text:
Once there was a little boy named Jack. He was only three years old and had lots of things he wanted to do. One day he saw something very special in the park - a big wheel! It was big and bright and looked very inviting.
Jack wanted to get on the wheel, so he ran to it. He put his hands on it and gently started turning it around. The wheel spun faster and he laughed as he felt the wind blowing in his face.
Once the wheel slowed down, Jack got off and looked around. He had such a great time on the wheel. He put his hands back on it and started turning it again, and soon he was spinning faster and faster. Every time he got off the wheel, he would put his hands back on it and start turning it again!
The more Jack put his hands on this big, bright wheel and gently turned it, the more he loved it. It was so much fun and he would happily keep turning it every day after that.

One day, Tom and Sue went to the park. They wanted to play a game. Tom said, "Let's tie our shoes and race to the big tree!" Sue said, "Ok, let's go!"
They tied their shoes and ran as fast as they could. Tom got to the tree first. He saw a hidden box. He called Sue, "Look, a hidden box! Let's open it!"
When they opened the box, they found lots of jam. They were surprised! They took the jam and shared it with their friends at the park. Everyone was happy and had a great time.

Lily loved her panda toy very much. She hugged it every night and played with it every day. She named it Pandy and gave it a red bow. Pandy was her best friend.
One day, Lily's mom said they had to move to a new house. She said they had to sell some of their things to get money. She said Lily had to sell some of her toys too. Lily was very sad. She did not want to sell Pandy.
She hid Pandy under her bed and hoped her mom would not find it. But her mom did. She took Pandy out and put it in a big box with other toys. She said she was sorry, but they had to sell Pandy. Lily cried and begged her mom to let her keep Pandy. But her mom said no.
The next day, a man came to buy the toys. He saw Pandy in the box and liked it. He said he would give five dollars for it. Lily's mom said yes and gave him Pandy. Lily ran after him and screamed, "No, no, give me back Pandy!"
But the man did not hear her. He got in his car and drove away. Lily fell on the ground and sobbed. She had lost her best friend. She wished she could see Pandy again.
But she never did. The man who bought Pandy did not care for it. He threw it in the trash. Pandy was dirty and torn. He had no bow and no friend. He was dead.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a kind girl named Lily. One day, she went for a walk in the fog. The fog was very thick, so she could not see very far.
As Lily walked, she heard a small voice. "Help!" said the voice. Lily looked around and saw a tiny bird. The bird was stuck in a cage. "Please help me unlock the cage," the bird asked.
Lily wanted to help the bird. She found a small key and used it to unlock the cage. The bird was very happy. "Thank you, kind girl!" said the bird. They became good friends and played together in the fog.

Once upon a time, there was a little yellow duck. The duck lived in a bathroom. The bathroom was very big for the duck. The duck liked to play in the water.
One day, the duck saw a toy boat. The boat was stuck in the water. The duck wanted to help the boat. The duck said, "I will help you, boat!" The duck tried to push the boat, but it was a struggle. The duck pushed and pushed, but the boat did not move.
Then, the duck had an idea. The duck asked a friend, a little green frog, to help. The frog lived in the bathroom too. The frog said, "Yes, I will help you, duck!" The duck and the frog pushed the boat together. The boat moved and was not stuck anymore. The duck and the frog were happy. They played with the boat all day.

One day, a famous cat named Tom went to the park. He saw a little girl named Sue. Sue had a pretty cup with her. Tom wanted to show Sue a fun game with the cup.
Tom said to Sue, "Hi Sue! Can I show you a fun game with your cup?" Sue smiled and said, "Yes, Tom! I like fun games." Tom was happy to show Sue the game. He knew she would love it.
Tom put the cup on the ground and hid a small ball under it. He moved the cup around and asked Sue to find the ball. Sue found the ball and laughed. She loved the game Tom showed her. They played the game all day and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a nice robot named Robby. Robby lived in a small house near a big pond. Every day, he went to the pond to play with his friends, the ducks.
One sunny day, Robby and the ducks were playing near the water. They took turns to splash in the pond. "Splash, splash!" said Robby. "Quack, quack!" said the ducks. They all laughed and had fun together.
A little duck named Ducky was scared to splash in the water. Robby saw this and went to help. "Don't be scared, Ducky. I will help you," said Robby. He held Ducky's wing and they splashed together. Ducky was happy and thanked Robby.
The other ducks clapped and cheered for Ducky and Robby. From that day on, Ducky was not scared to splash in the water anymore. The moral of the story is to always help your friends when they are scared or need help.

Once upon a time, there was a child named Tim. Tim had a big, empty box. He wanted to make something fun with it. So, he thought and thought about what to make.
One day, Tim's friend, Sue, came over to play. "What are you doing, Tim?" Sue asked. "I want to make something fun with this empty box," Tim said. "Let's make a fort!" Sue said with a big smile. Tim liked the idea.
Tim and Sue worked together to make the fort. They put the empty box on the floor and put a big blanket over it. They crawled inside and laughed. "This is the best fort ever!" Tim said. Sue agreed. They played in their fort all day long, happy with the fun they made.

Once upon a time, there was a clever dog named Spot. Spot had a big tooth. One day, Spot went for a walk in the park. He saw many trees, flowers, and birds. The sun was shining, and it was a beautiful day.
As Spot walked, he noticed a little girl crying by a tree. The girl had lost her ball. Spot wanted to help the girl. He looked around and saw the ball stuck high up in the tree.
Spot used his big tooth to grab the ball. He jumped and pulled the ball down from the tree. The little girl was very happy. She hugged Spot and said, "Thank you, clever dog!" Spot wagged his tail and smiled. He was happy to help his new friend. And from that day on, Spot and the little girl played together in the park every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play with her ball. One day, she played with her ball in the park. She would throw it high and try to catch it. But sometimes, she would miss the ball and it would fall on the dry grass.
Sue had a friend named Tom. Tom liked to play with Sue and her ball. They would take turns throwing the ball and saying a fun word when they catch it. Sue would say "happy" and Tom would say "jump". They laughed and played all day.
One day, Tom was not at the park. Sue felt sad and missed her friend. She played with her ball alone, but it was not as fun without Tom. The next day, Tom came back to the park. Sue was so happy to see him. They played with the ball and said their fun words again. Sue did not miss Tom anymore, and they played together on the dry grass every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She had a birdcage with a pretty bird inside. One day, it was very icy outside. Amy wanted to cook a warm meal for her family. She asked her mom, "Can I help cook?"
Her mom said, "Yes, but be careful near the stove." Amy was so happy to help. She cooked and cooked, but she forgot about her pretty bird in the birdcage.
As Amy cooked, the birdcage got too hot. The pretty bird inside the birdcage got very hot too. The bird tried to fly away, but it could not. The birdcage door was closed.
Amy smelled something bad. She saw the birdcage near the stove. She cried and cried. The pretty bird was gone. Amy was very sad. She learned to be more careful when she cooked.

Once upon a time, there was a big, hairy dog named Bobo. Bobo lived in a small house with his friend, a little girl named Lily. One day, Bobo and Lily found a shiny gold rock in their backyard. The gold rock had a magic glow. They were very happy to find the rock.
Bobo said to Lily, "Let's hide the gold rock so no one can take it." Lily agreed, and they hid the rock under Bobo's bed. But the gold rock's glow was so bright that it shone through the night. People saw the light and came to their house, asking about the glow.
Lily and Bobo decided to share the gold rock with everyone. People came from all around to see the glow. Everyone was happy and became friends. Lily said, "Sharing makes everyone happy!" And from that day on, Lily and Bobo always shared their things with others. The moral of the story is that sharing brings happiness to everyone.

Once upon a time there was a tomato. The tomato was ready to rot. One day it said hello to its friends, a carrot and a cabbage.
The tomato said to its friends, "Let's start an adventure. It'll be so fun!"
The carrot said, "That sounds funny. But where will we go?"
The tomato thought for a moment, then said, "Let's go to the beach!"
So, the three friends started their adventure. They raced over the sand, jumping and dancing. They felt so happy and free.
Soon, the sun began to set and the three friends knew it was time to go home. They hugged goodbye, and the tomato said, "Let's come back soon and start a new adventure!" 
The carrot and the cabbage agreed and they all waved goodbye until they were out of sight. The tomato knew that it had one more adventure before it was ready to rot.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to have a party. He was very excited. He asked his mom if he could invite his friends. His mom said yes, it would be easy to have a party.
Tim and his mom went to the store to buy things for the party. They bought hats, balloons, and a big cake. When they got home, they had to unpack the bags. Tim found a big box inside one of the bags. He did not know what was in the box.
At the party, Tim and his friends played games. They laughed and had fun. But then, Tim's little sister, Sue, wanted to open the big box. Tim said no, but Sue did not listen. She opened the box and lots of little balls came out. The balls went everywhere!
Tim was mad at Sue, but then he saw that his friends were having fun with the balls. They played a new game with the balls. The party was still fun, and Tim was not mad anymore. In the end, everyone had a great time at the party.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Mark. Mark lived in a house near a big park. He liked to play in the park with his friends.
One day, Mark saw a wild bird in the park. The bird was very pretty with many colors. Mark wanted to watch the bird fly and play. So, he sat on the grass and looked at the bird.
The wild bird saw Mark watching and came closer. Mark was very happy. He and the bird played together in the park all day. The bird became Mark's new friend. They had lots of fun together.

One day, a little boy named Tim looked up at the sky. He saw the big, bright moon. He had a question. "Why is the moon so big?" he asked his mom. His mom smiled and said, "The moon is not always big. It changes."
The next day, Tim saw the moon again. It was not as big as before. He found it difficult to understand. He asked his mom, "Why did the moon change?" His mom told him, "The moon has different shapes. It will grow big and then small again."
On the third day, Tim watched the moon. It was very small now. He remembered what his mom said. The moon would grow big and then small again. He was happy he knew the answer to his question. Tim smiled and said, "I understand the moon now, Mom!"

One day, Sam's parents mentioned a parade. Sam jumped up and down with excitement. He had never seen a parade before and it sounded interesting. 
On the day of the parade, Sam's family said goodbye and headed downtown. The buildings were tall, but Sam only had eyes for the parade. 
Sam saw lots of colorful and interesting things. There were clowns, jugglers, and musicians. The floats were decorated with balloons and flowers. Everyone was happy and waving. Sam noticed that even the animals were part of the parade. 
At the end of the parade, Sam was sad, but happy he had been able to see all the interesting things. He knew he would never forget his first parade.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in her clean, green garden. One day, she found a maze in the garden. Lily was very happy.
Lily went into the maze. She walked and walked. She saw pretty flowers and little bugs. She kept walking and found a big, clean pond. Lily loved the pond very much.
Then, Lily found her way out of the maze. She went home and told her mommy and daddy about the maze and the pond. They all went back to the maze to play together. They had lots of fun and Lily felt so much love.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved to help her mom in the kitchen. One day, Lucy's mom gave her a pretty apron to wear. Lucy was very happy and put on the apron right away.
Lucy and her mom were going to make a cake. Lucy's mom said, "Lucy, you can spray the pan with oil so the cake won't stick." Lucy was excited to help. She picked up the spray bottle and sprayed the pan. It was easy and fun!
When the cake was done, Lucy and her mom shared it with the family. Everyone loved the cake and said, "Thank you, Lucy, for helping!" Lucy felt proud and happy. The moral of the story is that helping others can be easy and fun, and it makes everyone feel good.

Once upon a time, there was a big tree in the forest. It had big branches and lots of leaves.
One day, a woodworker had a great idea. He took out his saw and started to work. He worked hard, cutting down the tree. He was going to make a beautiful bench.
But while he was sawing, the tree made a loud noise. Suddenly, some little birds flew out of the tree.
The woodworker was sorry he spoiled the birds' home. He wanted to make them a new one. So he worked even harder on the bench.
When he finished, the woodworker proudly stepped back to admire his work. He had created the perfect new home for the birds. They all flew in and found a safe and cozy place to stay.

Lily wanted to play with her friend Max, who lived next door. She asked her mom if she could go to his house. Her mom looked at the calendar on the wall and said, "Not today, Lily. Max is not available. He has a doctor's appointment."
Lily felt sad and said, "But I want to play with Max. He is my best friend. Can I go tomorrow?"
Her mom replied, "Maybe, if he is feeling better. But you have to wait and see. Why don't you play with your toys or read a book?"
Lily did not want to play with her toys or read a book. She wanted to play with Max. She decided to sneak out of the house and go to his house anyway. She put on her shoes and coat and opened the door.
But as soon as she stepped outside, she saw a big dog running towards her. The dog was angry and barked loudly. Lily was scared and tried to run back inside, but the dog was faster. The dog bit her leg and made her fall down. Lily screamed and cried. Her mom heard her and came outside. She saw the dog and Lily and shouted for help. She picked up Lily and took her inside. She called the doctor and said, "My daughter has been bitten by a dog. She needs help. Please come quickly." She hugged Lily and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I should have watched you better. You should have listened to me. Now you are hurt and in pain."
Lily felt sorry too. She wished she had listened to her mom. She wished she had not gone to Max's house. She wished the dog had not bitten her. She wished she could play with Max. But she could not. She had to go to the doctor and get a shot. She had to stay in bed and rest. She had to wait for her leg to heal. She had a bad day. She learned a hard lesson.

One day, a big bear and a small rabbit were friends. They wanted to play. The big bear said, "Let's go to the icy land to play!" The small rabbit was scared. He did not like the cold.
They went to the icy land. The big bear was happy. The small rabbit was cold and sad. The big bear saw the small rabbit was not happy. He said, "What can we do to make you happy?"
The small rabbit thought for a moment. He said, "I have an idea! Let's suggest a new game to play. We can play in the warm sun!" The big bear agreed. They went back to the warm sun and played all day. The small rabbit was happy again.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was an ancient well. The well had been there for many, many years. It would provide water for all the people and animals in the village.
One day, a little boy named Tim went to the well. He wanted to get some water for his family. When he reached the well, he saw an old man standing there. The old man said, "Hello, Tim. Be careful with the water you take from this well."
Tim didn't listen to the old man. He thought he knew better. He took a big bucket and filled it with water from the well. He carried the heavy bucket back to his house.
When Tim got home, he gave the water to his family. They all drank the water, but soon, they all started to feel sick. The ancient well's water had made them ill. Tim realized he should have listened to the old man's warning.

One day, a little girl named Amy found a camera. She wanted to take a picture of her cat. Amy did not know how to use the camera, but she wanted to try.
Amy tried to take the picture, but the camera did not work. She felt sorry for her cat because it was waiting for the picture. Amy asked her mom for help.
Her mom showed Amy how to use the camera. They took a picture of the cat together. Amy was happy and said "thank you" to her mom. The cat was happy too.

Mindy was a little girl who liked to draw. Every day she would take out her journal and draw something new in it. She was so proud of the things she created, but her brother Paul didn't always appreciate her work.
One day, Mindy was drawing in her journal when Paul came over and tried to take it away from her. "Give it to me!" he said. Mindy held on as tight as she could but Paul was too strong. He grabbed the journal that Mindy was so proud of and started to destroy it.
Mindy was very angry and tried to grab it back. Even though she tried very hard, Paul was too fast. After a few moments, the journal was in pieces on the floor. Mindy started to cry.
Paul felt bad and tried to apologize. "I'm sorry Mindy, I didn't mean to be so slow." he said. Mindy looked at Paul and told him that he didn't need to be sorry. She then hugged him and told him that it was OK. 
The two of them worked together to put the journal back together, and Mindy was once again able to draw her beautiful pictures. From that day on, Paul made sure to be extra careful when he was around Mindy's journal.

Once upon a time, there was a modest plane named Pete. Pete was not big, but he was very fast. Pete loved to fly in the sky and play with his friends.
One day, Pete was flying high in the sky when he saw a big ball. He wanted to play catch with the ball. So, he flew down to the ground and asked the ball, "Do you want to play catch with me?" The ball said, "Yes, I would like to play catch with you, Pete!"
Pete and the ball started to play catch. They were having so much fun. But then, the ball flew too high and too fast. Pete tried to catch the ball, but he could not. Pete went too high and too fast, too. He could not fly anymore and fell to the ground. Pete was very sad because he could not fly and play with his friends anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to dream. Every night, she would dream a new story. Her dreams were full of fun and happy things.
One day, Lily told her mom about her dreams. "Mom, I had a dream about a big, heavy ball. We played with it, and it was so much fun!" Her mom smiled and said, "That sounds like a wonderful dream, Lily."
The next day, Lily and her mom went to the park. They saw a big, heavy ball, just like in Lily's dream. They played with the ball all day, laughing and having fun. And from that day on, Lily's dreams became even more magical.

Once upon a time, there was a unique kid named Tim. He had a special power. He could disappear when he wanted to. Tim loved to play hide and seek with his friends.
One day, Tim and his friends were playing in the park. They decided to play hide and seek. Tim thought it would be fun to disappear and hide. So, he used his power and disappeared.
Tim's friends looked and looked for him, but they could not find him. They started to feel sad. They missed their friend Tim. Tim did not come back, and his friends never saw him again.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max loved to jog in the park with his friend, a little boy named Tim. They would jog and play all day long.
One day, while they were jogging, a stupid cat jumped out from behind a tree. The cat said, "You can't catch me!" Max and Tim laughed and started to chase the cat. The cat ran and ran, but Max and Tim were fast, too.
Finally, they caught the cat. The cat said, "Please don't hurt me!" Max looked at Tim and said, "We must protect the cat, not hurt it." So, they let the cat go, and they all became friends. From that day on, they would all jog and play together, and Max would always protect his friends.

One day, a child named Sam went to the park with his mom. He liked to play on the swings and the slide. He saw a big dog near the fence. He wanted to pet the dog, so he ran to it.
But the dog was not friendly. It barked and growled at Sam. It showed its teeth and jumped on him. Sam was scared and hurt. He felt helpless. He cried and shouted, "Mom, help me!"
His mom heard him and ran to him. She pushed the dog away and hugged Sam. She said, "It's okay, Sam. I'm here. You're safe." She checked his arms and legs for bites. She said, "We need to go to the doctor. The dog might have made you sick."
Sam nodded and held his mom's hand. He was still shaking and crying. He said, "I'm sorry, mom. I just wanted to pet the dog. I didn't know it was bad."
His mom said, "It's not your fault, Sam. Some dogs are not nice. You have to be careful and ask before you touch them. I love you, Sam. You're brave."
She took him to the car and drove to the doctor. The doctor gave him a shot and a band-aid. He said, "You'll be fine, Sam. Just keep the wound clean and take this medicine. And don't forget to hug your mom. She saved you."
Sam smiled and hugged his mom. He said, "Thank you, mom. You're the best." He learned a lesson that day. He never ran to a strange dog again. He always asked and listened to his mom. He was happy and safe.

Sara and Tom are playing in the park. They like to run and jump and slide. Sara has a net. She wants to catch some bugs. Tom has a ball. He wants to kick it and throw it.
"Let's play with my net," Sara says. "We can find some ants and bees and butterflies."
"No, let's play with my ball," Tom says. "We can score some goals and make some noise."
They do not agree. They start to argue. They pull and tug at each other's toys.
"Stop it, stop it!" a voice says. It is Mom. She is watching them from a bench. She is not happy. She walks over to them.
"You two need to share and take turns," Mom says. "Or else you will have to go home."
Sara and Tom do not want to go home. They want to play in the park. They look at each other. They nod.
"OK, Mom," they say. "We will share and take turns."
Mom smiles. She gives them a hug. She orders them to be nice and have fun.
Sara and Tom play with the net and the ball. They take turns. They find some bugs and score some goals. They laugh and cheer. They have fun.
But then, something happens. A big dog runs into the park. It sees the ball. It wants to play. It chases the ball. It grabs the ball. It runs away with the ball.
Sara and Tom are shocked. They are sad. They want their ball back.
"Hey, that's our ball!" they shout. They run after the dog.
Mom sees them. She is worried. She calls them to come back.
"Wait, wait!" she says. "Don't chase the dog. It might be dangerous."
But Sara and Tom do not hear her. They are too far away. They only see the ball.
They catch up with the dog. They try to get the ball. But the dog is strong. It does not let go. It growls and barks.
Sara and Tom are scared. They back away. They wish they had listened to Mom.
But then, Sara has an idea. She remembers her net. She still has it in her hand. She thinks of a plan.
She whispers to Tom. She tells him her plan. He nods. He likes her plan.
They act fast. They work together. Sara throws the net over the dog. Tom grabs the ball. They run away with the ball.
The dog is confused. It is trapped. It cannot see. It cannot run. It whines and wails.
Sara and Tom are happy. They have their ball back. They hug each other. They thank each other.
They run back to Mom. They show her the ball. They tell her what happened.
Mom is relieved. She is proud. She is glad they are safe.
She hugs them. She praises them. She orders them to be careful.
She also orders them to clean the ball. It is dirty and wet from the dog's mouth.
Sara and Tom agree. They clean the ball. They make it shiny and bright.
They play with the ball again. They also play with the net. They share and take turns. They have fun.
They are happy. Mom is happy. The dog is not happy. But it is free. Someone comes and takes the net off. It runs away. It looks for another ball.

Martin was walking in the park one day. He noticed everything seemed very peaceful and smooth. But as he walked, he noticed something strange. The ground seemed to be trembling a bit.
He told his mom about it, but she said it was nothing to worry about. So, Martin kept on walking.
Soon, he heard a loud noise and he felt like he was sinking. He was scared, but he kept walking until he saw a pond that looked so beautiful, yet it was trembling and shuddering.
Martin remembered suddenly what his mom had said earlier. He realized that the trembling he had felt earlier was a sign of the upcoming storm. He watched in awe as powerful waves moved across the smooth surface of the pond.
Martin used this experience to remember to respect and cherish nature for its power and beauty. He also learned to pay attention to warning signs in order to stay safe.

Once upon a time, there was a poor dolphin that lived on the beach. He loved to play in the sand and swim in the warm ocean. Whenever it got too warm, he would jump out of the water to cool off.
One day, the dolphin heard a loud noise coming from the sky. He looked up to see what was happening and saw a chunk of a ship falling from the sky. It landed in the ocean with a big splash. The dolphin was curious and swam towards the ship.
As he got closer he could see that it was welcoming him. The ship had a friendly face painted on its side. It had big welcoming arms that kept the dolphin safe. The dolphin was excited and hopped onto the boat.
He found a group of other dolphins who were just as poor as he was. They were all excited to welcome the poor dolphin to their new home. Together they all made a happy family and paddled away, off to explore the world.

Once upon a time, there was an enormous farm. On this farm, lived a special cow. This cow loved to lead her calf around, and everywhere she went, magical prints were left behind. Everywhere the cow and calf went, all the other animals could follow.
One day the cow and her calf walked around the farm and came upon an enormous lake. They noticed the magical prints from the cow leading around the lake. Seeing the prints made them feel excited and adventurous. 
The cow and her calf decided to follow the prints until it lead them to a huge cliff. At the cliff, the team could see how enormous the world was! All of the farm animals felt amazed and inspired witnessing the huge view. 
From that day on, the cow and her calf have lead all the other animals around the farm and have taught them the power of exploration.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play on the beach. One day, he went to the beach with his mom and dad. He saw many pretty shells on the sand. He wanted to catch them all.
Tim ran around the beach to catch the shells. He saw a big, naughty crab near a big shell. The crab was very fast. Tim wanted the big shell, but he was scared of the crab.
Tim's dad saw him and came to help. He caught the naughty crab and moved it away. Tim was happy and picked up the big shell. He thanked his dad and they all went back home with a big smile.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a weird tool in his toy box. He did not know what it was for. He showed it to his friend, Sue. Sue looked at it and said, "I don't know what it is, but it looks funny!"
Tim and Sue went to ask their friend, Dan, if he knew what the weird tool was for. Dan looked at it and laughed. "That's not a tool, it's a toy!" he said. Tim and Sue were confused. They did not know how to play with the toy.
Dan showed them how to use the toy. They all took turns playing with it and laughing. They had so much fun, they forgot about their fight. They were happy to have a new toy to play with and share.

Once upon a time there was a small, furry animal called Splitty. Splitty was hairy, and loved to explore the world. On this particular day, Splitty had come across a place called the Forest of Touch.
"What's this place?" asked Splitty. The forest was filled with strange plants and animals. 
Splitty was curious and ventured further in. Suddenly, Splitty felt something on his back. "Whoa!" Splitty jumped. When he looked, there was a big, hairy creature standing behind him. It was a giant green monster! 
The monster roared, and Splitty quickly ran away. He ran and ran, until he discovered a big tree. Splitty had an idea. He quickly split the tree in two. The vibrations from the tree shook the big monster, who fell down and couldn't get up. 
Splitty was safe! He rubbed his back and thanked the tree, before heading home.

One day, a tall girl named Lily went to yoga class. She liked yoga because it made her feel happy and strong. In the class, there were many kids who wanted to learn yoga too.
The teacher said, "Today, we will learn to balance on one foot." All the kids tried to balance, but it was hard. Some kids fell down and started to laugh.
Lily tried to balance too. She felt wobbly at first, but she kept trying. Finally, she could balance on one foot! She was so happy and said to her friend, "Look, I did it!" Her friend tried again and soon, she could balance too. They both smiled and had fun at yoga class.

Once upon a time, there was a little mouse named Tim. Tim was very adventurous. He liked to go to the park and play with his friends. One day, Tim heard about a big cheese that was in the park. He wanted to find it and share it with his friends.
Tim went to the park and started to look for the cheese. He looked under the slide, behind the swings, and in the sandbox. He asked his friends if they had seen the big cheese, but no one knew where it was. Tim did not give up. He was determined to find the cheese.
As Tim kept searching, he saw a big cat near the trees. The cat had the big cheese! Tim wanted to be brave and get the cheese for his friends, but the cat was very scary. Tim tried to sneak up, but the cat saw him. The cat chased Tim and caught him. Tim did not find the cheese and could not play with his friends anymore. The cat was happy, but Tim and his friends were sad.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Grace. She loved to travel. Every day, she would explore the world around her. One day, she decided to take a trip to the park.
She was very excited and put on her special sock. It made her happy. She ran out the door and began to explore.
At the park, she felt the wet grass beneath her feet. She loved the feeling of the dampness against her sock. She wanted to explore more, so she continued to travel around the park.
Suddenly, she heard a voice calling out to her. It was her mom! "Grace! Come here! You are getting your sock wet!"
Grace was surprised. She had not noticed that her sock was getting damp. She quickly ran back to her mom and went home.
The End.

Once upon a time, there was a polite lawyer named Tom. He liked to help people learn new things. One day, he met a little girl named Sue who was sad.
"Why are you sad?" asked Tom. Sue said, "I don't know how to say 'please' and 'thank you'. My friends say I am not nice." Tom smiled and said, "I can help you learn."
Tom taught Sue how to be polite. They practiced saying "please" and "thank you" together. Soon, Sue was very good at it. Her friends were happy and liked her more.
One day, Sue's friend Tim was sad. Sue asked, "Why are you sad?" Tim said, "I don't know how to share my toys." Sue smiled and said, "I can help you learn." She taught Tim how to share, just like Tom taught her.
The moral of the story is that when we learn good things, we can help others learn too.

Lena and Max were twins who liked to observe things. They had a big book with pictures of animals and plants. They learned their names and sounds. One day, they saw a new picture of a big cat with spots. It was called a leopard.
"Wow, look at the leopard!" Max said. "It has strong teeth and claws. It can run fast and climb trees. It is a hunter."
"I like the leopard too," Lena said. "But it looks sad. Why is its lip bleeding?"
Max looked closer and saw a red mark on the leopard's lip. He remembered something from the book. He said, "Maybe it fought with another leopard. The book says they are very territorial. That means they don't like to share their space."
Lena nodded. She felt sorry for the leopard. She said, "Maybe we can help it. We can give it some water and a band-aid. Then it will feel better."
Max agreed. He said, "Let's go and find it. Maybe it is in the zoo or the park. Come on, Lena, let's go!"
They put on their hats and shoes and ran outside. They did not see their mom, who was busy in the kitchen. She did not hear them leave. She did not know they were looking for a leopard. She did not know they were in trouble.

Once upon a time, there was a big white cat named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play outside. One day, Fluffy went to the west to find a new friend.
In the west, Fluffy met a small brown dog named Spot. Spot liked to stir in the dirt with his paws. Fluffy asked, "Can I play with you?" Spot said, "Yes, let's play together!"
Fluffy and Spot played in the dirt and stirred it up. They laughed and had lots of fun. They became best friends and played together every day. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a brave knight named Sir Peter. He was tall and strong and he had a bald head. Every day he would go on a new adventure and his most favourite adventure was looking for a magical sword. 
One morning, Sir Peter was ready to start his quest. He searched high and low until he reached a dark cave. Inside, he found the sword and started to pick it up. Suddenly, the ground started to rumble and a giant appeared before him.
The giant said, "If you wants the sword, you will have to fight me!"
Sir Peter looked up bravely and said, "I accept your challenge!"
The two battled for hours until finally Sir Peter struck true and the giant disappeared. He took his sword and started to go home proud of his victory.
The end.

"Mommy, why do you have to go to work?" asked the little 3-year old girl. 
"It's very important for me to go to work. I gain lots of things from working like money to buy the things we need and knowledge to help us understand the world better," Mommy said.
The little girl was not satisfied. "But why can't someone else do the work?"
"It's my job and I'm the one who is responsible," Mommy said, her voice calm and steady. 
"But wouldn't it be better if you stayed home with me mommy?"
"Yes, it would. But I also need to gain my own goals and dreams. Work helps me do that," Mommy said. 
The little girl thought about this and finally understood. "Ok, mommy. Go to work and come back soon."
"I will, I promise," Mommy said and gave the little girl a warm hug.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park. Lily felt very lucky because it was a sunny day. She saw a big pond with ducks. Lily wanted to play with the ducks, so she went close to the pond.
Lily saw a duck with dirty wings. She said, "Hi, duck! You need a wash. Can I help you?" The duck said, "Yes, please!" Lily was very happy to help. She took water from the pond and washed the duck's wings.
After the wash, the duck's wings were clean and shiny. The duck said, "Thank you, Lily! I feel lucky to have a friend like you." Lily smiled and said, "You're welcome, duck! I am lucky to have a friend like you too!" They played together in the park, and Lily and the duck became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a pair of shoes that he loved very much. But one day, the heel of his shoe broke. Tim was sad and frightened. He did not know what to do.
Tim went to his mom and said, "Mommy, my shoe is broken. Can you help me?" His mom looked at the shoe and said, "Don't worry, Tim. I can repair it for you."
Mom took the shoe and fixed the heel. Tim was so happy to see his shoe all better. He put it on and said, "Thank you, Mommy! Now I can play again."
From that day on, Tim was not frightened when something broke. He knew that with a little help, anything could be fixed. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was an honest dog named Bob. Bob loved to eat toast for breakfast. Every day, he would ask his mom for toast with butter and jam.
One day, Bob heard a noise. He paused and listened. The noise was coming from the kitchen. He thought, "Maybe it's a new friend!" So, he went to see who it was.
When he got to the kitchen, he saw a cat eating his toast! Bob was surprised. He had never seen a cat eat toast before. The cat looked up and said, "Hi, I'm Cat. Can we share the toast?" Bob smiled and said, "Yes, we can share." From that day on, Bob and Cat became best friends who loved to eat toast together.

Once upon a time, there was a very adventurous cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to talk to her friends. Her friends were a dog, a bird, and a fish. They played with their toys and had lots of fun.
One day, Kitty and her friends found some new stuff. It was a big box. They wanted to see what was inside. They opened the box and found a big, colorful ball. They were so excited and started to play with the ball.
As they played, something unexpected happened. The ball started to talk! It was a magic ball that could talk and play games. The ball became their new friend. Kitty and her friends had even more fun with the talking ball. They played together every day and had many more adventures.

Once upon a time, there was a pale boy named Tim. Tim loved to perform in his favorite sport, running. One sunny day, Tim went to the park to run with his friends.
At the park, Tim saw a new boy, Sam. Sam was sitting alone, looking sad. Tim went to Sam and asked, "Do you want to play with us?" Sam looked up and said, "I want to, but I am not good at any sport." Tim smiled and said, "That's okay! We can help you learn."
Tim and his friends helped Sam learn how to run and perform better in the sport. Soon, Sam was having fun and running with the others. They all became good friends.
The moral of the story is to always be kind and help others, even if they are not good at something. Everyone can learn and have fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a graceful jungle. It had tall trees and lots of lovely animals that lived happily there. In the middle of the jungle, there was a big fire. It burned and burned and burned.
The fire was so hot that it scared all the animals. They ran away and couldn't stop. The monkeys jumped from branches, the zebras galloped in all directions, and the birds flew high in the sky.
But then something amazing happened. A graceful elephant came up to the fire with a big, long trunk. It started to blow on the fire and the flames went lower and lower until the fire was totally out. And the animals all came back to the jungle and were happy again.

One day, a little cat went out to play. The cat saw a big, attractive tree. The tree had many leaves that could cover the cat. The cat thought it was a good place to hide.
The cat climbed up the tree and saw a bird. The bird had a snack in its mouth. The cat said, "Hi, bird! What is that snack for?" The bird replied, "This snack is for my babies. They are hungry."
The cat felt happy for the bird and its babies. They played together in the tree, with the leaves covering them. They had a great time and became good friends.

One day, a boy named Tim was playing in the mud. He got his legs very dirty. He liked to jump and splash in the puddles. His mom saw him and said, "Tim, you have dirty legs! Let's go wash them."
Tim and his mom went inside to wash his legs. His mom said, "Tim, remember to always clean your legs after playing in the mud." Tim nodded and said, "Yes, mom. I will."
The next day, Tim played with his friend, Sam. Sam had dirty legs too. Tim said, "Sam, let's go wash our legs. My mom says we should always clean them after playing in the mud." Sam agreed, and they both went to wash their legs.
The moral of the story is to always listen to your parents and keep yourself clean.

Anna liked to play with her mom's makeup. She wanted to look like a princess. She found a big box of makeup in her mom's room. She opened it and saw many colors and brushes. She was very happy.
She took some makeup and put it on her face. She used red for her lips, blue for her eyes, and pink for her cheeks. She looked in the mirror and smiled. She thought she was very impressive. She wanted to show her mom.
She ran to the living room, where her mom was reading a book. She said, "Mom, look at me! I am a princess!"
Her mom looked at her and was very surprised. She saw the makeup all over Anna's face. She said, "Oh, Anna, what have you done? You used my makeup without asking me. That is not nice. You made a big mess."
Anna felt sad. She did not want to make her mom angry. She said, "I'm sorry, mom. I just wanted to look like you. You are always so impressive. Can you forgive me?"
Her mom hugged her and said, "Of course, I forgive you, Anna. You are my sweet princess. But you don't need makeup to be impressive. You are impressive just the way you are. Now, let's go and restore your face. We need to wash off the makeup and use some cream. Then, we can play together. How does that sound?"
Anna nodded and said, "That sounds good, mom. I love you."
Her mom said, "I love you too, Anna." They went to the bathroom and restored Anna's face. Then, they played with some dolls and had fun. Anna learned to ask before using her mom's makeup. She also learned that she was impressive without makeup. She was happy.

Luke had a huge chess board. He had owned it for a long time and he loved to play with it. He had lots of little pieces that moved around on the board. He put the pieces in different places to make the shapes of animals and people. Every day, Luke and his mom played chess together. His mom was very good at it and would show Luke the different pieces and how they moved.
One day, Luke and his mom spotted a huge, giant chess set in the park. They had never seen a chess set that big before. They were so excited and had to go take a closer look. When they got to the chess set, they saw that every piece was made from wood and were much bigger than the pieces Luke had at home. Luke and his mom played the game together and Luke couldn't believe how much fun it was.
Luke asked his mom if they could get a huge chess set just like the one he saw in the park. His mom said that she would look for one and, the next day, she gave Luke the best present ever - a huge wooden chess set. He was so excited and spent hours playing the game with his mom. Now Luke owned his own, huge chess set that he could play with every day.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a big, heavy box. He wanted to examine it. It had a lock on it. Tim thought it must be a safe. He felt guilty for wanting to open it, but he was very curious.
Tim asked his friend, Sam, to help him open the safe. They tried and tried, but it would not open. They were sad, but then they had an idea. They would ask the wise old owl who lived in the tree.
The wise old owl told them a secret word to open the safe. Tim and Sam went back to the safe and said the secret word. The safe opened! But, something unexpected happened. Instead of finding treasure, they found a note.
The note said, "You are now the keepers of the safe. Use it to keep your things safe and help others." Tim and Sam were happy. They learned that it is good to help others and keep things safe.

Once upon a time, there was a slow snail named Sam. Sam loved to eat prunes. One day, Sam found a big prune under a tree. He was very happy and started to eat it.
Suddenly, a bird came down from the tree. The bird saw the prune and wanted to eat it too. The bird was fast, but Sam did not want to share his prune. Sam thought of a plan to keep the prune for himself.
Sam told the bird, "I will race you for the prune. If you win, you can have it." The bird laughed and agreed to the race. They started to race, but Sam knew he could not win. So, he quickly hid the prune under a leaf. The bird did not see him do it.
The bird won the race and looked for the prune. But the prune was gone! The bird felt regret because he did not watch Sam closely. Sam smiled and went back to the leaf where he hid the prune. He enjoyed the rest of the prune all by himself.

Once there was a pear tree. The tree was in a sunny garden and every spring it would bloom. And when it bloomed, it was so beautiful that the birds stopped singing to watch.
One day, a deaf little girl came to the garden. She saw the pear tree blooming and was so happy she clapped her hands. The pear tree was so happy that it bloomed even more, making the whole garden brighter.
The little girl reached up and picked a pear from the tree and ate it. She smiled and the tree bloomed even more. The girl thanked the tree and promised to come back whenever the pear tree bloomed. 
And from then on, whenever the little girl came to the garden, the pear tree would bloom so beautifully and the birds would stop singing again.

Once there was a little girl. She was frightened of the dark but she was brave. She owned a port; a long and tall one made of wood. One night, when it was very dark, she opened her port and peeked inside. Suddenly, something startled her; a big and hairy spider. She was so frightened that she screamed and shut the port quickly.
The next morning she decided to take a look again and it was not what she expected. There was now a huge hole in her port. The spider had eaten away a hole at the bottom of the port and crawled inside. She was so frightened and sad. She tried to fix the port but it was impossible. It was ruined. 
The little girl was very sad because she had lost her beloved port. She was so frightened and upset she ran away into the bright sunshine and never returned.

Once there was a little pig who loved to lay in the sun. One day, he saw something very fancy. It was an olive! He had never seen such a fancy olive before.
He couldn't resist, so he hopped out of the sunshine and carefully crept up to the olive. He snatched it up with his snout and took it to his favourite spot in the field.
He lay there in the sun and enjoyed his special treat. He chomped away at the fancy olive and ate it up until it was gone.
Then the pig hopped back into the sunshine and lay there dreaming of all the fancy olives he could find. What a happy pig he was!

Once upon a time, there lived a boy called Bob. Every day, he imagined flying away in a big aeroplane. One day in the market, Bob was excited to find an aeroplane for sale! His mum said he could buy it for five cents. Bob was so happy he ran to the lady and said, "I sell five cents aeroplane please!"
The lady smiled and said, "Here you go!" Bob quickly held the small aeroplane and said thank you. But then he felt embarrassed because the lady was still looking. He couldn't find any money! He had forgotten his five cents at home. Bob was very embarrassed and wished he could go home.
Suddenly his mum came, with the five cents. Bob smiled and said, "I sell aeroplane, five cents please." The lady nodded and said, "Of course, take it home and fly away." Bob was so happy! He thanked the lady and ran home with his new aeroplane. He was excited to fly away in it and go to wonderful places.

One day, a little cricket named Tim went to play at the park. He saw a big slide and wanted to go down it. Tim was very excited to try the slide, but he also felt a little scared.
As Tim climbed up the slide, he saw a terrible bug. The bug was big and mean. Tim was very brave and said, "Go away, bug! I want to slide!" The terrible bug looked at Tim and then flew away. Tim was happy that the bug was gone.
Now, Tim was ready to slide. He sat at the top and took a deep breath. Then, he slid down the slide very fast. Tim laughed and had so much fun. He went up and down the slide many times. The little cricket had a great day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a farm. On this farm, there was a chubby pig. The pig loved to play in the mud all day. One day, the sun was shining very bright. The pig was happy and said, "I love the sun! It makes everything shine!"
A little bird flew by and heard the pig. The bird said, "Did you know that the moon can shine too? At night, it makes everything bright and pretty." The pig did not know this. He was very surprised. He wanted to see the moon shine.
That night, the pig stayed awake to see the moon. It was very late, but he did not want to miss it. Finally, the moon came out. It was big and round and made everything shine just like the bird said. The pig was so happy. He danced and played in the moonlight. From then on, the pig loved the sun and the moon, because they both made the farm shine.

Once upon a time there were two best friends, Mo and Zee. They lived close to each other and loved to play. 
One day, Mo and Zee decided to have a picnic by the lake. They gathered some food and blankets and took off for the lake.
Once they arrived to the lake, they both jumped for joy and spread out the blankets. Then Mo said to Zee, "Let's gather some flowers for our picnic."
So, they grabbed a basket and picked some beautiful flowers in the meadow. When their basket was full, Mo and Zee admired their handiwork. They smiled brightly and gathered up their things to go back home.
On the way, Mo noticed a bumblebee buzzing around a flower. He pointed to it with his finger and said, "Look Zee! A bee gathering nectar from the flower. It's so happy!"
Zee's eyes were wide with wonder. She reached out with her finger to touch the bee's lip and said, "It tickles!" 
Mo and Zee laughed and enjoyed the rest of the way home. They were happy to have shared such an amazing adventure together.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a playful dog named Buddy. Buddy loved to jump, run, and play all day long. He had a favorite chair where he would rest after a long day of fun.
One sunny day, Buddy saw a big bow on his chair. He didn't know what it was, so he got closer to see. As he touched the bow, it fell on the floor. Buddy was scared and ran away. He didn't know that his friend, Kitty, put the bow there as a gift for him.
Later, Kitty came to the house and saw the bow on the floor. She felt sad that Buddy didn't like her gift. Buddy saw Kitty's sad face and felt bad too. He decided to make Kitty happy again. Buddy picked up the bow, put it on his chair, and then bowed to Kitty. Kitty smiled and they both played together.
The moral of the story is to always be kind and say thank you for the gifts you receive, even if they are unexpected.

Ben and Mia like to draw with crayons. They have many colors and papers. One day, they want to draw a bird. They look at the window and see a blue bird on a tree.
"Look, Mia, a bird!" Ben says. "Let's draw it!"
"OK, Ben, I like birds!" Mia says. "You draw the body and I draw the wings!"
They start to draw the bird. Ben makes a big oval for the body. Mia makes two triangles for the wings. They use blue and yellow crayons.
"Done!" Ben says. "We complete the bird!"
But Mia is not happy. She looks at the bird and shakes her head.
"No, Ben, the bird is not complete!" Mia says. "It needs a beak and eyes and feet!"
"Oh, you are right, Mia!" Ben says. "Let's add them!"
They use orange and black crayons to make the beak, the eyes and the feet. They also make a branch for the bird to sit on.
"Now the bird is complete!" Mia says. "It looks like the real one!"
They show their drawing to Mom. Mom smiles and hugs them.
"Wow, what a beautiful bird!" Mom says. "You are very good at drawing!"
Ben and Mia feel happy. They put their drawing on the fridge with a magnet. They see the blue bird fly away from the window.
"Bye, bird!" Ben and Mia say. "Thank you for being our model!"

Once upon a time, there was a big red car. The car loved to travel. One day, the car met a blue truck. The truck had an unusual cable. The car wanted to know more about the cable.
The car asked the truck, "What is that unusual cable for?" The truck said, "I use the cable to help my friends when they are stuck."
One day, while they were traveling together, they saw a big tree in the road. The car and the truck did not know what to do. Then, the truck had an idea. The truck said, "I can use my unusual cable to move the tree!"
The truck used the unusual cable to move the big tree. The car and the truck were happy. They could travel again. They traveled together and had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. He liked to jog every day. Tim was an honest boy, and he always remembered to say "please" and "thank you."
One day, while Tim was jogging, he saw a big box. It was a surprise! Tim did not know what was inside the box. He remembered that his mom told him to be careful with things he did not know.
Tim decided to take the box home. When he opened it, he found a big red ball. It was so big that it could bounce very high! Tim played with the ball all day. But then, something unexpected happened. The ball started to talk! It said, "Thank you for playing with me, Tim. I was lonely in the box."
Tim was surprised, but he was happy to have a new friend. He remembered to always be honest and kind, and he and the talking ball had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a curious little girl named Amy. Amy liked to pick flowers and play with her dog, Max. One day, Amy and Max went for a walk in the park.
Amy saw a big tree with many leaves. She wanted to pick a leaf from the tree. As she tried to reach the leaf, she heard a voice. "Hello! Can you help me?" said the voice. Amy looked around and saw a squirrel wearing pants. "I need help to pick nuts," said the squirrel.
Amy was surprised but wanted to help the squirrel. She picked nuts for the squirrel and put them in the squirrel's pants. They all laughed and played together. And from that day, Amy, Max, and the squirrel with pants became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, in the middle of a meadow, there was a port. It was a peaceful place, and birds could be heard singing in the trees. One day, a graceful deer decided to invite a group of friendly animals to come and have a picnic at the port. 
The deer went around to every animal he knew and extended the invitation. The animals were overjoyed, and immediately started making their way to the port.
When they arrived, they all saw the beauty of the port. The trees had bright green leaves, and the birds were chirping. Everyone smiled at the sight. 
The animals had a great time together. They ate, played, and even told stories. When it was time to go, the deer said, "It doesn't matter where we're going, or who we're going with. What matters is how we treat each other when we're there".
The animals all agreed and left the port with a feeling of joy and satisfaction. The moral of the story is that kindness is always important, no matter where we are.

Ben likes to build with his blocks. He has many blocks of different colors and shapes. He can make towers, bridges, houses and more. Sometimes he builds with his friends, and sometimes he builds by himself.
One day, Ben decides to build a kangaroo. He thinks kangaroos are fun and fast. He finds some brown blocks and some white blocks. He makes a big body, a long tail, a small head and two ears. He adds two legs and two arms. He looks at his kangaroo and smiles.
But something is missing. His kangaroo has no eyes, no nose, no mouth. It cannot see, smell or talk. Ben thinks hard. He looks around his room. He sees his stickers. He has stickers of eyes, noses, mouths and more. He runs to get his stickers.
He puts two eyes, a nose and a mouth on his kangaroo's face. He also puts a sticker of a pouch on its belly. He remembers that kangaroos have pouches to carry their babies. He wonders if his kangaroo has a baby. He looks inside the pouch. It is empty.
Ben feels sad. He wants his kangaroo to have a friend. He thinks of a way to make a baby kangaroo. He finds some smaller blocks and some more stickers. He makes a smaller body, a shorter tail, a tiny head and two ears. He adds two legs and two arms. He puts two eyes, a nose and a mouth on its face. He puts it inside the pouch.
He looks at his kangaroo and its baby. He feels happy. He hugs them and says, "Hello, kangaroo. Hello, baby kangaroo. You are my friends. I built you with my blocks and my stickers. I love you."

Once upon a time, there was a man and his wife. They lived in a small house with their dog. The man had to leave for work every day. His wife would stay home and take care of the house. She was very patient and kind.
One day, the man said to his wife, "I must leave for work now, but I will be back soon. Please take care of our dog." The wife said, "I will be patient and wait for you to come back. Our dog and I will be here."
The wife and the dog played together while the man was at work. They had a lot of fun. The dog was very happy to be with the wife. They laughed and played all day.
When the man came back from work, he saw his wife and the dog playing. He was very happy to see them. He said, "Thank you for being patient and taking care of our dog." The wife smiled and said, "We had a great day together."
From that day on, the man, his wife, and their dog lived happily together. They always had fun and the wife was always patient when the man had to leave for work.

Once upon a time there was a little kid. He was very excited to go exploring in the forest. As he walked, he suddenly saw something strange. It was a giant blue mushroom. He was curious and wanted to take the mushroom, so he snuck up to it very carefully.
Suddenly, the mushroom shrank! The little kid was so surprised, he shouted loudly. His mom came running and told him, "It's not nice to take things that don't belong to you. The mushroom was not yours, so it's not respectful to take it."
The little kid understood his mom's lesson. From then on, he was much more respectful and caring of things that weren't his. He learned that once things are taken away, they will not come back.
The end.

One day, a little boy named Tim was walking with his mom. They went to the mailbox to get the mail. Tim was nervous because he had never done this before. His mom said, "I will show you how to do it."
They walked up to the mailbox and Tim's mom opened it. Inside, there was a big, shiny ball. Tim was surprised! He asked, "Mom, why is there a ball in the mailbox?" His mom was also surprised and said, "I don't know, Tim. This is very strange!"
Just then, a little girl came running up to them. She said, "I'm sorry! That's my ball! I put it in the mailbox by mistake." Tim and his mom laughed. They gave the ball back to the little girl and got their mail. Tim was not nervous anymore. He was happy that he could help the little girl find her ball.

Once upon a time, in a land with lots of snow, there was a gentle boy named Tom. Tom loved to play in the snow. One day, he built a big igloo with his friends. They made it with snow and ice. It was very fun.
Tom had a pet dog named Max. Max was a big, white, and fluffy dog. Max liked to ride on a sled with Tom. They would go very fast in the snow. Tom and Max were best friends.
One day, Tom and Max went to the igloo with their friends. They played inside and had a big snowball fight. After playing, they sat in the igloo and shared yummy cookies. Tom, Max, and their friends had a great day in the snow.

One day, a clever cat named Tom found a rake in his yard. He wanted to use it to clean up the leaves. He started to rake the leaves into a big pile. 
Tom's friend, a dog named Max, came by and saw Tom with the rake. Max said, "Tom, can I help you with the rake?" Tom didn't want to accept Max's help. He thought he could do it all by himself. Max felt sad and walked away.
Tom kept raking the leaves. But he got very tired. The pile of leaves was too big for him to finish. He wished he had accepted Max's help. Now, the yard was still messy and Tom was too tired to play.

Once upon a time, there was a little black cat named Kitty. Kitty lived in a small house with a nice family. The family had a mom, a dad, and a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play with Kitty all day long.
One day, Sue wanted to play a new game with Kitty. She took Kitty upstairs to her room. Sue had a big box that she wanted to see if Kitty could fit in. Sue put the box on the floor and opened the top.
Kitty looked at the box and then looked at Sue. Sue said, "Come on, Kitty. Let's see if you can fit in the box!" Kitty jumped into the box and curled up inside. Sue laughed and clapped her hands. "Good job, Kitty! You fit in the box!" Sue and Kitty played upstairs with the box for the rest of the day.

Once upon a time, there was a cat named Tim. Tim was very good at balance. He could walk on thin walls and not fall. Tim loved to play outside in all kinds of weather. Rain or shine, he would go out and have fun.
One day, the weather was very windy. Tim wanted to show his balance skills to his friends. He climbed up a thin wall and started walking. The wind was strong, but Tim was persistent. He kept trying and did not give up.
Tim walked on the wall for a long time. His friends watched and clapped. They were so happy to see Tim balance in the windy weather. Even though it was hard, Tim's persistent heart made him a hero to his friends. And they all played together in the wind, having a great time.

One day, a tall girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. She saw a big slide and wanted to try it. She ran to the slide and climbed up the ladder.
"Can I go first, mom?" she asked.
"Sure, honey, but be careful," her mom said.
Lily sat on the slide and pushed herself down. She felt the wind in her hair and the sun on her face. She smiled and shouted, "Whee!"
But then, she saw a boy at the bottom of the slide. He was not moving and he looked angry. He had a red ball in his hand.
"Hey, you!" he yelled. "This is my slide! You can't use it!"
Lily was scared and stopped. She did not know what to do. She did not want to make the boy mad, but she also wanted to have fun.
"Who are you?" she asked.
"I'm Max, and this is my park," he said. "You have to ask me before you play here."
Lily did not think that was fair. She did not see any sign that said the park was his. She thought the park was for everyone.
"No, it's not," she said. "The park is for all the kids. You can't tell me what to do."
"Yes, I can," he said. "You're a girl, and girls are silly. You don't know how to play right."
Lily felt angry and sad. She did not like what he said. She thought girls were smart and fun. She wanted to tell him that, but she did not know how.
She looked at her mom, who was watching them. Her mom smiled and nodded. She gave her a sign to start talking.
Lily took a deep breath and said, "You're wrong, Max. Girls are not silly. Girls are awesome. And I know how to play right. I can slide and run and jump and throw. I can do anything you can do. And I can share, too. Do you want to play with me and my mom? We can take turns on the slide and play with your ball."
Max was surprised. He did not expect her to say that. He looked at her and her mom. They seemed nice and friendly. He felt a little sorry for being mean.
He thought for a moment and said, "Okay, Lily. I'm sorry for being mean. You can play with me and my ball. And we can share the slide. But you have to promise me one thing."
"What is it?" she asked.
"You have to promise me that you won't be taller than me," he said.
Lily laughed and said, "I can't promise that, Max. I don't know how tall I will be. But I can promise that I will be your friend."
Max smiled and said, "That's good enough for me. Come on, let's play."
And they did. They played on the slide and with the ball. They had fun and learned to get along. And their moms were happy, too. They watched them and talked to each other. They were glad that their kids started to be friends.

One day, a fat cat named Tom went for a walk. He wore a leash so he would not get lost. Tom liked to walk and see new things.
As Tom walked, he saw a big dog. The dog saw Tom and wanted to play. Tom was scared, but the dog just wanted to be friends. They played and had fun together.
Then, Tom found a spray bottle. He sprayed the water at the dog. The dog was surprised and ran away. Tom laughed and went back home. The dog came back with a ball, and they played together again. The dog and Tom became best friends.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play outside. One day, they go to the field near their house. The field has many flowers and trees. Anna and Ben run and laugh in the field.
"Look, Ben, a bird!" Anna says. She points to a big bird on a branch. The bird is brown and white. It has a long beak and a crest.
"Wow, Anna, it is beautiful!" Ben says. He wants to see the bird better. He walks slowly to the tree. He does not want to scare the bird.
Anna follows Ben. She is curious too. They reach the tree. They peek behind the trunk. They see the bird. The bird sees them. It makes a loud noise. It flaps its wings. It flies away.
"Bye, bird!" Anna and Ben say. They wave to the bird. They are not sad. They are happy. They saw a healthy bird. They learned something new. They hug each other. They go back to play in the field. They have fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He was a restless boy, always wanting to play and have fun. One sunny day, Tim went to the park with his best friend, Sam.
As they played, they found a treasure map under a big tree. The map showed them where to find a hidden treasure. Tim and Sam were very excited and started to look for the treasure. They dug and dug until they found a big box.
Tim said, "Let's open the box and see the treasure!" When they opened the box, they found shiny coins and pretty toys. Tim was so happy that he pinched Sam's arm. Sam laughed and said, "Ouch! That tickles!" They played with their new toys and shared their treasure with their friends. And from that day on, Tim and Sam had even more fun at the park.

Lily and Tom were playing in their room. They liked to pretend they were heroes and villains. Lily was the hero and Tom was the villain. Tom had a big closet where he hid his toys and clothes. He said it was his secret base.
"Give me back my teddy bear, you mean villain!" Lily shouted. She had a soft teddy bear that she loved very much. Tom had taken it and locked it in his closet. He was envious of Lily's teddy bear. He wanted it for himself.
"Never! It's mine now!" Tom said. He laughed and ran to his closet. He locked the door and turned off the light. He hugged the teddy bear and smiled.
Lily was angry and sad. She wanted to rescue her teddy bear. She knocked on the closet door and said, "Please, Tom, give me back my teddy bear. It's not nice to take things that are not yours."
"No, go away!" Tom said. He did not want to share. He did not care about Lily's feelings.
Lily tried to open the closet door, but it was too hard. She could not break it. She looked around and saw a chair. She had an idea. She dragged the chair to the closet and climbed on it. She reached for the doorknob and turned it. The door opened a little bit. She saw a crack of light and the teddy bear's ear.
"Ha! I got you!" Lily said. She grabbed the teddy bear and pulled it out. She was happy. She jumped off the chair and ran to her bed. She hugged the teddy bear and said, "I rescued you, my teddy bear. I love you."
But then, something bad happened. The chair fell over and hit the closet door. The door slammed shut and locked. Tom was still inside. He was scared and alone. He screamed and cried. He wanted to get out. He banged on the door and said, "Help! Help! Let me out!"
Lily heard Tom's voice. She felt sorry for him. She wanted to help him. She ran to the closet and tried to open the door. But it was too late. The door was stuck. She could not open it. She cried and said, "I'm sorry, Tom. I didn't mean to lock you in. Please, don't be mad at me."
But Tom did not hear her. He was too loud and too scared. He kept screaming and crying. He wished he had not taken Lily's teddy bear. He wished he had been nice to her. He wished he had a hero to rescue him. But there was no hero. There was only a closet. A dark and lonely closet. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a happy family who lived together in a house. This family loved to climb - they would often climb trees and rocks around their home, even when it was winter!
This day, the family decided to climb something different - a mountain! It was a very steep mountain and they would need to be careful. Everyone grabbed their climbing gear and started the climb. It was hard work, but soon they found themselves at the top.
The family looked around and were amazed. The view was so deep and beautiful - it was like nothing they'd ever seen before! They all climbed back down the other side.
On the way down, their youngest had a question.
"Daddy," the child asked, "Can we come back here again?"
"Yes," Daddy replied with a smile, "We can come back here anytime we want." 
The family continued their climb down and enjoyed the amazing views. They all said that this was the best mountain they'd ever climbed!

Tom and Lily were friends who liked to print pictures with their crayons. They had a big book of animals that they copied on paper. One day, they found a new animal in the book. It was called a zip. It had a long nose, a curly tail and black and white stripes.
"Wow, look at this zip!" Tom said. "It is so funny and jolly. I want to print it."
"Me too, me too!" Lily said. "Let's print it together."
They took a big sheet of paper and started to draw the zip. Tom drew the nose and the tail. Lily drew the stripes and the eyes. They used many colors and made the zip very bright.
But then, they had a problem. They both wanted to draw the zip's mouth.
"No, I want to draw the mouth!" Tom said. "I drew the nose, so I should draw the mouth too."
"No, I want to draw the mouth!" Lily said. "I drew the eyes, so I should draw the mouth too."
They started to pull the paper and the crayons. They were angry and sad. They did not want to share the zip.
"Stop, stop!" a voice said. It was their teacher, Miss Rose. She saw what they were doing and came to help them.
"What is going on here?" she asked. "Why are you fighting over the zip?"
Tom and Lily told her what happened. They both wanted to draw the zip's mouth.
Miss Rose smiled and said, "I have an idea. Why don't you draw two mouths for the zip? One on each side of the nose. Then, the zip will have a big smile and you will both be happy."
Tom and Lily liked the idea. They drew two mouths for the zip, one red and one blue. They looked at their picture and laughed. The zip was very funny and jolly. They were friends again.
They showed their picture to Miss Rose and said, "Thank you, Miss Rose. You are very smart and kind."
Miss Rose hugged them and said, "You are very welcome, Tom and Lily. You are very creative and sweet. I'm glad you learned to share and cooperate. Now, let's print some more pictures."

One day, a girl named Lucy went to the kitchen. She wanted to find something yummy to eat. She opened the refrigerator and saw a big cake. But the cake started to talk!
"Hello, Lucy! I am Cakey. Do you want to be friends?" said the cake. Lucy was very surprised. She had never met a talking cake before. But she liked to make new friends, so she said, "Yes, Cakey! Let's be friends."
Lucy and Cakey played and laughed all day. But then, something unexpected happened. Cakey started to get smaller and smaller. Lucy was so sad. She did not want to lose her new friend.
"Don't worry, Lucy," said Cakey. "I am not going away. I am just changing into something new." Suddenly, Cakey turned into a little toy cake. Lucy was so happy. Now she could keep her friend Cakey with her always.

Once upon a time, in a normal house, a little girl named Mia loved to eat dinner. Every day, her mom made yummy food for her. Mia's favorite dinner was spaghetti. She loved it so much!
One day, Mia came home and saw her mom was not there. Her dad said, "I will make dinner today." Mia was a little bit worried because her dad never made dinner before. But she thought it would be fun to try something new.
When dinner was ready, Mia saw something strange on her plate. It was not spaghetti. It was something green! Mia was scared, but she tried the green food. To her surprise, it was very yummy! She loved it! Mia learned that trying new things can be fun and yummy too.

Once there was a little girl named Amy. She had long purple hair and her favourite colour was purple. She loved to run around and explore her house.
One day, she wanted to go exploring but she couldn't reach the things high up in the cupboards. She asked her mum to lend her some help. Her mum smiled and said yes.
She gave Amy a tall purple chair and Amy ran over to the cupboard. She was so excited to see what was inside! But the chair was too tall. When she tried to climb onto it, she fell down onto the floor.
Amy's mum smiled. She kneeled down to the floor and helped Amy up onto the chair. Now Amy was tall enough to reach the top of the cupboards. She was so happy!
Amy thanked her mum for lending her a helping hand. Then, she happily went to explore the cupboards.

Once upon a time, there was a big room with a bookcase. Many books lived on the bookcase. In the room, there was also a clever cat named Tom. Tom liked to jump and play all day.
One day, as Tom was playing, he saw something strange. A new book appeared on the bookcase. It was a big, red book. Tom was very curious. He jumped up to look at the new book.
When Tom touched the book, something unexpected happened. The book opened and out came a funny little bird. The bird said, "I'm not a real bird. I am a magic bird!" Tom was surprised. The magic bird and Tom became best friends and played together every day.

Lily and Ben were twins who liked to play in the garden. One day, they saw a big melon on the table. It was green and round and smelled sweet.
"Let's eat the melon!" Lily said.
"No, we can't. Mom said it is for dessert. We have to wait," Ben said.
But Lily was naughty. She did not listen to Ben. She took the melon and ran away. Ben chased her, but he was too slow.
Lily ran to the swing and put the melon on the seat. She pushed the swing hard. The melon flew in the air. It flew over the fence and landed on the neighbor's roof.
"Uh-oh," Lily said.
"Uh-oh," Ben said.
The neighbor was angry. He came out and shouted. "Who threw this melon on my roof? It made a big mess. It is not nice to be naughty!"
Lily and Ben were scared. They ran back to the house and hid under the bed. They hoped Mom would not find out. They learned their lesson. They should not be naughty and play with the melon. They should wait for dessert.

Ben and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big melon on a table. The melon is green and round. It has black spots on it.
"Wow, look at that melon!" Ben says. "It is so big and unique. I want to touch it."
"Me too," Mia says. "But we have to ask first. Maybe it belongs to someone."
They walk to the table. They see a man sitting on a chair. He is reading a book. He has a hat and a beard.
"Excuse me, sir," Ben says. "Can we touch your melon? It is very pretty."
The man looks up. He smiles. He has kind eyes.
"Sure, you can touch it," he says. "But be careful. It is heavy. It is a watermelon. It has water inside. Do you want to try some?"
"Yes, please," Ben and Mia say.
The man takes a knife. He cuts the melon in half. He gives a piece to Ben and a piece to Mia. They see the red flesh and the black seeds. They smell the sweet juice.
"Thank you, sir," Ben and Mia say.
They bite the melon. It is cold and juicy. It tastes good. They make a mess. They laugh.
They lean on the table. They enjoy the melon. They share with the man. They make a new friend. They are happy.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the kitchen. He saw a big red box on the table. Tim was curious about what was inside the box. He wanted to unlock it. But his mom said, "Tim, don't unlock the box. It has spicy things inside. You won't like it."
Tim didn't listen to his mom. When his mom went away, he unlocked the box. Inside, he saw a red, spicy pepper. He thought it looked like a fun toy, so he took a big bite. Suddenly, Tim felt a lot of heat in his mouth. It was too spicy for him! He cried and ran to his mom.
His mom gave him a glass of milk to help with the heat. She said, "Tim, you should always listen to your mom and dad. We know what is best for you." Tim learned his lesson. From that day on, he always listened to his mom and dad, and he never unlocked things without asking.

Once upon a time, in a small place, there lived a harmless cat named Mimi. Mimi loved to play with her ball. One day, she saw a butterfly in the garden. Mimi wanted to play with the butterfly too.
Mimi asked the butterfly, "Do you want to play with me?" The butterfly said, "Yes, but first you must release me from this spider web." Mimi used her paw to help the butterfly get free.
After Mimi helped the butterfly, they played together all day long. They had so much fun in the garden. When it was time to go home, Mimi said, "Goodbye, butterfly! Let's play again soon!" The butterfly agreed and they both went to their homes, happy and tired.

One day, a big red ball was in the park. A little boy named Tim saw the ball and wanted to play. He ran to the ball and tried to pull it, but it was very big and heavy. Tim tried and tried, but he could not move the ball.
In the evening, Tim's friend, Sue, came to the park. She saw Tim and the big red ball. Sue had an idea. She told Tim, "Let's pull the ball together!" They held hands and pulled the big red ball together. It started to move!
But then, something unexpected happened. The big red ball was not a ball at all! It was a big red balloon! When Tim and Sue pulled it, the balloon flew up into the sky. They laughed and watched the big red balloon float away. They had fun, even though the ball was really a balloon.
