"Yes, please!" the snowman said. "I'm lonely here. Can you play with me?"
Anna and Ben were surprised. They did not know that snowmen could talk. They decided to play with him. They rolled snowballs, made snow angels, and sang songs.
They had so much fun that they did not notice the sun was getting warmer. The snowman started to feel hot and wet. He looked at his arms and saw that they were getting smaller.
"Oh no!" the snowman said. "I'm melting! I don't want to melt!"
Anna and Ben felt sad. They did not want to lose their new friend. They thought of a way to help him.
"Maybe we can take you to the store," Ben said. "They have a big freezer there. You can stay cool and not melt."
The snowman liked the idea. He asked Anna and Ben to help him get to the store. They put him on a sled and pulled him along the road. They saw the store in the distant. It looked far away, but they did not give up.
They reached the store and went inside. They saw the freezer and opened the door. They lifted the snowman and put him inside. He felt cold and happy.
"Thank you, Anna and Ben," the snowman said. "You are very kind and brave. You saved me from melting. I hope we can see each other again."
Anna and Ben hugged the snowman and said goodbye. They promised to visit him often. They left the store and went back home. They felt proud and glad. They had made a special friend.

Once upon a time, there was a fast rabbit named Bob. Bob loved to run and play in the big green field. One sunny day, Bob saw a big red apple on the ground. He was hungry, so he decided to grab the apple.
Bob took a big bite of the apple. It had a sweet taste that made him very happy. He loved the taste so much that he wanted to find more apples to eat. Bob ran fast to look for more apples in the field.
As Bob ran, he found more apples and grabbed them all. He shared the apples with his friends, and they all loved the sweet taste too. They played and ate apples all day long. And from that day on, Bob and his friends always looked for apples to enjoy together.

One day, a little boy named Tim and his furry dog, Max, were playing in the house. They were having fun running and laughing. Max liked to kick a ball, and Tim liked to watch television.
Tim said, "Max, let's play with the ball!" Max wagged his tail and jumped up and down. Tim threw the ball, and Max ran to get it. Max kicked the ball with his paw, but it went the wrong way!
The ball flew through the air and hit the television! Tim and Max were both very surprised. They looked at the television, and it was still working. They were happy that nothing bad happened.
Tim and Max learned to be more careful when playing inside the house. They decided to play outside instead, where there was more space. They had fun kicking the ball and running in the sun.

Once upon a time, there was a little star who loved to shine. Every night, the star would sing a happy song while shining bright in the sky. The star had many friends who also liked to shine and sing with it.
One night, a new star joined the sky. This star was very upset and did not want to shine or sing. The little star asked, "Why are you upset, new star?" The new star said, "I am shy and scared to shine and sing."
The little star wanted to help the new star feel better. So, it told all its friends to shine and sing a special song just for the new star. As they sang, the new star started to feel happy. Soon, the new star began to shine and sing along with the other stars. They all shined and sang together, making the night sky a beautiful place for everyone to enjoy.

George and his mom were in the park, looking around and enjoying the day. Suddenly, they heard a strange noise and saw an octopus. George's mom was surprised and said "Oh my! Look at that wild octopus!" She pointed to the octopus, which was all blue and purple. 
George was amazed. He wanted to get a better look, and he started to move closer. He couldn't believe his eyes as he got closer and closer to the octopus.
He reached out and touched it. Suddenly, the octopus made another noise and it started to move. George's mom said "Oh my! It's a wild octopus. Let's stay here and watch it." 
They stayed in place and gazed at the octopus. They watched it splash around and move its tentacles. It was so exciting! After a while they said goodbye to the octopus, and started walking home.

Once upon a time there was a pink elephant who loved to eat delicious food. Every day the elephant would walk into a magical tube in the forest and travel deep under the ground.
Inside the tube, the elephant would find a delicious feast with all kinds of food. The pink elephant believed this was the best food in the whole world and enjoyed it very much.
The elephant went through the tube every day, eating the delicious feast until it was full. On one day however, the elephant decided to stay in the tube a little longer.
Suddenly the tube started to shake and the elephant experienced the most thrilling roller coaster ride! The elephant smiled and laughed, believing the ride was much more fun than eating the delicious food.
The pink elephant was so happy that the tube took him on such an adventure, and kept coming back everyday. Now the elephant still enjoys the delicious food but he also looks forward to the amazing ride.

One day, a boy named Tim found a bright jacket. It was red, blue, and yellow. Tim was very happy. He wanted to wear the jacket all the time. It was a special jacket.
Tim put on the bright jacket and went outside to play. He saw his friend, Sam. Sam said, "Wow, your jacket is so bright!" They played and had fun. Then, something strange happened. The jacket started to weigh more and more. Tim felt heavy.
Tim and Sam were surprised. They took off the jacket and saw that it was full of candy! The jacket was magic! They shared the candy with their friends and had a great day. The bright jacket was very special.

Lola was a little girl and she was three years old. She loved to play outside all the time! But one day it was raining, so she was stuck inside the house.
Lola was very sad. She wanted to do something fun, but she didn't know what. Her mom saw her looking so sad and had an idea.
"Lola, why don't you try to grow some flowers?" asked her mom.
Lola thought that was a great task. She jumped around excitedly and immediately got to work. She filled up a flower pot with dirt and planted some seeds.
Every day, Lola would water her little flower pot and wait for the flowers to grow. After a few days, she saw little green pieces poking through the soil. She was so proud of her flowers!
Lola was no longer sad. She had a new task to grow the flowers, and she was having so much fun.

Once upon a time there was a little boy named Jake. Jake was three years old and liked to explore. One day he wanted to go for a walk outside.
"Let's go!" said Jake to his mom.
"It's very hot outside," suggested his mom. "Why don't you wear a hat?" 
"Ok," said Jake. He found a lovely hat and put it on.
The hat kept him cool as the went outside. He and his mom walked around the block. They saw lots of interesting things, like squirrels and flowers.
When they got back home, Jake took off the hat.
"Thanks for suggesting the hat," said Jake with a big smile.
"You're welcome," said his mom, smiling back at him. "I'm glad you had a lovely walk.

One day, a boy named Tim had a red wagon. He liked to skip and pull the wagon with him. Tim had a dog named Max. Max was big and smelly. They liked to play together.
Tim and Max saw a girl named Sue. Sue said, "Your dog is smelly. I don't want to play with you." Tim was sad. Max was sad too. But Tim had an idea. He told Max, "We will make you not smelly."
Tim and Max went to the pond. Tim said, "Get in the water, Max." Max got in the water. Tim thought Max would be clean now. They went back to Sue. But Max was still smelly.
Sue said, "I told you, I don't want to play with you." Tim and Max were very sad. They went home with their red wagon. They did not play with Sue that day.

One day, a little girl named Sara found a letter on the floor. She did not know what the letter said. She felt ignorant. Sara sighed and went to ask her mom for help.
"Mom, what does this letter say?" Sara asked. Her mom looked at the letter and smiled. "It says that we are going to the park today," her mom said.
Sara was so happy. She jumped up and down. She could not wait to go to the park. Sara and her mom went to the park and played all day. They had lots of fun. And Sara learned that it is okay to ask for help when she does not know something.

Once upon a time, there was a witch who lived in a little house. She had a big, dead tree in her yard. One day, a little girl named Sue walked by the house. She saw the witch outside and said, "Hi, witch! Why is your tree dead?"
The witch said, "My tree is dead because it is too old. I want to tear it down, but I am too weak." Sue wanted to help the witch, so she said, "I will help you tear it down!"
Sue and the witch started to pull and push the tree. Suddenly, the tree began to shake. They heard a loud noise and the dead tree turned into a beautiful, big, green tree! The witch was not a mean witch, but a kind witch who had been waiting for a friend to help her.
Sue and the witch became best friends. They played and laughed under the big, green tree every day. Sue learned that the witch was not scary but nice, and they lived happily ever after.

One day, a dog and a cat were playing in the yard. They liked to chase each other and roll in the grass. The dog had a big, red bow on his neck. The cat had a shiny, blue collar.
"Look at me, I am so pretty!" the dog said, wagging his tail. "I have a bow and you don't!"
The cat did not like the dog's bragging. She said, "I don't need a bow to be pretty. I have a collar and you don't!"
They started to argue and growl at each other. They did not notice a tiny, black flea that jumped on the dog's bow. The flea was hungry and wanted to bite the dog.
The flea crawled under the bow and found a spot on the dog's neck. He opened his wide mouth and bit the dog hard. The dog yelped and scratched his neck. He felt a sharp pain and a tickle.
"Ow, ow, ow! Something bit me!" the dog cried. "Help me, cat!"
The cat saw the flea on the dog's bow. She said, "It's a flea, you silly dog! He likes your bow. He thinks it's a snack!"
The cat jumped on the dog and tried to catch the flea. She used her claws and teeth to pull the bow off the dog's neck. The flea flew away and landed on the grass.
"Thank you, cat!" the dog said, rubbing his neck. "You are a good friend. I am sorry I was mean to you."
The cat said, "You're welcome, dog. I am sorry I was mean to you, too. You are a good friend. I like your bow. It's very pretty."
They smiled and licked each other. They decided to share the bow and the collar. They played happily in the yard. They did not see the flea that found a new home on a rabbit.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She was very enthusiastic about marrying a prince. One day, she met a boy named Tom. Tom said, "I am a prince, and I want to marry you, Lily!" Lily was very happy.
The day of the wedding came, and Lily wore a pretty dress. Tom wore a nice suit. They were ready to marry. Everyone was happy and clapping. Just then, something unexpected happened.
A frog jumped into the room and said, "Wait! Tom is not a prince. He is a frog like me!" Everyone gasped. Tom turned into a frog and hopped away. Lily was sad but still wanted to marry a prince. The other frog said, "Don't be sad, Lily. I am the real prince!" The frog turned into a prince, and they married. Everyone was happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a clever girl named Sally. She loved to read books. One day, she found a big book under her bed. Sally was very happy and excited to read it.
But, when she opened the book, she saw that the pages were all brown and yucky. The book had rot in it! Sally was very sad. She wanted to read the book, but she could not read the words on the yucky pages.
Sally had an idea. She asked her mom for help. Her mom was clever too. They went to the store and bought a new book. The new book was not rot. Sally and her mom read the new book together. They were both very happy. And the conflict was solved.

Sam and Mia liked to swim in the sea. They saw many fish and shells and rocks. They had fun in the water.
One day, they saw a big reef. It was far from the shore. It had many colors and shapes. Sam wanted to swim to the reef. He said to Mia, "Let's go to the reef. It looks cool. I dare you to go with me."
Mia was scared. She said, "No, Sam. The reef is too far. And there may be sharks or other bad things. We should stay close to the shore. Mom and Dad told us to be careful."
Sam did not listen. He said, "You are a baby. You are afraid of everything. I am not. I am brave. I will go to the reef by myself. You can stay here and be boring."
Sam swam to the reef. He left Mia behind. Mia was sad and worried. She watched Sam go farther and farther away.
Sam reached the reef. He saw many beautiful things. He was happy. He forgot about Mia and the shore. He forgot about the time and the danger.
He did not see the big shark that came from behind. The shark was hungry. It saw Sam as food. It opened its mouth and bit Sam. Sam screamed and bled. He tried to swim away, but it was too late. The shark ate him.
Mia heard Sam scream. She was terrified. She cried and called for help. But no one heard her. She was alone in the sea. She wished she had never dared Sam to go to the reef. She wished Sam had listened to her. She wished they had stayed together. But it was too late. The end.

One day, a mighty big cat named Tom met a tiny mouse named Jerry. Tom saw that Jerry was sad. Tom asked, "Why are you sad, Jerry?"
Jerry said, "I am sad because I can't reach my cheese on the high shelf."
Tom wanted to help. He offered to get the cheese for Jerry. Tom used his big paws to get the cheese. He gave it to Jerry. Jerry was happy.
Then, they saw a big mess in the room. They had an idea. They did an operation to clean the room. Tom and Jerry worked together. They picked up toys and put them away.
In the end, the room was clean. Tom and Jerry became friends. They played and shared the cheese. They were happy and had fun.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went to the park. She saw a pretty rose. The rose was red and smelled nice. Lucy wanted to pick the rose, but she saw a bee on it. She was scared and yelled, "Help!"
A nice boy named Tom heard Lucy yell. He came to her and said, "Don't be scared. The bee won't hurt you if you don't touch it." Lucy listened to Tom and did not pick the rose. They watched the bee fly away.
Lucy and Tom became good friends. They played in the park and had fun. They learned that it is good to help others when they are scared or in trouble. They also learned that it is better to be kind to all living things, even the poor little bees.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Tiny. Tiny loved to sing. Every day, he would fly around and sing to all his friends. One day, while singing, Tiny found a shiny metal in the grass.
Tiny was very proud of his find. He showed it to his friend, Bunny. Bunny said, "Wow, that's a pretty metal!" Tiny smiled and said, "I want to sing a song about it!"
So, Tiny flew to the top of a tree and began to sing. He sang, "I found a shiny metal, I am so proud and happy!" All the animals in the forest came to listen. They clapped and cheered for Tiny's song.
Tiny felt so happy and proud. He shared his shiny metal with all his friends, and they all took turns singing about it. And from that day on, Tiny and his friends always sang together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very enthusiastic. One day, he saw a big stand with many cups. He wanted to help fill the cups with water.
Tim asked his mom, "Can I help fill the cups, please?" His mom said, "Okay, but be careful. The cups can break." Tim was excited and started to fill the cups.
As he filled the last cup, he heard a loud noise. Tim looked around and saw that he had filled the cups too much. All the cups fell and broke. Tim was sad, and his mom was not happy. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to sleep all day and did not like to play. One day, Kitty met a wild bird named Chirpy. Chirpy was very good at flying and liked to have fun.
Chirpy said, "Kitty, why are you so lazy? Come, I will teach you how to fly and have fun!" Kitty looked at Chirpy and said, "But I am a cat, I can't fly like you." Chirpy smiled and said, "That's okay, we can still play and have fun together!"
So, Chirpy taught Kitty how to jump and run. They played together every day. Kitty was not lazy anymore and learned that playing with friends is fun. The other animals saw Kitty and Chirpy having fun and they all wanted to play too.
Soon, all the animals were playing together and having fun. They learned that being lazy is not good and that playing with friends makes everyone happy. And they all lived happily ever after.
The moral of the story is: Don't be lazy, and always be open to learn new things from your friends.

Ben likes to play in the garden. He likes to run and jump and smell the flowers. He likes the big yellow sunflowers best. They are so bright and happy.
One day, Ben sees a new sunflower in the garden. But it is not like the others. It is small and brown and droopy. It looks sad and ugly. Ben does not like it. He thinks it is a bad flower.
He runs to the ugly sunflower and kicks it hard. He wants it to go away. He kicks it again and again. He hears a snap. The ugly sunflower falls to the ground.
Ben feels happy. He thinks he has done a good thing. He looks around for more ugly flowers to kick.
But then he hears a cry. It is his mom. She is in the garden too. She sees the ugly sunflower on the ground. She sees Ben's foot. She is very angry and sad.
She runs to Ben and picks him up. She says, "Ben, what have you done? That was not a bad flower. That was a baby sunflower. It was just growing. It needed water and sun and love. You hurt it. You broke it. You made it die."
Ben feels scared. He does not understand. He thought he was playing. He thought he was helping. He says, "I'm sorry, mom. I didn't know. I didn't mean to."
Mom hugs Ben. She says, "I know, Ben. I know. But you have to be careful. You have to be gentle. You have to be kind. Flowers are living things. They have feelings too. They are not toys. They are not for kicking. They are for loving."
Ben nods. He feels sorry. He feels sad. He says, "I love you, mom. I love flowers too. Can we fix the baby sunflower? Can we make it happy again?"
Mom smiles. She says, "I love you too, Ben. I don't know if we can fix the baby sunflower. But we can try. We can give it water and sun and love. We can hope it will grow again. And we can plant more sunflowers. And we can watch them together. And we can learn from them. And we can be happy too."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her mom's makeup. One day, Lily found a big, ugly bug in her room. She was very scared of the bug. She thought the bug wanted to hurt her.
Lily had an idea. She took her mom's makeup and started to squeeze it on the bug. The bug did not like the makeup. It tried to run away, but Lily kept squeezing more makeup on it. The bug was getting slower and slower because of all the makeup.
Finally, the bug was covered in makeup and could not move. Lily was not scared anymore. She picked up the bug with a paper and put it outside. The bug was free, and Lily was happy that she was safe. She promised to never play with her mom's makeup again.

One day, a clumsy boy named Tim went to play outside. He saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. When he started climbing, he found a small screw on the ground. He picked it up and put it in his pocket.
As Tim climbed higher, he met a friendly bird. The bird said, "Hello, what is your name?" Tim replied, "My name is Tim. I found a screw. Do you know who it belongs to?" The bird looked at the screw and said, "I don't know, but let's ask my friends."
Tim and the bird went to ask other animals in the woods. They met a squirrel who was looking for nuts. Tim asked, "Do you know who lost this screw?" The squirrel replied, "No, but you can ask the rabbit. He knows many things."
So, Tim and his new friends went to find the rabbit. When they found him, they showed him the screw. The rabbit replied, "Oh, that's mine! I lost it when I was fixing my home. Thank you for finding it!" Tim was happy to help and gave the screw back to the rabbit. They all became good friends and played together in the woods.

Once upon a time, there was a modern town with many happy people. In this town, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his friends and learn new things. One day, while walking with his mom, he saw a man selling newspapers. Tim was curious about the newspapers and asked his mom if they could buy one.
Tim's mom bought a newspaper and they took it home. She read the newspaper to Tim, and he learned many interesting things. Tim wanted to know more, so his mom told him that they could get a new newspaper every day. This made Tim very happy, and his love for learning began to increase.
As Tim grew up, he continued to read newspapers every day. He learned about the world and shared his knowledge with his friends. Tim became a very smart and kind person, just like his mom. And the people in the modern town were proud of Tim, the boy who loved to learn from newspapers.

Sara and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big lock on the gate. They want to go inside, but they cannot.
"Who put the lock there?" Sara asks.
"I don't know. Maybe the park is closed," Ben says.
They are sad. They look around. They see a big tree near the fence. The tree has a branch that goes over the fence.
"Look, Sara! We can hop on the branch and go inside the park!" Ben says.
He runs to the tree and climbs up. He hops on the branch and swings over the fence. He lands on the grass.
"Come on, Sara! It is fun!" he shouts.
Sara is scared. She does not like to hop high. She stays on the ground.
"Ben, I don't want to hop. It is too high. I might fall," she says.
Ben sees that Sara is scared. He feels sorry. He hops back on the branch and climbs down the tree.
"It is okay, Sara. We don't have to hop. We can play here," he says.
He gives Sara a hug. He sees a candy shop across the street. He has some money in his pocket.
"Hey, Sara! Do you want some candy? They have sweet candy there. We can share," he says.
Sara smiles. She likes candy. She likes Ben.
"Yes, Ben! I want some candy. You are a good friend," she says.
They hold hands and cross the street. They buy some candy and eat it. They are happy. They forget about the lock.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to play with bubbles. One sunny day, she went to the park with her mom and her bubble wand. Mia was so happy to play with her bubbles.
As Mia blew the bubbles, a sad little bird watched from a tree. The bird wanted to play with the bubbles too. So, the bird flew down and popped a bubble with its beak. Mia was surprised and laughed. The bird was happy and not sad anymore.
Then, something unexpected happened. The bird flew up into the sky and returned with a big, shiny bubble. Mia and the bird played together, popping the big bubble. They became best friends and played with bubbles every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little bean who lived in a garden. This bean loved to nap under the warm sun. One day, while the bean was napping, a big bird flew by and saw the bean.
The bird looked at the bean and said, "Wow, you look so happy napping there. I am envious of you!" The bird wished it could nap like the bean, but it had to fly and find food.
The bean woke up and said, "You can nap with me, bird! We can both be happy together!" So the bird landed next to the bean and they both took a nice nap under the warm sun. From that day on, the bird and the bean became the best of friends, always napping together and enjoying each other's company.

Once there was a little girl named Julie. She was three years old and had big dreams. Every night, she dreamt of a cool country far away.
One morning Julie told her mom all about her dream.
"Mommy," she said, "I dreamt of a cool country."
Her mom smiled and said, "That sounds like an exciting dream. Where was it?"
Julie thought for a moment, then said, "It was far away and there were big mountains and lots of trees."
Her mom smiled and said, "That sounds like an amazing place. I'm sure we can visit one day." Julie smiled and hugged her mom.
Every night, Julie continued to dream of the cool country far away. She thought of all the things she would do and see when she finally got to visit. Until then, she was happy to use her imagination and dream of this wonderful place.

One day, a boy named Tim was very nervous. He wanted to watch his favorite show on TV. But, his mom said he had to wait. She needed to delay the TV time because they had to go to the store first.
Tim was sad, but he knew he had to listen to his mom. They went to the store and bought food for dinner. Tim saw his favorite candy and his mom let him have it. This made Tim happy.
When they got home, Tim's mom turned on the TV. Tim was not nervous anymore. He watched his favorite show and ate his candy. He was happy that he listened to his mom and waited for TV time.

Once upon a time, in a tidy little town, there lived a happy girl named Lily. She loved to play hide and seek with her friends. One day, while they were playing, the sky turned dark, and the wind started to blow. The town was about to face a big hurricane.
All the people in the town were scared. They knew they had to hide from the hurricane to stay safe. Lily and her friends stopped playing and quickly went to their homes. They hid inside their houses with their families, waiting for the hurricane to pass.
After the hurricane, the town was a mess. But everyone was safe because they hid in their homes. Lily and her friends learned that it is important to listen and stay safe when there is danger. They helped clean the town and make it tidy again. And from that day on, they always remembered to be careful and protect each other.

Tim and Sam were brothers who liked to play with their toys. They had a big ship that they could put in the water and make it go. They also had a small ship that they could hold in their hands and pretend it was flying.
One day, they went to the park with their mom and dad. They saw a big pond with ducks and fish. They wanted to play with their big ship in the pond.
"Can we put our ship in the water, mom?" Tim asked.
"Sure, but be careful not to lose it or get wet," mom said.
Tim and Sam ran to the edge of the pond and put their ship in the water. They pushed it gently and watched it float. They were happy and excited.
But then, a strong wind blew and made the water move. The ship started to drift away from them. They tried to reach it, but it was too far.
"Oh no, our ship!" Tim cried.
"Don't worry, we can get it back," Sam said. He had an idea. He took his small ship and threw it in the air. He made a sound like a plane.
"Look, Tim, our small ship is flying! It can help our big ship!" Sam said.
He pretended that his small ship was talking to his big ship. He said, "Hello, big ship, I am here to help you. Follow me and I will show you the way back. You can do it, big ship, you are strong and brave!"
Tim smiled and joined Sam. He also threw his small ship in the air and made a sound like a plane. He said, "Yes, big ship, listen to our small ship. He is your friend and he wants to help you. You are not alone, big ship, we are here for you. You can make it, big ship, you are hard and fast!"
The brothers encouraged their big ship with their small ships. They hoped that their big ship would hear them and come back to them. They did not give up.
And then, something amazing happened. The wind changed and the water calmed down. The big ship turned around and started to move towards them. It looked like it was following their small ships.
"Look, Sam, our big ship is coming back!" Tim shouted.
"Yay, Tim, our big ship is coming back!" Sam cheered.
They ran to the pond and caught their big ship. They hugged it and thanked their small ships. They were happy and proud.
"Good job, big ship, you did it!" Tim said.
"Good job, small ships, you helped big ship!" Sam said.
Mom and dad came and praised them. They said, "Good job, Tim and Sam, you encouraged your big ship with your small ships. You were creative and kind. We are proud of you!"
Tim and Sam smiled and said, "Thank you, mom and dad, we love you!"
They played with their ships some more and had a lot of fun. They learned that they could encourage each other and their toys with their words and actions. They learned that they could overcome hard things with their imagination and friendship. They learned that they could have big adventures with their small ships.

One day, a little cat was walking in the park. The cat saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. The cat climbed up the tree and sat on a branch. The cat felt safe up high in the tree.
A small bird came and sat next to the cat. The bird asked, "Do you want to play?" The cat nodded yes, and they started to play. They played a fun game where the bird would fly around and the cat would try to catch it.
As they played, the cat slipped and fell off the branch. But something unexpected happened! The cat landed on its knee and didn't get hurt. The bird helped the cat down from the tree. They both laughed and played more games on the ground. The cat learned that it is better to play safe games with friends.

One day, a wolf was walking in the mild woods. He saw a big tree with red apples. The wolf was hungry, so he picked an apple and ate it. It was very yummy.
As the wolf walked, he met a small rabbit. The rabbit said, "Hi, wolf! Can you help me find my home? I am lost." The wolf wanted to be kind, so he said, "Yes, I will help you, little rabbit."
They walked together and saw a big pond. The pond had ice on it. The wolf said, "Look, rabbit! The pond is freeze. We can slide on it!" The rabbit was happy and they slid on the ice. After they played, the wolf helped the rabbit find his home. The rabbit said, "Thank you, wolf!" The wolf smiled and walked back to the mild woods.

One day, a dog named Spot went for a walk. Spot liked to print his paw in the mud. He saw a big mud puddle and ran to it. Spot jumped in the puddle and got his paws all muddy. He felt very happy.
Spot saw his friend, a cat named Whiskers. Whiskers looked dizzy. "What's wrong?" asked Spot. "I don't know," said Whiskers. "I feel like I'm going round and round."
Spot wanted to help Whiskers. He had an idea. He told Whiskers to look at his paw prints in the mud. Whiskers looked at the paw prints and started to feel better. The paw prints made Whiskers stop feeling dizzy.
Just then, a big wind came and blew all the mud away. Spot and Whiskers were shocked. They thought the paw prints were gone forever. But when they looked down, they saw the paw prints were now on their fur! They laughed and laughed, happy that the paw prints were still with them.

Once upon a time, there was a dog. The dog had an ordinary heel. One day, the dog went for a walk. He could see many things. He saw a big tree, a small cat, and a red ball.
The dog wanted to play with the red ball. He ran fast to the ball. The dog did not see the cat near the ball. The cat was scared and jumped up. The dog was surprised and stopped.
Then, something unexpected happened. The cat landed on the dog's ordinary heel. The dog cried out loud. The cat ran away. The dog's heel was hurt. He could not play with the red ball. The dog went home sad.

Once upon a time, there was a big red strawberry. It lived in a garden with other fruits and flowers. The strawberry was sad and confused because it wanted to be saved from being eaten by bugs.
One day, a little boy named Tim came to the garden. He saw the strawberry and said, "Hi, strawberry! Why are you sad?" The strawberry replied, "I'm scared of bugs. They want to eat me. Can you save me?"
Tim wanted to help the strawberry. He thought for a moment and then picked up the strawberry very gently. He took it inside his house and put it in a safe place. The strawberry was so happy that it was saved from the bugs. From that day on, Tim and the strawberry became best friends.

One day, Tom was at his house. He was a little troubled. He could not find his watch. He looked and looked, but it was not there. He asked his friend, Sam, to help him find it.
"Sam, can you help me find my watch?" Tom asked. Sam said, "Yes, let's count how many places we look." They started to count and look all around the house.
They looked in one, two, three rooms. Then, something unexpected happened. A little dog came in the room with the watch in its mouth! The dog had taken the watch to play. Tom and Sam laughed and said, "Thank you, little dog!"

Once upon a time, there was a little girl. She was very intelligent and very excited. She had a surprise from her mom - a new game! She eagerly grabbed the box and unpacked it. Inside it was a colourful box with lots of pieces for her to play with. She was so excited that she immediately started to play with them. She explored the pieces and invented new games. She had so much fun, taking out the pieces and putting back together. She felt so smart and so clever while playing with her new game. And she never stopped playing all day!

Lily loved to slide. She liked to go fast and feel the wind in her hair. She had a big slide in her backyard that she played on every day. She wanted to slide on other things too, like the stairs and the couch. But her mom said no, that was not safe.
One day, Lily saw a plane in the sky. It was shiny and white and made a loud noise. She wondered what it was like to be on a plane. She asked her mom if they could go on a plane someday. Her mom said maybe, if they saved enough money and had a good reason to travel.
Lily had an idea. She went to her room and got her piggy bank. She shook it and heard the coins inside. She thought that was enough money for a plane ticket. She also got her backpack and stuffed it with her favorite toys and clothes. She was ready to go on a plane.
She went to the front door and opened it. She saw a big truck parked on the street. It had a long ramp on the back. Lily thought that looked like a slide. She ran to the truck and climbed on the ramp. She was going to slide down and then find a plane.
But before she could slide, she heard her mom calling her name. She turned around and saw her mom running to her. Her mom looked scared and angry. She grabbed Lily and hugged her. She asked her what she was doing. Lily said she was going to slide and then go on a plane. She said she had money and a backpack.
Her mom shook her head and said no, that was not how it worked. She said the truck was not a slide, it was a moving truck. She said the ramp was not for sliding, it was for loading and unloading. She said the plane was not for fun, it was for work or family. She said the money was not enough, it was for saving and spending wisely. She said the backpack was not for traveling, it was for school or sleepovers.
Lily felt sad and confused. She did not mean to do anything wrong. She just wanted to slide and see the world. She said she was sorry and that she was honest. She did not lie or steal. She just used her imagination.
Her mom smiled and said she understood. She said she was glad that Lily was honest and creative. She said she was not mad, she was just worried. She said she loved her and wanted her to be safe and happy. She said they could slide on the backyard slide and watch the planes in the sky. She said they could also read books and watch movies about planes and other places. She said maybe someday they could go on a plane together, but not today.
Lily nodded and said okay. She hugged her mom and said she loved her too. She said she was sorry for scaring her. She said she would slide on the backyard slide and watch the planes in the sky. She said she would also read books and watch movies about planes and other places. She said maybe someday they could go on a plane together, but not today.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby cat named Max. Max loved to play with his ball. He would play all day long. Max was very happy.
One day, Max's ball went under the bed. Max tried to get the ball, but he was too chubby to fit. He was very sad. Max had an idea. He would order his friend, the dog, to help him.
Max went to the dog and said, "Dog, please help me get my ball from under the bed." The dog was very helpful. He went under the bed and got the ball for Max. Max was very happy. He played with his ball and his friend, the dog, all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot liked to wonder about the big, unknown world. One sunny day, he went for a walk in the park.
While walking, Spot met another dog. "Hi, I'm Max," said the new dog. "Do you want to play?" Spot was scared of the unknown dog, but he said, "Yes, let's play!"
They played together all day and had lots of fun. Spot learned that making new friends was a good thing. From that day on, Spot never felt scared to meet unknown friends again.
The End.

One day, a boy named Tim found a band in the park. It was a colorful band with red, blue, and yellow. Tim liked the band very much. He put it on his arm and started to play with his friends.
While they were playing, Tim's friend, Sam, said, "Hey, Tim! Your band is so pretty!" Tim smiled and said, "Thank you, Sam! I found it in the park." They played and laughed together. They were very happy.
But then, Tim did a stupid thing. He dropped the band while running. The band fell on the ground and broke. Tim was very sad. Sam saw Tim was sad and said, "Don't worry, Tim. We can fix your band." They tried to fix the band, but they couldn't. Tim learned to be more careful with his things.

One day, a bald man named Tim was walking in the park. He saw a little boy named Sam playing with a toy car. Sam's car could go forward and reverse. Tim thought it was fun to watch the car move.
Sam saw Tim and said, "Hi, do you want to play with my car?" Tim said, "Yes, I would love to!" Sam showed Tim how to make the car go forward and reverse using his hand.
They played together and had lots of fun. Tim learned that even though he was bald, he could still make new friends and play. Sam was happy to have a new friend to play with, and they both laughed and played with the toy car all day.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jazz. She was three years old and loved to play in her garden. One day, she was playing when a magical door appeared. She was both scared and excited but she walked through it anyway.
When she went through the door, Jazz found herself in a strange room. There were lots of colours everywhere and toys all around. She looked around the room and noticed a big, serious looking man in a corner. He was wearing a hat and seemed to be waiting for something.
Suddenly, music started playing. The man started to dance. He jumped and spun, and it was the most fun Jazz had ever seen. He called it jazz and said it was a special kind of dancing. The man taught Jazz how to do the steps, and soon she was spinning and jumping too.
Jazz and the man danced together for hours. They laughed and smiled, and Jazz had a wonderful time. Eventually the music stopped and the man said goodbye. He said he would come back and they could dance again. Jazz said goodbye and watched the man disappear through the magical door. 
The next day Jazz was excited to see if the man would appear again, but instead a new family of birds flew into the garden. Jazz smiled and waved, happy to make new friends. 
The End.

One day, Tom and Lily went to the park with Mom. They saw many things. They saw birds and dogs and flowers. They saw a big slide and a swing. They wanted to play.
Tom ran to the slide. He climbed up and slid down. He laughed and said, "This is fun!" Lily followed him. She climbed up and slid down too. She smiled and said, "This is fun too!"
But then they saw a boy. He was on the swing. He swung very high. He let go of the rope. He flew in the air. He landed on the grass. He cried and said, "Ow, ow, ow!"
Tom and Lily stopped. They looked at the boy. They felt sorry for him. They said, "Are you okay?" The boy said, "No, I hurt my arm. I was foolish. I wanted to fly like a bird. But I can't."
Mom came over. She saw the boy. She said, "Oh, dear. You need a band-aid. Come with me. I have one in my bag." She took the boy to a bench. She put a band-aid on his arm. She said, "Don't do that again. Swings are for swinging, not flying. Flying is for birds, not boys."
Tom and Lily nodded. They said, "Yes, Mom. We won't do that. We will be careful." They went back to the slide. They played some more. They had a good day. They were not foolish.

Once upon a time, there was a little brown dog named Max. Max liked to follow his owner, Sam, everywhere. They loved to play in the park together.
One sunny day, Max and Sam went to the park. Max saw a big brown ball in motion. He wanted to play with it. But a big girl named Lucy was playing with the ball. Max barked, "Woof, woof!" Sam said to Lucy, "Can Max play with the ball too?"
Lucy did not want to share the ball. She said, "No, this is my ball!" Max felt sad. Sam had an idea. He found another ball and started to play with Max. Max was happy again.
Soon, Lucy saw how much fun Max and Sam were having. She decided to join them. They all played together and had a great time. Max learned that sharing can be fun, and Lucy learned that playing with friends is better than playing alone.

Once upon a time, there was a big, elderly lion. He lived in a forest with many trees. The lion had a loud roar. All the animals in the forest could hear him when he roared.
One day, the sky turned dark. The clouds made a loud noise and there was lightning. The animals were scared. The elderly lion saw a little bird shivering in a tree. The bird said, "Help, I am scared of the lightning."
The elderly lion roared very loud. His roar was so loud that it scared the dark clouds away. The lightning stopped and the sky became bright again. The little bird was happy and said, "Thank you, big lion, for making the sky bright again." The lion smiled and all the animals in the forest cheered. They were not scared anymore, thanks to the elderly lion's big roar.

Once upon a time, a chubby cat named Kitty lived in a small house. Kitty loved to play with her toy mouse. One day, Kitty saw a big red ball outside. She wanted to play with it.
Kitty went outside to play with the ball. She pushed the ball with her head, and it rolled away. Kitty ran after it. But then, she saw a fire in the grass. Kitty was scared. She said, "Oh no! The grass is burning!"
Kitty knew she had to do something. She ran back to her house and got a big bucket of water. Kitty carried the bucket to the fire and threw the water on it. The fire went out. Kitty was so happy. She said, "I did it! I stopped the fire! It's a success!"
Kitty went back to play with her big red ball. She felt proud and brave. She knew she could do anything.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. She was three years old and she loved to play outside. Every day she would go to the backyard and do her favorite exercise - jumping.
One day her mom saw her jumping and she said, "Jane, this looks like a wild exercise!" Jane smiled and said, "Yes, Mommy!"
Her mom then said, "Let's get an even wilder exercise. Let's go to the park and jump on the trampoline!"
Jane was so excited and said, "Yes, yes, yes! That's what I want to do!"
So they both went to the park and Jane had so much fun jumping on the trampoline. She jumped so high that it looked like she was flying!
When they were done, Jane's mom said, "See, wasn't it a wild exercise?"
Jane smiled and said, "Yes Mommy, it was really wild!"

One day, Tom and his dog, Spot, went for a walk. They saw a big bridge. Tom wanted to go over the bridge, but Spot was scared. "Don't be scared, Spot," said Tom. "It is safe."
Spot did not want to go. Tom had an idea. He took out his jacket and zipped it up. "Look, Spot!" said Tom. "I will zip up my jacket so I am safe too."
Spot felt better. They walked over the bridge together. They were safe and happy.

Once upon a time, there were two friends. Their names were Lola and Jacob. Lola loved to play with clay, and she was very persistent. Every day, she would go outside and play with clay in the dirt. She would make the most wonderful shapes with her clay.
One day, Jacob wanted to play with clay too, but he didn't have any. So he decided to steal some of Lola's clay. She didn't realize it at first, but when she did, Lola was very unhappy. She tried to take her clay back, but Jacob was too quick for her.
Lola was so upset, she told her mom what happened. Her mom said that Jacob should not have taken Lola's clay, and that stealing was wrong.
Lola was very persistent, so she went out every day to try to find her clay. One day, she found some clay in the dirt and realized it was her stolen clay. She was so happy!
Lola then went to find Jacob and told him never to steal again. Jacob said he was sorry, and from that day on, the two friends played with clay together!

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy old apple. The apple lived on the tip of a big tree. The tree was in a big yard with lots of sun and fun. The apple was grumpy because it did not want to play with the other apples.
One day, a big wind came and blew the grumpy apple off the tree. The apple fell on the ground with a soft thud. The apple felt sad and lonely on the ground. It missed the tree and the other apples, even though it was grumpy.
As days passed, the grumpy apple started to rot. It was not happy, but it was too late. The apple learned that it is important to be kind and play with others. Next time, it would not be a grumpy apple.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. He lived in a small house with his mom, dad, and his dog, Max. One day, Tom's mom asked him to help make dinner. She said, "Tom, please stir the pot on the stove." Tom was happy to help.
Tom went to the stove and saw a deep pot with yummy food inside. He stirred the pot carefully with a big spoon. While he stirred, he saw a sausage in the pot. Tom loved sausages! He wanted to eat it, but he knew he had to wait for dinner.
Max, the dog, saw the sausage too. He was hungry and wanted to eat it right away. When Tom wasn't looking, Max jumped up and took the sausage out of the pot. Tom turned around and saw Max eating the sausage. Tom was sad and said, "Max, you took the sausage! Now we don't have enough for dinner."
Tom's mom heard him and came to see what happened. She saw the empty pot and knew that Max had eaten the sausage. She was not happy. That night, Tom and his family had to eat dinner without the sausage. Everyone was still hungry and sad. Max knew he had done something wrong and felt sorry.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom, dad, and dog, Max. They were a very happy family. One day, Lily's mom told her they were going to have an amazing party for her birthday.
Lily was so excited! She helped her mom and dad make the party look pretty. They put up balloons, streamers, and a big sign that said "Happy Birthday, Lily!" Max was excited too. He wagged his tail and ran around the house.
On the day of the party, all of Lily's friends came to her house. They played fun games, like tag and hide-and-seek. When it was time to eat, Lily's mom said, "Come close, everyone! It's time for cake!" All the kids gathered around the table and sang "Happy Birthday" to Lily.
After they ate the cake, they opened presents. Lily got lots of toys and books. She was very happy. Max got a new ball too, and he was very happy as well.
When the party was over, Lily hugged her mom and dad. She said, "Thank you for the amazing party! I had so much fun!" Her mom and dad hugged her back and said, "We're so glad you had fun, Lily. We love you!" And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Eve. She was three years old and she loved to explore. On a lovely foggy day, she decided to add a little adventure to her day. 
She put on her little coat and set off. As she walked, the fog floated around her, like a magical mist. It tickled her nose and made her giggle. She kept walking until she reached the edge of the park.
She saw a big pile of leaves and thought it would be fun to add them onto her game. She started to jump and stomp on the leaves, making a crunchy sound. Suddenly, she heard someone speaking. 
"What are you doing, little one?" The man asked. 
Eve stopped and looked at him. "I'm adding leaves to the crunchy game!" She replied with a smile.
The man smiled back at her. "That sounds lovely," he said. 
Eve beamed and continued to add leaves until it was time to go home. She thanked the man and waved goodbye. 
On her way home, Eve breathed in the lovely fog and thought about how much fun she'd had. She smiled to herself, knowing she'd added a little bit of adventure to her day.

Once upon a time there was a penguin. He was a very special penguin - he was very black.
One day he was walking by a library and he decided to go inside. He saw so many books! He was surprised and wanted to find one to read.
The librarian saw him and said: "Hello, little penguin! Are you here to read?".
The penguin nodded his head and smiled. He was so excited to read a book. The librarian found the perfect book for him. It was a book about penguins!
The penguin sat down and started reading. He read and he read and he read. Soon he was finished and hopped back outside. He was so happy because he had learned so much. 
He waved goodbye to the librarian and said: "Thank you! Now I know all about penguins!". 
The librarian smiled and waved back.

Once upon a time, in a big green park, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy liked to take a walk every day. One day, while walking, she saw a big tree with a big bow on it. She thought it was very pretty.
Amy wanted the bow for herself. She thought, "I will take the bow home and keep it in my room." She took the bow off the tree and started to walk home. A little bird saw Amy and said, "That's my bow! It's not nice to be selfish."
Amy felt sad and said, "I'm sorry, little bird. I didn't know it was yours. Here, you can have it back." She gave the bow back to the bird. Then, something unexpected happened. The bird turned into a fairy!
The fairy said, "Thank you for being kind and giving the bow back. As a thank you, I will grant you one wish." Amy thought for a moment and wished for a new friend. The fairy waved her wand, and a cute puppy appeared. Amy and the puppy became best friends and they took walks together every day.

Once there was a girl who really liked to jump. She could jump up and down so high. One day, she got a modern package in the mail. She was so excited to open it. "What is it?" She asked.
"It's a special surprise!" her mom said.
So the girl opened the package and inside there was a special jump rope. It was modern and colorful too.
"Wow! I love it!" the girl said.
The girl grabbed the jump rope and started jumping up and down. She laughed with joy and jumped all around her house. She was having so much fun.
The girl loved her new jump rope and was so happy that she had such a modern surprise.

Once upon a time, there was a red house with a big door. In this house, lived a happy family. One sunny day, Mom said, "Let's gather apples from the tree!"
Dad, Mom, and little Tim went outside. They saw the big apple tree full of red apples. They all worked together to gather the apples. Tim picked up the apples that fell on the ground.
After they gathered many apples, Tim said, "Look, we have so many apples now!" They went inside the house through the big door. Mom made a yummy apple pie for everyone. They all ate the pie and were very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max loved to eat sausages. One day, Max went to the store with his mom. He saw a big sausage and wanted to eat it right away. His mom said, "Max, we have to measure the sausage first. Then, we can buy it and take it home."
Max was patient and waited for his mom to measure the sausage. She used a small ruler to see how long it was. "Wow, it's a big one!" she said. Max was very happy and wagged his tail. They bought the sausage and went home.
At home, Max shared the sausage with his friends. They all enjoyed it together. The moral of the story is to be patient and share with others.

Once there was a little boy named Bill. Bill liked to take long walks around town. One day as he was walking he saw something hanging from a tree. It was a small sack, full of something. Bill was a curious boy, so he got closer and decided to take a look inside.
He opened the sack and out came a big left hand! Bill was so shocked he screamed out loud. Suddenly, a man came running towards him. He had a big dark bag hanging from his hands.
The man yelled out to Bill, "Do not ever touch this again, it is an unknown hand!" Bill was so scared he started to cry. Then the man told Bill that if he ever touched the unknown hand again, something bad would happen to him. Then the man hung the bag back in the tree and disappeared.
Bill was so frightened he ran all the way back home and never walked around town by himself again. He vowed to never touch the unknown hand again, and unfortunately he never found out what the strange bag held.

Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play outside in spring. Spring is when the sun is warm and the flowers are pretty. Tom and Mia have a ball. They like to kick the ball and catch the ball.
One day, Tom and Mia go to the park. They see a big slide. They want to go on the slide. But there is a problem. The slide is too small for them. They are too big to fit on the slide.
"Let's try anyway," Tom says. He climbs up the ladder. He sits on the slide. He tries to slide down. But he gets stuck. He can't move. He feels sad.
"Mia, help me!" Tom cries. Mia runs to him. She tries to pull him. But he is too heavy. She can't move him. She feels scared.
"What's going on?" a voice says. Tom and Mia look up. They see a man. He is the park keeper. He has a hat and a whistle. He is not happy.
"You two are too big for this slide. You could get hurt. You have to get off now," the park keeper says. He blows his whistle. He climbs up the ladder. He pushes Tom. Tom slides down. He is free. He feels relieved.
"Thank you, sir," Tom says. The park keeper nods. He helps Mia down. He gives them a stern look.
"Next time, play with something that fits you. Like your ball. It's safer and more fun," the park keeper says. He walks away.
Tom and Mia look at each other. They feel sorry. They did not mean to cause trouble. They pick up their ball. They go to a different place. They kick the ball and catch the ball. They have fun. They are friends.

Anna liked to touch things. She liked to touch the soft fur of her cat, the smooth buttons of her coat, and the rough bark of the tree. But there was one place she was not allowed to touch: the attic. Her mom and dad said the attic was old and dusty and had many things that could hurt her.
But Anna was persistent. She wanted to see what was in the attic. She wanted to touch the things that her mom and dad hid there. One day, when they were busy in the kitchen, she sneaked up the stairs and opened the door to the attic. She saw a big box with a shiny ribbon. She touched the ribbon and pulled it. The box opened and a loud noise came out.
Anna was scared. She dropped the box and ran back to the stairs. But she tripped on a pile of books and fell down. She hurt her knee and cried. Her mom and dad heard her and came to the attic. They saw the box and the books and the ribbon. They were angry and sad.
They hugged Anna and said, "We are sorry you are hurt, but you should not have touched the attic. The box was a surprise for your birthday. It was a music box that played your favorite song. Now it is broken and we cannot fix it. You have to wait until next year for another surprise."
Anna felt sorry too. She said, "I am sorry, mom and dad. I was curious and persistent. I wanted to touch the attic. But I learned my lesson. I will not touch things that are not mine. And I will listen to you when you say no."
Her mom and dad kissed her and said, "We love you, Anna. We are glad you are okay. But please, stay away from the attic."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play outside. One day, she found a big box of clothes. The clothes were all different colors. Sue was very happy.
Sue wanted to try on the clothes, but her mom said, "Wait! We have to go to the store first." Sue was sad because she had to delay playing with the clothes. She wanted to play with them now, not later.
At the store, Sue saw a boy with bad clothes. They were dirty and had holes. Sue felt sad for the boy. When they got home, Sue shared her new clothes with the boy. They both looked nice and had fun playing together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily lived in a small house with her mom and dad. She had a room upstairs where she liked to play with her toys.
One sunny day, Lily went outside to play in the garden. She saw a pretty flower starting to bloom. "Look, Mommy!" she said. "The flower is blooming!" Her mommy smiled and said, "Yes, Lily. The flower is blooming just like you, growing big and strong."
After playing outside, Lily started to feel tired. She told her mommy, "I'm tired, Mommy. I want to go upstairs and take a nap." Her mommy said, "Of course, sweetie. Let's go upstairs and you can rest." They went upstairs, and Lily took a nice, long nap. When she woke up, she felt happy and ready to play again.

One day, a little cat was playing outside. The cat was happy and safe in her yard. She saw a big ball and wanted to play with it. The cat pushed the ball with her nose and it rolled away.
The ball rolled into the next yard. The cat ran after it, but she did not know where she was. She saw a big dog and asked, "Do you know where I belong?" The dog said, "I don't know, but I will help you find your home."
They looked and looked, but they could not find the cat's home. Then, they saw a big tree. The cat climbed up the tree to see if she could find her home. When she got to the top, she saw her yard! The dog and the cat were so happy. They played together and then the cat went back to her safe home.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big hill and wanted to climb it. He started to walk up the hill. Tim felt happy.
As he walked up, Tim saw a small cat. The cat looked worried. "Why are you worried?" Tim asked. The cat said, "I can't find my toy. It vanished!" Tim wanted to help the cat find the toy.
They looked and looked. Then, they found the toy under a bush. But the toy had changed! It was now a big, fun ball! The cat was not worried anymore. They played with the ball on the hill and had a great day.

Once upon a time, there was a little fish named Fin. Fin loved to play in the water. He loved to swim with his friends and make big waves. One day, Fin and his friends were playing and making the biggest waves they could.
As they played, they saw a small boat. The boat was rocking back and forth because of the big waves. Fin felt guilty, as he didn't want to make the people on the boat feel scared. So, Fin and his friends decided to stop making the big waves.
From that day on, Fin and his friends made sure to be careful when they played near boats. They still loved to make waves, but they didn't want anyone to feel scared. And so, Fin and his friends played happily ever after, always being kind to others.

Once upon a time, there was a big building in a peaceful town. The building was very pretty and all the people loved it. Inside the building, there were many rooms where people could play and have fun. It was a happy place for everyone.
One day, a little girl named Lily went to the building. She hoped to find new friends to play with. Lily walked around and saw a room with many toys. She went inside and found other kids playing there. They all smiled at her and asked her to join them.
Lily played with her new friends all day long. They had so much fun together. When it was time to go home, Lily felt happy and hoped to come back to the peaceful building again soon. She knew that she would always have friends to play with in the big, pretty building.

One day, a little girl named Amy went to the kitchen. She saw a high cabinet. She wanted to see what was inside.
Amy called her friend, Tom. "Tom, can you help me look inside the high cabinet?" she asked. Tom said, "Yes, I can help you!" They got a chair to stand on.
Tom opened the high cabinet. Inside, they found a toy that could speak! The toy said, "Hi, I am a talking toy!" Amy and Tom were very happy. They played with the talking toy all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max liked to lie down in the sun all day. He had a favorite spot in the yard where he would take his turn to lie down.
One day, Max saw a little cat walk into his yard. The cat had a serious look on her face. Max knew that something was about to happen. He watched the little cat very closely.
When the cat got close to Max's favorite spot, Max jumped up and barked. The little cat got scared and ran away. Max saved his favorite spot and the little cat learned not to go near Max's yard again. Max felt happy and went back to lie down in his favorite spot.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely shoe. It sat by the door, waiting for a friend. One day, a little girl named Lily saw the shoe. She said, "Hi, shoe! I will be your friend."
The shoe was very happy to receive a friend. Lily put the shoe on her foot and they played all day. They jumped, they ran, and they had so much fun.
At the end of the day, Lily said to the shoe, "You are not lonely anymore. You have me!" The shoe was so happy, it did a little dance. From that day on, the shoe and Lily were the best of friends.

One day, a boy named Tim was in his room. He saw the TV and turned it on. He wanted to watch his favorite show. His mom came in and said, "Tim, it's time to eat. Please shut the TV." Tim shut the TV and went to eat.
Mom made a new dish for dinner. It looked good, but Tim did not know it was spicy. Mom said, "Be careful, Tim. This food is spicy." Tim did not listen and took a big bite. His face turned red, and he felt hot. He ran to get water.
Later, Tim went back to his room. He turned on the TV again. This time, his favorite show was not there. Instead, there was a show about spicy food! Tim laughed and said, "Now I know to listen when Mom says something is spicy."

Once there was a girl who wanted to see a dolphin. She was very smart and she knew that dolphins were strong and fast swimmers. So she decided to try to find one. She went to the beach, looked carefully across the waves and tried to spot a dolphin. She looked and looked, but she couldn't find a single one. 
She kept trying and finally, she spotted a gray spot in the water. She shouted in excitement, "dolphin!" She had found a dolphin! The dolphin saw her too and swam closer. She was so happy to see the dolphin that she smiled and waved. The dolphin laughed, waved back and started to jump out of the water and spin around. 
The girl thought it was amazing how the dolphin was so smart and playful. She clapped and laughed and watched the dolphin for a long time. Eventually, she waved goodbye and the dolphin swam away. As she watched the dolphin swim away, she made a wish that she could see another one soon.

One day, a little girl named Sally felt dizzy. She was playing with her toy car. She could make it go forward and reverse. Sally loved her toy car very much.
Sally's mom saw her feeling dizzy. She said, "Sally, let's go to the hospital to see the doctor. The doctor will make you feel better." Sally nodded and held her mom's hand.
At the hospital, the doctor asked, "What's wrong, Sally?" Sally told the doctor about her toy car and how she felt dizzy. The doctor smiled and said, "Sally, just take a rest and you will feel better soon." Sally felt happy and thanked the doctor. She went home and took a nap. When she woke up, she felt better and played with her toy car again.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a perfect whistle. He blew it and it made a fun sound. Tim loved his new whistle and took it with him everywhere he went. He showed it to all his friends and they thought it was very cool.
One sunny day, Tim went to the park with his whistle. He saw a little girl named Sue. "Look at my perfect whistle!" Tim said to Sue. Sue looked at the whistle and said, "Can I try?" Tim gave Sue the whistle and she blew it. But when she blew it, a big wind came and took the whistle away!
Tim and Sue tried to catch the whistle as it flew through the air. They ran and jumped, but they could not catch it. Then, a big friendly dog saw the whistle and caught it in his mouth. The dog brought the whistle back to Tim and Sue. They were so happy! From that day on, Tim, Sue, and the dog became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim lived in a small house with his mom, dad, and a cat named Max. One day, Tim went to play outside. The sun was shining, and it was a very nice day.
Tim saw a little bug on the ground. The bug was not moving. Tim thought the bug was dead. He picked up the bug and went to tell his mom. "Mom, look at the bug! It's not moving," said Tim. Mom looked at the bug and said, "It's okay, Tim. The bug is just sleeping."
That night, Tim was in bed, and it was dark in his room. He was scared of the dark. So, he asked his mom to turn on the light. When the light was on, Tim felt safe and happy. He could see Max sleeping on the floor. Tim went to sleep, knowing that the light would keep him safe all night.

One sunny day, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. They saw many kids playing and heard the noisy swings going back and forth. Lily loved the park and wanted to play too.
Lily saw a big red ball near the slide. She ran to it and started to push the ball. As she pushed the ball around, she saw a fruit tree with lots of yummy fruits. "Mom, can I have some fruit?" Lily asked. Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, let's pick some together."
They picked some fruit from the tree and sat down on the grass to eat. Lily shared her fruit with her mom and they both enjoyed the sweet taste. After they finished eating, Lily went back to play with the big red ball, feeling happy and full.

Once there was a deaf girl named Sally. One day, she wanted to get some ice for her pitbull, Molly. So she went to the store to buy some. On the way there, she saw a mail truck and waved at the driver. But the driver didn't wave back. He was too busy mailing the letters. On the way back, Sally saw the mail truck again and waved even harder. But the driver still didn't wave back because he was mailing the letters.
When Sally got home, she put the ice in the freezer for Molly. Then she went outside and played with her dog. Molly loved the cold ice, and licked it until it was finished! Sally was so happy that Molly was happy. Maybe tomorrow, Sally would wave at the mail truck again on her way to the store. Maybe this time, the driver would wave back.

Once upon a time, there was an enormous helicopter. It had a name. Its name was Big Helicopter. It flew high in the sky. It loved to fly over the big trees and the blue sea. It was very happy.
One day, Big Helicopter saw a small bird. The bird had a name too. Its name was Tiny Bird. Tiny Bird was sad. It could not fly high like Big Helicopter. Big Helicopter wanted to help Tiny Bird.
Big Helicopter had an idea. It took a long string and tied it to Tiny Bird. Now, Tiny Bird could fly high with Big Helicopter. They flew together over the big trees and the blue sea. Tiny Bird was very happy. But then, something unexpected happened.
As they were flying, the string untied from Tiny Bird. Tiny Bird started to fall. But then, a big surprise! Tiny Bird spread its wings and flew all by itself! It did not need the string anymore. Tiny Bird was not sad anymore. It was happy and free.
Big Helicopter and Tiny Bird became best friends. They flew together, high in the sky, over the big trees and the blue sea. They were both very happy. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a big, dependable cat named Fluffy. Fluffy was very good at catching mice. One day, Fluffy found a shiny scale on the ground. He picked it up and showed it to his friend, the little bird.
"Look what I found!" said Fluffy. The little bird was very excited. "We should find where it came from!" So, they went on a long walk to find the owner of the shiny scale.
As they walked, the sky became dark. Fluffy and the little bird were very tired. "Let's sleep here," said Fluffy. They slept under a big tree. When they woke up, the sun was shining again.
They walked some more and soon found a big, sad fish. The fish was missing a scale. "Is this your scale?" asked Fluffy. The fish smiled and said, "Yes, thank you!" The fish was so happy that it jumped and splashed in the water.
Fluffy and the little bird went back home, happy they helped the fish. They knew they were a dependable team who could solve any problem together. And from that day on, they continued to have many more adventures.

Tom and Lily were playing in the yard. They liked to pretend they were lions, roaring and chasing each other. Tom was the big lion and Lily was the little lion. They had a lot of fun.
"Let's go inside and eat," Tom said. "I'm hungry."
"Me too," Lily said. "Maybe Mom made steak for us. I love steak. It's yummy and juicy."
They ran inside and saw Mom in the kitchen. She had a big plate of food on the table. But it was not steak. It was gray and mushy.
"What is that?" Tom asked.
"It's oatmeal," Mom said. "It's good for you. It has vitamins and fiber."
Tom and Lily made a face. They did not like oatmeal. It was bland and sticky.
"Can we have steak instead?" Lily asked.
"Sorry, sweetie, we don't have any steak today," Mom said. "Maybe another day. You have to eat what I made for you. It will make you strong and healthy."
Tom and Lily did not believe Mom. They thought oatmeal would make them weak and sick. They did not want to eat it. They wanted to be lions, not sheep.
They looked at each other and had an idea. They whispered in each other's ears. Then they smiled.
"Okay, Mom, we will eat the oatmeal," Tom said.
"Thank you, Mom, you are the best," Lily said.
They took their spoons and scooped up some oatmeal. But instead of putting it in their mouths, they threw it at each other. They laughed and screamed. They made a big mess.
"Hey, stop that!" Mom said. "What are you doing?"
"We are lions, Mom, not sheep," Tom said. "Lions don't eat oatmeal. They eat steak."
"And oatmeal is not good for us. It's gray and mushy," Lily said. "Steak is yummy and juicy."
Mom was not happy. She was angry and tired. She had to clean up the mess and make something else for them. She wished she had some steak. Or some lions.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to sort her candy. One day, she was very sad because she lost her candy.
Lily looked everywhere for her candy. She looked under her bed, in her toy box, and even in the kitchen. But she could not find her candy anywhere.
Then, Lily's mommy came in and saw her sad face. She knew Lily was sad because she could not find her candy. So, she helped Lily look for the candy. They found it in the living room, under the sofa. Lily was so happy to find her candy. She sorted her candy and shared it with her mommy. They both smiled and ate the candy together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her colorful toys. One day, Lily went to visit her aunt's house. Her aunt had a big garden with many pretty flowers.
In the garden, Lily saw a big ice cream cone on the ground. The ice cream was melting under the hot sun. Lily wanted to save the ice cream before it all melted away. She picked up the cone and ran to her aunt.
Lily showed the melting ice cream to her aunt. Her aunt smiled and said, "Don't worry, we can make more ice cream together!" Lily and her aunt made new, colorful ice cream. They had a fun day and ate the yummy ice cream together.

Once upon a time, there was a thoughtful little car. This car had four big tires. It loved to help others. One day, it saw a friend who needed help.
The friend was a small bike. The bike had a flat tire. The thoughtful car wanted to help. It tried to think of a way to manage the problem.
The car had an idea. It pushed the bike to a shop. At the shop, the bike got a new tire. The bike was happy. The thoughtful car felt good because it managed to help a friend. They both played together all day.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park. Tim saw a rough rock that he wanted to take home. On the rock, there was a small figure. The figure was a toy frog. Tim picked up the rock and started to walk home.
On his way, the toy frog began to talk. "Hello, Tim," said the frog. Tim was very surprised. He did not know that the frog could talk. "Take me to the pond, please," the frog asked.
Tim took the frog to the pond. When they got there, the frog jumped off the rock and into the water. Suddenly, the frog turned into a real frog! Tim was so happy that he had helped the frog. Now, the frog could live in the pond with its friends.

Tim and Lily were playing in the snow. They made a big snowman with a hat and a scarf. They gave him a carrot nose and two buttons for eyes. They were very happy.
But then Tim saw something on the ground. It was a thermometer. He picked it up and looked at it. It had a red line and some numbers. He did not know what they meant.
"Look, Lily, I found a thermometer!" he said. "What is it for?"
Lily came over and looked at it too. She remembered that her mom used a thermometer when she was sick. She put it in her mouth and waited for a beep.
"It tells you how hot or cold you are," she said. "Mom says you need to stay warm when you are sick."
"Can we use it for the snowman?" Tim asked. "Maybe he is cold too."
Lily nodded. She thought it was a good idea. They went to the snowman and tried to put the thermometer in his mouth. But it was too hard. The snowman's mouth was frozen.
"Uh-oh, it does not fit," Lily said. "Maybe we can put it in his ear."
They tried to put the thermometer in his ear. But it was too heavy. The snowman's ear broke off and fell to the ground.
"Uh-oh, it does not work," Tim said. "Maybe we can put it in his hand."
They tried to put the thermometer in his hand. But it was too slippery. The snowman's hand dropped the thermometer and it rolled away.
"Uh-oh, it does not stay," Lily said. "Maybe we should leave it alone."
They decided to leave the thermometer alone. They did not want to break the snowman more. They hugged him and said goodbye. They went back to their house and had some hot chocolate. They were still happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little toy car. It had a light battery inside. The car could go fast and slow. It was very fun to play with.
One day, the car wanted to learn something new. It saw a big hill and thought, "I want to go up there!" The car tried to go up the hill, but it was too hard. The car felt sad.
Then, something unexpected happened. A kind bird saw the car and wanted to help. The bird picked up the car and flew it to the top of the hill. The car was so happy! It learned that with a little help from friends, it could do anything.

One day, a shy cat named Tim went to a big park. In the park, there was a fun game. The game was to see who could jump the highest. Many kids and animals were there to play. Tim wanted to try, but he was too shy. He saw a small bird and said, "Hi, do you want to play with me?"
The bird said, "Yes! Let's play together and win!" So, Tim and the bird played the game. They jumped and jumped. It was hard, but they did not give up. They jumped higher and higher. In the end, they won the game! Everyone was happy and clapped for them.
After the game, there was a big party. At the party, there was a lot of food. Tim and the bird saw a big plate of toast. They both loved toast! But when they went to eat it, the toast started to talk! "Please don't eat us!" said the toast. Tim, the bird, and everyone at the party were very surprised. They did not eat the talking toast, but they all became friends and had a great time at the party.

Ben and Lily are twins. They like to play outside in the snow. They make snowmen and snowballs and snow forts. They have fun.
One day, they see a big billboard on the road. It has a picture of a hot chocolate and a cookie. It says: "Warm up with a treat at Joe's Cafe". Ben and Lily want to go to Joe's Cafe. They are cold and hungry.
They ask their mom if they can go. Their mom says: "Yes, but you have to use your coats and hats and gloves. It is very icy outside. Be careful."
Ben and Lily use their coats and hats and gloves. They hold hands and walk to Joe's Cafe. They are careful not to slip on the ice. They see the billboard again. It makes them smile.
They reach Joe's Cafe. They go inside. It is warm and cozy. They order hot chocolate and cookies. They say: "Thank you, Joe." Joe says: "You're welcome, Ben and Lily. Enjoy your treat."
Ben and Lily enjoy their treat. They sip their hot chocolate and nibble their cookies. They feel happy and warm. They look at each other and say: "This is the best day ever."

Ben was a boy who liked to fix things. He had a lot of tools and toys that he could fix. He was very good at fixing things, but he was not humble. He always bragged to his friends and family how he could fix anything. He thought he was better than everyone else.
One day, he saw a big truck parked outside his house. It had a sign that said "Do not touch". Ben was curious and wanted to fix the truck. He ignored the sign and climbed inside. He saw a lot of buttons and wires and levers. He started to pull and twist and push them. He thought he was making the truck better.
But he was not. He was making the truck worse. He broke the engine and the brakes and the lights. He also set off the alarm. The truck was not a normal truck. It was a police truck. It had a lot of bad people inside. They were in jail. They were going to court. But when Ben broke the truck, the jail doors opened. The bad people ran away.
The police came and saw what Ben had done. They were very angry. They caught the bad people again, but they also took Ben. They said he had to go to jail too. He had helped the bad people escape. He had caused a lot of trouble. He had broken the law.
Ben was very scared and sad. He wished he had not touched the truck. He wished he had been humble and listened to the sign. He learned a hard lesson. He learned that fixing things is not always good. He learned that he should respect other people's things. He learned that he should be careful and honest. He learned that being humble is better than being proud.
