One day, a girl named Lily went to the store with her mom. They needed to buy a cord for their TV. Lily was very excited to go shopping with her mom.
At the store, Lily saw an enormous ball. It was so big and round. She wanted to play with it. Her mom said they could buy the ball if she helped find the cord.
Lily and her mom looked all around the store. They found the cord they needed. Lily was happy they could buy the ball too. They went home and played with the enormous ball all day.

One day, a bull was walking in a farm. He saw a fence that was old and weak. He thought, "I can break this fence and go to the other side. There are more grass and flowers there. I don't want to stay in this ordinary place."
He ran to the fence and pushed it hard with his horns. The fence broke and the bull went to the other side. He was happy and ate a lot of grass and flowers.
But soon, he heard a loud noise. It was a big truck that was coming fast. The bull was scared and tried to run away. But he did not know where to go. He was not in his ordinary place anymore. He did not see the farmer or the other animals. He was alone and in danger.
The truck saw the bull and stopped. The driver got out and said, "Hey, you! You are not supposed to be here. You broke the fence and came to the road. You are a bad bull. You could have caused an accident. You have to go back to your place."
The driver took a rope and tied the bull. He dragged him back to the fence and fixed it. He said to the farmer, "I found your bull on the road. He broke your fence and escaped. You should watch him better. He is a troublemaker."
The farmer was angry and said to the bull, "You are a silly bull. You were not happy with your ordinary place. You wanted to see something new. But you did not think of the consequences. You put yourself and others in danger. You should be grateful for what you have and not break things that are not yours."
The bull felt ashamed and sorry. He realized that he made a mistake. He learned his lesson. He said to the farmer, "I am sorry, farmer. I was foolish and curious. I will not break the fence again. I will stay in my ordinary place and be happy with my friends."
The farmer forgave the bull and said, "I am glad you understand, bull. I hope you will be more careful and respectful from now on. You are a good bull, but you have to think before you act."
The bull nodded and thanked the farmer. He went back to his ordinary place and was happy with his friends. He never broke the fence again. He learned that ordinary is not bad. Ordinary is safe and good.

One day, a nosy dog named Spot went for a walk. He was very excite to see what he could find. He saw a big tree and sniffed it. Spot thought, "I bet I will find a fun friend today."
Spot saw a little boy named Tim. Tim was eating a snack. Spot walked up to Tim and said, "What are you eating?" Tim looked at Spot and said, "I am eating a yummy snack. It is a cookie. Do you want some?" Spot wagged his tail and said, "Yes, I would love some cookie!"
Tim gave Spot a small piece of cookie. Spot ate it and felt very happy. They both knew they would be best friends forever. And from that day on, Spot and Tim had many more fun adventures together.

One day, a nice pilot named Tom went to the park. He saw many kids playing together. They were having fun. Tom wanted to join them and make new friends.
Tom said, "Hi, I am a pilot. Let's unite and play together!" The kids were happy to have a new friend. They all played catch and laughed a lot.
At the end of the day, Tom and the kids were tired but happy. They had a great time together. The kids said, "Thank you, Tom, for playing with us. You are a nice pilot and friend!"

Once upon a time, there was a popular toy named Bob. All the kids loved to play with Bob. He was a big, red ball with a happy face. Bob lived in a big toy box with other toys.
One day, Bob saw a shiny nail in the toy box. He was scared that the nail might hurt his friends. Bob wanted to help, so he rolled over to the nail and tried to grab it with his tiny hands. But, Bob did not have hands, so he asked his friend, Sally the doll, for help.
Sally was a nice doll, and she wanted to help Bob. She used her hands to grab the nail. Then, she gave the nail to Bob's owner, a little girl named Amy. Amy thanked Sally and Bob for finding the nail. She put the nail away in a safe place. Now, all the toys in the toy box were happy and safe.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to wear her bathrobe all day. She would play in it, eat in it, and even sleep in it. It was her favorite thing to wear.
One day, Lily was eating a yummy snack in her bathrobe. She heard a loud noise outside. Lily started to panic. She didn't know what the noise was, and it scared her. She ran to her mommy and daddy, who were in the kitchen.
Mommy and daddy saw Lily was scared. They hugged her and told her not to panic. Daddy went outside to see what the noise was. He found out it was just a big truck driving by. Lily felt better knowing it was just a truck. She went back to eating her yummy snack in her bathrobe, happy and safe with her mommy and daddy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a dependable friend named Sam. They liked to play together every day. Tim had a big pipe in his backyard. They loved to play near the pipe, but it was not pretty.
One day, Tim said, "Let's decorate the pipe!" Sam agreed. They found some paint and brushes. They painted the pipe with many colors. It looked very nice.
As they were painting, a little bird came and sat on the pipe. The bird said, "Thank you for making my home pretty!" Tim and Sam were surprised. They did not know the bird lived in the pipe. They felt happy that they helped the bird. The moral of the story is that doing nice things for others can bring happiness to everyone.

Sara and Ben like to race. They race with their bikes, their feet, and their toys. They race to the park, to the store, and to the school. They always have fun, but sometimes they get tired.
One day, Mom gives them a snack. She gives them some cheese, some crackers, and some celery. Sara and Ben like cheese and crackers, but they do not like celery. They think celery is yucky and boring. They do not want to eat it.
Mom says, "Celery is good for you. It makes you strong and fast. If you eat your celery, you can race better than ever. Do you want to try?"
Sara and Ben look at each other. They want to race better than ever. They want to be the best. They think maybe Mom is right. Maybe celery is not so yucky and boring. Maybe celery is a magic food.
They decide to try. They take a bite of celery. They crunch and munch. They make a face. They do not like the taste. But they do not give up. They eat all their celery. They hope it will work.
They finish their snack. They thank Mom. They grab their bikes. They go outside. They are ready to race. They feel different. They feel strong and fast. They feel like they can do anything.
They race to the park. They race to the store. They race to the school. They race faster than ever. They pass everyone. They are the best. They are happy.
They do not know why. They do not know how. They do not know it is because of the celery. They think it is because they are regular. They think it is because they are Sara and Ben. And they like to race.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a rich man named Tom. He liked to drive his big, red car around the town. One day, while driving, he saw a little girl named Lily. Lily was sad because she did not have any toys to play with.
Tom wanted to help Lily, so he gave her a saw to play with. Lily was happy and thanked Tom. But when Lily tried to play with the saw, she got hurt. She cried and her mom came to help her.
Lily's mom told Tom that the saw was not a good toy for a little girl. Tom felt bad and wanted to make it right. He went to the store and bought a soft, safe toy for Lily. When he gave the new toy to Lily, she was very happy and thanked him. Tom learned that it is important to think about what is best for others, not just what makes us feel good. And from that day on, Tom always thought about how to help others in the best way possible.

One day, a girl named Sally went to the park. It was an ordinary day. She saw a big tree with something green on it. Sally looked closer and saw that it was an avocado! She was very happy.
Sally ran to her friend Tom and said, "Look, I found an avocado! Let's celebrate and have a party!" Tom was excited too. They started to plan their avocado party. They wanted to invite all their friends.
But when they went back to the tree, the avocado was gone! Sally and Tom were sad. Then, a little bird flew by with the avocado in its beak. The bird dropped the avocado into their hands. Sally and Tom were surprised and happy again. They had their avocado party and everyone had fun.

Once upon a time, there was a soft, little bunny named Ben. Ben lived in a small, cozy hole near a big tree. One sunny day, Ben decided to go out and play with his friends.
As Ben hopped through the woods, he saw a line of ants. The ants were marching. They looked so funny that Ben laughed. "Hello, ants!" Ben said, "Why do you march like that?" The ants replied, "We march to find food and bring it back to our home."
Ben hopped along and saw dust in the air. He sneezed, "Achoo!" The dust tickled his nose. He saw his friend, a tiny bird named Tina, sitting on a branch. "Hi, Tina!" said Ben. "Do you see the dust?" Tina nodded, "Yes, it makes me sneeze too!"
Ben and Tina played all day. They watched the ants march and laughed at the dust. As the sun began to set, they said goodbye and went back to their homes. Ben snuggled into his soft, cozy hole, happy after a fun day with his friends.

One day, a blue cat named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big puddle near his home. Tim thought it was fun to jump in the puddle. His friend, Sam the dog, saw him and said, "Don't leave me! I want to play too!"
Tim and Sam played in the puddle, but they did not know that the puddle had a secret. The puddle was magic! When they jumped in it, they both turned blue. They laughed and played, not knowing that they were now blue.
After playing, they went back to their homes. Tim's mom and Sam's mom were very surprised to see them blue. They asked, "What happened to you?" Tim and Sam looked at each other and said, "We played in the magic puddle!" Their moms did not believe them, but they all went to the puddle to see. When they got there, the puddle was gone! Tim and Sam stayed blue, but they were happy because they had fun and a great story to tell.

One day, a little red jeep was driving down a road. The jeep felt anxious because it was lost. It wanted to find its way home. The jeep saw a big tree and stopped.
A small bird was in the tree. The bird was singing a happy song. The jeep said, "Hello, bird! I am lost. Can you help me find my way home?"
The bird stopped singing and said, "Yes, I can help you, jeep! Follow me!" The bird flew and the jeep drove down the road together. They saw many things like flowers, dogs, and a big blue sky.
Finally, they found the jeep's home. The jeep was very happy and said, "Thank you, bird!" The bird started to sing again and flew back to its tree. The jeep was not anxious anymore and went to sleep in its cozy garage.

Once upon a time, there was a small cat named Tiny. Tiny loved to play with her toys and run around the house. One day, she found a card on the floor. It had a picture of a big red ball on it.
Tiny showed the card to her friend, Doggy. Doggy looked at the card and said, "I want to play with the big red ball!" Tiny replied, "Me too! Let's go find it!" So, they went on a fun adventure to look for the big red ball.
They looked everywhere in the house. They looked under the bed, behind the sofa, and even in the kitchen. Finally, they found the big red ball in the garden. Tiny and Doggy were so happy! They played with the ball all day long and had lots of fun. And they kept the card to remember their adventure.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. Tim was happy because he could ride his bike. The park was big and had many trees and flowers. Tim saw his friend, Sue, playing with her ball.
"Hi Sue, do you want to ride bikes with me?" Tim asked. Sue smiled and said, "Yes, let's ride bikes together!" They both rode their bikes around the park, laughing and having fun.
As they were riding, they saw a big, colorful bird in a tree. The bird looked at them and started to talk! "Hello, Tim and Sue!" said the bird. Tim and Sue were very surprised. They talked to the bird and had a great day at the park.

One day, a big ugly bug with a mustache went to the park. He saw a little girl playing with her ball. The bug felt sad because he thought no one would want to play with him because of his mustache.
The bug went up to the girl and said, "Can I play with you?" The girl looked at the bug and said, "You have a mustache! That's funny!" The bug felt happy that the girl did not think he was ugly. They played with the ball and had a lot of fun.
Later, a mean cat came to the park and tried to take the ball from the girl. The bug said, "No, you can't take her ball!" The cat saw the bug's mustache and got scared. The cat thought the bug was big and strong because of his mustache. The cat ran away, and the girl and the bug kept playing. The bug's mustache saved the day!

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the market with her mom. They bought some warm rice for dinner. Lily wanted to help her mom carry the rice home.
"Be careful, Lily," her mom said. "You need to balance the rice so it doesn't fall."
Lily tried her best to balance the rice. She walked very slowly and carefully. She didn't want the rice to fall.
On the way home, Lily saw her friend Tim. "Look, Tim," she said. "I am balancing the rice. It's for dinner."
"Wow, good job, Lily!" Tim said. "I want to try too."
Lily let Tim try to balance the rice. They both walked slowly and carefully all the way home. When they got home, they were very proud. They had balanced the rice and it didn't fall.

Sara and Ben are friends. They like to play with toys. Sara has a blue car. Ben has a red ball. They both think their toys are fun.
One day, Sara and Ben want to try something new. They decide to trade their toys. Sara gives Ben her blue car. Ben gives Sara his red ball. They say thank you and start to play.
But soon, Sara and Ben are not happy. Sara misses her blue car. She does not like the red ball. It is too hard and bouncy. Ben misses his red ball. He does not like the blue car. It is too fast and noisy.
They go to each other and say sorry. They ask to trade back their toys. They give back the blue car and the red ball. They say thank you and hug. They are happy again.
Sara and Ben learn a lesson. They learn to be happy with what they have. They learn to be careful with what they trade. They learn to be kind and honest with their friends. They are good friends.

Tim and Lily were playing with their toy cars in the living room. They liked to make them zoom and crash on the carpet. Tim had a red car and Lily had a blue car. They pretended they were racing on a big road.
"Let's see who can count to ten faster," Tim said. "Ready, go!"
"One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten!" Lily shouted.
"No fair, you skipped some numbers!" Tim said. "You have to count like this: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten!"
They argued for a while, but then they heard a loud noise from the kitchen. They ran to see what it was. They saw their mom holding a fire extinguisher and a burnt cake. There was smoke and a small flame on the stove.
"Mom, what happened?" Tim asked.
"I was trying to bake a cake for your dad's birthday, but I forgot to check the oven. The cake caught fire and made a big mess. I'm sorry, kids, I ruined the surprise," Mom said.
"It's okay, Mom, we still love you," Lily said. "And we can help you clean up."
"Thank you, sweeties, you are very tough and kind," Mom said. "But first, let's get out of the kitchen. It's not safe to be near the flame. Come on, let's go to the backyard and play with the dog."
Tim and Lily nodded and followed their mom. They forgot about their toy cars and their counting game. They decided to make a new surprise for their dad. They picked some flowers and drew some pictures. They hoped he would like them. They also hoped Mom would make a new cake. They liked cake.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a toy car. He was so happy and felt lucky. He loved to drive the toy car around his room. He would drive it on the floor, on his bed, and even on his dog's back!
One day, Tim's friend, Sam, came over to play. Sam saw the toy car and wanted to drive it too. But Tim didn't want to share. "No, it's mine!" Tim said. This made Sam sad and they began to fight.
Tim's mom heard the fight and came into the room. She saw the boys and the toy car. "Tim, you should share your lucky car with Sam," she said. Tim looked at his mom and then at Sam. He knew she was right. So, Tim let Sam drive the car too. They took turns driving the car and had lots of fun together. In the end, they both felt lucky to have each other as friends.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park with their toys. They liked to make cars and planes out of metal cans and boxes. They made a lot of noise with their metal toys, vroom-vroom and zoom-zoom.
A big boy came to the park and saw their toys. He wanted to play with them too, but he did not ask nicely. He grabbed Lily's car and Ben's plane and ran away. Lily and Ben were very sad and angry. They shouted at the big boy, "Give us back our toys! They are ours!"
The big boy did not listen. He laughed and said, "No, they are mine now. They are better than your toys. Your toys are old and ugly." He threw Lily's car on the ground and broke it. He bent Ben's plane and made a hole in it. He did not care about their toys. He was very mean and rude.
Lily and Ben cried and ran to their mom. Their mom saw their broken toys and hugged them. She said, "I'm sorry, my loves. That big boy was very wrong. He should not have taken your toys and hurt them. He should have asked you nicely and shared with you. That is what good friends do."
She took them to the shop and bought them new toys. They were happy and thanked their mom. They learned that sharing is good and stealing is bad. They also learned that metal toys are noisy and can break easily. They decided to play with softer and safer toys, like balls and dolls. They smiled and played nicely with each other.

Once upon a time there was a naughty monkey. She loved to play tricks and be silly.
One day the naughty monkey was playing outside when she heard a ringing sound. She looked around but couldn't tell where it was coming from. 
She decided to find out and went exploring. She soon found the source - a long thick cable on the ground. The monkey was curious and started to pull at the cable. She tugged and tugged, but then suddenly it started to smoke. Suddenly there was a loud bang and the cable flew right past the monkey's nose.
The naughty monkey had been too naughty this time. The ringing was an alarm and the cable was a power line. She was very lucky she didn't get hurt, but she learned her lesson to not be so naughty again.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went to the park. It was a cool day, and she was very happy. She saw her friend, Tom, playing with a ball. Lucy asked, "Can I play too?"
Tom said, "Yes, let's play together!" They played for a long time and had lots of fun. After playing, they were very thirsty. They saw a man selling drinks. Lucy and Tom bought a drink and used their paper money to pay.
As they drank, they saw a boy who was sad. He had no money for a drink. Lucy and Tom looked at each other and decided to share their drink with the boy. The boy was very happy and said, "Thank you!"
Lucy and Tom learned that it is good to share and help others when they need it. They made a new friend that day, and they all played together in the park.

One day, a boy named Tim wanted to get a haircut. He went with his mom to the haircut place. It was a fun place with lots of colors and toys.
Tim sat in the big chair and the haircutter started to cut his hair. Tim liked to observe what the haircutter was doing in the big mirror. He saw his hair getting shorter and shorter.
After the haircut, Tim and his mom went to the park. They saw a smelly dog. The dog was very happy and wanted to play. They had a fun day at the park after Tim's haircut.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Poppy. She loved to play with her toys and make new friends. One day, she found a small bird with a hurt wing. The bird could not fly and was very sad. Poppy knew she had to help the bird, so she decided to fix its wing.
Poppy took the bird to her dad, who was good at fixing things. Her dad was deaf, but he was very kind and smart. He looked at the bird's wing and knew just what to do. He carefully fixed the bird's wing with some soft bandages. Poppy was so happy that her dad could help the bird.
After a few days, the bird's wing was all better. Poppy and her dad took the bird outside and watched it fly away. Poppy felt so proud that she could help the bird. She learned that it is important to be kind and help others, even if they are different from us. And her dad taught her that being deaf did not stop him from doing good things.

Once upon a time, there was a unique bird. This bird was very special because it could talk. One day, the bird saw a big jar of salt. The bird was thirsty and wanted to drink the water in the jar. But the jar was too tall for the bird to reach.
The bird had an idea. It started to drop small stones into the jar. As the stones went in, the water came up. The bird was very happy and started to drink the water. People saw the bird and began to applaud. They clapped their hands and cheered for the smart bird.
But the bird did not stop drinking. It drank and drank until there was no water left in the jar. The bird felt sick from drinking too much water. It tried to fly away, but it was too heavy. The bird fell down and could not get up. It was sad because it could not fly anymore. And that was the bad ending for the unique bird.

Once upon a time, there was a cool cat named Tom. Tom had a friend named Sue. Sue was a pretty dog. One day, Tom and Sue were playing near an ashtray. They found a ring inside the ashtray.
Tom said, "Let's play marry with this ring!" Sue agreed, and they pretended to marry each other. But then, a big bird saw the ring and wanted it too. The bird tried to take the ring away from Tom and Sue. They did not want to give the ring to the bird.
Tom and Sue worked together to get the ring back from the bird. They talked to the bird and said, "We found the ring first. Please give it back." The bird listened and gave the ring back to them.
In the end, Tom and Sue learned that working together and talking can solve problems. They continued to play and have fun with their new ring. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a big, fierce dog saw a small cat. The cat was up in a tree. The dog wanted to play with the cat, but the cat was too high up. The dog said, "Come down, cat! Let's play!" The cat said, "No, you are fierce. I am scared."
The dog had an idea. He found a raft near the river. He tried to use the raft to reach the cat. The raft was not tall enough, so the dog tried to hang from a branch to get closer. But the branch broke, and the dog fell into the river.
The cat saw the dog in the river and felt bad. The cat said, "I will help you, dog!" The cat found a long stick and gave it to the dog. The dog held the stick, and the cat pulled the dog out of the river. The dog was happy and said, "Thank you, cat!" From that day on, the fierce dog and the small cat became best friends.

One day, a dependable dog named Buddy and a little cat named Kitty went for a walk. They saw a grill in the park. Buddy said, "Let's have a fun day and unite to make food on the grill!"
Kitty agreed, and they both started to gather wood for the fire. They worked together and soon had a nice fire going under the grill. Buddy and Kitty were very happy to unite and help each other.
Buddy cooked some yummy treats on the grill, and they shared their food with their friends in the park. All the animals enjoyed the food and thanked Buddy and Kitty for being dependable and kind. Buddy said, "We can do great things when we unite and work together!"

One day, a furry cat named Tim went for a walk. He saw his friend, a bird named Sam. Sam was sad because he could not fly high. Tim wanted to help Sam. He thought of a plan to support his friend.
Tim said, "Sam, I will help you fly high. You can sit on a toast, and I will push it up." Sam was happy with the idea. They found a big piece of toast and Sam sat on it. Tim started to push the toast up.
As Tim pushed the toast, something unexpected happened. The toast began to fly on its own! Sam was so surprised but happy. He flew high in the sky on the toast. Tim smiled, proud that he could help his friend.

Mandy jumped up high. She was so happy that she felt like she could jump higher than the sun! She was having the most amazing time.
Mandy's dad smiled as he watched her jump. He said "That's amazing, Mandy! Jump as high as you can!"
Mandy felt so excited and she jumped higher than ever before. She could feel the jump taking her up, up and away!
Suddenly, Mandy heard a loud crack. It was coming from one of the nearby trees. A branch had broken and it had landed right in front of Mandy! 
Mandy screamed with surprise. She was so scared, but her dad ran over to her and said "It's okay, Mandy. That was just a big crack, but it wasn't anything to be scared of." 
Mandy felt a bit better after hearing her dad's voice. She smiled and said "Let's jump again! It's so amazing!"

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the store with her mom. They needed to buy some food for dinner. Lily saw a big white cow outside the store. She asked her mom, "What is that?" Her mom said, "That is a cow, Lily. It gives us milk."
In the store, Lily's mom picked up some beef for dinner. Lily asked, "What is that?" Her mom said, "This is beef. We will eat it for dinner." Lily looked at the beef and then at the white cow outside. She thought they were the same.
When they got home, Lily's mom started to cook the beef. Lily went outside and saw the white cow was gone. She thought her mom had cooked the cow for dinner. She started to cry. Her mom asked, "Why are you crying?" Lily said, "I don't want to eat the white cow!"
Her mom smiled and said, "Don't worry, Lily. We are not eating the white cow. The cow is safe. The beef we are eating comes from a different place." Lily felt better and they finished making dinner together. They had a yummy dinner and Lily learned that the white cow was not the beef they ate.

One day, Tim went to the store with his mom. He saw a big jar of candy. Tim asked his mom, "Can you bring candy home?" His mom said, "No, too much candy can hurt your teeth."
Tim was sad but he wanted the candy. When his mom was not looking, he took some candy and put it in his pocket. He felt bad, but he wanted to eat the candy.
Later, at home, Tim ate the candy. His mom found out and was very angry. She said, "You should not take things that are not yours." Tim felt hurt and knew he did something bad.

Once upon a time, there was a warm, soft cat named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play with yarn. One day, Fluffy saw a long piece of yarn on the floor. Fluffy was very happy and wanted to play with it all day.
Fluffy started to follow the yarn. The yarn would lead Fluffy to many fun places. Fluffy went to the kitchen, the living room, and the bedroom. Fluffy was having so much fun playing with the yarn.
At the end of the yarn, Fluffy found a little mouse. The mouse was scared and shaking. Fluffy felt bad for the mouse. Fluffy decided to be kind and not play with the mouse. Fluffy and the mouse became good friends. They played with the yarn together and had lots of fun. The moral of the story is to be kind to others, even if they are different from you.

Once, there was a big police car. It had flashing lights and was very loud. It went all around the town traveling very fast. One day, the police car pulled up in front of a house. The police officers got out of the car and knocked on the front door.
Inside the house, a child was playing. The child was told to behave, so they sat quietly in the living room. The police officers asked the child's parents some questions, then left. The child was very afraid of the police, so they asked their parents why the police were there.
The parents explained to the child that the police were helping to keep the town safe. They said that it was important to always behave, as the police were there to help people. The child felt much better knowing that the police were there to help them, and they said a silent thank you.

One sunny day, a lively little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a small pebble on the ground. Tim picked up the pebble and said, "Hello, little pebble! You can be my friend."
Tim took the pebble to his house. He showed it to his mom. "Look, Mom! I found a new friend!" His mom smiled and said, "That's a nice pebble, Tim. But be careful, it's hot outside. Things can melt in the sun."
Tim didn't want his pebble to melt, so he took it inside. He put it in a cool place to keep it safe. Tim played with the pebble all day. They had lots of fun together. At night, Tim put the pebble next to his bed and said, "Goodnight, little pebble. I'm happy you didn't melt today."

Once there was a puppy. He was small and fuzzy, and he liked to play. One day, he found an empty room. He didn't know what it was, so he went in to explore. He saw some toys, so he started to play.
As he was playing, he heard a voice. It was a person who remembered the room! He started to tell the puppy all about the room. He said it was a place for fun, and lots of children used to come and play.
The puppy was so excited! He ran around the room, exploring all the little things. He saw funny toys and lots of interesting things. He also found some treats and he quickly ate them up. But then it was time for him to go.
The puppy wanted to remember the room, so he closed his eyes and tried to remember everything he had seen. When he opened his eyes, he was back in his own house. But he still remembered the empty room and all the fun he had.

Once upon a time, there were two best friends named Jack and Jill. They loved spending time together and playing in the park on sunny days. One day, Jack and Jill decided to take a walk around the neighbourhood.
On their journey, they soon learned about traffic. Jack said to Jill, "Look, that is a car and that one is a truck. When we walk around the neighbourhood, we need to be careful about the traffic around us."
Jill nodded in agreement. She said, "Yes, Jack. We must be very humble and careful when thinking about the traffic near us. We can learn a lot from it."
Together, Jack and Jill continued their walk around the neighbourhood. They learned much about traffic and the importance of being careful. They both knew that it was important for them to stay humble and safe. 
The End.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Max. Max had a hurt heel on his back foot. He felt sad and guilty because he had taken a toy from a little girl. Max wanted to give the toy back, but he did not know where the girl was.
One day, Max saw the little girl in the park. He limped over to her with the toy in his mouth. Max said, "I am sorry for taking your toy. I am guilty and want to give it back." The little girl looked at Max and said, "Thank you, Max. I forgive you."
Just then, something strange happened. Max's hurt heel started to feel better. Max was so happy that he could run again. But as he ran away, he tripped on a rock and fell. Max's heel was hurt even worse than before. The little girl and Max both felt sad, and Max knew he should have been more careful.

Tom and Lily were at the park with their mom. They saw a big maze made of green bushes. They wanted to go inside and play.
"Can we go, mom?" Tom asked.
"Okay, but be careful and stay together," mom said.
Tom and Lily ran into the maze. They had fun finding different paths and turns. They saw some flowers and birds. They laughed and shouted.
But then, they got lost. They could not find the way out. They tried to follow the signs, but they were confusing. They started to feel scared and tired.
"Mom, where are you?" Lily cried.
"I don't know, Lily. Let's keep looking," Tom said.
They walked and walked, but they only saw more bushes. They could not hear mom or anyone else.
They came to a fork in the maze. One path had a red sign that said "Exit". The other path had a blue sign that said "More Fun".
"Let's go to the red sign, Tom. Maybe that's the way out," Lily said.
"No, let's go to the blue sign, Lily. Maybe there's something cool there," Tom said.
He grabbed Lily's hand and pulled her to the blue sign.
"No, Tom, stop! I want to go to the red sign!" Lily said.
She tried to pull Tom to the red sign.
"Stop, Lily, you're hurting me! I want to go to the blue sign!" Tom said.
He tried to pull Lily to the blue sign.
They both pulled and pulled. They did not listen to each other. They were very stubborn. They insisted on their own way.
Suddenly, they heard a harsh voice behind them.
"What are you two doing? You're making a lot of noise and trouble!" the voice said.
They turned around and saw a man in a uniform. He was the maze keeper. He looked angry and mean.
He grabbed Tom and Lily by their arms and dragged them out of the maze. He scolded them for being naughty and rude. He told them to go back to their mom and never come back.
Tom and Lily felt ashamed and sorry. They wished they had listened to each other and to their mom. They learned a lesson that day. They hugged each other and said sorry. They ran to their mom and hugged her too. They told her what happened and said sorry again.
Mom was worried and relieved. She was glad they were safe and sorry they had a bad time. She hugged them and kissed them. She told them she loved them and forgave them. She also told them to be more careful and respectful next time.
Tom and Lily nodded and smiled. They loved their mom and each other. They decided to play somewhere else and have more fun. They also decided to listen more and insist less. They were happy again.

Once there was a little girl named Mia. She loved to play outside in the sun. One day, she saw an adorable little bug on a leaf. The bug was red with black spots. Mia wanted to be friends with the bug.
Mia said, "Hi bug, I am Mia. Will you be my friend?" The bug looked at Mia and thought she was nice. The bug decided to accept Mia as a friend. Mia was very happy to have a new friend.
Mia and the bug played together every day. They would run in the grass, jump in the air, and laugh with each other. Mia loved her new friend, and the bug loved Mia too. They were the best friends in the whole world.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily and her best friend, a fluffy cat named Whiskers, loved to play in the big, soft bed. They had a favorite pillow that was very big and soft. They liked to jump on the bed and play with the pillow. One day, they heard a loud sound from the pillow. Lily and Whiskers looked at each other and were very surprised.
Lily and Whiskers decided to discuss what the loud sound was. They talked and talked, trying to find out what made the noise. They thought maybe a toy was hiding inside the pillow, or maybe it was just their imagination. They decided to keep playing with the pillow, but they were a little bit scared.
The next day, Lily and Whiskers played with the pillow again. They jumped and laughed, and then they heard the loud sound again! This time, they found a small hole in the pillow. They looked inside and saw a tiny toy bird that could sing! The bird was making the loud sound. Lily and Whiskers were so happy to find the toy bird. They played with the bird and the pillow, and they all became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big house. In the house, there was a chimney. The chimney was very old and harsh. One day, a little girl named Lily wanted to sort her toys. She put them in a big box near the chimney. 
While Lily was sorting her toys, she heard a strange noise. It came from the chimney. She was scared but wanted to know what it was. She looked up and saw a big bird stuck in the chimney. The bird was trying to get out but could not.
Lily wanted to help the bird. She got a chair and climbed up. She reached into the chimney and gently pulled the bird out. The bird was so happy to be free. Then, the bird did something unexpected. It changed into a fairy! The fairy thanked Lily for her help and gave her a wish. Lily wished for more toys to sort and play with. The fairy made her wish come true and flew away.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy scale. He liked to play with the scale and lift things with it. He was a very clean boy and always kept his toys clean.
One day, Tim went to the park to play. He took his scale with him. He wanted to lift things with the scale at the park. Tim found a big rock and put it on the scale. The scale went up and down as Tim lifted the rock.
But then, a mean dog came running to Tim. The dog was not clean and had mud all over it. The dog jumped on Tim and the scale. The scale broke into pieces. Tim was sad and cried. The dog ran away, and Tim went home with his broken scale.

Once upon a time, there was a big pink palace. In the palace, there lived a happy king, queen, and their little girl, Lily. Lily loved to play with her soft toys and have fun in the big garden.
One sunny day, Lily found a small, cute dog in the garden. She named him Fluffy. Fluffy had soft fur and a wagging tail. Lily and Fluffy became best friends. They played together every day.
One day, while playing, Fluffy gently bit Lily's toy. Lily laughed and said, "No, Fluffy! Don't bite my toy!" Fluffy wagged his tail and stopped biting. They continued to play and have fun in the pink palace garden. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a tired cat named Tom went on a trip. He had a small bag and a passport. The passport had his name and picture on it. Tom walked a long way to a big gate. There was a kind man at the gate. The man looked at Tom's passport.
"Hello, Tom," said the man. "I see you are tired. Do you want to go through the gate?" Tom nodded and gave the man his passport. The man looked at the passport and smiled. "I accept your passport," he said. "You can go through the gate now."
Tom walked through the big gate and found a beautiful garden. There were many trees, flowers, and a big pond. Tom saw other animals playing and having fun. He met a new friend, a dog named Sam. They played together all day. Tom was not tired anymore. He was very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big lollipop in the sky. It was called a cloud. Every day a little girl, named Ann, came to see the cloud. She liked to look up and stare at it.
One day Ann said, "Cloud, I will protect you." She looked up and smiled.
The cloud heard what Ann said and was very glad. It said to her, "Thank you."
But then the wind started blowing and Ann felt uncomfortable. She held onto the cloud and said, "Don't worry, I will still protect you."
The cloud was glad and whispered, "Thank you."
From then on Ann made sure the cloud was safe and never felt uncomfortable. Ann and the cloud stayed together, happy and safe.

Once upon a time, there was a baby. The baby lived in a big house with his mom and dad. The baby had a toy he liked to play with. The toy was a small car. The baby liked to push the car around the house and make car noises.
One day, the baby saw a famous person on TV. The famous person was driving a big car. The baby thought the big car was cool. The baby looked at his toy car and then looked at the TV. The baby wanted a big car like the famous person.
The baby's mom saw that the baby wanted the big car. She smiled and said, "One day, when you are big, you can have a big car like the famous person." The baby smiled and clapped his hands. He was happy because he knew that one day, he would have a big car too.

Once upon a time, in a beautiful forest, there lived a little lion. The little lion loved to roar very loud. He would roar every time he saw a spoon because he loved to eat with it. One day, he found a big, shiny spoon in the forest. He was very happy and roared with joy.
The little lion knew that if he took the shiny spoon home, his friends would be very surprised. He decided to hide the spoon and show it to his friends later. He found a big tree with a hole and put the spoon inside. He roared softly and said, "I will come back for you, spoon."
The next day, the little lion brought his friends to the big tree. He told them he had a surprise for them. They were very excited. The little lion reached into the hole and pulled out the shiny spoon. His friends gasped and smiled. The little lion roared with happiness, and they all took turns using the beautiful spoon to eat their food. The end.

Lily and Tom were playing in the park with their ball. They liked to kick the ball to each other and catch it. The ball was soft and red and bounced high. They had a lot of fun.
But then, a big wind came and blew the ball away. The ball flew over the fence and into the sky. Lily and Tom ran after the ball, but they could not reach it. They saw the ball getting smaller and smaller, until it disappeared behind a cloud.
"Oh no!" Lily cried. "We lost our ball! What will we do?"
Tom started to panic. He felt sad and scared. He thought they would never find their ball again. He thought their mom and dad would be angry with them. He thought they had no more friends to play with. He said, "This is bad! This is very bad! We have to get our ball back!"
Lily tried to calm Tom down. She hugged him and said, "Don't worry, Tom. It's just a ball. We can get another one. Mom and dad won't be mad at us. They love us. And we still have each other. We are friends, remember?"
Tom looked at Lily and felt a little better. He realized she was right. He said, "You are right, Lily. It's just a ball. We can get another one. Mom and dad love us. And we are friends. Thank you, Lily. You are a good sister."
Lily smiled and said, "You are welcome, Tom. You are a good brother. Come on, let's go home and tell mom and dad what happened. Maybe they will help us find our ball. Or maybe they will buy us a new one. Or maybe they will have a surprise for us. Who knows?"
Tom nodded and said, "OK, let's go home. Maybe you are right. Maybe they will have a surprise for us. Who knows?"
They held hands and walked home, happy and hopeful.
The moral of the story is: Don't panic when you lose something. It's not the end of the world. There are more important things in life, like family and friends. They will always be there for you and help you. And sometimes, you might get a surprise that makes you smile.

Once upon a time, in a green forest, there lived a little bunny named Ben. Ben had a small tent where he would sleep at night. The tent was his favorite place because it was cozy and warm.
One day, while Ben was hopping around the forest, he met a big bear named Bob. Ben paused and said, "Hi, I am Ben. Do you want to see my tent?" Bob smiled and said, "Yes, I would like that."
Ben showed Bob his tent in the green forest. Bob said, "Your tent is very nice, Ben." They played together and became good friends. From that day on, Ben and Bob would always play and have fun in the green forest.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little boy named Tim. He was sad because it was a gloomy day. The sun was hiding behind the clouds, and it was too wet to play outside.
Tim went to his room and saw something shiny. A medal appeared on his bed! It was gold and pretty. Tim was very happy. He put the medal around his neck and felt like a winner.
He showed the medal to his mom and dad. They smiled and were proud of him. Even though it was still a gloomy day outside, Tim and his family had a fun day inside. They played games, and Tim wore his medal all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely girl named Sue. She had a pretty blouse that she loved to wear. One day, she went to the park to play. While she was playing, she felt very tired. So, she decided to take a nap under a big tree.
While Sue was napping, a squirrel came and took her pretty blouse. When Sue woke up, she saw that her blouse was gone. She felt very sad and started to cry. She looked around and saw the squirrel with her blouse. Sue wanted her blouse back.
Sue tried to get the blouse from the squirrel. But the squirrel was too fast and climbed up the tree. Then, something unexpected happened. A friendly bird saw Sue crying and wanted to help. The bird flew up to the squirrel and took the blouse from it. The bird gave the blouse back to Sue. Sue was so happy and thanked the bird. They became good friends and played together in the park.

One day, a little boy named Tim was very excited. He was going to the park with his mom. He put on his shoes and ran out of the house.
At the park, Tim saw a big structure. It had slides, swings, and places to climb. Tim ran to the structure and started to play. He went down the slide and laughed.
While playing, Tim began to hear a sound. It was a dog barking. He saw a cute dog playing with a ball. Tim smiled and played with the dog. They had a fun day at the park.

One day, Tom saw an empty box. He had an idea. "I can make an aeroplane!" he said. He put the box on the floor and went to find his friend, Tim.
"Look, Tim! I made an aeroplane!" Tom said. Tim looked at the box and smiled. "Let's fly!" said Tim. They both got inside the box and started to make flying sounds.
They were having so much fun. But then, Tim's foot began to slip. "Oh no!" said Tim. Tom laughed and helped Tim. They played with their aeroplane all day long.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a popular girl named Anna. She had a magic lock. The lock was very special. It could talk!
One day, Anna went to the park with the lock. They saw a boy named Ben. Ben was trying to zip his bag, but he could not. Anna and the lock wanted to help him.
"Hi, Ben!" said Anna. "Do you need help?"
"Yes, please!" Ben said. "I cannot zip my bag."
Anna used the magic lock to help Ben. The lock talked to the bag. "Zip, zip, zip!" said the lock. The bag listened and zipped up.
Ben was very happy. "Thank you, Anna and magic lock!" he said. They all played in the park together and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy named Rocko. Rocko was a rock that Tim loved to play with. They were best friends.
One day, Tim and Rocko went to the park. Tim saw a big tree with a lot of tape around it. Tim said, "Rocko, why is there tape on the tree?" Rocko said, "Maybe someone had a terrible fall, and the tape is to help the tree feel better."
Tim wanted to help the tree too. He picked up Rocko and gently rocked the tree back and forth. Tim said, "I hope this makes you feel better, tree." After a while, the tape fell off the tree. Tim and Rocko were very happy that they had helped the tree.
From that day on, Tim and Rocko always looked for ways to help others. They knew that even little things, like rocking a tree, could make a big difference. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a wise driver. He drove his big bus every day. He took people from one place to another. The wise driver was very good at his job. He was always on time and never got lost.
One day, while driving the bus, the wise driver saw a big tree in the road. He had to stop the bus. The tree was too big to move. The wise driver had an idea. He took out his tool to cut the tree.
He cut and cut and cut the tree. The people on the bus watched and waited. At last, the tree was cut into small pieces. The wise driver moved the pieces off the road. But then, something unexpected happened.
As the wise driver moved the last piece of the tree, he found a small box. He opened the box and inside was a shiny key. The people on the bus were very surprised. They had never seen a key like that before.
The wise driver took the key to the town's leader. The leader used the key to open a secret door. Behind the door, they found a big room full of toys for all the kids in the town. The wise driver and the town's leader gave the toys to the kids. Everyone was very happy, and they all thanked the wise driver for his help.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to play with her flexible toys. One day, she found a firework in her toy box. She did not know what it was, but she thought it was pretty.
Mia wanted to complete her toy collection, so she showed the firework to her mom. Her mom told her that fireworks can be dangerous and they should be careful with them. Mia listened to her mom and put the firework away.
Later, Mia and her mom went to a big party. At the party, they saw beautiful fireworks in the sky. Mia was happy she listened to her mom and kept the firework safe. The moral of the story is to always listen to your parents because they know what is best for you.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. He loved to perform for his friends. He liked to show them magic. Tim had a big hat and a wand. He could make a rabbit come out of the hat. His friends were very happy when they saw the magic.
One day, Tim felt uncomfortable. His tummy hurt. He did not want to perform magic that day. His mom said, "It's okay, Tim. You can rest today." Tim stayed in bed and his mom gave him warm soup. She told him he would feel better soon.
The next day, Tim felt better. He was ready to perform magic again. His friends came over and they all had fun. Tim showed them his magic and everyone was happy. Tim learned that it's okay to take a break when you feel uncomfortable.

Once upon a time, a mama and her son went out for a special day. They went to the park, and the little boy was so excited to play.
The little boy saw a bird in the tree and wanted to get closer. He saw a lollipop on the ground, so he grabbed it and put it in his pocket.
Mama saw the lollipop and said, "Oh no! We can't eat that - it looks very different from a regular one."
The little boy was sad, so mama said, "Let's find a way to use it!" She grabbed the lollipop and gave it a spray.
The little boy was amazed. Now the lollipop was pink and had sparkles all around! Mama said, "Now it looks better!" 
The little boy was so happy! He hugged mama tightly and said, "Thank you for making my lollipop different and special!"

In a little house on the corner there lived a very successful family. Everyday they watched a huge blue sky while they ate dinner. 
One day, the little boy watched with wide eyes as his father turned the faucet on in the kitchen. When the water started flowing he leapt up from his chair with excitement.
"What is it?" asked the little boy.
"It's a faucet" said his dad smiling. "It helps make cooking a lot easier."
The little boy kept watching the water come out of the faucet. He was amazed at how much it could do and how successful his father was for using it. 
"You are so successful, Daddy!" said the little boy, gazing up at his dad proudly.
His father chuckled, "Thank you. That means a lot."

One day, a tall man named Tom went to the store. He saw a big bin full of toys. Tom wanted to buy a toy for his little girl, Sue. He looked at the toys and saw a pretty doll. The doll was on display with a sign that said, "Share with friends."
Tom took the doll home and gave it to Sue. She was very happy. Sue played with the doll all day. Her friend, Lily, came over to play too. Lily saw the doll and said, "Wow! Can I play with it too?"
Sue remembered the sign on the display and said, "Yes, we can share the doll." They both played with the doll and had lots of fun. They learned that sharing is good and makes everyone happy.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He wanted to see the pretty park. The park had many attractive flowers and big trees. Tim loved to play there.
As Tim walked, he saw a girl named Sue. Sue was sad. "Why are you sad, Sue?" asked Tim. "I want to go to the park, but I always stumble and fall," Sue said. Tim thought for a moment. "Let's go together! I can help you if you trip," he said.
Tim and Sue walked to the park. They held hands, so Sue would not stumble. They had a fun trip to the park. They played all day and Sue did not fall. They were very happy and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a long, blue mailbox. It lived in front of a little red house. The mailbox had a friend named Tim. Tim was a small boy with short, brown hair. He liked to play with the mailbox every day.
One day, Tim wanted to study the mailbox. He looked at it up and down. He saw that it had a door and a flag. The flag went up and down too. Tim thought the mailbox was very smart.
The next day, Tim put a letter in the mailbox. He put the flag up. Soon, the mailman came and took the letter away. Tim was happy that the mailbox could help him. They were friends for a long, long time.

Once upon a time, in a big green park, there was a soft, fluffy bunny named Lily. Lily loved to hop and play with her friends. One day, while playing, she saw a bright, shiny thing on the ground. It was a shiny, round ball.
Lily hopped over to the shiny ball and picked it up with her soft paws. She decided to show her friend, Sam the squirrel. "Look, Sam," said Lily, "I found a shiny ball!" Sam looked at the ball in surprise. "Wow, that's a pretty sight!" he said.
As they played with the shiny ball, Lily accidentally let it drop. The ball rolled down a hill and into a big, blue pond. Lily and Sam looked at each other, sad that the ball was gone. But then, a kind duck swam over and brought the ball back to them. They all became friends and played together, having lots of fun with the shiny ball.

Once upon a time, in a deep, deep forest, there was a little girl named Ann. Ann loved to dream. Every day, she would sit under a big tree and dream about fun things. One day, she dreamt that she could give orders to the sun and the moon.
One sunny day, Ann was dreaming under the tree when a little bird came to her. The bird said, "I have a secret. If you want to give orders to the sun and the moon, you must find the magic rock." Ann was so excited! She wanted to find the magic rock and give orders to the sun and the moon.
Ann looked and looked for the magic rock. After a long time, she found it under a small bush. She picked it up and said, "Sun, go down! Moon, come up!" But nothing happened. Then, she saw that the rock was not a magic rock, but just a normal rock. Ann felt sad, but then she thought, "Even if I can't give orders to the sun and the moon, I can still dream about it." So, she sat under the tree and dreamt about all the fun things she could do with the sun and the moon. And that made her happy again.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a wealthy bunny named Bob. He had a big house made of roots. Bob was very happy.
One day, a big, scary wolf came to Bob's house. The wolf said, "I'm going to eat you, little bunny!" Bob was scared, but he had a plan to escape.
Bob said to the wolf, "I have a big party with lots of food in my house. You can eat all the food you want, but you have to let me go." The wolf thought about it and said, "Okay, I will let you escape, but you must give me all the food."
Bob opened the door to his house and showed the wolf all the food. The wolf was very happy and started eating. While the wolf was eating, Bob ran away as fast as he could.
In the end, Bob found a new home far away from the wolf. He was safe and happy again. And the wolf learned that it's not nice to try to eat others.

Once upon a time, there was a humble cat named Kitty. Kitty lived in a small house with her best friend, a little girl named Sue. They loved to play and have fun together. One day, Sue found a bottle of glue. She thought it would be fun to make something with the glue.
Sue said, "Kitty, let's welcome our new friend, Mr. Glue, and make a pretty picture together!" Kitty was very happy and meowed with excitement. They started to make a picture with the glue and some paper.
As they played with the glue, something unexpected happened. The glue bottle fell and spilled all over the floor! Sue and Kitty looked at each other, not knowing what to do. Just then, Sue's mom came in and saw the mess. She laughed and said, "Don't worry, we can clean this up together."
So Sue, Kitty, and Sue's mom cleaned up the glue and finished making their pretty picture. They all had a fun time, even when something unexpected happened. And they were happy to have welcomed Mr. Glue into their playtime.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who liked to peek. Every day she would peek around her bedroom to find the best places to hide. She would even peek around the house to find fun games. These games were always wild, and always lots of fun.
One day, the girl was peeking around her bedroom when she spotted something special. In the corner of her room was a box, and she knew that it held something special inside. She quickly opened the box and found a wild game.
The game was so much fun that the girl played it all day long. She peeked back and forth, and around and around, always trying to win the game. Finally, after an hour of wild play, the girl won the game. She was so excited that she jumped up and down while shouting "I won!"

Once upon a time, there was a famous boat. It had a big sail. One day, the boat met a bird. The bird liked to hang in the sky. They became friends.
"Hi, bird!" said the boat. "Do you want to sail with me?"
"Yes, boat! I like to hang in the sky, but sailing sounds fun too!" said the bird.
So, the bird and the boat sailed together. They had a lot of fun. The bird would hang in the sky, and the boat would sail on the water. They were happy and played all day long.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. The sun was bright and the grass was wet from the rain. Lily loved to play in the park, and she loved to eat pastries. Her mom had packed a yummy pastry for her snack.
While Lily was playing, she saw a little boy named Tim. Tim looked sad. Lily walked up to him and asked, "Why are you sad?" Tim said, "I dropped my pastry on the wet ground, and now I can't eat it." Lily wanted to help Tim, so she had an idea.
Lily said, "I have a pastry too! We can share it!" Tim smiled and said, "Thank you, Lily!" They sat on the grass and shared the pastry. They laughed and talked, and soon they became good friends. From that day on, Lily and Tim always played together at the park.

One day, a curious little girl named Mia went for a walk. She wanted to see what was in the big park. While walking, she found a shiny watch on the ground. She picked it up and looked at it. It was very pretty.
Mia saw an old man sitting on a bench. He looked sad. Mia walked over to him and asked, "Is this your watch?"
The old man looked at the watch and smiled. "Yes, it is! Thank you for finding it. This watch is very important to me. It has great value."
Mia was happy to help the old man. She gave him the watch and they both smiled. The old man thanked Mia for being kind and curious. They became good friends and played in the park together.

One day, a little dog named Max was very eager to go for a walk. He wagged his tail and jumped up and down. His owner, a little girl named Lily, put a leash on Max and they went outside.
As they walked, they saw a big tree. Max wanted to climb the tree, but Lily said, "No, Max! Dogs can't climb trees." Max was sad, but he listened to Lily.
Then, they saw a little cat stuck in the tree. The cat was scared and cried, "Meow! Help me!" Max saw the cat and wanted to help. He tried to climb the tree again, but Lily said, "No, Max! Dogs can't climb trees."
Suddenly, the leash broke and Max was free. He quickly climbed the tree and helped the cat come down. Lily was very surprised and said, "Wow, Max! I didn't know dogs could climb trees!" Max was happy he could help the cat.
The moral of the story is to never give up, even when someone tells you that you can't do something.

Tom and Sue love to ride their bikes to the beach. They like to see the big water and the sand. They also like to play with their red ball and make castles.
One day, they see some big waves on the water. They are called tide. Tom and Sue are curious. They want to go closer and see the tide. They leave their bikes and their ball and walk to the water.
"Look, Sue, the tide is coming and going. It is like a game," Tom says.
"Yes, Tom, it is fun. Let's throw some stones and see how far they go," Sue says.
They throw some stones and watch them splash. They laugh and clap. They are glad.
But then, they hear a loud noise. It is their dad. He is calling them.
"Tom! Sue! Come back! The tide is too strong. It is not safe. Hurry!"
Tom and Sue are scared. They drop their stones and run to their dad. He hugs them and tells them they are OK.
"Sorry, dad. We did not know. We just wanted to see the tide," Tom says.
"I know, son. But the tide can be dangerous. You have to be careful. Next time, stay with me or your mom. OK?" dad says.
"OK, dad. We are sorry," Sue says.
Dad smiles and takes them to their bikes and their ball. He says they can still play on the sand, but not too close to the water. Tom and Sue nod and say thank you. They are still glad. They love the beach.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom was a bit selfish. He always wanted to play with his toys alone and never shared with others. One day, his friend Sara came to play with him.
Sara said, "Tom, can I play with your toys, too?" Tom did not like this idea. He thought it was his opinion that only he should play with his toys. But Sara was sad, and she wanted to play with the toys as well.
Tom's mom saw them and said, "Tom, you should not be selfish. You should share your toys with your friends." Tom thought about it and decided to quit being selfish. He shared his toys with Sara, and they played together happily.
From that day on, Tom learned to share his toys and not be selfish. He and Sara became the best of friends, and they always played together. And Tom was happy because sharing made him feel good inside.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. He saw a small insect in the grass. The insect was green and had two big eyes. Tim was very surprised to see the insect. He bent down to look at it and said, "Hello, little insect!"
The insect looked at Tim and said, "Hello, big boy! I am a foolish insect. I lost my way home. Can you help me find my family?" Tim wanted to help the insect, so they started to look for the insect's family together.
As they walked, Tim and the insect talked and laughed. They looked under rocks and behind flowers. Then, they found the insect's family near a big tree. The family was so happy and surprised to see the insect! They all thanked Tim for his help. The foolish insect learned to be more careful and Tim made some new friends.

Once upon a time, there was a clever little bird. The bird lived in a tree near a big house. The house had a hanger outside where clothes were put to dry.
One day, the bird saw an apple on the ground. It was red and looked yummy. But when the bird tried to eat it, it tasted bad. The apple was old and had started to rot.
The clever bird had an idea. It picked up the rotten apple with its beak and flew to the hanger. It put the apple on the hanger to let it dry in the sun. After a few days, the apple was dry and not rotten anymore. The bird ate the apple and was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big turkey named Tom. Tom lived on a farm with many other animals. One day, Tom felt scared because he heard the farmer talk about Thanksgiving.
Tom did not want to be a part of Thanksgiving. He wanted to be free and play with his friends. So, Tom made a plan to release himself from the farm. He asked his friends, the cow, the pig, and the sheep, to help him open the gate.
With their help, Tom was able to release himself and run away from the farm. He found a new home in the woods with other wild turkeys. Tom was happy and free. The farmer missed Tom, but he learned that it was important to let animals be happy and free too. The moral of the story is to be kind and help others, even if they are scared.

Once upon a time, there was a furry cat named Tom. Tom liked to play with a sack. One day, Tom found a napkin inside the sack. He was very happy.
Tom took the napkin and played with it all day. He ran around the house and jumped up and down. The napkin was so much fun. Tom's friends, a dog and a bird, saw him playing and joined him.
After a long day of playing, Tom and his friends were tired. They all laid down on the soft, furry rug for a nap. They had a great day playing with the sack and the napkin. They all slept happily, dreaming of more fun days to come.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to count. She would count everything she saw. One day, Lily went to the park with her mom. It was an icy day. The ground was cold and white. Lily thought it was fun.
At the park, Lily saw a big box of plastic toys. The toys were all different colors. Lily was so happy! She started to count the plastic toys. She counted one, two, three, four, five, and six toys. Lily liked the red toy the best.
Lily played with the plastic toys in the icy park. She made a big tower with them. Her mom watched her play and smiled. They had a fun day at the park. When it was time to go home, Lily hugged her mom. She could not wait to count more things at home.

Once upon a time there was a family who had a very special bowl. Everyone in the family was very envious of it.
One day Grandma and Grandpa were visiting for dinner. Grandma said, "Let me serve the dinner with that special bowl."
Grandpa smiled and said, "That's a great idea!"
So Grandma set out the special bowl and began serving dinner. She gave each person a big portion and they all enjoyed it very much.
Little Lucy the three year old said, "That was the best dinner ever!" She was so happy that she even clapped her hands.
Everyone smiled with joy and grandpa said, "We're lucky to have such a special bowl that can serve us up such a delicious dinner every time."
The whole family was envious but also happy that they had such a special bowl in their home.
The end!

Once upon a time, there were two amazing twins, Tom and Tim. They loved to play with their toys all day. Every day, they would take out all their toys and make a big mess.
One day, their mom said, "Let's organize your toys!" Tom and Tim thought that was a good idea. They put all the cars in one box, all the blocks in another box, and all the dolls in a third box. Now, everything was neat and tidy.
The amazing twins were very happy. They could find their toys easily now. They played together and had lots of fun. Their mom was proud of them for organizing their toys. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small park, there was a gray swing and a long pipe. The swing loved to play with the kids, and the pipe liked to watch them have fun. They were good friends.
One sunny day, a little boy named Tim came to the park. He saw the gray swing and said, "Hi, Swing! Can I play with you?" The swing was very happy and said, "Yes, Tim! I love to play with kids!" Tim sat on the swing, and it went up and down, up and down. He was having so much fun.
Then, Tim saw the long pipe and said, "Wow, a big pipe! I want to play with you too!" The pipe was happy and said, "Climb on me, Tim!" Tim climbed on the pipe, and he slid all the way down. He laughed and played with the pipe and the gray swing all day long. The swing and the pipe were happy to have a new friend.

One day, a little octopus was playing in the sea. He felt helpless because he could not resist the strong waves. The waves pushed him far away from his home. The octopus was sad and scared.
A kind fish saw the octopus and asked, "Why are you sad, little octopus?" The octopus said, "I am lost and can't find my way home. The waves are too strong for me." The kind fish wanted to help the octopus.
The fish said, "Hold on to me, and I will swim with you." The octopus held the fish, and together they swam back to the octopus's home. The octopus was so happy and thanked the fish for the help. From that day on, they were the best of friends.

One sunny day, a lazy cat named Tom was lying under a big tree. He was very thirsty and needed water to drink. Tom looked around and saw a little girl named Emma playing near a small pond.
Tom walked to Emma and said, "Can you please help me? I need water to drink." Emma smiled and said, "Sure, I will help you, Tom." She took a small cup and filled it with water from the pond. Tom drank the water and felt much better.
After drinking the water, Tom and Emma played together near the pond. They became good friends and promised to play together every day. The lazy cat was not lazy anymore and always had water to drink because of his new friend, Emma.

One day, a little girl named Ann went to the park with her mom. She saw a big flag waving in the wind. Ann liked the pretty colors on the flag. She started to gaze at it for a long time. The flag made her feel happy.
Ann wanted to play a game with the flag. She started to spin around and around, trying to keep her eyes on the flag. After a while, Ann felt dizzy from spinning. She stopped and laughed.
Her mom came over and asked if she was okay. Ann said, "Yes, I am just a little dizzy from playing with the flag!" They both smiled, held hands, and walked home together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to draw. One day, he drew a big landscape with trees, a house, and a sun. Tim was very happy with his drawing. He wanted to attach it to the wall in his room, so he could see it every day. Tim felt safe when he looked at his drawing.
Tim's mom helped him attach the drawing to the wall. That night, Tim went to bed and looked at his drawing. He felt safe and happy. But then, something unexpected happened. The trees, house, and sun in the drawing started to move! Tim couldn't believe his eyes.
The trees waved at Tim, the house opened its door, and the sun smiled down on the landscape. Tim was scared at first, but then he realized that the drawing was still safe. It was a magic drawing! Tim smiled and waved back at the trees. From that day on, Tim knew that his drawing was special and it would always keep him safe.

Once upon a time, in a big purple house, there lived a kind mommy and her little girl, Lily. Mommy always loved to supply Lily with everything she needed. She gave her food, clothes, and lots of love. Lily loved her mommy very much too. They were always happy together.
One day, mommy told Lily that they were going to have a new baby. Lily was very excited. She couldn't wait to have a little brother or sister to play with. Mommy said that when the baby came, she would need to supply the baby with lots of love too. Lily promised she would.
A few months later, the baby came. It was a little boy! Lily was so happy. She remembered what mommy had told her about supplying love to the baby. So, she gave her little brother lots of hugs and kisses. The baby smiled at her. Lily knew that she was doing a good job. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sally. She liked to play near the river. The river was big and the sun made it look brilliant. Sally liked to throw stones in the river and watch them pass by. She had lots of fun doing this every day.
One day, Sally saw a big, red ball near the river. She wanted to play with it. She tried to pass the ball to herself, but it was too big. The ball rolled away and went into the river. Sally was sad, but she wanted to get the ball back.
Sally tried to reach for the ball, but she fell into the river. The water was deep and she could not swim. Sally was scared and cried for help. But no one was there to hear her. The river took her away and she never got her ball back.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, Bear and Squirrel. They had many fun adventures together.
One day, they went to the river to wash Bear's tail. Bear said to Squirrel, "My tail is so dirt, it needs to be washed." 
Squirrel said, "OK, let's wash it together."
So they got to the water and started to wash Bear's tail. It was so fun! The water was cool and the sun was warm.
Suddenly, a sneaky bird came and stole Bear's tail! "My tail!" shouted Bear. But it was too late. The bird was too fast.
Bear felt so sad. He had no tail. "It was so soft and sweet," he said sadly. 
Squirrel tried to make Bear happy by saying, "Let's go find another tail for you!" But Bear knew it wouldn't be the same. 
So Bear and Squirrel went home, and Bear never got his tail back.

Once upon a time, there was a smart little bunny named Bobo. Bobo lived in a big green forest. He loved to hop and play all day long.
One sunny day, Bobo found a long vine hanging from a tall tree. He thought it would be fun to swing on the vine. He grabbed the vine and swung back and forth, laughing happily.
While swinging, Bobo saw some pretty seeds on the ground. He decided to scatter them around the forest. He hopped from one spot to another, scattering the seeds everywhere. Soon, beautiful flowers grew all around the forest, making it even more lovely. Bobo was very happy with his work and continued to play every day.

Once upon a time, there was a big tent in the woods. A little boy named Tim went to the tent. He was scared, but he wanted to be brave. Inside the tent, there was a big, scary bear. The bear was sad and alone. 
Tim kneeled down and said, "Hello, Mr. Bear. Why are you sad?" The bear looked at Tim and said, "I am sad because I have no friends. Everyone is scared of me." Tim smiled and said, "I will be your friend, Mr. Bear. I am not scared of you."
From that day on, Tim and the bear played in the woods every day. They had lots of fun together. The bear was not scary anymore. He was now a happy bear with a friend. And Tim, the brave little boy, was happy too.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a boy named Tim. Tim loved to eat yummy candy. One day, Tim's mom said, "Let's make candy together."
Tim and his mom went to the kitchen. They put sugar, water, and yummy things in a big pot. Tim used a big spoon to stir the pot. He was very happy.
But, Tim's little sister, Sue, wanted to help too. Sue tried to stir the pot, but she was too small. She cried, "I want to stir too!" Tim didn't want to share. They both got mad.
Mom said, "We can take turns stirring the pot." Tim and Sue started to share. They took turns stirring and made the yummy candy together.
In the end, Tim and Sue learned that sharing was fun. They enjoyed the yummy candy they made together. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a bright, little bird. The bird had a red waist. It liked to sing and fly in the sky. One day, the bird saw a big tree. It wanted to sit on the tree.
The bird flew to the tree, but something strange happened. A big cat appeared! The cat wanted to catch the bird. The bird was very scared. It did not want to be caught by the cat.
But then, something unexpected happened. The cat did not want to hurt the bird. The cat just wanted to be friends. The bird was happy. Now, the bird and the cat are best friends, and they play together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a big cat named Tom. Tom was very lazy and loved to sleep all day. One day, Tom was walking in the park when he saw a crazy bird named Polly. Polly was flying around and singing a funny song.
Tom looked at Polly and said, "Why are you so crazy, Polly?" Polly stopped flying and said, "I am happy because I have a big heart. I love to sing and make others happy too."
Tom yawned and said, "I like to sleep, but I want to be happy like you." Polly smiled and said, "You can be happy too, Tom. Just open your heart and play with others."
Tom and Polly became good friends. They played together every day. Tom learned to be happy and not just sleep all the time. And Polly's big heart made everyone in the park happy too.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a sleepy cat named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play outside in the spring. The sun was warm, and the flowers were pretty. Fluffy had many friends, like the happy dog and the little bird.
One day, Fluffy's friend, the happy dog, mentioned a big tree in the woods. The big tree had a hole in it. Fluffy was curious and wanted to see the tree. The happy dog said not to go, but Fluffy didn't listen.
Fluffy went to the woods and found the big tree. Fluffy climbed up and saw the hole. But when Fluffy looked inside, a big, mean bear came out! The bear chased Fluffy all the way home. Fluffy was scared and never went to the woods again.

Tommy was feeling bad. He hated the page he was trying to do. It had lots of writing on it and Tommy found it very hard. He wanted to cry.
Tommy's mum saw he was feeling bad and said, "Don't worry Tommy. You know, it's OK to feel mad sometimes. You don't have to like the page you're doing, if you don't want to."
Tommy smiled. He didn't have to like the page or do the writing just because someone said so. He felt much better!
"You don't have to do anything you don't want to Tommy, as long as it's not bad for you," said his mum.
Tommy nodded. He still didn't like the page so he moved on to some of the other things he could do. He liked playing with his toys and singing songs better than doing pages he didn't like.
So, Tommy had some fun and he forgot all about the page he had been trying to do. He was sure he would never hate it again.

Once upon a time, there was a little aeroplane. The aeroplane was blue and loved to fly high in the sky. One day, the aeroplane saw a big, icy cloud. The aeroplane was scared but wanted to see what would happen if it flew through the icy cloud.
The aeroplane said, "I am scared, but I want to see what happens in the icy cloud." So, the brave little aeroplane flew into the icy cloud. Inside the cloud, it was very cold and the aeroplane started to shake.
When the aeroplane came out of the icy cloud, something amazing happened. The aeroplane had turned into a beautiful, shiny, silver aeroplane! The aeroplane was so happy and said, "I am so glad I went through the icy cloud. Now I am shiny and silver!" And the aeroplane flew happily ever after.

Once there was a little girl named Jenny. She was very intelligent and liked to pray every morning and night. One day, Jenny knelt down on the floor and put her hands together and closed her eyes.
She prayed, "Dear God, please help me always do my best and be a smart girl".
Suddenly, Jenny heard a voice say,"Yes, Jenny. I will do that for you". Jenny looked around, trying to find the voice, but nobody was in her room. 
The voice spoke again and said, "Jenny, look up at the ceiling". Jenny looked up and was amazed to see a beautiful angel with wings, smiling at her.
The angel said, "I am here to answer your prayers. God loves you and will help you be a smart, intelligent girl". Jenny thanked the angel and said goodbye.
