One day, it rained a lot outside. The store floor became wet. A little girl named Lucy came into the store with her mom. Lucy saw the jewel and wanted to look at it. Her mom said to be careful because the floor was wet.
As Lucy walked to the jewel, she slipped on the wet floor. She reached out her hand to stop her fall. Her hand hit the glass box, and the box opened. The pretty jewel fell out and landed in Lucy's hand. Everyone in the store was surprised. They thought the jewel would break, but it did not. The store owner was happy, and he let Lucy keep the pretty jewel because she saved it.

Sam and Mia were playing in the backyard. They liked to pretend they were soldiers and march around with sticks and hats. They made loud noises and sang songs.
"Let's go to the garage and find more things to play with," Sam said.
"OK, but we have to be quiet. Mom and Dad are working inside," Mia said.
They opened the door to the garage and saw a big box. It was smooth and shiny. They wondered what was inside.
"Maybe it's a new toy for us," Sam said.
"Or a surprise for our birthday," Mia said.
They lifted the lid and peeked inside. They saw a lot of bubble wrap and a small statue. It looked like a man with a beard and a robe.
"Who is that?" Sam asked.
"I don't know. He looks funny," Mia said.
They took out the statue and looked at it. It was heavy and cold. They saw a tag on it that said "Fragile. Handle with care."
"What does that mean?" Sam asked.
"I don't know. Maybe it means we can play with it," Mia said.
They decided to march with the statue and pretend he was their leader. They put a hat on his head and a stick in his hand. They made more loud noises and sang more songs.
They did not see Mom and Dad coming to the garage. They did not hear them gasp and shout.
"Sam! Mia! What are you doing?" Mom yelled.
They turned around and saw Mom and Dad looking angry and scared. They saw them run to the box and look inside.
"That's a very expensive and rare sculpture. It belongs to your uncle. He lent it to us for a week. He's a collector and an artist. He loves it very much. How could you take it out and play with it?" Dad said.
Sam and Mia felt bad. They did not know they did something wrong. They did not know the statue was important. They thought it was just a funny toy.
"We're sorry, Mom and Dad. We didn't know. We just wanted to have fun," Sam said.
"We didn't mean to hurt it. We thought he liked to march with us," Mia said.
Mom and Dad sighed. They looked at the statue and saw it was not broken or scratched. They were relieved. They looked at Sam and Mia and saw they were sad and sorry. They were not mad anymore. They were glad they were safe.
They hugged them and said, "It's OK, Sam and Mia. We know you didn't know. But you have to be more careful and respectful of other people's things. You can't just take them out and play with them. You have to ask first and listen to the rules. Do you understand?"
Sam and Mia nodded. They said, "We understand, Mom and Dad. We're sorry. We love you."
They put the statue back in the box and closed the lid. They said goodbye to the man with the beard and the robe. They said they were sorry to him too.
They went back to the backyard and played with their own toys. They still liked to march and sing, but they did it more quietly and gently. They learned their lesson.

One day, a boy named Tim found a big chest in his room. He did not know where it came from. Tim was very curious about what was inside the chest. He tried to open it, but it was locked. He asked his friend, Sam, to help him open the chest.
"Sam, can you help me open this chest?" Tim asked. Sam said, "Yes, let's try to break the lock!" They tried to destroy the lock with a big rock, but it did not work. They felt sad and did not know what to do.
Then, Tim's sister, Lily, came into the room. She saw the chest and said, "That chest is fake! I made it for a game. The lock is not real. You can open it with a simple twist." Tim and Sam were surprised. They twisted the lock, and the chest opened. Inside, they found fun toys and games that Lily had put there. They were happy and played with the toys together.

Once upon a time, there was a squirrel. This squirrel was very wealthy. He had many nuts in his home. One day, he went for a walk in the forest.
As the squirrel walked, he saw a big tree. The squirrel wanted to climb the tree to find more nuts. But as he started to climb, he began to stumble. He tried and tried, but he could not climb the tree.
A kind friend saw the squirrel and helped him. The squirrel shared his nuts with the friend. They became best friends and played together every day. And the squirrel never stumbled again.

Once upon a time, there was a little engine named Tim. Tim loved to collect things. He would go around the town and look for things to add to his collection.
One day, Tim met a big engine named Max. Max was very patient and kind. Tim asked Max, "Can you help me collect more things?" Max said, "Of course, Tim! Let's go together."
Tim and Max went around the town, collecting many things. They found pretty rocks, shiny buttons, and colorful flowers. Tim was very happy with his new friend and their collection.
At the end of the day, Tim said, "Thank you, Max, for helping me collect so many things." Max smiled and said, "You're welcome, Tim. I had fun with you today."
From that day on, Tim and Max were the best of friends. They continued to collect things together, and they always had a great time.

Once upon a time there was a young boy called Pete. He loved to play games and dreamed of being a great player.
One day his father gave him a new game. It was a rough game that involved rolling a ball around. Pete was very excited to try it out so he took the ball out to the garden and started to play.
He was having so much fun that the ball flew away and landed in a pile of leaves. Pete peered into the pile but he couldn't see the ball. He looked under the leaves and then he saw a rabbit!
The rabbit was scared and he ran away. Pete had a bright idea - he started to shake the pile of leaves and eventually the ball yielded.
He was so pleased that he shouted for joy! He had gotten his ball back and he thanked the rabbit for yielding it.

Once there was a little girl named Lucy. She was three years old and loved to explore different places.
One day, Lucy was looking out the window in her house. She saw the traffic go by and thought it was so interesting!
Lucy wanted to get out and explore, so she put on her shoes and went outside. There were lots of cars and trucks driving around.
Lucy watched the traffic go by. She liked watching the different colors and sizes of the cars.
Lucy wanted to get closer to the traffic, so she walked slowly and carefully across the street. She was careful to stay out of the way of the cars and trucks.
Finally, Lucy made it to the other side of the street. She had lots of fun exploring the traffic, and she learned a few things along the way. She was very proud of herself!

Once upon a time there was a black bird. Every morning the bird would wake up at the same time.
The bird would chirp and flutter. It would fly around in the black sky, even on rainy days. 
The bird flew faster and faster. It flew so far, until it couldn't fly any more.
When the bird was tired, it landed in the forest. The black bird had a good sleep and dreamed of flying again. 
When the bird finally woke up it flew away. Its wings were strong and it flew higher and higher. Eventually, the bird flew back home.
The bird's adventure had come to an end. The black bird was happy to be home and safe.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy rod that he loved to play with every day. He would run around the house with it and pretend to catch fish.
One day, Tim was playing with his rod when he saw a big, scary shadow on the wall. He was very scared and started to cry. He didn't know what to do, so he held his rod tight and hid under his bed.
Tim's mom heard him cry and came into the room. She saw the scary shadow too, and then looked out the window. She saw that it was just a tree branch moving in the wind. She told Tim not to be scared and helped him tear the shadow away. Tim felt better and went back to playing with his rod, happy that the scary shadow was gone.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was an obedient cat named Bella. She loved to play with her ball all day. One sunny day, Bella saw a cobweb in the corner of the room. She was curious and wanted to touch it.
Bella tried to balance on her back legs to reach the cobweb. She stretched her paw out, but it was too high. She tried again and again, but she could not touch the cobweb. Bella felt sad.
Then, Bella had an idea. She found her ball and put it under her feet. She stood on the ball and tried to balance again. This time, she could reach the cobweb. Bella was happy and proud of herself. She learned that if she tried hard, she could do anything.

Once upon a time, there was a little mouse. She loved to record things in her diary. One day she had a great adventure and wanted to write it down. 
She snuck into a kitchen and found a jar of cocoa. It was so yummy and smelled so good. She grabbed a little cup to carry it and ran away from the kitchen. 
She recorded her adventure in her diary with a pencil and paper. She wrote about how she found the cocoa and how she ran away with it. 
The little mouse was so proud of her great adventure and her diary was full of exciting stories. All because she had a great record of everything she had done in her little life!

Once upon a time, there was a little alligator. He was upset because he did not have any friends to play with. He lived in a pond with many fish, but they were all too scared to play with him.
One day, the alligator saw a small bird flying near the pond. He said, "Hi, bird! Will you be my friend?" The bird replied, "Sure, alligator! I can be your friend." The alligator was very happy.
The alligator and the bird played together all day. They played catch with a ball. When the alligator tried to catch the ball, he accidentally used his sharp teeth and tore the ball. The bird was not upset. Instead, they laughed and found a new game to play. From that day on, the alligator and the bird were best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He was very eager to eat a big ice cream from the refrigerator. He asked his mom, "Can I have ice cream, please?" His mom said, "Yes, but only after dinner."
Tim could not wait. He thought he could make the ice cream disappear before dinner. He opened the refrigerator and took the ice cream. He ate it all very fast. His tummy started to hurt.
His mom found out that the ice cream was gone. She was sad and told Tim, "You should have waited for dinner. Now, you will not have dessert for tonight." Tim felt bad and learned that he should wait for the right time to eat treats.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a little boy named Tim and his mom. Tim had a weird toy. It was a green ball with big eyes. He loved to hug it all the time. One day, Tim's mom said, "We are going to the park, Tim. Bring your toy!"
At the park, Tim played with his weird toy. He threw it up in the air and caught it. Then, he gave it a big hug. Suddenly, a big wind came and took the toy away. Tim was sad and started to weep. "Don't cry, Tim," said his mom. "We will find your toy."
They looked everywhere for the toy. Tim was still weeping when a little girl came up to him. "Is this your toy?" she asked, holding the weird green ball. Tim's eyes lit up and he hugged the girl. "Thank you so much!" he said. The girl smiled and said, "I saw it fly away and wanted to help." Tim and the girl became good friends, and they all played together at the park.

One day, a little chubby cat named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. Tim thought, "I bet I can see many things from up there!" So, he started to climb the tree.
As Tim climbed, he saw a bird sitting on a branch. The bird said, "Hi, Tim! What are you doing?" Tim said, "I want to see many things from up here!" The bird said, "Be careful, Tim! Don't grab the weak branch." Tim looked at the bird and nodded.
Tim kept climbing higher and higher. Then, he saw a weak branch. He remembered what the bird said. So, he did not grab it. Tim reached the top of the tree and looked around. He saw a beautiful point far away. He was very happy that he listened to the bird.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily had a green blanket that she loved very much. She took her blanket everywhere she went.
One day, Lily's mommy said they were going to meet a new friend. Lily was excited and wanted to introduce her green blanket to the new friend. She held her blanket tight and went with her mommy.
When they got to the park, Lily saw a little boy named Tim. Tim was playing with a red ball. Lily went up to Tim and said, "Hi, I'm Lily. This is my green blanket." Tim smiled and said, "Hi, I'm Tim. I like your blanket." They played together all day and became best friends.

One day, a little girl named Mia went for a walk. She saw a light cloth on the ground. It was very pretty. Mia picked it up and took it with her.
Mia saw her friend, Tim, playing with a ball. She showed him the light cloth. Tim liked it too. They decided to play a game with the cloth and the ball.
Mia and Tim threw the ball and caught it with the cloth. They had a lot of fun. The sun was shining and they played all day. Mia was happy she found the light cloth and could share it with her friend.

Lila and Sam liked to play in the garden. They had a big tent that they could put up and pretend they were camping. They had a lot of fun with their toys and books inside the tent.
One day, Lila and Sam had a fight. Lila wanted to play with the doll, but Sam wanted to play with the car. They both said "mine" and pulled the toy. The toy broke and they both cried. They were very angry with each other and said "I don't want to play with you anymore!"
Lila and Sam went to different corners of the garden. Lila took the doll and Sam took the car. They sat alone and did not talk to each other. They felt very lonely and sad.
After a while, Lila looked at the tent. She missed playing with Sam. She thought maybe they could fix the toy and say sorry. She walked to the tent and saw Sam sitting inside. He looked at her and said "Hi, Lila."
Lila said "Hi, Sam. I'm sorry I broke the toy. Can we be friends again?" Sam said "I'm sorry too. I was mean to you. Yes, we can be friends again." They hugged and smiled. They decided to work together and complete the toy. They used some tape and glue and made it look like new. They were very happy and proud.
They played with the toy and had fun in the tent. They said "We are the best friends ever!" They were not lonely anymore. They were very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big old tree in a small park. The tree was very old and had many branches. One day, a pretty bird came and lay a nest on a branch of the tree. The bird was very happy because she would soon have a family.
In the park, there was a nice couple who loved to visit the tree. They liked to sit under the tree and talk. One day, they decided to have a marriage under the tree. They invited their friends and family to the park. Everyone was happy and they all had a big party.
The pretty bird watched the marriage from her nest. She saw the couple and their friends dancing and laughing. The bird was happy to see the love under the tree. The old tree felt happy too, because it was a part of their special day. And so, the tree, the pretty bird, and the couple all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She had a favorite cushion that she loved very much. The cushion was soft, and it had a picture of a cat on it. Sue took her cushion everywhere she went.
One day, Sue met a boy named Tim. Tim saw Sue's cushion and said, "I like your cushion! Can I eat it?" Sue laughed and said, "No, you can't eat my cushion! Cushions are for sitting, not for eating!"
Tim and Sue played together all day, and they became good friends. They played with the cushion and had lots of fun. When it was time to go home, Sue told Tim, "You can come play with me and my cushion again tomorrow!" And they did, and they were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Tim. He had a big, soft cushion that he loved very much. He would sit on it, hug it, and tell it all his secrets.
One day, Tim was telling his cushion a story. Just then, something unexpected happened! The cushion began to tell Tim a story back! Tim was very surprised. He had never heard a cushion talk before.
As they talked more and more, Tim found out that the cushion was a magic cushion. It could tell him stories from all around the world. Tim and the cushion became best friends, and they shared many fun stories together.

Once upon a time, in a far away land, there was a great big sun. The sun loved to shine down on the land and make everything warm and bright. One day, a big cloud came and covered the sun. The sun could not shine anymore, and everything became dark and cold.
The animals were very sad. They missed the sun's warm light. They decided to march to the cloud and ask it to go away. The big lion, the tall giraffe, the fast cheetah, and the small mouse all marched together to the cloud. They worked together as a team.
When the animals reached the cloud, they asked it nicely to go away. The cloud saw how much the animals loved the sun and how they worked together. The cloud felt bad and decided to move away. The sun started to shine again, and everything became warm and bright. The animals were very happy and learned that working together can solve big problems.

Once upon a time, there was a smooth cat named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play with a soft ball. She would run and jump all around the house with the ball. Her motion was very fast and fun to watch.
One day, Fluffy saw a big dog named Spot. Spot saw Fluffy's ball and wanted to play too. But, Spot did not ask Fluffy if he could play. Instead, he did a bad thing. Spot tried to steal the ball from Fluffy.
Fluffy did not like that. She told Spot, "That is not nice! You should ask before you take my ball." Spot felt sorry and said, "I am sorry, Fluffy. Can we play together?" Fluffy agreed, and they both played with the ball, having lots of fun. From that day on, Spot never tried to steal again, and they became good friends.

One day, a girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. Lily loved to play on the swings and slide. She also loved to run around with her friends. Today, Lily saw a new girl at the park. The new girl looked sad and alone. Lily wanted to help.
Lily went to the new girl and said, "Hi, my name is Lily. What is your name?" The new girl looked up and said, "My name is Rose." Lily asked, "Do you want to play with me and my friends?" Rose smiled and said, "Yes, I would love that."
As they played, Lily noticed that Rose's shoes were very tight. Rose's feet hurt, but she didn't want to stop playing. Lily had an idea. She ran to her mom and asked if they could give Rose her extra shoes. Lily's mom agreed. They gave Rose the new shoes and her feet felt much better. The girls played all day and became good friends.
The twist in the story is that Lily was able to help Rose by giving her extra shoes, even though she was a new friend. The moral value of the story is that it's important to be kind and help others, even if they are new friends or strangers.

One day, a little pupil named Tim went to school. He had a heavy bag on his back. In the bag, there was a big bottle of water. Tim loved to drink water all day.
At school, Tim played with his friends. They ran and jumped, and Tim got very thirsty. He went to his heavy bag and opened it to drink his water. But, something unexpected happened! The bottle was empty. Tim was sad and did not know what to do.
Then, his friend Lucy came to him with a big smile. She had a twist in her hand - it was Tim's water bottle cap! Lucy had played a trick on Tim and drank all his water. They laughed and shared Lucy's water. From that day on, Tim and Lucy became best friends and always shared their drinks.

Once there was a cow. She had lots of milk. Every day, the cow gave milk to the farmer. The farmer was very successful.
One day, the cow stopped giving milk. The farmer said to the cow, "Why don't you give milk anymore?"
The cow said, "I'm tired and sad. I need to rest." 
The farmer said, "If you don't give milk, I won't be successful. You must remain here and give milk."
The cow said, "I'm sorry, but I can't."
The farmer got angry. He said, "If you don't give milk, you must leave."
The cow sadly left the farm. The farmer was no longer successful, and he had no milk.

Lily and Max were playing in the living room. They liked to build forts with the cushions from the sofa and the chairs. They had a lot of fun making walls and roofs and doors.
But then they had a problem. There was only one big cushion left, and they both wanted it for their fort. Lily said, "I saw it first. It's mine!" Max said, "No, it's mine! I need it more!" They started to pull the cushion from both sides.
"Stop it!" Mom said, coming into the room. "You are making a mess and a noise. Why are you fighting over the cushion?"
"Because it's the best cushion!" Lily and Max said at the same time.
Mom looked at the cushion. It was old and worn and had a stain on it. She did not think it was the best cushion. She said, "How about this? You can take turns with the cushion. Lily, you can have it for ten minutes, and then Max, you can have it for ten minutes. And then you can switch again. That way, you can both use it for your forts. Does that sound fair?"
Lily and Max thought about it. They did not like to share, but they did not want to fight either. They nodded and said, "Okay, Mom. We will take turns."
Mom smiled and said, "Good. I'm glad you agree. Now, let me set the timer for ten minutes. And remember, be nice to each other and have fun."
Lily and Max said, "Thank you, Mom." They hugged her and then went back to their forts. They decided to make a bridge with the big cushion, so they could visit each other's forts. They had a great time playing together.

Once upon a time, there was a little red train with a big engine. It liked to go fast and make loud noises. One day, the little red train saw a big blue train. The big blue train was very shiny and new. The little red train felt jealous.
The little red train said to the big blue train, "I can go faster than you!" The big blue train just smiled and said, "We will see about that." They decided to have a race to see who was the fastest. The little red train wanted to win so much that it started to ignore the other train.
During the race, the little red train went very fast, but it did not see the big rock on the tracks. The big blue train saw the rock and stopped. The little red train hit the rock and stopped too. The big blue train helped the little red train get back on the tracks.
After that, the little red train was not jealous anymore. It learned that it was better to be friends and help each other. The two trains became best friends and went on many adventures together, always looking out for each other.

Once upon a time, there was a little flower. The flower was scared to bloom. It was shy and did not want to show its pretty colors to the world.
One day, the flower saw a shiny diamond on the ground. The diamond was very bright and made the flower feel brave. The flower thought, "If the diamond can shine so bright, maybe I can bloom and show my colors too."
So, the scared little flower took a deep breath and started to bloom. It showed its beautiful colors to the world. Everyone loved the flower and the shiny diamond. The flower was not scared anymore, and it bloomed happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a sad man named Tom. Tom was miserable because he lost his favorite thing, a bottle of wine. He looked everywhere for it, but he couldn't find it. Tom was so sad that he started to cry.
A little girl named Sue heard Tom crying. She went to him and asked, "Why are you sad?" Tom told her about his lost wine. Sue wanted to help Tom. She said, "Don't worry, I will help you find your wine."
Sue and Tom looked everywhere for the wine. They looked under the bed, behind the couch, and in the kitchen. Finally, they found the wine in the garden. Tom was so happy! He thanked Sue for her help.
Tom and Sue sat in the garden and drank the wine together. They laughed and talked all day. Tom was not miserable anymore. He was happy because he had a new friend to help him. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely bookshelf. It was sad because it had no books to hold. One day, a little girl named Lily saw the bookshelf and decided to make it happy.
Lily had an idea. She went to her room and found her favorite books. She knew the books would make the bookshelf feel better. She put the books on the bookshelf, one by one. The bookshelf started to smile.
But then, a mean boy named Tom came and took all the books away. The bookshelf was sad again. Lily knew she had to do something. She talked to Tom and told him how the bookshelf felt. Tom didn't know the bookshelf was lonely. He felt bad and gave the books back.
Lily put the books back on the bookshelf. The bookshelf was so happy, it felt like it would burst. From that day on, the bookshelf was never lonely again. And Lily, Tom, and the bookshelf became the best of friends.

One day, a cute little cat named Kitty went to the kitchen. She wanted to find something to eat. She looked at the fridge and thought, "Maybe there is something yummy inside!"
Kitty opened the fridge and found a big surprise. There was a big cake inside! "Wow!" said Kitty. "I love cake!" She took the cake out of the fridge and put it on the table.
Just then, her friend Doggy came into the kitchen. "Hi, Kitty!" said Doggy. "What are you doing?" Kitty smiled and said, "I found a big surprise in the fridge! Look, a cake!" Doggy and Kitty were very happy. They sat down and ate the cake together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Daisy. Daisy was a very curious girl, and she always wanted to know what was around her. One day, Daisy found an interesting tube. She wondered what it was and wanted to find out. Daisy asked an adult to help her. The adult told Daisy that the tube was full of surprises and to wrap it up in some paper.
So, Daisy got some colorful paper and wrapped the tube carefully. Then, she ran back to the adult to ask what was inside. The adult said that Daisy would need to wait until she was ready. Daisy had to be patient and wait until it was time.
Finally, the day came when the tube was ready. Daisy impatiently opened it. Inside, she found lots of surprises! She was so excited about all the special presents inside the tube. Daisy smiled from ear to ear as she hugged the tube and thanked the adult for helping her.

Lily and Ben were friends who liked to play outside. One day, they saw a big gray animal with a long nose and wide ears. They did not know what it was.
"Hello, what is your name?" Lily asked the animal.
The animal looked at them and made a loud sound. "I am Ellie. I am an elephant. I live in the zoo, but I got lost. Can you help me find my way back?"
Lily and Ben nodded. They wanted to help Ellie. They took her hand and walked with her. They asked people if they knew where the zoo was. Some people were scared of Ellie, but some people were nice and showed them the way.
After a while, they reached the zoo. Ellie was happy to see her friends and family. She thanked Lily and Ben for helping her. She gave them a hug with her nose.
"Bye, Ellie. We are glad you are safe. You are a good elephant," Lily and Ben said.
"Bye, Lily and Ben. You are good children. You are brave and kind. Come and visit me anytime," Ellie said.
Lily and Ben waved goodbye and went home. They learned that animals have names and feelings too. They also learned that helping others is a good thing to do. They felt proud and happy.

Molly was a cute, little girl. She was three years old and had bright, twinkling eyes. Every day, her parents took her to the backyard to play. Her backyard was a huge, green field full of tall grass and wildflowers. 
One day, Molly saw something wonderful in her backyard. It was an adorable puppy! He was white and fluffy and had the cutest little face. Molly was so excited. 
Molly's mom and dad took the puppy home. They named him Fluffy and was very happy to have him in their family. 
Every day, Molly and Fluffy went to the backyard to play. Molly would load Fluffy up with her toys and they would chase each other around the garden. Fluffy was so adorable that Molly couldn't help but smile. 
Molly had the best times with her new friend, Fluffy. They ran around and played all day in the backyard until it was twilight and it was time to go in. Molly and Fluffy were two best friends that always had fun.

Once upon a time, in a small, humble house, there lived a little girl named Mia. Mia loved her soft pillow very much. Every night, she would hug it tight and sleep with a big smile.
One sunny day, Mia saw a tiny bird outside her window. The bird looked cold and lost. Mia wanted to help the bird, so she opened her window and said, "Welcome, little bird!" The bird flew inside and sat on Mia's pillow. Mia felt happy that she could help the bird.
Mia and the bird became best friends. They played together every day. The bird liked to sit on Mia's pillow and sing songs. Mia would clap and dance to the music. They were very happy in their humble home.

One day, a cheerful boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big slide and wanted to play on it. He ran to the slide and climbed up the steps. At the top, he saw a little girl named Sue.
"Hi," said Tim. "Do you want to play with me?"
Sue smiled and said, "Yes, let's play on the slide. It's my favorite!"
They both went down the slide and ran to the swings. They were having so much fun. But then, Tim felt a tickle in his nose. He tried to hold it in, but he couldn't. He sneezed really loud!
"Ah-choo!" Tim sneezed.
Sue laughed and said, "Bless you! Let's go play in the sand now."
They went to the sand and found a small shovel. Tim said, "Look, I found a shovel! We can dig a hole and make a sand mine."
They dug and dug until they had a big hole. They were very happy with their sand mine. Tim and Sue played all day at the park, and they became good friends.

One day, a little car named Zoom went for a ride. Zoom loved to go fast. He would zoom up and down the hills, laughing all the way. Zoom was very happy.
As Zoom was driving, he saw a big pit. It was full of mud and looked disgusting. Zoom was curious and wanted to see what was inside. So, he went closer to the pit.
Zoom slipped and fell into the pit. He was covered in mud and felt disgusting. But then, his friends came to help. They pulled Zoom out of the pit and cleaned him up. Zoom was happy to be out of the pit and back with his friends. And from that day on, Zoom stayed away from disgusting pits.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy animal named Bobo. Bobo liked to clean his home every day. One day, Bobo found a big box while he was cleaning. He did not know what was inside, but he knew it was special.
Bobo put the big box in a safe place and went to play with his friends. They played all day long. But Bobo could not stop thinking about the big box. He told his friends about it, and they all wanted to see what was inside.
The next day, Bobo and his friends went to the safe place to open the big box. They were all very excited. When they opened the box, they found many fun toys inside! Bobo and his friends played with the toys all day and had a great time. They were happy that Bobo found the big box while he was cleaning.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there lived a boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside with his friends. One day, Tim found a rough tool that could spring high into the air. He was very happy and wanted to show his friends.
Tim went to his friends and showed them the rough tool. They all took turns making it spring high into the sky. They had lots of fun, but soon, they started to argue about who could use the tool. The friends became very sad and stopped playing together.
Tim's mom saw the sad friends and told them, "Sharing is caring. If you all share the rough tool and play together, you will be happy again." The friends listened to Tim's mom and started sharing the tool. They played together, watched the tool spring high, and were happy again. The moral of the story is that sharing makes everyone happy.

Mia and Leo like to play in the snow. They make a big igloo with snow blocks. They go inside the igloo and pretend they are penguins. They waddle and slide and flap their arms.
One day, they hear a funny sound outside the igloo. They peek out and see a big white bear. The bear is playful. He rolls and jumps and sniffs the snow. He sees the igloo and comes closer. He is curious.
Mia and Leo are scared. They hide in the igloo and hope the bear will go away. But the bear does not go away. He pokes his nose in the igloo and growls. He wants to play.
Mia and Leo have an idea. They start to sing a song. They sing loud and happy. They sing about snow and ice and fish and friends. The bear likes the song. He stops growling and starts humming. He wags his tail and smiles.
Mia and Leo are brave. They come out of the igloo and sing with the bear. The bear is gentle. He licks their faces and nuzzles their hair. He is their new friend. They play and sing in the snow until it is time to go home. They hug the bear and say goodbye. They promise to come back and sing with him again.

Anna liked to draw. She had many colors and papers. She drew shapes and animals and people. She liked to make symbols. Symbols are things that mean something else. Like a heart means love, or a star means wish.
One day, Anna went to the park with her mom. She saw a boy with a big hat. He was drawing too. He had a pencil and a notebook. He drew symbols that Anna did not know. They looked like letters, but different. Anna was curious. She went to the boy and said hello.
The boy looked up and smiled. He said hello back. He told Anna his name was Leo. He said he was from another place. He said his symbols were his language. He said he could teach Anna some. Anna was happy. She said yes please.
Leo and Anna sat on the grass. Leo showed Anna his notebook. He pointed to a symbol and said what it meant. Anna repeated after him. She learned some new words. Leo learned some of Anna's words too. They laughed and had fun. They were playful. They drew more symbols and pictures. They made a new friend. They were glad they met.

Little Tommy was playing in his backyard one day when he found a big spade. He held it in his hands and he felt scared but also excited. He ran to his mom to show her.
"Mommy, look what I found!" he said.
His mom smiled and said, "That's a spade! Wow, you found a great one!"
Little Tommy was so happy and he held it tight. He loved the feeling of holding it in his hands.
"I'm going to look for more spades!" he said.
His mom said, "Okay, but be careful and stay close by. Don't be scared, okay?"
Little Tommy nodded and ran off to find more spades. He was sure that he was going to love each adventure.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a powerful girl named Lily. She had a big, friendly dog named Zero. They loved to play together in the park.
One sunny day, Lily and Zero were playing catch. Lily would throw the ball far and Zero would run to get it. They were having so much fun. Then, the ball went near a big tree.
When Zero went to get the ball, he saw a little bird stuck in the tree. The bird was scared and couldn't fly. Zero wanted to help, so he barked loudly. Lily heard Zero and came running to see what was wrong.
Lily saw the bird and knew she had to help. She shouted, "Zero, help me save the bird!" With her powerful arms, she lifted Zero up to the tree. Zero gently grabbed the bird with his mouth and brought it down. The bird was happy and flew away. Lily and Zero felt proud and went back to playing catch, knowing they had done a good thing.

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim found a hidden cord in his room. It was behind a big box. Tim was very curious. He wanted to know what the cord was for.
Tim pulled the cord and a door opened. A talking cat came out of the door. The cat said, "Hello! My name is Kitty. Thank you for finding me. Can you help me find my hat?"
Tim and Kitty looked all around the room. They looked under the bed and on top of the shelf. Finally, they found the hat behind a big chair. Tim gave the hat to Kitty.
Kitty was so happy. She said, "Thank you, Tim! You are a good friend." Tim and Kitty started to clap and dance. They had so much fun together. And from that day on, Tim and Kitty were the best of friends.

One day, a girl named Amy went to the park. She saw her friend, Tom. Tom was playing with a toy car. Amy wanted to play too. She asked Tom, "Can I use your toy car?" Tom said, "Yes, but be gentle with it."
Amy and Tom played with the toy car. They had fun. Then, they saw a big hill. They wanted to race the toy car down the hill. Amy put the car on the hill and let it go. The toy car went very fast down the hill. But, the car hit a rock and broke.
Amy and Tom were sad. They did not know what to do. Then, a nice man came. He had a bag of salt. He said, "Do not worry. I can help you." The man put some salt on the broken toy car. Something unexpected happened. The toy car was fixed! Amy and Tom were very happy. They said, "Thank you!" and played with the toy car again.

Once upon a time, there was a boy who loved to write. He had a big and impressive book that he liked to write in every day. The boy also loved food, and he would write about all the yummy food he ate.
One day, the boy saw a big, red apple on the table. He wrote in his book, "Today, I will eat a big, red apple." But, he did not know that the apple was not good. The apple looked nice, but it was bad on the inside.
The boy took a big bite of the apple. It did not taste good, but he did not want to stop eating it. He wanted to finish the apple, so he could write about it in his book. But after he ate the whole apple, the boy felt sick. His tummy hurt, and he did not want to write anymore. The boy learned that sometimes, even things that look impressive can be bad.

One day, a playful cat named Tom saw a big ashtray. The ashtray was round and shiny. Tom wanted to play with it.
Tom turned the ashtray with his paw. It spun around and around. Tom liked to watch it spin. He felt happy and excited.
Tom's friend, a little girl named Sue, saw him playing. She said, "Tom, what are you playing with?" Tom meowed and showed her the ashtray. Sue laughed and said, "That's funny! Let's play together."
So, Tom and Sue played with the ashtray all day. They took turns spinning it and watching it go around. They had lots of fun and were very happy.

Jill was walking in the park. The sun was shining and the grass was a bright green. She sees a frog sitting by the pond. 
"Hello, frog," said Jill with a big smile on her face. 
The frog hopped away, but he didn't seem to be scared. He just seemed like he was in a hurry. 
Jill kept walking and saw some ducks swimming in the pond. They were quacking and having fun. She was missing her swimming pool back home, but it was still nice to watch.
Suddenly, she saw something moving on the ground. She knelt down and saw a caterpillar walking across the grass. Its body was green and it was slowly moving along. 
Jill stayed with the caterpillar and watched until it crawled out of sight. She knew that she was going to miss the caterpillar and all the other animals she had seen. 
Jill got up and continued her walk in the park. She already knew that she wanted to come back again soon.

Once there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was playing with his ball in the park. He saw a stranger who was sad. The stranger had a big bag of candy but he had no friends to share it with. Tim thought the stranger might be fun to play with.
Tim went up to the stranger and said, "Hi, I'm Tim. Do you want to play with me and share your candy?" The stranger smiled and said, "Yes, I would like that. My name is Mr. Brown."
They played together and ate the candy. But soon, Tim started to feel bad. He remembered his mom told him not to talk to strangers. Tim began to regret not listening to his mom. He felt ashamed for not doing what she said.
Tim told Mr. Brown, "I'm sorry, but I need to go home now. My mom said not to talk to strangers." Mr. Brown understood and said, "That's okay, Tim. It's important to listen to your mom. I hope we can play again someday when your mom knows me too." Tim went home and told his mom everything. She was happy that he learned his lesson and would listen to her now.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a generous baker. He made the best pastry. Everyone loved it. The baker promised to give one pastry to every child in the town.
One day, a little girl named Lucy came to the bakery. She was so excited to get her pastry. The baker smiled and gave her the biggest pastry he had. Lucy was so happy.
Lucy took her pastry and shared it with her friends. They all loved it. The generous baker was happy to see the children enjoy his pastry. And he kept his promise to give a pastry to every child in the town.

Once upon a time, there was a gentle cat named Fluffy. Fluffy liked to supply her friends with toys. She had a pattern of giving them to her friends every day.
One day, Fluffy met a new friend named Spot, a small dog. Fluffy said, "Hi, Spot! I want to give you a toy. What do you like?" Spot said, "I like balls to play with!" Fluffy was happy and went to get a ball for Spot.
But when Fluffy came back, she saw that Spot had found a big pile of toys! Spot said, "Look, Fluffy! I found all these toys for us to share!" Fluffy was surprised but happy. They played with the toys together and became best friends.

One day, a little boy named Tom went to the park with his mom. They saw a big, green avocado tree. Tom's mom said, "Look, Tom! It's an original avocado tree!" Tom looked up and saw lots of avocados on the tree. He wanted to pick one, but they were too high up.
Tom had an idea. He saw a big puddle near the tree. He ran and jumped into the puddle. "Splash!" Tom's jump made the water go high up. The water touched the avocado tree's branches. This happened again and again. Tom's mom watched and smiled.
Finally, one avocado fell down from the tree. Tom picked it up and showed it to his mom. "I did it!" he said. His mom was proud of him. They took the avocado home and shared it. Tom learned that when he tried, he could do many things.

Lila and Ben are twins. They like to play in the garden. They have a small store. They sell herbs to their friends. Herbs are plants that smell good and taste good.
One day, a new friend comes to their store. His name is Sam. He has a big hat and a long coat. He looks unique. He says, "Hello, I am Sam. I like your store. Can I buy some herbs?"
Lila and Ben smile. They say, "Hello, Sam. We are Lila and Ben. We have many herbs. What do you want?"
Sam looks at the herbs. He sees mint, basil, rosemary, and thyme. He says, "I want mint. Mint is my favorite herb. It smells fresh and makes me happy."
Lila and Ben pick some mint for Sam. They put it in a paper bag. They say, "Here you go, Sam. That will be one coin, please."
Sam gives them a coin. He says, "Thank you, Lila and Ben. You have a nice store. I will come back soon."
Lila and Ben say, "You are welcome, Sam. We are happy to meet you. Come back anytime."
Sam waves goodbye. He walks away with his mint. He smells it and smiles. He thinks, "Lila and Ben are nice twins. They have a unique store. I like their herbs."

One day, a little boy named Tim found a big, shiny cube. It was very modern and had lots of colors. Tim wanted to play with the cube, but he didn't know how to organize the colors.
Tim asked his friend, Sue, "How do I make the colors look nice?" Sue tried to help Tim, but she didn't know how to organize the cube either. They both felt sad because they couldn't fix the cube.
Then, Tim's big sister, Jane, came to help. She said, "Let's all try together!" They worked as a team and organized the cube's colors. Tim, Sue, and Jane were so happy that they solved the problem. They played with the modern cube all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a brilliant red jacket. He loved to wear it every day. It was his favorite jacket.
One day, Tim went to the park to play. He played with his friends and had lots of fun. But when it was time to go home, he could not find his jacket. Tim was sad because he did not want to lose his favorite jacket.
So, Tim and his friends started to look for the jacket. They looked under the slide, behind the swings, and even in the sandbox. At last, they found the jacket near the big tree. Tim was so happy to get his jacket back. He thanked his friends for helping him and wore his brilliant red jacket all the way home.

Lily and Tom were friends who liked to pick berries in the forest. They had a big basket full of red, blue and black berries. They wanted to sell them to the people in the town and buy some candy.
They walked to the market and set up a small table. They put a sign that said "Berries for sale. One coin for five berries". They waited for customers to come.
Soon, a lady came and looked at their berries. She smiled and said "Hello, children. What a lovely basket of berries you have. Can I buy some?"
Lily and Tom nodded and said "Yes, please. One coin for five berries. They are very good and sweet."
The lady gave them a coin and took five berries. She tasted one and said "Mmm, these are incredible berries. They are so juicy and tasty. Where did you find them?"
Lily and Tom told her about the forest and how they picked them. The lady said "You are very clever and lucky. Thank you for sharing your berries with me. Have a nice day."
Lily and Tom thanked her and waved goodbye. They were happy that they sold some berries and made a new friend. They hoped to sell more berries and buy some candy. They also wanted to go back to the forest and pick more incredible berries.

Sam and Mia were playing in the garden. They liked to pretend they were lions and tigers. They would run and jump and roar at each other. They had a lot of fun.
One day, they found a big jug in the shed. It was empty and had a handle and a spout. Sam had an idea. He said to Mia, "Let's fill the jug with water and use it as a fountain. We can be lions and tigers who drink from the fountain."
Mia liked the idea. They took the jug to the tap and filled it with water. Then they carried it to the middle of the garden and put it on the ground. They took turns to tilt the jug and make the water come out of the spout. They pretended to lap up the water with their tongues and make happy noises.
They were having so much fun that they did not hear their mum calling them for lunch. She came out to the garden and saw them playing with the jug. She smiled and said, "You two look like very thirsty lions and tigers. Do you want to come inside and have some sandwiches and juice?"
Sam and Mia nodded and ran to their mum. They gave her a hug and said, "Thank you, mum. We love you." They left the jug in the garden. It was peaceful and quiet. The sun shone on the water and made it sparkle. The jug was happy too. It had made two new friends.

One day, a bright sun was shining in the sky. A boy named Tim saw a big yacht in the water. He wanted to go on the yacht and play.
Tim ran to his mom and said, "Mom, can we go on the yacht?" His mom smiled and said, "Yes, we can go on the yacht today."
They went on the yacht and Tim saw a big bird. The bird was loud and it yelled, "Hello!" Tim yelled back, "Hello, bird!" They had a fun day on the yacht under the bright sun.

Sara and Tom are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big mouse. The mouse has a piece of cheese in its mouth.
"Look, Sara, a mouse!" Tom says. "It has cheese. I like cheese. Do you like cheese?"
"Yes, I like cheese too," Sara says. "But the mouse is scary. It has sharp teeth. I feel afraid. Do you feel afraid?"
Tom nods. He feels afraid too. He holds Sara's hand. They back away from the mouse.
"Shoo, mouse, shoo!" Tom says. "Go away. Leave us alone. The cheese is not yours."
The mouse looks at them. It does not shoo. It does not go away. It makes a loud squeak. It runs towards them.
Sara and Tom scream. They run away too. They run to the slide. They climb up the slide. They slide down the slide. They feel safe. They feel fine.
"Are you okay, Sara?" Tom asks.
"I'm okay, Tom," Sara says. "Are you okay?"
"I'm okay, Sara," Tom says. "That was a bad mouse. It was mean. It wanted our cheese."
They hug each other. They smile. They do not see the mouse anymore. They see their moms. Their moms have snacks for them. They have cheese and crackers. They have juice and cookies.
"Come on, Sara, Tom," their moms say. "It's time for a snack. You must be hungry."
Sara and Tom run to their moms. They are hungry. They are happy. They eat their cheese and crackers. They drink their juice and cookies. They share their snack. They share their story.
The end.

John was out in the park one day with his mom. He saw a big orange bird flying in the sky. He wanted to follow it and he asked his mom if he could take it. His mom laughed and said, "No, you cant take away the bird."
John was disappointed, but then he noticed something else. There was a big rock sitting near a tree with a message written on it. He asked his mom if he could take it. She smiled and said, "Yes, of course you can take the message."
John was excited and quickly ran to the rock. He read the message on the orange rock out loud: "Keep smiling and have a good day!" John bellowed out a loud "Yay!" and ran to his mom to tell her the exciting message he had found. 
His mom smiled, gave him a big hug, and told him, "That's a wonderful message, John. Let's always remember it."

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toy soldiers. He would pretend the soldiers were in a war. Tim was a lucky boy because he had many friends to play with.
One sunny day, Tim went to the park. He saw a girl playing with her dolls. Her name was Lily. Tim wanted to meet her, so he walked up to her and said, "Hi, I'm Tim. Do you want to play with me?"
Lily smiled and said, "Sure, Tim! Let's play together." They decided to play war with Tim's toy soldiers and Lily's dolls. They had lots of fun, and their toys became friends too.
As the sun began to set, Tim and Lily knew it was time to go home. They said goodbye and promised to meet again at the park. Tim felt lucky to have a new friend, and he couldn't wait to play with Lily again.

Once upon a time, a boy named Tim found a weird guitar. It was big and had many colors. Tim loved to play with it. He made fun sounds by strumming the strings. One day, he met a girl named Sue who also liked the guitar.
"Can I play with your guitar?" Sue asked. Tim said, "Yes, but we need to unlock it first." They looked for a key to unlock the guitar case. They found the key under a rock. Tim and Sue were happy.
They unlocked the guitar case and played with the weird guitar. They made fun music and danced. Tim and Sue became good friends. They played with the guitar every day and had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play with his toys and eat yummy food. One day, Tim saw a big refrigerator in the kitchen. He wanted to see what was inside.
Tim opened the refrigerator and found a big cake. He believed the cake was for him, so he ate some. But, he felt ashamed because he did not ask his mom if he could have some cake. Tim knew he should always ask before taking something.
Tim went to his mom and said, "I ate some cake from the refrigerator. I'm sorry, I should have asked first." His mom smiled and said, "It's okay. I'm glad you told me. Next time, just ask." Tim felt better and promised to always ask before taking something.

Once upon a time, there was a popular dog named Spot. Spot loved to read. He would read all the time. He read books about cats, birds, and even fish. Spot was very happy when he read.
One day, while Spot was reading, there was a big earthquake. The ground shook and the trees moved. Spot was scared, but he knew what to do. He hid under a big table until the shaking stopped.
After the earthquake, Spot went outside. He saw that his friends were safe too. Spot was happy that everyone was okay. He told his friends about the books he read and how they helped him be brave during the earthquake. Now, all his friends wanted to read too.

Once upon a time, there was a wise fairy. She lived in a big tree in a pretty forest. The wise fairy had a special seat. This seat was magic. When someone sat on it, they would get a gift.
One day, a little boy came to the forest. He was lost and sad. The wise fairy saw the boy and wanted to help him. She knew that her magic seat could give him a gift to make him happy.
The wise fairy showed the boy her magic seat. The boy sat on the seat, and he got a gift. It was a map to help him find his way home. The boy was so happy and thanked the wise fairy. He followed the map and found his way back to his family.

One day, a cat found a big sack. The cat was very curious. It wanted to know what was inside the sack. The cat opened the sack and saw a lot of toys. The cat was very happy and wanted to play with the toys.
The cat took the toys to a big puddle. It put the toys in the puddle and played with them. The toys got wet and dirty. The cat did not care. It kept playing with the toys in the puddle. The cat had a lot of fun.
After a while, the toys became terrible. They were all broken and dirty. The cat was sad. It wanted to play with the toys again, but it could not. The cat should have taken care of the toys. Now, the cat has no toys to play with.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a pale girl named Lily. She loved to play with her doll and dress it up in pretty clothes. Lily liked fashion a lot. She had many dresses and shoes for her doll.
One day, Lily and her doll went to the park to play. They had so much fun running and playing with other kids. Lily was very happy. Then, it was time to go home. Lily put her doll in the bag and shut it tight.
When Lily got home, she opened the bag to play with her doll again. But, the doll was not there! Lily was very sad. She looked everywhere in her room but could not find her doll. Then, she heard a noise coming from the bag. Lily looked inside and saw a real, tiny girl wearing her doll's clothes! The girl said, "Hi, I am your doll. I turned into a real girl to play with you forever!" Lily was so surprised and happy. They played together all day and became best friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a nail on the ground. He picked it up and played with it. He felt happy to have found something new. Tim saw his big sister, Anna, frown at him. She looked guilty.
"Why do you frown, Anna?" Tim asked.
"I am sorry, Tim. I lost that nail when I was fixing my toy," Anna said. She felt guilty for making Tim feel bad.
Tim smiled and gave the nail back to Anna. They both felt happy again. They played together with their toys, and Anna fixed her toy with the nail. They shared their toys and had a fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a key that could open any door. Tim was very happy with his key. He liked to store it in a small box under his bed. Tim was a persistent boy, and he always tried to open new doors with his key.
One day, Tim found a big door in the woods. He took out his key and tried to open it. The door was very old and hard to open, but Tim was persistent. He tried and tried, and finally, the door opened. Behind the door, there was a dark room.
Tim was scared, but he wanted to see what was inside. He went into the dark room, and the door closed behind him. He tried to use his key to open the door again, but it didn't work. Tim was stuck in the dark room, and he could not get out. He was very sad and wished he had never found the big door in the woods.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to go to the store with his mom. One day, they went to the store to buy some toys.
At the store, Tim saw a big toy car. He wanted it so much. But when he looked at the price, it had increased. Tim was very upset. He said to his mom, "Mom, I want this car, but it costs more now."
His mom was kind and understood. She said, "Don't be upset, Tim. We can save more money and come back to the store later to buy the car." Tim felt better and they went home.
After a few weeks, they saved enough money and went back to the store. Tim was so happy when he could finally buy the big toy car. He played with it all day and forgot all about being upset.

Once upon a time, there was a hunter. He was very ugly. He lived in a small house near the woods. Every day, he went to the woods to find food. He looked for animals to eat. He was not a happy man.
One day, the ugly hunter found a big frog in the woods. The frog was green and slimy. The frog looked at the hunter and said, "If you kiss me, I will turn into a beautiful princess." The hunter was very surprised. He had never heard a frog talk before.
The ugly hunter thought about it. He decided to kiss the frog. He closed his eyes and gave the frog a big kiss. But, something unexpected happened! Instead of the frog turning into a princess, the ugly hunter turned into a handsome prince! The frog laughed and hopped away. The hunter, now a prince, was very happy. He went back to his small house and lived happily ever after.

One day, a shy cat named Tom went to the park to have some fun. He saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. Tom started to climb the tree, but he was scared. He heard a whistle sound and looked around to see where it was coming from.
A little bird was sitting on a branch, whistling a happy song. The bird saw Tom and asked, "Why are you scared, friend?" Tom said, "I am shy and scared to climb this big tree." The bird smiled and said, "I will help you, just follow me and whistle like I do."
Tom started to whistle like the bird, and he felt less shy. He climbed the tree with the bird, having fun and whistling all the way up. When they reached the top, they saw a big surprise. There was a big party with many animals, all whistling and having fun.
Tom was so happy to see the party and was not shy anymore. He made many new friends and had lots of fun. From that day on, Tom was never scared to climb trees, and he always remembered to whistle a happy song.

Once upon a time there was a happy little girl. She was marching around her house, feeling very excited. She had a hook in her hand which she loved. She used the hook to pick up things, give hugs and even help make food.
One day, the little girl went on a long march through the woods. She was so excited! She kept her hook close by in case she needed it.
The little girl eventually arrived at a big lake. It was so peaceful and beautiful. She was so grateful to have come and see it.
The little girl decided to use her hook to catch some fish. After a few tries, she caught one! She was so proud of herself and even more grateful for her hook.
The little girl was so happy that she had gone on her march that day. Even though she was tired, she was also very content. She picked up her hook and marched back home.

Anna and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to slide, swing and run. But they also liked to look for bugs and flowers. They had a small box to keep their finds.
"Look, Anna, I found a purple flower!" Ben said, holding a tiny blossom.
"Wow, it is so pretty! Can I see?" Anna asked, reaching for the flower.
Ben gave her the flower and she smiled. She put it in the box with a green leaf and a red bug.
"Let's find more!" Ben said, and they ran to another spot.
They found a yellow flower, a blue bug and a brown leaf. They were very happy with their box.
But then they saw a big dog. It was black and white and had a loud bark. It ran towards them, wagging its tail.
"Uh-oh, Anna, look!" Ben said, pointing at the dog.
"Uh-oh, Ben, run!" Anna said, grabbing the box.
They ran to the slide, but the dog followed them. It jumped on the slide and slid down with them. It licked their faces and wagged its tail.
"Stop, dog, stop!" Ben said, trying to push the dog away.
"Go away, dog, go away!" Anna said, holding the box tight.
But the dog did not listen. It wanted to play. It saw the box and sniffed it. It smelled the flowers and the bugs. It opened its mouth and took the box.
"Hey, dog, no!" Ben said, pulling the box.
"Give it back, dog, give it back!" Anna said, pulling the box too.
But the dog did not let go. It pulled the box too. It tore the box apart. The flowers and the bugs flew out. The dog ate some of them and spat some of them out.
"Bad dog, bad dog!" Ben said, crying.
"Mean dog, mean dog!" Anna said, crying too.
They lost their box and their finds. They were very sad.
Then they heard a voice.
"Rover, Rover, come here!" The voice said.
It was a man. He was the dog's owner. He saw the dog and the kids. He saw the torn box and the flowers and the bugs.
"Oh, I'm so sorry, kids. Rover didn't mean to hurt your things. He just wanted to play. He likes to play with everything. He even plays with my pants. He makes them purple with his tongue. See?" The man said, showing them his pants.
They were purple indeed.
The kids looked at the man and the dog. They looked at his pants. They stopped crying. They started to giggle.
"Your pants are purple!" Ben said, laughing.
"Your dog is silly!" Anna said, laughing too.
The man smiled. He was glad they were not mad. He gave them a new box. He gave them some cookies. He said he was sorry again.
The kids forgave him and the dog. They ate the cookies. They played with the dog. They found new flowers and bugs. They put them in the new box.
They were happy again.
They learned to settle with what they had. They learned to share and to forgive. They learned to laugh and to play.
They had a good day.

Once upon a time, in a big blue sea, there lived a playful little boat. The boat loved to sail all day long. It would go fast, then slow, then fast again. The boat had so much fun!
One sunny day, the boat met a friendly fish. The fish swam up to the boat and said, "Hello, boat! Let's play together!" The boat was very happy and said, "Yes, let's play! We can sail and swim all day long."
After playing for a long time, the boat and the fish got hungry. They decided to have a snack. The boat had some yummy bread, and the fish had some tasty seaweed. They shared their food and enjoyed the snack together.
The boat and the fish became best friends. They would sail and swim together every day. And they always had a playful and fun time.

One day, a big dog named Max was playing in the park. He saw a little girl named Lily with a box. The box had a clear top, so Max could see inside. Lily was happy to show Max what was in the box. She opened it and showed him many colorful balls in a nice order.
Max liked the balls and wanted to play with them. But Lily told Max, "We must keep them in order, so we do not lose any." Max did not listen and took the balls out to play. The balls went everywhere, and they were not in order anymore.
Lily was sad, but Max had an idea. He helped Lily find all the balls and put them back in the box. They worked together to make the order nice again. In the end, Max learned that it is important to listen and follow the rules. And Lily learned that sometimes, even big dogs need help too.

Once upon a time, there was a group of eager toddlers. They were always on the lookout for any fun activity that they could do together. One day, they gathered around their teacher and asked, "Teacher, can we do something fun?". Their teacher smiled and said, "Yes, why don't you rub your tummies with both hands?"
The toddlers were excited and started to rub their tummies. None of them wanted to stop, so they kept going and going. They rubbed and rubbed until they started to get tired.
Suddenly, one of the toddlers got too eager and he rubbed so hard that he started to cry. The others in the group immediately stopped and they gathered around their crying friend.
The teacher came to the rescue. She knelt down and comforted the toddler while gently taking his hands away from his tummy. She said, "It's ok. You can be eager, but you must be gentle." 
The group of toddlers looked relieved and they all promised to be more gentle when rubbing their tummies. The group went back to having fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who loved to sing. She lived in a country where all the birds liked to sing too. They would sing together every day.
One day, the little bird found a tree that looked dead. It had no leaves and no other birds sang on it. The little bird felt sad for the tree and decided to sing to it.
Every day, the little bird sang to the dead tree. Soon, the tree started to grow leaves and other birds came to sing too. The dead tree was happy again, all because of the little bird's singing.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max liked to organize his toys. He had a toy made of steel. It was his favorite. Max was very proud of his toys.
One day, Max went to play with his friend, a cat named Lucy. Lucy saw Max's steel toy and wanted to play with it too. Max was ashamed because he did not want to share his favorite toy. But Lucy was sad, so Max decided to share.
Then, something unexpected happened. When Max and Lucy played with the steel toy, it broke in two. Max was very sad, but Lucy had an idea. They took the broken toy to a wise old owl. The owl fixed the toy, and now Max and Lucy could both play with it. Max was happy he shared, and they all played together.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who liked to fly. The bird had a friend named Fan. Fan could not fly, but he could spin around fast. Fan liked to help his friends stay cool on hot days.
One day, the bird saw a light up in the sky. It was very pretty and shiny. The bird wanted to touch the light, so it started to fly higher and higher. But the light was too far away. The bird felt sad and came back down.
Then, Fan had an idea. He started to spin very fast, making a big wind. The bird flew into the wind and, suddenly, the bird could fly higher and higher! The light came closer and closer. It was not a star, but a shiny kite! The bird was so happy, and it was all thanks to Fan.

One day, a little girl named Sue went to the kitchen. She saw a big mixer on the table. Sue thought it was boring. She wanted to play with her toys instead.
Sue's mom said, "Sue, we need to use the mixer to make a cake. Can you help me?" Sue nodded and put her toys away. She stood next to her mom, ready to help.
Sue's mom turned on the mixer. It went round and round. Sue put in the cake mix. The mixer made a funny noise. "Stop!" Sue said, laughing. Her mom turned off the mixer. They both smiled and finished making the cake. Sue learned that the mixer was not boring after all.

Once there was a little girl who was very excited. She was about to go on a picnic with her family and she couldn't wait. Her Mom allowed her to carry her own basket. She ran outside and started gathering all of the things she wanted to take on the picnic. 
But as soon as she stepped outside, she saw a crazy monkey. It was jumping around, screeching and climbing on things. The little girl was so scared that she dropped her picnic basket! 
Mommy heard the noise and went to check on her. She asked her daughter, "What happened?" 
The little girl told her mom about the crazy monkey, and Mommy said, "Well, that's too bad. We can't take the picnic now. You know better than to go outside alone, so no picnic for you today." 
The little girl was so disappointed and she wanted to cry. But instead, she simply said, "Okay, Mommy." She sadly picked up her basket and went back inside, not even wanting to look at the crazy monkey again.

Once upon a time, in a warm summer, there lived a little boy named Tim. He loved to play outside with his friends. They would run, jump, and laugh all day. One day, Tim saw a new kid in the park. He was unknown to Tim and his friends.
Tim walked up to the new kid and said, "Hi! I am Tim. Do you want to play with us?" The new kid smiled and said, "Yes, I am Sam. I would love to play with you!"
They all played for a long time. After a while, they got tired. Tim said, "Let's take a nap under the big tree." Sam and the other friends agreed. They lay down under the tree and took a short nap.
When they woke up, they felt better and played some more. Tim and Sam became best friends. From that day on, they played together every summer day. And they always took a nap under the big tree when they were tired.

Tom and Sam were friends who liked to ride their bikes. One day, they saw a big temple on a hill. They wanted to go there and see what was inside.
"Let's ride our bikes to the temple," Tom said. "It looks fun and cool."
"OK, but we have to be careful," Sam said. "The road is steep and bumpy. We have to wear our helmets and hold our bikes tight."
They put on their helmets and started to ride up the hill. They went fast and slow, up and down, left and right. They had fun and laughed.
But when they got to the temple, they saw a sign that said "No bikes allowed". They felt sad and confused.
"Why can't we ride our bikes here?" Tom asked. "We came all this way to see the temple."
"Maybe because the temple is old and special," Sam said. "Maybe the bikes can hurt the temple or make it dirty. We have to respect the temple and follow the rules."
Tom nodded. He understood. He and Sam parked their bikes outside the temple and walked inside. They saw many beautiful things and learned many new things. They were happy and amazed.
They thanked the temple and went back to their bikes. They rode down the hill carefully and safely. They smiled and talked.
"We had a good adventure today," Tom said. "We rode our bikes to the temple and saw something new."
"Yes, we did," Sam said. "And we learned something too. We learned to be careful, respectful and curious."
They hugged and went home. They were good friends who liked to ride their bikes.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Mia had a doll. Mia loved her doll very much. Every day, Mia played with her doll in the park. One day, Mia saw a big tree that was easy to climb. She thought it would be fun to play with her doll up in the tree.
Mia climbed the tree with her doll. She was very happy. But then, something happened. A big bird came and took her doll. Mia was very sad. She wanted her doll back. The bird flew far away with the doll.
Mia thought of a plan to get her doll back. She found a stick and waited for the bird to come back. When the bird came back, Mia tried to strike the bird with the stick. But, the bird dropped the doll before Mia could hit it. The doll fell into a bush, and Mia found a new toy hidden in the bush. Mia was happy to have her doll back and now she had a new toy to play with too. The big bird flew away, and Mia learned to be careful when playing in the tree.

One morning, a big cat named Tom was very sad. It did not rain for many days, and the grass was dry. Tom wanted to find water for his friends, so he left his home to look for it.
Tom walked and walked until he saw a big tree. Behind the tree, he found a big, shiny rock. Tom was very happy because he thought the rock could help him. He picked up the rock and started to walk back to his friends.
But then, something unexpected happened. The shiny rock started to shake and made a loud noise. Suddenly, water came out of the rock! Tom was very surprised. He ran back to his friends and told them about the magic rock. Now, they all had water to drink and the dry grass turned green again. Tom and his friends were very happy and played together all day.

Molly and Tom were great friends. Every day, they would gather together and play. One day, Tom had an idea. He said, "Let's build a fort over the faucet!" Molly was envious - she hadn't thought of such a great plan.
So the two best friends began to work together. They piled up blankets and stuffed animals. They spread out blankets and pillows inside the fort. They even built a secret door.
When they finished, they had the most fun adventure ever. They pretended they were brave pirates, searching for treasure. And they drank water from their secret faucet inside the fort.
Molly and Tom stayed there until it was time to go home. They hugged and said goodbye, not realizing they had made such a wonderful day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big, soft chair. It was so comfortable that everyone liked to sit on it. In that town, there was also a judge who wore a big hat. One day, the judge decided to go and sit in the big, soft chair.
On the way to the chair, the judge saw a little girl. She was playing with her toy, but she looked sad. The judge asked her, "Why are you sad?" The girl said, "I lost my toy's hat, and now she's cold." The judge felt bad for the girl, so he gave her his big hat. The girl was so happy, she let out a little scream of joy.
Later, the judge went to sit in the big, soft chair. But when he sat down, he felt something was missing. He remembered he gave his hat to the little girl. Just then, the girl came running with a small, soft pillow. She said, "Thank you for the hat, now you can be comfortable too." The judge smiled and put the pillow on the chair. They both sat on the chair, feeling happy and comfortable.

Once upon a time, there was a playful dog named Max. Max loved to run and jump all day long. He had many friends, and they would play together in the big park.
One day, Max met a new friend named Sam. Sam was a cat who knew a special spell. Max was very curious and wanted to learn the spell too. Sam said, "To do the spell, you must say 'meow' three times and spin around."
Max tried the spell, and suddenly, he started to shiver. He shivered and shivered, and then something magical happened. Max turned into a cat, just like Sam!
Max and Sam played together as cats, jumping and running in the park. They had so much fun, and Max loved being a cat for a day. When the sun went down, Max said "meow" three times and spun around again. He stopped shivering and turned back into a dog. Max and Sam were happy to be friends, no matter if they were a dog or a cat.

Once upon a time, there was a fragile zero. It glowed so brightly that it lit up the sky like a star. Everyone wanted to hold it, even though it was so fragile.
One day, a little girl named Mia saw the zero. She reached out and touched it, and it felt so warm and comforting in her hands. She smiled and hugged it so tightly, but the zero was still so fragile.
Mia wanted to take the zero home, so she picked it up and ran. Everyone shouted to her not to drop the zero, and to be careful, but she didn't notice.
When she reached home, the zero was still glowing in her hands. It lit up her room, and everyone crowded around it. They all said it was beautiful.
Mia smiled and hugged the zero again. She knew it was fragile, but it also made her feel very special. From that day on, she always kept the zero close.

Once upon a time there was a woman who was going on an adventure. She was very proud that she was going to do something very special.
Before she left on her adventure, she went to the store to pick the things she would need. She had to pick the special foods she wanted to bring.
When she had everything she needed, she took it back home. There, she prepared her food for the journey. She wanted to make sure it would last for her special day.
Once she was all prepared, she said goodbye to her family. Her family was very proud of her and wished her a safe trip.
"You be careful," said her father. "And have a great time!" 
"I will!" said the woman and she smiled.
With that, she set off on her adventure, feeling proud and ready to go.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play with toys and books. One day, they find a big book with many pictures. The book is about history. History is what happened before they were born.
Lily and Tom look at the pictures. They see people and animals and things. Some pictures are old and some are new. Some are happy and some are sad. Lily and Tom ask questions about the pictures. They want to know more about history.
Lily and Tom see a picture of a thin girl. She has no shoes and no coat. She looks cold and hungry. Lily and Tom feel sorry for her. They wonder why she is so thin. They care about her. They wish they could help her.
Lily and Tom turn the page. They see a picture of a big cake. It has candles and frosting and fruit. It looks yummy and fun. Lily and Tom smile. They wonder who is having a birthday. They hope the thin girl can have some cake too.
Lily and Tom close the book. They hug each other. They are happy to have toys and books and friends. They are happy to learn about history. They are happy to care about others. They say thank you to the book. They say thank you to each other. They say thank you to history.

Once there was a boy who was very stubborn. He wouldn't listen to anyone. One day, he was walking down the street when he came across a big puddle. He was determined to jump over the puddle. But when he got to the edge of the puddle, he paused. He thought it looked very big, like it might be too hard to jump over. 
He kept pausing, wondering what to do. Finally, he decided to go around it. As he walked around the puddle, he noticed a very small one. He smiled and decided it was worth trying to jump over it. 
So he ran and jumped, but he missed. He tried again, but he still missed. He was so stubborn and determined, he kept trying until finally he did it. He smiled with pride. He knew that being stubborn could get him far.

Jill looked at the broken toy on the floor. She didn't know why it was broken.
"Guess what I saw outside?" said her mom.
"A cat," Jill said, her eyes getting wide.
"Oh, you guessed it," said her mom.
Jill jumped up and grabbed her coat. She was excited at the thought of playing with a cat.
When Jill and her mom got outside, the cat was already far away. Jill looked at the broken toy and wondered if the cat had anything to do with it.
"Maybe the cat knocked it over," said Jill.
Her mom smiled. "You can guess all you want, but we'll never know why it's broken," she said.
Jill thought about it for a moment and then smiled. "Let's go play with the cat," she said. 
Her mom laughed and agreed. Together, they went on their way, in search of the friendly cat.

One day, a little seed was in the ground. It wanted to grow big and strong. The sun was shining and the rain fell. The seed started to grow. It grew into a small plant. The plant was very happy.
One day, a bird came to visit the plant. The bird said, "Do you want to go on a journey?" The plant was excited. "Yes, I want to go on a journey!" The bird told the plant about a distant place with big trees and pretty flowers.
