The plant wanted to go, but it could not move. The bird had an idea. It picked up the plant and carried it on its back. They went on a long journey to the distant place. The plant saw many new things.
When they reached the distant place, the plant was amazed. It saw the big trees and pretty flowers. The plant was very happy. The bird put the plant down in the soft ground.
The plant grew bigger and stronger in its new home. It made many new friends. The plant thanked the bird for taking it on the journey. They were friends forever.

Once upon a time, there was a fish named Bob. Bob lived in a reef. The reef had many colors and pretty things. Bob had a lot of friends in the reef. They liked to play and swim all day.
One day, a new fish came to the reef. His name was Max. Max was big and rude. He did not say "please" or "thank you." Max did not want to be friends with the other fish.
Bob said to Max, "Why are you so rude? We can be friends if you are nice." Max thought about it and said, "Okay, I will try to be nice." Max started to complete tasks for others, like helping them find food.
Soon, Max was not rude anymore. All the fish in the reef were friends. They played and swam together every day. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a tough helicopter was flying in the sky. The helicopter liked to help people. It would keep them safe when they needed it. The helicopter saw a little boy who was lost.
The little boy looked up and saw the helicopter. He said, "Helicopter, can you help me find my mom?" The helicopter smiled and said, "Yes, I will keep you safe and help you find your mom."
They looked and looked for the little boy's mom. The helicopter flew high and low. Finally, they found her. The little boy was happy to see his mom. The tough helicopter had kept him safe, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there was a little boy. His name was Sam. One day Sam decided to explore the jungle. He remembered his mom telling him to be careful.
As Sam crept along the path, he saw a lion! It was big, but Sam knew he could be brave. He remembered his mom saying that lions were easy to scare away.
Sam called out to the lion, "Hello there! Please don't be scared, I won't hurt you!"
The lion said, "What do you want? I am very hungry!"
Sam thought for a moment and then said, "I have some yummy food in my pocket. You can have it if you want."
The lion was very happy and said, "Thank you, Sam! You are very kind!"
Sam smiled, remembering the lion's thank you. He ran home, knowing he had been brave.

Lily and Ben were very happy. They were going to the zoo with Mom and Dad. They wanted to see the animals, especially the rabbits. They liked rabbits because they were soft and cute and they could hop.
At the zoo, they saw many animals. They saw lions and tigers and bears. They saw monkeys and elephants and giraffes. They saw birds and fish and snakes. But they did not see any rabbits. They asked Mom and Dad, "Where are the rabbits?"
Mom and Dad said, "The rabbits are in a special place. They are very popular, so many people want to see them. We have to wait in a long line to get in. Do you want to wait?"
Lily and Ben said, "Yes, yes, we want to see the rabbits!"
So they waited in the long line. They waited and waited and waited. They were bored and tired and hungry. They asked Mom and Dad, "When can we see the rabbits?"
Mom and Dad said, "Soon, soon, we are almost there."
But they were not almost there. The line was very slow. Some people left the line. Some people pushed and shouted. Some people cried and complained. Lily and Ben did not like the line. They did not like the people. They did not like the zoo.
Finally, they got to the front of the line. They were excited. They thought they would see the rabbits. They thought they would pet them and feed them and watch them hop. But they did not see the rabbits. They saw a sign. The sign said, "Sorry, the rabbits are gone. They escaped last night. We are looking for them. Please come back another day."
Lily and Ben were very sad. They did not see the rabbits. They did not have fun. They wasted their time. They wanted to go home. They said, "This is the worst day ever!"

Once upon a time, there was a farmer. He lived with his cows on his farm. One day, he noticed that one of the cows was walking funny. He realised that the cow had a broken leg. So, the farmer was very sad.
The farmer looked for help. He called upon his neighbour, who was a kind and compassionate man. The neighbour said he knew what to do. He went to his shed and brought out a toolbox. The neighbour got to work and tried to fix the cow's leg.
The neighbour worked hard to mend the cow's leg. The farmer was very thankful. As time went on, the cow's leg healed. The kind neighbour had used his compassion and the tools from his toolbox to help the farmer and the cow.
At the end of the story, the farmer learned a lesson. He vowed to always be kind and compassionate, just like his neighbour. Being kind and helpful could fix many things.

Once upon a time, in a normal town, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily liked to play with her friends outside. One day, she saw a tall post near her house. She thought it would be fun to climb the post.
Lily's mom saw her and said, "Lily, don't climb the post. You might fall and weep." But Lily did not listen to her mom. She wanted to show her friends that she could climb the post. So, she started to climb up.
As Lily climbed higher, she began to feel scared. She looked down and saw her friends far below. Suddenly, she slipped and fell to the ground. Lily started to weep because she was hurt.
Her mom came running and said, "I told you not to climb the post, Lily. Now you are hurt and sad." Lily's friends went home, and she had to stay inside to rest.
The moral of the story is to always listen to your parents. They know what is best for you, and they are trying to keep you safe. If Lily had listened to her mom, she would not have been hurt and could have kept playing with her friends.

Jack and Jill were twins who liked to play outside. They liked to dig in the dirt, climb on the rocks, and chase the birds. But they did not like to bathe. They hated the water, the soap, and the towel. They always ran away when their mom called them to wash.
One day, they found a big pile of coal near the road. They thought it was impressive. It was black and shiny and hard. They wanted to touch it and take some home. They did not see the sign that said "Danger. Do not touch."
They ran to the coal and started to pick it up. They did not know that coal was dirty and sharp. They got coal dust on their hands, faces, and clothes. They also got cuts and scrapes from the coal. They did not care. They were having fun.
But then, a big truck came. It was loud and fast. It was the truck that brought the coal. The driver did not see Jack and Jill. He wanted to dump more coal on the pile. He pushed a button and the coal came out of the truck.
Jack and Jill were scared. They tried to run away, but it was too late. The coal fell on them and buried them. They could not breathe. They could not move. They could not cry. They were gone.
Their mom came looking for them. She saw the coal pile and the truck. She saw the sign that said "Danger. Do not touch." She saw a bit of red and blue under the coal. It was Jack and Jill's clothes. She screamed and cried. She wished they had listened to her. She wished they had bathed.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily was very excited because she was going on a tour with her family. They were going to visit the zoo. Lily loved animals and couldn't wait to see them.
When they arrived at the zoo, Lily's mom said, "Lily, you must behave and stay close to us." Lily nodded and promised to be good. They saw many animals like lions, monkeys, and birds. Lily was very happy.
But then, they went to see the big snakes. Lily was frightened. She held her dad's hand tight and said, "Daddy, I'm scared." Her dad hugged her and said, "Don't worry, Lily. The snakes can't hurt you. They are behind the glass." Lily felt better and enjoyed the rest of the tour.

Tim and Mia are adventurous kids. They like to play outside and explore new things. One day, they find a big hill with a long zip line. A zip line is a rope that you can slide on with a seat.
"Wow, look at that!" Tim says. "Do you want to try it?"
Mia nods. She is not afraid. She likes to have fun. They climb up the hill and find the seat. Tim goes first. He puts on a helmet and a belt. He sits on the seat and holds the rope.
"Ready, set, go!" Mia shouts. She pushes Tim and he zips down the line. He feels the wind in his face and the sun on his skin. He sees the trees and the flowers and the birds. He smiles and laughs.
"Yay, this is awesome!" he says.
Mia watches him from the top. She claps and cheers. She wants to zip too. She waits for Tim to reach the end and get off the seat. Then she puts on a helmet and a belt. She sits on the seat and holds the rope.
"Ready, set, go!" Tim shouts. He pushes Mia and she zips down the line. She feels the wind in her hair and the sun on her eyes. She sees the sky and the clouds and the butterflies. She giggles and squeals.
"Wow, this is amazing!" she says.
Tim and Mia have a lot of fun on the zip line. They zip back and forth many times. They feel happy and free. They love to be adventurous. They do not want to stop. But soon, it is time to go home. They say goodbye to the zip line and the hill. They walk down to the ground and hold hands.
"That was the best thing ever!" Tim says.
"I agree!" Mia says. "Can we come back tomorrow?"
"Sure!" Tim says. "We are adventurous kids, remember?"
They smile and hug. They are the best friends ever. They go home and tell their mom and dad about their adventure. They can't wait to zip again.

Once upon a time, there was a messy little boy named Tim. He loved to play with his toys and make a big mess. One day, Tim saw a potato on the table. He thought it was a toy, so he picked it up and started to play with it.
Tim's mom saw him playing with the potato. She said, "Tim, that's not a toy. It's a potato. We need it for dinner." Tim looked at the potato and then at his mom. He nodded and said, "Okay, Mom. I will put it back."
Tim tried to put the potato back on the table, but it rolled away and made an even bigger mess. His mom laughed and said, "Well, at least you tried. Let's clean up together." Tim nodded again and helped his mom clean up the mess. After they were done, they cooked the potato for dinner and enjoyed a nice meal together.

Once upon a time, there was a little ant. The ant had a weird home. It was a small hole in a big tree. One day, the ant went out to find food. The ant's mom said, "Stay close to the tree, little ant."
The little ant found a big, sweet berry. The ant tried to carry the berry back to the tree. But the berry was too big for the ant. The ant felt sad. Then, a big, strong ant came along. The big ant helped the little ant carry the berry. They worked together to get the berry back to the tree.
After they got the berry back, the little ant said, "Thank you, big ant!" The big ant said, "You're welcome! It's good to help each other." The little ant learned that it's important to stay close to home and help each other. And the weird tree hole became a happy place for all the ants to live.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max loved to lay in the sun. One day, he found a strip of soft grass in the park. Max thought it was easy to lay there all day.
While Max was laying on the strip, a tiny bug crawled on him. Max did not see the bug. The bug was very small. The bug whispered in Max's ear, "I can help you find yummy treats." Max was surprised but happy.
Max followed the bug, and they found a big pile of treats. Max was so happy and thanked the bug. They became best friends and shared the treats. From that day, Max and the bug always played together in the park.

One day, a clean little bird was sitting on a tree. It started to rain. The bird saw a small screw on the ground. The bird picked up the screw with its beak.
The bird met a squirrel. The squirrel said, "Hi bird! What do you have?" The bird said, "I have a screw. It's clean and shiny. I found it in the rain." The squirrel said, "Wow! Can I see?"
The bird showed the squirrel the clean screw. They played with the screw in the rain. They were happy and had fun. The rain stopped, and the sun came out. The bird and the squirrel were still friends.

One day, a little girl named Lily went for a walk. She saw a wet rose on the ground. Lily picked up the rose and said, "Oh no, the pretty rose is wet. I will pray for the sun to come out and make it dry."
Lily held the rose and prayed, "Please sun, come out and make this rose dry." The sun heard Lily's prayer and came out from behind the clouds. The sun shone on the wet rose, and it started to become dry.
Lily's friend, Tom, saw her with the rose and asked, "What are you doing, Lily?" She said, "I prayed for the sun to come out, and it did! Now the rose is dry and pretty again." Tom smiled and said, "That's great! Let's go play together." So Lily and Tom played together, holding the pretty rose that was no longer wet.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jim. Jim wanted to be successful, so he decided to get a hammer and build something. He worked hard but couldn't make it right.
Jim got frustrated and wanted to quit. But then he thought about how much he wanted to be successful. So he kept trying and trying.
Finally, after many tries, Jim was successful! He made a beautiful shelf with his hammer. Everyone was so impressed by what he had built.
Jim was so proud of himself. He had never quit no matter how hard it was. His hard work paid off and he became successful.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. Lily loved to do yoga. She was an honest girl who always told the truth. One day, Lily met a boy named Tom. Tom was new to the town and he did not know anyone.
Lily said to Tom, "Hi, my name is Lily. I like to do yoga. What do you like to do?" Tom said, "Hi Lily, my name is Tom. I like to play with my ball." Lily was happy to meet Tom and they became friends.
Every day, Lily and Tom played together. Lily taught Tom how to do yoga and Tom taught Lily how to play with a ball. They were honest friends and always shared their toys. They had lots of fun and were happy to have each other.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a fast wolf. He liked to run and play all day. The wolf had many friends, like birds, rabbits, and squirrels.
One day, the fast wolf saw a little rabbit stuck in a hole. The rabbit was very sad and scared. The fast wolf wanted to help his friend, but the hole was too small for him. He thought and thought about how to save the rabbit.
Then, the fast wolf had an idea. He managed to find a long stick. He gave the stick to the rabbit. The rabbit held the stick, and the fast wolf pulled him out of the hole. The rabbit was very happy and thanked the fast wolf. They played together and had fun all day.

One day Jems was walking in the forest looking for some fun. She soon heard a noise coming from under a bush.
"What's that?" Jems said.
Jems bravely looked under the bush. Her eyes opened wide with panic. There was a small, sad rabbit. It had no one to help it.
Jems quickly scooped up the rabbit. "Don't worry," she said to it. "It's going to be alright."
She took the rabbit home and took care of it. The rabbit soon became happy, running around in the garden.
Jems was glad she could help the poor, sad creature. She was proud of herself that she could help a creature in need.

Tom is a small boy. He likes to play with water. He has a bucket and a cup. He fills the bucket with water from the tap. He pours the water into the cup. He pours the cup on the grass. He laughs.
Tom sees a bird. The bird is red and yellow. It is on a tree. Tom wants to name the bird. He thinks of a name. He says, "Hello, bird. Your name is Sun. You are bright and pretty. Do you want some water?"
Tom pours some water from the cup on his hand. He holds his hand up to the bird. The bird flies down. It lands on Tom's hand. It drinks some water. It sings a song. Tom smiles.
Tom and Sun are friends. They play with water. They have fun. They are happy.

Once upon a time, in a warm and cozy nest, there was a little bird named Tim. Tim was a worried bird because he had to stay and watch his mom's big egg. Tim's mom told him that he must not leave the egg alone.
One day, while Tim was watching the egg, he saw a pretty butterfly. He wanted to play with the butterfly, but he knew he had to stay with the egg. The butterfly flew away, and Tim felt sad. He thought that maybe if he left for just a minute, nothing bad would happen to the egg.
Tim decided to follow the butterfly, leaving the egg alone. He played with the butterfly and forgot about the egg. When he came back, he saw that the egg was gone! Tim was very worried and sad. He knew he should have stayed with the egg like his mom told him. Now, the egg was lost, and Tim couldn't find it. The bad thing happened because he didn't stay with the egg.

One day, a little girl named Sue went to the park with her mom. Sue saw a vendor selling pretty balloons. The vendor had many colors, like red, blue, and yellow. Sue wanted a balloon very much.
Sue's mom bought her a big, red balloon. Sue was so happy. She held the balloon and admired it. It was so big and pretty. She felt like the luckiest girl in the park.
As Sue played, a harmless little bug landed on her balloon. Sue was not scared. The bug was tiny and nice. Sue, the bug, and the big red balloon had lots of fun in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a train. This train was very excited. It loved to go fast and take people to new places. One day, the train wanted to organize a fun trip for all its friends.
The train told all its friends, "Let's go on a fun trip!" All the friends were excited too. They all got on the train, ready to go. The train started to go fast, and everyone was happy.
But then, something unexpected happened. The train saw a big hill. It had never seen a hill this big before. The train was scared, but it wanted to be brave for its friends. So, the train went up the hill. When it got to the top, the train saw a big surprise. There was a big, fun park at the top of the hill! The train and all its friends had a great time playing at the park. The train was very proud that it was brave and organized a fun trip for everyone.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a calm mule named Molly. Molly lived on a farm with her best friend, a little boy named Timmy. They liked to play in the big field together.
One day, while playing, Timmy noticed something shiny in the grass. "Look, Molly!" he said, "What is that?" Molly walked over and saw the shiny thing too. They both felt excited and knew it was a clue for a big surprise.
They followed the shiny things in the grass, and each one led them closer to the big surprise. When they found the last shiny thing, they saw a big, beautiful rainbow. "Wow, Molly! We found the rainbow!" Timmy said with a big smile. Molly felt happy too, and they both knew they had an amazing adventure together.

One day, a boy named Tim went to a bright room to play. In the room, there were many toys and other kids. Tim was happy to play with the toys and make new friends.
Tim saw a girl named Sue playing with a big doll. He went to Sue and said, "Hi! Can I play with you?" Sue said, "Yes, let's play together!" They played with the doll and had a lot of fun.
But then, Tim did something that made him feel embarrass. He tripped on a toy car and fell down. All the kids in the room looked at him. Tim's face turned red. Sue helped him up and said, "It's okay, Tim. We all fall sometimes." Tim smiled and they continued to play, feeling happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to draw pictures. One day, she drew a big, orange sun. She showed the picture to her mom and dad. They liked it a lot.
That night, Sue went to her room to sleep. She looked out her window and saw the dark sky. Sue wanted to see the orange sun again. So, she closed her eyes and began to pray. She prayed for the orange sun to come back soon.
The next morning, Sue woke up and looked out her window. The sky was bright and the orange sun was shining. Sue was very happy. She knew her prayer worked. She ran to show her mom and dad the orange sun. They all smiled and enjoyed the beautiful day together.

One day, a little girl named Lucy found a map in her room. The map was old and had many lines on it. Lucy wanted to know where the map could take her. She felt a bit troubled because she could not read the map. She asked her mom for help.
"Mom, can you help me read this map?" Lucy asked. Her mom looked at the map and saw that it was very old. "Let's try to clean it first," her mom said. They put the map in water to soak. The water made the map look new again.
After the map was clean, Lucy and her mom looked at it. They saw that the map showed the way to a park. Together, they followed the map and found the park. The park had a big slide and swings. Lucy played and had so much fun. She was happy that she found the map and that her mom helped her.

John was three years old and his mother taught him a new word. It was the word "hate". John asked her why the word hate was so bad. His mother said that it was an orange word, but it didn't mean something nice.
John was confused. He asked, "Why do people hate if it's orange?" His mother smiled and explained to him, "Sometimes people are mean and do hateful things. That's why it's important to be kind and loving to each other. That's how we can make the world a better place."
John thought about it for a moment and then said, "I don't like hate. I want to teach it how to be nice like me!" His mother smiled and hugged him.
John was happy and ran outside to play with his friends. He knew that spreading kindness was the best way to make the world a better place. And he was proud to be a teacher of love.

Anna and Ben are brother and sister. They like to play in the garden. One day, they plan to make a meal for their mom and dad. They pick some flowers, some grass, some mud and some rocks. They put them in a big pot. They stir and stir. They think it looks yummy.
They take the pot to the kitchen. They see mom and dad are not home yet. They put the pot on the stove. They turn on the fire. They wait and wait. They want to surprise mom and dad.
But the pot is too hot. The fire is too big. The pot starts to smoke. The smoke fills the kitchen. The smoke makes the alarm beep. The alarm makes Anna and Ben scared. They run out of the kitchen. They are worried.
Mom and dad come home. They see the smoke. They see the pot. They see Anna and Ben. They are angry. They are sad. They are not surprised. They are not happy. They have to clean the kitchen. They have to throw away the pot. They have to order pizza. They have to talk to Anna and Ben. They have to teach them a lesson.
Anna and Ben are sorry. They are ashamed. They are hungry. They are not proud. They did not make a meal. They made a mess. They made a mistake. They learned a lesson. They will not plan to make a meal again. They will help mom and dad instead.

One day, a sleepy soldier went to the town. The soldier was tired and wanted to find a place to rest. He saw a big sack and thought it would be a good place to sleep. So, the soldier went inside the sack and closed his eyes.
While the soldier was sleeping, a big, mean dog came by. The dog saw the sack and thought it was a toy. It started to play with the sack, not knowing that the sleepy soldier was inside. The dog bit the sack and shook it, making the soldier wake up.
The soldier tried to get out of the sack, but it was too late. The dog had torn the sack and the soldier was stuck inside. The sleepy soldier could not get out and had to stay in the torn sack all day. The soldier was sad and wished he had found a better place to sleep.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play with her toys. One day, she got a new toy. It was a rubber ball. Sue was so happy to own it.
Sue played with her ball all day. But then, the ball got lost. It was hidden behind a big box. Sue looked everywhere for her ball. She was very sad. She asked her mom, "Mom, have you seen my ball?"
Her mom said, "No, Sue. Let's look for it together." So, they looked and looked. Finally, they found the hidden ball behind the big box. Sue was so happy to find her rubber ball.
Sue and her mom played with the ball together. They had a lot of fun. Sue learned that when she lost something, she could ask for help. And she knew that her mom would always be there to help her.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Anna. She had a lucky red ball that she loved to play with. One day, Anna saw a small hole in her backyard. She thought, "Maybe I can find something fun in there."
Anna took a peek into the hole. It was very dark and she could not see anything. She reached in and felt something hard. It was a nail! Anna was so happy to find a nail. She thought it was a lucky nail because it was shiny and pretty.
Anna took the lucky nail and put it with her lucky red ball. She played with them every day. The lucky nail and the lucky red ball became her favorite toys. They made Anna very happy and she always had fun playing with them.

Once upon a time, there was a charming duck. This duck liked to print pretty pictures. The duck had a little house where he kept all his nice pictures. He loved to show his friends the pictures he made.
One day, the charming duck wanted to print a new picture. He had a big idea for a beautiful picture with many colors. He was very excited to start. The duck went to his little house to find his printer.
But when the charming duck got to his little house, he saw that his printer was broken. He was very sad. The duck tried to fix the printer, but he could not. He could not print his new beautiful picture.
The charming duck was very sad that his printer was broken. He could not make pretty pictures for his friends anymore. The duck's friends tried to help him, but they could not fix the printer either. The duck's house was quiet and there were no more pretty pictures.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He liked to imagine things. One day, Tim found a big, modern towel in his room. It was blue and soft.
Tim said to his mom, "Mom, I found a big towel. Can I play with it?" His mom said, "Yes, but be careful and don't make a mess."
Tim played with the towel all day. He imagined it was a cape, a blanket, and even a tent. But then, Tim had a bad idea. He imagined the towel was a rope and tied it to the door.
Tim tried to swing on the towel like a monkey. But the towel broke and Tim fell down. He cried and his mom came running. Tim was sad and hurt. The big, modern towel was broken too. Tim learned to be more careful when he played.

One day, a dolphin named Dee lived in the big water. Dee was sad because the water was empty. There were no fish to play with. Dee swam and swam, looking for a friend.
Dee saw a big ice. It was cold and white. Dee touched the ice and said, "Brrr, this ice can freeze me!" Dee saw a little fish stuck in the ice. The fish was cold and scared. Dee wanted to help the fish.
Dee pushed the ice with his nose. The ice moved and the fish was free. The fish said, "Thank you, Dee! You saved me from the freeze!" Dee and the fish became best friends. They swam and played together in the big water. Now, the water was not empty anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little hut in the woods. It was a very useful hut. Many animals lived in the hut to stay warm and safe.
One day, a big storm came. The rain fell hard, and the wind blew strong. The hut started to rot. The animals were sad because they loved their useful hut.
A kind squirrel saw the rotting hut and wanted to help. He gathered his friends, and they all worked together. They fixed the hut, and it was useful again. The animals were so happy, and they thanked the squirrel and his friends. They all lived happily ever after in their nice, useful hut.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lucy loved to travel. She traveled with her family to many places. They saw big mountains, long rivers, and pretty flowers. Lucy loved to keep memories of all the places she had been.
One day, Lucy met a poor old man. The man was sad because he could not travel like Lucy. He said, "I wish I could travel and see the world like you, Lucy." Lucy wanted to help the poor man.
Lucy had an idea. She showed the man her memory box. In the box, she had pictures and things from her travels. She said, "You can look at my memories, and it will be like you are traveling too." The poor man was happy. He looked at Lucy's memories and smiled. Now, he could travel in his heart.

Ben and Lily are best friends. They like to play in the park with their dogs, Max and Ruby. Max is a big dog with black fur. Ruby is a small dog with brown fur. They are both very fierce. They bark at the birds and the cats and the squirrels.
One day, Ben and Lily see a new dog in the park. It is a white dog with spots. It looks friendly, but Max and Ruby do not like it. They run towards it and growl. The white dog is scared and runs away. Ben and Lily call their dogs to come back, but they do not listen. They chase the white dog out of the park and into the street.
Ben and Lily are worried. They run after their dogs, but they are too fast. They see a big car coming. The car honks and brakes, but it is too late. The car hits Max and Ruby. They fall to the ground and do not move.
Ben and Lily cry. They hug their dogs and say sorry. They wish they had stayed in the park. They wish their dogs had stayed with them. They love their dogs very much. They do not want to lose them.
A man gets out of the car. He is sorry too. He says he did not see the dogs. He says he will take them to the vet. He says he will help them. He lifts Max and Ruby into his car. He tells Ben and Lily to get in too. He drives them to the vet.
The vet is a nice lady. She checks Max and Ruby. She says they are hurt, but they will be okay. She gives them medicine and bandages. She says they need to rest and be careful. She says they are lucky dogs. She says they have good friends.
Ben and Lily are happy. They thank the man and the vet. They hug Max and Ruby. They say they are sorry again. They say they will stay with them. They say they love them very much. They say they are fierce, but they are also sweet.

Once upon a time, there was a small pond. It was a gloomy pond. Near the pond, there was a big tree. A duck lived by the pond. The duck liked to enjoy the pond every day. The duck would swim and play in the water.
One day, a big fish came to the pond. The fish was very big and very fast. The duck was scared of the big fish. The duck did not want to swim in the pond anymore. The big fish liked the pond too. It would swim and jump in the water.
The duck missed enjoying the pond. The duck had an idea. It would talk to the big fish. The duck went to the pond and talked to the fish. The big fish said it was just lonely. The fish wanted a friend to play with. The duck and the fish started to play together in the pond.
But one day, while they were playing, the big fish got stuck in some plants. The duck tried to help, but the fish was too big. The duck went to find help, but it was too late. The big fish could not breathe and it was very sad. The duck was sad too.
The moral of the story is to be careful when you play. Sometimes, even if you have good friends, bad things can happen. It is important to always be safe and watch out for each other.

One day, a toy cat and a toy dog were in a room. It was a hot day. The cat and the dog did not like each other. They always fight.
"Give me that toy!" said the cat. "No, it's mine!" said the dog. They started to fight over the toy. They pulled and pulled.
Then, something unexpected happened. The toy broke! The cat and the dog stopped fighting. They looked at the broken toy and felt sad.
"Maybe we should not fight," said the cat. "Yes, let's be friends," said the dog. They hugged and played together. From that day on, they did not fight anymore.

Once upon a time, there were two friends named Jack and Jill. They were playing a game one day when Jack noticed something big ahead of them. When he asked Jill what it was, she said it was a huge rock. 
Jack was worried that he wouldn't be able to move the rock, but Jill reassured him that together they could do it. Jack was still worried, but he agreed to try and help. 
Jack and Jill joined hands and together they pushed the huge rock. After a few tries, the huge rock started to move! The two friends cheered in delight.
They had so much fun moving the rock, that they decided to play another game. They ran around the huge rock, taking turns to try and knock it over. Every time they could move the rock a little, they cheered and clapped. 
They had such a great time playing the game, that they decided to play it again the next day!

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a long, dark tunnel. Many animals lived near the tunnel. They liked to play and have fun together. One day, a kind and generous rabbit named Bobby decided to make a new friend.
Bobby hopped into the tunnel and saw a sharp rock. He thought it looked like a nose. "Maybe it's a new animal friend!" he said. Bobby was very happy and excited. He touched the sharp rock and said, "Hi, I'm Bobby! Do you want to play with me?"
But the sharp rock didn't talk or move. It was just a rock. Bobby was sad, but he didn't give up. He hopped out of the tunnel and saw a little bird. He shared some of his food with the bird. The bird was very happy and became Bobby's new friend. They played and had fun together, and Bobby learned that being generous and kind can help make new friends.

Timmy was out walking in the park with his mom. He saw a pile of junk in the corner of the park and ran towards it, excited to see what was inside.
"Be careful, Timmy," his mom warned. "That junk might be harmless and you can look through it, but don't touch it."
Timmy nodded and started looking through the junk. After a few minutes, he saw something he believed was special. He reached in and picked it up.
Mom said, "Timmy! Don't do that, it could be dangerous!"
But it was too late. As soon as Timmy touched the object, he knew it wasn't harmless. A loud noise filled the air and his hand started to hurt. He dropped the object and ran to his mom, crying.
Mom hugged him tight and tried to make him feel better. But Timmy knew that his mistake was not harmless. He had done something bad and it had not ended well.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to eat salad. One day, Mia saw her mom pour dressing on the salad. Mia thought she could do it too. She was excited to try.
The next day, Mia's mom was busy. Mia saw the salad and the dressing. She tried to pour the dressing on the salad. But, she spilled it all over the table. Mia felt frustrated.
Mia's mom saw the mess and came to help. She showed Mia how to pour the dressing slowly. Mia tried again and poured the dressing just right. Mia was happy and enjoyed her salad.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy was very independent. She liked to do things all by herself. One day, Amy saw a pretty glass on the table. She wanted to drink water from the glass.
Amy tried to reach the glass, but it was too high for her. She climbed on the chair to get it. When she finally got the glass, she was very happy. But, as she climbed down, she slipped and the glass fell. The glass broke into many pieces and Amy started to tear.
Amy's mom came and saw what happened. She hugged Amy and told her not to cry. She said, "Next time, ask for help when you need it. It's okay to be independent, but sometimes it's good to ask for help too." Amy learned that it's important to ask for help when things are hard or dangerous. And she lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a watch that he loved very much. It was a special watch because it could talk. The watch was very kind and compassionate. It always helped Tim when he needed it.
One day, Tim met a girl named Sally. Sally was mean and did not like Tim's watch. She said, "I hate your watch. It's not fun." Tim felt sad and asked his watch what he should do. The watch told Tim, "Be nice to Sally even if she is mean."
So, Tim tried to be kind to Sally. He shared his toys and played with her. After some time, Sally started to change. She became nicer and more compassionate too. Sally said, "I'm sorry I was mean. I like your watch now."
In the end, Tim and Sally became good friends. They learned that being compassionate and kind is more important than hating things. And Tim's watch was always there to help them remember this lesson.

Lisa and Tom are friends. They like to play together at school. One day, they go on a trip with their class. They ride on a big bus. The bus has many seats and windows. Lisa and Tom sit next to each other. They look out the window and see many things.
"Look, Tom, a cow!" Lisa says. She points to a black and white cow in a field.
"Moo!" Tom says. He makes a cow sound. He likes animals.
The bus takes them to a park. The park has a big lake and many ducks. Lisa and Tom feed the ducks with bread. They have fun. They also see a boat on the lake. The boat has a sail and a flag.
"Wow, a boat!" Tom says. He likes boats.
"I want to ride on the boat!" Lisa says. She likes boats too.
But the teacher says they cannot ride on the boat. The boat is not for them. The boat is for someone else. Lisa and Tom are sad. They are envious of the person who can ride on the boat.
They return to the bus. They sit in their seats. They are quiet. They do not look out the window. They do not talk.
"Are you okay, Lisa?" Tom asks. He sees that Lisa is not happy.
"I'm okay, Tom," Lisa says. She tries to smile. "But I wish we could ride on the boat."
"Me too, Lisa," Tom says. He hugs Lisa. "But we still have each other. And we can play with our toys when we get home."
Lisa nods. She hugs Tom back. She feels a little better. She thinks Tom is a good friend. She is glad they are friends.

Once upon a time, in a little garden, there was a tiny seed. The seed wanted to grow big and strong. It was ready to grow, but it needed help from the sun and rain.
One day, a thief came into the garden. The thief was looking for something to take. He saw the tiny seed and thought it was a good thing to steal. But the sun and rain saw the thief too. They knew they had to help the seed grow before the thief could take it away.
The sun shone very bright and the rain fell very soft. The tiny seed started to grow. It grew and grew, and soon it was a big, strong plant. The thief came back to take the seed, but it was too late. The seed had grown into a big plant that the thief could not steal. The sun and rain were happy they had helped the seed grow, and the plant was safe and strong in the little garden.

Once upon a time, there was a kind tutor named Mr. Fox. He was very useful to the kids in his town. He helped them learn new things every day. One day, Mr. Fox met a little girl named Sally.
Sally said, "Mr. Fox, can you help me learn how to cut paper?" Mr. Fox said, "Of course, Sally! But be careful, the scissors are sharp."
Sally and Mr. Fox sat down at the table. Mr. Fox showed Sally how to hold the scissors and cut the paper. Sally was very happy. She said, "Thank you, Mr. Fox! You are so useful!"
From that day on, Sally and Mr. Fox became good friends. They learned many new things together. And Sally always remembered to be careful with the sharp scissors.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved playing outdoors. One day, before school, she looked at the calendar. She saw that it was the first day of spring. She was very excited and decided to go out and explore the woods.
So she hurried outside. But it was very tiring. She was so tired, she just wanted to sit down. Just then she saw a little tree with lots of juicy apples. She was starving and wanted to eat. But then she heard a voice.
"If you take just one apple, the rest of us can yield to your hunger," said the tree.
Lucy was surprised. She had never seen a talking tree before.
"Thank you very much," replied Lucy, as she carefully chose one apple from the bunch and enjoyed it.
When she was done, she thanked the tree for her generosity and went off feeling happy and content.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy called Tom. Tom was very impatient, he wanted to go upstairs and play. Everyday, his mommy and daddy say 'not yet'. 
One day, when Tom was three, his mommy and daddy said 'finally, you can go upstairs!'. Tom was very excited and he ran upstairs as fast as he could. 
When Tom reached the top of the stairs, he saw something funny. It was a big brown bear! Tom cried "Wow!" and the bear said "Hullo!" Tom was amazed and he said: "Are you real?". The big brown bear said "Yes, I am real - let's play together!" 
Tom was so happy! He and the bear played together for hours and hours. Tom was having so much fun, he did not want to stop. 
Finally, it was time for Tom to go home. He said goodbye to the big brown bear, hugged him, and then ran all the way down the stairs. All the way home, Tom was happy to have met a real bear.

Once upon a time there was a bear who was very brave and curious. Her name was Daisy. Daisy wanted to go and explore the world.
One day, Daisy decided to start an adventure. She packed her food and water and set off. Eventually, Daisy stumbled across a beach and stopped to investigate.
The water of the beach sparkled in the sunshine and the sand was soft. But Daisy noticed something strange. All of the other animals were running away!
Daisy was confused, so she asked a seagull why the beach looked so dangerous. The seagull replied, "The beach is dangerous because there are big waves and they can be scary."
Daisy was determined to explore the beach, so she took a deep breath and started wading into the water. After playing in the waves for a while, Daisy had so much fun that she decided to come back every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very happy because he was going to the library with his mom. He loved the library because it had many books with fun pictures. Today was a perfect day to read.
At the library, Tim found a big ball. He wanted to play with it. He picked it up and kicked it very hard. The ball flew across the room and hit a tall shelf. The shelf shook and many books fell down.
Tim's mom came over and saw the mess. She was not happy. They had to leave the library and Tim could not read any books. Tim was sad. He learned that it is not good to play with a ball inside a library.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy was an adventurous girl who liked to explore her house. One day, she found a big bookcase in her room. The bookcase was full of colorful books.
Amy wanted to read a book, so she said, "Mom, can you help me open a book?" Her mom came and opened a book with many pictures. Amy and her mom sat down and started to read the book together.
As they read, Amy felt like she was going on an adventure. She saw animals, trees, and big mountains in the pictures. Amy was very happy and said, "I love reading books from my bookcase!" From that day on, Amy and her mom read a new book from the bookcase every day.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy cat named Tom. Tom lived in a big city. He liked to play with his friends, but sometimes they made him mad.
One day, Tom and his friends were playing near the tall buildings. They were having fun, but then they heard a loud noise. The noise scared them, and they all started to panic. They did not know what to do.
Tom, even though he was grumpy, knew he had to help his friends. He told them to be brave and not to panic. They all listened to Tom and stopped panicking. They found out the noise was just a big truck. They all laughed and went back to playing. Tom was happy he could help his friends, and they all had a great day.

One day, a fast dog named Spot went to play in the park. Spot liked to run and jump. He was very happy. Spot saw a little girl named Jane. Jane was sad. She lost her toy.
Spot said, "Don't be sad. I will help you find your toy." They looked high and low. They looked near and far. They could not find the toy. Jane was still sad.
Then, Spot had an idea. He said, "I can change into a toy for you." He changed into a toy dog. Jane was happy. She played with her new toy dog, Spot. They had a fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little otter. He lived in a big, ancient tree by the water. He was a happy otter, and he loved to play with his friends.
One day, the otter found a sharp stick. He wanted to show it to his friends. But when he tried to play with it, he hurt his friend's paw. His friend cried, and the otter felt very sad.
The otter knew he had to make it right. He threw the sharp stick away and found a soft leaf instead. He wrapped his friend's paw in the leaf, and they hugged. They played together again, and the otter knew he would never play with sharp things again.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Annie. She was only 3 years old. One day, Annie wanted to believe in the sky. She looked up and the sky was blue. But she wasn't sure if she should believe that the sky was really blue.
Annie asked her big sister if she should really believe that the sky was blue. Her big sister told her: "Yes, you should believe what you can see with your own eyes." Annie thought about this and decided to believe that the sky was blue.
She was so happy that she started to dance. Seeing this, her big sister became very upset. Annie asked her big sister why she was so angry. Her big sister replied: "Believing in something is never a bad thing. It's important to believe in yourself too!"
Annie understood the lesson and learned to always trust and believe in herself. Soon after, she went off to explore the world with the trust that she could believe in whatever she saw.

Once upon a time, there was a playful dog named Spot. Spot loved to play outside with his friends. They would run, jump, and have so much fun together.
One day, Spot's friend, a little bird named Tweet, wanted to improve her flying. Tweet asked Spot, "Can you help me fly better?" Spot, being a helpful friend, said, "Yes, let's play a game to help you."
They played a game where Tweet would fly, and Spot would chase her. This made Tweet fly faster and higher. They played this game every day, and soon, Tweet improved her flying.
Spot and Tweet were so happy that they could help each other. They knew that playing together could make things better. And from that day on, they had even more fun playing with their friends.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a polite car named Bobby. Bobby was a very good car who loved to help others. One day, as he was driving down the street, he saw his friend, Timmy the truck, stopped by the side of the road.
"Hi Timmy!" said Bobby. "Why are you stopped here?"
"Hi Bobby," said Timmy sadly. "I have no fuel. I can't go anymore."
Bobby wanted to help Timmy. He thought for a moment and then said, "Can I borrow some fuel for you? I will go to the fuel store and bring it back."
Timmy was very happy. "Yes, please! Thank you, Bobby!"
Bobby went to the fuel store, borrowed some fuel, and brought it back to Timmy. He shared his fuel with Timmy, and soon, Timmy was able to drive again. Timmy thanked Bobby for his help. They were both very happy, and they continued down the road together, the best of friends.

Tom and Lily are friends. They like to play with toys and draw pictures. One day, Tom draws a note for Lily. He writes "I like you" and draws a heart. He is happy and wants to give the note to Lily.
He goes to Lily's house and knocks on the door. Lily's mom opens the door and says "Hello, Tom. Lily is in her room. You can go and see her." Tom says "Thank you" and runs to Lily's room. He has the note in his hand.
He opens the door and sees Lily. She is playing with a doll. Tom says "Hi, Lily. I have something for you." He shows her the note and smiles. He hopes she will like it.
But Lily does not smile. She looks at the note and says "What is this? I can't read. This is silly. I don't want it." She takes the note and tears it into pieces. She throws the pieces on the floor and says "Go away, Tom. I don't like you. You are not my friend."
Tom feels sad and angry. He says "You are mean, Lily. You are not my friend either. You are a bad girl." He turns around and runs out of the room. He cries and goes back to his house. He does not want to see Lily again. He is not happy.

One day, a little boy named Tim was playing in his yard. He saw a hole in the ground. It was a normal hole, not too big and not too small. Tim was curious about the hole, so he went to his mom.
"Mom, can you tell me about the hole in the yard?" Tim asked. His mom looked out the window and saw the hole. "It's just a normal hole, Tim. Maybe a small animal made it," she said.
The next day, Tim went to play in the yard again. But this time, the normal hole was much bigger! Tim was surprised and ran to his mom. "Mom, the hole is big now!" he said. His mom came outside to look. 
As they stood near the big hole, a friendly rabbit popped out! "Hello, Tim and Mom! I made the hole bigger to share with my rabbit friends," the rabbit said. Tim and his mom were happy to meet the rabbit and his friends. From that day on, Tim played with the rabbits and they all had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to draw. She would draw all day. Her favorite thing to draw was a big, brown dog. She would show her drawings to her mom and dad. They would smile and say, "Good job, Sue!"
One day, Sue drew a brown dog, but it didn't look right. The dog had a big head and tiny legs. Sue was sad. She didn't know how to fix the image. She showed her mom the drawing. Her mom said, "Don't worry, Sue. I can help you fix it."
Sue's mom took a pencil and showed Sue how to make the dog's head smaller and the legs longer. Sue watched and learned. Then, Sue tried to draw the brown dog again. This time, it looked just right! Sue was so happy. She showed her new drawing to her mom and dad. They both said, "Great job, Sue! You fixed the image!"
From that day on, Sue kept drawing and learning. She knew that if she ever needed help, her mom and dad would be there to help her fix her drawings. And Sue and her brown dog lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a happy kid called Tim. He loved exploring and playing outside. He valued the time he had with his family and friends, and he enjoyed being outside in nature.
One day, Tim went to the park with his mom and dad. He had so much fun playing on the swings and slides. He also loved picking flowers and chasing butterflies.
At the park, Tim noticed a lonely kid playing by herself. He wondered why she wasn't playing with anyone else. So Tim went up to her and said "Hi!" The girl smiled and introduced herself. Tim and the girl played together all afternoon. They ran around and laughed, and had lots of fun. 
When it was time to go home, Tim and his parents said goodbye to the girl. He valued the time he spent with her and was happy that he could make a new friend. He and his parents smiled, waved, and said goodbye.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to ride the subway with his mom. They would take the subway to visit his grandma.
One day, while riding the subway, Tim saw a weak, sad dog. The dog looked hungry and tired. Tim asked his mom, "Can we help the dog, please?" His mom said, "Yes, let's give the dog some food and water."
Tim and his mom took the dog home, fed it, and gave it a nice bed to sleep in. The weak dog became strong and happy. Tim named the dog Buddy. From that day on, Tim, his mom, and Buddy would ride the subway together, always looking for new friends to help.

One day, a boy named Tim saw a big motorcycle. It was red and shiny. Tim felt very proud of his new toy. He wanted to show it to his friends.
Tim went to his friend, Lily. He said, "Look at my motorcycle! It's big and red!" Lily smiled and said, "Wow, it's very nice!" Tim felt even more proud.
Then, Tim had an idea. He said to Lily, "Do you want to play with my motorcycle?" Lily was happy. She said, "Yes, thank you!" They played together and had lots of fun with the big red motorcycle.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. Tim saw a girl named Sue. Sue had sunglasses on her head. Tim wanted to play with the sunglasses, but he was too short to reach them.
"Can I play with your sunglasses?" Tim asked Sue. Sue said, "No, my mom told me not to share them." Tim was sad, but he had an idea. He found a modest stick and tried to reach the sunglasses with the stick.
Sue saw what Tim was doing and got mad. "Stop that!" she yelled. Tim felt bad and said sorry. They decided to play together in the park and became friends. From that day on, they learned to share and play together.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby dog named Spot. Spot loved to play and run around in the park. One sunny day, Spot went to the park with his friend Timmy.
As they played, Spot saw something big in the bushes. It was a chest! Spot and Timmy went to discover what was inside. They pushed the chest out of the bushes and opened the lid.
Inside the chest, they found many toys and treats. Spot and Timmy were very happy. They played with the toys and shared the treats. It was the best day ever for the chubby dog and his friend.

Once upon a time, there was a wide tree. It had many twisty branches. A cat lived in the tree. Her name was Kitty. She loved to play with yarn.
One day, Kitty found a ball of yarn on the ground. She was very happy. She picked it up with her mouth and started to play. She twisted the yarn around the tree branches. The yarn made the tree look very colorful.
A little boy named Timmy saw the tree. He walked over and said, "Wow! What a pretty tree!" Kitty looked at Timmy and meowed. Timmy asked, "Did you twist the yarn around the tree?" Kitty nodded her head.
Timmy and Kitty became good friends. They played with the yarn and twisted it around the wide tree every day. The tree looked more beautiful than ever. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Lucy found a big, mysterious box in her room. She did not know where it came from. She thought, "What is this big box?" She opened the box and saw a lot of clothes. It was a wardrobe!
Lucy wanted to improve her look. She put on a pretty dress from the wardrobe. She looked in the mirror and said, "Wow, I look nice!" Lucy's mom came in and saw her in the new dress. She asked, "Where did you find that pretty dress, Lucy?"
Lucy said, "I found it in a big box in my room." Mom smiled and said, "That is a special wardrobe, Lucy. It has clothes that make you look and feel better." Lucy was happy and thanked the mysterious wardrobe for helping her improve her look.

One day, a girl named Lucy went to the store with her mom. They wanted to buy a blue ball for Lucy to play with. Lucy was very happy because blue was her favorite color. She could not wait to play with her new blue ball.
At the store, Lucy saw a big blue ball. She said, "Mom, I want this one!" Her mom said, "Okay, let's buy it." They went to the counter and paid for the blue ball. Lucy held the ball tightly as they walked home.
When they got home, Lucy went outside to play with her new blue ball. She threw it up in the air and it came back down. She did this many times, but on her last turn, the ball went very high. It went so high that it hit a tree and popped! Lucy was very sad because her new blue ball was broken.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy liked to play with her noisy toy car. She would rush it around her room and make it go really fast. One day, Amy's mom told her that she would get a big surprise soon. Amy was very excited and could not wait for the surprise.
The next day, Amy saw her mom was making something with a stitch. It looked like a big, soft thing. Amy asked her mom, "Is that my surprise?" Her mom just smiled and said, "You will see soon, my dear." Amy was even more excited now.
Finally, the day came when Amy got her surprise. It was a big, soft race track for her noisy toy car! Amy was so happy. She rushed her car around the track and had so much fun. She knew her mom had used the stitch to make the race track just for her. And they played together all day long.

Once upon a time, in a small icy land, there lived a fairy named Lily. Lily was a kind and loving fairy. She had many friends in the land. One day, Lily was playing with her best friend, a little girl named Mia.
Mia and Lily were running in the icy park. They were laughing and having fun. But suddenly, Mia slipped on the ice and fell down. She started to cry. Lily felt very bad for Mia. She wanted to help her friend.
Lily said, "I'm sorry, Mia. Please forgive me for not being careful." Mia looked at Lily and said, "It's okay, Lily. I forgive you." Then, something unexpected happened. Lily used her magic to make the ice go away. The park became warm and full of flowers.
Mia and Lily were very happy. They played in the park all day long. And from that day on, they were even better friends. They learned that saying sorry and forgiving each other made their friendship stronger.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom was a lucky boy. He had many friends and a big dog named Sam. One day, Tom and his friends went to play in the park. Sam came too. They played with a ball and had lots of fun.
While playing, Tom saw a big rock. He wanted to lift it, but it had a lot of weight. Tom said to his friends, "Help me lift this big rock!" His friends came and they all tried to lift it. They used their hands and knees to try and lift the heavy rock.
As they tried to lift the rock, Tom's friend Tim started to kneel. He said, "I can't lift it, it's too heavy!" Tom didn't want to give up. He said, "I'm a lucky boy, I can do it!" But as Tom tried to lift the rock, he lost his balance and fell down. The rock fell on his foot and it hurt very bad. Tom cried and his friends felt sad. They knew they should have been more careful.

Once upon a time, there was a king. The king was very big and sometimes harsh. He lived in a large castle with his people.
One day, the king wanted to make a cake. He took flour, sugar, and eggs to mix them all together. The king was not very good at mixing, so he asked his friend, a small bird, for help.
The bird helped the king mix the cake. They mixed and mixed until it was just right. Then, they baked the cake and shared it with everyone in the castle. The king was happy and not harsh anymore.

Lily and Mummy were in the restaurant, waiting for their food. Lily was very curious. She was looking all around the restaurant, watching the other people eating. Suddenly, she noticed that one of the people was using a knife.
Lily said in a loud voice, "Mummy, look! He is cutting his food!" 
Mummy smiled and said, "Yes, that man is cutting his food, but you mustn't do that. You can only use the spoon and fork." 
Lily nodded and said, "OK." She was still very curious and wanted to know more about cutting. She asked, "Mummy, why does the man need to cut his food?" 
Mummy replied, "He is cutting his food so that it is smaller, and more easy to eat."
Lily smiled and said, "That's nice." She was now even more curious and wanted to try cutting too. Mummy said, "No, you mustn't do that. We will only use the spoon and fork in the restaurant." 
Lily sighed but said, "OK." She had to wait until they were at home to try cutting her food.

Lily was a wealthy girl who had many toys and dresses. She liked to play with her dolls and pretend they were her friends. One day, she saw a new doll in the shop window. It had long hair and a shiny dress. Lily wanted the doll very much.
She asked her mother, "Can I have the doll, please? It is so pretty and I love it."
Her mother looked at the doll and said, "No, Lily, you have enough dolls already. You don't need another one. Besides, that doll is very expensive. You should be happy with what you have."
Lily was sad and angry. She said, "But I want it, I want it, I want it! You are mean and stingy. You don't love me."
Her mother sighed and said, "Lily, that is not true. I love you very much, but I also want you to learn some wisdom. Wisdom is knowing what is good and what is bad, what is enough and what is too much, what is important and what is not. Wisdom is better than having many things. Wisdom makes you happy and kind."
Lily did not understand. She said, "How can I learn wisdom? Where can I find it?"
Her mother smiled and said, "Wisdom is not something you can buy or lift. Wisdom is something you can grow in your heart and mind. You can learn wisdom by listening to your elders, by reading books, by observing nature, by being kind to others, by saying sorry when you are wrong, by being thankful for what you have, by sharing with those who have less, by being honest and brave. Wisdom is a gift from God, and He gives it to those who seek it."
Lily thought about what her mother said. She felt a little ashamed of her tantrum. She said, "I'm sorry, mother. I was selfish and rude. I don't need the doll. I want to learn wisdom. Will you help me?"
Her mother hugged her and said, "Of course, my dear. I'm proud of you. You have taken the first step to wisdom. Come, let's go home and read a story together."

One day, in a small house, there were two children playing. The were talking and laughing, when suddenly the little brother smelled something burning. He ran to the kitchen and found the big brother holding a rag, and blue flames coming out of it. The little brother was scared and shouted "Fire!". The big brother quickly put the burning rag in a pot, and the fire stopped.
After that, both children were very grateful that the fire had been put out before it spread to the rest of the house. The big brother had been very brave and quick.
The mother was also grateful to the big brother for trying to keep them safe. She hugged them both and said she was proud of him. He smiled and the three of them hugged happily.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play outside. One day, while playing, she saw a wild bird. The bird was hungry. Sue wanted to feed the bird.
Sue went to her house and got some bread. She broke the bread into small pieces. She went back outside to feed the wild bird. The bird was happy and ate all the bread. Sue felt happy too.
After the bird ate, it let Sue give it a rub. Sue rubbed the bird's head gently. The bird liked Sue and stayed with her. They became good friends and played together every day.

Once there was a snail who lived in a garden. Every day he would mail letters. One day he was getting ready to mail one of his letters, when he noticed a wave coming towards him! At first he was scared, but then he realized that it was just a friendly wave. He took the letter and waved it around in the air. He was so happy.
But then he realized that the letter was fragile and he was afraid he was going to break it! So he quickly put the letter away and looked around for something else to mail. He found a twig and decided to mail it instead. He waved the twig around in the air and then put it in the mail. Phew!
The snail was so relieved that he hadn't broken the letter. He waved goodbye to the postman and happily continued on with his day.

Once upon a time, there was a hairy dog named Max. Max lived in a small house with a big yard. He loved to play outside with his ball.
One day, Max found a skull in the yard. The skull was white and had big teeth. Max was not scared of the skull. He thought it was a fun toy to play with.
Max showed the skull to his friend, a little bird named Sam. Sam was scared of the skull at first. But Max told Sam, "Do not be scared. It is just a toy." Sam and Max played with the skull all day, and they were not scared anymore. They had a lot of fun together.

Bob was a little boy who loved to travel. One day his Mommy said, "Bob! Put on your orange coat, let's go to the airport!".
Bob was really excited; he had never been to an airport before. He ran through the doors and looked around. He saw so many people and planes! 
Bob said, "Wow! This is cool!". But then, he saw a big sign saying "Flight Delayed". Bob started to panic.
Mommy saw thee sign too and said, "Don't worry Bob, we'll still be able to get on the plane". She hugged him and said, "Let's go get a snack!". 
Bob felt better and he knew that everything would be ok. He was glad he had his Mommy with him!

One day, Tom and his mom go to the station. They want to ride the big train. Tom is very happy. He likes to watch the trains come and go. He sees a big red train and a small blue train. Tom wants to ride the red train.
At the station, Tom meets a nice man. The man is deaf. He cannot hear Tom. But the man can talk with his hands. Tom thinks this is fun. They talk and laugh together. Tom learns how to say "hello" with his hands.
Soon, it is time to go. Tom and his mom get on the red train. They wave goodbye to the nice man. The train goes fast. Tom looks out the window and sees the station get smaller and smaller. He cannot wait to come back and see his new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a gray cat named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to measure things. Every day, Fluffy would find new things to measure with his little ruler.
One day, Fluffy found a big book. The book was full of stories about history. Fluffy wanted to measure the book, so he opened it and started to measure the pages.
As Fluffy measured the history book, he learned about all the fun things that happened a long time ago. He saw pictures of big castles, happy people, and pretty dresses. Fluffy loved the history book and measured it every day.

It was a sunny day and Rose was so excited. She was going to hear a story about a white rabbit. It was her favorite kind of story, so she could not wait to hear it. 
First, the storyteller said, "Once upon a time, there was a white rabbit who hopped through the forest." Rose's eyes sparkled and she clapped her hands. 
The storyteller continued. "The white rabbit wanted to find someone special. He looked high and he looked low, but he could not find the special someone he was looking for." 
Rose was very excited. She loved hearing stories like this one and could not wait to hear what would happen next. 
Then, the storyteller said, "The white rabbit kept hopping until he came to a bright and shining garden. There he found a special friend who was just like him!"
Rose beamed. She was so happy to hear that the white rabbit found a special friend. She could not wait to find out what happened next. 
Suddenly, the storyteller said, "And they lived happily ever after!" 
Rose was delighted. She could not wait to hear more exciting stories.

Tom and Sam are friends. They like to play with toys and sing songs. One day, they find a microphone in the garage. It is big and shiny and makes loud noises.
"Wow, look at this!" Tom says. "We can sing with this!"
"Let me try!" Sam says. He grabs the microphone and sings a silly song. Tom laughs and claps.
They take turns singing and having fun. But then, they hear a loud bark. It is Rex, the big dog from next door. He is tough and mean and likes to chase them.
"Uh-oh, Rex is here!" Tom says. "He will take our microphone!"
"Run!" Sam says. They drop the microphone and run to the house. Rex runs after them and picks up the microphone in his mouth.
"Hey, that's ours!" Tom says. He is angry and scared.
"What can we do?" Sam says. He is sad and worried.
They think hard. They have an idea. They unite and make a plan.
They go back to the garage and find a big ball. They throw the ball to the other side of the fence. Rex sees the ball and drops the microphone. He runs to get the ball.
"Quick, get the microphone!" Tom says. They run to the microphone and grab it.
"Yay, we did it!" Sam says. They hug and cheer.
They go back to the house and sing more songs. They are happy and proud. They are friends. They are brave. They are smart. They are united.

One day, a boy named Tim and his mom went to the port. They saw big boats and little boats. Tim loved to watch the boats. He saw a man with a big stick. The man was using the stick to measure the boats.
"Mom, what is he doing?" Tim asked.
"He is measuring the boats to see if they are fair," his mom said. "If a boat is too big or too small, it can't go in the water."
Just then, a very big fish jumped out of the water. It was so big, it made a big splash! Everyone at the port was wet. Tim and his mom laughed. The man with the big stick measured the fish. The fish was fair, so it could stay in the water. Tim and his mom had a fun day at the port.

One day, a boy named Tim had to help his mom pack a bag. They had to go to the hospital because Tim's dad was sick. Tim's room was very messy, but he tried to make it tidy before they left.
On their way to the hospital, Tim and his mom had a small fight. Tim wanted to bring his toy car, but his mom said no. Tim was sad and said, "But I want my car, please!" His mom replied, "We need to go fast, Tim. No time for toys now."
When they got to the hospital, they saw Tim's dad. He looked very sick and tired. Tim and his mom stayed with him, but he did not get better. In the end, Tim's dad went to sleep and did not wake up. Tim and his mom were very sad, and they missed him a lot.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. He lived in a big house with his mom, dad, and dog, Max. Tom loved to play with Max all day long. They would run, jump, and have fun together.
One day, Tom and Max were playing outside when a harsh wind blew. Max got scared and ran away. Tom called, "Max, come back!" But Max did not come back. Tom was full of worry. He went to his mom and said, "Mom, Max is gone!"
Mom said, "Do not worry, Tom. We will find Max." So, Tom and his mom went outside and called, "Max, where are you?" They looked and looked, but they could not find Max. Tom was very sad.
Just as they were about to give up, they heard a bark. It was Max! He was hiding behind a big tree. Tom and his mom were so happy to see Max. They hugged him and said, "Max, we love you. Do not run away again." From that day on, Tom, Max, and their family lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a big ship. The ship was smelly because it had many animals on it. The animals were going to a new home.
One day, a lion on the ship roared very loud. The other animals got scared. The lion felt bad for scaring his friends. He knew he had to be kind and help them feel better.
The lion said sorry to his friends. He told them that he would not roar loud anymore. The other animals were happy. They all learned that being kind and saying sorry can make things better. And they all lived happily on the smelly ship.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Daisy. Daisy's mom was very weak and always stayed in her bed. One day, Daisy's mom said to her, "Let's go put me in a hospital so I can get better."
Daisy was very sad that her mom was so weak, so she agreed. They got in the car and drove to the hospital.
When they arrived at the hospital, Daisy's mom said to her, "Thank you for helping me, Daisy. I know it's hard to see me like this, but try to be strong. I'll be back home soon!"
At the end of the day, Daisy's mom came back home, feeling better and stronger. Being strong even in tough times brings courage, and courage can help you get through anything.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to play outside with his dog, Spot. One day, Tim and Spot went to the park to play.
While they were playing, Tim saw a big chair. "I want to sit on the chair," said Tim. But when Tim sat on the chair, he felt uncomfortable. The chair was too hard and not nice to sit on.
Tim saw a man walking by. "Can you help me?" asked Tim. The man tried to make the chair more comfortable, but it did not work. Tim became sad because he could not sit on the chair. The end.

Once upon a time there were two children who were best friends. The children were named Sam and Tammy. Sam had curly black hair but Tammy was bald. 
On a sunny day, Sam asked Tammy what her favorite story was. Tammy said her favorite story was about a princess.
Sam asked Tammy to tell them the story. Tammy took a deep breath and began to tell the story. 
Tammy said, "Once upon a time, there was a beautiful princess who lived in a castle by the sea. One day, she set off on a journey to find her true love. On her journey, she met a Dragon and a Wizard who helped her and gave her advice. In the end, the princess found her true love and they lived happily ever after!" 
Sam was very happy when Tammy told the story and they both smiled.

Once upon a time, there was a little shrimp. The shrimp lived in a big sea with lots of other fish. The shrimp had an interesting shell that was very pretty.
One day, the shrimp met a big fish. The big fish said, "I like your shell. It is interesting." The shrimp was happy and said, "Thank you, big fish." They swam together and played in the sea.
While they were playing, the big fish had to sneeze. "Achoo!" sneezed the big fish. The shrimp laughed and said, "Bless you, big fish." They kept playing and having fun, and they became good friends.

One day, a cat named Tom and a dog named Max were playing in the yard. They were very good friends. Tom saw a dish on the grass. It was a big, shiny dish. Tom wanted to catch the dish, but it was too high for him.
Max saw Tom looking at the dish. He said, "Tom, I can help you catch the dish. I am tall and strong." Tom was happy and said, "Thank you, Max!" So, Max jumped high and caught the dish with his mouth. He gave the dish to Tom.
They played with the dish, making it noisy by banging it on the ground. They laughed and had a lot of fun. Their friends, the birds, joined them, and they all played together. Tom and Max were very happy that they could catch the dish and share it with their friends.

Daddy was getting ready for a big meeting. He was very excited, so he checked his tie to make sure it looked nice. It was a unique tie, his favourite one.
Mummy watched him from the corner of her eye and smiled. "Do you need any help getting ready, honey?" she asked.
Daddy sighed. "No sweetheart," he said. "I have to do this all by myself."
When Daddy was finished, he gave Mummy a big kiss goodbye and went out the door.
Mummy stood in the hallway and watched him go. She wished she could go with him and help, but Daddy always wanted to do everything himself.
Suddenly, she heard someone in the room. It was the three-year-old twin boys! They were running around and playing while Daddy was getting ready. She had ignored them while Daddy was getting ready.
Mummy shouted to the boys, "Come here and give Daddy a hug before he goes!" But the boys ignored her and kept playing.
Mummy was so upset. The boys had forgotten Daddy was leaving and ignored her. She was about to get angry when she realized the boys were just being kids.
Mummy smiled and said softly, "It's ok. Daddy will be back soon." And even though the boys ignored her, Mummy knew that her unique little family was worth it.

Once upon a time, there was a small hut in the woods. A weird bird lived in the hut. The bird had a big beak and funny feathers. One day, the wind blew very hard. The wind broke the hut. The weird bird was sad.
A kind squirrel saw the sad bird. The squirrel said, "Don't cry, bird. I will help you fix your hut." The bird was happy. The squirrel and the bird worked together. They used sticks and leaves to fix the hut.
After they fixed the hut, the squirrel and the bird became friends. They played together every day. The squirrel liked the bird's funny feathers. The bird liked the squirrel's bushy tail. They were happy in their little hut in the woods.

One day, a little girl named Sue went to her friend Tom's house. They wanted to play a fun game. They decided to play on the stairs. Sue said, "Let's clap every time we jump on a stair!" Tom agreed and said, "Yes, that sounds fun!" So, they started to play.
As they jumped and clapped, they laughed and had a great time. But then, something unexpected happened. A big, guilty-looking dog came running up the stairs. The dog was chasing a small, scared cat. Sue and Tom stopped playing and looked at the animals.
Sue said, "Oh no! The dog wants to catch the cat!" Tom thought for a moment and said, "Let's clap really loud to scare the dog away!" They clapped as loud as they could. The dog stopped chasing the cat and ran away. The cat was safe, and Sue and Tom felt like heroes. They laughed and continued to play their stair game, happy that they had saved the day.

Lily and Ben are twins. They share a big bed with a soft mattress. They like to sleep and play on the bed. But sometimes they fight over the toys and the blankets. They make the bed very messy.
One day, Mom says, "Lily and Ben, you are too big to share a bed. You need to separate. You will have your own beds in your own rooms. You can choose your own toys and blankets. But you have to keep them tidy."
Lily and Ben are sad and angry. They do not want to separate. They like to be together. They say, "No, Mom, we do not want to separate. We want to share. We do not care if the bed is messy. We love our bed and our mattress."
Mom says, "I know you love each other, but you also need your own space. You will still see each other every day. You can play together in the living room or the garden. But you have to sleep in your own beds at night. It will be good for you. Trust me."
Lily and Ben are not happy, but they listen to Mom. They help Mom to move the mattress and the bed to their new rooms. They pick their own toys and blankets. They say goodbye to their old bed and their old room. They hug each other and say, "We will always be twins. We will always be friends. We will always share our love."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Jane. Jane was very obedient, always listening to her parents. One day, Jane wanted to do something naughty.
She sneaked into the kitchen and saw a box of tea. She wanted it, so Jane thought she could take it without her parents noticing.
Suddenly, her mother appeared. "What are you doing, Jane?" her mother asked. Jane hung her head in shame, "I'm stealing the tea," she said meekly. Her mother looked disappointed, but she said, "That wasn't very nice, was it? Come with me".
Jane followed her mother quietly. As they walked, Jane thought about what she had done. She knew that she had done something naughty, and that she might face a punishment.
When they arrived at her bedroom, her mother said, "I'm not mad, Jane. You can keep the tea. But I want you to think about why it was not a good idea to steal it".
Later in the evening Jane was obedient and polite, her mother was sure she had learned her lesson. From that day on, Jane never wanted to steal anything again.

Once upon a time, there was a yellow car. The yellow car liked to spin. One day, the yellow car went to the track. The track was big and fun. The yellow car was very happy.
