At the track, the yellow car met a blue car. The blue car said, "I like to spin too! Let's spin together!" The yellow car and the blue car spun around the track. They went fast and laughed.
But then, the yellow car spun too fast. The yellow car went off the track. "Oh no!" said the blue car. The yellow car was sad and broken. The end.

One day, a little girl named Lily wanted to buy some sweet juice. She went to the store with her mom. At the store, she saw many bottles of juice. She picked the one that looked the most sweet.
"Mom, can we get this juice?" asked Lily. Her mom looked at the juice and said, "Yes, we can get that juice. It looks very sweet. Let's pay for it now."
They went to the man at the store to pay for the juice. But something unexpected happened. The man said, "Today, you don't need to pay for the juice. It's a special day. You can have the juice for free!"
Lily and her mom were very happy. They said thank you to the man and went home. They drank the sweet juice together and it was the best juice they ever had.

Once upon a time, there was a thin cat named Tom. Tom lived in a small house with his friend, a big dog named Max. They liked to play outside and look at the sky. One day, they saw a shiny thing in the sky. It was a comet!
The comet was very bright and moved fast. Tom and Max were very happy to see it. They wanted to see the comet again, so they waited for it to return. They waited and waited, but the comet did not come back.
Then, one day, something unexpected happened. The comet returned, but it was not alone! With it came many small, bright stars that danced in the sky. Tom and Max were so surprised and happy. They laughed and played under the sky full of stars. The comet had brought them a beautiful show, and they would always remember the day the comet returned with its new friends.

Once upon a time, there was a creative little girl named Lily. She loved to draw and make things with her hands. One day, she saw a shiny razor in the bathroom. She thought it would be fun to use it in her art.
Lily's mom saw her and said, "No, Lily! Don't enter the bathroom and don't touch the razor. It is sharp and can hurt you." Lily was sad, but she listened to her mom.
Instead, Lily found some paper and crayons. She made a beautiful picture for her mom. Her mom was so happy and proud. She said, "Lily, you are so creative! You don't need a razor to make great art." And they hung the picture on the fridge. Lily learned that she can make beautiful things without using dangerous tools.

Mama and Sandy were very excited. They had just unpacked a big, gray box. Inside the box were lots of special things. 
"What do you think is inside?" asked Mama. 
"I hope it's something pretty," said Sandy. 
Mama opened the box. Sandy peered inside. She couldn't believe her eyes. There were lots of different toys. They were colorful and sparkly.
"Oh! They are so pretty! I love them!" squealed Sandy. 
Mama smiled. She had found the perfect gift for Sandy. They unpacked the toys and soon began to play. Sandy was overjoyed. 
Mama hugged Sandy and said, "I think you will love these toys for a very long time." 
Sandy smiled and replied, "Yes, I will. They are so, so pretty."

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to play outside in the cold. One day, it was very cold and Tim wanted to play. He put on his coat, hat, and gloves. Tim went outside to play in the snow.
Tim saw a big case on the ground. It was dull and looked old. He wanted to see what was inside the case. He opened it up and found a magic wand. Tim was very happy and wanted to play with the wand.
Tim waved the magic wand and said, "freeze!" Suddenly, everything around him froze. The trees, the animals, and even the snow froze. Tim was scared and didn't know what to do. He tried to unfreeze everything, but the wand didn't work. Tim was sad and cold. He went back home, but everything stayed frozen. Tim's world was now a cold and icy place.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big factory. The factory made toys for all the kids. One day, a little girl named Lily was walking by the factory. She saw a toy she really liked. Lily wanted that toy now, so she went to the factory door.
Lily knocked on the door and a nice man came out. Lily said, "I want that toy, please!" The man knew Lily was not ready to have the toy yet. He said, "You must wait until your birthday, then the toy will be ready for you."
Lily was not happy. She said, "I demand that toy now!" The man was kind and said, "I understand, but good things come to those who wait. When the toy is ready, it will be the best gift ever." Lily thought about it and decided to wait. When her birthday came, she got the toy and it was the best gift ever.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy boat that he loved to play with in the water. One day, Tim went to the park with his mom and dad to play near the pond.
As Tim played with his boat, he saw a small leak in it. He felt ashamed that his boat was not perfect. He urged his mom and dad to help him fix the leak. They tried their best, but the leak was still there.
Tim was sad, but then something unexpected happened. A duck swam up to Tim's boat and pushed it with its beak. The duck liked the leak because it made the boat go slower, so the duck could play with it too. Tim was happy that the duck liked his boat, even with the leak. He was not ashamed anymore and played with the duck all day long.

One day, a cheerful girl named Lucy found a chess set in her toy box. She did not know how to play chess, but she liked the pretty pieces. She decided to show her friend, Tom, the new toy.
"Look, Tom!" Lucy said, holding the chess set. "I found this in my toy box. Let's play!"
Tom looked at the chess set and smiled. "Okay, Lucy! But first, we need to learn how to play."
Lucy and Tom stood by the table and put the chess pieces on the board. They tried to learn the rules, but they were too hard. So, they made up their own game with the chess pieces.
They took turns moving the pieces and laughing as they played their new game. They had a lot of fun with the chess set, even if they didn't know the real rules. And they played happily together all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. She had a toy box with many plastic toys. Jane loved her toys very much. One day, she saw a new toy at her friend's house. It was a big, shiny car. Jane felt envious because she wanted the car to belong to her.
Jane asked her friend, "Can I have your car?" Her friend said, "No, it's mine. You can play with it, but it doesn't belong to you." Jane was sad but played with the car anyway. While they were playing, something unexpected happened.
Jane's friend had to go inside the house for a moment. Jane thought, "I want the car to belong to me." So, she took the car and ran back to her house. When her friend came back, he saw the car was gone and was very sad.
Jane played with the car in her room, but she didn't feel happy. She knew the car didn't belong to her, and she had made her friend sad. Suddenly, the car broke. It was not a strong car, after all. Jane felt bad for taking the car and making her friend sad. Now, she had a broken toy, and her friend had nothing.
In the end, Jane learned that taking things that don't belong to her can make others sad and lead to bad endings.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to cook. One day, she found a big pan in the kitchen. Sue wanted to make a yummy cake for her family.
Sue started to mix the cake. But it was difficult. The pan was too big and heavy for her. She tried and tried, but she could not mix it well. Then, something unexpected happened. A little bird came in through the window. The bird saw Sue and wanted to help her. The bird used its beak to mark a line on the pan.
Sue saw the line and got an idea. She put some of the cake mix in a smaller pan. Now it was not difficult to mix the cake. Sue and the bird worked together to make the cake. When it was done, they shared it with Sue's family.
The moral of the story is that when things are difficult, it is good to ask for help or find a new way to do it. And sometimes, help can come from unexpected places.

Sam and Lily were playing with their blocks in the living room. They liked to build towers and bridges and houses with the blocks. They had many colors of blocks, but their favorite was green. Green was the color of the grass and the trees and the frogs.
Mom was in the kitchen, making lunch. She was happy to see Sam and Lily playing nicely together. She did not want to disturb them, so she was very quiet. She made sandwiches and cut some fruit and put them on a tray. She also poured some juice in two cups.
She carried the tray to the living room and said, "Lunch is ready, kids. Come and eat." Sam and Lily looked up and smiled. They saw the sandwiches and the fruit and the juice. They were hungry and thirsty. They said, "Thank you, Mom."
They left their blocks on the floor and went to the table. They sat on their chairs and ate their lunch. They talked to Mom and to each other. They said how much they liked the food and the juice.
But they did not see the cat. The cat was gray and furry and sneaky. The cat liked to play with the blocks, too. The cat saw the green blocks and wanted to touch them. The cat jumped on the floor and ran to the blocks. The cat knocked down the towers and the bridges and the houses. The cat made a big mess.
Sam and Lily heard a noise. They turned around and saw the cat and the blocks. They were surprised and angry. They said, "Bad cat! You disturbed our blocks! Go away, cat!"
The cat did not listen. The cat kept playing with the blocks. The cat did not care. The cat was having fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She was a lonely daughter because she had no friends to play with. Her mommy and daddy were always busy working. One day, Lily went outside to play in the big park near her house.
While she was playing, Lily saw a small cat. The cat was very cute and had soft fur. Lily wanted to be friends with the cat, so she called out, "Hey, little cat! Do you want to play with me?" The cat looked at Lily and meowed happily.
As they were playing, the cat started to run fast, and Lily tried to catch it. But she did not see a big rock in front of her. She stumbled and fell down, but the cat came back to help her. The cat rubbed against Lily, and she felt better. Now, Lily was not a lonely daughter anymore. She had a new friend, the cat, and they played together every day.

One sunny day, Max was playing in the park with his best friend Alex. They were playing hide-and-seek. Max was hiding behind a tree while Alex tried to find him. Alex shouted "Where are you, Max?". Max replied, "I'm hiding!"
Soon, Max heard Alex's footsteps getting closer and closer and he knew he had been found. Alex said with a huge smile, "I found you!" Max was very proud and gave Alex a big hug. 
When they returned home, Max's parents surprised him with a birthday party. He had lots of presents, cake and ice cream. His parents were so proud of him!
Max and Alex had a wonderful time at the birthday party. Everyone was laughing and having a good time. In the middle of the party, Max decided to hide again. This time, no one could find him! He had to eventually come out of his hiding spot when all the adults began shouting "Max! Max, where are you?!"
Max was very proud of himself for being so clever.

Once upon a time, there was a pale little girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to help her mom bake cookies. One day, they made the yummiest cookies and put them in the oven. Lucy was so excited to eat them!
While they waited, Lucy stared at the oven. She wanted the cookies to be ready now. But her mom said, "Be patient, my dear. Good things take time."
Then, something unexpected happened. A tiny mouse came out and stared at the oven too! Lucy was scared, but the mouse said, "I just want a cookie. Please share with me." Lucy thought about it and decided to share. She learned that being kind and patient is important. And they all enjoyed the cookies together.

Once upon a time, there was a little ball named Bounce. Bounce loved to jump and play all day. Bounce had many friends who liked to play with him. They would laugh and have fun together.
One day, Bounce saw a big mouth. The mouth looked crazy and scary. Bounce wanted to be brave, so he jumped into the mouth. The mouth closed and Bounce was inside. Bounce's friends were worried and did not know what to do.
Then, something unexpected happened. The crazy mouth started to laugh. Bounce tickled the mouth from inside and made it laugh. The mouth opened and Bounce jumped out. Bounce and his friends learned that being brave and making others laugh can help when things seem scary.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She had a pretty rose in her garden. Lily loved her rose so much. She wanted to take it with her on a trip.
Lily tried to pack her rose in a bag. But the rose was too big. Lily felt frustrated. She wanted to take her rose with her.
Lily's mom saw her and said, "Lily, don't be frustrated. We can put the rose in a little pot. Then, you can carry it with you." Lily smiled and said, "Thank you, Mom!" They put the rose in a little pot and Lily took it with her on the trip. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a thin cat named Tom. Tom liked to play with a rod. One day, Tom went outside to find his rod.
Tom looked and looked for his rod. He saw it under a big tree. Tom was so happy! He picked up the rod and started to play.
While Tom was playing, he saw a big dog. The dog was very nice. The dog did a bow to Tom. Tom did a bow back. They became friends and played with the rod together. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a smart girl named Lisa. She loved to learn and count numbers. One day, Lisa found a box with a secret inside.
Lisa wanted to reveal the secret, but she needed a special number to open the box. She tried many numbers, but the box stayed closed. Lisa was sad and asked her friend, Tom, for help.
Tom was good at guessing, so he tried some numbers too. Finally, the box opened with the number "7". Lisa and Tom were very happy. They revealed the secret inside the box: a big, shiny star. They put the star in the sky and it became the brightest one of all.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a broken hoop in his yard. He was sad because he could not play with it. But Tim had an idea. He thought he could fix the hoop and make it like new.
Tim worked hard to fix the hoop. He used tape and glue to make it strong again. When he was done, the hoop looked good. He was very happy and wanted to surprise his friends with the fixed hoop.
He called his friends to come and play. They were all surprised to see the hoop was not broken anymore. They played a fun game with the hoop. But then, the hoop broke again! Tim's friends were sad, but Tim just laughed. He knew he could fix the hoop again and they could play more games.

One day, a mighty cat named Max was walking down the street. He saw a newspaper on the ground. Max picked up the newspaper and started to read. He saw a picture of a big cake. Max loved cake! He wanted to find the cake in the picture.
Max walked and walked until he saw a little bird named Tina. Max said, "Tina, do you know where the big cake is?" Tina looked at the newspaper and said, "I think the cake is in the big house over there." Max and Tina went to the big house to find the cake.
When they got to the big house, they opened the door. Inside, they saw a big party! There were balloons and games. Max and Tina were so happy. But then, Max saw the cake and screamed, "The cake is not real! It is just a picture on the wall!" Max and Tina laughed and laughed. They stayed at the party and had lots of fun.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big truck in the park. The truck has a cage. Inside the cage, there is a leopard. The leopard is angry and loud. It wants to get out.
Anna and Ben are curious. They go near the truck. They want to see the leopard better. They wave at the leopard. The leopard waves back with its paw. Anna and Ben think the leopard is friendly. They do not know the leopard is hungry and mean.
The man who drives the truck is careless. He does not lock the cage well. He goes to buy a drink. He does not see Anna and Ben. He does not hear the leopard.
The leopard sees a chance. It pushes the cage door. The door opens. The leopard jumps out. It runs to Anna and Ben. It roars. It bites. Anna and Ben scream. They try to run away. But they are too slow. The leopard is too fast.
The man hears the screams. He drops his drink. He runs to the truck. He sees the leopard. He sees Anna and Ben. He is shocked and sad. He calls for help. But it is too late. The leopard has hurt Anna and Ben. They are not moving. They are not breathing. The leopard has killed them.

Once upon a time there was a big kiss. It was a purple kiss that had a tip. The tip was pointing in the sky. One day, a little girl saw it and ran over to it. She closed her eyes, puckered her lips, and gave the kiss a big smooch. The kiss was so surprised that it tipped back. Then the little girl opened her eyes and saw the purple kiss tip running around the sky. She laughed and giggled and chased after it. Finally, the kiss tired and stopped. The little girl caught it and kissed it again. The kiss was so happy that it showered her with purple sparkles. And they both lived happily ever after.

Mira and Leo like to play dress-up. They have many clothes and hats and shoes in a big box. They like to make funny fashion shows for their mom and dad. They put on the clothes and walk and pose and smile.
One day, they find a new dress in the box. It is long and white and has many flowers on it. Mira thinks it is very pretty. She wants to wear it for the fashion show. Leo thinks it is very old. He says it is ancient. He does not want to wear it.
Mira and Leo start to fight. They pull and tug on the dress. They yell and cry. They disturb their mom and dad, who are working in the other room. Their mom and dad come to see what is wrong. They see the dress and they look surprised.
They tell Mira and Leo that the dress is very special. It is the dress that their grandma wore on her wedding day. It is a family treasure. They say they are sorry for putting it in the box. They did not mean to disturb the dress.
Mira and Leo feel bad. They say they are sorry too. They hug their mom and dad. They hug each other. They put the dress back in the box. They say they will not disturb it again. They find other clothes to wear for the fashion show. They have fun and make their mom and dad laugh.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw a big statue of a man on a horse. Tim looked at the statue with serious eyes. He asked his mom, "Who is that man?"
His mom told him, "That man was very fast. He would speed on his horse to help people in need. He was a good man."
Tim wanted to be like the man on the horse. So, he and his mom played a game. They pretended to be on horses and speed around the park, helping others. They helped an old lady find her lost hat, and they helped a little girl find her mom.
At the end of the day, Tim's mom said, "You were a good helper today, just like the man on the horse."
Tim learned that being fast and helpful was a good thing. He wanted to help people like the man on the horse did. The moral of the story is to be kind and help others when they need it.

Once upon a time, there was a heavy bell in a big tree. The bell loved to ring and make loud sounds. Every day, the wind would blow and ring the bell. The bell was very happy.
One day, a little bird came to live in the tree. The bird liked the bell, but the loud sound was too much for her. She had an idea to make the bell not so heavy. The bird pushed some soft earth up into the bell. The earth made the bell lighter and not so loud.
The next day, the wind blew again, but the bell did not ring. The bird was so happy that her idea worked. But then, something unexpected happened. The wind blew the soft earth out of the bell, and it became heavy again. The bell rang loud and clear. The bird and the bell became friends, and they learned to love the sound of the ringing bell.

One day, a boy named Tim found a small, fragile bird in his yard. The bird was sad and scared. Tim wanted to comfort the bird, so he gave it a soft hug. "Don't worry, little bird," said Tim. "I will take care of you."
Tim brought the bird inside and gave it some food. He thought the bird might like some jelly. The bird looked at the jelly and chirped. It was happy to eat the sweet treat. Tim and the bird became very good friends.
One day, Tim woke up and saw that the bird was not in its cage. He looked all around the house, but he could not find it. Suddenly, he heard a loud noise from the kitchen. When he went in, he saw the bird sitting on a big pile of jelly! The bird had found the jelly jar and opened it. Tim laughed and said, "Silly bird, you really love jelly, don't you?"

One day, a cool little bug named Bob went out into the world. He liked to glow at night. His glow was very pretty. All his friends liked his glow too. They all played together and had fun.
One night, Bob saw a big, dark place. He wanted to make it glow too. So, he called all his friends to help him. They all went to the dark place and started to glow. But, the dark place did not become bright. They all felt sad.
Then, a wise old bug came to them. He told them that they did not need to make the whole world glow. They just needed to make their own little part of the world bright and happy. The bugs learned that they did not need to do big things to make a difference. They just needed to be kind and happy in their own little world. And that was enough.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play and sing. One day, they find a big box in the park. The box has a picture of a guitar on it. Anna and Ben are happy. They want to play music.
They open the box and see a guitar. It is red and shiny. It has strings and buttons. Anna and Ben take turns to hold the guitar. They try to make sounds with it. They sing along. They have fun.
But then, a man comes to the park. He is angry. He sees the box and the guitar. He says, "Hey, that's my guitar! You stole it from my car! Give it back to me now!"
Anna and Ben are scared. They say, "No, we didn't steal it. We found it in the park. We just wanted to play music. Please don't be mad at us."
The man does not listen. He grabs the guitar from Ben. He pushes Anna and Ben to the ground. He says, "You are bad kids. You are liars. You are thieves. Go away and never come back!"
He puts the guitar and the box in his car. He drives away. He leaves Anna and Ben alone in the park. They are hurt and sad. They cry.
They go home. They tell their moms what happened. Their moms hug them and say, "We are sorry. That was a mean man. He should not have done that to you. You are good kids. You are honest. You are brave."
Their moms make them soup for lunch. The soup is spicy. It burns their mouths. They don't like it. They say, "Yuck! This soup is too spicy. We don't want it. We want something else."
Their moms say, "No, you have to eat it. It is good for you. It will make you feel better. You have to finish it. No more music. No more playing. Just soup."
Anna and Ben are unhappy. They eat the soup. They finish it. They don't play music. They don't play anything. They have a bad day. The end.

One day, a boy named Tim found a toy knife in his toy box. He liked to play with it because it was colorful and fun. Tim's dog, Spot, would watch him play and wag his tail.
One day, while Tim was playing with the toy knife, he felt uncomfortable. He didn't know why, but he wanted to shake the toy knife. So, he shook it and something unexpected happened. The toy knife opened up and there was a small note inside.
Tim couldn't read the note, so he showed it to his mom. His mom read the note and smiled. She told Tim that the note was from his grandpa, who had hidden it in the toy knife long ago. The note said, "I love you, Tim. Always be happy and play with a smile." Tim felt happy and hugged his mom. He knew his grandpa was watching him from the sky and smiling too.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play in the mud and get dirty. His clothes always had a pattern of dirt on them.
One day, Tim's mom saw his dirty clothes and requested him to stop playing in the mud. She said, "Tim, please play with your toys inside the house, so you won't get dirty."
Tim listened to his mom and started to play with his toys inside. He found out that he could make patterns with his blocks too. Tim learned that he could have fun without getting dirty.
Soon, Tim's clothes were clean, and his mom was happy. Tim also felt good about keeping his clothes clean. The moral of the story is to listen to our parents and try new things, as they know what is best for us.

Tom was a loyal dog who loved his farmer and his farm. He liked to run and play in the fields of wheat, and watch the birds and rabbits. Sometimes he would wander too far and get lost, but he always found his way back by following his nose.
One day, Tom saw a big truck on the road. He was curious and wanted to see what was inside. He ran after the truck and left the farm behind. He did not notice that the truck was going to the city, where there were many cars and noises and people.
Tom was scared and confused in the city. He could not smell the wheat or the farmer. He could not find a place to rest or a friend to play with. He missed his farm and his farmer very much. He wished he had not wandered away from them.
He was hungry and tired, and he looked for some food in a trash can. But a mean cat hissed and scratched him, and a loud horn honked and scared him. He ran away and hid under a bridge. He felt very sad and lonely.
Then he heard a familiar sound. It was the truck that he had followed. It was going back to the farm. Tom ran after the truck again, hoping it would take him home. He barked and wagged his tail, and the driver saw him in the mirror. He stopped the truck and opened the door. He recognized Tom as the farmer's dog and smiled. He said, "You're a lucky dog, Tom. I'm taking you back to your farm. You must have wandered very far. Your farmer must be worried about you."
Tom was happy and grateful. He jumped into the truck and licked the driver's face. He could smell the wheat and the farmer again. He learned his lesson and never wandered away from his farm again. He stayed loyal to his farmer and his farm, and they loved him very much.

Once upon a time, there was a big bear named Bobo. Bobo was very strong and could carry many things. One day, Bobo met a little bird named Tweet. Tweet was very small, but she was very good at talking. She knew many words and could speak in a special language that only birds knew.
Bobo and Tweet became friends, and they played together every day. One day, they found a tree with fruit that looked tasty. The fruit was a new kind for them. Tweet told Bobo to carry the fruit back to their homes so they could share it. Bobo was happy to help and carried the fruit in his big paws.
When they got home, they tried the fruit. But it was bitter! Bobo and Tweet did not like the taste at all. They were sad, but then Tweet had an idea. She used her special bird language to call her bird friends. The birds came and took the bitter fruit away. They brought back sweet fruit for Bobo and Tweet to enjoy. The friends learned that sometimes things don't go as planned, but working together can make everything better.

Once upon a time, there was a hoop. The hoop loved to spin all day long. It would spin in the sun and spin in the rain. The hoop was very happy.
One day, the hoop met a little girl. The girl was sad and said, "I am sorry, but I lost my toy." The hoop wanted to help the girl. So, the hoop spun around and around, looking for the lost toy.
Finally, the hoop found the toy under a big tree. The girl was so happy and said, "Thank you, hoop!" The hoop felt good for helping the girl. From that day on, the hoop and the girl became best friends, and they would spin and play together every day.

The morning was calm. Tom was at the beach with his dad. They saw a wooden cross on the sand. His dad said "Let's shoot the cross!" 
Tom looked confused. His dad explained. He said: "We can shoot a toy at the cross. Do you want to try it?" 
Tom was excited and nodded. 
His dad gave him a toy gun and showed him how to shoot. He told Tom to hold the gun tight, aim at the cross and take a deep breath. Then he said "Okay, now shoot!" 
Tom closed one eye and took aim. He slowly squeezed the trigger and shot. The toy bounced off the arms of the cross! 
Tom smiled. He and his dad shot at the cross for a while and had lots of fun. When they were finished they walked away, still smiling and feeling calm.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved to play outside in the sun. One day, Lucy found a small, light crystal on the ground. It was very pretty and shiny.
Lucy showed the crystal to her friend, Tim. "Look, Tim! I found a pretty crystal," she said. Tim wanted to see how shiny it was, so he held it up to the sun. The crystal made many colors scatter all around them.
Lucy and Tim played with the crystal all day. They laughed and danced in the colorful light. They knew they found something special and would always remember the day they found the light crystal.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a girl named Lily. She had a pretty veil that she loved to wear. One sunny day, Lily felt dizzy while playing outside. She sat down on the grass and called for her mom.
"Mommy, I feel dizzy," said Lily. Her mom came running and gave her a hug to comfort her. "Don't worry, my dear. Let's go inside and rest," said her mom.
As they walked into the house, Lily's veil got caught on a branch. The veil pulled off her head and flew away. Lily was sad, but her mom said, "We will find it later."
After a short nap, Lily felt better. She and her mom went outside to look for the veil. When they found it, they saw a baby bird snuggled in the veil, keeping warm. Lily smiled and said, "The bird can keep the veil. It's a nice home for it." Her mom hugged her and they went back inside, happy to have helped a new friend.

One day, a little bird named Tim was playing in the mud. He liked to whistle a sweet song while he played. As he whistled and played, a small frog named Sam hopped by.
"Hi, Tim!" said Sam. "I like your sweet song. Can you teach me to whistle?"
"Sure!" said Tim. "Just pucker your lips and blow air out gently."
Sam tried to whistle, but he could only make a funny sound. They both laughed and tried again. After a while, Sam could whistle just like Tim.
Now, Tim and Sam play in the mud together, whistling their sweet song. They are the best of friends, and everyone loves to hear their happy tunes.

Anna and Ben went to the beach with Mom and Dad. They liked to play in the sand and look for shells. They saw many things in the water, like fish and seaweed and rocks.
One day, they found a black crab on the sand. It had big claws and small eyes. It moved very fast and made a funny noise. Anna and Ben were curious and wanted to touch it, but Mom and Dad said no.
"Black crabs can pinch you with their claws," Mom said. "They are not friendly like some other crabs. You have to be careful and leave them alone."
Anna and Ben nodded and watched the black crab from a distance. They saw it dig a hole in the sand and hide inside. They wondered what it was doing and what it ate.
They decided to make a sandcastle near the hole, but not too close. They used their buckets and shovels and some shells and seaweed to decorate it. They were very proud of their sandcastle and showed it to Mom and Dad.
"Wow, that's a beautiful sandcastle," Dad said. "You are very creative and smart. Do you remember what we told you about the black crab?"
"Yes, we remember," Anna and Ben said. "We don't touch it, we just look at it. It can pinch us with its claws."
"Very good," Mom said. "You are very brave and respectful. The black crab is happy in its hole and you are happy in your sandcastle. You can be friends from afar."
Anna and Ben smiled and waved at the black crab. They hoped it liked their sandcastle too. They had a lot of fun at the beach with Mom and Dad and the black crab. They would always remember that day.

Once upon a time, there was an old table in a room. The table had not been used in a very long time, since it was so old.
One day, a little girl came into the room and saw the table. She looked at it for a moment, thinking about what she could do with it. She decided that she wanted to use it to draw a picture.
The little girl got out a piece of paper and a crayon and sat at the table. She was very excited! She drew a beautiful picture at the table.
When she was done, she decided that she would keep the old table in the room as a special place for her to draw pictures. Every day, she would come over and make something new. The old table was very happy in its new job.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She loved to climb trees and eat sweet candy. One day, she saw a big tree with a candy bag on top. Mia wanted to get the candy.
Mia started to climb the tree. It was a little hard, but she did not give up. When she reached the top, she grabbed the candy bag. Mia was so happy. She climbed down the tree to share the candy with her friends.
Mia saw her friend Tom and said, "Look, I found a candy bag in the tree! Let's eat it together." Tom was excited and they both ate the sweet candy. They laughed and played all day, happy to share their sweet treat.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside in the morning. He would run, jump, and enjoy the warm sun on his face. He played with his friends and they all had fun together.
One day, Tim saw a small bird on the ground. The bird looked sorry and sad. Tim wanted to help the bird. He picked up the bird and took it to his mom. His mom said, "Good job, Tim. We will take care of the bird."
Tim and his mom helped the bird get better. They fed the bird and made it a warm bed. Soon, the bird was happy and could fly again. Tim learned that it was good to help others when they are sorry and sad. The bird flew away, but always came back to visit Tim in the morning. They both enjoyed their new friendship.

Lily and Ben were playing hide and seek in the old house. Lily counted to ten and then looked for Ben. She opened a door and saw a dark room. She walked inside and saw a big bed with a blanket. She thought Ben might be hiding under the blanket, so she lifted it up.
"Boo!" Ben shouted and jumped out. He made Lily scream and laugh. He hugged her and said, "I got you!"
They looked around the room and saw many things. They saw a lamp, a chair, a mirror, and a dresser. They also saw many cobwebs on the walls and the ceiling. Cobwebs were like thin strings that spiders made. They were dusty and sticky.
"Look at the cobwebs!" Lily said. "They are so many and so big!"
"Let's touch them!" Ben said. He reached out his hand and touched a cobweb. It felt soft and tickly. He pulled it and it stretched. He wrapped it around his finger and said, "Look, I have a ring!"
Lily wanted to try too. She touched a cobweb and pulled it. But she pulled too hard and the cobweb broke. It fell on her face and covered her mouth and nose. She tried to get it off, but it stuck to her skin and hair. She felt scared and angry. She opened her mouth and tried to spit it out. But she tasted the cobweb. It tasted bad. It tasted like dirt and bugs. She made a face and said, "Yuck! The cobweb tastes yucky!"
Ben saw Lily and laughed. He thought she looked funny with the cobweb on her face. He said, "You look like a mummy!"
Lily did not like that. She felt dizzy and sick. She ran to the door and said, "I don't like this room! I don't like the cobwebs! I want to go out!"
Ben followed her and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I was just joking. Don't be mad at me."
They left the room and closed the door. They decided to play somewhere else. They did not want to taste the cobwebs again.

One day, a boy named Tim found a hidden paper under his bed. He was very happy to find it. The paper had a big tree on it. Tim ran to show it to his mom. "Look, mom! I found a paper with a tree!" said Tim.
His mom looked at the paper and smiled. "That's a nice tree, Tim. Let's put it on the wall." Tim and his mom put the paper on the wall. They were very happy.
Later, Tim's friend, Lily, came to play. They played near the paper with the tree. Lily saw the paper and said, "Wow! That's a pretty tree!" Suddenly, she tripped and fell. Her hand hit the paper and it made a loud snap. The paper fell down, but there was a surprise. Behind the paper was a hidden door! Tim and Lily were very excited. They opened the door and found a secret room full of toys. They played all day and had lots of fun.

Mandy sat on the yellow cube. She could feel the warmth of the sun on her face.
"What are you doing?" asked her brother.
"I'm sitting," Mandy said.
Her brother smiled. He pointed to the yellow cube. "What is that?"
Mandy looked at the cube. "That's a cube," she said.
Her brother touched the cube. "It's soft," he said.
Mandy touched it too. She nodded. "It's yellow," she said.
They both looked at the cube. Suddenly, Mandy had an idea. "Let's play!" she said, smiling.
So they took turns sitting on the yellow cube and they laughed and played the whole afternoon.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a very enthusiastic kid who loved to play with his toys. One day, he found a long cable in his room. He didn't know what it was for, but he thought it would be fun to play with.
Tim decided to play a game with the cable. He pretended it was a snake that he needed to catch. He chased the cable-snake around his room, trying to grab it. But the cable-snake was very fast and didn't want to surrender. 
After a while, Tim got tired and sat down. He looked at the cable-snake and said, "I give up. You win, cable-snake!" The cable-snake seemed happy that Tim surrendered. They became friends and played together every day. And that's the story of Tim and the cable-snake.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog. The dog was very interesting. It had three legs, but it was still a happy dog. The dog liked to run and play with its friends.
One day, the interesting dog went for a walk. It walked far away from its home. The dog saw many new things. It played with other animals and had a lot of fun. But then, the dog started to miss its home.
The interesting dog wanted to go back home. It walked and walked until it was very tired. At the end of the day, the dog found its home. The dog was very happy to be back. It played with its friends and slept in its warm bed. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. He liked to explore and examine things. One day, he went for a walk with his mom in the park.
In the park, Tom saw a smooth stick. It looked like a cane. He picked it up and showed it to his mom. "Look, mom! I found a cane!" said Tom.
Tom and his mom walked and talked. Tom used the cane to help him walk. They had a fun day in the park. The end.

One day, a blue bird named Bob was flying in the sky. He saw a big tree and sat on it. Bob saw his friend, a little girl named Sue, playing in the park. She was feeling the texture of the leaves and grass with her hands. Bob wanted to play with Sue too.
Bob flew down to Sue and said, "Hi Sue, do you want to play with me?" Sue was happy to see Bob. She said, "Yes, let's play together!" They played and laughed for a long time. They had a lot of fun.
Then, Sue saw a big box. She said, "Look, Bob, a big box!" They went to see the box. Inside the box, there was a note. The note said, "Send this box to the moon." Sue and Bob looked at each other. They did not know how to send the box to the moon. But then, a big, friendly giant came and said, "I can help you send the box to the moon!" The giant picked up the box and threw it high up into the sky. Sue and Bob watched as the box went all the way to the moon. They were very surprised and happy.

One day, little Johnny went to the park with his Daddy. He was excited to go there and play. He saw a brick wall and decided to go and look at it. As he got closer, he noticed that it was full of terrible cracks and he was really scared. 
"Daddy, this wall looks terrible," he said to his Daddy. 
"Don't worry, Johnny. It's just an old brick wall; nothing to be afraid of," his Daddy replied.
Little Johnny was still scared, but he was also a brave boy and decided to go closer. He started touching the bricks, and the more he touched, the more the wall became stable again. 
"See, Johnny," said his Daddy, "you can make things better with your own hands. You are very brave."
Little Johnny smiled and said, "Thanks Daddy. I am so proud of myself for being brave."

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy cat named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play with her ball of yarn. One day, Fluffy was playing when she saw a shiny needle on the floor. She wanted to play with it, but she knew it was not safe.
So Fluffy went to her friend, a wise owl named Hootie. Fluffy asked, "Hootie, I found a needle on the floor. Can I play with it?" Hootie said, "No, Fluffy. Needles are not for playing. They can hurt you."
Fluffy listened to Hootie and did not play with the needle. Instead, she gave it to her mom. Her mom was very happy to receive the needle. She said, "Thank you, Fluffy. This will help me fix your favorite blanket."
Fluffy learned that it is important to be careful with things that can hurt you. She also learned that listening to her wise friend Hootie was a good thing to do. And from that day on, Fluffy stayed away from needles and played safely with her ball of yarn.

One day, a lively cat and a dog were playing in the park. The cat saw a rope hanging from a tree. The cat said, "I want to play with the rope!" The dog said, "Me too! Let's see who can reach it first."
The cat and the dog ran to the tree. They tried to jump and shoot their paws up to grab the rope, but it was too high. They were sad and did not know what to do. Then, a little bird flew by and saw them. The bird said, "Why are you sad?" The cat and the dog told the bird about the rope.
The bird had an idea. It flew up and grabbed the rope with its beak. The bird pulled the rope down so the cat and the dog could reach it. They were so happy and started to play with the rope. But then, something unexpected happened. The rope was not just a rope, it was a snake!
The snake said, "Please do not play with me. I am not a rope." The cat, the dog, and the bird were all very surprised. They said sorry to the snake and let it go. They learned that not everything is what it seems, and they became friends with the snake. They all played together in the park and had a fun day.

One day, a little girl named Sue went to the store with her mom. Sue saw a pretty shirt with flowers on it. She wanted the shirt very much. Sue asked her mom, "Can I have the pretty shirt, please?"
Her mom said, "We don't have enough money to pay for the shirt today. We can come back another day."
Sue was sad. She wanted the pretty shirt now. She thought of a plan. Sue asked her mom, "Can I help you with work to get money for the shirt?"
Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, you can help me at home. We can save money together for the pretty shirt."
Sue was happy. She knew she would have to work hard, but she would get the pretty shirt soon. Sue and her mom went home to start working and saving money together.

Once upon a time, there was a little player named Tim. Tim was an unusual boy because he could run very fast. He loved to play with his friends in the park. They would run and jump all day long.
One day, Tim's mom told him not to forget to come home early. She needed him to help with dinner. Tim said "yes" and went to the park to play.
Tim was having so much fun that he forgot what his mom said. He played all day and all night. When he finally went home, it was very late. His mom was sad and dinner was cold. Tim felt bad for forgetting, but it was too late. The day had a bad ending.

One day, a little bunny named Tim went for a hop in the hot sun. Tim hopped and hopped until he saw a big tree. Under the tree, there was a gift. Tim was very happy.
Tim said, "Wow! A gift for me!" He opened the gift and found a big hat. Tim put the hat on his head. Now, he was not hot anymore.
Tim hopped back home with his new hat. He showed his mom and dad the gift. They all laughed and hopped around the yard. Tim loved his new hat and had a fun day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big flood. The water went up and up. People were scared. They needed help.
One day, a fair girl named Lily found a big ball. She had an idea. She told her friends, "Let's roll the ball to stop the flood." Her friends said, "Yes, let's do it!"
They all pushed the ball and it started to roll. The ball rolled and rolled. It stopped the water. The flood was gone. Everyone was happy and said, "Thank you, Lily!" And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mary. She liked to go and play in the garden. One day she noticed a small spider in the garden. While looking at it, Mary heard a loud noise coming from a nearby tree.
"Oh no!" said Mary, "I think the tree needs to be repaired!"
Mary looked around and saw a toolbox. She grabbed a hammer and some nails and tried to repair the tree. 
After a few tries, she was able to get the tree back into place. She was so proud of her repair job! 
The little spider was very happy too. He thanked Mary for her help and offered her a sour apple. Mary happily accepted and thanked the spider for the sour apple. 
The two said their goodbyes and Mary ran off to enjoy her sour treat.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toys. One day, Tim saw a big tube in the park. He was very happy and decided to play with it.
Tim rolled the tube and said, "Tube, let's play together!" The tube rolled down the hill with Tim. They played all day long. Tim had so much fun with the tube.
But then, a spoiled girl named Sally saw Tim playing with the tube. She wanted the tube for herself. Sally said, "Tim, give me the tube! I want it!" Tim didn't want to share, but he didn't want to be mean, so he let Sally play with the tube too.
Sally played with the tube, but she didn't take care of it. The tube got dirty and broken. Tim was sad to see the tube like that. He said, "Sally, you spoiled the tube. We can't play with it anymore."
From that day on, Tim and Sally learned to share and take care of their toys. They played together and became good friends. And they never spoiled their toys again.

Once upon a time, there was a big gym. In the gym, there lived an elderly cat named Mr. Whiskers. Mr. Whiskers liked to watch the kids play and run around. He loved to sit and nap in the sun.
One day, a little boy named Timmy came to the gym. He saw Mr. Whiskers and said, "Hi, Mr. Whiskers! Do you want to play with me?" Mr. Whiskers opened one eye, looked at Timmy, and said, "Meow."
Timmy started to play with a toy car. He made the car zoom back and forth on the floor. Mr. Whiskers watched Timmy play and thought it looked fun. So, he decided to join in. Mr. Whiskers jumped down and started to chase the car as it zoomed by.
Together, Timmy and Mr. Whiskers played and had lots of fun in the gym. They zoomed around all day until they both got tired. At the end of the day, they sat down and took a nap in the warm sun. They were very happy friends.

Once upon a time, there was a gentle dog named Max. Max lived in a big house with his best friend, a little boy named Tim. They loved to play together all day. One day, they saw a big truck outside. The truck had many toys in it. Max and Tim wanted to trade their old toys for new ones.
They went to the truck and asked the man if they could trade. The man said, "Yes, but you must do something for me first." Max and Tim had to find a red ball that the man lost. They looked and looked, but could not find the red ball. They did not know how to manage this task.
Then, something unexpected happened. A little bird flew by and saw Max and Tim looking sad. The bird said, "I can help you find the red ball!" The bird knew where the ball was and took them to it. Max and Tim were so happy! They gave the ball to the man, and he let them trade their old toys for new ones. Max and Tim learned that with a little help, they could manage anything.

Once upon a time, there was a little spider named Sally. Sally liked to add things to her web. One day, Sally saw a helpless bug stuck on a leaf. The bug was sad and wanted to go home.
Sally said, "Don't worry, I'll help you!" She added a new part to her web and used it to lift the bug from the leaf. The bug was happy to be free and thanked Sally.
Together, Sally and the bug became friends. They played and talked all day long. Sally learned that sometimes, even a little spider can help someone who feels helpless.

Once upon a time, there was a little crab who lived on the beach. He was a happy and content little creature, but he was always a bit bitter.
One day, the crab decided to take a journey. He went up the beach as far as he could, until he reached the edge of the jungle. It was a scary place, and the crab felt a bit afraid.
The crab kept walking until he saw a bright light in the distance. He was drawn to it, and he inched closer and closer until he reached the source. The bright light was coming from a bowl of delicious oranges.
The crab was so excited and forgot about his fear. He reached out to take one of the oranges, when suddenly out of the shadows a big crab appeared. He was so much bigger than the little crab, and he was also very bitter.
The little crab was frightened and dropped the orange. He scurried away, back down the beach, as fast as he could.
The little crab learned a valuable lesson that day: never be too trusting of something that seems too good to be true!

One day, a little cat named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big hedge in his way. Tim tried to jump over the hedge but he was too small. He felt dizzy when he looked up at the tall hedge. Tim decided to find another way to get past the hedge.
As Tim walked around the hedge, he met a big dog named Max. Max wanted to play with Tim, but Tim did not want to play. They disagreed on what to do. Max had an idea. He said, "I can help you get over the hedge if you play with me." Tim thought about it and agreed.
Max and Tim ran and played together. After a while, they went back to the hedge. Max jumped over the hedge and then helped Tim get over it too. But when they got to the other side, they found out they were back where they started! The hedge was a big circle! They laughed and continued to play together, now as friends.

Lily and Max were going to camp with Mom and Dad. They were very happy. They packed their bags, their toys, and their snacks. They got in the car and drove for a long time.
When they got to the camp, they saw a big orange tent. Mom and Dad said that was their home for the night. Lily and Max ran to the tent and went inside. It was cozy and warm. They saw two sleeping bags, one for each of them.
"Let's play!" Lily said. She grabbed her doll and pretended to feed her. Max grabbed his truck and drove it around the tent. He saw a small hole in the tent wall. He was curious. He poked his finger through the hole.
"Ow!" he cried. He pulled his finger back. It was red and sore. He had poked a bee. The bee was angry. It flew out of the hole and buzzed around the tent.
"Max, what did you do?" Lily asked. She saw the bee and was scared. She hugged her doll and hid under her sleeping bag.
"Sorry, Lily. I poked a bee. It hurt me. It is mad at me." Max said. He tried to swat the bee with his truck. But the bee was fast and dodged him.
Mom and Dad heard the noise and came to the tent. They saw the bee and the hole. They understood what happened. They helped Max and Lily. They put some ice on Max's finger. They shooed the bee away. They patched the hole with some tape.
"It's okay, Max. It was an accident. You learned your lesson. Don't poke things you don't know." Mom said.
"It's okay, Lily. The bee is gone. You are safe. You can come out now." Dad said.
Lily and Max came out of their sleeping bags. They hugged Mom and Dad. They said they were sorry and they were brave. Mom and Dad said they were proud of them and they loved them.
They all cuddled in the tent and ate some snacks. They told stories and sang songs. They had fun. They forgot about the bee. They were happy. They were a family. They were at camp.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy cat named Tom. Tom liked to sleep all day and play all night. He had a friend named Jerry, who was a little mouse. Jerry liked to run and play, but Tom was too lazy to play with him.
One day, Jerry found a shiny thing on the floor. It was a license! Jerry knew that the license was important, so he wanted to save it. He tried to give it to Tom, but Tom was too lazy to get up and take it.
Jerry decided to save the license himself. He carried it in his little mouth and ran to find the person who lost it. When he found the person, they were so happy to see their lost license. They thanked Jerry and gave him a big piece of cheese to share with Tom. In the end, Tom learned that it was good to help and not be lazy.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly cat named Sam. Sam liked to play with his ball and run in the sun. One day, Sam saw a big fire in the woods. He was scared but wanted to help.
Sam found a long stick and used it to split the fire into two smaller fires. He thought this would make the fire go away. But, something unexpected happened. The two small fires joined together and became a big friendly fire dog named Sparky.
Sparky and Sam became good friends. They played with the ball and ran in the sun together. And from that day on, the woods were safe because Sparky could control the fire.

Once upon a time, in a big house, there lived a wealthy man. He had many animals. He liked to pull their tails. The animals did not like this. They were sad.
One day, the man saw a new animal. It was big and had a long tail. The man was happy. He wanted to pull its tail too. The animal looked at him with big eyes. It knew what he wanted to do.
The man pulled the animal's tail. The animal got very mad. It chased the man around the house. The man was scared. He fell and hit his head. The animals were now free from the man who pulled their tails.

Once upon a time, there was a big, loud elephant named Ella. Ella loved to play with her friends in the forest. They would run, jump, and laugh all day long.
One day, Ella's friends wanted to send her on a special adventure. They made a big map for Ella to follow. The map showed Ella where to go to find a big surprise.
Ella followed the map and found a big, loud party just for her! All her friends were there to play and have fun. They danced, ate yummy food, and played games. Ella was so happy her friends sent her on this great adventure. They all had a wonderful time together.

Once upon a time, in a big farm, there was a little horse named Tom. Tom was a dull horse, and he did not like to play with the other horses. One day, Tom saw a big pile of hay near the barn. He thought, "I want to eat all this hay by myself."
Tom decided to make a plan. He told the other horses, "There is a big, fun game on the other side of the farm. You should all go play there." The other horses were happy and ran away to play the game. Tom went to the big pile of hay and started to eat it all by himself.
As Tom ate the hay, he felt sad and lonely. He realized that being greedy and not sharing with his friends was not fun. He decided to call the other horses back and share the hay with them. They all ate the hay together, and Tom learned that sharing with friends is much better than being greedy.

One day, a little dog named Max wanted to go to the park. Max was a good dog, but he did not have a license. He knew that it was bad to go without a license. Max was sad.
Max's friend, a cat named Luna, saw Max looking sad. Luna asked, "Why are you sad, Max?" Max told Luna, "I want to go to the park, but I don't have a license. It's bad to go without one."
Luna had an idea. She said, "Let's ask our friend, the wise owl, for help!" Max and Luna went to see the wise owl. The owl listened to Max and said, "I will give you a license, Max. Now you can go to the park and have fun!" Max was very happy. He thanked the owl and went to the park with Luna. They played all day and had lots of fun.

One day, a boy named Tim was with his mom. They went to the food place. Tim's mom said, "Tim, what do you want?" Tim said, "I want a big ice cream!" So, his mom ordered a big ice cream for him.
While they waited, Tim saw a man. The man could not hear. He was deaf. The man was trying to order food too. But he had a hard time. Tim thought, "I want to help the man."
Tim walked up to the man. He used his thumb to point at the food he wanted. The man smiled and said, "Thank you!" The man ordered his food with Tim's help. Then, Tim and the man ate their food together. They both were happy.

Joe was a happy boy. He loved playing with his cart. Every day he would bring it outside and pretend to shop. He would push it around outside, full of imaginary treats!
It was a gloomy day, and he told his mommy, "Mommy, I don't want to go outside today". But his mommy just said, "Joe be brave, you can go outside and play". 
So Joe reluctantly took his cart outside. He was sad and pushed his cart slowly, repeating over and over, "I don't want to go outside". Suddenly, he heard something that made him stop.
It was a puppy, barking and playing in the grass. Joe's eyes lit up, and he ran over to the puppy. They played together for an hour. At the end of it, Joe looked up at his mommy with a big smile and said, "Mommy, can we please keep this puppy?".
His mommy smiled and said, "Yes Joe, we can keep the puppy". Joe was so excited! His gloomy day had brightened in an instant thanks to his new puppy.

One day, a pink cat went for a jog. The cat loved to jog in the park. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing. The cat saw a little boy with a pencil. The boy was sad.
"Hi, little boy," said the pink cat. "Why are you sad?"
"I lost my pencil," said the boy. "I need it for school."
The pink cat wanted to help the boy. The cat remembered seeing a pencil on the ground while jogging. The cat told the boy, "I think I saw your pencil. Let's go find it together."
They jogged back to where the cat saw the pencil. The boy was very happy when they found it. He said, "Thank you, pink cat!" They both smiled and jogged together in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful bookshelf in a big room. It was full of colorful books. A little girl named Lily loved to read these books every day. She would sit near the bookshelf and read her favorite stories about animals, fairies, and heroes.
One day, Lily's mom came home with a new book. She said, "Lily, I have a special book for you to add to your bookshelf!" Lily was very happy and excited. She took the book from her mom and put it on the bookshelf.
That night, when everyone was sleeping, something unexpected happened. The new book started to glow and shake on the bookshelf. Suddenly, all the characters from the books came to life! There were animals, fairies, and heroes all around the room. They were all very friendly and wanted to play with Lily. When Lily woke up the next morning, she found herself surrounded by her favorite characters. She was so happy and could not believe her eyes. From that day on, Lily's life was full of magic and fun with her new friends from the beautiful bookshelf.

Ben and Mia were playing in the kitchen. They liked to pretend they were chefs and make yummy food. Ben found a big potato and cut it into pieces. Mia found some cheese and butter. They put them in a bowl and mixed them.
"Let's make mashed potato!" Ben said. "It will be so good!"
"Yes, let's do it!" Mia agreed. "But we need to cook the potato first. Where is the pot?"
They looked around and saw a big pot on the stove. It was shiny and red. Ben reached for it, but Mia stopped him.
"Wait, Ben! That pot is hot! Mommy was using it to make soup. You will burn yourself!"
Ben paused and looked at Mia. He was angry. He wanted to make mashed potato now. He did not want to wait.
"But I'm hungry!" Ben whined. "And I want to use that pot! It is the best pot!"
"No, Ben! You can't use that pot! It is not ready! It is dangerous!" Mia shouted. "We have to find another pot!"
They started to argue and tug at the pot. They did not see Mommy coming into the kitchen. She heard them and saw them fighting over the hot pot. She was scared and angry too.
"Stop, stop, stop!" Mommy yelled. "What are you doing? You could have hurt yourselves! That pot is very hot! You are not allowed to touch it!"
She took the pot away from them and put it on a high shelf. She looked at Ben and Mia. They were quiet and sad. They knew they had done something bad.
"I'm sorry, Mommy," Ben said.
"I'm sorry, too," Mia said.
Mommy sighed and hugged them. She was not mad anymore. She was glad they were safe.
"I know you were trying to have fun, but you have to be careful in the kitchen. There are some things that are not for playing. You have to ask me before you use anything. Do you understand?"
"Yes, Mommy," Ben and Mia said.
Mommy smiled and kissed them. She had an idea.
"How about we make mashed potato together? I have another pot that is ready. And I have some more cheese and butter. And maybe some bacon too. Would you like that?"
Ben and Mia nodded and smiled. They liked that idea. They liked mashed potato. And they liked Mommy.
"Okay, then. Let's go and wash our hands. And then we can be chefs together. But no more fighting, okay?"
"Okay, Mommy," Ben and Mia said.
They followed Mommy to the sink. They washed their hands and dried them. They were ready to make mashed potato. And they were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little fish named Fin. Fin lived in the big sea with his family. He was a bossy fish. He liked to tell his friends what to do.
One day, a new fish named Gilly came to the sea. Gilly was shy and did not have any friends. Fin saw Gilly and wanted to be her friend. But he was still bossy. He told Gilly to swim fast, but Gilly did not like that.
Gilly was sad and cried. Fin saw Gilly cry and felt bad. He wanted to make her feel better. Fin swam to Gilly and gave her a big kiss. Gilly smiled and they played together.
Fin learned that being bossy is not nice. He learned to be kind and play with his friends. And they all lived happily in the big sea.

Once upon a time, there was a little black cat named Kitty. Kitty liked to play and observe things around her. One day, she saw a pretty lamp in the living room.
Kitty walked up to the lamp and looked at it. The lamp was shiny and bright. She wanted to touch the lamp, but she was a little scared. She sat and watched the lamp for a while.
Kitty saw her friend, a little boy named Tim, walk into the room. Tim saw Kitty and the lamp, and he smiled. He turned on the lamp, and the room became bright and happy. Kitty felt brave and touched the lamp too. They both laughed and played near the lamp all day long.

Once upon a time, in a big cool pond, there lived a goose named Gigi. Gigi had many friends who lived in the pond too. They liked to swim and play all day long.
One day, Gigi saw a big party near the pond. People were eating and having fun. Gigi wanted to join the party too. So, she walked to the party and asked a man, "Can I join the party? I can serve food to people."
The man smiled and said, "Yes, Gigi, you can help us serve food." Gigi was very happy. She took a plate and served food to the people at the party. Everyone loved Gigi and said, "Thank you, Gigi!"
After the party, Gigi went back to the cool pond. She told her friends about the fun day she had. All the animals in the pond were happy for Gigi and they all played together until the sun went down.

Once upon a time, there was a pale blue car. This car loved to make circles in the park. One day, the car met a little red bike.
"Hi, bike!" said the car. "Do you want to make circles with me?"
"Yes!" said the bike. "That sounds fun!"
So, the car and the bike made circles in the park together. They were very happy. But then, something unexpected happened. The car and the bike went too fast and bumped into each other. 
"Oh no!" said the car. "We need to fix this!"
The car had an idea. It could reverse and go back in time before they bumped. So, the car reversed very fast, and they went back in time. The car and the bike did not bump into each other again. They learned to be more careful and continued to make circles in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a kind little girl named Poppy. She loved to play in the park and mind her own business. One day, while playing with her ball, she saw a sad dog.
Poppy wanted to help the dog, so she went close to it. The dog looked up at her with big, sad eyes. Poppy tried to be kind and give the dog a hug. She thought it would make the dog happy.
But the dog got scared and bit Poppy's hand. She cried and ran back to her mom. Her mom took her to the doctor, and Poppy learned to be careful with dogs she didn't know. The end.

Once upon a time there lived a three-year-old girl named Mona. Mona lived in a beautiful house with her mom and dad. One day, Mona was playing with her computer in the living room when she accidently spilt her dad's coffee. The coffee spilled on the computer and the coffee was smelly. Mona was very scared and started crying. 
Mona's mom came and asked her what had happened. Mona told her mom the whole story. Mona's mom knew that her husband would be angry but she said,"Mona, you have to learn to forgive yourself. It was an accident and we can replace the computer".
Mona's father came home a few minutes later and saw the smelly mess. He was very angry. Mona was even more scared. But Mona's mom talked to her husband and asked him to forgive Mona. Mona's father looked at her, smiled and said, "Yes, it was just an accident".
From that day, Mona learned that it's important to forgive and be kind to others. She also learned that it's important to learn from your mistakes and not be so hard on yourself.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. It was a cold day, so Tim wore a big warm coat. At the park, Tim saw a big motor truck. The truck was there to weigh things. Tim was very excited to see the truck.
"Mom, can I go see the truck?" Tim asked. His mom said, "Yes, but be careful and stay close." Tim walked up to the truck and saw a man. The man was putting things on the truck to weigh them. Tim watched as the man weighed a big box.
Suddenly, the truck started to move! The man had not put the motor off. Tim was very scared. He yelled, "Mom, help! The truck is moving!" Tim's mom heard him and ran to the truck. She quickly turned off the motor and stopped the truck.
Tim's mom hugged him and said, "I'm so glad you're safe!" Tim was happy that his mom saved him. They went home and had some hot cocoa to warm up from the cold day. From that day on, Tim always made sure to be extra careful around big trucks.

Lily and Tom were playing in the backyard. They saw a big barrel near the fence. They wanted to see what was inside.
"Let's go look, Lily!" Tom said.
"OK, Tom!" Lily said.
They ran to the barrel and peeked inside. It was empty, but it had a small hole at the bottom.
"I have an idea, Lily!" Tom said. "Let's take turns to fit in the barrel and roll around!"
"That sounds fun, Tom!" Lily said.
Tom went first. He climbed into the barrel and curled up. Lily pushed the barrel and it started to roll.
"Yay, Tom!" Lily cheered.
Tom laughed and shouted. He liked the feeling of rolling in the barrel.
But then, the barrel rolled too fast. It hit a rock and bounced. The hole at the bottom got bigger. Tom fell out of the barrel and landed on the ground.
"Ow, ow, ow!" Tom cried.
Lily ran to Tom and saw that he was hurt. He had a big cut on his knee and a bump on his head.
"Oh no, Tom!" Lily said. "Are you OK?"
Tom was not OK. He was in pain and he was angry. He blamed Lily for pushing the barrel too hard.
"It's your fault, Lily!" Tom yelled. "You made me fall out of the barrel! You are a bad sister!"
Lily was shocked and sad. She did not mean to hurt Tom. She wanted to play with him and have fun.
"I'm sorry, Tom!" Lily said. "I didn't know the barrel had a hole! I didn't want to make you fall!"
But Tom did not listen. He was too grumpy. He pushed Lily away and ran to the house. He told their mom that Lily hurt him on purpose.
Mom came out and saw Tom's wound. She was worried and angry. She scolded Lily for being careless and mean.
"Lily, how could you do this to your brother?" Mom said. "You should know better than to play with a barrel! You could have hurt yourself too! Go to your room and think about what you did!"
Lily felt terrible. She did not do anything wrong, but no one believed her. She went to her room and cried. She wished Tom would forgive her and Mom would understand her. She wished the barrel would go away. She wished the day would end.

Once upon a time, there was an elephant who was very clumsy. He lived in a magical forest and liked to take walks in the woods.
And one day, while walking through the forest, he stumbled upon a corner of the forest where the trees became very tall and the air became misty.
He then met a friendly fairy who said, "Hello! Do you want to play?"
The elephant was very excited and replied, "Yes!"
The fairy waved her wand and said, "By the waving of my wand, you shall become something new!"
Instantly, the elephant began to change. His body grew larger and longer and his skin turned gray.
He was amazed at the change and asked the fairy, "What's happening to me?"
The fairy answered, "You are being transformed. You are no longer a clumsy elephant but a graceful and powerful elephant. You will be very strong and wise."
The elephant was so happy that he thanked the fairy and promised to always use his strength and wisdom for good.
The fairy smiled and waved goodbye as the elephant walked back into the woods, forever changed.

Sam and Ben were brothers who liked to build things with lumber. They had many pieces of lumber in their backyard. One day, they decided to make an enormous fort with lumber. They worked hard and added more and more lumber to their fort.
"Look, Ben, our fort is almost done!" Sam said. "It is so big and strong!"
"Yes, Sam, it is!" Ben said. "But we need one more piece of lumber to finish the roof. Can you get it for me?"
Sam nodded and ran to get the last piece of lumber. But when he came back, he saw a problem. A big dog was in their fort. The dog was barking and growling. It did not want to leave.
"Hey, dog, get out of our fort!" Sam shouted. "This is our fort, not yours!"
But the dog did not listen. It bared its teeth and snarled. Sam was scared. He dropped the piece of lumber and ran to Ben.
"Ben, Ben, there is a dog in our fort!" Sam said. "It is a mean dog! It won't let us in!"
Ben looked at the dog and frowned. He did not like the dog. He wanted to finish their fort. He picked up a stick and waved it at the dog.
"Go away, dog, go away!" Ben said. "This is our fort, not yours!"
But the dog did not go away. It barked louder and louder. It jumped at Ben and tried to bite him. Ben dodged the dog and hit it with the stick. The dog yelped and ran away.
"Ha, ha, we did it, Sam, we did it!" Ben said. "We scared the dog away!"
"Yes, we did, Ben, we did!" Sam said. "Now we can finish our fort!"
They hugged each other and smiled. They added the last piece of lumber to their fort. They climbed inside and looked around. Their fort was enormous and cozy. They were happy and proud.
The end.

One day, a reindeer named Remy was in the forest. Remy was a naughty reindeer. He liked to play tricks on his friends. He would hide their things and make them look for them.
One day, Remy saw his friend, a squirrel named Sam. Sam was sad because he could not find any nuts to eat. Remy had an idea. He said, "I will provide nuts for you, Sam." Sam was happy and thanked Remy.
But Remy played a trick on Sam. He gave Sam rocks instead of nuts. Sam tried to eat the rocks, but they were too hard. Sam was sad again. Just then, a wise old owl saw what happened. The owl told Remy that being naughty was not nice. Remy felt bad and decided to change. He found real nuts for Sam and they became good friends. From that day on, Remy was a helpful reindeer.

Once upon a time, there was a funny little alien. He lived on a far away planet. The planet was very pretty and had lots of colors.
One day, the alien met another alien. The new alien said, "I can lie and tell funny stories." The funny little alien asked, "Can you tell me a funny story?" The new alien told a story that made them both laugh.
But then, the new alien lied about something important. The funny little alien felt sad. He learned that lying is not good. From that day on, they both only told true stories and had lots of fun. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a big pink house, there lived a little girl named Lily. She loved pink stuff. Her room was full of pink toys, pink clothes, and even a big pink bed. Lily was very happy in her pink world.
One day, Lily found a little lost cat outside her house. The cat was not pink, but Lily loved it anyway. She brought the cat inside and gave it some food. She wanted to keep the cat and insisted that it stay with her.
Lily's mom said, "We have to find the cat's home." Lily was sad but she knew her mom was right. They asked around and found the cat's owner. Lily said goodbye to her new friend and gave it one last hug. The owner thanked Lily and gave her a pink ribbon for her hair. Lily smiled, knowing she did the right thing.

Once upon a time, in a big green forest, there lived a small squirrel named Sam. Sam loved to find nuts and hide them in his little home. One day, Sam found a big, white nut. He had never seen a nut like this before. Sam was so happy!
Sam wanted to keep his white nut safe, so he decided to cover it with soft leaves. He worked hard to find the best leaves and gently placed them on top of his special nut. Sam felt proud of his work and knew the nut was safe now.
Soon, Sam met another squirrel named Lucy. Lucy was sad because she had no nuts to eat. Sam thought about his white nut and decided to share it with Lucy. They uncovered the nut and ate it together. It was the yummiest nut they ever had! From that day on, Sam and Lucy became best friends. They learned that sharing with others makes everyone happy. And that was the most important lesson of all.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She had a big box of jewelry. She loved her jewelry very much. She had a friend named Tom. Tom was a reliable friend. He always played with her and helped her.
One day, Sue and Tom played a game. They wanted to scare each other. They hid behind doors and trees. They jumped out and said "Boo!" They laughed and had fun. But Sue had an idea. She wanted to scare Tom more.
Sue took her jewelry and hid it. Then she told Tom that her jewelry was gone. Tom was scared. He thought he lost it. Sue laughed and said, "I scared you!" Tom was sad. He didn't like the joke.
Sue saw Tom was sad. She said sorry and gave him a hug. They found the jewelry and put it back in the box. Sue learned that it's not nice to scare friends with bad jokes. From that day, Sue and Tom played nice games and stayed good friends.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was only three and very brave. She loved to dance.
One day the little girl decided to explore the big factory near her house. When she got inside, she was amazed. The factory was brilliant. Everything sparkled and shone!
The little girl began to dance through the factory. She pirouetted around the machines, spinning and leaping. She was so happy!
Suddenly, the factory started to shake. The machines began to roar. The lights blinked out. The floor began to rumble.
The little girl ran as fast as she could, but the factory was too dangerous. A huge beam of metal crashed to the floor, trapping her underneath. She was never seen again.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play in the park. He loved to splash in the big puddles after it rained. He put on his boots and ran outside. Tim was very happy.
As he splashed in the puddles, he saw a big box near a tree. Tim was worried. What could be inside? He opened the box and found a TV! Tim thought it was strange to find a TV in the park, but he was excited.
Tim took the TV home and showed his mom. She was surprised too. They turned on the TV and saw a message on the screen. It said, "You won a prize!" Tim and his mom laughed. They never thought they would find a prize in a puddle at the park.

One day, a pretty cat named Lucy went for a march in the park. She saw a long cord on the ground. Lucy thought it was a fun toy to play with. She jumped on the cord and started to play.
"Hi, Lucy!" said a big dog named Max. "What are you playing with?" Lucy told Max about the cord and they both played with it. They pulled and ran with the cord, having lots of fun.
But then something unexpected happened! The cord moved on its own! Lucy and Max were very surprised. It was not a cord, but a long snake! The snake said, "Please don't play with me. I was sleeping." Lucy and Max said sorry to the snake and went back to their march in the park, looking for other fun things to play with.

One day, a girl named Sue and her friend Tom wanted to make a picnic. They took bread, cheese, and apples. They went to the park to have a unique picnic under a big tree.
As they were eating, a big bird came and said, "Can I join your picnic?" Sue and Tom looked at each other and said, "Yes, you can join us!" This was the first time they talked to a bird. They gave the bird some bread and cheese.
Then, something unexpected happened. The bird became a little girl! She said, "Thank you for sharing your picnic with me. I am a magic bird, and now I can be your friend." Sue, Tom, and their new friend had lots of fun at their unique picnic.

One day, there was a small fish. He wanted to be strong, so he requested a tank for his birthday. He thought maybe the tank would make him strong.
His parents agreed and got him the tank! They filled it up with water and put in some decorations.
The little fish was so excited! He smiled and said thank you to his parents.
He hopped into the tank to check it out and swam around. Soon, he felt a lot stronger and enjoyed swimming around in his new tank.
He went back to his parents and requested one more thing. He asked for a friend, someone to swim around with him.
His parents agreed and got him some other little fishies. The small fish was so excited and swam quickly around the tank. He was happy to have some friends and the tank made him feel very strong.
