Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big dance party. Everyone was excited to dance to jazz music. They all wore pretty clothes to look attractive. 
A little girl named Lily wanted to go to the dance party too. She put on her favorite dress and asked her mom, "Can I go to the dance party, please?" Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, you can go, but be back before bedtime."
At the dance party, Lily saw her friends and they all danced together. They laughed and clapped their hands to the fun jazz music. Everyone thought Lily and her friends looked very attractive in their pretty clothes. They danced and danced until it was time to go home.
When Lily got home, she told her mom all about the dance party. She said, "I had so much fun dancing to jazz music with my friends!" Her mom hugged her and said, "I'm glad you had a great time, sweetie. Now, it's time for bed." Lily went to sleep with a big smile on her face, dreaming of the fun dance party.

Once upon a time, there was a big pond. In the pond, there were many fish. They liked to play and have fun. One day, a new fish came to the pond. The new fish did not behave. He was not nice to the other fish. He took their toys and made them sad.
The other fish did not like the new fish. They wanted him to be nice and behave. They told the new fish, "Please be nice and play with us. We will be your friends if you behave." The new fish thought about it and decided to try.
The new fish started to behave. He shared his expensive toys with the other fish. They all played together and had fun. The fish in the pond were happy again. They became good friends with the new fish, and they all lived happily in the pond.

One day, a poor cat named Tom was walking down the street. He was very hungry. He saw a shop with a big sign that said "Muffin Shop". Tom's eyes got big and his tummy rumbled.
Tom went into the shop and asked the lady, "Can I have a muffin, please?" The lady said, "Yes, but you have to print your name on this paper first." Tom was sad because he did not know how to print his name. He went outside and sat on the ground, feeling very sad.
Just then, a kind dog named Max came by. He saw Tom and asked, "Why are you sad?" Tom told him about the muffin and the paper. Max said, "I can help you print your name!" They went back into the shop and Max helped Tom print his name. The lady gave Tom a big, yummy muffin. Tom and Max became best friends and shared the muffin together.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty bird who lived near a river. The bird had many friends, but she liked to play with a crocodile the most. They would swim, run, and have lots of fun together.
One day, the bird and the crocodile had to separate because the bird's family was moving to a new home far away. They were very sad, but they knew they had to say goodbye. The bird gave the crocodile a big hug and flew away with her family.
After many days, the bird and her family found a new home. They made new friends, but the bird always remembered the fun times she had with the crocodile. She knew that even though they were separated, they would always be friends in their hearts.

Once upon a time, there were two little boys in the town. One of them was Jack, who was three years old. He went to the dock early every morning with his father. Today, dad had given Jack an important task.
"Jack," said dad, "It's your turn to load up the boat with the fish."
Jack was excited. He grabbed the net and started to load the boat. With each fish, his score improved. 
But suddenly, he heard a loud noise. 
"Oh no," said Jack.
The noise was coming from inside the boat - it was his dad! He had fallen on the fish and was now miserable.
Jack ran over to help. He tried to help his dad but it was too late. Dad had broken his arm and needed to go to the hospital immediately. 
Jack was sad. He had to go home without his dad. His score was low, and his dad was in pain. It was a miserable ending.

Once upon a time, there was a big race. All the cars were fast and loud. One car was calm and slow. The calm car's name was Tim.
The race began. All the cars went fast, but Tim went slow. The other cars laughed at him. Tim felt sad, but he did not give up. He saw a trophy at the end of the race. He wanted the trophy.
Suddenly, one fast car got stuck. It could not move. Tim came to help. He put his car in reverse and pushed the stuck car. The car was free. They raced together to the end. Tim won the race and got the trophy. All the cars were happy for Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to ride his bike. His bike had pedals that he would push with his feet to make the bike go fast. One day, Tim felt very hungry. So, he decided to ride his bike to the store to buy a yummy snack.
As Tim was riding his bike, he saw his friend, Sue. Sue was trying to raise her kite high in the sky. Tim stopped his bike and said, "Hi, Sue! I am hungry. I am going to the store to buy a snack. Do you want to come with me?"
Sue smiled and said, "Yes, Tim! I am hungry too. Let's go to the store together." So, Tim and Sue rode their bikes to the store. They used their pedals to go fast. When they got to the store, they bought some yummy snacks to eat.
After they ate their snacks, they felt much better. Tim and Sue went back outside to play. Tim helped Sue raise her kite high in the sky. They laughed and played together all day long. And that was the end of their fun day.

Sara was a little girl who liked to play with her toys. She had a charming clock that she got from her grandma. The clock had a face with two hands and numbers. It also had a bird that came out and said "cuckoo" every hour.
One day, Sara decided to name her clock. She thought of different names, but none of them seemed right. She asked her mom for help.
"Mom, what should I name my clock?" she said.
Her mom smiled and said, "Why don't you name it after someone you love?"
Sara thought for a moment. She loved her mom, her dad, her grandma, and her dog. But she wanted a special name for her clock.
"I know!" she said. "I will name it Cuckoo!"
Her mom laughed and said, "That's a perfect name for your clock. Cuckoo is a funny and sweet name."
Sara hugged her mom and ran to her room. She put her clock on her nightstand and said, "Hello, Cuckoo. You are my new friend. I will take good care of you."
Cuckoo nodded and said, "Cuckoo, cuckoo, cuckoo."
Sara and Cuckoo were very happy. They played, read, and sang together. Every hour, Cuckoo would say "cuckoo" and Sara would say "cuckoo" back. They had a lot of fun. Sara loved her charming clock named Cuckoo.

Once upon a time, in a cute little house, there lived a girl named Sally. Sally loved to play outside with her friends. One sunny day, Sally and her friend Timmy decided to have a race. They each had a bag.
Sally said, "Let's balance the bag on our heads and see who can run to the tree and back without dropping it!" Timmy agreed and they both put the bags on their heads.
They started to run, but the bags kept falling off their heads. They tried again and again, but they could not keep the bags balanced. Sally and Timmy laughed and had a lot of fun trying.
Finally, Sally found a way to balance her bag. She walked slowly and carefully, and she made it to the tree and back without dropping her bag. Timmy clapped and said, "Good job, Sally! You won the race!"
Sally and Timmy played with their bags all day, trying new ways to balance them. They had a great time and were happy to be friends.

Once upon a time, there was a kind girl named Lily. She had a big heart and always helped others. Lily was very compassionate.
One day, Lily went for a walk and found a little infant. The baby was all alone and crying. Lily wanted to help, so she took the baby home. She gave the baby food, a bath, and a warm bed to sleep in.
Then, something unexpected happened! The baby's mom came to Lily's house. She was so happy to see her baby safe and sound. The mom was so thankful that she gave Lily a big hug. Lily received a new friend that day, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a fragile spring. It was small and so very light.
One day, a gentle breeze came and turned the spring round and round. It spun faster and faster until it flew up into the air and away!
The fragile spring flew for miles over the countryside and found a new home in a tree. It twirled and swirled from branch to branch until it made a nest in between the leaves.
The little spring was happy to have a new home amongst the leaves. And the gentle breeze kept turning it round and round.
The End.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to count. One day, while playing in the park, he found a shiny gem. Tim was very happy and wanted to show it to his friend, Sam.
"Look, Sam!" said Tim. "I found this gem. Let's count how many colors it has." Sam looked at the gem and saw red, blue, and green. "One, two, three," they counted together. "Wow, three colors!" Sam was excited too.
But then a big, mean boy came and said, "You two are stupid! That's not a gem, it's just a rock." Tim and Sam were sad, but they knew they were not stupid. They kept the gem and continued to count and play together, having fun and enjoying their special find.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big purple ball. This ball had a long history. Many kids loved to play with it.
One day, a little boy named Tim found the purple ball. He wanted to play with it too. Tim started to push the ball around the yard. He pushed it here and there, and had lots of fun.
But then, Tim pushed the ball too hard. The ball went rolling down a hill. It rolled faster and faster. Tim ran after it, but he could not catch it. The purple ball hit a tree and broke. Tim was sad. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She loved to play outside with her friends. One sunny day, Amy saw a big lemon tree in her yard. The tree had many bright yellow lemons. Amy wanted to pick one lemon for her mom, but the tree was very tall.
Amy tried to reach the lemon, but she was too small. She felt stubborn and did not want to give up. She asked her friend, Tom, for help. Tom said, "Let's rest for a while and think of a good plan." They sat under the tree and thought about how to get the lemon.
After they rested, Amy and Tom found a long stick. They used the stick to pick the lemon from the tree. Amy gave the lemon to her mom, who was very happy. Amy learned that it is good to ask for help and to rest when you need it. And they all lived happily ever after.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the field near their house. The field has many flowers and bugs and birds. Anna and Ben like to look at them and question them.
One day, they find a big, smooth stone in the field. It is round and shiny and has many colors. Anna and Ben are curious. They touch the stone and feel how cold and hard it is.
"Where did this stone come from?" Anna questions.
"I don't know," Ben says. "Maybe it fell from the sky. Maybe it is a star."
"A star?" Anna says. "But stars are hot and bright. This stone is not."
"Maybe it is a special star. Maybe it has magic," Ben says.
Anna and Ben decide to keep the stone. They think it is a treasure. They take it to their house and show it to their mom and dad. Their mom and dad are surprised. They tell Anna and Ben that the stone is not a star, but a marble. A marble is a toy that some children play with. They roll it and bounce it and make games with it.
Anna and Ben are happy. They like their new toy. They thank their mom and dad and go back to the field. They play with their marble and make up stories about it. They think it is the best stone in the world.

Once upon a time, there was an adorable little elephant named Elly. Elly loved to travel with her family. They went to many fun places.
One day, Elly and her family went to a big park. Elly saw a big tree with a hole in it. She went to the tree and found a small trunk inside the hole. Elly was so happy. She picked up the trunk with her long nose and showed it to her mom.
"Look, Mom! I found a trunk!" said Elly. Her mom smiled and said, "That's great, Elly! Let's take it with us on our travels." Elly was so excited to have a new trunk to carry her things in.
From that day on, Elly and her family traveled to many more fun places. Elly always had her new trunk with her. She loved her trunk and the adventures they had together. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a loyal dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his best friend, a little boy named Tim. They played all day, every day, and had lots of fun together.
One day, Tim and Spot saw a big motorcycle on the street. They were so excited! Tim's dad let them sit on the motorcycle, but he said, "Don't touch anything, and be careful." Tim and Spot were very happy.
Then, a big wind came and shut the door of the house. Tim and Spot were scared. They ran back to the house and tried to open the door, but they couldn't. Tim's mom saw them and opened the door for them. They went inside and felt safe again. Tim and Spot hugged each other and knew they would always be loyal friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play at the park. One day, he went to the park with his mom. They saw a big, red ball. Tim wanted to play catch with the ball.
Tim asked his mom, "Can we play catch, please?" His mom said, "Yes, of course!" They started to play catch with the big, red ball. They threw the ball back and forth. Tim was very happy.
Suddenly, a dog came running into the park. The dog saw the big, red ball and wanted to play too. The dog jumped and caught the ball in its mouth. Tim felt a little guilty because he thought the dog was not allowed to play.
Tim's mom said, "It's okay, Tim. The dog just wants to play too." So Tim, his mom, and the dog played catch together at the park. They all had a great time and went home with a big smile.

Once there was a little girl. She was only three and she liked to dress up. One day she found a mitten on the ground outside. It looked very dirty.
The little girl picked up the mitten and asked her mother, "Mama, can I keep this?"
Her mother looked at it and said, "No honey, it's too dirty. Maybe if you wash it you can keep it."
The little girl thought it was a good idea, so she got a basin filled with warm water and some soap. She scrubbed and scrubbed the mitten until it was clean.
She held up the clean mitten and said, "Look, Mama! It's clean."
Her mother smiled and said, "Yes it is! Now you can dress up and wear it."
So the little girl put on the mitten and went outside to play. She was very happy to have a nice, clean mitten to dress up with.

Once upon a time, there was a huge, friendly bear named Ben. Ben lived in a big forest with his friends. One day, Ben had a very bad dream. He saw a scary monster in his dream. This dream was called a nightmare.
When Ben woke up, he was very sad because of the nightmare. He told his friends about the scary monster. His friends wanted to help him feel better. So, they made a plan to say "thank you" to the sun for scaring away the nightmare.
The next day, when the sun came up, Ben and his friends went outside. They all looked up at the sun and said, "Thank you, Sun, for scaring away the nightmare!" Ben felt much better and was very happy. Now, every time the sun comes up, Ben and his friends say "thank you" to the sun, and they don't have nightmares anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a big ranch. On the ranch, there were many animals. There were cows, horses, and sheep. They all lived together and played in the thick, green grass.
One day, a little boy came to the ranch. He wanted to play with the animals. He asked the animals, "Can I please play with you?" The animals said, "Yes, please come and play with us!"
The little boy and the animals played all day. They ran through the thick grass and had so much fun. When the sun went down, the little boy said, "Thank you for playing with me. I had a great time at the ranch!" The animals were happy too, and they all went to sleep.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a guitar. He liked to play it every day. One day, Tim found a big, sour lemon under his bed. He didn't know how it got there. Tim thought it was very strange.
Tim took the sour lemon and his guitar to the park. He sat under a big tree and played his guitar. A little girl named Sue came by. Sue liked the sound of Tim's guitar. She asked if she could play too. Tim said yes and gave Sue his guitar. Sue played the guitar very well. Tim was happy to have a new friend.
As they played, a big bird came down from the sky. The bird took the sour lemon from Tim's hand. Tim and Sue were very surprised. They didn't know what to do. Tim wanted his lemon back. Sue had an idea. She yelled at the bird, "Hey, give it back!" The bird heard Sue and dropped the sour lemon. Tim and Sue laughed and kept playing the guitar. They were happy that the bird didn't take their guitar too.

Once upon a time, there were two brave friends called Jack and Jill. One day, they decided to go on an adventure. They grabbed their hats and started walking through the forest.
As they were walking, they saw something very strange in the distance. It looked like a mushroom. Jack and Jill were curious, so they decided to take a closer look.
They went over to the mushroom and examined it. Jack asked Jill,"What's this?"
Jill grinned. She said,"That's a mushroom! It will remind us of our brave adventure."
Jack and Jill smiled. They continued their adventure and soon forgot all about the strange mushroom. But every now and then, when Jack and Jill passed by the same spot, the mushroom would remind them of their brave adventure.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She liked to explore and discover new things. One day, she went on a walk with her mom in the deep forest near their house.
As they walked, Lily saw something shiny on the ground. She picked it up and said, "Mom, look what I found!" It was a beautiful crystal. It was so pretty and sparkly. Lily's mom smiled and said, "Wow, that's a very nice crystal, Lily."
They took the crystal home and put it on a shelf in Lily's room. Every night, Lily would look at the crystal before she went to sleep. The crystal made her feel happy and safe. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a bright sun in the sky. It made the day very happy. A little boy named Mark loved to play outside in the sun. He liked to wear his apron when he played because it was his favorite thing to wear.
One day, Mark went outside to play with his ball. He wore his apron and it made him feel like a superhero. He threw the ball high in the sky and it went very far. Mark ran to get the ball and he was very fast.
While playing, Mark saw a big tree. He decided to sit under the tree and rest. The tree was very big and gave him shade. Mark looked at his apron and smiled. He loved his apron and the bright sun. He played all day and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to eat his meals. One day, he felt uncomfortable in his tummy. He did not want to eat his meal. Spot's mom said, "You must eat your meal, Spot." But Spot chose to ignore her.
The next day, Spot felt more uncomfortable in his tummy. He still did not want to eat his meal. Spot's mom said, "Spot, you need to eat." But Spot ignored her again. Spot's mom was sad because she wanted Spot to feel better.
On the third day, Spot's mom gave him a new meal. It looked yummy! Spot felt a little less uncomfortable. He tried the meal and liked it. Spot's mom was happy to see him eat. From that day on, Spot did not ignore his meals and his tummy felt good.

Once upon a Tuesday, there was a little girl who was very brave and bold. She was a bit different to other children and liked to do things her own way.
"Today I'm going to settle the pond," she said. She put on her original hat and went outside. She grabbed a large net and began to look around the pond.
"What are you looking for?" asked one of her friends.
"Fish!" said the little girl. 
The other children watched in curiosity as the girl started to catch fish and put them in a bucket. After some time, she had enough fish to fill the pond.
"It looks like you have settled the pond," smiled her friend.
The little girl was very proud of what she had done. Her courage and spirit had made something wonderful. 
From then on, every Tuesday the little girl was reminded of all that she had achieved. She was proud to know that she had made the pond special and original.

Once upon a time, in a busy park, there was a pigeon. The pigeon was hungry and wanted to find food. It saw some kids playing near a bench. The pigeon flew closer and saw some bread on the ground.
The pigeon tried to get the bread, but it was a struggle. The bread was too big for the pigeon to carry. A little girl saw the pigeon and said, "Oh no! The pigeon cannot get the bread."
The girl picked up the bread and broke it into small pieces. She gave them to the pigeon. The pigeon was happy and ate the bread. The girl said, "There you go, pigeon! Now you can eat the bread."
The pigeon finished eating and flew away. The girl felt good for helping the pigeon. She went back to play with her friends, and they all had a fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a graceful soldier named Tom. Tom liked to slide down the big hill near his house. One sunny day, Tom met a little girl named Sue at the top of the hill.
"Hi!" said Sue. "Can I slide with you?"
"Yes!" said Tom. "Let's slide together!"
Tom and Sue slid down the hill, laughing and having fun. They played all day, sliding and running back up the hill. The sun began to set, and it was time for Sue to go home.
"Bye, Tom!" said Sue. "I had fun sliding with you!"
"Bye, Sue!" said Tom. "Let's slide again soon!"

One day, a little girl named Ann found a hidden box under her bed. She opened the box and saw a lot of colorful soap inside. Ann was very happy and excited to play with the soap.
Ann's friend, Tim, came over to play with her. They wanted to have more fun with the soap. Tim suggested they should make bubbles with the soap. Ann agreed, and they went outside to play with the soap and water.
Ann and Tim took turns dipping the soap in the water and blowing bubbles. They laughed and chased the bubbles all around the yard. The sun was shining, and they had a great time playing with the hidden soap.

One day, a little dog named Spot was playing outside. Spot was having fun, but he did not know that it was going to rain. Spot was ignorant about the weather. Soon, it started to rain very hard. Spot was all wet and cold.
Spot ran back to the house. He saw his friend, a little girl named Sue. Sue saw that Spot was wet and cold. She said, "Oh no, Spot! You suffer from the rain. I will help you."
Sue got a big, soft towel from the bathroom. She wrapped the towel around Spot to make him warm and dry. Spot was happy that Sue helped him. Now, Spot knew that he should check the weather before playing outside.

Once upon a time, there was a small dog named Bob. Bob was a happy dog, but one day he started to feel miserable. He did not want to play or eat. All he wanted to do was lie down and sleep.
Bob's best friend, a little girl named Sue, saw that he was sad. She tried to find a way to make him happy again. Sue thought and thought, and then she had an idea. She would take Bob to the park, where they always had fun.
At the park, Sue found a stick with a point. She knew Bob loved to play fetch, so she threw the stick as far as she could. Bob saw the stick and his eyes lit up. He ran as fast as he could to get the stick and bring it back to Sue. They played for hours, and Bob was no longer miserable. Bob and Sue went home, happy and tired from their day at the park.

One day, a cute little dog named Max saw a cart. The cart had many toys. Max wanted to play with the toys. He ran to the cart and picked up a ball. He threw the ball and it rolled away.
Max ran to get the ball and brought it back to the cart. He did this many times. Max liked to repeat this game. He was having so much fun. Then, a little girl named Sarah saw Max playing with the cart.
Sarah said, "Hi, Max! Can I play with you?" Max wagged his tail and barked. Sarah picked up the ball and threw it. Max ran to get the ball and brought it back to Sarah. They played together and had lots of fun with the cart and the toys.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Ben. Ben was only three years old, and very curious about everything.
One day, Ben went for a walk in a new place. As he was looking around, he heard a strange sound. He was starting to panic, when suddenly he saw something soft!
It was a big, soft, furry dog! It was very friendly and so Ben felt safe.
"What's your name?" asked Ben.
The dog barked and said, "My name is Spot. Where are you going?" 
"I don't know," said Ben.
"Let's explore this new place together!" said Spot.
Ben and Spot had a great time exploring the new place. They found lots of interesting new things. Ben felt excited but not scared anymore. He had made a new friend!

Once upon a time, there was a big dance. All the animals were excited. They put on nice clothes and got organized. They wanted the dance to be fun and happy.
But, the big bear and the little bunny could not settle on a dance to do. The big bear wanted to do a slow dance. The little bunny wanted to do a fast dance. They were sad because they could not dance together.
Then, something unexpected happened. A wise old owl came and showed them a new dance. This dance was both slow and fast. The big bear and the little bunny tried the new dance. They were happy and had lots of fun. In the end, they all danced together and had a great time.

Once upon a time, there was a wealthy man who liked to invite people to his house. One day he asked his friends to come over for a playdate. Everyone was so excited and went to his house. 
When they got there, the man took them to the backyard. He had set up a huge pile of laundry on the grass. Everyone started playing and had so much fun. They jumped, rolled, and made shapes with the laundry. 
The man was so pleased that he invited them to come back the next week. He said he would have another fun surprise for everyone. All the friends had a blast and couldn't wait to come back. The end.

One day, a little girl named Amy went to visit her friend Tom. Tom had a big, scary dog named Max. Amy was scared of Max, but Tom said, "Don't worry, I will hold Max."
Amy and Tom played with Max in the yard. Amy was still scared, but she saw that Tom was not scared. Tom said, "Max is not scary. He is a good dog."
Amy started to feel better. She even touched Max and played with him. She learned that sometimes things are not as scary as they seem.

Once upon a time, a creative cat named Kitty loved to travel. One day, she decided to go on a week-long trip. She told her friend, Doggy, "I will travel for one week."
Doggy was sad. He said, "Please don't go, Kitty. I will miss you." Kitty wanted to go, but she didn't want to make Doggy sad. They had a big problem.
Kitty thought hard. She had an idea. She said, "Doggy, you can travel with me!" Doggy was happy. They traveled together and had fun. They learned that sharing fun times with friends is the best way to be happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had many different toys. One day, he found a box of matches. Tim did not know what matches were, so he went to his friend, Sally.
"Look, Sally! I found this," Tim said. "What is it?" Sally looked at the matches and said, "These are matches, Tim. They can make fire, but we should not play with them."
Tim and Sally discussed the matches. They talked about how different they were from their other toys. They knew that matches were not for kids to play with.
Tim decided to show the matches to his mom. "Mom, look what I found. Sally said they can make fire. What should I do with them?" Tim's mom smiled and said, "Thank you for showing me, Tim. I will keep them safe. Matches are not for playing."
From that day, Tim and Sally learned that some things are different and not for kids to play with. They continued to play with their safe toys and had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy called Peter. He was three years old and wanted to learn how to be polite.
One day, Peter saw a frog near a pond. The frog had big eyes and a big lip. Peter decided that he wanted to be friends with the frog and asked, "Will you be my friend?"
The frog smiled and said, "Yes!" Then, it led Peter away from the pond. Peter followed the frog and they went to find some flowers.
The frog taught Peter how to be polite. It told him to always say please and thank you. The frog also taught him to be kind and gentle. 
Peter was very happy to learn how to be polite. Maybe one day he would be able to teach other kids how to be polite, too. He said goodbye to his new friend and thanked it for leading him and teaching him.

Sara and Tom were friends who liked to play together. One day, Sara's mom said they could go to the pool. Sara was very happy and ran to tell Tom.
"Tom, Tom, come with me to the pool! My mom said we can go!" Sara shouted.
Tom looked sad. He wanted to go to the pool, but his mom did not allow him. She said he had to stay home and do his homework.
"Sorry, Sara, I can't go. My mom said no. You have fun, okay?" Tom said.
Sara felt sorry for Tom, but she was also envious. She wished she did not have to do homework. She thought it was not fair that Tom had to miss the pool. She decided to go anyway and have fun.
Sara and her mom went to the pool. Sara put on her swimsuit and jumped into the water. She splashed and swam and laughed. She saw other kids playing with toys and floats. She wanted to play with them too.
She saw a big yellow duck float. It looked very fun. She swam over to it and tried to grab it. But another kid was already on it. He was a big boy with a mean face.
"Hey, that's mine! Go away!" he yelled.
Sara did not listen. She wanted the duck. She pulled on it and tried to take it away. The boy got angry. He pushed Sara off the duck and into the water. Sara was scared. She could not breathe. She tried to scream, but no one heard her. She sank to the bottom of the pool. She closed her eyes and felt very cold. She did not have fun anymore. She wished she had listened to Tom and stayed home.

One day, a boy named Tim went to play outside. It was cold and there was rain. Tim put on his coat and boots. He liked to play in the rain. He saw a big puddle and jumped in it. The water splashed all around.
Tim found a toy in the puddle. It was a toy that could print. He was very happy. He took the toy inside his house. Tim wanted to print a picture of the rain for his mom. He pressed the button on the toy.
But instead of a picture, the toy printed a real, tiny rain cloud! The cloud floated in the room and made it rain inside the house. Tim was surprised. He did not know what to do. His mom came in and saw the little cloud. She laughed and hugged Tim. They opened the window and let the tiny cloud go outside to join the big rain clouds. They watched it fly away and smiled.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to think about things. One day, he found a big metal box in his yard. He was very patient and tried to open it, but it was hard.
A little bird saw Tim and wanted to help. The bird flew down and sat on the box. Tim and the bird tried to open the box together. They pushed and pulled, but the box did not open. They were both very patient.
Then, something unexpected happened. The box started to shake and make noise. Tim and the bird were scared. But they were brave and stayed by the box. The box opened, and out came a big, friendly dog! The dog was happy to see Tim and the bird. They all became good friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a small dog named Max. Max was a very dirty dog because he loved to play in the mud. One day, Max saw a big ball in the park. He wanted to play with it, so he ran to it and started to spring up and down.
"Hey, Max!" said a little girl named Lily. "That's my ball!" Lily was the owner of the ball. Max felt bad for taking her ball, so he gave it back to her. "Thank you," said Lily. "Do you want to play with me?"
Max and Lily played with the ball all day. They had so much fun, and Max learned that it was important to share and be kind to others. At the end of the day, Max was even dirtier than before, but he was very happy. He had made a new friend, and he knew that sharing was a good thing to do.

Once upon a time, there was a lion. This lion was different. He had a big, red nose. 
One day, the lion set out to find a friend. He walked and walked until he met a bird. The bird was blue and small. They played all day in the sun. 
At the end of the day, the lion and the bird were happy. They were friends now. The different lion with the big, red nose had found a friend in the small, blue bird. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little enthusiastic toy car. It loved to run around and make everyone laugh. It had a small battery inside that made it go fast. The toy car was very happy.
One day, the toy car saw a big toy truck. The truck was sad because it could not go as fast as the car. The enthusiastic car wanted to help the truck. It thought of a plan to make the truck laugh and be happy too.
The little car shared its battery with the big truck. Now, the truck could go fast and have fun too. They both laughed and played together. The other toys saw them and joined in the fun. Everyone was happy because the little car was kind and shared its battery. The moral of the story is that sharing can make everyone happy.

Tommy likes to play with his cars. He has many cars of different colors and sizes. He likes to make them zoom and crash on the floor. He does not like to wear pants. He thinks pants are too tight and hot. He likes to wear shorts or nothing at all.
One day, his mom tells him to put on his pants. She says they are going to the park. Tommy does not want to go to the park. He wants to stay home and play with his cars. He says no to his mom. He refuses to put on his pants. He runs away and hides under his bed.
His mom is not happy. She looks for Tommy. She finds him under his bed. She tries to pull him out. Tommy kicks and screams. He holds on to his cars. He says he hates pants. He says he hates the park.
His mom is very patient. She talks to Tommy. She tells him that pants are good. They keep him warm and safe. She tells him that the park is fun. He can play with other kids and see the birds and the flowers. She tells him that she loves him. She asks him to please put on his pants.
Tommy listens to his mom. He thinks for a while. He loves his mom too. He does not want to make her sad. He decides to try his pants. He finds a pair of pants that are blue and soft. He puts them on. They are not so bad. They are not too tight or hot. They are just right. He tells his mom he is ready. He gives her a hug. He takes one car with him. He says he is sorry. He says he wants to go to the park.
His mom smiles. She hugs him back. She tells him she is proud of him. She tells him he is a good boy. She takes his hand. They go to the park. Tommy has a lot of fun. He plays with other kids and sees the birds and the flowers. He also zooms his car on the grass. He is happy. He likes his pants. He likes the park. He likes his mom. He likes his cars. He likes his life.

Lily and Ben were restless. They did not want to go to bed. They wanted to play more with their toys and books.
"Mom, can we stay up a little longer?" Lily asked.
"No, sweetie, it's past your bedtime. You need to sleep and rest," Mom said.
"But we're not sleepy. We're bored," Ben said.
Mom smiled and hugged them. "I have an idea. How about we read a story together? A cozy and fun story that will help you settle down."
Lily and Ben liked that idea. They ran to their room and picked a book. They put on their jackets and snuggled under the blanket. Mom sat on the bed and opened the book.
"Once upon a time, there was a little bear who loved to explore the forest. He was curious and brave, but sometimes he got into trouble. One day, he found a big honeycomb and wanted to taste it. But he did not see the bees that lived there. When he poked the honeycomb, the bees came out and chased him. He ran and ran, but the bees were faster. He was scared and hurt. He wished he was home with his mom and dad."
"What happened next?" Lily and Ben asked.
"You'll have to wait and see," Mom said. "But I'll give you a hint. He learned a lesson and found a friend."
Lily and Ben were curious and excited. They listened to Mom as she read the rest of the story. They forgot about being restless and bored. They felt warm and happy. They settled down and fell asleep. Mom kissed them good night and turned off the light. She smiled and whispered, "Sweet dreams, my little bears."

One day, a fish named Bob lived near a reef. Bob had a friend named Tim. Tim was a deaf crab. They liked to play and swim together.
One sunny day, Bob and Tim found a big rock. The rock was dirty. Bob said, "Let's wipe the rock clean!" Tim could not hear, but he saw Bob cleaning the rock. So, Tim helped Bob wipe the rock too.
After they cleaned the rock, they played on the reef. They were very happy. Bob and Tim were good friends. They always helped each other and had fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a little bug who loved to crawl. He crawled all day long. One day, he saw a big parent bug. The parent bug was very scared.
The little bug asked, "Why are you scared, parent bug?" The parent bug said, "I lost my baby bug. I can't find her anywhere!" The little bug wanted to help the scared parent bug.
So, they crawled together to look for the baby bug. They crawled under leaves and over rocks. Finally, they found the baby bug. The scared parent bug was happy again. They all crawled back home, and they lived happily ever after.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big slide and wanted to go on it. Tim was very excited. He took his spade with him, because he liked to play with it in the sand.
Tim went up the slide and then slid down very fast. He laughed and had fun. Then, he saw a girl named Amy. Amy was sad because she did not have a spade to play with. Tim thought it was not fair for Amy to be sad.
Tim had an idea. He gave his spade to Amy and they played together. They made a big sand castle with the spade. Tim and Amy became best friends. They shared the spade and had lots of fun at the park. The day was full of joy and they both went home happy.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a huge dog. The dog is brown and fluffy. It has a long tail and big ears.
"Wow, look at that dog!" Anna says. "It is so big and soft. Can we pet it?"
Ben is not sure. He is a little scared of the dog. He shrugs. "I don't know. Maybe it is not nice. Maybe it will bite us."
Anna wants to try. She walks slowly to the dog. She holds out her hand. The dog sniffs her hand. Then it licks her hand. Anna giggles. "See, it is nice. It likes me. Come on, Ben, don't be afraid."
Ben follows Anna. He also holds out his hand. The dog sniffs his hand. Then it licks his hand. Ben smiles. "You are right, Anna. It is nice. It likes us. It is a good dog."
The dog wags its tail. It barks happily. It wants to play with Anna and Ben. Anna and Ben laugh. They pet the dog. They throw a ball for the dog. The dog runs and catches the ball. It brings it back to Anna and Ben. They have fun with the dog.
The end.

Jack had a wallet. He liked it very much. He kept it safe in his pocket. Jack's wallet was filled with money. Every day, Jack went to work. He was a kind man and always worked hard. At work, Jack would do harmless things like help others and make them laugh.
At the end of each day, Jack would come home with his wallet filled with money. He'd be so happy! He'd hug his wallet and be very proud of what he'd earned.
Jack knew his wallet kept his money safe. He was always taken care of. That made Jack very happy and he worked hard every day. Jack's wallet was his favorite because it kept him safe.

Once, there was a bossy cat named Tom. Tom liked to play with a big, red crayon. One sunny day, Tom went outside to play with his crayon in the park.
In the park, Tom found a tall slide. He wanted to slide down with his crayon. So, Tom climbed up the slide with his crayon in his mouth. When he reached the top, he put the crayon on the slide and sat down.
Tom slid down the slide very fast with his crayon. He had so much fun! He went up and down the slide many times. At the end of the day, Tom was tired but happy. He walked home with his crayon, ready for a good nap.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Daisy. Daisy had an umbrella and she liked to take it with her everywhere. One day, a big wind came and it made Daisy's umbrella fly high up in the sky.
Daisy chased it, but it flew so fast that she couldn't catch it. She kept running, but it flew even farther away. She started to feel sad and then she heard a voice.
The voice said, "Stay right here. I can help you get your umbrella back." Daisy looked around and she saw a friendly bird. The bird flew after the umbrella and then it came back with it in its beak.
Daisy was so happy that she thanked the bird and gave it a big hug. From then on, whenever Daisy goes outside with her umbrella, the friendly bird always stays close by.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy town. In the town, all the people and animals were always tired. They liked to sleep a lot. One day, a big war started between the dogs and the cats. They were very loud and woke everyone up.
A little boy named Tim went to his mom and said, "Mom, why are the dogs and cats fighting?" His mom said, "They are fighting because they are not sleepy like us. We must teach them to be sleepy too."
So, Tim and his mom decided to punish the dogs and cats. They made a big, soft bed and put it in the middle of the town. They said, "Dogs and cats, if you stop fighting, you can sleep on this big, soft bed."
The dogs and cats stopped their war and tried the big, soft bed. They became sleepy like the people in the town. From that day on, the dogs and cats were friends and the sleepy town was peaceful again.

Once upon a time, there was a little pig. The little pig was very fast. He loved to run and play with his friends. One day, the little pig was playing with his friends when he saw a big hill. The little pig wanted to see how fast he could run up the hill.
The little pig started to run up the hill. He ran very fast. But, when he got to the top of the hill, he saw something unexpected. There was a big, fast river. The little pig did not remember how to swim. He was scared.
Just then, a big, friendly frog came by. The frog said, "Don't be scared, little pig. I can help you." The frog helped the little pig learn how to swim. The little pig was very happy. Now, the little pig could run fast and swim too.

Once upon a time, there was a proud girl named Mia. She had a pretty veil that she loved to wear. Mia thought she was the best, and she always wanted to remain the best.
One day, Mia went to the park with her veil. She saw another girl with a veil too. Mia got mad. She wanted to be the only one with a veil. Mia and the other girl started to fight over who was the best.
In the end, both veils got torn and dirty. Mia and the other girl were sad. They both lost their pretty veils, and they did not remain friends. The day ended bad for Mia and the other girl.

Once upon a time, there was a gentle puppy named Spot. Spot loved to play with his friends in the park. One day, Spot saw a new friend. The new friend was a little bird named Tweet.
"Hi, Tweet!" said Spot. "Do you want to play with me?" Tweet said, "Yes, Spot, I want to play too!" So, Spot and Tweet played all day long. They ran, jumped, and had so much fun together.
But then, something unexpected happened. Spot found a sharp stick on the ground. He picked it up with his mouth and started to run. He didn't see that Tweet was flying very low. Spot accidentally poked Tweet with the sharp stick. Tweet fell to the ground.
Spot felt very bad. He said, "I'm sorry, Tweet. I didn't mean to hurt you." Tweet said, "It's okay, Spot. I know you didn't mean it." Spot and Tweet learned to be more careful when they played. And they lived happily ever after, playing together in the park.

One day, a young dog named Spot went to play with his group of friends. They all loved to play in the big park near their homes. Before they could play, Spot's mom told him to search for his little red ball. Spot knew that he would need the ball to play with his friends.
Spot searched and searched for the ball. He looked under his bed, in the toy box, and even in the kitchen. But he could not find the ball anywhere. Spot felt sad and worried that he would not be able to play with his friends without the ball.
Just as Spot was about to give up, he heard a noise coming from the living room. He went to see what it was and found his little sister playing with the red ball! Spot was so happy to find the ball and went to play with his friends. The unexpected noise had led him to the ball, and now Spot and his friends had the best day playing together in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to run and rush all day. He had a big red ball that he would play with. Spot's best friend was a cat named Fluffy. Fluffy and Spot would trade toys and play together.
One day, Spot could not find his original red ball. He looked everywhere for it. He asked Fluffy if she had seen it. Fluffy said she had not seen the red ball, but she had a blue ball they could play with. Spot and Fluffy played with the blue ball, but Spot still missed his original red ball.
The next day, Spot and Fluffy went to the park. They saw a big tree and decided to climb it. When they got to the top, they found Spot's original red ball! It was stuck in the tree from when Spot had rushed and played with it the other day. Spot and Fluffy were so happy they found the red ball. They played with it all day and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a cat named Tom. Tom was an ancient cat. He loved to stretch in the sun. One day, Tom had a task to do. He had to find a big red ball.
Tom asked his friend, a dog named Max, "Max, have you seen the big red ball?" Max said, "I think I saw it near the big tree." Tom went to the big tree to look for the ball. He saw something red behind the tree.
Tom was excited. He thought he found the big red ball. But it was not the ball, it was a red hat. Tom was sad. He kept looking for the ball.
Then, Tom saw his friend, a bird named Lily. "Lily, have you seen the big red ball?" Tom asked. Lily said, "Yes, I saw it near the pond." Tom went to the pond and found the big red ball.
Tom was very happy. He finished his task. He played with the big red ball all day. Then, he stretched in the sun and took a long nap.

One day, a little girl named Sue got some yarn from her mom. Sue was a very obedient girl. She always did what her mom told her to do. Her mom said, "Sue, you can play with the yarn, but do not go near the big tree."
Sue played with the yarn all day. She made a long line with the yarn. She went to her friend Tom and said, "Look at my yarn, Tom! It is so long and fun to play with." Tom said, "Wow, Sue! That is a great yarn. Let's play with it near the big tree."
Sue remembered what her mom said, but she wanted to have fun with Tom. They went near the big tree and played with the yarn. The yarn got stuck in the tree. Sue and Tom tried to get it out, but the yarn broke.
Sue was very sad. She knew she should not have gone near the big tree. When she went home, her mom saw the broken yarn. Sue's mom was not happy. She said, "Sue, you did not listen to me. Now your yarn is broken, and you cannot play with it anymore." Sue learned that she should always listen to her mom.

Once upon a time, there was a bright red train. The train had big wheels and went on the rail. It went fast and made a loud "choo-choo" sound. People liked to ride the train to go places. They would wave to the train when it went by.
One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to ride the train. He put on his hat and went to the train station with his mom. They waited for the train to come. Tim was very excited to ride the train for the first time. He jumped up and down as he waited.
But Tim and his mom were late. The train came and they were not at the station yet. The train did not wait for them. It went "choo-choo" and left. Tim and his mom missed the train. They were sad and had to walk home. Tim did not get to ride the bright red train that day.

Lily and Sam were friends who liked to sing and dance. They joined a show at school where they could sing and dance on a big stage. They practiced a lot and learned a song and some moves.
On the day of the show, Lily and Sam were very excited. They put on their costumes and waited for their turn. When they heard their names, they ran to the stage and smiled at the people who came to watch.
Lily started to sing the song and Sam followed. They sang very well and did their moves. But then, Sam forgot some words and felt scared. He stopped singing and looked at Lily.
Lily saw that Sam was scared and wanted to help him. She did not laugh or get angry. She was honest and kind. She pointed at the words on a big paper behind the stage and whispered to Sam, "Look, Sam, the words are there. You can do it. I am here with you."
Sam looked at the words and remembered them. He started to sing again and felt better. He smiled at Lily and said, "Thank you, Lily. You are a good friend." They finished the song and the people clapped and cheered. Lily and Sam hugged and felt happy. They did a good show and had fun.

Once there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to wander around the town. One day, he wandered too far from home and got lost. He saw a lot of traffic and became confused. Spot did not know what to do.
Spot saw a big girl named Sue. He walked up to her and said, "Help, I am lost." Sue looked at Spot and said, "Don't worry, I will help you find your home." They walked together, looking for Spot's home.
As they walked, they saw another dog that looked just like Spot. The other dog said, "Hi, I am Dot. I am lost too." Sue was surprised! She said, "Wow, you two look the same! Let's all find your homes together." They all became friends and soon found their homes. Spot and Dot were happy to be home and thanked Sue for helping them.

Once upon a time, there was a funny dog named Bob. Bob loved to play with a ball. He was a great player. One day, Bob saw a cat named Sally. Sally was playing with a toy mouse.
Bob said, "Sally, let's play together!" But Sally refused. She did not want to share her toy mouse. Bob was sad, but he had an idea. He went to find his ball.
Bob came back with his ball and said, "Sally, let's play with my ball!" Sally looked at the ball and thought it was fun. She agreed to play with Bob.
They played together and had a great time. They laughed and ran around. In the end, Sally and Bob became good friends. They learned that playing together was more fun than playing alone.

Once upon a time, there was a soldier. He was an original soldier, not like the others. He liked to help people. One day, he went to a town. The town did not have much food.
The soldier wanted to supply food to the town. He went to the big store. He bought a lot of food. He put the food in a big bag. He carried the big bag back to the town.
The people in the town were happy. They said, "Thank you, soldier!" The soldier smiled. He was happy to help. The town had food now, and everyone was happy. The original soldier went on to help more people.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely city. In the city, there were many people, but they did not talk to each other. They did not play or laugh together. They felt sad and alone.
One day, a kind girl named Lily came to the city. She saw how lonely everyone was and wanted to help. She went to a park and started to play with a ball. A boy saw her and asked, "Can I play with you?" Lily smiled and said, "Yes, let's play together!"
Soon, more kids came to the park and started to play with Lily and the boy. They all had fun and laughed. The grown-ups saw this and began to talk to each other too. The people in the city started to unite and become friends. They were no longer lonely, and the city became a happy place where everyone played, talked, and laughed together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play ball with his friends. They would throw the ball and try to score points. Tim was good at the game, but his shoes were tight. He wanted to win, but his feet hurt.
One day, Tim saw a sign that said, "New Shoes!" He wanted new shoes, but he didn't have enough money. Tim asked his mom for help. She said, "You can do jobs around the house to earn money for the shoes." So Tim worked hard and got the money for new shoes.
Tim got his new shoes and played ball with his friends. His feet didn't hurt anymore, and he scored many points. Tim learned that working hard can help you get what you need. He was happy and his friends were happy too. The moral of the story is that hard work pays off.

One day, a squirrel was in the park. The squirrel was not very smart. He wanted to improve and be better. He saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. But he did not know how. 
A bird saw the squirrel and wanted to help. The bird flew down and said, "Hello, squirrel! I can help you climb the tree. Just follow me." The squirrel looked at the bird and said, "Thank you, bird! I want to improve and be a better climber."
The bird showed the squirrel how to climb the tree. They went up, up, up, and the squirrel got better at climbing. Soon, they were at the top of the tree. The squirrel was so happy! He said, "I did it! I improved and now I am not a stupid squirrel. Thank you, bird!" The bird smiled and said, "You're welcome, squirrel! You can always improve if you try."

One day, a big oak tree in the park was sad. It felt embarrassed because it couldn't resist the strong wind. The wind blew its leaves away, and the oak tree felt naked.
A little boy named Tim saw the oak tree and asked, "Why are you so sad, tree?" The oak tree said, "The wind took my leaves. I feel silly now."
Tim wanted to help the oak tree. He ran home and got a big bag. He picked up all the leaves around the tree and put them in the bag. Then, he brought the bag to the oak tree.
"Look, tree! I found your leaves!" Tim said. The oak tree smiled and said, "Thank you, Tim! You made me happy again!"
From that day on, the oak tree and Tim became best friends. They played together in the park every day, and the oak tree was never embarrassed again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy faucet that he believed was magic. He thought it was a weird faucet because every time he turned it on, something special happened.
One day, Tim was playing with his toy faucet in his room. He turned it on and said, "I wish for a big cake!" To his surprise, a big cake appeared on his bed. He was so happy and shared the cake with his mom. His mom asked, "Where did this cake come from?" Tim said, "My magic faucet made it!"
The next day, Tim played with his faucet again. This time, he wished for a new toy car. Just like before, the toy car appeared in front of him. He showed it to his dad, who asked, "How did you get this toy car?" Tim said, "My magic faucet gave it to me!" His dad just smiled and thought it was a fun game.
But then, something unexpected happened. The faucet stopped working! Tim tried to make a wish, but nothing happened. He was sad and didn't know what to do. His mom and dad saw him and asked, "What's wrong, Tim?" He told them the faucet wasn't magic anymore.
His mom and dad smiled and said, "Tim, the faucet was never really magic. We saw you playing and wanted to make you happy, so we made your wishes come true. But you know what? You don't need a magic faucet to be happy. You have us, and we love you very much." Tim hugged his mom and dad and realized that he didn't need a magic faucet after all.

Once upon a time, there was a large egg. The large egg had a small crack. The crack was very small, but it grew bigger and bigger each day. The large egg was on a soft nest in a tall tree.
One sunny day, the animals in the forest decided to unite. They wanted to help the large egg with the crack. The bird tried to fix the crack with its beak. The squirrel tried to cover the crack with leaves. The rabbit tried to hold the egg together with its paws. But the crack kept getting bigger.
Then, on a windy day, the large egg with the crack fell from the tree. It fell down, down, down to the ground. The animals in the forest watched as the large egg broke into many pieces. They were very sad because they could not save the large egg. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a big toy box with a lock on it. One day, he heard a soft voice coming from inside the toy box. Tim was very curious about the voice and wanted to find out who it was.
Tim tried to open the lock, but it was not easy. He asked his mom for help. His mom had a key and she could open the lock. With a clear smile, she opened the toy box for Tim. Inside the toy box, they found a small bird.
The bird was the one with the soft voice. It was so happy to be free. Tim and his mom took the bird outside, and it flew away. Tim learned that it's important to listen to the voices around him. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a big, red couch in his room. Tim loved to jump and play on his couch. One day, he felt a tickle in his nose. He knew it was time to sneeze.
Tim sneezed very loud. "Achoo!" he said. His sneeze was so big that it made him fall on the couch. The couch was soft and safe. Tim did not get hurt. He laughed and smiled because the sneeze was funny.
Tim's mom heard the sneeze and came to check on him. She saw Tim was safe on the couch. She smiled and said, "Bless you, Tim!" Tim said, "Thank you, Mom!" Then, they both laughed and played on the big, red couch together.

Once upon a time, there was a big road. On this road, there were many cars, bikes, and buses. They all went fast on the road. The road was very long and fair.
One day, a small toy car was on the road. The toy car did not belong there. It was lost. The toy car wanted to go back to its home. The toy car was sad.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and blew the toy car up into the sky. The toy car flew over the road and saw its home. The wind put the toy car back in its home. Now the toy car was happy. It belonged in its home, not on the big road.

Sam likes to roll on the grass. He rolls up and down, left and right, and makes funny noises. He feels the sun on his face and the wind in his hair. He is happy.
Mom watches Sam from the window. She smiles and goes back to her work. She has to write a letter on the computer. She has to send it to her boss. She does not like her work, but she needs it. She is grateful.
Dad comes home from his work. He works at the store. He sells things to people. He likes his work, but he is tired. He is grateful.
Sam sees Dad and runs to him. He hugs him and says, "Dad, I rolled on the grass! It was fun!" Dad hugs him back and says, "I'm glad, Sam. You are a good boy." He picks him up and takes him inside. He says, "Let's go see Mom. She worked hard today. We can thank her and help her." Sam nods and says, "Okay, Dad. I love Mom. I love you too." He is happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy had a mitten that she liked very much. It was soft and warm. One day, Amy lost her mitten. She was very sad. She asked her mom, "Mom, can you help me find my mitten?"
Her mom said, "Yes, I will support you in finding your mitten." They looked all around the house. They looked in the bedroom, the kitchen, and the living room. Finally, they found the mitten under the table. Amy was so happy!
That day, Amy's mom made a tasty snack for them. They ate the snack together and talked about how they found the mitten. Amy learned that with support from her mom, she could find anything. And from that day on, Amy always kept her mitten close.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to spend time with his mom. They liked to cook together. One day, they wanted to make rice for dinner.
Tim was very excited. He helped his mom put the rice in a big pot. But, oh no! Tim dropped the rice on the floor. Tim felt sorry. His mom said, "It's okay, Tim. We can clean it up."
They cleaned the rice from the floor. Then, they cooked new rice together. Tim was happy again. They had a yummy dinner. Tim learned that it's okay to make mistakes, and he was not sorry anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little pig named Piggy. Piggy lived in a small house made of hay. He felt safe in his hay house. One day, Piggy saw that his hay was starting to rot. He did not know what to do.
Piggy had a friend, a wise old owl named Ollie. Piggy went to Ollie and told him about the rot in his hay house. Ollie thought for a moment and said, "Piggy, you need to find new hay to make your house safe again."
Piggy went on a journey to find new hay. Along the way, he met a friendly cow named Bessie. Bessie had lots of fresh hay and was happy to share it with Piggy. Piggy thanked Bessie and took the new hay back to his house.
Piggy worked hard to fix his house with the new hay. Soon, his house was safe and strong again. Piggy was happy and knew that his house would not rot anymore. And from that day on, Piggy and Bessie became very good friends.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a cat named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to climb and play all day. One day, Fluffy saw a ladder near a tree. Fluffy thought it would be fun to climb the ladder and perform tricks for her friends.
Fluffy called her friend, Bunny. "Bunny, come see me perform on the ladder!" she said. Bunny hopped over quickly and watched Fluffy climb up and down the ladder. Fluffy did flips and jumped from one step to another. Bunny clapped and cheered for her friend.
After Fluffy's performance, they both felt hungry. They went to find something yummy to eat. They found a big, red apple under the tree. They shared the yummy apple and enjoyed their snack together. Fluffy and Bunny were happy to have spent the day having fun and eating yummy food.

Once there was a kangaroo, and he wanted to do something fun. So he hopped around looking for something. One day he came across a calm meadow, and he saw that there were no people around. He hopped in, and felt the grass and the sunlight on his back. He felt happy and relaxed. He started to hop around and explore the meadow. He found some tall grass to play in and some trees to jump over. He was having so much fun!
But then all of a sudden he heard a voice. It was a kind voice, and it said "Are you enjoying yourself, little kangaroo?". He looked around and saw a man. His face was gentle, and he smiled. The man asked the kangaroo if he could stay and play in the meadow, and the kangaroo was excited. The man said that he would allow him to stay, as long as he was calm and respectful. The kangaroo thanked the man and promised to be calm and he hopped off to play.
He continued to explore the meadow, hopping around and enjoying the sunshine and the wind on his back. He felt very calm and content, and he thanked the man for allowing him to stay and play in the meadow. He had a wonderful time!

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim went to the theater with his mom. They wanted to see a movie about wild animals. Tim was very excited because he loved wild animals. They bought popcorn and sat in their seats.
In the movie, there was a magic potion that made things shrink. Tim wished he could have a potion like that. When the movie ended, they walked out of the theater.
As they left, Tim found a tiny bottle on the floor. It looked like the magic potion from the movie. He took it home and tried it on his toy car. The car began to shrink! Tim was amazed. But then, the potion spilled on Tim's foot, and he started to shrink too! Tim's mom found another potion to make him big again, and they both laughed at the wild adventure they had.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Timmy. Timmy loved to sing all day long. He lived in a small cage in a smelly room. Timmy did not like the smelly room. He wanted to escape and see the big world outside.
One day, while Timmy was singing, a little girl named Lily came into the smelly room. She heard Timmy singing and said, "Wow, you have a pretty song, Timmy! Do you want to come out and play with me?" Timmy nodded his head and sang even louder.
Lily opened the cage and Timmy flew out. They played together and Timmy sang happily. Timmy was so glad to escape the smelly room. He and Lily became best friends and Timmy never had to go back to the smelly room again.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Mel. She was three years old and lived with her family in a small village.
One day, Mel asked her Mommy why the seasons changed. Her Mommy said, "Each season has something special, sweetheart. Winter has snow and summer has the beach. Fall has leaves and spring has blooming flowers."
Mel thought the seasons were beautiful and asked, "How can I make them even better?"
Mommy laughed and said, "That's a very good question. You can improve the seasons by being kind to others and helping out around the house. That way, each season can be even more special."
Mel was very happy. She ran around the house, trying to help everyone. She was bossy but in a very loving way. Mel was determined to make every season the best it could be.
The End.

Once upon a time, there was a big dragon. The dragon was green and lived in a cave. People in the town did not believe the dragon was real. They said it was a story.
One sunny day, a little girl named Lily went to find the dragon. She walked and walked until she found the cave. The sky was very clear. Lily went inside and saw the dragon. The dragon was sad.
Lily talked to the dragon and found out he was a nice dragon. She told the town that the dragon was real and nice. The people were surprised. They became friends with the dragon and played with him. Now, everyone believed in the dragon and they all lived happily together.

Once upon a time, there was a charming little dog named Spot. Spot loved to run and play with his friends. One day, Spot heard about a big race. The winner would get a prize. Spot wanted to win the prize, so he practiced running every day.
On the day of the race, Spot met a new friend named Lily. Lily was a small, shy cat. Spot said, "Lily, will you watch my race? I want to win the prize." Lily nodded and wished Spot good luck. Spot felt happy and ready to run.
The race began, and Spot ran as fast as he could. He remembered all the practice he did. At the finish line, Spot won the race! He got the prize, a big, shiny toy. Spot was so happy, and he wanted to share his prize. He went to Lily and said, "Lily, I won the prize! Let's play with it together." Lily smiled, and they played happily ever after.

One day, a little dog named Max went for a walk with his boy, Tom. Tom put a leash on Max to keep him safe. They walked to the park where they saw their friends.
At the park, they played a silly game. They ran and jumped and laughed. Max was very fast and Tom was very happy. Max wanted to win the game and make Tom proud.
Max ran faster and faster. He won the game and everyone clapped. Tom gave Max a big hug and said, "Good job, Max!" Max was so happy that he wagged his tail. They walked back home, smiling and laughing, after a fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play with her favorite toy, a long string. She would jump and run to catch the string all day. Kitty also loved to eat yummy food that her owner gave her.
One day, Kitty saw the string hanging from a high shelf. She wanted to play with it so much. Kitty jumped and jumped but could not reach the string. She felt sad and wanted the string very much.
Then, Kitty had an idea. She climbed on a chair to get closer to the string. But when she jumped, the chair fell and Kitty fell too. Kitty got hurt and cried. Her owner came and saw what happened. The owner was sad too. They could not play with the string anymore.

Tom and Mia were playing with their marbles on the floor. They had many marbles of different colors and sizes. Some were big and shiny, some were small and dull. Tom liked the bright ones best. He picked up a red marble and held it in his hand.
"Look, Mia, this is my favorite marble. It is bright like a fire. Do you want to see?" he said.
Mia nodded and came closer. She looked at the red marble and smiled. "It is very pretty, Tom. Can I hold it?" she asked.
Tom nodded and gave her the marble. Mia felt its smoothness and weight. She rolled it on her palm and watched it sparkle. "Thank you, Tom. It is very nice. Do you want to trade?" she said.
Tom thought for a moment. He liked his red marble, but he also liked Mia. He wanted to make her happy. He looked at her marbles and saw a blue one that was bright like the sky. He pointed at it and said, "Can I have that one? It is bright like the sky. I like it."
Mia nodded and gave him the blue marble. She took the red marble and put it in her pocket. She said, "Now we both have bright marbles. They are our special marbles. We can play with them later."
Tom smiled and put the blue marble in his pocket. He said, "Yes, they are our special marbles. We can wrap them in paper and keep them safe. They are our friends."
They hugged each other and went back to playing with their other marbles. They were happy. They had bright marbles and each other.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a mighty dog named Max. Max had many friends, and they all liked to play together. Every day at noon, they would meet at the big tree near the pond.
One sunny day, Max's friend, a little bird named Lily, wanted to borrow Max's ball to play with. Max said, "Yes, you can borrow my ball, Lily." Lily was very happy, and they played with the ball together.
After they played, Lily gave the ball back to Max and said, "Thank you for letting me borrow your ball, Max. You are a very good friend." Max smiled and said, "You're welcome, Lily. I'm always happy to share with my friends." And they all played happily together under the big tree.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat named Mimi. Mimi was a serious cat who loved to dance. She would dance all day and all night to the rhythm of the wind blowing through the trees.
One day, Mimi went outside to dance. The wind was very cold, and Mimi started to shiver. She didn't want to stop dancing, but she knew she needed to find a way to stay warm.
Mimi had an idea. She put on a big, warm coat and went back outside. Now, Mimi could dance to the rhythm of the wind without shivering. She was a happy, warm, and serious dancing cat.

One day, a little boy named Tim went with his mom to the subway. They wanted to go to the park. Tim was very excited to play there. He liked to run and play with his ball.
On the subway, Tim saw a big dog. The dog looked frustrated. It did not want to be on the subway. Tim asked his mom, "Can I play with the dog?" His mom said, "No, Tim. The dog is not happy right now."
Then, something unexpected happened. The dog started to run around the subway. It wanted to play with Tim! Tim's mom said, "Okay, you can play with the dog, but be careful." Tim and the dog played together and had fun. The dog was not frustrated anymore. Everyone on the subway was happy and laughed.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Dare. Dare was a very hungry dog. He wanted to find some food to eat. Dare looked around and saw a big tree with a lot of fruit.
Dare wanted to get the fruit, but he didn't know how. Then, a big bird named Teach saw Dare. Teach was very smart and knew how to help. Teach said, "Dare, I can show you how to get the fruit."
Dare was very happy. Teach showed Dare how to jump high and get the fruit. Dare ate the fruit and was not hungry anymore. Dare and Teach became best friends and played together every day.

One day Timmy and his mum went to the park. Timmy ran around in the grass, chasing little flies. He was so happy that he shouted out loud. 
Mum: "What are you so happy about, Timmy?"
Timmy pointed to the sky and said, "Look Mum! An enormous fly!"
Mum looked up and there it was, an enormous fly circling in the sky, so large that it seemed like a bird. Timmy watched in wonder.
Mum: "That's amazing, Timmy. Applaud for the fly!"
So Timmy cheered for the fly, clapping his hands together and laughing the whole time. The enormous fly heard the applause and flew off, taking with it all the joy Timmy had felt that day. 
Mum: "Let's go home now, Timmy. It's time for dinner." 
Timmy nodded and waved goodbye to the fly as they left the park.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby monkey called Timmy. He was always climbing trees and exploring the jungle. One special day, Timmy came across a huge pile of newspapers. He had never seen newspapers before.
Curious Timmy grabbed a newspaper and said, "What's this?" He shook the newspaper fiercely and saw something cool. Timmy said, "Wow! Look at all these shapes! I must climb up and take a closer look."
Timmy started to climb the tallest tree in the jungle and suddenly everything went dark. As he hung onto the branch, the darkness started to light up with colourful shapes and patterns. "It's the same shapes I saw on the newspaper!" exclaimed Timmy. 
When Timmy arrived home, he showed his friends the shapes he found, and they all laughed. "What silly shapes! Those are actually fireworks! We're going to have a party tomorrow and they'll be lit up!"
And that's why Timmy was so excited when he saw the newspaper. He had unknowingly seen a sneak peek of the wonderful fireworks that would be lit up and enjoyed by everyone tomorrow night!

Once upon a time, there was a big hedge. In the hedge, there was a small bird. The bird was very selfish. He did not like to share his things with others.
One day, a little mouse came to the hedge. The mouse was very hungry. He asked the bird, "Can I have some food, please?" The selfish bird said, "No, this is my food. You cannot have it."
The mouse was sad and went away. Later, a big storm came. The wind blew away the bird's food. The bird was now hungry too. He asked the mouse, "Can you help me find food?"
The mouse said, "Yes, but you must learn to share." The bird agreed. They found food together and shared it. From that day on, the bird was not selfish anymore. They both lived happily in the hedge.

Once upon a time there was a turkey who was very persistent. Every day, he would look for food in the meadow and he wouldn't stop until he found something tasty to eat. 
But one day, the turkey decided he had had enough of looking for food. He wanted to stay in one place and not move until he found something yummy. 
The turkey searched for the perfect spot to stay. He found a big, shady tree and he stayed there for days.
But no matter how long the turkey stayed, he couldn't find the food he longed for. He was very persistent and tried everyday, but still no food appeared.
Finally, the turkey had a bright idea! He decided to walk around and search for food once more. And then he spotted some yummy berries on the ground!
The turkey was so happy that he gobbled up all the berries. He was glad he had been so persistent, and he learnt an important lesson - sometimes you have to move around to find the things you are looking for.

Once upon a time, there lived a Jay and his friend, Tom. 
Jay wanted to borrow Tom's toaster to make some toast one day. Tom agreed and let Jay borrow the toaster. 
Jay worked hard to make the toast and managed to get it just right-golden brown. Before he knew it, the house was filled with the smell of the toast.
He went back to Tom's house and returned the toaster. Tom was surprised to see the toast was so brown. 
From then on, Jay learned an important lesson - always be careful when you borrow something. If you use it wisely, you'll be able to return it and make everyone happy!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play in his room all day. His room was like a chamber full of toys. One day, his mom asked him to include his sister, Sue, in his playtime. Tim didn't want to, but his mom said it was important to share.
"Tim, please let Sue play with you," his mom said. Tim agreed, but he was not very happy about it. Sue was very happy and excited to play with Tim. She was persistent and never gave up when they played games. At first, Tim was not happy, but soon he found that playing with Sue was fun.
Together, they played with cars, dolls, and blocks. They laughed and had a great time. Tim learned that including others and being kind made him happy too. The moral of the story is that sharing and being nice to others can bring happiness to everyone.

One day, a little boy named Tim was playing with his toy car. He was racing it up and down the living room. He felt very happy. Suddenly, he remembered that his mom told him about an emergency. She said, "If you see red lights outside, come to me right away." Tim didn't know what an emergency was, but he knew it was important.
As Tim played, he saw red lights outside the window. He stopped playing and ran to his mom. "Mom, I see red lights! Is it an emergency?" he asked. His mom looked outside and started to laugh. Tim felt embarrassed. He didn't understand what was funny.
His mom said, "Tim, that's not an emergency. That's just our neighbor's red lights for their party." Tim felt better and laughed too. He went back to playing with his toy car, happy that there was no emergency after all.

Once upon a time, there was a lovely forest. In this forest, there lived a hunter. The hunter liked to catch animals. But he was a good hunter and only caught what he needed.
One day, the hunter went to the forest to find food. He saw a big deer. He tried to catch the deer, but it was too fast. The hunter was sad because he could not catch the deer.
That night, the hunter prayed. He asked for help to catch the deer. The next day, he went back to the forest. This time, he was able to catch the deer. The hunter was happy and thanked the forest for helping him.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a troubled boy who liked to poke things. One day, he found a belt in his room. It was a big belt with a shiny buckle. Tim thought it was a fun toy to poke.
Tim wore the belt and went outside to play. He saw his friend, Tom, and wanted to show him the belt. As they played, Tim poked the belt buckle with a stick. Suddenly, the belt started to glow and something unexpected happened.
The belt made Tim and Tom fly up in the sky. They were scared at first, but then they had fun flying like birds. They flew all around the town and saw their houses from the sky. After a while, the belt stopped glowing and they gently landed back on the ground. Tim and Tom were happy and decided to keep the magic belt a secret. They couldn't wait to fly again another day.
