Once there was a little boy called Jack who was very jolly. He loved finding a quiet corner to read his favourite magazine. One day, his mum decided to allow Jack to go on a bike ride with his friends. Jack was so excited and pedaled away as fast as he could. 
But when Jack stopped for a rest, he realised he'd left his magazine behind. He was so sad, but then he realised that having fun with his friends was an even better treat. 
Finally, Jack and his friends made it safely back home and Jack was happy again. He liked the feeling of making new memories far better than reading his magazine. 
Jack asked his mum if he could go on the bike ride again, and she said "Yes". Jack had learned a valuable lesson about having fun with his friends and enjoying special moments together.

Once upon a time there was a little boy called Bob. Bob liked to zoom around in his car. One day he was zooming around the garden when he suddenly ran into the fence. He was very sad but he finished his zoom and left the car by the fence. Bob's parents were waiting at the door. 
"What have you been doing?" they asked.
"I was zooming in my car," said Bob sadly.
Then his dad had an idea. He said, "let's finish your zoom by tidying up the mess. Then you can drive your car safely!"
So Bob and his parents tidied up the fence and Bob's car stayed safe. 
The next day Bob was zooming around the garden again. His parents were very proud of him, because they knew his car was tidy and safe. And Bob had a great day zooming around! The end.

One day, a little ball named Bounce went outside to play. The weather was nice and sunny. Bounce loved to play with his friend, a big and useful box named Boxie. They were very happy together.
"Let's play catch!" said Bounce to Boxie. Boxie nodded and they started to play. Bounce jumped high and Boxie caught him. They laughed and played for a long time.
But then, something unexpected happened. The nice weather changed, and it started to rain! Bounce and Boxie were sad because they could not play catch anymore. But Boxie had an idea.
"Look, Bounce! I can be useful in the rain too!" Boxie opened his lid and Bounce jumped inside. Now, they could still play together even in the rain. Bounce and Boxie were happy again. They knew that they could always find a way to play, no matter the weather.

Once upon a time, there was a very smart dog named Max. Max loved to play with his friends in the park. One day, Max and his friends had a big part to play in a game.
Max had a good memory. He remembered where to find all the hidden toys in the park. He helped his friends find the toys by showing them the way. They were all very happy because Max was so intelligent.
In the end, Max and his friends won the game. They were all very proud of Max for being such a good helper. They played and laughed until it was time to go home. Max felt happy to have his friends and his special memory.

Tim and Sam are best friends. They like to play pirates in the park. They have a big ship made of wood and ropes. They pretend to sail the ship and look for treasure.
One day, they see a new boy in the park. He has a red hat and a big smile. He comes to their ship and says hello.
"Hi, I'm Ben. Can I play with you?" he asks.
Tim and Sam look at each other. They don't want to share their ship with a stranger. They think he might break it or take their treasure.
"No, you can't. This is our ship. Go away!" Tim says.
"Yeah, go away! We don't need you!" Sam says.
Ben feels sad and angry. He thinks Tim and Sam are mean and rude. He wants to play with them, but they don't want him.
He decides to hide behind a tree and wait for them to leave. He thinks of a plan to get back at them.
He sees a big stick on the ground. He picks it up and sneaks to their ship. He swings the stick and hits the ropes. The ropes break and the ship falls apart.
Tim and Sam hear a loud noise. They turn around and see their ship in pieces. They see Ben running away with a red hat and a big smile.
They are shocked and angry. They cry and shout.
"Hey, stop! You broke our ship! You are bad!" Tim says.
"Yeah, stop! You are bad! We hate you!" Sam says.
They run after Ben, but he is too fast. He escapes and hides behind another tree. He thinks he is clever and brave.
He doesn't know that Tim and Sam have an adorable dog named Spot. Spot is smart and loyal. He smells Ben's red hat and follows him. He barks and bites his leg.
Ben screams and drops his hat. He sees Spot and Tim and Sam. He is scared and sorry.
"Ow, ow, ow! Stop, stop, stop! I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry!" he says.
Tim and Sam catch up with him. They see his hat and his leg. They see his tears and his fear.
They feel sorry for him too. They realize he was lonely and wanted to play. They realize they were mean and rude.
They stop and let go of him. They ask Spot to stop too. Spot stops and wags his tail.
They look at each other and say sorry.
"Sorry, Ben. We were mean and rude. We shouldn't have said go away. We should have said hello." Tim says.
"Yeah, sorry, Ben. We were mean and rude. We shouldn't have broken your hat. We should have shared our ship." Sam says.
"Sorry, Tim and Sam. I was bad and angry. I shouldn't have broken your ship. I should have asked nicely." Ben says.
They hug and smile. They become friends. They go to their ship and fix it. They play pirates and look for treasure. They have fun and share. They are happy.

One day, a little girl named Lucy wanted to help her mother. Her mother was busy in the kitchen. "What can I do to help?" Lucy asked.
Her mother said, "Lucy, can you please load the big basket with fruits from the garden?" Lucy was alert and ready to help. She put on her shoes and went outside.
In the garden, Lucy picked red apples, yellow bananas, and green grapes. She loaded the big basket with all the fruits she could find. Lucy was happy to help her mother.
Lucy carried the heavy basket back to the kitchen. Her mother was so proud of her. "Thank you, Lucy!" her mother said. "Now we can make a yummy fruit salad together."
Lucy and her mother made a big fruit salad. They laughed and had fun. Lucy felt good helping her mother and being alert in the garden.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was very frightened of big dogs. One day, he saw a big dog in the park. He wanted to run and hide, but he knew he needed to face his fear.
Spot took a deep breath and started to walk towards the big dog. The big dog looked at Spot and wagged its tail. Spot was still scared, but he kept going. As he got closer, he saw that the big dog was not mean, but friendly.
In the end, Spot made friends with the big dog. He was not frightened anymore. This was a big success for Spot. From that day on, Spot was not scared of big dogs and made many new friends in the park.

Once upon a time, there were two children, John and Sarah. They were playing in the park together. Suddenly, Sarah saw a beautiful butterfly flying around.
With a big smile, Sarah shouted, "Look!"
John was amazed and watched the butterfly as it made quick motions in the air. He felt smart for noticing the butterfly's motion.
John said to Sarah,"It's so pretty!"
Sarah agreed with a nod and said, "thank you" for noticing it.
The children watched the butterfly together until it flew away. Then, they continued to have fun in the park. 
John was happy. He wanted to thank Sarah for noticing the butterfly, so he ran over and gave her a big hug.
Sarah laughed and hugged him back. They both felt very smart.

Once upon a time, there was a big missile. It did not belong with the toys. One day, the missile met a little boy. The little boy was glad to see the missile. He thought it was a toy.
The little boy said, "Hi, missile! Do you want to play with me?" The missile said, "I am not a toy. I do not belong here. I am a big missile."
The little boy was sad. He wanted to play with the missile. The missile said, "Do not be sad. I will find a place where I belong."
The missile went away to find a place to belong. The little boy waved goodbye. He was glad that the missile would find a place to belong. And the little boy went to play with his toys.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was adventurous and loved to play outside. One day, he wanted to go on an adventure.
Before he went out, Tim had to dress. He put on his favorite shirt and pants. He also wore his big boots. Now, he was ready for his adventure.
As Tim walked outside, he saw a big storm coming. The clouds were dark and there was lightning in the sky. Tim knew it was not safe to be outside, so he went back home. He promised himself that he would go on another adventure when the sky was clear and bright.

Once upon a time, there was a little soldier. He was very brave. Every night, he would dream of big adventures. He was always grateful for his dreams.
One day, the little soldier went on a walk. He met a big dog. The dog was lost. The little soldier wanted to help the dog. They looked for the dog's home together.
They found a big house. The door was open. They went in. They saw a big party. The dog's family was there! They were so happy to see the dog. They were grateful to the little soldier. But then, the little soldier woke up. It was all a dream! The little soldier smiled. He was grateful for his dreams.

Once upon a time there was a driver who was very harsh. He drove around with a big truck, picking up lots of things for people. One day, he drove to a farm to receive some hay for the farmer. 
When he opened the door of the truck, he saw that the farmer was waiting for him. The driver was very rude and spoke in a harsh tone. He said, "You have to pay for the hay before I give it to you." The farmer said in a passive tone, "I'm sorry, I don't have enough money today." 
The driver didn't like this and he yelled, "If you don't pay, you won't receive the hay!" His voice was very loud and the farmer was scared. But then, the driver realized how harsh he was being, and he apologized. He said, "I'm sorry, I didn't mean to be so mean. I will still give you the hay." 
He placed the hay in the truck, and the farmer thanked him. The driver felt bad for being so harsh, so he said to the farmer, "Don't worry about paying today. Just have a nice day." 
The farmer smiled, and said thank you. The driver drove away feeling happy. It made him feel good to be kind and receive something in return.

Once upon a time, there was a white mouse named Peter. Peter had a very special task. Every day he would go out to search for very special things. 
At first, Peter didn't know what he was looking for. He searched high and low but couldn't find anything. Peter was very frustrated.
But one day something very special happened. When Peter was searching he found something very exciting. It was shiny and round and he knew it was what he had been searching for all along.
The next day Peter proudly showed his friends the special thing. It was a bright white marble, perfect for playing games and everyone was delighted. 
From that day forward, Peter was very pleased that all his searching had paid off. He was so proud that his friends got to enjoy the special marble and have lots of fun.

One day, a little boy named Tim was eager to help his mom. His mom asked him to gather all the old magazines in the house. Tim said, "Okay, Mom! I will find them all!"
Tim looked in the living room, the kitchen, and his bedroom. He found many magazines. He put them in a big pile. Tim's mom saw the pile and said, "Good job, Tim! You found so many magazines!"
Tim was very happy to help his mom. They took the magazines to a place where they could give them away. Tim felt proud that he could help his mom and make her smile.

Once upon a time, in a calm little town, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to go up a hill and look at the view. He could see the whole town from there. One day, Tim saw a new girl named Sue. Sue was sad because she did not know anyone in town.
Tim said, "Hi Sue, I know a great place to see the view. Do you want to come with me?" Sue smiled and said, "Yes, I would love to!" So, they walked up the hill together.
When they reached the top, they saw a big, colorful bird. They had never seen a bird like that before! The bird said, "Hello, I can talk!" Tim and Sue were so surprised. They had a new friend, and now Sue knew someone in town too. They all enjoyed the beautiful view together.

One day, a small ant named Tim was walking in the park. He saw a big tree with a sign that said, "Climb and Win." Tim was calm and brave, so he wanted to try it.
As he started to climb, a big bird named Sam saw him. Sam asked, "What are you doing, little ant?" Tim said, "I am climbing the tree to win." Sam thought it was funny and said, "I can fly to the top. I will win for sure!"
Tim and Sam raced to the top of the tree. Sam was fast, but Tim was calm and did not give up. When they got to the top, they saw a big cake with a note. The note said, "The winner must share the cake with a friend."
Sam was sad because he did not have any friends. But Tim was kind and said, "We can share the cake and be friends." So, they both won and shared the cake. They became best friends, and they learned that winning is more fun when you share with others.

One day, a creative cat named Jazz loved to play with her ball. She played all day and all night. She had many friends in her town. They all liked to play with her.
One day, Jazz got a big box. She had to deliver it to her friend, Dog. "I will help you," said her friend, Bird. They took the big box to Dog's house. But, when they got there, Dog was not happy. "I don't want this box," he said. Jazz and Bird were sad.
Then, something unexpected happened. The box started to move! They opened the box and found a big, bouncy ball inside. Jazz, Bird, and Dog played with the ball all day. In the end, they all became happy friends again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved her modern fan. It was big and white. The fan helped her stay cool on hot days. The fan had a light that would glow when it was dark. Sue liked to watch the light glow.
One day, Sue's friend, Tim, came to visit. They played all day. When it was dark, Sue said, "Look, Tim! My fan can glow!" Tim looked up and saw the glowing light. "Wow!" said Tim, "It's so pretty!"
Sue and Tim lay on the floor, watching the fan spin and the light glow. They were very happy. They talked and laughed until they fell asleep. The glowing fan kept them cool all night.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia had big eyes that saw everything. She loved to play with her toys and make plans for fun games.
One day, Mia saw a modern toy car in the shop. She wanted the car so much! Mia thought and thought about how she could get the car. She made a plan to save her money to buy it.
Mia did small jobs to save money. She helped her mom clean the house and helped her dad in the garden. Mia's eyes would light up every time she thought about the modern toy car.
After a few weeks, Mia had enough money to buy the car. She went to the shop with her mom. Mia's eyes were full of joy when she saw the car. She gave her money to the shopkeeper and took the car home.
Mia played with her modern toy car all day. She was so happy that her plan worked. Her big eyes sparkled as she raced the car around her room. And from that day on, Mia knew that if she made a plan and worked hard, she could make her dreams come true.

Sara and Ben are friends. They like to play in the pool. They have fun with the water and the toys. But one day, Sara sees a big bug in the pool. She is scared of the bug. She screams and runs away.
"Come back, Sara!" Ben says. "It is just a bug. It will not hurt you."
But Sara does not listen. She hides behind a tree. She is uncomfortable. She does not want to go near the pool.
Ben feels sad. He wants to play with Sara. He cares about her. He thinks of a way to help her. He gets a net and catches the bug. He puts the bug in a jar.
"Look, Sara," he says. "I have the bug. It is not in the pool anymore. You can come back now."
Sara peeks from behind the tree. She sees the bug in the jar. She is still scared, but she is also curious. She walks slowly to Ben.
"Can I see the bug?" she asks.
"Sure," Ben says. "But be careful. Do not touch it. It might bite."
Sara looks at the bug. It has many legs and eyes. It is brown and hairy. She thinks it is ugly, but also interesting.
"Thank you, Ben," she says. "You are a good friend. You made me feel better."
"You are welcome, Sara," Ben says. "Do you want to play in the pool again?"
Sara nods. She is not uncomfortable anymore. She is happy. She and Ben go back to the pool. They splash and laugh. They forget about the bug. They have fun.

Once upon a time, in a big tree, there were many flies. They all looked the same. But there was one original fly. This fly was very different. It was bigger and had bright colors. The other flies did not like the original fly.
One day, the flies had to choose a new home. They found two trees. One tree was small and safe. The other tree was big and scary. The original fly wanted to go to the big tree. The other flies wanted to go to the small tree. They all had to choose.
The original fly went to the big tree. The other flies went to the small tree. The big tree was not safe. A big bird lived there. The bird saw the original fly. The bird ate the original fly. The other flies were safe in the small tree. The original fly should have chosen the small tree too.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty girl named Lily. She had nice, soft skin and big, happy eyes. Lily loved to play outside in the sun with her friends.
One day, Lily and her friends went to the park. They saw a big swing set. Lily loved to swing. She ran to the swing and sat down. Her friends pushed her, and she went up high in the air. Lily felt like she was flying like a bird.
After a while, Lily got tired. She stopped swinging and went to sit under a tree. The tree had pretty flowers that smelled nice. Lily and her friends laughed and played until it was time to go home. They all had a fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, in a big room called a chamber, there lived a lazy cat named Tom. Tom liked to sleep all day and not do much. One day, Tom's friend, a little bird named Sue, came to visit him.
"Tom, let's play!" said Sue. "I want to imagine we are in a big castle, and I am the queen and you are the king!" Tom yawned and said, "Okay, Sue, let's play that."
As they played, something unexpected happened. A real queen and king walked into the chamber! They were lost and needed help to find their way back to their castle.
"Can you help us?" asked the queen. Tom and Sue were so surprised, but they wanted to help. Together, they led the queen and king back to their castle. The queen and king were so happy, they invited Tom and Sue to stay and play in the real castle. Tom was no longer a lazy cat, and he and Sue had lots of fun playing with the queen and king every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big toy box in her room. One day, she wanted to find a toy that was at the bottom of the box. She tried to lift the toys out, but they were too heavy for her.
Lily thought of a new style to get the toy. She took a long, dry stick and put it into the box. She moved the stick around and around until the toy came up to the top. She was very happy to have found her toy.
Lily played with her toy all day long. She was proud that she found a new style to get her toy. She told her friends about her idea, and they all thought it was very smart. From that day on, Lily knew that she could always find a way to solve her problems.

One day, a little dog named Tim went for a run in the park. He saw a big, bossy cat named Max. Max was always telling Tim what to do. Today, Max wanted to play a game before dinner.
"Tim, you have to run and catch me!" said Max. Tim did not like this game, but he played anyway. He ran and ran, trying to catch Max. They ran around trees and over hills. Tim was getting very tired.
Finally, Tim stopped and said, "I don't want to play this game anymore, Max. I want to go home and eat my dinner." Max was surprised, but he knew that Tim was right. They both went home and enjoyed their dinner. From that day on, Max was not so bossy, and they played games that both of them liked.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom had a toy with a zip. He loved to zip and unzip it all day. One day, Tom's mom told him they needed to sell some of their old toys. Tom was sad but he knew he had to help his mom.
Tom took his toy with a zip to the park where he saw his friend, Sue. "I have to sell my toy with a zip," he told Sue. Sue looked at the toy and said, "I want to buy your toy, but I don't have any money." Tom felt bad for Sue, but he needed to sell his toy.
Then, a selfish boy named Max came to the park. Max had a lot of money. He saw Tom's toy with a zip and wanted it. "I will buy your toy," said Max. Tom thought about Sue and how she wanted the toy too. He decided to give the toy to Sue instead of selling it to Max. Tom learned that being kind is more important than being selfish.

One day, a little girl named Amy found a gentle cat in her room. The cat was playing with a crayon. Amy was happy and wanted to play with the cat too. They played with the crayon, making fun drawings on paper.
Amy's mom called her for lunch. "Hurry, Amy! It's time to eat!" Amy told the cat to wait and went to eat. When she came back, the cat was gone. She was sad and looked for the cat everywhere.
Then, Amy saw her crayon move on the floor. The crayon was drawing by itself! The cat was not a real cat, but a magic crayon. It could change into a cat and play with Amy. Now, Amy had a magic friend to play with every day.

Once upon a time there was a green page. The page was such a fun page. It made shapes and squiggles. But it was all alone. So it went out to look for friends.
It found one friend, then another, and another, until soon there were lots of friends. They all wanted to join the green page. So they joined together and made a big, fun party.
They drew on the page and laughed and danced. The green page was so happy that it had lots of friends. The friends stayed together and played on the page all day. 
They all had so much fun that they wanted to join even more. So the green page got bigger every day as more and more friends joined. And they all lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time, there was a chicken. The chicken lived on a farm. The farm had a big, modern house. The chicken liked to play near the house.
One day, the chicken met a boy. The boy was nice. He smiled at the chicken. The chicken smiled back. They played together all day.
The boy and the chicken became best friends. They played together every day. They made each other smile. And they lived happily ever after.

Once there was a small boy who wanted to go and play in his yard. He was very alert and ran outside. As he ran, he started counting the trees, 1, 2, 3...He was so excited he kept counting, 4, 5, 6 as he looked around the yard. He was so lost in his counting, he didn't notice the big, scary dog until it was too late. He screamed and tried to run away but the dog chased him, barking loudly. He was so scared, he almost forgot to count. 7, 8, 9 he shouted as he raced down the street.But the dog was faster and ran right up to him.The boy stopped counting, 10, as the dog cornered him. He was too scared to move and he knew he would never be able to make it back to the yard. The bad dog had won. The boy sadly cried and went home, never to play in the yard again.

Once upon a time, there was a big, ugly bug. The bug wanted to build a bed. He found a soft mattress and put it on the ground.
One day, a little girl saw the ugly bug. She said, "Ew! You are ugly! I don't like you!" The bug was sad. He didn't want to be ugly. He wanted to be pretty.
The little girl had an idea. She said, "Let's build a bed together. Maybe then you won't be ugly." The bug was happy. They started to build the bed. But the little girl didn't help. She just watched the bug work.
The bug was tired. He said, "Please help me build the bed." The little girl said, "No, you are ugly. I don't want to help you." The bug was sad again.
The bed was not good. The bug was still ugly. The little girl didn't help. The moral of the story is: don't judge others by how they look, and always help your friends.

Once upon a time, there was a kind boy named Jake. Jake liked to help people. One day, Jake saw a hungry little bird in a tree. Jake got some food and helped the bird eat.
The next day, Jake and the bird flew to a volcano. The volcano was big and the bird was scared. Jake held the bird in his arms and said, "Don't worry, I'll help you."
Jake thought carefully and realized that the volcano was hungry. He got some food from the tree and fed the volcano. Soon the volcano calmed down and smiled.
The bird was so happy and thanked Jake for helping them. From then on, Jake and the bird were good friends, helping each other with everything.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to read books. In her room, she had a big bookcase full of her favorite books. Every day, she would pick a book and read it before bedtime.
One day, Lily was feeling upset. She did not know what to do. She went to her bookcase to find a book that would make her happy. As she pulled a book, something surprising happened. The bookcase moved and showed a secret door!
Lily was so surprised! She opened the door and found a room full of toys and games. She played with the toys and forgot why she was upset. From that day on, whenever Lily was sad, she would go to the secret room and play. And every time, she would leave with a big smile on her face.

Lily likes to stare at the letter on the fridge. It is a big letter A. It is red and shiny. Lily thinks it is a friendly letter. She wants to learn more letters.
One day, Lily's mom gives her a book. It has many letters and pictures. Lily is happy. She opens the book and stares at the first page. She sees a friendly letter A and a picture of an apple. She points and says, "A, apple!"
Mom smiles and hugs Lily. She says, "Good job, Lily! You are very smart. Do you want to see more letters and pictures?" Lily nods and says, "Yes, please!"
Mom turns the page and shows Lily another letter and picture. She says, "This is B. B is for ball. Can you say B, ball?" Lily stares at the letter B and the picture of a ball. She tries to say, "B, ball!"
Mom claps and says, "Very good, Lily! You are learning fast. Let's see what else we can find." Lily and mom have fun reading the book and staring at the letters and pictures. Lily learns many new words. She loves the friendly letters.

Sara and Ben are playing with their toys in the living room. Sara has a tea set and Ben has a truck. Sara wants to play tea party with Ben, but Ben wants to play with his truck.
"Ben, come here. I have tea for you. It is sweet and hot," Sara says, holding a cup and a pot.
"No, thank you. I want to play with my truck. It is fast and loud," Ben says, zooming his truck on the floor.
Sara feels sad. She wants to share her tea with Ben. She thinks of a way to make him come. She sees a book on the shelf. It has prints of animals on it. Sara likes animals. She has an idea.
"Ben, look at this book. It has prints of animals. Do you like animals?" Sara asks, showing him the book.
Ben stops his truck. He likes animals too. He sees a print of a lion on the cover. He wants to see more.
"Yes, I like animals. Can I see the book?" Ben asks, walking towards Sara.
Sara smiles. She gives him the book. They sit on the couch and look at the prints of animals. They see a lion, a monkey, a giraffe, and a penguin. They make sounds like the animals.
"Roar!" Ben says, pointing at the lion.
"Ooh-ooh-ahh-ahh!" Sara says, pointing at the monkey.
They have fun with the book. Sara remembers her tea. She still wants to share it with Ben.
"Ben, do you want some tea now? It is still sweet and hot," Sara says, holding the pot.
Ben looks at the pot. He sees steam coming out of it. He thinks it looks nice.
"OK, I will try some tea. Thank you, Sara," Ben says, taking the cup.
Sara pours some tea for Ben. She pours some for herself too. They sip their tea. They like it. It is sweet and hot.
"Yum, this is good tea. You are a good friend, Sara," Ben says, smiling.
"You are a good friend too, Ben. I am glad you like my tea," Sara says, smiling back.
They hug each other. They are happy. They play with their toys and their book. They share their tea and their prints. They have a good time.

One day, a little girl named Amy found a big, red ball in her yard. She wanted to play with it, but her mom said, "Amy, you need to clean your room first." Amy did not want to clean her room, but she could not resist playing with the big, red ball. So, she went inside to clean her room.
Amy's mom wanted to test if Amy would clean her room. She put a fake bug on Amy's bed to see if Amy would find it. When Amy saw the bug, she yelled, "Mom, there's a bug on my bed!" Her mom came into the room and laughed, "That's a fake bug, Amy. I put it there to test if you were really cleaning."
Amy laughed too and said, "You got me, Mom!" She finished cleaning her room and then went outside to play with the big, red ball.

One day, a big dog named Max went for a jog. He liked to run in the park. Max saw a small cat named Lily. Lily was laying on the grass. Max wanted to play with Lily.
Max ran to Lily, but Lily was scared. Lily got angry and hissed at Max. Max was sad. He did not want to make Lily angry. He wanted to be friends with Lily.
Max walked away and laid down on the grass. Lily saw that Max was sad. She walked to Max and sat next to him. They became friends. The moral of the story is to be kind and not scare others.

Once upon a time, there was a kind boy named Tim. Tim had a big sack. He liked to put toys in the sack and take them to his friends.
One day, Tim's mom said, "Tim, let's go to the park." Tim took his sack with him. At the park, he met a girl named Sue. Sue was sad because she had no toys to play with.
Tim wanted to make Sue happy. He opened his sack and let Sue pick a toy. Sue was so happy, and they played together all day. Tim was glad to be kind and share his toys with his new friend.

Tom and Jen are friends. They like to play games. Today they play a game with rice. They have two bowls of rice. They take turns to move some rice from one bowl to another. The one who has more rice in their bowl at the end wins.
Tom is deaf. He cannot hear Jen. He uses his eyes and his hands to talk to her. Jen can hear, but she also knows how to talk with her eyes and her hands. They are good at talking to each other.
Tom and Jen play the game. They move some rice. They count the rice. They smile and laugh. They have fun. Jen has more rice in her bowl. She wins. She shows Tom. She says, "I win!"
Tom is not sad. He is happy for Jen. He claps his hands. He says, "You win! Good job!" He gives Jen a hug. He says, "You are my friend. I like you."
Jen hugs Tom back. She says, "You are my friend too. I like you. You are good at the game. Next time, you can win."
They share the rice. They eat some rice. They are full and happy. They play another game. They are friends.

One day, a little taxi was on the road. The taxi liked to roll fast and easy. It made the taxi very happy. The taxi saw a big hill and thought, "I will roll down the hill fast and easy!"
As the taxi started to roll down the hill, it saw a big rock on the road. The taxi did not want to hit the rock, so it tried to roll around it. But the rock was too big, and the taxi could not roll around it fast and easy.
Just when the taxi thought it would hit the rock, a big wind came and moved the rock away. The taxi was so happy! It rolled down the hill fast and easy, all the way to the bottom. The taxi learned that sometimes, things can look hard, but they can still be easy with a little help.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Go-Go. She was three years old and very silly. One day in her room, Go-Go opened up a drawer that belonged to her mom. Inside the drawer, Go-Go found a funny stuffed animal. She was so excited to show it to her mom.
Go-Go was about to walk out of the room when she heard her mom's voice. "Go-Go," her mom said, "don't touch things that are not yours." Go-Go felt bad, like she had done something wrong.
Go-Go's mom walked into the room and saw the stuffed animal in Go-Go's hands. She began to scold Go-Go, but then she stopped. She smiled and said, "Let's go show this funny thing to your dad."
Go-Go's mom encouraged her to take the toy out of the drawer, and then she and Go-Go walked downstairs where Go-Go's dad worked. Go-Go showed him the toy and he laughed. He even made a funny face!
Go-Go's mom was glad she had encouraged her daughter to follow through with her curiosity. That day, Go-Go knew it was okay to explore, as long as she was always respectful. From that day forward, she was very careful with the things she found in drawers.

One night, Zipper, a small puppy, wanted to sleep. He hopped around and couldn't find a place to lay down. He hopped across the yard before stopping in front of a large tree and said, "Maybe I should sleep here." 
Suddenly, a voice answered, "No, Zipper, you mustn't sleep here. It isn't safe!" Startled, Zipper turned to see a harmless little rabbit peeking out from behind a bush. 
"What's not safe?" Zipper asked.
"Sleeping under the tree," the rabbit said. "It gets cold in the night and you won't be warm here. Come, I know a safe place for you to sleep."
The rabbit led Zipper over to a nearby hollow, lined with grass and leaves. "This is a safe, warm place to sleep," said the rabbit. So Zipper curled up in the hollow and fell fast asleep. 
The little rabbit watched him all through the night, to make sure he was safe and warm.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big puddle. Tim was happy. He jumped in the puddle. Splash! Water went everywhere.
A small bird was near the puddle. The bird was sad. Its wings were wet. "Why did you splash me?" said the bird. Tim felt bad. He did not want to make the bird sad.
"I am sorry, bird," Tim said. "I will help you." Tim took the bird home. He used a soft towel to dry the bird's wings. The bird was happy again. They became good friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He liked to play with his toy hook. One day, Tim saw his mom in the kitchen. She was making a cake. He wanted to help her.
"Can I help?" Tim asked. Mom smiled and said, "Yes, you can stir the mix." Tim took a big spoon and began to stir. He stirred and stirred. The mix went round and round. Tim started to feel dizzy. He giggled and said, "I'm dizzy, mom!"
Mom laughed and said, "Take a break, Tim." Tim stopped stirring and sat down. He played with his toy hook. Soon, the cake was ready. Tim and his mom enjoyed the yummy cake together. They were both happy.

Sammy was a small bear who lived in a cozy bedroom. His bedroom was usually very tidy, but one morning he woke up to find it very messy! He looked all around and couldn't figure out what had happened. He asked his mom, "Mom, why is my bedroom so messy?"
His mom replied, "I think a little mouse came in and made a mess while you were asleep."
Sammy asked, "What is a mouse?"
Mom explained, "A mouse is a tiny animal. It can be very sneaky, and it likes to bite things."
Sammy was a bit scared. He asked, "Will the mouse bite me?" 
Mom said, "No, the mouse won't bite you. I will keep you safe."
Sammy felt better and started cleaning his bedroom. He was very careful to make sure the mouse didn't come back.

Once upon a time, there was a fat cat. Every day, the fat cat woke up just before daybreak, when the sky turned purple and the sunrise peeked over the horizon. The cat loved to greet the sunrise, and would meow and purr when the sun's light shined into her eyes.
One day, the cat was curious and wanted to find out what surprises the sunrise would bring. She looked around carefully, but all she could see was the same purple sky, with no hint of what was coming.
This morning the sun peeked more brightly than ever before, and with it came the most wonderful surprise. From behind the horizon, a basket of fluffy kittens crawled out and spread across the grass. The fat cat was delighted, and she cuddled the kittens for hours. It was the best sunrise ever!

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Lucy. She loved to play and explore around her house. Today, she decided to venture outside. She stepped out into her garden and saw a neat, tidy bush. She headed straight for it and twisted it around. Suddenly, Lucy heard a tinkling sound and she saw something glittering in the bush. She reached in and found a beautiful, shiny gem. She squealed in excitement and quickly put the gem in her pocket. 
Lucy's mom was close by and heard her laugh. She said, "What did you find, Lucy?". Lucy smiled brightly and pulled the gem out of her pocket. She showed it to her mom and said, "Look, a gem!" 
Her mom smiled and said, "Wow! What a bright gem. Where did you find it?". Lucy pointed to the bush and said, "Twist it around and out came the gem". Her mom replied, "That's amazing! Let's keep the gem safe and show it to everyone later". 
Lucy happily agreed and she couldn't wait to show off her special, tidy gem.

One day, a black cat named Sam was walking in the yard. He saw a big tree with a lot of prunes on it. Sam was hungry and wanted to eat the prunes. He jumped and tried to get them, but he could not reach them. Sam felt sad.
A little bird saw Sam and said, "Why are you sad, Sam?" Sam told the bird, "I want to eat the prunes, but I can't reach them." The bird said, "I can help you get the prunes, but first, you must take a sleep under the tree." Sam agreed and went to sleep under the tree.
While Sam was sleeping, the bird flew up and picked the prunes. The bird dropped the prunes down to Sam. When Sam woke up, he saw all the prunes on the ground. He was very happy and said, "Thank you, little bird!" The bird said, "You're welcome, Sam! Enjoy your prunes!" And they both became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. He had very long hair. Tim's mom said, "You need a haircut." Tim did not want a haircut. He liked his long hair. But his mom said, "Long hair is not good for you. It is careless."
Tim went to the hair place. He sat in the big seat. The hair person said, "What kind of haircut do you want?" Tim said, "I want a short one, please." The hair person used scissors to cut Tim's hair. Tim was scared, but he did not cry.
After the haircut, Tim looked in the mirror. He liked his new short hair. He felt clean and neat. Tim's mom was happy too. She said, "Now you look nice and not careless." Tim learned that it is good to listen to his mom. The moral of the story is to listen to your mom because she knows what is best for you.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy was very hungry. She wanted to eat a big apple. Lucy asked her mom, "Can I have an apple, please?"
Her mom said, "Yes, but first you must do a job. Pick up your toys in your room." Lucy went to her room and picked up all her toys. She did a good job.
When Lucy finished, her mom gave her a big apple. Lucy was so happy. She took a big bite. Her mom and dad saw her and began to applaud. "Good job, Lucy!" they said. Lucy smiled and enjoyed her apple.

Once upon a time there was a factory. It was a very big factory with lots of workers inside. One day the workers started working real hard. They twisted and twisted things all day long. But things did not get better, in fact, they got worse.
The factory workers kept twisting and twisting but the factory stayed the same. Then one day a small boy saw them. He asked them why they were twisting and twisting.
The workers told the boy that they were trying to make the factory better but they were not. The boy thought for a while and then he had an idea. He asked the workers to work together and stop twisting things.
The workers thought the boy was silly but they decided to try it. To their surprise, the factory got better. Everything looked fine and the workers were so happy. They all thanked the boy for his help.
The moral of this story is that working together is much better than working alone.

Once, there was a big lion who lived in the jungle. One day, the lion was very, very tired. He yawned a big yawn and looked for a place to sleep.
He found a tree with a long vine. The lion said, "I will sleep here." He lay down on the soft vine and closed his eyes.
A little bird was in the tree. The bird was impatient. She wanted to play. She said, "Hey, lion! Wake up! Let's play!" The lion yawned again and said, "Not now, little bird. I am tired." The bird waited and then they played together when the lion woke up.

Once upon a time, there was a girl who wanted to go to a castle. She was so excited that she ran up to the castle gate and peeked inside. The girl noticed how hot it was in the castle and she began to feel uncomfortable. She wanted to go in and see what it was like, so she stepped inside.
A voice called out from inside the castle - "Who are you?!" The girl was scared, but she spoke up bravely and said "I am a girl who wanted to visit your castle."
The voice answered back "You need to leave right away. This is my castle and no one is allowed in here." The girl felt so disappointed and began to cry. She begged the voice, "Please let me stay for a little while, I just wanted to explore the castle."
But the voice shouted back "No! You cannot stay here, now go away!" So the girl sadly walked away from the castle and never returned.

Once upon a time, there was a bald man named Tom. Tom loved to make pies. One day, he made a big, yummy pie. He was very happy.
Tom went to his friend, Sam, and said, "Look at my pie! Do you want to eat some?" Sam looked at the pie and said, "Yes, let's eat the pie together!" But, they needed something to cover the pie.
Tom and Sam went to find a big cloth to cover the pie. They found a pretty, blue cloth. They put the cloth on the pie. Now, the pie was covered and safe.
Tom and Sam sat down and ate the yummy pie together. They were very happy and full. They laughed and talked while they ate. It was a good day for Tom and Sam.

One day, a girl named Lucy wanted to help her dad. Her dad was fixing the fence in their yard. The fence was old and not modern. Lucy was very excited to help her dad make the fence look nice and new.
As they worked, a big wind came and blew the fence down. Lucy and her dad were very surprised. They did not know what to do. They thought they would have to build a new fence from scratch.
But then, a friendly neighbor came over with a big, modern fence. The neighbor said, "I have an extra fence that I don't need. You can have it!" Lucy and her dad were so happy. They thanked the neighbor and put up the new fence. The yard looked great, and Lucy was proud that she could help her dad.

Once upon a time, there was a bright sun in the sky. A little boy named Tim went to the park to play. He saw a big tree with a hole in it. Tim thought, "There must be treasure in the tree!"
Tim tried to lift a big stick to poke the hole. But the stick was too heavy, and he couldn't lift it. He was sad, but then he saw a small stick on the ground. Tim picked up the small stick and poked the hole in the tree.
And guess what? Out came a shiny treasure! It was a bright, shiny coin. Tim was so happy that he found the treasure. He took the bright coin home and showed it to his mom and dad. They were very proud of him. And from that day on, Tim knew that sometimes, small things can help you find big treasures.

Once upon a time there was a modest castle. A little girl wanted to explore it, so she started to climb up the walls. She made her way around the castle, feeling so excited. Everywhere she looked, she saw something new and curious.
At the top of the castle was a beautiful garden with lots of flowers. The little girl wandered around, smelling the fragrant flowers. She felt so happy and relaxed.
Climbing down the walls, the little girl wanted to explore more of the castle. Everywhere she went, she found something new to discover. She stayed until the sun went down, and then it was time to go home. 
The little girl smiled, remembering all of the fun she had climbing around the modest castle. She was happy and excited for the next time she got to visit it again.

Once upon a time, there was a graceful kitty who loved to zip around. Everywhere she went, she moved very quickly, like the wind. She especially liked to zip around the kitchen because there was always something to explore. 
One day, the kitty found a big pot full of soup on the stove. She couldn't believe her eyes. She had never seen so much soup in one place before. She licked her lips and started to zip around the pot. All of a sudden, the pot started to boil and the kitty quickly zipped away. 
The kitty was so graceful with her zig-zag pattern that she soon realized she had nothing to fear. She kept zipping around and eventually she was brave enough to come back to the soup pot. The soup smelled so good that she couldn't help herself. She zipped around the pot a few more times until she had enough courage to take a sip of the soup. 
After that day, the kitty was always zipping around the kitchen. She tasted lots of other things and was always so graceful in her movements. The kitty had a special love for soup and so every time she saw the pot, she would zip around it with a smile on her face.

Sara and Tom are playing in the park. They see many leaves on the ground. They are red, yellow and brown. They are pretty.
"Let's gather the leaves and make a pile!" Sara says.
"OK!" Tom says. They run and pick up the leaves. They put them in a big pile. They are happy.
They see a little dog. The dog is black and white. It has a long tail and floppy ears. The dog runs to the pile of leaves. It jumps on it. The leaves fly everywhere.
"Hey, stop!" Sara says. She tries to catch the dog. The dog runs away. It barks.
"Come back, dog!" Tom says. He follows the dog. The dog runs to a man. The man is sitting on a bench. He has a book.
"Is this your dog?" Tom asks.
"Yes, he is. His name is Spot. He likes to play with leaves. I'm sorry he messed up your pile." The man says.
"It's OK. He is cute. Can we pet him?" Sara asks.
"Sure, you can. He likes to be petted. Especially on his ears. He has soft ears." The man says.
Sara and Tom pet the dog. The dog wags his tail. He licks their faces. They giggle.
They make friends with the man and the dog. They gather more leaves and make another pile. They play together until it is time to go home. They say goodbye and hug. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Tim. Tim liked to play at the park. His favorite thing to do was to slide down the big slide. One day, Tim saw a tough-looking dog at the park. The dog was big and had a loud bark.
Tim wanted to be friends with the dog, but he was scared. He thought the dog was too tough for him. Then, he had an idea. He would show the dog how to slide down the big slide. Maybe then the dog would want to be friends with him.
Tim went up the slide and called the dog over. The dog watched as Tim slid down the slide. The dog barked and wagged his tail. Then, the dog tried to slide too. The dog slid down the slide and landed on the ground with a big smile. Tim and the tough dog became best friends and they played on the slide every day.

One day, a boy and a girl went to the lake. They saw a boat. The boy said, "Let's count the boats!" The girl was glad.
They counted one, two, three boats. The girl said, "I see a big boat!" The boy said, "I see a small boat!" They laughed and played near the water.
Then, a big fish jumped out of the water and landed in one of the boats! The boy and the girl were very surprised. The big fish said, "Thank you for counting the boats. I needed a new home." The boy and the girl smiled and waved goodbye to their new fish friend.

One day, it began to rain. The rain was unusual, it was very big and very wet. It rained all day and all night.
In a little house, there was a pipe. The pipe was old and small. The unusual rain made the pipe very full. The pipe started to make a funny sound.
A little boy heard the sound. He looked at the pipe and saw the rain. He knew he had to help. The boy found a big bowl and put it under the pipe. The rain went into the bowl and the pipe was not full anymore. The boy was happy and the house was safe.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a girl with long hair. She was a restless girl, always running and playing. One day, she saw a big tree with many leaves. She wanted to climb it.
She started to climb, but her hair got stuck in the branches. She tried to get free, but she could not. She was sad and scared. She believed she would be stuck there forever.
Then, a kind bird came and helped her. The bird pulled and pulled, and the girl's hair came loose. The girl was happy and free. But when she looked at her hair, it was short now! The girl was surprised, but she liked her new short hair. She thanked the bird and they played together all day.

One day, a boy named Tom went to the beach with his mom and dad. He loved the water and the sand. He saw many fish and shells. He also saw a big, bright dolphin. The dolphin was jumping and playing in the waves. Tom wanted to touch the dolphin. He asked his mom and dad, "Please, can I go to the dolphin?"
His mom and dad said, "No, Tom. The dolphin is not a toy. The dolphin is a wild animal. He may be scared or hurt by you. You have to be kind and respectful to the dolphin. You can watch him from here, but do not go near him."
Tom was sad. He really wanted to go to the dolphin. He thought the dolphin was friendly and fun. He did not listen to his mom and dad. He ran to the water and swam to the dolphin. He tried to grab the dolphin's tail. The dolphin did not like that. He felt Tom's hand and he got angry. He slapped Tom with his tail and made a loud noise. Tom was scared and hurt. He cried and swam back to the shore.
His mom and dad were worried and angry. They hugged Tom and checked if he was okay. They also scolded him for not obeying them. They said, "Tom, you were very naughty and foolish. You did not listen to us. You did not respect the dolphin. You could have been hurt more. You have to learn to follow the rules and be careful with animals. They have feelings and needs too. They are not here for your amusement. They are here to live and be free."
Tom felt sorry and ashamed. He said, "I'm sorry, mom and dad. I'm sorry, dolphin. I was wrong. I will not do that again. I will be good and kind and respectful. I will watch the dolphin from far away and enjoy his beauty and grace. I will not bother him or hurt him. I will be his friend, not his enemy."
His mom and dad smiled and kissed him. They said, "That's good, Tom. We are proud of you. You have learned a valuable lesson today. You have grown a little bit more. We love you, Tom."
The dolphin heard Tom's words and saw his smile. He felt happy and calm. He jumped and played in the waves again. He waved his tail and made a friendly noise. He said, "Thank you, Tom. You are a good boy. I forgive you. I hope you have a nice day. Goodbye, Tom."
Tom waved back and said, "Goodbye, dolphin. Thank you for being so bright and beautiful. You are amazing. I hope to see you again. Goodbye, dolphin."
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to joke and make people laugh. One day, he found a microphone in his toy box. He was very happy and decided to make a show for his family.
Tim called his mom, dad, and little sister to the living room. He said, "I will tell you some jokes with my microphone." His family sat down and Tim started his show. He told many jokes, but some were bad. His family did not laugh at the bad jokes.
Tim felt sad. His dad saw this and said, "It's okay, Tim. We all have bad jokes sometimes. Keep trying and you will get better." Tim smiled and tried again. This time, he told a funny joke and everyone laughed. Tim was happy, and he kept telling jokes with his microphone.

Once upon a time, there was a huge ice cream cone. It was so big that everyone in the town wanted to eat it. But there was a problem. The ice cream cone was on top of a tall hill, and no one could reach it.
One day, a little boy named Tim tried to climb the hill to get the ice cream cone. He tried and tried, but he could not reach the top. Tim was sad because he wanted the ice cream cone so much. Then, he had an idea. He would ask his friend, the magic bird, to help him.
The magic bird tried to help Tim by using its magic to shrink the ice cream cone. But something went wrong. Instead of shrinking the cone, the magic bird made it even bigger! The huge ice cream cone started to melt and it fell down the hill. The town was covered in ice cream, and no one could eat it because it was too messy. Everyone was sad, and the magic bird felt bad for making a mistake.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a clumsy cat named Tom. Today, Tom wanted to play outside. The sun was bright, and it was a dry day. Tom was very happy.
Tom went outside and saw a big ball. He tried to play with the ball, but he was clumsy. He fell down many times. But Tom did not give up. He got up and tried again and again.
At the end of the day, Tom was tired but happy. He had a lot of fun playing with the ball. Even though he was clumsy, he learned that he could still have a good time. And that made today a great day for Tom.

Once upon a time there was an elderly man who had to work every day. He didn't get to play like the other children in his town. One day he asked his boss if he could have the day off, but his boss said no. The elderly man was very sad.
The next day the elderly man went back to work and he got in trouble because he was not following the boss's orders. The boss was very angry and he screamed at the elderly man.
The elderly man got so scared that he started crying. He wanted to go home but the boss said he had to stay and work until the day was done. The elderly man was very sad and he did not have a good time.
At the end of the day the boss said the elderly man could go home. The elderly man was so relieved that he was allowed to leave.
He never went back to work again. Even though the elderly man was sad, he was thankful that he did not have to work anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a big pit. It was full of water. The water was dull and not clear. A little dog named Spot liked to play near the pit. Spot's mom told him to stay away from the pit. But Spot did not listen.
One sunny day, Spot was playing with his ball. He threw the ball too far. The ball went into the pit. Spot was sad. He wanted his ball back. He looked at the pit and thought of a plan.
Spot found a big stick. He thought he could use the stick to get the ball. He put the stick in the water to reach the ball. But the stick was not long enough. Spot leaned in closer to the pit. Suddenly, he fell in! The water soaked Spot. He could not swim. Spot's mom heard him cry and came running. She tried to help him but she could not. The pit was too deep. Spot was gone forever.

Once upon a time, there was a nice little dog named Spot. Spot loved to play in the park with his friends. One day, Spot saw a small flame in the park. He knew it was not good, so he wanted to help.
Spot ran to the flame and tried to heal it with his tiny paws. But something unexpected happened. Instead of healing the flame, Spot's paws started to glow and the flame grew bigger! Spot was scared, but he didn't give up.
Suddenly, a friendly bird saw Spot and flew down to help. The bird flapped its wings and blew the flame away. Spot's paws stopped glowing and he was so happy. The bird and Spot became best friends and played together in the park every day. And from that day on, Spot knew that even when something unexpected happens, friends can always help make things right.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Daisy. Daisy was sad because her toy had broken and she didn't know how to fix it.
Daisy's mom saw her crying and came over to comfort her. 
"Don't worry, Daisy," said her mom. "We can easily find another toy in the cupboard."
Daisy was still a little sad and replied, "My toy was not stupid! I don't want another one!"
Mom understood Daisy's painful feelings and said, "It's okay, Daisy. We can keep the broken toy and I'll find a new one in the cupboard. That way you'll still have both!"
Daisy hugged her mom and smiled. They looked in the cupboard until they found the perfect toy for Daisy. 
And they lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time, there was a funny boy named Tim. He loved to collect jokes from his friends. Every day, he would go to the park and listen to all the jokes his friends told. He would laugh and clap his hands. Tim was very happy when he heard a new joke.
One day, Tim was careless. He forgot his book of jokes at the park. When he got home, he looked everywhere for it, but he could not find it. Tim was very sad. He wanted to laugh and share his jokes with his family.
The next day, Tim went back to the park. He saw his friend, Sally. Sally had found Tim's book of jokes! She gave it back to him. Tim was so happy. He thanked Sally and told her a funny joke. They both laughed and played together. From that day on, Tim was more careful with his book of jokes.

One day, a little girl named Lily went for a walk. She saw a gray cat sitting on a path. The cat looked sad. Lily wanted to help the cat. She thought of using her toy to make the cat happy.
Lily showed the cat her toy. "Do you want to play with my toy?" she asked. The cat looked at the toy and then at Lily. The cat meowed softly. Lily knew the cat wanted to play.
But then, a big dog came running. The dog wanted the toy too. The cat hissed, and the dog barked. They both wanted the toy, but there was only one toy. Lily had an idea.
"Why don't we all play together?" Lily asked. The cat and the dog looked at each other. They stopped hissing and barking. They all played with the toy on the path. The gray cat was happy, the dog was happy, and Lily was happy too.

Once upon a time, there was a brave dog named Spot. Spot loved to observe things. One day, he saw a big cross on the ground. He did not know what it was, but he wanted to find out.
Spot went to his friend, Kitty. "Kitty, I saw a big cross on the ground. Do you know what it is?" Kitty said, "I don't know, but we can look for clues together!" So, Spot and Kitty went back to the cross.
As they got closer, they saw a big box near the cross. They were both very brave and opened the box. Inside, they found lots of yummy treats! Spot and Kitty were so happy that they had found the big cross and the treats. They shared the treats with all their friends and had a big party. And from that day on, Spot and Kitty were known as the bravest friends in the land.

There was a sad barber who had no customers. He was very concerned and did not know what to do. He decided to go for a walk and try to think of a plan.
As he was walking, he saw two children playing with a ball in the park. After watching them for a bit, he decided to ask for help. He asked the children: "Do you know how I could make some customers?".
 
The children thought for a bit and then one of the children said: "Try being kind and make people happy".
The barber thought for a bit and then smiled. He thanked the children for the advice. He opened his shop that same week and started treating his customers very nicely. Soon enough, people started coming to his shop as word spread about his kind nature. 
The barber was no longer sad and his shop was always busy. The moral of the story is that being kind and making others happy will bring a smile to everyone's face.

One day, a creative girl named Lily wanted to perform a show. She decided to do the show at the hospital to make the sick kids happy. She wore a funny hat and danced around.
Lily went to the hospital and saw a sad boy named Tim. She said, "Hi Tim, I'm here to perform for you!" Tim smiled and said, "Okay, I want to see your show!"
Lily danced and told funny jokes. Tim laughed and clapped his hands. Soon, other kids came to watch too. They all had fun and forgot they were in the hospital. Lily was happy that she could make them smile.

Once upon a time, there was a human named Tom. Tom lived in a tiny house. He had a big heart and loved to give things to others. One day, it was very dry outside. Tom saw a sad tree with no leaves. He wanted to help the tree.
Tom had an idea. He took a big cup and filled it with water. He walked to the tree and gave the water to the tree. The tree drank the water and started to feel better. Tom was very happy to see the tree feel better.
But then, something unexpected happened. The tree began to grow very big and tall. It grew so big that it gave shade to Tom's tiny house. Tom was happy because the tree helped him too. The moral of the story is that when you give to others, they can give back to you in surprising ways.

Once upon a time, in a wide, wide park, there was a boy named Tim. Tim was a happy boy who liked to play in the sun. One day, his mom gave him sunglasses to wear when he played outside. Tim did not like the sunglasses. He thought they looked silly.
Tim's mom insisted that he wear the sunglasses to protect his eyes from the sun. Tim did not want to listen to his mom. He left the sunglasses at home and went to play in the wide, wide park with his friends. They played ball and ran around all day.
At the end of the day, Tim's eyes hurt from the bright sun. He wished he had listened to his mom and worn the sunglasses. Tim went home with sore eyes and learned his lesson. Sometimes, it is good to listen to mom, even if you think you know better.

Once upon a time, there was a swan named Sally. Sally loved to swim in the big pond near her home. One day, she met a frog named Freddy. Freddy was very careful when he hopped from one lily pad to another.
"Hi, Sally!" said Freddy. "I like to measure how far I can hop. Can you help me?"
Sally was happy to help. She used her long neck to measure the distance between the lily pads. "One, two, three," she counted as she stretched her neck.
"Wow!" said Freddy. "I can hop three big steps! Thank you, Sally!"
Sally and Freddy became best friends. They played together every day. They were very careful when they measured and played in the pond. And they lived happily ever after.

Lily and Tom are going on a big trip with Mom and Dad. They are very excited to see new things and meet new people. But first, they have to go to a big building where they get their passports.
A passport is a small book with your name and picture on it. It tells other people who you are and where you are from. Mom and Dad have passports, and Lily and Tom need them too.
When they get to the big building, they have to wait in a long line. Lily and Tom are bored and hungry. They want to play and eat. They start to whine and cry.
Mom and Dad try to calm them down. They tell them that they have to be patient and quiet. They say that getting passports is very important and they have to follow the rules. They discuss with them what they will do on the trip and how much fun they will have.
Lily and Tom listen to Mom and Dad. They try to be good and stop whining and crying. They look at the pictures in their books and imagine the places they will see. They smile and hug Mom and Dad.
Soon, it is their turn to get their passports. They go to a nice lady who asks them some questions and takes their pictures. She gives them their passports and says they are ready to go. They are very happy and proud. They have their own passports, just like Mom and Dad.
They thank the nice lady and leave the big building. They go to the car and put their passports in their backpacks. They are not regular books, they are special books. They are their passports to the big trip. They can't wait to start their adventure.

Once upon a time, there was a comet in the sky. The comet was very tired from flying so fast. It wanted to rest for a while. The comet saw a big tree on the ground and thought it would be a nice place to rest. So, the comet came down from the sky and landed on the tree.
The tree was home to a little bird. The bird saw the comet and said, "Hello, comet! Why are you here?" The comet replied, "I am tired and want to rest. Can I stay in your tree for a while?" The bird was happy to have a new friend and said, "Yes, you can rest here."
But the comet was very big and heavy. It took up a lot of space on the tree. The bird's nest got spoiled because of the comet. The bird said, "Oh no, my nest is spoiled! What can we do?" The comet felt sad and said, "I am sorry, little bird. I will help you fix your nest." So, the comet and the bird worked together to fix the spoiled nest. After they were done, they both sat on the tree and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a polite cat. The cat lived in a small house with a girl. One day, the girl lost her fork while eating her food. The cat wanted to help her find it.
The cat looked all around the house to find the fork. He found a toy, a ball, and a hat, but he could not find the fork. Then, the cat had an idea. He could replace the fork with a stick from the yard.
The cat brought the stick to the girl. She tried to eat with the stick, but it was not like a fork. The girl was sad, and the cat felt bad. They never found the fork, and the girl had a hard time eating her food. The cat learned that not all things can be replaced.

One day, a little girl named Lucy wanted to put on a show. She got a stage ready in her room. She wanted it to be perfect. Lucy asked her friend, Tom, to help her.
Lucy said, "Tom, please wipe the stage clean." Tom wiped the stage with a big cloth. Now, the stage was perfect and clean.
Lucy and Tom started their show. They sang and danced on the stage. But then, something unexpected happened. A little dog ran on the stage! The dog danced with them. Lucy, Tom, and the dog had a fun show together.

One day, a little dog named Zip saw a big, scary cat. The cat said, "Zip, follow me. We will have fun." Zip was not sure, but he followed the cat anyway.
As they walked, Zip saw a scary shadow. He was scared, but the cat said, "Don't be scared, Zip. It's just a shadow." Zip felt better and kept following the cat.
Then, something unexpected happened. The big, scary cat led Zip into a dark room. Zip could not see anything. Suddenly, the door closed and Zip was trapped. The cat laughed and said, "Now, you will stay here forever!" Zip was very sad and scared.

One day, Tom and Sue were playing in the park. They were very enthusiastic. They saw a big blanket on the grass. They wanted to have a picnic on the blanket.
Tom said, "Let's eat on the blanket!" Sue agreed and they sat down. They had yummy food like apples, cheese, and bread. They were very happy.
Suddenly, a little bug came to join them. The bug pinched Tom's arm. Tom was surprised and said, "Ouch! That hurt!" The bug said, "I'm sorry, I just wanted to play too." Tom and Sue laughed and let the bug join their picnic. The bug was nice and they all became friends.

Alice was out in the park looking for something fun to do. Suddenly she saw an old, wet statue that she had never seen before. She walked closer and realized the statue had an amazing shape. It was an angel with wings!
"Oh, how magical!" she exclaimed. Then, all of a sudden, the statue spoke.
"Alice, I am made of stone but I need to be healed. Can you help me?"
Alice was very surprised but she was brave.
"Yes! What can I do?" she said.
"Sing to me," said the statue. So Alice sang her favourite song as loud as she could and as she did, the statue slowly healed and became sparkly and dry.
Alice was so happy and thanked the statue. Then the statue smiled and said,
"Thank you, Alice. Now I am healed."
Alice smiled and waved goodbye as she ran off to find more adventures.

One day, a little boy named Tim and his mom went to the park. Tim was very impatient. He wanted to play with his ball right away. His mom said, "Wait, Tim. We need to find a seat first."
They found a seat under a big tree. Tim's mom sat down and said, "Now you can play with your ball, but be careful not to kick it too far." Tim was happy and started to play.
Tim kicked the ball high and far. It went near a small pond. A duck saw the ball and thought it was food. The duck tried to eat the ball, but it was too big. Tim ran to the duck and said, "No, duck! That's my ball!" The duck let go of the ball, and Tim kicked it back to the seat where his mom was. They both laughed and played until it was time to go home.

One day, a famous cat named Kitty and a famous dog named Doggy went for a walk. They were best friends. They liked to talk and play together.
While walking, they saw a bathroom. Doggy said, "I need to go to the bathroom." Kitty said, "Me too!" So they went inside and used the bathroom. They washed their hands and continued their walk.
They started to discuss their favorite games. Kitty said, "I like to chase mice." Doggy said, "I like to fetch sticks." They laughed and played their games. They had lots of fun and were happy friends.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a little girl named Lily. She had a friend named Angel. Angel was a dependable friend, always there for Lily when she needed help.
One sunny day, Lily and Angel were playing in the park. They were having so much fun. They ran, jumped, and laughed together. But then, Lily saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. Angel was scared, but Lily was brave. She started to climb the tree.
Lily climbed very high, but then she got scared. She was too high and didn't know how to get down. She called out, "Angel, help me!" Angel heard her call and came to help. She found a big, strong man to help Lily get down from the tree.
Lily was so happy to be safe on the ground. She hugged Angel and said, "Thank you for being a dependable friend." From that day on, Lily learned to be careful when climbing trees and to always listen to her friend Angel. And they both learned that it's important to help each other when they need it.

One day, a boy named Tim found a magnet. It was a big, strong magnet. He liked to play with it. He would put it on things and watch them stick. Tim thought it was fun.
Tim had a friend named Sally. They liked to play together. Tim showed Sally his magnet. Sally thought it was fun too. They played with the magnet and laughed.
One day, Tim and Sally found a dead bug. Sally was scared. She shouted, "Eww! I don't like it!" Tim said, "Don't worry, I will use my magnet to move it away." Tim put the magnet near the bug and it moved away. Sally was happy and said, "Thank you, Tim!" They went back to playing with the magnet and had a great day.

Once upon a time, there was a blue machine. It was a very big machine. It could talk and think. It liked to wonder about many things.
One day, a little boy named Tim saw the blue machine. He asked, "What do you do, blue machine?" The blue machine said, "I wonder about the world and how it works."
Tim thought that was fun. He and the blue machine wondered together. They wondered about the sun, the moon, and the stars. They wondered about the trees and the animals. They wondered about everything.
As they wondered, they became very good friends. They played and laughed together. And they lived happily ever after, always wondering and learning new things.

Once upon a time, there was a squirrel named Sam. Sam was sad and miserable, because he had no friends. One day, Sam had an idea. He thought if he could count and number all the nuts in the forest, he would be famous, and everyone would want to be his friend.
Sam started to count nuts. He counted one nut, two nuts, three nuts, and so on. He counted and counted, and the other animals in the forest started to watch. They saw Sam working very hard, and they started to help him too. The birds, rabbits, and even the big bear helped Sam count and number the nuts.
As they all worked together, Sam started to feel happy. He was not miserable anymore. The other animals liked Sam, and they became friends. In the end, Sam did not need to count all the nuts to be famous. He just needed to be kind and work with others, and he found many friends in the forest.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She lived in a small house with her mom, dad, and her dog, Max. Lucy loved to play with Max. One day, she found a big box in her room. It was a mystery what was inside.
Lucy called Max to help her open the box. They both pulled and pulled. Inside the box, they found a fierce toy lion. Lucy thought it was very cool. She named the lion Leo. Lucy and Max played with Leo all day long.
The next day, Lucy's mom told her they were going to welcome a new friend to their house. Lucy was very excited. When the doorbell rang, Lucy and Max ran to the door. There stood a boy with a toy lion, just like Leo. His name was Tom. Lucy, Max, and Tom all played together with their toy lions, and they became the best of friends.

Once, there was a little girl who wanted to make music. She had a toy piano and some pots and pans and she banged them together in all sorts of ways. But her mother didn't permit it. She said it was too noisy and very ugly. 
The little girl just wanted to make music, and it made her very sad when her mother said she couldn't. But her mother wouldn't permit it. 
One day, the little girl thought of a plan. She would make music only when her mother wasn't home. So, she waited until her mother had left and then she started to play her lovely tunes. 
Suddenly, her mother came through the door and shouted, "No more music! You haven't been allowed to play, and now you must be punished." The little girl cried, but her mother wouldn't let her make music. It was an ugly end to the little girl's dreams.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to help her mom in the kitchen. One day, her mom was making a big cake. Sue wanted to help, so her mom let her stir the mix. Sue was happy to stir and stir. She felt safe and big like her mom.
Sue's mom told her to say a special word when she stirred. The word was "love". Sue said the word "love" every time she stirred the mix. Sue's mom said the cake would taste good because they put love in it.
When the cake was done, Sue and her mom ate it. It was the best cake they ever had. They knew it was because they put love in it. Sue felt safe and happy with her mom, and they had a great day together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. He loved to jog every day. One day, while jogging, Tom found a wet prune on the ground. He picked it up and looked at it.
Tom saw a little girl crying near a tree. He went to her and asked, "Why are you sad?" The little girl said, "I lost my prune, and I am hungry." Tom smiled and gave her the wet prune he found. The little girl was happy and said, "Thank you, Tom!"
From that day on, Tom and the little girl became good friends. They played and jogged together every day. Tom learned that it is good to help others and share what we have. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Spot. Spot lived in a big house with a nice family. One day, the family went away, and Spot was left alone. He felt sad and missed his family.
Spot decided to go for a walk to find his family. On his walk, he saw a big, unknown dog. The unknown dog was very big and looked a little scary. Spot thought, "Should I trust this dog?" He remembered that his family told him to be careful with new friends.
Spot decided to trust the big, unknown dog. They played together and had lots of fun. The unknown dog knew where Spot's family was, and he took Spot to them. Spot was so happy to see his family again. The family was happy, too, and they all went home together. Spot learned that sometimes, trusting new friends can lead to good things.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Ben. Ben loved to play outside in the sun. One day, he found a small, helpless bug under a rock. The bug was sad and wanted to go home.
Ben wanted to help the bug. He picked it up and asked, "Where is your home?" The bug said, "My home is by the big door." So, Ben hopped and hopped with the bug until they found the big door.
Near the door, the bug showed Ben where to bury him in the soft dirt. Ben gently put the bug in the hole and covered him with dirt. The bug was happy to be home. Ben felt good because he helped the bug. They said goodbye and Ben hopped back to play in the sun.

Once upon a time, there was a bossy cat. The cat loved to tell everyone what to do. One day, the cat saw a little grape on the ground. The cat wanted the grape, but it was too far away. The cat told a dog to get the grape for her. The dog did not want to help the bossy cat.
Then, a big bird came and took the grape. The cat and the dog were very sad. They wanted the grape back. The cat and the dog decided to work together to get the grape from the bird. They made a plan to get the grape.
The cat climbed a tree, and the dog barked at the bird. The bird got scared and dropped the grape. The cat caught the grape and gave it to the dog. The dog was very happy and started to applaud. The cat learned that being bossy was not good. They became friends and shared the grape.

One day, Tom was hungry. He went to the big cold box, the refrigerator. He opened it to look for food. He saw a big cake. Tom was very happy.
Tom's sister, Sue, saw him. Sue said, "No, Tom! That cake is for later. We must wait." Tom was sad. He did not want to wait. He wanted the cake now.
Tom and Sue looked at the cake. They examined it. It was so big and yummy. Tom said, "Please, Sue. Just a small piece?" Sue thought about it. She said, "Okay, just a small piece. We can share it." They both smiled and were happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a big fish named Fin. Fin was a very incredible fish. He loved to dive deep into the water. One day, while diving, he found a network of fish friends.
Fin wanted to play with his new fish friends. They all swam and played together. They had lots of fun diving in and out of the water. The network of fish friends grew bigger and bigger.
Fin and his fish friends were very happy. They had the best time diving and playing together. And they all lived happily ever after in their incredible network of friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to a big building with his mom. They needed to go up to the top floor. Tim saw a wide door and asked his mom, "What is that?" His mom said, "That is an elevator. It can take us up and down the building."
Tim and his mom walked to the elevator. Tim saw a button with an up arrow. He pointed to it and asked, "Do I press this?" His mom said, "Yes, press the button." Tim pressed the button and the elevator door opened. They went inside and Tim pressed another button for the top floor.
As the elevator went up, Tim felt his tummy tickle. He laughed and said, "That feels funny!" When they reached the top floor, the elevator door opened again. Tim and his mom stepped out, and Tim said, "I like the elevator!" His mom smiled and said, "I'm glad you had fun, Tim."
