Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy named Top. Top was Tim's best friend. They played all day, every day. Tim hoped that they would always be friends.
One day, Tim met a girl named Sue. Sue had a pretty doll. Tim liked the doll and wanted to play with it. Top felt jealous. Top didn't want Tim to play with the doll. Top wanted Tim to play with him.
Top decided to do a fun dance to make Tim happy. Tim saw Top's dance and laughed. Tim played with Top again. Sue joined them, and they all played together. Top was happy and not jealous anymore.

One day, a girl named Mia wanted to build a big tower. She wore her favorite skirt and went to play outside. Mia saw a big hill and thought it would be the best place to build her tower.
On the hill, Mia met a boy named Tom. Tom said, "Be careful, Mia. This hill can be dangerous." But Mia didn't listen and started to build her tower. The tower got very tall and looked like it might fall.
Suddenly, the wind blew hard, and the tower began to shake. Mia was scared and called for help. Tom quickly came and helped her hold the tower. They worked together to make the tower safe. In the end, Mia learned that it's important to listen to others and work together to stay safe.

Once upon a time, in a mild and pretty landscape, there was a big tree. Under the tree lived a kind dog named Bow. Bow loved to play with his ball all day.
One day, Bow met a little bird. The bird said, "Bow, can you help me? I lost my nest. Can you find it for me?" Bow nodded and said, "Yes, I will help you, little bird."
Bow and the little bird looked for the nest all day. They asked other animals if they saw it, but no one knew. Then, Bow saw the nest high up in the big tree. Bow jumped and jumped, and finally got the nest. The little bird was so happy and said, "Thank you, Bow!"

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful cat named Lucy. Lucy loved to play with yarn. One day, Lucy found a big ball of yarn in the house. She was very happy and started to play with it.
"Look, Mom!" said Lucy. "I found yarn! Can I play with it?" Mom looked at the yarn and said, "Yes, Lucy, but be careful. That yarn is for my work. I need it later." Lucy promised to be careful and started to play with the yarn.
Lucy played and played with the yarn. She rolled it around the house and had so much fun. But then, something unexpected happened. The yarn got all tangled and messy. Lucy tried to fix it, but she couldn't. When Mom saw the messy yarn, she was sad. "Oh, Lucy, I can't use this yarn for my work now," said Mom. Lucy felt bad and said sorry, but the yarn was still ruined, and Mom could not finish her work.

Once upon a time, there was a big red umbrella. The umbrella was eager to find a friend. One sunny day, the umbrella met a little girl named Lily. Lily loved the umbrella and they became best friends.
One day, Lily said to the umbrella, "I want to marry you, my big red umbrella. We will be together forever." The umbrella was very happy and said, "Yes, Lily, I would love to marry you."
So, Lily and the big red umbrella had a fun wedding. They danced and played together. All their friends came to celebrate with them. From that day on, Lily and the big red umbrella were always together, and they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there was a young girl named Sarah. She was three-years-old and very strong for her age. One day Sarah was playing in the yard with her friends when she saw something sparkly in the junk pile. She went over to take a closer look and it was a big, bright, glass bottle! She knew it was special, but when she tried to pick it up, it broke into little pieces. 
Everyone around her gasped, but Sarah knew she could fix it. She whispered something to herself and then she began to gather the pieces. With her strong hands, she carefully placed each bit of glass and tried to fit them back together. When she was finished, the bottle was almost good as new! Everyone clapped and cheered for Sarah. 
"How did you do that, Sarah?" her friend asked.
Sarah smiled and said, "With strong hands and determination, anything can be fixed."

One day, a girl named Lily wanted to print a big heart for her mom. She went to the printer with a mild smile on her face. Lily was very excited to give her mom the heart.
But, her big brother Tom was not happy. He wanted to use the printer too. Tom and Lily began to fight. They both wanted to print something for their mom.
Lily and Tom pulled the heart picture. They pulled and pulled. The heart ripped in half. Both Lily and Tom were sad. They could not give their mom the heart. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a fragile peach tree. It had green leaves and smooth skin. One day, the tree was feeling very thirsty, so it decided to go for a drink.
The tree found a puddle and started to drink the water. It was so refreshing! All of a sudden, the tree heard a voice. It was a gardener and he had a special surprise!
The gardener sprayed the peach tree with a magical mist. Suddenly, the tree felt so fresh and happy! From that day on, the tree was filled with lots of juicy and delicious peaches.
The peaches were so sweet and fragrant. Everyone in the neighbourhood came to pick them and enjoyed a small, tasty treat. The fragile peach tree had been saved!

Tim and Ben are friends. They like to play outside. One day, they find a big stick. They want to write their names on the ground with the stick.
"Look, I can write Tim!" Tim says. He makes a T, an I and an M.
"Me too, I can write Ben!" Ben says. He makes a B, an E and an N.
They are happy. They write more things. They write cat, dog, sun, moon.
But then, they see a bald man. The bald man is angry. He shouts at them.
"Hey, you little brats! What are you doing? You are ruining my lawn! Go away!"
Tim and Ben are scared. They drop the stick. They run away.
But they run too fast. They do not see the river. They fall into the water. The water is cold and deep. They cannot swim. They cry for help.
But the bald man does not hear them. He is busy fixing his lawn. He does not care.
Tim and Ben drown. They are gone. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a brave little girl named Jenny. She wanted to do something really hard. So she made a plan.
Jenny worked hard to make the plan just right. She practiced and practiced and finally she was ready.
When the day came, Jenny knew she could do it. She was excited and she walked boldly to the place where she would act.
Jenny's goal was to jump across the pond. She stood and took a deep breath, then she leaped as far as she could.
Jenny made it! She jumped with all her might and made it safely across! All the people around her cheered in joy. 
Jenny had achieved her hard goal! She was proud that she had made it.

One day, a nice girl named Lily got an invitation to a party. She was very happy. She put on her pretty dress and went to the party.
At the party, Lily saw her friend Tom. Tom said, "Hi Lily! Let's play catch!" They played catch with a big, round ball. They laughed and had fun.
Then, Lily tried to catch the ball but she did not see a toy on the floor. She tripped on the toy and started to fall. Tom quickly caught her and saved her from falling. Lily said, "Thank you, Tom!" They smiled and continued to play at the party.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a boy named Tom. Tom had a toy razor that he liked to play with. He pretended to shave like his dad. It made him feel comfortable and happy.
One day, Tom's friend Sam came to play. Sam saw the toy razor and wanted to play with it too. But Tom didn't want to share. They started to quarrel over the toy razor. Both boys were not happy.
Tom's mom came to see what was wrong. She told the boys that friends should share and not quarrel. Tom and Sam listened to her. They took turns playing with the toy razor. Soon, they were both comfortable and happy again.

One day, a little girl named Sally found a big coat with a zipper. Sally liked to play with the zipper. She would zip it up and down, up and down. She was very happy.
Sally's friend, Timmy, saw her playing with the zipper. Timmy was a bit ignorant and did not know what a zipper was. He wanted to tease Sally. So, he said, "Sally, that thing you play with is silly!"
Sally looked at Timmy and said, "No, Timmy, this is a zipper. It is fun to play with. You can try too!" Timmy tried to play with the zipper, and he liked it. They played together, and they both were happy.

One day, a helpful dog named Spot heard a sound. It was a little bird. The bird was sad because it could not find its mom. Spot wanted to help the bird find its mom.
Spot and the bird went to the park. They saw a big tree. "Is your mom in this tree?" asked Spot. The bird looked and said, "No, my mom is not here." They kept looking for the bird's mom.
Spot and the bird came to a pond. They saw ducks and asked, "Do you know where the bird's mom is?" The ducks said, "Yes, she is by the red flowers." Spot and the bird had to choose which way to go. They went to the red flowers and found the bird's mom. The bird was very happy and thanked Spot for being so helpful.

Once upon a time, there was a little brown dog named Buddy. Buddy loved to play with his rag. He would bite it, tug it, and carry it everywhere. One day, Buddy lost his rag. He looked and looked, but could not find it.
Buddy asked his friend, a cat named Whiskers, "Have you seen my rag?" Whiskers said, "No, but let's look together." So, they looked high and low for the brown rag. Buddy felt sad and worried that he would never find his favorite toy.
As they were looking, they heard a tiny squeak. They followed the sound and found a little mouse holding Buddy's brown rag. The mouse said, "I found this rag and thought it was lost. I am sorry." Buddy was so happy to have his rag back. He thanked the mouse and shared his rag with his new friend. From that day on, Buddy, Whiskers, and the mouse played together with the brown rag, and they all became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful little girl named Amy. Amy loved to wake up early every day. She liked to help her mom and dad in the garden. They grew many things like flowers, tomatoes, and squash.
One day, a big squash grew in the garden. It was so big that Amy could not lift it alone. She asked her mom and dad for help. They all worked together to pick the big squash. Amy was very happy because she was helpful.
They took the big squash to the kitchen. Mom and dad cut the squash and made a yummy soup. Amy helped by stirring the soup. They all ate the soup together. It was the best squash soup they ever had.

One day, a little boy named Tim went outside to play. The sky was big and blue. Tim saw his friend, the hard-working ant. The ant was carrying a big leaf. Tim said hello to the ant and asked if he could help.
The ant nodded and told Tim to follow him. They went to a big hill where many ants were working hard. Tim helped the ants by picking up leaves and sticks. The ants were happy and thanked Tim for his help.
Tim looked up at the sky and saw a big, white cloud. He waved goodbye to his ant friends and ran back home. He told his mom about his day and how he helped the ants. His mom smiled and gave him a big hug.

Once upon a time, there were two little brothers who loved to play together. One day, they were jumping in the living room when their mom said, "Boys, why don't you go outside and play!"
So they ran outside and had lots of fun. They saw a big fan in the garden, and they ran to it. One of the brothers said, "Let's see if it works!" So he pushed the button and the fan spun around so fast. It scared them!
But then they started to see the fan in a different way. They thought it was playful and began to jump around it. Soon they were laughing and having a good time, while the fan kept spinning around them.
They played together for hours, until their mom called them inside for dinner. They were sad. But they knew that the fan was always there, and they would play with it again soon!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a big, expensive toy car. He loved his car very much and took care of it every day. One day, Tim was playing with his car in the living room. He saw a newspaper on the floor. Tim thought it would be fun to make a road for his car with the newspaper.
Tim's mom saw him playing with the newspaper. She said, "Tim, be careful with the newspaper. It is not a toy. We need it to read." Tim listened to his mom and put the newspaper back on the table.
Later that day, Tim's dad came home from work. He saw the newspaper on the table and said, "Thank you, Tim, for taking care of the newspaper." Tim smiled and went back to playing with his expensive toy car, knowing that he had listened to his mom and helped his dad.

One day, a boy named Tom found a razor. He knew it was a bad thing to play with. So, he went to his mom and showed her the razor. "Mom, I found this. I know it is bad. What should I do?" Tom asked.
His mom said, "Good job, Tom! You are right. It is bad to play with a razor. Let's put it away where it is safe." They went to the bathroom and put the razor high up on a shelf.
Later, Tom and his mom decided to make a cake. They had to mix the ingredients together. Tom loved to mix. He mixed and mixed until the batter was smooth. They put the cake in the oven and waited.
When the cake was done, they took it out and let it cool. Tom and his mom were happy they made a cake together. They were also happy that Tom knew not to play with the bad razor. They enjoyed their cake and had a great day.

Once upon a time, there was a cute little dog named Spot. Spot liked to run and play all day. One day, Spot saw a big ice block. Spot thought it would be fun to play with the ice block, so he ran and jumped on it.
Spot started to slide on the ice block, but he could not stop. He went very fast and started to feel scared. Spot's mom saw him and yelled, "Freeze!" Spot tried to stop, but he just slid faster and faster. His mom ran after him, trying to catch him before something bad happened.
Spot's mom got close, but just as she reached out her shoulder to grab him, Spot slid off the ice block and hit a tree. Spot was hurt, and his mom was sad. She wished she could have caught him in time. From that day on, Spot never played on ice blocks again.

Once upon a time there was a young boy who loved collecting stamps from all around the world. He was always looking for new stamps to add to his collection. One day he was walking home from school, and he noticed an old looking stamp on the sidewalk. He picked it up, excited to add it to his collection.
The young boy ran home and showed the stamp to his mom. He told her: "I found this new stamp. I know it's a rare one." His mom smiled and said: "That's wonderful. Let me take a look." She carefully studied the stamp and said: "You know, I think this stamp is more valuable than you think. I'm certain you'll get a great deal of money if you sell it."
The young boy's eyes lit up. In his mind, he was already planning what he would do with the money. He didn't know it, but his mom could tell that this stamp was very special. He had no idea what this stamp was worth. 
The young boy was delighted when he sold the old stamp, and he was able to buy some new things with the money. But what he didn't know was that the stamp was actually a very rare, valuable stamp, and he could have gotten a lot more money if he had sold it to the right person. 
The young boy never knew the true value of the old stamp and was never able to reap the full rewards.

One day, a boy named Tom found a wallet on the ground. It was a dull, brown wallet. Tom frowned because the wallet was not his. He wanted to find the person who lost it.
Tom went to his friend, Lily, to ask for help. Lily said, "Let's look for clues!" They opened the wallet and found a picture of a dog. They knew the dog in the picture. It was their friend, Sam's dog!
They went to Sam's house and showed him the wallet. Sam was happy to see it. But then, Sam frowned too. He said, "This is not my wallet, but the dog in the picture is mine!" They all laughed because they did not expect that. In the end, they kept looking for the wallet's owner together.

Once upon a time, there was a humble duck. This duck liked to swim in the pond. The duck had many friends who liked to play with him.
One day, the duck and his friends wanted to play a new game. They all tried to think of a fun game to play. The duck had an idea. He said, "Let's settle down and play hide and seek!"
All the friends thought it was a great idea. They all took turns counting while the others hid. The duck found a big bush to hide behind. He was very good at hiding.
When it was the duck's turn to count, he found all of his friends quickly. They all laughed and played hide and seek until the sun went down. The humble duck and his friends had a very fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to skip. She skipped all day long. One day, Lily went to a big building with her mom. They needed to go up to the top floor.
Lily saw an elevator and said, "Mom, can we use the elevator?" Her mom said, "Yes, but it is very slow and makes a terrible noise." Lily didn't care. She just wanted to ride the elevator.
They got in the elevator and started to go up. The elevator made a terrible noise, just like her mom said. But Lily didn't mind. She started to skip inside the elevator. Her mom laughed and said, "Lily, you make the elevator ride fun!" When they got to the top floor, Lily and her mom left the elevator, still smiling and skipping.

One day, a brown dog named Max went for a walk at sunrise. The sky was pink and pretty. Max liked walking in the park. He saw a big tree and wanted to put his ball under it.
Max saw a little girl named Lily by the tree. She was sad. "Why are you sad?" asked Max. "I lost my toy," said Lily. Max wanted to help her find her toy.
Max and Lily looked and looked for the toy. They did not find it. Then, something unexpected happened. Max found his ball under the tree! It was not his ball he put there, but Lily's toy! Lily was so happy. They played together and became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a bald man named Tom. He liked to design things. He had many ideas in his head. Tom wanted to make a model of his favorite animal, a cat.
Tom got some clay and started to design the cat. He made a big round ball for the body and a smaller round ball for the head. He added two pointy ears, four legs, and a long tail. He was happy with his model cat.
One day, Tom showed his model cat to his friends. They liked it very much. They asked Tom to design more animals for them. Tom was happy to help his friends and made many more model animals. Everyone loved Tom's designs and he became very popular in his town.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to play outside. She was walking and saw a big scary tree. Lily was scared but wanted to be brave. She took a deep breath and said, "I will not be scared."
Lily started to sing a happy song to make her feel brave. She sang, "I am brave, I am strong, I will not be scared for long." As she sang, a friendly bird came and sat on a branch near her. The bird liked her song and started to sing with her.
Lily and the bird sang together, and the scary tree didn't seem so scary anymore. The bird said, "Thank you for your song, it made me happy." Lily smiled and said, "Thank you for singing with me, now the tree is not scary." They became friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a little boy named Tim. Tim was a very sorry boy because he had a big problem. He did not know how to tie his shoes. Every day, he tried and tried, but he could not solve it.
One day, Tim met a wise old man. The old man saw Tim's sad face and asked, "Why are you so sorry, little boy?" Tim told him about his problem. The wise old man said, "Do not worry. I will help you. Let us try together for one week."
So, every day for one week, Tim and the wise old man practiced tying shoes. They tried and tried, and finally, Tim could tie his shoes! Tim was so happy, and he said, "Thank you, wise old man! You helped me solve my problem!"
The wise old man smiled and said, "You are welcome, Tim. Remember, if you have a problem, do not be afraid to ask for help. Together, we can solve anything."
From that day on, Tim was never afraid to ask for help. He learned that with patience and teamwork, any problem can be solved. And he was never a sorry boy again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She liked to play outside and find hidden things. One day, while playing in the park, she saw a small box. Sue was very curious and wanted to open it.
Sue asked her friend, Tom, "Should I open the box?" Tom said, "I don't know. Maybe it's not safe." But Sue really wanted to see what was inside. So, she opened the box anyway.
Inside the box, there were lots of bugs! The bugs came out and started to run all around. Sue and Tom were scared and cried. They ran away from the bugs, but the bugs followed them. The day was not fun anymore, and Sue wished she had not opened the box.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved the color pink. She had pink clothes, pink toys, and even a pink room. One day, Lily's mom told her they were going to have a party to celebrate her birthday.
Lily was so excited. She wanted to have a pink party. She asked her mom to get pink balloons, pink cake, and pink party hats. Her mom said yes, and they started to get ready for the big day.
On the day of the party, Lily's friends came over. They all wore pink clothes and played with pink toys. They ate the pink cake and laughed a lot. One of Lily's friends put a party hat on their dog. The dog looked a little stupid, but everyone thought it was funny. They all had a great time celebrating Lily's pink birthday party.

Jill and Ben were friends who liked to play with dolls. They had a big group of dolls that they dressed up in fancy clothes and hats. They pretended that the dolls were going to a party or a picnic or a wedding.
One day, Jill and Ben went to the park with their dolls. They found a nice spot under a tree and set up their dolls on a blanket. They were having fun, until they saw a big dog running towards them. The dog was barking and wagging its tail. It wanted to play, too.
But the dog did not know how to play with dolls. It jumped on the blanket and knocked over the dolls. It grabbed some of the dolls in its mouth and shook them hard. It tore off their fancy clothes and hats and made them dirty. It spoiled the dolls and the game.
Jill and Ben were very sad and angry. They cried and shouted at the dog. They tried to get their dolls back, but the dog ran away with some of them. They had to leave the park with their ruined dolls. They did not want to play with dolls anymore. They did not want to be friends anymore. The dog spoiled everything.

One day, a boy named Tim found a big waffle on the table. He thought it was boring, but he was hungry. So, he ate it. As he ate the waffle, he heard a noise. It was a tiny voice saying, "Please, don't eat me!"
Tim looked around and saw a little man on the table. The man said, "I can help you unlock a fun game if you don't eat me." Tim thought about it and said, "Okay, I won't eat you."
The little man led Tim to a door he had never seen before. "Unlock the door," said the man. Tim did, and inside was a room full of toys and games. Tim played all day and was very happy. He learned that even boring things can lead to fun surprises.

One day, a girl named Sally went for a ride on her bike. She liked to listen to her small radio while she rode. Sally was a very silly girl who loved to make her friends laugh. Today, she was going to meet her friends at the park.
On the way to the park, Sally saw a big box. She thought it would be funny to put the box on her head and ride her bike. She knew her friends would laugh when they saw her being so silly.
But as she rode with the box on her head, she couldn't see where she was going. Suddenly, she heard her radio make a loud noise. It was telling her that there was a surprise party at the park for her! Sally was so excited that she forgot about the box on her head and kept riding.
When Sally got to the park, she saw all her friends waiting for her. They were all laughing and clapping because they thought the box on her head was part of the surprise. Sally laughed too, and they all had a great time at her surprise party. The big box on her head had made the party even more fun and silly than she could have ever planned.

Once upon a time, there was a brilliant pirate named Jack. Jack had a big hat and a red shirt. He loved to go on his ship and look for treasure.
One day, Jack met a little girl named Lucy. Lucy wanted to go on an adventure with Jack. She asked, "Can I go with you, Jack?" Jack said, "Yes, let's go find treasure together!"
They went on the ship and sailed the sea. They saw fish and birds. Jack and Lucy found a treasure chest on an island. They were very happy and went back home. They told everyone about their adventure and shared the treasure.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved to imagine and play with her toys. One day, she was playing with her toy kitchen and decided to make some toast for her teddy bear, Mr. Snuggles.
"Mr. Snuggles, would you like some toast?" Lucy asked. Mr. Snuggles didn't say anything, but Lucy knew he wanted some. She put the pretend bread in the toy toaster and pushed the button. When the toast popped up, she put it on a little plate.
Lucy wanted to be generous, so she put lots of pretend butter on the toast. "Here you go, Mr. Snuggles," she said, giving him the plate. Lucy and Mr. Snuggles had a fun tea party with their toast and pretend tea.
After they finished eating, Lucy and Mr. Snuggles played more games. They went on a big adventure in the backyard, imagining they were in a magical forest. They had lots of fun together, and Lucy was always kind and generous to her friend Mr. Snuggles.
At the end of the day, Lucy gave Mr. Snuggles a big hug and said, "I love you, Mr. Snuggles. We had so much fun today!" And they both went to sleep, dreaming of more adventures and fun times together.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called May. May was very tired and wanted a nap.
So she grabbed her teddy bear and went outside. She found a pretty stream at the side of the forest. May was happy and started to play in the stream. The water was cold and it made May shiver.
Then May got more and more tired. She slowly put her head down to try and get some rest. But then she heard a noise, it was a big bear!
May got scared and shivered even more. She held onto her teddy bear tight and quickly ran away from the stream. She ran home and was very happy to be safe.
May was so tired that she went straight to bed. She snuggled up with her teddy bear and soon fell asleep.

One day, a little girl named Lucy was playing in her room. She was very happy. She looked up and saw a big bug on the ceiling. Lucy did not like bugs. She was scared and ran to her mom.
"Mommy, there is a big bug on the ceiling! Please help me!" Lucy cried. Her mom came to the room and saw the bug. She knew what to do.
Mom took a big cup and put it over the bug. Then, she slid a paper under the cup and took the bug outside. Lucy was so happy that the bug was gone. She gave her mom a big hug.
"Thank you, Mommy! You are the best!" Lucy said. Her mom smiled and gave her a glass of water. "Here, drink this. It will help you feel better." Lucy drank the water and felt much better.
From that day on, Lucy was not scared of bugs anymore. She knew her mom would always be there to help her. They were both very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a playful mosquito. He liked to fly around in the sun. He had many friends to play with.
One day, the mosquito wanted to get a new toy. He asked his friends to help him find one. They all looked around the big tree.
By the tree, they found a pretty leaf. The mosquito was very happy. He played with the leaf all day long with his friends. And they all had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to help her mom in the kitchen. One day, Lily's mom asked her to balance a big plate of food to the table. Lily was so excited that she could help her mom.
As Lily walked to the table, she tried her best to keep the food balanced on the plate. But then, something unexpected happened. A tiny mouse ran across the floor, and Lily got scared. She tripped and the food fell off the plate. Lily felt so embarrassed.
But then, the mouse picked up a piece of food and brought it back to Lily. She realized the mouse was just hungry and wanted to share the food with her. Lily wasn't embarrassed anymore. She and the mouse became friends, and they all ate the food together.

Once upon a time, there was a compassionate girl named Lily. She had a magic microphone that could make things grow. Lily loved to sing and help her friends.
One day, Lily's friend, Sam, had a small flower that needed to grow. Lily took her magic microphone and started to sing a happy song. As she sang, the flower began to grow bigger and bigger. Sam was so happy to see his flower grow.
Lily felt good about helping her friend. She knew that her magic microphone was special. Lily promised to keep using her microphone to help others and make the world a better place.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play outside. One day, she found a cheap toy in the park. It was a shiny chain. Sue was very happy.
Sue wanted to hang the chain on a tree. She found a big tree with strong branches. She climbed up and hung the chain on a branch. The chain sparkled in the sun. Sue was proud of her new toy.
Every day, Sue would visit the tree and play with the chain. She would swing it, make it dance, and pretend it was a magic necklace. Sue and the chain became best friends. They had lots of fun together. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She had a small, cute dog. The dog's name was Fluffy. Amy loved Fluffy very much. They played together all the time.
One day, Fluffy was naughty. He took Amy's toy and ran away. Amy was sad and cried. She called for Fluffy, but he did not come back. Amy looked for Fluffy everywhere.
Finally, she found Fluffy under a big tree. He looked sorry for being naughty. Amy hugged Fluffy to comfort him. They went back home together, happy again. And Fluffy never took Amy's toys again.

Once upon a time, there was a little rabbit. The rabbit was sad because he was lost. He could not recognize where he was. He wanted to find his way back home.
One day, the little rabbit met a big, terrible wolf. The wolf wanted to eat the rabbit. The rabbit was very scared. But then, something unexpected happened. The wolf did not eat the rabbit. Instead, he became friends with the rabbit.
The terrible wolf helped the little rabbit find his way back home. They became best friends and played together every day. And the little rabbit was not sad anymore.

One day, a nosy cat named Tom wanted to know what was inside a big building. His friend, a dog named Max, told him, "That is a hospital. People go there when they are sick or hurt."
Tom was curious and went inside. He saw a girl with a hurt leg. The girl said, "My mom sent me here because I fell and hurt my leg." Tom felt sad for the girl.
Max came in and said, "Tom, we should not be nosy. We should help people when they need it." So, Tom and Max decided to help the girl by making her laugh and feel better. They learned that it is better to help others than to be nosy.

Once upon a time, there was a hairy bear who liked to explore. He went on a journey one day, deep underground. He was so excited to see what he could find. When he came to a cave, he heard a strange sound. It was a voice!
The voice said, "Bear, why are you here? I am the king of this cave and you need to fold if you want to stay." The bear shook his head. He did not know what to do. He had never heard of "folding".
The voice said, "If you can figure out how to fold, then I will let you stay. Otherwise, you must go." The bear thought really hard. Suddenly, he remembered how to fold his big, hairy arms. He quickly started folding them up and down. 
The voice said, "Good job! You can stay in my cave as long as you like." The bear cheered. He was so excited to explore this underground cave. He could not wait to see what other surprises he could find.

Once upon a time, there was a warm day. On this day, a boy named Tim wanted to organize his toys. He had many vehicles, like cars and trucks. He put them all in a big box. Tim felt happy because his room was now clean.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came through the window. It blew the box away! Tim's vehicles were all over the floor. He was sad because he had just organized them.
But then, Tim saw something special. One of his toy vehicles started to move on its own! It was a magic car! The magic car helped Tim pick up all the other vehicles. They put them back in the box together. Tim was happy again and now had a new friend, the magic car.

One day, a modest seal named Sam lived by the sea. Sam was very good at ordering fish from his fish friends. He would say, "Fish friends, please bring me some food." The fish friends would always listen and bring Sam his food.
One day, Sam met a big fish named Billy. Billy told Sam, "I can help you order even more fish to eat." Sam was happy and said, "Yes, please help me, Billy." But Sam did not know that Billy was not a nice fish.
The next day, Sam tried to order fish from his fish friends. But they did not come. Sam was very sad and hungry. Then, Billy came and said, "I can bring you fish, but you have to give me your shiny rock." Sam did not want to give away his shiny rock, but he was very hungry.
Sam gave Billy his shiny rock, and Billy brought him some fish. Sam learned that it is important to be careful who you trust. And from that day on, Sam only ordered fish from his true fish friends.

One day, a red bird named Tim wanted to go to the museum. He asked his mom, "Can we go to the museum today?" His mom said, "Yes, let's go!"
At the museum, Tim saw many things. He saw big animals, small animals, and colorful paintings. Then, he saw a big red button with the word "raise" on it. Tim was very curious and wanted to know what the button did.
Tim pressed the button and something unexpected happened! The floor started to raise up, taking Tim and his mom higher and higher. They were scared but also excited. They looked down and saw the whole museum from above. When they got back down, they laughed and had a great day at the museum.

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and sing happy songs. One day, Lily went to the kitchen to get some cookies. She saw the stove and wanted to touch it.
Her mommy had told her not to touch the stove, but Lily wanted to ignore her mommy's words. She thought it would be fun to touch the stove, but she did not know it could be hot and hurt her.
Just then, her mommy walked into the kitchen and saw Lily near the stove. She picked her up and moved her away from the stove. Lily's mommy told her again not to touch the stove because it could be hot. Lily listened to her mommy and promised not to ignore her words again. They both went to the living room and played with toys, feeling happy and safe.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sadie. She was three years old and very special. One day, she was playing in the park with her mom. 
Sadie wanted to climb a tree but her mom didn't think it was a good idea. "You're too young, Sadie," her mom said. 
Sadie disagreed. "I'm a big girl!"
Her mom smiled. "I know, but you'll need to be a bit older to climb that tree. I'll buy you some lotion if you stay away from the tree."
Sadie thought about it. She really wanted the lotion. It smelled so good!
In the end, Sadie decided to listen to her mom and stay away from the tree. Her mom was still proud of Sadie for how good she was. 
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in her mom's room. One day, she saw a pretty bottle. It was her mom's perfume.
Lily wanted to smell the perfume, so she tried to get it. But she was too small to reach it. She climbed on a chair, but it was very messy. There were toys and clothes everywhere.
Lily fell off the chair and the perfume bottle broke. The perfume spilled all over the floor. Lily started to cry. Her mom came in and saw the mess. She was not happy. The end.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to swing and slide and run. They saw a big tree with many leaves. They wanted to see what was behind the tree.
They walked around the tree and saw a small house. It was old and dirty. It had a broken door and a window with no glass. They wondered who lived there.
"Hello?" Lily called. "Is anyone home?"
There was no answer. Lily and Ben went closer to the house. They heard a loud noise. It sounded like a growl.
"Roar!" A big dog jumped out of the window. It had black fur and sharp teeth. It looked angry and hungry.
Lily and Ben were scared. They screamed and ran away. The dog chased them. It barked and snarled.
"Help!" Lily and Ben cried. "There is a bad dog!"
A man heard them. He was walking his own dog. His dog was small and white. It had a red collar and a leash.
"Hey, stop!" The man shouted. "That's my dog! He's not bad, he's just lonely. He likes to surprise people."
The man caught his dog and held him. He said sorry to Lily and Ben. He told them his dog's name was Rex. He said he lived in the old house. He said he was fixing it.
Lily and Ben stopped running. They looked at the man and his dog. They were not angry anymore. They were curious.
"Can we pet your dog?" Lily asked.
"Sure, he's friendly. He just wants to play." The man said.
Lily and Ben went closer to Rex. They touched his fur. He licked their hands. He wagged his tail.
Lily and Ben smiled. They were not scared anymore. They were happy.
They made a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a yellow duck. The duck lived in a pond. The pond was near a big tree. The duck loved to splash in the water all day long.
One day, the yellow duck found a soft velvet cloth near the pond. The duck didn't know what it was, but it felt nice. The duck decided to make the velvet cloth its new bed.
Every day, the yellow duck would splash in the pond and then rest on the soft velvet bed. The duck was very happy. The end.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a grumpy bear named Bobo. Bobo was always grumpy because he thought no one liked him. One sunny day, Bobo saw a line of ants marching on the ground. He watched them as they marched, and he felt a little less grumpy.
Bobo decided to follow the ants. Along the way, he met a friendly squirrel named Squeaky. Squeaky gave Bobo a big wink and said, "Hi, Bobo! Let's play together!" Bobo was surprised but happy. They played and laughed all day long, and Bobo forgot to be grumpy.
Soon, more animals joined them. They all played together, and Bobo made many new friends. He learned that being grumpy didn't make him happy, but having friends and being kind did. And from that day on, Bobo was never grumpy again.

Sam and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big sign. The sign says: "East Fair. Fun and games for all!"
"Let's go to the fair!" Sam says. "Maybe we can ride the horses or eat some candy."
"OK!" Mia says. "But we have to ask our moms first."
They run to their moms, who are sitting on a bench. They ask if they can go to the fair. Their moms say yes, but they have to stay together and be careful.
Sam and Mia are happy. They hold hands and walk to the fair. They see many people and colors and sounds. They smell popcorn and cotton candy. They hear music and laughter.
They go to a booth that sells lemonade. They are thirsty. They each get a cup and drink. But the lemonade is not sweet. It is bitter. They make a face and spit it out.
"Yuck!" Sam says. "This lemonade is bad. It tastes like soap."
"I don't like it either," Mia says. "It hurts my tongue."
They throw away their cups and look for something else to drink. They see a man with a big jug. He says he has apple juice. They think apple juice is good. They ask for some.
The man smiles and pours them some juice. They take a sip. But the juice is not good. It is bitter too. They make another face and spit it out.
"Yuck!" Mia says. "This juice is worse than the lemonade. It tastes like dirt."
"I agree," Sam says. "It is awful. Why is everything so bitter here?"
They look at the man. He is not smiling anymore. He is angry. He says: "You are rude kids. You don't appreciate my drinks. They are not bitter. They are sour. Sour is good. Sour is east. East is the best. You are west. West is the worst."
Sam and Mia are confused. They don't understand what the man is saying. They don't know what east and west are. They only know that they don't like his drinks. They are scared of him. They run away from him.
They find their moms and tell them what happened. Their moms hug them and say they are sorry. They say they will take them to another fair. A fair that has sweet drinks and nice people. A fair that is not east or west. A fair that is just fun.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a rich youth named Tom. He had a big house and many toys. He loved to play all day.
One day, Tom went outside to look for his friends. He walked around the town and saw a small park. There, he found a little girl playing alone. She was not rich like Tom, but she was happy.
Tom decided to share his toys with the little girl. They played together and had a lot of fun. After that day, Tom and the little girl became best friends. They learned that being rich in friendship is more important than having many toys.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to ride his red bike. One day, it rained and the street was wet. Tim wanted to ride his bike, but Mom said, "No, Tim. It's too wet outside."
Tim was sad, but he waited for the sun to come out. When the sun came out, the street was still wet. Tim asked his mom, "Can I ride now?" Mom said, "Yes, but be careful."
So, Tim went outside and rode his bike on the wet street. The water splashed as he rode. Tim laughed and had fun. When he was done, he went back inside and told Mom all about his ride. Mom was happy that Tim had fun, but she reminded him to always be careful.

Once upon a time there was a beautiful barrel. All the animals in the forest wanted to play with it. But none of them dared to go near it.
One day a brave little bunny hopped up to the barrel. He was very curious. He hopped closer and closer until he reached the barrel. Then he carefully poked his nose inside. He was so excited!
But just then a loud noise scared the little bunny. He jumped back. A little bear had come up to the barrel too. The bear wanted to play but he was too scared to do it alone. He was so happy to see the bunny.
Together they dared each other to go inside the barrel. The bear was the first one to do it. He looked so silly! After a few moments, the bunny got brave enough and dared to go inside the barrel too. It was so much fun playing in there.
Eventually the animals came out of the beautiful barrel, tired but very happy. It was the best game ever.

One day, a clever little bird named Tim was in his nest. He felt the wind blowing on his face. He saw the leaves dance in the wind. Tim liked the wind. It made him happy.
Tim started to wonder what was far away. He wanted to see new things. So, he spread his wings and flew away. The wind helped him fly fast. Tim saw big trees, a river, and many new friends.
Tim met a big, kind bear. The bear and Tim played all day. They had so much fun. When the sun went down, Tim said goodbye to his new friend. He flew back to his nest, happy and tired.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play with books and toys. One day, they find a big book with many letters and pictures. They want to learn new words.
"Look, Tom, this is a cat. C-A-T. Cat." Lily says, pointing to a picture of a furry animal.
"Wow, Lily, you can spell! You are so smart." Tom says, clapping his hands.
"Thank you, Tom. You can spell too. Try this one. This is a lemon. L-E-M-O-N. Lemon." Lily says, showing him a yellow fruit.
Tom tries to say the letters. "L-E-M-O-N. Lemon." He repeats, smiling.
"Good job, Tom. You did it." Lily says, clapping again.
They take turns to spell more words, like dog, sun, and hat. They have fun and learn a lot.
But then, they see a word they do not know. It is B-I-T-T-E-R. Bitter.
"What is bitter, Lily?" Tom asks, curious.
"I don't know, Tom. Maybe it is something good. Like sweet or soft." Lily guesses.
They look at the picture next to the word. It is a lemon again. But this time, it has a face. And the face is not happy. It is making a sour face.
"Maybe we can try it. Maybe it is not bad." Tom says, brave.
He picks up a lemon from the table. He cuts it in half. He gives one half to Lily and keeps one for himself.
They both take a bite of the lemon. They make the same sour face as the picture. They spit out the lemon. They do not like it. It is not good. It is bitter.
"Yuck, yuck, yuck. Bitter is bad. Bitter is not good. Bitter is yucky." They say, rubbing their tongues.
They throw away the lemon. They do not want to spell bitter anymore. They want to spell something else. Something nice. Like cake. C-A-K-E. Cake. They clap and smile. They are friends. They like to play with books and toys.

Once upon a time, there was a humble dog named Spot. Spot was a good dog who always helped his friends. One day, Spot's friend, Cat, was very sad. Cat's fur was dirty and tangled. Spot wanted to help Cat feel better.
Spot said, "I can help you, Cat. Let's include shampoo to wash your fur." Cat agreed, and they went to find shampoo. They found a big bottle of shampoo and went to the river. Spot and Cat took turns washing each other's fur with the shampoo. Soon, Cat's fur was clean and shiny.
Cat was very happy and said, "Thank you, Spot. You are a good friend." Spot was happy too because he could help his friend. The moral of the story is that helping others can make you feel good too.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia had a big box of stamps. She loved to play with them. One day, Mia felt troubled. She could not find the match for her favorite stamp.
Mia asked her friend, Tom, "Can you help me find the match for my stamp?" Tom said, "Yes, I can help you." They looked and looked, but they could not find the match.
Then Mia's mom came and said, "Why don't you share your stamps with Tom? Maybe you will find the match together." So, Mia and Tom shared the stamps and played together. Soon, they found the match for Mia's favorite stamp.
Mia and Tom were so happy. They learned that sharing and helping each other can solve problems. And they played with the stamps all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a playful cat named Lily. She loved to crawl under the table and chase her ball. One day, while playing, she found a shiny gem. The gem was red and sparkly. Lily was so happy to find it.
She wanted to show her friend, Max the dog. Max was big and kind. Lily crawled to him with the gem. Max wagged his tail and barked. He was happy to see the gem too. They decided to play with the gem together.
They played with the gem all day. They would take turns, Lily would crawl and bat the gem, and Max would chase it. They had so much fun. When the sun went down, they were tired. Lily and Max curled up together and slept, dreaming of more playful days with their shiny gem.

Tom and Lily liked to play soccer with their friends. They had a big ball and a small goal. They went to the park every day after school. Sometimes they saw other kids playing soccer too. They wanted to play with them, but they were too shy.
One day, they saw a big sign near the park. It said: "Soccer Tournament. Come and join. Win a ticket to the stadium." Tom and Lily were very excited. They wanted to see the stadium. It was where the best soccer players played. They decided to join the tournament.
They practiced hard with their friends. They learned how to pass, shoot, and defend. They were ready for the tournament. They wore their red shirts and their blue shorts. They went to the park early. They saw many other teams. They were nervous, but also happy.
They played well in the first game. They scored two goals and won. They played well in the second game too. They scored three goals and won. They made it to the final. They were very proud. They saw their parents cheering for them. They smiled.
They played against a team with yellow shirts and black shorts. They were bigger and faster. They scored a goal in the first minute. Tom and Lily did not give up. They tried to score too. But they made a careless mistake. They lost the ball near their goal. The yellow team scored again. It was 2-0.
Tom and Lily felt sad. They thought they would lose. They wanted to cry. But then they saw their friends. They saw their parents. They saw the sign. They remembered why they played. They played for fun. They played for the stadium. They played for each other.
They looked at each other. They nodded. They knew what to do. They had to solve the problem. They had to work together. They had to play harder. They had to believe.
They kicked off. They passed the ball. They ran. They shot. They scored. It was 2-1. They cheered. They kicked off again. They passed the ball. They ran. They shot. They scored. It was 2-2. They cheered louder. They kicked off again. They passed the ball. They ran. They shot. They scored. It was 2-3. They won. They jumped. They hugged. They screamed.
They got the ticket to the stadium. They were very happy. They thanked their friends. They thanked their parents. They thanked the yellow team. They were good sports. They went to the stadium. They saw the best soccer players. They learned from them. They dreamed of being like them. They loved soccer. They loved each other. They were happy.

One day, a little girl named Lucy found a fragile bathrobe in her room. She was very happy. She thought it was a magic bathrobe. Lucy wanted to dress in the bathrobe and see if it had magic powers.
Lucy put on the bathrobe and went to show her mom. Her mom said, "Wow, Lucy! You look so pretty in your new bathrobe." Lucy smiled and said, "Thank you, Mom! I think it is magic."
Lucy went outside to play with her friends. She wanted to show them her magic bathrobe. But when she got outside, it started to rain. The rain made the bathrobe wet and heavy. Lucy was sad because she thought the magic was gone. But then, the rain stopped and a big rainbow appeared in the sky. Lucy and her friends were so happy. They played under the rainbow, and Lucy knew the magic was still there.

One day, a big, dependable dog named Max found a long cord. Max liked to move the cord around with his mouth. He would play with the cord all day long. Max's best friend, a little boy named Tim, liked to watch Max play with the cord. They would laugh and play together.
One day, Max and Tim were playing with the cord when they saw a little girl named Sue. Sue was sad because her kite was stuck in a tree. Max had an idea. He gave the cord to Tim and said, "Help Sue with the cord!"
Tim tied the cord to the kite and Max pulled it. With a big tug, Max moved the kite out of the tree. Sue was very happy and said, "Thank you, Max and Tim!" They all became good friends and played together every day.
The moral of the story is that being dependable and helping others can make new friends and bring happiness.

Once upon a time, there was a unique taxi. It was big and yellow. The taxi driver was a nice man. He liked to make people happy. One day, he saw a sad boy on the street.
"Hi, little boy," the taxi driver said. "Why are you sad?"
"I lost my toy," the boy said. "I can't find it."
The taxi driver wanted to help. He said, "Let's make a fun game. We will look for your toy together in my taxi."
The boy smiled and got in the taxi. They drove around and looked for the toy. They found it under a tree. The boy was very happy.
The moral of the story is: when you help others, you make them happy and you feel good too.

Once upon a time, there was a wealthy man named Tim. He lived in a big house by the beach. Tim loved to play in the sand all day.
One day, while playing in the sand, he found a small box. He poked the box and it opened. Inside the box, there was a tiny frog. The frog said, "Hi, I am a magic frog! I will give you one wish."
Tim thought and thought. He wished for more sand to play with. The magic frog said, "Okay, your wish is my command." Suddenly, there was sand everywhere! The sand filled up Tim's big house and even the whole town.
Everyone was surprised and didn't know what to do. Tim felt bad for his wish. He asked the frog to take back the wish and make everything normal again. The frog agreed, and with a wave of its little hand, the sand went away. Tim learned to be happy with what he had and to think before making a wish.

Once upon a time, there was a big brown giant. He lived in a big house in the woods. The giant was nice and liked to play with the small animals in the woods. One day, the giant met a little squirrel. The squirrel had a big, bushy tail. The squirrel said, "Hello, giant! Let's play a game."
The giant and the squirrel played all day. They laughed and had fun. The giant felt a tickle in his nose. He knew he was going to sneeze soon. He told the squirrel, "I have to sneeze. When I sneeze, it is very big and loud. You should hide."
The squirrel did not believe the giant. He thought the giant was joking. The squirrel said, "I am not scared. I want to see you sneeze." The giant felt the sneeze coming. He tried to hold it in, but he could not.
The giant sneezed a big and loud sneeze. The squirrel was surprised. He flew up into the air and landed on a tree branch. The squirrel said, "Wow, giant! That was a big sneeze!" The giant smiled and said, "I told you so!" They both laughed and continued to play in the woods.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to build things. He had a big box of toy blocks. Tim was very happy when he played with his blocks.
One day, Tim found a long cord. He had a great idea. "I will use this cord to make my block tower even better!" he said. Tim tied the cord to his blocks and started to build a big, tall tower.
Tim's friend, Sue, came to play. "Wow, Tim!" she said. "Your tower is so tall and strong!" Tim smiled and said, "Thank you, Sue! I used the cord to make it even better." They both laughed and played together, building more towers with the blocks and cord. They were very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little square. The square was troubled because it was not like the other shapes. It had four sides, all the same size. The other shapes would laugh and play, but the square felt alone.
One day, a kind circle saw the square and decided to care for it. The circle took the square to play with the other shapes. They all laughed and had fun together. The square was not so troubled anymore.
Later, the square and the circle found a puzzle. The puzzle had a spot that was just the right size for the square. The square fit in the puzzle and helped the other shapes finish it. The shapes were happy and the square felt special. The square was not troubled anymore because it knew it had a place where it belonged.

One day, a girl named Lucy found a rare veil. The veil was very pretty. It was red, blue, and yellow. Lucy loved the veil very much. She put it on her head and went to show her mom.
"Mom, look at my veil!" Lucy said. Her mom looked at her and smiled. "That is a very nice veil, Lucy," her mom said. Lucy felt happy that her mom liked the veil too.
Lucy wore the veil all day. She played with her toys, ate her lunch, and took a nap with the veil on her head. When it was bedtime, Lucy took off the veil and put it next to her bed. She could not wait to wear it again the next day.

Once upon a time, in a big green garden, there was a brown avocado tree. The tree had many avocado friends hanging from its branches. One day, a little girl named Ava came to the garden. She saw the brown avocado tree and smiled.
Ava walked up to the tree and said, "Hello, Mr. Avocado Tree. I like your brown avocados. Can I have one, please?" The tree shook its branches, and a brown avocado fell into Ava's hands. She thanked the tree and took the avocado home.
At home, Ava showed her mom the brown avocado. They cut it open and shared it. It was yummy. They were happy. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside with her friends. One day, while playing with her friends, she fell down and hurt her knee.
Lily called out to her mommy, "Mommy, I hurt my knee!" Her mommy came running to help her. She looked at Lily's knee and saw a small scratch. "Don't worry, sweetie. It's just a little scratch. Let's clean it up and put a band-aid on it."
Lily's mommy cleaned her knee and put a pretty band-aid on it. Then, she gave Lily a big hug and a kiss. Lily felt much better. She smiled and said, "Thank you, Mommy!" After that, Lily and her friends continued to play, and they all had a wonderful day.

Once upon a time, there was a jungle. In this jungle, there were many animals. One of the animals was a bird. The bird was very popular. All the other animals liked the bird.
One day, the bird had an idea. The bird said to the monkey, "Let's record our voices." The monkey said, "That's a good idea!" So, the bird and the monkey went to find a big leaf. They used the leaf to record their voices.
The bird and the monkey sang a song. They sang, "We love the jungle, it's our home. We play here, we are never alone." The other animals heard the song and they liked it. The bird and the monkey became more popular. All the animals in the jungle were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big toy zebra. He loved to ride it all day long. The zebra was soft and fun to play with. Tim and the zebra were best friends.
One day, Tim and the zebra went outside to play. They saw a big rock. Tim said, "Let's ride over the rock, zebra!" But when they tried, the zebra got hurt. It made a sad sound.
Tim felt bad. He hugged the zebra and said, "I'm sorry, zebra. I didn't mean to hurt you." The zebra felt better. They played more, but stayed away from the big rock. Tim and the zebra were happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She loved to eat tasty treats. One day, she saw a big, red apple on a tree in her yard. She wanted to eat it because she knew it would be very tasty.
Amy asked her friend, Tom, to help her get the apple. Tom was very tall, so he could reach the apple. Amy pointed to the apple and said, "Tom, can you please get that apple for me?" Tom smiled and said, "Sure, Amy! I will help you."
Tom reached up and picked the apple from the tree. He gave it to Amy, and she took a big bite. It was the tastiest apple she had ever eaten. Amy and Tom were very happy with their success. From that day on, they always helped each other when they needed it.

Once there was a little girl who wanted to do her own ironing all by herself. She asked her mommy, "Can I iron some clothes?" Her mommy said, "Yes, you can try."
The little girl went to the ironing board and picked up the iron. She felt so big, so grown up. She plugged in the iron without any help.
When she started to iron the clothes, she moved the iron too quickly and suddenly the room started spinning. She felt a little dizzy and she had to stop.
The little girl started to cry and her mommy came running. Her mommy asked, "What's wrong?"
The little girl said, "I was ironing but I moved it too fast and it made me dizzy. I can't do it!"
Her mommy said, "It's ok sweetheart, you don't have to do it. You're too little. Just leave the iron and I'll do it for you."
But it was too late, the little girl had already removed the iron from the plug before her mommy arrived and by the time her mommy came back, the iron had already caused some damage.
The little girl was so sad and she started to cry even harder. Her mommy felt so sorry for her. She hadn't meant for her little girl to get hurt.

One day, a lively cat named Sam got an invitation. It was a card with pretty colors. The card said, "Come to my party!" Sam was very happy.
Sam put on his best hat and took a big step out of his house. He walked to the party. On the way, he met a dog named Max. Max said, "I got an invitation too! Let's go together!" So, Sam and Max went to the party together.
At the party, there were games, food, and music. Sam and Max played with their friends. They danced and had fun. Sam said, "This is the best party ever!" Max agreed, "Yes, I love this party!" They had a great time and went home with big smiles on their faces.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a girl named Mia. Mia's mom told her to make food. Mia wanted to be big and helpful. She got a pan from the cupboard. The pan was filthy. Mia did not like the dirty pan, but she wanted to help her mom.
Mia's cat, Whiskers, saw the dirty pan. Whiskers wanted to encourage Mia to clean the pan. So Whiskers jumped on the table and knocked the dirty pan onto the floor. Mia was sad and surprised. She did not know why Whiskers did that.
Mia's mom came into the room and saw the dirty pan on the floor. She understood what Whiskers was trying to do. She told Mia that they needed to clean the pan first. Together, Mia and her mom cleaned the pan. Then, Mia made food with the clean pan. Whiskers was happy to help and Mia felt proud.

Lily and her mom went to the museum. Lily liked the museum. It had many things to see and learn. Mom said they could unpack their lunch and eat in the garden.
They found a nice spot under a big tree. Lily opened her backpack and took out her sandwich and juice. Mom opened her bag and took out her salad and water. They smiled and said, "Bon appetit!"
But then, Lily heard a loud roar. She looked around and saw a big dinosaur. It was not a real dinosaur. It was a statue. But it looked very scary. It had sharp teeth and claws. It was moving its head and tail. It was making noise.
Lily was afraid. She dropped her sandwich and juice. She ran to her mom and hugged her. She said, "Mom, mom, there is a dinosaur! It is going to eat us!"
Mom looked at the dinosaur. She was not afraid. She knew it was not a real dinosaur. It was a robot. It was part of the museum show. She said, "Lily, Lily, don't be scared. It is not a real dinosaur. It is a robot. It is pretending to be a dinosaur. It is for fun. It is not going to eat us."
Lily looked at the dinosaur again. She saw that it was not a real dinosaur. It was a robot. It was pretending to be a dinosaur. It was for fun. She felt silly. She said, "Oh, I see. It is not a real dinosaur. It is a robot. It is pretending to be a dinosaur. It is for fun. I am sorry, mom. I was scared."
Mom hugged Lily and said, "It's okay, Lily. I understand. Sometimes things can look scary, but they are not. You are brave, Lily. You are my brave girl."
Lily smiled and said, "Thank you, mom. You are my best mom. Can we finish our lunch and see more things in the museum?"
Mom said, "Of course, Lily. Let's finish our lunch and see more things in the museum. Maybe we can find some friendly dinosaurs."
They picked up their lunch and ate in the garden. They were happy. They enjoyed the museum. They had a good day.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was an obedient dog. He would always listen to his owner, Jane. Jane loved Spot very much.
One day, Jane and Spot went for a walk. They took many steps together. Spot would stop and sniff the flowers. Jane would giggle and wait for him.
Suddenly, a big cat came out from behind a tree. The cat wanted to fight Spot. But Spot was a good dog and did not want to fight. Jane told Spot to come to her, and he did. The cat went away, and Jane and Spot continued their walk. They were happy and safe together.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max was an obedient dog. He always listened to his owner, Sam. Sam loved Max very much. They liked to play together in the park.
One day, Sam and Max went to the park to play. Sam saw a big hole in the ground. He told Max to mix the dirt around the hole. Max was happy to help. He used his paws to mix the dirt. The hole was soon gone.
Sam was very proud of Max for being so obedient. They had a great day at the park. After that, they went back home. Max got a big treat for being such a good dog. They lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a baby. She was very miserable. She cried and cried. Then, mama and daddy said to her, "What is wrong, baby?". But the baby was too miserable to say anything.
She put her head down in her lap and started to cry. Mama and Daddy were very worried for the baby. So, Daddy said, "Let's try to make the baby feel better".
Mama went to the kitchen and made a special treat for the baby. She brought in a bowl of ice cream and put it in front of the baby. When the baby saw it she stopped crying and smiled. She looked up at mama and daddy and said, "Yum".
Mama and Daddy were so happy that the baby was feeling better. They gave her lots of hugs and kisses. From then on, the baby was never miserable again.

Once upon a time, there was a playful cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play with her toys and run around the house. One day, Kitty found a bottle of shampoo in the bathroom. She did not know what it was, so she decided to ask her friend, Doggy.
"Doggy, can you tell me what this is?" Kitty asked, holding the shampoo bottle. Doggy looked at the bottle and said, "That is shampoo. We use it to wash our fur and make it clean and soft."
Kitty and Doggy decided to use the shampoo to wash their fur. They filled a big tub with water and poured some shampoo in it. They took turns washing each other's fur, making lots of bubbles and having fun.
When they were all clean and dry, Kitty and Doggy felt very soft and smelled very nice. They were so happy that they found the shampoo and learned how to use it. From that day on, they always took baths together and stayed clean and soft. And they lived happily ever after.

John and his mommy were at the store looking for something special. John saw something he liked - a big leather ball.
"Mommy, can I have it?" he asked.
"Let's have a look at it," said Mommy.
John was thrilled when Mommy said yes! He hugged it tight and it made him happy.
John and his mommy went home. John was playing with the leather ball in the yard when something yummy appeared - a picnic with sandwiches, chips, and cake! He started eating it all up.
But then the picnic quickly disappeared! He was confused and asked, "Where did it go?"
Mommy smiled. "I didn't buy the leather ball for you to play - I bought it for us to make a picnic basket. So I took it back inside and filled it with yummy food for us to eat."
John was overjoyed. He realized that if he had been patient and waited, his picnic wouldn't have disappeared.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to his friend's house to play. They had a normal day, playing with their toys and laughing. Tim's friend, Sam, had a big jar of cookies on the table. Tim loved cookies, and he really wanted one.
But then, a problem happened. Tim and Sam both reached for the last cookie at the same time. They both wanted it, but there was only one left. Tim thought it would be fun to joke with Sam. He said, "Let's play a game to see who gets the cookie."
They played a simple game of rock, paper, scissors. Tim won the game, so he got the cookie. Sam was sad, but Tim had an idea. He broke the cookie in half and shared it with Sam. They both smiled and ate their half of the cookie. They learned that sharing made them both happy, and they continued to play and have fun for the rest of the day.

Once upon a time there was a boy named Jake. Jake was very intelligent and loved to jog. One day he decided to jog through the park. As he jogged along, he saw another little boy. His name was Peter. Peter was eating a piece of candy and watching Jake jog by. 
"Hi," said Peter. 
"Hi," replied Jake.
"I like your jogging," said Peter. "It looks fun."
"It is fun!" said Jake. "Do you want to join me?"
"Yes!" said Peter.
So Peter and Jake started to jog together around the park. They were having so much fun they forgot they were both tired. After they were done running, they sat down together and ate some more candy.
"You're very intelligent, Jake," said Peter.
"Thank you," said Jake. "I think you're really smart too!" 
Peter smiled. He was so happy to have a friend like Jake. And ever since then, they've been jogging together every day.

One day, a girl named Mia wanted to paint a big building. She had many colors to use. The sky was clear and blue. Mia was very happy.
Mia started to paint the building with red, blue, and yellow. She painted all day. Suddenly, a big wind came and blew all the paint away. Mia was sad.
But then, something unexpected happened. The wind mixed all the paint together and made a beautiful rainbow on the building. Mia was so surprised and happy. The building was now more pretty than before.

Once upon a time, there was a little red car. The car loved to go fast. One day, the car went down the road with an open door.
The car saw a big tree on the left side of the road. The car wanted to stop and look at the tree. So, the car stopped near the tree with its door still open.
A little bird saw the open door and flew inside the car. The car liked the bird and they became friends. They went on fun rides together, always stopping to look at things they liked. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a big, powerful dog named Max. Max lived in a little house with a girl named Anna. Max loved to play with Anna in the yard. They played ball and ran around the yard together. Max was very strong, so Anna always used a leash when they played outside.
One day, Max wanted to improve at playing ball. He wanted to be the best ball player in the whole world. Max practiced and practiced, but he still couldn't catch the ball very well. Max was sad because he wanted to make Anna happy.
Anna saw Max trying to improve and had an idea. She found a longer leash for Max, so he could run and jump better. Max was so happy with the new leash! He practiced and practiced, and soon, Max was the best ball player in the whole world. Max and Anna played ball every day, and they were both very happy.

One day, a big red car wanted to drive. The car saw a fly sitting on a spicy snack. The car said, "Hello, fly! Do you want to drive with me?"
The fly said, "Yes, I would love to drive with you, car!" So the car and the fly started to drive. They drove fast and slow, and they had a lot of fun.
But then, something unexpected happened. The car turned into a big red boat! The fly was very surprised. "Wow, car! Now you are a boat!" said the fly.
The big red boat said, "Yes, now we can go on the water too!" So the boat and the fly went on the water and had even more fun.
In the end, they went back to the land, and the big red boat turned back into a car. The car and the fly were very happy and said, "We had the best day ever!"

Once upon a time, there was a lively little girl named Lily. She loved to play and dance all day long. One day, her mom told her to clean her room. Lily did not want to clean her room, but she knew she must yield and do as her mom said.
Lily found her soft, pink bathrobe on the floor. She picked it up and put it away. As she cleaned, she found more things she had forgotten about. She put away her toys and clothes, making her room nice and neat.
When Lily finished cleaning her room, her mom was very happy. She gave Lily a big hug and said, "Good job, Lily!" Lily felt proud of herself. She learned that yielding to her mom and doing what she was told made everyone happy. And that was the moral of the story: it is good to listen and do what we are told.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toys. One day, Tim found a flexible snake toy. The snake could bend and twist in many ways.
Tim played with the snake all day. He made the snake strike his toy blocks. The blocks fell down with a big noise. Tim laughed and clapped his hands. He was having so much fun.
Then, Tim found his mom's stamps. He decided to make the snake stamp on paper. Tim dipped the snake's head in paint and pressed it on the paper. The snake made pretty stamp shapes. Tim showed his mom the art he made. She smiled and hugged him. They had a happy day together.

Once upon a time, there was a clever little boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch his favorite show on the TV. Every day, he would sit on the floor and wait for the sun to rise. When the sun was up, he knew it was time for his show.
One day, Tim woke up early. He saw the sun rise, and he got very excited. He ran to the TV and turned it on. But, the TV was not showing his favorite show. He was sad and did not know what to do.
Then, Tim had a clever idea. He looked at the big clock on the wall. He saw that it was too early for his show. So, he waited and played with his toys. When it was the right time, he turned on the TV again. This time, his favorite show was on! Tim was happy and watched his show with a big smile.

Once upon a time, there was a kid named Tim. Tim liked to play by the sea. One day, he found a small boat. He wanted to sail the boat on the water.
Tim put the boat in the water. The wind blew and the boat began to sail. Tim was happy to see the boat move. But then, the wind blew harder and it got cold.
Tim felt cold and went back home. He told his mom about the boat and how he sailed it. His mom hugged him and made him warm. Tim was happy and couldn't wait to sail the boat again on a warm day.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Max. Max loved to eat steak. One day, he saw a big, juicy steak on the table. Max wanted the steak very much. He felt envious when he saw the cat, Whiskers, looking at the steak too.
Max said to Whiskers, "I dare you to take the steak!" Whiskers thought about it, but she did not want to get in trouble. She said, "No, I won't do it. You take the steak if you dare!"
Max was scared, but he wanted the steak. He jumped on the table and took the steak. Whiskers watched and felt envious. Max ate the steak and it was very yummy. Whiskers wished she had dared to take the steak too.
In the end, Max and Whiskers learned that sometimes it is good to dare, but sometimes it is better to be safe. They both knew that they should not take things that do not belong to them. They promised to be good and share their treats next time.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Sara and Jack. Sara was very strong because she had lots of muscle. Jack was not as strong, but he was very clever.
One day, they were playing together and Sara offered to lend Jack some of her muscles! Jack was so excited and thanked her with a big hug.
Jack took the muscle and started lifting. He worked so hard and soon he was lifting things that he would have never been able to do before.
The next day, Sara saw the muscle was empty. Jack had used it all up! Sara laughed and gave Jack a big hug. They both felt so happy and proud of each other.

Once upon a time, there was a big, dull frog. The frog liked to eat bugs. One day, the frog found a whistle on the ground. He picked it up with his mouth.
The frog saw a little boy. The little boy said, "Hi, frog! Can you blow the whistle?" The frog tried to blow the whistle, but he could not. The frog was sad.
The frog tried to eat a bug, but the bug flew away. The frog tried to blow the whistle again, but he still could not. The frog was very sad. The end.

Once there were two friends, Mike and Jacob. They both were quite uncomfortable because the air was filled with gas. Mike started to worry and said, "We must find away to increase the air!" 
Jacob looked around and noticed a big window. He said, "Let's open the window and the gas should go away." 
So Mike and Jacob opened the window and soon enough the air started to increase. They both cheered and hugged each other happily because the gas was gone. 
They had made the air much more comfortable with their bravery.

Once upon a time, there was a polite dog named Max. Max had a big heart and never wanted to worry anyone. One day, he went for a walk in the park. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing. Max was very happy.
As Max walked, he saw a little cat stuck in a tree. The cat was scared and cried for help. Max wanted to help the cat, so he tried to climb the tree. But Max was not good at climbing trees, and he started to worry.
Max tried harder and harder to climb the tree, but he could not do it. He was very sad and worried. The cat was still stuck in the tree, and Max could not help. In the end, Max had to go home, leaving the cat in the tree. The cat was sad, and Max's heart was heavy with worry.

One day, an elderly cat named Mr. Whiskers was walking in the park. He saw a big mattress lying on the grass. Mr. Whiskers felt tired and wanted to take a nap on the mattress.
As he lay down, a little dog named Spot came running up. Spot wanted to play, so he kicked the mattress. "Hey, Mr. Whiskers, let's play!" said Spot. But Mr. Whiskers did not want to play. He just wanted to sleep.
Mr. Whiskers and Spot talked about what to do. "I am old and need to rest," said Mr. Whiskers. "I am young and want to play," said Spot. They could not agree. So Mr. Whiskers had an idea. He found a stick and threw it for Spot to fetch. Spot was happy to play by himself, and Mr. Whiskers could sleep on the mattress. In the end, they both got what they wanted and were happy.

Once upon a time, in a modern house, a little boy named Tim found an envelope on the table. He was curious and wanted to see what was inside. Tim tried to open the envelope, but it was too hard for him. So, he decided to chew on it to make it easier to open.
As Tim chewed on the envelope, something unexpected happened. The envelope started to wiggle and jiggle. Tim was surprised and stopped chewing. He watched as the envelope opened up all by itself.
Out of the envelope came a tiny, friendly mouse. The mouse said, "Thank you for helping me, Tim! I was stuck inside that envelope, and your chewing helped me get out." Tim and the mouse became best friends and played together every day. And they never chewed on envelopes again.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot had a long tail that he liked to wag when he was happy. One day, Spot went for a walk with his friend, a little girl named Sue.
One day, Spot and Sue went to the park to play. They played for a whole week, and they had so much fun! They ran, jumped, and laughed together. But then, Spot started to sneeze. He sneezed and sneezed, and Sue was worried.
Sue took Spot to the doctor, who said that Spot was okay, but he needed to rest. So, Spot and Sue went home and took a long nap. When they woke up, Spot felt better and stopped sneezing. They were both very happy and went back to the park to play some more. And they played happily ever after.
