Once upon a time, there was a fast cat named Speed. Speed was not happy because he was poor. He had no toys to play with. One day, Speed heard a big sound. He went to see what it was.
Speed saw a big box. It was closed. "What is in the box?" he thought. "I must see!" He tried to open it. A kind dog came and said, "Be careful, Speed. That box has a secret."
Speed did not listen to the kind dog. He wanted to see what was inside. He opened the box and it revealed many toys! Speed was very happy, but he did not know that the toys were not good.
Speed played with the toys all day. The kind dog watched him and felt sad. He knew something bad would happen. The toys began to break and fall apart. Speed was sad and cried.
In the end, Speed learned that he should have listened to the kind dog. The toys were not good, and now he was still poor and had nothing to play with. The kind dog tried to help, but it was too late.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Minty. She wanted to go to the park, but her mom said no. Minty was sad and thought it was terrible. Her mom said, "I promise you can go to the park tomorrow."
The next day, when Minty woke up, she was so excited and ran to get ready. However, when she arrived at the park she saw it was pouring rain. She thought it was very terrible.
But Minty's mom told her to wait and that it might get better. Sure enough, after a few minutes the rain stopped, and Minty could finally play at the park. She was so happy, and thought that her mom was very kind to make such a nice promise.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a group of animals. They were always rude to each other. They never shared their food and did not play together. They always fought over small things.
One day, a wise old owl saw how the animals were acting. He wanted to help them unite and be friends. So, he came up with a plan. He invited all the animals to a big party with lots of food. The animals were very excited.
At the party, the animals started to share their food with each other. They talked and played together. They realized that being rude was not fun. They learned to be nice and united as friends. From that day on, the animals were always kind to each other and lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small, modern house, there lived a boy named Tom. Tom had a toy car that he loved very much. He would play with it every day and take it everywhere he went. Tom's car was the best toy he owned. 
One day, Tom's mom told him to clean his room. As a result, Tom had to put away all his toys, including his car. He was not happy about it, but he knew he had to do it. So, Tom cleaned his room and put his car on a shelf.
The next morning, Tom woke up and couldn't find his car. He looked everywhere, but it was gone. Tom felt sad and didn't know what to do. Then, he remembered putting it on the shelf. Tom was so happy to find his car again. He picked it up and started to play, promising to always remember where he put his toys.

Once upon a time, there was a clever dog named Spot. Spot loved to help his friends. One day, his friend, a little bird, had a broken wing. Spot wanted to help fix it.
Spot and the little bird went to the wise old owl for help. They had to wait for a while because the owl was sleeping. When the owl woke up, he saw Spot and the little bird. He knew what to do to fix the bird's wing.
The owl gave Spot some special leaves to put on the bird's wing. Spot put the leaves on the wing and the bird felt better. The bird could fly again! Spot was so happy he could help his friend. They all played together and had a fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a grandma who loved to take her naps in her cold armchair. She often would nap for hours, covered in her big blanket. One day, when she was almost asleep, she heard a gentle knock at the door. When she opened it, a little girl was standing there with a big smile.
The little girl asked, "Can I come in and take a nap? It's so cold outside and I don't have anywhere to go." Without a second thought, grandma opened the door wider and said, "Come in! You can share my armchair and blanket."
The little girl was so happy. She snuggled up in the armchair under the blanket with grandma. As they drifted off to sleep, grandma could feel the warmth of the little girl's hug.
The moral of the story is that it's important to be kind and open your heart to help people in need. Showing love can make others feel warm and comforted. If we all do our part in this world, we can make it a little less cold.

Once upon a time, there was a clumsy boy named Tim. He loved to draw with his crayons. One day, he found a big box of crayons in many colors. Tim was very happy.
Tim started to draw a big picture on the wall. He used all the colors from the box. His mom saw the wall and said, "Oh no, Tim! We don't draw on walls! Let's clean it up and draw on paper instead."
Tim and his mom cleaned the wall. Then, they put the crayons in a special box. Tim's mom said, "We will lock the box, so the crayons stay safe." Tim agreed and they locked the box together. From that day on, Tim only drew on paper and was more careful with his crayons.

One day, a big melon was in the garden. A little boy named Tim saw it and felt uncomfortable. He did not know why the melon was there. He called his mom and said, "Mom, there is a big melon in the garden!"
His mom came out and saw the melon. She said, "Oh, that is a gift from our neighbor, Mr. Brown. He wanted us to have it." Tim felt better now. He wanted to eat the melon with his mom.
They cut the melon and started to eat it. But something unexpected happened! Inside the melon, there were many little bugs. The bugs started to dance and sing. Tim and his mom were very surprised. The bugs were so good at dancing and singing that Tim and his mom started to applaud. They laughed and clapped their hands. The bugs took a bow and went back into the melon. Tim and his mom decided to give the melon back to Mr. Brown and thank him for the fun surprise.

Once upon a time, there was a rich man who lived in a big house. He had a lot of things and was very happy. One day, he wanted to prepare a big meal for his friends. He asked his helper to make the food.
The helper made the food, but he was not careful. He left the fire on for too long, and the food burned. The house filled with ash from the fire. The rich man was very sad because his house was dirty and his friends could not come.
The rich man learned that he should be more careful and not leave the fire on for too long. He also learned that having a lot of things does not always make you happy. Sometimes, being careful and safe is more important.

Once upon a time, there was a family. This family had a mom, a dad, a big sister, and a little brother. They all lived in a small house. They loved each other very much.
One day, big sister found a special rock. The rock would glow when she held it. It made her very happy. She showed the glowing rock to her family, and they were all amazed.
Little brother was jealous. He wanted a glowing rock too. So, he went outside and looked for one. After a while, he found a rock that glowed just like big sister's. He showed it to his family, and they were all happy together. They played with their glowing rocks and shared many fun times as a family.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toy whip. He would run around and pretend he was a cowboy. One day, Tim saw a delicious cake on the table. It looked so yummy.
Tim wanted the cake, but he knew he should wait for his mom to say it was okay. But the cake looked so good, and he couldn't wait any longer. He used his whip to pull the cake closer to him. The cake fell on the floor and broke into pieces.
Tim felt regret for what he did. He knew he should have waited for his mom. When his mom saw the mess, she was sad, but she still loved Tim. They cleaned up the cake together and made a new one. Tim promised to always listen to his mom and wait for her.

Once upon a time, there was a wide garden full of melons. A little boy was running around, playing in the garden. He always picked the ripest and most ripe melons to eat.
One day the little boy walked up to a melon, but he could tell that this melon was different. It had an extra wide shape and a bright green color. He sniffed it, and he knew it was perfect for eating! He picked it up and started to run with it.
Just then, the little boy heard a voice from behind. "Stop!" The voice said. It was the boy's father. He could tell the boy had taken the best melon. The boy's father said, "This melon is too special to eat. If you take it, I will have to punish you." 
The boy thought for a moment, and he chose to put the melon back in the garden. His father watched him with approval.
A few days later, the little boy saw his father take the same melon, but this time the father did not punish him. Instead, the father thanked him for his kindness and said, "You were so wise to leave the melon. I'm going to give it as a present."
 
The little boy was so happy that his good behavior was rewarded! He learned that by being kind, he can get nice surprises.

Once there was a gifted mechanic named Tom. He could fix any car or bike. One day, a man came to Tom with a broken bike. The man said, "Can you fix my bike, please?"
Tom looked at the bike and saw a cut in the tire. He knew he could fix it. But he wanted to do something special. He had a plan to make the bike go very fast. Tom worked all day and night to make the bike better.
The next day, the man came back to get his bike. Tom said, "I fixed your bike, and now it's very fast!" The man was happy and rode away on his bike. But as he rode, the bike went too fast. The man could not stop and crashed into a tree. The bike was broken again, and the man was sad. Tom's special fix was not a good idea after all.

Once upon a time, there was a little infant named Lily. She loved to paint with her mommy and daddy. Lily's favorite color was purple.
One day, Lily and her mommy decided to paint a big picture together. They used purple paint to make a beautiful flower. Lily was so happy to see the purple flower on the paper.
After they finished painting, Lily showed her daddy the purple flower. He was so proud of her. They hung the painting on the wall for everyone to see. Lily's purple flower made everyone smile.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty flower called Lily. Lily lived in a big garden with many other flowers. They all lived happily together.
One day, a new flower came to the garden. Her name was Daisy. Daisy was not fit and had a hard time playing with the other flowers. Lily saw Daisy and said, "Hi, Daisy! My name is Lily. Do you want to play with me?" Daisy smiled and said, "Yes, I would like that."
Lily and Daisy played together every day. They played hide and seek, and tag. Soon, Daisy became fit and strong. All the flowers in the garden became friends with Daisy. The moral of the story is that we should always be kind and help others when they need it.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play with her toys and go to the park. One day, she saw a bottle of perfume on the table. She knew it was her mom's, but she wanted to smell it too. She tried to reach for it, but it was not available for her to get.
Lily tried to climb on the chair to get the perfume, but she was not careful. She started to fall from the chair. Her mom saw her and caught her just in time. Lily was safe, but she was scared. Her mom told her that she should not try to get things that are not available for her.
Lily learned that it was not good to take things that were not available for her. She knew that she should always ask for help if she needed something. From that day on, Lily always asked her mom or dad for help when she wanted something. And they were always happy to help her.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big dream. He wanted to swim in the deep sea with his friend, a big fish named Fin. Every night, Tim would dream of his adventures with Fin.
One day, Tim told his mom about his dream. "Mom, I want to go to the deep sea with my friend Fin," he said. His mom smiled and said, "That's a nice dream, Tim, but you can't go to the deep sea. It's too far away."
But Tim insisted, "Mom, please let me go. I really want to see Fin." His mom thought about it and said, "Okay, Tim. But you have to promise to be careful and come back soon." Tim was very happy and thanked his mom.
Tim went to the beach and called for his friend Fin. Soon, Fin came swimming up to the shore. They played in the water and had so much fun. After a while, Tim remembered his promise to his mom. He waved goodbye to Fin and went back home.
When Tim got home, he told his mom all about his adventure with Fin. His mom was happy that Tim had fun, but she was even happier that he kept his promise and came back home safely. From that day on, Tim and Fin were the best of friends, and they had many more adventures together.

Once there was a girl called Lucy. She was 3 years old. One day she was in her bedroom getting dressed. Her mum said, "You need to look fit for the party". Lucy smiled and nodded. 
Suddenly, she felt something prick her finger. She looked down to see a needle. She blinked in surprise. She didn't know what it was doing there. 
A few days later, it was the party. Lucy put on her special dress and arrived with her mum and dad. She saw lots of happy people and kids having fun. 
Suddenly, she spotted a puppet show. She blinked with excitement. She had seen the same needle at the puppet's outfit. She smiled as she realised why the needle was in her bedroom. Her Mum had put it there for a surprise. 
Lucy was so excited. Now her dress was fit for the party and the needle made things even more special. She giggled with glee. 
The Moral: Be prepared!

Once upon a time there was a little girl whose name was Lucy. She was ready to eat dinner with her family but she was in a bit of a delay. Her mom had made her a plate of vegetables. 
"Oh no!" Lucy exclaimed. "I do not want to eat my vegetables!"
"But why not?" her mom asked. "You love eating vegetables; they're so delicious and good for you!"
Lucy crossed her arms and stuck out her tongue. "No way!" she said. "They are yucky!"
Her mom smiled and said, "Just try one bite. I promise, if you don't like it, you don't have to eat it!"
Lucy was ready to take the challenge. She slowly lifted her fork and took a small bite of the vegetable. Surprisingly, it wasn't as yucky as she thought it would be. She smiled and said, "Mom, you were right, these vegetables are delicious!"

Ben and Lily were playing in the park. They liked to hop on the grass and zoom on the slide. They saw an elderly man sitting on a bench. He had a big hat and a long beard.
"Hello, sir," Ben said. "Can we play with you?"
The elderly man smiled. He took off his hat and showed them a rabbit. The rabbit was white and fluffy.
"This is my friend, Snowy," he said. "He likes to hop too. Do you want to pet him?"
"Yes, please!" Lily said. She reached out her hand and touched the rabbit's fur. It was soft and warm.
"Snowy is very nice," Ben said. "Can he zoom with us?"
The elderly man laughed. He put his hat back on and picked up the rabbit.
"Sure, he can zoom with you," he said. "But be careful. He is very fast. He can zoom faster than a car!"
Ben and Lily were excited. They took turns holding the rabbit and zooming on the slide. The rabbit seemed to enjoy it too. He wiggled his nose and twitched his ears.
The elderly man watched them and smiled. He was happy to share his friend with them. He liked to see them hop and zoom. They reminded him of when he was young.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a smooth rock and picked it up. He liked how it felt in his hand. Tim walked and walked until he saw a big cable on the ground.
Tim thought it would be fun to play with the cable. He tried to snap it like a whip, but it was too heavy. Just then, a big wind came and blew Tim's hat away. He was sad and wanted his hat back.
Tim had an idea. He tied the cable to his smooth rock and threw it at his hat. The rock and cable wrapped around the hat. Tim pulled and pulled, and the hat came back to him. Tim was happy and went home with his hat, the cable, and the smooth rock.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue had a small, pretty purse that she loved. She took it everywhere she went.
One day, Sue saw a tiny, harmless bug on the ground. She wanted to show her mom the bug. Sue insisted that the bug should come home with her. So, she put the bug in her purse and went home.
When Sue got home, she showed her mom the bug. Her mom smiled and said, "What a nice, harmless bug!" Sue was happy that her mom liked the bug too. She let the bug go outside to play. Sue knew she would always remember the little bug she found.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and have fun. One day, she found a big machine in her room. It was red, blue, and yellow. Lily was very happy and wanted to know what the machine could do.
Lily asked her mommy, "What does this machine do?" Her mommy smiled and said, "This is a magic machine. It can make anything you want when you point at it and say what you want." Lily was very excited and wanted to try it right away.
Lily pointed at the machine and said, "I want a big ball to play with!" Suddenly, a big, bouncy ball appeared in front of her. She played with the ball all day and had lots of fun. The next day, she pointed at the machine and asked for a teddy bear. The machine made a soft, cuddly teddy bear for her to hug. Lily loved her magic machine and played with it every day.

One day, a big, hairy dog went for a walk. He saw a bridge and wanted to cross it. As he walked, he met a small, scared cat. The cat said, "Help! I am stuck on this bridge and I want to go home."
The dog wanted to help the cat, so they walked together. As they walked, they saw ice on the bridge. They were scared, but the dog said, "Don't worry, we can do it together." They tried to walk, but the ice made them freeze. They could not move!
Then, a big, warm sun came out from behind the clouds. The sun saw the dog and the cat and wanted to help. The sun shone very bright and warm, and the ice began to melt. The dog and the cat could move again. They said, "Thank you, sun!" The sun smiled and the dog and the cat walked off the bridge, happy and safe.

Once upon a time, there was a big, bald man named Tom. He had a black cat named Spot. Tom and Spot were best friends. They liked to play and eat together. One day, they went for a walk in the park.
In the park, they saw a little girl. She was crying. Tom asked her, "Why are you sad?" The girl said, "I lost my toy. I can't find it." Tom and Spot wanted to help her. They started to look for the toy.
They looked and looked but could not find the toy. Then, Spot heard a noise. He went to see what it was. Spot found a big box. He opened the box and saw the toy inside! The little girl was so happy. She said, "Thank you, Tom and Spot!" 
But then, something unexpected happened. The toy started to talk! It said, "I was hiding in the box because I was scared." Everyone was surprised. They all laughed and played together. And they all became friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom had a big pocket on his shirt. He liked to put things in his pocket. It was a comfortable pocket.
One day, Tom found a small toy car. He wanted to care for it and keep it safe. He put the toy car in his pocket. But, there was a hole in the pocket. The toy car fell out and got lost. Tom was sad.
Tom looked for the toy car. He searched everywhere. Finally, he found it under a big tree. Tom was so happy! He learned to care for his pocket and fix the hole. Now, Tom and his toy car were comfortable and safe together.

One day, a girl named Lucy found a bracelet on the floor. She looked at it and saw that it was very pretty. Lucy wanted to attach the bracelet to her wrist, but she didn't know how to do it.
Lucy asked her friend, Tom, for help. Tom was sometimes rude, but he knew how to attach the bracelet. He helped Lucy and the bracelet was on her wrist. Lucy was very happy and said thank you to Tom.
But then, something unexpected happened. The bracelet started to glow and it made Lucy and Tom very small! They were now the size of bugs. They looked at each other and laughed. Now, they could go on a new adventure together as tiny friends.

Once upon a time, there was a generous man who lived in a small village. He was always friendly and kind to everyone. One day, while the man was walking down the village street, he encountered a broccoli plant that had been neglected by its owner. The generous man decided to care for the broccoli. He watered it and made sure it got enough sunlight.
The man's efforts paid off, and soon enough the little broccoli plant was growing bigger and bigger. As it grew, the man tended to it every day with love and care. After a few months, the broccoli was thriving and full of life.
One day, the man's generous nature was put to the test. Another man living in the village wanted to buy the broccoli. The generous man thought long and hard, and in the end, he decided that he should reverse his kind gesture and give away the broccoli that he'd nurtured. He was selfless and didn't accept any money for it.
The other man was really grateful for the generous man's thoughtful deed. He thanked him and gave him a generous reward. The generous man was very happy and learned a valuable lesson from his experience - kindness always comes with a reward.

Once there were two little kids, a boy and a girl. They loved to go and play in the park. One day, the girl had an unusual idea. "Let's make a twist sandwich!", she said. She grabbed her bag and found some slices of bread, and began to twist them together.
The boy watched as she wiggled and twisted the slices, until her sandwich was all done. He smiled, excited about the unusual sandwich.
The two kids went to sit on a bench in the park, and then the girl handed the twist sandwich to the boy. He took a big bite, and his eyes lit up. "It's so delicious!" he said.
The two of them laughed and talked and ate, enjoying the unusual twist sandwich. Even though it was simple to make, it was one of the best snacks they had ever had.

Once upon a time, there was a bird. He was scared and he wanted to find some help. Then, he spotted two children. He flew over to them and he started to talk.
The first child said, "Hi, Bird! What can we do for you?"
The bird replied, "I'm scared. Can you help me?"
The second child said, "Sure! But first, you have to listen."
The Bird listened carefully. The first child said, "We can show you a safe place to sleep. A place where nobody can hurt you!"
The Bird was happy. He said, "Thank you! I will listen to you always!"
The children took the Bird to a safe place and made sure he was happy. From then on, the Bird was safe and never scared again.

Once upon a time, an old man was walking through a gloomy forest when he saw a tiger. The tiger was sad and scared. The old man kindly said, "What happened, tiger?" 
The tiger told him that he was lost and didn't know the way. The old man smiled and said, "Don't worry little tiger, I promise to keep you safe and show you the way." 
The tiger was glad, so he followed the old man through the forest until they got to the tiger's home. The old man said, "Now that you are safe and back home, remember to always keep your promises as this will make others happy too." 
The tiger thanked the old man and hugged him before happily walking back home. 
The moral of this story is to always keep your promises as it will make others happy.

Once upon a time, there was a happy dog named Spot. Spot liked to run and play with his ball. One day, Spot saw the mailman enter his yard. The mailman had a big bag of mail.
Spot was curious and wanted to see what was in the bag. He ran to the mailman and wagged his tail. The mailman smiled and gave Spot a big pat. Spot was very happy.
In the next yard, there was a cat named Whiskers. Whiskers saw Spot and the mailman. Whiskers was envious because he wanted a pat too. So, Whiskers ran to the mailman and got a pat. Now, both Spot and Whiskers were happy and played together in the yard.

Once upon a time, there was a huge frog. The frog had big eyes that would blink all the time. He lived near a pond and loved to jump and play all day.
One day, the frog saw a sack near the pond. He hopped over to see what was inside. He used his strong legs to open the sack. Inside, he found many colorful balls.
The frog was so happy! He took the balls out of the sack and played with them. He would blink his big eyes and smile. All his friends came to play with him too. They had a fun day playing with the colorful balls from the sack.

Lily and Tom were best friends. They liked to play in the park with their toys. One day, they saw a big tiger in a cage. The tiger looked sad and lonely.
"Let's go and see the tiger," Lily said.
"OK, but be careful. He might bite," Tom said.
They walked up to the cage and saw the tiger's yellow eyes and black stripes. He had a big mouth and sharp teeth. He was very quiet and still.
"Hello, tiger. What is your name?" Lily asked.
The tiger did not answer. He just looked at them with his sad eyes.
"Maybe he is hungry. Do you have any food for him?" Tom asked.
Lily nodded. She had a sandwich in her bag. She took it out and threw it to the tiger. The tiger sniffed it and ate it. He licked his lips and smiled.
"Thank you, little girl. You are very kind. My name is Raju," the tiger said.
Lily and Tom were surprised. They did not know that the tiger could talk.
"Wow, you can talk! How do you know our language?" Lily asked.
"I learned it from the people who stole me from my home. They put me in this cage and took me to different places. They made me do tricks for money. They were very mean and cruel. I miss my family and friends," Raju said.
Lily and Tom felt sorry for the tiger. They wanted to help him.
"That's not fair. You should be free and happy. We will help you escape," Lily said.
"How can you do that? The cage is locked and the people are watching," Raju said.
Lily and Tom had an idea. They ran to the other side of the park and found a big stick. They brought it back to the cage and used it to break the lock. The cage door opened and Raju came out.
"Thank you, little girl and boy. You are very brave and loyal. You are my friends," Raju said.
He hugged them with his big paws and licked their faces.
"Come on, Raju. Let's go and find your home. We will go with you," Lily said.
"OK, but we have to be careful. The people might see us and chase us," Raju said.
They ran away from the park and into the woods. They looked for Raju's home and his family. They hoped they would find them soon. They were happy and free. They were friends.

Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Lucy. She loved to play in her garden, especially on the swing. She hopped onto the seat, gripping the smooth cord tightly. As she pushed off with her feet, she began to swing. 
Lucy laughed with happiness as she felt the breeze on her face. She went higher and higher into the sky, all the while feeling content and calm.
Then, a voice called out, "Lucy, would you like some juice?" 
"Yes, please!" Lucy said, as she slowed her swing. 
Moments later, Lucy was sitting on the ground next to her swing, sipping her juice. She looked at the cord with a smile. She knew she could always use it for a short ride or a moment of calm.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby cat named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play outside with his friends. One day, dark clouds came, and Fluffy heard a loud noise. It was thunder!
Fluffy was scared, but his friend, a kind bird, urged him to be brave. "Don't worry, Fluffy," said the bird. "We can hide under the big tree until the thunder goes away." Fluffy felt better with his friend by his side.
They hid under the tree, and soon the thunder stopped. The sun came out, and Fluffy and the bird played happily together. They knew that they could always help each other when they were scared. And they were best friends forever.

Once upon a time, there was a kind and compassionate girl named Lily. She had many friends, but her best friend was a little dog named Max. One day, Lily found a pretty scarf in her room. It was red and blue, and she thought it would be a good match for Max.
Lily put the scarf on Max, and they went outside to play. They saw a big tree with a small hole in it. Lily and Max were curious, so they went closer to look. Inside the hole, they saw a tiny bird shivering from the cold. The bird looked very sad and cold.
Lily knew what to do. She took the scarf off of Max and gently wrapped it around the little bird. The bird felt warm and happy now. Lily and Max knew they had done a good thing. They played together all day, knowing that they had helped a friend in need.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw a deep hole in the ground. Tim was curious and wanted to know what was inside the hole. His mom said, "We need to fix this hole so no one falls in."
Tim and his mom went home to get some dirt to fix the hole. While they were there, Tim's dad was cooking a big steak for dinner. Tim loved steak and couldn't wait to eat it. His mom put the dirt in a bag, and they went back to the park.
At the park, Tim and his mom filled the deep hole with the dirt from the bag. They fixed the hole so no one would fall in. After they were done, they went home to eat the yummy steak that Tim's dad had cooked. Tim was happy that he helped fix the hole and got to eat his favorite food.

Tom and Mia were having breakfast with their mom. They liked waffles with honey and butter. Mom made them hot and crispy in the toaster.
"Can I have more honey, please?" Mia asked.
"Sure, sweetie. Here you go." Mom poured some honey on Mia's waffle.
Tom was about to take a bite of his waffle, when he felt a tickle in his nose. He tried to hold it in, but he couldn't. He sneezed loudly and sprayed his waffle with snot.
"Yuck!" Mia said. "Tom, you sneezed on your waffle!"
"Sorry, Mia. I didn't mean to." Tom said. He wiped his nose with a napkin.
Mom smiled and said, "It's okay, Tom. Sometimes we sneeze when we have a cold or an allergy. Do you want a new waffle?"
Tom nodded. He didn't want to eat his snot waffle. Mom threw away his old waffle and gave him a new one.
"Thank you, Mom. You're the best." Tom said.
"You're welcome, Tom. I love you." Mom said.
Tom and Mia finished their breakfast and got ready for the day. They hoped to have some wild fun in the park later. They loved to play on the swings, the slide and the sandbox. They also liked to watch the birds, the squirrels and the flowers. They were happy and healthy kids.

One day, a little girl named Sue found her mom's makeup. She thought it would be fun to put the makeup on her face. But she didn't know how to use it right. So, her face looked very funny.
Sue's mom saw her and was grumpy. "You should not play with my makeup," she said. Sue felt sad and went to her room. As she sat there, she had an idea. She took a paper and colors. Sue drew a big, bright sun. She strike lines all around the sun to show it was shining.
Sue showed her mom the sun picture. Her mom smiled and said, "I'm sorry for being grumpy. We can learn to use makeup together." They both laughed and had fun with the makeup. Sue learned that it's better to share and learn together.
The moral of the story is to share and learn things together with your loved ones.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She loved to play at the park. Her favorite thing to do was to slide down the big, red slide. Mia would hold her waist and laugh as she slid down.
One sunny day, Mia saw a serious-looking cat sitting near the slide. The cat wore a tiny hat and a bow tie. Mia had never seen a cat like this before. She thought it was funny, so she giggled.
As Mia went to slide down, the cat jumped up and slid down first. Mia was very surprised! She never saw a cat slide before. The cat then took off its hat and bowed. Mia clapped and laughed. From that day on, Mia and the serious cat became best friends, and they always played together at the park.

Lily liked to play with her mom's jewelry. She liked the shiny rings, the pretty necklaces, and the colorful earrings. She would put them on and pretend to be a princess or a fairy. But she was not careful with them. She would leave them on the floor, on the table, or in the sofa. She did not put them back in the box where they belonged.
One day, her mom came home and saw her jewelry all over the place. She was very upset. She said to Lily, "Lily, what have you done? You have made a big mess with my jewelry. You could lose them or break them. They are very special to me. Some of them were given to me by your grandma and grandpa. You have to protect them and take care of them. You have to be organized and put them back in the box when you are done playing with them. Do you understand?"
Lily felt sorry. She did not want to make her mom sad or angry. She said, "I'm sorry, mom. I did not know they were so special to you. I just wanted to play with them. They are so beautiful. I will protect them and take care of them. I will be organized and put them back in the box. I love you, mom."
Her mom hugged her and said, "I love you too, Lily. Thank you for being sorry and for listening to me. You can play with my jewelry sometimes, but you have to ask me first and be gentle with them. They are not toys. They are treasures. And you are my treasure too."
Lily smiled and nodded. She helped her mom pick up the jewelry and put them back in the box. She learned to respect her mom's things and to be more organized. She also learned that some things are more valuable than others, and that the most valuable thing is love.

Lily and Tom were playing in the garden. They liked to run and jump and laugh. They saw a big fire in the sky. It was orange and red and yellow. It was the sun.
"Look, Lily, the sun is a fire!" Tom said.
"No, Tom, the sun is not a fire. It is a big ball of light and heat. It makes us warm and cheerful," Lily said.
"How do you know, Lily?" Tom asked.
"Mommy told me. She knows many things. She is very smart. You should mind her," Lily said.
"OK, Lily, I will mind Mommy. But I still think the sun is a fire. It looks like a fire," Tom said.
They kept playing in the garden. They did not mind the sun. They were happy and cheerful. They liked the sun. It was their friend.

One day, a little ant was walking in the grass. He was gentle and kind. He liked to help his friends. He saw a big ant who was sad. The big ant had a problem. The big ant did not trust anyone.
The little ant wanted to help the big ant. He went to the big ant and said, "I can help you. Please trust me." The big ant looked at him and thought about it. The big ant was scared, but he saw that the little ant was gentle and kind.
The big ant decided to trust the little ant. They worked together to solve the big ant's problem. They became good friends. The big ant was not sad anymore. He was happy that he trusted the little ant.

Once upon a time, there was a regular boy named Tom. Tom had a toy skull. He loved to play with his toy skull every day.
One day, Tom saw a big hill. He wanted to gain the top of the hill. So, Tom and his toy skull started to climb the hill. It was hard, but Tom kept going.
When Tom and his toy skull reached the top, they were very happy. They looked at the pretty view. Tom and his toy skull went back home and played more. They had a fun day.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a yellow bird named Sunny. Sunny loved to fly high in the sky. He had many friends, like the red fox, the brown bear, and the green frog.
One day, Sunny was flying very fast. He loved to speed through the trees. As he was flying, he saw his friend, the green frog. The green frog said, "Sunny, be careful! There is a big storm coming!" Sunny did not listen and kept flying fast.
Suddenly, the wind blew hard and the rain started. Sunny was scared. He tried to fly back, but the wind was too strong. Just then, the red fox and the brown bear saw Sunny in trouble. They worked together to help Sunny. The red fox climbed a tree, and the brown bear caught Sunny when the wind blew him down. They all went back to their homes, safe and sound. From that day on, Sunny was always careful when he flew.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Spot. Spot was a very good dog who loved to create things. One day, Spot found a perfect bone. He was so happy! He wanted to make something special with the bone.
But, Spot had a problem. His friend, the cat named Fluffy, also wanted the perfect bone. They both wanted the bone, and they didn't know what to do. This made Spot and Fluffy very sad.
Then, Spot had a great idea. He said, "Let's create something together with the bone!" Fluffy liked the idea. So, Spot and Fluffy worked together, and they made a beautiful toy with the perfect bone. They played with the toy all day and were very happy. And from that day on, Spot and Fluffy always shared their toys and played together.

Ben likes to play guitar. He has a red guitar that his dad gave him. He can play some songs that he learned from his dad. He likes to sing along with his guitar.
One day, his dad asks him if he wants to play guitar in front of some people. Ben feels nervous. He does not like to play in front of people. He thinks they will laugh at him or say he is bad. He shakes his head and says no.
His dad smiles and hugs him. He says, "It's okay to be nervous, Ben. I get nervous too sometimes. But you know what? You are very good at playing guitar. And the people who will listen to you are your friends and family. They love you and they will be happy to hear you play. You can trust them. They will not laugh at you or say you are bad. They will clap and cheer for you. Do you want to try?"
Ben thinks for a moment. He loves his dad and he loves his guitar. He wants to make them happy. He nods and says yes. He takes his red guitar and follows his dad to the living room. He sees his mom, his grandma, his uncle, and his cousin. They smile and wave at him. He feels a little less nervous.
He sits on a chair and puts his guitar on his lap. He looks at his dad, who gives him a thumbs up. He takes a deep breath and starts to play. He plays a song that he and his dad like. He sings along with his guitar. He forgets about being nervous. He feels happy and proud. He finishes the song and looks up. He sees the people clapping and cheering for him. He hears them say, "Good job, Ben! You are amazing! We love you!" He smiles and says, "Thank you!" He hugs his dad and his mom. He feels very happy. He trusts them. He loves them. He loves his guitar.

One day, a clumsy cat named Sam was playing in his room. He liked to jump on his bed and poke his toys. Sam had a big, soft bed that he loved to play on. He had many toys, but his favorite toy was a little bear. The bear was blue and very cute.
"Come play with me, bear!" said Sam. The bear did not talk, but Sam liked to pretend it did. They played and jumped on the bed together. Soon, Sam and the bear were very tired from playing.
It was time for a nap. Sam and the bear lay down on the bed. Sam poked the bear one more time and said, "Good night, bear. I love you!" Then, Sam closed his eyes and went to sleep. The clumsy cat and his little bear had a nice nap on the big, soft bed.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside with his friends. One day, while playing in the park, Tim found a big stick. He loved the stick and started to admire it. He thought it was the best stick in the whole world.
Tim met a new friend in the park named Sam. Sam was deaf, but he was very good at reading lips. Tim showed Sam his big stick and said, "Look at my big stick! Isn't it great?" Sam smiled and nodded his head. He thought the stick was great too.
Tim and Sam played with the stick all day long. They used it as a sword, a wand, and even a fishing pole. At the end of the day, Tim told Sam, "Thank you for playing with me and my stick. I had so much fun!" Sam smiled and said, "Me too!" Tim learned that even though Sam was deaf, they could still be great friends and have lots of fun together. The moral of the story is that we can be friends with anyone, no matter how different they are from us.

Sara and Tom were playing in the park. They liked to run and slide and swing. But they did not like to share. Sara had a ball in her pocket. She wanted to play with it by herself. Tom saw the ball and wanted to play with it too.
"Give me the ball!" Tom said.
"No, it's mine!" Sara said.
They both pulled the ball. The ball flew out of Sara's pocket and rolled away. They ran after the ball. But they did not see the big dog.
The big dog saw the ball and wanted to play with it too. He ran faster than Sara and Tom. He reached the ball and picked it up with his mouth. He wagged his tail and barked.
"Hey, that's our ball!" Tom said.
"Give it back!" Sara said.
They tried to get the ball from the dog. But the dog did not want to give it back. He growled and snapped. He slapped Sara and Tom with his paw. They fell down and cried.
A distant voice called the dog. It was the dog's owner. He saw what happened and ran to the park. He took the ball from the dog and scolded him. He said he was sorry to Sara and Tom. He gave them the ball and a band-aid.
Sara and Tom stopped crying. They looked at the ball and at each other. They felt sorry too. They said they were sorry to each other. They decided to share the ball and play together. They thanked the dog's owner and waved goodbye. They learned a lesson that day. Sharing is better than fighting. And dogs are not toys.

Once upon a time, a cheerful woodcutter named Tom lived in a small house near the woods. He had a big axe that he used to chop wood every day. One day, he found a tiny door in a tree.
Tom said, "I wonder if my axe can fit in the door." He tried to put the axe in the door, but it was too big. Tom was sad because he wanted to see what was inside the tree.
A squirrel came by and saw Tom. The squirrel said, "Hi, Tom! Why are you sad?" Tom told the squirrel about the tiny door and his big axe. The squirrel said, "I can help you open the door!" With a little push, the door opened, and Tom and the squirrel became good friends. They played together in the woods every day, and Tom was cheerful again.

One day, a little girl named Lily was playing in her room. Her mom was in the living room watching TV. Lily heard her mom whisper to herself, "This show is fine, but I wish Lily would watch with me."
Lily went to her mom and asked, "Mom, can I watch TV with you?" Her mom smiled and said, "Of course, Lily! Come sit with me."
They watched a fun show together. Lily liked the show and thought it was fine. She whispered to her mom, "I like this show, Mom." Her mom whispered back, "I'm happy you like it, Lily."
From that day on, Lily and her mom would watch the fine show together. They would whisper and laugh, enjoying their time together. And they both knew that watching TV was more fun when they did it together.

One day, a colorful bird named Bob came to play with Tim. Tim was a little boy who loved fun. They played with toys and had a great time. But then, Tim's friend Sue came over. Sue wanted to play with Tim, but she did not want to play with Bob the bird. 
Tim said, "Sue, please accept Bob to play with us. He is nice and fun." Sue did not like birds, so she said, "No, I don't want to play with a bird. I want to play with you, Tim." This made Tim very sad.
Then, something unexpected happened. Bob the bird started to sing a beautiful song. Sue had never heard a bird sing like that before. She was surprised and said, "Wow, Bob is a very special bird. I will play with both of you." They all played together and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful butterfly. She had big, colorful wings. She loved to fly around in the sun. One day, she met a mosquito. The mosquito was not as pretty as the butterfly. He felt bad about it.
The butterfly wanted to help the mosquito. She had an idea. She told the mosquito to fly with her. They flew together, and everyone saw them. But something unexpected happened. The mosquito started to fly better than the butterfly. He was fast and could do tricks. The butterfly was embarrassed because she couldn't keep up.
But then, the mosquito did something nice. He stopped showing off and flew next to the butterfly. They became good friends. The butterfly was not embarrassed anymore. They both learned that it's not about how you look or how you fly. It's about being kind to each other. And they lived happily ever after.

Ben and Lily were playing in the park with their ball. They liked to kick the ball to each other and run after it. Sometimes the ball would go near the road, where the big cars and buses went fast. Ben and Lily knew they had to be careful and look both ways before they crossed the road.
One day, a wild dog came into the park. It saw the ball and wanted to play with it too. It ran after the ball and barked loudly. Ben and Lily were scared of the dog. They wanted their ball back, but they did not want to go near the dog.
"Go away, dog!" Ben shouted. "That is our ball!"
The dog did not listen. It picked up the ball in its mouth and ran away. Ben and Lily chased the dog, hoping to get their ball back. They did not see the red light or the big truck that was coming.
The truck driver saw the children and the dog running into the road. He tried to stop, but he could not. He honked his horn and yelled, "Stop! Yield!"
But it was too late. The truck hit the dog and the ball. The ball popped and the dog yelped. Ben and Lily stopped and saw what happened. They were very sad and scared. They cried and hugged each other.
A nurse who was walking by saw the accident. She ran to help the dog and the children. She called for an ambulance and a police officer. She tried to comfort Ben and Lily and tell them it was not their fault.
But the dog was badly hurt. It did not move or bark anymore. The nurse said it was dead. Ben and Lily felt very sorry for the dog. They wished they had not chased it. They wished they had stayed away from the road. They wished they had listened to their mom and dad. They learned a hard lesson that day.

One day, a little girl named Mia was playing in the park. She saw her friend Tom sitting on a bench. Tom had a bad eye. Mia asked, "What happened to your eye, Tom?"
Tom said, "I was playing with my ball, and it hit my eye. Now it hurts and looks bad. Can you help me guess what I can do to feel better?"
Mia thought for a moment and said, "Maybe we can ask my mom for help. She knows how to make things better."
So Mia and Tom went to find Mia's mom. Mia's mom put a cold pack on Tom's bad eye. Soon, Tom's eye felt better, and they could play again. They were both very happy and thanked Mia's mom for her help.

Once upon a time, there was a white dog named Spot. Spot loved to run, play, and speak to his friends. He had many friends in the park. They would have fun all day.
One day, Spot found a bottle with a strange smell. It had a label that said "poison." Spot did not know what it was, but he knew it was not good. He wanted to keep his friends safe.
Spot went to his friends and told them about the poison. They all listened and stayed away from the bottle. They thanked Spot for helping them. They played together and had fun, knowing they were safe because of Spot.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to make a cake. He found some black dough in the kitchen. Tim was not sure if he could make a cake with black dough, but he wanted to try.
Tim asked his mom, "Can I make a cake with this black dough?" His mom said, "I don't know, but you can try." So, Tim tried to make the cake. He put the black dough in a pan and put it in the oven.
After some time, the cake was ready. Tim took it out of the oven and it looked very black. He tried a piece and it tasted very bad. Tim was sad that his cake did not taste good. His mom said, "It's okay, we can try to make a better cake next time."

One day, a dull cat named Tom saw a thief in his home. The thief was taking Tom's toys. Tom did not like this at all. He wanted to stop the thief and get his toys back.
Tom thought of a plan. He decided to shout very loud to scare the thief away. So, Tom took a big breath and shouted, "Stop, thief!" The thief was so scared of the loud noise that he dropped the toys and ran away.
Tom was very happy that his plan worked. His toys were safe and the thief was gone. Tom learned that sometimes, even a dull cat can be very brave and smart.

Once upon a time, there was a generous fish named Bob. Bob lived in a big pond. He loved to help all his friends.
One day, Bob saw his friend, Sam the turtle, trying to use a hook to get some yummy food. Sam was having a hard time. Bob wanted to help Sam.
Bob swam up to Sam and said, "Let me help you with that hook." Bob used the hook and got the food for Sam. Sam was so happy. They both shared the food and had a great day.

One day, a charming little cat was playing outside. The cat saw a big tree with white leaves. The cat wanted to climb the tree and see the white leaves up close.
The charming cat climbed the tree and sat on a branch. A bird was also sitting on the branch. The bird said, "Hello, cat! Be careful, don't fall!" The cat said, "I will be careful, bird."
But the wind started to blow very hard. The cat was scared and tried to hold on tight to the branch. The bird said, "Hold on, cat! I will help you!" The bird flew to the cat and tried to help, but the cat started to fall. The bird quickly caught the cat and they both landed safely on the ground. The cat and the bird became good friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little pig. The pig was pale and lived on a big farm. The pig had many friends, like the cow, the dog, and the cat. They liked to play and have fun together.
One day, the pig saw a big tree. He looked up and saw a bird. The pig said, "Hello, bird! What are you doing?" The bird said, "I am singing a song. Do you want to sing with me?" The pig said, "Yes, I would like that!"
The pig and the bird sang a happy song. They had a lot of fun. The pig started to wonder what other fun things he could do on the farm. He asked his friends, the cow, the dog, and the cat, to join him. They all played and had a great time together. The pig was very happy to have such good friends.

Once upon a time, there lived a beautiful fairy called Daisy. Daisy was very kind and always looking out for the needs of her friends. She was so nice that she was always invited to special parties and festivals.
One special day, Daisy was invited to a magical Rhythm Festival. She was so excited to feel the rhythm of the music and dance with her friends.
When Daisy arrived at the festival, something strange happened. All of the rhythm that once filled the air was gone. Everyone around her was confused andvery worried.
Daisy knew what to do. She had a special wand that could restore rhythm. With a flick of her wand, the music started again and everyone was relieved.
Daisy smiled and said to her friends, "I knew I could restore the rhythm!" From that day on, Daisy knew that if there was ever a time she was confused, her wand could help her out. She was very grateful for her special powers!

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Bob. Bob lived in a small house with his friend, Tom the cat. They liked to play all day. One day, they wanted to go outside and explore.
Bob said, "Tom, let's go play in the dark woods!" Tom was scared, but he wanted to be brave. So, they walked into the woods. The ground was rough, and it was hard for them to walk. But they kept going, holding each other's paws.
As they walked, they heard a sound. "Who is there?" asked Tom. A big, friendly bear came out from behind a tree. "I am Ben the Bear!" he said. "I live in the dark woods. Do you want to be my friend?" Bob and Tom smiled and said, "Yes, we want to be your friend!" And they all played together in the woods until it was time to go home.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Spot. Spot was a very patient dog. He liked to play with his leash. One day, Spot's family planned a big celebrate. They were all very excited.
Spot's family was busy getting ready for the celebrate. They hung up pretty things and made yummy food. Spot wanted to help, but he had to wait. He played with his leash to pass the time.
Finally, the celebrate began. Spot's family put his leash on and took him outside. There were many friends, games, and treats. Spot was so happy that he had been patient. The celebrate was the best day ever.

Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Tim. Tim liked to play with his toy cars. One day, Tim's mom said, "We are going to visit Grandma. Let's unpack your toys and put them in a bag."
Tim said, "Okay, Mommy." He unpacked his toy cars and put them in a bag. Tim and his mom went to Grandma's house.
At Grandma's house, Tim met a girl named Sue. Sue was young too. They played with Tim's toy cars. Tim said, "This car is mine." Sue said, "That car is pretty." They shared the cars and had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a slow snail named Sam. Sam had a friend named Tom. Tom was a turtle. They liked to play together.
One day, Sam and Tom found a nail. They did not know what to do with it. Tom had an idea. He said, "Let's tie a string to the nail and make a toy."
Sam and Tom tied the string to the nail. They pulled the string and the nail moved. They laughed and played with their new toy all day.
At the end of the day, Sam and Tom were tired. They said goodbye to each other and went to sleep. They were happy they had so much fun with the nail and the string.

Once upon a time, in a big farm, there lived a little chicken named Chicky. Chicky was a careless chicken who loved to play all day long. One day, Chicky was playing near the fence when he saw a big ball on the other side.
Chicky wanted to get the ball, so he tried to jump over the fence. But he couldn't jump high enough. A kind cow named Molly saw Chicky and said, "Chicky, you should kneel down and crawl under the fence."
Chicky listened to Molly and kneeled down. He crawled under the fence and got the ball. Chicky was so happy that he said, "Thank you, Molly! You are a good friend." From that day on, Chicky was more careful and always asked for help when he needed it.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a little rabbit named Remy. Remy had a special pocket on his tummy. In this pocket, Remy would supply food for his friends when they were hungry.
One day, Remy met a big bear named Benny. Benny was ignorant about the forest and didn't know where to find food. Remy showed Benny his pocket and gave him some food. Benny was very happy and thanked Remy.
From that day on, Remy and Benny became best friends. They helped each other and shared everything they had. All the animals in the forest learned from Remy and Benny's friendship. The moral of the story is to always be kind and help others, even if they are ignorant about something.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a helpful little bug named Benny. Benny had many friends, like birds, mice, and frogs. Benny liked to crawl on the ground and on the trees. One day, Benny found a skull on the ground. The skull was from a big animal that lived long ago.
Benny saw that his friend, the mouse, was sad. The mouse said, "I lost my home. I have no place to sleep." Benny thought for a moment and said, "I have an idea! You can live in the big skull. It will be a safe place for you to sleep."
The mouse was very happy and thanked Benny. Benny and the mouse worked together to make the skull a nice home. They put soft leaves inside for a bed and found food to eat. Soon, the mouse had a cozy new home.
All the other animals in the forest saw how helpful Benny was. They learned that being kind and helping others can make everyone happy. And from that day on, all the animals in the forest tried to be more helpful, just like Benny.

Once upon a time, there was a big, colorful couch. It was the nicest couch in the room. It was soft and had many colors. Everyone loved the couch.
One day, a cat and a dog wanted to sit on the couch at the same time. The cat wanted to sleep, and the dog wanted to play. They both seemed to want the couch very much. They did not know how to share the couch.
The cat and the dog began to fight. They both jumped on the couch and tried to push each other off. The couch shook and the colorful pillows fell on the floor.
Then, a small girl came into the room. She saw the cat and the dog fighting on the couch. The girl knew how to fix the problem. She picked up the pillows and put them back on the couch.
The girl told the cat and the dog to share the couch. She showed them how to sit on the couch together. The cat and the dog listened to the girl. They sat on the couch, side by side. The cat slept, and the dog played with a toy. They were both happy on the colorful couch. And they became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big old house with a green lawn. It was a sunny day, and a little boy named Tim was playing outside. He liked to run and jump on the lawn. Tim had a pet cat named Mittens. Mittens was a very pretty cat with soft fur. She liked to play with Tim on the lawn too.
One day, Tim saw Mittens run up a tall tree. Mittens was scared and could not come down. Tim called out, "Mittens, come down, please!" But Mittens just meowed and stayed in the tree.
Tim's dad saw that Mittens was in the tree. He said, "Tim, don't worry. I will save Mittens." Tim's dad got a long ladder and put it against the tree. He climbed up, picked up Mittens, and brought her back down. Tim was very happy and said, "Thank you, Dad, for saving Mittens!" They all went back to playing on the lawn, and they had a fun day together.

One day, Tom hopped out of bed. He was so cheerful because it was a beautiful day. The sun was shining, and there was a gentle breeze in the air.
Tom went outside to play. He saw a big field of daisies. He hopped around them and plucked some of the bright yellow petals. Then he put them behind his ear like a special flower crown.
After he finished, Tom decided to have a picnic lunch. He laid out a blanket and spread all kinds of delicious treats. He ate some cheese and crackers and shared his lunch with a butterfly.
When it got too warm, Tom headed back home. He was so happy because he had such a cheerful day. He hopped along the way, singing a happy tune. When he got inside, Tom skipped off to bed with a smile on his face.

Once upon a time, there was a small boy named Tom. He was feeling lonely, so he decided to go for a walk. With his voice, he called out "Show me your friends!".
Suddenly, a mouse appeared from the bush. "Hello, I'm Mary the Mouse!" she said. "I'm looking for friends too. Will you join me?"
Tom was a bit frightened at first, but he knew he shouldn't be. With a smile, he said, "Yes, let's join together!" Mary was very excited.
They went on a long journey together, and soon enough, they came across a delicious-looking muffin. Mary said, "Let's split it. I'd love to share this muffin with you!" Tom was so happy that they both joined together.
He enthusiastically said: "Yes, let's join together and eat the muffin!" So they ate the muffin together, and had lots of fun. From then on, Tom and Mary became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a special girl called Daisy. Daisy was very strong, and she liked chocolate. One day, Daisy wanted to go on an adventure. So, she decided to leave her home. She grabbed all her special things, like her toy, her storybook and some chocolate.
When she left, Daisy saw a big, strong tree. She wanted to climb it, so she touched the trunk. It was so strong, she knew she could do it! When she reached the top, Daisy was rewarded with a delicious surprise - a giant chocolate bar!
Daisy wanted to share the treat, so she started to look around for her friends. She saw a bunny, a squirrel and a bear, and happily offered them a piece of her chocolate. They all loved it so much, they thanked Daisy and asked her to climb the strong tree with them again!
So, Daisy became friends with all the animals - and they all shared the delicious chocolate until they were no longer hungry. Daisy was happy and full, so she said goodbye to her new friends and left.

Once upon a time, there was a smooth ball. The ball loved to roll and play in the sun. One day, the ball saw a hedge. The hedge was big and green.
The smooth ball wanted to gain new friends. It asked the hedge, "Can I play with you?" The hedge was happy and said "Yes! Let's play together!" They had lots of fun playing in the sun.
After playing, the smooth ball and the hedge were tired. They sat near a tree and talked. The ball was happy to gain a new friend. They promised to play together every day. And they lived happily ever after.

Ben and Mia were playing in the park with their kites. They liked to watch them fly high in the sky and make shapes with the strings. Ben had a red kite with a long tail and Mia had a blue kite with a star.
"Look, Mia, my kite is flying faster than yours!" Ben said.
"No, mine is flying higher than yours!" Mia said.
They ran and laughed and tried to make their kites fly better. But then a big wind came and blew Ben's kite away. It flew over the trees and the road and the houses. Ben was sad and scared. He did not want to lose his kite.
"Ben, don't worry, maybe we can find your kite later," Mia said. She tried to be a good friend.
But Ben did not listen. He ran after his kite. He did not see the cars and the bikes and the people. He only saw his kite. He crossed the road without looking. He did not hear the horn of the truck. He did not feel the bump.
Mia saw what happened. She screamed and cried. She ran to Ben. He was lying on the ground. He was not moving. He was bleeding. Mia was very scared. She did not know what to do.
A man saw them. He had a phone. He called for help. He said Ben needed to go to the hospital. He said the hospital was a place where doctors and nurses could make Ben better. He said Mia could go with Ben. He said he was sorry.
Soon a big car with lights and sounds came. It was an ambulance. The ambulance took Ben and Mia to the hospital. Mia held Ben's hand. She hoped he would wake up. She hoped he would be okay.
At the hospital, the doctors and nurses looked at Ben. They gave him medicine and bandages. They said he had a mild head injury. They said he would be fine. They said he was lucky. They said Mia was brave.
Ben opened his eyes. He saw Mia. He smiled. He said he was sorry. He said he was silly. He said he loved his kite. He said he loved Mia more.
Mia hugged Ben. She said she was happy. She said she was sorry too. She said she loved his kite. She said she loved him more.
They smiled and laughed and cried. They were happy. They were friends. They still liked to fly kites. But they learned to be careful. They learned to look and listen. They learned to be safe.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his ball. One day, Spot was playing with his ball inside the house. The ball rolled onto the thick carpet. Spot tried to get the ball, but he started to struggle.
Spot's mom saw him struggle and came to help. She told Spot that playing with the ball inside the house was not a good idea. Spot should play with the ball outside, where there is no carpet. Spot listened to his mom and took the ball outside.
From that day on, Spot always played with his ball outside. He never struggled with the thick carpet again. Spot learned that it is important to listen to his mom because she knows what is best for him.

Once upon a time, there was a little angel named Amy. She had long, soft wings that she loved to flap. Amy lived in a big, pretty house in the sky.
One day, Amy wanted to bake a cake. She put some flour, sugar, and eggs in a big bowl. She mixed them all together and put the mix in a round pan. Amy put the pan in the oven and waited.
After a while, the cake was ready. It smelled so good! Amy shared the cake with all her friends. They all said it was the best cake they ever had. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little white cat named Snow went for a walk. She walked to the east where the sun comes up. Snow loved to play in the warm sun. As she walked, she saw a big tree with a red ball stuck in it.
Snow tried to get the ball, but it was too high. A friendly bird saw Snow and asked, "Do you need help?" Snow said, "Yes, please. I want the red ball." The bird flew up and tried to drop the ball, but it was stuck tight.
The bird had an idea. He called more birds to help. They all flew up and pushed the ball together. The ball began to move. Snow was so happy. Finally, the ball dropped to the ground. Snow played with her new red ball all day, and she knew she had made some great friends.

One day, a colorful bird named Mimi wanted to learn yoga. She went to the big tree where all the animals did yoga. Mimi saw her friend, a little frog named Fifi. Fifi was very good at yoga.
"Hi Fifi! Can you teach me yoga?" asked Mimi. Fifi smiled and said, "Yes, Mimi! Let's start with a fun pose!" They tried the tree pose, the cat pose, and the dog pose. Mimi found it hard, but she kept trying. Fifi helped her and they had lots of fun.
After yoga, Mimi and Fifi went to eat ice cream. It was a hot day, so their ice cream started to melt fast. They laughed and licked their ice cream quickly. Mimi and Fifi were happy they did yoga and ate ice cream together.

Once upon a time, there was a big tiger. The tiger had a friend, a little boy. The boy was sometimes foolish. They liked to play together in the forest.
One day, the big tiger said, "I have a secret." The little boy was very excited. He wanted to know the secret. But the big tiger told him, "You must wait. You will see it later."
The little boy and the big tiger played more games. The boy tried to be good and not foolish. He wanted to know the secret. Then, the big tiger took him to a hidden place. There, they found a big surprise. It was a huge pile of toys! The little boy was very happy. The big tiger's secret was a special gift for his friend. And they played together all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside in the dirt. One day, he found a dirty tomato in his yard. Tim was very happy to find the tomato.
Tim wanted to eat the tomato, but it was too dirty. He tried to clean it, but he had a struggle. The dirt was very hard to get off. Tim did not give up. He tried and tried to clean the tomato.
Finally, Tim got all the dirt off the tomato. He was so proud of himself. He showed his mom the clean tomato. She was happy for Tim. They ate the tomato together, and it was the best tomato they ever had.

Jim was taking a walk in the mountains when he noticed a fake mountain. He was curious and decided to go closer. When he got to the mountain, he noticed a bunny chewing a piece of purple grass. 
Jim asked the bunny, "What are you doing?" The bunny replied, "I'm chewing this grass, it's really tasty!" Jim said, "But why did you choose a fake mountain?". The bunny replied, "I like it here because it's big and I can have some peace and quiet!" 
Jim smiled and took a seat next to the bunny. He said, "Chewing grass is fun! Can I try?" The bunny nodded and handed him a piece of grass. Jim chewed the grass and it was surprisingly sweet. He thanked the bunny before getting up and continuing his walk in the mountains.

Sara and Lily are best friends. They like to play with dolls and dress up. They hug each other every day. They are popular in their class. Everyone wants to be their friend.
One day, a new girl comes to their class. Her name is Mia. She has long hair and pretty clothes. She brings a big box with her. She says it is a gift for the class. She asks who wants to help her open it.
Sara and Lily raise their hands. They want to see what is in the box. But Mia says no. She says she only needs one helper. She picks Sara. She says Sara is the prettiest and the nicest. She says Lily is not.
Lily feels sad and angry. She thinks Mia is mean and rude. She thinks Sara is not her friend anymore. She runs away to the corner. She cries.
Sara sees Lily crying. She feels sorry and confused. She likes Mia, but she loves Lily. She does not want to hurt Lily. She tells Mia to wait. She goes to Lily. She says she is sorry. She says she is still her friend. She says she wants to share the gift with her. She hugs Lily.
Lily hugs Sara back. She says she is sorry too. She says she is still her friend. She says she wants to see the gift with her. They smile.
They go back to Mia. They ask her to open the box. Mia opens the box. Inside the box are many books and crayons and stickers. They are for the class to use and enjoy. Mia says she is sorry for being mean and rude. She says she was nervous and lonely. She says she wants to be their friend. They say yes. They hug Mia.
They share the books and crayons and stickers with the class. They have fun. They are happy. They are friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big jug. The jug was very loyal. It always stayed in one place and never moved. It liked to help people. It held water for them to drink.
One day, a group of friends came to the jug. They wanted to unite and play together. The jug was happy to see them. The friends played near the jug and had so much fun. They laughed and jumped around.
But then, something unexpected happened. The loyal jug began to move! It started to dance with the friends. Everyone was surprised and laughed even more. The jug had become a part of their play. They all united and had the best day ever.

Lily and Ben were playing with their dolls in the living room. They liked to make up stories and act them out. Lily was the princess and Ben was the knight. They had a castle, a dragon and a magic wand.
"Help me, help me!" Lily cried. "The dragon is coming to eat me!"
"Don't worry, princess!" Ben said. "I will save you with my magic wand. Repeat after me: dragon, go away!"
"Dragon, go away!" Lily repeated.
Ben waved his wand and pretended that the dragon flew away. He ran to Lily and hugged her.
"You are very brave, princess!" he said. "Now you are safe and we can have a feast."
"Yes, let's have a feast!" Lily said. "I am very hungry."
They went to the kitchen and looked for some food. They saw a big cake on the table. It had pink frosting and sprinkles. It looked very yummy.
"Wow, look at that cake!" Ben said. "Can we have some, please?"
"I don't know," Lily said. "Mommy said we have to wait until after dinner. She said it is for Daddy's birthday."
"But we are very hungry," Ben said. "And we saved the princess. We deserve a reward."
He winked at Lily and smiled.
"Maybe we can have a little bit," he said. "Mommy won't notice. Come on, it will be fun."
Lily was not sure. She did not want to make Mommy angry. But the cake looked so good. And Ben was her best friend. She winked back and nodded.
"OK, let's have a little bit," she said.
They climbed on a chair and reached for the cake. They cut a big slice and ate it. It was very sweet and soft. They liked it a lot.
"Yum, yum, yum!" they said.
They cut another slice and ate it. And another. And another. They did not stop until they ate the whole cake. They felt very full and happy.
"That was the best cake ever!" Ben said.
"Yes, it was!" Lily said.
They wiped their mouths and went back to the living room. They did not see Mommy coming home with Daddy. They did not hear them open the door. They did not see their angry faces when they saw the empty plate on the table.
They only heard Mommy's voice when she shouted.
"Lily! Ben! What have you done?"

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful girl named Lily. She had a diary where she drew pictures of her day. Lily loved to share her diary with her friends.
One sunny day, Lily went to the park. She saw her friend, Tom, near a big tree. Tom was sad because he lost his toy car. Lily wanted to help Tom find his toy car. "Let's look for your car together," she said.
Lily and Tom looked high and low. They leaned down to look under the slides and leaned over to look in the bushes. Finally, they found the toy car near the swings. Tom was so happy, and they played together all day.

Once upon a time, there was a big rock. It was icy and cold. The rock liked to play at noon, when the sun was high in the sky. Every day, the rock would wait for noon so it could play with its friend, the sun.
One day, the rock woke up and saw the sky was dark. It was noon, but the sun was not there. The rock felt sad because it could not play with the sun. It waited and waited, but the sun did not come.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big cloud came and hugged the rock. The cloud was warm and soft. The rock felt happy again. The cloud said, "I am sorry the sun is not here today. But I will play with you!" And so, the icy rock and the big cloud played together all day long, and they became good friends.

Ben and Lily were playing with their ball in the park. They liked to kick the ball and run after it. They were having fun.
But then, a big dog came out of nowhere. It saw the ball and wanted to play too. It ran towards the ball and barked loudly. Ben and Lily were scared of the big dog. They tried to get their ball back, but the dog was faster and stronger.
"Go away, dog!" Ben shouted. "That is our ball!"
The dog did not listen. It picked up the ball in its mouth and shook it hard. The ball popped and made a loud noise. The dog was surprised and dropped the ball. It looked at Ben and Lily with angry eyes.
"Run, Lily, run!" Ben said. He grabbed Lily's hand and they ran away from the dog. But the dog was not happy. It chased after them and tried to bite them.
Ben and Lily ran as fast as they could, but they could not find their mom or dad. They did not know where to go. They were lost and afraid.
The dog caught up with them and jumped on them. It bit Ben's head and made him bleed. Ben screamed and cried. Lily tried to help him, but the dog bit her too. She fell to the ground and did not move.
The dog left them alone and ran away. Ben and Lily were hurt and alone. They did not know what to do. They wished their mom and dad would come and save them.
But no one came. They were too far from the park. No one saw them or heard them. They were in trouble.
Poor Ben and Lily. They just wanted to play with their ball. But something bad happened to them. They were very unlucky. The end.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy was a tough girl who loved to play outside with her friends. One day, Amy and her friends were playing near a big tree, when they saw a little bug.
Amy wanted to pick up the bug, but her friend told her to be careful not to pinch it. Amy was very gentle and picked up the bug without hurting it. She and her friends looked at the bug and saw that it was very pretty. They decided to show it to their mom.
As they walked home, there was a moment of silence. Suddenly, the bug started to grow bigger and bigger! It was not a bug anymore, but a beautiful fairy! The fairy thanked Amy for being so gentle and not pinching her. She gave Amy and her friends a wish for being so kind. The kids were very happy and knew that being gentle and kind was always the best thing to do.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play in a big maze. It was a cold, icy day. Tim put on his warm coat and hat. He was excited to find his way through the maze.
In the maze, Tim saw a big truck. The truck was loading big blocks of ice. Tim wanted to help. He picked up a block of ice and put it on the truck. But, the ice was very slippery. It fell off the truck and blocked the path.
Tim was sad. He wanted to fix it. He thought and thought. Then, he had an idea. Tim found a stick and pushed the ice out of the way. The path was clear again. Tim was happy. He finished the maze and went home to tell his mom about his icy adventure.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a wet leopard. The wet leopard was sad because he was all wet from the rain. He wanted to be dry and happy like the other animals.
One day, the wet leopard met a kind monkey. The monkey saw that the leopard was suffering. "Why are you so wet and sad?" asked the monkey. The wet leopard said, "I don't like being wet. I want to be dry like you and the other animals."
The kind monkey decided to help the wet leopard. They found a big tree with lots of leaves to hide under. The wet leopard started to become dry and happy. The monkey and the leopard became good friends.
The other animals saw how the kind monkey helped the wet leopard. They learned that it is good to help others who are suffering. The animals in the forest became kinder to one another, and they all lived happily ever after.
The moral of the story is to always help others who are suffering, even if they are different from you.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. The park had a big gate. Tim saw his friend, Sam, near the gate. Sam was looking for a place to play.
"Hi Sam," said Tim. "I recommend we play near the swings. It's a perfect spot!"
Sam agreed and they played together on the swings. They had lots of fun. Tim's mom watched them and was happy to see them play together.
At the end of the day, Tim and Sam said goodbye at the gate. They promised to play together again soon.

One day, a cat named Tom and a dog named Max were playing in the sun. They were very happy and had big smiles on their faces. They liked to play because they were both flexible and could jump and run.
After a while, Tom said to Max, "I am tired. Let's take a nap." Max agreed, and they both lay down under a big tree. The tree had many leaves that made a nice shade for them to rest in. They closed their eyes and took a nice nap.
When they woke up, they felt much better. Tom and Max smiled at each other and said, "That was a good nap!" They went back to playing, jumping, and running, happy to be together and enjoy their day.

Once upon a time, there was a pink chicken. This pink chicken lived on a farm with many other animals. The pink chicken had a lot of friends, like the cow, the pig, and the duck.
One sunny day, the pink chicken went for a walk. She saw a big, red ball. She was so happy! The pink chicken wanted to play with the ball. But the ball started to roll away. The pink chicken tried to catch it, but it was too fast.
The pink chicken began to scream. Her friends heard her scream and came to help. The cow, the pig, and the duck all ran to the pink chicken. Together, they stopped the ball from rolling away. The pink chicken was so happy that her friends helped her. They all played with the big, red ball and had a fun day.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the store with his mom. They wanted to buy a new chair. Tim saw a big, comfortable chair that he liked. It was very soft and nice to sit on. Tim's mom said they could buy it.
When they got home, Tim put the chair in the living room. He was so happy to have a comfortable chair to sit in. Tim liked to read books and watch TV in his new chair.
