One day, when it was fall, Tim saw a big pile of leaves outside. He wanted to play in the leaves. Tim ran outside and jumped into the pile. He had so much fun playing in the leaves. After playing, Tim went back inside and sat in his comfortable chair. He was very happy.

One day, a boy named Tim was very excite to go to the store with his mom. They walked hand in hand to the big store with lots of toys. Tim loved to look at all the fun toys and pick one to take home.
Inside the store, Tim saw a big, fearful dog toy. It was big and had a loud bark. Tim was scared at first, but his mom said, "Don't be scared, it's just a toy." Tim smiled and started to play with the dog toy.
After playing with the dog toy, Tim and his mom bought a small, cute toy car. Tim was so happy and couldn't wait to play with it at home. They left the store, and Tim knew he would not be scared of the big dog toy anymore.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big house with a garden. They want to see the flowers and the birds.
"Let's go inside," Ben says. "Maybe we can find some tea and cookies."
Anna is not sure. She thinks the house looks scary. But Ben is brave. He holds her hand and they walk to the door.
They knock on the door. A lady opens it. She has a big smile and a nice dress. She looks friendly.
"Hello, children," she says. "Do you want to come in? I have some tea and cookies for you."
Anna and Ben are happy. They follow the lady into the house. She leads them to a room with a table and chairs. She pours some tea and gives them some cookies.
"Sit down and relax," she says. "You must be tired from playing."
Anna and Ben sit down and eat the cookies. They are very sweet and yummy. They drink the tea. It is very hot and bitter. They start to feel sleepy and dizzy.
The lady watches them with a wicked grin. She has a big knife in her hand. She is not friendly. She is a witch. She wants to cook them for dinner.
"Sleep well, my pretties," she says. "You will make a delicious meal."
Anna and Ben try to scream, but they can't. They fall asleep on the table. The witch laughs and turns on the oven. She is going to eat them. The end.

One day, a modest dog named Mark went for a walk. He had a big stomach because he loved to eat. While walking, he met a small cat. The cat was sad and hungry.
"Hello, cat," said Mark. "Why are you sad?"
The cat looked at Mark and said, "I am hungry. I have no food."
Mark wanted to help the cat. He thought about his big stomach and knew he could share his food. So, Mark took the cat to his house. They ate together and became good friends.
From that day on, Mark and the cat played and shared food every day. The cat was not sad anymore, and Mark was happy to have a new friend.

One day, a big tiger was walking in the forest. He saw a little boy. The little boy was sad. The tiger asked, "Why are you sad?"
The little boy said, "I want to play, but I am too small. No one wants to play with me. I am not fit to play big games."
The tiger had an idea. "Let's play a game called 'guess'," said the tiger. "I will think of something, and you will guess what it is."
The little boy was happy. They played the game. The boy guessed right, and the tiger guessed wrong. They played and played until the sun went down. The boy was not sad anymore. The tiger and the boy became best friends. They learned that being fit does not matter when you have a friend who cares.

Sam and Lily like to fish with their dad. They have a big lake near their house. They have poles, hooks, worms and a bucket. They go to the lake in the morning.
"Look, dad, I have a fish!" Sam says. He pulls his pole hard. The fish is big and shiny. It jumps and splashes in the water.
"Wow, good job, Sam!" dad says. He helps Sam take the fish off the hook and put it in the bucket. "That is a nice fish. Do you want to keep it or let it go?"
"I want to keep it. I want to show mom," Sam says. He is happy and proud.
Lily watches Sam and wants to catch a fish too. She throws her hook in the water and waits. She hopes a fish will bite. She thinks fishing is easy.
But no fish comes to her hook. She waits and waits. She feels bored and sad. She looks at Sam's fish in the bucket and wishes she had one too.
"Dad, why can't I catch a fish?" Lily asks. She sounds sad.
"Don't worry, Lily. Fishing takes time and patience. Sometimes you catch a fish, sometimes you don't. You just have to keep trying," dad says. He gives Lily a hug and a smile. "Here, let me help you. Maybe we can find a better spot."
Dad takes Lily's pole and walks with her to another part of the lake. He shows her how to cast her hook and how to watch for signs of fish. He tells her stories about fishing when he was a kid.
Lily listens and learns. She feels better and more hopeful. She tries again and again. She enjoys fishing with dad.
Soon, she feels a tug on her pole. She gets excited and pulls hard. She has a fish too! It is small and colorful. It wiggles and flaps on the hook.
"I have a fish! I have a fish!" Lily shouts. She shows dad her fish. He helps her take it off the hook and put it in the bucket.
"Yay, Lily! You did it! That is a beautiful fish. I'm so proud of you," dad says. He hugs Lily and kisses her cheek. "You see, fishing is not easy, but it is fun. And you never know what you will catch."
Lily smiles and hugs dad back. She looks at her fish and Sam's fish in the bucket. She feels happy and proud too.
"Can we show mom our fish?" she asks.
"Of course we can. Let's go home and show mom our fish. And maybe we can cook them for dinner," dad says.
"Yummy!" Sam and Lily say. They pick up their poles and their bucket. They walk with dad to the car. They had a good day fishing. They can't wait to tell mom.

Once upon a time, in a land of peace, there lived a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to measure things with her small ruler. She would measure her toys, her bed, and even her dog. One day, Lily felt upset because she could not find her ruler.
Lily looked everywhere for her ruler. She looked in her room, the kitchen, and the backyard. She asked her mom, dad, and brother if they had seen it. They all said no. Lily was so upset that she started to cry.
Just then, her dog, Spot, came running with something in his mouth. It was her ruler! Spot had taken it to play with. Lily was so happy and hugged Spot. From that day on, Lily and Spot measured things together, and they all lived happily in their land of peace.

Sara and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. Sara has a toy gun. Ben has a ball.
"Let's play shoot and jog!" Sara says. "You jog and I shoot. Then we switch."
"OK!" Ben says. He throws the ball in the air and starts to jog. Sara aims her toy gun and says "Bang! Bang! Bang!" She pretends to shoot the ball and Ben.
Ben laughs and dodges. He catches the ball and says "Now it's my turn!" He gives Sara the ball and takes the toy gun. He says "Bang! Bang! Bang!" He pretends to shoot Sara and the ball.
Sara giggles and runs. She throws the ball back and forth. She says "You can't catch me!"
They play shoot and jog for a long time. They have fun. They get tired and hungry.
"Let's go home and eat!" Sara says. "Mom made cookies. They are delicious!"
"Yum!" Ben says. "I love cookies!"
They hold hands and walk home. They share the cookies and milk. They are happy. They are friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big, happy dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his best friend, a little girl named Lily. They would run and jump all day long. One day, while they were playing, they found a shiny diamond in the grass.
Spot picked up the diamond with his mouth and showed it to Lily. "Look, Lily!" he barked, "We found a diamond!" Lily clapped her hands and laughed. "Wow, Spot! That's so pretty!" she said.
They decided to share the diamond with their friends. They took it to their friend Sam, who was a little boy. Sam was very happy to see the diamond. "Thank you for sharing, Spot and Lily!" he said with a big smile.
Next, they shared the diamond with their friend Kitty, who was a small cat. Kitty purred and said, "Thank you, Spot and Lily! I love the diamond!" Spot and Lily felt very happy that they could share the diamond with their friends.
At the end of the day, Spot and Lily put the diamond in a special box. They knew that they could share it with more friends tomorrow. They both felt very happy and went to sleep, dreaming about all the fun they would have the next day.

Once upon a time, there was a big mixer. It was unusual because it could talk. It loved to teach little kids how to bake. One day, the mixer was teaching a girl named Lucy how to make a cake. Lucy was happy and excited.
As they were mixing the cake, something unexpected happened. The mixer started to sing a funny song. Lucy laughed and danced along. The mixer and Lucy were having a great time. They didn't know that the cake mix was getting bigger and bigger.
Finally, the cake mix was so big that it filled the whole room. Lucy and the mixer were surprised! They thought the cake mix would stay small. But they were not sad. They had fun playing in the big cake mix. The unusual mixer had taught Lucy a very important lesson: sometimes, unexpected things can be fun too.

Bob was walking on the beach, when he saw something strange in the sand. He bent down and tried to guess what it was. Suddenly he heard someone calling him. He turned around and saw his friend Lizzie.
Lizzie asked him, "What are you doing Bob?"
Bob replied, "I'm trying to guess what this is!"
He pointed to the sand, and Lizzie said, "Let's find out! Come on, follow me!"
So, Bob and Lizzie started digging in the sand. They kept digging until they finally found something. It was a basket filled with tasty snacks!
Bob said, "Wow! This looks really tasty."
Lizzie smiled and said, "Let's enjoy it together. After all, we found it together!"
So, Bob and Lizzie enjoyed their tasty snacks on the beach, with sand everywhere. They had a lot of fun!

Once upon a time, there was an adventurous little bird named Tim. Tim lived in a small birdcage in a big house. Every day, he would look out of his birdcage and wish he could go outside.
One day, Tim saw that the door of his birdcage was open. He thought, "This is my chance to go outside and see the world!" So, he flew out of the birdcage and into the big house. He saw many things he had never seen before. He saw a big chair, a big table, and a big window.
Tim flew to the big window and said, "Wow, I can see the whole world from here!" Just then, a big cat named Tom came into the room. Tom saw Tim and said, "Hello, little bird! Do you want to play with me?" Tim was scared of the big cat, so he flew back to his birdcage as fast as he could. He found that he could still fit inside his safe birdcage. Tim was happy to be back in his birdcage, but he knew he would always remember his adventurous day outside.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside with his big red ball. One day, while playing, his ball rolled near a tall pole. Tim felt dizzy looking up at the pole.
A friendly bird, named Sally, saw Tim and flew down. "Hi, Tim! Why are you dizzy?" asked Sally. Tim said, "I was looking at the tall pole. My ball is near it, and I want to get it back." Sally wanted to help Tim.
Sally had an idea. She told Tim to open his eyes wide and look at the pole again. Tim did as Sally said, and he didn't feel dizzy anymore. They both smiled and laughed. Tim got his ball back, and they played together all day.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty fish named Fin. Fin lived in a reef with many other fish friends. They liked to swim and play all day long.
One day, Fin met a new fish named Bubbles. Bubbles was shy and didn't talk much. Fin wanted to be friends with Bubbles. So, Fin told Bubbles, "Let's play together!"
Bubbles smiled and said, "Okay!" They played hide and seek in the reef. They had lots of fun. After that, Fin and Bubbles were best friends. They always told each other stories and played together in their pretty reef home.

Lily and Ben were very happy. They were going to a hotel with Mom and Dad. They packed their bags and got in the car. The hotel was big and shiny. It had many rooms and a pool.
"Can we go to the pool, Mom?" Lily asked.
"Yes, but first we have to check in and put our bags in our room," Mom said. "The hotel is very organized. They have a map and a key for us."
Lily and Ben followed Mom and Dad to the front desk. They got their map and key and went to the elevator. The elevator went up and up. Lily and Ben pressed their noses to the glass and saw the city below.
"Wow, look at all the cars and buildings!" Ben said.
They reached their floor and found their room. It was cozy and clean. It had two beds, a TV, and a window. Lily and Ben put their bags on their beds and ran to the window. They saw the pool and the garden.
"Look, there's the pool!" Lily said. "It's blue and sparkly. And there's a slide and a fountain!"
"Let's go, let's go!" Ben said. "I want to jump in the water!"
"Okay, but first we have to change into our swimsuits and put on sunscreen," Mom said. "The sun is very bright and hot. We don't want to get burned."
Lily and Ben nodded. They changed into their swimsuits and put on sunscreen. Mom and Dad did the same. They grabbed their towels and hats and went to the elevator. They pressed the button for the pool.
"Are you ready to have fun?" Dad asked.
"Yes, yes, yes!" Lily and Ben said.
They got out of the elevator and ran to the pool. They saw other kids and families playing and splashing. Lily and Ben smiled and jumped in the water. They felt the cool and wet on their skin. They laughed and kicked and swam. They went on the slide and the fountain. They had a lot of fun.
They stayed at the pool until it was time for dinner. They dried off and went back to their room. They changed into their clothes and went to the restaurant. They ate yummy food and drank juice. They talked about their day and what they wanted to do tomorrow.
They went back to their room and brushed their teeth. They got into their beds and snuggled with their stuffed animals. Mom and Dad kissed them goodnight and turned off the light.
"Did you have a good day, Lily?" Ben asked.
"Yes, I did, Ben," Lily said. "I liked the hotel and the pool. They were very nice."
"Me too," Ben said. "I liked jumping in the water. It was very fun."
They closed their eyes and fell asleep. They dreamed of the hotel and the pool. They were very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a small chair. The chair was very pretty. Everyone liked the chair because it was attractive.
One day, the chair went to a big room. In the room, there were many things to see. The chair wanted to display how nice it was. So, the chair stood in the middle of the room.
All the people came to see the chair. They said, "Wow, what a pretty chair!" The chair was very happy. It liked to show off and make people smile. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a bottle on the ground. He picked it up to examine it. It was a high bottle, much taller than his hand. Tim thought it was very pretty and wanted to show his friend, Lily.
"Look, Lily!" said Tim, holding the bottle high. "I found this big bottle! Isn't it nice?" Lily looked at the bottle and wanted it too. They both started to fight over the bottle. They pulled and pulled, but the bottle was too strong.
Just then, the bottle popped open and out came a tiny bird! The bird looked at Tim and Lily and said, "Thank you for setting me free! I was stuck in the bottle. Because you helped me, I will give each of you a pretty stone." The bird gave them each a shiny stone and flew away.
Tim and Lily looked at their stones and then at each other. They felt bad for fighting over the bottle. They learned that it is better to share and be kind to each other. From that day on, Tim and Lily promised to always share and be good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Bobo. He wanted to learn how to hop high. But every time he tried, he felt helpless. He could not hop high at all.
One day, Bobo met a big bunny named Bubu. Bubu was good at hopping high. Bubu said, "I can help you, Bobo!" So, they went to the park to start hopping.
With Bubu's help, Bobo tried and tried again. At first, he was still helpless. But Bubu did not give up on him. After many tries, Bobo began to hop higher and higher. In the end, Bobo succeeded in hopping high. Bobo was very happy, and he thanked Bubu for being a good friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was feeling miserable one day because it was raining outside, and he could not play with his friends. So, he stayed inside his house and looked for something fun to do.
Tim found a big toy called a microscope in his room. He put small things under the microscope to see them up close. He saw a tiny bug and said, "Wow! You look so big and funny!" The bug seemed to laugh with its tiny legs moving fast.
Just then, Tim's mom came into the room and saw him playing with the microscope. She said, "Tim, you found a great way to have fun inside!" Tim smiled and felt happy again. From that day on, even when it rained, Tim was never miserable because he could always play with his microscope and laugh at the tiny, funny things he saw.

One day, a girl named Sue got a job to deliver a note. The note was very important. Sue had to be careful with it. She put the note in her bag and went on her way.
As Sue walked, she met a boy named Tom. Tom asked, "What do you have in your bag?" Sue said, "I have a note to deliver. It is very important." Tom wanted to help Sue. They walked together to deliver the note.
When they got to the house, they gave the note to the lady who lived there. The lady opened the note and laughed. "This note is not for me," she said. Sue and Tom looked at each other, surprised. They had delivered the note to the wrong house! They said sorry and went to find the right house. In the end, Sue and Tom became good friends and laughed about their mistake.

Once upon a time, there was a big forest. In the forest, many animals lived. They all liked to play and have fun. One day, they wanted to find a new place to play. They looked for the best spot available.
They saw a nice open space near the trees. The animals decided to settle there. They were very happy. They played games like tag and hide-and-seek. The sun was shining, and the sky was blue.
As the day went on, the animals became tired. They said goodbye to their friends and went back to their homes. The forest was quiet again. The animals knew they could always come back to play in their new spot.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She liked to collect pretty things. One day, she saw a big heart. The heart was red and shiny. Lily wanted to have it.
Lily was very tired, but she walked and walked to get the heart. She picked it up and started to go home. The heart was heavy, but Lily did not want to let it go. She was so tired, but she kept walking.
At last, Lily could not walk anymore. She fell down and the heart broke. She was sad because she could not keep the pretty heart. And now, she was tired and had nothing.

One day, a little frog named Tim went for a walk. It was a calm Tuesday. Tim liked to jump high and far. He saw a big rock and wanted to jump over it. But the rock was very big, and Tim was scared.
Tim met a small bird named Sue. Sue said, "Hi Tim! What's wrong?" Tim said, "I want to jump over the big rock, but I am scared." Sue said, "I can help you, Tim. Let's jump together!"
Tim and Sue walked back to the big rock. They took a deep breath and jumped as high and far as they could. They made it over the rock! Tim was so happy. He thanked Sue and they played together for the rest of the calm Tuesday.

Amanda and Max were best friends, and always played together. Today, they wanted to go to the park. Amanda was very ready to go, but Max wanted to play at home, so they began to quarrel. 
Amanda spoke, her voice getting louder and louder. Max didn't agree and raised his own voice in response.
Mama heard their voices and went to their room to talk to them. She asked them why they were having a quarrel. With a sigh, Amanda said that she wanted to go to the park, but Max wanted to play at home. Mama smiled and said it was a good idea to go to the park. 
Max agreed and smiled. Amanda was so happy, and they both danced as they got ready to go to the park together.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy cat named Tom. Tom loved to sleep all day and play all night. One day, Tom found a mint plant in the garden. He liked the smell of the mint, so he decided to play with it.
Tom played with the mint plant every day. One day, Tom's friend, a little mouse named Jerry, saw Tom playing with the mint and wanted to play too. But Tom did not want to share the mint with Jerry. He was very selfish.
Jerry was sad and asked Tom to remove the mint plant so they could play together. Tom did not listen to Jerry and continued to play with the mint alone. One day, while Tom was playing with the mint, he accidentally broke the plant. The mint was gone, and Tom was left all alone. No one wanted to play with the lazy and selfish cat anymore, and Tom was sad forever.

Once there was a man who had a very fancy wife. One day the man went outside on a very cold day so his wife said, "Don't go out. You will freeze." The man didn't listen and went out anyway. 
When he got outside, the man's joints quickly started to freeze up and he was stuck. He couldn't move. He called out to his wife, "Help me! I'm frozen!" The wife quickly ran outside and grabbed her husband's arm. She said, "Let's go inside and get you warm." 
The man and his wife went inside and he drank some warm fluids and snuggled up under blankets. After a few minutes, he was feeling better. The man thanked his wife for taking care of him. She said, "Well, you should listen to me next time!" The man agreed that it was a good idea. They both had a good laugh and went to get some fancy dinner.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful bird. The bird lived on a roof. The bird liked to sing and play all day.
One day, the bird saw something strange. It was a big, cold thing. The bird did not know what it was. The bird went to see the cold thing. When the bird touched it, the bird began to freeze.
But then, something unexpected happened. The cold thing started to talk. It said, "I am a magic ice cube. I can help you if you are nice to me." The bird was nice to the ice cube, and the ice cube made the bird warm again. They became good friends and played together every day.

Anna was a smart daughter. She liked to learn new things and play with her toys. She also liked to help her mom and dad with chores. One day, her mom asked her to water the plants in the garden. Anna was happy to do it. She took the big can and filled it with water from the tap.
She went outside and saw the flowers and the trees. They were pretty and colorful. She started to pour water on them, gently and carefully. She saw how they drank the water and looked happy. She smiled and said, "You're welcome, plants. I hope you grow big and strong."
She moved to the next plant, but she did not see the rock on the ground. She tripped and fell. The water can flew out of her hand and splashed all over her. She was wet and cold. She cried and said, "Ouch! That hurt!"
Her mom heard her cry and ran to her. She saw her daughter on the ground, soaked and sad. She hugged her and said, "Oh, Anna, are you okay? I'm sorry you fell. Let me help you up."
She lifted her up and checked her for injuries. She saw a small scrape on her knee, but nothing serious. She said, "You're a brave girl, Anna. You only have a little boo-boo. Let's go inside and get you dry and warm. Then we can have some cookies and milk. How does that sound?"
Anna nodded and said, "Okay, mom. Thank you. I love you." She hugged her mom and smiled. She was still a smart daughter, and a good helper. She learned to be more careful next time. And she enjoyed the cookies and milk.

One day, a little girl named Lucy found a big spoon. She thought it was fun to play with. Lucy liked to hit the spoon on a pot. It made a loud sound. It made her laugh and clap her hands.
Lucy showed her friend, Tom, the big spoon. Tom did not know what to do with it. He just gave a shrug. Lucy smiled and showed him how to make the loud sound. Tom tried it and laughed too.
They played with the spoon all day. They made loud sounds and danced. It was a fun day for Lucy and Tom. They were happy to find the big spoon.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Lily who had a very special memory. It was of a wide, beautiful lake that she had visited with her family when she was younger.
On a hot summer's day, Lily decided to go back to the lake and have a swim. She splashed her way in and the cool water felt amazing.
As Lily swam around, she laughed and smiled remembering all the happy times she had shared at the lake with her family. She closed her eyes and felt the sunshine on her face as she remembered.
Suddenly, she heard a voice calling out to her. "Hey, Lily! Are you going to continue swimming or are you going to just float around?" she heard her dad ask.
Lily opened her eyes and smiled. "I'm going to splash around for a bit longer!" she shouted back.
So, she and her dad splashed and played together in the wide lake, creating special memories that Lily would never forget.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She had a fancy dress that she loved very much. Mia wore her fancy dress every day. One day, Mia saw a boy who was sad. He was sad because he did not have a fancy dress like Mia.
Mia wanted to help the sad boy. She thought about what she could do. Then, Mia had an idea! She could give the boy her fancy dress. But Mia loved her dress so much, she did not want to give it away.
Mia thought about the sad boy all day. She knew she had to do something. So, Mia took off her fancy dress and gave it to the boy. The boy was so happy! He did not scream, but he said "thank you" with a big smile. Mia felt a warm feeling of love in her heart.
The next day, Mia's mom gave her a new fancy dress. It was even more beautiful than the first one! Mia learned that when you share love and help others, good things come back to you.

Once upon a time, in a big sky, there was a little star. The star was very nice and kind. Everyone liked the star because it was so compassionate. It always helped others and made them feel good.
One day, a big cloud came and wanted to cover the star. The star was sad and scared. It did not want to be hidden away. But it did not know how to protect itself from the big cloud.
Then, a strong wind came along. The wind saw the star and the big cloud. The wind wanted to help the star. So, it blew the big cloud away. The star was happy and safe again. The wind had protected the star. From that day on, the star and the wind were the best of friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play by the river. He liked to watch the water and throw rocks. The sun was shining and it was an easy day to have fun.
Soon, the sky turned dark and it started to rain. The rain made the river rise and Tim saw a flood coming. He was scared and did not know what to do. He requested help from his friend, the big tree, but the tree could not move.
The flood came and took Tim away. The big tree was sad, and the easy day was gone. Tim should have gone home when the rain started. Now, the big tree will miss Tim and the fun they had.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom was an adventurous boy. He loved to play outside with his dog, Max. One day, Tom and Max went to the park to play.
At the park, they saw a big steel box. Tom said, "Max, let's see what's inside!" They kneeled down and opened the box. Inside, they found a big red ball. Tom and Max were very happy and started playing with the ball.
Suddenly, the ball started to talk! It said, "Hi, I'm Bob the Ball! I can grant you one wish!" Tom and Max were surprised. Tom thought for a moment and said, "I wish for more fun adventures with Max!" Bob the Ball smiled and said, "Your wish is granted!" From that day on, Tom and Max had many more fun adventures together.

Once upon a time, there was a small rabbit called Bouncy. He was very happy and loved to bounce. Every day he woke up early and went to the park to bouncing.
One day, Bouncy went to the park and he saw a ticket on the ground. He picked up the ticket and thought it was very special. He decided to keep it.
At night, when the park closed, Bouncy went home with the ticket. But when he arrived, he was very upset. His family had moved away and his house was empty. Bouncy was so upset that he wanted to bounce away his sadness.
So Bouncy bounced away through the night and day until he finally found a new home. Inside the house was filled with warmth and love. Bouncy was so happy and he felt safe. He knew he had finally found a home. He put the ticket in a special place, something to remember his old home.

Once there was a thin parrot named Polly. Polly lived in a big tree with her friend, a little boy named Tim. They liked to play and talk together every day.
One day, Tim and Polly were sitting under the tree. Tim said, "Polly, let's discuss what we want to eat today." Polly thought for a moment and said, "I want to eat some fruit." Tim agreed and said, "Yes, fruit sounds good."
So, Tim and Polly went to find some fruit to eat. They found apples, bananas, and grapes. They ate the fruit and had a fun day playing together.

One sunny day, a little boy named Tim went to the park. It was an ordinary day, just like any other. At the park, Tim saw a big, red ball. He wanted to play with it, but another little girl named Lily also wanted the ball.
"I want the ball!" said Tim. "No, I want it!" said Lily. They both tried to grab the ball at the same time, but it split in two! Tim and Lily were both very sad.
Just then, an old man came by. He saw the two sad kids and said, "Why don't you both take a nap under that tree and think about how to fix the ball?" Tim and Lily agreed. After their nap, they had an idea. They used tape to put the ball back together. Now, they could both play with the ball and became good friends.

One day, a boy named Tim found a big, red box. The box had a lock on it. Tim wanted to open the box, but he did not have a key. He looked around and saw a nail on the floor. He thought, "Maybe I can use this nail to open the lock."
Tim tried to use the nail to open the lock. He pushed the nail into the lock and wiggled it around. Suddenly, there was a loud noise. The box popped open! Tim was very happy. Inside the box, he found a toy car and a ball.
Tim's friend, Sam, came over and saw the open box. Sam asked, "How did you open the box?" Tim said, "I used a nail to open the lock!" Sam and Tim played with the toy car and the ball all day. They had lots of fun with their new toys.

Molly was feeling a bit nervous. It was her first day of school! She was excited to meet all the new children but she was also a little scared. 
Molly put on her pink dress and matching shoes and took a deep breath. She was ready! 
Molly's mom and dad walked her to school. When they arrived, her mom knelt down and gave Molly a big hug. Then she poured a packet of sunflower seeds into Molly's pocket. 
Molly said goodbye to her parents, feeling a bit nervous. She walked into the school and heard the other children laughing. She closed her eyes and took a deep breath. 
Molly soon began making lots of new friends. She forgot she was ever nervous and had a lovely first day at school.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. She had a sister named Sue. Sue was bigger than Jane, but Jane was very smart. They loved to play together every day.
One day, Jane and Sue found a big, heavy ball in their yard. They didn't know where it came from. They wanted to play with it, but it was too heavy for Jane. Sue tried to help Jane lift the ball, but it was too heavy for both of them.
Then, something strange happened. The heavy ball began to move all by itself! It rolled and rolled until it hit a tree. The tree shook, and a big bird came out. The bird flew down and gave Jane a little slap on her hand. Jane was surprised but not hurt. The bird then picked up the heavy ball with its strong feet and flew away. Jane and Sue looked at each other, laughed, and went back to playing their own games.

One day, a helpful cat saw a row of birds. The cat had a question. "Why are you in a row?" the cat asked the birds. The birds said, "We are in a row to get food."
The cat wanted to help the birds get food. But the birds did not trust the cat. They knew cats sometimes eat birds. The cat was sad, but he still wanted to help. The cat had an idea. He went away and came back with a big bag of seeds.
The birds saw the cat and the seeds. They were happy. The cat showed the birds he was helpful and not mean. The birds and the cat became friends. The birds knew they could trust the cat. The cat learned that being helpful is good.

Tom and Sue were twins who liked to play in the barn. There were many things to see and touch in the barn, like hay, tools, and animals. But their favorite thing was the big barrel that was high on a shelf. The barrel was full of apples that their dad had stored for the winter. They wanted to taste the apples, but they could not reach the barrel.
One day, they had a plan. They found a ladder and put it near the shelf. Tom climbed the ladder and Sue held it. Tom was almost at the barrel when he heard a loud noise. It was their dad, who had come to the barn to feed the horses.
"Tom, Sue, what are you doing?" he asked in a stern voice.
Tom and Sue were scared. They knew they had done something wrong. Tom tried to get down from the ladder, but he slipped and fell. The barrel also fell and broke. Apples rolled everywhere.
Tom and Sue cried. They had hurt themselves and wasted the apples. Their dad was angry, but also worried. He ran to them and checked if they were okay. He hugged them and said, "I'm glad you're not badly hurt, but you should never play with the barrel. It's dangerous and the apples are for later. You have to listen to me and follow the rules. Do you understand?"
Tom and Sue nodded. They said they were sorry and they loved their dad. Their dad said he loved them too, but they had to help him clean up the mess. He also said they could have one apple each as a treat, but only after they finished their chores. Tom and Sue agreed. They learned their lesson and never played with the barrel again.

Once there was a puppy. He was very brave. He wanted to save the world! He had special power to do it too.
His friend, a kitty, wanted to help. She was careful as she walked around. She was always watching and listening. 
One day, the puppy said, "Let's go and save the world!" 
But the kitty didn't think it was a good idea. She said, "We should be careful!" But the puppy kept on going.
They got lost and they couldn't find the way back home. They were all alone and the puppy had no power to save them. The kitty was sad and scared. 
They never found their way back home. The puppy and kitty were lost forever.

Once there was a wise painter called Peter. Peter had been painting for many years and was really good at it. 
One day, Peter was walking around the town looking for a new colour to use in his next painting. He walked along the street until he saw a bright blue door. Peter was surprised and said to himself: "That looks interesting." 
He turned the handle and opened the door. To his surprise, there was a big room filled with paint of all colours. Peter opened his big paintbox and started to choose the colours he liked for his painting. 
Suddenly, he heard a voice. It was coming from outside the door. A small boy was standing there and said: "Hello, Painter! What are you doing?" Peter smiled and said: "I'm choosing the colours for my painting." The boy looked at the bright paints and said: "That looks fun! Can I help?"
Peter smiled and said: "Sure! We can turn this room into a beautiful painting together." He showed the boy how to mix the colours and how to use the brushes. 
The boy was very happy and his eyes lit up. Together, they worked until it was dark outside. The painting they made was very beautiful and everyone said it was the best painting in town!
Peter and the boy looked at the painting and Peter said: "That was fun. You are a very wise painter, young one!"

One day, a loyal dog named Max was walking with his friend, a little boy named Tim. They went to the park to play ball. While they were playing, Max saw a magazine on the ground. He stared at the magazine and barked.
Tim looked at Max and said, "What is it, Max?" Max kept barking and staring at the magazine. Tim picked up the magazine and saw a picture of a lost dog that looked like Max. Tim showed the picture to Max and asked, "Is this you, Max?"
Max nodded his head and wagged his tail. Tim knew they had to help find the lost dog's home. They walked around the park, asking people if they knew the dog in the picture. Finally, a kind old lady said she knew the dog and showed them the way to the lost dog's house.
When they found the lost dog's house, Max and the lost dog were so happy to see each other. The lost dog's family was very thankful to Tim and Max for finding their dog. Tim and Max learned that it is important to help others when they are in need, and they felt very proud of themselves.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a lot of toys, but her favorite was a plastic dog. The dog was always available to play with her. One day, Lily was playing outside with her dog, and she met a new friend named Tim.
Tim saw Lily's plastic dog and said, "I like your dog! Can I play with it too?" Lily smiled and said, "Yes, you can play with my dog, but be careful, it can bite!" They both laughed and started to play with the dog together.
As they played, Lily pretended that the plastic dog was biting Tim's hand. Tim giggled and said, "Ouch, your dog bit me!" Lily and Tim laughed and continued to play. They became the best of friends and played with the plastic dog every day. And from that day on, Lily always shared her toys with Tim.

Sara was sleeping in her bed when she heard a loud noise. It sounded like a big truck or a plane. She opened her eyes and saw a bright light outside her window. She was scared and curious at the same time.
She got out of her bed and ran to the window. She saw a big rocket flying in the sky. It had fire and smoke behind it. It was impressive and beautiful. Sara wanted to see more of it.
She ran to her mom and dad's room and knocked on the door. She said, "Mom, dad, wake up! There is a rocket in the sky! Come and see!"
Her mom and dad were sleepy and grumpy. They did not want to wake up. They said, "Sara, go back to bed. It is too late for rockets. It is just a dream. We will see it tomorrow."
Sara was sad and angry. She did not like that her mom and dad did not believe her. She did not like that they did not want to share her excitement. She said, "No, it is not a dream. It is real. It is amazing. You are missing it. You are boring."
She ran back to her room and watched the rocket until it disappeared. She wished she had someone to watch it with. She felt lonely and misunderstood. She hoped that the rocket would come back another night. She hoped that her mom and dad would wake up and see it with her. She hoped that they would be impressed and proud of her.

Once there was a small mouse. He was very brave and loved to explore. One day, he decided he wanted to climb a cliff to see what was on top.
The cliff was enormous, and it was very high. The mouse was scared, but he kept climbing. He was almost at the top when he heard a growling noise. He looked around and saw a large wolf.
The wolf was very hungry, and he wanted the mouse to be his dinner. He opened his big mouth and the mouse tried to run away. But the wolf bit him and the poor mouse couldn't move anymore.
The moral of the story is that you should not take risks. Sometimes, things are far too dangerous to try.

Jonny was walking through the park when he noticed something big. It was a mustache! He stopped and looked at it. "I want that," said Jonny. 
Then, he saw a man walking towards him. He was wearing the big mustache! 
"Hello there," the man said. "Would you like to try it on?"
Jonny smiled and said, "Yes please!" He was so excited. 
The man put the mustache on Jonny and said, "You look great!" 
Jonny smiled as he looked in a mirror. He felt very special wearing the big mustache. He thanked the man and then ran off. Whenever Jonny passed a mirror, he would look at himself with the mustache and giggle.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Mark and Tim. Mark had a club and he was very excited about it. He wanted to show Tim how to march with it. 
"Let's march with the club," said Mark.
"I don't know how," said Tim.
Mark tried to teach Tim how to march with the club and waved it around, but Tim wasn't having any of it. 
Mark got mad. He shouted "Come on, Tim. It's easy. Just move the club like this!"
Tim felt scared. He didn't know what to do. 
Just then, a lady walking nearby saw them and smiled. She walked over and said "It's ok, Tim. You can do it. Take the club from Mark and have a go."
Tim was excited and took the club from Mark. He tried to march with it and it worked. Mark cheered for him and they both marched together. They had a lot of fun and laughed the whole time. 
The end.

Once upon a time there were two groups of friends. The first group was big, and the second group was small.
One day, the small group was playing and having lots of fun. But suddenly, one of the members became sad. She started to cry.
The big group was a bit scared. They didn't know what to do.
Then the leader of the big group stepped forward. She found a way to make the small group smile again. She called out, "Let's share!"
The big group shared their toys, games and stories with the small group. The small group was very happy and so were the big group.
The big group was proud of their leader. They saw how she spread happiness by sharing.
The small group learned that when you share, it can make everyone feel calm and happy.
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a fierce dog called Rex. He loved running and jumping around outside.
One day, he heard thunder in the sky. He thought "This can't be a good thing! I'm worried!"
He went to his daddy, and asked, "Daddy, why is the sky making a noise?"
His daddy said, "Rex, it's just a storm. It's nothing to be scared of. In fact, it's a good thing, because the rain helps the plants grow."
Rex wanted to find out more, so he went outside and watched the sky carefully. He could see how the storm brought out colours from the clouds. He could feel the rain drops on his fur. It made him feel calm.
Rex realized that sometimes things that look fierce can actually be good. He realized that he should never be afraid of something because it looks different.
The moral of this story is that it's important to remember that things that look tough may have beautiful lessons to teach us. You don't have to be scared of something just because it looks different.

One day, a popular toy named Tim wanted to polish his shoes. He liked to look nice for his friends. Tim lived in a small house with his best friend, a toy named Sue. They played all day and had lots of fun.
One day, a big wind came. Tim and Sue called it a "hurricane." The wind blew and blew. Tim and Sue were scared. They held hands and said, "We need to find a safe place to hide!"
Tim and Sue ran to a big tree. They hid under the tree and waited. The big wind stopped. They looked around and saw that the hurricane had made their small house very clean. It was a big surprise!
Tim and Sue were happy. They went back to their clean house and played. They told their friends about the big wind and how it helped them. Now, they were not scared of hurricanes anymore.

Lily and Tom were friends who liked to play near the creek. They liked to splash in the water, catch frogs and look for shiny stones. One day, they saw a big log across the creek. They wanted to cross it and see what was on the other side.
Lily said, "Let's go, Tom. It looks fun." But Tom said, "Wait, Lily. We need to prepare. The log is wet and slippery. We need to find a stick to help us balance. And we need to tell our moms where we are going."
Lily did not want to wait. She said, "I don't need a stick. I can balance by myself. And our moms know we are near the creek. Come on, Tom. Don't be scared." She ran to the log and stepped on it.
But Tom was right. The log was wet and slippery. Lily lost her balance and fell into the creek. She got wet and cold and scared. She cried for help. Tom heard her and ran to the log. He found a stick and used it to cross the log. He helped Lily out of the creek and gave her his jacket.
Lily said, "Thank you, Tom. You are a good friend. I am sorry I did not listen to you. You were smart to prepare. I was silly to rush. I learned my lesson. Next time, I will be more careful and follow your advice."
Tom said, "You are welcome, Lily. I am glad you are okay. You are a good friend, too. I forgive you. Let's go back to our moms and tell them what happened. They will be happy to see us and give us a hug."
Lily and Tom walked back to their moms, holding hands and smiling. They learned that it is better to prepare than to rush, and that it is good to listen to your friends.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Mia. Mia lived near the sea. Every day, she would go to the beach and play in the sand. She loved to look at the big boats on the water. One day, Mia saw a yacht. It was big and white. Mia smiled when she saw the yacht. She wished she could ride on it.
A kind man named Jack owned the yacht. He saw Mia looking at his boat and walked over to her. "Hello, little girl! Do you like my yacht?" he asked. Mia nodded her head and said, "Yes, I love it! Can I ride on it?" Jack smiled and said, "Of course! Let's go for a ride."
Mia and Jack went on the yacht. They sailed on the sea and had a fun time. The sun was shining and the water was calm. Mia smiled the whole time. She was so happy to be on the yacht. When they came back to the beach, Mia thanked Jack for the ride. She couldn't wait to tell her friends about her sweet adventure on the yacht.

One day, a black cat named Tom was walking in the park. He saw a big bell hanging on a tree. Tom thought, "I want to play with that bell." So, he jumped up and hit the bell with his paw. The bell made a loud sound, "Ding! Ding!"
A little girl named Sally heard the bell and came running. She saw Tom and said, "Hi, cat! Why did you hit the bell?" Tom felt embarrass and didn't know what to say. He just looked down at the ground.
Just then, a big dog came running and barking at Tom. Sally said, "Stop, dog! Don't hurt the cat!" The dog stopped and looked at Sally. She picked up Tom and said, "You are safe now, cat." Tom was happy that Sally saved him.
The dog said, "I'm sorry, cat. I didn't mean to scare you." Tom looked at the dog and said, "It's okay. I'm not scared now." They all became friends and played together in the park.
The moral of the story is: Don't be afraid to ask for help when you need it, and always be kind to others, even when they make a mistake.

Tom and Mia like to create things with pasta. They have many shapes and colors of pasta in a big box. They glue the pasta on paper to make pictures and patterns.
One day, Tom and Mia want to create a pasta house. They take out the pasta and look for the best pieces. Tom says, "We need long pasta for the walls and round pasta for the windows. And we need small pasta for the roof."
Mia says, "OK, let's be organized. We can sort the pasta by shape and color. Then we can find what we need faster." They make piles of pasta on the table. They have a pile of long pasta, a pile of round pasta, a pile of small pasta, and a pile of other pasta.
They start to glue the pasta on a big paper. They make a square with long pasta for the wall. They make four windows with round pasta. They make a triangle with small pasta for the roof. They use other pasta to decorate the door, the chimney, and the flowers.
They are very happy with their pasta house. They show it to their mom and dad. Mom and dad say, "Wow, what a beautiful and organized pasta house! You are very creative, Tom and Mia. We are proud of you." Tom and Mia smile and hug their mom and dad. They love to create things with pasta.

Lily and Tom are playing with paper and scissors. They like to cut shapes and make pictures. Lily cuts a big circle and a small triangle. She puts the triangle on the circle and says, "Look, I made a hat!"
Tom cuts a long strip and a small square. He puts the square on the tip of the strip and says, "Look, I made a flag!"
Lily and Tom want to show their pictures to Mom. They run to the kitchen, where Mom is making a cake. They hold up their pictures and say, "Mom, look what we made!"
Mom smiles and says, "Wow, you are very creative! I like your hat and your flag. But be careful with the scissors, they are sharp. And don't cut anything else, okay?"
Lily and Tom nod and say, "Okay, Mom." They go back to the living room, where they have more paper and scissors. They want to make more pictures.
But then, Tom sees Lily's hat. He likes it more than his flag. He says, "Lily, can I have your hat? It is so pretty."
Lily says, "No, Tom, it is mine. I made it. You have your flag. It is nice, too."
Tom says, "But I want your hat. It is better than my flag. Give it to me."
Lily says, "No, Tom, you can't have it. It is not fair. Go away."
Tom gets angry. He grabs Lily's hat and tries to pull it. Lily holds on to her hat and tries to push him. They fight over the hat. They yell and cry.
Mom hears the noise and comes to the living room. She sees Lily and Tom fighting over the hat. She says, "Stop, stop, stop! What are you doing? Why are you fighting?"
Lily and Tom stop and look at Mom. They are both sad and sorry. They say, "Mom, we are sorry. We wanted to share, but we couldn't."
Mom says, "It is okay to want something, but it is not okay to take it without asking. And it is not okay to fight over it. You have to respect each other and your things. You have to ask nicely and wait for an answer. And you have to accept it if the answer is no. Do you understand?"
Lily and Tom nod and say, "Yes, Mom, we understand. We are sorry."
Mom says, "It is okay. I forgive you. But you have to forgive each other, too. Can you do that?"
Lily and Tom look at each other. They say, "Yes, Mom, we can do that. We are sorry, too."
They hug each other and say, "I forgive you. I love you."
Mom smiles and says, "I love you, too. Now, why don't you make a new picture together? You can use both the hat and the flag. And you can use some of the cake decorations, too. They are colorful and shiny. Do you want to try?"
Lily and Tom say, "Yes, Mom, we want to try. Thank you, Mom."
They take the paper, the scissors, the hat, the flag, and the cake decorations. They sit on the floor and start to make a new picture. They share and help each other. They have fun and laugh.
Mom watches them and says, "That's better. You are good children. I am proud of you."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She was a very honest girl. She always told the truth. One day, Amy's mom wanted to teach her how to go up and down the stairs.
Amy and her mom went to the stair. Amy's mom held her hand and showed her how to go up. Amy tried and tried. She was a little scared, but she did it! Then, Amy's mom showed her how to go down the stairs. Amy tried and tried again. She did it!
Amy was so happy. She told her mom, "I did it, Mom! I went up and down the stair!" Her mom was very proud of her honest little girl. They hugged and laughed. From that day on, Amy could go up and down the stairs all by herself.

One day, Anthony wanted to visit his brother's house. As he opened the door, he felt scared. He was about to enter the bathroom when an alien popped out of the toilet! 
"Let me out!", the alien said in a loud voice. Anthony was frightened and ran away. 
The next day, Anthony told his brother what had happened. His brother replied, "You were just careless. It was only a toy alien!" 
Anthony felt bad and ran back to the bathroom. He opened the door, expecting to see the little alien, but he couldn't find it. He looked around, but he couldn't find anything. 
Suddenly, he heard a loud noise coming from inside the toilet. He was scared, so he closed the door and ran away. 
The next morning, Anthony decided to take a look in the bathroom again. When he opened the door, he saw the alien lying on the floor, motionless. Sadly, Anthony realized that he had left the alien in the bathroom by accident and it had died. He felt very sad and he knew he was being careless.

Lila and Tom were twins who liked to play in the park. They had a toy boat that they could sail on the pond. One day, they saw an elderly man sitting on a bench. He had a big hat and a long beard.
"Hello, sir," Lila said. "Can we talk to you?"
The man smiled and nodded. "Of course, little ones. What do you want to talk about?"
"We want to know about your hat," Tom said. "It is very big and funny."
The man laughed. "This is my sailor hat. I used to sail on a big boat across the sea. I saw many things and had many adventures."
"Wow," Lila said. "That sounds amazing. Can you tell us a story?"
The man thought for a moment. "Well, let me see. Once, I was sailing near a small island. I saw something shiny on the shore. I decided to spring off the boat and swim to the island. I wanted to see what it was."
"What was it?" Tom asked.
The man winked. "It was a treasure chest full of gold and jewels. I opened it and took some of the treasure. But then, I heard a loud roar. It was a dragon guarding the chest. It was very angry and scary."
"What did you do?" Lila asked.
The man shrugged. "I ran back to the boat as fast as I could. I threw the treasure on the boat and sailed away. The dragon chased me for a while, but I managed to escape. That was one of the most exciting days of my life."
Lila and Tom clapped their hands. "That was a great story. Thank you, sir."
The man smiled. "You're welcome, little ones. I'm glad you liked it. Now, why don't you go and sail your toy boat on the pond? Maybe you'll find your own adventure."
Lila and Tom nodded. They said goodbye to the man and ran to the pond. They sailed their toy boat and pretended to be sailors. They had a lot of fun.

One day, a little girl named Sue was playing with her toy doll. She saw her mom kneel down next to an infant in the park. The baby was crying and Sue thought the baby might be bitter because it was very cold outside. Sue walked over to her mom and the baby, holding her doll.
"Mom, why is the baby crying?" Sue asked. Her mom looked at her and said, "The baby is cold, Sue. We need to make the baby warm."
Sue had an idea. She took the blanket from her doll and wrapped it around the baby. Suddenly, the baby stopped crying and started to laugh. Sue was so happy that she could help the baby feel better. She looked at her mom and said, "Mom, I think the baby likes my doll's blanket!"
Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, Sue, you did a great job. Now the baby is warm and happy!" Sue felt proud and knew that even little things can make a big difference.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She lived in a peaceful house with her mom, dad, and her dog, Buddy. They all loved to play outside in the sun. Mia liked to run and jump in the grass with Buddy.
One day, Mia saw a shadow on the ground. It moved when she moved. She started to worry. "What is this?" she asked her mom. Her mom smiled and said, "That is your shadow. It follows you when the sun is shining."
Mia felt better. She played with her shadow and Buddy. They all had fun in the sun. Mia was not worried anymore. She knew her shadow was just a part of her. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy crab. The crab lived near the sea. Every day, the crab would try to find food and play with his friends.
One day, the crab was very tired. He wanted to take a nap. He found a soft spot on the sand and closed his eyes. But then, something unexpected happened. A big wave came and took the crab away from his home.
The crab was scared, but he did not give up. He tried very hard to swim back to his home. And guess what? The crab found out he could swim! He swam back home and told his friends about his adventure. From that day on, the sleepy crab was not only good at finding food and playing, but also at swimming. The crab and his friends had more fun than ever before.

Once upon a time, there was a street. On this street, there were two friends who liked to spread butter on bread. They were very fast at it. They were competitive and always tried to be the best.
One sunny day, they had a big race. The whole town came to watch them. The friends were excited and ready to show how fast they could spread butter on bread. They started to spread the butter as fast as they could. Everyone was cheering for them.
But then, something unexpected happened. A big, friendly dog came running down the street. The dog saw the butter and wanted some too. He jumped up and licked the butter off the bread. The friends stopped racing and laughed. They realized that it was more fun to share their butter with the dog than to be competitive. And they all became the best of friends.

Tom and Anna are friends. They like to play together. They want to have fun and be healthy. They know that exercise is good for them.
One day, they go to the park with their moms. They see a big slide, a swing, a seesaw and a sandbox. They run to the slide and take turns to slide down. Whee! They laugh and scream.
"Let's go to the swing next!" Tom says.
"OK!" Anna agrees.
They run to the swing and push each other. They feel the wind in their hair and the sun on their faces. They smile and sing.
"Let's go to the seesaw now!" Anna says.
"OK!" Tom agrees.
They run to the seesaw and balance each other. They go up and down, up and down. They giggle and clap.
"Let's go to the sandbox last!" Tom says.
"OK!" Anna agrees.
They run to the sandbox and make castles, mountains and animals. They use their hands, shovels and buckets. They decorate their creations with stones, leaves and flowers. They admire and hug.
"Wow, you are brilliant!" Tom says to Anna.
"Thank you, you are brilliant too!" Anna says to Tom.
They are happy and proud. They have done a lot of exercise and had a lot of fun. Their moms come to them and praise them.
"Good job, Tom and Anna! You have played so well and done so much exercise! You are brilliant!" their moms say.
"Thank you, mom!" Tom and Anna say.
They hug their moms and each other. They are tired but satisfied. They go home with their moms and have a nice nap. They dream of playing together again. They are friends. They like to play together. They want to have fun and be healthy. They know that exercise is good for them.

Once upon a time, there was a rough bear named Bobo. He had a friend named Lulu, a little girl. They liked to play in the sun. One day, Lulu's mom told her, "Put on lotion before you play."
Lulu asked Bobo, "Will you allow me to put lotion on you too?" Bobo nodded yes. Lulu put lotion on her arms and legs. Then, she put lotion on Bobo's rough fur. They went to play outside.
While they played, something unexpected happened. The lotion made Bobo's fur soft, not rough! They were both very happy. Now, Bobo was a soft bear and they could hug and play even more. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to play with her toy animals. One day, she decided to have a tea party with her toys. She put all the yummy food on a tray and invited her toy friends to join her.
As they were having the tea party, Mia's toy elephant said, "I want to try something new. Can we have something spicy?" Mia thought for a moment and said, "I have an idea! Let's add some spicy sauce to our food." So, Mia put a little spicy sauce on the food.
As they continued to eat, the toy elephant took a bite of the spicy food. Suddenly, the elephant started to sneeze. "Achoo! Achoo!" sneezed the elephant. Mia laughed and said, "Oh no, I think the spicy food made you sneeze!"
The toy elephant kept sneezing, and Mia decided to give it some water. After drinking the water, the elephant stopped sneezing. Mia and her toy friends continued their tea party, but this time without the spicy sauce. They all laughed and had a great time together. And from that day on, they knew that spicy food could make them sneeze.

Anna and Ben are playing in the park. They see a big animal with a horn on its nose. It is a rhinoceros. They want to touch it, but it is sleeping.
"Shh, do not wake it up," Anna says. "It is napping."
"Why is it napping?" Ben asks. "Is it tired?"
"Maybe it is hungry," Anna says. "It is very thin. Look at its bones."
They go closer to the rhinoceros. They see some grass and leaves near it. They pick some and put them next to its mouth.
"Here, eat this," Ben says. "It is good for you."
The rhinoceros opens one eye. It sees the children and the food. It makes a loud noise. It is angry.
"Run!" Anna says. "It does not like us!"
They run away from the rhinoceros. They hide behind a tree. They are scared.
"Sorry, rhinoceros," Ben says. "We just wanted to help you."
The rhinoceros snorts. It goes back to sleep. It is not hungry. It is just thin. It is a special kind of rhinoceros. It is called a white rhinoceros.

One day, a little dog named Spot went for a walk. He saw a brilliant red ball on the ground. Spot wanted to pick up the ball with his paw. He ran to the ball and tried to grab it with his paw. But it was too big for him.
A big dog came by and saw Spot. The big dog said, "I can help you pick up the ball." Spot was happy and said, "Thank you, big dog!" The big dog used his big paw to pick up the ball. Spot was very excited to play with the ball now.
But the big dog did not want to share the ball with Spot. The big dog said, "This is my ball now!" and ran away with it. Spot was very sad because he could not play with the brilliant red ball. He went home and cried.

Once upon a time there was a small bunny called Max. One day, Max was hopping around in the garden when suddenly his friend, a squirrel named Sam, called out to him.
"Max! Max!" said Sam, "There's something I need to tell you!"
"What is it?" Max asked, hopping over to the squirrel.
"You must stay out of the attic," Sam said, "It's a huge, dark place and it's very dangerous."
Max was very confused, he had never been in the attic. "What do you mean, Sam?" he asked.
"It's like a huge cave up at the top of the house and a lot of strange things happen there," Sam warned. "It's best not to go there. Promise me you won't?"
Max was certain he would never go there and he promised Sam he would stay away. He waved goodbye to his friend and hopped away, vowing to never go near the attic.

Once upon a time, there was a big office. People worked there every day. They were patient and nice. They liked their office a lot.
One day, a big monster came to the office. The monster was very angry. It wanted to destroy the office. The people were scared. They did not want their office to be destroyed.
The people tried to stop the monster. They thought they could make the monster go away. But the monster was too big and strong. The monster destroyed the office. The people were very sad. They did not know that the monster was their friend, who just wanted to play a game.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Sam. She was three years old and was very bright. She had long, blonde hair and loved to play outside.
One day, Sam was playing in her backyard. Suddenly, she saw a big, white box that had never been there before. She ran to it and opened it. Out of the box came a big, shiny film. Sam clapped her hands and said "Wow!"
Sam picked up the film and took it inside her house. She put it in the DVD player and pressed the play button. The film came to life and showed trees with bright flowers, a river with lots of fish and an amazing sunset. Sam loved it!
At the end of the film, Sam saw a big tree and a bright lemonade stand. She said to her mummy "Can I drink lemonade?" Mummy said "Yes, of course. But only a little bit because it's very sweet!"
So Sam had a little drink of the delicious lemonade and thought to herself that it was the best day ever. She watched the film once again and smiled. Suddenly, the sun outside was that little bit brighter.

One day, a boy named Tim went outside to play. He saw a hose lying on the ground. Tim thought it would be fun to grab the hose and spray water. He wanted to make a big puddle for his toy ducks to swim in.
As Tim grabbed the hose, he felt something was wrong. The hose was very hard and heavy. He tried to pull it, but it did not move. Tim thought this was a stupid hose because it did not work like a normal hose should.
Suddenly, the hose started to wiggle and move! Tim was scared and let go of the hose. To his surprise, the hose was not a hose at all. It was a big snake! The snake was not mean, though. It just wanted to play with Tim. They became friends and played together in the yard. Tim learned that day that not all things that look scary are bad.

One day, a girl named Amy was going on a trip with her mom and dad. They were all very happy. They had to pack their bags and get ready. Amy's mom said, "We need to rush, or we will be late!"
While they were packing, Amy found her mom's passport. She knew it was important, but she didn't know what it was for. She felt a little guilty for looking at it. Amy asked her mom, "What is this?" Her mom replied, "It is a passport. We need it to go on our trip."
When they got to the big place where the planes were, they could not find the passport. Amy's mom and dad looked worried. They looked and looked, but it was not there. Suddenly, Amy remembered that she had seen it before. She said, "I know where it is!" and ran to her bag.
Amy found the passport in her bag and gave it to her mom. They were all so happy that they could go on their trip. They rushed to the plane and got on just in time. Amy felt proud that she had helped her family.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim did not love vegetables. He only liked to eat soft foods like ice cream and cake. One day, Tim's mom said, "Tim, you need to eat vegetables too. They are good for you."
The next day, Tim went to the park with his mom. He saw a big, green vegetable on the ground. Tim picked it up and said, "Mom, what is this?" His mom said, "That's a vegetable called a zucchini. It can be soft when you cook it." Tim decided to take the zucchini home and try it.
That night, Tim's mom cooked the zucchini and made it soft. Tim tried it and loved it! From then on, Tim loved eating vegetables, especially soft ones like zucchini. He learned that vegetables can be yummy and good for him too.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a lazy cat named Sam. Sam loved to sleep all day and play all night. One sunny day, Sam went outside to play.
As Sam played, he saw a small bug. The bug was in the dust. Sam stepped closer to the bug and said, "Hi, little bug! Do you want to play with me?" The bug looked up at Sam and said, "Sure, Sam! Let's play!"
Sam and the bug played all day in the dust. They ran, they jumped, and they laughed. As the sun went down, Sam said, "Thank you for playing with me, little bug." The bug smiled and said, "Thank you, Sam! Let's play again soon." And from that day on, Sam and the bug became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was an old man who liked to teach. Every day, he went to a big school to help little kids learn. The old man was very good at his job, and all the kids loved him.
One day, the old man tried to open a big, heavy door. He pushed and pushed, but the door would not open. The kids saw him and wanted to help. They all ran to the door and pushed together. Suddenly, the door opened!
Behind the door, they found a big, fun playroom. The old man did not know it was there. Everyone was very happy and surprised. They played together all day and had lots of fun. The old man was not just a teach, but also a good friend.

Lily and Ben were playing with their toys in the living room. Lily had a big doll and Ben had a small car. Lily liked her doll very much, but she wished she had a car too. She asked Ben if she could play with his car for a while.
"No, this is my car. You have your doll. Go away!" Ben said. He was not very friendly.
Lily felt sad and angry. She wanted to have a car too. She thought of a plan. She saw a bottle on the table. It had a label that said "shrink". She did not know what that word meant, but she thought it might make things smaller. She took the bottle and sprayed some of it on Ben's car.
Ben saw what Lily did and shouted. "Hey, what are you doing? Stop it! You are making my car shrink!"
Lily laughed. She thought it was funny to see Ben's car get smaller and smaller. She picked up the tiny car and put it in her pocket. She said, "Now I have a car too. And you have nothing. Ha ha!"
Ben was very upset. He cried and ran to his mom. He told her what Lily did. Mom was not happy. She scolded Lily and made her give back the car. She said, "Lily, that was not nice. You should not take things that are not yours. And you should not use things that you do not understand. You could have hurt Ben or yourself. You need to say sorry to Ben and share your toys."
Lily felt sorry. She realized she was wrong. She gave back the car to Ben and said, "I'm sorry, Ben. I was mean. I should not have sprayed your car. Can we be friends again?"
Ben was still sad, but he forgave Lily. He said, "It's okay, Lily. I'm sorry too. I should have been more friendly. Can we play together?"
Lily and Ben hugged and smiled. They decided to play together and share their toys. They learned a lesson. They wished they would never fight again.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was a very foolish dog. He loved to play with his dish. One day, Spot wanted to go outside and play with his dish, but his mom did not permit him to go. Spot was sad.
Spot sat by the window and watched his friends play outside. He wanted to join them, but he remembered his mom did not permit him to go out. Spot thought of a plan. He would take his dish and sneak outside.
Spot took his dish and quietly opened the door. He was outside with his friends now! They all played with Spot's dish and had lots of fun. But soon, Spot's mom found out and was not happy. She told Spot to come inside.
Spot knew he was foolish for not listening to his mom. He promised to always ask for permission before going outside. From that day on, Spot and his dish played outside only when his mom permitted him to do so.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved warm things. She loved her warm bed, her warm socks, and her warm hugs from her mom. One day, Sue's mom told her they were going to the store to buy a warm pastry. Sue was very happy.
On the way to the store, Sue saw a big dog. The dog was running very fast. Sue's mom told her that the dog was in a rush. Sue thought the dog was funny. She wanted to rush like the dog too. Sue's mom said they could rush together after they got the warm pastry.
When they got to the store, Sue and her mom found the warm pastry. It smelled so good! They bought the pastry and went outside. Sue's mom said, "Now we can rush like the dog!" Sue and her mom held hands and rushed all the way back home.
Sue was so happy to eat the warm pastry. She thought about the big dog that was in a rush. She was glad that she and her mom rushed like the dog too. It was a fun day for Sue and her mom. And the warm pastry was the best part of all.

Once upon a time, in a small garden, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play with his toy car and eat yummy vegetables. One day, Tim saw a big, green vegetable. He did not know what it was. He thought it was a toy.
Tim picked up the big vegetable and tried to play with it like a toy car. He pushed it and pulled it, but it did not move like a car. It was very foolish to think it was a toy car. Tim felt sad and wanted to find out what the vegetable was.
He asked his mom, but she was busy. So, Tim went to the garden and tried to put the vegetable back where he found it. But he could not find the right place. He tried and tried, but the vegetable would not fit anywhere. Tim felt very foolish and sad. The big, green vegetable was lost, and Tim did not know what to do.

Once upon a time, there was a little bulb. This bulb was very impatient. It wanted to grow up and be a big, bright flower. But it had to wait for the sun to come out and for the rain to fall. It wanted to be big and bright right now!
One day, the sun was shining and the rain had fallen. All the little bulbs in the garden decided to unite. They wanted to grow together and become big, bright flowers. They knew that if they worked together, they could grow faster and become even more beautiful.
As the bulbs grew, they became less impatient. They learned that growing takes time and that it is okay to wait. Soon, they were big, bright flowers, shining in the sun. The little bulbs were happy they had united, and they knew that together, they were even more beautiful.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was sad because he could not sleep. Every night, he would lay in his bed and listen to the faucet drip, drip, drip. The sound of the water made it hard for him to sleep. Tim wished he could sleep like his friends.
One day, Tim's mom went to the store. She said, "I will find something to help Tim sleep." Tim was excited and hoped his mom would find something to help him. He thought about what his mom could bring home to help him sleep.
That night, Tim's mom came home with a surprise. She had a soft, fluffy pillow for Tim. She said, "This pillow will help you sleep, Tim." Tim put the pillow on his bed and laid down. The faucet still went drip, drip, drip, but the soft pillow made Tim feel cozy.
Tim closed his eyes and listened to the faucet. The drip, drip, drip did not bother him anymore. He felt safe and warm with his new pillow. Soon, Tim was fast asleep. He was not sad anymore. Tim's mom was happy too, because her little boy could finally sleep.

Once upon a time, there was a yellow bird named Bob. Bob liked to nap all day. One day, Bob was napping on a tree when he heard a loud noise. It was a man with a big hammer. The man was fixing a house.
Bob said, "Please stop the noise! I want to nap!" The man said, "I am sorry, but I need to fix the house." Bob was sad, and he could not nap. He had an idea. "Can I help you fix the house?" he asked. The man said, "Yes, you can help me."
Bob helped the man fix the house by finding nails for the hammer. They worked together, and soon the house was fixed. The man thanked Bob and left. Now, Bob could nap in peace.

Ben and Lily like to play in the garden. They look for bugs and make houses for them. They use sticks, leaves and stones. They are very careful not to hurt the bugs.
One day, they find a big green bug with long legs and wings. They think it is a grasshopper. They make a house for it in a shoebox. They put some grass and water inside. They poke some holes in the lid so the bug can breathe.
"Hello, Mr. Grasshopper," Ben says. "We are your friends. We will take care of you. You can stay in our house as long as you want."
"Thank you, Ben and Lily," the bug says. "You are very kind and dependable. You have made me a nice home. I am happy here."
Ben and Lily are surprised. They did not know the bug can talk. They are very curious. They ask the bug many questions. They learn that the bug is not a grasshopper, but a cricket. He tells them his name is Chirpy. He tells them how he makes music with his wings. He tells them about his family and friends. He tells them stories and jokes.
Ben and Lily have a lot of fun with Chirpy. They laugh and sing and play. They share their snacks and toys with him. They become good friends. They are glad they found the bug in the garden. He makes them happy.

Once upon a time, there was an elderly lady who liked to perform yoga. She did yoga every day in her big garden. She was very good at it.
One sunny day, while she was doing yoga, a little cat came into her garden. The cat watched the elderly lady perform yoga. The cat thought it looked fun and wanted to try it too.
The cat started to copy the elderly lady's yoga moves. But then, something unexpected happened. The cat was so good at yoga that it could do even better moves than the elderly lady! The lady was so happy to see the cat do yoga. They became friends and did yoga together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to lift big rocks in his yard. One day, while lifting a rock, he found a small, incredible bug. The bug was shiny and had many colors.
Tim called his friend, Sue. "Sue, come see this incredible bug!" Sue ran to Tim and looked at the bug. They both stared at it, amazed. They decided to show it to their friends, so they put the bug in a jar and went to the pass where everyone played.
As they walked through the pass, something unexpected happened. The incredible bug started to grow! It grew and grew until it was too big for the jar. Tim and Sue were scared but the bug smiled at them. It spoke, "Thank you for finding me. I am a magic bug. I will grant you one wish."
Tim and Sue thought hard. They wished for a big playground for all their friends to play. The magic bug lifted its wings, and with a flash, a huge playground appeared in the pass. The magic bug flew away, and Tim and Sue played happily with their friends on the new playground.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy cat named Tom. Tom liked to act like a big boss. He always went around the farm telling other animals what to do. The animals did not like Tom's bossy ways.
One day, Tom saw a big box near the farm. He thought it was a gift for him. He told all the animals to come and see. The animals were tired of Tom's nosy ways, but they came to see the big box. They all felt a big shock when they saw what was inside.
Inside the box was a big dog named Max. Max was strong and fast. He did not like nosy cats like Tom. Max chased Tom around the farm until Tom was too tired to act like a big boss. The animals were happy that Tom learned his lesson. They all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Mia went to sleep. When she woke up, she saw a tiny fairy in her room. The fairy was sad and frustrated. She could not find her way back home.
Mia asked the fairy, "Why are you sad?" The fairy said, "I can't find my home. I am lost." Mia wanted to help the fairy. They looked around the room for a way to get the fairy home.
They saw a tiny door in the wall. Mia opened the door and the fairy went inside. The fairy was happy now. She thanked Mia and flew back to her home. Mia felt good for helping her new friend.

One day, a girl named Lucy and her mom went to church. The church had a big, deep pool. People went in the pool to get wet. Lucy saw the pool and said, "Mom, can I go in the pool?"
Her mom said, "Not today, Lucy. But soon, we will have a special day. On that day, you can go in the pool."
