Lucy was sad but she waited. She went to church every week and looked at the deep pool. One day, her mom said, "Today is the special day, Lucy!" Lucy was so happy.
Lucy put on a pretty dress and went to the church. Her mom and dad were there. They smiled at her. The man in the church said nice words. Then, he took Lucy to the pool. He poured water on her head. Lucy felt happy and loved. Now, she was a part of the church, just like her mom and dad.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had an ordinary eraser. She liked to draw and use the eraser to fix her mistakes.
One day, Lily saw her cat, Whiskers, peek behind a big box in her room. She went to the box and peeked too. She found a big book with lots of colors.
Lily opened the book and started to draw. She used her ordinary eraser to make her pictures perfect. Whiskers sat next to her and watched. They had a fun day drawing together.

One day, a curious cat named Tom went for a walk. He saw a big red barn. Tom went inside the barn to look. He saw a lot of hay. Tom liked to play in the hay.
Tom saw a cow named Daisy. Daisy was eating the hay. Tom asked, "Can I hang with you, Daisy?" Daisy said, "Yes, Tom! Let's play in the hay together!"
As they played, they heard a loud noise. A big truck came to the barn. The truck took all the hay away! Tom and Daisy were sad. But then, they saw a big pile of new hay! They were happy again and played all day.

Once upon a time, in a nice little town, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toys. He had a big toy cooler that he liked to put all his toys in when he was done playing. One day, Tim's toy cooler was full and he couldn't fit any more toys in it.
Tim had an idea. He wanted to destroy his old toy cooler and get a new, bigger one. He tried to break it, but it was very hard. Then, something unexpected happened. Tim's dog, Spot, came and bumped into the cooler. The cooler fell down and broke into pieces.
Tim was sad at first, but then he saw that inside the broken cooler was a secret box. He opened the box and found new, fun toys! Tim's mom helped him clean up the mess and they got a new cooler for his toys. Tim was happy and played with his new toys all day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She had a big box full of toys. Mia loved playing with her toys, but she wanted to improve her box to make it more useful. She thought about what she could do to make her box better.
One day, Mia asked her friend Tom to help her. They thought and thought about how they could improve the box. They decided to make a secret door in the box. This would make the box even more fun to play with. They worked hard to make the secret door, and when they were done, Mia was very happy. She knew this would make her box more useful.
But one day, Mia's little brother, Sam, found the secret door in the box. He opened it and all the toys fell out. Mia was sad and worried that her box was not useful anymore. But then, something unexpected happened. Sam had an idea to fix the secret door and make the box even better. He added a lock to the door so it would not open by accident. Mia was so happy and thanked Sam for his help. They played together with the improved box and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue liked to design pretty pictures. She would draw every day. One day, her mom said they were going on a trip next week. Sue was very happy.
Sue was a little anxious about the trip. She had never been away from home before. But her mom said it would be fun. They would see new things and meet new people. Sue felt better.
On the trip, Sue took her paper and crayons. She designed new pictures every day. She showed her new friends her art. They liked her pictures. Sue had a great week on the trip. She was not anxious anymore.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to bake cakes with her mom. One day, they decided to go to the park and bake a cake.
It was a sunny day and the grass was soft and green. Lily and her mom started to bake. The mom mixed all the ingredients in a bowl and Lily poured it in a pan.
Lily was excited. She asked her mom, "What will the cake taste like?"
Mom said, "It's a surprise. You'll have to wait and find out!"
Lily waited and waited. Finally, the cake was done baking. Mom took it out of the oven and put it on the grass. She said, "Let's cut it open and see what it looks like!"
They cut open the cake and it was filled with unknown ingredients. Lily tasted it and it was delicious! She couldn't believe her mom had made such a delicious and unknown cake.
Lily and her mom enjoyed the delicious cake in the park. From then on, Lily loved to bake with her mom.

Once there was a little girl called Jenny. Her favourite thing to do was to play in the garden with her pet dog Benji. Every day, Jenny would run around and explore all the secret corners of her garden.
One day, Jenny noticed that her garden's hose was broken. She was very upset, because she liked playing in the water. She called out to her mom and asked her to fix the hose.
"I'll fix it soon, sweetheart" her mom said, "but first I have to finish some work."
Jenny was disappointed and asked why she had to delay. Her mom smiled and said "I know you love to play in the water, so I'm trying to make sure that whenever we fix the hose, it doesn't break again. Otherwise, we will have to do it over and over. That's why we have to fix it properly the first time and it takes some time to do that."
Jenny understood and waited patiently for her mom to finish the work. Finally, the hose was fixed and was as long and strong as ever! Jenny was so happy she started to dance around the garden with Benji. 
This story teaches us that patience is important. We should not be too eager to get results and jump to conclusions. If we wait patiently and work hard, we will have the desired results in the long run.

One day, a little boy named Tim was playing with his toy cars. He had a big blue car and a small red car. Tim liked to make the cars race each other. He would push them fast across the empty room.
Tim's mom came in and asked him a question. She said, "Tim, did you finish eating your lunch?" Tim looked at his plate and saw that it was empty. He said, "Yes, mom. I finished eating my lunch."
Just then, something unexpected happened. The big blue car and the small red car started talking to each other! The blue car said, "I am faster than you, red car!" The red car said, "No, I am faster than you, blue car!" They decided to race each other to find out who was the fastest.
The cars raced around the room, going faster and faster. Tim and his mom watched in surprise. In the end, the big blue car and the small red car crossed the finish line at the same time. They both laughed and said, "We are both fast!"
Tim and his mom laughed too. They were happy to see the cars being friends. From that day on, Tim played with the talking cars every day, and they all had lots of fun together.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk in the woods. He saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. But as he got closer, he saw a small cut on the tree. Tim was scared, but he wanted to know what made the cut.
Tim saw a little bug near the cut. The bug was red and had a long nose. Tim thought the bug looked funny, so he poked it with a stick. The bug moved and Tim laughed. He was not scared anymore.
Tim and the bug became friends. They played together in the woods all day. When it was time to go home, Tim said bye to the bug. He was happy he made a new friend and was not scared of the cut on the tree anymore.

One day, a boy named Tim found a parrot. The parrot was bad. It was sad and did not talk. Tim wanted to help the parrot. He said, "I will make you happy, parrot."
Tim took the parrot home. He gave it food and water. He played with the parrot every day. The parrot started to feel better. Tim was happy to see the parrot happy.
One day, the parrot said, "Thank you, Tim. You restore my joy." Tim smiled and said, "You are welcome, parrot. I am glad you are not bad anymore." They played and laughed together. Tim and the parrot were best friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a harmless bug. The bug had a zipper on its back. Tim was very happy to find a new friend.
Tim took the bug home to show his mom. She told him that the bug could change. Tim was excited to see what would happen. He played with the bug all day.
At night, the bug went inside its zipper. When it came out, it had changed into a beautiful butterfly. Tim and his mom were so happy. They let the butterfly go outside to play. And they knew they would always remember their special friend.

Once upon a time there was a boy called Sam. Sam was three years old and loved playing with his toys. One day he was playing with his toy car when it suddenly stopped working.
Sam was very confused. He couldn't figure out why his car wasn't working. He checked the battery, but it seemed alright. He then asked his dad for help.
His dad looked at the car and said "I think the battery needs to be changed". 
Sam looked up at his dad and said "How do I change it?"
His dad answered "You need to unscrew the cover and take out the old battery first".
Sam was still confused, but he did as he was told and eventually he managed to change the battery. After he put the old one in the bin, his car started to work again.
Sam was so happy he gave his dad a big hug. He had learnt an important lesson that day: when things don't work, ask for help!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had many toys, but they were all over the floor. His mom said, "Tim, let's unite your toys and put them on the shelf."
Tim was impatient. He wanted to play, not clean. But he knew his mom was right. So, he picked up his toys and put them on the shelf. As he did this, the toys became friends with each other.
Now, Tim's toys were happy on the shelf. They were not alone anymore. Tim liked seeing his toys together. He learned that when we unite, we can make everyone happy.

One day, a creative dog named Max found a rake in the yard. He wanted to play with it. He picked it up with his mouth and ran to his friend, a cat named Luna.
Max said, "Luna, look at my rake! Let's play with it!" Luna thought it was a fun idea. They took turns using the rake to make a big pile of leaves. They jumped in the leaves and laughed.
After playing, they were dirty. Luna said, "Max, we need to bathe now." They went to the pond and jumped in. They splashed water on each other to get clean. When they were done, they went back to the yard to play more games.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to swing, slide and run. They saw a big red ball near the fence. They wanted to play with it.
"Can we have the ball?" Lily asked a boy who was sitting on the grass.
"No, it's mine. Go away!" the boy said. He was mean and rude.
Lily and Ben felt sad. They went to another part of the park. They saw a wide pond with ducks and fish. They wanted to feed them.
"Look, I have some bread. Let's share it with the ducks and fish," Ben said. He broke the bread into small pieces.
"Okay, that sounds fun," Lily said. She took some bread from Ben.
They walked to the edge of the pond. They threw the bread into the water. The ducks and fish came to eat it. They made happy noises.
Lily and Ben smiled. They forgot about the mean boy and the ball. They enjoyed watching the ducks and fish.
But then, something bad happened. Lily leaned too far over the water. She lost her balance. She fell into the pond.
"Help! Help!" Lily cried. She was scared and wet. She could not swim.
Ben was shocked. He wanted to save Lily. He looked around for help. He saw the mean boy with the ball. He ran to him.
"Please, help me! My friend fell into the pond. She can't swim. You have to save her!" Ben begged.
The mean boy looked at Ben. He looked at Lily. He looked at his ball. He made a choice.
He shook his head. He said, "No, I won't help you. I don't care about your friend. I only care about my ball. Go away!"
He turned his back on Ben. He kicked his ball. He laughed.
Ben felt angry and sad. He did not know what to do. He cried.
Lily was still in the pond. She was still scared and wet. She could not breathe.
She sank under the water.
She did not come up again.
The end.

Once upon a time, in a big city, there lived a tiny mouse named Tim. Tim had a friend named Sam, who was a big cat. They liked to play and eat tasty food together.
One day, Tim and Sam found a big piece of cheese. Tim said, "Wow, this cheese is so tasty! Let's eat it together!" But as they started to eat, the ground began to tremble. They were scared and didn't know what was happening.
The trembling stopped, and they saw that the cheese had turned into a big, friendly dog named Daisy. Daisy said, "I was stuck as cheese, but you two helped me become a dog again. Thank you!" Now, Tim, Sam, and Daisy were all friends, and they played and ate tasty food together in the big city.

Mommy and Daddy went out for the night. They were going to have dinner and discuss an important decision. When they arrived they sat down at the table. Daddy said, "I think we should have some wine to drink tonight." Mommy agreed and the waitress brought two glasses of cold wine.
Daddy and Mommy talked and discussed the decision. It was a difficult decision and it took a long time for them to make up their minds. Eventually, Mommy said, "I think I know what we should do. It's best for everyone this way." 
Daddy smiled and said, "Alright then. If that's what you think, we'll do it." 
They drank their cold wine and left the restaurant. They were happy with their decision and Mommy said, "I think that went really well!"

One day, a little white dog named Spot went for a walk. The sun was shining and the birds were singing. Spot felt happy and had an urge to run and play.
As Spot ran through the park, he stumbled on a small rock. He fell down, but he was not hurt. He got up and saw a little girl named Lily. Lily saw Spot fall and asked, "Are you okay, doggy?"
Spot barked and wagged his tail to show he was fine. Lily and Spot played together in the park. They ran, jumped, and laughed. After a while, they were both tired and sat down under a big tree. They had a fun day and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a cold day. A boy named Tim went for a walk. He wore a big coat to stay warm. Tim saw something on the ground. It was a wallet. He picked it up.
Tim went to his mom and said, "Look, I found a wallet. What should I do?" His mom said, "We need to care for it and find the owner. Let's go to the lost and found."
They went to the lost and found. Tim gave the wallet to the lady there. She said, "Thank you for caring and bringing the wallet here. The owner will be happy to get it back."
Tim felt good that he helped someone. He knew that caring was important. Tim and his mom went home, feeling warm inside even on that cold day.

Little Jack wanted a bucket. He asked his dad for one and was so excited when he gave Jack a lovely bucket. But then his dad said he wasn't allowed to go outside with the bucket. Jack didn't want to listen and tried to resist his dad's request. He wanted to fill the bucket with water from the garden hose.
His dad saw Jack was resisting, so he said it was okay for Jack to take the bucket outside, but he must be careful. Jack grinned and let out a little cheer. He filled the bucket with water and enjoyed splashing it over the grass and himself.
Jack's dad saw how much Jack was enjoying himself and smiled. He was glad to see his son loved the bucket. He hoped that one day Jack would learn to respect his parents and not always try to resist them.

Once upon a time, there was a compassionate bird named Bella. She loved to spread her wings and fly high in the sky. Bella had many friends in the big forest.
One day, a big storm came. The wind blew and the rain fell hard. All the animals in the forest were scared. But Bella was brave. She wanted to help her friends.
Bella flew through the storm to find her friends. She saw a little mouse hiding under a leaf. Bella picked up the mouse and flew to a safe, dry place. Then, the storm stopped, and the sun came out. But, surprise! The mouse was a fairy in disguise! The fairy was so happy that Bella helped her. She gave Bella a wish. Bella wished for the forest to always be a happy and safe place for her friends. The fairy made her wish come true, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to ride her bike in the park. One sunny day, while she was riding, she saw a pretty bird. The bird was very smart and could talk. It was an intelligent bird.
The bird said, "Hi, Lily! I have a secret for you." Lily stopped her bike and listened. The bird told her, "There is a shiny gem hidden in the park. Let's go find it together!" Lily was very excited and said, "Yes, let's go!"
They looked and looked, and finally, they found the shiny gem under a big tree. The gem was so pretty and made Lily very happy. Lily thanked the intelligent bird and they became best friends. They had lots of fun riding and playing in the park together.

One day, a girl named Amy went to the market with her mom. They wanted to buy some grapes. Amy loved grapes a lot. They were her favorite snack. At the market, they saw a lady with lots of grapes. Amy and her mom went to her.
"Hello! Can we get some grapes?" Amy's mom asked. The lady smiled and said, "Yes, of course!" She gave them a big bag of grapes. Amy's mom paid the lady and they went home.
When they got home, Amy opened the bag to eat some grapes. But something unexpected happened. Instead of grapes, there were gold coins in the bag! Amy and her mom were very surprised. They had received a bag of gold coins instead of grapes.
Amy's mom said, "Wow, we are rich now! Let's go back to the market and thank the lady." So they went back to the market. They found the lady and told her about the gold coins. The lady was surprised too.
She said, "I didn't know there were gold coins in the bag. But I am happy you received them. You are a nice family. Enjoy your new riches!" Amy, her mom, and the lady all laughed. They bought more grapes and went home. From that day on, Amy's family was rich and they always shared their grapes with the lady at the market.

Sara and Ben are twins. They like to play with their toys in the living room. Sometimes they make a big mess with their blocks, dolls, cars and books. Mom does not like that. She tells them to clean up before dinner.
One day, Sara and Ben are playing with their toys. They hear a loud noise outside. They run to the window and see a big truck. The truck has a new couch for their house. Sara and Ben are curious. They want to see the new couch.
Mom opens the door and lets the men bring the couch inside. She tells Sara and Ben to stay away from the door. They watch from the window. The men put the couch in the living room. It is blue and soft. Mom thanks the men and gives them some money.
Mom calls Sara and Ben. She says, "Come and see the new couch. It is very comfortable. You can sit on it and read your books or watch TV. But you have to be careful and not spill anything on it. Do you like it?"
Sara and Ben come to the living room. They touch the couch. It is smooth and fluffy. They climb on it and sit down. It is cozy and warm. They like it very much. They hug Mom and say, "Thank you, Mom. We love the new couch. It is the best couch ever." Mom smiles and hugs them back. She says, "You are welcome, my sweeties. I love you too. Now, let's clean up your toys and have dinner." Sara and Ben nod and help Mom. They are happy and proud. They have a new couch and a nice family.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to play outside and find new things. One day, he went to the park with his mom.
At the park, Tim saw a large rock. He thought it was a special thing. He wanted to take it home. Tim tried to pick up the rock, but it was too heavy. He asked his mom for help.
Tim's mom helped him carry the large rock home. They put it in the yard. Tim was so happy to have a new thing to play with. He played with the rock every day and had lots of fun.

Jill was excited to go to the carnival with her parents. She wore her favorite purple dress and her shiny black shoes. She wanted to look her best. 
When they got there, Jill saw lots of things to do and see. She asked her parents if she could ride all the rides. 
Her Dad smiled and said, "Yes, but you need a ticket first." 
They went to the ticket booth and bought a ticket. Jill felt so excited to have her very own ticket. 
On the way to the first ride, she noticed how comfortable the carnival was. Everything was bright and inviting. They stopped and sat down to watch a show and Jill laughed and smiled. 
At the end of the day, Jill was tired but happy. She said to her parents, "What a comfortable day. I'm very happy I had my ticket!"

Once upon a time, there was a chubby cat named Tim. Tim lived near a big temple. Every day, he would play near the temple and watch people come and go.
One sunny day, while Tim was playing, he saw a man trying to pay the temple with a big, shiny rock. The man looked sad because the temple did not want his rock. Tim felt bad for the man, so he had an idea.
Tim picked up the shiny rock with his mouth and ran very fast to the other side of the temple. There, he found a man who wanted the rock! The man was so happy that he gave Tim some yummy food. Tim took the food back to the first man, and they both smiled. The man could now pay the temple with the food, and everyone was happy. The twist was that the shiny rock was not needed to pay the temple, but the food was.

One day, a cat named Tom found a cooler. The cooler was big and blue. Tom was very happy because it was a hot day and he wanted something mild.
Tom opened the cooler and saw many cold things inside. There was ice and cold juice. Tom drank some juice and felt much better. He did not want to quit playing with the cooler.
Tom called his friend, a dog named Max, to come and see the cooler. Max was happy to find the cooler too. They both played with the cooler all day long. They did not quit playing until the sun went down. The cooler made their day mild and fun.

Sandy and Joe were best friends. They lived together and did lots of fun things. One day, Sandy and Joe had a plan. They wanted to go on a jog. They put on their shoes and started to run.
But then there was a delay. It started to rain. So they had to wait until it stopped. After a while, the rain stopped and it was clear again. They hurried outside and started jogging! They ran round and round and had lots of fun.
Finally, the morning was over. Sandy and Joe went back home, tired and happy. What a great morning they had!

Once upon a time, there was a kind king. He lived in a big castle with many friends. The king loved to taste new food. One day, he heard about a special fruit that was very yummy. He wanted to taste it too.
The king asked his friends to find the fruit for him. They looked everywhere but could not find it. The king was sad but did not give up. He decided to go find the fruit himself. He put on his shoes and went for a long walk.
The king walked and walked until he found the fruit. It was on a high tree. He climbed the tree and picked the fruit. The king was very happy. But when he tasted the fruit, it was not yummy at all. In fact, it was very bad. The king felt sick and had to go to bed. The kind king's friends were sad because their king was sick and they could not help him.

Once upon a time, there was a happy boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside. One day, he found a big tent in his yard. Tim was very excited to play in the tent.
Tim went into the tent and had lots of fun. He played with his toys and ate a yummy snack. Then, Tim thought it would be fun to lock the tent door. So, he locked it tight.
But, oh no! Tim didn't know how to unlock the door. He tried and tried, but it just wouldn't open. Tim started to feel sad. Then, something unexpected happened. A friendly dog came by and saw Tim in the tent. The dog knew how to unlock the door. The dog opened the door and Tim was free again. Tim and the dog played together and became best friends.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play in the park with their mom and dad. They swing, slide, and run on the grass. They have fun with their friends, too.
One day, Anna feels sick. She has a fever and a sore throat. She does not want to play. She stays in bed and cries. Mom gives her a pill to make her feel better. Anna does not like the pill. It is big and bitter. She spits it out.
"Anna, you have to take the pill," Mom says. "It will help you get well. Ben took his pill when he was sick, remember?"
Anna looks at Ben. He nods and smiles. He remembers when he had a cold and Mom gave him a pill. He did not like it either, but he swallowed it. He was brave. He got well soon and went back to the park.
"Okay, Mom, I will try," Anna says. She takes the pill again and drinks some water. She makes a face, but she does not spit it out. She is brave, too.
Mom hugs her and says, "Good girl, Anna. You are very helpful. I am proud of you. If you rest and take your pills, you will get well soon and go back to the park, too."
Anna smiles and says, "Thank you, Mom. I love you. And I love the park. I want to play with Ben and our friends again."
Mom kisses her and says, "I love you, too, Anna. And I love the park, too. We will go there again when you are better. Now, sleep well and dream of the park."

Once upon a time, there was a friendly cat named Tom. He lived in a small house with a big yard. The yard had many trees and flowers on every side. Tom loved to play in the yard and chase butterflies.
One day, Tom saw a ball in his yard. He did not know where it came from. He looked around and saw a little girl named Lucy on the other side of the fence. Lucy was sad because she lost her ball. Tom wanted to help her.
Tom decided to borrow the ball and give it back to Lucy. He picked up the ball with his mouth and jumped over the fence. Lucy was so happy to see her ball again. She thanked Tom and they became best friends. They played together every day and had lots of fun.

One day, a big dog named Sam went on a picnic with his friends. They went to a nice park with lots of trees and grass. Sam was very happy because he loved picnics. He knew it would be easy to have fun with his friends.
At the picnic, Sam saw a little girl with a pretty ribbon in her hair. He wanted to be friends with her too. Sam's friend told him, "If you bow to her, she might like you." Sam thought about it and decided to try it later.
After they ate, Sam saw the little girl again. He remembered what his friend said. He walked up to her and bowed. The little girl smiled and clapped her hands. She became Sam's new friend, and they all played together for the rest of the day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had a special glove that she liked to wear all the time. One day, she lost her glove and felt very sad.
Lucy asked her friend Tommy, "Do you know where my glove is?" Tommy thought for a moment and said, "No, but let's look for it together." They looked under the bed, in the closet, and even in the toy box, but they could not find the glove.
Finally, Lucy's mom came into the room and saw them looking. She asked, "What are you looking for?" Lucy felt embarrassed and said, "I lost my glove." Her mom smiled and said, "I found it in the laundry. Here you go." Lucy was so happy to have her glove back and thanked her mom and Tommy for helping her.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She loved to play by the river. One day, she found a pretty crystal. It was shiny and made her smile.
Lily saw a turtle by the river. The turtle was slow and had a green shell. Lily said, "Hi, turtle! Look at my crystal!" The turtle paused and looked at the crystal. "Wow! It is so pretty!" said the turtle.
Lily and the turtle became friends. They played by the river every day. They liked to pause and look at the pretty crystal together. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a toy that he liked a lot. It was a fake toy dog. He played with the toy dog every day.
One day, Tim's mom saw him and his friend Sam play. They were having a fight with their toys. Tim's mom did not like this. She said, "I do not permit you to fight with your toys."
Tim and Sam stopped fighting. They were sad. Then, something unexpected happened. The fake toy dog started to talk! The toy dog said, "I don't like fights. Let's all be friends."
Tim, Sam, and the toy dog played happily together. They never had a fight again.

Once upon a time, in a happy town, there was a big fountain. The fountain was very pretty. It had water that went up and down. Everyone in town loved the fountain. They would come and watch the water every day.
One day, a nice girl named Lily wanted to have a fun day at the fountain. She thought it would be a good idea to invite her friends to play with her. So, she asked her friends, Tom and Sue, to come to the fountain. They were very excited to go and play.
When they got to the fountain, they saw that it had a lot of healthy fruits near it. They picked the fruits and ate them. They were very yummy! After eating, they played games and had a lot of fun. They laughed and splashed in the water. At the end of the day, they were very happy and tired. They went home and told their families about the great day they had at the fountain.

Sophie was very excited to go on her first camping trip with her family. They had been walking for hours when night began to fall. Suddenly, Sophie saw a small stream between two trees and said, "Daddy, can we stop here for the night?" 
Her father smiled and said, "Let's investigate." Sophie eagerly followed her father as he revealed an old drain which had been hidden under the trees. She noticed that the moss and leaves were damp and it was difficult to pull the drain open. 
Her father said, "There is some water inside. We can use it to make dinner. Shall we try and see if there is enough?" 
Sophie and her father carefully opened the drain and soon enough, they found enough water to make dinner. Sophie was so excited as she followed her father back to the camp site. This was her first experience of finding hidden water and it would definitely be remembered.

Once upon a time, there was a big cloud up in the sky. It was a sunny day, and the cloud was white and fluffy. A little bird named Timmy liked to sit on the cloud and look down at the world below.
One day, while Timmy was sitting on the cloud, he saw something broken on the ground. It was a toy car. Timmy felt sad for the broken toy and wanted to help fix it. He flew down from the cloud to the ground to get a closer look.
As Timmy got close to the broken toy car, something unexpected happened. The wind blew very hard, and the cloud started to move away. Timmy tried to fly back up to the cloud, but the wind was too strong. He couldn't reach his cloud and got lost. Timmy never saw his cloud again, and he felt very sad.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Anna. She had many letters to learn. Every day, she would learn a new letter. Anna was very grateful for her mom, who taught her the letters.
One day, as Anna learned more letters, she found a magic letter. It was a letter that could increase anything it touched. Anna was so excited! She showed the magic letter to her mom, and they decided to use it to make their garden grow.
But then, something unexpected happened. Every time Anna used the magic letter to increase the garden, it would also increase something else in their house. First, it was their cat, who grew very big. Then, it was Anna's toy, which became as big as her room. Anna and her mom were surprised and didn't know what to do.
In the end, they decided to give the magic letter back to where they found it. They were grateful for the fun they had, but they learned that sometimes, too much of something can be a problem. Anna continued to learn her letters, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there were two furry friends - Jimmy and Sally. They were very excited to go to school together because they were best friends.
On their way to school, Jimmy and Sally saw a warning sign. It said: "Slowdown - School Ahead". Jimmy and Sally knew they had to be careful, so they slowed down their walking.
When they arrived at school, Jimmy and Sally were warmly greeted by their teacher. She said, "Welcome to school! It's so nice to have you both here!" Then she warned Jimmy and Sally to stay safe.
The first day of school was lots of fun for Jimmy and Sally. They played games, sang lots of songs, painted pictures and even learnt new things.
At the end of the day, Jimmy and Sally said goodbye to their teacher and ran off home, thinking about all the good times they had at school.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max had a key to his toy box. He loved his toy box because it was full of fun toys. Max was a happy dog, but sometimes he was spoiled. He did not like to share his toys.
One day, Max's friend, a little cat named Luna, came to play. Max did not want to share his toys with Luna. He hid the key under his bed so Luna could not find it. Max thought he was very smart.
But Max did not know that hiding the key would delay their playtime. Max and Luna looked and looked for the key, but they could not find it. They were sad because they could not play with the toys. Max learned that being spoiled and not sharing made both him and Luna unhappy.

Lily and Max are friends. They like to play games. On Wednesday, they go to the park with their moms. They see a big slide. They want to go on the slide.
"Let's race to the slide!" Max says. He is competitive. He likes to win.
"OK!" Lily says. She is competitive too. She likes to have fun.
They run to the slide. Max is faster. He gets to the slide first. He climbs up the ladder. He smiles.
"I win!" he says.
Lily is not happy. She gets to the slide second. She climbs up the ladder too. She frowns.
"No fair!" she says.
They both sit on the slide. They look at each other. They are quiet.
Then, they hear a sound. It is a dog. The dog is big and brown. The dog barks. The dog runs to the slide. The dog wants to play.
Lily and Max are scared. They scream. They slide down the slide. They run to their moms. They hug their moms.
The dog follows them. The dog wags his tail. The dog is friendly. He just wants to say hello.
The moms see the dog. They laugh. They pet the dog. They say he is cute.
Lily and Max look at the dog. They look at each other. They smile.
They are not competitive anymore. They are friends. They play with the dog. They have fun. They like Wednesday.

Once upon a time, there was a little bug. The bug was rare and very special. It lived in a big garden with many flowers.
One day, the bug wanted to change. It wanted to be different from the other bugs. So, it went to find a magic flower in the garden that could help it.
The bug searched and searched until it found the magic flower. The magic flower changed the bug into a beautiful butterfly. Now, the rare bug was even more special and it flew happily in the garden.

One day, a little girl named Lily heard a noise in her room. She looked around and saw a small, fake toy brain on the floor. Lily was not scared, but she was curious about the toy brain. She picked it up and decided to keep it.
The next day, Lily heard the same noise again. She looked around and saw that the fake toy brain was gone! She searched her room and found it under her bed. Lily thought it was strange, but she put the toy brain back in its place.
On the third day, Lily heard the noise once more. This time, she saw the fake toy brain move! It had tiny legs and was walking around her room. Lily was surprised but happy to have a new friend. She played with the toy brain all day, and they had lots of fun together.

Tommy likes to play with his toys in his room. He has cars, trains, blocks and a clock. The clock is big and round and has numbers and hands. Tommy likes to watch the clock and make noises like tick-tock, tick-tock.
But one day, Tommy's mom tells him to stop playing and get ready for bed. Tommy does not want to go to bed. He wants to play more with his clock. He says no to his mom and holds the clock tight. His mom tries to take the clock from him, but he pulls it back. They tug and tug on the clock.
Then, something bad happens. The clock breaks. The hands fall off and the numbers fly away. The clock makes a loud noise like BANG! Tommy and his mom are scared and sad. They look at the broken clock and the mess on the floor.
Tommy feels sorry for what he did. He knows he should have listened to his mom and released the clock. He says sorry to his mom and gives her a hug. His mom says sorry too and hugs him back. She tells him she loves him and that they can fix the clock together tomorrow. Tommy nods and says he loves her too. He is ready to go to bed now. He hopes the clock will be okay.

Ben and Lily were playing with their blocks. They liked to build towers and houses and bridges. Sometimes they shared their blocks and sometimes they wanted their own.
"Can I have the red block, please?" Lily asked Ben.
"No, it is mine. I need it for my tower," Ben said.
"But I want it for my roof. Please, Ben, give it to me," Lily said.
They both pulled the red block and it broke in half. They heard a loud crack and saw the pieces on the floor.
"Oh no, we broke it!" Ben said.
Lily started to cry. She was sad and angry. She did not want to play with Ben anymore.
Ben felt sorry. He did not mean to break the block. He wanted to make Lily happy again.
He looked around and saw a blue block. He picked it up and gave it to Lily.
"Here, you can have this one. It is a nice blue. You can use it for your roof," he said.
Lily stopped crying and looked at the blue block. She smiled and took it from Ben.
"Thank you, Ben. That is very thoughtful of you. I like the blue block. It is a pretty blue. You can use the red pieces for your tower," she said.
They hugged and said sorry to each other. They started to play with their blocks again. They were happy and friendly. They learned to share and be kind.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw a big tree with a sign that said, "Do not poke the tree." Tim asked his mom, "What does the sign say?" His mom said, "It is a law. We should not poke the tree. It can be difficult for the tree."
Tim saw a squirrel on the tree. He wanted to poke the squirrel, but he remembered the law. Tim said to the squirrel, "I will not poke you or the tree. It is a law and it can be difficult for you."
The squirrel said, "Thank you, Tim, for not poking me or the tree. It is a good law." Tim and his mom went home, happy that they followed the law and did not poke the tree.

Once upon a time, there was a big red dog named Max. Max had a special job to do. He had to protect a shiny trophy that was very important to his family.
One day, a naughty cat named Whiskers saw the trophy and wanted to take it. Whiskers said, "I want that trophy, and I will take it from you!" Max didn't want to let Whiskers take the trophy, so he stood in front of it and said, "No, you can't have it! I must protect it!"
Whiskers tried to jump over Max to get the trophy, but Max was too big and strong. Whiskers tried to go around Max, but Max moved to block him every time. Finally, Whiskers gave up and said, "Okay, you win. I won't take the trophy."
Max was happy that he protected the trophy and kept it safe for his family. His family was very proud of him, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once there was a little girl called Daisy who wanted to be a princess. She loved pink and wanted to be surrounded by it all of the time. One day her mom came in with a big box from the store. Daisy ran up to it and carefully opened it. Inside was a pink crown with feathers and glitter. Daisy's eyes opened wide with surprise! She was so delighted that she put it on straight away. 
However, soon Daisy started to realize that the crown was a bit fake. She asked her mom, "Is this crown real?" 
Her mom smiled and said, "No, Daisy, it's not real. But even if it isn't real, you can still trust in the feeling it gives you. You don't need a real crown to feel like a princess."
Daisy smiled. She knew that was true. Even though the crown was fake, it still made her feel special. She decided to trust in that feeling. 
And so Daisy proudly wore her fake pink crown and became the princess she always wanted to be!

Once upon a time, there was an enormous dog. His name was Old. Old was very big and very strong. He lived in a small house with a little girl named Sue. Sue loved Old very much.
One day, Sue and Old went to the park. They played catch with a big red ball. Old could run very fast. Sue laughed as Old chased the ball. They played until the sun went down.
When it was time to go home, Sue and Old walked together. They were tired but happy. They had so much fun at the park. Sue gave Old a big hug and said, "I love you, Old." And Old wagged his tail, happy to be with his best friend Sue.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. He had a big, pretty flag. His friend, Sam, saw the flag and was envious. He wanted a flag too. Sam felt sad and upset.
One day, Sam decided to poke Tim's flag. He thought it would make him feel better. But when he poked the flag, it fell down. Tim was angry and yelled at Sam. They both felt bad.
Later, Sam said sorry to Tim. Tim forgave him. They went to the store and bought a flag for Sam. Now, both friends had flags and were happy. They played together with their flags and had lots of fun.

One day, a pale boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big tree with strong roots. Tim sat under the tree and looked around. He saw a little bird with a broken wing. The bird was sad.
Tim said, "Little bird, why are you sad?" The bird said, "I can't fly. My wing is broken." Tim wanted to help the bird. He thought of a way to restore the bird's wing.
Tim found a stick and some leaves. He put the leaves on the bird's wing and tied it with the stick. The bird tried to fly, but it couldn't. Tim was sad too. Then, a big wind blew and something unexpected happened. The wind took the leaves and stick away, but the bird's wing was now fixed!
The bird said, "Thank you, Tim!" The bird flew away, happy. Tim learned that sometimes, when we try to help, things might not go as planned. But if we keep trying, good things can happen.

Once upon a time, there was a strong boy named Tom. He loved to play outside in the sun. One day, he saw a beautiful tree with a green lime on it.
Tom wanted the lime, so he asked his friend, Sue, to help. Sue was a smart girl who always had good ideas. She said, "Let's kneel down and make a plan to get the lime."
Tom and Sue kneeled down and thought hard. Then, they decided to climb the tree together. Tom went first, and Sue followed him. They reached the top and picked the lime.
"Yay! We did it!" Tom said with a big smile. Sue smiled too, and they both went down the tree. They shared the lime and enjoyed the sweet taste. They were happy that they could work together and have fun.

Once upon a time, in a big zoo, there was a happy zebra. The zebra had black and white stripes. It liked to run and play with its friends.
One day, the zebra saw a door. The door was open. The zebra went through the door and found a new place to play. It was a big park with green grass and tall trees.
The zebra played all day in the park. It was getting dark. The zebra wanted to go back to the zoo. It went back to the door, but the door was shut. The zebra was sad.
A kind man saw the zebra and opened the door. The zebra went back to the zoo. It was so happy to be home with its friends. The zebra learned to always shut the door when it goes out to play. And they all played happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a farm. On the farm, there was a pig named Piggy. Piggy was a helpless pig. He could not twist his tail. All the other pigs could twist their tails, but not Piggy.
One day, a man came to the farm. The man said, "I need a pig with a twisty tail." All the pigs twisted their tails. But Piggy could not twist his tail. The man saw Piggy and said, "I will take this pig."
Piggy was scared. He did not want to go with the man. But then, something unexpected happened. The man took Piggy to a nice farm. There were many animals, and they all had something different about them. Piggy was happy. He learned that it is okay to be different.

Once upon a time, there was a flea named Fifi. Fifi was a new flea, and she loved to jump. She would jump from one place to another, having fun all day long.
One day, Fifi met a big dog named Dodo. Dodo was sad because he could not jump as high as Fifi. Fifi said, "I can help you jump high like me, Dodo!" Dodo was happy and wanted to learn from Fifi.
Fifi and Dodo practiced jumping together every day. Soon, Dodo could jump high too. They both had fun jumping around and playing. They became best friends and lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a hungry little bird named Tim. Tim lived in a big tree with his family. One day, Tim saw a shiny ornament hanging on a small tree. It was very pretty and he wanted to take it home.
Tim took the ornament in his beak and flew back to his tree. But, he started to regret taking it. He knew it was not his and he should not have taken it. Tim felt sad and wanted to make it right.
So, Tim took the ornament back to the small tree and hung it back up. He said sorry to the tree and flew back home. Tim felt much better now. He learned that it is not good to take things that are not yours. And from that day on, Tim only took what he needed and shared with his friends.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a new toy. The toy was very happy to meet a little boy named Tim. Tim and the toy played all day long. They had so much fun together. They would laugh and run around the house.
One day, Tim and the toy went outside to play. They saw a big door. Tim said, "Let's go close the door!" They ran to the door and closed it together. After they closed the door, they felt peace. They liked the quiet and calm feeling.
Tim and the toy went back inside the house. They felt tired after playing all day. They sat down and took a deep breath. They felt the peace again. Tim hugged the toy and said, "I am happy we are friends." The toy smiled and hugged Tim back. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim lived in a small town with his mom and dad. One day, Tim's mom said, "We will go to the festival today!" Tim was very happy.
At the festival, there were many fun games and yummy food. Tim's mom gave him some money to buy a toy. Tim saw a hidden shop with a big, red ball. He used his money to buy the ball. Tim played with the ball all day.
As it got dark, Tim and his family started to go home. On the way, Tim saw a big hill. He wanted to play with his ball one more time. He threw the ball down the hill, but it went too far. Tim ran after the ball, but he could not find it. The ball was lost. Tim was very sad.

One day, a group of ants went on a march. They wanted to gather food for their home. They walked and walked, feeling tired but happy to help their friends.
As they walked, they saw a big, tasty cookie. "Look!" said one ant, "Let's take this back home!" The other ants agreed and started to carry the cookie together. They were very excited to show their friends what they found.
But, as they carried the cookie, it started to rain. The cookie got wet and broke into little pieces. The ants were sad, but then they saw something unexpected. Inside the cookie were many yummy treats! They gathered the treats and took them back home. Their friends were so happy, and they all shared the tasty surprise.

Once upon a time, there was a small boy named Tim. He lived in a tiny house with his mom and dad. Tim loved to eat pastry. His mom would make the best pastry in their little town.
One day, Tim saw a big bird flying in the distant sky. The bird was so big and pretty. Tim wanted to be friends with the big bird. So, he decided to pray. He closed his eyes and said, "Please, big bird, come be my friend."
The next day, the big bird came to Tim's house. Tim was so happy! He gave the bird some pastry to eat. The bird loved the pastry too. From that day on, Tim and the big bird were the best of friends. They played together every day and shared many pastries.

Once upon a time, there was a big snowman. He lived in a distant place where it was very cold. He was a very smart snowman. He liked to give lectures to his snowman friends about how to stay cold and not melt.
One day, the sun came out and it was very warm. The snowman was worried because he knew that if it got too warm, he and his friends would melt. He gave a lecture to his friends about how to find shade under trees and stay away from the sun.
The snowman's friends listened to his lecture and they all went to find shade under the trees. They stayed cold and did not melt. The sun went away and it got cold again. The snowman and his friends were happy because they did not melt. They thanked the smart snowman for his lecture and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue was a normal girl who liked to play with her toys. One day, Sue saw a big cross on the ground. She did not know what it was for.
Sue's friend, Tim, came over to play. "What is this cross?" asked Sue. Tim said, "I don't know, but we should not touch it. It might delay our playtime." Sue and Tim decided to play with their toys near the cross, but they did not touch it.
As they played, a big wind came and blew their toys away. Sue and Tim tried to catch them, but they could not. The wind blew the toys far away. Sue and Tim were sad because they lost their toys. They wished they had not played near the cross.

Once upon a time, there was a kind girl named Sue. Sue had a friend named Tim. Tim was scared because he had a bad dream. The bad dream made Tim cry at night. Sue wanted to help Tim feel better.
Sue had an idea. She told Tim to clap his hands when he felt scared. Clap, clap, clap, went Tim's hands. Tim felt a little better when he clapped. Sue was happy that she could help her friend.
Every time Tim had a bad dream, he would clap his hands. Clap, clap, clap. Sue was always there to help him. They became best friends. Tim learned that with a kind friend like Sue, he didn't have to be scared of bad dreams anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot lived in a warm house near a big station. Every day, he saw big trains come and go. Spot wanted to see the world outside the station.
One day, Spot saw an open door. "I can escape!" he thought. So, he ran out of the house and into the station. There, he met a little girl named Sue. Sue was going on a train ride with her mom.
"Hi, Spot!" said Sue. "Do you want to come with me?" Spot wagged his tail and jumped on the train with Sue. They had a fun day seeing new places together. At the end of the day, they went back to the warm house by the station. Spot was happy he had escaped and seen the world with his new friend, Sue.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved pumpkins. He had a big pumpkin in his yard. Tim was careful with his pumpkin. He wanted it to grow big and round.
One sunny day, Tim's friend, Lily, came over to play. "Wow!" said Lily, "Your pumpkin is so big!" Tim smiled and said, "I know! Let's set it in a special spot so it can get more sun." So, Tim and Lily were careful and moved the pumpkin to a sunny place.
Days went by, and the pumpkin got bigger and bigger. Tim and Lily were so happy. They played with the pumpkin every day. They knew that being careful and setting it in the sun helped it grow. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there was a daddy who loved his little girl very much. Every evening he would ask her what she wanted to do, and she would always smile and say that she wanted to play with him. 
One evening, the little girl asked her daddy to go to the park. He said yes, as long as they were polite while they were there. So they put on their shoes and went to the park. They had a lot of fun, throwing the ball around, riding the swings and running around. 
When the little girl was ready to go home, she remembered that her daddy asked her to be polite while they were there. So she thanked the other people at the park, and apologized if her ball had ever hurt anyone accidentally. 
When they got home, the daddy hugged his girl and told her that he was proud of her for being polite. He said that being polite was loving and showed respect for other people. 
This story had a moral value - that being polite is an act of love and shows respect for other people.

One day, a mild cat named Mimi saw a big star in the sky. Mimi made a wish on the star. She wished to lead all the cats and dogs to a fun park.
The next day, Mimi told all her cat and dog friends about her wish. They all wanted to go to the fun park too. But, there was a big, mean dog named Bubba who did not want to go.
Mimi and her friends tried to find a way to the fun park without Bubba. They saw a path in the woods that would lead them there. They walked and walked on the path, but Bubba found them.
Bubba tried to stop Mimi and her friends from going to the fun park. But Mimi was brave and told Bubba that he could come too if he was nice. Bubba thought about it and said yes.
In the end, Mimi's wish came true. She led all the cats and dogs to the fun park. They all had a great time, even Bubba. And they all learned that being kind and working together was the best way to have fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to design things. One day, he wanted to make a tunnel. He got a big box and started to work.
Tim cut a hole in the box to make the tunnel. The tunnel was dark and gloomy inside. Tim was not scared. He took a flashlight and went into the tunnel.
In the tunnel, Tim had fun. He played with his cars and trucks. He made the cars go in and out of the tunnel. Tim was happy with his tunnel design. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a loyal dog named Max. They loved to play together. One day, Tim found a flashlight in his room. He was very happy.
At night, Tim and Max went outside with the flashlight. Tim turned it on, and it made a big, bright light. Max barked, "Woof! Woof!" Tim said, "Look, Max! We can play with the flashlight in the dark."
They played a fun game. Tim would shine the flashlight on the ground, and Max would chase the light. They laughed and played until they were very tired. Then they went back inside and went to sleep. Tim and Max had the best time with their new flashlight.

Once upon a time, in a hidden small house near the woods, there lived a little girl named Lily. She had a pretty white dove as her pet. Lily and the dove played together every day. They loved to run and jump in the green grass.
One sunny day, Lily and the dove were playing near the woods. They were having a lot of fun. But then, the dove flew away into the trees. Lily looked and looked, but she could not find her dove. Lily started to weep because she was very sad.
As Lily wept, a kind squirrel heard her and wanted to help. The squirrel climbed the trees and found the dove. The squirrel told the dove that Lily was sad and looking for her. The dove flew back to Lily quickly. When Lily saw her dove, she was so happy, and they hugged each other. They played together for the rest of the day, and they knew they would always be best friends.

Anna and Ben were playing in the garden. They had a toy gun and a basket of roses. They pretended to be spies and bad guys.
"Bang, bang, I shoot you, Ben!" Anna said, pointing the toy gun at Ben.
"No, you miss, I shoot you, Anna!" Ben said, pointing the toy gun at Anna.
They ran around the garden, shooting and dodging. They had fun.
Then they saw a big rose bush. It had many roses of different colors. They stopped shooting and looked at the roses.
"Wow, look at all the roses!" Anna said.
"They are pretty," Ben said.
They picked some roses and put them in their basket. They saw one rose that was different from the others. It was white with red spots.
"Look, this rose is original," Anna said.
"What does original mean?" Ben asked.
"It means it is not like the others. It is special," Anna said.
"Can I have it?" Ben asked.
"OK, you can have it. It is a gift for you," Anna said.
"Thank you, Anna. You are a good friend," Ben said.
They hugged and smiled. They went back to their game, but they did not shoot anymore. They gave roses to each other instead. They were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a shy little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her blocks. One day, she built a big tower with her blocks in the living room.
Lily's big brother, Tom, saw the tower and said, "Wow, that's a big tower, Lily! Can I play too?" Lily just shrugged and said, "Okay, but be careful."
Tom started to play with the blocks, but he was not careful. He knocked down Lily's tower by accident. The blocks fell all over the floor. Lily was sad and cried.
Tom tried to say sorry, but Lily was too upset. She did not want to play with her blocks anymore. The day ended with Lily feeling sad and her tower broken.

One day, a little girl named Lily went for a wander in her backyard. She loved to explore and see what she could find. The sun was shining, and the grass was green. Lily felt happy and healthy as she walked and played.
As she wandered, she found a big, colorful cube. It was red, blue, and yellow. She picked it up and saw that it had numbers and letters on it. Lily liked to learn, so she thought the cube was fun.
Lily played with the cube all day. She showed it to her mom and dad, and they were happy to see her having fun. At the end of the day, Lily put the cube in her room. She knew she would play with it again tomorrow.

Once upon a time, there was a delicate little ant named Andy. Andy lived by the sea with his ant family. One day, Andy found a big anchor near the water. He thought it was a great place to play.
Andy called his friend, Benny the beetle, to play with him. "Benny, come see the big anchor I found!" Andy said. Benny marched right over to see the anchor. They marched around the anchor, playing and laughing.
As the sun began to set, Andy and Benny knew it was time to go home. They said goodbye and marched back to their families. They had a fun day playing by the big anchor and couldn't wait to play again tomorrow.

Tom and Anna are friends. They like to play outside. One day, they see a big puddle of mud. They want to have fun.
"Let's run in the mud!" Tom says. He runs fast and jumps in the puddle. He splashes mud all over himself. He laughs.
"Me too!" Anna says. She runs after Tom and jumps in the puddle too. She splashes mud all over herself and Tom. She giggles.
They run and jump and splash in the mud. They are very happy. They do not care that they are dirty.
But then, they hear a voice. It is Mom. She is not happy.
"Tom! Anna! What are you doing? You are covered in mud! Come here right now!" Mom says. She is angry.
Tom and Anna stop playing. They look at Mom. They look at each other. They are scared.
They walk slowly to Mom. They try to smile. They hope Mom will not be too mad.
Mom looks at them. She sees their muddy clothes and faces and hair. She sees their charming smiles. She sighs.
"You two are very naughty. But you are also very cute. Come on, let's go inside and get clean. And then we can have some cookies." Mom says. She is not so angry anymore.
Tom and Anna are relieved. They hug Mom. They say sorry. They love Mom.
They go inside and get clean. They have some cookies. They are still friends. They are still happy.

One day, a little bunny named Max was in the garden. He saw a big carrot. Max was hungry, so he wanted to eat the carrot. He pulled and pulled, but the carrot did not come out. Max was sad.
Then, Max saw his friend, a little bird named Lily. Max asked Lily, "Can you help me get the carrot?" Lily said, "Yes, I can help you!" So, Max and Lily pulled and pulled together. The carrot came out!
But, something strange happened. The carrot started to glow! Max and Lily were frightened. They did not know what to do. Then, the glowing carrot turned into a beautiful fairy! The fairy said, "Thank you for helping me! I was stuck as a carrot. Now, I will give you both a wish!" Max and Lily were happy, and they wished for more yummy food to share with their friends.

Once upon a time, a young girl named Alice was walking through the forest. All of a sudden, she saw something scary in the distance. She was very frightened and started running. As she was running, Alice tripped and fell into a deep, dark hole. 
Alice began to panic as she realised she had no way of getting out of the hole. "Help!" she shouted but no one was around to hear her. Suddenly, Alice heard a noise from further down the hole. It was a furry, wriggling creature. 
Alice saw that the creature was carrying a long, strong rope. She was so relieved, she quickly grabbed the rope and began to climb. But then, the creature started to tug on the other end. Alice got even more frightened and shouted for help again. 
Just then, Alice could hear the sound of footsteps. It was her family who had heard her call for help and had come to rescue her. Alice was so happy, she quickly climbed out of the hole with the rope. Her family were so glad she was safe. What a relief!

Once upon a time, there was a nice girl named June. She enjoyed going for walks and playing with her friends. One day, June was walking by the lake when she heard an unusual sound. She stopped to listen and it sounded like something was melting. She got close to the noise and saw that an ice block was melting. It made a normal sound but June was very excited to see the ice melting. 
Just then, June heard a voice. "Hi June, why have you stopped walking?" It was her friend, Jack. 
June explained the strange sound she heard and Jack got excited too. They watched the ice melt until it became a small puddle on the ground. They smiled and walked away together. 
The end!

One day, a boy named Tim went to the beach with his mom. They wanted to play in the sand and have fun. Tim's mom said, "Stay close to me, so you are safe."
Tim saw a big sand hill and wanted to try to climb it. He asked his mom, "Can I try to climb the big sand hill?" His mom said, "Yes, but be careful!"
Tim started to climb the sand hill. When he got to the top, he saw a big crab. The crab said, "Hello, I am a talking crab! I can make a wish come true for you." Tim was very surprised. He wished for a big sand castle. The crab clapped his claws, and a big sand castle appeared. Tim and his mom played in the sand castle all day, and they had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a toy named Mr. Bear. Mr. Bear was very comfortable to hug. Lily loved Mr. Bear a lot.
One sunny day, Lily saw a shape on the ground. It was a circle. She wanted to find more shapes. So, she took Mr. Bear and let him lead her around the yard. They found a square and a triangle too.
Lily and Mr. Bear had a fun day finding shapes. They felt tired and went back to their cozy home. Lily hugged Mr. Bear and fell asleep, happy with the shapes they found.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Anna. Anna was three years old and she loved to explore. One day, she decided to explore the office next door. She slowly opened the door and walked inside.
When she opened the door, Anna realized the office was empty. She looked around and noticed that the room was filled with troubles. Everything was in disarray. 
Just then, Anna heard a voice. "Hello! Is anyone here?" She asked.
A kind old man stepped out from behind a desk. He said, "Yes, I'm here. What can I help you with?"
Anna smiled at him. "I was just trying to fill up this office," she said.
The man smiled and said, "Let me help you. I can show you how to fill this place up with nice things."
Anna happily followed the old man around the office, learning how to add a little bit of joy in each corner. Soon enough, the office was filled with bright and cheerful things.
Anna thanked the old man for his help and said goodbye. She walked out of the office with a smile on her face, knowing she had made the troubled office a lot brighter.

Tom and Anna are twins. They like to play with their cars. Tom has a red car and Anna has a blue car. They have a race track in their room.
"Ready, set, go!" Tom says. He pushes his car on the track. His car goes very fast. He likes speed.
Anna pushes her car too. But her car is slow. It stops in the middle of the track. Anna is frustrated. She wants to win.
"Ha ha, I win!" Tom says. He picks up his car and shows it to Anna. "My car is the best!"
Anna is angry. She grabs Tom's car and throws it on the floor. The car breaks. Tom is sad. He cries.
"Anna, why did you do that?" Tom says. "You spoiled my car!"
Anna feels sorry. She did not mean to break Tom's car. She hugs Tom and says, "I'm sorry, Tom. I was mad. I like your car too."
Tom stops crying. He hugs Anna back and says, "It's okay, Anna. I'm sorry too. I was mean. I like your car too."
They look at their cars. They are both broken. They are sad. But they are still twins. They love each other. They decide to share their cars and fix them together. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had a pink dress that she loved to wear every morning. One day, she woke up and could not find her pink dress.
Lucy started to complain to her mom, "I want my pink dress now!" Her mom looked everywhere, but the dress was still lost. Lucy was very sad and did not want to wear any other dress.
The next day, Lucy's mom found the pink dress, but it was dirty. Lucy learned that she should not complain too much and should be happy with what she has. Sometimes, things do not go the way we want, but we need to be patient and wait.

Once upon a time, there was an old white cat. She lived in a small house with a little girl and her mom. The girl and her mom loved the cat very much. They would play with her, pet her, and fill her bowl with yummy food.
One day, the old white cat went outside to play. She found a big box in the yard. The cat was curious and jumped into the box. The box was filled with white, soft things. The cat liked the soft things and fell asleep in the box.
While the cat was sleeping, the little girl came outside to play. She saw the box and thought it was a great place to hide. She did not know the cat was inside. The girl picked up the box and carried it into the house. When the old white cat woke up, she was back inside her home. The cat was surprised, but happy to be back with her family. The girl and her mom laughed when they found the cat in the box. They all played together and had a fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy little fish named Fin. Fin loved to swim around and talk to his friends in the sea. One day, Fin met a new friend named Bubbles. Bubbles was a tiny crab who lived in a pretty shell.
"Can I borrow some oxygen?" asked Fin. "I want to see what is inside your pretty shell." Bubbles said yes, and Fin took a big breath of oxygen. He swam inside the pretty shell and saw many beautiful things.
Fin and Bubbles played together all day and had lots of fun. They swam and laughed, and Fin learned that it was okay to be nosy sometimes, as long as he asked first. And Bubbles learned that sharing his oxygen and pretty shell with a new friend made him very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a country that had an evil king. The king was very mean and he did not like when people disagreed with him. He wanted everyone to do everything his way.
One day, a wise old man visited the country. He was very kind and had a good heart. He saw the way the king was acting and he was very worried. So one day, he decided to warn the people about the evil king.
The wise old man spoke to all the people in the country and told them about the king's bad behavior. He told them that the king wanted everyone to do everything his way and that it was not good. He asked them to be brave and to stand up to the king when it was necessary.
The people were all very worried, but they listened to the wise old man and thanked him for taking the time to warn them about the evil king. They promised to do the right thing and to never forget the wise old man's warning.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max was sad because his toy was broken. He sighed and looked out the window, dreaming of going on a vacation.
One day, Max's family decided to go on a vacation to the beach. Max was so happy! He wagged his tail and jumped into the car. The family drove to the beach, and Max looked out the window, excited to play in the sand and water.
When they got to the beach, Max ran and played with his family. He splashed in the water and dug holes in the sand. Max even found a new toy to play with! He forgot all about his broken toy and sighed happily, enjoying his fun vacation with his family.

One day, a little dog named Spot went for a walk with his owner, Tom. Tom put a leash on Spot to keep him close. They walked to the park, where many interesting things were happening. Spot saw other dogs playing and children laughing.
In the park, Tom saw a big stage. There was a show going on. Spot and Tom sat down to watch the show. They saw people singing and dancing. The music was fun, and the dancers wore pretty clothes. Spot liked the show very much.
When the show was over, everyone clapped their hands. They did this to say "good job" to the people on the stage. This is called applauding. Spot wanted to applaud too, so he barked and wagged his tail. Tom laughed and patted Spot on the head. They had a great day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a bald man. He was very nice. The bald man had a big scale. He liked to weigh things with his scale. One day, he wanted to unite all his friends for a party.
At the party, the bald man had a fun game. He asked his friends to guess the weight of a big box. The friends used the scale to weigh the box. They all guessed different weights.
Then, something unexpected happened. The big box started to move! The friends were very surprised. They opened the box and found a big, happy dog inside. The dog wanted to play with everyone. The bald man and his friends laughed and played with the dog. The party was so much fun, and they all became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a rich cow. The cow had lots of cream and wanted to do something fun.
One day, the cow had an idea, "I will sail in my cream!" So the cow gathered all the cream and made a little boat. She put all her cream in it and climbed inside.
The cow sailed away in her boat. She sailed far and wide and saw many wonderful things. Along the way, she said hello to the fish, birds and other creatures.
The cow sailed until she came to a beautiful beach. She enjoyed the sunny day and ate some of her rich cream.
At the end of the day, the cow sailed back home in her cream boat and fell asleep with a big smile on her face.

Once upon a time, there was a peaceful bird. The bird lived on a cliff near the sea. The bird liked to clean its nest every day.
One day, the bird saw a big mess on the cliff. The bird felt sad. The bird wanted the cliff to be clean and peaceful again.
The bird called its friends to help clean the cliff. All the birds worked together. They picked up the mess with their beaks. Soon, the cliff was clean and peaceful again. The birds were happy and sang a beautiful song.

Once upon a time, there was a big red barn on a farm. The barn was old and had a broken door. Inside the barn, there were many animals. They all liked to talk and play together. One day, a new animal arrived at the farm. It was a little, lost duck.
The duck went to the barn and said, "Hello, I am new here. Can I stay in the barn with you?" The other animals looked at the duck and said, "Yes, you can stay with us. We like new friends."
The duck was very happy. The animals helped fix the broken door of the barn. They all worked together and soon the door was fixed. The little duck had found a new home and new friends in the big red barn. They all lived happily together and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby bird named Bobo. Bobo was not like other birds. He had a special power. He could shoot lasers from his eyes!
One day, Bobo went to fly in the sky. He saw a big tree. The tree had a big, red apple on it. Bobo wanted the apple but it was too high. So, he used his laser eyes to make the apple fall.
The apple fell down and Bobo ate it. It was very yummy. Bobo was happy. He flew back to his nest and took a nap. And from that day on, Bobo always used his lasers to get yummy food.

One day, a little girl named Lily woke up very excited. She ran to the window and saw fog outside. The fog was so thick, she could not see her friend's house across the street.
Lily put on her coat and went outside. She hoped to play with her friend, Tim. She walked through the fog and knocked on Tim's door. "Tim, let's play in the fog!" she said.
Tim came out, and they both played in the fog. They laughed and had so much fun. They hoped the fog would stay all day, but soon it went away. They were still happy because they had a great time playing together.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sue. Sue had a big job. She had to collect all the socks in her home. Sue loved socks, especially long ones.
One day, Sue found a long sock under her bed. "Who does this sock belong to?" she asked her cat, Max. Max just looked at her and said, "Meow." Sue knew that Max didn't wear socks, so she kept looking.
Sue went to her brother, Tim, and asked, "Is this your long sock?" Tim shook his head and said, "No, it's not mine." Sue was sad, but she didn't give up. She went to her mom and dad and asked them the same question. They both said, "No, it's not ours."
Finally, Sue went to her grandma, who was visiting. "Grandma, is this your long sock?" Sue asked. Grandma smiled and said, "Yes, dear, it's mine! Thank you for finding it." Sue was so happy that she had collected all the socks and found the owner of the long sock. From that day on, Sue and her grandma always had fun collecting socks together.

One day, a little girl named Lily found a package in her room. It was difficult for her to open it, but she tried very hard. Inside the package, there were many colorful candies. Lily was very happy.
Lily wanted to share the candies with her friend, Tom. She went to his house and knocked on the door. "Tom, I have a package of candies! Do you want to taste them with me?" she asked. Tom was excited and said, "Yes, I want to taste the candies too!"
Together, they opened the package and tasted the candies. Some were sweet, and some were sour. They laughed and enjoyed tasting the candies. After they finished, they decided to play outside. It was a fun day for Lily and Tom.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She had a notebook that she loved very much. Lily liked to draw wild animals in her notebook. She drew lions, tigers, and bears. Lily took her notebook everywhere she went.
One day, Lily went to a party. She saw a boy named Tom. Tom was nice and funny. Lily and Tom played and laughed together. They became good friends. Lily showed Tom her notebook with the wild animals. Tom liked the drawings a lot.
After many years, Lily and Tom grew up. They still liked each other very much. One day, Tom asked Lily to marry him. Lily said yes! But, on their wedding day, something unexpected happened. The wild animals from Lily's notebook came to life! They all came to the wedding to celebrate with Lily and Tom. Everyone was surprised, but they all had a great time with the wild animals. And Lily and Tom lived happily ever after.

One day, a nice boy named Tim wanted to eat pizza. He asked his mom, "Mom, can we have pizza today?" His mom said, "Yes, Tim. We can receive pizza from the pizza shop."
Tim and his mom went to the pizza shop. They saw many yummy pizzas. Tim picked a big pizza with cheese. His mom picked a pizza with vegetables. They were very happy.
They took the pizzas home and ate them. Tim said, "This pizza is so nice and yummy!" His mom said, "I'm glad you like it, Tim." They both enjoyed their pizza and had a great day.

Once upon a time, there was a bright sun in the sky. It was very big and shiny. A little mouse named Timmy loved to gaze at the sun.
One day, Timmy went on a walk to find some cheese. Cheese was his favorite food. He looked and looked, but he could not find any cheese.
Finally, Timmy found a big piece of cheese under a tree. He was so happy! Timmy ate the cheese and gazed at the bright sun. It was a good day for Timmy the mouse.

Once upon a time, there was a big cat. The cat liked to wear a hat. The hat was unusual. It was very big and very red. The cat was happy with the hat.
One day, the cat went to the park. He saw a dog. The dog was not wearing a hat yet. The cat said, "You should wear a hat too!" The dog thought it was a good idea.
So, the cat and the dog went to find a hat for the dog. They found a hat that was unusual too. It was very big and very blue. Now, the cat and the dog both wore unusual hats. They were happy and played together in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a big dragon. The dragon was deaf, so he could not hear anything. He lived in a cave near a small town. The people in the town were scared of the dragon, but he was a nice dragon.
One day, the dragon saw a little girl walking near his cave. The girl was cold because she did not have a coat. The dragon wanted to help her. He found a big leaf and used it to cover the girl. The girl was happy and warm now.
The people in the town saw the dragon help the girl. They were not scared of the dragon anymore. They knew he was a nice dragon. The dragon and the people became friends. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He liked to walk on the path near his house. One day, he saw an enormous tree. It was so big, it made him frown. He had never seen such a big tree before.
Tim walked up to the tree and said, "Hello, tree. Why are you so big?" The tree smiled and said, "I'm big because I'm very old. I've been here for a long, long time."
Tim thought for a moment and then said, "I want to be big like you one day." The tree laughed and said, "You will be, Tim. Just eat your food, play, and sleep. You'll grow big and strong."
Tim smiled and said, "Thank you, tree. I'll do that!" And from that day on, Tim made sure to eat, play, and sleep well, so he could grow up to be big and strong like the enormous tree.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a sweet lizard on a rock. The lizard was green and small. Tim was happy to see the lizard.
Tim said, "Hi, lizard! You look nice." The lizard did not talk, but it did display a big smile. Tim smiled back at the lizard. They were friends now.
