Tim and the lizard played in the park. They ran and jumped. The sun was warm and bright. After a fun day, Tim said goodbye to his new friend. The sweet lizard waved and went back to its rock.

Once upon a time, there was an intelligent dog named Spot. Spot had a job to deliver things. He was very good at it. Spot liked to help people.
One day, Spot had to deliver a big box. The box was many inches long. It was heavy too. Spot tried to carry the box, but it was hard. He was sad because he wanted to help.
Spot thought and thought. He had an idea! Spot pushed the box with his nose. It was not easy, but he did it. Spot delivered the box to the person. They were happy. Spot was happy too. He was a very helpful dog.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max was very intelligent. One day, Max saw a little cat stuck in a tree. The cat was in panic. Max wanted to help the cat.
Max had an operation in mind. He called his friend, a bird named Chirpy. Max said, "Chirpy, help me get the cat down from the tree." Chirpy was happy to help Max.
Chirpy flew up to the cat and told her not to panic. Then, Chirpy used her beak to hold the cat's paw. Slowly, Chirpy and the cat came down from the tree. The cat was safe, and Max and Chirpy were happy. They all became good friends.

Once upon a time there was a man who was very thirsty. He needed to drink something. He looked around and saw some news on the table. The news was not very cold and made him a bit dizzy.
But the man was so thirsty he decided to drink the news. He drank and drank and drank until his thirst was satisfied.
But then his head started to spin. He was feeling very dizzy! He ran outside and took a big gulp of fresh air. That's when the dizziness went away and he felt better. 
He learned an important lesson that day - never drink news!

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park. He wanted to play with his ball. While he was playing, he heard a voice. The voice said, "Hello, do you want to play with me?" Tim looked around and saw a girl named Lily. She was holding a toy.
Tim said, "Yes, I want to play with you." So, Tim and Lily played together. They had lots of fun. They ran and laughed. They became good friends. They liked to meet at the park every day.
One day, Tim and Lily found something on the ground. It was green and slimy. It looked disgusting. Tim said, "Yuck! What is that?" Lily said, "I don't know, but it's gross!" They laughed and went back to playing. They were happy to have each other as friends.

One day, a big red car went for a drive. The car liked to go fast. It wanted to speed through a pretty valley. In the valley, there were green trees, soft grass, and blue sky. The car felt happy when it saw the valley.
As the car went faster, it saw a small dog. The dog had a serious face. The car slowed down and stopped. The dog looked at the car and said, "Please be careful when you speed. There are many animals in the valley."
The big red car listened to the dog. It wanted to be a good friend to the animals in the valley. So, the car drove slowly and carefully. The dog was happy, and they became friends. The car and the dog had fun in the valley together.

One day, a little girl named Sue was playing in the park. She was glad to be outside. She saw a big tree with leaves. Sue wanted to make a print with the leaves.
Sue picked up a leaf and ran to her mom. "Mom, can I make a print with this leaf?" Sue asked. Mom said, "Yes, but we need to go home to get paper and paint."
On the way home, they saw a big dog. The dog wanted the leaf. Sue did not want to give the leaf to the dog. The dog tried to take the leaf from Sue. Sue was scared.
Mom said, "Leave, dog! This is Sue's leaf." The dog left. Sue was happy. They went home and Sue made a print with the leaf. Sue was glad she got to make her leaf print.

Once upon a time, there was a big rhinoceros named Remy. Remy lived in a green forest with many friends. He liked to play with his friends, but he felt jealous when they played without him.
One day, Remy saw his friends playing near the river. They were having so much fun, but they did not ask Remy to play. Remy felt sad and angry. He wanted to play too.
Remy walked up to his friends and said, "I want to play too!" He then let out a loud roar to show he was upset. His friends stopped playing and looked at Remy. They said, "We are sorry, Remy. We did not know you wanted to play. Please come play with us."
Remy felt happy that his friends cared about him. They all played together near the river, and Remy's roar made everyone laugh. From that day on, they always played together and no one felt jealous anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Spot. Spot loved to play and dive into the big blue sea. He liked to find things in the water. One day, Spot saw something incredible in the sea. It was a big, white bone. Spot wanted that bone very much.
Spot said to his friend, the fish, "Look at that big bone! I want it!" The fish said, "Let's go get it, Spot!" So, Spot and the fish swam fast to the bone. They had to dive deep down into the sea to reach it.
Spot grabbed the bone with his mouth. He was so happy! He swam back up to the top of the water with the fish. Spot said, "Thank you, fish, for helping me find this incredible bone!" The fish said, "You're welcome, Spot! Let's play together again soon!" And they both played happily in the big blue sea.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the church with his mom. They went there to see the big, pretty balloons. Tim saw a red balloon that was very high up. It was so high that it started to rise and touch the sky. Tim was sad because he wanted the red balloon.
"Why does the red balloon rise so high?" Tim asked his mom. His mom said, "The balloon wants to be with the other balloons in the sky. It is envious of them."
Tim saw a girl named Sally with a blue balloon. Sally's balloon was not rising. Tim wanted Sally's balloon, but she did not want to give it to him. They argued about the balloon.
In the end, Tim's mom bought him a green balloon. Tim was happy with his green balloon, and Sally kept her blue balloon. They both played together with their balloons, and the red balloon in the sky was not envious anymore.

One day, a little boy named Tim was eager to play outside. He saw that the sun was shining and the snow was melting. He put on his coat and ran out the door.
Tim found a big stick with a handle. He said, "I will use this stick to make a path in the snow!" He started to push the snow away, making a path for his friends to walk on. As he worked, he saw his friend Sue coming down the road.
"Hi Sue!" Tim called. "Look at the path I made with my stick!" Sue smiled and said, "Wow, that's a great path, Tim! Now we can play together without getting our feet wet in the melting snow!" They both laughed and played on the path Tim had made, happy to enjoy the sunny day.

Once upon a time there was a grumpy baby. His brain was always thinking. One day he had a question, but no one seemed to have the answer.
The baby became so sad and grumpy about not having an answer. Then he had an idea. He decided to ask his brain for the answer!
So the baby closed his eyes, thought really hard and waited for an answer. Suddenly, his brain started working! He opened his eyes and smiled. He had his answer.
The baby felt so proud of himself. He couldn't believe his brain had answered his question. He felt very happy, and went to find someone to tell them what he had found out.

Once upon a time, there was a gifted mole. The mole was very good at finding things. One day, he met a little boy. The boy was sad because he lost his toy. The mole wanted to help the boy.
The mole said, "I will show you where your toy is." The boy and the mole started to look for the toy. They looked under rocks and behind trees. The mole used his gifted nose to sniff out the toy.
At last, the mole found the toy. He showed it to the boy. The boy was so happy to have his toy back. He hugged the mole and said, "Thank you, gifted mole!" The boy and the mole became best friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely sunflower. She had no friends to play with. 
Every day she watched the other sunflowers in the meadow and longed to be like them.
One day, the sunflower saw a beautiful lady walking in the meadow. The sunflower looked at the lady, who was wearing a bright yellow dress. The sunflower was filled with joy and happiness!
The lady sat down beside the sunflower and started to talk to her. The sunflower was so happy to have someone to talk to. The lady told the sunflower stories and taught her about the wonderful things in the meadow.
Now the sunflower had friends to play with, and she was no longer lonely. Whenever the sun was shining, the sunflower was happy to be surrounded by all her new friends!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily lived in a small house with her mom and dad. She loved to play outside in the garden. One day, she found a tiny mushroom. It was not a boring mushroom, it was a magic mushroom!
Lily wanted to see what the magic mushroom could do. She took a peek at the mushroom and said, "Mushroom, please show me your magic!" Suddenly, the mushroom started to grow bigger and bigger. Lily was so happy to see the magic.
Lily played with the magic mushroom all day. She showed it to her mom and dad, and they were very surprised. They all had fun with the magic mushroom. When the day was over, the mushroom became small again. Lily said goodnight to the mushroom and went to bed. She couldn't wait to play with the magic mushroom again tomorrow.

One day, a big bear and a little mouse were in the woods. They saw a tall tree with sweet fruits on top. The bear wanted to get the fruits, but the tree was too high. The mouse had an idea. "Let's use a ladder to reach the fruits!" said the mouse.
The bear didn't know what a ladder was. He was ignorant about many things. The mouse showed him a ladder and they put it against the tree. The mouse said, "First, we must measure the ladder to see if it is long enough to reach the fruits."
The big bear and the little mouse measured the ladder. It was just the right size! They climbed up the ladder and picked the sweet fruits from the tree. The bear was very happy and thanked the mouse for teaching him about ladders and measuring.
From that day on, the big bear and the little mouse became best friends. They learned many new things together and shared their sweet fruits with each other. The bear was no longer ignorant, and they both enjoyed their time in the woods.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a big guitar. He was very happy. Tim loved to play with the guitar every day. He would play and sing songs. He was an honest boy who always told the truth.
One day, Tim's friend, Sam, came to play. Sam saw the big guitar and wanted to play too. "Can I play with the guitar?" Sam asked. Tim said, "Yes, but please be careful. We don't want to break it." Sam promised to be careful.
While they were playing, Sam started to get hungry. He looked at the guitar and thought it looked like a big cookie. Sam took a bite of the guitar. The guitar broke! Tim was sad. Sam said, "I'm sorry, I thought it was a cookie." Tim knew Sam was an honest friend, so he forgave him. They decided to fix the guitar together and continued to play and sing happily.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very hungry. Today was his birthday. He was so excited!
His mom made him a big cake. The cake was very yummy. Tim could not wait to eat it! He wanted to chew the cake fast.
But Tim did not listen to his mom. She told him to chew slow. He ate the cake too fast. Tim felt bad. His tummy hurt a lot. His birthday was not fun anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She liked to help her mom in the kitchen. One day, they decided to make cookies. Amy was very excited.
First, they mixed all the ingredients in a big bowl. The dough was wet and sticky. Amy loved to touch it. Then, they put the cookies on a tray and put the tray in the oven. The oven was hot, so Amy's mom did it.
While they waited for the cookies to bake, Amy and her mom cleaned up. They put the leftover dough in a box to store it for later. When the cookies were done, they took them out of the oven. The cookies were warm and yummy. Amy and her mom shared the cookies with their family, and everyone was happy.

One day, a happy little girl named Lily was playing in her room. She wanted to wear her favorite dress, but she couldn't find it. She looked in her closet, but it was not there. She felt sad and asked her mom, "Mom, where is my favorite dress?"
Her mom said, "I had to replace it with a new dress because it was too small. You can pick a new dress from the closet." Lily looked at the new dress, but she still missed her favorite one. She didn't want to wear the new dress and started to cry.
Her mom hugged her and said, "It's okay to miss your old dress, but sometimes we need to let go of old things to make room for new ones." Lily thought about it and decided to try the new dress. She put it on and saw that it was very pretty. She felt happy again and learned that sometimes change can be good.

Once upon a time, there was a gray cat. The gray cat lived in a small house with a backyard. The cat liked to play in the backyard every day. It was a happy cat.
One day, a big dog came into the backyard. The gray cat was scared. The dog and the cat did not like each other. They wanted the backyard all to themselves. They started to fight.
The gray cat tried to slap the big dog. But the dog was too strong. The dog slapped the cat back. The gray cat was sad and hurt. It could not play in the backyard anymore. The big dog stayed, and the gray cat had to find a new place to play.

One day, a cat named Mark and a dog named Cord went for a walk. They were best friends. The sun was shining and they were very happy. Mark was very flexible and could jump high in the sky. Cord was strong and could pull heavy things.
As they walked, they found a big box. "What's in the box?" asked Mark. "I don't know, let's open it!" said Cord. They opened the box and found a big, long rope. The rope was also flexible, just like Mark. They were excited to play with the rope.
Mark and Cord played jump rope with the flexible rope. They had lots of fun. Then, something unexpected happened. The rope started to talk! "Thank you for playing with me," said the rope. Mark and Cord were very surprised but happy to have a new friend. From that day on, the three friends played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big, red couch in her living room. Lily loved to play on the couch. She would jump on it and sit on it. But one day, her toy bunny vanished. Lily was very sad and did not know where it went.
Lily asked her mom, "Mommy, where did my bunny go?" Her mom said, "Maybe it is hiding. Let's look for it." So, they both looked for the bunny. They looked under the couch, behind the couch, and on top of the couch. But they could not find the bunny.
Finally, Lily's mom said, "Lily, maybe your bunny is lost because you did not take care of it. You must learn to put your toys away when you are done playing." Lily felt bad and promised to take better care of her toys. The next day, Lily found her bunny under a hard pillow. She was very happy and remembered to put her toys away from then on. The moral of the story is to take care of your things, so they do not vanish.

Once upon a time, there was a mighty vehicle named Big Red. Big Red was a big, red truck who loved to help people. One sunny day, Big Red saw a little girl named Lily who was sad.
"Hi, Lily! Why are you sad?" asked Big Red.
"I want to go to the park, but I'm too tired to walk," said Lily.
Big Red smiled and said, "I can help you! Hop on, and I will take you to the park."
Lily climbed onto Big Red, and they went to the park. They had so much fun playing on the swings and slides. After a while, Lily got tired again. So, they found a nice spot under a big tree, and Lily and Big Red took a rest. They were both very happy and had a great day at the park.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a shy girl named Lily. She liked to pick flowers in the big park. One day, while picking flowers, she saw something shiny on the ground. It was a diamond! She was very happy and surprised.
Lily wanted to show her mom the diamond. On her way home, she met her friend, Tom. "Look, Tom! I found a diamond!" she said. Tom looked at the diamond and said, "Wow, that's pretty! Let's go show our moms."
When they showed their moms the diamond, the moms were very surprised too. But then, something unexpected happened. The diamond started to glow and change colors! The moms and kids were all amazed.
Suddenly, the diamond turned into a beautiful fairy! The fairy said, "Thank you for finding me, Lily and Tom. I was lost and needed help. As a thank you, I will give you both one wish." Lily and Tom were so happy and made their wishes. From that day on, they knew that sometimes, even the shyest people can have the biggest adventures.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Hanna. She was three years old and loved to explore new places.
One day her parents took her to a country far away. Hanna was very excited, but also a bit scared. 
When they arrived, Hanna saw many tall buildings and busy streets. She wanted to explore, but her parents said no.
Hanna asked "Why can't I explore?"
Her father said, "Hanna, this country can be a dangerous place. We have to wrap you up in a blanket so that you stay safe."
Hanna was a little scared at first, but she soon felt comfortable. Her parents wrapped her up in the blanket and held her close.
Hanna realized she was safe, and explored the new country with her parents. She saw lots of new and exciting things!

Anna and Ben were playing in the living room. They saw a big candle on the table. It was red and smelled nice.
"Let's play with the candle," Ben said. "It looks fun."
"No, Ben, we can't," Anna said. "Mom said candles are not toys. They are hot and can hurt us."
But Ben did not listen. He climbed on the table and reached for the candle. He pulled it hard and it fell on the floor. It broke into many pieces.
"Oh no!" Anna cried. "You broke the candle! Mom will be angry!"
Ben was scared. He tried to pick up the pieces, but they were sharp and sticky. He cut his finger and it bled.
"Ow!" Ben said. "It hurts!"
Anna ran to get a band-aid and some tissue. She helped Ben clean his finger and put the band-aid on. She also wrapped the tissue around the broken candle.
"Let's hide the candle under the sofa," Anna said. "Maybe Mom won't see it."
They pushed the candle under the sofa and hoped Mom would not find out. They learned their lesson. They would never play with candles again.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to a fun park with his mom. They wanted to go on a big ride. To get on the ride, they needed a ticket. Tim was very happy when his mom gave him a ticket.
When they got close to the ride, Tim saw a bad man. The bad man tried to take Tim's ticket. Tim was scared and held his ticket tight. His mom saw the bad man and told him to go away.
The bad man went away and Tim felt safe. Tim and his mom got on the big ride and had lots of fun. After the ride, they went home and Tim told his dad about the bad man. Tim's dad was proud of him for keeping his ticket safe.

One day, a boy named Tim was playing with his ball. He threw the ball up to the ceiling. He missed the ball and it fell down. Tim thought it was funny. He laughed and said, "Oops! I missed the ball!"
Tim's mom heard him and came into the room. She saw the ball on the floor. "Tim, be careful with the ball," she said. "You might break something." Tim said, "Okay, Mom. I will be careful."
Tim threw the ball up again. This time, he hit the ceiling. The ball made a loud noise. Tim looked up and saw that the ceiling was tough. It did not break. But then, something unexpected happened. The ceiling opened up, and a big, friendly bear came down. The bear looked at Tim and said, "Hello! You found my secret door! Let's play together!" Tim and the bear played with the ball and became good friends.

One day, a boy named Tim felt bad. His tummy hurt a lot. He told his mom, "Mom, my tummy feels yucky!" His mom said, "Oh no, let's go see the doctor."
At the doctor's place, Tim had to lie down on a big bed. The doctor looked at Tim's tummy and said, "Hmm, I think you ate something disgusting." Tim thought about the green, gooey thing he ate from the ground. He didn't tell the doctor.
The doctor gave Tim some yucky medicine to drink. Tim didn't want to drink it, but his mom said, "You must drink it to feel better." Tim drank the medicine, but it was very disgusting. He made a face and said, "Yuck!"
After a few days, Tim's tummy still hurt. The medicine didn't help. Tim's mom was sad, and Tim felt bad. They went back to the doctor. The doctor said, "I'm sorry, but I can't help you." Tim and his mom went home, and Tim still felt bad.

Susy and Tim were playing in the park. Tim had a shiny phone and Susy was curious. She asked him, "Can I see your phone?"
Tim happily said "Yes!" and handed it to Susy. She carefully held the phone and saw how wide the screen was. She was so happy!
She smiled and said "Can I cover it with this leaves?" She pointed to a pile of leaves nearby.
Tim said, "Sure, why not!"
Susy carefully put the leaves over the phone and started giggle. She then ran off to find more leaves to cover it with. Tim just laughed and said, "Look at Susy go."

Once upon a time, there was a wild box full of surprises! Little Jen and her mom were so excited to open it together. 
"Mom, what can be in the box?" Jen asked.
"I don't know," her mom replied. "Let's open it and find out!"
Jen opened the lid and inside the box she found a furry, wild animal. 
"Oh!" Jen exclaimed. "What is it?"
"It's a wild cat," her mom said. "Let's see if it will come to us." 
The wild cat slowly walked over to Jen and her mom. Jen petted the cat and said, "I'm so glad you came!" 
Jen and her mom laughed and hugged each other as they watched the wild cat come out of the box.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. He saw a big door in his yard that he had never seen before. The door was old and looked hurt. Tim wanted to know what was behind the door.
Tim called his friend, Sue. "Sue, come see the door!" he said. Sue came running and saw the door too. "Let's open it and reveal what's inside," Sue said. They both pushed the door open.
Behind the door, they found a beautiful garden with many colorful flowers. Tim and Sue played in the garden all day, and they were very happy. They promised to come back to the garden every day to play and take care of the hurt door.

Once upon a time, there was a smelly arm. The arm belonged to a girl named Jane and it was very smelly. One day, Jane decided to do something about it. 
So, she ordered a big box from the store. When she opened it up, there was a jar of special soap inside. Jane was excited! She opened the jar and it was a very nice smell.
Jane put the soap on her smelly arm and scrubbed it all over. Then, she washed it off in the sink. She smiled when she saw how clean her arm was! 
Jane was very happy that she had ordered the special soap. Now, her arm smelled better than ever.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to zip his coat up and down. One day, he found a thin tape on the floor. He thought it was fun to play with the tape.
Tim took the tape and started to zip it around his coat. He zipped it up and down, in and out. He was having so much fun! But then, the tape got stuck on his coat. Tim tried to pull it off, but it was too hard.
Tim's mom saw what happened and said, "Oh no, Tim! You should not play with things you find on the floor." Tim was sad because his coat was ruined. The moral of the story is to be careful with what you play with, and always ask for help if you are not sure.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to pretend and play with her toys. One day, she made a game with her toy animals. She said, "Let's play a dangerous game!"
In the game, the toy lion was the boss. He put the other animals in a cell because they did not listen to him. The lion said, "You must stay in the cell because it is dangerous outside."
But the toy elephant was very smart. He found a way to open the cell door. The elephant said to the other animals, "We can be free now! Let's go and have fun!"
The animals left the cell and played together. They were happy and safe. Lily watched them and smiled. She knew that even in a dangerous game, her toys could always find a way to be happy.

Once upon a time, in a small, quiet town, there lived a noisy dog named Barky. Barky loved to run and play all day long. One day, while Barky was playing, he saw a shiny star on the ground. Barky thought it was the most beautiful thing he had ever seen.
Barky picked up the star with his mouth and carried it around the town. As Barky passed by his friends, they all wanted to see the star too. They took turns holding the star and playing with it. They were all very happy.
Soon, it was time for Barky to go home. He wanted to keep the star all to himself, but he remembered how happy his friends were when they shared the star. Barky decided to leave the star in the park so that everyone could enjoy it. The moral of the story is that sharing with others can make everyone happy.

One day, a little cat named Mimi felt very dizzy. She walked around the room and saw lots of furniture. Mimi wanted to take a nap but did not know where to sleep.
Mimi asked her friend, a dog named Bobo, "Where should I nap?" Bobo said, "You can nap on the soft chair or the big bed." Mimi looked at the chair and the bed, but she wanted something different.
Mimi looked around and saw a warm, sunny spot on the floor. She thought, "This is a good place to nap!" So, Mimi laid down in the sunny spot and took a nice, long nap. When she woke up, she did not feel dizzy anymore. Mimi was happy she found the perfect place to nap.

Once upon a time, a little cat named Kitty loved to play. She had soft paws and liked to chase her tail. One sunny day, Kitty saw a tight ball of yarn. She thought it would be fun to play with it.
Kitty played with the tight ball of yarn all day. She rolled it here and there, and her paws got tangled in the yarn. The more she played, the tighter the yarn got around her paws.
Soon, Kitty's paws were stuck in the yarn. She could not play anymore. She tried to get her paws out, but the yarn was too tight. Kitty was sad and could not play anymore.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a microscope in his room. He did not know what it was, so he asked his mom. "Mom, what is this?" Tim said. His mom smiled and said, "That is a microscope, Tim. It helps you see very small things."
Tim wanted to use the microscope. He put a small leaf under it and looked. He saw many tiny things on the leaf. Tim was very proud. He called his mom and said, "Look, Mom! I can see small things with the microscope!"
His mom was happy and proud too. She said, "That's great, Tim! But remember to share the microscope with your friends, so they can see small things too." Tim learned that sharing with friends is good. He took the microscope to his friend's house and they all had fun seeing small things together. The moral of the story is to always share with others.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to run and slide and swing. They saw a big red ambulance parked near the gate. They were curious and enthusiastic. They wanted to see what was inside.
They ran to the ambulance and opened the door. They saw a lot of buttons and wires and machines. They did not know what they were for. They started to push the buttons and pull the wires. They thought it was fun.
But they did not know that they were making a big mistake. The ambulance was there for a reason. It was waiting for a sick person who needed help. The buttons and wires and machines were important. They helped the sick person breathe and stay alive.
The ambulance driver heard a loud noise. He saw that the lights and sirens were on. He ran to the ambulance and saw Lily and Ben. He was angry and scared. He shouted at them to get out. He tried to fix the damage. But it was too late.
The sick person did not get the help he needed. He died in the park. Lily and Ben felt very sad and sorry. They did not mean to hurt anyone. They learned a hard lesson. They should not play with things they do not understand. They should listen to grown-ups and follow the rules.

One day, a little girl named Sue went to play in the park. It was a peaceful, sunny day. She saw a big tree and lay down under it to rest. As she lay there, she saw a bird in the tree. The bird said, "Hello, Sue! Do you want to play a quiz game?"
Sue was surprised. She didn't know birds could talk! But she liked quiz games, so she said, "Yes, please!" The bird asked, "What is big and yellow, and shines in the sky?" Sue thought for a moment and said, "The sun!" The bird clapped its wings and said, "Good job!"
Then, something unexpected happened. The bird changed into a fairy! The fairy said, "Sue, you won the quiz! Now, I will give you a special gift. You can talk to animals for one day!" Sue was so happy. She spent the whole day talking and playing with her new animal friends.

In a small town, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a toy called Mr. Bear. Mr. Bear had brown skin. One day, Tim was worried. He could not find Mr. Bear.
Tim looked everywhere for Mr. Bear. He asked his mom, "Mom, have you seen Mr. Bear?" Mom said, "No, but I saw some brown skin in the yard. Maybe Mr. Bear is outside."
Tim ran to the yard. He saw a brown dog. The dog's skin did not match Mr. Bear's skin. Tim was sad. But then, he saw something under a big tree.
There, he found Mr. Bear! Tim was so happy. He hugged Mr. Bear and said, "I was so worried! I will never let you go again." From that day on, Tim and Mr. Bear were always together.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Ben. Ben loved to hop all day. One day, he hopped all the way to class. In class, there were many other little animals who liked to hop too.
In the class, Ben saw a delicate butterfly named Bella. Bella had pretty wings that were so soft. Ben wanted to be friends with Bella. So, he hopped over to her and said, "Hi, I am Ben. Do you want to hop with me?"
Bella smiled and said, "Yes, I would love to hop with you, Ben!" So, Ben and Bella hopped together in the class. They hopped high and low, and they laughed a lot. All the other animals in the class joined them too. They all had a great time hopping together.

Once upon a time, there was a mighty dog named Max. Max loved to watch films with his best friend, a little boy named Tim. They would sit on the couch and laugh and play while they watched their favorite films together.
One day, Tim and Max wanted to watch a new film. So, Tim's mom helped them order a new film from the store. They were very excited and could not wait for the film to arrive.
When the film came, they put it in the TV and started to watch. But, something unexpected happened! The film was not about a mighty hero like they thought. Instead, it was about a funny cat who made them laugh even more. Tim and Max loved the new film and watched it many times. They learned that sometimes surprises can be fun and make things even better.

Jack was walking in the park. It was a sunny day and he felt comfortable. Suddenly, he saw a stranger. The stranger was trying to fix their bike. Jack stopped and looked at the stranger.
The stranger smiled at Jack and asked if he wanted to help. Jack was a bit scared of the stranger, but he was also curious about the bike. He wanted to help fix the bike. 
So, together they worked on the bike. Jack was happy to help and was surprised at how quickly they fixed the bike. The stranger thanked Jack for his help. 
Jack felt proud as he watched the stranger ride away on the fixed bike. Now, he felt even more comfortable when walking in the park.

One day, a gifted dog named Spot wanted to help his owner, Jane. Spot could do many things that other dogs could not do. He was a very special dog.
Spot thought, "I want to succeed in making Jane happy." So, Spot went to find a ball to play with Jane. He looked and looked, and at last, he found a big red ball. Spot picked up the ball and went to Jane.
Spot said, "Look, Jane! I have a ball!" Jane smiled and said, "Good job, Spot! Let's play!" They played with the ball all day long. Spot was happy that he succeeded in making Jane happy. They were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved peanuts. She would eat them every day. Mia's mom would give her a small bag of peanuts to take to school.
One day, Mia saw a squirrel in her yard. The squirrel looked hungry. Mia thought the squirrel might want some peanuts too. So, she took a peanut from her bag and gave it to the squirrel. The squirrel was very happy and ate the peanut.
Mia and the squirrel became friends. They would play together every day after school. Mia would share her peanuts, and the squirrel would try to fit them in its tiny hands. They were both very happy and had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a furry cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play with her ball and chase the birds in the yard. She had a lot of fun every day.
One day, Kitty found some paint and decided to spread it on the floor. She made a big mess! The paint was all over the place, and it was not in order at all. Kitty's owner saw the mess and was not happy.
Kitty's owner told her, "Kitty, you need to clean up this mess and put everything in order." So, Kitty got a towel and began to clean up the paint. It took a long time, but she finally put everything back in order. Kitty learned that spreading paint was not a good idea, and she never did it again.

Once upon a time, there was a fat cat named Tom. Tom had a big waist and he liked to eat a lot. He lived in a small house with a nice lady. The nice lady always gave him food, and Tom loved to eat.
One day, Tom saw a little mouse named Jerry. Jerry was very fast and he liked to run around the house. Tom wanted to catch Jerry, but he was too fat. Tom said to Jerry, "I will catch you, little mouse!" But Jerry just laughed and said, "You can't catch me, fat cat. You don't understand how fast I am!"
Tom tried to catch Jerry, but he was too slow. He ran and ran, but Jerry was always faster. Tom got very tired and sad. He wanted to catch Jerry, but he couldn't. The nice lady saw Tom was sad and said, "You need to eat less and exercise more, Tom. Then you might catch Jerry." But Tom didn't listen. He just kept eating and eating.
In the end, Tom never caught Jerry. He got even fatter and couldn't move at all. The nice lady tried to help him, but Tom didn't understand that he needed to change. And so, Tom stayed fat and sad, and Jerry kept running around the house, always too fast for Tom to catch.

Once upon a time, there was a little caterpillar named Carl. Carl was eager to play with his friends every day. He loved to climb trees and eat leaves.
One sunny day, Carl's friends wanted to climb the tallest tree in the park. Carl was scared, but he did not want to miss the fun. So, he insisted on going with them. They all climbed up, up, up the tree.
When they reached the top, they saw a big surprise. There was a giant cake! Carl and his friends were so happy. They ate the cake and had a great time. They never expected to find a cake in a tree, but it was the best day ever.

Sara was a spoiled girl. She had many toys and clothes, but she never shared or said thank you. She did not like to clean her room or help her mom. She only wanted to play and watch TV.
One day, Sara's mom told her they were going to have a party for her birthday. Sara was very happy. She wanted a big cake, balloons, and presents. She told her mom to invite all her friends and buy her a new dress.
But Sara's mom had a plan. She said, "Sara, you can have a party, but first you have to clean your room. It is very messy and dirty. You have to put away your toys, make your bed, and throw away the trash. If you don't clean your room, you can't have a party."
Sara was very angry. She did not want to clean her room. She thought it was boring and hard. She said, "No, I won't clean my room. You can't make me. I want a party now!"
Sara's mom said, "Sara, you are being very rude and selfish. You have to learn to be responsible and respectful. If you don't clean your room, you will have no party, no cake, no balloons, and no presents. You will stay in your room all day and think about your behavior."
Sara did not believe her mom. She thought her mom was bluffing. She said, "Fine, I don't care. I don't need a party. I have enough toys and clothes. I will play and watch TV in my room. You are a mean mom."
Sara's mom said, "Okay, Sara, you have made your choice. I hope you will change your mind and clean your room. But if you don't, you will miss your party and your friends. I will go and prepare everything for the party. You have one hour to clean your room."
Sara's mom left the room and closed the door. Sara was alone in her messy and dirty room. She looked around and saw her toys, clothes, and trash. She felt a little sad and lonely. She wondered if her mom was really going to have a party without her. She wondered if her friends would come and have fun without her. She wondered if she was being a spoiled girl.
She thought for a while and then decided to clean her room. She said to herself, "Maybe mom is right. Maybe I should be more responsible and respectful. Maybe I should clean my room and say sorry to mom. Maybe I can still have a party and enjoy my birthday."
She got up and started to clean her room. She put away her toys, made her bed, and threw away the trash. She worked hard and fast. She wanted to finish before the hour was up. She wanted to show her mom that she could be a good girl.
She finished cleaning her room and looked around. She felt proud and happy. She saw her room was neat and tidy. She said to herself, "I did it! I cleaned my room! It looks so nice and cozy. I hope mom will be proud of me too. I hope I can still have a party and celebrate my birthday."
She opened the door and ran to find her mom. She saw her mom in the living room, with a big cake, balloons, and presents. She saw her friends waiting for her, with smiles and hugs. She saw her mom looking at her, with love and joy.
She ran to her mom and said, "Mom, I'm sorry. I was a spoiled girl. I cleaned my room. I want to be a good girl. I love you, mom."
Her mom hugged her and said, "Sara, I'm so proud of you. You did a great job. You are a good girl. I love you too, Sara. Happy birthday, Sara."
Sara smiled and said, "Thank you, mom. Thank you for the party. Thank you for the cake, balloons, and presents. Thank you for being my mom."
She joined her friends and had a wonderful party. She shared and said thank you. She had fun and learned a lesson. She was a happy girl.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful city. In the city, there was a big park with many trees and flowers. Lots of kids played there every day. They ran, laughed, and had fun. It was a happy place.
One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park. She saw a small flower on the ground. It was all alone. Lily wanted to help the flower grow big and strong like the other flowers. She knelt down and touched the flower gently.
Lily came back to the park every day to take care of the flower. She gave it water and sang to it. The flower grew bigger and more beautiful. Soon, other kids saw Lily and the flower. They started to help too. The park became even more beautiful and full of flowers. Everyone was happy and loved their city.

Once upon a time, there was a little flea. The flea lived in a big, heavy blanket. The blanket was on a bed in a small room.
One day, the flea met a tiny bug. The bug said, "I am cold. Can you help me?" The flea wanted to help, but the heavy blanket was too big for the flea to move. The flea tried to cover the bug with the blanket, but it was too hard.
The flea asked a big ant to help. The ant was very strong. The ant helped the flea move the heavy blanket. Together, they covered the bug with the blanket. The bug was warm and happy. The flea, the bug, and the ant became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his friends. One day, Tim saw his friends eating an amazing sandwich. He wanted to join them, but he didn't have a sandwich.
Tim went home and asked his mom to make him a sandwich. His mom made a big, amazing sandwich. Tim was very happy. He ran back to his friends with the sandwich.
When Tim joined his friends, he saw that one of his friends didn't have a sandwich. Tim decided to share his amazing sandwich with his friend. Everyone was happy and enjoyed their sandwiches together. The moral of the story is to always share with others.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. Tim was excited, but he was also a little scared. The tree was very tall, and its branches looked fragile. Tim thought, "What if the branches break when I climb?"
Tim's friend, Lily, was at the park too. She saw Tim looking at the tree. Lily said, "Hi, Tim! Do you want to climb that tree?" Tim said, "Yes, but the branches look weak. I am scared." Lily said, "We can climb together. It will be fun!"
Tim and Lily started to climb the tree. They were careful not to break the fragile branches. They climbed higher and higher. When they reached the top, they looked around. They could see the whole park! Tim and Lily smiled at each other. They were happy they climbed the tree together.

One day, a little boy named Tim was playing with his toy car. He was driving it up and down the couch. He was having lots of fun. His mom was in the room too, doing her work.
Then, something unexpected happened. Tim's toy car fell down and hit his mom's cup. The cup fell and water went all over her work. Tim felt guilty. He looked at his mom and started to cry. "I'm sorry, Mommy," he said.
His mom looked at him and smiled. "It's okay, Tim. It was just an accident," she said. She hugged him and they cleaned up the water together. Then, they went back to playing with the toy car, but this time, they played on the floor.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was ready to play a fun game with his mom. The game was called "Match the Shapes". Tim was very excited to play.
In the game, Tim had to match shapes in one minute. He saw a red circle, a blue square, and a yellow triangle. Tim was very fast at matching the shapes. His mom was so proud of him.
At the end, Tim matched all the shapes in just one minute! His mom gave him a big hug and a little prize. Tim was very happy and couldn't wait to play the game again.

One day, a cat and a dog were playing outside. The cat saw some food on the ground. It looked disgusting, but the cat was hungry. The dog wanted the food too. They both wanted to eat it, but they did not want to share. This made them very mad at each other.
The cat had an idea. He said, "Let's stretch our legs and run to the tree. Whoever gets there first can have the food." The dog agreed. They got ready to race.
They ran as fast as they could. But when they got to the tree, they saw a big bird eating the disgusting food. The cat and the dog were shocked. They did not get to eat the food, but they learned that sharing is better than fighting.

Tom is a lawyer. He likes to help people with their problems. He works in a big building with many rooms. Every day, he wears a suit and a tie.
One day, Tom has a day off. He decides to go to the park with his son, Ben. Ben is three years old. He likes to play with his ball and his truck. He is eager to have fun with his dad.
At the park, Tom and Ben find a grassy spot. They throw the ball to each other. Tom says, "Catch, Ben, catch!" Ben tries to catch the ball, but sometimes he misses. He laughs and runs after the ball. Tom smiles and runs with him.
Then, they see a dog. The dog is big and brown. It has a collar and a leash, but no owner. The dog sees the ball and runs towards it. It grabs the ball with its mouth and runs away.
"Hey, that's our ball!" Ben shouts. He chases the dog. Tom chases Ben. The dog is fast and clever. It dodges and weaves through the trees and the people.
"Stop, dog, stop!" Tom calls. He tries to catch the dog, but it is too quick. He worries that Ben might get lost or hurt.
Finally, the dog stops at a bench. On the bench, there is a lady. She is reading a book. She looks up and sees the dog, the ball, and the two chasers.
"Is this your dog?" Tom asks, panting.
"No, I've never seen it before. Is this your ball?" the lady asks, pointing.
"Yes, it's ours. The dog took it from us. We've been trying to catch it for a long time," Tom explains.
"I'm sorry. Here, let me help you," the lady says. She puts down her book and reaches for the ball. The dog lets go of the ball and wags its tail. The lady gives the ball to Ben.
"Thank you, lady," Ben says, smiling.
"You're welcome, little boy. What's your name?" the lady asks.
"I'm Ben. And this is my dad. He's a lawyer," Ben says, proudly.
"Really? That's interesting. I'm a lawyer, too," the lady says, surprised.
"Wow, what a coincidence!" Tom says, amazed.
They start to talk. They find out that they work in the same building, but on different floors. They have many things in common. They like the same books, the same movies, and the same food.
They decide to sit on the bench and chat some more. The dog sits next to them and licks their hands. Ben plays with the ball and the truck. He is happy. He thinks that the dog is friendly, the lady is nice, and his dad is cool. He hopes that they can be friends. He thinks that today is a good day.

One day, a big, tough dog named Max put on a nice suit. He wanted to look good for his friend's birthday party. Max walked to the party and saw all his friends there. They played games and ate yummy cake.
Max's friend, a little cat named Lucy, said, "Max, your suit is so nice! Will you stay and play with me?" Max smiled and said, "Yes, I will stay and play with you, Lucy."
As they played, something unexpected happened. Max's suit started to change colors! It turned red, then blue, then green. All the friends laughed and clapped. Max didn't know his suit could do that. He was happy to have a magic suit that made his friends smile.

One day, a girl named Sue saw a spider. The spider was amazing. It had eight legs and it could walk on walls. Sue liked to watch the spider. She would say, "Wow, spider, you are so good at walking on walls!"
One day, Sue saw a boy named Tim. Tim was scared of the spider. He said, "Sue, I do not like the spider. It is scary." Sue said, "No, Tim, the spider is not scary. It is amazing. Look at how it walks on walls."
Tim watched the spider with Sue. He saw how the spider walked on walls. He saw that it was not scary. Tim said, "You are right, Sue. The spider is amazing. I admire the spider now." Sue and Tim became friends and they both liked the spider.

Once upon a time, there was a naughty dinosaur. He liked to play tricks on his friends. One day, he found a big rock. He had a fun idea.
The dinosaur decided to cover the rock with leaves. He wanted his friends to trip on it. This would make him laugh. He hid and waited for his friends to come.
A little dinosaur friend walked by. He did not see the rock and tripped. He fell and got hurt. The naughty dinosaur felt bad. He went to say sorry, but his friend was very mad. They did not want to play with him anymore. The naughty dinosaur was sad and all alone.

Once upon a time, there was a big cat named Tom. Tom was a rich cat because he found a big box of food. He ate lots of food every day and did not like to exercise. Tom became very big and slow.
One day, Tom met a small cat named Tim. Tim was very fast and liked to exercise. Tim said, "Tom, you should exercise with me. It is fun and good!" Tom did not want to, but he agreed to try.
Tom and Tim went to the park to exercise. At first, Tom was tired and wanted to lie down. But as they played, Tom started to have fun. They ran, they jumped, and they climbed trees. Tom felt better and was happy. From that day on, Tom and Tim exercised together and became best friends.

One day, a little girl named Mia went to the park with her mom. Mia was very excited to play with her friends. She was a bit impatient, so she ran ahead of her mom.
At the park, Mia saw a big tree. She gazed up at the tree and saw a bird with a note in its beak. The bird dropped the note, and Mia picked it up. The note had a picture of a toy on it.
Mia showed the note to her mom and her friends. They all looked for the toy together. They found it under the slide. Mia and her friends were very happy and played with the toy all day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lisa. She was very patient and loved animals. Her mom and dad told her they had a big surprise for her. They said it was something she would love. Lisa could not wait to find out what it was.
One day, they took Lisa to a special place called a safari. There were lots of animals there, like lions, elephants, and monkeys. Lisa was so happy to see them all. She remembered her mom and dad's surprise and knew this was it. She was excited to celebrate her special day with the animals.
As they walked through the safari, Lisa saw a big tree with a sign that said "Party Time!" She knew her mom and dad had planned a big party for her. They had cake, balloons, and even a special dance with the animals. Lisa's patient waiting had paid off, and she had the best day ever at the safari.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch movies. One day, he saw a bad movie. He did not like it. Tim wanted to make a better movie. He had an idea.
Tim asked his friend, Sam, to help. They made a movie about a dog who could deliver things. The dog was very fast. The dog could bring things to people far away. Tim and Sam were very happy with their movie. They wanted to show it to their friends.
Tim and Sam invited their friends to watch the movie. They were excited. But when they played the movie, something unexpected happened. The dog in the movie started to deliver bad movies to people. Tim and Sam were surprised. Their friends laughed. The movie was funny. Tim and Sam learned that sometimes, a bad movie can be fun too.

Once upon a time, there was a big cat named Tom. Tom was very selfish. He never shared his toys with his friends. One day, Tom went to the park to play. He took his favorite ball with him.
In the park, Tom saw a new friend. His name was Sam. Sam was a judge. Tom wanted to play with Sam, but he did not want to share his ball. Sam knew that Tom was selfish, but he still wanted to meet him and be his friend.
Sam had a plan. He told Tom, "Let's play a game. We can share your ball and have fun together." Tom thought about it and agreed. They played and had a great time. Tom learned that sharing was fun and made new friends. From that day on, Tom was not selfish anymore. He shared his toys with all his friends and everyone was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a stubborn cat named Tom. Tom liked to play in the subway. He did not listen to his mom when she told him to stay away from the subway.
One day, Tom went to the subway to play. He saw many people and trains. Tom was having fun, but he did not know that he was disturbing others. The people were not happy with Tom.
Tom did not stop playing in the subway. He was very stubborn. One day, a train came and Tom was in the way. Sadly, Tom got hurt by the train. His mom was very sad. The people wished Tom had listened to his mom.

Once upon a time, there was a stubborn cat named Tom. Tom loved to play with his shadow. One sunny day, Tom went outside to play.
As Tom played, he saw his shadow on the ground. He decided to chase it. Tom ran after his shadow, but it was always just out of reach. Tom was not happy.
Tom tried and tried to catch his shadow. He was very stubborn. But no matter how hard he tried, he could not catch it. Finally, Tom decided to lay down and take a nap. When he woke up, his shadow was still there, waiting to play.

Once upon a time, there was a wealthy family who lived in a big, beautiful house. One sunny morning, the family were busy washing all the windows to let the sunshine light up the house. They were all very happy and excited as the sun shone brighter and brighter. 
Suddenly, there was a knock on the door. Mama opened the door and a friendly old man stood there. "Hello. I have come to share a secret with you," he said. Mama, who was curious, asked the old man to tell her the secret.
The old man replied, "If you wash each light in the house with a special soap, it will become very bright and powerful." Mama thanked him and the old man winked as he said goodbye. 
The family were not sure what the old man meant, but they did as he said. So every day, they carefully and lovingly washed each light. As a result, the house grew incredibly bright and sparkly. The wealth of the family grew and they were all very happy.

Lily liked to make bows with her mom. They used ribbons of many colors and shapes. They put the bows on clips and pins and bands. Lily had a lot of bows for her hair.
One day, Lily and her mom went to the park. They saw a table with a sign that said "Sell". There were toys and books and clothes on the table. A girl and her dad were selling them. Lily wanted to see what they had.
She saw a doll with a pretty dress and a bow in her hair. Lily loved the doll. She asked her mom if she could buy it. Her mom said they had to ask the girl how much it was.
The girl saw Lily and her mom. She said the doll was five dollars. Lily's mom said that was too much. She asked if the girl would take three dollars. The girl said no. She said the doll was her favorite and she only sold it because she needed money for a new bike.
Lily felt sad. She really wanted the doll. She looked at her own bows. She had an idea. She asked the girl if she would trade the doll for some of her bows. The girl looked at Lily's bows. She saw one that was pink and sparkly. She liked it a lot. She said she would trade the doll for that bow and two more.
Lily was happy. She gave the girl the pink bow and two other bows. She took the doll and hugged it. She thanked the girl and her dad. She and her mom left the park.
The girl was happy too. She had three new bows for her hair. She put the pink one on right away. She thought it was very pretty. She did not care about the doll anymore. She only wanted the money for the bike.
But the girl's dad was not happy. He saw what the girl did. He said she was selfish. He said she should not have traded the doll for the bows. He said the doll was worth more than the bows. He said she should have asked for more money or kept the doll.
The girl did not understand. She said she liked the bows better than the doll. She said Lily liked the doll better than the bows. She said they both got what they wanted. She said that was fair.
The girl's dad shook his head. He said the girl did not know the value of things. He said she had to learn to be more careful and smart. He said he would not buy her the bike until she sold more things. He said she had to earn it.
The girl felt sad. She wished her dad would be happy for her. She wished he would let her have the bike. She wished he would not be so selfish.

Once upon a time, there was a busy bee named Billy. Billy loved to fly around and visit his friends in the garden. Every day, he would buzz from flower to flower, making them happy.
One sunny day, while Billy was flying, he saw a little spark. The spark was coming from a tiny rock. Billy was curious and wanted to see what it was. He flew closer and saw that the rock had a little door. He knocked on the door and a tiny ant named Andy came out.
Andy told Billy that the spark was magic. If they worked together, the magic spark would yield a big surprise. So, Billy and Andy decided to help each other. They spent the whole day moving the rock to a special place in the garden. 
At the end of the day, the magic spark grew bigger and bigger. Suddenly, it turned into a beautiful rainbow bridge! The bridge connected all the flowers in the garden, making it easier for Billy to visit his friends. Billy and Andy were so happy that they had worked together. From that day on, they became the best of friends and had many more adventures in their garden home.

Once upon a time, there was a loyal dog named Spot. Spot loved to help his best friend, Tim. Tim had a task to do every day. He had to clean his room before they could celebrate and play.
One day, Tim said to Spot, "Let's do the task fast, so we can celebrate and play!" Spot barked and wagged his tail. Together, they picked up toys and put them away. They made the bed and put dirty clothes in a big bag.
When the task was done, Tim and Spot went outside. They played catch with a ball and ran in the grass. They laughed and had fun. The sun was shining, and it was a great day to celebrate. And that's what loyal friends do - they help each other and have fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a little baby named Tim. He lived in a small house with his mom and dad. Tim had a crib in his room. The crib was perfect for him to sleep in at night.
One day, Tim's mom gave him a toy to play with. It was a soft, round ball. Tim liked to throw the ball up in the air and catch it. But one time, he did not catch the ball. He let it drop on the floor. "Oh no!" Tim said.
Tim's mom heard him and came into the room. "What happened?" she asked. Tim looked at her and said, "I drop my ball." His mom smiled and picked up the ball. She gave it back to Tim and said, "Be careful next time." Tim was happy and played with his ball again, making sure not to drop it.

One day, a big rain came. It rained a lot. A flood arrived in the town. The water was everywhere. People were sad and scared.
A boy named Tim saw a rude duck. The duck said, "Quack! This is my water now. You can't play here!" Tim was sad. He wanted to play in the water too.
Tim went to his friend, a kind dog. The dog said, "Woof! Let's talk to the duck together." They went to the rude duck.
The dog said, "Woof! We can all play in the water. Be nice." The duck thought about it. Then, the duck said, "Quack! Okay, we can all play together."
The flood went away. Tim, the dog, and the duck played in the water. They were all happy and had fun. The rude duck learned to be nice.

One day, a happy bunny named Bob wanted to go outside and play. The sun was shining and the sky was blue. Bob felt excited and couldn't wait to have fun.
Bob ran out and started to skip in the grass. He saw his friend, a bird named Tim. Tim flew to Bob and landed on his arm. They laughed and played together all day.
As the sun started to set, Bob and Tim said goodbye. They were tired but very happy. Bob went back to his home and slept, dreaming of the fun day he had with his friend.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a man with a cart. The cart had many toys. The man was doing a trade with the people. They gave him old toys and he gave them new ones.
Tim had a dull toy car. He wanted to trade it for a new one. He said to the man, "Can I trade my dull car for a new one?" The man looked at the car and said, "Yes, you can trade it."
Tim was very happy. He traded his dull car for a new, shiny one. He played with it all day. His friends saw his new car and wanted to trade too. They all went to the man and traded their dull toys for new ones.

Once upon a time, there was a big house with a small attic. In the attic, there were two toys: a red ball and a blue car. They were friends, but sometimes they felt jealous of each other.
One day, the red ball wanted to play outside. The blue car wanted to play too, but he was too big to fit through the attic door. The red ball rolled down the stairs and set off to the yard. The blue car was jealous and sad because he couldn't play outside with his friend.
The blue car thought of a plan. He asked the red ball to help him. Together, they pushed and pushed until the blue car could fit through the door. They both set off to the yard to play. They were not jealous anymore, and they played happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy liked to go to the store with her mom. One day, they went to the store to buy some toys. Amy saw a big, pretty toy she wanted. She asked her mom, "Can I have this toy, please?" Her mom looked at the price tag and said, "No, Amy, this toy is too expensive."
Amy was sad, but she saw another toy. This toy looked dangerous. It had sharp points and looked like it could hurt someone. Amy asked her mom, "Can I have this toy?" Her mom said, "No, Amy, this toy is dangerous. It could hurt you or your friends."
Amy was sad, but she kept looking. She saw a small, safe toy that was not expensive. It was a cute, little doll. Amy asked her mom, "Can I have this toy?" Her mom looked at the price and saw that it was not dangerous. She said, "Yes, Amy, you can have this toy."
Amy was very happy with her new toy. She played with it all day and shared it with her friends. The moral of the story is to always choose things that are safe and not too expensive.

One day, a little boy named Tom found a tooth on the ground. He picked it up and showed it to his mom. "Look, Mom! I found a tooth!" he said. His mom smiled and told him, "That's great, Tom! It looks like a healthy tooth."
Tom wanted to keep the tooth, so he put it in his pocket. He went to play with his friends. They were playing a game where they had to break things. Tom thought it would be fun to break the tooth, so he took it out of his pocket.
Tom threw the tooth on the ground, but it didn't break. Instead, it started to grow bigger and bigger! Tom and his friends were surprised. The tooth became as big as a house! Tom's mom came outside and saw the huge tooth. "Wow, Tom! That's a very big, healthy tooth!" she said. Tom and his friends laughed and played in the giant tooth's shadow.

Once upon a time, there was a stubborn boy named Tim. Tim did not like to listen to anyone. One day, his mom asked him to help her cook. She said, "Tim, please stir the soup for me."
Tim did not want to help. He said, "No, I want to play!" But his mom told him a story. The story was about a little boy who stirred a magic soup. The soup made everyone happy and strong.
Tim liked the story. He wanted to make everyone happy and strong too. So he picked up the spoon and began to stir the soup. As he stirred, he thought about the story his mom told him. He felt happy and strong.
In the end, Tim learned that helping others can be fun. And from that day on, he was not so stubborn anymore.

One day, a green frog named Bob was hopping in the forest. He saw his friend, a smelly skunk named Sam. Sam was sad.
"Why are you sad?" Bob asked Sam. Sam tried to explain, "I am smelly and my friends don't want to play with me."
Just then, a big wind came and blew all the smelly away! Sam was not smelly anymore. "Wow!" said Bob. "The wind made you not smelly!"
Now Sam was happy. He and Bob played together all day. Their friends saw them playing and joined them too. And that's how Sam the skunk became friends with everyone again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch TV all day long. He would watch his favorite shows and not play with his toys. His mom said that he was a spoiled boy.
One day, Tim's mom decided to lock the TV. She wanted Tim to play outside and make friends. Tim was sad at first, but then he went outside to play.
Tim met a girl named Sue. They played with a ball and had lots of fun. Tim learned that playing outside was fun too. He was not a spoiled boy anymore. Tim and Sue became best friends, and they played together every day.

One day, a boy and a girl had a quarrel. The boy had a fan. The girl wanted the fan. The boy said, "No, it is mine!" The girl said, "But I want it too!" They were not happy.
The boy and the girl went to their mom. She was a normal mom. She said, "You must share the fan." The boy and the girl did not like this. They did not want to share.
They went back to their room. The boy and the girl pulled the fan. They pulled very hard. The fan broke. The boy and the girl were sad. They could not use the fan anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a poor girl named Lucy. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Lucy loved to play and laugh with her friends.
One day, Lucy's mom asked her to go to the store. "Lucy, can you bring some sugar home for me?" she asked. Lucy said, "Yes, mom!" and went to the store.
At the store, Lucy saw a small cat. The cat was sad and hungry. Lucy decided to help the cat. She kneeled down and gave the cat some sugar. The cat ate the sugar and smiled. "Thank you, Lucy!" said the cat. They became friends and went home together. Lucy's mom was happy to see the cat and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy made of metal that he loved to play with. One day, Tim was troubled because his toy was dirty.
Tim thought, "I will give my toy a bath to make it clean!" So, Tim filled a bowl with water and put the metal toy in it to soak. The toy was in the water for a little while, and then Tim took it out.
The metal toy was all clean and shiny again. Tim was so happy to see his toy looking like new. He played with his toy all day long and promised to keep it clean. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue had a favorite hat. She wore it every day. Her hat was red and blue. She loved her hat very much.
One day, Sue saw a big cake. She wanted to taste it. But the cake was on a high shelf. It was difficult for her to reach. Sue tried and tried, but she could not get the cake. She was sad.
Sue's friend, Tom, saw her. He wanted to help. Tom was taller than Sue. He could reach the cake. Tom got the cake for Sue. They both tasted the cake. It was yummy. They were happy.
Sue learned that sometimes, things can be difficult. But when we help each other, we can do anything. And that is what friends are for.

Once upon a time, in a far away land to the east, there was a wise old frog named Fred. Fred was very wise and knew many things. He lived in a small pond with his best friend, a fish named Finn.
One sunny day, Fred and Finn wanted to play a game. Fred said, "Let's see who can soak the other one first!" They both got ready and splashed water at each other. They were having so much fun.
Fred had a big smile on his face as he thought of a plan. He told Finn, "I will jump high and when I come down, I will make a big splash!" Finn was excited to see this. Fred jumped high, and as he came down, he made a huge splash. The water soaked Finn completely! Finn laughed and said, "You are very wise, Fred!" They both continued to play and have fun in the pond.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to play outside with her friends. One day, she saw her mom doing laundry. She wanted to help but was afraid she would do it wrong. So, she just shrugged and went to play.
While playing, Lucy saw her friend Timmy looking sad. Timmy was ashamed because he had lost his toy. Lucy wanted to help him find it. They looked and looked, and finally, they found the toy under a big tree. Timmy was so happy, and Lucy felt good for helping him.
That night, Lucy told her mom about helping Timmy. Her mom was proud of her. Lucy then asked if she could help with the laundry. Her mom said yes, and they did it together. Lucy learned that it's good to help others and not be afraid to try new things.

One day, a little girl named Sally went to the ice cream shop with her mom. Sally was nervous because she did not know what flavor to pick. She looked at all the colors and felt a little scared.
Sally's mom saw her worry and said, "Sally, it's okay to be nervous. Just take a deep breath and pick the one you like best." Sally sighed and looked at the flavors again. She picked a pink cone and smiled.
Sally learned that it is okay to be nervous when trying new things. She was happy with her pink ice cream cone and shared it with her mom. They both enjoyed it and had a great day together. The moral of the story is that it's okay to be nervous, but trying new things can be fun and exciting.

Ben and Lily were playing in the park with their dog, Spot. They liked to run, slide, swing and throw the ball for Spot. But then the sky became dark and loud. It was a storm. Ben and Lily were scared. They wanted to go home.
"Come on, Spot, we have to go!" Ben said. He took Lily's hand and ran to the gate. But the gate was locked. They could not get out. They looked around for a place to hide from the storm.
They saw a big box near the trash cans. It was a shelter. They ran to the box and crawled inside. Spot followed them. The box was dirty and smelly, but it was better than the rain and the thunder.
"Are you okay, Lily?" Ben asked.
"I'm okay, Ben. But I don't like this shelter. It's terrible," Lily said.
"Me neither. But we have to stay here until the storm is over. Maybe Mom and Dad will come and find us," Ben said.
He hugged Lily and Spot. They tried to be brave. They hoped the storm would end soon. They missed their home.

Once upon a time, there was a messy room. Toys were everywhere. In this room lived a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play and throw his toys. One day, Tim found a big scale in the corner of his room. The scale was used to weigh things.
Tim thought it would be fun to throw his toys on the scale. He picked up his teddy bear and threw it on the scale. The teddy bear bounced off and landed on the floor. Then, he threw his toy truck on the scale. This time, the scale tipped over and fell on the floor too. Tim laughed and thought it was funny.
But then, Tim's mom came into the room. She saw the messy room and the broken scale. She was not happy. Tim knew he was in trouble. He had been told many times to not throw his toys. Now, the scale was broken, and Tim was sad. The moral of the story is to listen to your parents and not to throw things in the house.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sara and a nice policeman named Jim. One day, Sara wanted to go see her best friend who lives far away. She asked her mom if she could drive there.
Her mom said, "No, you must be very polite and ask Jim the policeman to drive you there." 
Sara went to Jim and said please, "Can you drive me to my best friend?"
Jim smiled and said, "Of course I can! You are being very polite and I'm happy to help you." 
Jim put Sara in his car, and with big smiles, they drove to Sara's best friend. 
When the arrived, Sara waved to Jim and said, "Thank you for the nice drive!" Jim smiled and said, "You're welcome, my little friend."
Sara told her best friend the story, and they both learned the lesson from it. Be polite, and use your hand when you ask for help - you might get what you wanted.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a little girl named Lily. She was a troubled girl because she had no friends to play with. One day, she decided to go to the park to find some friends.
At the park, she saw a boy named Tim. He was playing with a ball. Lily walked up to Tim and asked, "Can I play with you?" Tim smiled and said, "Yes, let's play together!" They played all day and became good friends.
After playing, they were very thirsty. So, they went to Lily's house to get a drink. Lily's mom made them some hot cocoa. But there was only one big cup of cocoa. Lily's mom said, "You can split the cocoa and share it." So, they shared the cocoa and had a great time. Now, Lily was not troubled anymore because she had a new friend to play with.

Once upon a time, there were two friends named Ben and Daisy. Daisy wanted to play a game with Ben so she suggested they play with the giant plastic box. 
Ben thought that was a great idea and he rushed over to the box and opened it up. Inside was a lovely pink toy elephant. They both smiled and hugged each other.
Daisy said, "Let's play a game! We can split the elephant into two pieces."
Ben thought this was a great idea and he carefully split the plastic elephant into two pieces. Both Ben and Daisy squealed with delight and hugged the two halves of the elephant. 
They played with the elephant for hours. Ben and Daisy had a lovely time together, playing and laughing with the elephant. When it was time to go, they said goodbye and both hugged the plastic elephant goodbye. 
And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there were two brothers named Jack and Simon. They were getting ready for their holiday.
Jack said, "Let's go pack our things so we can go on holiday!" Simon said, "OK!"
They both went to their bedroom and started to pack. Jack said, "I'm almost done packing! Let's go!" 
But then Simon said, "Wait! I'm not done yet!" Jack said, "We can't wait, the sun is hot and we have to go now!"
Simon was very upset and wanted to take more things with them on their holiday. They argued for a few minutes and finally Jack said, "Ok, you can bring one more thing, but that's it!"
The two brothers packed the last of their things and went to the airport. When they arrived, they were met with a big welcome sign and lots of happy faces.
They smiled and waved as they went to their plane. Soon they were in the air, heading for their holiday adventure!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big, open folder with pretty pictures inside. One sunny day, Lily went to the park with her mom and dad. She took her folder with her to show her friends the pretty pictures.
While playing, Lily left her folder on a bench. A little squirrel saw the folder and thought it was a fun toy. The squirrel took the folder and ran up a tree. Lily saw the squirrel and said, "Oh no, my folder!" She told her mom and dad what happened.
Lily, her mom, and dad tried to get the folder back, but the squirrel was too fast. They had to hurry before the squirrel took it away. Then, something unexpected happened. The squirrel opened the folder and saw the pretty pictures. It liked them so much that it brought the folder back to Lily. Lily was so happy, and she even let the squirrel keep one pretty picture as a thank you gift. From that day on, Lily and the squirrel became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a furry cat named Tom. Tom had a red cap that he loved to wear. One day, he went for a walk in the park. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing. Tom was very happy.
As Tom walked, he met a big dog named Max. Max looked at Tom's red cap and said, "I like your cap. Can I try it on?" Tom said, "Sure, you can try it on." Max put on the red cap, but it was too small for him. The cap fell off and rolled away.
Tom and Max ran to get the cap, but a small bird picked it up and flew away. Tom and Max were sad. Then, they had an idea. They worked together to gain the bird's trust. Tom sang a song, and Max danced. The bird liked the song and dance, so it gave the cap back to Tom. Tom and Max were happy and became best friends.

The sun was shining warmly. Danny and his mom were walking in the park, when they spotted a tiny bug. Danny loved bugs and wanted to catch it. He was persistent, and ran after it. 
"Don't catch it!" His mom said, but Danny was too fast. He scooped it up and cupped it in his hands. 
"What are you going to do with it?" Mom asked. Danny thought for a moment, then blew a kiss onto his hands. 
The bug flew away, and Danny was sad. He was sure he had hurt the little bug. 
"You didn't hurt it," Mom said. "It was scared, so it flew away. You can show kindness to something by being gentle and blowing a kiss. Be persistent in showing love and kindness, and things will work out." 
Danny smiled, understanding what Mom had said. From that moment on, he was persistent in showing kindness and love to everyone and everything he encountered.

Once upon a time, in a big house, there was a rich man. He had a big smile and loved to laugh. He had many toys and games that he liked to play with.
One day, the rich man wanted to go to the park. He put on his hat and went outside. He saw a long line of people waiting to play on the swings. He wanted to play too, so he joined the wait.
While he was waiting, the rich man started to laugh. His laugh was so funny that it made everyone else laugh too. They all laughed together and had a great time. The wait didn't seem long anymore, and they all became good friends. And they all played happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there lived a little girl named Jane. Every day, Jane would go with her parents to the theater. She loved the theater! She would listen to the wonderful music, laugh and clap along with the actors on stage.
One day, Jane woke up to find that it was raining outside. The sky was a very dark and gloomy grey. No one was in the mood for the theater, so Jane was disappointed.
So, instead of going to the theater, Jane's parents decided to spoil her. They bought her a big chocolate bar and played a game at home. Jane was so happy and it helped brighten up their rainy day. 
They all laughed and played until the rain stopped and the gloomy sky started to clear up. They all were glad that even though it was raining outside, they made the most of their time together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a bike with pedals. He loved to ride his bike every day. One day, it rained and Tim felt miserable. He couldn't ride his bike outside.
Tim had an idea. He could still have fun inside. He took a piece of paper and a crayon. Tim began to rub the crayon on the paper. He drew a big sun and a happy face. Tim showed his mom the drawing. She smiled and hugged him.
The next day, the sun came out. Tim was happy as he rode his bike again. He learned that even when days are miserable, he could still find a way to have fun. The moral of the story is that you can always find something to make you happy, even on a bad day.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a boy named Tim. Tim was a little troubled because he wanted to find a good idea to make his mom happy.
One day, Tim asked his friend, Sam, "Do you have any idea to make my mom happy?" Sam thought for a moment and said, "Why not make her a nice card? Moms value cards made with love."
Tim liked the idea and made a beautiful card for his mom. He drew a big heart and wrote, "I love you, Mom!" inside. When he gave it to his mom, she smiled and hugged him tight.
From that day on, Tim knew that even a small idea could make someone very happy. He learned to value the simple things in life and always tried to find new ways to make his mom and friends smile. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a playful cat named Tom. Tom loved to play with his ball all day long. He would jump and run in the yard, making everyone laugh.
One day, Tom saw a big jar of salt in the kitchen. He wanted to take it and play with it. So, Tom jumped up and knocked the jar down. The salt spilled all over the floor.
