One night, Max saw a big, shiny star in the sky. He thought, "I want to touch that star!" Max decided to jog as hard as he could to reach the star. So, Max started to jog very fast. He jogged and jogged, trying to get closer to the star.
But Max could not reach the star. He jogged so hard that he got very tired. Max was sad that he could not touch the star. He lay down in the grass, tired and sad. Max learned that no matter how hard he jogged, he could not touch the stars in the sky.

Once upon a time, there was a farm. On the farm, there was a turkey. The turkey was very smooth. It had smooth feathers and a smooth beak. The turkey liked to walk around the farm and eat seeds. The seeds helped the turkey grow big and strong.
One day, the turkey saw a little girl. The little girl was sad. She looked at the turkey and said, "Hi, turkey. I want to grow like you. How do you grow so big and strong?" The turkey thought for a moment and then said, "I eat seeds. They help me grow."
The little girl smiled and said, "Thank you, turkey. I will eat seeds too." So, the little girl and the turkey ate seeds together. They had fun and laughed. The little girl felt happy again. She knew that eating seeds would help her grow big and strong, just like the smooth turkey.

Once upon a time in a big tree, there was a normal owl. He had big eyes and soft feathers. He liked to fly at night and look for food. The normal owl had a lot of friends who lived in the tree too.
One day, the normal owl found a small toy. The toy was soft and fun to play with. The normal owl liked to squeeze the toy with his wings. He would squeeze the toy and then let it go. It made him happy and he liked to share the toy with his friends.
One by one, the normal owl's friends tried to squeeze the toy too. They all liked the toy and took turns squeezing it. They played with the toy all day and had a lot of fun. The normal owl was happy that he found the toy and could share it with his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue had a toy violin. She played it every day. Sue loved her violin very much.
One day, Sue went to school. She took her violin with her. At school, Sue met a boy named Tom. Tom had a toy too. It was a number toy. Sue liked Tom's toy.
Sue felt jealous of Tom's number toy. She wanted to play with it. Tom saw Sue's violin and felt jealous too. They both decided to share their toys. Sue played with the number toy, and Tom played the violin. They had a lot of fun together. From that day on, Sue and Tom became best friends. They learned to share and not be jealous anymore.

Once upon a time, in a quiet forest, there lived a raven. The raven had many friends. One day, the raven found a big red ball.
The raven wanted to give the ball to a friend. First, he went to the little frog by the pond. "Do you want the ball?" asked the raven. The frog said, "No, thank you. I like to jump and swim, not play with balls."
Next, the raven went to the tall tree where the squirrel lived. "Do you want the ball?" asked the raven. The squirrel said, "No, thank you. I like to climb trees and eat nuts, not play with balls."
Finally, the raven went to the small mouse in the grass. "Do you want the ball?" asked the raven. The mouse said, "Yes, thank you! I love to play with balls!" The raven was happy to give the ball to the mouse.
The raven, the mouse, and their friends played together in the quiet forest. They were all very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. She was three years old and loved to explore her backyard. One day, as she ran around, she saw a flower. She stopped and wondered what it was and if she could pick it. She stepped closer and asked her Mum, "Mum, what is this little flower?"
Mum smiled and replied, "That's a daisy. Would you like to pick it?" Jane nodded and reached out to pick the flower. She twirled around with the daisy in her hand, spinning and twirling with joy.
The next day, Jane was back in the backyard. This time she saw a different kind of flower and stopped to wonder what it was. She also noticed that this flower was even littler than the daisy from yesterday. Jane couldn't wait to pick it and show her Mum. She smiled and softly said, "Look Mum, a little flower!"

Once upon a time, there was a planet filled with smelly creatures. 
One of them was Bow, who was very small but brave.
One day, Bow decided that she wanted to explore the planet.
So, she took a deep breath and started to run around the planet.
Just as she was running, she heard a loud voice.
"Bow, where are you going?"
Bow looked around to see who was talking. 
She saw a huge creature standing in front of her.
"I am just exploring the planet," Bow replied, bowing her head. 
"Ah, I see," said the creature. "Well, be careful!"
Bow thanked the creature and continued running around the planet. 
She explored the planet until it was dark. 
Then, she bowed her head in satisfaction and started to go back home. 
Bow had had a wonderful adventure!

Once upon a time, there was a little fairy. She was very intelligent. She loved to fly around and play with her friends in the forest.
One day, the fairy and her friends decided to joke with each other. They took turns making funny faces and telling silly stories. They laughed and laughed until their bellies hurt.
In the end, the intelligent fairy and her friends were happy. They learned that joking and laughing together made their days brighter. They promised to always be there for each other and to keep sharing their laughter.

Once upon a time, there was a creative little girl named Sue. Sue loved to make things with her hands. One day, she found a hammer in her dad's toolbox. She thought it would be fun to use the hammer to make something new.
Sue took the hammer and some wood. She started to hit the wood with the hammer. Bang, bang, bang! Sue was having so much fun. She made a little house for her toys to live in.
As Sue was playing, she did not see her toy car on the floor. She took a step and stumbled on the toy car. Sue fell down, but she was okay. She looked at the mess and decided to clean up. Sue put her toys in the little house she made. She was very happy with her creative work.

One day, a man and his wife lived in a big, modern house. They loved to hug each other every day. They were very happy together.
One sunny day, the wife heard a knock on the door. She opened it and saw a big bear outside. The bear looked sad. The wife asked, "Why are you sad, bear?" The bear said, "I am sad because I have no one to hug."
The wife felt bad for the bear. She had an idea. She called her husband and said, "Let's hug the bear and make him happy!" The husband agreed. They both hugged the bear, and the bear felt happy.
The bear was so happy that he started to glow. Suddenly, the bear turned into a beautiful fairy. The fairy said, "Thank you for your kindness. I was a fairy trapped in a bear's body, and your hug broke the spell. As a thank you, I will make your house even more modern and beautiful!" The man and wife were very surprised and happy. They thanked the fairy, and they all hugged together.

One day, a boy named Tim found a toy missile in his room. He was very happy. Tim wanted to fill the missile with air, so it could fly high in the sky. He was impatient to see it fly.
Tim went to his dad and said, "Dad, can you help me fill the missile with air?" His dad smiled and said, "Yes, let's do it together."
They went outside and filled the missile with air. Tim and his dad counted, "One, two, three!" and let the missile go. The missile flew high in the sky. Tim was so happy and clapped his hands. They played with the missile all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a kind, elderly lady. She lived in a small, quiet house with a pretty garden. She loved to sit outside and watch the birds come and go.
One sunny day, she saw a little bird come to her garden. The bird was sad because it had lost its family. The elderly lady wanted to help the bird, so she gave it some food and water.
As the days went by, the bird became happy and strong. It loved to sing and play in the quiet garden with the elderly lady. They became the best of friends, and the bird never felt lonely again.

Once upon a time, in a big landscape with many trees and flowers, there lived a harmless little squirrel. The squirrel loved to play and run around all day long. One day, the squirrel met a big, friendly bear.
"Hello, little squirrel," said the bear. "Do you want to play with me?"
"Yes, I would love to!" said the squirrel, and they played together all day.
As they played, the squirrel found a big tree with many yummy nuts. The squirrel wanted to share the nuts with the bear, but the tree was too tall for the bear to reach. So, the squirrel climbed the tree and started to yield the nuts for the bear. The bear was very happy and thanked the squirrel.
From that day on, the squirrel and the bear became best friends. They played in the beautiful landscape every day and shared many more adventures together.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy duck. He lived by a big pond. The duck did not like to play with other ducks. He liked to be alone.
One day, the grumpy duck found a big red apple. He wanted to taste it. He took a big bite of the apple. It was very good. The duck was happy.
Soon, other ducks saw the grumpy duck with the apple. They wanted to taste it too. The grumpy duck shared his apple with them. They all tasted the apple and liked it. The grumpy duck was not grumpy anymore. He made new friends and they all played together by the pond.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful theater. It was a very big place where people went to see fun shows. Many kids loved to go there with their moms and dads.
One day, a girl named Sally went to the theater with her family. They were going to watch a show about a big cake. Sally was very excited because she loved cake.
During the show, Sally saw people on the stage. They were stirring a big bowl to make the cake. The cake grew bigger and bigger. Everyone laughed and clapped. Sally and her family had a great time at the beautiful theater.

Once upon a time, there was a little brown bunny named Bob. Bob loved to play in the sun. One day, he found a big green hedge near his home. The hedge was very tall and had many leaves.
Bob thought it would be fun to count the leaves on the hedge. He started at one and went on and on. As he counted, he hopped around the hedge. He saw red birds and yellow bugs as he counted.
When he got to the end, Bob counted one hundred leaves! He was so happy to finish counting. He hopped back to his home, excited to tell his friends about the big green hedge and all the leaves he counted.

Once upon a time, there was a funny tomato named Tom. Tom loved to roll all day long. He would roll up the hill and then roll down the hill. All the other tomatoes would watch and giggle.
One day, Tom met a little girl named Lily. She saw Tom rolling and laughed. "Hi, funny tomato!" she said. "I like how you roll. Can I roll with you?" Tom was very happy to have a new friend to roll with.
So, Tom and Lily rolled up and down the hill together. They rolled fast and slow. They rolled in the sun and under the clouds. And they laughed and had so much fun. Tom and Lily became the best of friends, and they rolled happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Lily found a big white box. She did not know what was inside. She asked her mom, "Mom, can I open the box?" Her mom said, "Yes, you can open it."
Lily opened the box and saw a white cat inside. The cat lay very still. Lily was very happy and said, "Hello, cat! I will be your friend." But the cat did not move or say anything. Lily was sad and thought the cat did not like her.
Then, Lily's mom came in and saw the cat. She said, "Oh, Lily, that is not a real cat. It is a toy cat!" Lily was surprised and laughed. She picked up the toy cat and hugged it. Now, she knew that the cat was not real, but she still loved it. And they played together all day.

One day, a cricket named Jack was feeling very lively. He wanted to stretch his legs and explore the world. He went for a walk and saw lots of things. But Jack was too small to go far.
He soon found a big field where he could really stretch his legs. He jumped and hopped around, working all his muscles. He was feeling very excited and happy.
Then, Jack heard something. It was a lively sound of chirping. He looked around and saw lots of other crickets just like him. He went over to the group and joined in their singing. He stayed with them until the sun went down and then he started the journey back home.
Jack felt really good after his stretch and he vowed to go out more often. Whenever he went out, he would always be lively, like the crickets he'd heard.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big tree with a strange symbol on it. The symbol was incredible. It was a happy face with a big smile. Tim wanted to know what the symbol meant.
Tim asked his friend, Amy, to help him find out what the symbol meant. They asked the other kids at the park, but no one knew. Tim and Amy felt sad. They wanted to solve the mystery of the incredible symbol.
Then, a kind old man saw them and asked what was wrong. Tim and Amy told him about the symbol. The old man smiled and said, "That symbol means that this park is a happy place for everyone to play and have fun." Tim and Amy felt happy. They had solved the mystery and helped make the park a happier place for all the kids.

Once upon a time, there was a big hill. On top of the hill, there was a little house. In the house, there was a nosy cat named Tom. Tom liked to watch everything that happened outside.
One day, Tom saw a little boy named Tim playing near the hill. Tim was trying to roll a ball up the hill, but it was too hard. Tom wanted to help Tim, so he went outside and said, "Let me help you, Tim!"
Together, Tom and Tim pushed the ball up the hill. When they reached the top, they let the ball roll down the other side. They laughed and played all day long. From that day on, Tom and Tim were the best of friends. They always had fun on the big hill.

Lily and Ben are playing knights and dragons in the garden. Lily has a pink shield and a wooden sword. Ben has a green shield and a plastic horn.
"Roar! I am a big dragon and I will eat you!" Ben says, running towards Lily.
"No, you will not! I am a brave knight and I will stop you!" Lily says, holding her shield and sword.
They pretend to fight, but they are careful not to hurt each other. They laugh and have fun.
Suddenly, they hear a loud noise. It is their dog, Rex, who has jumped over the fence and is barking at them.
"Rex, no! You are not a dragon, you are a dog!" Lily says, dropping her shield and sword and running to hug Rex.
"Rex, bad! You scared us!" Ben says, dropping his shield and horn and joining Lily.
Rex licks their faces and wags his tail. He does not know he did anything wrong. He just wanted to play with them.
Lily and Ben forgive Rex and pet him. They decide to let him join their game.
"OK, Rex, you can be a dragon, but a nice dragon, OK?" Lily says, picking up her pink shield.
"OK, and we will be your friends, not your food, OK?" Ben says, picking up his green shield.
"OK, OK, OK!" Rex says, or that is what they think he says.
They resume their game, but this time they are all on the same team. They pretend to explore the garden, looking for treasure and adventure. They are happy and they love each other. They know they have the best shields in the world.

One day, a boy named Tim saw a big open box. In the box, he found a suit. It was a very nice suit. Tim wanted to wear the suit to his friend's party. So, he put on the suit and looked in the mirror. He was very happy with how he looked.
Tim went to his friend's house for the party. When he got there, he knocked on the door. His friend, Sam, opened the door and said, "Wow! You look great in that suit! Come in!" Tim went inside and they played games and ate cake.
After a while, Tim started to feel hot in his suit. He wanted to take it off, but he didn't know how. He asked Sam for help, but Sam didn't know how to take off the suit either. They tried and tried, but the suit just wouldn't come off.
Tim started to cry. He was very sad and very hot. Sam didn't know what to do. The party was not fun anymore. Tim's mom came to pick him up and she saw him crying. She tried to help him take off the suit, but she couldn't either. They went home, and Tim was still stuck in the suit. It was a bad ending for Tim's day.

Once upon a time, there was a farmer. He had a big red barn. The farmer had many animals. He had cows, pigs, and chickens. They all lived on the farm.
One day, a big dangerous wolf came to the farm. The wolf wanted to eat the animals. The animals were scared. They did not want the wolf to eat them.
The farmer saw the wolf and said, "No, you cannot eat my animals!" The farmer chased the dangerous wolf away. The animals were safe and happy. The farmer took care of them, and they all lived happily on the farm.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a little boy named Tim. Tim was a happy boy, but today he felt miserable. He wanted to find something to make him feel better.
Tim went for a walk and saw a big tree with a pear hanging from it. The pear was so high up that he could not reach it. Tim marched up to the tree and tried to jump, but he still could not get the pear. He felt even more miserable.
Just then, a tall man walked by and saw Tim looking sad. The man asked, "Why are you so sad, little boy?" Tim replied, "I want that pear, but I can't reach it." The tall man smiled and picked the pear for Tim. Tim ate the pear and felt happy again. From that day on, whenever Tim felt miserable, he would remember the kind man and the pear, and it would make him smile.

Once upon a time, there was a good goose. The goose lived near a big pond. The goose liked to play with the other animals. They would run and jump all day long.
One day, the good goose got very dirty. His feathers were not white anymore. The good goose wanted to be clean again. So, he went to the pond to bathe. He splashed the water with his wings and kicked with his feet.
After the good goose finished his bath, he was clean and white again. His friends were happy to see him clean. They all played together by the pond. The good goose was very happy. And they all had a fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a big gorilla named Bobo. He lived in a big forest with many trees. Bobo was very nice and liked to talk to his friends.
One day, Bobo met a small bird named Chirpy. Chirpy told Bobo, "I lost my mom. Can you help me find her?" Bobo said, "Yes, I will help you." So, they talked to many animals in the forest to find Chirpy's mom.
At last, they found a big tree with a nest. Chirpy's mom was there! Chirpy was very happy and said, "Thank you, Bobo!" Bobo was happy too because he helped his new friend. From that day, Bobo and Chirpy were best friends and loved to talk and play together.

Once upon a time, there was a big stage in the middle of a park. A little cat named Kitty loved to play on the stage. One day, it rained a lot. The stage got wet and the ground became muddy. Kitty wanted to play on the stage, but it was too wet.
Kitty saw her friend, a dog named Buddy. Buddy was smelly because he played in the mud. Kitty said, "Buddy, you are smelly!" Buddy laughed and said, "Yes, I played in the mud. It was fun!"
Kitty and Buddy decided to play on the wet stage together. They jumped and danced in the rain. They got all soak and smelly, but they had a lot of fun. After playing, they went home to get clean and dry. They couldn't wait to play on the stage again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big hat that he loved very much. One day, he went for a walk in the park with his hat on his head. He saw a girl named Mia, and they became friends.
"Wow, your hat is incredible!" said Mia. Tim felt very happy and said, "Thank you! I hope you like it too." They played together and had lots of fun. Suddenly, a big wind blew Tim's hat away. Tim and Mia were very sad.
They looked for the hat everywhere but could not find it. Then, they saw a little bird wearing the hat on its head. The bird looked very happy. Tim and Mia thought about it and decided to let the bird keep the hat. They learned that sharing can make others happy too. And from that day on, Tim and Mia became best friends who always shared their things with each other.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She was an ordinary girl who loved to play, eat cereal, and hug her teddy bear. Mia was very happy.
One day, Mia was eating her cereal when her teddy bear said, "Mia, can I have a hug?" Mia looked at her teddy bear and said, "Of course, Teddy! I love to hug you!"
So Mia hugged her teddy bear very tight. They both felt very happy. From that day on, Mia knew that even ordinary things like eating cereal and hugging her teddy bear could make her feel very special.

Tim and Mia are playing with their toys in the living room. Tim has a laser that makes red lights and sounds. Mia has a doll that can talk and sing. Tim likes to poke Mia's doll with his laser and make it stop talking. Mia does not like that.
"Stop it, Tim!" Mia says. "You are hurting my doll!"
"No, I'm not!" Tim says. "It's just a laser. It's fun!"
Mia grabs her doll and hugs it. She sees that the laser has made a filthy mark on its dress. She is very angry.
"That's not fun, Tim! That's mean! You made my doll dirty!"
She pokes Tim with her finger and makes him drop his laser. The laser falls on the floor and breaks. Tim is very sad.
"Look what you did, Mia! You broke my laser!"
He starts to cry. Mia feels sorry for him.
"I'm sorry, Tim. I didn't mean to break your laser. I just wanted you to stop poking my doll."
She picks up the laser and tries to fix it. She can't. She gives it back to Tim.
"Maybe we can ask mom to buy you a new laser. Or we can share my doll. She can talk and sing again. Do you want to play with her?"
Tim wipes his tears and nods. He takes the doll and presses a button. The doll says, "Hello, I'm Lily. I love you!"
Tim and Mia smile and hug each other. They play with the doll and forget about the laser. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue liked to help her mom in the kitchen. One day, Sue put on her apron and said, "Mom, I want to try to make cookies!"
Sue and her mom got all the things they needed to make the cookies. They mixed the dough and rolled it out. Sue used a cookie cutter to make fun shapes like stars and hearts. Then, they put the cookies in the oven to bake.
When the cookies were done, Sue and her mom took them out of the oven. They smelled so yummy! Sue and her mom put icing on the cookies and shared them with the family. Everyone said, "Sue, you did a great job! These cookies are very yummy!" Sue was so happy that she tried to help her mom and make cookies.

Once upon a time, there was an attractive cat named Fluffy. Fluffy was very pretty and liked to play. One day, Fluffy saw a big tree and wanted to climb it.
As Fluffy climbed the tree, she saw a bird on a branch. The bird said, "Hi, Fluffy! Do you want to be my friend?" Fluffy said, "Yes, I want to be your friend." They played together and had lots of fun.
Then, the bird told Fluffy about a prize hidden in the tree. Fluffy and the bird looked for the prize. They found a big, shiny ball. Fluffy and the bird were very happy to find the prize. They played with the ball all day and became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a tall tree. It had many green leaves. Under the tree, there was a small bean. The bean was green too. Every day, the bean looked at the tall tree and wanted to be tall too.
One day, a little girl found the bean. She picked it up and gave it a squeeze. The bean felt happy. The little girl took the bean home to show her mom. She put the bean in a cup with some dirt.
The next day, the bean started to grow. It grew and grew. It became taller and taller. The bean was so happy! It was almost as tall as the tall tree. But then, something unexpected happened. The bean started to shrink. It became smaller and smaller. The bean was sad.
Then, the little girl came to the bean. She gave the bean another squeeze. The bean felt happy again. It started to grow once more. This time, it did not stop. It grew taller than the tall tree. The bean was so happy and it knew that the little girl's squeeze was its magic power.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a big, soft armchair. It was a famous armchair because it was very comfy. Many people wanted to sit in it, but the armchair belonged to a kind girl named Sally.
One day, Sally's friend, Tim, came over to play. They played with toys and ran around the house. Tim saw the famous armchair and really wanted to sit in it. He could not resist the soft and comfy chair. But, Sally said, "Wait! I have a surprise for you."
Sally went to her room and came back with a big, soft blanket. She put the blanket on the armchair and said, "Now we can both sit on the famous armchair together!" Tim was very happy, and they both sat on the armchair, feeling warm and comfy. They knew they would always remember this special day.

Once upon a time, there was a big, red mop. The mop was very expensive. It was special because it could set itself up to clean. One day, the mop was in a house with a little boy named Tim.
Tim saw the mop and wanted to play with it. He set the mop up and began to clean. But Tim did not know how to use the expensive mop. He played with it too much and made a big mess.
Tim's mom came home and saw the mess. She was very sad. The expensive mop was broken, and the house was dirty. Tim felt bad and knew he should not have played with the mop.

Once upon a time, there was a big tree. It had many leaves. The tree was very old. It had been there for many years. The tree was in a park where kids loved to play.
One day, a little boy named Tim came to the park. He saw the tree and wanted to hang from a branch. He held on tight with his hands. Tim was happy and laughed as he swung back and forth.
Tim came back to the tree every day. He loved to hang on the branch and swing. The tree and Tim became good friends. They spent many happy days together in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a yellow rose. The rose lived near a big tree. The tree had a little door. The door had a lock.
One day, a girl named Amy found a key. Amy wanted to unlock the door. She put the key in the lock and turned it. The door opened.
Behind the door, Amy found a beautiful garden. The yellow rose was very happy. Amy picked the rose and took it home. They were friends forever.

Once there was a little girl named Maria. She was only 3 years old and she was very curious. She was always asking her mom questions about everything.
Her mom always had time to teach Maria something new every day.
One day, Maria asked her mom why she couldn't go to school like the other children.
Her mom smiled and said, "One day when you are a little older I will teach you some special things that you can learn at school. If you learn them well, I will give you a special reward."
Maria was very anxious to learn what these special things might be, but she had to wait.
Finally, the day came when her mom began to teach her all of the things that she could learn at school. Maria was so excited to learn, and did her best to remember everything.
When she was done, her mom rewarded her with a big hug and a cookie - her favorite treat! Maria had learned something new, and was excited to continue learning more and more.

One day, a little cat named Sam went for a walk. He was very hungry and wanted a snack. He looked around and saw an empty box. Sam thought there might be a snack inside.
Sam walked to the box and tried to jump in. But, he did not see the water on the ground. Sam's foot did slip and he fell. He was wet and a little sad.
Then, Sam saw a nice girl with a snack. She saw Sam was wet and sad. She gave Sam some of her snack. Sam was happy and not hungry anymore. They became friends and played together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. Jane had a big, clear jar. She loved to put things in the jar. One day, she found a pretty candle. Jane did not know what it was for, but she put it in the jar.
Later, Jane's mom came into the room. She saw the candle in the jar. "Jane, do you know what that is?" she asked. Jane shook her head no. "That is a candle," her mom said. "We can use it to make light when it is dark."
That night, the sky got dark and the room was very dark too. Jane's mom took the candle out of the jar. She lit the candle, and the room filled with a warm, soft light. Jane smiled and said, "Now I know what a candle is for! Thank you, Mom."

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk with his mom. They walked in the park and saw a big tree. The tree had dry leaves on the ground. Tim wanted to play with the leaves.
Tim said, "Mom, I want to play with the dry leaves!" His mom smiled and said, "Okay, Tim. Let's play together." They jumped and laughed as they played in the dry leaves.
After playing, they saw a squirrel. The squirrel wanted a nut. Tim found a nut and gave it to the squirrel. The squirrel was happy. Tim and his mom went home, feeling happy too.

Once upon a time, there was a restless little dog named Mark. Mark loved to run and play all day long. He liked to find small things to play with, like a pebble.
One sunny day, Mark found a shiny pebble in the park. He was so happy and wanted to show it to his best friend, a cat named Luna. Mark picked up the pebble with his mouth and ran to find Luna.
When he found Luna, he showed her the shiny pebble. Luna liked the pebble too. They played with the pebble all day, rolling it around and chasing after it. At the end of the day, Mark and Luna were tired but happy. They put the pebble in a special place, so they could play with it again later.

Once upon a time, in a gloomy space, there lived a little girl named Mia. Mia had a friend named Tom. They liked to play together. One day, they found a big, red ball in the park. They wanted to play with it, but it was stuck in a tree.
Mia and Tom tried to get the ball down. They threw sticks at it, but it did not work. Then, Tom had an idea. He said, "Let's pinch the tree and maybe it will let go of the ball!" Mia thought it was a funny idea, but they tried it anyway.
They pinched the tree together, and something unexpected happened. The tree started to laugh! It said, "That tickles!" The tree shook and the big, red ball fell down. Mia and Tom were very surprised. They thanked the tree and played with the ball all day long.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw a smelly old man sitting on a seat near the swings. Tim wanted to play on the swings, but he was scared of the smelly old man.
"Mom, can I go on the swings?" Tim asked. "Yes, but be nice to the old man," his mom said. Tim went to the swings and started to play. The smelly old man smiled at him and said, "Hi, I am Mr. Brown. I like to watch kids play."
Tim was still scared, but he talked to Mr. Brown. Then, something unexpected happened. Mr. Brown stood up and walked to a big box near the seat. He put a key in the box and turned it. The box opened, and there were lots of toys inside! Mr. Brown had locked the toys in the box to keep them safe.
Tim and Mr. Brown played with the toys and had lots of fun. Tim learned that even if someone looks smelly, they can still be nice and fun to play with.

One day, a cute little cat named Kitty went to a food place with her mom. They sat at a table and looked at the menu. The menu had many yummy foods like pizza, cake, and ice cream. Kitty wanted to eat everything!
"Mom, can I have pizza and ice cream?" Kitty asked. Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, but you need to eat your pizza first, and then you can have ice cream."
As they waited for their food, Kitty saw a big box on the floor. She wanted to see what was inside, so she tried to lift it. But the box was too heavy for her. She tried and tried, but she couldn't lift it.
Just then, a big, friendly dog walked in. He saw Kitty trying to lift the box and came to help. With one strong push, the dog lifted the box, and something unexpected happened. The box was full of balloons, and they all flew up into the air!
Kitty and the dog laughed and played with the balloons. They became good friends. And after playing, Kitty ate her pizza and shared her ice cream with her new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful man with a big mustache. He liked to perform tricks for the kids in the park. One sunny day, he put on a funny hat and went to the park.
As he started to perform, a small cat came to watch. The cat had a mustache too! The kids laughed and clapped. They thought the cat was part of the show. But the helpful man did not know the cat.
Then, something unexpected happened. The cat started to perform tricks too! It jumped high and did flips. The kids were very happy. The helpful man smiled and knew that the cat with the mustache was his new friend.
From that day on, the helpful man and the cat with the mustache performed tricks together. They made many kids happy and had lots of fun. And they became the best of friends, always helping each other.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play with toys. One day, they find a big box in the park. The box is full of old things. There are clothes, books, cups, and a lamp.
"Wow, look at all these things!" Lily says. "We can use them for our game. This lamp can be a rocket. These clothes can be costumes. These books can be treasure maps."
Tom shakes his head. "No, these things are waste. They are dirty and broken. We can't play with them. We need modern toys. Modern toys are shiny and new. They have buttons and sounds. They are fun."
Lily and Tom disagree. They start to argue. They pull and push the things in the box. They make a big mess.
"Stop it, stop it!" a voice says. It is the park keeper. He is angry. He sees the box and the things. He sees Lily and Tom. "What are you doing? You are making trouble. You are not supposed to touch this box. This box is for recycling. Recycling means making new things from old things. It is good for the earth. It is not waste."
Lily and Tom feel sorry. They did not know about recycling. They help the park keeper to put the things back in the box. They say sorry to him. He smiles and forgives them. He tells them about recycling. He shows them some modern toys that are made from recycled things. Lily and Tom are amazed. They learn something new. They decide to play with the modern toys. They agree that recycling is fun.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a spring with water. The water was not wet, but dry. Tim was very surprised.
Tim met a rude frog near the spring. The frog said, "This is my spring. I made the water dry because I don't like sharing." Tim was sad but had an idea. He said, "If we share the spring, we can make new friends."
The frog thought about it and agreed. They worked together to make the water wet again. Soon, many animals came to drink and play. The frog and Tim became good friends.
The moral of the story is: Sharing is good because it helps us make friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toy cars and eat potatoes. One day, Tim saw a big potato in the kitchen. He was very happy and wanted to eat it.
Tim's big brother, Ben, saw him with the potato. Ben liked to tease Tim. Ben said, "Tim, that potato is fearful. It will scare you!" Tim was scared, but he still wanted to eat the potato.
Later, Tim's mom came into the kitchen. She saw Tim looking scared at the potato. She asked, "Why are you scared, Tim?" Tim said, "Ben said the potato is fearful." Mom laughed and said, "Don't worry, the potato is not scary. Ben was just teasing you." Tim smiled and ate the potato. It was very yummy.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play with Lily's dog, Max. Max is a big dog with brown fur and black spots. He is very alert and likes to run and bark.
One day, Lily and Tom find a big box in the garage. They wonder what is inside. They open the box and see a lot of paper and ribbons. They have an idea. They decide to wrap Max with the paper and ribbons. They think it will be a funny surprise for Lily's mom.
They call Max and he comes to them. He is happy to see them and wags his tail. They start to wrap him with the paper. They put a big bow on his head. They laugh and say, "You look so pretty, Max!"
But Max does not like to be wrapped. He feels hot and itchy. He tries to shake off the paper and ribbons. He growls and barks. He runs around the garage and makes a mess.
Lily and Tom hear Lily's mom coming. They are scared. They try to hide behind the box. But Lily's mom sees them and Max. She is not happy. She says, "What have you done to Max? He looks like a giant present! This is not nice. You have to unwrap him and clean up the garage. Now!"
Lily and Tom feel sorry. They say, "We are sorry, mom. We wanted to make you laugh. We love Max. We will unwrap him and clean up. We promise."
They unwrap Max and hug him. He licks their faces. He is glad to be free. They clean up the garage and put the paper and ribbons back in the box. They say, "We will not wrap Max again. We will play with him in the yard. He is a good dog. We are sorry, mom."
Lily's mom smiles and says, "It's okay. I know you meant well. But next time, please ask me before you use my things. And remember, Max is not a toy. He is a living being. He needs love and care. Not paper and ribbons."
Lily and Tom nod and say, "We understand, mom. We love you and Max. Can we go play with him in the yard?"
Lily's mom says, "Yes, you can. But be careful and gentle. And have fun."
Lily and Tom say, "Thank you, mom. We will." They take Max to the yard and play with him. He is happy and alert. He runs and barks. He is a good dog. And Lily and Tom are good friends. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Emma. She was three years old. Emma had a special place which she liked to spend time in. One day, she decided to go there.
When Emma arrived, she saw a big shower of stars twinkling in the sky. She was so excited! Emma stared in amazement and then decided to get even closer. She looked for the safest way to go, but it seemed so far away.
Suddenly, she heard a voice from behind her. "Hi Emma!" It was her Grandma. She had come to see her grandaughter's special place. Grandma said "Let's go where the stars are. That way you'll stay safe."
Emma and Grandma made their way to the stars. Emma stared in wonder as they got nearer and nearer. Finally, they were standing under the shower of stars. Emma smiled happily and hugged Grandma. They would always remember their special night.

One day, a cat and a dog were playing near a tree. The cat found a file under the tree. The dog said, "What is that?" The cat said, "I don't know. Let's play with it."
They played with the file, and it made a loud sound. The sound was so loud that it scared the birds in the tree. The birds flew away, and the cat and dog laughed. They played with the file some more, and it made more loud sounds.
Then, a big bird came. The big bird was not scared of the loud sound. The big bird said, "This is a fake file. It is not real. You should not play with it." The cat and dog did not listen. They kept playing with the fake file.
The big bird got mad. It flew down and struck the fake file with its beak. The fake file broke into pieces. The cat and dog were sad. The big bird said, "You should listen when someone tells you something is not good."
The cat and dog learned their lesson. They said, "We will listen next time." They played with other things and did not play with fake things anymore.

Once upon a time, in a wild forest, there lived a small bird named Tim. Tim hurt his wing one day and could not fly. He was sad and sat on a tree branch.
One day, a kind deer named Daisy came by. She saw Tim and asked, "Why are you sad, little bird?" Tim said, "I hurt my wing and cannot fly. I want to heal and be happy again." Daisy wanted to help her new friend.
Daisy told Tim, "Take small steps and be patient. Your wing will heal soon." Tim listened to Daisy and took small steps. He rested, ate well, and played with Daisy. After some days, his wing healed, and he could fly again. Tim and Daisy were very happy and played together in the wild forest.

Once upon a time, there was a soft bunny named Bobo. Bobo lived in a small hotel with his friends. They liked to play and have fun together.
One day, Bobo wanted to jump high and touch the sky. He tried and tried, but he could not do it. His friend, Toto the turtle, came and said, "Bobo, don't give up! You can succeed if you keep trying."
Bobo smiled and said, "Thank you, Toto. I will try again." With Toto's help, Bobo practiced every day. He got better and better at jumping.
Finally, one sunny day, Bobo jumped so high that he touched the clouds. His friends cheered and clapped for him. Bobo was very happy because he succeeded. From that day on, Bobo and his friends played and had fun in their hotel, knowing that they can do anything if they try hard.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to build things. He would build towers, cars, and even little houses with his blocks. One day, Tim found a new game called "chess". He wanted to learn how to play.
Tim asked his mom to help him play chess. She said, "Let's build the perfect chess board first!" So, they took out all the pieces and put them in their right spots. There were big pieces and little pieces. Tim liked the horse piece the most.
Tim and his mom played chess together. They moved the pieces and tried to win. Tim was very happy because he learned a new game. He thought the chess game was perfect. Now, Tim could build and play chess whenever he wanted.

Once upon a time, there was a brave little boy named Tim. He loved to play outside with his friends. One day, Tim saw a big tree with a lot of fruit. He wanted to get the fruit for his friends to share.
Tim tried to reach the fruit, but it was too high. He did not give up. He tried to yield the fruit by jumping and using a stick. Suddenly, a friendly bird flew by and saw Tim. The bird gave Tim a wink and said, "I can help you get the fruit."
With the help of the bird, Tim got the fruit and shared it with his friends. They were all very happy and enjoyed the sweet fruit. Tim thanked the bird for being so kind. From that day on, Tim and the bird were best friends.

One day, a warm sun was in the sky. A little bird was happy to fly high. The bird saw a big cloud up in the sky. The cloud was very cold, and it could freeze things.
The bird wanted to see the cloud up close. But when the bird got near the cloud, something unexpected happened. The cloud was not a cloud at all! It was a big, cold ball of ice. The bird was surprised.
The bird did not want to freeze, so it flew away from the cold ball of ice. The bird went back to the warm sun in the sky. The bird was happy to be warm again and kept flying high.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a jog in the fog. He liked to jog because it made him feel happy. As he jogged, he saw a noisy dog. The dog was barking and jumping around.
Tim said, "Why are you so noisy, dog?" The dog stopped barking and said, "I am noisy because I am lost in the fog. I can't find my way home."
Tim felt sad for the noisy dog. He wanted to help the dog find his home. So, he said, "Don't be sad, dog. I will help you find your home."
Together, they jogged through the fog, looking for the dog's home. After a while, they found it. The dog was very happy and said, "Thank you, Tim, for helping me find my home."
From that day on, Tim and the noisy dog became best friends. They would jog in the fog and play together every day. And they were both very happy.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a big volcano. Many people lived near the volcano. They were happy but sometimes they felt troubled. They knew the volcano could be dangerous.
One day, a wise old man came to the village. He saw the people and said, "I must warn you. The volcano can wake up and make a big boom. You should move away to be safe."
The people listened to the old man. They were scared but they knew he was right. They said, "Thank you for warning us. We will move away to be safe."
So, the people moved to a new village far away from the volcano. They were happy and not troubled anymore. They knew they were safe now, thanks to the wise old man's warning. And they all lived happily ever after.

Tommy was a boy who liked to talk a lot. He talked to his mom, his dad, his sister, his friends, his toys, and even his dog. He talked when he was happy, when he was sad, when he was bored, and when he was curious. He talked so much that sometimes he forgot to listen.
One day, Tommy went to the park with his mom and his sister. He saw a big slide and ran to it. He talked to the other kids who were waiting for their turn. He talked about his favorite games, his favorite foods, his favorite colors, and his favorite pants. He talked so much that he did not hear his mom mention that it was time to go home.
When Tommy finally got to the top of the slide, he looked around and saw that his mom and his sister were gone. He felt scared and alone. He cried and called for them, but no one answered. He wished he had listened to his mom when she mentioned that they had to leave.
Luckily, a nice lady who worked at the park saw Tommy and helped him. She asked him his name and where he lived. She called his mom on her phone and told her where Tommy was. She gave him a hug and a cookie and told him to be quiet and wait for his mom.
Soon, Tommy's mom and sister came to the park. They were very worried and angry. They hugged Tommy and scolded him for not listening. They told him that he had to pay attention when they talked to him, and that he had to be quiet sometimes and let others talk too. They told him that talking too much can get him into trouble and make him miss important things.
Tommy felt sorry and ashamed. He apologized to his mom and his sister and thanked the nice lady. He learned his lesson and promised to be more quiet and listen more. He realized that talking is good, but not all the time. He understood that being quiet can be good too.

Once upon a time, there was an old mountain. It seemed to stretch up forever and ever.
One day, a small boy was walking around the mountain. He was only three years old and couldn't see the top!
"How do I get up there?" the boy asked.
A wise old man who was sitting nearby heard the boy's question. He smiled and said, "If you want to get up there, you must stretch your legs and climb!"
The boy was excited and started to climb the old mountain. He worked his way up step by step until he made it to the top.
Looking out from the top of the mountain, the boy had a wonderful view. From up here, he could see far and wide. The boy smiled with joy and could not believe how high he had climbed.

Once, there was a little girl named Jane. Jane was only three years old, and she was curious about the world around her. Every day, Jane liked to look out her window and watch the people walking by. One day, she saw a man walking by with a letter in his hand. Jane thought it was very pretty and wondered what was inside.
Mommy came over to Jane and said, "That is called mail. It is special paper with a message written on it." Jane was so confused. "What is the message?" she asked.
Mommy smiled and said, "It could be anything. It could be a message from someone far away telling someone else how much they love them." Jane smiled and asked, "Can I write mail to someone?"
Mommy sighed, "Well, that's the thing. You can't write mail until you learn how to write. But there is something special that you can do. You can pray for the person who you want to send a message to. Praying is like sending someone a message from your heart, and your prayer will always be heard."
Jane thought about this for a moment and then smiled. She bowed her head and said a little prayer for the person that she saw with the mail in his hands. Then she opened her eyes and smiled. Jane felt that her prayer was heard.

Once upon a time, there was a famous pen. This pen could talk! It lived in a big box with other pens. One day, a little girl named Lily found the pen.
Lily said, "Hello, pen. Can you talk?" The pen replied, "Yes, I can talk. I am a famous pen."
Lily and the pen became good friends. They would talk and draw pictures together. One day, they drew a big, pretty sun. Lily was very happy with her famous pen friend.

One day, a big wild bear named Bobo went to the clean river. He was very thirsty. He saw his friend, a small bird named Lulu, sitting on a tree branch. Lulu said, "Hi, Bobo! Let's play a game!"
Bobo said, "Okay, Lulu! What game do you want to play?" Lulu thought for a moment and said, "Let's play hide and seek!" So, they started to play. Bobo closed his eyes and counted, while Lulu flew away to hide.
As Bobo looked for Lulu, he saw a big cave. He thought Lulu might be hiding there. He was about to enter the cave when he heard a loud roar. Out came a big lion! The lion was not happy to see Bobo near his cave. Bobo was scared and ran away.
Bobo found Lulu and told her about the lion. Lulu said, "Oh no! I am sorry, Bobo. I did not know there was a lion in the cave." They decided to play a different game, far away from the cave. They had fun and stayed safe together.

Lily and Ben were twins who liked to play outside. They had a big yard with a swing, a slide and a sandbox. They also had a friendly neighbor, Mr. Lee, who had a garden with many flowers and vegetables.
One day, Lily and Ben were playing in the sandbox when they saw Mr. Lee picking some tomatoes from his garden. They waved to him and said hello. Mr. Lee waved back and smiled. He walked over to them and gave them each a ripe tomato. "These are for you, my little friends. Enjoy!" he said.
"Thank you, Mr. Lee! You are very generous!" Lily and Ben said. They took the tomatoes and bit into them. They were juicy and sweet. They liked them very much.
But then, Ben saw that Lily's tomato was bigger than his. He felt jealous and angry. He wanted the bigger tomato. He snatched Lily's tomato from her hand and said, "This is mine! You can have the smaller one!"
Lily was shocked and sad. She started to cry. She said, "That's not fair, Ben! Mr. Lee gave us both a tomato. You can't take mine!"
Mr. Lee heard the commotion and came back to see what was wrong. He saw Ben holding two tomatoes and Lily crying. He said, "What's going on here, children? Why are you fighting over a tomato?"
Ben looked down and felt ashamed. He realized he had been greedy and mean. He said, "I'm sorry, Mr. Lee. I'm sorry, Lily. I wanted the bigger tomato, but I shouldn't have taken yours. Here, you can have it back."
He gave Lily her tomato and hugged her. Lily stopped crying and hugged him back. She said, "It's okay, Ben. I forgive you. We can share the tomatoes if you want."
Mr. Lee smiled and said, "That's very kind of you, Lily. And that's very brave of you, Ben. You both learned a valuable lesson today. It's better to be generous and share than to be selfish and grab. Now, why don't you go inside and wash your hands? It's almost time for dinner."
Lily and Ben nodded and said, "Okay, Mr. Lee. Thank you for the tomatoes. We'll see you later."
They ran inside and washed their hands. They told their mom about the tomatoes and how they made up with each other. Their mom was proud of them and said, "You are good children. I'm glad you have such a nice neighbor. Now, let's have dinner. We have some more tomatoes in the salad."
Lily and Ben smiled and said, "Yay! We love tomatoes!"
They sat down and enjoyed their dinner. They also remembered to wave to Mr. Lee through the window and say thank you again. Mr. Lee waved back and said, "You're welcome, my little friends. Have a good night!"

Once there was a little girl called Lucy. She was very playful. She loved to lay down in the sun outside her house. One day Lucy wanted to be even more playful. She went to the store and bought some paint.
Back at home, Lucy quickly got ready. She got some newspapers to lay on the floor, then she opened the paint. She chose some bright colours and started drawing. She made happy, colourful shapes around her.
Lucy was so happy with her artwork. She lay on the floor and smiled at the stuff she had made. She was so proud. Lucy laughed and danced around in her colourful world. It was very playful and lots of fun. 
When it was time to go inside, she looked around and smiled. This was the best, most playful day ever!

Once upon a time, in a big green forest, there was a fat fox. The fat fox liked to play with his friends. One day, the fat fox was sad because he lost his ball. He looked for it everywhere, but he could not find it.
The fat fox went to his friend, the kind bear, and asked, "Can you lend me your ball?" The kind bear said, "Of course, I will lend you my ball, but you must give it back to me later." The fat fox was very happy and started to play with the ball.
As the fat fox was playing, he found his lost ball under a big tree. He was very surprised! Now he had two balls to play with. The fat fox went back to the kind bear and gave him back his ball. The kind bear was happy to have his ball back, and they both played with their own balls together. From that day on, the fat fox and the kind bear became best friends and always shared their toys.

Once upon a time, there was a big, rough cauliflower. It lived in a garden with many plants. One day, a little boy named Tim came to the garden. He saw the cauliflower and said, "Wow! You are so big and rough!"
The cauliflower replied, "Yes, I am! Do you want to know my secret?" Tim was very curious and said, "Yes, please tell me!" The cauliflower told Tim that it would drink a lot of water every day to grow big and strong.
One day, while the cauliflower was drinking water, something unexpected happened. A tiny, thirsty bird came to the garden. The bird saw the water and wanted to drink too. The cauliflower was kind and shared the water with the bird. They both drank water together and became good friends.
The moral of the story is that sharing is caring. When we share with others, we can make new friends and be happy.

One day, a small boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. He had a yummy lollipop in his hand. Tim liked the park because he could play on the swings and slides.
While Tim was playing, a small dog came up to him. The dog wanted to be friends. Tim liked the dog and wanted to play with him, but the dog saw the lollipop. The dog wanted to eat the lollipop too.
Tim didn't want to part with his lollipop. But then, something unexpected happened. The dog jumped and took the lollipop from Tim's hand! Tim was sad, but the dog was happy. Tim's mom saw what happened and gave Tim a new lollipop. Tim and the dog became friends and shared the new lollipop.

Once upon a time, there was a loyal dog named Buddy. Buddy had a best friend, a crocodile named Crikey. They played together every day.
One day, Buddy and Crikey were playing by the river. Buddy said, "Crikey, let's play fetch!" Buddy threw a stick into the river. Crikey swam fast to get the stick. But the stick went too far away. Crikey said, "I can't get it, Buddy. I quit."
Buddy did not want Crikey to quit. He had an idea. Buddy found a big leaf and put it on the water. Buddy said, "Get on the leaf, Crikey. It will help you." Crikey got on the leaf and swam to the stick. He got the stick and swam back to Buddy.
Buddy and Crikey were happy. They played fetch all day. They were loyal friends who always helped each other.

One day, a group of animals was playing in the park. They were very creative and liked to make up new games. They played and laughed all day long. The sun was shining, and the grass was green.
As they played, a big wind came and made the leaves fall from the trees. The animals thought this was fun and started to catch the falling leaves. They jumped and ran, trying to grab as many leaves as they could.
But then, something unexpected happened. A little mouse, who was very good at catching leaves, saw a big leaf falling from the sky. He ran to catch it, but when he got close, he saw that it was not a leaf at all! It was a butterfly! The butterfly had been hiding in the trees, watching the animals play. The mouse and the butterfly became friends, and they all played together in the park.

Once upon a time in summer, a rabbit and a turtle had a race. The rabbit was very fast, but he was not serious. The turtle was slow, but he was very serious.
The rabbit said, "I will win the race because I am fast!" The turtle said, "We will see who wins."
The race started. The rabbit ran very fast, but then he stopped to play. The turtle kept walking, slow and serious. The rabbit saw the turtle and said, "I can still win!" But he played more.
At the end of the race, the turtle won. The rabbit was sad. The turtle said, "Being serious is important. If you do not try, you will not win."
The rabbit learned that being serious is good. They both became friends and played together in the summer sun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big red ball. He liked to play with the ball every day. One day, the ball got stuck in a tree. Tim was in trouble.
Tim saw a long rope. He had an idea. He would pull the rope to get the ball. Tim tied the rope to the ball. Then, he pulled and pulled. The ball came out of the tree! Tim was very happy.
Tim was successful. He got his ball back. He played with the ball all day. He was careful not to let the ball get stuck again. Tim knew that if he ever had trouble, he could use the rope to pull and fix it.

One day, a small cat named Tim saw a weak bird near the gate. The bird could not fly. Tim felt bad for the bird and wanted to help.
Tim said to the bird, "I can lend you my toy car so you can move around." The bird was happy and said, "Thank you, Tim!"
But then, a big dog came and wanted the toy car too. The dog and the bird did not want to share. Tim was sad and did not know what to do. But then, the bird had an idea. The bird said, "Let's all play together with the toy car!" The dog liked the idea, and they all played happily. The weak bird felt better and could fly again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. One day, she saw an old lady who needed help. The old lady was trying to carry a big bag of apples. Lily went to her and said, "Can I help you with your bag?"
The old lady smiled and said, "Thank you, dear. You are very kind." Lily helped the old lady carry the big bag of apples to her house. They talked and laughed as they walked together.
When they got to the old lady's house, they saw a foolish dog. The dog was trying to catch its tail but kept falling down. Lily and the old lady laughed at the silly dog. The old lady said, "Thank you for your help, Lily. You made my day better." Lily smiled and went back home, feeling happy that she could help someone.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a cave. In the cave, there lived a bear named Ben. Ben was a fair bear, always kind to his friends.
One sunny day, Ben went out to play with his friends. He met a small bird named Tim. Tim was sad because he couldn't speak like the other birds. Ben wanted to help Tim.
Ben said to Tim, "Let's go to the wise old owl. He can help you speak." So they went to the wise old owl's tree. The owl listened to their problem and said, "Tim, you must be patient and practice every day. Soon, you will speak like the other birds."
Tim listened to the wise old owl and practiced every day. Soon, he could speak like the other birds. Ben and Tim were very happy.
The moral of the story is: be patient and practice, and you can learn anything.

One day, a little boy named Tim was playing in his room. He saw his bookcase and wanted to add a new book to it. But, the bookcase was very high, and Tim was too small to reach it. He felt ashamed that he could not do it by himself.
"Mom, can you help me?" Tim asked. His mom came into the room and saw Tim looking sad. "What's wrong, Tim?" she asked. Tim told her about the bookcase and how he wanted to add a new book.
Mom smiled and said, "Don't be ashamed, Tim. You are still growing. I will help you now, and soon you will be big enough to do it by yourself." She picked up Tim and helped him add the new book to the bookcase.
Tim was so happy that his mom helped him. He knew that he would grow bigger and stronger one day. For now, he was glad that his mom was there to help him when he needed it. And together, they enjoyed reading the new book.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy girl. She liked to stick her nose in other people's business. One day, she heard that a special poem was being written. She wanted to learn the words of the poem.
She tried and tried to find out what the poem said, but no one would tell her. This made the girl very worried. What was so special about this poem? She wished that she knew the answer.
One day, the girl's wish came true. She peeked out the window to see a parade with flags and banners flowing in the breeze. On the banners were the very words of the poem.
The girl read the words and smiled. She was happy that she finally knew what was so special about the poem. She went outside to join in the parade. She was no longer nosy. She was now just happy and excited to share the poem with the world.

Once upon a time, there was a polite girl named Lily. She loved to help her mom in the kitchen. One day, Lily's mom was making a big cake. Lily wanted to help her mom cut the cake.
When Lily's mom was not looking, Lily tried to cut the cake by herself. But she was not careful, and she cut her hand. It hurt a lot, and Lily started to cry. Her mom heard her cry and came to see what happened.
Lily's mom saw the cut on her hand and quickly put a bandage on it. She told Lily that she should always ask for help when using sharp things. Lily learned her lesson and promised to be more careful next time. From that day on, Lily always asked her mom for help and they had lots of fun in the kitchen together.

Once upon a time there was a faucet who was very intelligent. She used to record record things around her. 
One day she used her recorder to document her day in the garden. Suddenly a three year old appeared! "Hi, faucet!" said the 3 year old happily. 
The faucet was very surprised. She had not expected to meet a 3 year old. She stopped recording and asked, "what are you doing here?" 
The 3 year old replied, "I like to play here because it's so much fun. Also I learn a lot about plants!" 
The faucet was impressed by the 3 year old's intelligence. She smiled and said, "That's amazing! I'm sure you'll learn a lot of new things in the garden." 
The 3 year old nodded happily, and the faucet resumed recording. They said goodbye, and the 3 year old went off to have more fun in the garden.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who wanted to be independent and go on an adventure. She went to stay at a hotel to have some fun.
At the hotel, the girl met a kind lady who mentioned something amazing. She said there was a magical place nearby with lots of critters and creatures.
The little girl was so excited! She ran to her best friend and they both raced to the magical place. When they got there they saw all sorts of animals like dogs, cats, rabbits, and birds.
They had so much fun! The little girl was so proud that she'd been able to go out and explore by herself. She felt independent and happy.
The girl and her best friend enjoyed the magical place for hours, until it was time to go back to the hotel. The little girl knew she would remember her independent adventure forever.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a man who liked to sell new things. One day, he got a big box of new masks. He was very happy.
He went to the park with the new masks to sell them. He put the masks on a table and called out, "New masks for sale! Come and get a new mask!" Many people came to see the new masks.
A little boy saw the masks and wanted one. He asked his mom if he could get a new mask. His mom said yes, and they bought a new mask. The little boy was so happy with his new mask that he wore it all day long. The man was happy too, because he sold many new masks to the people in the town.

One day, a boy named Tim had a notebook. He liked to draw in it. Tim had a friend named Sam. Sam was deaf, but they still played together.
One day, they were playing with the notebook. Tim drew a picture of a cat. Sam liked the cat. He wanted to keep it. Tim said, "OK, you can have it."
Sam tried to tear the page out of the notebook. But he tore it a little too much. The cat picture ripped in half. Sam was sad. Tim saw Sam's sad face and said, "Don't worry, I can draw another one for you."
Tim drew another cat for Sam. This time, he helped Sam tear the page out. They both smiled and continued to play together.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a path in the woods. He wanted to see where it went. Tim walked on the path and saw a big tree. The tree had many crazy shapes on it. Tim thought it was a funny tree.
Tim saw a big pile of leaves on the path. He decided to cover the path with the leaves. He thought it would be fun to jump in them. As he was playing, a little girl named Sue came by.
"Hey! Why did you cover the path with leaves?" Sue asked. Tim said, "I thought it would be fun to jump in them. Want to try?" Sue looked at the leaves and thought for a moment.
"No, I want to see where the path goes," Sue said. "Can you help me move the leaves?" Tim felt bad. He didn't want to stop Sue from having fun. So, they worked together to move the leaves off the path.
With the path clear, Tim and Sue walked together. They found a beautiful pond at the end of the path. They became friends and had a great day playing by the pond.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max liked to run and play all day. One day, Max met a big dog named Sam. Sam was very tired and wanted to relax.
Max said, "Sam, why don't you play with me?" Sam replied, "I am too tired, Max. I need to relax my mouth and body." Max thought for a moment and then had an idea.
Max brought Sam a soft pillow. Sam lay down on the pillow and relaxed. Max learned that it was important to help others and let them rest when they are tired. And so, Max and Sam became good friends, always caring for each other.

Once, there was a brother and sister. The brother loved to sing. The sister liked to make things organized. They played in their room every day. The sister would always clean up after they played. The brother would always sing a song when they were done.
One day, the brother and sister found a big box. They did not know what was inside. The sister said, "Let's make the box organized!" The brother agreed and sang a happy song while they worked. They put all their toys in the box and made them neat.
The next day, the brother and sister found a note on the box. It said, "Thank you for making me organized! I am a magic box, and now I will grant you a wish." The brother and sister were so happy! The brother wished to sing even better, and the sister wished for more things to make organized. They played and sang all day, knowing that their hard work made the magic box happy.

All of a sudden, a blue chamber opened and a group of animals started to run out. One of them was a small, furry mouse. He started to jump around the chamber and squeak, showing how happy he was. 
The other animals cheered and applauded loudly. They started to clap along with a rhythm, stomping their feet. As they kept stomping, everything seemed to vibrate. 
The mouse stopped and looked around the chamber. He saw a little girl in the corner, popping her head out from behind the big blue door. She smiled, and the mouse squeaked back. 
The little girl and the mouse stared at each other for a while in a comfortable silence. The other animals were still clapping and tapping but it felt like if time had stopped. 
Then, the girl started to clap along with the beat of the animals. She smiled and started to laugh. The animals stopped and joined in the laugh. They all applauded for the shared joy. 
The chamber was filled with laughter and happiness. The little mouse also jumped alongside the animals and the little girl, as they all celebrated the moment.

One day, a little girl named Lily was very excited. She knew her daddy was coming home from work soon. Lily wanted to make a surprise for her daddy.
Lily thought, "I will draw a pretty picture for daddy!" She took her crayons and paper and began to draw. She drew a big sun, a green tree, and a blue bird.
When Lily was done, she heard her daddy come in the door. She ran to him and said, "Daddy, look! I made you a surprise!" Daddy looked at the picture and smiled. He was so happy.
Daddy said, "Thank you, Lily! I love it!" He gave her a big hug. Lily knew her daddy liked the surprise and she was very proud.

Once upon a time, in a cold and snowy place, there lived a little penguin. All the other animals were so excited to meet him that they welcomed him with open arms! 
The fox was the first to greet the little penguin. He said, "Hello! It's so nice to meet you. Welcome to our home!"
The cub was just about to answer when the wiry hare came bouncing in. "My word!" he exclaimed. "Look at you! You're so hairy!"
The penguin smiled and said, "Yes, I am hairy! It keeps me warm in this cold place. Nice to meet you too!"
Suddenly, the bear walked in. He was the biggest animal of all and he said, "Welcome, little penguin! We all can't wait to be your friends!" 
The little penguin felt so happy. He had never met so many animals before and he was so glad to be welcomed by them.
From then on, the little penguin was part of the family and they all lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time, in a small village, there lived a human named Tom. Tom was a wild boy who loved to run and play all day. He had many friends, but he did not like to share his toys.
One day, Tom found a magic ball. The ball could increase in size when he played with it. Tom loved his new toy and played with it all the time. But he did not want to share it with his friends.
Tom's friends were sad because they wanted to play with the magic ball too. They tried to tell Tom that sharing is good, but he did not listen. The magic ball kept increasing in size, and soon it was too big for Tom to play with alone.
Tom's friends saw that he was having trouble with the big ball, so they came to help him. They all played with the big ball together and had lots of fun. Tom learned that sharing is important and makes everyone happy.
From that day on, Tom shared all his toys with his friends, and they all played happily together. The moral of the story is that sharing with others makes life more enjoyable for everyone.

Once upon a time, there was a little yellow bird. The bird lived in a small cell made of sticks. The cell was in a tree, high up in the sky.
One day, the yellow bird wanted to find a friend. So, the bird left the cell and started to fly. The bird flew and flew until it saw a big, green frog. The frog was sitting on a rock near a pond.
The yellow bird said, "Hello, frog! Will you be my friend?" The frog said, "Yes, bird! I can be your friend!" The two friends played all day. They would turn around and around, having lots of fun. At the end of the day, the yellow bird and the frog went back to the cell in the tree. They were happy to be friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy arrow that he loved to play with. It was a very special arrow, given to him by his mom. She told him it was expensive and to be careful with it. Tim liked to pretend he was a brave hero with the arrow.
One day, Tim was playing with his arrow outside. He saw a big ant on the ground. Tim thought it would be fun to pinch the ant with his fingers. But when he tried to pinch the ant, it ran away very fast. Tim chased after the ant, still holding his arrow.
Tim chased the ant all around the yard. He was having so much fun that he forgot about his expensive arrow. Suddenly, Tim tripped on a rock and fell down. His arrow flew out of his hand and broke. Tim was very sad. He learned that it is important to take care of the things we love.

Tom and Lily are twins. They like to play with their toys in their room. One day, they see a big orange ball in the shop window. They want the ball very much.
"Look, Lily, the ball is so big and orange!" Tom says. "Can we have it, please?"
Lily nods. "Yes, Tom, it is very pretty. But we need money to buy it. How can we get money?"
Tom thinks for a moment. He remembers that their mom gives them money when they help her with chores. He has an idea.
"Let's decide to help mom with chores. Then she will give us money. Then we can buy the ball!" he says.
Lily agrees. "That is a good idea, Tom. Let's go and ask mom what we can do."
They run to the kitchen, where their mom is making lunch. They tell her their plan.
"That is very nice of you, Tom and Lily," their mom says. "You can help me by setting the table, washing the dishes, and putting away your toys. Then I will give you some money for the ball."
Tom and Lily are happy. They decide to help their mom with chores. They work hard and do a good job. Their mom is proud of them. She gives them some money for the ball.
"Thank you, mom!" they say. "We love you!"
They run to the shop and buy the big orange ball. They play with it in the park. They have a lot of fun. They are glad they decided to help their mom.

One day, a dog named Buddy found a bone. It was a big, yummy bone. Buddy was very happy. He wanted to take the bone to his dog house. But, he did not know the way back home.
Buddy was patient. He thought and thought. Then, he had an idea. He would walk reverse. He walked back the way he came. He walked slow and careful. Buddy did not want to lose the bone.
Soon, Buddy saw his dog house. He was so happy! He walked inside and put the bone down. Buddy was proud of himself. He had found his way home by walking reverse. Now, he could enjoy his bone.

Once upon a time, there was a foolish dog called Sammy. He always wanted to follow the jet flying high in the sky. One day he decided to try and catch it. He ran and ran as fast as he could but the jet was too far away and moved too quickly.
Sadly, Sammy's dream of catching the jet seemed impossible. He became very sad and thought about giving up. But then he remembered something his mommy had told him. She said if he tried hard enough, his dreams could come true.
So, Sammy kept running and running, no matter how tired he got. Finally, he reached the jet! Then, using all his strength, he leaped into the sky and grabbed onto the jet. 
He had done it! Sammy was so proud of himself and the jet flew Sammy around the world! From that day on Sammy decided to never give up on his dreams, even if it seemed foolish.

One day, a little boy, Sam, went out to play. He was wearing a black shirt, and he was feeling a bit troubled. 
He started to skip through the park. He jumped over the grass, and skipped around the trees. Suddenly, one of the trees spoke to him! It said, "Will you help me?" 
Sam was so surprised that the tree could talk. He asked, "What do you need help with?" 
The tree answered, "I need you to cut the old rope off my wood. See? It's stuck around my branches and won't come off."
Sam didn't have anything to cut the rope with. But, he also noticed that the old rope was black. He thought for a minute and then he smiled. "I know where I can get something to help us," he said. 
Sam ran off to find a way to free the tree. As he went, he could feel that something exciting was about to happen. He was hopeful that he could help the tree, and he was sure that something good was going to come!

Once upon a time, there was a little mouse named Mimi. Mimi loved to watch the big world outside her tiny hole. Every day, she would peek out and see all the fun things going on. Birds would fly, and dogs would play. Mimi wanted to join them, but she was too small.
One day, Mimi saw a big piece of cheese outside her hole. She wanted the cheese so much because it was her favorite food. Mimi was very brave and ran out to get the cheese. She was so happy to have it and couldn't wait to eat it.
As Mimi was eating the cheese, a noisy cat named Tom came by. He saw Mimi and wanted to catch her. But Mimi was very fast. She ran back to her hole with the cheese, and the noisy cat couldn't catch her. Mimi was safe and enjoyed her yummy cheese. From that day on, Mimi knew she could be brave and fast, just like the big world outside her hole.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia had a delicate toy bunny that she loved a lot. She would take it everywhere she went. One day, Mia and her bunny went to the park to play.
While at the park, Mia needed to go to the bathroom. She took her bunny with her. When she finished, she washed her hands and went back to play. But Mia did not know that she had dropped her delicate bunny in the bathroom.
Mia played for a while, then she noticed her bunny was gone. She looked everywhere but could not find it. She was very sad. Just then, a kind boy found the delicate bunny in the bathroom and gave it back to Mia. Mia was so happy to have her bunny back, and they played together for the rest of the day.

Once upon a time in a beautiful garden, there lived a little caterpillar called Peter. Peter loved nothing more than to explore the garden. One day as he was going out, he saw a big sun in the sky. "Wow! That sun looks so beautiful!" Peter exclaimed. 
Just then, Peter heard a voice behind him saying "You can toil under the sun all day and never get anywhere." It was the voice of a wise old caterpillar. "If you really want to be happy, you must surrender to the sun's power," the old caterpillar continued.
"But why?" asked Peter, slightly confused.
The old caterpillar smiled and said,"The sun is pale, and no matter how hard you work, you will never be able to defeat it. The sun is so powerful that if you try to fight it, you will only grow weaker. So you must surrender to the sun's strength and use it to your advantage instead."
Peter thought about this long and hard. He realized that when he surrendered to the sun's power instead of trying to fight it, he felt more fulfilled and energetic. 
The moral of Peter's story is simple. We must all surrender to the power of nature. Even though it may be difficult and different from our ideal path, learning to listen to the wisdom of nature can truly enhance our lives.

Once upon a time, there lived a beautiful princess. Every day, she would go outside to play. One day while she was playing, she met a little goblin. He asked her if she would like to come to a party. The goblin showed the princess an invitation and she accepted the offer right away.
At the party, the princess had the best time with all of the other guests. Everyone was very friendly and kind, except for the nasty goblin. He was being so rude, that the princess did not know what to do. She told him she was going to leave, which made the goblin stop being mean.
