He politely asked her to stay and offered to show her a good time. The princess accepted the offer and kept on playing. She even sang and danced with the goblin. They had the best time and the princess never forgot about it.

One day, a mule named Max was walking down a path. Max felt helpless because he could not find his way home. He saw a big tree and sat under it to rest. The sun was hot, and Max was tired.
A little bird named Bella saw Max and flew down to him. She asked, "Why do you look so sad, Max?" Max said, "I am lost and can't find my way home." Bella wanted to help Max, so she told him, "I can zip through the sky and look for your home."
Bella zipped away and found Max's home. But when she came back, she saw a big surprise. There were now two mules under the tree! One was Max, and the other was his twin brother, Sam. Bella was confused but happy to see that Max was not helpless anymore. Together, they all went back to Max and Sam's home, and they became good friends.

One day, little Robert was at the playground. He saw a big slide on the right side, and wanted to slide down it. He asked his dad: "Dad, can I slide down the big slide?" 
His dad said "Yes, of course you can. Just go ahead and slide down. Be careful now". 
Little Robert was so excited. He ran to the slide and quickly slid down it. He enjoyed it so much, he decided to slide down it again. 
When he reached the bottom, he saw a bald man next to him. The bald man said to him: "Hi little one. Now, why don't we try the slide on the left? That one is even bigger".
Little Robert smiled. He liked the idea, so he and the bald man went to the left side of the playground. The slide there was huge and looked like so much fun. Robert and the bald man both slid down the slide, having a great time. 
When they finally came to the last slide, Robert thanked the bald man for taking him to the playground. The bald man smiled and said: "You're welcome. It was great fun and I'm so glad you had a good time".

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very adventurous. He liked to play outside and find new things. One day, Tim saw a tall pole in his yard. He thought it would be fun to climb it.
Tim's mom saw him looking at the pole. She said, "Tim, be careful. That pole is very high. I will provide you with a rope to help you climb." Tim was happy that his mom was helping him.
Tim tied the rope to the pole and started to climb. He went up and up, higher and higher. When he got to the top, he saw a beautiful bird's nest. Tim said, "Wow, this is so cool!" Then, he climbed back down and told his mom all about the nest. They both smiled and were happy with their adventurous day.

Once upon a time, there was a furry bear and a furry rabbit. The bear and the rabbit were best friends.
The bear wanted to go on a safari, so he asked the rabbit. "Let's go on a safari, my friend!" shouted the bear excitedly.
But the rabbit was scared of the safari. He said, "No way! I don't want to go on a safari!"
The bear was sad. He thought his friend didn't want to have fun. He said, "Please, please, come on the safari with me!"
The rabbit hesitated, but then he thought maybe it might be fun. He said, "Okay, I'll go with you on the safari."
The bear was so happy. He clapped and said, "Let's go!" and they set off on their safari.
They explored the wide and wild safari, looking at animals and plants. The bear and the rabbit laughed and shouted and had a wonderful time.
At the end of the day, they applauded their own adventure. They were glad that they had gone together on a safari, and had so much fun!

One day, a zebra was walking in the forest. The zebra saw a smelly sock on the ground. The zebra was sad because the sock made the forest look bad.
A bird saw the zebra and asked, "Why are you sad?" The zebra replied, "I am sad because of the smelly sock." The bird wanted to help the zebra, so it picked up the sock in its beak and flew away to throw it in the trash.
The zebra was happy now. The bird came back and the zebra said, "Thank you for helping me." The bird replied, "You're welcome, friend." From that day on, the zebra and the bird were best friends and they always helped keep the forest clean.

One day, a little girl named Amy went to the farm with her mom. They saw many animals like cows, pigs, and chickens. Amy saw a big field with tall green plants. "Mom, what is that?" she asked.
Her mom said, "That is corn. We can pick some and put it in our basket." So they went to the corn field and picked the yellow corn. They put the corn in their basket. Amy liked to be organized, so she put the big corn on one side and the small corn on the other side.
As they walked back to the car, a friendly farmer saw Amy's organized basket. He smiled and said, "Wow, you did a great job with the corn!" Amy felt happy and proud. She went home and shared the corn with her family. They all enjoyed the tasty corn for dinner.

Molly was excited for the parade to start. She saw all the bright colors, balloons, and treats everywhere. She couldn't wait to join in the fun!
But then she noticed something smelly. She looked around and saw that a monster had crashed the parade!
The monster threw his arms down and shouted, "The parade is cancelled!"
Molly was not happy. She glared at the monster and said, "No way!" But the monster just laughed and threatened to tear the parade apart.
Molly had to act quickly. She stepped forward and said, "Please don't do that! People were looking forward to this day."
The monster sighed. "Alright. I'll let you keep your parade, but only if you promise to take care of it better next time."
Molly smiled with relief. She promised she would take care of the parade from then on, and the monster stepped away. Just then, the parade started and everyone cheered. Molly joined in, happy that the parade had been saved.

Once upon a time there was a deer. He was very ordinary, but he was happy. Every day he would lock himself in the barn. The barn was very cozy, and he liked it there.
One day, the deer heard some loud noises coming from the window. He opened the barn door and peeked out. He saw an ordinary little boy! The boy was looking in the window at the deer. The deer thought the boy was friendly, so he said hello.
The boy was so excited to see the deer that he ran away! The deer chased him, and eventually the deer caught up. He wanted to be friends with the boy. So, the deer asked if he could lock the door. The boy agreed and helped the deer lock the door.
The deer was so happy. Finally he had a friend who he could lock in the barn with him. They spent hours in the barn, playing together and enjoying each other's company.
The deer and the boy were best friends. They were both very happy every time they locked themselves in the barn together.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a modest girl named Lily. She liked to help others and always had a smile on her face. One day, Lily went to the forest to pick some flowers for her mom.
As she walked through the forest, she saw a tiny flame near a tree. The flame was scared and alone. Lily knew she had to help the flame, so she went closer. She found a small stick and gently carried the flame back to her home.
Lily's mom saw the flame and told her that it was special. The flame could grow big and strong if they took care of it. So, Lily and her mom took care of the flame, and it grew bigger and brighter. They shared the flame's warmth and light with everyone in the town. The people were grateful and learned that helping others can make the world a better place. And Lily felt happy knowing she had done something good.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big billboard with a picture of a scary monster on it. Tim felt fearful when he saw the monster. He wanted to be brave, so he decided to march up to the billboard and face his fear.
As Tim marched closer, he met a little girl named Sue. Sue was also scared of the monster. Tim said, "Let's march to the billboard together and not be scared." Sue agreed and they both marched up to the billboard.
When they got close, they saw that the monster was not real. It was just a picture. They laughed and felt happy that they were not scared anymore. Tim and Sue became friends and marched back home, knowing they could face their fears together.

One day, Jack and his mommy went to the store to buy a new pair of shoes. Jack saw a shiny pair of red shoes that he liked and his mommy said, "Let's try them on!". 
Jack was so excited and he tried the shoes on. He stamped his feet and said, "They fit!". His mommy smiled and said, "I have one more thing to do. I need to check the buckle." 
Jack was frustrated but he waited while his mommy buckled the straps on his shoes. When she was done, she said, "Let's go to the bakery and get a tasty snack to celebrate your new shoes." 
Jack was delighted and he hopped up and down. He skipped to the bakery and when he got there, he found his favorite treat - chocolate chip cookies. Jack ate two and said, "They taste very tasty!" 
His mommy laughed and said, "I'm glad that you like your new shoes!"

One day, a little girl named Amy went to play outside. She saw a big, ugly bug on the ground. Amy was not scared of bugs, so she went closer to look at it. The bug had many legs and a funny face. Amy wanted to know how the bug would taste, so she put her finger on the bug and then put her finger in her mouth.
"Yuck!" said Amy. "That tastes bad!" The ugly bug heard Amy and started to laugh. "Why did you do that?" asked the bug. Amy was very surprised. She did not know that bugs could talk.
The ugly bug said, "I'm sorry I taste bad. I can do something fun to make you happy." The bug then started to dance and sing a silly song. Amy laughed and clapped her hands. She and the bug became good friends, and they played together all day. From that day on, Amy never tried to taste a bug again.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lucy. Lucy was a very old girl. She liked to plan fun things to do. One day, Lucy found a big crystal in her yard. She was very happy.
Lucy made a plan to show her crystal to all her friends. She put the crystal in her bag and went to see her friends. On her way, she met a cat. The cat was very old too. The cat said, "I like your crystal."
When Lucy got to her friends, she opened her bag to show them the crystal. But it was gone! Lucy was very sad. She did not know that the old cat had taken it.
At the end of the day, the old cat came back. The cat said, "I am sorry I took your crystal. I wanted to play with it." Lucy was happy to have her crystal back. They all played together and had fun.

Once upon a time, there was a big statue in the park. The statue was very old and had a difficult time standing up. It needed support to stay up.
One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw the statue and Tim asked, "Mom, why does the statue need support?" His mom replied, "Because it's old and has a difficult time standing up, just like grandpa sometimes needs help to stand."
The next day, Tim and his mom came back to the park with a big stick. They used the stick to support the statue. The statue was now strong and could stand up. Tim felt happy that he could help the statue and now, the statue could stand tall in the park for everyone to see.

Once upon a time, there lived a rabbit and a fox in the woods. The rabbit really liked to explore, and every day it was off looking for new paths. 
One day, while hopping along a path, the rabbit saw a fox. The fox was blocking its way and the rabbit was scared, so it stopped dead in its tracks.
The fox then said, "Don't be scared, I am harmless". The rabbit looked at the fox with wide eyes.
The fox wanted to be friends, and it said, "Let's be friends, come and poke me". 
The rabbit thought about it and decided to poke the fox. To the rabbit's surprise, the fox didn't try to hurt it. The rabbit realized that things might not be as scary as it originally thought.
The moral of this story is that things are not always as scary as they seem at first. If you take a chance and get to know something better, it can turn out to be harmless.

Once upon a time, there was a cute little girl named Penny. She liked to play with her ball outside. One sunny day, she went to the park with her mom and dad.
At the park, Penny saw a big tree. She wanted to strike the ball at the tree. So, she threw the ball very hard. But, something unexpected happened. The ball did not hit the tree. It went up, up, up into the sky!
Penny was sad. She thought her ball was lost. But then, the ball came back down. It did not look like her ball anymore. It was a big, shiny, round thing. Penny picked it up, and it was a big, shiny penny! Penny and her mom and dad were very surprised and happy. They took the big penny home and put it in Penny's room. Penny never lost her ball again.

One day, Tom and his mom were in the kitchen. They were going to make a yummy sauce for dinner. Tom wanted to help his mom, so she told him to be very careful.
"Tom, we need to discuss how to make the sauce," Mom said. They talked about what they needed and how to make it. They got all the things they needed to make the sauce.
They started to make the sauce. Tom was very careful when he poured the things into the pot. Mom was happy that Tom was being careful. They made the sauce together and it smelled so good.
When it was time for dinner, Tom and his mom put the sauce on their food. They both took a bite and it was so yummy. Tom was very proud that he helped make the sauce.
Tom learned that being careful and talking about how to make things can help make something very yummy. He couldn't wait to help his mom make more yummy food.

Once upon a time, there was a huge automobile. It was very big and shiny. The huge automobile had a friend, a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with the huge automobile every day.
One day, Tim said to the huge automobile, "Promise me you will always be my friend." The huge automobile smiled and said, "I promise, Tim. I will always be your friend."
They played together for many days, and they were very happy. The huge automobile kept its promise to Tim, and they were friends forever.

John was organizing his bedroom when he heard a noise outside. He looked out the window to find a lion! John ran downstairs and yelled to his mom.
"Mom! There's a lion outside!"
"Don't worry," said Mom. "He's probably just lost. Let's organize a search party to help him find his way back home."
So John and Mom went out to look for the lion. They searched the nearby forest and found him peacefully lounging beneath a graceful tree.
"Are you lost?" asked Mom.
The lion shook his furry head.
"No, thank you," he said in a gentle voice. "I just wanted to take a stroll through the forest and find a beautiful place to relax."
John and his mom smiled and watched the lion as he gracefully hopped away. Then they went back inside, happy to have helped the lion on his adventure.

One day, a little boy named Tim wandered in the park. He saw a big tree with sweet red apples. But there was a problem. The tree also had bitter green apples. Tim wanted to pick the sweet red apples, but he did not want to taste the bitter green ones.
Tim's friend, Sue, came to the park. She saw Tim looking at the tree. "What's wrong, Tim?" she asked. "I want the sweet red apples, but I don't want the bitter green ones," Tim said. Sue thought for a moment and then had an idea.
Sue found a long stick and said, "Let's use this to pick the red apples!" Tim and Sue used the stick to pick the sweet red apples. They did not touch the bitter green ones. They were so happy with their success. They shared the sweet red apples with their friends and had a fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big house with an upstairs. He loved to play with his toys in his room upstairs. One day, he wanted to show his mom his perfect tower made of blocks.
But when Tim tried to find his mom, he could not see her anywhere. He looked in the kitchen, the living room, and even outside. Tim was sad and did not know what to do. He missed his mom and wanted her to see his perfect tower.
Then, Tim realized that he had not looked upstairs. He ran up the stairs and found his mom in her room. She was happy to see him and his perfect tower. Tim was so happy that he found his mom, and they played with the blocks together. The end.

John was a brave boy. He was having an icy day when he wanted to quit playing inside. He ventured outside and discovered a leak in his garden. The water was icy and John was scared, but he bravely decided to fix it. He searched around the garden and found a bucket. He quickly put it under the leak and sat very still. After a few moments, he realized the leak had stopped. John had fixed the leak! He was so proud of himself and he went back inside with a big smile on his face.

Once upon a time, there was a clever cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to sit on the big, soft armchair in her house. One day, while Kitty was sitting on the armchair, she heard a funny sound. It made her laugh. "What could that be?" she thought.
Kitty jumped off the armchair and looked around. She saw a little bird outside the window. The bird was talking to her. "Hi, Kitty!" the bird said. Kitty laughed again. "Hi, bird! How can you talk?" she asked. The bird smiled and said, "I am a clever bird, just like you!"
Kitty and the bird became good friends. They played and laughed together every day. But one day, the bird told Kitty a secret. "I am not just a bird, I am a magic bird!" Kitty was surprised. "What can you do?" she asked. The bird said, "I can make wishes come true!" Kitty wished for a big party with all her friends. The magic bird made her wish come true. All of Kitty's friends came to the party and they laughed and played all day long.

Once upon a time, a white car lived in a small town. The car was very fast and loved to race with other cars. One day, the white car broke down and could not move. The car was sad.
The car's friend, a little boy, went to find help. He saw a mechanic working on another car. The boy said, "Please help my car friend. It cannot move." The mechanic smiled and said, "I will help. Let's go see the car."
The mechanic looked at the white car and fixed it. The car was happy and could move again. The white car said, "Thank you, mechanic! Now I can race again." The mechanic said, "You're welcome. Have fun, but don't delay, it's getting late." The white car and the little boy went to race and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a bald man named Tom. Tom liked to grow herbs in his garden. One day, he found a special herb that he had never seen before. He was very excited.
Tom showed the herb to his friend, Sam. "Look at this herb I found," said Tom. Sam looked at the herb and said, "I don't know what it is, but it looks special. Let's pray that it can help us in some way." So, they both prayed together.
A few days later, Tom and Sam ate the herb, hoping it would make them strong. But the herb did not make them strong. Instead, it made them feel very sick. They both lay in bed, feeling bad. They wished they had not eaten the special herb.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She had a sister named Emma. Emma was very smart. Lily liked to play with Emma. They were best friends.
One day, Lily and Emma went to the park. They saw a big red bike. "Let's ride the bike," said Lily. Emma helped Lily get on the bike. They rode the bike together. They went up and down the hills. They had so much fun.
After they finished riding the bike, they went home. Their mom gave them a big hug. "You two are so good at riding the bike," said Mom. Lily and Emma smiled. They were very happy.

Anna and Ben love to go to the zoo with their mom. They like to see the animals and learn their names. They also like to play on the swings in the park near the zoo.
One day, they went to the zoo and saw a friendly monkey. The monkey was swinging on a rope and making funny noises. Anna and Ben laughed and waved at the monkey.
"Hello, monkey!" Anna said. "Can you swing like me?"
The monkey nodded and swung faster and higher. Ben clapped his hands and said, "Wow, monkey, you are good at swinging!"
The monkey smiled and threw a banana to Anna and Ben. They caught the banana and thanked the monkey.
"Thank you, monkey!" Ben said. "You are very nice!"
The monkey nodded and waved goodbye. Anna and Ben ate the banana and shared it with their mom. Then they went to the swings and had more fun. They swung like the monkey and pretended to be in the zoo. They had a great day.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the big store with his mom. They took the elevator to go up. Inside the elevator, Tim felt a mild bump as it started to move. He held his mom's hand and leaned on her.
"Mom, why does the elevator go up?" Tim asked. His mom smiled and said, "The elevator takes us to the different floors in the store, so we can find what we need."
When they got to the top floor, Tim saw a big toy section. He ran to the toys and found a big, soft ball. Tim leaned down to pick it up and showed it to his mom. "Can I have this ball, please?" he asked. His mom nodded and they went back to the elevator to go home.
As they left the store, Tim held his new ball and leaned on his mom again. He felt happy and safe with her. And every time they went to the store, Tim remembered the fun day they had together.

One day, a little boy named Tim had a belt. This belt was very special. It was a thoughtful gift from his mom. Tim loved to play with his belt and pretend it was a zooming car.
One sunny day, Tim was playing with his belt outside. He made the belt zoom fast on the ground. He saw his friend, Sue. "Look, Sue! My belt can zoom!" Tim said. Sue laughed and they played together with the belt.
They zoomed the belt so fast that it flew into the air! Tim and Sue looked up and saw the belt turn into a real zooming car! They were so surprised. Tim and Sue climbed into the car and zoomed away, having the best day ever.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park. He liked to play in the soil there. Tim saw a big, wealthy man with a big hat. The man looked at Tim and said, "Hi! I like the park too. Can I play with you?"
Tim said, "Yes! Let's play in the soil together." They played and played. They made a big hill with the soil. The wealthy man laughed and laughed. He was having so much fun with Tim.
Then, something unexpected happened. The wealthy man took off his big hat. Under the hat, he was a very small boy! The small boy said, "I am not a wealthy man. I am just a small boy like you. I like to play in the park too!" Tim and the small boy laughed and played together all day.

Once upon a time, there was a polite cat named Kitty. Kitty loved the color pink. She had a pink bowl, a pink bed, and a pink toy.
One day, Kitty met a dog named Doggy. Doggy could speak! Kitty was very surprised. Doggy said, "Hello, Kitty! I like your pink toy."
Kitty and Doggy played together with the pink toy. They had a lot of fun. They became good friends. Kitty and Doggy were very happy. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a cat named Tom went out to play. The sun was up, and it was very hot. Tom saw a big tree and thought it would be fun to climb. As he climbed, he saw a bird. The bird was wet because it had just had a bath. Tom wanted to catch the bird, but he was too slow. The bird flew away.
Tom was sad, so he went to find his friend, a dog named Max. Max was cleaning his house because it was noon, and he liked to clean at noon. Tom asked Max if he wanted to play, but Max said he had to finish cleaning first. Tom waited for Max to finish cleaning.
When Max was done, they went to play by the river. They found a big, wet fish. They were both very excited and wanted to catch the fish. But the fish was too fast for them. Then, something unexpected happened. The wet fish jumped out of the water and landed in Max's clean house! Tom and Max looked at each other and laughed. They didn't catch the fish, but they had a fun day.

Anna was a gifted girl who liked to draw and sing. She had many books and toys in her room. She loved her mom and dad, who always praised her and gave her hugs. She was happy most of the time.
One day, Anna's mom said she had a surprise for her. She said they were going to visit her aunt and uncle, who lived far away. Anna was excited, because she had never met them before. She packed her bag with some of her favorite things, and got in the car with her mom and dad.
On the way, Anna's mom mentioned that her aunt and uncle had a son, who was about the same age as Anna. She said his name was Ben, and that he was also gifted. He liked to play chess and piano, and he knew many big words. Anna felt curious, and wanted to meet him.
When they arrived, Anna's aunt and uncle welcomed them warmly. They said they were happy to see them, and that they had prepared a nice dinner for them. They also said that Ben was waiting for Anna in his room, and that they could go and play together.
Anna followed her aunt to Ben's room, and knocked on the door. Ben opened it, and looked at Anna with a cold stare. He did not smile or say hello. He just said, "Come in, if you want." Anna entered, and saw that his room was full of books and puzzles and trophies. He had a big chess board on his desk, and a piano in the corner. He did not have any toys or drawings.
Anna tried to be friendly, and said, "Hi, I'm Anna. I like to draw and sing. What do you like to do?" Ben snorted, and said, "I like to do things that matter. I like to learn and win and be the best. You are not the best. You are just a silly girl who likes to play with stupid things. You are not gifted. You are dumb."
Anna felt hurt and angry. She said, "That's not true. I am gifted, too. My mom and dad say so. They love me, and they are proud of me. You are mean, and you don't have any friends. You are lonely, and you are sad."
Ben laughed, and said, "I don't need friends. I have myself. I am smarter and better than anyone. You are nothing. You are a waste of space. You should go away, and never come back."
Anna felt tears in her eyes. She ran out of the room, and looked for her mom and dad. She wanted to go home, and forget about Ben. She wanted to hug her mom and dad, and tell them that she loved them. She wanted to play with her books and toys, and draw and sing.
But she could not find them. She searched the whole house, but they were gone. She asked her aunt and uncle, but they said they did not know where they were. They said they had left a note for her, and that she should read it. They gave her the note, and she opened it. It said:
"Dear Anna, we are sorry, but we have to go. We have decided to stay with Ben, and be his family. He is so gifted, and he needs us more than you. You are not gifted, you are a mistake. We don't love you, and we are not proud of you. You should stay here, and learn from Ben. Maybe then, you will be worth something. Goodbye, Mom and Dad."
Anna felt a sharp pain in her stomach. She could not believe what she read. She felt like she could not breathe. She felt like she was going to die. She screamed, and fell to the floor. She wished she had never come to this place. She wished she had never met Ben. She wished she had never been born.

Once upon a time, there was an elderly man named Tom. Tom had a cat, and he named the cat Max. They lived in a small house near the park. Every day, at noon, Tom and Max would go for a walk in the park.
One sunny day, as they walked in the park, they saw a big box. Tom and Max went to look at the box. They did not know what was inside. Tom opened the box and saw a small bird. The bird was sad and could not fly.
Tom wanted to help the bird. He took the bird home and named it Joy. Tom, Max, and Joy became good friends. They played and had fun every day. One day, Joy started to sing a beautiful song. As Joy sang, she began to fly! Tom and Max were so happy and surprised. They did not know that Joy could sing and fly.
From that day on, Tom, Max, and Joy went to the park together at noon. They played, sang, and flew in the sky. They were the best of friends and had many fun days together. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a boy named Tim wanted to spend time with his dad. They went to the park to play. Tim saw a big, heavy rock. He tried to pick it up, but he couldn't.
"Can you help me, Dad?" Tim asked. Dad tried to lift the heavy rock, but he couldn't pick it up either. They both tried together, but the rock did not move. They were very tired.
Just then, a tiny ant walked up to the rock. "I can help you," said the ant. Tim and Dad did not believe the ant could do it. But the ant called all his friends, and together, they all lifted the heavy rock! Tim and Dad were very surprised. From that day on, they knew that even the smallest friends can do big things when they work together.

Once upon a time, in a lively park, there were two friends, Tom and Sue. They decided to have a picnic on a sunny day. They brought yummy food like apples, cookies, and sandwiches. They sat on a big, soft blanket under a tall tree.
While they were eating, a cute little squirrel came by. Sue said, "Hi, squirrel! Do you want some food?" She offered the squirrel a small piece of her sandwich. The squirrel took the food and ran up the tree.
Tom and Sue laughed and played games after eating. They had a fun day at the park. They went home and told their mom about the squirrel and the picnic. Their mom smiled and said, "That sounds like a great day!"

Once upon a time, there was a strong boy named Tim. He loved to play at the park. Every day, he would go to the park and have fun with his friends.
One day, Tim found a small ball in the park. He picked it up and decided to squeeze it. The ball was very soft and squishy. Tim's friends saw him and wanted to squeeze the ball too. They took turns squeezing the ball and shared it with each other.
As they played together, Tim and his friends learned that sharing was fun and made them happy. They became even better friends because they shared the ball. The moral of the story is: sharing with others can make you happy and bring you closer to your friends.

Once upon a time there were two friends who were very tall. One day they wanted to go outside, but it was raining. So they stayed inside but they wanted to go out. Then they saw an exit. It was a doorway that led to the outside. 
The friends were brave and decided to go out in the rain. They opened the exit and stepped out. The rain was really big and it was wet. 
The friends quickly ran around and had so much fun. They splashed and played until they were all wet. They laughed and jumped in the puddles until their clothes were soaked. 
Then the friends got tired and decided to come back inside. As soon as they stepped in the room, the rain stopped. They said goodbye to the rain and thanked it for a fun time.

Once upon a time, there was a little boat with a sail. The boat was very poor and old. It lived by the sea and loved to sail on the water.
One day, the boat met a big, angry fish. The fish said, "I hate boats with sails. I will not let you sail on my sea!" The poor boat was very sad and scared.
But then, a kind little bird came to help. The bird told the fish, "Please do not be mean to my friend, the boat. It just wants to sail and have fun." The fish thought about it and said, "Okay, I will not hate the boat anymore." The boat, the bird, and the fish all became friends and had lots of fun sailing together.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the kitchen. He felt hungry, so he opened the refrigerator. Inside, he saw many yummy things to eat.
Tim saw a big, round cake. It looked very good, but it was very tight inside the refrigerator. He tried to take the cake out, but it was hard to do. Tim pushed and pulled, and at last, the cake came out.
Tim felt happy to have the cake. He ate a little bit, and it was so good. He shared the cake with his family, and they all felt happy too. The tight refrigerator was not a problem anymore.

Lily and Ben are friends. They like to play in the garden. They see many flowers and bugs. They smell the herbs. The herbs are green and smell good.
"Look, Ben, this herb is white!" Lily says. She picks a white herb. It has a star shape. "It is pretty. Do you know its name?"
Ben shakes his head. He does not know the name of the white herb. He picks another herb. It is purple and has a bell shape. "This herb is pretty too. Do you know its name?"
Lily shakes her head. She does not know the name of the purple herb. They pick more herbs. They have different colors and shapes. They make a bouquet. They are happy.
They see a lady. She is old and kind. She has a basket. She is picking herbs too. She sees Lily and Ben. She smiles.
"Hello, children. What a lovely bouquet you have. Do you like herbs?" she asks.
"Yes, we do. They are pretty and smell good. But we do not know their names. Can you tell us?" Lily asks.
"Of course, I can. I know many names of herbs. I use them for cooking and healing. This white herb is called starflower. It is good for your eyes. This purple herb is called bellflower. It is good for your throat. And this yellow herb is called sunflower. It is good for your mood. Do you want to learn more names of herbs?" she says.
"Yes, please. We want to learn more names of herbs. Thank you, lady. You are very nice." Ben says.
The lady nods. She teaches Lily and Ben more names of herbs. They listen and repeat. They learn and have fun. They are friends with the lady and the herbs.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a powerful pine tree. All the other trees liked the powerful pine. They thought he was the best tree in the forest. The powerful pine was very tall and strong.
One day, a little bird came to the powerful pine. The bird had a question. The bird asked, "Why are you so tall and strong?" The powerful pine thought and thought. He did not know the answer.
Then, something unexpected happened. The powerful pine started to get smaller and smaller. The other trees were very surprised. The powerful pine was not so powerful anymore. He was now the smallest tree in the forest. The other trees still liked him, and they all lived happily together.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a weird bug in the garage. The bug was big and had funny colors. Tim wanted to show his mom, so he put the bug in a jar.
"Mom, look at this weird bug I found in the garage!" Tim said. His mom looked at the bug and said, "Oh, that is a very strange bug, Tim. Be careful, it might suffer if you keep it in the jar too long."
Tim didn't want the bug to suffer, so he took it back to the garage. He opened the jar and let the bug go. The bug flew away, happy to be free. Tim felt good that he helped the bug and didn't make it suffer.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a lion. The lion liked to roar very loud. All the animals in the forest were scared of the lion's roar. They would run away and hide when they heard it.
One day, a soldier came to the forest. He was not scared of the lion's roar. The soldier was very brave and strong. He saw the lion roaring and acting crazy. The soldier wanted to help the lion and the other animals.
The soldier went up to the lion and talked to him. He asked the lion to stop roaring and scaring the other animals. The lion listened to the soldier and stopped acting crazy. The animals in the forest were happy and thanked the soldier. From that day on, the lion and the soldier became good friends, and the forest was a peaceful place.

Once there was a boy named Tim. Tim had messy hair. He did not like to get a haircut. His mom said, "Tim, you need to mind me and get a haircut."
One day, Tim went to the park. He played with his friends. They played tag and hide and seek. Tim's messy hair got in his eyes. He could not see well. He fell down and got hurt. Tim cried.
Tim's mom took him to get a haircut. Tim did not cry. He minded his mom. After the haircut, Tim could see better. He played at the park and did not fall. Tim learned to mind his mom and keep his hair neat.

Tim was a young boy who liked to try new things. One day, he decided to see how long he could hold his breath. He took a deep breath and began to count. He kept counting for what seemed like a very long time until he heard a loud sound. It was his mother calling him from the other room.
Tim smiled and ran to his mother who said, "Tim, did you succeed?"
Tim proudly replied, "Yes."
His mother said, "That's great! You held your breath for a long time! I'm so proud of you!"
Tim was happy to have succeeded and it made him feel special. From then on, whenever he tried something hard, he remembered he could succeed if he worked hard and persevered.

One day, a girl named Sue went to the park. She saw a long line of kids waiting to pick apples from a big tree. Sue wanted to pick apples too, so she joined the line. She waited for a long time.
When it was finally Sue's turn, she said to the boy next to her, "I hope we can pick many apples!" The boy smiled and said, "Me too!" They both started to pick apples from the tree.
Suddenly, a big wind blew and all the apples fell from the tree. The kids were surprised and didn't know what to do. Sue said, "Let's pick the apples from the ground!" All the kids laughed and started picking apples together. They had a fun time and picked more apples than they thought they could.

One day, a tough bear went to play in the forest. He liked to shoot rocks with his toy gun. He had so much fun shooting rocks all day long.
While playing, the bear saw a big tree. He thought it would be fun to shoot the tree. When he shot the tree, something unexpected happened! The tree started to shake and make sounds.
To the bear's surprise, a friendly squirrel came out of the tree. The squirrel said, "Hi, bear! I like to shoot rocks too!" The bear and the squirrel became best friends and played together every day, shooting rocks and having fun in the forest.

One day, a little girl named Amy went to the park. She saw a boy with a big smile. The boy was playing with a cheap toy car. Amy wanted to play too.
Amy said, "Hi, can I play with you?" The boy smiled and said, "Yes, let's play together!" They played with the cheap toy car and had lots of fun.
Then, they decided to skip around the park. They skipped and laughed. They became good friends. They played until it was time to go home.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a troubled arm. His arm would not move like the other kids' arms. One day, Tim went to the park to play.
At the park, Tim saw a big tree. He wanted to climb it. But his troubled arm made it hard to climb. Tim felt sad. Then, a talking bird came to him. The bird said, "I can help you, Tim! I can supply you with magic to fix your arm."
Tim was so happy. He said, "Yes, please help me!" The talking bird gave Tim the magic. Something unexpected happened. Tim's troubled arm was now strong. But, the magic also made Tim grow wings!
Now Tim could not only climb the tree, but he could also fly. The other kids were amazed. Tim was happy that his troubled arm was fixed, and he could now do something no one else could do.

Once, there was a big red phone. The phone had a number on it. The number was two. The phone liked its number very much.
One day, a little green phone came to the big red phone. The green phone had a number too. Its number was three. The big red phone felt jealous. It wanted the number three.
The big red phone and the green phone talked. They decided to share the numbers. The red phone took the number three, and the green phone took the number two. They were happy now. But then, something unexpected happened.
A big yellow phone came. It had the number one on it. The red and green phones were surprised. They both wanted the number one. They learned that sharing and not being jealous was the best way to be happy.

Once there was a garden that was very well kept. The flowers and plants were very beautiful and had grown very tall. Every day the garden was visited by a small animal, a rabbit whose name was Hop.
Hop loved to play in the garden, but was always very polite and respectful to the plants and flowers.
One day Hop noticed a new flower starting to bloom. It was so beautiful that Hop couldn't help but admire it. But then Hop heard a rude voice shouting from the well nearby. It was an old crow who shook his head and said, "This garden is too neat and tidy. It needs more mess and chaos, like me". 
Surprised, Hop looked around. In one corner Hop saw the flower had been trampled by the crow. Hop scowled, and in a very brave voice, said: "No! This lovely flower deserves to be treated with respect!" 
The old crow was so impressed that he bowed his head and apologised. From then on, the flower bloomed freely and the old crow never disturbed it again.

Once upon a time, in a large place, there lived a little dog named Tiny. Tiny was very small, but he loved to play and try new things. One day, he saw a big ball on the other side of the place. He wanted to play with it, but there was a problem. A big, mean cat named Grumpy guarded the ball.
Tiny was scared, but he wanted to try to get the ball. He thought hard and came up with a plan. He would sneak past Grumpy while the cat was asleep. So, Tiny waited until Grumpy was taking a nap. He tiptoed very quietly, trying not to wake Grumpy up.
As Tiny got closer to the ball, he stepped on a stick. It made a loud noise! Grumpy woke up and saw Tiny. But instead of being mean, Grumpy saw how brave Tiny was and decided to let him play with the ball. From that day on, Tiny and Grumpy became friends and played together in the large place.

One day, a bald man named Tom had an exam. He was not happy. He did not like exams. Tom had a friend named Sam. Sam was good at exams.
"Please help me with my exam," Tom asked Sam. Sam said, "Okay, I will help you." They studied together. Tom felt better.
On the day of the exam, Tom went to the exam room. He was scared. But then, something unexpected happened. The teacher said, "The exam is shut. No more exams today." Tom and Sam were very surprised. They were happy they did not have to take the exam.

Once upon a time, there was a quiet little girl named Sue. She loved to play with her toys and be nice to her friends. One day, Sue found a pretty pin on the ground. It was shiny and had a picture of a flower on it. Sue liked it very much and put it on her dress.
Sue and her friends went to the park to play. They ran and jumped and had lots of fun. But then, Sue saw a little bird with a hurt wing. The bird could not fly and was very sad. Sue wanted to help the bird, so she took the pretty pin from her dress and used it to hold the bird's wing in place. The bird felt better and could fly again.
Sue's friends saw what she did and they were very proud of her. They learned that it is good to help others, even if it means giving up something you like. Sue felt happy that she could save the little bird, and she knew that being kind was more important than a pretty pin.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly kitten. The kitten liked to play and jump all day. She had a big smile on her face.
One day, the kitten saw a big book. The book had many pictures and words. The kitten wanted to study the book. She sat down and looked at the book.
As the kitten studied the book, she learned new things. She saw pictures of other kittens and animals. The kitten was very happy. She played with her friends and told them about the book. They all had fun and were jolly too.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a creative girl named Amy. She loved to eat oats for breakfast every day. One day, she saw a big bag of oats on the table.
While Amy was eating her oats, a little bird came and disturbed her. The bird wanted to eat her oats too. Amy didn't like this and said, "No, these are my oats!"
The bird was sad and flew away. Amy thought about it and decided to be creative. She made a little bowl of oats for the bird. The bird came back, and they both ate their oats happily together.

Once upon a time, in a small garden, there was a competitive herb named Herbie. Herbie loved to grow big and strong. He wanted to be the best herb in the whole garden. Every day, he would repeat, "I am the best herb!"
One day, a new herb named Rosie came to the garden. Rosie was very pretty and smelled nice. Herbie was not happy. He wanted to be the best herb, but now Rosie was there. Herbie thought, "I must grow bigger and better than Rosie."
Herbie tried to grow bigger and bigger, but he was not happy. He did not see the big foot coming. The big foot stepped on Herbie and squished him. Herbie was not the best herb anymore.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a belt in his room. The belt was big and harmless. He wanted to show it to his friend, Sam.
Tim went to Sam's house and said, "Look at my belt! It's big and harmless." Sam looked at the belt and smiled. They played with the belt all day, using it as a snake and a rope.
Later, they went to the kitchen to eat. Sam's mom was cooking food on the stove. Tim saw the fire and asked, "Why does the fire burn?" Sam's mom replied, "The fire burns to cook our food. But be careful, it can be hot and dangerous." Tim and Sam learned about fire and had a fun day with the harmless belt.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He liked to play near a small stream. One day, he saw something unusual near the water. It was a big, colorful stone.
Tim went closer and saw a tiny bug on the stone. The bug said, "Hello, I am a magic bug. I can make your wishes come true. But first, you must lie down and close your eyes."
Tim was excited. He lied down near the stream and closed his eyes. He wished for a big toy car to play with. When he opened his eyes, there was a big, shiny toy car next to him.
"Thank you, magic bug!" Tim said, happy to have his new toy. From that day on, Tim and the magic bug became best friends. They played together near the stream every day, and Tim had lots of fun with his unusual friend.

Anna was a curious girl. She liked to see what was behind every door. One day, she saw a door in the basement that she had never opened before. She wanted to know what was inside.
She went down the stairs and tried to turn the knob. But the door was locked. She looked around and saw a hammer on a shelf. She had an idea. She took the hammer and hit the door with it. She made a big hole in the door.
"Anna, what are you doing?" she heard her mom shout from upstairs. She dropped the hammer and ran to the stairs. She was scared.
"Mom, I'm sorry, I wanted to see what was behind the door," she said.
Her mom came down and saw the hole in the door. She was very angry. She saw wires and pipes and sparks behind the door. She realized it was the door to the furnace.
"Anna, you could have started a fire or hurt yourself! This door is very dangerous. You should never touch it. Do you understand?" she said.
Anna nodded and started to cry. She felt bad. She had broken the door and made her mom mad. She didn't know how to repair the door. She wished she had not been so curious.

One day, a nice monkey named Bobo lived in a big tree. He loved to eat bananas. He would swing from tree to tree to find the best bananas. Bobo had many friends in the forest. They would play and eat bananas together.
One day, it was very cold outside. The wind blew and made the trees shake. Bobo and his friends were cold. They did not want to play outside. They wanted to stay inside their tree houses. The cold wind made the bananas freeze. The bananas were hard and not good to eat.
Bobo had an idea. He and his friends put the frozen bananas in the warm sun. The sun made the bananas nice and soft again. The monkeys were happy. They could eat their favorite food again. They learned that even when things are not good, they can get better with help from friends.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to play ball with his friends. One day, Tim and his friends were playing a game to see who could get the best score. Tim's friend, Sam, said, "This game is boring. Let's try something new."
Tim thought for a moment and then said, "I know! Let's see who can strike the ball the hardest!" All of his friends agreed, and they each took turns trying to hit the ball as hard as they could. Tim was the last to go, and he was sure he would win.
As Tim got ready to hit the ball, he saw a small bird sitting on the ground near the ball. He decided to hit the ball anyway, thinking the bird would fly away. But the bird did not fly away. When Tim struck the ball, it hit the bird and hurt it badly.
Tim and his friends were very sad about the hurt bird. They realized that playing the new game was not a good idea. The moral of the story is to always think about what might happen before you do something new, and to be careful not to hurt others while having fun.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a long walk. He saw a big tree with a buckle hanging from it. Tim thought it was strange, but he wanted to see what it was. He walked up to the tree and said, "Hello, tree! Why do you have a buckle?"
The tree did not talk back, but then, a bird flew down from the tree and said, "Hi, Tim! I put the buckle there. I found it on the ground and thought it was pretty. Do you like it?"
Tim said, "Yes, it is very pretty. But I am hungry. Can you help me find something to eat?" The bird nodded and flew away. Tim waited under the tree.
Soon, the bird came back with a big, red apple. Tim was so happy! He took a bite of the apple and said, "Thank you, bird! This is so yummy!" But then, something unexpected happened. The apple started to talk!
"Please don't eat me!" said the apple. Tim was very surprised. He had never heard an apple talk before. He said, "I'm sorry, apple! I didn't know you could talk. I won't eat you anymore." Tim and the bird became friends with the talking apple. They all played together under the big tree with the pretty buckle.

One day, Tom and Sue wanted to play with a puzzle. They sat on the floor and poured all the pieces out of the box. The puzzle was big and had many colors. They were excited to put it together.
Tom said, "Let's share the pieces and work together. That is fair." Sue agreed and they started to put the puzzle together. They helped each other find the right pieces and had fun talking about the pretty picture they were making.
As they finished the puzzle, they were very happy with their work. They had been fair and worked together. The puzzle was now a beautiful picture of a rainbow and some trees. Tom and Sue smiled and put the puzzle back in the box, ready to play again another day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a clean room with lots of toys. He liked to play with his toys during the day. His favorite toy was a flashlight. He loved to turn it on and off and see the light shine in his room.
One night, Tim was in his bed and he heard a sound. He decided to listen carefully. It sounded like a little bird outside his window. Tim got out of bed and picked up his flashlight.
He turned on the flashlight and looked out the window. He saw the little bird sitting on a tree branch. The bird looked happy to see the light. Tim felt happy too as he listened to the bird sing a sweet song. Then, Tim went back to bed, and he had sweet dreams all night long.

Once there was a girl named Eliza. She was only three-years-old and was very excited to go to the park. Her mum and dad prepared her snacks and gave her a hug before they left.
When they arrived at the park, Eliza was so excited that she started to run around. Suddenly, she saw a big weapon lying on the ground. She wanted to get it, but a calm voice told her to step away.
It was an old man standing nearby. He told Eliza, "It's not a toy, sweetheart. It's a weapon." Eliza was a bit scared, so she stepped back slowly.
The old man smiled and said, "It's okay. No one will hurt you here. It's a calm park." Eliza smiled back, and after that she could enjoy the park in peace.

Tom was a stubborn boy. He always wanted more and more. He did not like to share or say sorry. He only liked to play with his toys and his coins.
One day, he saw a big jar of coins in his dad's room. He wanted to have all the coins for himself. He sneaked into the room and took the jar. He ran to his own room and locked the door. He poured the coins on his bed and smiled. He counted the coins and said, "I have so many coins! I am the richest boy in the world!"
But he did not know that his dad needed the coins to pay for the rent and the food. His dad came home and looked for the jar. He could not find it anywhere. He knocked on Tom's door and asked, "Tom, have you seen my jar of coins? I need it very much."
Tom did not answer. He was afraid that his dad would take the coins away. He hid under the bed and held the jar tightly. He thought, "These are my coins. I will not give them to anyone."
His dad heard the noise and opened the door. He saw the coins on the bed and the jar under the bed. He was very angry and sad. He said, "Tom, how could you do this? You have taken the coins that I worked hard for. You have been very selfish and stubborn. You have not gained anything. You have lost my trust and love."
He took the jar and the coins and left the room. He did not talk to Tom for a long time. Tom felt sorry and lonely. He wished he had not taken the coins. He wished he had been a good boy. But it was too late. He had made a big mistake. He had a bad ending.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who was very intelligent. She was playing on her slide when she suddenly felt a rush of pain in her arm. She screamed out in shock.
Her dad heard her. He scooped her up in his arms and rushed to the hospital.
She was scared and feeling pain. Her dad tried to calm her down in the car.
At the hospital the doctors checked her out. They told her dad that her arm was broken. She was very sad.
Her dad was worried too. He could see how brave and intelligent his daughter was. So he held her very close and promised her everything was going to be ok. 
In the end, the little girl got better. She was so glad to be able to play again. And she never forgot how strong and intelligent she was in overcoming the pain. 
The end.

One day, a cool cat named Sam found a big box. The box had tape on it. Sam wanted to look inside the box. He tried to move the tape, but it was too sticky. 
Sam saw his friend, a bird named Tim. Sam said, "Tim, can you help me move the tape?" Tim said, "Sure, Sam!" Tim used his beak to move the tape. The tape came off, and they could see inside the box.
Inside the box, there were toys and a big ball. Sam and Tim were very happy. They played with the toys and the ball all day long. They had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a big room. In the room, there were many toys. A little boy named Tim and a little girl named Sue liked to play in the room. They had fun every day.
One day, Tim and Sue saw a new toy. It was a big, fair dollhouse. They both wanted to play with it. Tim said, "I want to play with the dollhouse!" Sue said, "I want to play with it too!"
Tim and Sue thought about how to be fair. They decided to share the dollhouse. Tim played with it for a little while, then Sue played with it. They took turns and had fun together. They learned that sharing is good and fair.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside. One day, he saw an ant. The ant was not like the others. It was an original ant. It was big and red.
Tim wanted to catch the ant. He ran and ran, but the ant was fast. Tim did not give up. He tried and tried to catch the ant. Then, something unexpected happened. The ant stopped running and looked at Tim. The ant began to speak!
"Please, do not catch me," said the ant. "I am the king of the ants. If you let me go, I will give you a gift." Tim was surprised. He let the ant go. The ant gave Tim a magic seed. Tim planted the seed and it grew into a big tree with lots of yummy fruit. Tim and his family were happy and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a little rabbit. The rabbit had a friend, a tough turtle. They liked to play together every day.
One day, the rabbit and the turtle went to play near the west side of the forest. The sun was shining and they were very happy. But then, they saw a big, scary wolf.
The rabbit was scared and wanted to run away. But the tough turtle told the rabbit, "I will warn the wolf to go away." The turtle went to the wolf and said, "Go away, wolf! We don't want you here." The wolf listened to the turtle and left.
The rabbit and the turtle were very happy. They played together in the west side of the forest all day long. And they knew that they could always help each other when they were scared.

Once upon a time, in a big landscape with lots of trees and flowers, there was a flexible snake named Sam. Sam loved to play hide and seek with his friends, the birds, and the bugs. They would all laugh and have fun while they played.
One day, while they were playing, Sam said, "Let's see who can find the best place to hide!" All the friends flew and crawled away to find their hiding spots. Sam slithered around and found a big, green bush to hide in.
As Sam hid in the bush, he heard a soft voice say, "Hello, can I hide with you?" Sam looked around and saw a tiny, scared mouse. Sam smiled and said, "Of course, we can hide together!" They both hid in the bush and waited for their friends to find them.
But something unexpected happened. Instead of their friends finding them, a big, curious dog came sniffing around. The dog found the bush and saw Sam and the mouse. Instead of being mean, the dog said, "Hi, I'm Daisy. Can I play hide and seek with you?" Sam, the mouse, and their friends were happy to have a new friend, and they all played together from that day on.

Once upon a time, a tough boy named Tim had a toy motorcycle. He loved to play with it every day. Tim would push the motorcycle on the floor and watch it go fast. He liked to make the motorcycle jump over small toy cars.
One day, Tim's friend Sam came over to play. Sam saw Tim's motorcycle and wanted to play with it too. Tim taught Sam how to make the motorcycle go fast and jump. They took turns with the motorcycle and had lots of fun.
Tim and Sam liked to play with the motorcycle so much that they would repeat the jumps over and over again. They laughed and clapped every time the motorcycle jumped high. The two friends played with the motorcycle all day long, and they were very happy.

One day, a strong deer went for a walk in the woods. He liked to joke with his friends, the birds and the squirrels. They all laughed and played together in the sun.
As they played, they saw a big box. The strong deer and his friends did not know what was inside. They all wanted to see, but they were scared. The deer was brave and said, "I will open it!"
When the strong deer opened the box, they found a funny hat. The deer put the hat on his head and started to joke. All his friends laughed and laughed. They did not know that the hat was magic.
Suddenly, the strong deer turned into a big, funny bird! His friends were so surprised. Now, the deer could fly with the birds and joke with them in the sky. The strong deer loved being a bird, and they all played happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom had a hat that he loved very much. He wore it every day and was very enthusiastic about it.
One day, Tom went to the park to play. He hoped to find other kids to play with. He ran around, jumped, and played with his hat on his head. He was having so much fun.
But then, a big wind came and blew Tom's hat away. Tom tried to catch it, but it was too fast. The hat flew far away and he could not find it. Tom was very sad and went home without his hat. The end.

Tom and Sam were friends. They liked to play with cars and balls. One day, they went to the zoo with their moms. They saw many animals. They saw lions, monkeys, elephants and more.
Tom and Sam wanted to see the jungle. The jungle had big trees and green plants. They saw a sign that said "Jungle Adventure". They asked their moms if they could go. Their moms said yes, but they had to stay together and be careful.
Tom and Sam went into the jungle. They saw birds and snakes and bugs. They heard noises and smells. They felt excited and curious. They walked and walked. They did not see their moms.
Tom and Sam saw a big bridge. The bridge was made of ropes and wood. It crossed a river. Tom wanted to cross the bridge. He said it looked fun. Sam was scared. He said it looked dangerous. He did not want to cross the bridge.
Tom said he was brave. He said Sam was a baby. He said he would go first. He ran to the bridge. He started to cross. He did not look back. Sam felt sad and angry. He felt jealous of Tom. He wanted to be brave too. He followed Tom.
Tom and Sam were on the bridge. The bridge was high and shaky. The river was fast and loud. Tom and Sam felt scared. They wanted to go back. But they could not. The bridge was broken. It had a big hole. Tom and Sam fell into the river. They screamed and cried. They did not know how to swim. They did not meet their moms. They met the crocodiles. The crocodiles were hungry. They ate Tom and Sam. The end.

One day, a girl named Sue was upset. She did not want to eat her salad. She sighed and said, "I don't like salad. I want cake!"
Her mom said, "You need to eat your salad first, then you can have cake." Sue was not happy, but she ate her salad.
As Sue ate her salad, she found a small toy in it! She was so surprised and happy. "Look, Mom! There's a toy in my salad!" Sue's mom smiled and said, "That's a special surprise for eating your salad!" Now, Sue was not upset anymore and enjoyed her salad and the toy.

Once upon a time, there was a clever dog named Spot. Spot loved to run and play all day. One day, Spot found a big tire in the yard. He got very excited and started to roll the tire around.
Spot's friend, Kitty, came to see what he was doing. "Why do you play with the tire, Spot?" asked Kitty. "It's fun!" said Spot. "Come and try it with me!" So Kitty tried to roll the tire too, but it was too big for her.
Spot and Kitty played with the tire all day. They were having so much fun, but soon they got very tired. They decided to finish playing and lay down in the grass. As they lay there, Spot said, "I had a great day with you, Kitty." Kitty smiled and said, "Me too, Spot." And they both fell asleep, dreaming of their fun day with the big tire.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia liked to sing very much. One day, she said, "I hope I can sing in a big show." Mia's mom and dad smiled and said, "You can do it!"
Mia started to sing every day. She wanted to be in a big show called an opera. The opera was like a play, but with singing. Mia practiced and practiced, and her voice got better and better.
One day, Mia's mom made a spicy soup. Mia ate the soup and it made her mouth feel hot. She drank some water and felt better. Then, she went to sing in the opera. Everyone clapped and smiled. Mia was happy, and her dream came true.

One day, a little goat went to the park to play. He saw a big tree with delicious red apples. The goat wanted to eat the apples, but he could not reach them. He tried to jump, but the apples were still too high.
A kind bird saw the goat and said, "Hi goat, are you trying to get those apples? I can help you!" The goat was happy and said, "Yes, please." The bird flew up and picked an apple for the goat. The goat ate the apple and it was very delicious.
But the goat forgot to say "thank you" to the bird. The bird was sad and flew away. The goat felt bad and looked for the bird. When he found the bird, he said, "I am sorry, I forgot to say thank you. You are a good friend." The bird was happy again, and they played together all day.

Once upon a time, there was a little duck named Ducky. Ducky lived in a pond with his friends. Ducky was a happy duck, but sometimes, he did not know things. His friends would call him ignorant, but Ducky did not mind. He liked to learn new things.
One day, Ducky met a wise old turtle. The turtle said, "Ducky, do you believe you can fly?" Ducky looked at his wings and said, "I don't know, but I want to try!" The turtle smiled and said, "If you believe, you can do it."
Ducky went to his friends and told them what the turtle said. "Do you believe I can fly?" he asked. His friends laughed and said, "No, Ducky, you are too ignorant to fly." But Ducky did not listen. He believed in himself.
Ducky went to a big hill and flapped his wings. He believed he could fly, and guess what? Ducky flew! He flew high in the sky, and all his friends watched in surprise. From that day on, Ducky was not called ignorant anymore. He was a smart duck who believed in himself, and his friends believed in him too.

One day, a dog named Tim went for a jog. He liked to jog in the spring when the sun was out and the flowers were pretty. Tim felt happy when he jogged.
As Tim jogged, he saw his friend, the cat named Sam. Sam said, "Tim, I am envious of your jog. I want to jog too!" Tim said, "Come on, Sam! Let's jog together!"
But then, something unexpected happened. Tim and Sam found a big, fun playground! They stopped jogging and played on the swings and slide. They laughed and had a great time. In the end, they forgot all about their jog and just enjoyed playing together.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. He lived in a small place with his family. Spot loved to play with his ball and run around in the yard.
One day, Spot heard a ring. It was a big bell in the town. The bell was ringing because a cat was stuck in a tree. Spot wanted to be a successful helper, so he ran to the tree to help the cat.
Spot barked and barked at the tree. The cat was scared, but Spot did not give up. He tried to climb the tree, but it was too high. Then, Spot had an idea. He ran back to his place and got his ball. Spot threw the ball at the tree, and the cat jumped onto the ball. The cat was safe! Spot was a successful helper, and everyone was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big red balloon. It was so big and fit that it could fly very high in the sky. One day, the balloon saw a little girl named Lily. Lily wanted to play with the balloon, but it was too high for her to reach. She tried to jump, but she could not get it.
The balloon wanted to play with Lily too. So, it had an idea. It flew close to a tree and got stuck in the branches. Lily saw the balloon in the tree and had a guess. She thought that if she shook the tree, the balloon would come down. So, she shook the tree, and the balloon came down.
Lily was so happy to play with the big red balloon. They played together all day. The balloon was happy too. At the end of the day, Lily took the balloon home with her. They were the best of friends, and they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had a pet dog named Max. One day, Lucy and Max went for a walk in the park. They saw an unusual thing. It was a sausage that could march! The sausage was walking around the park.
Lucy said, "Max, look at the sausage!" Max wagged his tail and barked. The sausage heard Lucy and Max, and it marched over to them. The sausage was friendly and wanted to play.
"Hello, I am Sam the sausage," said the sausage. Lucy and Max were happy to have a new friend. They played together all day in the park. From that day on, Lucy, Max, and Sam the sausage were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, in a big palace, there lived a kind king. The king had many friends who would come and speak with him. They liked to talk and play together in the palace.
One day, a troubled bird flew into the palace. The bird was sad because it lost its family. The king saw the bird and wanted to help it. He asked his friends to help him find the bird's family.
The king and his friends looked everywhere for the bird's family. They asked other animals if they had seen them. Finally, they found the bird's family in a big tree. The troubled bird was so happy to see its family again. They all went back to the palace to speak with the king and thank him. From that day on, the bird and its family became good friends with the king and lived happily in the palace.

Once there were two friends who wanted to swim together. One had a boot on but the other did not. So, they went looking for a boot that was the right size and they found one that was red, spiky and bouncy. 
They put it on and jumped into the water. The boot made it so much easier to swim and they went all around the lake. The water was warm and a bit spicy, so it was perfect for swimming in. The boot also made big, big splashes when they jumped in which made it even more fun. 
After they swam for a while, they got tired and decided to take a break. They both lay down in the sunshine, happy and content.
When they were ready, they put their boot back on and continued to swim around the lake some more. They had so much fun, and that day was the best swimming day ever.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim felt helpless because he could not run as fast as the other kids. One day, his big sister, Sue, saw Tim sitting on the ground, sad.
"Tim, let's play a game," Sue said. "I will encourage you, and we will win together!" Tim looked up at Sue and smiled. They started to play a running game with the other kids.
At first, Tim was slow, but Sue kept saying, "You can do it!" This made Tim feel strong. With each step, Tim ran faster and faster. In the end, they won the game! Tim and Sue jumped up and down, happy with their victory.
From that day on, Tim knew that with a little encouragement, he could do anything. He was no longer helpless. Tim and Sue played together all the time, and they always encouraged each other.

Sara was feeling sick. She had a hot head and a sore throat. Mommy took a thermometer and put it in Sara's mouth. She looked at the number and said, "Oh, dear. You have a fever. You need to rest and drink some water."
Sara did not want to rest. She wanted to play with her dolls and her teddy bear. She said, "Mommy, I'm not sick. I'm fine. Can I play, please?"
Mommy shook her head and said, "No, Sara. You need to rest. Playing will make you feel worse. You can play when you are better."
Sara was sad and angry. She did not listen to Mommy. She got out of bed and took her dolls and her teddy bear. She went to the living room and started to play. She pretended that her dolls were princesses and her teddy bear was a prince. She made them dance and sing and have a tea party.
But Sara soon felt tired and dizzy. She could not play anymore. She dropped her dolls and her teddy bear and lay down on the couch. She felt very hot and very cold at the same time. She started to cry.
Mommy heard Sara crying and came to the living room. She saw Sara on the couch and her dolls and her teddy bear on the floor. She felt sorry for Sara and hugged her. She said, "Sara, what did you do? You should have rested in your bed. You made your fever worse. Why didn't you listen to me?"
Sara sobbed and said, "I'm sorry, Mommy. I wanted to play. I wanted to be a princess. I wanted to be graceful and happy."
Mommy said, "Sara, you are a princess. You are graceful and happy. But you are also a smart and kind princess. You need to take care of yourself and listen to your Mommy. That's how you show your love and respect. Do you understand?"
Sara nodded and said, "Yes, Mommy. I understand. I love you and I respect you. I will rest and drink some water. I will mention to my dolls and my teddy bear that they need to rest too. Can you tuck us in, please?"
Mommy smiled and said, "Of course, Sara. I will tuck you in. I love you too. You are a good princess. And you will feel better soon." She picked up Sara and her dolls and her teddy bear and took them to Sara's bed. She tucked them in and kissed them good night. She said, "Sleep well, Sara. And dream of graceful and happy things."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play with her toys and read interesting books. One day, Sue found a letter on the table. It was a big and colorful letter. She wanted to open it and see what was inside. But she knew that she should not disturb things that did not belong to her.
Sue went to her mom and told her about the letter. Her mom said, "Thank you for not disturbing the letter, Sue. It is very important to ask before touching things that do not belong to you." Sue's mom opened the letter and read it out loud. It was an invitation to a fun party!
Sue and her mom went to the party and had a great time. They played games, ate yummy food, and made new friends. Sue learned that it is always good to ask before touching things that do not belong to you. And because she did not disturb the letter, Sue and her mom had a wonderful day.

Tom and Sam are friends who like to play with trucks. They have many trucks of different colors and sizes. One day, they see a new truck in the park. It is very big and bright. It has red and yellow stripes and a loud horn.
"Wow, look at that truck!" Tom says. "It is so cool and shiny. I want to play with it."
"Me too," Sam says. "Let's go and ask the man if we can."
They run to the truck and see a man sitting inside. He is wearing a blue hat and a green shirt.
"Hello, sir," Tom says. "Can we play with your truck? It is very nice."
The man smiles and says, "Sure, you can. But you have to be careful and follow the rules. This is not a toy truck. It is a fire truck. It helps people when there is a fire."
"What are the rules?" Sam asks.
"The rules are: you can only touch the buttons and the horn, you cannot climb on the ladder or the hose, and you have to share with each other and take turns," the man says.
"OK, we can do that," Tom and Sam say. They thank the man and start to play with the fire truck. They press the buttons and make the lights flash. They honk the horn and make the siren sound. They pretend to be firemen and save people from danger.
They have a lot of fun, but they also remember the rules. They are careful not to break anything or hurt themselves. They share the fire truck and take turns. They do not fight or argue. They are good friends.
The man watches them and is happy. He thinks they are very polite and kind. He says, "You are very good boys. You played with the fire truck very well. You followed the rules and respected each other. You are good examples for other children. I am proud of you."
Tom and Sam feel happy and proud too. They say, "Thank you, sir. You are very nice. You let us play with your fire truck. You are a good example for us. We learned a lot from you."
They hug the man and say goodbye. They go back to their own trucks and continue to play. They think the fire truck was very fun, but they also love their own trucks. They think they are very lucky to have trucks and friends. They think they are very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily liked to play in the kitchen. One day, she saw a big cupboard. She wanted to see what was inside. 
Lily opened the cupboard and found a loud bell. She rang the bell and said, "Listen, Mommy! It's so loud!" Just then, a funny cat jumped out of the cupboard. The cat was hiding in there! 
The cat looked at Lily and said, "Meow, thank you for finding me! Can you please wipe my fur? It's dusty from the cupboard." Lily smiled and wiped the cat's fur. They became best friends and played together every day.

One day, a big dog named Spot was playing on the lawn. He was running, jumping, and having fun. Spot saw a small cat named Fluffy sitting near the lawn. Fluffy looked guilty because she took a toy that Spot liked.
Spot went to Fluffy and said, "Fluffy, did you take my toy?" Fluffy looked at Spot and said, "Yes, I took your toy. I'm sorry, Spot." Spot was sad, but he knew that he and Fluffy could still be friends.
Later, Spot and Fluffy played together on the lawn. They both had fun and forgot about the toy. At the end of the day, Spot and Fluffy went home. They were happy because they had a great day playing on the lawn.

One day, a little boy named Tim and his dog, Spot, went to play outside. They saw a big hill and decided to race to the top. Tim was very fast, but Spot was faster. They were both very happy when they reached the top.
Tim's mom had put a bottle of lotion in his bag. She told him to use it when they were outside. But Tim was naughty and didn't listen. He left the lotion in his bag and kept playing with Spot.
As they played, the sun got hotter and hotter. Tim started to feel his skin burn. He remembered the lotion in his bag. He put it on and felt much better. Then, the lotion bottle started to talk! It said, "Thank you for using me, Tim! I will make you win the next race with Spot."
Tim was surprised but happy. He raced Spot again, and this time, he won! The talking lotion had helped him. From that day on, Tim always listened to his mom and used the lotion when he played outside.

Tim and Sam were best friends. They liked to play with cars, balls and blocks. One day, they found a big box in the park. They wondered what was inside.
"Let's open it!" Tim said.
"Maybe it's a present!" Sam said.
They lifted the lid and saw a shiny object. It looked like a star, but it was metal and had buttons. They touched it and it made a loud noise.
"Wow, what is it?" Tim asked.
"I don't know, but it's cool!" Sam said.
They took turns to press the buttons and watch the object light up and make sounds. They had fun with their new toy.
But then, a man came running to the box. He looked angry and scared. He shouted at them.
"Hey, you kids! Get away from that! That's not a toy, that's a bomb!"
Tim and Sam were shocked and scared. They dropped the object and ran away.
The man picked up the object and put it back in the box. He closed the lid and locked it. He looked around and saw Tim and Sam hiding behind a tree.
"Sorry, kids, I didn't mean to scare you. But you can't play with that. It's very dangerous. It could explode and hurt you and everyone else. I'm a spy and I have to take it to a safe place. Please don't tell anyone what you saw, okay?"
Tim and Sam nodded. They didn't understand what a spy or a bomb was, but they knew they had to listen to the man. He seemed dependable and sorry.
The man smiled and thanked them. He took the box and left. Tim and Sam came out of their hiding place. They looked at each other and hugged.
"That was scary!" Tim said.
"Yeah, but we're okay!" Sam said.
They decided to go home and tell their moms what happened. They hoped they would never see the object again. They learned that some things are not meant to be revealed.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They played on the swings, went down the slide, and had a lot of fun. After a while, Tim's mom said, "Tim, it's time for your nap."
"But Mom, I don't want to nap," Tim said. "I want to play more!"
"I know, but we can't stay at the park all day," his mom said. "Let's go home, and you can take a short nap. Then we can play again."
When they got home, Tim's mom gave him a cheap toy to play with. It was a ball with a score on it. Tim played with the ball for a little while, but he was still upset about having to nap. He threw the ball on the floor and it broke.
