"Oh no, Tim," his mom said. "Now your toy is broken. You should have been more careful."
Tim was sad that his toy was broken, but he knew his mom was right. He should have been more careful. He went to take his nap, and when he woke up, his mom had a new toy for him. This time, Tim was very careful with his toy and played nicely.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a unique cat named Tim. Tim liked to go outside and play. One day, he saw a bottle with a word he did not know. It said "poison."
"Mom, what is poison?" asked Tim. His mom said, "Poison is bad. It can make you sick. Do not touch it."
Tim wanted to be good. He did not want to be sick. So, he went far away from the poison. He saw his friend, a big dog named Sam. "Sam, do you know about poison?" Tim asked.
"Yes, I do," said Sam. "We should close the bottle and tell our moms."
So, Tim and Sam closed the bottle and told their moms. They were safe and happy. They played together all day long.

One day, a boy and his mom went to a museum. The boy was very excited to see all the things inside. His mom told him to keep calm and hold her hand.
As they went in, they saw a big dinosaur. The boy was so happy, he jumped up and down. His mom told him to keep calm and not touch anything. The boy tried to listen, but it was hard.
Then, the boy saw a big, shiny rock. He wanted to touch it, so he let go of his mom's hand. He touched the rock and it fell on the floor. The boy was sad and scared. The museum people were mad. The boy and his mom had to leave the museum and never come back.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a nice big tree and wanted to climb it. As he climbed, he saw something shiny on the ground. "I wonder what that is," Tim thought. He climbed down the tree to see what he found.
When he got to the ground, Tim discovered a small cage. The cage was empty, but it had a little door that could open and close. Tim thought, "Maybe I can find something to put in the cage." He looked around and saw a little bird. "Would you like to come in my cage?" Tim asked the bird.
The bird looked at the cage and then at Tim. "No, thank you," said the bird. "I like to fly free in the sky." Tim thought about it and realized that the bird was right. So, he opened the cage door and let the bird fly away. As the bird flew away, it sang a happy song. Tim smiled and knew that he had done a nice thing by letting the bird be free.

Lily and Sam were playing in the snow. They made a big snowman with a hat and a scarf. They gave him a carrot nose and two rocks for eyes. They named him Frosty.
"Hello, Frosty!" Lily said. "Do you like the snow?"
"Yes, I do!" Sam said in a funny voice, pretending to be Frosty. "But I don't like the sun. It makes me feel hot and melt."
"Don't worry, Frosty," Lily said. "The sun will go away soon. It is almost noon. Then it will be dark and cold again."
They hugged Frosty and went inside for lunch. They ate soup and bread and drank hot chocolate. They told their mom about their snowman.
"That sounds fun," their mom said. "But be careful, Frosty might disappear if the sun stays out too long."
Lily and Sam looked out the window. The sun was still shining bright. They saw Frosty's hat and scarf on the ground. His carrot nose and rock eyes were gone. His snow body was smaller and smaller.
"Oh no!" Lily cried. "Frosty is disappearing!"
"Let's go save him!" Sam said.
They put on their coats and boots and ran outside. They tried to fix Frosty with more snow. But the snow was too icy and hard. They couldn't make it stick.
"I'm sorry, Frosty," Lily said. "We tried to help you."
"Goodbye, Frosty," Sam said. "We will miss you."
They gave Frosty one last hug and went back inside. They felt sad and cold. They hoped Frosty would come back next winter.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly cat named Tom. Tom loved to play and have fun. One day, Tom went on a tour of the big park with his friends. They were all very happy and excited.
While on the tour, Tom saw a big tree. He wanted to peek at what was behind it. As he peeked, he saw a big, shiny ball. Tom was so happy, and he called his friends to see the ball. They all wanted to play with it.
But when they touched the ball, something unexpected happened. The ball turned into a big, yummy cake! Tom and his friends were so surprised. They laughed and enjoyed the cake together. It was the best tour they ever had.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a special toy that she loved very much. It was a cute, intelligent teddy bear named Mr. Hugs. Lily would always say, "Mr. Hugs is mine."
One day, Lily lost Mr. Hugs. She looked everywhere but could not find him. She felt very sad and missed her bear. Then, she saw her dog, Spot, playing with Mr. Hugs in the yard. Lily ran to Spot and said, "Mr. Hugs is mine!"
Lily picked up Mr. Hugs and gave him a big hug. She was so happy to have her intelligent bear back. From that day on, Lily and Mr. Hugs were always together, and they hugged each other every day.

Once upon a time, in a pretty nation, there lived a kind queen. She loved to talk to her people and answer their questions. Every day, she would sit on her big chair and listen to what they had to say.
One day, a little girl came to see the queen. She was very shy, but she had a question for the queen. The queen smiled and asked her what she wanted to know. The little girl wanted to know how the flowers in the garden grew so pretty.
The queen thought for a moment and then gave her answer. She said that the sun, the rain, and the love from the people helped the flowers grow pretty. The little girl was happy with the queen's answer and went back to her home. She told her friends and family about the pretty flowers and the kind queen who answered her question.
From that day on, the little girl and her friends took care of the flowers in the garden. They gave them lots of love, and the flowers grew even more pretty. The queen was very proud of her people, and they all lived happily in their pretty nation.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very enthusiastic about a big event happening in his town. It was a fun fair with lots of games and yummy treats. Tim could not wait to go to the fair.
On the day of the event, Tim's mom said, "Tim, don't forget to take your hat and put on sun cream. It's very sunny outside." Tim listened to his mom and put on his hat and sun cream. Then, he went to the fair with his friends.
At the fair, Tim played games and ate ice cream. He had so much fun that he forgot to go home. When the sun went down, Tim's mom came to find him. She said, "Tim, you forgot to come home!" Tim was sorry and hugged his mom. They went home together, and Tim promised to never forget again.

One day, a nice bee went to find food. It saw a big flower and said, "I will get food from you!" The flower did not yield food. The bee was sad.
The bee saw a boy. The boy said, "Do not be sad, bee. I will help you find food." The bee was happy. They went to find food together.
They found a big tree with lots of flowers. The bee said, "Thank you, boy!" The bee went to the flowers. But the flowers were not real. They were fake! The bee was sad and could not find food. The boy was sad too. They did not find food that day.

Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Tim. Tim liked to play in the garden. One day, Tim went to the garden to find his toy car.
In the garden, Tim met a young girl named Sue. Sue was looking for her doll. Tim said, "I will help you find your doll." Sue smiled and said, "Thank you, Tim."
They looked and looked. Soon, they found the doll and the toy car. Tim and Sue were very happy. They played together in the garden every day. And they became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a thoughtful girl named Lily. She loved to watch the sky. One day, she saw a big lightning in the air. It was so big and bright. Lily thought it was pretty.
The lightning saw Lily too. It liked how kind and thoughtful she was. The lightning wanted to be friends with her. So, it came down from the sky to meet her. They played together and had fun.
After some time, the lightning and Lily became best friends. They decided to marry and be together forever. From that day on, Lily and the lightning lived happily ever after, lighting up the sky with their love.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. The sun was shining, and the grass was wet from the rain that fell last night. Tim saw a big, metal slide and ran to play on it.
As Tim climbed up the slide, he heard a small voice yell, "Wait!" He looked down and saw a tiny frog. The frog said, "I need help! My home is wet and I need a new one." Tim wanted to help the frog, so he took it with him.
Tim and the frog looked for a new home. They found a big, dry rock under a tree. The frog said, "Thank you, Tim! This is perfect!" Tim felt happy that he helped the frog. He went back to the slide and played all day, knowing he had made a new friend.

Sandra was walking along the path when she saw a small brick lying on the ground. She wanted to pick the brick up but it was too heavy.
Suddenly, a kind voice said: "Can I help you?"
She looked up to see an old man with a compassionate smile. He said, "I know you can't pick it up but I can."
He carefully picked the brick up and said, "Tell me why you wanted this brick?"
Sandra told him, "I want to make a house for my toys. I need lots of bricks to make it strong."
The old man smiled and said, "Let's find more bricks so you can make the strongest house in the world."
So Sandra and the old man went on a hunt for bricks. Together, they found enough bricks for her to build her wonderful house. And Sandra thanked the old man for his kindness and compassion.

Once there was a girl named Molly. She liked to make new things on her own. Today, she wanted to make something special.
Molly went to the kitchen and took out some pepper and flour. She was very impatient and she wanted to mix the ingredients together quickly.
So, Molly grabbed the spoon and started to twist the ingredients together. She felt happy when she saw that the mixture was smooth.
Molly took a spoonful of the mixture and tasted it. She was surprised when she felt the pepper in her mouth. It tasted like a special treat. 
Molly was very happy with her creation and she enjoyed it very much.

Once upon a time, there was a unique boy named Tom. Tom loved to paint. He painted all day long. His room was full of colors. Tom also loved music. He had a guitar that he played every day.
One day, Tom had an idea. He wanted to paint his guitar. He thought it would be fun. So, he got his paint and started to paint his guitar. He painted it with red, blue, and yellow. It looked very pretty.
Tom showed his guitar to his friends. They all liked it very much. They thought it was unique too. Tom was very happy. He played his painted guitar every day. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a big pool. In the pool, there were many fish. One fish was very graceful. Her name was Grace. Grace liked to swim and play with her friends.
One day, Grace and her friends wanted to play a game. They decided to play "guess". One fish would hide, and the others would try to guess where the fish was. It was Grace's turn to hide.
Grace swam fast and hid behind a rock. Her friends tried to guess where she was. They looked high and low. Finally, one of her friends found her. They all laughed and played more games in the pool. They had a lot of fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a vendor. The vendor was sad and gloomy. He sold toys to kids. The kids did not like his toys. The vendor wanted to make the kids happy.
One day, a little boy came to the vendor. The boy wanted a toy. The vendor gave him a toy. The toy was a stick that could snap. The boy was happy with his toy.
The boy played with the toy. He snapped the stick. The stick broke. The boy was sad. The vendor was sad too. The gloomy vendor could not make the kids happy. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She was an adorable girl who loved to play outside. One sunny day, she went to the park with her mom. In the park, she saw a well. The well was very old and had a bucket.
Lucy asked her mom, "Mom, can I look in the well?" Her mom said, "Yes, but be careful." Lucy looked into the well and saw her face in the water. She thought it was fun and started to laugh.
As Lucy played by the well, a little boy named Tim came over. He saw Lucy and said, "Hi, I love playing by the well too!" Lucy and Tim became friends and played together by the well all day. They had so much fun and couldn't wait to come back to the park again.

Once upon a time, there was an old cat named Tom. Tom liked to play with his crayon. He would draw on the ground with it. One day, Tom's crayon broke. He was very sad.
Tom tried to fix his crayon. He pushed the pieces together. But it was a struggle. The crayon was still broken. Tom did not know what to do.
Then, a little girl saw Tom. She saw that he was sad. She gave Tom a new crayon. Tom was happy. He drew a big heart on the ground to say thank you. And they played together all day.

Once upon a time, there was a big rhinoceros named Remy. Remy lived in a distant land with many trees and a big river. He loved to play in the mud all day long. 
One day, Remy met a little bird named Bella. Bella was very clean and pretty. She said, "Remy, you are very dirty from playing in the mud. Let me help you wash." Remy liked Bella, so he said, "Okay, Bella, let's wash together."
They went to the big river, and Bella helped Remy wash his big body. The water felt nice and cool on Remy's skin. After they were done, Remy looked clean and shiny. Remy and Bella became best friends, and they played and washed together every day.

Lily and Ben love to perform for their mom and dad. They like to sing songs, tell jokes, and make funny faces. Sometimes they use toys and costumes to make their show more fun.
One day, they decided to perform a shape show. They found some paper and scissors and cut out different shapes. They had circles, squares, triangles, and stars. They also found some glue and glitter and made their shapes shiny and colorful.
They put on their hats and capes and went to the living room. They said, "Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to our shape show! We will show you some amazing shapes and what they can do!" They held up their shapes and said their names. Then they made them into different things. They made a circle into a sun, a square into a house, a triangle into a mountain, and a star into a fairy.
Their mom and dad clapped and cheered. They said, "Wow, what a great shape show! You are very clever and creative!" They gave Lily and Ben hugs and kisses. They also gave them a yummy treat. It was a cookie with icing and sprinkles. It had the same shapes as their paper shapes. Lily and Ben smiled and said, "Thank you, mom and dad! We love you and we love shapes!" They ate their cookies and shared them with their shapes. They were very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little flower. It wanted to bloom and be big like the other flowers. But it was too small and not strong yet.
One day, a fierce wind came. It blew hard and tried to push the little flower down. The flower was scared. It did not want to be hurt by the wind. It hid behind an old tree for help.
The old tree was kind and strong. It protected the little flower from the fierce wind. The wind could not hurt the flower anymore. The little flower was safe and happy. It thanked the old tree.
After some time, the little flower grew bigger and stronger. It bloomed into a beautiful flower. The old tree and the flower became good friends. They lived happily ever after.

Once, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to play with his toy hook. One day, Tim went outside to play in the snow. He was very happy.
Tim saw his friend, Sam. Sam said, "Let's play freeze tag!" Tim was impatient and wanted to play with his hook. But, he said, "Okay, let's play!" They ran and laughed. Tim tried to tag Sam, but Sam was fast.
Then, something unexpected happened. Tim's hook got stuck in the snow. He tried to pull it out, but it was frozen. Tim was sad. Sam saw Tim and said, "Don't be sad. We can help." They worked together and got the hook out. Tim was happy and they played freeze tag with the hook.

One day, a big dog and a small cat had a race. They wanted to see who was the fastest. The prize was a big win. The dog thought he would win because he was big and strong. The cat was not sure, but she wanted to try.
Halfway through the race, the dog was very far ahead. He looked back and saw the cat far behind. He laughed and said, "You are too slow! I will win!" The cat felt bitter but she did not give up.
Then, something unexpected happened. The dog got tired and stopped to rest. The cat kept going and passed the dog. She ran and ran until she won the race. The dog was surprised and learned to value the cat's hard work.

Once upon a time, there was a hunter who was hopping through the forest. Then he saw something bouncing. He thought it was a tasty snack! So he chased after it. But it was too fast. So he got out a big net to try to catch it. Then the bouncy thing saw the net, so it changed its mind and began to bounce away. The hunter had to run very fast to try to get the bouncing thing. It was hard work, but the hunter kept running. Eventually, he managed to catch the tasty, bouncy thing. It was a delicious strawberry! The hunter was very happy. He collected more strawberries and enjoyed a tasty snack. The End.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play with his toy cart. One day, Tim took his cart outside to play in the park. He pushed the cart up and down the hills. It was so much fun!
Tim saw a big puddle of water. He thought it would be crazy to push his cart through the puddle. So he did! The cart went splash! It got all wet and dirty. Tim laughed and laughed. He thought it was very funny.
When Tim went back home, his mom saw the dirty cart. She said, "Tim, we need to wash the cart." Tim helped his mom wash the cart with water and soap. They scrubbed and scrubbed until the cart was clean again. Tim was happy to have a clean cart to play with. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max loved to eat pastry. One day, Max found a big, yummy pastry on the table. He was very happy and wanted to eat it all.
But there was a big, fearful cat named Luna near the table. Max was scared of Luna. He wanted the pastry, but he didn't want the cat to see him. So, Max thought of a plan to get the pastry without the cat knowing.
Max decided to make Luna sneeze. He found a feather and tickled Luna's nose with it. Luna sneezed so loud that she ran away. Max quickly jumped on the table and ate the pastry. He was very happy and his tummy was full.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a nice girl named Lily. Lily had a pretty vase that she loved very much. The vase was blue and had pretty flowers on it. One day, Lily met a wise cat named Tom. Tom was a smart cat who knew many things.
Tom said to Lily, "I can lead you to a place with more pretty vases, do you want to go?" Lily was very happy and said, "Yes, please! I love vases." So, Tom and Lily went on a walk to find more pretty vases.
They walked and walked until they found a big store with many vases. Lily was very happy and picked out a new vase. It was pink with pretty flowers on it. Lily said, "Thank you, Tom, for leading me here!" Tom said, "You're welcome, Lily. I'm glad I could help you find a new vase." And they went back home, happy and wise.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She lived with her mommy and daddy in a small house near the sea. One day, the sky became dark, and the wind started to blow hard. It was very cold outside. Lucy's mommy said, "A big storm called a hurricane is coming."
Lucy was scared and asked, "Will the hurricane hurt us, Mommy?" Her mommy hugged her and said, "I promise we will be safe, Lucy. We will go to a safe place where the hurricane cannot reach us."
So, Lucy, her mommy, and daddy went to a big strong building with many other people. They all stayed there together, talking and playing games until the hurricane went away. The wind stopped blowing, and the sun came out again. Lucy was happy that her mommy kept her promise and that they were all safe.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big race. All the kids were excited to complete the race. The winner would get a beautiful flag. Tim was a little boy who wanted to win the flag. He wore his comfortable shoes and was ready to run.
The race started, and all the kids ran fast. Tim was in the front, but then he saw a small puppy on the side of the road. The puppy looked sad and lost. Tim stopped running and went to help the puppy. He thought he would lose the race, but he wanted to help.
Tim and the puppy walked to the finish line together. When they got there, Tim was surprised! He was the first one to complete the race! The other kids got lost because they didn't see the right way. Tim won the flag and made a new friend, the puppy. They were both very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a very important paper. Lily loved her paper because it had a big, pretty rainbow on it. She wanted to show it to her friend, Tim.
Lily and Tim liked to play at the park together. One sunny day, they played on the swings and the slide. They had so much fun! But Lily forgot her important paper at home. She needed to remember to bring it next time.
The next day, Lily remembered her important paper. She took it to the park and showed it to Tim. He thought the rainbow was very pretty too. They both smiled and played together. And from that day on, Lily always remembered her important paper when she went to the park.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a boy named Tom. Tom had a tray. It was not a big tray, but it was an original tray. It was his favorite tray. He used it every day to eat his food. He loved his tray so much.
One day, Tom's mom told him to stay in his room and play. Tom took his original tray with him. He played with his toys on the tray. But then, something unexpected happened. A bird flew in through the window and took Tom's tray! Tom was sad and didn't know what to do.
Tom's mom saw the bird with the tray and went outside. She talked to the bird and asked it to give the tray back. The bird was sorry and gave the tray back to Tom's mom. Tom learned that day that it's important to be kind and share with others, even if they take something from you. And from that day on, Tom shared his original tray with his friends and family, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved going to the gym with her mommy. The gym was very old, but Lily didn't mind. She liked to play and run around in the big room.
One day, Lily saw an ancient toy in the corner of the gym. It was a small, old teddy bear. She picked it up and said, "Hello, teddy bear! Do you want to play with me?" The teddy bear didn't say anything, but Lily pretended it was talking to her.
Lily played with the teddy bear all day. When it was time to go home, she started to cry. Her mommy came to comfort her. "What's wrong, Lily?" she asked. "I want to take the teddy bear home," Lily said. Her mommy smiled and said, "Okay, you can take the teddy bear home. But we have to bring it back next time we come to the gym." Lily was happy and hugged the teddy bear tight. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a dog named Spot found a toy in the park. The toy was unknown to him. He did not know what it was. Spot was very curious and wanted to play with the toy.
Spot saw another dog named Max. Spot said, "Max, do you know this toy? I found it in the park." Max looked at the toy and said, "No, I don't know it. But we can play with it together!" So, Spot and Max played with the unknown toy.
While they were playing, the toy broke. Both dogs were sad. Spot said, "I am sorry, Max. I did not want the toy to suffer." Max said, "It's okay, Spot. We can find a new toy to play with." They went to look for a new toy and found a ball. Spot and Max played with the ball and were happy again.

Once upon a time, in a distant far away place, there was a big school called a university. Many people went there to learn and play. One day, a little girl named Lucy found a pretty ribbon. She wanted to attach it to her bag, so she could show her friends.
But, a boy named Tom saw the ribbon too. He wanted it for his hat. Lucy and Tom both held the ribbon and did not want to let go. They did not know what to do. They were both sad and upset.
Then, their teacher came and saw the problem. She had an idea! She took the ribbon and cut it into two pieces. She gave one piece to Lucy to attach to her bag and the other piece to Tom for his hat. Now, both Lucy and Tom were happy and they became good friends. They learned that sharing can make everyone happy.

One day, a little girl named Sue was scared. She had lost her favorite toy, a small doll with pretty hair. Sue looked under her bed, in her toy box, and all around her room, but she could not find her doll. She felt very sad.
Sue's mom saw her sad face and asked, "Sue, why are you so sad?" Sue told her mom about her lost doll with pretty hair. Her mom said, "Let's pray together and ask for help to find your doll." So they sat down and prayed.
After they prayed, Sue went to the kitchen for a snack. And there, on the table, was her doll! Sue was so happy and surprised. She never thought to look in the kitchen. Sue hugged her doll and said, "Thank you for helping me find my doll." From that day on, Sue was not scared to ask for help when she needed it.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a compassionate girl named Mia. Mia loved to help others. One day, she saw a lost cat near her home. The cat was scared and did not know where to go. Mia wanted to help the cat find its way back to its family.
Mia said to the cat, "Don't worry, little cat. I will help you find your family." She decided to take the cat around the town to look for its home. They walked and walked, looking for the cat's family. Mia and the cat went through many exits, but they still could not find the cat's home.
Finally, they reached a big exit with a sign that said "Welcome to the Cat Park." Mia thought that maybe the cat's family was inside. They went into the park and saw many cats playing and having fun. Mia asked a big cat, "Do you know this little cat?" The big cat said, "Yes! That's my sister!" The little cat was so happy to see her family again. Mia felt good that she could help the cat find its family. From that day on, Mia and the cat became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a peaceful squirrel. He would spend his days warmly snuggled up in the tree outside of his little house. One day, he heard a loud noise coming from outside his house. He knew he had to investigate.
The squirrel crept closer and closer to the loud noise and saw a group of small children. They were holding sticks, and hitting a round object back and forth between them. As the object came closer he realized it was a large nut. The nut was almost as big as the squirrel. He watched them playing and was no longer scared. 
The children noticed the squirrel and were excited to see him up close. One of them shouted, "Let's see if we can hit the squirrel with the nut!" They started throwing the nut in the squirrel's direction. The squirrel tried to run away, but it was too late. The nut struck the squirrel and he felt pain all over his body. He managed to crawl back to his home and lay still, but he never woke up again.
The children were very sorry and ran away. They never played with nuts and sticks again, and the peaceful squirrel was never seen again.

One day, a brave little girl named Mia went outside to play. She saw a big pile of soil and decided to make a mud pie. Mia put her hands in the soil and started to mix it with water. She saw a worm and smiled. "Hello, worm!" she said. The worm wiggled and smiled back.
Mia's friend Ben came over and saw her playing with the soil. "What are you doing?" he asked. Mia smiled and said, "I'm making a mud pie! Do you want to help?" Ben nodded and they both started to make the mud pie together.
As they were playing, a big rain cloud came and it started to rain. Mia and Ben tried to be brave, but the rain was too heavy. Their mud pie was washed away by the rain. They both felt sad and went inside their houses. The worm in the soil wiggled away, looking for a new friend to play with.

Lily and Ben love to play in the pool. They splash each other with water and laugh. They also like to dance in the pool. They move their arms and legs and make funny faces.
One day, they see a big frog on the edge of the pool. The frog is green and slimy. Lily and Ben are curious. They swim closer to the frog and say hello.
"Hello, frog. Do you want to play with us?" Lily asks.
"Ribbit, ribbit. No, thank you. I don't like to splash and dance. I like to jump and catch flies," the frog says.
"Jump and catch flies? That sounds boring. Splashing and dancing is more fun. Come on, frog. Try it with us," Ben says.
The frog shakes his head. He does not want to leave his spot. Lily and Ben decide to show him how to splash and dance. They splash water at the frog and dance around him. The frog gets wet and annoyed. He jumps into the air and lands on Lily's head.
"Ah! Get off me, frog!" Lily screams.
"Ribbit, ribbit. That was a successful jump. I caught a fly on your head," the frog says.
Lily and Ben are surprised and scared. They swim away from the frog and get out of the pool. They run to their mom and tell her what happened.
"Mom, mom, there is a frog in the pool. He jumped on Lily's head and ate a fly," Ben says.
"Oh, dear. That sounds like a naughty frog. Let's go and see if we can get him out of the pool," mom says.
They go back to the pool and see the frog still on the edge. He looks happy and full. He sees them and says, "Ribbit, ribbit. Hello, again. Do you want to jump and catch flies with me?"
Lily and Ben shake their heads. They don't like the frog. They like to splash and dance. They say goodbye to the frog and go to play somewhere else. The frog does not mind. He likes to jump and catch flies. He thinks he is very successful.

Once upon a time, there was a gifted little boy named Tim. Tim lived in a small house near the woods. He loved to play with his dog, Spot, and find pretty things in the woods.
One day, Tim and Spot found a shiny pebble. Tim said, "Look, Spot! A pretty pebble! Let's give it to Mom as a gift." So, they took the pebble home to give to Mom. Mom was very happy and said, "Thank you, Tim and Spot! I love it!"
The next day, something unexpected happened. The shiny pebble started to glow! Tim, Spot, and Mom were surprised. The pebble turned into a beautiful butterfly! The butterfly said, "Thank you for finding me. I was lost, but now I can fly home." And with that, the butterfly flew away, leaving Tim, Spot, and Mom with a happy memory.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max liked to tease a little cat named Luna. One day, Max saw a symbol on a tree. It was a picture of a cat and a dog being friends. Max felt guilty for teasing Luna.
Max wanted to be friends with Luna, but Luna was scared of Max. Max tried to be nice, but Luna always ran away. Max was sad and didn't know what to do.
One day, Max found a toy that Luna liked. He brought the toy to Luna and they played together. Luna saw that Max was being nice and they became friends. The symbol on the tree came true and Max never teased Luna again.

One day, a little red dog named Max went for a walk. Max saw a big tree and decided to rest under it. As he sat down, he started to shiver because it was very cold outside.
Max was hungry and wanted to eat something warm. He thought about his favorite food, spaghetti. Max had an idea and ran back to his house to get some spaghetti.
When Max came back to the tree, he had a big bowl of hot spaghetti. He ate the spaghetti and felt warm and happy. Max did not shiver anymore. He played under the tree all day and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little red train. It had a big engine that made it go fast. The train loved to help people and always did a good job.
One sunny day, the train saw a little boy crying by the side of the tracks. The train stopped and asked, "Why are you crying?" The little boy looked up and said, "My toy car fell into the water, and it's all wet now."
The train wanted to help the little boy, so it said, "Don't worry, I can help you dry your toy car." The train took the toy car and put it in the warm sun. They waited together, and soon the toy car was dry and good as new.
The little boy was very happy and thanked the train. The train felt good because it helped the little boy. From that day on, they became good friends, and the train always stopped to say hello to the little boy when it passed by.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Spot. Spot lived in a small house with a nice family. They had a pretty rug in the living room. Spot liked to sit on the rug and play with his toys.
One day, Spot's family had a party. They asked Spot to serve food to the people. Spot was happy to help. He carried a plate of cookies to the guests. But, Spot was frustrated because he couldn't hold the plate right. The cookies kept falling on the rug. Spot tried again and again, but he still couldn't do it.
Finally, Spot's family saw how hard he was trying. They decided to help him. They picked up the cookies and served the guests together. Spot was happy to be part of the team. After the party, they all sat on the rug and ate cookies together.

Mia and Sam were going to the park with Mom. They got in the car and Mom helped them with their buckles. "Click, click, click," they said as they heard the buckles snap. "Are you ready to go?" Mom asked. "Yes, yes, yes!" Mia and Sam shouted.
At the park, they saw a big slide, a swing, and a sandbox. Mia wanted to go on the slide, and Sam wanted to go on the swing. They took turns and had fun. Then they saw a little girl crying on a bench. She had a scraped knee and no one was with her.
Mia and Sam felt sorry for her. They walked over to her and said, "Hi, what's your name?" The little girl sniffed and said, "I'm Lily. I fell down and hurt my knee. And I can't find my dad." Mia and Sam looked around and saw a man looking for someone. They waved at him and said, "Is that your dad?" Lily nodded and smiled.
The man ran over to them and said, "Lily, there you are! I was so worried. Thank you for finding her, kids. You are very kind." He hugged Lily and checked her knee. He said, "It's not too bad. Let's go get a band-aid and some ice cream." He looked at Mia and Sam and said, "Do you want to come with us?" Mia and Sam looked at Mom and she nodded. They said, "Yes, please!"
They all went to the ice cream truck and got their favorite flavors. Lily shared her chocolate with Mia and Sam. They became friends and played together. Mom watched them and felt proud. She hugged Mia and Sam and said, "You are very good kids. I love you." Mia and Sam hugged her back and said, "We love you too, Mom." They had a happy day at the park.

Once there was a boy named Tom. He was weak but wanted to be strong. Tom had a friend named Sam. Sam was strong and liked to help Tom.
Sam said, "I will teach you at the club." The club was a place where kids played and learned. Tom was happy to go there with Sam.
At the club, Sam taught Tom how to run, jump, and play. Tom became strong and not weak anymore. They had fun and were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big cat named Tom. Tom was a good cat who liked to serve healthy food to his friends. One day, Tom invited his friend, a little mouse named Jerry, to have dinner with him.
At night, Tom cooked a healthy meal for Jerry. They sat down to eat and Tom said, "Here you go, Jerry. I made this healthy food for you." Jerry was very happy and said, "Thank you, Tom. This food looks yummy!"
But, while they were eating, a big dog named Spike came into the house. Spike was angry and wanted to eat Tom and Jerry. Tom and Jerry tried to run away, but Spike was too fast. He caught them and ate them up.
The moral of the story is: Always be careful and watch out for danger, even when you are having a good time with friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside. One day, Lily saw a little light blink far away. It was very distant. She wanted to find what made the light blink.
Lily started her search. She walked and walked. The light kept blinking. She got closer and closer. Lily was very excited to see what it was.
At last, Lily found a small, shiny rock. The rock was making the light blink. She picked it up and took it home. Lily showed the blinking rock to her mom and dad. They all smiled and were happy.

Once upon a time lived a brave girl called Erin. She was proud and worked very hard in school. Every night before bed she would close her eyes and pray for the future.
One day, Erin went on a special trip to the city. She was so excited to go and learn about the law from all of the clever people.
When she arrived, Erin's eyes opened wide as she saw all that was around her. People leaped and jumped around, laughing and having fun. But despite the joy, Erin knew there was something more to learn.
That night, Erin went to her hotel room and prayed with even more passion than before. She put all of her happy thoughts and wishes into one big prayer.
The next day, she awoke to a surprise. She had been chosen to speak in front of all the people at the city's courthouse. Her dream was coming true!
Erin stood tall and proud in her beautiful blue dress and spoke loudly, sharing all that she had learned about the law. It was a very special day.
Since then, Erin has kept her brave spirit and continues to pray for better things. She knows that anything is possible if you believe in yourself.

Once upon a time, there was a little monkey named Momo. Momo was very restless. He loved to jump and play all day long. One day, Momo saw a big yellow banana on a tree. He wanted to eat it because it looked yummy.
Momo climbed up the tree and said, "I have to get that banana!" He tried to reach for the banana, but it was too high. Momo called his friend, Lulu the bird. Lulu flew up to the banana and said, "I can help you, Momo!"
Lulu pushed the banana with her beak, and it fell down to Momo. Momo was so happy. He shared the banana with Lulu, and they both enjoyed their tasty snack. After eating, Momo was not restless anymore. He sat with Lulu and they talked and laughed together.

Once upon a time, in a lively town, there lived a little girl named Lily. She liked to welcome all her friends to play at her house. One sunny day, Lily and her friends found a shiny metal toy car in the park.
They were excited and decided to play with the metal car. They took turns pushing the car around the park. They made the car go fast and slow. They had so much fun together.
As the sun started to set, Lily welcomed her friends to her house to play more. They played with the metal car until it was time to say goodbye. Lily and her friends were happy and couldn't wait to play together again.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max liked to relax under a big tree in the park. One day, while Max was relaxing, he saw a scary cat.
The scary cat wanted to take Max's treat. Max did not want the cat to take his treat. So, Max barked at the scary cat. The cat ran away, and Max was happy.
Max went back to relax under the tree. He ate his treat and felt very good. From that day on, Max knew he could be brave and protect his treats from scary cats.

Once there was Maria and John. Maria was a very lively girl and John was her best friend. 
One day the sun started to disappear and big raindrops started to fall from the sky. Maria had only ever seen rain one other time before and so she was excited.
John and Maria put on their raincoats and ran outside together. They twirled around in the rain and laughed. The rain was so lively it made them everything seem so wonderful! 
John took off his hat and bowed to Maria. Suddenly there were two rainbows in the sky in all the colors of the rainbow. Maria and John looked at them and smiled. 
The rainbows were so beautiful Maria and John bowed to them. After that happened the rain stopped and the sun shined again. Maria and John stayed outside and played until the sun went down. 
Maria and John always remembered the special day they had together with the lively rain.

Once upon a time, there was a little sheep named Fluffy. Fluffy lived in a peaceful field with lots of green grass. Every day, Fluffy would eat grass and play with his friends.
One sunny day, Fluffy saw a big hill. He had an idea. "I will roll down the hill!" he said to his friend, Bunny. Bunny was scared, but Fluffy was brave. "I will try it first, then you can try," Fluffy told Bunny.
Fluffy went to the top of the hill and started to roll. He rolled down the hill, laughing and having fun. When he reached the bottom, he looked up at Bunny and said, "That was fun! You should try it too!" Bunny hopped to the top of the hill and rolled down just like Fluffy. They both laughed and played, rolling down the hill all day long.

Once upon a time there was a sheep in a meadow. The sheep heard something that disturbed it. It was interesting. The sheep looked around and saw a big bird. The bird was looking at the sheep. The sheep got scared and ran away! The bird followed the sheep. The sheep ran as fast as it could. The bird could not keep up. The sheep was safe again. The sheep was very happy. It went back to eating the grass in the meadow. The bird was gone. The sheep was not disturbed any more.

Once upon a time, there was a clever little penguin named Pippin. Pippin loved to supply ice to his friends. Every day, he would bring ice to them, and they would say, "Thank you, Pippin!"
One day, Pippin went to get ice for his friends. But when he got to the ice, there was none! Pippin was sad. He went to tell his friends the bad news. They were all sad too.
But then, something unexpected happened. The sun came out and started to shine. The ice started to come back! The friends all cheered. Pippin could supply ice to his friends again. They were all happy, and Pippin was the cleverest penguin of all.

Once upon a time, in a land far away, there was a big war. Many people fought in the war. They were very brave. They wanted to keep their land safe. They knew they belonged to their land and had to protect it.
One day, a brave boy named Tim saw the war. He wanted to help. Tim was not big, but he had a big heart. He knew he belonged with the brave people fighting for their land.
Tim fought in the war too. He was brave and strong. The people were happy to have Tim with them. They won the war and kept their land safe. They knew they belonged together, and they were all very brave.

Once upon a time, there was a busy little town. In this town, there was a big newspaper shop. Every day, many people came to buy the newspaper to read.
One sunny day, a small cat climbed a tall tree. The cat was scared and could not come down. The people in the town saw the cat and wanted to help. They tried to think of a way to rescue the cat from the tree.
A kind man found a long ladder. He put the ladder against the tree and climbed up. The man was brave and rescued the cat. The people in the town were happy. The cat was safe, and everyone went back to their busy day. The next day, the story of the brave man and the cat was in the newspaper.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat. The cat had a tight collar around its neck. The collar was so tight that it made the cat very sad.
One day, a kind boy saw the sad cat. He said, "Don't worry, cat. I will help you." The boy tried to take the tight collar off the cat. But it was not easy.
Finally, the boy took the tight collar off the cat. The cat was so happy! The cat said, "Thank you, boy!" And they became best friends forever.

Once upon a time, there was a little car who loved to drive fast. The car had four legs with wheels. One day, the car went for a drive in the park. It saw many friends, like the dog and the cat. They all played together.
But the car wanted to go faster. It drove so fast that it did not see a big rock. The car hit the rock and broke one of its legs. It could not drive fast anymore. The car was very sad.
Then, the dog and the cat came to help the car. They found a new leg for the car. The car learned that going fast is not always good. It is better to be safe and have friends to help when you need them. The car, the dog, and the cat all played together happily ever after.

One day, Sam and his mom went to the park. Sam liked to play all the games. But his mom wanted to take a nap! So she told Sam to stay close and not wander too far. 
Sam agreed and scooted over to the swing set. He saw a folder under the swing and he was very curious. He asked his mom if he could take a look. She smiled and said "It's alright, but be careful." 
Sam was excited to explore the folder. Inside, he saw some animals, but he was nervous. It looked like someone had been taking them from their homes! 
Sam went to find his mom to show her the folder, but someone else was gone. She was gone too! It was a bad man, trying to take the folder. 
Sam tried to protect the folder, but the bad man was too strong. Sam was too little to fight back. So the bad man took the folder and Sam's mom never came back.

Once upon a time, there was a big orange cat named Fluffy. Fluffy liked to play and rush around the house. He would run up and down the stairs, making lots of noise.
One day, Fluffy found a new toy. It was a soft massage brush. Fluffy loved the way the brush felt on his fur. He would rub against it all day long.
Fluffy's owner saw how much he liked the massage brush. She would use it to brush Fluffy every day. Fluffy was a very happy orange cat. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a boy named Tim and a girl named Sue were playing in the park. They saw a big table with a game on it. The game was called chess. Tim and Sue wanted to play, but they didn't know how. So, they asked an old man sitting on a bench nearby. The old man had a sour face, but he agreed to teach them.
As they played, the old man became more and more sour. Tim and Sue didn't like it. They wanted to have fun, not be sad. So, they tried to make the old man smile. They made funny faces and told jokes. But the old man stayed sour.
Suddenly, the sky turned dark and cold wind blew. Tim, Sue, and the old man started to freeze. They didn't know what was happening. Then, a voice from the sky said, "You have played chess with a sour wizard! Now, you will all freeze forever!" The old man was a wizard! Tim and Sue were scared.
The old man said, "I am sorry, Tim and Sue. I didn't mean to make you freeze. But I cannot undo the spell." Tim and Sue were very sad. They wanted to go home and play, but now they were stuck in the park, frozen with the sour wizard.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a gifted little girl named Lily. Lily loved to gather things. One day, she found a shiny nail on the ground.
Lily ran to her friend Tom, and said, "Look, I found a nail. Let's gather more and build something fun!" Tom was excited and said, "Yes, let's do it!"
Together, Lily and Tom gathered more nails and wood. They built a nice little house for their toys. They were very happy and played with their new house all day.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a big rain. The rain made a flood. The flood was everywhere. It made the village very wet and muddy. The people in the village were sad because their homes were filthy.
One day, a kind man came to the village. He saw the people were sad. He wanted to help them. The kind man had a magic cloth. The magic cloth could clean the flood and make the village nice again.
The kind man went to the first house. He kneeled down and used the magic cloth to clean the filthy floor. The flood went away. The house was clean and dry. The people in the house were happy. The kind man went to every house in the village. He kneeled down and cleaned all the houses with the magic cloth. The village was clean and dry again. All the people were happy and thanked the kind man for his help.

Lila and her mom walk to the store. They need to buy shampoo. Lila likes shampoo. It makes her hair soft and shiny.
"Mom, can I pick the shampoo?" Lila asks.
"Sure, honey. You can pick the shampoo. But we have to hurry. It is almost time for lunch." Mom says.
Lila and her mom go to the aisle with the shampoo. There are many bottles of shampoo. Some are big, some are small. Some are red, some are blue. Some have pictures of flowers, some have pictures of animals.
Lila looks at all the shampoo. She sees one bottle that she likes. It is pink and has a picture of a bunny. Lila loves bunnies. She takes the bottle and shows it to her mom.
"Mom, look! I want this shampoo. It has a bunny. Bunnies are cute and fluffy." Lila says.
Mom smiles and looks at the bottle. She reads the label. It says "Dry Shampoo. No Water Needed. Just Spray and Brush."
"Uh-oh, Lila. This is not the shampoo we need. This is dry shampoo. It is for people who do not have water. We have water at home. We need shampoo that we can use with water." Mom explains.
Lila frowns. She does not understand. She likes the bunny shampoo. She does not want to put it back.
"But mom, I want the bunny shampoo. It is pretty and pink. Please, mom, please." Lila begs.
Mom shakes her head. She knows that Lila will not like the dry shampoo. It will not make her hair soft and shiny. It will make her hair dull and sticky.
"Sorry, Lila. We cannot buy the bunny shampoo. It is not good for your hair. Let's find another shampoo that you like. How about this one? It is yellow and has a picture of a duck. Ducks are cute and quacky." Mom suggests.
Lila looks at the duck shampoo. She likes ducks. They are cute and quacky. She nods and smiles.
"Okay, mom. I like the duck shampoo. It is yellow and bright. Thank you, mom." Lila says.
Mom hugs Lila and puts the duck shampoo in the cart. They walk to the cashier and pay for the shampoo. Then they walk back home. Lila is happy. She can't wait to use the duck shampoo. She hopes it will make her hair soft and shiny. And maybe a little quacky.

Once upon a time, there was a big sunflower. It was old and lived in a garden. One day, a little girl named Lily came to see the sunflower. She liked to stare at it because it was so big and pretty.
One day, when Lily came to see the sunflower, something strange happened. The sunflower started to talk! It said, "Hello, Lily. I am a magic sunflower." Lily was very surprised. She did not know sunflowers could talk.
The magic sunflower told Lily that it could make a wish come true. Lily thought for a moment and then wished for a big, fun party. Suddenly, the garden was full of people, music, and yummy food. Everyone had a great time, and the old sunflower was happy to see Lily smile.

Once upon a time, a little train named Timmy lived in a small town. Timmy was a very good train. He would supply people with things they needed. He carried food, toys, and clothes from one town to another. Timmy loved his job.
One day, Timmy met a big, terrible train named Grumpy. Grumpy was always sad and angry. He did not like to help people. Grumpy said to Timmy, "Why do you supply things to people? It is no fun!" Timmy replied, "I like to make people happy. It is a good thing to do."
Timmy wanted to help Grumpy be happy too. So, Timmy asked Grumpy to join him and supply things to people. At first, Grumpy did not want to. But then, he saw how happy it made Timmy and the people in the towns. Grumpy decided to try it. Soon, Grumpy was not terrible anymore. He was a good train, just like Timmy. They both supplied things to the towns and made many people happy.

Once upon a time, there was a bossy cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to paint. One day, she decided to paint a big sun on a wall. She painted the sun in the east, so it would look like it was rising.
Kitty asked her friend, Doggy, to help her. "Doggy, come paint with me," said Kitty. Doggy agreed and started to paint with her. They painted the sun together. Kitty was bossy and told Doggy how to paint. "No, paint here!" said Kitty.
In the end, they finished painting the sun in the east. It looked very pretty. Kitty and Doggy were proud of their work. Even though Kitty was bossy, they had fun painting together. From that day on, they painted more pictures and became best friends.

One day, a boy named Tim went to a nice place with his mom. This place had a big tree and many toys. Tim saw another boy named Sam playing with a red ball. Tim wanted to play with the red ball too.
Tim went to Sam and said, "Can you tell me if I can play with the red ball?" Sam said, "No, I want to play with it alone." Tim was sad and went to his mom.
Tim's mom saw that he was sad and asked, "What is wrong, Tim?" Tim said, "Sam won't let me play with the red ball." Tim's mom said, "Let's find a new toy to play with." They found a blue car and Tim was happy again.

One day, a boy named Tim found a wallet on the ground. Tim thought the wallet was amazing because it was very colorful. He wanted to find the owner of the wallet, so he asked his friend, Sam, to help him.
Tim said to Sam, "I found this amazing wallet. Let's find who it belongs to!" Sam agreed, and they started to look for the owner. They asked people they met if the wallet was theirs, but no one said yes.
Finally, they found a girl named Sue who was painting a picture. Sue said, "That's my wallet! I use it to buy paint for my pictures." Tim and Sam gave the wallet back to Sue. She was very happy and thanked them. Sue showed them her paintings, and they all became good friends.

Once upon a time, in a small brown house, there lived a little girl named Mia. Mia had a best friend named Tom. They liked to play together in the yard. One day, they saw some dust on the ground.
Tom said, "Mia, let's speak to the dust and see if it can talk back to us!" They both spoke to the dust, but it did not speak back. They tried again and again, but still, the dust did not talk.
Mia and Tom felt sad. Mia's mom saw them and asked, "Why are you sad?" Mia told her mom about the dust. Her mom said, "Not everything can speak. But it's good to try and learn new things."
Mia and Tom learned that even if something does not speak, it is still fun to try. They also learned that it is good to be kind to everything, even if it cannot talk. And they played happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Lily went for a walk in the woods. She saw a big mushroom. It was red and white. She thought it was very pretty.
Lily met a friendly squirrel. The squirrel said, "Don't touch that mushroom, Lily! It is dangerous!" Lily was surprised. She did not know some mushrooms could be dangerous.
Just then, a big wind came and blew the dangerous mushroom away. Lily and the squirrel were happy. They played in the woods all day and had lots of fun.

Once there was a little girl who wanted to build something special. She had a box full of blocks and she thought maybe it would be fun to build something good with them. Every day, she took out the blocks and tried to come up with something original. But she couldn't. She got frustrated and wanted to give up.
Then her dad came in and saw her lying there with her blocks. He said, "Why don't you take it easy? Being patient and taking your time will help you build something good." So the little girl took a deep breath and decided to be patient. She thought about what she wanted to build for a long time until one day, it finally came to her.
The little girl took her blocks and the idea that had been in her head, and she slowly built the most fantastic thing ever. She was so proud of herself because she was patient and was able to create something that she was proud of. Being patient allowed her to build something truly original!
Moral of the story: Being patient can help you build something really special.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toy cars. One day, she found a big metal car in her toy box. It was shiny and new. Lily was very happy.
Lily wanted to play with her friend Tom. She went to his house and showed him her new metal car. Tom wanted to play with it too, but Lily didn't want to share. She insisted that it was her car and only she could play with it. Tom felt sad and went to play alone.
Lily played with her metal car, but she started to feel embarrassed. She knew it was not nice to not share with her friend. So, she went to Tom and said sorry. They played together with the metal car and had lots of fun. Lily learned that sharing is caring and it makes everyone happy.

Once upon a time, in a big school, there was a small pupil named Tim. Tim was very thin. He liked to play with his friends and learn new things. One day, Tim saw a big ball in the playground. The ball belonged to his friend, Sam. Tim wanted to play with the ball too.
Tim went to Sam and asked, "Can I borrow your ball?" Sam was a kind friend, so he said, "Yes, you can borrow my ball, but be careful. It is very big and heavy." Tim was excited and thanked Sam for letting him borrow the ball.
Tim started to play with the big ball. He pushed it and kicked it. But because Tim was very thin, the big ball was too heavy for him. The ball rolled very fast and hit a tree. The tree shook and many leaves fell down. Tim was surprised, but he learned that he needed to be more careful with the big ball. He played with the ball more gently and had lots of fun with his friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the train station with his mom. They wanted to go on a train ride to visit grandma. Tim was very excited because he loved trains a lot.
At the train station, they saw a big, fit man who worked there. Tim's mom asked the man, "Can you recommend a good train for us to take?" The man smiled and said, "Yes, the blue train is the best one to take. It will take you right to grandma's house."
So, Tim and his mom got on the blue train. They sat by the window and watched the trees go by. Tim was very happy to be on the train. Soon, they reached grandma's house and had a great day together.

Jimmy was so excited when his birthday finally arrived. He woke up early and couldn't wait to see what presents he would receive. When he opened his bedroom door, he was greeted with a wonderful surprise. His family and friends were waiting for him with presents and big smiles.
Jimmy was trying to contain his excitement, but it was difficult! He tried to stay calm as his family and friends shouted out a big "Welcome!". Jimmy was overjoyed when he received his presents. There were toys, some clothes and even a game. He was sure he was going to have a lot of fun with them.
But suddenly Jimmy's excitement turned to sadness when his best friend William said that he couldn't stay for the party. Jimmy was sad, but his family and friends quickly cheered him up. Everyone agreed to have the party another day and have William join them then. 
Jimmy was very happy to receive an extra day to enjoy with his best friend. He had been so excited to receive his presents, but he was even more excited that he would now get to have twice the fun!

Once upon a time, three year old Tommy lived in a small house with his mom and dad. One day, Tommy saw something ordinary outside - it was a brick! He was curious and asked his mom what it was.
"Mommy, what's that?"
"It's a brick," she said.
"What can I do with it?"
"You can build houses with bricks," she replied.
Tommy was excited. He quickly grabbed the brick and ran inside the house. He grabbed some other ordinary things like paper, sticks and blocks, and started to build. It was a bit of a struggle, but he soon figured it out.
He worked and worked until he was done. His mom was very impressed when she saw what he had made.
"Look at this, Mommy! I made a house with the brick!"
Tommy smiled with pride and hugged his mom. From that day onward, Tommy was sure that ordinary things like bricks could be used for something special.

Once upon a time, there was a little worm. The worm lived in a big garden with many friends. The worm liked to play with his friends all day.
One day, the worm saw a big pot. The pot was hot. The worm did not know what it was. The worm asked his friend, the bird, "What is this?" The bird said, "That is a pot to cook food. Be careful, it can hurt you."
The worm was sad. He did not want to get hurt. So, the worm and his friends played far away from the pot. They had a fun day and the worm was safe. The worm was happy and so were his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a wide, big tree. Under the tree, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily was playing with her toys. She had a small fan that she liked a lot. She played with it every day.
One day, Lily saw a little bird on the ground. The bird looked sad and could not fly. Lily wanted to help the bird. She decided to care for it. She gave the bird water and some food. The bird started to feel better.
Lily used her fan to make a soft breeze for the bird. The bird liked it very much. After a few days, the bird could fly again. It was so happy! The bird flew up into the wide, big tree and sang a song for Lily. Lily knew she had made a new friend.

One day, a little dog named Max went for a walk. He saw a big gate. Max was confused. He did not know what was behind the gate. Max wanted to find out, so he pushed the gate open with his nose.
Max saw a long path. He decided to follow it. As he walked, he saw many pretty flowers and tall trees. Max was very happy. He liked this new place.
But then, something unexpected happened. Max found a big pond with a family of ducks. The ducks were not scared of Max. Instead, they asked Max to join them. Max was surprised but happy. He jumped into the pond and swam with the ducks. They all became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a bird named Bob. Bob loved to sing every day. One day, he wanted to record his song. He asked his friend, the cat, to help him.
The cat had a wake-up box. When the box hears a loud sound, it wakes up. They put the wake-up box near Bob. Bob sang a lovely song, and the wake-up box heard it. Now, Bob's song was inside the box.
Bob and the cat were very happy. They wanted to celebrate. They had a big party with all their friends. They ate spicy food and danced all night. Bob's song made everyone smile.

Once upon a time, a thoughtful boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big object on the ground. It was round and had many colors. Tim was very happy to find it.
One day, Tim wanted to play with the object. He tried to strike it with his hand. But the object did not move. Tim was sad and did not know what to do.
Then, a little bird came and told Tim to try again. Tim struck the object with love and kindness. This time, the object moved, and many toys came out for all the kids to play with. The moral of the story is to always be kind and loving, even when something is hard.

Once upon a time, in a big palace, there lived a charming king. He had a big hat and a long robe. He was very nice to all the people in his land.
One day, the charming king wanted to let all the kids come to the palace to play. He said, "Let all the kids come and have fun in my palace!" The kids were very happy to hear this.
The kids came to the palace and played with the charming king. They played with balls, ran around, and laughed a lot. The charming king was very happy to see the kids having fun. And from that day on, the palace was always filled with joy and laughter.

Once upon a time, there was an adventurous little girl named Sally. Sally loved to go on fun walks in the park with her mom. One day, it was very cold outside, so Sally wore her favorite scarf. It was a warm and pretty scarf that her grandma made for her.
While walking in the park, Sally saw two squirrels. The squirrels were not happy. They started to quarrel about who could have the big nut they found. Sally wanted to help them, so she walked over to the squirrels. 
Sally gave each squirrel half of her nut. The squirrels stopped their quarrel and said thank you to Sally. They were now happy and became friends with Sally. Sally felt good because she helped the squirrels, and they all played together in the park until it was time to go home.

Once upon a time, there was a big red car. The car loved to drive fast. One day, the car went on a long drive with its friend, the blue car. They were very happy.
But then, they saw a lot of traffic. The cars had to drive slow. The big red car and the blue car were not happy. They wanted to drive fast again.
After some time, the traffic went away. The big red car and the blue car started to drive fast again. They were very happy. But soon, the big red car felt sleepy. The big red car stopped and took a nap. The blue car stayed with the big red car. When the big red car woke up, they both drove home. They were happy and sleepy, ready to rest after their long drive.

Mia likes to create things with her crayons and paper. She draws flowers, animals, and houses. She also likes to cut the paper with her scissors. She makes shapes and patterns with the paper.
One day, she sees a big bowl of prunes on the table. They are dark and wrinkly. She thinks they look like funny faces. She takes some prunes and puts them on her paper. She uses her scissors to cut out eyes, noses, and mouths. She makes prune people.
She shows her mom her prune people. "Look, mom, I create prune people!" she says proudly.
Mom smiles and says, "That's very creative, Mia. But prunes are not for playing. They are for eating. They are good for you. Do you want to try one?"
Mia picks up a prune and takes a bite. She makes a face. The prune is sour and sticky. She does not like it. She spits it out. "Yuck, mom, I don't like prune people. They are sour and icky. I like crayon people better. They are pretty and nice."
Mom laughs and says, "That's okay, Mia. You don't have to eat the prune people. But you can't waste the prunes. Let's put them back in the bowl. Maybe dad will like them. He likes sour things. And you can keep your crayon people. They are very pretty and nice. You are a good artist, Mia."

Lily and Ben were playing in the garden. They saw a big peach tree with many ripe peaches. Lily wanted to pick a peach and eat it. She asked Ben to help her.
"Ben, can you lift me up? I want a peach," she said.
"OK, Lily. I will lift you up. But be careful. Don't drop the peach," Ben said.
Ben lifted Lily up and she reached for a peach. She pulled it hard and it came off the branch. But it was too heavy for her and she dropped it on the ground. The peach broke into two pieces and the juice spilled out.
"Oh no! The peach is broken! It is all messy!" Lily cried.
Ben put Lily down and looked at the broken peach. He felt sorry for Lily. He wanted to make her happy.
"It's OK, Lily. We can still eat the peach. Look, it is still sweet and juicy. Here, you can have this half. I will have the other half," he said.
He gave Lily one half of the peach and took the other half for himself. They both bit into the peach and smiled. It was delicious.
"Thank you, Ben. You are a good friend. I like the peach. It is yummy," Lily said.
"You're welcome, Lily. I like the peach too. It is yummy. And I like you. You are a good friend too," Ben said.
They finished the peach and hugged each other. They were happy. They went back to playing in the garden.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy had a big screen in her room. She liked to watch her favorite show on the screen. But one day, the screen started to show bad things. Amy did not like the bad things on the screen.
Amy went to tell her mom about the bad things on the screen. Her mom did not believe her. Amy felt sad because her mom did not trust her. So, Amy went back to her room and saw the screen was good again. The bad things were gone.
Then, Amy had an idea. She took a picture of the screen with her toy camera when the bad things came back. She showed the picture to her mom. Her mom was surprised and said, "I am sorry, Amy. I trust you now." Amy was happy that her mom trusted her, and they fixed the screen together.

Sam and Mia like to fish with Dad. They have a rod, a hook, a worm and a bucket. They go to the lake and find a good spot. Dad helps them put the worm on the hook and throw the rod in the water.
They wait and wait for a fish to bite. Sam sees a big fish near his rod. He gets excited and pulls hard. The fish jumps out of the water and lands on the grass. Sam runs to get it, but the fish is slippery and wiggles away. Sam chases the fish and tries to catch it with his hands.
Mia sees another fish near her rod. She is ready and pulls gently. The fish comes out of the water and into the bucket. Mia is happy and proud. She shows Dad and Sam her fish. Dad says she did a good job. Sam is sad and jealous. He wants a fish too.
Dad tells Sam not to give up. He says there are more fish in the lake. He gives Sam a new worm and helps him throw the rod again. Sam waits and hopes for a fish. He sees a small fish nibble his hook. He pulls slowly and carefully. The fish comes out of the water and into the bucket. Sam is happy and proud. He shows Dad and Mia his fish. Dad says he did a good job too.
Sam and Mia hug and smile. They have two fish in the bucket. They are ready to go home and cook the fish. They shut the lid of the bucket and carry it together. They thank Dad for taking them fishing. They had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a nice little frog named Tim. Tim lived near a well. He loved to jump all day long. One day, while Tim was jumping, he met a small bird named Sue. Sue was sitting near the well.
"Hi, Sue!" said Tim. "Do you want to jump with me?"
Sue smiled and said, "Yes, Tim! I would love to jump with you."
So, Tim and Sue jumped and played near the well all day. They had so much fun together. The sun was shining, and they were very happy. After a long day of jumping, they sat by the well to rest.
"Thank you for playing with me, Tim," said Sue. "You are a very nice friend."
Tim smiled and said, "You are a nice friend too, Sue. Let's play again tomorrow!"
And from that day on, Tim and Sue played and jumped near the well every day. They were the best of friends and always had a great time together.

One day, a boy named Tim went on a safari with his mom and dad. They saw many animals like lions, elephants, and monkeys. The sky was clear and the sun was bright. Tim was very happy.
Tim had a toy car that could zoom very fast. He loved to play with it all the time. His mom told him not to play with the car near the animals, but Tim did not listen. He wanted to see the car zoom with the animals.
While they were watching the lions, Tim let his car zoom near them. The lions got scared and ran away. Tim was sad and his mom was angry. The safari was over and they had to go home.
The bad ending of the safari made Tim learn that he should always listen to his mom. The moral of the story is to listen to your parents, as they know what is best for you.

Lily and Tom were playing in the park. They liked to run and jump and slide. They saw a big wire fence near the trees. It looked like a fun thing to climb.
"Let's go to the wire fence!" Lily said. "We can pretend we are monkeys!"
"OK!" Tom said. "But be careful. The wire might be sharp."
They ran to the wire fence and started to climb. They made funny noises like monkeys. They felt happy and brave.
But then Lily felt something poke her hand. She looked and saw a drop of blood. She had cut herself on the wire. She felt a big pain and started to cry.
"Ow! Ow! Ow!" Lily cried. "The wire hurt me!"
Tom heard Lily cry and climbed down. He saw her hand and felt sorry for her.
"I'm sorry, Lily!" Tom said. "The wire is bad. It is not fun. It is uncomfortable. Let's go to the nurse. She will make you feel better."
He took Lily's hand and ran with her to the nurse. The nurse cleaned Lily's hand and put a band-aid on it. She gave Lily a hug and a sticker.
"There, there," the nurse said. "You are a brave girl. The wire will not hurt you anymore. You can play again. But stay away from the wire fence. It is not safe."
Lily nodded and smiled. She felt better. She thanked the nurse and took Tom's hand. They ran back to the park. They decided to play on the swings instead. They did not like the wire fence anymore. They liked the swings. They were fun and comfortable.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who had a magical jelly. Whenever the jelly was happy, it glowed different colors.
One day, the little girl was looking at her jelly in awe. She talked to it, saying, "My jelly, you are so charming. I love how you glow."
The jelly heard the little girl and was so full of joy that it started glowing in all colors. The little girl laughed and clapped her hands.
"Oh, you are so fun!" she said to her jelly.
The jelly kept glowing until the night. It was so bright that the little girl could see her way around the darkness. And she was so happy to have a jelly like this that glowed with joy.

Mommy was cold. It was very icy outside and there were snowflakes everywhere. She was wearing her big, warm coat. She wanted to make something nice to keep her warm so she decided to make hot chocolate. She put some milk in a pan, then added sweet, sticky syrup.
She put the pan on the fire and waited. Soon, steam started from the pan and it smelled like hot chocolate. Mama was so happy!
Suddenly, a black ashtray fell off the table. Mama ran to grab it, but it was too late. The ashtray had disappeared! Mama was so sad and looked around for it, but it was gone.
Mama held back the tears and wondered where the ashtray had gone. She finished the hot chocolate but it didn't taste as good without the ashtray. She sat for a while, feeling icy and sad, until she finally decided it was time to go inside.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He lived with his mom in a small house. One day, Tim's mom told him, "Today is your birthday, Tim! You will receive a yummy cake!" Tim was very happy and excited.
Later, Tim and his mom went to the wide park to play. They played with a ball and ran around. After playing, Tim's mom said, "Let's go home for your yummy cake!" Tim was very excited and ran to the house.
When they got home, they saw that the yummy cake was gone! A big dog had come in and eaten the whole cake. Tim was very sad, and his mom said, "I'm sorry, Tim. We can't have cake today." Tim cried, but he knew that next year, he would receive another yummy cake.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely oyster. He lived in a big sea. The lonely oyster had no friends. He was very sad.
One day, a friendly fish swam by. The fish saw the lonely oyster and said, "Hello, oyster! Why are you so sad?" The oyster replied, "I am sad because I am lonely. I have no friends."
The friendly fish had an idea. "I will help you release your beautiful pearl. Then, you will have many friends!" The lonely oyster agreed. Together, they worked to release the pearl.
After the pearl was out, many sea animals came to see the pretty pearl. The lonely oyster was not lonely anymore. He had many friends and was very happy. The oyster and the friendly fish were best friends, and they lived happily ever after.

One day, a big honest dog and a small cat were playing. The dog said, "Let's play with my toy missile!" The cat said, "Yes, let's play!"
The dog got his toy missile and they started to play. The cat was very fast and could catch the missile easily. The dog was a little slow, so he had to try harder. The dog started to yawn, he was getting tired.
The dog said, "I want to try one more time!" The cat said, "Okay, but be careful!" The dog tried to catch the missile, but he was too tired. The missile hit a big tree and the tree fell on the dog's house. The house broke and the dog was very sad.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a smelly cat. The cat liked to eat a lot. It would eat anything it found. One day, the cat went outside to look for food.
As the cat walked, it saw something shiny on the ground. It was a piece of gold! The cat did not know what gold was, but it thought it might be good to eat. So, the cat picked up the gold and took it back to the small house.
The cat tried to eat the gold, but it was too hard. The cat's mouth hurt, and it could not eat the gold. The cat was sad. Then, a nice girl saw the cat and knew what to do. She gave the cat some soft food to eat, and the cat was happy. The smelly cat and the nice girl became good friends, and they played together every day. The cat never tried to eat gold again.

Once upon a time, there was a big, ancient square. In this square, there were many toys. The toys loved to play with each other. But one day, a new toy came. This toy did not know how to play with the other toys.
The old toys did not like the new toy. They said, "You cannot play with us." The new toy was sad. He wanted to show the old toys that he could play too. So, he thought of a fun game.
The new toy showed the old toys how to play the game. They all played together and had fun. The old toys saw that the new toy was good at playing. They said, "You can play with us now." The new toy was happy, and they all played together in the ancient square.

Once upon a time, there was a cute little boat. The boat loved to sail on the water every day. The boat was very happy when it could sail.
One day, a hero came to the water. The hero needed help to get to the other side. The cute little boat wanted to help, so it let the hero sail with it. The hero was very happy, and they sailed together.
When they got to the other side, the hero said, "Thank you, cute little boat!" The boat was very happy to help. The hero and the boat became good friends. The moral of the story is to always help others when you can, and you will make good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to read his favorite book every day. One day, he took his book to school to show his friends.
At school, Tim's teacher asked him to read his book in front of the class. Tim was scared and felt embarrassed. He didn't want to read in front of everyone, but his teacher said it would be fun.
Tim tried to read his book, but he got frustrated because some of the words were hard to say. His friends helped him with the hard words, and soon, Tim was happy. He finished reading his book, and everyone clapped for him. Tim wasn't embarrassed anymore and felt proud of himself.

Once upon a time, there was a dependable dog named Max. Max liked to run and play with his friends. One day, Max saw a shiny medal high up in a tree. He wanted to get it for his friend, Sam.
Max tried to jump and reach the medal, but it was too high. Just then, a big bird flew down and took the medal in its beak. Max was sad and thought the bird would fly away with the medal. But something unexpected happened.
