The bird did not fly away. Instead, it dropped the medal right into Max's paws. Max was very happy and thanked the bird. He took the medal to Sam, who was very surprised. They both laughed and played, knowing that sometimes, good things can fall from the sky.

Once upon a time, there was a small town with a pretty church. The church had a big bell that rang every Sunday. People in the town loved to go to the church and listen to the bell.
One day, the people in the town decided to have a big party. They wanted to include everyone, even the little kids. So, they made yummy food for everyone to share. They had sweet cakes, juicy fruits, and tasty sandwiches.
At the party, the people learned that sharing and including everyone made them all happy. They saw that when they were kind and shared yummy food, everyone had a good time. The moral of the story is that including others and sharing can make everyone happy.

One day, a fish named Bob wanted to swim. He saw a tire in the water. The tire was big and round. Bob thought it would be fun to swim through the tire.
"Hi, tire!" said Bob. "Can I swim through you?" The tire did not talk, but Bob swam through it anyway. As he swam, he started to feel frustrated. He did not know why he felt this way.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big wave came and pushed Bob and the tire far away. Bob tried to swim back, but he could not. He was too tired and the tire was too heavy. In the end, Bob and the tire were lost at sea.

Once, there was a loyal little bird. He liked to live in a temple. Every day, he would fly around the temple and whistle. All the animals in the temple liked his whistling. It was sweet and gentle.
One day he sang so loudly that even people in the nearby town could hear him. Everyone was amazed by his beautiful music. They thought that he must be very special.
From then on, the bird would wake up early each morning and whistle from the top of the temple. His music made everyone in the town very happy. Even the animals would dance around whenever he sang.
The bird was so loyal that no matter how many times he flew away, he always came back to the temple. He would never forget about the place or the people who enjoyed his music for so many years.

Tom and Anna are twins. They like to play with their toys in their room. But sometimes they make a big mess and do not put their toys away.
One day, Mom comes to their room and sees toys everywhere. She is not happy. She says, "Tom and Anna, you need to tidy your room. It is very messy. Please put your toys in the box and make your beds."
Tom and Anna do not want to tidy their room. They want to keep playing. They say, "Mom, please, can we play a little more? We are having fun."
Mom shakes her head. She says, "No, you cannot play more. You need to tidy your room now. It is your job. If you tidy your room, I will give you a treat. But if you do not tidy your room, you will have no treat and no TV."
Tom and Anna do not like this. They want a treat and TV. They look at each other and nod. They decide to tidy their room. They put their toys in the box and make their beds. They work fast and hard.
Mom comes back and sees their room. She is very happy. She says, "Tom and Anna, you did a great job. Your room is very tidy. I am proud of you. You can have a treat and TV. But first, come here and give me a hug."
Tom and Anna run to Mom and hug her. They say, "Thank you, Mom. We love you. You are the best." Mom smiles and kisses them. She says, "I love you too. You are the best. Now, how about a rub on your back? It will make you feel good." Tom and Anna say, "Yes, please. We like a rub on our back." Mom rubs their back and they feel good. They are happy and cozy. They enjoy their treat and TV. They have a good day.

Ben and Mia like to play with cars. They have many cars of different colors and sizes. They like to make them go fast and make noises. Vroom, vroom!
One day, they find a big box in the garage. They open it and see a big car inside. It is red and shiny. It has four wheels and a motor. Ben and Mia are very happy. They want to play with the big car.
"Can we ride the big car, Mom?" Ben asks.
"Sorry, sweetie, the big car is broken. The motor does not work. It is Daddy's old car. He wants to fix it someday." Mom says.
Ben and Mia are sad. They want to make the big car go. They have an idea.
"Maybe we can find something to make the motor work. Let's search in the garage." Mia says.
They search in the garage. They find many things. They find a hammer, a screwdriver, a wrench, a flashlight, a battery, and some wires. They do not know what they are, but they think they can help.
They take the things to the big car. They open the hood and see the motor. It is dirty and rusty. They try to use the things they found. They hit the motor with the hammer. They twist the wires with the screwdriver. They connect the battery with the flashlight. They make a big mess.
Suddenly, the big car makes a loud noise. It starts to shake and smoke. The motor is working!
"Wow, we did it! We fixed the big car!" Ben says.
"Let's ride the big car, Ben!" Mia says.
They jump in the big car and turn the key. The big car moves forward. It goes very fast. It goes out of the garage and into the street. Ben and Mia are scared. They do not know how to stop the big car.
"Help, Mom! Help, Dad!" They scream.
Mom and Dad hear them. They run out of the house and see the big car. They are shocked. They chase the big car and try to catch it.
"Ben, Mia, stop the big car! Press the brake!" Mom shouts.
"What is the brake?" Ben asks.
"The pedal on the floor!" Dad says.
Ben and Mia look at the floor. They see two pedals. They do not know which one is the brake. They press both of them. The big car stops. Mom and Dad catch up and open the door. They hug Ben and Mia.
"Are you okay, sweetie? That was very dangerous. You should never play with the big car. It is not a toy." Mom says.
"We are sorry, Mom. We just wanted to make the motor work. We did not know it would go so fast." Mia says.
"We are proud of you for being smart and brave, but you have to be careful. The big car is not safe. It needs a lot of work. Maybe when you are older, you can help Daddy fix it." Dad says.
Ben and Mia nod. They are sorry for making trouble. They are also happy for making the motor work. They learned a lot about the big car. They still like to play with cars, but they prefer their small ones. They are more fun and easier to control. Vroom, vroom!

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the store with her mom. They needed to buy some cream for their cake. Lily loved the store because she could choose her favorite snacks.
While they were at the store, Lily saw a big spinning wheel. She wanted to try it, so her mom let her. Lily spun the wheel round and round. When she stopped, she felt dizzy. She laughed and her mom laughed too.
Then, something unexpected happened. A little mouse came out from behind the wheel. It was dizzy too! The mouse had been spinning with the wheel. Lily and her mom were surprised. They gave the dizzy mouse some cream to make it feel better. The mouse was happy and ran away. Lily and her mom went home with a fun story to tell.

Once upon a time, there was an obedient girl named Lily. She loved to measure things. One day, Lily went to the park to play. She saw a big flower and decided to measure it.
Lily took her ruler and started to measure the flower. As she was measuring, something unexpected happened. The flower started to grow bigger and bigger! Lily was very surprised.
The big flower then said, "Thank you, Lily, for measuring me. I am a magic flower, and your ruler made me grow!" Lily and the magic flower became best friends, and they played together in the park every day.

Once upon a time, there was a modest angel named Amy. She lived in a pretty house in the sky. Every day, she would sit on a big, fluffy cloud and watch the children play below.
One day, Amy saw a little girl named Lily. Lily was sad because she had no friends to play with. Amy decided to visit Lily and help her find friends. She flew down from her cloud and said, "Hi, I am Amy. Why are you sad?"
Lily looked up and saw the angel. She said, "I am sad because I have no friends to play with." Amy smiled and said, "I will be your friend, and I will help you find more friends."
Amy and Lily played together every day. They had so much fun, and soon other children joined them. Lily was happy because she had many friends now. And Amy, the modest angel, was happy too because she helped Lily.

Once upon a time there was a girl called Sarah. She was very good at school, and her teacher thought she was the best student ever.
One day Sarah's teacher said: "Today Sarah you have an exam. Please try to do your best!".
Sarah was a little bit scared because it was her first exam.
She worked hard and tried her best, and in the end she did really well.
When Sarah had finished the exam, her teacher said: "Well done Sarah, you did very good! I'm very proud of you".
Sarah learnt an important lesson that day: with hard work and trying her best, good things can happen. Always try your best and never give up, and in the end you will get a good result.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play with their toys and make a lot of noise. But their mom does not like it when they make a mess in their room. She tells them to tidy up their stuff before they can go outside.
"Anna, Ben, it is time to clean your room!" mom says. "You have too many toys on the floor and on the bed. Put them in the box or in the shelf. Then you can go outside and play with your friends."
"But mom, we are having fun!" Anna says. "We are playing with our dolls and cars and blocks. We do not want to stop."
"Me too!" Ben says. "We are making a castle and a town and a zoo. We do not want to tidy up."
Mom shakes her head. "You can play with your toys, but you have to keep your room tidy. If you do not tidy up your stuff, you will not be allowed to go outside. Do you understand?"
Anna and Ben look at each other. They do not want to miss the fun outside. They want to play with their friends and run and jump and slide. They decide to listen to mom and tidy up their stuff.
"Okay, mom, we will tidy up our stuff," Anna says.
"Sorry, mom, we will put our toys away," Ben says.
They start to pick up their toys and put them in the box or in the shelf. They work together and help each other. Soon, their room is tidy and neat.
"Good job, Anna and Ben!" mom says. "You have tidied up your stuff very well. Now you can go outside and play with your friends. But remember to keep your room tidy next time, okay?"
"Okay, mom, we will," Anna and Ben say. They hug mom and run outside. They are happy and proud. They have tidied up their stuff and they are allowed to play.

Jack was a happy two year old boy, who loved to dance. He played music every day and moved his feet. One day, he heard a mysterious sound coming from the empty forest nearby. He was inspired to explore it and started to dance his way toward the sound. When he reached the center of the forest, he saw an empty lake surrounded by a beautiful meadow. The sound he heard was enchanting, and he decided to dance around the lake. 
Jack was so focused on the music and enjoy his dance, that he didn't notice a big rock right in front of him. Suddenly, he tripped and fell, and started to cry. His mother, who had been following him, ran to his side and hugged him tight. She said to him: "Jack, you should have been careful and not let the mystery of this place consume you. You should always stay away from danger, even if they seem exciting."
Jack was sad but he learnt his lesson. From that day on, he always checked his surroundings when he went somewhere new. He reminded himself that it is important to stay alert and never get too distracted, even when something mysterious and exciting appears.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful bird named Blue. Blue loved to soar high in the sky. One day, while Blue was flying, he saw a little girl named Mia.
Mia was sad because she lost her watch. She looked everywhere, but she could not find it. Blue saw Mia and wanted to help her. He flew down and said, "Hi Mia, I can help you find your watch."
Together, Blue and Mia looked for the watch. They looked under the trees and behind the rocks. Finally, Blue saw something shiny near the pond. He soared down and picked up the watch with his beak.
Mia was so happy to have her watch back. She thanked Blue for being so helpful. From that day on, Blue and Mia became the best of friends. They played together every day, and Blue always helped Mia when she needed it.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch cartoons on TV. His favorite cartoon had a funny cat and a silly dog. They would always play and have fun.
One day, Tim saw a sharp pencil on the floor. He picked it up and decided to draw his own cartoon. He drew a cat and a dog just like the ones on TV. He showed his drawing to his mom and said, "Look, Mom! I made a cartoon!"
His mom looked at the drawing and smiled. "That's great, Tim! But be careful with that sharp pencil. It can hurt you."
Tim didn't listen to his mom because he was deaf. He couldn't hear her warning. He went back to drawing, but he accidentally poked his finger with the sharp pencil. It hurt a lot, and he started to cry.
His mom came over and saw what happened. She hugged him and said, "I told you to be careful, Tim." She took the sharp pencil away, and Tim learned that he should listen to his mom, even if he couldn't hear her.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a tough boy who loved to play outside. One cold day, he saw ice on the ground. Tim thought it was fun to slide on the ice.
Tim's mom saw him playing on the ice. She said, "Tim, be careful! The ice can make you fall." Tim did not listen to his mom. He kept playing on the ice. Then, he fell down and hurt his knee.
Tim's mom came to help him. She said, "I will teach you how to be safe on the ice." Tim listened to his mom this time. She showed him how to walk slowly and hold onto things. Tim learned that it is important to listen and learn from others.

One day, a boy named Tim got a new camera. He was so happy. He wanted to take pictures of everything. He saw a cat, a dog, and a big tree. He took pictures of them all.
Tim had a game called "Match the Picture". He wanted to play with his friend, Sally. They would take turns looking at the pictures and finding the match. But Tim was foolish. He did not know how to play the game right.
Tim showed Sally the cat picture. Sally found the match. Then, Tim showed Sally the dog picture. Sally found the match again. Tim was sad because he did not win. He thought he was supposed to find the match too.
Then, Sally told Tim how to play the game right. They played again and had lots of fun. Tim was not foolish anymore. He learned to play the game right and they both had a great day with the camera and the matching game.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. He was very glad because it was a sunny day. Tim saw a big tunnel in the park. He wanted to sit inside the tunnel and play with his toys.
But there was a problem. A big dog was sitting in the tunnel. Tim was sad because he wanted to sit there too. He thought of a plan to make the dog leave the tunnel.
Tim got a big ball and threw it far away. The dog saw the ball and ran to get it. The dog was happy to play with the ball. Now, the tunnel was empty. Tim went inside and sat down. He played with his toys and was very glad. The dog was happy too, playing with the ball.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful poppy field. Every day, children from the village would go there to play. 
One day, two children went to the poppy field. One was an older brother and the other was his three-year-old sister. 
As the two children approached the field, the three-year-old said, "I want to stir the poppies!" The older brother said no, "That's dangerous!" 
But the three-year-old wouldn't listen. She started stirring the poppies with a stick. Suddenly, the poppies started to swirl around her. She gasped in surprise!
The older brother ran over and pulled her out of the poppy whirlwind. He said, "You should have listened! Stirring the poppies can be very dangerous."
The three-year-old nodded and apologized. She promised never to stir the poppies again. From then on, they just admired the beautiful poppy field from outside.

Once upon a time, there was a charming little girl named Lily. She loved to play and lead her friends on fun adventures. One sunny day, Lily and her friends went to the park to play.
As they played, they saw a big oven in the middle of the park. They were very surprised because they had never seen an oven outside before. They all wondered why it was there. Lily decided to lead her friends to the oven to take a closer look.
When they got to the oven, they saw a big cake inside. The oven door opened, and out came a talking cat! The cat said, "Surprise! I made this cake for you, Lily, and your friends. I heard you are a great leader, and I wanted to thank you for being so charming and kind." Lily and her friends were so happy, they laughed and shared the cake with the talking cat. They all had a great day at the park.

One day, a sunflower and a little boy went for a walk. The sunflower was tall and yellow. The boy liked the sunflower a lot. It was a warm day and the sun was shining bright. They decided to march together to the park.
At the park, the boy said, "Sunflower, let's play!" The sunflower nodded and they played catch. They were both very happy. The sunflower liked playing with the boy too.
But then, a big wind came. The wind was too strong for the sunflower. The sunflower tried to stand tall, but it couldn't. The wind broke the sunflower. The boy was sad and cried, "Oh no, my sunflower!" The sunflower could not play with the boy anymore. The boy went home with a broken heart.

Once upon a time there was a spoiled little boy named Andy. He was only three, but his parents gave him anything he asked for. One day they gave him a special present: a box with something in it. Andy opened the box, but he couldn't see what was inside. He peeked.
He saw a bright light that made him blink. "What is it?" he asked.
His dad chuckled and said, "It's a surprise, Andy. Just wait and you'll see."
But the little boy was impatient and he kept peeking. Then one day he saw something he should not have seen. It was a sight that was too dangerous for a little boy like him.
Andy was scared, but he wanted to peek again. He was about to when suddenly his mom ran up to him, grabbed the box, and scolded him.
"Andy, never peek at things that you're not supposed to!" she said sternly.
The little boy learned his lesson. He was too spoiled, and often he couldn't fight the temptation to peek.
The moral of the story is that it can be dangerous to peek at things that we're not supposed to. Knowing our limits and exercising restraint can save us from many things.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to eat sandwiches. One day, he wanted to try a different sandwich. He asked his mom to make a new one for him.
His mom said, "Okay, Tim, let's make a different sandwich today." She took out bread, cheese, and some apple slices. Tim helped his mom put the sandwich together. He felt happy to make something new.
Tim took a big bite of his different sandwich. He smiled and said, "Yummy! I like this sandwich, Mom!" His mom smiled back and said, "I'm glad you like it, Tim." From that day on, Tim loved to eat different sandwiches.

Once there was a farmer who had a very flexible nap. He could nap anywhere, anytime of day. One day, the farmer went to his field to nap. He was feeling so tired that he completely forgot about his work. He napped for so long that his crops didn't get harvested in time and so he lost all of his money. The farmer was so sad and he never wanted to nap so late again.

One day, a boy and a girl wanted to have a picnic. They felt very happy. They asked their mom to order some food for them. "Mom, can you order food for our picnic?" the boy asked. Mom said, "Yes, I will order food for you."
The boy and the girl went to the park. They found a nice spot to sit. They put a big blanket on the grass. But the grass was wet and it made them feel uncomfortable. They did not like it. But they still wanted to have their picnic.
Then, a big wind came. It blew their food away. The boy and the girl were sad. They did not have a picnic anymore. They went back home with no food and no fun. They learned that sometimes things do not go as planned.

Once upon a time, a little white dog named Spot lived in a small house. Spot had a big yellow ball that he loved to play with every day. One day, Spot could not find his yellow ball. He looked everywhere but it was not there. Spot was very sad.
Spot went outside to look for his yellow ball. He asked his friend, the brown cat named Whiskers, "Did you see my yellow ball?" Whiskers said, "No, I did not see your yellow ball. But let's look for it together!" So Spot and Whiskers looked for the yellow ball all around the yard.
As they were looking, they noticed a little girl crying near the fence. Spot and Whiskers went to her and asked, "Why are you crying?" The little girl said, "I lost my red toy car." Spot and Whiskers decided to help her find her red toy car. They searched together, and soon they found the red toy car under a bush. The little girl was so happy, and she said, "Thank you, Spot and Whiskers!"
Then, the little girl noticed something behind the bush. It was Spot's yellow ball! Spot was so happy to have his yellow ball back. The little girl, Spot, and Whiskers played together for the rest of the day.
The moral of the story is that when you help others, good things happen to you too.

Lily and Ben love to play with their kayak. They have a big lake near their house, where they can paddle and splash. Sometimes they see fish and ducks and frogs in the water.
One day, they decide to go on an adventure. They pack some snacks and water in their kayak, and put on their hats and life jackets. They say goodbye to their mom and dad, and push their kayak into the lake.
"Where should we go?" Lily asks Ben.
"Let's go to the island!" Ben says. "It has a big tree and a cave. Maybe we can find some treasure!"
They paddle hard with their oars, and soon they see the island. They are very excited. They land their kayak on the shore, and tie it to a branch. They grab their snacks and water, and start to explore.
They find the big tree and the cave. They also see some flowers and berries and rocks. They eat some of their snacks, and drink some of their water. They have a lot of fun.
"Look, Lily!" Ben says. "I found a stick. I can use it to mark our names on the tree. Then everyone will know we were here!"
He takes the stick and writes "Lily and Ben" on the bark. He makes the letters big and clear.
"That's a good idea, Ben!" Lily says. "But we should also mark our kayak, so we can find it again. The lake is very big, and there are many kayaks. We don't want to lose ours!"
She takes the stick and writes "Lily and Ben" on the side of their kayak. She makes the letters big and clear.
They are very proud of their marks. They feel like powerful explorers. They hug each other and smile.
They decide to go back to their mom and dad. They untie their kayak, and get in. They paddle hard with their oars, and soon they see their house. They are very happy.
They tell their mom and dad about their adventure. They show them their marks on the kayak and the tree. Their mom and dad are very proud of them. They hug them and kiss them.
They have a big dinner, and then go to bed. They dream of their kayak and their island. They are very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a lady who was kind and original. She always tried to support her neighbors by offering them help. One day, she offered to babysit for a family in her neighborhood. The family was so grateful that they couldn't thank her enough. 
The lady said, "It's my pleasure to help. It's the least I can do." So, the family went off and the lady stayed at their house taking care of the little ones.
But the lady was not as original as the family had hoped. She spent the entire time playing the same old game with the kids, even though the kids asked her to do something different. The kids didn't really have fun, but the lady kept insisting on the same old game.
Eventually, the family came back home and saw how unhappy the kids were. Furious, they scolded the lady for not being more original. The lady was so embarrassed that she could not even apologize.
The moral of the story is: No matter how much you want to help, it's always important to be original and support in a way that's enjoyable for everyone.

Once upon a time, in a small pond, there was a little fish named Fin. Fin loved to swim all day with his friends. They would play games and have lots of fun.
One day, Fin saw a big rock in the pond. He wanted to swim to the top and shout to his friends. But every time he tried, he could not reach the top. Fin felt frustrated and sad.
Fin's friends saw him and wanted to help. They all swam together, and with their help, Fin reached the top of the rock. He shouted with joy, and they all laughed and played together. Fin was happy and not frustrated anymore.

Once upon a time there was a leopard named Leo. He lived alone in the jungle with his friends the birds and the monkeys.
One day, Leo was walking through the jungle, feeling curious and alert as ever. Suddenly he heard something strange in the trees. He climbed up, and saw a small green creature. The creature was shrinking and shrinking and shrinking!
Leo said, "What's going on? What are you doing?". The creature replied, "I'm shrinking away from danger. There is a bigger leopard in the trees and he is trying to catch me!".
Leo was very brave and he said,"No, he won't get you! I will protect you!" Leo raced around the trees and chased away the bigger leopard. The small creature thanked Leo and said goodbye.
Leo was very proud of his brave act. He felt so proud that he roared to tell the jungle of his bravery. He felt brave and alert with a feeling of joy.

Once upon a time there was a man who had a smelly old cooler. It smelled so bad that the man had to keep it outside. One day, he went and opened the cooler. He was so surprised to see that there were lots of gross bugs inside! The man was disgusted. He quickly took the cooler and released all of the gross bugs outside. They all flew away into the sky.
Suddenly a gust of cold air came out of the cooler. It was so cold that the man could feel it all the way up his arms! He reached in and felt the sides of the cooler. It was very cold! He reached his hand in further and felt something round and bumpy. He pulled it out and saw that it was a huge block of ice! 
The man was very excited and raced inside his house to get a container for the ice. He put it all in the container and brought it inside, where it quickly melted and made the air nice and cool. 
He was so happy that he was able to release the bugs and find the cold block of ice in his old and disgusting cooler!

Tom and Sue wanted to go on a trip to the big hill. They had a mule to help them carry their things. They had to pack their bags with food, water, clothes and toys.
"Let's pack our bags, Sue," Tom said. "We have to leave soon."
"OK, Tom," Sue said. "I will pack my doll and my book. What will you pack?"
"I will pack my ball and my hat. And some snacks for the mule. He likes carrots and apples."
They packed their bags and put them on the mule. The mule was strong and could carry a lot. They said goodbye to their mom and dad and started to walk to the big hill.
But on the way, they saw a man with a weak mule. The weak mule had too many things on his back. He looked tired and sad. The man was mean and hit the weak mule with a stick.
"Stop, stop!" Tom and Sue shouted. "You are hurting the mule. He is too weak to carry so much. You have to take some things off his back."
The man looked at them and laughed. "You are silly kids. This is my mule and I can do what I want. He has to work hard for me. Go away and mind your own business."
Tom and Sue felt angry and sorry for the weak mule. They wanted to help him. They had an idea. They took some things from their own bags and put them on the weak mule's back. Then they took some things from the weak mule's back and put them in their own bags. They did this quickly and quietly, while the man was not looking.
"Come on, Sue, let's go," Tom whispered. "We have to hurry before the man sees us."
They ran away with their mule and the weak mule followed them. The man did not notice until it was too late. He shouted and cursed, but he could not catch them.
Tom and Sue reached the big hill and found a nice spot to camp. They shared their food and water with the weak mule and gave him some hugs and strokes. The weak mule felt happy and grateful. He decided to stay with them and be their friend.
They had a fun and cozy night under the stars. They played with their toys and read their book and told stories. They were glad they had helped the weak mule. They knew they had done a good thing.

Once upon a time, there lived two friends, a bear and a hedgehog. The hedgehog was very clever and he had a great idea.
One day, the hedgehog said to the bear, ""Let's design something together! I can make spikes and you can make something else."
The bear was excited but he didn't know what to make.
"Come on," said the hedgehog, "Let's make a mountain. I will make spikes to go on the mountain and you can make something else."
The bear thought for a moment and then he said, "Let's make a disgusting soup to go in the mountain."
The hedgehog thought this was a wonderful idea and so the two friends got to work. The hedgehog made the spikes and the bear made the soup. They put them all together and it looked amazing. The friends were very proud of what they had designed.
The end.

One day, a thoughtful cat and a dog went to play. They wanted to have fun together. The cat said, "Let's play with the ball!" The dog agreed, and they started to play.
While they were playing, a big car arrived. The car was very noisy. The cat and the dog stopped playing and looked at the car. The dog said, "I don't like the noisy car. Let's keep playing." The cat was scared and didn't want to play anymore.
The dog got mad and didn't want to be friends with the cat. The cat was sad and went home. They didn't play together again. This was a bad ending for the cat and the dog.

One day, a boy named Tim found a shiny copper coin on the ground. He was very happy and showed it to his friend, Sally. Sally said, "Wow, that's a pretty coin! We should send it to someone."
Tim and Sally went to the post office and put the copper coin in a small box. They wrote a note that said, "We found this pretty coin. We hope you like it!" They sent the box to a far away place.
A few days later, Tim and Sally heard a loud noise outside. They went to see what it was. A big truck had come to their house. The driver said, "I have a big surprise for you!" He opened the truck, and it was full of shiny copper coins! The person who got their coin sent them a thank you gift. Tim and Sally were so happy and shared the coins with all their friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia had a new purse. She loved her purse very much. It was pink and had a big bow on it. Mia took her purse everywhere she went.
One day, Mia went to the park with her mom. While playing, she met a big, fluffy cat. The cat said, "Meow, I am very tired." Mia saw the cat yawn. She wanted to help the cat feel better.
Mia opened her new purse and found a soft blanket inside. She gave the blanket to the cat. The cat said, "Thank you, Mia." The cat curled up on the blanket and went to sleep. Mia was happy she could help her new friend.

Tom and Lily were twins who liked to play outside. One day, they saw a big church with a tall tower. They wanted to go inside and see what was there.
"Let's go, Tom!" Lily said. "Maybe we can find some toys or books or candy."
Tom nodded and followed Lily. They ran across the street and opened the door of the church. It was dark and quiet inside. They saw some benches, some candles, some pictures and some statues.
"Wow, this is cool!" Tom said. "Look at that statue, Lily. It has wings and a halo. Is it an angel?"
Lily looked at the statue. It was made of stone and stood on a pedestal. It had a long dress, a smile and a sword.
"I think so, Tom. It looks pretty. Can I touch it?" Lily said.
She walked towards the statue and reached out her hand. She wanted to feel the wings and the halo. But she did not see the rope that was around the pedestal. She tripped over the rope and fell down. She hit her head on the floor and started to cry.
"Ow, ow, ow!" Lily cried. "It hurts, Tom. Help me!"
Tom heard Lily's cry and ran to her. He saw blood on her forehead and tears in her eyes. He was scared and sad. He hugged Lily and tried to comfort her.
"I'm sorry, Lily. I'm here, I'm here. Don't cry, please. You'll be okay." Tom said.
But Lily did not stop crying. She was in pain and afraid. She wanted to go home and see her mom and dad.
Then they heard a loud voice behind them.
"Hey, you two! What are you doing here? This is not a playground. This is a church. You have no respect. You have broken the statue. You have made a mess. You have to leave. Now!" The voice said.
Tom and Lily turned around and saw a man in a black suit. He had a cross on his chest and a frown on his face. He was the priest of the church. He was angry and stern. He walked towards them and pointed at the door.
"Go, go, go! And don't come back. You are bad children. You are not welcome here." The priest said.
Tom and Lily were more scared and sad. They did not mean to break the statue or make a mess. They just wanted to see the church and have some fun. They did not like the priest or his words. They wanted to say sorry, but they could not. They got up and ran to the door. They left the church and went back to their home. They never went to the church again.

Once upon a time, in a cheerful little town, there lived a girl named Lily. Lily loved to create things. She would make pretty pictures with her crayons and build tall towers with her blocks.
One sunny day, Lily went to the garden with her mom. They saw a strawberry plant with big, red strawberries. Lily was very happy. She wanted to create something with the strawberries.
Lily and her mom picked the strawberries and went back home. They made a yummy strawberry cake together. Lily was so proud of her creation. She shared the cake with her friends and family, and everyone was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Bob. He loved to play outside in the sunshine. One day, Bob was playing in the garden when he decided to fly his sharp kite. The kite zoomed up in the sky, catching the popular sunshine.
Bob was so proud of his kite. He took it to his school to show his friends. All of his friends were amazed and wanted to try fly a kite too.
Bob said to them, "I will help you fly it! It's easy."
His friends were very excited and they all ran to the park. Bob showed them how to use the kite's sharp string to make the kite fly high in the sky. Everyone clapped with joy as their kites took to the air.
Bob and his friends had lots of fun playing in the sunshine with the kites. From then on, Bob was one of the most popular kids in school. He was proud of his sharp kite and of the friends he had made.

Once upon a time, in a dark room, there was a big print on the wall. The print was of a happy sun and a pretty flower. The room was dark, but the print made it feel like a sunny day.
One day, a little girl named Lily found a ball in the room. She liked to play with the ball. She would throw it and then run to catch it. Lily was having so much fun!
But then, Lily threw the ball too high. It hit the print on the wall. The print started to fall down. Lily saw this and was scared. She did not want the print to break. So, she ran as fast as she could and caught the print before it could drop on the floor. Lily was happy that she saved the print. She hung it back on the wall, and the room was sunny and pretty again.

Once upon a time, there was a dancer. She was very impressive. She could jump high and spin fast. Everyone loved to watch her dance. She danced in a big room with a shiny floor.
One day, the dancer was sad. She could not dance because the floor was wet. She needed the floor to be dry. She tried to wait, but she wanted to dance now. She thought and thought about how to dry the floor.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big, friendly dog came into the room. The dog saw the wet floor and knew the dancer was sad. The dog had an idea. He ran around the room and wagged his tail. His tail was like a mop, and it dried the floor!
The dancer was so happy. She could dance again, thanks to the dog. From that day on, the dancer and the dog were best friends. They danced together, and everyone loved to watch them. And they always made sure the floor was dry.

It was a beautiful day outside and Jack wanted to ride his bike. He hopped on and quickly pedaled outside. He waved to his mom and said goodbye.
Jack enjoyed riding around the neighbourhood and he loved being able to go fast. He wanted to try something different so he decided to join a network of high bike riders. He asked some people if he could join and they said yes! 
Jack was excited and he tried to keep up with the other riders. But he quickly realized he couldn't go as high as them. He felt embarrassed and he stopped trying.
The other riders noticed he was struggling and they started to make fun of him. Jack felt angry and he started to ride away. But one of the riders zoomed past him and blocked his way. Jack was scared and tried to get around him, but the rider wouldn't move.
Jack started to cry and he ran home. He never joined the network again and he was too scared to ride his bike anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very sleepy one night, but he wanted to eat his soup before going to bed. He had a big spoon to eat his soup with.
As Tim was eating his soup, he saw a tiny bug on his spoon. He was scared and screamed, "Mom, there's a bug on my spoon!" His mom came running to see what was wrong.
Mom looked at the spoon and said, "It's just a little bug, Tim. It won't hurt you. But it's important to be calm and not scream when you see something scary." Tim listened to his mom and learned that screaming doesn't help when you're scared. He finished his soup and went to bed, feeling brave and ready for a good night's sleep.
The moral of the story is to stay calm when you're scared and not to scream, because it won't help you feel better.

Once upon a time, there was a unique cat named Tom. Tom had a big, colorful lollipop. He loved his lollipop very much.
One day, Tom went for a walk with his lollipop. He met a small dog named Tim. Tim saw the lollipop and wanted it, too. Tom said, "Let's be friends, and I will share my lollipop with you."
Tom and Tim walked together. Tom held the lollipop, and Tim followed. Tom was happy to lead the way. They walked and shared the lollipop. They became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big temple. In the temple, there lived a kind man named Tom. Tom was a little anxious because he had lost his cat. The cat was gray and very soft.
One day, Tom met a girl named Sue. Sue said, "Hi, Tom! Did you call your cat?" Tom said, "Yes, I called and called, but she did not come back." Sue wanted to help Tom find his cat. So, they went to look for the cat together.
They looked near the temple and in the trees. Then, they heard a soft sound. It was the cat! She was stuck in a bush. Tom and Sue helped the cat get out of the bush. Tom was happy, and he thanked Sue. They all went back to the temple, and they were not anxious anymore.

Once upon a time there was a farm. The farm had lots of animals and people that played in the fields. One day, a frog appeared. He was big, green and wobbly. Everyone was very surprised to see him.
The people on the farm were embarrassed. They had never seen a frog like this before! The frog watched them with his big round eyes.
The farmer tried to make the frog go away, but it didn't go. He offered it food, but the frog didn't move. Everyone was starting to get worried.
Suddenly, the frog hopped away and disappeared into the grass. Everyone was relieved and smiled. They learnt that it was ok to be different and not to be embarrassed by it.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Anna. She loved to play and imagine with her toys. One day, Anna's aunt came to visit. Anna was very happy and excited to see her aunt. Her aunt always brought her fun surprises.
Anna's aunt gave her a big box wrapped in pretty paper. Anna opened the box and found a beautiful dress inside. The dress was so pretty, it looked like a princess dress. Anna's aunt told her, "Put on the dress, and we will go to a special place." Anna couldn't wait to see the special place.
Anna put on the beautiful dress, and her aunt took her to a big, colorful room. There were lots of other kids there, all dressed up like princesses and princes. They played games, danced, and had yummy treats. Anna had so much fun, just like she imagined. At the end of the day, Anna thanked her sweet aunt for the best surprise ever.

Once upon a time, there was a tidy sunflower. It lived in a big garden with many other flowers. The sunflower was happy because it had a lot of sun to grow big and strong.
One day, a big cloud came and covered the sun. The sunflower was sad because it needed the sun to grow. It wanted to celebrate its birthday, but it was not happy without the sun. All the other flowers tried to help the sunflower feel better, but the sunflower was still sad.
Then, a little girl came to the garden. She saw the sad sunflower and wanted to help. The little girl blew on the cloud, and the cloud went away. The sun came out again, and the sunflower was happy. The sunflower and all the other flowers celebrated the sunflower's birthday in the warm sun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was playing with his toys near a big bowl of water. His mom said, "Be careful, Tim! Don't spoil your toys."
Tim didn't listen and put his toy car in the water. The toy car got all wet, and Tim felt miserable. He knew he had made a mistake. He said, "I'm sorry, Mom."
His mom gave him a towel to dry the toy car. Tim wiped the toy car with the towel until it was dry. He promised to be more careful next time. From that day on, Tim never spoiled his toys again, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to ask questions. He would ask his mom, dad, and friends for answers. One day, Tim saw a big table in his house. He wanted to know what it was for. He asked his mom, but she was busy. So, Tim decided to find the answer himself.
Tim thought and thought about what the table could be for. He tried to put his toys on it, but they fell off. Then, he tried to sit on it, but it was too high. Finally, Tim had an idea. He remembered that his mom would put food on the table for him to eat. So, he went to the kitchen and found some food.
Tim put the food on the table and called his mom. She came and saw the food on the table. She was happy and said, "Good job, Tim! You found the answer! The table is for eating." Tim felt successful and proud of himself. From that day on, Tim always knew what the table was for, and he continued to find answers to all his questions.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved tasty ice cream. One day, Lily's ice cream fell on the ground. She was very sad.
Lily's friend, Tim, saw her crying. He asked, "Why are you sad, Lily?" Lily said, "My tasty ice cream fell on the ground." Tim had an idea. He said, "Let's go to the magic tree. It can restore things."
Lily and Tim went to the magic tree. They asked the tree to restore Lily's ice cream. The tree said, "I can help, but first you must find my lost youth." The tree was old and needed its youth back.
Lily and Tim searched for the tree's youth. They found it hidden in a small cave. They gave the youth back to the tree. The tree became young and strong again.
The tree was happy and restored Lily's tasty ice cream. Lily and Tim thanked the tree and enjoyed their ice cream together. They were very happy.

One day, a boy named Tim and his dog, Spot, went to play outside. They wanted to play in a tent. Tim said, "Spot, we need to find a tent to play in."
Tim and Spot looked and looked. They found a tent in the park. They went inside and started to play. Spot ran around and around. Tim laughed and laughed. Then, Spot felt dizzy from running so much. He stopped and sat down.
A girl named Sue came by and saw them playing. She said, "That's my tent! You can't play in there." Tim and Spot felt sad. Sue saw how dizzy Spot was and felt bad. She said, "I'm sorry. You can play in my tent if you want."
Tim, Spot, and Sue played together all day. They learned that sharing is good and makes everyone happy.

One day, a boy named Tim played baseball with his friends. They liked to play and have fun. Tim was very impressive at hitting the ball. His friends were happy to watch him hit the ball far away.
One day, while they were playing, a big dog came to the field. The big dog picked up the baseball in his mouth. Tim and his friends were surprised. They did not know what to do. The big dog looked at them and wagged his tail.
Tim had an idea. He asked the big dog to lead them to his house. The big dog took them to a new field with many more baseballs. Tim and his friends were very happy. They played with the big dog and had more fun than ever. The big dog was now their new friend.

Once there was a little mouse who wanted to escape his hole. He had a special file which he was proud of. He asked his mother, "Can I go outside?"
"No," she said, "It is dangerous. Stay here and be safe."
The little mouse wasn't happy. He thought and thought, and eventually an idea popped into his head. He would use his file to dig a tunnel and escape his hole!
He grabbed the file, and began to dig. He dug and dug and dug, until finally he had made a tunnel big enough to walk through.
He grabbed his mother's hand and said, "Come with me! Let's go outside, together."
His mother said, "Ok, if you promise to be careful, we can explore together."
The little mouse was so proud to be able to escape his hole. He and his mom, went exploring and had a wonderful time. Never again did he doubt his escape plan, and never again did he want to stay inside.

One day, a boy named Tim found a big candle. He thought it was a fine candle. He wanted to show it to his friend, Sam. Tim ran to Sam's house with the candle.
"Look, Sam!" said Tim. "I found a fine candle. Let's fill it with pretty colors!" Sam was happy. They found some colors and started to fill the candle with them. They used red, blue, and yellow.
As they filled the candle, something unexpected happened. The candle started to talk! "Thank you for filling me with colors," said the candle. Tim and Sam were very surprised. They had never seen a talking candle before. From that day on, Tim, Sam, and the talking candle became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy fork. He loved to play with his fork all day. One day, he lost his fork. Tim was very sad.
Tim looked for his fork everywhere. He asked his mom, "Mom, have you seen my fork?" His mom said, "No, but keep looking, you will find it." Tim did not give up. He knew he would find his fork.
Later, Tim saw a big box in the corner. He had not looked there yet. He thought, "Maybe my fork is in there!" Tim opened the box and there was his fork! He was so grateful. Just then, he saw a little bug by the box. The bug blinked at him. Tim smiled and said, "Thank you, bug!"

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a thin tree. This tree had a name, and its name was Pear. Pear was very sad because he was not like the other trees. The other trees were big and strong, but Pear was thin and weak.
One day, a big wind came to the forest. The wind was so strong that it could blow the trees down. Pear was scared, but he knew that he had to be brave. He knew that his name meant something special, and he wanted to show the other trees that he was just as strong as they were.
The wind blew and blew, and the big trees tried to stay up. But they were too big and too heavy for the wind. One by one, they fell down. But Pear was still standing. He used his thin body to bend with the wind, and he did not fall. The other trees were so happy for Pear, and they knew that he was strong too. From that day on, Pear was no longer sad, and the other trees loved him just as much as they loved each other.

Once upon a time, there was a dancer named Lily. Lily was a normal girl who loved to dance. One day, Lily went to a big dance show. She was very excited to dance on the stage.
Before the show, Lily's mom said, "Don't forget your dance shoes!" Lily looked for her shoes, but she could not find them. She was very sad. Then, her friend Sam came and said, "I have an extra pair of dance shoes. You can use them!"
Lily was happy and wore Sam's shoes. When it was her turn to dance, something unexpected happened. The shoes started to make her dance in a funny way. Everyone laughed, but Lily kept dancing. At the end of the show, everyone clapped and cheered for Lily. She was happy that she did not forget to dance, even when something unexpected happened.

Once upon a time, there was a big, deep forest. In the forest, there was a small rabbit named Remy. Remy had a best friend, a bird named Betty. Every morning, Betty would wake up Remy to play together.
One day, Remy said, "Betty, please remind me to find food for my family." Betty nodded and flew away. Later, Betty found Remy playing with other animals. "Remy, remember to find food," Betty said. Remy smiled and said, "Thank you, Betty!"
Remy found food for his family and they were all happy. The moral of the story is to always listen to your friends and help them when they need it. It is good to be kind and helpful.

Lily and Ben are playing with their toy soldiers. They like to make them march and fight. They have two teams: red and blue. Lily has the red team and Ben has the blue team.
"Ready, set, go!" Lily says. She makes her soldiers march to the middle of the rug. Ben does the same with his soldiers. They pretend to shoot and hit each other. "Bang, bang, pow!" they say.
Lily and Ben count how many soldiers are left on each team. Lily has four red soldiers and Ben has three blue soldiers. "I have more than you!" Lily says. "You have less than me!"
Ben does not like to lose. He grabs one of Lily's red soldiers and hides it under his pillow. "Now we have the same!" he says. "Three and three!"
Lily sees what Ben did. She is angry. She grabs two of Ben's blue soldiers and throws them in the trash. "Now I have more again!" she says. "Three and zero!"
Ben sees what Lily did. He is sad. He starts to cry. "That's not fair!" he says. "You cheated!"
Lily feels sorry for Ben. She knows she was wrong. She takes the blue soldiers out of the trash and the red soldier out of the pillow. She gives them back to Ben. "I'm sorry, Ben," she says. "You can have your soldiers. Let's play nice."
Ben stops crying. He smiles at Lily. He hugs her. "Thank you, Lily," he says. "You are a good friend. Let's play nice."
Lily and Ben hug each other. They decide to make their soldiers march together. They are quiet and happy. They are good friends.

One day, a boy named Tim found a weird toy. It was a laser. Tim did not know what it did. He wanted to keep it. He asked his mom, "Can I keep this laser, please?"
His mom said, "Okay, but be careful with it."
Tim played with the laser. He pointed it at the wall. The wall changed color! Tim was very surprised. He pointed the laser at his toy car. The toy car turned into a real car! Tim was so happy.
Then, Tim's little sister, Sue, came into the room. She saw the laser and wanted to play too. Tim said, "No, it's mine!" Sue was sad, but then she found a button on the laser. She pushed it. The laser made a funny sound. All of a sudden, the car turned back into a toy and the wall was normal again.
Tim and Sue were surprised, but they decided to keep the laser and share it. They had a lot of fun playing with the weird laser together.

Once upon a time, there was a fisherman. He liked to catch fish. One day, he went to the sea. He wanted to find the best fish.
The fisherman saw a big cave. He said, "I will enter the cave. Maybe there are available fish inside." So, he went in. Inside the cave, he found a big, shiny fish.
The shiny fish said, "Hello, fisherman! If you let me go, I will give you a gift." The fisherman smiled and said, "Okay, shiny fish. I will let you go." The shiny fish was happy and gave the fisherman a big net. The fisherman said, "Thank you, shiny fish!" Now, he could catch more fish than ever before.

One day, a cool cat named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big red tomato in the garden. Tim was hungry, so he ate the tomato. It was very yummy!
After eating the tomato, Tim felt sleepy. He found a soft and warm spot under a tree. Tim decided to take a nap there. He closed his eyes and started to dream.
In his dream, Tim met a kind bird who gave him more tomatoes to eat. They played together and had lots of fun. When Tim woke up from his nap, he was happy and full of energy. He went back home to tell his friends about his cool adventure.

Lily hated smoke. It made her eyes water and her nose hurt. She did not like when her dad smoked in the car. She wished he would stop.
One day, she saw a sign at the store. It had a picture of a cigarette and a red cross. It said, "Smoking is bad for you and your family. Quit today and get a free candy." Lily liked candy. It was sweet and yummy. She had an idea.
She ran to her dad and showed him the sign. "Dad, look! You can get candy if you stop smoking. Please, please, please, can you try?" she begged.
Her dad looked at the sign and then at Lily. He saw her hopeful eyes and her cute smile. He felt a pang in his chest. He knew smoking was bad for him and for Lily. He loved Lily more than anything. He decided to try.
"Okay, Lily, I will try. But you have to help me. You have to remind me not to smoke and to do something else instead. Like play with you or read a book or go for a walk. Can you do that?" he asked.
Lily nodded and hugged him. "Yes, dad, I can do that. I will help you. And then we can share the candy. Thank you, dad. I love you." she said.
Her dad smiled and hugged her back. "I love you too, Lily. And I'm proud of you. You are a very smart and brave girl. Come on, let's go get our candy." he said.
They walked to the store and gave the sign to the lady. She smiled and gave them a big bag of candy. Lily and her dad were happy. They did not need smoke. They had each other and sweet candy.

Once upon a time, there was a nurse named Jane. She loved to help people feel better. One day, she saw a little boy named Tim who had a weird looking cut on his arm. Tim was sad and his arm hurt.
Nurse Jane said, "Don't worry, Tim. I will help you." She took a cloth, soaked it in water, and gently put it on Tim's arm. The cool water made Tim feel better. He smiled and said, "Thank you, Nurse Jane."
As they talked, Tim learned that it's important to be careful when playing. Nurse Jane told him, "Always watch where you are going, so you don't get hurt." Tim promised to be more careful next time. From that day on, he played safely and never got hurt again. And Nurse Jane was always there to help when someone needed her.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily loved to dream. She had a big, dark room where she slept at night. In her dreams, she would go on fun adventures.
One night, Lily had a dream about a world made of spaghetti. She met a talking spaghetti named Mr. Noodle. "Hello, Lily!" said Mr. Noodle. "Welcome to our spaghetti world!"
As they walked and talked, the sky turned dark. Suddenly, the spaghetti world started to shake! Lily and Mr. Noodle were scared. They held hands and hid under a big spaghetti tree.
Then, the shaking stopped. It was a giant meatball rolling by! It was just playing and didn't mean to scare them. Lily and Mr. Noodle laughed and played with the giant meatball. They had a great time in the spaghetti world.

Once upon a time, there was a smelly dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his stick. He would run, jump, and catch the stick all day long. Spot was a happy dog.
One day, Spot went to the park with his friend, Sam. Sam was a little boy with a big heart. Sam would throw the stick, and Spot would mind Sam by bringing it back to him. They played and laughed in the sun.
As they played, a smelly skunk came by. The skunk saw Spot's stick and wanted to play too. Spot and Sam didn't mind. They all played together, and they didn't care about the smell. They were happy friends, having fun with the stick.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to eat gum. One day, he saw an amazing big gum on a high shelf. Tim wanted the gum very much. He tried to reach the gum, but he was too small.
Tim had an idea. He brought a chair and stood on it to reach the amazing gum. But the chair was still not high enough. Tim felt sad. He really wanted the gum.
Then, something unexpected happened. Tim's cat, Fluffy, jumped up and knocked the amazing gum down. Tim was so happy! He picked up the gum and shared it with Fluffy. They both enjoyed the gum together. And that day, Tim and Fluffy became the best of friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a mask. The mask was very pretty. Tim wanted to play with the mask. He put it on his face and looked in the mirror. He was very happy.
Tim went to his friend, Sue. He said, "Look at my mask!" Sue liked the mask too. She asked, "Can I play with it?" Tim said, "Yes, let's play together!" They played with the mask all day. They were grateful to have fun.
At the end of the day, Tim and Sue were tired. They took off the mask and put it away. They said, "Thank you for the fun day!" They were very happy and went to sleep.

Once there was a clever little boy. He wanted to make some food, so he went to ask his mom if he could borrow her pan. She said yes, so he took the pan and went to his kitchen.
He reached for some ingredients and started to mix them together. He added the right amount of salt and sugar and stirred the mixture. After a few minutes, he put the pan on the stove and turned on the heat.
The boy watched and waited. Soon, the mixture started to sizzle and bubble, just like his mom did it. Then the pan began to fill up with yummy smelling steam. 
The little boy had made a delicious snack for himself. He smiled, proud of his work. He had done it all on his own, using the pan he had borrowed from his mom. How clever of him!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim was an honest boy. He always told the truth. One day, Tim found a toy gun. It was a bright red gun. He was very excited to play with it.
Tim went to his friend, Sam. He said, "Sam, come play with me and my new toy gun!" Sam was happy and came to play. They played cops and robbers and had lots of fun.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and took the toy gun away. Tim and Sam were sad. But, they decided to play a new game without the toy gun. They played hide and seek and had even more fun.

Once upon a time, there was a tiny mouse called Bob. He was very modest, and he lived in a small house with no decorations at all. One day, Bob decided he wanted to decorate his home.
Bob got a hammer and started to work. He put up a new mantelpiece, some wallpaper, and a shiny new door. But then, something terrible happened - the hammer slipped from Bob's hands and broke!
Bob was so sad. He had worked hard to decorate his house, but now he had no hammer. He didn't know what to do. He tried to ask his friends for help, but it was no use. The hammer was gone forever. 
In the end, Bob's modest home stayed plain and lonely. He never managed to decorate it.

Lily and Ben like to play sport in the park. They run and kick a ball. They have fun. But one day, the park is very quiet. Lily and Ben do not see any other kids. They wonder why.
They see a big tent near the trees. It has many colors and shapes. They hear some music and laughter. They are curious. They want to see what is inside the tent.
They walk to the tent. They hold hands. They are a little scared. They peek inside the tent. They see many clowns and animals. They see a lion and a monkey. They see a clown with a big red nose and a funny hat. They see a clown with a bike and a horn.
The clowns and animals see Lily and Ben. They smile and wave. They invite them to join the show. Lily and Ben are happy. They forget their fear. They go inside the tent. They have more fun. They laugh and clap. They play sport with the clowns and animals. They have a great day.

Once upon a time there was a dry field. Casey the Cow was walking through it when she came across a fuel truck. The fuel truck was red and shiny. Casey the Cow had never seen a fuel truck before.
She wanted to play with it, so she walked up to it and touched it. Suddenly, a noise came from above and Casey the Cow ran away. It was an airplane flying overhead!
The airplane had a man on board and he was shooting fire out of it! The fire was landing on Casey's dry field, and it was starting to burn.
Casey the Cow was scared and ran around in circles looking for help. Finally, Harrison the Horse showed up and was able to put out the flames before the whole field caught fire. He used buckets of water to put out the fire.
Casey the Cow was so happy that Harrison the Horse had been able to help save the day. The fire was out and the dry field was safe again!

One day, Zoe found an incredible mineral in the garden. She was so excited that she brought it inside to show her mom.
"Mom, look what I found!" Zoe said, holding up the mineral.
Her mom smiled. "That really is incredible! Let's keep it in a safe place."
The next day, Zoe wanted to show her friends the mineral. She quickly returned to the garden, but when she got there the mineral was gone. 
Disappointed, Zoe asked her mom what happened.
"Oh, that's strange!" her mom said. "Maybe an animal took it and will return it soon."
Sure enough, a few days later, the incredible mineral was back in the garden. Zoe was so happy that she hugged it tightly.
"I'm so glad it returned," said Zoe. 
"Yes," said her mom. "It's great when something so special comes back to us."

Lily and Sam were best friends. They liked to play in the park. One day, they saw a big tower in the park. It was made of wood and had a slide and a ladder.
"Let's go to the tower!" Lily said.
"OK!" Sam said.
They ran to the tower and climbed the ladder. They reached the top and looked around. They saw birds and trees and people.
"Wow, this is fun!" Lily said.
"Shh, let's whisper," Sam said. "Maybe we can hear a secret."
They leaned over the edge and listened. They heard a man and a woman talking.
"I'm sorry, I lied to you," the man said. "I'm not honest."
"Why did you lie to me?" the woman said. "I trusted you."
The man and the woman sounded sad and angry. Lily and Sam felt sorry for them.
"Let's go down," Lily said. "This is not a good secret."
"OK," Sam said.
They slid down the slide and ran away from the tower. They decided to play something else.
They learned that lying is bad and honesty is good. They promised to always tell the truth to each other. They were happy and stayed best friends.

One day, a little girl named Lucy had a plan. She wanted to help her mom with the laundry. Lucy was an obedient girl and always did what her mom asked.
Lucy went to the laundry room and found a big pile of clothes. She put the clothes in the washing machine. Then, she added soap and turned it on. The washing machine made a loud noise as it cleaned the clothes.
When the washing machine stopped, Lucy took the wet clothes out. She hung them up to dry in the sun. Her mom was very happy and proud of her. Lucy felt good because she was helpful and obedient.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Mary. She was always playing in the park, and she loved looking for the little squirrels in the trees.
One day, while she was in the park, a squirrel ran up to her. Mary was so happy, but the squirrel seemed scared. Mary wanted to help the little squirrel, so she carefully held out a nut to it.
But as soon as she did, the squirrel ran away. Mary was sad and called out after it: "Don't be scared, little squirrel!"
Mary quickly forgot about the scared squirrel and ran back to the playground to play. But when she got there, she discovered that she had forgotten her teddy bear!
"Oh no," she said. She was so sad and scared. She closed her eyes and wished that the squirrel would be back to help her find her toy.
Suddenly, she heard a rustling in the trees. When she opened her eyes, the squirrel was there. It ran up to her holding her teddy bear in its tiny paws. 
Mary was so relieved and she thanked the little squirrel. From that day on, the squirrel was her best friend!

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Bobo. Bobo loved to spring and play all day long in the green grass. He was a very enthusiastic bunny and always had fun with his friends.
One day, Bobo felt sick. His mom took him to see the doctor. The doctor was a wise old owl who helped animals feel better. Bobo was a little scared, but the doctor was very kind.
The doctor gave Bobo some medicine to make him feel better. Soon, Bobo was back to his old self, springing and playing with his friends. Bobo and his friends were very happy, and they all played together in the sunshine.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to join his friends at the park. One day, he saw an incredible tree with big green leaves. The leaves were as big as his palm. Tim thought it was amazing.
Tim told his friend, "Look at the big tree! Let's go near it." His friend said, "Okay, let's go!" They walked to the tree and looked up. They saw a big nest in the tree. They could not see what was inside, but they were excited.
The next day, Tim and his friend went back to the park. They wanted to see the nest again. But when they got there, they found a baby bird on the ground! It must have fallen from the nest. Tim and his friend were surprised. They knew they had to help the baby bird. They picked it up and put it back in the nest. The baby bird was safe, and Tim and his friend felt very happy.

John was a patient boy. He was worried because he didn't know how to paint. His mum wanted him to paint a picture of his dog, but he couldn't. 
So then his mum took him to the art shop. She showed him the paint and the brushes. John was so excited! He chose all the colours he liked. 
When he went home, his mum said, "Now you can try to paint the picture of your dog." But as soon as John started to paint, he made a big mess. His mum said, "This is not how you paint. I'm very disappointed in you." 
John was so sorry that he had made a mess. He was so worried that he wanted to cry. He had to go to bed without trying again to paint the picture of his dog. It was a very bad ending.

One day, a little girl named Lily saw a disgusting bug. She wanted to run away, but she was brave. She knew she had to help the bug. The bug was stuck in a spider web. Lily said, "Don't worry, bug, I will help you."
Lily tried to release the bug from the spider web. She was careful not to hurt the bug. The bug was free and said, "Thank you, Lily!" The bug flew away and then came back with a gift for Lily.
The bug gave Lily a rainbow. The rainbow was big and pretty. Lily and the bug played under the rainbow all day. They were happy and had fun. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to run. He would run every day, all year long. He ran fast, like a big cat.
One day, Tim saw a disgusting bug. The bug was green and slimy. Tim thought it was yucky. But he was brave and wanted to make friends with the bug.
Tim said, "Hello, bug. Let's run together." The bug looked at Tim and smiled. They ran and ran, and had lots of fun. Tim and the bug became best friends, and they ran together every day.

Lily was playing with her dolls in the garden. She liked to make them talk and have tea parties. She saw a blue spider on a flower. She was not afraid of spiders. She thought they were pretty and smart.
"Hello, blue spider," she said. "Do you want to join my tea party?"
The blue spider looked at Lily. He was shy and scared. He did not know if Lily was nice or mean. He had seen other children scream and run away from spiders. He had seen some children try to hurt spiders.
"Hello, little girl," he said. "I don't know if I can join your tea party. I am very small and you are very big. You might not see me or hear me. You might step on me or spill tea on me."
Lily smiled. She was gentle and kind. She wanted to be friends with the blue spider. She said, "Don't worry, blue spider. I will be careful with you. I will make a tiny cup and plate for you. I will listen to what you say. I will not hurt you. I like you. You are pretty and smart."
The blue spider felt happy. He decided to trust Lily. He said, "Thank you, little girl. You are nice and kind. I will join your tea party. I like you. You are gentle and friendly."
Lily and the blue spider had a fun tea party. They talked and laughed. They shared stories and jokes. They became good friends. They learned that they had a lot in common. They learned that they could be friends even if they were different.
The moral of the story is: Don't judge someone by how they look. Be open and friendly to everyone. You might find a new friend in an unexpected place.

Once upon a time, there was a little mosquito named Mo. Mo was very enthusiastic and liked to fly around a lot. One day, Mo met a big frog named Fred. Fred was sad because he didn't know what to eat.
Mo thought about it and said, "I recommend you try some yummy bugs!" Fred agreed and they went to look for bugs together. Mo showed Fred all the best bugs to eat. They had a lot of fun and became good friends.
But one day, something unexpected happened. Fred saw Mo and thought, "Hmm, Mo is a bug too!" So, Fred tried to eat Mo! But Mo was quick and flew away. Mo was sad that his friend tried to eat him. Then, Fred realized what he did and said sorry. They both learned to be more careful and stayed friends, but Fred never tried to eat Mo again.

One day, a little girl named Sue found a hoop in the park. She loved to play with it. She would spin it around and jump through it. Sue was very happy with her hoop.
Sue's mom told her, "Don't forget your hoop when we go home." Sue said, "I won't, Mom. I am serious." But Sue was having so much fun that she forgot her hoop at the park. When they got home, she realized her hoop was not with her.
The next day, Sue and her mom went back to the park. Sue was sad because she thought her hoop was gone forever. But when they got to the park, Sue saw her hoop waiting for her under a big tree. She was so happy to have her hoop back. Sue said, "I will never forget my hoop again!" And from that day on, she always remembered to bring her hoop home.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who lived near a cliff. The bird was very dirty because it always played in the mud. It liked to be dirty, but it also wanted to be clean.
One day, the bird saw a shiny pond near the cliff. The bird wanted to take a bath in the pond to be clean. It flew down to the water and started to splash around. The bird was very happy.
But then, something unexpected happened. The pond was not just water, it was also full of mud! The bird got even dirtier than before. The bird was sad because it wanted to be clean, but now it was dirtier than ever. And so, the little bird stayed dirty and never found a way to be clean.

One day, a girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. Lily saw her friend, Tom, playing with a ball. She wanted to play too. "Mom, can I play with Tom?" Lily asked. Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, let's go play!"
Lily and Tom played with the ball, and they had a crazy fun time. They laughed and ran around the park. Then, Lily had an idea. "Tom, let's play with the ball in a new style!" she said. Tom was excited and said, "Okay, let's try!"
They started to play with the ball in a funny way, bouncing it on their heads and rolling it with their noses. Everyone at the park stopped to watch them and laughed. But then, something unexpected happened. A big dog came running and took the ball in its mouth!
Lily and Tom were surprised, but they were not scared. They knew the dog just wanted to play too. So, they played with the dog and had even more fun. In the end, they all became good friends and played together every day at the park.

Tom and Lily were playing in the park. They liked to pretend they were spies and had secret missions. Tom had a stick that he used as a weapon. Lily had a hat that she used as a disguise.
"Tom, we have to find the bad guy and stop him," Lily said. "He is hiding in the big slide."
"OK, Lily, let's go. But be careful, he might have a trap," Tom said. He held his stick tightly and followed Lily.
They climbed up the stairs and looked down the slide. They saw a boy with a red shirt and a mask. He was holding a water gun.
"Ha ha, I got you, spies!" the boy shouted. He sprayed water at Tom and Lily.
"Ah, he is the bad guy!" Lily said. She pulled her hat over her eyes and slid down the slide. She tried to kick the boy, but he dodged.
Tom was slow. He was afraid of the water. He stayed at the top of the slide and watched.
"Tom, help me!" Lily cried. She was wet and cold. The boy laughed and sprayed more water.
Tom felt bad. He wanted to help Lily. He remembered his weapon. He threw his stick at the boy. It hit him on the head.
"Ow, that hurts!" the boy said. He dropped his water gun and ran away.
Lily picked up the water gun. She smiled and climbed up the slide. She hugged Tom.
"Thank you, Tom, you saved me!" she said. "You are a brave spy. You can keep the water gun as a reward."
Tom was happy. He hugged Lily back. He liked the water gun. He forgot his fear.
"Thank you, Lily, you are a smart spy. You can keep the hat as a gift," he said.
They played with their new weapons and had more fun. They were the best spies in the park.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a kind boy named Tom. Tom liked to spend time outside on the lawn. One day, he saw a little bird with a hurt wing on the lawn.
"Hello, little bird," Tom said. "Why are you sad?"
"I hurt my wing and I can't fly," the little bird cried.
Tom wanted to help the bird. He was very kind. He picked up the bird and took it to his mom. "Mom, can we help the bird?" he asked.
His mom said, "Yes, we can help." They took care of the bird until its wing was better. The bird was happy and flew away.
Tom learned that it is good to be kind and help others when they are in need.

Anna loves her kitten, Sunny. Sunny has yellow fur and green eyes. Sunny likes to play with Anna and chase balls and mice. Anna and Sunny are best friends.
One day, Anna and Sunny go outside to play in the garden. The sun is very bright and warm. It shines on the flowers and the grass. Anna and Sunny run and jump and have fun. Anna sees a butterfly and tries to catch it. Sunny sees a bird and tries to catch it.
"Come here, butterfly!" Anna says. "You are so pretty and colorful!"
"Meow, meow, bird!" Sunny says. "You are so fast and noisy!"
But the butterfly and the bird are too clever. They fly away from Anna and Sunny. Anna and Sunny are sad. They want to play with the butterfly and the bird.
"Where did they go?" Anna asks.
"I don't know," Sunny says.
Anna and Sunny look around. They see a big tree with many leaves. The leaves are green and shiny. They move in the wind. Anna and Sunny think they see something in the tree.
"Look, Sunny!" Anna says. "There is something in the tree!"
"Let's go and see!" Sunny says.
Anna and Sunny run to the tree. They are curious and impatient. They want to know what is in the tree. They look up and see a big, round, shiny thing. It is silver and bright. It shines like the sun.
"What is that?" Anna asks.
"I don't know," Sunny says.
Anna and Sunny try to reach the shiny thing. Anna stands on her toes. Sunny climbs on the tree. But the shiny thing is too high. They cannot touch it.
"Please, come down!" Anna says.
"Please, come down!" Sunny says.
But the shiny thing does not come down. It stays in the tree. It does not talk or move. It just shines.
Anna and Sunny are confused and disappointed. They do not understand what the shiny thing is. They do not know why it is in the tree. They do not know how to play with it.
They decide to leave the shiny thing alone. They go back to the garden. They find other things to play with. They forget about the shiny thing.
But the shiny thing is still in the tree. It is a balloon. A balloon that someone lost. A balloon that flew away. A balloon that got stuck in the tree. A balloon that shines.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who was anxious about her special day. She was wearing a pretty blue skirt and was eager to go out.
The little girl was so excited that she asked her mom, "Can we go now?"
Her mom replied, "Yes, but first we have to rest. It will be a long day and you will need energy for all the fun things we'll be doing."
The little girl was disappointed, but listened to her mom and took a nap. After she got her rest, the little girl was ready to head out and have a wonderful day! She was no longer anxious and was excited to show off her new skirt.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a hidden door. Behind the door lived a kind and friendly cat. The cat liked to play and have fun with the kids in the house.
One day, a little girl named Lucy was playing near the hidden door. She saw the door open just a little bit. The cat came out to play with her. They played together all day and had lots of fun.
Later, Lucy saw that the cat was stuck in a tree. She wanted to save the cat. Lucy called her big brother, Tom. Tom climbed the tree and saved the cat. The cat was very happy. They all played together and had a great day. The cat went back to the hidden door, and Lucy and Tom knew they had a special friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to dance in her pretty dress. One day, she met a bossy cat who lived near her house. The bossy cat said, "You can't dance here! This is my place!"
Lily was sad but she wanted to dance. She asked the bossy cat, "Can I please dance with you?" The bossy cat thought for a moment and said, "Fine, but you must follow my rules." They started to dance together, but the bossy cat kept telling Lily what to do.
Suddenly, a big wind came and blew the bossy cat's hat away. The bossy cat was scared and ran after the hat. Lily saw that the cat needed help, so she stopped dancing and helped catch the hat. When they got the hat back, the bossy cat said, "Thank you, Lily. I'm sorry for being bossy. Let's dance together and have fun!" From that day on, Lily and the bossy cat became good friends and danced happily together.
