Kitty went into the chamber and lay down on the bed. It was so soft and warm. She closed her eyes and began to rest. Kitty had sweet dreams about playing with her ball. When she woke up, she felt happy and ready to play again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Jack. Jack was a very hungry boy. He was so hungry that he could not help but stick out his tummy so everyone could see. 
One day, while Jack was out in the park, he walked past a stand. He saw a yummy cake on top of it and his tummy grumbled. He looked around to see if anyone was watching, and then he reached his hand out.
Suddenly a voice called out, "Stop right there!" Jack was embarrassed and he quickly moved away from the stand. He looked back to see his mom standing there with a frown.
Jack's mom said, "Jack, you know better than to take things without asking! That cake is not for you, it's for the party. You were supposed to wait until after dinner, but you were too hungry."
Jack hung his head and said sorry. His mom hugged him and said, "It's ok. Just remember next time to wait until you are given food."
Jack nodded and smiled. He had learned a lesson not to be embarrassed.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a soft, small board. He picked it up and saw a tiny ant on it. Tim said, "Hi, little ant! What are you doing on this board?"
The ant replied, "Hello, Tim! I'm trying to find my way home. Can you help me?"
Tim wanted to help the ant, so he decided to follow the ant's lead. They walked and walked, passing trees and flowers. The ant would point, and Tim would follow. They talked and laughed as they went on their way.
Finally, they reached a tiny ant hill. The ant said, "Thank you, Tim! This is my home!" Tim smiled and said, "You're welcome, little ant! I'm happy I could help you."
Tim waved goodbye to the ant and went back home with the soft board. He knew that he had made a new friend that day.

Once upon a time, there was a silly dog named Spot. Spot loved to run and play all day. One day, Spot saw a big hill. He thought it would be fun to run up and down the hill.
Spot ran up the hill as fast as he could. When he got to the top, he looked around. He did not recognize where he was. Spot was a little scared, but he knew he had to find his way home.
Spot ran down the hill and saw a little girl. The girl knew Spot and took him home. Spot was so happy to be back. He knew that next time, he would not be so silly and would stay close to home.

One day, a red fox was walking in the woods. The fox saw an open door. The open door was on a big tree. The fox was very curious and wanted to see what was inside the tree.
Inside the tree, there were many animals. They were all talking and having fun. The fox wanted to join them. So, the fox went inside and started to discuss with the other animals. They talked about the weather, the food they liked, and the places they had been.
After a while, it was time for the fox to go home. The fox said goodbye to all the new friends. The fox felt happy and had a great day. The end.

Lily and Ben are playing with their toys in the living room. They have cars, dolls, blocks and books. They make a lot of noise as they zoom, talk, build and read. They are having fun.
Mom is in the kitchen. She is making lunch. She hears the noise from the living room. She thinks it is too loud. She wants Lily and Ben to be quiet. She walks to the living room. She says, "Lily, Ben, you are too noisy. Please play quietly. I need to finish lunch."
Lily and Ben look at Mom. They say, "Sorry, Mom. We will play quietly." They try to make less noise. They whisper, they slow down, they put down their toys gently. They are still having fun.
But then, Ben feels something tickle his nose. He tries to ignore it. He does not want to make noise. He does not want to bother Mom. But the tickle gets stronger. He feels a sneeze coming. He covers his mouth with his hand. He says, "Achoo!"
The sneeze is very loud. It makes the toys move. It makes the books fall. It makes the blocks tumble. It makes Lily jump. It makes Mom come back. She says, "Ben, are you okay? What was that noise?"
Ben says, "Sorry, Mom. I had to sneeze. Something tickled my nose." He rubs his nose with his hand. He smiles.
Mom says, "It's okay, Ben. Sneezing is not noisy. It is healthy. It means you are getting rid of something that bothers your nose. You don't have to be sorry for sneezing. But you do have to say 'excuse me'."
Ben says, "Excuse me, Mom. Excuse me, Lily." He says it politely.
Mom says, "That's good, Ben. Now, why don't you and Lily help me set the table for lunch? It is almost ready."
Lily and Ben say, "Okay, Mom. We will help you." They put away their toys. They follow Mom to the kitchen. They are hungry. They are happy.

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim went to the mall with his mom. They were happy to buy new toys and clothes. Tim saw a big, red ball and wanted it. His mom said, "No, Tim, we can't buy it today." Tim felt sad and helpless.
Tim's mom saw a soft, blue teddy bear and asked, "Do you like this teddy bear, Tim?" Tim nodded his head, but he still wanted the big, red ball. When his mom was not looking, he took the ball and hid it under his shirt.
As they left the mall, Tim's mom saw the big, red ball under his shirt. She was upset and said, "Tim, we must not take things without paying. That is not right." Tim felt bad and said, "I am sorry, mom. I will not do it again." They went back inside the mall to return the ball.
Tim's mom said, "I forgive you, Tim. We all make mistakes. It's important to say sorry and learn from them." Tim hugged his mom and said, "Thank you, mom. I will be good." They left the mall with the soft, blue teddy bear and a big lesson learned.

Anna and her dad were in the living room. Anna wanted to watch some cartoons, but her dad wanted to play with her. She was not happy about this.
"Dad, I want to watch cartoons," Anna complained.
Her dad smiled and said, "Let's both watch cartoons together on the couch."
Anna smiled and ran to the couch. It was big and orange. She jumped on it and said, "Yay!"
Anna and her dad watched cartoons together on the couch. Anna was very happy, and didn't complain anymore.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a compassionate rhinoceros named Remy. Remy had many friends because he was kind and always helped others. One day, Remy found a small, lost bird who couldn't fly. Remy knew that the bird needed to be in its nest, so he decided to help.
Remy and the bird went on a journey to find the bird's family. They walked and walked until they found a tall tree with many nests. Remy thought, "This must be where the bird's family lives!" But the nests were too high for Remy to reach.
Remy had an idea. He asked his friend, the elephant, to help him put the bird back into its nest. The elephant used its long trunk to lift the bird up and put it into the right nest. The bird was so happy to be back with its family. They all thanked Remy and the elephant for their help.
From that day on, Remy, the elephant, and the bird became the best of friends. They played together in the forest every day. And every time someone needed help, Remy was there with his compassionate heart, ready to lend a hand.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big toy box with lots of toys. One day, Tim wanted to play with his favorite toy car. But the car was not easy to find. So, Tim started a search for his toy car.
Tim looked in his toy box, but he could not find his car. He looked under his bed, but it was not there. Tim felt sad and wanted to give up. But then, his mom came in and said, "Keep trying, Tim. Don't give up."
Tim listened to his mom and kept looking. He looked in the closet, and there it was! His favorite toy car was hiding behind a big box. Tim was so happy that he did not give up on his search.
The moral of the story is to never give up, even when things are not easy. Keep trying and you will find what you are looking for.

Once upon a time, there was a hotel. The hotel was very big and pretty. Many people came to stay at the hotel. One day, a kind man came to the hotel. He wanted to stay there, too. The people at the hotel said, "Yes, we accept you to stay here."
The kind man was very happy. He went to his room and saw a big yummy cake on the table. He said, "Wow, that cake looks so yummy!" The kind man took a big bite of the cake. It was very sweet and yummy. He smiled and ate the whole cake.
The next day, the kind man said goodbye to the hotel. He was very happy with his stay. He told all his friends about the hotel and the yummy cake. All his friends wanted to go to the hotel, too. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to play outside with his friends. They would run, jump, and have lots of fun.
One day, Tim and his friends played a crazy game called "Freeze Club." In this game, one friend would hold a toy club and run around. When they touched someone with the club, that person had to freeze like a statue. The other friends would try to unfreeze them by touching them with their hands.
Tim loved playing "Freeze Club" with his friends. They would laugh and shout as they ran around. It was a fun game that made everyone happy. And that's how Tim and his friends spent their days, playing and having a great time together.

One day, a brave dog named Max went for a walk. He liked to play and run in the park. Max noticed a big tree and wanted to see what was behind it. He walked around the tree and saw a big bottle of shampoo.
Max saw a little girl named Lily near the shampoo. Lily said, "Max, can you help me open this shampoo? I want to wash my hair." Max tried to open the bottle, but it was too tight. He tried again and again, but he could not open it.
Max and Lily were sad. They wanted to use the shampoo, but they could not open it. Max said, "I am sorry, Lily. I tried my best." Lily said, "It's okay, Max. Thank you for trying." Max and Lily went home feeling sad that they could not open the shampoo bottle.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in her backyard. One sunny day, she found a package in the garden. It was big and had pretty colors on it. Lily was very excited to see what was inside.
Lily decided to bury the package in the sand and dig it up later. She thought it would be a fun game. But when she came back to dig it up, the package was gone! Lily was very upset. She looked and looked, but she could not find her package.
She told her mommy about the missing package. Her mommy helped her look for it. They found the package behind a big rock. Lily was so happy to have her package back. She opened it and found a beautiful toy inside. From that day on, Lily and her mommy played with the toy together and they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Mary. She liked to play dress-up and wanted to become a princess. One day, she found a big, pretty dress in her mom's room. It was expensive and shiny. Mary put on the dress and felt like a real princess.
Mary saw a boy named Tom playing outside. She ran to him and said, "Let's play marriage!" Tom said, "Yes!" They pretended to have a big wedding with all their friends. They had so much fun.
After the game, Mary and Tom became best friends. They played together every day. They were happy and always had fun. And sometimes, they still played dress-up and marriage.

Once upon a time, in a small house in the east, there lived a little girl named Amy. Amy had a big dog named Max. Max was a filthy dog because he loved to play in the mud.
One day, Amy and Max went outside to play. They saw a big tree and decided to have a race to see who could get to the tree first. "Ready, set, go!" said Amy. They both ran as fast as they could. Max got to the tree first and Amy laughed.
Amy looked at Max and said, "Max, you are so filthy! Let's go home and get you clean." They walked back to their house and Amy gave Max a bath. After the bath, Max was clean and shiny. They both went to bed, happy and tired from their fun day.

Ben and Lily were bored. It was a dull day. The sun was hiding behind the clouds. They could not play outside.
"Let's send a letter to the sun," said Ben. "Maybe he will come out and play with us."
"How do we send a letter to the sun?" asked Lily.
"We need a big balloon and some paper and a pen," said Ben. "We can write our letter and tie it to the balloon. Then we can let it go and it will fly up to the sun."
Ben and Lily found a big balloon and some paper and a pen. They wrote their letter. It said:
Dear Sun,
We miss you. Please come out and play with us. We have a ball and a kite and a bike. We want to see your bright smile.
Love,
Ben and Lily
They tied their letter to the balloon. They went outside and looked for a clear spot in the sky. They let go of the balloon and watched it fly up.
"Bye bye, balloon!" they said. "Say hello to the sun for us!"
They waited and waited. They hoped the sun would read their letter and come out. But the sky stayed dull and gray. The balloon got smaller and smaller until they could not see it anymore.
They felt sad. They went back inside.
"Maybe the sun is busy," said Lily. "Maybe he has a lot of work to do."
"Maybe he is sleeping," said Ben. "Maybe he needs a nap."
They decided to draw some pictures of the sun and hang them on the wall. They used bright colors and made the sun happy and smiling. They felt a little better.
They did not know that their balloon had reached the sun. The sun saw their letter and read it. He felt touched by their words. He wanted to play with them too. He decided to wake up and shine.
He pushed the clouds away and sent his warm rays down to the earth. He looked for Ben and Lily's house. He found it and peeked through the window.
He saw their pictures of him on the wall. He smiled back at them. He knocked on the glass with his light.
Ben and Lily heard the knock. They looked up and saw the sun. They could not believe their eyes. They ran to the window and waved at him.
"Hello, sun!" they said. "Thank you for coming out and playing with us!"
The sun winked at them. He said:
"You're welcome, Ben and Lily. Thank you for sending me your letter. It made me very happy. Now let's go outside and have some fun!"
Ben and Lily grabbed their ball and their kite and their bike. They put on their hats and their sunscreen. They went outside and played with the sun. They had a wonderful time.
It was not a dull day anymore. It was a bright and happy day.

Once upon a time, there was a gifted person named Tim. Tim was very good at looking at things closely. One day, Tim went to the park to play.
At the park, Tim saw a big tree. He wanted to examine the tree. He looked at the tree and saw a little bird. The bird was singing a pretty song. Tim smiled and talked to the bird. "Hello, little bird! I like your song," said Tim.
The bird was happy that Tim liked its song. So, the bird sang more for Tim. They became good friends. Tim and the bird played in the park every day. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a fat cat named Tom. Tom was very good at giving hugs. One day, Tom met a little girl named Lily.
"Hi, Tom!" said Lily. "Can I have a hug?"
"Of course!" said Tom, and gave Lily a big, warm hug. Lily was very happy.
Lily wanted to record Tom's hugs, so she could show her friends. She took out her camera and asked Tom to give her another hug. Tom hugged Lily again, and she took a picture. Lily and Tom spent the whole day together, taking pictures and giving each other hugs. They were the best of friends.

One day, a little dog named Spot was walking down the street. He was a popular dog in the town because he was very friendly. Spot liked to say hello to everyone he met. As he walked, he felt a pain in his heel. He stopped and looked at his paw.
A small boy named Tim saw Spot and walked over. "What's wrong, Spot?" Tim asked. Spot looked at his paw and tried to explain. "My heel hurts," Spot said in a sad voice.
Tim wanted to help Spot feel better. "Let's go see my mom. She can help you," Tim said. They went to Tim's house, and Tim's mom looked at Spot's heel. She put a bandage on it and gave him a treat. Spot felt much better and thanked Tim and his mom. They all played together in the yard, and Spot's heel didn't hurt anymore.

Once upon a time there was a smelly wizard called Bob. He had a magical bag full of tricks. One day it was raining outside and Bob had nothing to do, so he started to whistle.
He whistled so loud that he woke up his pet dragon, Sparky. Sparky flew out from under Bob's bed and shouted 'What are you doing, Bob?'.
Bob replied 'I'm just whistling, Sparky. I'm so bored I thought maybe I'd use my magic bag to do something special. Wanna try?'
Sparky nodded and said 'Yeah, that sounds like fun. What do you want me to do?'.
Bob said 'Let's go out and find some smelly things and put them in my bag. Maybe then we can make some magic with them!'
Sparky was so excited and he shouted 'Let's go!'. They grabbed Bob's magical bag and flew off in search of smelly things. 
That day they found some things that were super smelly, like old socks and stinky fruit! Then they went back home and Bob put all of their smelly finds in his magical bag.
Bob said 'Now use your dragon breath to make some magic!' and sure enough, when Sparky blew his dragon breath onto the magical bag, a big cloud of magical sparkles filled the air!

One evening, a big red ball sat on the soft green grass. A nosy little cat saw the ball and wanted to play. The cat went close to the ball and gave it a little push with its paw.
The ball started to roll away, and the cat ran after it. The cat was so happy to play with the ball. They played together until the evening sun began to set.
As the evening turned dark, the cat and the ball sat down on the grass, tired from playing. They looked at the pretty sky and felt happy. The nosy cat and the big red ball became best friends.

One day, a boy named Tom found a fine marble. It was very pretty and shiny. Tom loved his new marble and showed it to his friend, Sam. Sam liked the marble too and wanted to play with it. Tom said, "Let's play a game with the marble later today."
Later, Tom and Sam went to the park to play with the marble. They saw a big tree with a hole in it. Tom said, "I have an idea! Let's try to strike the marble into that hole." Sam agreed and they took turns trying to hit the marble into the hole.
After many tries, Sam finally struck the marble just right and it went into the hole. Tom and Sam were very happy. They played with the marble all day and had lots of fun. In the end, they learned that playing together made the game even better.

Tim and Jen are friends. They like to play hide and seek. Tim is the seeker. He counts to ten. Jen runs to find a spot to hide.
Jen sees a big box. She thinks it is a good spot. She goes inside the box. The box is dark. Jen is not scared. She waits for Tim to find her.
Tim looks for Jen. He looks under the bed. He looks behind the door. He looks in the closet. He cannot find Jen. He says, "Where are you, Jen? I give up!"
Jen hears Tim. She wants to surprise him. She stands up in the box. She pushes the lid open. She says, "Boo!"
Tim sees Jen. He jumps. He laughs. He says, "You scared me, Jen! You found a good spot. You win!"
Jen and Tim are happy. They hug. They say, "Hide and seek is fun!" They play again.

One day, a red automobile was driving down a road. It was a very boring day. The sun was shining, but the automobile wanted to have fun.
The automobile saw a tree with a big branch. "I have an idea," the automobile said. It decided to hang from the tree branch to make the day more fun. The automobile drove up a small hill and jumped onto the branch. It hung there, swinging back and forth.
A little boy saw the automobile hanging from the tree. "Wow!" he said. "Look at the automobile in the tree!" The boy ran to the tree and pushed the automobile to make it swing higher. The automobile and the boy had a lot of fun together. The boring day became a fun day for both of them.

Once upon a time, there was a clever girl named Lily. She loved to print pictures of animals. One day, she found a glove on the ground. The glove was very special.
Lily put on the glove and said, "Wow, this glove makes my hand feel warm!" Then, she had an idea. She went to her room and started to print a picture of a cat. When the cat was on the paper, she touched it with the special glove.
To her surprise, the cat came to life! It jumped off the paper and said, "Thank you, Lily! You are very clever." Lily and the cat became best friends. They played together every day, and Lily used her special glove to print more animal friends.

Tom and Lily are friends. They like to play with toys and books. One day, they find a big box in the park. It has many things inside: a ball, a doll, a car, a book, and a massage tool. It is a tool that makes you feel good when you rub it on your back or your feet.
"Wow, look at this!" Tom says. He picks up the massage tool and turns it on. It makes a buzzing sound and vibrates. He puts it on his back and smiles. "This feels nice!"
"Can I try?" Lily asks. She is curious about the massage tool. She wants to feel good too.
"Okay, but only for a little bit. Then you have to give it back to me," Tom says. He is not very good at sharing. He likes the massage tool a lot.
Lily takes the massage tool and rubs it on her feet. She giggles. "This tickles!" She likes the massage tool too. She does not want to give it back to Tom.
"Hey, give it back to me! It's my turn!" Tom says. He is angry. He tries to grab the massage tool from Lily.
"No, it's mine! I found it first!" Lily says. She is stubborn. She holds the massage tool tight.
They start to fight over the massage tool. They pull and push and shout. They are not friends anymore.
A lady sees them fighting. She is the owner of the box. She left it in the park by mistake. She is hungry and wants to go home and eat. She walks over to Tom and Lily and says, "Stop fighting, children. That is not nice. That is my massage tool. I use it when I am tired or sore. You can't have it. But you can have something else from the box. There are many things to share. Why don't you pick one thing each and play nicely?"
Tom and Lily stop fighting. They feel sorry. They say, "We are sorry, lady. We were bad. We want to be friends again. Can we share your things?"
The lady smiles. She says, "Yes, you can. But you have to be careful and kind. Sharing is good. It makes you happy and makes others happy too. Here, you can have the ball and the doll. They are fun to play with. And you can have the book too. It has stories and pictures. And you can have the car. It goes fast and makes noises. But you have to give them back to me when you are done. Okay?"
Tom and Lily nod. They say, "Okay, lady. Thank you. You are nice. We will share your things and play nicely. We will give them back to you when we are done."
They take the things from the box and go to a bench. They play with the ball and the doll and the book and the car. They take turns and say, "Please" and "Thank you". They are friends again. They are happy.
The lady watches them play. She is happy too. She says, "Good children. You learned a lesson today. Sharing is good. And so is massage. Maybe I will give you a massage later. But first, I need to eat. I am hungry." She takes the massage tool and goes home. She is hungry and tired and sore. But she is also glad. She found her box and helped two children. She is a good lady.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big brother named Tom. They liked to eat dinner together with their mom and dad.
One evening, as they were eating, Lily felt restless. She kept moving in her chair. Tom noticed and wanted to help her. He asked Lily to pass the salt to him. Lily reached for the salt and passed it to Tom. This made her feel important and a little less restless.
After dinner, Lily and Tom went to play outside. They ran and jumped until Lily was tired. When it was time for bed, Lily felt much better. She was not restless anymore. The next day at dinner, Lily passed the salt again, and they all smiled.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Jane. Jane loved to pick berries in the forest near her house. Every day she would take a basket with her and wander around the forest, filling the basket with delicious berries. 
One day, when Jane was out picking berries, she heard an excited voice shouting: "Come and see, come and see!" Jane wandered over to find a very wealthy man. He had lots of different kinds of berries and he was offering them to anyone who wanted them.
Jane was so excited! She took a big basket of the delicious juicy berries and ran home. She was so excited she couldn't wait to share her find with the family.
The next day, when Jane's family sat down for dinner, everyone was delighted to find themselves enjoying a delicious feast of berry pie. What a surprise! The family thanked Jane for finding such tasty berries in the forest. They all realized that Jane's discovery was the beginning of a rich and wonderful journey.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Sue went to the beach with her mom. Sue loved to play in the sand and look for shells. One day, she found a very pretty shell. It was not like other shells. It looked expensive.
Sue showed the shell to her mom. Her mom said, "That's a special shell, Sue. Let's take it home and cook something yummy with it." Sue was very happy and excited. She couldn't wait to see what they would cook with the special shell.
At home, Sue and her mom cooked a big pot of soup. They put the special shell in the pot. When the soup was ready, they tasted it. The soup was the best soup they ever had! The special shell made the soup taste amazing. Sue and her mom knew they found something very special. They would always remember the day they found the expensive shell at the beach.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a mysterious box. It had many shapes on it. One day, a little boy named Tim found it. He wanted to open it, but his mom said, "No, Tim. I don't permit you to open the box."
Later, Tim met his friend, Sue. He showed her the box. Sue said, "Wow! What's inside?" Tim said, "I don't know. My mom didn't permit me to open it." Sue looked at the shapes and said, "Let's ask the wise old man. He knows many things."
The wise old man looked at the box and said, "This box has a secret. If you open it, you must be ready for what's inside." Tim and Sue thought about it. They decided to wait until they were older and wiser.
Years later, Tim and Sue remembered the mysterious box. They went to the wise old man and said, "We are ready now." The wise old man smiled and said, "You have learned patience and wisdom. You may open the box." Inside, they found a note that said, "You have learned the value of waiting for the right time. This is the greatest gift."
And so, Tim and Sue learned that sometimes it's better to wait and be wise. The mysterious box taught them an important lesson.

Once upon a time, there was a kind neighbor named Mr. Brown. He had a smooth stone that he liked to protect. He would keep the stone in a safe place and always look after it.
One day, a little girl named Susie came to visit Mr. Brown. She saw the smooth stone and asked, "What is this, Mr. Brown?" Mr. Brown replied, "This is my special stone, Susie. I protect it because it is very important to me."
Susie wanted to help Mr. Brown protect the smooth stone. They decided to build a small fence around it. They worked together and made a nice, safe place for the stone. Now, Mr. Brown and Susie protect the smooth stone together. They are very happy neighbors.

One day, Tom and his dog, Spot, went for a walk. They saw a big, red jeep. The jeep was smooth and shiny. Tom said, "Wow, I want to get a ride in that jeep!"
Tom asked the jeep's owner, "Can we get a ride in your smooth jeep?" The owner said, "Sure, hop in!" So, Tom and Spot got in the jeep. They were very happy.
As they were riding, something unexpected happened. A big bird came and sat on the jeep. The bird said, "Hello, I am a talking bird!" Tom and Spot were very surprised. The bird asked, "Can I join your ride?" They all laughed and said, "Yes, let's go on a fun ride together!" And they all had a great time in the smooth, red jeep.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty bird. The bird liked to display her beautiful wings. One day, she saw a post in the park. The post was very tall. The bird thought it was a nice place to show her wings.
The pretty bird sat on the post. She spread her wings wide. Many people came to see her. They said, "What a pretty bird!" The bird was very happy. She liked to make people smile.
One day, a small bird came to the post. The small bird wanted to sit there too. The pretty bird thought about it. She knew the post was big enough for both of them. So, the pretty bird let the small bird sit with her. They both displayed their wings. People loved to see them together. The pretty bird learned that sharing makes everyone happy.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He loved to look at the big landscape around him. He saw trees, grass, and flowers. Tim felt happy and excited.
As he walked, he saw a little bird. The bird was standing on a branch. Tim said, "Hi bird! I like how you stand on the branch. You look so alert!" The bird looked at Tim and chirped, "Thank you, Tim! I have to be alert to find food and stay safe."
Tim and the bird became friends. They played and talked all day. When it was time to go home, Tim said, "Goodbye, bird! I will come back and see you soon." The bird chirped, "Goodbye, Tim! I will be here, standing alert on my branch."

One day, a little dove was flying in the sky. The sun was hot, and the dove felt very tired. The dove wanted to find some cool water to drink.
The dove saw a boy near a pond. The boy was trying to reach a ball that was in the water. He tried and tried, but he could not get the ball. The dove flew down and said, "I will help you get the ball."
The dove flew over the water and picked up the ball with her beak. She gave it to the boy, and he was very happy. He said, "Thank you, dove! You helped me succeed!"
The boy then gave the dove some cool water to drink. They both sat by the pond and played together. The dove and the boy became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a restless boy who always wanted to play. One day, Tim saw a big fence near his house. He wanted to climb it and see what was on the other side.
Tim asked his mom, "Can I climb the fence, please?" His mom said, "No, Tim. There is a mean dog on the other side. He will spoil your fun and maybe bite you." But Tim did not listen to his mom. He wanted to climb the fence and play.
When his mom was not looking, Tim climbed the fence. On the other side, he saw the mean dog. The dog barked and ran towards Tim. Tim was scared and tried to climb back. But it was too late. The dog bit Tim and spoiled his fun. Tim cried and wished he had listened to his mom.

Once upon a time, there was a reliable dog named Spot. Spot loved to help his family. He was very good at picking up things and carrying them. One day, Spot's family needed meat for dinner. They asked Spot to go to the store and get some meat.
Spot went to the store and got the meat. He held it in his mouth and started to walk back home. On his way back, he saw a cat. Spot wanted to play with the cat, but he knew he had to bring the meat back to his family. So, he kept walking.
As Spot got closer to home, he saw a big ball. He wanted to play with the ball, but he knew he had to bring the meat back to his family. Then, Spot accidentally dropped the meat. He quickly picked it up and ran home. When he got home, his family was happy to see him and the meat. They all had a yummy dinner together.

The twin boys, Dave and Don, were playing outside. Dave was looking through a magnifying glass when he spotted something very strange in the grass.
He said to Don, "Look at this. It looks hairy."
Don took the magnifying glass and looked at the small, furry creature hopping around in the grass.
"Wow! What is it?" Don said.
The twins suddenly realized that it was a mouse.
Don quickly ran inside to tell his mom what he had found.
His mom ran outside and shouted, "Ahhh! It's a mouse!" 
She said to the twins, "You must be more careful when you are playing outside."
The twins were sad, because they felt bad for not being more careful.
They now realized how dangerous it was to be outside without an adult.

One day, Jack the driver was driving down the road with a few passengers. It had been a long day and he was feeling very tired. Suddenly, in the distance he saw an old horse and carriage. The driver of the carriage had an old hat and a big white beard. The driver of the carriage smiled at him and said, "Take it easy, my friend. You look tired. You should take a moment to relax." 
Jack thanked the old man and said," Thank you for your advice. But I still have a few passengers I need to take to their destination." 
The old man nodded and said, "No problem. If you're persistent, you can do it!" 
Jack nodded and continued to drive. He drove with renewed energy and eventually got to the destination. He thanked the old man one last time, before driving on. 
When Jack looked back, he saw the old man was still standing there with a big smile on his face. Jack smiled too, feeling relaxed and happy that he had been so persistent.

Once upon a time, there was a big red truck named Tim. He was a very flexible truck. He could bend and twist easily to go through tight spaces.
One day, Tim met a small blue car named Sam. Sam asked Tim, "How can you be so flexible?" Tim just shrugged and said, "I don't know, I just am." Sam wished he could be flexible like Tim.
One day, they saw a big pile of rocks blocking the road. All the cars were stuck and could not go through. Sam said, "Tim, you can help!" Tim used his flexibility to bend and twist around the rocks. He made a path for all the cars to go through. Sam and all the other cars were very happy. Tim was proud to be a flexible truck.

Once upon a time, there was a pink train. This train had a big engine. The train loved to wear a pink hat on its engine. Every day, the train went on a trip to see its friends.
One day, the pink train met a blue car. The blue car also liked to wear a hat. They became good friends. They played together and had lots of fun.
Then, they met a yellow bus. The yellow bus didn't have a hat to wear. The pink train and the blue car felt sad for the yellow bus. They all went to the hat store and got a hat for the yellow bus. Now, all three friends wore hats and played together every day. They were very happy.

Once upon a time there was a boy named Mikey. He loved to record things; he recorded everything that caught his eye. One day, he found something very special - a small purple flower. "It's so pretty!" he said as he carefully recorded it. "I want to take it home with me!"
Just then a voice said, "No,Mikey! You must be careful with nature. You don't need to pick it up and take it home. Leave it here for others to enjoy and record it for your memory."
Mikey listened to what he was told, and he decided to take a picture of it instead. He recorded it for his memory, and everyone was happy.
The moral of the story is that nature is precious and it's important to be careful with it.

One day, a boy called John was playing in his garden. Suddenly, he saw a pretty scarf on the ground. He ran to get it and put it around his neck. Then he began to act like a princess. He twirled around, pretending he was wearing a beautiful gown.
John's mom saw him and smiled. But she said, "John, where did you get that scarf?"
"I found it in the garden," he replied.
His mom was a bit frustrated. "John, you can't just take things that don't belong to you. You need to learn to ask before you act," she said.
John felt a little sad. He put the scarf back on the ground and silently went back inside. He realized that he had done something wrong, and that made him very frustrated.
His mom hugged him and said, "It's ok, John. Just remember to always ask before you act. That way, you won't get in trouble."
John nodded and said, "I won't forget, Mom. I promise."

One day, there was a little boy who wanted to make something. He had a saw. He was very lucky because he found it. Every day he went outside and pulled it with him.
He used the saw to cut some wood. He made the wood into shapes. He made a cube, a triangle and a square. He was so happy with his work.
The little boy put his shapes in a row. He looked at them every day and was proud of his work. One day, he even shared his shapes with his friends. They all said that he was so lucky to have made such nice shapes.
The little boy was pleased with his saw. He had a lot of fun making shapes and he was very proud.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a little bird named Tim. Tim lived in a tree with many branches. One day, Tim saw a big, harmless bug on a branch. The bug was very colorful and slow.
Tim's friend, Sam the squirrel, came by and saw the bug too. Sam was hungry and wanted to eat the bug. But Tim liked the harmless bug and didn't want it to be eaten. So, Tim had an idea. He would suggest a different snack for Sam.
Tim said to Sam, "Let's not eat the bug. I know where we can find some yummy nuts instead." Sam liked nuts, so he agreed. They left the harmless bug on the branch and went to find nuts together. Tim and Sam had a fun day, and the harmless bug stayed safe on the branch.

Once upon a time, there was a gym. The gym was different. It had many colors and fun things to play with. Many kids went to the gym to play and have fun. They liked the gym a lot.
One day, something unexpected happened. The gym started to change. The colors became brighter and the toys became bigger. The kids were very surprised. They looked around and saw that the gym was even more fun now.
But then, the gym changed again. This time, it became a big playground. The kids were so happy. They played on the swings, slides, and seesaws. They had a great time at the new playground. And they learned that change can be fun and exciting.

Once upon a time there was a young girl named Julia. She was a very obedient child, but she didn't always listen when her parents told her to do something. One day Julia's parents were busy and asked her to tie some knots for them. 
When she looked up at them she said, "What does knot mean?" 
Her parents laughed and explained, "A knot is when you take two pieces of rope and tie one end to the other. Can you do that?" 
Julia was curious, so she decided to give it a try. She took two pieces of rope and started to practice. She figured out how to tie the knot in no time. She was so proud of herself!
The day went on and Julia started to wonder what else she could do. She took the ropes and started to create different kinds of knots. She created big knots, little knots, simple knots, and complicated knots. Every knot she made was perfect and she was so pleased with herself!
At the end of the day, Julia was exhausted but she felt like the proudest child in the world. She had been obedient and she learned something new. Her parents were very proud of her, too. Julia's face glowed with pride as she sent off to bed, dreaming about knots.

Once upon a time, there was a big white dog named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play and run in the park. One day, Fluffy saw a little girl named Lily. Lily was sad because she lost her ball.
Fluffy wanted to help Lily. He looked around and saw the ball under a table. Fluffy kneeled down and got the ball with his mouth. He brought it back to Lily. Lily was very happy and said, "Thank you, Fluffy!"
From that day on, Fluffy and Lily became best friends. They played together every day, and Lily always had a big smile on her face. And Fluffy was happy to have a new friend to play with.

Once upon a time, there was a big field. In the field, there were many animals. One day, a little dog named Max came to the field. Max was an obedient dog, but he liked to play a lot.
In the field, Max met a big cat. The cat did not want to play with Max. Max was sad. He wanted to play, but the cat said "no". Max tried to play with the cat, but the cat got mad. The cat tried to bite Max.
Max ran away from the cat. He found a nice rabbit in the field. The rabbit was kind and wanted to play with Max. They played all day and became good friends. Max was happy and the cat did not bite him anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little fish named Fin. Fin lived in a peaceful lake with his family. They all loved to play and swim together in the clear blue water. One day, Fin saw a big fish with a sad face. Fin swam over to the big fish and asked, "Why are you sad?"
The big fish said, "I can't act in the fish show tonight. My lung hurts, and I can't swim fast." Fin wanted to help the big fish. He thought for a moment and then said, "I will act for you in the fish show!"
That night, Fin acted in the fish show. He swam fast and made the other fish laugh. Everyone clapped for Fin when the show was over. The big fish was very happy and thanked Fin for helping him. From that day on, Fin and the big fish became best friends, and they swam together in the peaceful lake.

Once upon a time, there was a small gray cat named Kitty. Kitty lived in a big house with her best friend, a girl named Lucy. Lucy's mom and dad worked in the house with other people. These people were called the staff.
One day, Kitty wanted to play with a ball. She saw the staff's ball and took it without asking. Later, Lucy asked, "Kitty, did you take the staff's ball?" Kitty said, "Yes, I did. I wanted to play." Lucy told Kitty, "That's not nice. You should ask before taking things."
Kitty felt bad and knew she made a mistake. She had regret in her heart. Kitty gave the ball back to the staff and said, "I'm sorry. I should have asked first." The staff smiled and said, "It's okay, Kitty. Thank you for giving it back." From that day on, Kitty always asked before taking things. She learned that it is important to be kind and ask before taking something that does not belong to you.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to collect pretty things like rocks, leaves, and flowers. One day, she found a very important shiny rock. It was special because it could make people dance.
Mia took the important rock to her friends. She showed them the rock and told them that it could make them dance. They all laughed and wanted to see if it was true. Mia held the rock and said, "Dance!" and everyone started to dance.
They danced all day and all night. They danced in the park, they danced in the street, and they danced in their homes. The important rock made everyone happy. Mia and her friends loved the rock, and they all collected more important things together. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the store with his mom. He wanted to buy a toy car. When they got to the store, Tim found the car he liked. It was red and shiny. He was so excited!
Tim's mom said, "We need to pay for the car before we can take it home." Tim didn't know what "pay" meant. His mom explained, "We give the store some paper money, and then we can take the car home."
They went to the man at the counter. Tim's mom gave him the paper money for the car. Tim felt a little dizzy because he was so happy. He couldn't wait to play with his new toy car.
When they got home, Tim played with his car all day. He made it go fast and slow, and even made it jump over things. Tim was so happy he got his toy car. He thanked his mom for helping him pay for it.
At bedtime, Tim put his car next to his bed. He said goodnight to his mom and his new toy car. Tim couldn't wait for the next day to play with his car again. And that night, he had sweet dreams about all the fun they would have together.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max liked to make a lot of noise. He would bark and bark all day. Some of his friends did not like the noise. They thought it was rude.
One day, a small cat named Luna came to the park. Luna did not make noise like Max. She was very quiet. Max saw Luna and wanted to be her friend. He tried to say hello, but his barking scared Luna away. Max felt sad.
Max wanted to be like Luna. He tried to be quiet and not bark. It was hard, but he did it. Luna saw Max being quiet and came to play with him. They played all day and had fun. Max learned that being quiet can be good too. Now, Luna and Max are best friends. They admire each other and play together every day.

Sammy was walking through the park, when he found a big note. He picked it up, and saw that it said 'Dig Here'. He looked around for a shovel, and soon enough he spotted one lying in the sand. Sammy grabbed it and started digging, and soon enough he found a big box. He opened the box, and it was full of toys! Sammy was so happy, he started to play with them all day. 
Then night came, and Sammy had to bury the toys. He started to cry, but then he remembered the note. He decided to read it one last time, and then he heard a voice. It was coming from the tree. 
"Don't cry Sammy. You can hide the toys here, if you want," said the tree. 
"Oh thank you! That would be so nice" said Sammy. 
Sammy buried the toys, and then he went home with a big smile. From then on, he visited the tree every week, to make sure that the toys were still safe.

One day a mysterious vehicle arrived in the village. Everyone was curious about who it belonged to, but no one had seen anyone new. The village elders decided to replace their old wagon with the mysterious vehicle to help them get around. 
The villagers were excited about their new ride, but soon found out that the vehicle had a mind of its own! It never went where they wanted, taking them around in circles and up and down hills. 
The elders were surprised and confused by the vehicle's behavior. They tried different ways to control it, but nothing seemed to work. 
One day, the elders had an idea. They decided to listen to the mysterious vehicle for once, and followed its directions. As a surprise, the vehicle took them to the village's best-kept secret - a hidden lake in the middle of the woods! 
The villagers were thrilled and celebrated their discovery. They thanked the mysterious vehicle for showing them this beautiful place, and from then on always let it choose its destination.

Once upon a time, in a modest little house near the edge of a cliff, there lived a kind rabbit named Remy. Remy loved to play with his friends near the cliff. One day, Remy saw a sharp rock near the cliff. He knew it was not safe for his friends to play there.
Remy called his friend, a little bird named Benny. "Benny, we must move the sharp rock. It is not safe for our friends to play near the cliff." Benny agreed, and they started to think of ways to move the rock.
Together, Remy and Benny asked their friends for help. The strong bear, the fast deer, and the wise owl all came to help. They pushed and pulled the sharp rock until it was far away from the cliff. Now, Remy and his friends could play near the cliff without any worry. They were all very happy and thankful for their teamwork.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a delicate bird named Blue. Blue liked to carry things in her beak. One day, she saw some lumber near a big tree. Blue wanted to carry the lumber to build a nest.
Blue tried to carry the lumber, but it was too big and heavy for her. She did not give up. She tried again and again, but she could not carry it. Blue was very sad.
As Blue tried to carry the lumber one last time, she fell down. The heavy lumber hurt her wing. Blue could not fly anymore. She was very sad and her new nest was never built.

Once upon a time there was a boy called Tom. Tom was a very polite boy; he always said please and thank you to his parents and his friends. His parents were very proud.
One day, Tom went to his room and saw some stuff on the floor. He picked it all up and put it away so his room was tidy. After tidying his room, Tom went to the drawer, opened it slowly and closed it again. That's when he noticed something was missing. 
Tom went to his parent's room to ask them what had happened, and that's when he saw the politeest gift ever! It was a brand new toy for him - something he had wanted for so long. 
Tom was so excited, he hugged his parents and thanked them for being so kind. He now understood why his parent had asked him to close the drawer, so they could surprise him with the best gift.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. He loved to ride his bike. But Tom was foolish. He did not like to wear his helmet. His mom and dad told him, "You must wear your helmet when you ride your bike."
One day, Tom went for a bike ride. He was having so much fun. He went up and down the hills. But then, he fell off his bike. Tom was sad and hurt. He wished he had worn his helmet.
Tom's mom and dad came to help him. They said, "You must wear your helmet to be safe." Tom knew they were right. From that day on, Tom always wore his helmet when he rode his bike. Tom was not foolish anymore. And that is the end of the story.

Once upon a time, there was a little train. The train was very good at his job. He loved to carry people and things from one place to another. Every day, the train would go very fast on the tracks.
One day, the train saw a small bug. The bug was trying to crawl across the tracks. The train wanted to help the bug, so he stopped. The train tried to crawl like the bug, but he could not. He was too big and heavy.
Just then, a big wind came and blew the bug up into the air. The bug landed on the train's back. The train was happy to give the bug a ride. So, the train and the bug went on their way, and they became very good friends. The train learned that even if he couldn't crawl, he could still help his new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy bear. All he wanted to do was sleep and take naps, day and night. His mom was so worried about him.
One day, his mom said, "Come on, bear! Let's go for a walk!"
But the lazy bear just stared at her and said, "I don't want to."
His mom smiled and said, "It's important that we get some fresh air and oxygen. You need to be healthy. Please come outside with me!"
So, the lazy bear slowly got out of bed and followed his mom. He was surprised by how good it felt to get some fresh air and breathe in the oxygen. Before long, he was running around, feeling very energized and alive!
The lazy bear realized that his mom was right, fresh air and oxygen was important for his health. From that day forward, he stopped being so lazy and always made sure to get outside every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to write. One day, she wrote a story about a great big pillow. This pillow was very special. It could talk!
The pillow said, "Hi, Sue! I am a great big pillow. I can help you be good to others." Sue was happy to have a new friend. They played and talked together. Sue learned that being kind to others made her feel good too.
One day, Sue saw a boy who was sad. She asked the pillow, "What should I do?" The pillow said, "Give him a hug and share your toys." Sue did what the pillow said. The boy smiled and they played together. Sue learned that being kind and sharing made everyone happy.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a gentle farmer named Tom. Tom had a big farm with many animals and plants. One day, he planted some corn in the field.
As the days went by, the corn grew tall and strong. The animals liked to play near the corn field. Tom was happy to welcome his friends to see the corn, too. They all loved the corn and the farm.
One sunny day, Tom and his friends had a big party near the corn field. They played games, laughed, and had fun. The animals joined them, too. Everyone was happy and felt welcome at the gentle farmer's home. And they all enjoyed the tasty corn that Tom had grown.

One day, a boy named Tom found a pencil. It was a very boring pencil. It was not red, blue, or green. It was just brown. Tom did not like the boring pencil, but he took it with him to play.
Tom went to the park with the boring pencil. He saw his friend, Sue. Sue asked, "What do you have, Tom?" Tom said, "I have a boring pencil." Sue wanted to see the pencil. She touched it and something strange happened. Sue could not move! She was like a statue. The pencil made her freeze.
Tom was scared. He did not know what to do. He thought hard and had an idea. He touched the boring pencil to Sue again. Suddenly, Sue could move! She was not frozen anymore. Tom and Sue knew that the boring pencil was not so boring after all. They put the pencil in a safe place, so no one else would freeze.

Once upon a time, there was a little red bird. The bird loved to scatter rice for her friends. Every day, she would fly and scatter rice for them to eat. She had many friends who loved her.
One day, the little red bird met a big blue bird. The big blue bird was sad. The little red bird asked, "Why are you sad?" The big blue bird said, "I have no friends. No one shares with me."
The little red bird felt sad for the big blue bird. She decided to share her rice with him. She scattered the rice on the ground, and the big blue bird ate it. The big blue bird was very happy.
From that day on, the little red bird and the big blue bird were best friends. They shared everything together. The other birds saw this and learned to share too.
The moral of the story is: sharing makes everyone happy.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was an obedient boy who always listened to his mom and dad. He loved to play with his friends, eat yummy food, and sleep in his cozy bed.
One day, while Tim was playing, he heard a strange noise. "What's that?" he asked his friend, Sam. Sam didn't know either. They decided to look for the source of the noise.
As they walked, they found a big, sleepy bear in the middle of the village. Everyone was scared, but Tim had an idea. He said, "Let's sing a song to help the bear sleep!" The bear heard the song and began to close its eyes. The village was safe, and everyone thanked Tim and Sam for their help.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue was very sleepy one night. She put on her soft bathrobe and got ready for bed. But Sue could not find her favorite toy, Mr. Bear. She felt sad and began to weep.
Sue's mommy heard her weep and came into the room. She saw Sue in her bathrobe, with tears in her eyes. "What's wrong, Sue?" asked her mommy. Sue said, "I can't find Mr. Bear, and I can't sleep without him." Her mommy gave her a hug and said, "Don't worry, we will find Mr. Bear."
Together, they looked all around the house for Mr. Bear. They looked in the living room, the kitchen, and even the bathroom. Then, they saw something behind the couch. It was Mr. Bear, hiding behind a pillow! Sue was so happy to see her toy. She hugged Mr. Bear, put on her bathrobe, and went to bed. That night, Sue and Mr. Bear slept very well, and Sue did not weep anymore.

Mama and Tommy were in the park on a sunny day. Tommy wanted to play but he was still a bit too young. Mama saw a hoop near the edge of the playground and smiled.
She took Tommy's hand and pointed to the hoop. "That's a hoop. Do you want to play with it?" she asked.
Tommy was very excited and nodded. He took the hoop and ran to an open space in the middle of the playground. Mama watched him carefully and smiled.
Tommy held the hoop up above his head and smiled. He concentrated hard trying to make it move, but it was too heavy. His little arms were too weak to lift it above his head.
Mama saw his disappointment and crouched down next to him. She whispered softly, "Don't worry, you will be able to do it when you are bigger and stronger. One day you'll be so strong that you can lift that hoop with ease."
Tommy smiled up at his mama and hugged her. Then he kept playing with the hoop and the sun kept shinning in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play a sport with his friends. The sport was hitting a ball with a stick. Tim and his friends played this sport every day after school.
One sunny day, Tim and his friends were playing their sport. Tim was waiting for his turn to hit the ball. He wanted to hit the ball very hard. He knew it would be hard, but he wanted to try. His friends cheered for him.
Finally, it was Tim's turn. He held the stick and got ready. The ball came towards him. He swung the stick and tried to strike the ball as hard as he could. The ball flew far, far away. Tim and his friends were so happy. They knew that practice made them better at their sport. They played until the sun went down, and they all went home tired but happy.

Ben and Sam were playing with their toy soldiers in the garden. They liked to make them march and fight and pretend they were brave. They had a big box of soldiers, some green and some brown, and they put them in different teams.
"Look, Sam, my soldiers are winning!" Ben said, pointing to his green team. "They are stepping on your brown soldiers and making them fall!"
"No, they are not!" Sam said, getting angry. "My soldiers are stronger and smarter than yours! They are hiding behind the rocks and waiting for the right time to attack!"
Sam grabbed a handful of brown soldiers and threw them at Ben's green team. Some of them hit the green soldiers and knocked them over, but some of them missed and landed on the grass.
"Hey, stop that!" Ben shouted. "That's not fair! You are cheating!"
"I am not cheating!" Sam shouted back. "You are cheating! You have more soldiers than me!"
They started to argue and push each other. They forgot about their toy soldiers and only cared about winning the fight.
While they were fighting, a big dog came into the garden. He saw the toy soldiers on the grass and thought they were snacks. He ran towards them and started to bite and chew them. He did not care if they were green or brown, he liked them all.
Ben and Sam heard the dog's barks and stopped fighting. They turned around and saw the dog eating their toy soldiers. They were shocked and scared. They ran to the dog and tried to get their soldiers back, but the dog was too big and strong. He growled and snapped at them and made them run away.
The dog ate all the toy soldiers and left none for Ben and Sam. He was full and happy. He licked his lips and wagged his tail. He did not know he had done something bad.
Ben and Sam were sad and angry. They had no toy soldiers left to play with. They had lost their game and their toys. They blamed each other and started to cry. They were tired and hurt. They did not want to be friends anymore.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a little mushroom. The mushroom was very alert. It always watched what was happening around it. One day, a rabbit came to the forest.
The rabbit saw the mushroom and said, "Hello, little mushroom! Can I cut you and take you home to eat?" The mushroom thought for a moment and said, "No, please don't cut me. I want to stay in the forest and watch everything."
The rabbit felt sad but understood. So, the rabbit and the mushroom became friends. They played together and watched the forest together. They were always alert and saw many things in the forest.
One day, they saw a big bird coming to the forest. The bird wanted to eat the rabbit. But the mushroom helped the rabbit hide from the bird. The bird flew away, and the rabbit was safe.
The rabbit thanked the mushroom for being such a good friend. They continued to be alert and watch the forest together. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Joe. Joe was 3 years old and he loved to play. One day, Joe decided to go to the park with his mom. 
As he was walking to the park, Joe heard a strange noise. He stopped and looked around. Suddenly, he heard the noise again. "Snap!" it said.
Joe was curious, so he followed the sound. He soon found a bossy turtle. The turtle was snapping its beak together. "What are you doing?" Joe asked. 
The turtle replied, "I'm making the time go faster! It's lonely in the park all by myself, so I'm trying to hurry up the day." 
Joe smiled at the turtle and said, "That's a great idea!" Joe gave the turtle a big hug, and then went off to play in the park. 
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a soft bunny. This bunny lived in a big green field. Every day, the bunny would pass by a big tree. The tree was the bunny's friend.
One day, the bunny and the tree played a game. They called it "war". The bunny would try to hop past the tree, and the tree would try to catch the bunny with its long arms. They laughed and had fun.
In the end, the soft bunny always won the war. The tree was happy for the bunny. They both knew that they were best friends. And they played together every day, as the sun shined on the big green field.

One day, a gray cat named Tom went for a jog in the park. He liked to run and play with his friends. While jogging, he saw a big, shiny thing on the ground. It was a microphone!
Tom picked up the microphone and said, "Hello, I am Tom the cat. Can anyone hear me?" His voice was loud and clear. All his friends came running to see what was going on.
His friend, a dog named Max, said, "Wow, Tom! You found a microphone! Let's sing a song together." So, Tom and Max sang a fun song while all their friends danced and clapped. They had a great time at the park with the microphone.

Once upon a time, there was a gray cat. The cat liked to move and play all day. The cat had a friend, a little girl. The little girl loved the cat very much.
One day, the little girl wanted to have a tea party. She asked the gray cat to come and play. The cat was happy to move and join the tea party.
At the tea party, the little girl and the gray cat drank tea and ate cake. They laughed and had fun. When the tea party was over, the gray cat moved back to its bed. They were both very happy and had a great day.

One day, a graceful cat named Lily went for a walk. She saw a big box with a label on it. The label had a picture of a toy. Lily wanted to know what was inside the box.
Lily saw a boy named Tim. She asked him a question, "What is in the box?" Tim looked at the label and said, "It is a toy for you to play with."
Lily and Tim opened the box together. Inside was a soft, colorful ball. They played with the ball all day, and had lots of fun.

One day, a little gray cat named Luna went for a walk. She saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. Up, up, up she went. When she got to the top, she looked around and smiled.
Luna saw a small wet pond near the tree. She climbed down and walked to the pond. At the pond, she met a happy frog. The frog said, "Hi, I am Fred. Do you want to play?"
Luna smiled and said, "Yes, let's play!" They played all day, splashing in the wet pond and running around the tree. Luna and Fred became best friends, and they had fun every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny. He was always very hungry and he loved to chew.
One day, the bunny found a pan in the grass. It was a shiny, metallic pan. He was so excited! He picked it up and he started to chew on it. 
But the pan did not taste very good. It tasted bad! The bunny made a yucky face and stopped chewing the pan. 
At that moment, the bunny had an idea. He hopped over to a nearby apple tree and he grabbed an apple. He started to chew on the apple and it tastes much better! The bunny happily ate his apple and never looked back at the bad pan again.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby bunny named Bob. Bob loved to play in the big maze near his home. One sunny day, Bob met a new friend named Tim, who was a fast turtle.
"Let's play in the maze," said Bob. Tim agreed and they went into the maze together. They played for a long time, but soon they got lost. Bob and Tim started to feel scared.
Just then, a little bird flew by. The bird saw Bob and Tim and wanted to help. The bird was very fast, so it sped through the maze to find the way out. The bird came back and showed Bob and Tim the way out. They were so happy and thanked the bird. From that day on, Bob, Tim, and the bird became best friends, and they played in the maze every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy loved to count. She would count everything she saw. One day, Amy's dad said they would go on a trip in their big red automobile. Amy was very happy because she could count things on the way.
As they started their trip, Amy counted many things. She counted trees, birds, and other automobiles. It was easy for her to count because she loved it so much. Her dad smiled as he listened to her count. He knew that something fun was waiting for them at the end of their trip.
Finally, they arrived at a big park. Amy was so excited! Her dad had planned a surprise for her. In the park, there were one hundred colorful balloons tied to a tree. Amy's eyes lit up, and she started to count the balloons. She counted all the way to one hundred. Amy's dad knew she would love this surprise because she loved to count. They laughed and played with the balloons, and it was a great day for both of them.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a magic bottle. She was very glad to have this bottle. The bottle was her best friend. They played together every day.
One day, Lily found out that her magic bottle could make things increase. When she said "increase," things would grow bigger! She tried it on her toy bear. "Increase!" she said. The bear grew big, big, big! Lily was so happy.
Lily and her big bear played all day. When it was time for bed, she said "increase" again. Her bed grew big, big, big! Lily, her big bear, and her magic bottle all slept in the big bed. They were very glad to be together.

One day, a little boy named Tim went into a smelly room. The room was full of old toys and clothes. Tim wanted to clean the room, so he started to pick up the toys and put them away. He wanted to complete his task before his mom came home.
As Tim was cleaning, he found a big, old teddy bear. The teddy bear was dirty and smelly, but Tim thought it was cute. He picked it up and said, "Hi, Teddy! You are smelly, but I will clean you up!" The teddy bear came to life and said, "Thank you, Tim! I have been waiting for someone to find me!"
Tim and the teddy bear cleaned the room together. They were almost done when they heard Tim's mom coming home. The teddy bear said, "Hide me, Tim! Your mom will not like me because I am smelly!" Tim hid the teddy bear under his bed and went to show his mom the clean room.
But when Tim's mom came into the room, she smelled the smelly teddy bear. She was angry and said, "Tim, this room is still smelly! You did not complete your task!" Tim was sad and looked at the teddy bear under his bed. The teddy bear was sad too, because he knew he was the reason Tim got in trouble.

Once upon a time, in a jolly farm, there was a big pumpkin. The pumpkin was very happy. It liked to play with the other pumpkins in the farm. They all had fun together.
One day, a big machine came to the farm. The machine was called a printer. The printer wanted to print on the pumpkins. The pumpkins did not like this. They were scared. They did not want the printer to print on them.
The big pumpkin tried to help the other pumpkins. It tried to stop the printer. But the printer was too strong. It printed on the big pumpkin and all the other pumpkins too. The pumpkins were very sad. They did not want to have prints on them.
The jolly farm was not jolly anymore. The pumpkins were not happy. They did not like the prints on them. They could not play anymore. They missed the fun they had before the printer came. And so, the big pumpkin and the other pumpkins lived sadly ever after.

Sam and Mia liked to play in the park. They had a toy boat that they could sail on the pond. They also liked to look for mushrooms under the trees. They knew they could not eat them, but they liked their shapes and colors.
One day, they found a big mushroom that looked like an umbrella. It was red with white spots. Sam had an idea. He said to Mia, "Let's sail on the mushroom. It can be our boat."
Mia thought it was a fun game. She said, "OK, let's do it." They lifted the mushroom and carried it to the pond. They put it on the water and climbed on it. They pretended to be pirates and explorers.
But the mushroom was not a good boat. It was wobbly and slippery. It spun around and around on the water. Sam and Mia felt dizzy. They said, "Whoa, this is not fun. This is scary. Let's get off."
They tried to jump off the mushroom, but they fell into the pond. They got wet and cold. They cried for help. A nice lady saw them and helped them out. She said, "You should not play with mushrooms. They are not toys. They can be dangerous. Come, I will take you to your mom and dad."
Sam and Mia were sorry. They said, "Thank you, lady. We are sorry. We will not play with mushrooms again. We will only sail with our toy boat." They learned their lesson. They hugged their mom and dad and went home. They had a warm bath and a hot chocolate. They said, "We love you, mom and dad. We will be careful next time."

One day, a little squirrel named Sam went to the park. He wanted to find a peanut. Sam saw his friend, Tim the turtle, at the park too. They both wanted to find the peanut first. They were very competitive.
Sam said, "Let's jog to find the peanut!" Tim agreed and they started to jog together. They jogged up and down the hills, looking for the peanut.
Finally, they found a big peanut under a tree. Sam and Tim were so happy. They decided to share the peanut and eat it together. They learned that sharing was more fun than being competitive.

One day, a blue train was ready to go. The train was very big and strong. Many people came to ride the train. They had to choose where to sit. Some people sat in the front, and some people sat in the back.
As the train moved, it went very fast. The people were happy and having fun. They looked out the window and saw trees, houses, and animals. But then, something unexpected happened. The train stopped moving!
The people were confused. They did not know why the train stopped. But then, they saw a big, friendly elephant in front of the train. The elephant wanted to join the ride too! The people laughed and let the elephant on the train. They all became friends and continued their journey together.

Once upon a time, there was a humble cat named Tom. Tom was very good at talking in cat language. He was proud of his language and loved to talk to his cat friends.
One day, Tom was walking on a wall. He was talking to his friends in cat language. He was not looking where he was going. Suddenly, he did not see a hole in the wall. Tom started to fall down.
Tom was scared, but his friends helped him. They caught him before he hit the ground. Tom was happy and thankful. He learned that being humble and having good friends is very important. From that day, Tom was always careful and thankful to his friends. And they all lived happily together.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Spike. Spike was a very selfish dog. He did not like to share his toys with other dogs. One day, Spike went to the park to play with his ball.
At the park, Spike met two other dogs named Spot and Fido. They wanted to play with Spike's ball too. But Spike did not want to share. Spot and Fido tried to discuss with Spike about sharing, but he just would not listen.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and blew Spike's ball far away. All three dogs ran to find it. When they found the ball, they all grabbed it at the same time. They realized that playing together was more fun than being selfish. From that day on, Spike learned to share and they all became best friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They took a wide cooler with them. Inside the cooler, there were yummy snacks and cold drinks. Tim was very happy.
At the park, Tim saw a girl named Jane. She was sad because she forgot her snack at home. Tim went to his cooler and said, "I can share my snack with you!" Jane smiled and said, "Thank you, Tim!" They sat down and shared the snacks from the wide cooler.
After they ate, Tim and Jane played together. They ran and jumped, and had lots of fun. When it was time to go home, Tim zipped up the cooler. He learned that sharing is good, and it can make new friends. Jane and Tim were now best friends, all because Tim shared his snacks.

Once upon a time, there was a big nation. In this nation, there were many people who were very proud. They liked to sing, dance, and play with their friends.
One day, a little girl named Mia started to cry. She lost her toy and could not find it. Her friends saw her cry and wanted to help her. They looked high and low for the toy, but they could not find it.
Then, the proud people of the nation heard Mia cry. They all came together to help her find her toy. They looked under rocks, in trees, and behind houses. Finally, they found Mia's toy. Mia stopped crying and was very happy. The proud nation was happy too, because they helped their friend.

One day, a big cat saw a cabinet. Inside the cabinet, there were many yummy treats. The big cat wanted to eat the treats, but the cabinet was closed. The big cat thought, "How can I get the treats?"
The big cat had a friend, a small dog. The big cat asked the small dog, "Can you help me open the cabinet?" The small dog said, "Yes, I can help you. We can steal the treats together."
So, the big cat and the small dog worked together. They pushed the cabinet until it opened. They were successful! They took the treats out and shared them with each other. They were very happy and full.

Once upon a time there was a pumpkin. He was a big, round and enthusiastic fellow. He wanted to stretch and move around, but he was too heavy and stuck in one spot.
He asked the birds and rabbits to help him, but they could only do so much. Then an idea came to him. He asked a friendly caterpillar to crawl up his stem and blow some air. 
The caterpillar blew and blew and blew. The pumpkin felt the air filling him up. He stretched and stretched until he was as long and skinny as a snake.
The animals were so happy. They all gave the enthusiastic caterpillar a big hug and thanked him for his help. 
From then on, the pumpkin was the happiest pumpkin in the world. He was able to stretch and move around as much as he wanted. And everyone was happy to have him in the garden!

Once upon a time, there was a clumsy fairy. She had tiny wings and a big heart. She loved to ride on the back of her friend, a green bug.
One day, the fairy and the bug went for a ride near the big tree. The sun was shining, and they were very happy. They saw many pretty flowers and played with other bugs.
But then, the clumsy fairy fell off the bug's back. She tried to fly, but her tiny wings were not strong enough. The fairy fell down and got hurt. The bug tried to help her, but he was too small.
The fairy cried and cried. Her tiny wings could not lift her up. She and the bug were very sad. They could not play together anymore.
From that day, the clumsy fairy never rode on the bug's back again. She stayed near the ground, and her friend the bug stayed with her. They were still friends, but they missed their happy rides.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a pair of brilliant red pants that he loved to wear. One day, Tim decided to dare himself to do something new and fun.
Tim went to the playground and saw his friend, Sally. Sally was playing on the swing. Tim said, "Sally, I dare you to jump off the swing!" Sally smiled and said, "Okay, Tim! I'll try!" Sally jumped off the swing and landed on the soft grass. She was okay, and they both laughed.
Then, Tim and Sally saw a big slide. They both dared each other to go down the slide together. They climbed up and sat at the top. Tim said, "Ready, Sally?" Sally said, "Ready, Tim!" They both slid down the slide in their brilliant pants, laughing all the way. They had a great day at the playground, daring each other to try new things and having lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Daisy. Daisy had a rag which she loved more than anything else in the world, it was her most secret treasure.
One day, Daisy decided to record something with her rag. She was careless and didn't think about what would happen with the recording. 
Daisy's mum saw her with the rag and said, "Daisy! What are you doing with that rag?". Daisy told her mum that she was recording something. Her mum smiled and asked, "What did you record?". Daisy smiled and said, "Something really special and I want to keep it forever".
And that's what exactly happened. Daisy kept her recording and still treasures it to this day.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. Tim wore his favorite shirt. It was a rare shirt with a big red ball on it. He loved to play with his friends at the park.
While playing, Tim saw a girl named Sue. She was sad. Tim asked, "Why are you sad, Sue?" Sue said, "I lost my toy. Can you help me find it, please?" Tim wanted to help, so they looked for the toy together.
They looked near the slide, by the swings, and under the bench. Finally, they found Sue's toy by the big tree. Sue was very happy. She said, "Thank you, Tim!" Tim felt good for helping his friend. They played together for the rest of the day, and Tim's rare shirt with the big red ball made him easy to find.

Once upon a time, there was a thin cat named Tim. Tim loved to play with his friend, a dog named Sam. One sunny day, Tim and Sam went to the park to play. They ran and jumped, having lots of fun.
As they played, Tim saw a book on the ground. He picked it up and said, "Look, Sam! A book!" Sam wagged his tail and said, "Let's read it!" They sat down under a big tree and opened the book.
As Tim read the book out loud, a bug flew into his nose. "Achoo!" Tim sneezed. Sam laughed and said, "Bless you, Tim!" They continued to read the book and had a great day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a pilot. The pilot flew a big plane. One day, the pilot saw a distant bird in the sky.
"Hello, bird!" the pilot said. The bird did not reply. The pilot felt sad. He wanted to be friends with the bird.
The next day, the pilot saw the bird again. This time, the bird said, "Hello, pilot!" The pilot was happy. They talked and talked. But then, the bird flew away.
The pilot did not see the big tree. The plane hit the tree. The pilot was sad. The bird did not help.

Once there was a cat. The cat was very attractive. The cat liked to slap things. One day, the cat went outside to play.
The cat saw a big red ball. The cat thought, "I want to slap that ball." The cat ran up to the ball and slapped it. The ball went flying. The cat was happy.
Later, it was time for lunch. The cat went home. The cat saw the big red ball again. The ball was by the cat's lunch. The cat slapped the ball again and laughed. The cat ate lunch and played with the ball all day.

Once upon a time, there was a hunter named Tom. Tom lived in a peaceful forest with many animals. He liked to rub his hands together when he was happy.
