One day, Tom met a big, brown bear named Ben. Ben was very friendly. Tom said, "Hi Ben! Do you want to play with me?" Ben nodded his head and they played together all day long.
At the end of the day, Tom and Ben were very tired. They sat under a big tree and watched the sun go down. Tom rubbed his hands together and smiled. They were happy to be friends in the peaceful forest.

One day, a big storm came to a small town. The rain fell hard, and the wind blew strong. Everyone in the town was scared. They knew it was important to stay inside their homes.
A little girl named Mia wanted to help her mom. Her mom needed to buy food for dinner. Mia put on her rain boots and her coat. She said, "Mom, I can go buy the food for you!" Her mom smiled and said, "Thank you, Mia. Be careful in the storm."
Mia went to the store and bought the food. On her way back, she saw a small dog in the rain. The dog was scared and wet. Mia knew she had to help the dog. She took the dog home with her.
When Mia and the dog got home, her mom was surprised. Mia said, "Look, Mom! I found a dog in the storm. Can we keep him?" Her mom looked at the dog and smiled. She said, "Yes, we can keep him. You did a good thing, Mia."
Now, Mia and her new dog are best friends. They play together every day. And when there is a storm, they both stay inside where it is safe and warm.

Once upon a time, there was a noisy horse named Bob. Bob loved to run and play with his friends. One day, while running in the field, Bob found a big feather. He picked it up and put it on his back.
As soon as the feather touched his back, Bob started to fly! He was very surprised and a little scared. But soon, he was having fun flying high in the sky. Bob's friends looked up and saw him. They were very happy and clapped their hands.
But then, the feather fell off Bob's back, and he started to fall down. Just in time, a big bird caught him and put him back on the ground. Bob was safe, and his friends cheered. The bird picked up the feather and flew away. Bob was not a flying horse anymore, but he was still a noisy and happy horse.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to slide, swing and climb on the equipment. They saw a big truck come and stop near the equipment. The truck had a lot of boxes and tools.
"What is that?" Lily asked.
"I don't know. Let's go see," Ben said.
They ran to the truck and saw a man get out. He wore a yellow hat and a blue shirt. He opened a box and took out some wires and a machine.
"Hello, kids. Do you know what I'm doing?" the man asked.
"No. What are you doing?" Lily and Ben said.
"I'm fixing the equipment. It's broken and needs new wires and a machine. Do you want to help me?" the man said.
"Yes, please!" Lily and Ben said.
"OK, but you have to be careful. The wires and the machine can be dangerous. You can help me by laying these wires on the ground, like this," the man said. He showed them how to lay the wires on the ground in a neat way.
Lily and Ben nodded and started to lay the wires on the ground. They liked to help the man. The wires were warm and soft. They made a long line from the box to the equipment.
"Good job, kids. You're very helpful. Now I need to connect the wires to the machine and the equipment. You can watch me, but don't touch anything, OK?" the man said.
"OK," Lily and Ben said.
They watched the man connect the wires to the machine and the equipment. He turned on the machine and pressed some buttons. The equipment made a noise and started to work again.
"Yay! You fixed it!" Lily and Ben said.
"Yes, I did. Thanks to you, too. You were very good helpers. Now you can play on the equipment again. Have fun!" the man said.
He packed his tools and boxes and got back in the truck. He waved to Lily and Ben and drove away.
Lily and Ben ran to the equipment and played on it. They were happy and proud. They had helped the man fix the equipment. They had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play outside in the bright sun. She liked to point at the pretty birds and the big trees.
One day, Lily found a small box under her bed. She opened the box and saw a beautiful bow. The bow was red and shiny. Lily was very happy and put the bow in her hair.
Lily went outside to show her friends her new bow. They all liked it very much. They played games and pointed at the bright sky. Lily felt so happy with her friends and her pretty bow.

Once upon a time, there was a rich man who lived in a big house. He had a long road in front of his house. He liked to walk on the road every day. He knew everyone who lived near the road.
One day, the rich man saw a little dog on the road. The little dog looked sad and lost. The rich man did not know the little dog. But he wanted to help the little dog find his home.
The rich man asked his friends if they knew the little dog. No one knew the little dog. The rich man was sad, but then something unexpected happened. The little dog started to talk! The dog said, "I am not a real dog. I am a magic dog! I will give you one wish for trying to help me." The rich man wished to know everyone's name on the road. Now, the rich man knew everyone and was very happy. The magic dog went back to his magic home, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a big valley, there lived a creative little bird named Tim. Tim loved to test new ways to build his nest. He used sticks, leaves, and even mud to make his home warm and cozy.
One day, Tim had a fun idea. He wanted to test if he could make a nest using flowers. So, he flew around the valley and picked the prettiest flowers he could find. Tim put the flowers together in a creative way to make a beautiful, colorful nest.
When Tim's friends saw his flower nest, they were very happy. They thought it was a great idea and wanted to make their own creative nests too. Soon, the whole valley was filled with nests made of flowers, leaves, and sticks. Tim was proud that he could help his friends and make the valley a more beautiful place to live.

Once upon a time, there was a great big sun. The sun was very hot and bright. One day, the sun saw a cold ice cube on the ground. The sun wanted to help the ice cube, so the sun shined very bright and warm.
As the sun shined, the ice cube started to melt. It turned into a puddle of water on the ground. The sun was happy to help the ice cube. The ice cube was now a puddle, and it could move and play with the other puddles.
Soon, the puddle turned into mist. The mist floated up into the sky, and it joined the clouds. The clouds were happy to have a new friend. The great big sun smiled and shined even brighter. The sun, the mist, and the clouds all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a dark night. The dark was not scary, but it was hard to see. A little boy named Tim was playing outside. He saw a big fog coming. The fog was white and fluffy like a cloud.
Tim saw a little cat in the fog. The cat was lost and scared. Tim wanted to save the cat. He walked into the fog to find the cat. He called out, "Here, kitty, kitty!"
The cat heard Tim and came to him. Tim picked up the cat and walked back to his house. The fog went away and the dark was not so dark anymore. Tim saved the cat and they became best friends. They played together every day, and they were very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Jane. Jane was three years old and was very curious. One day, Jane decided to explore the dark attic of her family home. She opened the door and noticed a mysterious old box. She carefully opened it and was surprised to find an image. It was a bad image - it made Jane scared. 
So, she quickly closed the box and placed it back in the attic. She went downstairs and asked her mom to take away the box. Jane's mom agreed, and requested Jane to never touch the box again.
But Jane couldn't forget about the image inside the box. After a few days, Jane decided to go back to the attic to open the box. She opened it and found the bad image again. This time, the image had changed. Instead of looking bad, it looked beautiful. 
Jane was so surprised. She no longer felt scared, instead she felt inspired by the image. Jane understood that what she thought was bad at first, was actually something she could appreciate. 
Ever since that day, Jane ventured up to the attic often and made more requests to her mom. Jane started to explore the attic and noticed many new things - she was no longer scared.

Lily was a baby girl who liked to play with her toys in her crib. She had a doll, a ball, a book and a teddy bear. She talked to them and made them have fun. She was happy in her crib.
One day, her mom came to her room and said, "Lily, we have to go to grandma's house. We will stay there for a few days. You can bring your toys with you, but not your crib. Grandma has a crib for you there."
Lily did not like this. She loved her crib. It was cozy and safe. She did not want to sleep in another crib. She said, "No, mom, I want my crib. I don't like grandma's crib. It is cheap and old. It makes noise and it hurts."
Mom tried to calm her down. She said, "Lily, don't be sad. Grandma's crib is not cheap and old. It is the crib that I used to sleep in when I was a baby. It is very special and it has a lot of stories. It does not make noise and it does not hurt. It is just different from your crib. You will like it when you see it."
Lily was not sure. She still wanted her crib. But she trusted her mom. She said, "Okay, mom, I will try grandma's crib. But can I have my toys with me?"
Mom smiled and said, "Of course, Lily, you can have your toys with you. They will keep you company and make you feel at home. Now, let's pack your bag and get ready to go."
Lily hugged her mom and her toys. She said goodbye to her crib. She hoped that nothing bad would happen to it while she was away. She hoped that she would like grandma's crib. She was a little scared, but also a little curious. She wondered what stories grandma's crib had to tell.

One day, a little girl named Lucy found a magic ruler. She could measure anything with it. Lucy wanted to measure her toy box. The ruler said it was very big. Lucy was happy because she had a big toy box.
But then, Lucy's friend Tom came to play. He saw the magic ruler and wanted it. Tom said, "Give me the magic ruler, Lucy!" Lucy said, "No, it is mine!" They were both very sad and tight in their hearts.
Lucy thought for a moment. She said, "Let's share the magic ruler, Tom. We can take turns measuring things." Tom agreed, and they both had fun measuring all the things in Lucy's house. They were happy again, and their hearts were not tight anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy bear named Bob. He lived in a big forest. Bob did not like to smile. He liked to be alone.
One day, a happy bunny named Lily hopped by. She saw Bob and said, "Hi, Mr. Bear! Why are you so grumpy? You should smile!" Bob did not want to talk to Lily. He said, "I don't like to smile. Go away, please."
The next day, Lily came back. She had a big cake with her. She said, "I made this cake for you, Mr. Bear. I hope it makes you smile." Bob looked at the cake and started to smile. He had never had a cake before. He said, "Thank you, Lily. This is very nice."
Just then, a big wind came and blew the cake away. Bob and Lily were sad. But then, they both started to laugh. They had fun watching the cake fly away. From that day on, Bob was not grumpy anymore. He learned that it was fun to smile and have friends.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sally. Sally had a brother named Tom. They liked to play together and do silly things. They would run, jump, and laugh all day long. Sally and Tom were best friends.
One day, Sally and Tom met a frog. The frog said, "If you help me, I will make you very happy." Sally and Tom thought it would be fun to help the frog. They carried the frog to a pond and set it free. The frog was very happy and said, "Thank you! Now I will make you happy."
The frog told Sally and Tom that they could marry each other. Sally and Tom thought this was a silly idea. They laughed and said, "We are brother and sister! We can't marry each other!" The frog was very angry. It said, "If you don't marry, I will make you very sad."
Sally and Tom did not want to be sad, but they knew they could not marry each other. They said, "No, frog, we cannot marry." The frog was very, very angry. It jumped into the pond and splashed water on Sally and Tom. They were wet and cold.
Sally and Tom went home, feeling sad and wet. They knew that they had made the frog angry, and now they were not happy. The frog had given them a bad ending. But they still had each other, and they knew that they would always be best friends.

One day, a tutor named Tim went to the park. Tim was very weak, but he loved to talk. In the park, he saw a big tree. Tim sat under the tree to rest.
A little bird came to sit on the tree. The bird said, "Hi, I am a bird who can talk." Tim was very surprised. He said, "Wow, I did not know birds could talk."
The bird said, "I can teach you to be strong if you teach me more words." Tim agreed. They spent many days together. Tim taught the bird many words, and the bird helped Tim get strong.
One day, a strong wind came and the tree almost fell. But Tim was strong now, and he held the tree up. The bird was very happy. They both learned that helping each other can make them better.

One day, a little dog named Max wanted to go on a walk. He saw a trail in the woods. The trail was rough with many rocks and sticks, but Max was brave.
Max started to walk on the trail. He had to manage to jump over the rocks and sticks. It was hard, but Max was happy. He liked the adventure.
As Max walked on the rough trail, he met a friend. It was a small cat named Lily. They both managed to walk on the trail together. They had a fun day and became the best of friends.

One day, a little leopard named Lenny was walking in the forest. He was very nervous because it was his first time alone. Lenny saw many trees, flowers, and animals. He wanted to make new friends.
Lenny saw a big bunny named Benny. Benny was chewing on a carrot. Lenny walked up to Benny and said, "Hi, I am Lenny the leopard. Can we be friends?" Benny looked at Lenny and said, "Yes, we can be friends. I am Benny the bunny."
Lenny and Benny played together in the forest. They laughed and had fun. Lenny was not nervous anymore. He was happy to have a new friend. At the end of the day, they both went home. Lenny knew he would see Benny again soon.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved to gather pretty rocks in her garden. One day, she found a rock that looked like a heart. It was very special to her.
Lucy showed the heart rock to her friend, Tim. Tim said, "Wow, that's a cool rock! Can I hold it?" Lucy felt uncomfortable giving her special rock to Tim, but she wanted to be a good friend. So, she said, "Okay, but please be careful."
Tim held the heart rock and smiled. He knew it was important to Lucy, so he gave it back to her after a moment. Lucy felt happy that she shared her special rock with her friend. They played together all day and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a big leopard. The leopard was very proud. He loved to run fast and jump high. He lived in the jungle with his friends.
One day, the leopard met a small rabbit. The rabbit was trembling. The leopard asked, "Why are you trembling, little rabbit?" The rabbit said, "I am scared of the loud noise." The leopard said, "Don't be scared. I will help you find the noise."
They walked through the jungle together. The proud leopard and the trembling rabbit became good friends. They found the noise. It was just a big bird flapping its wings. They all laughed and played together. The rabbit was not scared anymore.

One day, a happy and enthusiastic girl named Lucy went to the park with her mom. They were going to see a display about the earth. Lucy was very excited because she loved learning about the world around her.
At the park, they saw a big display with pictures of the earth. Lucy saw mountains, rivers, and animals. She pointed at the pictures and said, "Look, Mom! Earth is so pretty!" Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, it is, Lucy."
They spent the whole day at the park looking at the display and talking about the earth. Lucy learned a lot and had so much fun. When it was time to go home, she hugged her mom and said, "Thank you for the best day ever!"

Once upon a time, there was a wealthy man named Tom. Tom lived in a big house and had many friends. One day, Tom wanted to cook a yummy meal for his friends. He needed a pepper to make the meal perfect.
Tom went to the store to buy a pepper. When he got there, he saw a little girl crying. He asked her, "Why are you sad?" She said, "I don't understand why I can't find the pepper I need for my mom." Tom wanted to help the girl, so he looked around the store with her to find the pepper.
As they looked for the pepper, something unexpected happened. Tom and the girl found a hidden door behind the peppers! They opened the door and found a room full of toys and games. The girl was so happy that she forgot about the pepper. Tom and the girl played with the toys and had a great time. In the end, Tom and the girl became best friends and shared the pepper for their meals.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max wanted to go on a journey to find a big bone. He was jealous of his friend, a big dog named Sam, who had found a big bone before. Max knew he would struggle, but he wanted to try.
One sunny day, Max started his journey. He walked and walked, looking for a big bone. He struggled to look under rocks and in bushes. Max was tired, but he did not give up. He wanted the big bone so much.
Then, something unexpected happened. Max found a little cat stuck in a tree. Max forgot about the big bone and helped the cat. The cat was very happy and said, "Thank you, Max!" Max felt so good, he did not feel jealous of Sam anymore. Max learned that helping others is better than being jealous. And they both went on a new journey together, as friends.

Lily liked to help her mom in the kitchen. She was a big girl now and she could do many things. One day, her mom asked her to fill a bowl with olives. Lily loved olives. They were small and round and salty. She went to the pantry and found a big jar of olives. She opened the lid and poured some olives into the bowl. She filled the bowl until it was full of olives.
She carried the bowl to the table, where her mom was making a salad. Her mom was busy chopping lettuce and tomatoes and cheese. She smiled when she saw Lily and the bowl of olives. She said, "Thank you, Lily. You are such a good helper. Do you want to add some olives to the salad?" Lily nodded and picked up a spoon. She scooped some olives from the bowl and sprinkled them over the salad. She liked how they looked with the other colors.
When the salad was ready, her mom said, "Let's go to the living room and wait for dad. He will be home soon and we can have dinner together." Lily followed her mom, holding the bowl of olives. She was proud of her work. She hoped her dad would like the salad and the olives. She also hoped he would save some olives for her. She still wanted to eat some. She loved olives.

One day, a little girl named Mia went for a walk. She saw a dull, gray rock. Mia wanted to pick it up, but she saw a tiny insect on it. The insect was very small and had many legs. Mia did not want to hurt the insect, so she let it pass by.
The insect was trying to get to its home. It was a long way away. Mia wanted to help the insect. She thought of a plan. Mia would pick up the rock and carry it to the insect's home. This way, the insect could ride on the rock and not get hurt.
Mia picked up the rock and started to walk. She was very careful not to drop the rock. The insect held on tight. After a long time, Mia found the insect's home. The insect was very happy. Mia put the rock down and the insect went inside. Mia felt good that she helped her new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely sausage named Sam. Sam lived in a big pan with other sausages. Sam didn't have any friends, because he was different. He was a little bit smaller than the other sausages.
One day, a kind girl named Lucy came into the kitchen. She saw Sam in the pan and said, "Hello, little sausage. Why are you so lonely?" Sam looked up at Lucy and said, "I don't know. I think I am too small."
Lucy smiled and said, "I think you are just right. I will be your friend." Sam was so happy to have a friend. They played and laughed all day long. From that day on, Sam was never lonely again.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named May. She was three years old and loved to explore the world around her. One day she ventured out of her house and climbed up a big mountain. 
May was amazed at how high she had climbed. Suddenly, she came across a giant image. It was huge and difficult to understand. 
"What is this image?" May asked. 
A voice from behind her said, "If you want to know the answer, you have to pay me something." 
Startled, May turned around and saw an old man. He had a twinkle in his eye and a big smile on his face. 
May thought long and hard about what to give the old man as payment. Then she remembered that she had something special at home, so without hesitation she ran off to get it. 
When May returned, she presented the old man with an apple. The old man smiled and thanked her for the kind gesture. 
"The image is a map," said the old man. "The map will show you the way back home". 
May thanked the old man and followed the map back home. She learned a valuable lesson - if you want to learn something new, you have to be willing to pay something.

Once upon a time, there was a silly dog named Max. Max liked to watch videos on the big TV. His favorite video was about a funny cat who did foolish things. Max would laugh and clap his paws when he saw the cat do something silly.
One day, Max found a big box. He thought it was a good idea to jump on the box. But the box broke and Max fell down. He felt a little foolish, just like the cat in the video. Max looked around to see if anyone saw him fall. He was happy that no one was there.
Max learned that it was okay to be foolish sometimes. He still liked to watch the funny cat video and clap his paws. Max also learned to be careful when playing with boxes. And from that day on, Max and his friends would laugh and clap together while watching funny videos on the big TV.

One day, a lovely girl named Amy found a puzzle on the floor. She wanted to play with it. She tried to resist, but she could not. She sat down and started to put the pieces together.
As she played, her friend Tom came over. He saw the puzzle and said, "Can I help you, Amy?" Amy smiled and replied, "Yes, Tom! Let's put the puzzle together." They worked together and talked about their favorite toys.
Soon, the puzzle was done. It was a picture of a big, happy sun. Amy and Tom were very proud of their work. They clapped and laughed. They had a great time playing with the lovely puzzle.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was an honest man named Tom. Tom had a big dog named Max. They were best friends. Tom and Max liked to play and go for long walks together. One day, while they were walking, Max found a shiny rock. The rock was gold!
Tom took the gold rock home and showed it to his mom. His mom said, "We should go back to where Max found the gold rock. Maybe there is more gold there." Tom and Max went back to the place where Max found the gold rock. They looked and looked, but they did not find any more gold.
The next day, Tom and Max went for a walk again. Max led Tom to a different place this time. Max started to dig and dig. What did Max find? More gold rocks! Tom and Max found a lot of gold rocks that day. They were very happy. They knew that Max had led them to the gold rocks. From that day on, Tom and Max were known as the best gold finders in the town.

Tim and Sue like to play hide and seek. They take turns to hide and search. One day, they play in the park. Tim hides behind a big tree. Sue counts to ten and then starts to search.
She looks behind the swings, under the slide, and in the sandbox. She does not find Tim. She looks in the bushes, behind the bench, and in the trash can. She does not find Tim. She starts to feel sad. Where is Tim?
She hears a loud roar. She turns around and sees a big, ugly bear. The bear has Tim in its mouth. Sue screams and runs away. The bear chases her. Sue is very scared. She thinks this is the end.
But then, she sees a man with a gun. The man shoots the bear. The bear falls down and lets go of Tim. Tim is hurt, but alive. The man helps Tim and Sue. He is a park ranger. He says he was searching for the bear, because it escaped from the zoo. He says he is sorry for their fright.
Tim and Sue hug each other. They are happy to be safe. They thank the man. They decide to stop playing hide and seek. They think it is too dangerous. They go home with their mom. They have cookies and milk. They feel better. They say they will play something else tomorrow. The end.

One day, a boy named Tim went to play at the park. He saw a smelly bottle on the ground. Tim picked it up and decided to fill it with water from the pond. When he filled the bottle, a small frog jumped out of it!
"Thank you for helping me," said the frog. "I was stuck in the smelly bottle and could not get out. You are a good friend."
Tim was happy to help the frog. They played together all day at the park. When it was time to go home, the frog had a surprise for Tim. The frog turned into a magic fairy!
"I was a fairy all along," said the fairy. "Because you helped me, I will give you a wish. What do you want?"
Tim thought for a moment and said, "I want to be kind and helpful to everyone I meet." The fairy smiled and granted his wish. From that day on, Tim was always kind and helpful to others.
The moral of the story is to always be kind and helpful, because you never know who you might be helping.

One day, two twin cats went to play. They were very hairy. They liked to count things. They found a big tree with many leaves. The twins wanted to count the leaves.
The first twin started to count. "One, two, three..." He was very happy. The second twin also started to count. "One, two, three..." She was also very happy. They counted and counted, until they were tired.
The hairy twins went home. They told their mom about the big tree and how they counted the leaves. Mom was very proud of them. The twins went to bed, happy and tired from their fun day of counting.

Johnny was outside playing with his truck when a tornado came. He was careless and didn't run away. He was sucked up in the tornado and felt scared. He wished he had been more careful.
The tornado carried Johnny over the town and his truck fell and broke. Johnny felt very sad and regretted not listening to his mom.
The tornado eventually stopped and Johnny landed gently in a tree. He was thankful to be safe and sound. He looked around him and all the damage the tornado had done. He was sorry for his carelessness and knew to be more careful in the future.

One day, a big dog named Max went for a walk. He saw a bone on the ground. The bone was very big. Max was very happy and wanted to take the bone home.
On his way home, Max met a little cat named Bella. Bella saw the big bone and wanted to play with it too. Max did not want to share the bone. He yelled, "No, it's my bone!"
Bella was sad, but she had an idea. She found another big bone and brought it to Max. Now, they both had a big bone to play with. Max and Bella were happy and played together all day.

Once upon a time, there was a bossy taxi. The taxi liked to tell all the other cars what to do. The taxi would say, "You go here, you go there!" The cars did not like the bossy taxi.
One day, a little girl needed a ride to the park. She asked the bossy taxi, "Can you take me to the park, please?" The bossy taxi said, "No, I will not allow it. I am too busy telling the other cars what to do." The little girl was sad.
A kind car saw the little girl and said, "I can take you to the park." The little girl was happy. They went to the park and had a fun day. The bossy taxi saw them and learned that being kind is better than being bossy. From that day on, the taxi was nice to all the cars and they all became friends.

Once upon a time, in an ordinary park, there was a swing. The swing was very happy because it loved to play with kids. Every day, many kids would come to the park and play on the swing.
One sunny day, a little girl named Mia came to the park. She saw the swing and ran to it. Mia sat on the swing and started to go back and forth, higher and higher. She was having so much fun.
Mia wanted to tell her friends about the swing. She called her friends Tom and Lily to come to the park. They all played on the swing together, laughing and having a great time. The swing was so happy to have new friends to play with. And they all lived happily ever after.

Mia was a little girl who loved to admire the world around her. She was always looking at the birds flying in the sky and the flowers blooming in the garden.
One day, Mia's mom took her for a walk in the park. Mia was so excited, she saw so many things that she wanted to explore. She noticed a little bird sitting on a tree. She stopped and watched the bird.
Suddenly, the bird flew away and Mia's mom said, "That bird was so pretty, wasn't it?".
Mia smiled and said, "Yes mommy, I wondered if I could give the bird a hug!".
Mia's mom smiled, "I'm sure if it was here, it would love a little hug from you."
Mia and her mom continued their walk and Mia admired all the little things she saw along the way. She felt so happy and content.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a light blue dress that she loved very much. One day, while playing outside, she heard a small voice.
"Help me, please!" said the voice. Lily looked around and saw a tiny bird with a tear in its wing. "I can't fly," the bird cried.
Lily wanted to help the bird. She picked it up and took it home. Her mom helped her fix the tear in the bird's wing with a soft bandage. They took care of the bird until it felt better.
One day, the bird said, "Thank you, Lily, for helping me. Now I can fly again!" The bird flew up into the sky, and Lily waved goodbye. She felt happy that she could help her new friend. From that day on, Lily and the bird would always play together, and they became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a tidy room with a big, soft blanket on the floor. A little dog named Spot liked to play in this room. Spot loved to spin around and around on the blanket. One day, Spot saw a small ball in the corner of the room. He knew it would be fun to play with.
Spot ran to the ball and started to spin around with it. He was having so much fun. But, as he spun, the ball rolled under the blanket. Spot stopped spinning and looked for the ball. He knew it was hiding under the big, soft blanket.
Spot went to the blanket and started to dig. He dug and dug until he found the ball. When he pulled it out, the blanket was all messy and not tidy anymore. Spot felt bad for making a mess. So, he tried to fix the blanket by spinning around on it again. This time, when Spot stopped spinning, the blanket was tidy once more. Spot was happy that he could make the blanket tidy again and went back to playing with his ball.

Once upon a time, in a big town, there was a doctor. His job was to work and help people feel better. One day, a different kind of patient came to see him. This patient was not a person, but a small, sad cat.
The doctor was kind and patient with the cat. He looked at the cat and saw that it had a hurt leg. The doctor knew just what to do to help the cat feel better. He put a soft band on the cat's leg.
After some days, the cat started to feel better. It could walk and play again. The cat was happy and so was the doctor. They became good friends. The cat would visit the doctor every day, and they would play together. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a fine castle. The people of the castle had a thief they feared. One day, the thief came and stole some of their things.
The people were sad and scared. They had to find him and stop him from taking their belongings. A brave knight said, "We must have the thief. I will find him for you." 
So the knight gathered his horse and went off to search for the thief. After hours of travelling, he came across a deserted hut. He could hear someone inside, so he slowly opened the door. He saw the thief, who was trying to hide from him. 
The brave knight shouted at the thief, "Stop. You have to give back the things you have taken! Now give them back!" The thief was scared, so he dropped all of the things he had taken and ran away. 
The brave knight and the people were happy. The thief was gone, and all of their things were safe. The people thanked the knight and said, "We are so lucky to have you!" The knight smiled and said, "No, you are lucky to be so fine."

John was a 3 year old and he was excited. He had just seen a beautiful butterfly and he wanted to go and explore. He looked down and saw that his lips were cold. John couldn't help but wonder why his lips were cold while the butterfly was warm.
He asked his mum, but she didn't know the answer. So, John kept on wondering. He decided to take off on a little adventure and see what he could find.
He saw all kinds of animals, flowers and trees. But he still couldn't figure out why his lips were cold. Then he saw a pond and he wondered if that had the answer.
So he walked up to the pond and looked inside. He saw the reflection of the sun and he finally understood why his lips were cold. He realized that the sun had been behind him the whole time and the coldness on his lips was from the shade.
He smiled and was happy he solved the mystery. Now, he was ready to keep exploring and discover new things.

Sara and Ben are friends. They like to play at the park. They like to slide on the big slide. Sara has long hair. Ben has short hair.
One day, Sara's mom says, "Sara, you need a haircut. Your hair is too long. It gets in your eyes and your mouth. Come, let's go to the salon."
Sara does not want a haircut. She likes her long hair. She says, "No, mom. I don't want a haircut. My hair is pretty. Ben likes my hair."
Sara's mom says, "Sara, a haircut will not make your hair less pretty. It will make it easier to play and to see. Ben will still like your hair. Trust me, you will like it too."
Sara is scared. She does not want to go to the salon. She does not want to see the scissors. She cries and runs away. She runs to the park. She sees Ben. She says, "Ben, help me. My mom wants to cut my hair. I don't want a haircut. I want to keep my hair."
Ben says, "Sara, why are you crying? A haircut is not bad. I have a haircut. It does not hurt. It makes me feel cool and clean. Your mom loves you. She wants you to be happy and healthy. A haircut will not change who you are. You will still be Sara. You will still be my friend. You will still be pretty."
Sara listens to Ben. She thinks about what he says. She says, "Ben, you are right. A haircut is not bad. It is just hair. It will grow back. My mom loves me. She knows what is best for me. I will go to the salon. I will try a haircut. Thank you, Ben. You are a good friend."
Sara and Ben hug. They go back to Sara's mom. Sara says, "Mom, I am sorry. I was scared. I will go to the salon. I will get a haircut. I trust you, mom. You know what is best for me."
Sara's mom smiles. She says, "Sara, I am proud of you. You are brave. You are smart. You are kind. You are my pretty girl. Come, let's go to the salon. I will be with you. It will be fun."
Sara and Ben go to the salon. Sara gets a haircut. She looks in the mirror. She likes her haircut. It is short and cute. It does not get in her eyes and her mouth. It is easy to play and to see. She says, "Mom, I like my haircut. It is pretty. Thank you, mom."
Sara's mom says, "You are welcome, Sara. I love you. You are pretty. You are pretty with long hair. You are pretty with short hair. You are pretty with any hair. You are pretty because you are you."
Sara and Ben go back to the park. They slide on the big slide. They have fun. They are happy. They are friends.
The end.
Moral value: The story teaches that haircuts are not scary or bad, that they do not change who you are, that you should trust your parents, and that you are pretty no matter what.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a strong friend, a big lion named Leo. They liked to play games and have fun together. One day, they went on an adventure in the forest.
While in the forest, they found a tree with yummy fruit. Tim and Leo decided to taste the fruit. They both took a bite, and it was so sweet and tasty. They laughed and ate more of the fruit.
As they walked through the forest, they found a big hill. Tim and Leo took a big breath with their lungs and ran up the hill. They felt so strong and happy. At the top of the hill, they looked at the beautiful view and smiled. Tim and Leo had the best day ever.

One day, a kind boy named Tom went to the beach to play. He liked to play in the sand and make big sand castles. Tom saw a little girl named Sue who was sad.
"Hi, Sue! Why are you sad?" Tom asked. Sue looked at him and said, "I don't know how to make a sand castle. It's too hard for me."
Tom wanted to help Sue. He said, "I can show you how to make a sand castle. It's easy and fun!" Sue smiled and said, "Thank you, Tom!"
Tom showed Sue how to make a sand castle. They played together all day and made the biggest sand castle on the beach. Sue was very happy and thanked Tom for being so kind. They became best friends and played in the sand every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little nut named Nutty. Nutty was very adventurous. He liked to go on big trips and see new things. Nutty lived in a big tree with his family. They were all happy nuts.
One day, Nutty went on a walk. He saw a big hill and wanted to go up. Nutty went up the hill and saw a big, pretty butterfly. The butterfly had many colors, like red, blue, and yellow. Nutty and the butterfly became friends. They played together and had fun.
After playing, Nutty and the butterfly went back to the tree. Nutty's family met the butterfly and they all became friends. They played and laughed together. Nutty was happy to have a new friend and to share his adventure with his family.

Once upon a time, in a big pond, there was a noisy otter named Ollie. Ollie liked to call out to his friends to play. He would shout, "Hey, friends! Come play with me!" All the animals in the pond would hear him and come to play.
One day, a new animal came to the pond. Her name was Lily, and she was a quiet little frog. Ollie saw Lily and called out, "Hey, Lily! Come play with us!" But Lily was shy and did not like the noisy otter. She hid behind a big rock and did not come out to play.
Ollie felt sad that Lily did not want to play. He thought about it and decided to try something new. The next day, Ollie called out to Lily in a soft voice, "Hi, Lily. Would you like to play with me?" This time, Lily came out from behind the rock and joined Ollie and his friends.
Ollie learned that being too noisy can scare others away. He learned to be kind and gentle with his new friend Lily. From that day on, Ollie and Lily played together every day, and they were the best of friends. And the moral of the story is to be kind and gentle with others, so you can make new friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She was a nurse who helped people feel better. One day, she was walking in the park. It was a sunny day and the birds were singing. Lily was happy.
Suddenly, a big, frightened dog came running towards her. Lily was scared, but she did not run away. She knew she could help the dog feel better. The dog stopped in front of her and started to cry. Lily hugged the dog and told him it was okay.
Then, something unexpected happened. The dog talked! He said, "Thank you, Lily. I was scared because I lost my way home." Lily could not believe it. She thought dogs could not talk. But she did not disagree with the dog. Together, they found the dog's home and became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue liked to play with her toys in her warm room. One day, she found a small, green leaf. It was a mint leaf. Sue thought it was pretty, so she put it with her toys.
Later, Sue and her toys wanted to have a tea party. They all gathered around the table, and Sue tried to settle her toys in their chairs. But the mint leaf would not sit still. It kept moving and dancing around the table. Sue was surprised and couldn't believe what she saw.
Suddenly, the mint leaf stopped dancing and turned into a little fairy. The fairy said, "Thank you for finding me, Sue. I was lost, and now I can go home." With a wave of her wand, the fairy turned back into a mint leaf and flew away. Sue and her toys were amazed by what happened. They had a great tea party and never forgot their new fairy friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She loved to play in her old yard. One sunny day, she found a cherry tree in her yard. The tree had big red cherries on it.
Mia called her friend Tom. "Tom, come see the cherry tree!" she said. Tom ran to the yard and saw the tree. "Wow, Mia! This is a nice tree," he said.
Mia and Tom picked cherries from the tree. They lay on the grass and ate cherries. The sun was warm and the cherries were sweet. They both had a fun day in the old yard with the cherry tree.

Once upon a time, there was a large bear. The bear was very sleepy. He went to sleep in a big cave.
One morning, a little bird came to the cave. The bird wanted to play with the bear. The bird tried to wake the bear up. The bird sang a pretty song to urge the bear to wake up.
The large bear woke up and saw the little bird. The bear was happy to have a friend. They played all day in the warm sun. And from that day on, they became the best of friends.

The sky was gloomy and dark, and it was starting to pour rain. Mama and Sammy were outside, playing together. Mama said, "Let's try to measure how much it is raining." She got a long ruler, and Sammy helped her to hold it up straight. Slowly, the ruler filled up with water.
"Wow! Look at that," said Mama. 
Sammy smiled. "Wow!" he said. 
Mama then looked at Sammy and said, "Sometimes, bad things like rain may happen, but there is something wonderful that comes out of it. Do you know what that is?"
Sammy thought for a minute and then said, "It's fun to measure rain!"
Mama smiled. "Yes, but also there is something else. When it rains, we can plant new flowers and the world becomes a beautiful place." 
Mama and Sammy hugged and went inside. They had learned that even in difficult times, the world can become brighter if you try to make the best out of it.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to read a book. He went to his room and looked for a book with pictures of big trucks. He loved big trucks because they had a lot of power.
Tim found a book and sat down to read it. He looked at the pictures and tried to read the words. But some words were hard, and he felt stupid. Tim's mom saw him looking sad and asked, "What's wrong, Tim?"
"I can't read the words in this book. I feel stupid," Tim said. His mom hugged him and said, "You are not stupid. Reading is hard when you are little. I will help you." They read the book together and Tim felt happy. He knew that with his mom's help, he would learn to read and not feel stupid anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a lovely little bird. The bird liked to sing. It sang all day long. The bird lived near a creek. The creek was full of water and fish.
One day, the bird flew to the creek. It saw a big fish. The bird wanted to be friends with the fish. The bird sang a song for the fish. The fish liked the song.
The bird and the fish became friends. They played near the creek every day. The bird sang songs, and the fish swam in the water. They were very happy together.

Anna loves to dance. She dances at home, at school, and at the park. She wants to be a dancer when she grows up. She thinks dancers are brilliant and beautiful.
But not everyone likes Anna's dancing. Some kids at school laugh at her and call her names. They say she is silly and clumsy. They make her feel sad and angry.
One day, Anna has a chance to show her dancing at the school show. She is very excited and nervous. She practices a lot and wears a pretty dress. She hopes the kids will like her dancing.
But when she goes on the stage, she sees the mean kids in the front row. They start to boo and jeer at her. They throw paper balls and pencils at her. They make her feel scared and hurt.
Anna wants to run away and cry. But then she hears a voice from the back of the room. It is her best friend, Ben. He shouts, "Go, Anna, go! You are a brilliant dancer! I support you!" He claps and cheers for her. He makes her feel happy and brave.
Anna smiles and thanks Ben. She ignores the mean kids and dances with all her heart. She shows them her moves and her grace. She makes them feel surprised and quiet.
Anna finishes her dance and bows. The whole room claps and cheers for her. Even the mean kids look ashamed and sorry. Anna feels proud and joyful.
She runs to Ben and hugs him. He hugs her back and says, "You are amazing, Anna! You are my best friend!" She says, "You are amazing, Ben! You are my best friend, too!" They laugh and celebrate. They support each other. They are brilliant dancers.

Steven was an intelligent 3 year old boy. He loved playing with his train set, and he wanted to build a track.
His father asked, "What do you want to build, Steven?"
Steven answered, "I want to build a track."
His father smiled and said, "Okay, let's start building the track!"
Steven and his dad started putting the track together. They put some pieces here and some pieces there. They made sure to keep it nice and straight.
After a few hours, Steven's track was complete. He beamed with joy and said, "Look at my track!"
His father said, "It looks great. You're so smart, Steven!"
Steven smiled proudly and said, "Thank you, Daddy. Let's play trains now!"

One day, an ordinary insect named Tim was walking in the park. Tim was not a big insect, but he was very friendly. He liked to count and number things he saw.
As Tim walked, he saw a little girl named Amy. Amy looked sad. Tim asked, "Why are you sad, Amy?" Amy said, "I lost my toy. I can't find it." Tim wanted to help Amy. He said, "Let's look for your toy together."
Tim and Amy walked and looked for the toy. They counted the steps they took. One, two, three, four. Tim saw a red ball under a bush. "Is this your toy?" he asked. Amy smiled and said, "Yes, that's my toy! Thank you, Tim!" They were both happy and played with the toy together.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a fragile cup. This cup was very sad. It sat on a high shelf with a dirty rag. The cup wanted to escape the shelf and see the world.
One day, a friendly bird flew into the house. The bird saw the sad cup and said, "Hello, cup! Why are you sad?" The fragile cup replied, "I am sad because I want to escape this shelf and see the world, but I am too fragile."
The bird thought for a moment and said, "I have an idea! I can use this rag to help you. I can wrap you in the rag, and then I can fly with you to see the world!" The fragile cup was very happy.
The bird carefully wrapped the fragile cup in the rag and held it in its beak. Together, they escaped the shelf and flew around the world. The fragile cup saw many beautiful things and was no longer sad. The bird and the cup became the best of friends and had many more adventures together.

Anna loved to play with her toys in the bathtub. She had a yellow duck, a blue boat, a green frog and a pink fish. She was eager to enter the bathtub every night and make bubbles and splashes with her toys.
One night, she saw a new toy on the shelf. It was a big, black, furry toy. It had two pointy ears, two shiny eyes and two sharp teeth. Anna thought it was a funny toy and wanted to play with it. She asked her mom, "What is this toy, mom?"
Her mom said, "That is not a toy, Anna. That is a real bat. It flew into the house through the window and I caught it with a towel. I put it on the shelf until I can take it outside. Do not touch it, Anna. It might bite you."
Anna nodded, but she was curious about the bat. She wondered how it felt and how it moved. She thought it might be fun to play with it in the bathtub. She waited until her mom left the bathroom and then she reached for the bat.
The bat was not happy to be grabbed by Anna. It squeaked and flapped its wings and tried to escape. Anna was scared and dropped the bat into the bathtub. The bat splashed into the water and got wet and angry. It flew out of the bathtub and chased Anna around the bathroom. Anna screamed and ran to the door. She opened the door and ran to her mom.
Her mom heard Anna's scream and came to the bathroom. She saw the bat flying around and Anna crying. She grabbed a towel and threw it over the bat. She wrapped the bat in the towel and took it outside. She came back to the bathroom and hugged Anna.
She said, "Are you okay, Anna? Did the bat hurt you?"
Anna said, "No, mom. I'm sorry, mom. I wanted to play with the bat, but it was not a toy. It was scary and mean. I don't like bats."
Her mom said, "It's okay, Anna. I'm glad you are not hurt. Bats are not toys. They are wild animals. They do not like to be touched or wet. They like to fly in the dark and eat bugs. You should never play with bats. You should only play with your toys in the bathtub."
Anna said, "Yes, mom. I understand. I will only play with my toys in the bathtub. Can I still play with my duck and my boat and my frog and my fish?"
Her mom said, "Of course, Anna. You can play with your toys in the bathtub. But first, let me dry you and put on your pajamas. Then you can enter the bathtub and have fun."
Anna smiled and hugged her mom. She was eager to enter the bathtub and play with her toys. She did not want to see the bat ever again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom liked to play outside with his dog, Spot. One day, they were playing near a big ice block. Spot was running very fast and hit the ice. Poor Spot got a bad hurt on his leg.
Tom was sad to see Spot in pain. He wanted to help his friend heal. So, Tom went to his mom and told her about Spot's bad hurt. His mom knew what to do. She got a soft cloth and wrapped it around Spot's leg.
After a few days, Spot started to feel better. The soft cloth helped his leg heal. Tom was so happy to see his friend running and playing again. Now, they both knew to be careful when playing near the ice. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a big, dependable dog named Spot. Spot loved to go for walks in the park. One sunny day, Spot and his little friend, Tim, went for a walk together.
As they walked, Tim said, "Spot, you are my best friend. You always make me feel happy and safe." Spot wagged his tail and barked, showing that he was happy too.
They played and ran in the park until it was time to go home. Tim and Spot walked back, feeling tired but happy. They knew they would always be there for each other, no matter what.

Once upon a time, there was a shy little dog named Spot. Spot lived in a small house with a porch. He loved to sit on the porch and watch the world go by.
One day, it was very cold outside. Spot went out to the porch and started to shiver. He wanted to play, but he was too cold. So, he went back inside the house to get warm.
Spot's friend, a nice girl named Sue, saw him shivering on the porch. She felt sad for Spot. She went home and got a warm, soft blanket for him. Sue put the blanket on Spot, and they both sat on the porch. Spot was happy and warm, and they played all day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a man named Tom. Tom liked to work. He had a shop where he would trade toys with the kids in town. Tom was very organized. He had all his toys in nice rows on the shelves.
One day, a little girl named Sue came into the shop. She wanted to trade her old toy for a new one. Tom looked at the toy and said, "Okay, let's trade." But when Tom tried to find a new toy for Sue, all the toys were gone! Tom was sad and didn't know what to do.
Then, they heard a small giggle. They looked around and saw a little boy named Tim. Tim had played a trick on Tom and hid all the toys. Tom and Sue laughed, and Tim helped put all the toys back on the shelves. In the end, Sue traded her old toy for a new one, and they all became good friends.

One day, a big sun rose in the sky. It was a bright and happy day. A little bird named Tim flew around and saw a tight nest. The nest was made of soft leaves and small sticks. Tim thought it was a perfect place to rest.
Tim went to his friend, a little cat named Sam. "Sam, I have news!" said Tim. "I found a tight nest. We can play there!" Sam was excited and wanted to see the nest too. So, they both went to the nest and played together.
As the sun began to set, Sam and Tim felt tired. They said goodbye to each other and went to their homes. Tim went back to the tight nest, and Sam went to his warm bed. They both slept well, dreaming of more fun days playing together.

Once upon a time, there was a wise old dog named Spot. Spot had a small flea friend named Tiny. They lived in a big house with a nice family.
One day, Spot felt a poke on his back. It was Tiny, the flea. Tiny said, "Spot, I need your help! I lost my way home." Spot wanted to help his friend. They started to look for Tiny's home together.
As they looked, they found a hidden door in the house. They went inside and saw a big room filled with toys and treats. It was a surprise! The family made this room just for Spot and Tiny. Now, they had a new place to play and live happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Mia. Mia loved to play with her toy car. She would make it zoom all around her room. Mia was very happy when she played with her toy car.
One day, Mia saw a boy named Tom. Tom was sad because he did not have a toy to play with. Mia wanted to help Tom. She thought hard about what to do. Then, Mia had a great idea. She would give Tom a toy from her sack of toys.
Mia went to her room and got her sack. She looked inside and found a toy car just like hers. Mia gave the toy car to Tom. Tom's face lit up with joy. He was so happy to have a toy to play with. Now, Tom could zoom his toy car around just like Mia.
Mia and Tom became good friends. They played together with their toy cars every day. They learned that sharing can make others happy. And when you make others happy, you feel happy too. The moral of the story is to always share with others and spread happiness.

Once upon a time, a shy little dog named Tim lived in a small house. Tim had zero friends because he was too scared to talk to the other animals. One day, Tim decided he wanted to make friends, so he went to the park.
At the park, Tim saw a big cat named Max. Tim was scared, but he wanted to be friends with Max. He walked up to Max and said, "Hi." Max looked at Tim and said, "Hi," back. Tim was so happy that he didn't cry.
From that day on, Tim and Max were best friends. They played together every day. Tim wasn't shy anymore, and he never felt like crying again. And he never had zero friends again.

One day, a girl named Ann went to the park with her mom. Ann loved to play in the sun. She would run, jump, and look at all the pretty flowers. Ann's mom knew that playing in the sun could make her skin feel hot, so she always brought a thick lotion with them.
Before they played, Ann's mom put the thick lotion on Ann's arms and face. Ann liked the way the lotion felt. It was cool and soft on her skin. She knew the lotion would help her stay safe in the sun.
Ann and her mom had a fun day at the park. They played until the sun went down. Ann was happy that her mom put the thick lotion on her. She knew it was important to take care of her skin, and she loved spending time with her mom.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went to the park. It was a peaceful day. The sun was out, and the birds were singing. Lucy sat down under a big tree and took out her lunch. In her lunch box, there was an orange and a wrap.
A boy named Tim saw Lucy and walked over to her. "Hi, can I sit with you?" he asked. "Yes!" said Lucy, and they sat together under the tree. They talked and laughed while they ate their lunch.
After a while, a big wind came and blew Lucy's wrap away. Lucy was sad. Tim tried to help by giving her his last orange, but Lucy was still upset. The day was not so peaceful anymore, and they both went home feeling sad.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a big dog named Spot. Spot was enormous and loved to play outside. One day, Spot was playing in the grass with his friends, a cat named Fluffy and a little bird named Chirpy.
Spot was having fun, but he saw that Fluffy and Chirpy did not like the grass. They said they hate the grass because it was itchy and made them sneeze. Spot felt sad because his friends were not happy. So, he thought of a way to make them feel better.
Spot found a big blanket and put it on the grass. Fluffy and Chirpy were happy because they could play without the itchy grass. They all played together and had a great time. The moral of the story is to always help your friends and find ways to make everyone happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big, heavy box that lived in the corner of a small room. It had a little letter on top of it. The letter said, "Do not open the box!" Everyone who saw the box and the letter was very curious about what was inside.
One day, a little boy named Tim came into the room. He saw the big, heavy box and the letter on top. Tim wanted to know what was inside the box, but he remembered the letter said not to open it. He thought and thought about what could be inside.
Finally, Tim decided to open the box. He lifted the heavy lid and looked inside. To his surprise, there were many colorful balloons! The balloons floated out of the box and filled the room. Tim was so happy he opened the box, and he played with the balloons all day long. The letter was not right, opening the box made Tim very happy.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a long, tall tree. The tree was very big and old. All the kids loved to play near the tree.
One day, a big storm came to the town. The storm was very strong and it started to destroy things. The people in the town were scared. The wind blew hard and the rain fell fast.
After the storm, the people of the town asked the judge to help them. The judge was a wise man. He told the people to work together and fix what the storm destroyed. The people listened to the judge and they all worked together. They fixed their town and the long, tall tree was still there for the kids to play. Everyone was happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who loved to fly. Every day after dinner, she would go to the kitchen and open the window. Then, with a big jump, she would fly out of the window into the sky.
On one day, the little girl wanted to fly higher than ever before. She jumped out of the window, but then realized that she had forgotten to open it. She bumped into the window hard.
But she was not discouraged. She tried again and this time the window opened. She flew higher and higher, past the clouds and up into the sky.
At last, she came back to the kitchen window and flew through. She was so happy and proud of her flying that she did not mind the hard landing.

Once upon a time, there was a family of four. They were all happy together. One day, the family tried to find ways to improve. They thought it would be easy.
But it wasn't easy. The father wanted to do one thing, the mother another, and the kids wanted yet another thing. Everyone wanted something different. 
There was a lot of arguing. No one could agree. The family was getting worse, not better.
Finally, the father got angry and shouted at everyone. The mother and kids were so sad, they all ran away.
The family never got better. They never saw each other again.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went to a big food place with her mom. They sat down and looked at the menu. Lucy noticed a big picture of a yummy cake on the menu. She said, "Mom, I want that cake!"
Just then, a big dog walked into the food place. Lucy was frightened. She hid under the table and said, "Mom, I am scared of the big dog!" Her mom said, "Don't worry, the dog is nice. He just wants to say hi."
The dog came to Lucy and gave her a big, wet kiss. Lucy laughed and was not scared anymore. She learned that sometimes things that look scary can be nice. She enjoyed the yummy cake and made a new friend in the big dog.

One day, a boy named Tim saw a big jeep. The jeep was red and shiny. Tim really wanted the jeep, but his mom said no. Tim was sad and felt spoiled.
Later, Tim went to play with his friend, Sue. Sue noticed the spoiled look on Tim's face. She asked, "Why are you sad, Tim?" Tim told her about the red jeep he wanted.
Just then, they heard a loud noise. They looked and saw the red jeep! But the jeep was not shiny anymore. It was dirty and had a flat tire. Tim and Sue laughed. They were happy they did not have the spoiled jeep.

Once upon a time, there was a little cabin in the woods. It was a dependable cabin, always safe and warm for the people who lived there. The cabin was made of wood and had a big door.
One day, a girl named Lucy wanted to display her pretty toys. She put them on a table near the window so everyone could see. She was so happy to share her toys with the people who passed by the cabin.
Lucy's friends came to see her toys. They all liked the display and played together. The cabin was full of laughter and fun. Lucy and her friends knew they could always count on the dependable cabin for a good time.

Once upon a time, there was a unique bug named Max. Max was a mosquito. Max had a friend named Sam. Sam was a frog. Max and Sam liked to play by the pond.
One day, Max and Sam had a big fight. Max wanted to play hide and seek, but Sam did not. Sam was mad. Max had an idea. Max said, "Let's ask our friends what to play." Sam thought it was a good idea.
They asked all their friends. Some friends said they should play tag. Some friends said they should play hide and seek. Max was sad. Sam was sad too. They could not agree on a game to play. So, Max and Sam stopped being friends. The end.

One day, a dog named Max went for a walk. Max liked to walk on his legs and feel the calm wind on his face. The sun was shining and the sky was blue. Max was very happy.
As Max walked, he saw a big tree. Under the tree, there was a cat. The cat was also calm and happy. Max wanted to say "hi" to the cat. But as he walked closer, he felt a tickle in his nose.
Max tried to stop it, but he could not. He sneezed very loud! The cat jumped up and looked at Max. Max said, "I'm sorry, I did not mean to scare you." The cat looked at Max and said, "It's okay, I sneeze too sometimes." They both laughed and became friends. Now, Max and the cat like to walk together and enjoy the calm days.

Once upon a time, there was a woman who lived in a small house. She had a big red balloon that she loved very much. One day, she decided to go outside and play with her balloon.
While she was playing, the wind started to blow very hard. The woman held her balloon tight so it would not fly away. But the wind was too strong, and the balloon started to rise high in the sky.
The woman was sad, but she did not give up. She jumped and reached for her balloon. As she held it tight, the balloon helped her rise too. They both went up and up, and soon, they were flying together in the sky. The woman and her balloon had a great time, and they became the best of friends.

One day, a cheerful girl named Lily was playing outside. She liked to play in the sun. Lily had a big smile on her face. She was very happy.
But then, it started to rain. The rain made Lily sad. She did not like to play in the rain. She wanted the sun to come back.
Lily had an idea. She sang a happy song to make the rain go away. As she sang, the rain stopped. The sun came back out. Lily's face was happy again. She could play in the sun once more.

One day, a big dog and a little dog were playing near a gate. The big dog was fast and strong. The little dog was envious. He wanted to be fast and strong too.
The big dog said, "I can jump over the gate." He ran and jumped high, over the gate. The little dog tried to jump too, but he was too small. He was sad.
Later, it rained. The big dog was wet and cold. The little dog found a warm, dry place under a tree. The big dog said, "I wish I was small like you." The little dog smiled and they played together, happy and dry.

Tim and Lily like to play in the park. They run and jump and slide and swing. They have fun with their friends and their toys. But they also like to look for animals in the park. They see birds and squirrels and bugs. Sometimes they see a frog.
One day, they find a big frog near the pond. It is green and brown and has spots. It looks funny and friendly. Tim and Lily want to touch it and play with it.
"Hello, frog!" Tim says. "Do you want to be our friend?"
The frog does not say anything. It just sits on a rock and blinks.
"Can I hold you, frog?" Lily asks. She reaches out her hand to the frog.
But the frog is not a nice frog. It is a dangerous frog. It has poison on its skin. If Lily touches it, she will get sick and hurt.
"Stop, Lily!" a voice says. It is their mom. She sees the frog and knows it is bad. She runs to them and pulls Lily away from the frog.
"That is a dangerous frog, Lily. You cannot touch it. It will make you sick and hurt. We have to stay away from it."
Lily is scared and sad. She does not understand why the frog is dangerous. She thinks it looks funny and friendly.
"Why is the frog dangerous, mom?" she asks.
"Because it has poison on its skin, Lily. Some frogs have poison to protect themselves from other animals. But we are not other animals. We are people. We have to be careful with animals we do not know. Some of them can be dangerous."
Tim and Lily nod. They understand now. They do not want to touch the frog. They do not want to get sick and hurt. They say goodbye to the frog and go back to play with their friends and their toys. They still like to look for animals in the park. But they also listen to their mom and stay away from the dangerous ones.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a brown dog named Max. Max loved to play with his friends in the park. One sunny day, Max and his friends found a big, round melon near a tree.
Max's friend, a little cat named Lily, said, "Let's share the melon!" Max agreed and they all kneeled down to look at the melon. They decided to cut the melon into pieces so everyone could have some.
As they shared the melon, a small bird named Timmy flew down and asked, "Can I have some too?" Max and Lily said, "Of course!" and gave Timmy a piece of the melon. They all enjoyed the sweet melon together.
After they finished eating, Max, Lily, and Timmy felt happy and full. They learned that sharing with friends is a good thing to do. From that day on, they always shared their treats with each other and made even more friends in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a big hoop. It was red and shiny. A little boy named Tim loved to play with it. He would roll the hoop, jump through it, and even dance around it. Tim and the hoop were best friends.
One day, a slow turtle named Tom came to the park. He saw Tim and the hoop playing together. Tom thought the hoop looked fun, so he said, "Hi, can I play with the hoop too?" Tim smiled and said, "Yes, you can play with us!"
Tim and Tom played with the hoop all day. They laughed and had so much fun. But then, something unexpected happened. The hoop started to talk! It said, "I love playing with you both!" Tim and Tom were so surprised. They never knew the hoop could talk. From that day on, the three friends played together every day, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a very amazing boy named Tim. Tim had a toy sack. He loved to play with the toys in his sack every day. He shared his toys with his friends, and they all had fun together.
One day, Tim saw a big operation happening in the town. The workers were fixing a broken house. Tim wanted to help, so he took his sack and went to the operation. He gave his toys to the workers and said, "Please use my toys to fix the house!"
The workers laughed and said, "Thank you, Tim, but we can't use toys to fix the house." Tim felt sad, but then something unexpected happened. The workers showed Tim their tools, and they looked like big toys! Tim learned that helping others is important, even if it's not with toys.
From that day on, Tim helped the workers with their operation. He learned how to use the big tools and became a great helper. The moral of the story is that even if you don't have the right things to help, you can still learn and be a good friend.

Once upon a time, there was a barber. He had a big, red chair. He liked to cut hair. One day, a weird man came to the barber. The weird man had long hair. The barber wanted to cut it.
The barber took out his scissors. He started to cut the weird man's hair. But, the weird man did not want short hair. He wanted the barber to wrap his hair.
The barber was confused, but he wanted to help. So, he wrapped the weird man's hair around his head. The weird man was happy. He thanked the barber and left. The barber smiled and went back to cutting hair.

One day, a weak little ant named Andy was walking in the hot sun. He was very thirsty and wanted to find water. As he walked, he saw a big network of lines on the ground. The lines were all dry, but Andy thought they might lead to water.
Andy followed the dry network until he met a big, strong ant named Benny. Benny was also looking for water. "Hello," said Andy. "Do you know where to find water?"
Benny smiled and said, "Yes, I do. Follow me." They walked together until they found a small puddle. The water was not much, but it was enough for the two ants. They drank the water and felt better.
After they drank, Andy and Benny decided to help each other. They would work together to find more water and make the network wet again. They knew that if they worked together, they could make their home a better place for all the ants.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy. He was only 3 years old. His name was Johnny.
One day he wanted to escape. He crawled to the refrigerator and opened it. Inside he saw many strange things. He saw a regular apple, a banana and a pear. 
Johnny picked up the regular apple. He was about to take a bite when he heard a strange voice. 
"Hey! Stop!" shouted the voice. It was coming from the refrigerator. Johnny froze. 
The voice said again: "Johnny, who said you could take my apple? Put it back!" 
Johnny gulped. He put the apple back into the refrigerator and escaped quickly. 
Johnny didn't even bother to close the refrigerator. He ran to his mom and never went back to the refrigerator ever again!

Once upon a time, there was a polite cat named Kitty. Kitty had a toy she loved. It was her own toy. The toy was a little match. Kitty played with her match all day.
One day, a dog named Max came to visit. Max saw the match and wanted to play too. Max asked Kitty, "Can I play with your match?" Kitty thought about it and said, "Yes, you can play with my match."
Max and Kitty played with the match together. They had so much fun. They took turns and shared the match. Max was happy and said, "Thank you for sharing your match, Kitty." Kitty smiled and said, "You're welcome, Max." They played until it was time for Max to go home. Kitty and Max were good friends and they always remembered to be polite and share.

Ben and Lily went to the zoo with their mom. They saw many animals, like lions, giraffes, and elephants. But their favorite was the monkey house. They liked to watch the monkeys swing, jump, and play.
One monkey was very young and curious. He saw Ben and Lily and wanted to say hello. He climbed down from his tree and ran to the fence. He reached out his hand and grabbed Ben's hat. He put it on his head and made a funny face.
"Hey, that's my hat!" Ben shouted. He tried to get his hat back, but the monkey was too fast. He ran away with the hat and showed it to his friends. The other monkeys laughed and clapped.
Lily giggled and said, "Look, Ben, the monkey likes your hat. He thinks he is a boy like you."
Ben felt embarrassed. He did not like that the monkey took his hat and made fun of him. He wished he had a different hat, or no hat at all.
Their mom saw what happened and said, "Don't worry, Ben, the monkey is just playing. He doesn't mean to hurt your feelings. Maybe he will give your hat back if you ask him nicely."
Ben nodded and said, "Okay, mom, I will try." He walked to the fence and said, "Excuse me, monkey, can I have my hat back, please? It is mine and I need it."
The young monkey heard Ben and looked at him. He saw that Ben was sad and not angry. He felt sorry for him. He took off the hat and threw it back to Ben. He waved his hand and made a friendly sound.
Ben caught his hat and said, "Thank you, monkey, you are very kind. I'm sorry I yelled at you. You are very cute and smart."
The young monkey smiled and nodded. He was happy that Ben was not mad at him. He went back to his tree and joined his friends.
Ben and Lily hugged their mom and said, "We love the zoo and we love the monkeys. They are very funny and nice."
Their mom smiled and said, "I'm glad you had fun, my sweeties. Let's go see some more animals, shall we?"

One day, a small boy named Tim was playing outside. He liked to touch the soft grass and the warm dirt. The sun was shining bright, and Tim was very happy.
Suddenly, Tim felt the ground shake. It was an earthquake! Tim was scared, but he knew he had to be brave. He ran to his mom and said, "Mom, the ground is shaking! What do we do?"
Mom hugged Tim and said, "Don't worry, Tim. It's just a small earthquake. We will be safe inside our house." They went inside and waited for the shaking to stop. Soon, the earthquake was over, and Tim felt better.
Tim and his mom went back outside to play. They touched the grass and the dirt again, and everything felt normal. Tim was happy that the small earthquake was over, and he could play outside with his mom again.

One day, a big circle and a small circle were friends. They liked to play together. The big circle said, "Let's see who can roll down the hill the fastest!" The small circle answered, "Yes, that sounds fun!"
They went to the top of the hill. The big circle thought it would be easy for him to win. He was so much bigger than the small circle. They started to roll down the hill. The small circle went very fast. The big circle was surprised!
The small circle got to the bottom first. He was very happy. The big circle said, "You did it! You succeed!" The small circle smiled and said, "Thank you! It was fun!" The big circle learned that being big does not always mean you will win. They played together all day and had a great time.

Lila and Tom were playing in the park. They liked to swing and slide and run. They saw a big black bird on a tree. It was a raven. The raven had shiny eyes and a sharp beak.
The raven saw Lila and Tom. It wanted to scare them. It flew down and made a loud noise. "Caw, caw, caw!" It flapped its wings and pecked at their hair. Lila and Tom were afraid. They cried and ran away. The raven was bad.
Lila and Tom found their mom. They told her about the raven. Mom hugged them and said, "Don't worry, my loves. The raven can't hurt you. It is just a bird. It is not nice, but it is not strong. You are brave and smart. You can end the raven's game. Next time, you can say, 'Go away, raven. We don't like you. You are not our friend.' Then, you can ignore it and keep playing. The raven will get bored and fly away."
Lila and Tom felt better. They thanked their mom and went back to the park. They saw the raven again. It tried to scare them. But Lila and Tom did what their mom said. They said, "Go away, raven. We don't like you. You are not our friend." They ignored it and kept playing. The raven got bored and flew away. Lila and Tom smiled and high-fived. They ended the raven's game. They had fun. The end.

One day, a little boy named Tim was playing with his toy car. His mom called him to come and eat. She had made a yummy sandwich with jam for him. Tim loved jam, so he ran to the table.
Tim took a big bite of his sandwich. But, something was wrong. The jam tasted disgusting! Tim did not want to eat it. He wanted to part the sandwich and take out the jam. His mom saw him and asked, "What's wrong, Tim?"
Tim said, "The jam is disgusting. I don't want to eat it." His mom looked at the sandwich and saw that the jam had gone bad. She felt sorry and made Tim a new sandwich with fresh jam. Tim ate the new sandwich and it was very yummy.
The moral of the story is to always check your food before you eat it. Sometimes, things can go bad and taste disgusting. It's better to be safe and make sure your food is good before you eat it.

Tim and Lily were playing in their room. They liked to pretend they were soldiers and march around with their toy guns. They wore hats and boots and made loud noises.
"Left, right, left, right!" Tim shouted, leading the way.
"Left, right, left, right!" Lily repeated, following him.
They marched across the room and stopped at the window. They saw a big curtain that was colorful and shiny. It had red, blue, yellow and green stripes.
"Wow, look at that curtain!" Lily said. "It's so pretty! Can we touch it?"
"No, we can't!" Tim said. "Mom said it's not ours. It belongs to Mrs. Jones, the lady who lives next door. She lent it to us for the party. We have to be careful with it."
"But I want to touch it!" Lily said. "It looks so soft and fun! Please, Tim, let me touch it!"
Lily reached out her hand and pulled the curtain. Tim tried to stop her, but it was too late. The curtain came down with a loud rip. They heard a scream from the kitchen.
"Tim! Lily! What have you done?" Mom yelled, running into the room. She saw the curtain on the floor, torn and dirty. She saw Tim and Lily, scared and sorry.
"Mom, we're sorry!" Tim said. "We were just playing and Lily wanted to touch the curtain and..."
"It's not my fault!" Lily said. "Tim was mean and he didn't let me touch the curtain and..."
Mom was very angry and sad. She picked up the curtain and looked at it. She shook her head and sighed.
"This curtain was very special to Mrs. Jones. She made it herself. She was very kind to let us use it for the party. How can we fix it? How can we say sorry to her?"
Tim and Lily felt very bad. They knew they had done something wrong. They wanted to make Mom happy and Mrs. Jones happy. They looked at each other and nodded.
"Mom, we're sorry!" they said together. "We'll help you fix the curtain. We'll go to Mrs. Jones and say sorry. We'll be good soldiers and march to her house. Please, Mom, don't be mad at us!"
