The big fish went first and the scale said he was very big. Then the small fish tried, but he was too small for the scale. He was sad and asked the big fish, "Can you help me be big like you?" The big fish thought for a moment and said, "Yes, I will help you."
The big fish told the small fish to eat lots of food, so he would grow big. The small fish ate and ate, but he did not grow. One day, while they were swimming, they found a magic fish. The magic fish said, "I can make one of you bigger, but the other must yield their size." The big fish loved the small fish and wanted him to be happy. So, the big fish said, "I will yield my size for you."
The magic fish made the small fish big and the big fish small. They were both happy with their new sizes and swam away together. From that day on, they learned that helping each other can make them both happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little brown dog named Max. Max loved to play in the park. One sunny day, Max went to the park with his best friend, a little girl named Lily.
While they were playing, Max saw a bee. The bee was small and busy. Max wanted to introduce the bee to Lily. He barked at the bee and the bee flew to Lily.
"Hi, bee!" said Lily. "I'm Lily and this is Max." The bee buzzed around them, happy to meet new friends. They played together all day, and Max, Lily, and the bee became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who loved dinosaurs. Every day she played with her dinosaurs, each one carefully sorted by type. One day, she went to pick up her most favourite one, a triceratops. When she put it in her hands, she noticed it felt quite alert. 
She shook the toy a little. "What's wrong, triceratops?" she asked. The triceratops let out a low rumble.
 
Suddenly, the girl heard a loud noise coming from the bedroom. She quickly ran and peeked inside. There, she saw a real live dinosaur! It was very alert, looking all around for anything that could hurt it. 
The girl was so excited. She knew this was a once in a lifetime experience! She gathered her courage and stepped inside. All of a sudden, the dinosaur lunged towards her. The girl quickly stepped back, but the dinosaur stopped, peering down at her in a very friendly way.
The girl smiled. As it turns out, this dinosaur just wanted someone to play with. She and the dinosaur played together, running around and sorting all her toy dinosaurs. When it was time to go, the girl said goodbye to her new friend, who waved her goodbye until she left.

Once upon a time, in a fair land, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play near the water. Every day, she would go to the river and watch the fish swim. Lily had a big job to do. She had to protect the water from the bad things.
One day, a mean man came. He wanted to take the water away. Lily tried to protect the water, but the man was too strong. She asked her friends, the fish, for help. The fish tried to help, but they were too small.
In the end, the mean man took the water away. The fish had no place to swim, and Lily was very sad. She could not protect the water, and the fair land was not so fair anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a furry cat named Kitty. Kitty liked to play outside in the sun. One day, Kitty saw a big red ball and started to wonder where it came from.
Kitty played with the ball all day. She did not see the open door to her house. The door was her escape when she was scared. Soon, the sun went down and it got dark. Kitty was scared and wanted to go home.
Kitty looked for her escape but could not find it. She was very sad. Then, she saw a light in the window. It was her home! Kitty ran to the light and found her escape. She learned to always remember where her escape was. The moral of the story is to always remember your way home.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a walk with his mom. They went down a quiet path. Tim saw a big billboard. He wanted to read it. But he was too small to see the words on the billboard. Tim was sad.
A nice man saw Tim and his mom. He asked, "Why is Tim sad?" Tim's mom told the man about the billboard. The man had an idea. He picked up Tim and put him on his shoulders. Now, Tim could read the billboard.
The billboard said, "Play at the new park today!" Tim was so happy. But when they got to the park, it was closed. Tim was sad again. Then, the nice man said, "I know a secret park!" They all went to the secret park and played all day. Tim was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby cat. The cat liked to play with a spoon. One day, a dog saw the cat playing with the spoon. The dog wanted the spoon too. So, the dog tried to steal the spoon from the cat.
"Give me the spoon," said the dog. The cat did not want to give the spoon. They both pulled the spoon. They were not happy.
Then, something funny happened. A bird flew down and took the spoon. The cat and the dog looked at the bird. They both laughed. The bird dropped the spoon. The cat and the dog played with the spoon together. They were happy and became friends.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived an elderly lady named Grandma Sue. She had a kind spirit and loved to encourage everyone around her. Grandma Sue had a little cat named Whiskers. Whiskers was her best friend.
One sunny day, Grandma Sue and Whiskers went for a walk in the park. They saw a sad little boy sitting on a bench. Grandma Sue asked, "Why are you sad, little boy?" The boy said, "I lost my toy and can't find it."
Grandma Sue wanted to help. She said, "Let's look for your toy together." As they searched, they found a big, colorful box. They opened the box and found not only the boy's toy but many more new toys! The boy was so happy and thanked Grandma Sue and Whiskers.
From that day on, Grandma Sue, Whiskers, and the boy became good friends. They played together and encouraged each other to be kind and share their toys with other kids in the park. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Billy. He was very excited because his mom said she was going to deliver him a very special present. Billy ran around the house, almost too excited to sit still.
The next day, mom came and said, "Billy! I have something for you!"
"What is it?" Billy asked.
"Well, let's find out!" mom said, opening the box. Inside was a delicate and fragile banana.
Billy was puzzled. "A banana?"
"Yes," mom said. "Remember, I said I was going to deliver you something special and this is it."
When Billy looked closer, he saw something shiny around the banana. It was a necklace. Billy was delighted!
He wore his necklace proudly, never forgetting that special moment when his mom delivered him a special present.

One day, a red balloon was floating in the sky. It felt gloomy because it was all alone. It wanted to find a friend to play with. As it floated, it saw a little boy on the ground. The boy saw the balloon and wanted to catch it.
The little boy jumped and tried to grab the balloon, but it was too high. He did not stop trying. He climbed on a chair, but still could not reach the balloon. The balloon felt bad for the boy and floated lower to the ground.
As the balloon got closer, a mean cat saw it. The cat jumped and popped the balloon with its sharp claw. The balloon was gone, and the boy was sad. The cat walked away, not caring about the boy's feelings. The boy cried because he lost his new friend, the red balloon.

Tom and Lily were playing in the park. They liked to run and jump and slide. They had a big sack with them. They put some leaves and sticks and stones in the sack. They wanted to make a fort.
"Look, Lily, I found a big stick. We can use it as a flag," Tom said. He put the stick in the sack.
"Good, Tom. I found some yellow flowers. We can use them as decorations," Lily said. She put the flowers in the sack.
They carried the sack to a big tree. They started to build their fort. They used the leaves and sticks and stones to make walls. They used the flowers to make it pretty. They used the stick as a flag.
They were very happy with their fort. They went inside and pretended to be kings and queens. They had a lot of fun.
But then they heard a loud noise. They looked outside and saw dark clouds. They saw lightning in the sky. They heard thunder. They were scared.
"Tom, Lily, come here. It's going to rain. We have to go home," their mom called from the bench.
They ran out of their fort. They grabbed their sack. They followed their mom to the car.
They were sad to leave their fort. They hoped it would not be ruined by the rain.
"Maybe we can come back tomorrow and play again," Tom said.
"Maybe. But we still have our sack. We can make another fort at home," Lily said.
They smiled at each other. They hugged their sack. They were not scared anymore.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a walk. It was a gloomy day. The sky was gray and the trees looked sad. Tim saw a big puddle on the ground. He thought it was water, but it was not water. It was oil!
Tim called his friend, Sue. "Sue, come see this!" he said. Sue came running to see what Tim found. She looked at the oil and said, "It's so shiny and slippery!" They both wanted to examine the oil closer.
As they looked at the oil, they saw a little frog jump out of the puddle. The frog was covered in oil, too. Tim and Sue decided to help the frog. They gently wiped the oil off the frog and set it free. The frog hopped away happily, and Tim and Sue felt good for helping their new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a small, enthusiastic dog named Buddy. Buddy loved to play with his red ball. He would run and jump to catch the ball in his mouth.
One day, Buddy saw a big box in the yard. He wanted to see what was inside. Buddy tried to lift the box with his mouth, but it was too heavy. He tried again and again, but he could not lift it.
Buddy's friend, a little bird named Chirpy, saw him trying to lift the box. Chirpy flew down and helped Buddy lift the box. Together, they opened the box and found a new, blue ball. Buddy was so happy, and he played with both balls all day long.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. He saw a big red box with a lock on it. Tim wanted to know what was inside the box, so he looked for a key to open the lock.
Tim found a small shiny key and tried to open the lock. "This is a stupid lock!" he said, as he tried to turn the key. Suddenly, the lock clicked and the box opened. A lot of steam came out of the box. Tim was very surprised.
Inside the box, Tim found a little talking frog. The frog said, "Thank you for opening the box! I was stuck inside with all that steam. Now I can go home." Tim and the frog became good friends and played together every day.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to go outside and play. It was cold, so his mom said, "Tim, you need to wear a warm wrap before you go out." Tim did not like the warm wrap. He wanted to play without it.
Tim went outside and saw his friend, Sue. She was wearing a warm wrap too. They started to play, but Tim started to feel cold. He told Sue, "I don't want to wear the wrap, but I am cold." Sue said, "You should listen to your mom and wear the wrap to stay warm."
Tim and Sue went back inside. Tim's mom saw him shivering and said, "You need to stop going out without a warm wrap. You will get sick." Tim put on his warm wrap and went back outside with Sue. They played all day and Tim stayed warm and happy.

Once upon a time, there was a large hill. On the hill, there lived a big rabbit named Bob and a small bird named Sue. They were good friends and played together every day.
One day, while they were playing near the hill, Bob said, "Sue, I want to go to the top of the hill. Will you come with me?" Sue nodded and said, "Yes, let's go together!" So they started to climb the hill. It was a long walk, but they were happy.
When they reached the top, they looked around and saw their homes far away. They played for a while and then it was time to go back. But, they forget the way home. Bob and Sue were scared. They held hands and walked down the hill. After some time, they found their way back home. They were happy to be safe and learned not to forget their way.

Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Tom. Tom loved to visit his grandma's house. He would play with her cat and eat her yummy cookies.
One day, Tom went to his grandma's house for a visit. They had a fun day playing games and eating cookies. But then, Tom spilled his juice all over the floor. Tom felt embarrassed because he made a big mess.
His grandma saw that Tom was embarrassed and gave him a big hug. She said, "Don't worry, Tom. We all make mistakes sometimes." Tom smiled and helped his grandma clean up the mess. They continued to have a great visit, and Tom learned that it's okay to make mistakes.

Once upon a time, there was a powerful dog named Max. Max was big and strong. He loved to run and play with his friends. One day, Max found a ball in the park. It was a very nice ball, but it had a small leak. Max wanted to play with the ball, but he knew it would not last long.
Max needed to accept that the ball was not perfect. He decided to ask his friend, the wise cat, for help. The cat told Max that even though the ball had a leak, they could still have fun with it. Max listened to the cat and decided to play with the ball, even if it was not perfect.
Max and his friends played with the ball all day long. They had a great time, and the ball made them very happy. In the end, Max learned that it is okay to accept things that are not perfect. He also learned that he was a powerful dog who could still have fun, even with a leaky ball.

One day, a big cat named Tom went for a walk. He saw a small dog named Tim. Tim was sad because he had a toy stuck in his mouth. Tom wanted to help, so he said, "I will help you get the toy out of your mouth."
Tom tried to step on the toy to pull it out. But, he stepped too hard and hurt Tim's mouth. Tim cried, "Ow! That hurt my mouth!" Tom felt very embarrassed because he did not want to hurt Tim.
In the end, Tom could not help Tim. Tim's mouth hurt more, and he was still sad. Tom walked away, feeling bad about what he did. He knew he should have been more careful.

Once upon a time, a tall bunny named Bobo loved to bounce. One day, Bobo went to visit his friend, a little bird named Tweet. Tweet lived in a small nest up in a tree.
Bobo and Tweet played a fun game. They bounced a big red ball. The ball went high up in the sky. Suddenly, the ball did not come back down. Bobo and Tweet were sad.
Then, a big wind came and blew the ball back to them. The ball was not a ball anymore. It was a big red balloon. Bobo and Tweet were happy. They played with the big red balloon all day long.

Tom and Mia like to play in the sand near the water. They make castles and dig holes and look for shells. One day, they find a small shrimp in a puddle. The shrimp is pink and has many legs and eyes.
"Look, Mia, a shrimp!" Tom says. He picks up the shrimp and shows it to Mia.
"Wow, Tom, it is so tiny and cute!" Mia says. She touches the shrimp gently with her finger. The shrimp wiggles and tries to get away.
"Let's keep it as a pet!" Tom says. He puts the shrimp in a bucket with some water and sand.
"No, Tom, we can't do that!" Mia says. "The shrimp is not happy in the bucket. It needs to go back to the water. It is helpless here."
"But I like the shrimp!" Tom says. He holds the bucket tightly.
"Please, Tom, be nice to the shrimp. It is not a toy. It is a living thing. It has feelings too. How would you feel if someone took you away from your home and put you in a bucket?" Mia says.
Tom thinks for a moment. He remembers how he felt when he got lost in the park and could not find his mom. He felt scared and sad and lonely. He did not like that feeling.
"Okay, Mia, you are right. I don't want to make the shrimp feel bad. Let's take it back to the water." Tom says.
He and Mia walk to the puddle and gently put the shrimp back. The shrimp swims away quickly and disappears in the water.
"Bye, shrimp! Thank you for letting us see you!" Tom and Mia say.
They smile at each other and go back to playing in the sand. They are happy and proud that they did the right thing.

Once upon a time, there was a duck. This duck was very selfish. He did not like to share his toys with other ducks.
One day, the duck found a big cookie. He wanted to eat it all by himself. But then, he saw his friend, a little duck, who was hungry too. The little duck asked if they could share the cookie.
The selfish duck thought about it for a moment. He decided to split the cookie in half. He gave one half to the little duck and kept the other half for himself. The little duck was so happy and thanked the selfish duck.
From that day on, the selfish duck learned how to share. He became a good friend to all the ducks. They played together and shared their toys and food. And the selfish duck was not selfish anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy bird with a big wing. The bird lived in a big tree. The bird liked to bring food to its friends.
One day, the bird saw a cat near the tree. The cat wanted to eat the bird. The bird was scared. The bird had an idea. The bird tried to bring the cat some food too.
The cat liked the food the bird brought. The cat did not want to eat the bird anymore. They became friends. The cat helped the bird bring food to its friends. The bird was not sleepy anymore. They were all happy.

Tom and Sam are friends. They like to play with blocks. They build towers and bridges and houses. They have fun.
One day, Tom has a new block. It is big and red and shiny. Sam likes the new block. He wants to play with it. He asks Tom, "Can I have the new block, please?"
Tom says, "No, this is my block. I got it from my mom. It is mine."
Sam is sad. He says, "But we can share. Sharing is good. We can take turns. Please, Tom, let me have the new block for a little while."
Tom thinks. He likes Sam. He does not want to make him sad. He says, "Okay, Sam, you can have the new block for a little while. But only for an inch of time. Then you have to give it back to me. Do you understand?"
Sam nods. He is happy. He says, "Thank you, Tom. You are a great friend. I understand. I will give it back to you after an inch of time."
Tom and Sam smile. They play with the new block and the other blocks. They build more towers and bridges and houses. They have more fun.

Tom and Lily were playing in the garden. They liked to run and jump and laugh. They saw a hose on the grass. Tom had an idea.
"Let's play with the hose!" he said. "We can make water come out and spray each other!"
Lily nodded. She thought it would be fun. They ran to the hose and picked it up. Tom turned the tap and water came out. He pointed the hose at Lily and made her wet. Lily squealed and ran away. She grabbed the hose from Tom and sprayed him back. They giggled and chased each other with the hose.
But then they heard a loud roar. They turned and saw a big dog. It had sharp teeth and angry eyes. It was barking and running towards them. It wanted the hose. Tom and Lily were scared. They dropped the hose and ran to the house. The dog picked up the hose and shook it. Water went everywhere.
"Help! Help!" Tom and Lily shouted. "There is a scary dog in the garden!"
Mum heard them and came out. She saw the dog and the hose. She was not happy.
"Who left the hose on?" she asked. "You are wasting water and making a mess!"
Tom and Lily looked at each other. They did not know what to say. They sighed.
"Sorry, Mum," they said. "We were playing with the hose and then the dog came and took it."
Mum looked at the dog. It was still playing with the hose. It looked happy.
"That is not our dog," she said. "It must have escaped from somewhere. Let's go inside and call the owner. Maybe they are looking for it."
Tom and Lily nodded. They followed Mum inside. They hoped the owner would come soon and take the dog away. They did not want to play with the hose anymore. It was too scary.

Once upon a time, there was a rare dragon who loved to soar high in the sky. He was so happy! His wings were strong and colorful, and sparkled in the sun. 
One day, as the dragon was flying, he slipped. He was so surprised as he tumbled downwards towards the ground. He quickly righted himself and pulled his wings back up. 
"Woah! What was that?" said the dragon. 
Just then, a tiny fairy flew up. She was so small the dragon could barely see her. "Oh dear! You slipped! That was a surprise," said the fairy. 
The dragon smiled and thanked the fairy for her help. He promised to be more careful in the future, and soon the dragon was soaring high in the sky again. 
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a healthy dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his metal ball. He would run and jump all day long.
One day, Spot was playing outside when he saw a big door. The door was close to his home. Spot wanted to see what was behind the door. He pushed the door with his nose, but it did not open. He tried to jump and hit the door, but it still did not open.
Spot saw a little bird on a tree. He asked the bird, "Can you help me open the door?" The bird said, "Yes, I can help you." The bird flew down and pushed the door with its beak. The door opened, and Spot was very happy. He thanked the bird and went inside to explore.

Once upon a time, there lived a 3 year old called Jay. He was playing in his garden when he heard a scary noise coming from the forest. Jay was scared so he stopped what he was doing and he began to cry.
His mom heard him and rushed outside to comfort him. "What's wrong Jay?", she asked. Through his tears he replied, "I heard something scary in the forest and I'm scared".
His mom smiled and said, "It's okay, don't be scared. That'll be the birds tearing up their meal in the forest".
Jay wasn't sure whether he liked the sound or not, but it didn't sound so scary anymore.
"Come on, let's go inside and have a nice meal together", said his mom.
Jay nodded, feeling much better now. With his mom's help, he was no longer scared of the scary noise he had heard in the forest.

One day, a boy named Tim saw a big, fake tornado. It was not real, but it looked real. Tim was not scared, but he wanted to play with it. He urged his friends to come and see the tornado too. They all ran to the fake tornado, laughing and having fun.
As they played, something strange happened. The fake tornado started to move and make noise. Tim and his friends stopped and looked at it. They were confused. How could a fake tornado move and make noise?
Then, a man came out from behind the fake tornado. He was a magician! He told the kids that he made the tornado for a magic show. The kids were excited and clapped their hands. The magician let them watch the show, and they had a great time. Tim and his friends learned that sometimes, unexpected things can lead to fun surprises.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy was very smart. She loved to learn new things.
One day, Lucy found a tiny bug. The bug was in a small hole, like a cell. Lucy sighed because the bug looked sad. She wanted to help the bug be happy.
Lucy thought of a plan. She used her intelligent mind to make the bug a new home. The bug was so happy in its new home. Lucy and the bug became best friends, and they played together every day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a theater. In the theater, many people would come together to watch shows. The theater had a big stage and a pale blue curtain.
One day, a group of animals decided to unite and put on a show for the town. There was a dog, a cat, a bird, and a rabbit. They practiced and practiced, and soon they were ready for the big show.
On the day of the show, the animals were very excited. But then, something unexpected happened. The dog found out that he could sing! The cat could dance, the bird could tell funny jokes, and the rabbit could do magic tricks. The town loved the show, and the animals became famous. They were happy that they had united to make the best show ever.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play outside when it is cold and there is snow. They make snowmen and snowballs and have fun.
One day, they see a big hill with lots of snow. They want to slide down the hill on their sleds. Anna says, "Let's do it! It looks attractive and exciting!" Ben says, "I don't know. It looks dangerous and steep. Maybe we should not do it."
Anna does not listen to Ben. She thinks he is scared and silly. She takes her sled and runs to the hill. She slides down very fast and screams with joy. Ben watches her and feels worried.
But then, something bad happens. Anna does not see a big rock hidden by the snow. She hits the rock with her sled and falls off. She hurts her leg and cries. Ben runs to help her. He calls for help and stays with her until help comes.
Anna is sorry for not listening to Ben. She says, "You were right, Ben. It was not attractive and exciting. It was dangerous and steep. I should have listened to you. I was foolish and reckless." Ben says, "It's okay, Anna. I'm glad you are not hurt more. I'm still your friend. Next time, let's be careful and safe when we play with snow." Anna says, "Yes, Ben. Thank you for being my friend. Next time, I will listen to you." They hug and smile. They learn a lesson.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a boy named Tim. He loved to play with his toy cars and trucks. One day, Tim saw an impressive big truck outside his house. It was so big and shiny that he wanted to have a toy just like it.
Tim asked his mom, "Can I have a toy like that big truck?" His mom said, "Yes, but first you have to do something for me. You need to pay me with a bar of soap." Tim was confused but he agreed. He looked everywhere in the house to find a bar of soap.
Finally, he found the soap and gave it to his mom. She smiled and said, "Now, let's go to the store and find that toy truck for you." When they got to the store, Tim saw the most unexpected thing. The big, impressive truck from outside his house was now a toy! It was right there on the shelf, waiting for him.
Tim was so happy that he hugged his mom and said, "Thank you, Mom! I love my new toy truck!" From that day on, Tim played with his impressive toy truck every day and always remembered the magic of the soap that helped him get it.

Once upon a time, there was a big red barn. Inside the barn, there were many animals. There were cows, pigs, and chickens. They all lived happily together.
One day, it was time to wash the barn. All the animals were eager to help. The cows used their tails to sweep the floor. The pigs used their noses to push the dirt away. The chickens flapped their wings to make the dust fly out the door.
When they finished washing the barn, the animals were very happy. They had worked together and made their home clean and bright. They all went back inside the barn and enjoyed the rest of the day.

Anna and Ben wanted to decorate the station for their mom's birthday. They had balloons, stickers, and crayons. They ran to the station with their things.
"Look, Ben, this is a good spot to put the balloons!" Anna said. She tied the balloons to a pole.
"OK, Anna, I will put stickers on the wall!" Ben said. He peeled off the stickers and stuck them on the wall.
They were having fun, but they did not see the clock. It was almost time for their mom to come back from work.
"Oh no, Ben, we have to hurry!" Anna said. She looked at the clock and felt worried. "We have to finish decorating and hide!"
"OK, Anna, let's go!" Ben said. He grabbed the crayons and drew a big heart on a paper. He wrote "Happy Birthday Mom" with the crayons.
They finished decorating and hid behind a bench. They waited for their mom to come.
Soon, they heard the train. Their mom got off the train and saw the station. She saw the balloons, the stickers, and the paper. She smiled and looked around.
"Surprise!" Anna and Ben shouted. They jumped out from behind the bench and hugged their mom.
"Wow, thank you, Anna and Ben!" their mom said. She kissed them and thanked them for the decoration. "You made my birthday very special!"
Anna and Ben were happy. They had decorated the station for their mom's birthday. They had made their mom smile. They had done a good job.

Once upon a time, there was a small white bird.
The bird loved to whistle and sing beautiful songs.
One day, the bird saw an old wreck in the distant sky.
The bird was curious, so it flew closer, whistling happily.
Suddenly, the bird saw a big net near the wreck.
The bird realised that the net was dangerous, so it quickly flew away, whistling a warning song.
The bird learnt an important lesson: to be careful and listen to warnings as soon as they are heard.
The bird was proud of its discovery and continued to whistle a happy tune as it flew away.

Once, there was a big jungle. In the jungle, there were many animals. There was a kind monkey named Momo. One day, Momo saw a small bird with a hurt wing. The bird was sad because it could not fly.
Momo said, "Do not go near the harsh rocks. They are not safe for you." He wanted to warn the bird. The bird listened to Momo and stayed away from the rocks. Momo helped the bird by finding food and water.
After some time, the bird's wing got better. It was happy and could fly again. The bird thanked Momo for his help. They became good friends and played together in the jungle.

One day, a gentle cat named Tom and a little girl named Sue wanted to bake a cake. They went to the kitchen and put all the things they needed on the table. They mixed the cake stuff in a big bowl.
While they were baking, the lights went out. It was very dark. Sue said, "Don't worry, Tom. I have a flashlight." She turned it on and they could see better. They kept making the cake and put it in the oven.
When the cake was done, they took it out of the oven. But, oh no! The cake was not a cake at all. It was a big, scary monster! The monster jumped out of the oven and ran away. Tom and Sue were sad they didn't get to eat their cake.

One day, a girl named Sue found a good book. She liked to look at the pictures and hear the story. Sue wanted to show the book to her friend, Tim. But when she went to find Tim, he was not there.
Sue looked for Tim and found him with a big dog. The dog was loud and it scared Sue. "Tim, I want to show you my book, but the dog is too loud," Sue said. Tim looked sad, but he had an idea.
Tim said, "Let's go to the tree house and read the book there." Sue liked the idea, so they went to the tree house. The dog could not get up there. When they started to read the book, they found a secret map inside! They were so happy and excited. Now, they could go on a fun adventure together.

Anna liked to play on the computer. She liked to watch videos of animals and draw pictures with colors. But her mom said she could only play for a little time. Then she had to do other things, like read books or play with toys.
One day, it was very cold outside. The wind was blowing and the snow was falling. Anna looked out the window and saw everything was icy and white. She wanted to go out and make a snowman, but her mom said no.
"It's too icy, Anna. You could slip and hurt yourself. And you need to wear warm clothes and boots, but they are in the laundry. Maybe tomorrow we can go out and play in the snow."
Anna was sad. She did not want to wait until tomorrow. She wanted to play in the snow now. She asked her mom if she could play on the computer instead.
"OK, Anna. You can play on the computer for a little time. But then you have to do something else, OK?"
Anna nodded. She ran to the computer and turned it on. She clicked on her favorite video of a polar bear and a penguin. She smiled and watched them play in the icy snow. She wished she could join them. She thought they looked like fun. She said to the computer, "Hello, polar bear. Hello, penguin. Can I play with you?"

Jimmy and Sally were playing in the park. They were playing with the leaves and Jimmy thought it was so much fun. They noticed a big, messy bookcase and Jimmy asked Sally what it was. Sally said it was a special place for books. Jimmy disagreed and thought of something else he wanted to do. But Sally insisted it was for books.
Jimmy was about to give in and follow Sally, but then he saw a pile of leaves next to the bookcase. He thought it looked like so much fun, so he disagreed with Sally and went to play with the leaves.
At first, they had fun gathering the leaves and throwing them up into the air. But the leaves covered the bookcase and the ground around it. It was so messy! It didn't take long for Sally to get mad at Jimmy. She said he was going to have to clean it all up.
Jimmy didn't like being yelled at. He felt sad and angry, and he decided he would never disagree with Sally again. But it was too late. The bookcase was a mess, and Jimmy was guilty. He had to clean it all up, and he was too sad to enjoy the rest of his time at the park.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a small fairy. Her name was Lily. Lily was a good fairy, but she had a difficult job. She had to mix magic dust to make the forest beautiful.
One day, Lily met a big bear. The bear said, "I want to help you mix the magic dust, but it looks difficult." Lily smiled and said, "It is difficult, but we can do it together!" So, the bear and Lily became friends.
They worked together to mix the magic dust. The bear was strong, and Lily was smart. They knew that something special would happen when they finished. And it did! The forest became more beautiful than ever before. All the animals were happy, and Lily and the bear were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a thin bird with big wings. The bird lived in a tree near a big garden. The bird loved to fly high in the sky and play with its friends.
One day, a big wind came and blew the bird's nest away. The bird was very sad and wanted to prevent this from happening again. So, the bird asked its friends for help to make a new nest.
The friends worked together to make a strong nest. But, something unexpected happened. A cat came and tried to take the nest away. The thin bird used its big wings to prevent the cat from taking the nest. The cat ran away, and the bird and its friends were safe. They all lived happily in the big garden.

One day, a family went on a picnic in the countryside. Everywhere the children looked there were rolling hills, lush green trees and wildflowers.
"Mommy, it's so cute!" said the little girl, as she looked out at the landscape.
Her mom smiled and said, "Yes sweetie, it sure is."
They stopped at a field of wildflowers and the children started picking them. They mixed the pretty petals together and made a colourful bouquet.
The girl's brother had an idea and said, "Let's make a game! Let's mix the petals and try to make the prettiest landscape!"
The children had so much fun playing the game. They mixed and matched different flowers in all kinds of patterns.
At the end, the little girl said, "It's so cute!" as she looked at their creation.
"Yes it is!" her mom said and smiled proudly at her children. They had created a beautiful landscape with their own two hands.

Lily and Ben were playing with their toys in the living room. They liked to pretend they were mail carriers and deliver letters and packages to each other. Lily had a big box that she wrapped with paper and a bow. She put it on her bike and rode to Ben's house.
"Knock knock! Mail for you!" she said, ringing the bell.
Ben opened the door and saw the box. He was very excited. He took it from Lily and thanked her.
"What is it? What is it?" he asked, tearing the paper.
He opened the box and saw a furry belt inside. It was brown and soft and had a buckle. He put it around his waist and smiled.
"It's a bear belt! I love it! Thank you, Lily!" he said, hugging her.
Lily was happy that Ben liked her gift. She had made it herself with her mom's help. She had cut some furry fabric and sewed it together. She had found an old buckle in the garage and attached it.
"You're welcome, Ben. I'm glad you like it. It looks good on you," she said.
But then, they heard a growl from behind the couch. It was Ben's dog, Rex. He had seen the furry belt and thought it was a real bear. He was angry and scared. He jumped out and barked at Ben.
"Rex, no! It's not a bear! It's a belt!" Ben said, trying to calm him down.
But Rex did not listen. He ran towards Ben and tried to bite the belt. Ben screamed and ran away. Lily followed him. They ran to the kitchen and closed the door.
"Help! Help! Rex is crazy! He thinks my belt is a bear!" Ben cried.
Lily was scared too. She did not know what to do. She looked around and saw a phone on the counter. She picked it up and dialed a number.
"Hello? Mom? Can you come home? Rex is attacking Ben's belt!" she said.

Once upon a time there was a little mouse called Bradley. He lived in a tiny village near a sunny field. Every day, Bradley wanted to meet the other animals who lived in the field, but he was too scared. One day, he mustered all his courage and hopped through the grass into the field. 
The butterflies were fluttering and the bees were buzzing. At first, everyone was too busy to notice Bradley, until a kind squirrel spotted him. She welcomed Bradley and said she wanted to be his friend. Everyone in the field was so happy to see Bradley and welcomed him in with open arms.
The animals had lots of fun together, like chasing each other and playing tag. Soon, the animals learned that by uniting they could do more. They worked together to build homes and look after the field while they played.
The animals had discovered that by working together they could have even more fun. Bradley had grown more confident too and learned that, no matter how little you are, you can unite with others to reach great things.

Once upon a time, there was a big zoo. All the animals at the zoo were very happy because the zoo had a big glowing ball.
All the animals wanted to get closer to the glowing ball. One day a monkey came to the zoo and said to the other animals, "Let's jump over the fence and play with the glowing ball!"
The animals were very excited, so they all jumped over the fence. As they got close to the glowing ball, they were amazed by it's beauty.
The lion said, "Let's touch the glowing ball! It looks fun!"
The elephants agreed and they all touched the glowing ball together. It was warm and bright and they felt a happy glow inside them.
The animals all had so much fun with the big glowing ball until the sun went down. Then they all went back to their homes in the zoo, looking forward to their next big adventure.

Once upon a time, there was a big round ball. It was very happy and loved to play with all the other toys. One day, the round ball met a new friend. This new friend was a bitter lemon. The lemon was not happy like the round ball. It was always sad and did not like to play.
The round ball wanted to help the lemon become happy. It thought of a fun game they could play together. The game was called "split". In this game, they had to split into two pieces and then try to come back together. It was a fun game, and the round ball was very good at it.
The lemon tried to play the game, but it was not good at splitting. The round ball kept trying to help the lemon, but the lemon was still sad. Finally, the round ball realized that it was better to be happy with what you have and not try to change others. The round ball and the lemon stayed friends, and the round ball learned that everyone is different and that's okay.

Once upon a time, there was a bear who was very furry. One day, he was walking in the woods when he heard someone laughing. He was curious, so he decided to see where the laughter was coming from.
He followed the sound until he came to a small clearing in the woods and there he discovered two little bunnies. They were giggling and having lots of fun, and the bear wanted to join in. He asked them what they were doing and one of the bunnies said, "We are telling jokes!"
The bear was so excited, he asked if he could join in. The bunnies said yes, so the bear started to tell them a joke. The bunnies laughed and laughed, then they told the bear their own jokes. They all had a lot of fun together, telling jokes and laughing.
The furry bear was so happy he'd discovered the bunnies in the woods. He waved goodbye to them and happily walked back home, still laughing.

One day, a girl named Lily wanted to ride her bike. She put on her fancy hat and went outside. Her friend Tom was there too. They both had fun riding their bikes up and down the street.
"Let's have a picnic," said Lily. She ran inside her house and got a big blanket. She spread the blanket on the grass, and they sat down. They had yummy food like apples, cookies, and juice. They laughed and talked while they ate.
As they were eating, a big wind came. It blew Lily's fancy hat away! Oh no! But then, something unexpected happened. The big blanket lifted up like a magic carpet and flew after the hat. Lily and Tom jumped on the blanket and rode it like a magic ride. They caught the hat and laughed. They knew it was a special day they would never forget.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a secret. He found a big, disgusting bug in his room. He thought it was fun to play with the bug. Tim liked to part its legs and watch it wiggle.
One day, Tim's friend, Sam, came to play. Tim wanted to show Sam his secret bug. They went to Tim's room and found the bug. Sam was scared of the disgusting bug. But Tim said, "Don't worry, it's fun to play with."
Tim and Sam played with the bug. They part its legs and watched it wiggle. But then, the disgusting bug got angry. It bit Tim and Sam. They cried and ran to Tim's mom. She was mad and said, "You should not play with bugs!"
Tim and Sam learned a lesson. They should not play with disgusting bugs. They felt sad and hurt. The bug was not a good secret to have. From that day on, they never played with bugs again.

One day, a little girl named Amy found a huge box. She was very excited. She opened the box and saw many cards inside. The cards were pretty and colorful.
Amy had an idea. She wanted to make a big picture on the wall with the cards. She started to add one card, then another, and another. Her mom and dad came to see what she was doing.
They all smiled and clapped. The huge picture made with cards was beautiful. They were all happy and had a fun day together.

Once upon a time, there was a big bathtub. It was a scary bathtub because it had a big, mean face. It lived in a small house with a nice family.
One day, the family wanted to take a bath. They filled the bathtub with warm water. But when they tried to get in, the scary bathtub did not want them to. It made a loud noise and scared the family away.
The family was sad and did not know what to do. Then, they had an idea. They took a big tool and split the scary bathtub into two smaller bathtubs. The two smaller bathtubs were not scary at all. They were happy and friendly. The family could now take baths and everyone was happy. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her delicate toys. Her favorite toy was a small pretty doll. Lily played with her doll every day.
One sunny day, Lily took her doll to the park. She was so happy to play with her doll outside. As they played, a big wind came and took her doll away. Lily was sad and screamed, "Oh no, my doll!"
Just as Lily was about to cry, a small bird flew down with her doll in its beak. The bird had saved her doll from the wind! Lily was so happy, she hugged the bird and thanked it. From that day on, Lily and the bird became best friends and played together all the time.

Once upon a time, in a small pond, there lived a grumpy frog named Fred. Fred liked to sit on a big rock and lean on a net he found. He would watch the other animals play and have fun, but Fred never wanted to join them.
One sunny day, a little fish named Fin swam by and saw Fred leaning on the net. Fin said, "Hi Fred! Come play with us in the water!" But Fred just frowned and said, "No, I like to sit here and be grumpy."
As the days passed, the other animals kept asking Fred to come play, but he always said no. Then one day, while Fred was leaning on the net, it fell into the pond. Fred was very sad and didn't know what to do. The other animals saw Fred's problem and decided to help him.
The animals all worked together to get the net out of the pond. They showed Fred that they could have fun and help each other too. Fred was so happy to have his net back, and he finally decided to join the other animals in their games. From that day on, Fred was not a grumpy frog anymore. He learned that playing with friends was much more fun than being alone.

One day, a boy named Tim had a cake. The cake was big and yummy. He wanted to display the cake for all his friends to see. He put the cake on a table outside his house. Tim was very happy and excited.
A little bird came and saw the cake. The bird wanted a small piece, but the bird was too small to eat the cake. The bird flew away and told his friends about the yummy cake. Many birds came to see the cake, but they could not eat it too.
Then, a big wind came and blew the cake off the table. The cake fell and was broken. Tim was sad, but the birds were happy. They could now eat the broken cake. Tim and the birds became friends and shared the cake. The end.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a scary flood. The water came up very high. People were scared and did not know what to do. Animals like cats, dogs, and birds were scared too.
One day, a little girl named Mia saw something big and strange in the water. She could not believe it. She ran to tell everyone in the village. They all came to see the big thing in the water.
When they got there, they saw it was not a scary thing at all. It was a big, friendly elephant. The elephant had come to help them. The elephant used its long nose to take the water away from the village. Everyone was happy and thanked the elephant. The village was safe again, and they all became friends with the elephant.

Lily was a lawyer who helped people with problems. She liked her job, but she also liked to have fun. Sometimes, she went to the park with her dog, Spot. Spot was a big, fluffy dog who was very adorable. He liked to run, jump and play with Lily and other dogs.
One day, Lily and Spot met a new friend at the park. His name was Ben, and he was a boy who had a red ball. Ben threw the ball for Spot, and Spot ran after it. Lily and Ben talked and laughed. They had a lot in common. They both liked books, music and pizza.
"Can I twist your hair?" Ben asked Lily. He liked her long, brown hair. It looked soft and shiny.
"Sure, you can twist my hair," Lily said. She smiled and let Ben take some of her hair in his hands. He twisted it gently and made a braid. He tied it with a blue ribbon.
"You look pretty," Ben said. He gave Lily the ribbon.
"Thank you, you are very kind," Lily said. She gave Ben a hug.
Spot came back with the ball. He wagged his tail and barked. He wanted to play more.
"Let's play some more," Ben said. He took the ball and threw it again.
"Okay, let's play some more," Lily said. She ran after Spot.
They played until the sun went down. They had a lot of fun. They became good friends. They agreed to meet again at the park. Lily, Ben and Spot were happy.

Tom and Lily are friends. They like to play with crayons and draw pictures. One day, Tom has a new crayon. It is big and shiny and red. He shows it to Lily and says, "Look at my crayon. It is the best crayon ever. You can't have it. It is mine."
Lily feels sad. She likes red crayons too. She says, "Please, Tom, can I try your crayon? Just for a little bit. I want to draw a flower."
Tom says, "No, you can't. You are stupid. You don't know how to draw. You will break my crayon. Go away."
Lily starts to cry. She runs to her mom and tells her what Tom said. Her mom hugs her and says, "Don't listen to Tom. He is not a good friend. He is mean and selfish. You are not stupid. You are smart and kind. You know how to draw very well. I will teach you how to draw even better. Come, let's get some crayons and paper."
Lily smiles and follows her mom. They sit on the floor and draw together. Her mom teaches her how to draw different shapes and colors and patterns. Lily has fun and learns a lot. She draws a beautiful flower with many crayons. She says, "Thank you, mom. You are the best teacher ever. I love you."
Her mom says, "I love you too, Lily. You are a wonderful artist. You can draw anything you want. And you can share your crayons with anyone who wants to play with you. That is what good friends do."

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy he liked a lot. It was a round object that he could play with. He would throw it and catch it all day. Tim needed his toy to be happy.
One day, Tim was playing with his toy. He threw it very far and it went into the woods. Tim felt foolish for throwing it so far. He went into the woods to find his toy. He looked and looked but could not find it.
Just when Tim was about to give up, he saw a big bear. The bear had Tim's toy in its paw. Tim was scared, but the bear gave the toy back to him. The bear was not mean, but it was nice! Tim and the bear became friends and played together. The unexpected thing was that the bear was nice and they had fun.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. At the park, Lily saw a cherry tree. The tree was very big and full of red cherries. Lily wanted to pick some cherries to take home. She asked her mom, "Can I pick some cherries, please?"
Lily's mom said, "Yes, but be careful not to get dirty." Lily started to pick the cherries. She was very happy. But then, she saw a filthy cat stuck in the tree. The cat was scared and could not get down. Lily wanted to help the cat, so she shouted, "Mom, there is a cat in the tree! Can we help it?"
Her mom came to see and said, "Oh no, poor cat! Let's help it." They found a long stick and used it to help the cat get down. The cat was very happy and clean again. Lily and her mom took the cat home with the cherries. They all became good friends and had a fun day.

Once upon a time, in an ancient house, there lived a kind mom and her little daughter. They loved to play together in their big garden. One day, while they were playing, they found a small bird with a hurt wing.
The daughter urged her mom, "Mom, let's help the bird!" The mom agreed, and they took the bird inside their house. They gave it food and made a warm bed for it. The bird was very happy and soon started to feel better.
After a few days, the bird's wing was not hurt anymore. The mom and daughter let the bird go free in the garden. The bird flew up into the sky and sang a beautiful song for them. The mom and daughter were happy they could help the bird.
The moral of the story is that when we help others, it brings happiness to everyone.

Once upon a time, there was a bossy mule. He lived on a farm with other animals. The mule liked to tell everyone what to do. He was not very nice.
One day, the sun was very hot. The mule wanted to find a cool place to rest. He saw a big tree and set his mind on resting under it. The mule walked to the tree and lay down in the shade.
But soon, the other animals came to the tree too. They wanted to rest in the shade as well. The bossy mule did not like to share. But the animals did not listen to him. They all lay down together under the tree. The mule learned that it was better to be nice and share with his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a fast car. The car liked to speed down the road. The car was very proud of its expensive look and fashion. It thought it was the best car in the town.
One day, the car saw a slow bike. The car laughed at the bike and said, "You are so slow! I am much better than you!" The bike felt sad, but it did not say anything. The car sped away, leaving the bike behind.
Later, the car and the bike had a race. The car was winning, but it saw a big hole in the road. The car was going too fast and could not stop. It fell into the hole. The bike was going slow, so it could stop and go around the hole. The bike won the race. The car learned that being fast and expensive is not always the best. It is important to be careful and kind to others.

Sara liked to help her mom in the kitchen. She liked to put things in bowls and stir them with a spoon. One day, her mom gave her a bag of prunes and said, "Sara, can you put these prunes in this empty jar for me? They are very good for you and you can have one later."
Sara nodded and opened the bag. She took out a prune and looked at it. It was wrinkly and dark and smelled funny. She did not think it was very good. She put it in the jar and took out another one. She put it in the jar too. She did this until the bag was empty and the jar was full.
She looked at the jar and felt proud. She had helped her mom and done a good job. She wanted to show her mom and get a hug. She picked up the jar and started to walk to the living room. But she did not see the toy car on the floor. She stepped on it and slipped. The jar flew out of her hands and crashed on the floor. The prunes spilled everywhere and the glass broke into pieces.
Sara started to cry. She was scared and sad. She had made a big mess and wasted the prunes. She thought her mom would be angry and not hug her. She heard her mom running to her and saying, "Sara, Sara, are you okay? What happened?"
Sara looked up and saw her mom's face. She was not angry. She was worried and kind. She hugged Sara and said, "It's okay, Sara, it's okay. You are not hurt and that's what matters. The prunes and the jar are not important. We can clean them up later. I love you, Sara, and I'm proud of you for helping me. You are a good girl."
Sara felt better and hugged her mom back. She said, "I love you too, mom. I'm sorry for the mess. I wanted to show you the jar. I did not like the prunes. They are wrinkly and funny."
Her mom smiled and said, "That's okay, Sara. You don't have to like the prunes. You can have something else later. But you know what? Sometimes wrinkly and funny things are very good for you. Like grandma and grandpa. They are wrinkly and funny, but they love you very much. And so do I."
Sara smiled too and said, "I love grandma and grandpa. They are wrinkly and funny and nice. Can we call them later?"
Her mom said, "Yes, we can. And maybe we can send them some prunes too. They like them very much."
Sara said, "Okay, mom. But can I have a cookie instead?"
Her mom said, "Yes, Sara. You can have a cookie. But first, let's clean up this mess together."
Sara said, "Okay, mom. Let's do that." And they did.

One day, a girl named Lily went to the mill with her mom. They wanted to buy flour to make bread. The mill was a normal place where people went to get flour. Lily liked going there because she could see the big wheel outside the mill go round and round.
At the mill, Lily and her mom met a nice man named Mr. Brown. He said, "Hello! How can I help you today?" Lily's mom said, "We want to buy flour to make bread." Mr. Brown smiled and went to get the flour for them.
But when Mr. Brown came back, he had a big bag of candy instead of flour! Lily and her mom were surprised. Mr. Brown said, "Oops! I made a mistake. Let me get the flour for you." They all laughed, and Mr. Brown gave them the flour. Lily was happy because she got to see the big wheel and they had a fun time at the mill.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. He loved to play all day long. One day, Tom saw a big table in the park. He wanted to play with the table all by himself. He did not want to share the table with other kids. Tom was being selfish.
Tom played and played until he was very tired. He needed to rest. But when he sat down to rest, something unexpected happened. The table started to shake and then it moved away from him! Tom was very sad and scared.
The table moved close to a group of kids who were playing together. They all shared the table and had fun. Tom realized that sharing was better than being selfish. He joined the other kids and played with them. They all had a great time together. Tom learned that sharing with others is more fun and makes everyone happy.

Jimmy was walking in the park one day when it started to rain. He had no umbrella and he began to fret.
Just then, he heard a voice say, "Don't worry, I have an umbrella". It was Grandma. She handed Jimmy her umbrella, and said, "I promise that I'll take care of you".
Jimmy smiled. He thought it was very nice of Grandma to do that.
The next day, Jimmy wanted to do something nice for Grandma. He wanted to give her a present, but he didn't have any money. He finally decided to do something foolish - he promised to wash all the windows in Grandma's house so that she wouldn't have to.
Grandma was very surprised, but she accepted the offer. Jimmy spent the whole day washing each and every window in Grandma's house.
By the end of the day, Grandma was very pleased and told Jimmy, "You have kept your promise - you are a very good grandson!"

One day, a boy named Tim had a cold. He did not feel good. He wanted to play with his friends, but his mom said, "No, Tim. You need to rest."
Tim was sad. He stayed in his room and started to draw. He drew a big sun to feel warm. But he made a mistake. He needed an eraser to fix it. He looked in his bag, but he could not find it. He started to think. "Where did I put my eraser?" he asked himself.
Then, he remembered he gave it to his friend, Lily. Tim felt sad again. But then, he heard a knock on the door. It was Lily! She said, "I heard you were sick, so I brought you soup and your eraser!" Tim was so happy. He thanked Lily and they both smiled.
The moral of the story is: good friends help each other when they need it.

Once upon a time, there was a place. All the toys there were broken. Little Jane, who was three years old, was very sad.
"Why are all of the toys broken," Jane asked her mom.
"Well, they were all stored away for a long time," Mom said. "We need to find another place to store them so they don't get broken again."
Jane was excited. Together, they went on a hunt to find another place to store the toys. After a long search, they found a place to store the toys.
It was a very special place, and the toys stayed safe and sound. Jane played with them all the time, and she felt happy and content. She no longer had to worry about the toys getting broken again.
Foreshadowing and setup with the first introduction to the broken toys and the introduction of the concept of finding a 'place' to store the toys payoffs with the satisfaction of having found a safe place to store the toys.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to eat pasta. Her mommy would make it for her all the time. Mia was a happy girl.
One day, Mia saw a big plate of pasta on the table. She wanted to eat it. But, there was a big angry cat on the chair. The cat did not want Mia to eat the pasta.
Mia had an idea. She started to spin around and around. The angry cat watched her and began to spin too. The cat spun so much that it got dizzy and fell off the chair. Mia sat down and ate her pasta. She was happy again. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy cat named Tom. He liked to drive his little red car. One day, Tom wanted to go on a fun ride. He put on his hat and got ready to start his car. Tom was very excited.
As Tom started to drive, he saw a big blue ball in the road. He did not know where it came from. He stopped his car and got out to look at the ball. Just then, the ball started to move! Tom was surprised.
The big blue ball opened up, and out came a tiny green frog! The frog said, "Hi! I am Fred. I was inside the ball because I am a magic frog!" Tom was very happy to meet Fred. They became best friends and drove around in the little red car together, having lots of fun.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big festival. Everyone was happy and excited. The festival had lots of fun games, yummy food, and pretty colors.
A thin boy named Tim was shy and scared to join the games. His friend, Sam, saw him and said, "Tim, come play with me! I will encourage you." Tim looked at Sam and smiled. They played games together, and Tim was not scared anymore.
At the end of the festival, Tim and Sam won a big prize for playing well together. They learned that when you encourage your friends, you can do great things. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He was scared of the dark. One night, Tim was playing with his toys. He saw a thermometer on the table. He picked it up and looked at it.
Tim's mom came into the room and said, "Tim, it's time to lock the door and go to bed." Tim nodded and locked the door. He felt a little scared, but he remembered the thermometer.
In bed, Tim held the thermometer tight. He thought, "If I get scared, I can look at the thermometer and feel better." The thermometer made him feel safe. Tim fell asleep and had sweet dreams. The next morning, Tim was happy and not scared anymore.

Once there was a shy little girl named Lucy. She had a strange job - she had to order symbols. Everyday, Lucy would go to the store and pick out symbols that were at her feet. She would take them home and stack them up in piles.
Lucy was so shy that none of her friends knew about her job. When she went to the store to order symbols, none of them came along with her. But whenever Lucy got her symbols, she was always so proud.
One day, Lucy was ordering a lot of symbols. She had more than she could carry. She went to the checkout desk and asked the clerk how she could get them all home. The clerk smiled and said "Wait a few minutes, I'll take care of it!".
The clerk took all of the symbols and wrapped them in a box with pink ribbons. He tied the ends of the box with a nice bow and handed it to Lucy. She thanked the clerk and smiled.
That day, Lucy had something extra to show her friends. She was no longer too shy to talk about her job - she proudly showed them the box of symbols!

The hunter was running. He wanted to see what was over the hill. It was icy outside, but he wanted to see what was there. He ran faster and faster and eventually reached the top of the hill. He looked around, and he saw a beautiful icy lake! He thought it would be a great place to catch fish. So, the hunter kept running down the hill towards the lake. He was so excited to find such a wonderful place. He ran to the edge of the lake, picked up his fishing pole, and started fishing.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a bottle. The bottle was very pretty. Tim was very happy. He showed the bottle to his friend, Sue. Sue was envious. She wanted a pretty bottle too.
Tim and Sue went to the park. They saw a big dog. The dog wanted to play with them. But Tim and Sue wanted to play with the bottle. They ignore the dog. The dog was sad.
In the end, Tim and Sue shared the bottle. They played with the dog too. The dog was happy. Tim and Sue were happy too. They all played together in the park.

Ben and Mia were best friends. They liked to play in the park with their toys. One day, Ben brought a new toy to show Mia. It was a green crocodile with big teeth and a long tail.
"Look, Mia, this is my new toy. It is a crocodile. It can snap and bite. Do you like it?" Ben said.
Mia looked at the crocodile and felt scared. She did not like it. She liked her pink bunny more. It was soft and cuddly. She hugged her bunny and said, "No, Ben, I do not like your crocodile. It is scary and mean. I like my bunny. It is nice and sweet."
Ben felt sad. He liked his crocodile. He thought it was cool and fun. He wanted Mia to like it too. He said, "But, Mia, my crocodile is not scary and mean. It is just a toy. It cannot hurt you. It can be nice and sweet too. See, it can smile and wink. Watch this." He made the crocodile smile and wink at Mia.
Mia watched the crocodile and felt curious. She saw that it was not so scary and mean. It was just a toy. It could smile and wink. She said, "Oh, Ben, your crocodile can smile and wink. That is funny and cute. Can I touch it?"
Ben felt glad. He saw that Mia was not scared and mean. She was just curious and cute. He said, "Yes, Mia, you can touch it. It is soft and smooth. Here, you can hold it."
He gave the crocodile to Mia. Mia held the crocodile and felt happy. She said, "Thank you, Ben, for showing me your crocodile. It is cool and fun. I like it. Do you want to hold my bunny?"
Ben said, "Yes, Mia, I want to hold your bunny. It is soft and cuddly. I like it. Thank you for sharing with me."
They hugged each other and their toys. They were best friends. They liked to play in the park with their toys. They were glad.

One day, a fat cat went for a walk. The cat saw a twig on the ground. The cat wanted to play with the twig.
The cat looked at the twig and hit it with its paw. The twig rolled around and the cat chased it. The cat was happy.
After playing, the fat cat was tired. It went back home to take a nap. The cat had a fun day with the twig.

One day, a happy boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They walked and saw a big ice-cream truck. Tim's mom said, "If you are good today, we can get ice-cream later." Tim was very excited and wanted to be good.
Tim played on the swings and the slide. He saw a little girl crying. She lost her toy. Tim wanted to help. He looked and found the toy. The little girl was happy again. Tim's mom saw him help and smiled.
At the end of the day, Tim's mom said, "You were very good today. Let's go get ice-cream!" Tim was so happy. They went to the ice-cream truck and Tim got to use his mom's money to buy a big ice-cream cone. Tim and his mom sat on a bench and enjoyed their ice-cream together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom had a job to deliver socks to people in his town. He was a very helpful boy and everyone liked him.
One day, Tom met a rude dog on his way to deliver socks. The dog barked and growled at Tom. Tom was scared, but he knew he had to deliver the socks to the people.
Tom thought of a plan. He took a sock and threw it far away. The rude dog ran to get the sock. While the dog was away, Tom quickly delivered the socks to the people. Everyone was happy, and Tom was proud of his work.

Lily and Max are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big dog. The dog is brown and furry. It has a long tail and sharp teeth.
"Look, a dog!" Lily says. She likes dogs. She wants to pet the dog.
"Wait, Lily!" Max says. He is scared of dogs. He thinks the dog will bite him. He holds Lily's hand.
The dog runs to them. It barks loud. Lily and Max are scared. They run away. The dog chases them.
"Help, help!" Lily and Max shout. They look for a safe place.
They see a bench. On the bench, there is a bag. In the bag, there are treats. There are cookies and candies and chips.
"Let's hide behind the bench!" Lily says. She has an idea. She takes some treats from the bag.
She throws the treats to the dog. The dog stops barking. It smells the treats. It eats the treats. It likes the treats.
Lily and Max watch the dog. They see that the dog is not mean. It is hungry and lonely.
"Maybe the dog is nice," Lily says. She walks to the dog. She gives the dog a cookie. The dog licks her hand.
"Can we be friends?" Lily asks the dog.
The dog wags its tail. It nods its head.
Lily and Max smile. They pet the dog. They give the dog more treats.
They hear a voice. It is a man. He is looking for his dog.
"Spot, Spot, where are you?" the man says.
The dog hears the man. It barks. It runs to the man.
The man sees the dog. He sees Lily and Max. He sees the bag of treats.
"Thank you for finding my dog," the man says. "He ran away from me. He loves treats. You are very kind."
He gives Lily and Max a hug. He gives them some treats.
"Can we play with Spot again?" Lily asks.
"Of course," the man says. "Spot likes you. You are his new friends."
Lily and Max are happy. They are not scared of dogs anymore. They like Spot. They like treats. They like the park.

One day, a curious little boy named Tim went to his dad's work. His dad worked at a big store with lots of toys. Tim loved to play with toys, so he was very happy to go with his dad.
At the store, Tim saw a big box with a sign that said, "Do not spoil." Tim was curious and asked his dad, "What does spoil mean?" His dad said, "It means to break or ruin something. We don't want to spoil the toys inside the box."
Tim promised his dad he would not spoil the toys. But, when his dad was busy helping people, Tim opened the box. Inside, he found a big, shiny robot toy. He was so excited, he played with it right away. But, as he played, he broke the robot's arm by accident. He felt sad and scared that he spoiled the toy.
Tim told his dad what happened. His dad was not angry. He smiled and said, "It's okay, Tim. That box was full of toys we need to fix. You didn't spoil the robot, it was already broken. Now we know what to fix on it." Tim felt better and learned that it is important to be careful with things and not spoil them.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty lily. The lily was in a big garden with many other flowers. All the flowers were glad to be in the sun.
One day, a big wind came. The wind made the lily bow. The lily was scared. The other flowers were scared too. They did not like the wind.
The wind did not stop. The lily and the other flowers bowed more and more. Then, the wind broke the lily. The lily was very sad. The other flowers were sad too. The wind did not let the lily be glad again.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big, filthy sack. He loved to play with it, even though it was very dirty. One day, Tim's friend, Sue, came over to play. She saw the sack and wanted to play with it too.
Sue asked Tim if she could borrow the sack. Tim said yes, and they both played with the sack all day. They filled it with toys and carried it around the yard. They had so much fun together.
At the end of the day, Sue gave the sack back to Tim. They were both very happy. Even though the sack was filthy, they had a great time playing with it. And from that day on, Tim and Sue were the best of friends.

Lila and Ben are friends who live on a farm. They like to play with the sheep. The sheep are soft and white and make funny sounds. But one sheep is not happy. His name is Tim and he is very thin. He does not eat much and he does not run with the other sheep.
One day, Lila and Ben see Tim sitting alone under a tree. They go to him and say hello.
"Hi, Tim. Why are you so sad?" Lila asks.
"I don't feel good. I am too thin and weak. The other sheep don't like me. They say I am not a real sheep," Tim says.
"That's not true, Tim. You are a real sheep and we like you. You are just different and that's okay. You can improve if you eat more and play more. Then you will be strong and happy," Ben says.
"Really? Do you think so?" Tim asks.
"Yes, we do. Come on, Tim. Let's go to the barn and find some hay. It is yummy and good for you. Then we can play tag with the other sheep. It is fun and good for you too," Lila says.
"Okay, I will try. Thank you, Lila and Ben. You are good friends," Tim says.
Lila and Ben smile and help Tim get up. They walk to the barn and find some hay. Tim eats some and feels better. Then they run to the field and play tag with the other sheep. Tim has fun and feels better too. He is not so thin and sad anymore. He is improving and happy. He is a real sheep and he has good friends.

Once upon a time there was a child named Ruth. Ruth was a very keen girl who loved to sort things. Every day she would sort her toys, her clothes and everything else in her room.
Then one day, Ruth's parents took her on a tour. It was a very exciting day and Ruth was full of delight. Everywhere the family went, she would look around and admire the neat and tidy things. "Look how nice those things are", she thought.
The tour ended and Ruth went back home. She returned to her messy room and she sighed. She knew that she couldn't keep her room neat and tidy like she had seen on the tour. Suddenly she had an idea! Maybe she could sort everything in her room and make it neat and tidy like the things she had seen on the tour.
So she got to work, neatly sorting her toys, clothes and books. It took her some time to get everything sorted out, but eventually she got it done. Ruth was very proud when she had finished and it was even neater and tidier than the things she had seen on the tour! The pay off was worth it!

One day, a little bird was in a tree. The bird saw a big cat. The bird wanted to talk to the cat. The bird said, "Hi, cat! Do you want to play?" The cat looked at the bird and smiled.
The cat said, "Yes, bird! I want to play too." The bird was very happy. But the bird did not know that the cat was not a nice cat. The cat was thinking of a bad plan.
The bird flew down from the tree. The bird was embarrassed because it fell. The cat laughed at the bird. The bird did not like that. The bird said, "Why did you laugh at me?"
The cat said, "I am sorry, bird. I will not laugh again." The bird believed the cat. They started to play together. But the cat was still thinking of the bad plan.
At the end, the cat caught the bird. The bird was very sad. The cat was not a nice friend. The bird should not have talked to the cat.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Benny and Jane. They had a special flashlight that was different than any other flashlight. Every night they would play a game with the flashlight. They would shoot the flashlight around the room and try and find each other, hiding in the shadows.
One night, Benny was very excited when it was time for the game because he had a very special plan. He thought he would hide in the hallway, and then shoot the flashlight when Jane went by. When he heard her footsteps, Benny quickly shot the flashlight and saw Jane in the hallway! She was so surprised she laughed out loud.
The next day, Benny and Jane told all of their friends about their game with the flashlight. They all agreed it was a lot of fun, and the friends all wanted to play when the sun went down again.
So, every night Benny and Jane played with the special flashlight, shooting it around the room, pretending to find each other in the dark. It was so fun, they were always ready to play.

One day, a cat and a dog had a quarrel. They were both being silly. They were fighting about who could use the saw first. The cat said, "I want to use the saw!" The dog said, "No, I want to use the saw!"
While they were fighting, a little bird came by. The bird saw the saw and thought it was a toy. The bird picked up the saw and started to fly away with it. The cat and the dog stopped fighting and looked at the bird.
The cat and the dog said, "Hey, that's our saw!" They both ran after the bird. The bird dropped the saw, and the cat and the dog picked it up together. They both realized that they could share the saw and stopped being silly. From that day on, they became good friends and never had a quarrel again.

Once upon a time, there was a class full of kids. They all liked to draw, paint, and make things. They were very creative. One day, their teacher, Miss Sue, had a fun idea. She said, "Let's all improve our drawings today!"
The kids were excited and started drawing. They drew cats, dogs, trees, and houses. Suddenly, a little girl named Lucy found a magic crayon. When she drew with it, her pictures came to life! She showed her friends and they all gasped, "Wow, Lucy! That's amazing!"
Lucy's friends wanted to try the magic crayon, too. They took turns drawing with it, and soon the class was filled with real animals, plants, and even a little car! The kids were so happy and couldn't believe the magic crayon was real. Miss Sue smiled and said, "This is the most creative class ever!"

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his baseball. He was an adventurous boy who liked to explore.
One day, Tim went to the park with his baseball. He threw it high into the air and caught it again. Tim was having lots of fun. But then, a big dog came and took Tim's baseball. The dog did not want to give it back. The dog wanted to steal the baseball.
Tim was sad, but he was also adventurous. He chased the dog to get his baseball back. They ran around the park until Tim caught up to the dog. Tim got his baseball back and the dog was sorry. Tim and the dog became friends and played together every day.

Lily and Max were twins who liked to play pretend. They had a large box that they used as an elevator. They would take turns pushing the buttons and going up and down. Sometimes they would pretend to be doctors, or astronauts, or spies.
One day, their mom said she had to sell some of their old toys. She said they had too many and they could make some money for new ones. Lily and Max did not like this idea. They loved all their toys, even the ones they did not play with much. They did not want to give them away.
They decided to hide their favorite toys in the elevator box. They thought mom would not look there. They put their dolls, their cars, their books, and their puzzles in the box. They also put a sign on the box that said "Do not sell".
Mom came to their room and saw the box. She did not see the sign. She thought it was just a box. She picked it up and carried it to the car. She said she was going to the yard sale.
Lily and Max saw mom take the box. They ran after her and shouted "Mom, mom, stop! That's our elevator! That's our toys!"
Mom heard them and stopped. She looked at the box and saw the sign. She felt sorry. She said "Oh, I'm sorry, kids. I did not know. You can keep your elevator and your toys. But maybe you can choose some other toys to sell. You don't need so many, do you?"
Lily and Max nodded. They were happy to have their elevator and their toys back. They hugged mom and said "Thank you, mom. We love you. We will find some other toys to sell. But not our elevator. That's our special toy."

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sue. Sue had a toy sword that she loved to play with. She was always glad when she played with her sword.
One day, Sue was playing with her sword in the yard. She saw a little path that she had never seen before. The path seemed to lead somewhere fun. Sue thought maybe it would lead to a big adventure.
Sue followed the path and found a big box at the end. She was so excited! She used her toy sword to open the box. Inside the box, there were lots of fun toys and games. Sue was so glad she had her sword to help her find this big surprise.

Once upon a time there was a bear who lived in an igloo. He was very happy in his home, until one day a bitter snowstorm came and he had to go outside. The bear tried to resist the storm and go back inside, but the snow was coming down too fast. He was cold and alone and tried his best to get back home.
Then, suddenly, he heard a voice calling to him. It was a warm and friendly voice. "Come here! I can help you!" the voice said. The bear looked and saw a big, furry animal. It was a walrus!
The walrus offered the bear a place to stay, and the bear accepted. The walrus had made an igloo that was warm and safe. The bear was so happy to find a warm place to stay in. From then on, the bear and the walrus were the best of friends. 
They never had to resist the storms again!
