The pig was very happy on the farm. It played with the other animals and ate good food. Soon, the pig grew big and fat like the farmer. They all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little boy named Ben wanted to play with his toy soldiers. He liked to make them march and fight and pretend he was a strong leader. He did not like to share his toys with his sister, Mia, who was younger than him. He said she was too small and silly to play with soldiers.
Mia felt sad and lonely. She wanted to play with Ben, but he always pushed her away. She went to her room and found her stuffed monster. She hugged it and said, "You are my friend, monster. You are big and strong and nice. You can play with me."
Ben heard Mia talking to her monster. He thought it was a stupid toy. He decided to scare Mia and take her monster away. He sneaked into her room and shouted, "Boo! I am a real monster! I will eat your toy monster and you!"
Mia was scared and cried. She held her monster tight and said, "No, no, no! Go away, Ben! You are a mean monster! You are not my brother!"
Ben felt bad. He saw that Mia was really afraid and hurt. He realized that he was not a strong leader, but a bully. He said, "I'm sorry, Mia. I was just joking. I don't want to eat your monster or you. I want to be your brother and your friend. Can I play with you and your monster?"
Mia stopped crying and looked at Ben. She saw that he was sorry and kind. She said, "Yes, Ben. You can play with us. But you have to be nice and share. And you have to say sorry to my monster, too."
Ben nodded and hugged Mia and her monster. He said, "I'm sorry, monster. You are a good friend to Mia. You are big and strong and nice. Can I march with you and Mia?"
Mia smiled and said, "Yes, Ben. You can march with us. We can be a team and have fun."
And so they did. They marched and played and laughed together. They learned that being nice and sharing is better than being mean and selfish. They learned that monsters can be friends, too.

Anna and Ben were friends who liked to play with snow. They made a big snowman with a hat, a scarf and a carrot nose. They gave him a name: Sam.
One day, the sun was very bright and warm. Anna and Ben went to see Sam, but they saw something bad. Sam was melting! His hat was falling, his scarf was loose and his carrot nose was gone. Anna and Ben were very sad and angry.
They saw a boy with a big smile and a carrot in his hand. He was the one who took Sam's nose. He said: "Ha ha, your snowman is gone. He can't talk to you anymore. He doesn't have a language."
Anna and Ben did not like the boy. He was mean and rude. They wanted to get Sam's nose back, but they did not know how. They thought hard and had an idea.
They said to the boy: "You are right, our snowman can't talk to us. But he can talk to you. He is very wise and knows many things. He can tell you a secret, if you give him his nose back."
The boy was curious and greedy. He wanted to know the secret. He gave Sam his nose back and leaned close to his ear. But Sam did not say anything. He was just a snowman.
Anna and Ben laughed and said: "The secret is: you are a fool. You should not take things that are not yours. You should be kind and share. That is what wise people do."
The boy felt ashamed and sorry. He said: "I am sorry, Anna and Ben. I was wrong and mean. Can we be friends?"
Anna and Ben smiled and said: "Yes, we can be friends. But you have to promise to be nice and good."
The boy promised and they all played together. They had fun and learned a lesson. The sun was still bright and warm, but they did not mind. They knew that Sam was happy too.

Once upon a time there was a bunny. He was so happy and playful. Every day he hopped along, looking for adventure. He would hop and hop until he saw something that made him wonder.
One day he saw a flower stand and he hopped over to it. He sniffed each flower and touched the soft petals. He was almost ready to move on when he noticed something strange.
It was a caterpillar! It was lying in the grass beside the stand. The bunny had never seen a caterpillar before, so he was curious. He prodded it gently, but the caterpillar didn't move.
The bunny realised that the caterpillar was lying there, taking a nap. The caterpillar had been very playful before, but now it was asleep. But the bunny was very happy that he had come across such a wonderful surprise.

One day, a little girl named Mia went to the park. She saw a harmless bug and waved at it. The bug waved back with its tiny legs. Mia was very happy.
Mia saw a doctor walking in the park. She ran to the doctor and said, "Hi, doctor! I saw a bug and we waved at each other!" The doctor smiled and said, "That's very nice, Mia. Bugs can be good friends."
Mia and the doctor walked together in the park. They saw more bugs and waved at them too. The bugs waved back, and everyone was happy. Mia and the doctor became good friends, just like Mia and the harmless bugs.

One day, a little wolf went for a walk. He saw a big tree far away. The little wolf wanted to go to the tree, but he was scared.
His mom saw him and said, "You can do it! I will be right here." She wanted to encourage him. The little wolf felt brave and started walking.
As he walked, the tree got closer and closer. The little wolf was happy. He looked back and saw his mom. She was smiling and said, "Good job!" The little wolf knew he could do anything with his mom's help.

Once upon a time, there was a famous cat. Everyone loved the cat and he was very smart. One day, the cat went walking outside and he slipped on some waste. He fell and hurt himself badly. He cried out in pain.
The cat limped back home but he wouldn't get better. He was very sick and soon, he passed away. Everyone was sad. They wrote stories and songs to remember the famous cat, but nothing could take away the sadness of his slip.

Once upon a time, there was a big ship. The ship was very original. It was red, blue, and yellow. Many people liked to look at the ship. The ship would sail on the water, and everyone would wave and smile.
One day, the ship was sailing, and it saw a big fish. The fish was very friendly. The fish swam up to the ship and said, "Hello, ship! I want to be your friend!" The ship was very happy to have a new friend. They played together in the water.
But then, something unexpected happened. The sky turned dark, and the wind began to blow. The waves started to strike the ship. The big fish said, "Don't worry, ship! I will help you!" The fish swam very fast and pushed the ship to a safe place. The wind and waves stopped, and the sky turned bright again. The ship and the fish were now best friends. They were very happy and played together every day.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw a big bin. Tim's mom said, "Tim, put your trash in the bin." Tim picked up his trash and put it in the bin. The bin was hard.
At the park, Tim saw a dog. The dog was playing with a ball. The dog ran fast and hit the bin. The bin fell down. Tim yelled, "Oh no, the bin!"
Tim's mom came over and said, "It's okay, Tim. Let's pick up the bin." They picked up the hard bin together. Then, Tim and his mom went home. They were happy they helped the park stay clean.

One day, a big shark named Sam swam in the sea. Sam was very organized. He liked to keep his sea home clean. Sam had a big box. He always kept his toys inside the box. Sam had a friend named Tim. Tim was a little fish.
Sam said to Tim, "I will open my toy box. Let's play with my toys." Tim was happy. They played with the toys for a long time. The sun was going down. Sam knew it was time to put the toys back in the box.
Sam and Tim put the toys in the box. They closed the box. Sam knew that his sea home was clean again. They were happy because they had fun playing. Sam said, "We can play again tomorrow!" Tim smiled and swam away.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a small, fragile leaf. The leaf lived on a tall tree with many friends. They all liked to dance and play when the wind blew.
One day, dark clouds came, and it started to rain. The wind blew stronger, and the fragile leaf was scared. It held on tight to the tree, not wanting to let go.
The rain stopped, and the sun came out. The fragile leaf was happy again. It started to dance and play with its friends in the warm sunlight. They all knew that they were safe and loved in their big forest home.

Once upon a time, in an ancient forest, there was a big lion. The lion loved to lie under a big tree and taste the sweet fruit that fell from it. He was very happy.
One day, while the lion was lying under the tree, a little bird came and sat on a branch. The bird sang a pretty song. The lion liked the song and wanted to be friends with the bird. The bird liked the lion too and they became good friends.
The lion and the bird played together every day. But one day, something unexpected happened. The bird found a magic fruit on the ground. The bird shared the magic fruit with the lion. As they tasted the fruit, they both changed into beautiful butterflies! Now, they could fly and play together in the sky, and they were even happier than before.

Once upon a time, there was a red ball. The red ball had a friend named Page. Page was a book. They lived in a big toy box with other toys.
One day, the red ball and Page decided to play a game. They wanted to make the other toys laugh. So, the red ball started to bounce up and down. Page opened and closed, making funny faces.
All the toys in the toy box started to laugh. They laughed and laughed. The red ball and Page were very happy. They loved making their friends laugh. And they all played together happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was an old woman who found a small kitten by the side of the road. She felt it was her duty to protect the kitten and keep it safe so she decided to take it home. She showed it to her daughter and said, "Isn't this kitten so cute and small? We should support it and give it a place to live."
Her daughter was so excited that she hugged the kitten and said, "Yes, let's take care of the kitten! I want it to be safe and happy here."
The old woman was so glad her daughter supported her decision. She made a warm, comfortable bed for the kitten in the corner of the room, gave it some food and water and told it that it was now safe in their home.
The kitten was so relieved and happy, it ran around the room, meowing with joy. From that day on, it stayed with the old woman and her daughter, playing and purring and feeling safe and loved.

Once upon a time there was a brave boy. He lived by an icy river. Every morning he would go to the river and lift the stones. He was very brave. One day the boy decided to go further down the river.
He walked and walked carefully until he saw a big chunk of ice. He had never seen anything like it before! He was scared but he was brave and wanted to see what it was.
He reached out to touch the ice but suddenly a voice called out, "Don't touch the ice! It's too cold and it can be dangerous."
The boy was surprised; he looked around and saw that it was an old man. The man said, "If you promise to be careful and brave I'll show you a much better way to lift the ice."
The boy promised and the old man showed him how to use a rope to pull the ice out of the river. Thanks to the old man's help, he was able to lift the ice and take it home with him. From that day on, the boy was always brave and never stopped exploring.

One day, a purple cat named Tom found an ashtray. Tom did not know what it was, so he made a plan to find out. He asked his friends, a dog and a bird, to help him. They all said yes.
First, the dog sniffed the ashtray. He did not know what it was. Next, the bird looked at it from high up. She did not know what it was either. Tom felt sad because his plan was not working.
Then, a big man saw the purple cat, the dog, and the bird with the ashtray. The man knew what it was. He told them that an ashtray is used to put out fires. Tom was happy that his plan worked. The purple cat, the dog, and the bird thanked the man and went to play.

One day, a curious little boy named Tim went for a walk with his mom. They saw a big red ambulance on the street. Tim asked his mom, "Mom, what is that big red car?" His mom said, "That is an ambulance, Tim. It helps sick people."
Tim wanted to see more of the ambulance. They walked closer and saw the ambulance display many bright lights. Tim was very happy and clapped his hands. He said, "Mom, I like the pretty lights on the ambulance!"
Then, the ambulance made a loud noise. "Wee-oo, wee-oo!" Tim was scared and held his mom's hand. His mom said, "Don't worry, Tim. The ambulance is just saying it needs to go help someone." Tim felt better and waved goodbye to the ambulance as it drove away.

One day, a big rocket was in the sky. It was very high up. A little boy named Tim saw the big rocket and wanted to fly in it. He asked his mom, "Can I go in the big rocket, please?"
His mom said, "No, Tim. The big rocket is not for little boys. You need to be big and strong to fly in the rocket."
Tim was sad, but he wanted to gain the strength to fly in the big rocket. So, he ate his fruits and veggies, and played outside every day. He became big and strong, just like his mom said.
One day, the big rocket came back. Tim was so excited! He ran to the rocket, but when he got there, he saw a little girl crying. She said, "I lost my toy in the rocket. Can you help me?"
Tim thought about flying in the rocket, but he decided to help the little girl instead. They found her toy, and she was very happy. Tim learned that helping others is more important than just having fun by yourself.

Once upon a time, there was an incredible hunter. He always went into the woods to find and hunt animals. One day, he was out in the woods when he came across a stream. He decided to wash in it to clean of the dirt from his hunt. As he was washing, he heard a voice from the other side of the stream. It said: "Come closer, hunter!"
The hunter was curious. He slowly approached the other side of the stream and saw a frog. The hunter thought it was incredible, so he asked the frog: "What are you doing here?"
The frog said: "I'm here to wash! It's much better than washing in a bowl. Will you wash with me?" The hunter found the idea amusing, so he said yes. So the hunter and the frog washed together in the stream. They both felt clean, refreshed and incredible after their wash. The end.

One day, a small cat named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big, red ball in the sky. It was very hot. Tim felt the heat on his fur. He thought, "I need to find some shade."
Tim saw a big tree with a lot of shade. He went to the tree and sat down. He heard a small voice say, "It's dangerous here." Tim looked around and saw a tiny bug. The bug was scared. Tim asked, "Why is it dangerous?"
The bug said, "A big bird lives in this tree. The bird likes to eat bugs and small animals like you." Tim was scared, but he didn't want to go back into the heat. He thought of a plan. He said, "Let's work together. I will help you hide, and you can help me hide too."
The bug agreed. They found a safe place to hide under the tree. The big bird came back, but it did not see Tim and the bug. They stayed safe and cool in the shade. They were happy they worked together.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Lola. She was 3 years old and loved to play outside. One day, Lola went out to the park with her mom. They had so much fun! They played on the swings and then went to explore the forest. 
As they were walking, Lola noticed something different in the ground. She looked closer and saw that it was a patch of bumpy, soft texture. She asked her mom, "What is this?" Her mom smiled and said, "It's a special mushroom, with powerful healing powers."
Lola was so excited that she wanted to pick the mushroom right away. But her mom warned her, "Let's leave it alone. We don't want to worry the magical mushroom."
The little girl listened and they continued their journey. As they walked away, Lola kept looking back at the special mushroom with awe and admiration.

Once upon a time, in a quiet little town, there lived a girl named Amy. Amy loved to dance to the rhythm of her favorite songs. She would dance all day and all night.
One day, while Amy was dancing, she saw a tiny bird outside her window. The bird was scared and needed help. Amy wanted to protect the little bird, so she brought it into her house.
Amy and the little bird became best friends. They danced together and played games. The bird loved the rhythm of Amy's music and they lived happily ever after in their quiet little town.

Once upon a time, there was a little rabbit named Bob. Bob loved to eat carrots. One day, he found a big, yummy carrot in the garden. But, when he tried to pull it out, it was stuck! Bob felt hurt because he couldn't get his favorite food.
Then, Bob saw his friend, the squirrel named Sam. Bob asked Sam, "Can you help me get this carrot?" Sam said, "Yes, let's unite and pull together!" So, Bob and Sam held the carrot and pulled with all their strength.
Finally, the carrot came out of the ground! Bob and Sam were so happy. They shared the big carrot and enjoyed their tasty snack. From that day on, they always helped each other and knew that when they unite, they can do anything.

Once upon a time, in a big palace, there lived a king. The king was very foolish. He liked to play all day and did not do his work. One day, he decided to start a big party for all his friends. He was very excited and happy.
The king invited all his friends to his palace. They came to the party and played many fun games. They ate lots of yummy food and danced all night. The king was having the best time ever.
But then, something unexpected happened. A big, friendly bear came to the party. Everyone was scared at first, but the bear just wanted to have fun too. The bear played games and danced with the king and his friends. The king learned that even if something seems scary, it can turn out to be fun and nice. He became a wise king and made friends with everyone, even the big friendly bear.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. Tim had a sandwich in his bag. It was a very yummy sandwich with cheese and ham. Tim was very happy to have his sandwich.
While playing in the sand, Tim's hands got very dirty. He wanted to eat his sandwich, but he didn't want to get it dirty too. Then, Tim saw a useful cloth in his bag. He used the cloth to wipe his hands clean. Now, his hands were clean and ready to eat the sandwich.
Tim sat down on the grass with his mom and ate his yummy sandwich. His mom was happy that Tim had used the cloth to wipe his hands. They both enjoyed their time at the park, and Tim learned that it is important to have clean hands before eating.

Lily and Ben like to play outside when it rains. They put on their boots and coats and run to the park. They find a big puddle and jump in it. They splash and laugh and make waves.
They see a tree with a swing. They run to the swing and take turns. Lily pushes Ben and Ben pushes Lily. They go higher and higher. They feel the wind and the rain on their faces. They are happy.
But then, something bad happens. The rope of the swing breaks. Ben falls off the swing and lands in the puddle. He hurts his arm and his leg. He cries and cries. Lily runs to him and tries to help him. She is scared and sad.
They hear a loud noise. It is a car. The car is coming fast. The driver does not see them. They are in the middle of the road. They try to get up and run, but they are too late. The car hits them. They fly in the air and hang on a branch. They do not move. They do not breathe. They are gone.
The rain stops. The sun comes out. The puddle dries up. The swing is empty. The tree is silent. The car drives away. No one knows what happened. No one cares. It is a bad ending.

Once upon a time, in a big blue sea, there lived a big octopus. The octopus was very nice and loved to play with fish. One day, the octopus met a small fish who was sad. The octopus asked, "Why are you sad, little fish?"
The little fish said, "A dangerous shark is scaring all my friends away. I have no one to play with now." The octopus wanted to help the fish and all his friends.
The octopus thought hard and came up with a plan. He told the fish, "I will play with you and your friends. When the dangerous shark comes, I will make my arms increase and look very big. The shark will be scared and go away."
The little fish was happy and called all his friends to play. Just as they were playing, the dangerous shark came. The octopus made his arms increase and looked very big. The shark was scared and swam away quickly.
From that day on, the octopus, the little fish, and all his friends played happily together in the big blue sea. The dangerous shark never came back, and they all became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a chair. It was a very beautiful chair and it hung in the sky. Every morning, the chair would hang there, high up in the sky, because it had been a successful chair.
Every day, lots of animals would come to the chair. They would all hang from it, like bobbing apples. Some of them would swing from the chair, and others would just sit and have a big chat. 
Then one day, a little bird came. He said, "I want to hang too." But he was too small to hang from the chair. So he asked if anyone had a better idea. 
Then a big elephant said, "I have an idea! Maybe you could hang from my trunk." So the little bird thought it was a great idea and he hugged the elephant's trunk. He was the happiest little bird in the world. 
So from then on, the chair hung there, high in the sky and the bird hung from the elephant's trunk. He was very happy and so was the chair because it had been a successful chair.

Lily and Tom were playing pirates in the garden. They had a big box for a ship, a stick for a sword and a hat for a captain. Lily was the captain and Tom was the mate.
"Look, mate, there is a treasure island!" Lily said, pointing to a pile of rocks. "We have to get there before the bad pirates do!"
They jumped out of the box and ran to the rocks. They pretended to fight with the bad pirates, who were really just bushes and flowers. They made loud noises and swung their swords.
"Yield, you scurvy dogs!" Lily shouted. "This treasure is ours!"
They reached the rocks and looked for the treasure. They saw a shiny thing sticking out of the dirt. It was a hook!
"Wow, a real pirate hook!" Tom said. "Maybe it belonged to a strong pirate who lost his hand in a battle!"
He tried to pull the hook out of the dirt, but it was stuck. He pulled harder and harder, but it did not move.
"Help me, captain!" Tom said. "It's too hard for me!"
Lily came to help him. She grabbed the hook with both hands and pulled with all her might. She was very strong for a little girl. She pulled so hard that the hook came out of the dirt, but she also lost her balance and fell backwards.
"Ouch!" Lily said. "That hurt!"
Tom ran to her and helped her up. He saw that she had a scratch on her arm from the hook.
"Are you okay, captain?" Tom asked. "You were very brave!"
Lily smiled and hugged him. She did not mind the scratch. She was happy that they had found the treasure.
"I'm okay, mate!" Lily said. "Thank you for helping me! We make a great pirate team!"
They held the hook in the air and cheered. They had the best pirate adventure ever.

Poppy was a curious cat. She liked to explore and find new things. One day, she saw a big box in the kitchen. It had a picture of a bird on it. Poppy wanted to see what was inside.
She jumped on the box and pushed it with her paws. The box moved and made a noise. Poppy was scared and ran away. She hid under the sofa and watched the box.
Soon, she heard a voice. It was her human, Lisa. Lisa came into the kitchen and saw the box. She smiled and opened it. She took out a cage with a bird in it. The bird was yellow and blue and sang a pretty song.
"Hello, Sunny," Lisa said to the bird. "You are my birthday present from Grandma. I'm so happy to have you. I will serve you some seeds and water and make you a cozy home."
Poppy peeked out from under the sofa. She saw the bird and felt curious again. She wanted to play with the bird. She crept closer to the cage and reached out her paw.
But Lisa saw her and said, "No, Poppy, you can't touch Sunny. He is not a toy. He is my friend. You have to be nice and gentle with him. Do you understand?"
Poppy nodded and meowed. She understood, but she still felt curious. She wondered what the bird would do if she opened the cage. Maybe he would fly and sing with her. Maybe he would be her friend too.
She waited until Lisa left the room. Then she jumped on the cage and tried to open it. But the cage was locked and strong. Poppy could not open it. She pushed and pulled and scratched and bit. But nothing worked.
The bird saw Poppy and felt scared. He flapped his wings and squawked. He wanted Lisa to come back and save him. He did not like Poppy. He did not want to play with her.
Lisa heard the noise and came back. She saw Poppy and the bird and was angry. She picked up Poppy and said, "Bad cat, bad cat. You can't do that. You scared Sunny. You have to leave him alone. Go to your bed and stay there."
She put Poppy in her bed and closed the door. Poppy felt sad and sorry. She did not mean to be bad. She just wanted to be curious. She wished Lisa would forgive her and let her out. She wished the bird would like her and be her friend.
But the bird did not like her. And Lisa did not forgive her. Not for a long time. Not until Poppy learned to be nice and gentle with Sunny. And not until Sunny learned to trust and sing with Poppy. And not until they both learned to serve and love Lisa.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was an enormous tree. The tree was very old and full of wisdom. Many animals lived near the tree and they loved it very much.
One day, a little bird wanted to start a new life. The bird flew to the enormous tree and asked for its wisdom. The tree told the bird to be kind and share with others.
The little bird listened and started to share its food with other animals. The animals became friends with the bird. They all lived happily together near the enormous tree. The tree was proud of the little bird for using its wisdom.

Once upon a time, there was a funny bird who loved to joke. He lived in a big tree near a beautiful pond. Every day, he would fly high in the sky and look for friends to play with.
One day, while the bird was flying in the sky, he saw an ugly frog sitting by the pond. The frog looked sad, so the bird decided to make a joke to cheer him up. The bird flew down from the sky and landed next to the frog.
"Hey, frog!" said the bird. "Why did the chicken cross the road?" The frog looked up, curious. "I don't know, why?" he asked. The bird laughed and said, "To get to the other side!" The frog laughed too, and they became good friends. From that day on, they played together by the pond, under the big, blue sky.

One day, Johnny's mom took him to the park. While there, Johnny saw a kite that he wanted to fly. His mom smiled and said, "Let's wrap the string around your face!" Johnny was excited and let his mom tie the string around his little face. Then, they ran to the hill as fast as they could. As they ran, the kite started to fly higher and higher in the sky. 
Suddenly, Johnny's mom shouted out, "Let go, Johnny! Let go!" He did not do it fast enough and the kite flew away! Johnny was sad, but his mom said, "Don't worry! We can try again next week." Johnny smiled and hugged his mom. He knew that she would make sure the kite wrapped around his face next time.

One sunny day, Mama and Bob the Baby Bird were walking in the meadow. Bob looked around and saw a turtle. He was very bossy and walked right towards the turtle, saying "Hi!" 
The Turtle stopped, surprised by the Baby Bird. He asked, "What do you want?" 
Bob replied, "I want success!" 
Mama smiled and said, "That's an excellent answer, Bob. But success takes a lot of hard work and practice." 
The Turtle thought for a moment then said, "Success come from not being too bossy. If you are willing to listen and be patient, it will pay off in the end." 
Bob thought about it and decided to take the Turtle's advice. He thanked the Turtle for his wise words and continued his journey with Mama, searching for success.

once upon a time there was a girl who had a photo. she liked the photo and thought it was the prettiest ever. one day, she decided to twist it. however, she twisted it a bit too much and it ripped! the girl was so uncomfortable because she had destroyed something she liked. she decided to be more careful next time. the end.

Once upon a time, there was a big bear named Bob. Bob loved to make waffles. One day, he wanted to make the best waffle ever. He got all the things he needed and started to make the waffle.
As Bob was making the waffle, he saw a little bunny named Ben. Ben looked sad. Bob asked, "Why are you sad, Ben?" Ben said, "I am sorry, but I lost my toy." Bob wanted to help Ben find his toy.
Bob and Ben looked for the toy together. They found it under a big tree. Ben was very happy. He said, "Thank you, Bob!" They went back to Bob's house and ate the waffle together. It was the best waffle ever. And they became the best of friends.

One day, a green frog named Sam hopped near a fire. He saw a big log. Sam wanted to carry the log to his pond. But the log was too big for him.
Sam asked his friend, a red bird named Tim, for help. "Tim, can you help me carry this log to my pond?" Sam asked. Tim said, "Yes, I can help you, Sam!"
As they tried to carry the log, they saw a mean blue snake. The snake wanted the log too. The snake hissed, "This log is mine! Go away!" Sam and Tim were sad and scared.
But then, they had an idea. Sam jumped high and splashed water from the pond on the snake. The snake got wet and went away. Sam and Tim carried the log to the pond together. They were happy and safe.

Mittens was a very difficult cat. He thought it was fun to do things in reverse. He would run backwards and meow backwards. His owners would turn their heads and shake them in confusion.
One day, his owners decided to have a picnic in the park. Mittens wanted to come along, but he was having trouble finding his green mitten. He searched and searched, but could not find it. Then, he had an idea. He decided to try and find it in reverse.
He started running backwards from one corner of the park to another. He stopped to take a look at trees, bushes, and even the grass. He was determined to find it. After a few minutes, he spotted the mitten!
He took the mitten and ran back to the picnic. His owners were very happy to see him, with his mitten in his mouth. They all had fun enjoying their picnic. Mittens was even happier knowing he had found his mitten in a very difficult way!

The wind was zipping through the trees. Jeremy watched with an interesting smile. He loved the sound of the wind and how it made the trees dance.
Suddenly, a scoop of ice cream flew out of the window and landed on Jeremy's head. He looked up and saw two children laughing.
"Who are you?" Jeremy asked.
"We are twins," said the first one. His name was Mark.
"And my name is John," said the second one.
Jeremy was amazed. He had never seen twins before!
"Can I join you?" Jeremy asked.
The twins said yes and they all laughed and zipped around the park. They played and explored, and Jeremy learned a lot of interesting things. 
Soon it was time to go home. The twins said goodbye and they all hugged.
"I had a lot of fun today," Jeremy said.
The twins smiled and zipped away, leaving Jeremy with a smile on his face.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her friend, Poppy. They liked to play with toys and run in the park. One day, they had to separate because Poppy was going to visit her grandma. Lily was sad but she was patient. She knew Poppy would come back soon.
While Poppy was away, Lily found a big box in her room. She opened it and saw many toys. She thought, "I can't wait to show Poppy when she comes back!" Lily played with the toys and had fun, but she missed Poppy. She knew she had to wait.
Days went by and Poppy came back from her grandma's house. Lily was so happy to see her friend. She said, "Poppy, I found a big box of toys! Let's play together!" They opened the box and started playing. But then, something unexpected happened. The toys started to move and talk! Lily and Poppy were surprised but they laughed and played with their new talking toy friends. They knew they would have many more fun days together.

Once upon a time, there was a clever cat named Tom. Tom liked to stir things in the kitchen. One day, Tom found a big pot and started to stir, stir, stir. He was having so much fun!
Outside, there was a big storm. The wind blew hard, and the rain fell fast. Tom didn't know about the storm because he was busy stirring the pot. The storm made a loud noise, and Tom got scared. He ran away from the pot and hid under the bed.
But then, something unexpected happened. The storm stopped, and the sun came out. Tom came out from under the bed and saw that the pot he was stirring was now full of yummy soup! The storm had brought all the things needed for the soup. Tom was so happy, and he shared the soup with his friends. They all laughed and enjoyed the tasty soup that the storm helped make.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a folder on the floor. Inside the folder, there were many pretty pictures. Tim wanted to keep the folder for himself because he was selfish. He did not want to share it with his friends.
Tim showed the folder to his mom. "Mom, I found this folder. Can I keep it?" he asked. His mom looked at the folder and smiled. "This folder belongs to your friend, Anna," she explained. "You should give it back to her."
Tim felt sad. He did not want to give the folder back. But he knew it was the right thing to do. He went to Anna's house and gave her the folder. Anna was so happy to see it. "Thank you, Tim!" she said. "I lost this folder and I was very sad."
Then, something unexpected happened. Anna opened the folder and took out a picture. "This is for you, Tim," she said, giving him a pretty picture. Tim was surprised and happy. He learned that sharing was better than being selfish.

One day, a cat and a dog were playing in the park. The cat was very impatient. He wanted to play a new game. The dog thought for a moment and then suggested, "Let's play hide and seek!"
The cat agreed and they began to play. The dog closed his eyes and started counting. The cat ran to hide behind a big tree. But the dog was very good at the game. He quickly found the cat with his sharp sight.
The cat was sad and said, "You are too good at this game. I want to play a different game." The dog thought again and suggested, "Let's play tag!" The cat agreed and they played tag for a while. But the cat was still not happy. He wanted to win a game.
The dog saw that the cat was sad and said, "Don't worry, friend. We can play more games until you find one you like." The cat smiled and they continued to play in the park, trying different games until they both had fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play near the creek. He liked to watch the fish swim and the water flow.
One day, a big bird came to the creek. The bird wanted to steal the fish. Tim felt dizzy watching the bird fly round and round. He didn't want the bird to take the fish away.
Tim picked up a stone and threw it at the bird. The bird was scared and flew away. Tim saved the fish and felt happy. He played by the creek every day, making sure the fish were safe.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom was a good boy who always tried to behave. One day, Tom went to the park with his mom. It was a warm day, and the sun was shining bright.
At the park, Tom saw a big boy named Jack. Jack was showing off his muscles to his friends. Tom wanted to be like Jack, so he asked him, "How can I have big muscles like you?" Jack laughed and said, "You need to eat lots of food and play a lot."
So, Tom ate lots of food and played every day. But instead of getting big muscles like Jack, Tom got very tired and felt sick. His mom saw this and told him, "Tom, you don't need big muscles to be a good boy. What matters is that you behave and be kind to others."
Tom learned that being kind and behaving well was more important than having big muscles. And the next time he went to the park, he played with his friends and shared his toys. Everyone liked Tom because he was a good boy who behaved.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Amy. She was very happy and loved life. Every day she would play in the park and would sometimes take her special teddy bear.
One day a new meeting was happening. Amy was very excited and decided to join. It was very unusual because it was in a secret place under a tree.
When she arrived at the meeting, many people were there. But Amy noticed that no one wanted to talk to her. They just wanted to talk to each other. She was very sad and felt left out.
But then one lady noticed Amy and smiled at her. She told her that she loved her and could join the meeting. Amy was so happy and went to speak to the others.Everyone was very friendly and started to love her.
From then on, Amy was always invited to the meetings. The people there would always love to speak to her and she was no longer excluded. 
Amy had learned that even when things are unusual, love can help make them better.

Once upon a time, in a warm and sunny place, there was a good little lizard. The lizard loved to play with his friends all day long. They would run, jump, and laugh together.
One day, while playing, the lizard fell and hurt his leg. He felt sad because he could not play with his friends. His mom told him, "Don't worry, your leg will heal soon."
The little lizard stayed in his cozy home and waited for his leg to heal. His friends came to visit him and brought him yummy bugs to eat. They told him stories and made him laugh. The lizard felt happy even though he could not play outside.
After a few days, the lizard's leg healed and he could play with his friends again. They were all so happy to have their good friend back. They played and laughed together, and the little lizard knew that even when things go wrong, good friends and time can help make everything better.

Once upon a time, there was a charming cat who wore a vest. The cat loved to play with a sack. One day, the cat went outside to play with his sack. He was very happy.
As the cat played, a dog came by. The dog saw the cat playing with the sack and wanted to play too. The cat was scared because the dog was big. But the dog was nice and just wanted to have fun.
Then, something unexpected happened. The dog put on the vest and started to play with the sack too. The cat and the dog played together and became best friends. They both loved the sack and the vest, and they shared them every day. The charming cat and the nice dog were very happy.

Once upon a time, in a big zoo, there was a gorilla named Gogo. Gogo was a very friendly gorilla, but sometimes he felt sad because people would ignore him. He wanted to play with the kids who visited the zoo, but they were always busy looking at other animals.
One day, a little girl named Lily came to the zoo with her mom. Lily saw Gogo and thought he looked fun. She asked her mom, "Can I play with the gorilla? He looks sad and lonely." Her mom said, "Sure, as long as you are careful and stay close to me."
Lily walked up to Gogo and said, "Hi, Mr. Gorilla! My name is Lily. Do you want to play with me?" Gogo was so happy that someone finally noticed him. He nodded his head and started to play with Lily. They played catch with a ball that was available in the zoo.
As they played, more kids saw them and joined in. Gogo was so happy that he was no longer being ignored. From that day on, Gogo was always the center of attention at the zoo, and he had many new friends to play with.
And so, Gogo the gorilla and Lily became best friends. They played together every time Lily visited the zoo, and they taught everyone that even the biggest animals need friends too.

One day, a frog and a duck were at a pond. The frog said, "I will show you how to jump." The duck said, "I will show you how to swim." They were good friends and liked to help each other.
The frog showed the duck how to jump. The duck tried to jump, but it was bad at it. Then, the duck showed the frog how to swim. The frog tried to swim, but it was bad at it too. They both laughed and had fun.
Then, something unexpected happened. The duck jumped very high and the frog swam very fast. They were both very happy. Now, they were good at jumping and swimming. They played together all day at the pond.

Once upon a time, there was a gray bunny named Bob. Bob loved to bake yummy treats for all his friends. One day, he decided to bake a big cake and share it with everyone.
Bob went underground to his cozy little home to start baking. He mixed flour, eggs, and sugar in a big bowl. The cake batter smelled so good! Bob put the cake in the oven and waited for it to bake.
When the cake was done, Bob took it out of the oven. It was big and yummy! He shared the cake with all his friends. They loved it and thanked Bob for the tasty treat. From that day on, Bob was known as the best baker in the forest.

Once upon a time there was an enormous hospital. All the doctors and nurses were filled with worry. One day there was a call for help from a tiny village far away. The hospital was the only hope the villagers had, so the doctors and nurses raced to help. 
One tiny nurse called Lilly was getting ready to leave. As the other nurses said goodbye, Lilly stopped. She asked her friends, "What if there's not enough medical supplies? We might not be able to help everyone!"
Everyone was silent. Eventually one doctor said, "You're right - we must go out with enough supplies to help as many people as possible! Don't worry, Lilly, we'll fill as many bags as we can!"
And so they did. With great bravery they filled their bags with as many supplies as they could and raced to the village. They filled the villagers with hope and saved many lives.
The moral of the story is that it's better to be prepared for something rather than rush in without thinking first. Planning ahead can help us to fill the world with more love and help the people who need it most.

Once upon a time, there was an elderly lady named Sue. Sue liked to read books every day. She also liked to do exercise. One day, she went for a walk in the park.
In the park, Sue saw a big dog. The big dog wanted to play with Sue. But Sue was scared. She did not want to play with the dog. The dog jumped on Sue and took her book. Sue was sad.
Sue's friend, Tom, came to help. Tom asked the dog to give the book back. The dog listened and gave the book to Tom. Sue was happy. Tom told Sue not to be scared of dogs. Dogs just want to play and be friends. The moral of the story is to not be scared of new things and to ask for help when needed.

Once upon a time there was a girl called Jane. Jane wanted to make something special so she got a needle and thread. As Jane tried to thread the needle, the thread kept slipping away. Jane became very angry and threw the needle away. 
Just then, Jane's Mum came into the room. Jane's Mum asked her why she was so angry. Jane picked up the thread again and showed her Mum the needle and thread. 
Mum said, "It's ok Jane, let me show you how to do it." She showed Jane how to thread the needle, carefully and slowly. 
Jane smiled and said, "Thank you Mum!" Jane threaded the needle and started on her project. She was so happy she was able to make something special all by herself!

Once upon a time, there were two little twins named Tim and Tom. They lived in a small house with their mom and dad. Tim and Tom liked to play outside in the sun. One day, they went outside to play with their red ball.
While they were playing, Tim said, "I feel hot. The sun is burning today!" Tom agreed, "Yes, it is very hot. Let's go inside and get a drink." They went inside, and their mom gave them a cold drink. The twins felt better after drinking the cold water.
Suddenly, they heard a loud noise outside. Tim and Tom were scared, but they went to see what happened. To their surprise, they found a big, wet elephant splashing water everywhere! The elephant had come to cool the hot ground. The twins laughed and played with the elephant, and they all had fun on the hot day.

One day, a big, loud dog named Max met a small, quiet cat named Lily. Max liked to tease Lily and make jokes. Lily did not like Max's jokes, but she was too shy to say anything.
One day, Max said, "Hey Lily, why don't you try to bark like me? It's fun!" Max barked very loud. Lily did not like the loud noise. She felt scared, but she tried to be brave. "I don't want to bark, Max. I like to be quiet," Lily said.
Max felt sad that he scared Lily. He said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I won't tease you anymore. Let's be friends." Lily felt happy and said, "Okay, Max. We can be friends." From that day on, Max and Lily played together and had lots of fun. They learned to be friends and enjoy each other's company.

Lily was miserable. She did not like the dark. She wanted to sleep with her teddy bear, but she could not find it. She looked under her bed, in her closet, and behind her curtains. She did not see her teddy bear anywhere.
She heard a noise outside her window. She was scared. She ran to her door and tried to open it. But it was locked. She forgot that her mom and dad locked it every night. She cried and banged on the door. She wanted them to come and help her.
She saw a shadow on the wall. It was big and scary. It looked like a monster. She screamed and hid under her blanket. She wished her teddy bear was with her.
The shadow moved closer to her bed. It reached out its hand. Lily was too afraid to look. She closed her eyes and hoped it would go away.
But then she heard a familiar voice. It was her brother, Tom. He said, "Lily, don't be afraid. It's me. I'm sorry I scared you. I was trying to find my flashlight. I dropped it under your bed. Look, I found your teddy bear too. Here, take it. I'm sorry I locked your door. I was playing a prank on you. But I won't do it again. I promise. Do you forgive me?"
Lily opened her eyes and saw Tom's face. He was holding her teddy bear and his flashlight. She was angry at him, but also happy to see him. She took her teddy bear and hugged it. She said, "Tom, you are mean. But I forgive you. Thank you for finding my teddy bear. I love you. But don't lock my door again. Or I will tell mom and dad."
Tom smiled and said, "I love you too, Lily. And I won't lock your door again. I'm sorry I made you miserable. Do you want me to stay with you until you fall asleep?"
Lily nodded and said, "Yes, please. And can you turn on your flashlight? I don't like the dark."
Tom said, "Sure, Lily. I will turn on my flashlight. And I will tell you a story. A nice story. Not a scary one."
Lily smiled and said, "Okay, Tom. Tell me a story. A story about a princess and a dragon. And a happy ending."
Tom said, "Okay, Lily. I will tell you a story. A story about a princess and a dragon. And a happy ending. Once upon a time..."

Once upon a time, a little girl named Sue lived in a small house. Sue was an independent girl. She liked to do things by herself. One day, Sue found a pretty skirt in her room. The skirt had many colors and she loved it very much.
Sue wanted to examine the skirt closely, so she put it on her bed. She looked at the colors and touched the soft fabric. Then, her friend Tom came over to play. Tom saw the skirt and said, "Wow, that's a nice skirt! Can I wear it too?"
Sue thought for a moment and said, "No, Tom. This is my special skirt. I want to keep it nice and clean." Tom felt sad but he understood. They played with their toys instead.
Later, Sue wore the skirt outside to play. She was very careful, but she tripped and fell in the mud. Her beautiful skirt was dirty and ruined. Sue was very sad and cried. She wished she had shared the skirt with Tom, so they could have enjoyed it together before it got dirty.

One morning, a little girl woke up. She felt excited because she knew that today was cereal day. She jumped out of bed and ran to the kitchen. There, she saw two big, brown boxes. She knew one was filled with her favorite cereal. She quickly opened the box and looked inside. It was full of her favorite brown cereal. She smiled and grabbed a big handful. She felt so happy because she knew she could get to eat her favorite cereal. She filled her bowl and added milk. Then, she sat down and ate it. She felt so happy as she ate her cereal.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Tom. He loved to play in the tunnel near his home. One day, he saw his friend, the squirrel, stirring a big pot of soup.
"Hi, squirrel!" said Tom. "What are you doing?"
"I am stirring my soup," said the squirrel. "I am making it for lunch."
Tom felt envious. He wanted to stir the soup too. So, he asked, "Can I help you stir the soup?"
"Of course!" said the squirrel. They stirred the soup together and had a fun time.
After they finished, they ate the soup together. Tom was happy that he could help his friend. From that day on, they always played in the tunnel and stirred soup together.

One day, a little dog named Blink was playing in the park. He was very happy. Suddenly, he saw a big sign. The sign said, "No dogs in the park. It is the law." Blink did not understand what the sign meant. He felt miserable.
A little girl named Sue saw Blink and asked, "Why are you sad?" Blink told her about the sign. Sue said, "Don't worry, I will help you." They came up with a plan to hide Blink in a big box so he could still play in the park.
Sue and Blink played in the park with the box. They were having so much fun. But then, a police officer came and said, "You can't have a dog in the park. It is the law." Sue and Blink looked at each other and started to laugh. The police officer was confused. Sue opened the box and showed the officer that it was empty. Blink was hiding behind a tree the whole time. The officer laughed too and let them play in the park.

One day, a polite dog named Tim went to play near a tap. He saw a big truck with many things to load. Tim was very happy because he wanted to help load the truck.
Tim asked the truck driver, "Can I help you load the truck?" The truck driver said, "Yes, you can help me. Thank you, polite dog!" So Tim started to help load the truck. They loaded toys, food, and clothes.
But then, the truck driver was not careful. He loaded a heavy box on top of the tap. The tap broke and water went everywhere. Tim and the truck driver were sad because they could not fix the tap. The water made a big mess, and they could not finish loading the truck.

Once there was a rat who lived under the stairs. One day he became very curious and decided to explore. He crawled out of his home and spotted a big box. The rat opened the box but inside was something very serious. He was about to turn around but it was too late - the box was burning! 
He tried jumping out the box but it was no use. He was burning and it hurt. Luckily, a good samaritan saw the rat and ran over to help. He got a bucket of water and threw it on the box. The rat was free! 
The rat thanked the kind stranger and ran back under the stairs. From then on he decided to explore but be more careful - no more burning.

Once upon a time, there was a lovely lion named Leo. Leo lived in a big forest with many trees and animals. He was a friendly lion, and all the animals liked him.
One day, Leo saw a big bird in the sky. The bird could soar high and fast. Leo wanted to be friends with the bird, so he called out, "Hello, bird! Can we play together?" The bird heard Leo and came down to meet him.
They played together all day long. Leo would run and jump, and the bird would soar above him. They had so much fun and became very good friends. From that day on, the lovely lion and the soaring bird were always seen playing together in the big forest.

Once upon a time, there was a pink wagon. A little girl named Amy loved her wagon very much. She played with it every day. Amy liked to put her toys in the wagon and pull it around the yard.
One day, Amy was playing with her pink wagon when she saw a big hill. She wanted to see how fast her wagon could go down the hill. So, she put her toys in the wagon and started to push it.
As the pink wagon went down the hill, it went faster and faster. Amy ran after it, but she could not catch up. She yelled, "Stop, wagon, stop!" But the wagon did not stop. It hit a big rock at the bottom of the hill. The pink wagon broke and all the toys fell out. Amy was very sad because her favorite wagon was broken.

Once upon a time, there was a big yellow sun in the sky. It was very hot, and it made the day bright and happy. In a small house, a mommy and her little child wanted to do something fun. They decided to bake a cake for a party.
Mommy and the child put on their aprons and started to bake. They mixed flour, sugar, and eggs in a big bowl. The child liked to stir the mix with a big spoon. When the cake was ready, they put it in the oven to bake. The house smelled yummy!
Soon, friends came over for the party. They all wore yellow hats and played fun games. When the cake was ready, mommy and the child took it out of the oven. It was a big, yellow cake! Everyone at the party loved the cake, and they all had a great time.

Once upon a time, in a small village, lived a kind girl named Lily. She had a big cat named Tom. Lily loved to draw with ink. She would raise her hand and make pretty pictures. Tom liked to watch her draw.
One day, Lily wanted to draw a mild sun. She took her ink and started to draw. Tom sat next to her and said, "Meow! I want to help you draw." Lily smiled and said, "Okay, Tom. You can help me."
Lily gave Tom some ink. They both raised their hands and drew a mild sun together. The sun was happy and bright. Lily and Tom were proud of their drawing. They showed it to their friends, and everyone loved it. From that day on, Lily and Tom always drew together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to ride her bike. One day, she saw a big mountain. She wanted to ride her bike up the mountain.
Sue was an honest girl. She asked her mom if she could ride her bike up the mountain. Her mom said, "Yes, but be careful."
So, Sue rode her bike up the mountain. It was hard, but she made it to the top. She was very happy. Sue rode her bike back down and told her mom all about it. They were both proud of her.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She had a violin that she loved to play. Amy was very patient when she practiced. She would observe her fingers and make sure they were in the right place.
One day, Amy's friend Tim came to her house. "What is that?" Tim asked. "It's a violin!" said Amy. "I like to play it and make music." Tim was excited and wanted to try it too. Amy let him hold the violin and showed him how to play.
Tim was not as patient as Amy. He wanted to play the violin fast, but he did not observe his fingers. The music did not sound good. Amy showed him again how to be patient and watch his fingers. Soon, Tim was playing the violin better, and they made beautiful music together.

John and his mum were getting ready to go to school. John put on his shoes and his mum put on her coat. She then took his hand and they drove to school in their car.
When they arrived, John noticed the school was very tidy. There were no litter or leaves on the ground.
John's mum smiled and said, "John, I like that the school is so tidy. Let's keep it clean, ok?"
John smiled and said, "Ok, Mum. I will be careful."
John and his mum then walked into the school, ready for a day of learning. John was excited and couldn't wait to see all of his friends.

Once upon a time there was a dependable bear. He really liked candy. Every day he would find some candy to eat. One day he found a special kind of candy. It was brighter and sweeter than any candy he had ever tasted before. He loved it so much that the bear decided to take it home with him.
When he got home, the bear gave his candy to a little girl. The girl was so happy that she hugged the dependable bear and kissed his furry head. The bear was so happy too. Then the girl shared her special candy with the bear. They both enjoyed it very much.
The bear and the little girl became very good friends. They shared candy and hugged every day. They both knew that they could always depend on each other for hugs and kisses and candy.

Once upon a time, there was a big gym. In the gym, there were many fun things to do. There were balls to play with and ropes to climb. All the kids loved going to the gym to play.
One day, a little boy named Tim went to the gym. He saw a big, red ball that he wanted to play with. But the ball belonged to another kid named Sam. Tim asked Sam if he could borrow the ball. Sam said yes, but only if Tim promised to give it back. Tim said he would, but he was not really sorry for taking the ball.
Tim played with the ball all day, and it was so much fun. But then, something unexpected happened. The ball popped! Tim was very scared. He knew he had to tell Sam what happened, but he was afraid Sam would be mad.
When Tim told Sam about the ball, Sam was very sad. Tim felt bad, but it was too late. The ball was gone. The moral of the story is that you should always be sorry when you borrow things, and take care of them as if they were your own.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a boy named Tom. Tom had a spear. He liked to play with it. One day, Tom saw a big mess on his spear. He felt ashamed. He wanted to make it clean.
Tom went to his mom and said, "Mom, my spear is dirty. Can you help me wash it?" His mom smiled and said, "Of course, Tom! Let's wash it together."
They went to the river and washed the spear. It was now clean and shiny. Tom was happy. He thanked his mom and promised to keep his spear clean always. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a little boy named Mark went to play outside. It was a wet day, and the rain had left big puddles all over the ground. Mark loved jumping in the puddles and watching the water splash.
As he played, Mark found a small box. He decided to cover the box with a big leaf to keep it dry. He showed the box to his friend, Sally. "Look what I found!" said Mark. "Wow!" said Sally, "What's inside?"
Together, they lifted the leaf and opened the box. Inside, they found a beautiful, shiny stone. Mark and Sally were very happy with their new treasure. They took the stone home and kept it safe, always remembering their fun, wet day outside.

Once upon a time, there was a brave little cat named Kitty. Kitty lived in a small house with her best friend, a rag doll named Dolly. They loved to play together all day long.
One day, Kitty and Dolly were playing outside when they saw a big dog. The dog looked at them and said, "Woof, woof! I am so tired!" Kitty looked at Dolly and said, "Let's help the dog find a place to sleep." Dolly nodded and they all went to find a cozy spot.
After searching for a while, they found a soft bed under a tree. The dog lay down and let out a big yawn. "Thank you, Kitty and Dolly," said the dog, "I can sleep now." Kitty and Dolly smiled, happy that they could help their new friend. And from that day on, the brave little cat, the rag doll, and the big dog were the best of friends.

Little Lucy was so excited. She was out in her backyard looking at the flower garden her mom had planted. She loved how colourful it was. Suddenly, she saw something sparkling in the soil.
"Mommy, mommy, what's this?" Lucy asked while carefully pushing away the dirt around it.
"That's a mineral, sweetheart," her mom replied. "It's very special and it can make something even prettier."
Lucy started to collect the mineral and put them into a special box. "I'm going to make something lovely," she announced as she admired the different shapes and colours of the minerals.
When she was done collecting all the minerals, Lucy brought her box inside the house. Then she went back to the garden and carefully arranged the minerals between the flowers. She smiled as she stepped back and admired the results.
"It's so lovely," she said with a huge grin on her face.
Her mom nodded and smiled back. "You did a wonderful job, Lucy. I'm so proud of you. Now let's go inside and have some nice tea, okay?"
Lucy happily grabbed her mom's hand, and they went back inside the house. Little Lucy was proud of her new creation and knew that she would always love it - as if it were part of her very own flower garden.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful princess. She was going to marry a prince from another land. They both liked each other a lot. Everyone in the kingdom was happy, except for a jealous frog who lived in a pond near the castle.
One day, the frog found a hook. He wanted to use it to stop the wedding. He thought if he could catch the prince's ring with the hook, the prince would not marry the princess. The frog waited for the right time to use the hook.
On the wedding day, the frog used the hook to try and catch the ring. But, something unexpected happened. Instead of catching the ring, the hook caught a big fish. The fish jumped out of the pond and landed on the wedding cake! Everyone laughed and clapped. The frog felt silly and was not jealous anymore. The prince and princess got married and they all lived happily ever after.

One day, Tom and his mom went for a drive. They wanted to go to the museum. It was a cold day, so they wore warm coats. Tom was happy because he loved to see new things.
At the museum, they saw many things. There were big animals and pretty stones. Tom's mom said, "Look, Tom! That is a big dinosaur!" Tom was excited to see the dinosaur, but then something unexpected happened.
The dinosaur started to move! It was not a real dinosaur, but a big toy. The toy dinosaur said, "Hello, Tom and mom! I can dance!" Tom and his mom laughed and danced with the toy dinosaur. They had a fun day at the museum.

Once upon a time, there was a family who lived in a big building. They had many rooms to play and explore, but one of their favorite parts was the rooftop. The rooftop was special because it included big open spaces, lots of light and lots of room to run and play. 
One day, a strong wind blew in from the east and the family noticed that their rooftop was in danger of being damaged. So, Mama and Papa made a decision to do something to protect the rooftop. They worked hard and eventually included boards on the roof to keep the wind out. 
The whole family was celebrating their work the next day when the wind blew again and this time it was stronger. All of a sudden, the boards that were included started to make some noise and the wind stopped, just like Mama and Papa had planned. 
From that moment, the family knew that it is always important to include protection in order to keep something safe, like the rooftop. They also learned that sometimes it requires hard work, but will always be worth it in the end.

Once upon a time, in a big tree, there lived an orange monkey. The monkey loved to bounce all day long. He would bounce from one branch to another, laughing and having fun.
One day, the orange monkey met a little bird. The bird said, "Hello, monkey! Can I bounce with you?" The monkey smiled and said, "Yes, little bird! Let's bounce together!"
So, the orange monkey and the little bird bounced from branch to branch, laughing and playing. They were very happy and became the best of friends. And from that day on, they always bounced together in the big tree.

One day, Tom and his dog, Spot, found a big gray rug. They wanted to make it pretty. "Let's decorate the rug," said Tom. Spot wagged his tail.
They found red, blue, and yellow paint. Tom and Spot began to paint the rug. They made fun shapes like stars, hearts, and circles. They were very happy with their work. "Look, Spot! The rug is so pretty now!" said Tom.
Just then, the wind blew hard. It picked up the gray rug and carried it away. Tom and Spot chased after it, but they could not catch it. They were sad, but then they saw the rug land on a big tree. The tree now looked pretty, too. Tom and Spot smiled and decided to find more things to decorate.

Once upon a time, there was a shy bunny named Ben. Ben loved to eat salad. One day, he went to find some salad in the big green field. Ben saw a big pile of salad and was very happy.
As Ben started to eat the salad, he saw it move. He didn't know why the salad was moving. Ben started to struggle to eat the salad. He was scared and didn't understand what was happening.
Then, a little caterpillar named Carl came out from under the salad. Carl was eating the salad too. Ben was not scared anymore. They both laughed and ate the salad together. Ben and Carl became best friends and shared their salad every day.

Once upon a time, there were two twin flowers. They were very curious. They lived in a big garden. One day, they saw a big tree. The tree had many pretty leaves. The twin flowers wanted to have pretty leaves too.
The first twin flower said, "Let's ask the tree how to have pretty leaves." The second twin flower said, "Yes, let's go!" So, they went to the tree. The tree told them they needed to bloom to have pretty leaves. The twin flowers were happy.
The next day, the twin flowers tried to bloom. The first twin flower bloomed and had pretty leaves. But the second twin flower could not bloom. The first twin flower said, "Don't worry, we will try again tomorrow." The next day, the second twin flower bloomed too. Both twin flowers were happy and had pretty leaves.

Once upon a time, there was a little pumpkin. This pumpkin was very independent. It did not want to stay with the other pumpkins. It liked to roll around the farm all by itself. One day, the pumpkin rolled too far away from the farm.
The pumpkin got lost and felt scared. It did not know how to get back to the farm. It started to cry. A big, friendly dog heard the pumpkin cry and came to help. The dog said, "Don't be sad, little pumpkin. I will help you get back to the farm."
The dog pushed the pumpkin with its nose. They rolled back to the farm together. The pumpkin was happy to be back with the other pumpkins. It learned that it is good to be independent, but it is also important to stay close to your friends and family. The pumpkin now rolls around the farm with the dog, having fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She had a big, red door in her house. Sue liked to open and close the door all day long. One day, when she was playing with the door, she saw a small, harmless bug on the floor. The bug was green and had tiny legs.
Sue looked at the bug and said, "Hello, little bug. Do you want to be my friend?" The bug seemed to nod its head, so Sue was very happy. She picked up the bug and put it on her hand. They played together all day long.
When it was time for bed, Sue put the bug on a leaf outside the door. She said, "Goodnight, little bug. I will see you tomorrow." And every day after that, Sue and the harmless bug played together and became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a zebra named Mark. Mark was a very fast zebra. He loved to run and play with his friends in the big field.
One day, Mark and his friends had a race. They wanted to see who was the fastest. Mark ran and ran. He was so fast that he won the race. All his friends clapped and cheered for him.
After the race, Mark and his friends went to eat some yummy grass. They laughed and talked about the fun day they had. Mark was happy to be a fast zebra and have such good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a smiley boy named Tim. Tim loved to help his mom in the kitchen. One day, his mom asked him to put the dirty cups and plates in the dishwasher. Tim was very happy to help.
Tim found a big, spoiled apple by the cups and plates. He did not want the spoiled apple to make the cups and plates dirty again. So, he threw the spoiled apple in the trash. Then, he put all the cups and plates in the dishwasher.
When the dishwasher was done, Tim and his mom took out the clean cups and plates. They both smiled because the cups and plates were so clean. Tim was proud that he could help his mom and make her smile.

Once upon a time, there was a new camera. It was a very nice camera. It could take pictures of everything.
One day, the camera wanted to know how much it weighed. So, it asked its friend, the toy car, for help. The toy car said, "I can help you weigh yourself."
The toy car took the camera to a scale. The camera got on the scale and found out how much it weighed. The camera was very happy to know its weight. It thanked the toy car and they played together all day long.

Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. Today, they see a big slide. Mia wants to go on the slide, but Tom is scared.
"Come on, Tom, it is fun!" Mia says. "You can go after me."
Mia climbs up the ladder and sits on the slide. She smiles and waves at Tom. Then she pushes herself down the slide. She goes very fast and laughs.
Tom watches Mia. He thinks the slide looks fun, but he is still scared. He climbs up the ladder slowly. He sits on the slide, but he does not push himself. He holds on to the sides.
"Tom, what are you doing?" Mia asks. "You have to push yourself!"
Tom shakes his head. He does not want to push himself. He wants to go back down the ladder.
But then he sees a big dog. The dog is barking and running towards the slide. The dog looks angry and hungry. Tom is more scared of the dog than the slide.
"Help, Mia, help!" Tom cries. "There is a big dog!"
Mia sees the dog too. She is scared, but she is also wise. She knows what to do.
"Tom, listen to me!" Mia says. "You have to push yourself now! The dog cannot catch you if you go down the slide!"
Tom trusts Mia. He pushes himself down the slide. He goes very fast and feels the wind in his hair. He reaches the bottom and runs to Mia. They hug each other.
The dog stops at the slide. He cannot climb up the ladder. He cannot go down the slide. He barks and growls, but he cannot reach Tom and Mia.
Tom and Mia are safe. They are happy. They are brave.
"Thank you, Mia," Tom says. "You are a good friend. You are very wise."
"You are welcome, Tom," Mia says. "You are a good friend too. You are very brave."
They smile and hold hands. They go to play on the swings. They have fun. They are not scared of the slide or the dog anymore. They are friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big fish with a shiny scale. The fish was very bossy. He liked to tell the other fish what to do. One day, the bossy fish met a little fish. The little fish was sad.
The bossy fish said, "Why are you sad, little fish?" The little fish said, "I lost my scale. I regret not being more careful." The bossy fish wanted to help the little fish find the scale.
They looked for the scale together. They asked other fish if they saw the scale. Finally, they found the scale under a rock. The little fish was happy again. The bossy fish learned that helping others felt good. From that day on, the bossy fish was not so bossy anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. He was an ordinary boy who liked to play outside. One day, Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw many trees and flowers there. Tim loved the wind that blew through the park. It made him feel happy.
Tim saw a big, tall tree. He said to his mom, "I admire that tree, Mom! Can we go sit under it?" His mom smiled and said, "Yes, let's go sit under the tree and enjoy the wind."
They sat under the tree and felt the wind on their faces. Tim looked up at the tree and said, "I like this tree, Mom. It's nice to sit here with you." His mom hugged him and said, "I like it too, Tim. It's a special day for us." They stayed at the park all day, feeling the wind and admiring the big, tall tree.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to ride his bike. He put on his helmet and went outside. Tim was very excited to ride around the block.
As he rode his bike, he saw his friend, Sue. "Hi, Tim!" Sue said. "Do you want to race?" Tim nodded and they began to race. Tim was going very fast, but he fell off his bike. His pants got caught on the bike and showed his waist. Tim felt ashamed.
Sue stopped her bike and went to help Tim. "It's okay, Tim," she said. "We all fall sometimes." Tim smiled and they both got back on their bikes. They rode together around the block, laughing and having fun. Tim was not ashamed anymore.

Once upon a time, in a big farm, there was a turkey. The turkey was anxious. He did not have many friends. He wanted to be liked by the other animals.
One day, the turkey saw a group of animals watching a show. The animals were applauding and having fun. The turkey had an idea. He thought, "If I can make them laugh, they will like me."
The turkey put on a show for the animals. He danced and made funny faces. The animals laughed and applauded. They liked the turkey's show. The turkey was happy. He made new friends.
The moral of the story is: Be yourself and make others happy, and you will find friends who like you.

Once upon a time there lived a mighty dragon. The dragon was so mighty that it never yielded to any other creature. One day, a little rabbit ran into the dragon's cave. To the dragon's surprise, the rabbit didn't run away. Instead, it stayed and made friends with the dragon.
The dragon's heart filled with joy and it decided to yield to the rabbit and be friends with it. From then on, the two were inseparable, and the dragon would often take the rabbit on its mighty wings and go on adventures.
One day, the dragon had a surprise in store. It brought the rabbit to the top of a hill and showed it a glorious sight - a big lake, with a castle in the middle of it. The rabbit was so delighted that it jumped and clapped with joy! From then on, the dragon and the rabbit were the very best of friends, and they would often go to the lake and swim in it together.

Once upon a time there was a elderly lady who loved mysteries. She loved reading them, listening to them and even solving them. One day she was trying to solve a mystery when her little grand daughter came to visit. The little girl had an idea that would solve the mystery. The elderly lady disagreed with the idea and shook her head no. 
The little girl was upset, so she decided to solve the mystery herself. She thought and thought until a idea came to her. She repeated her idea to the elderly lady and the lady agreed this time.
The elderly lady was surprised and excited to see how the little girl could solve a mystery. They worked together and solved the mystery. They were both super happy!
The end.

One day, Gail was really excited to go outside and explore the world. She took her first steps and stumbled over a slippery rock. She looked to her right and saw a green tree. It looked very tall, and was so amazing to Gail! She stood back up and started to walk towards the tree, stumbling a few more times. 
When Gail reached the tree, she leaned against it. She took a deep breath and looked around. The leaves of the tree rustled as the wind blew by. Gail could feel the dependability of the tree and she began to feel safe.
Gail reached up and grabbed onto a branch to pull herself up a bit higher. She reached and pulled with all her might and she was able to climb onto the lowest branch! Gail was very proud of herself and the dependable green tree.
Gail spent the whole afternoon playing in the tree and when it was time to go inside, she smiled and gave the tree a kiss. She thanked it for being so dependable and helping her to have such a great day!

Once there was a girl named Sue. Sue liked to play with her toy boat. She would rock the boat in the water, and it made her very happy. One day, Sue saw a big rock in the water. She thought it would be fun to put her boat on the rock.
"Boat, you will be safe on the rock," Sue said. She put the boat on the rock and went to play with her ball. But when she came back, the boat was gone! Sue was sad and looked for her boat. She saw a clean spot on the rock where her boat was. 
Sue looked and looked, and then she saw something. Her boat was with a new friend, a frog! The frog had jumped on the rock and pushed the boat back into the water. Sue was happy to see her boat again, and now she had a new friend to play with.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to dance. She would dance all day and all night. One day, Mia met a big, fierce lion in the woods. The lion looked very scary, but Mia was not afraid.
"Hello, Mr. Lion," said Mia. "Do you want to dance with me?" The fierce lion looked at Mia and said, "Yes, I would love to dance, but I have a big rub on my foot. It hurts to walk and dance."
Mia felt sad for the lion. She had an idea. "I can help you, Mr. Lion. Let me see your foot." The lion showed Mia his foot, and she saw the big rub. Mia went to find a soft leaf and gently put it on the lion's rub. The lion felt much better.
"Thank you, Mia," said the lion. "Now we can dance together!" Mia and the fierce lion danced and danced. They became best friends. And from that day on, they danced together in the woods every day, showing that even the fiercest creatures can be gentle and kind.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She had a toy car that she loved to play with all the time. Amy was very stubborn and did not like to share her toys with others.
One day, Amy's friend Tom came over to play. Tom saw the toy car and asked, "Can you lend me your car, please?" Amy thought for a moment and said, "Okay, but only for a short time." Tom was very happy and played with the car carefully.
After some time, Tom gave the car back to Amy. They both had fun playing together. From that day on, Amy learned to share her toys with her friends. The moral of the story is that sharing can make playtime more fun for everyone.

Once upon a time, there was a little green frog. The frog lived a happy life by a big pond. The pond was his home, and he had many friends there. They played together every day.
One day, the little green frog saw something new in the pond. It was a big leaf, but there was something under it. The frog was very curious. He swam closer and closer to the leaf. He wanted to reveal what was hiding there.
When the little green frog moved the leaf, he found a tiny fish! The fish was scared at first, but the frog was nice. They became good friends and played together in the pond. The frog's life was even happier now that he had a new friend.

One day, a boy named Tim found a monkey. The monkey was very funny. Tim liked the monkey a lot. They played together all day. Tim wanted to keep the monkey, so he attached a leash to him. The leash was expensive, but Tim didn't care.
"Monkey, will you be my friend?" Tim asked. The monkey smiled and nodded.
They went to a park. There, they saw a man. The man looked at the monkey and was very surprised. He said, "That's my monkey! I lost him a long time ago."
Tim was sad. He didn't want to give the monkey back. But he knew it was the right thing to do. The man was very happy to see his monkey again. He thanked Tim and gave him a big hug.
