As a thank you, the man gave Tim a gift. It was a toy monkey. It wasn't as fun as the real monkey, but Tim liked it. He learned that sometimes, doing the right thing can be hard, but it's always worth it.

Once upon a time, in a bright, sunny land, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big mind and loved to think. One day, Tim wanted to have success in making a big, tall tower with his toy blocks. He worked hard, placing one block on top of another.
As Tim was building, his friend Sue came over. "What are you doing?" Sue asked. "I am making a tower," Tim said. "I want to have success and make it very tall." Sue wanted to help Tim, so they worked together. The tower grew taller and taller.
Suddenly, a little dog named Spot ran into the room. Spot was excited and wagged his tail. He didn't mind the tower and bumped into it. The tower fell down with a big crash! Tim and Sue looked at each other and laughed. They didn't mind starting all over again. Together, they began to build a new tower, even bigger and better than before.

One day, a little girl named Sue went to the park to play. She saw a big tree with many colors on it. Sue was very happy and wanted to see the colors up close. She was polite and asked her mommy if she could go to the tree. Her mommy said yes.
Sue walked to the tree and saw that the colors were birds. There were red birds, blue birds, and yellow birds. Sue wanted to say hi to the birds. She yelled, "Hello, birds!" But the birds did not say anything back. Sue was sad.
Then, something unexpected happened. The tree started to shake and the birds flew away. Sue was surprised to see that the tree was not a tree at all. It was a big, green giant! The giant smiled at Sue and said, "Hello, little girl. I am a friendly giant who loves colors too." Sue was not scared anymore and they became friends.

One day, a little girl named Lily went outside to play. It was a wet day, and there were many puddles on the ground. Lily liked to jump in the puddles and watch the water splash. She wore her red boots and smiled as she played.
Lily saw a small vehicle on the ground. It was a toy car that her friend Tom had left in her yard. The car was wet from the rain. Lily picked up the car and dried it with her hands.
Lily took the vehicle to Tom's house. Tom was happy to see his toy car again. He smiled and thanked Lily for finding it. They played with the car together, racing it through the puddles. They both had a fun, wet day.

One day, a bird sat on a branch. The bird saw a cat near the tree. The bird was alert. The bird said to the cat, "I warn you, don't come up the tree!"
The cat did not listen. The cat climbed the tree fast. The bird flew away. The cat was sad. The cat wanted to be friends with the bird.
Then, something unexpected happened. The branch broke! The cat fell down, but it was okay. The bird came back and said, "I am sorry. I did not know you wanted to be friends." The cat and the bird became friends and played together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a loyal dog named Max. They loved to play together in the sun.
One day, Tim and Max found a lime tree. They saw many green limes on the tree. Tim wanted to learn how to pick the limes. Max helped him by jumping up and down.
Tim learned to pick the limes with Max's help. They had a fun day and shared the limes with their friends. Tim and Max were happy and went home to rest.

Mary was looking out of the window and saw the strong sunrise. She smiled and grabbed a spoon from the kitchen. She ran outside and started to stir. She stirred and stirred, as the bright sunrise lit up the sky. She stirred and stirred until the sky was bright. Mary looked up and said, "Beautiful!" The strong sunrise was so beautiful, Mary wanted to keep looking up at it forever. She wanted to remember this sunrise forever.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely zebra named Zara. Zara lived in a big forest, but she had no friends to play with. Every day, she would walk around the forest, looking for someone to be her friend.
One sunny day, Zara saw a group of animals playing together. They were laughing and having fun. Zara wanted to join them, but she was too shy. She watched from far away, wishing she could be a part of their fun.
A kind little monkey named Momo saw Zara watching them. Momo walked over to her and said, "Hi, Zara! Would you like to play with us? We would love to welcome you to our group!" Zara felt so happy that someone wanted to be her friend. She joined the group, and they all played together.
Zara learned that it's important to be kind and welcoming to others. She also learned that even if you feel lonely, there are always friends waiting to welcome you with open arms. And from that day on, Zara was never lonely again.

Once upon a time there was a robot called Bob. Bob was a friendly robot and he always said hello to everyone he met. One day, Bob saw a peaceful meadow and decided to lay down in the grass. While he was there, he heard a voice.
"Hi Bob," said the voice.
Bob looked up and saw a little girl. She was smiling at him.
"What are you doing here?" asked the girl.
"I'm just enjoying the peacefulness of this meadow," Bob replied. 
The little girl smiled again. "It is very peaceful here," she said. 
Bob and the little girl talked for a while before Bob said goodbye and walked away. As he went, he felt peaceful and content. Bob had made a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sally. She loved to help her mom in the kitchen. One day, her mom made some incredible jam. Sally wanted to spread the jam on her toast all by herself.
But Sally was not feeling well. She had a little cold. Her mom gave her a pill to help her feel better. Sally didn't like the taste, but she knew it would help her get well.
Sally learned that sometimes, we have to do things we don't like to feel better. She also learned that spreading love and care, just like she did with the jam, can make everyone feel incredible.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a little fox. The little fox was very impressive. He could run fast, jump high, and do many fun things. One day, he met a big bear.
The big bear said, "Hey, little fox! Can you bow to me?" The little fox thought about it and then bowed to the big bear.
The big bear was not happy. He wanted the little fox to bow lower. The little fox tried to bow lower, but he could not. The big bear got mad and chased the little fox.
The little fox ran and ran, but the big bear was too fast. In the end, the big bear caught the little fox. The little fox was very sad and the big bear was still mad. And that is the end of the story.

Once upon a time, in a peaceful little town, there was a girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to play with her mom's makeup. One day, when mom was away, Lucy found the makeup box and started to play.
Lucy put makeup on her face and felt like a big girl. She saw her cat, Mr. Whiskers, and had an idea. Lucy thought it would be fun to put makeup on Mr. Whiskers too. She put makeup on the cat, but Mr. Whiskers did not like it. He started to tremble because he was scared.
Lucy did not know that the makeup was making Mr. Whiskers feel bad. Later, when mom came home, she saw what Lucy had done. She told Lucy that it was not safe to put makeup on pets. Sadly, Mr. Whiskers got sick from the makeup and went to sleep forever. Lucy felt very sad and learned her lesson.

Anna likes to read books. She likes to learn new words. She has a big book with many words and pictures. She likes to pick a word and say it out loud. She thinks it is fun.
One day, she is reading her book in the living room. She sees a word she does not know. It has four letters. It starts with F and ends with K. She thinks it is a funny word. She wants to say it out loud. She picks the word and says it very loud.
"Look, Mommy, I know a new word! F-K!"
Mommy hears Anna and drops her cup. She is very surprised. She runs to the living room and sees Anna with her book. She sees the word Anna said. She is very angry.
"Anna, that is a bad word! You should not say that word! That word is rude and mean. Where did you learn that word?"
Anna does not understand. She is very confused. She points to her book and says, "I learned it from my book. It is a word. See?"
Mommy looks at the book and sees the word. She sees that it is not a word. It is a mistake. The book has a typo. The word should be F-O-R-K, not F-K. It is a picture of a fork, not a bad word. Mommy feels very embarrassed. She made a big fuss over nothing.
She hugs Anna and says, "I'm sorry, Anna. You did not do anything wrong. The book is wrong. That is not a word. That is a mistake. The word is fork, not F-K. Fork is a thing you use to eat. Can you say fork?"
Anna nods and says, "Fork. Fork. Fork."
Mommy smiles and says, "That's right. Fork. You are a smart girl. You know many words. But some words are not nice. Some words are bad. You should not say bad words. Do you understand?"
Anna nods and says, "I understand. I will not say bad words. I will say nice words. Like fork. And book. And Mommy."
Mommy kisses Anna and says, "I love you, Anna. You are a good girl. And I love your book. But maybe we should get a new one. One without mistakes."

Once upon a time, there was a little fish named Fin. Fin lived in the big sea with his family. They loved to play and swim together. One day, Fin saw a big wave coming. He was scared, but he knew what to do.
Fin went to his friend, the crab. He asked the crab if he could borrow his strong shell. The crab said yes, and gave Fin his shell. Fin put on the shell and felt safe. The big wave came, but Fin was not scared anymore.
The big wave passed, and Fin was okay. He said thank you to the crab for letting him borrow the shell. Then, Fin went back to his family. They were all happy and safe. They played and swam together again.

Once upon a time, in a gloomy town, there was a big steel box. The big steel box was hard and cold. Many people in the town did not like the big steel box because it made them feel sad.
One day, a small girl named Lily found the big steel box. She wanted to change it and make the town happy. Lily had an idea. She went home and got her paint and brush. She started to paint the big steel box with bright colors.
As she painted, something unexpected happened. The big steel box started to shake and move. It changed into a big, friendly robot! The robot thanked Lily for changing him. Now, the town was not gloomy anymore. The people loved the robot, and they were all happy.

Once upon a time, there was an igloo. In the igloo, there lived a happy little boy named Tim and a small, angry cat named Max. They liked to play together every day.
One day, Tim found a magic toy. He showed it to Max and said, "Look, Max! Let's play with this toy!" But when they played with the toy, it made Max shrink! Max became very tiny, and he was not happy.
Tim wanted to help Max. He said, "Don't worry, Max. I will make you big again." Tim found another magic toy, and it made Max grow back to his normal size. Max was happy and not angry anymore. They played together in the igloo and lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there was a very big spider, it was an enormous spider. He was walking around, looking for something to eat. All of a sudden he saw a small girl playing with her toys. She was so happy and having fun.
The spider became quite curious, he slowly crept closer, getting ready to pounce if necessary. He creeped and snuck until he was very close to her.
The girl saw the spider and became scared. She started to clap her hands loudly to scare the spider away, but the spider wouldn't move. He just kept staring at her.
Suddenly her brother came running into the room. He had heard his sister clapping, and quickly noticed the huge spider. He ran closer to her, scooped her up and looked at the spider with a stern expression.
The spider became very scared. He quickly ran away and they never saw him again. The sister rejoiced and clapped her hands because she had been saved by her brother. She was no longer scared of the enormous spider.

Once upon a time, there was a big sea. The sea had a tide that would come and go. One day, the tide went out and left a big hole in the sand. A little boy named Tim saw the hole and wanted to fill it up.
Tim found a small bucket and began to fill it with wet sand. He poured the sand into the hole. He did this many times, but the hole was still not full. The sand was rich and dark, and Tim liked how it felt in his hands.
As Tim kept filling the hole, the tide began to come back in. The water slowly filled the hole with Tim's rich, wet sand. Tim watched and smiled as the water and sand mixed together. He knew he had helped the sea fill the hole, and he felt happy.

Once upon a time, there were two friends - a rabbit and a fox. They liked to spend time together and have fun.
One day, the fox said to the rabbit, "Let's go and explore the underground!" The rabbit was excited and the two of them set off together.
When they reached the underground, the rabbit and fox were amazed by what they saw. They saw all sorts of useful things, like tunnels and burrows, which were perfect for hiding. 
The two friends had a great time exploring the underground. After a while, they both decided that it was time to leave. 
Just before they were about to go, the rabbit stopped and said to the fox, "Let's meet here tomorrow and explore the underground again!" The fox thought this was a great idea and the two of them agreed to meet there the next day. 
The fox and the rabbit had lots of fun exploring the underground. And, with the useful things they found, they became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a light brown horse named Happy. He lived in a big field with his friend, a little girl named Lily. They loved to play together every day.
One day, Happy and Lily had a small fight. Happy wanted to run fast, but Lily wanted to play hide and seek. "I want to run!" said Happy. "No, I want to hide!" said Lily. They were both very sad.
Then, Happy's mom came and reminded them, "You are best friends. Friends don't fight." Happy and Lily looked at each other and said sorry. They decided to play both games. First, they ran fast in the field. Then, they played hide and seek. They were happy again and played together all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was very filthy because he loved to play in the mud. His mom always told him to stay clean, but he just couldn't help it. The mud was too much fun!
One day, Spot's mom mentioned that they were going to a big party. Spot was so excited! He knew he had to get clean for the party. So, he jumped in a big tub of water and got all the mud off. He was now a clean and shiny dog.
At the start of the party, all the other dogs were amazed at how clean Spot was. They played games, ate yummy treats, and had lots of fun. Spot was so happy that he listened to his mom and got clean for the party. And from that day on, he tried his best not to get too filthy.

One day, a boy named Tim went to play golf with his dad. Tim was a little clumsy, but he loved to play. They took their golf sticks and went to the big green field.
Tim's dad hit the ball very far. "Wow, dad! You're so good!" said Tim. Then it was Tim's turn. He swung the golf stick, but he was so clumsy that he missed the ball. Tim felt sad.
Suddenly, a big wind came and moved the ball. The ball rolled and rolled, and then it fell into the hole! Tim and his dad were so surprised. "Good job, Tim!" said his dad. They both laughed and kept playing golf, happy that something unexpected had happened.

Once upon a time, in a cold, wild forest, there lived a little bunny named Binky. Binky loved to hop and play with his friends. One day, while Binky was playing, he saw his friend, a tiny bird, stuck in a tall tree.
"Oh no!" said the tiny bird, "I can't fly down. Help me, please!" Binky wanted to save his friend. He looked around and saw a big bear named Benny. Binky hopped over to Benny and asked, "Can you help save my friend, the tiny bird?"
Benny, who was kind and strong, agreed to help. He stood up tall and reached the tiny bird in the tree. Benny saved the tiny bird, and they all became good friends. The moral of the story is that we should always help our friends in need, no matter how big or small the problem may be.

Once upon a time, there was a child. His name was John. One day, he was playing with toys in the living room when he decided to explore the house. He got up and walked to the hallway. There was a staircase and at the top of the stairs was a door with a handle. It looked unknown to John. He got really curious and went closer to it. He grabbed the handle, turned it and opened the door. He had never seen the room before! John climbed the stair and entered the mysterious room. Inside the room there was a table and a chair, a toy chest and a window. He started to play with the toys and he was having a great time.
Suddenly, he heard his mom calling his name. "John! Come down the stairs!" John left the toy chest and went down the stairs. He heard his mom again. She said, "I told you not to go up to that unknown room!" John felt a little scared but he was happy he was able to explore something new.

One day, a blue bird named Bob was sitting in a tree. He saw big, dark clouds in the sky. Bob knew that rain was coming soon. He wanted to find a friend to play with before the rain started.
Bob flew down and saw a little girl named Sue. She was sad because she had no one to play with. Bob said, "Hi Sue, let's play together and smile before the rain comes." Sue looked up and saw the blue bird. She smiled and said, "Yes, let's play!"
They played and laughed under the tree. Soon, the rain came down. But they were happy because they found a friend to play with. They smiled at each other and knew they would have more fun in the rain together.

Once upon a time, there was a large bear named Ben. Ben lived in a big forest with his friends. One day, Ben had an idea. He wanted to have a party. He thought it would be fun to invite all his friends.
Ben started to plan the party. He made a list of all his friends to invite. He asked his friend, Cat, to help him. Cat was very happy to help. Together, they sent out invitations to all their friends.
On the day of the party, all the friends came. They played games, ate yummy food, and had a great time. Everyone was happy and thanked Ben for inviting them. The party was a big success.
The moral of the story is that it is good to share fun times with friends. When we invite others to join us, we can make everyone happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to sleep. She had a very nice aunt who was loyal and kind. Mia and her aunt lived in a small house near the woods.
One day, Mia and her aunt went for a walk in the woods. They saw a big, scary dog. The dog was angry and barked at them. Mia's aunt told her to stay close. Mia was scared, but she knew her aunt would keep her safe.
Mia and her aunt walked away from the dog. They went back to their house and went inside. Mia's aunt locked the door. The dog tried to get in, but he couldn't. Mia and her aunt were safe.
That night, Mia and her aunt went to sleep. They were happy to be together and safe. The next day, Mia's aunt taught her to be brave and not to be scared of dogs. Mia learned that it's important to trust the people who love you and to be loyal to them.

Once upon a time, there was a sailor. The sailor lived near the sea. He had a modern boat. He loved to go out and sail on the water.
One day, the sailor heard a ring. It was a bell on his boat. The bell told him it was time to sail. He put on his hat and went to his boat.
The sailor sailed on the water. He saw fish, birds, and big waves. He had fun on his modern boat. The sun set, and the sailor went home. He was happy and tired from his day of sailing.

Sara and Tom are playing in the park. They see a big pile of mud. Sara wants to jump in it. She urges Tom to join her.
"Come on, Tom, it will be fun!" she says. "We can make mud pies and mud castles."
Tom is not sure. He knows his mom will be angry if he gets filthy. He looks at his clean clothes and shoes.
"But Sara, we will be very dirty," he says. "Mom will be mad. She will make us take a bath."
Sara does not care. She thinks mud is fun. She runs to the pile and jumps in. She splashes and laughs. She makes a mud pie and throws it at Tom.
"Ha ha, Tom, catch!" she says.
Tom dodges the mud pie. He does not want to play with Sara. He thinks she is silly. He walks away from the pile. He looks for a clean place to sit.
Sara is sad. She wants Tom to play with her. She does not understand why he is mad. She thinks he is boring. She stays in the mud. She makes more mud pies and castles.
Soon, their moms come to pick them up. They see Sara and Tom. They are shocked. Sara is filthy. Tom is clean.
"What happened here?" Sara's mom asks. "Sara, you are a mess!"
"I was playing in the mud, mom," Sara says. "It was fun."
Tom's mom looks at Tom. She is proud. Tom is clean.
"Good job, Tom," she says. "You stayed out of the mud."
Sara and Tom look at each other. They do not smile. They do not say anything. They go home with their moms. The result is that they are not friends anymore.

Once upon a time, there lived a grumpy little girl. She was always too tired to help her parents with chores. One day, her mom asked her to carry a jar of sauce from the store. The girl didn't want to help and she said no.
The next day, the mom asked the girl to carry one more jar of sauce. The girl still didn't want to help but her mom said it was important. So the grumpy girl carried the jar back home.
Once the girl was done carrying the jars of sauce, she saw how happy her mom was. She realized that it felt good to help and make others smile. So from that day on, she helped her mom every day with her chores and her grumpiness disappeared.
The moral of the story is that helping others makes us happy and it can even get rid of our grumpiness.

Ricky was very chubby and he loved to explore. Whenever his mom wasn't looking, he would run around the house, playing and discovering. But this time, Ricky had spotted something unusual: a trunk, sitting in the corner. It was a big, brown trunk and it was full of secrets!
Ricky decided he needed to test it. He hopped over and lifted the trunk, but it was too heavy. He asked his mom to help, and together they opened the trunk. Inside was an exciting surprise! It was full of toys - soft blocks, a spinning top, and a stuffed animal.
Ricky was so excited that he started to squeal. He picked out the stuffed animal and hugged it close. He thanked his mom for helping him test the trunk, then began to play with his new toy. Ricky was thankful to have such a fun surprise, and now he was ready to explore even more!

One day, a little dog named Max went for a walk in the park. Max was a regular dog, with soft fur and a wagging tail. He liked to run and play with his friends.
While playing, Max heard a sound. It was a buzzing sound. He looked up and saw a big, round, yellow bee. The bee flew close to Max and said, "Hello, I am Bee. Let's be friends!" Max was happy to have a new friend.
Max and Bee played together in the park. They ran and played hide and seek. But then, Max accidentally bit Bee. "Ouch!" cried Bee. "I am sorry," said Max. "I did not mean to bite you." Bee forgave Max and they continued to play. They were great friends and had lots of fun together.

One day, a big, smelly dog went into an office. The dog saw a man sitting at a desk. The dog went up to the man and said, "Give me a treat!" The man looked at the dog and said, "I don't have any treats."
The dog was sad, but then he had an idea. He decided to demand treats from the man. The dog said, "Give me treats or I will make a big mess in your office!" The man looked scared, but then he smiled. He had a plan.
The man said, "Okay, I will give you a treat, but first, I have to go to the store to buy some." The dog agreed and waited for the man to come back. But when the man came back, he had a big surprise. He brought a cat! The dog was so scared of the cat that he ran away and never came back to the office again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom who loved to eat oats. One day, Tom's mom made a big bowl of thick, warm oats for breakfast. Tom was very happy because he liked thick oats the best.
Tom wanted to share his oats with his friend, Sue. So, he went to Sue's house and asked, "Do you want to eat oats with me?" Sue said, "Yes, I like oats too!" They both sat down and ate the thick oats together.
After they finished, Tom and Sue played outside. They had so much fun that they forgot about the time. When it was time for dinner, they were very hungry. They asked their moms for more thick oats. Their moms said yes, and they all had a big dinner with lots of thick oats. Tom and Sue were very happy.

One day, a little girl named Amy found a big, interesting box. She wanted to know what was inside. So, she opened the box and saw many small cards. Each card had a word on it. Amy thought it would be fun to play with the cards.
Amy started to load the cards into her toy truck. She wanted to show her friends the words. As she was loading the cards, a small bird flew into her room. The bird saw the cards and thought they were food. The bird picked up a card and flew away.
Amy was sad that the bird took her card. But then, something unexpected happened. The bird came back with more friends. All the birds started to bring her more cards with words on them. Now, Amy had even more words to play with and share with her friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved to play with her blocks. She would build tall towers and big houses. But sometimes, she would get frustrated when the blocks fell down.
One day, Lucy saw a little ant on the floor. The ant was trying to escape from her room. Lucy wanted to help the ant, so she used her blocks to make a path for the ant to follow.
The ant followed the path of blocks, and soon it was near the door. Lucy opened the door, and the ant was able to escape outside. Lucy felt happy that she helped the ant. She went back to playing with her blocks, feeling proud of her good deed.

Once upon a time, there was a scared cat named Tom. Tom loved to eat tuna. One day, he met a pretty cat named Tina. Tina also loved to eat tuna. Tom and Tina became very good friends.
One day, Tom said to Tina, "I love tuna and I love you. Let's marry and eat tuna together every day." Tina was very happy and said, "Yes, I want to marry you, Tom!"
They had a big party with all their friends. Tom and Tina were not scared anymore. They ate tuna and lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Amy loved to play outside. One day, she saw a big, sharp rock near her house. She wanted to touch it, but her mom said, "No, Amy! That rock is sharp. It can hurt you."
Amy was sad. She wanted to play with the rock, but she knew her mom was right. So, she went to play with her toys inside the house. She saw that her toys were covered in dust. "Mom, my toys are dirty," Amy said. Her mom told her, "Amy, you should clean your toys. It is good to keep them clean."
Amy used a cloth to clean the dust off her toys. She played with her clean toys and felt happy. Later, she went outside and saw the sharp rock again. She remembered what her mom said and didn't touch it. Instead, she played with her clean toys in the yard.
One day, her friend Tom came to play with her. Tom saw the sharp rock and wanted to touch it. Amy told him, "No, Tom! That rock is sharp. It can hurt you. Let's play with my clean toys instead." Tom listened to Amy and they played together with her toys.
Amy learned that it is good to listen to her mom and keep her toys clean. She also learned to help her friends stay safe by not touching sharp things. And they all played happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a caterpillar. The caterpillar was very wealthy. He had many leaves to eat. One day, he went on a walk to find more leaves.
On his walk, he met a little girl. The girl liked to clap. She clapped when she was happy. The caterpillar wanted to make the girl happy. So, he gave her some of his leaves.
The girl was very happy. She clapped and clapped. But then, she wanted more leaves. The caterpillar gave her more leaves, but soon he had no more leaves left. The caterpillar was sad, and the girl was not happy anymore.

One day, a cool cat named Tom went to the park. He saw a quick dog named Max. Tom wanted to be friends with Max. So, Tom went to Max to introduce himself.
"Hi, I'm Tom," said the cool cat. "I'm Max," said the quick dog. They played and had fun. They ran and jumped in the park. They were happy to meet each other.
Soon, it was time to go home. Tom and Max said goodbye. They knew they would see each other again. The cool cat and the quick dog were now best friends.

Once upon a time there was a lady. She was very cold because it was wintertime. She needed to find a place to stay so she could stay warm. She looked all around and finally saw a house. The lady walked closer to the house and saw that the door was locked. She knocked on the door and waited, and waited. Finally, the door opened and the lady smiled. A man and a little girl were standing at the door. They let the lady come inside to stay with them and get warm. She was so happy. She spent the night with them and in the morning she thanked them for their kindness. Now she was not so cold anymore!

One day, a little girl named Mia went to the theater with her mom. They were excited to see a show with singing and dancing. Mia clapped her hands and jumped up and down.
In the show, there was a helpless kitten stuck in a tree. The kitten cried and cried. A kind boy named Tom heard the kitten and said, "Don't worry, little kitten. I will help you." Tom closed his eyes and began to pray for help to save the kitten.
As Tom prayed, a big, strong bird came and carried the kitten down from the tree. The kitten was safe! Everyone in the theater clapped and cheered. Mia hugged her mom and said, "I'm so happy the kitten is okay!"

One day, a pilot named Tom went to the park. He saw a big tree with delicious red apples. Tom wanted to eat the apples, but they were too high up. So, he had an idea. He would use a stick to cut the apples down.
Tom found a long stick and tried to cut the apples. He hit the tree, but nothing happened. Then, a little bird came by and said, "I can help you get the apples." Tom was happy and said, "Yes, please help me!"
The little bird flew up and cut the apples with its beak. The apples fell down, and Tom picked them up. But when he looked at the apples, they were not real apples! They were made of paper! Tom was surprised and laughed. The little bird laughed too, and they both played with the paper apples in the park.

One day, a little robot went for a wander. The robot liked to walk and see new things. The sun was bright and the sky was blue. The robot was happy.
As the robot walked, it saw a big tree. The robot wanted to climb the tree. But, when the robot tried to climb, it fell down. The robot felt ashamed. It was sad that it could not climb the tree.
Then, something unexpected happened. A little bird flew down from the tree. The bird said, "Don't be ashamed, robot. You can't climb, but you can do other things." The robot smiled and walked with the bird. Together, they found new things to do and the robot was happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big, red bag. He loved his bag very much. He took it with him everywhere he went. One day, Tim went to the park with his bag.
At the park, Tim saw a big tree with a nice shade. He wanted to sit under the tree with his bag. But when he got there, he saw a small, sad girl sitting alone. She was crying very loud.
Tim wanted to help the sad girl. He thought for a moment and then opened his big, red bag. Inside, he found a toy. He gave the toy to the girl, and she stopped crying. They both sat under the tree and played with the toy. They became good friends.
The moral of the story is to be kind and share with others.

Bob was being naughty. He had been told many times that he should not pack his station. He just couldn't seem to help himself though. He wanted to make sure he was always ready for a surprise adventure.
One day, Bob's mom caught him with a big bag full of stuff. She said in a stern voice, "Bob, what are you doing with all this stuff? I told you not to pack your station."
Bob tried to be brave and said, "But I wanted to be ready for an adventure. I don't want to miss out on anything fun!"
His mom sighed and said, "Oh, Bob. You are being so naughty today. But I understand why you wanted to be prepared. Let's go on a special surprise adventure right now, if you would like?"
Bob was so excited. He smiled and nodded his head. He quickly got ready and they were on the adventure in no time. From then on he learned not to pack his station and only take the things he needed.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a noisy little dog named Spot. Spot loved to sing and bark all day long. He had a toy microphone that he played with all the time. One day, Spot could not find his microphone. He looked everywhere and started to miss his favorite toy.
Spot asked his friend, Cat, "Have you seen my microphone?" Cat shook her head and said, "No, I have not seen it. Let's look together." So, Spot and Cat searched the house, looking under the bed and behind the sofa. But they still could not find the microphone.
Finally, they went to the garden to look for it. There, they found the microphone under a big tree. Spot was so happy! He picked up the microphone and sang a loud, happy song. Cat laughed and joined in the noisy fun. From that day on, Spot and Cat always played with the microphone together, and they never missed it again.

One day, a lazy cat named Tom was lying under a big tree. He saw a little bird with a gift in its beak. The bird was trying to open the gift, but it was too hard for her. Tom went to the bird and said, "Can I help you open the gift?"
The bird was happy and said, "Yes, please!" Tom used his strong teeth to open the gift. Inside the gift, there was a shiny stone. The bird said, "Thank you, Tom! This is a very special stone. It will help us find food when we are hungry."
Tom and the bird became friends. They played and looked for food together. They learned to value each other's help. The bird taught Tom to fly high, and Tom taught the bird how to run fast. They were very happy and had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a big bear named Bob. Bob was a serious bear who did not like to be disturbed. One day, while Bob was taking a nap, he heard a loud sound. It was the sound of lumber being cut by a man with a big saw.
Bob woke up and said, "Who is making that loud sound? I do not like to be disturbed when I am napping!" He went to see who was making the noise. As he walked through the forest, he thought about what he would say to the person who was disturbing him.
Finally, Bob found the man cutting the lumber. He said to the man, "Please stop making that loud sound. You are disturbing my nap!" The man looked at Bob and said, "I am sorry, Bob. I did not know I was disturbing you. I will stop cutting lumber now."
Bob was happy that the man listened to him. He went back to his nap, and the forest was quiet again. From that day on, the man never disturbed Bob's nap, and they became good friends.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to drink milk every day. One day, when she opened the fridge, she saw that there was no milk left. Mia was sad and did not know what to do.
Mia's mommy saw her sad face and asked, "Why are you sad, Mia?" Mia said, "There is no milk, and I am thirsty." Mommy smiled and said, "Don't worry, Mia. I will make milk appear!"
Mia was curious and watched her mommy. Mommy took a big cup, poured some white powder into it, and added water. Mia did not know what it was, but she was happy to see that it looked like milk. She felt ignorant because she did not know how her mommy made the milk appear.
Mia drank the milk and felt happy again. She thanked her mommy and learned that the white powder was called "milk powder." Now, Mia knew how to make milk appear too! From that day on, Mia and her mommy always had milk to drink, and they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a King and Queen. Every morning, the King and Queen had breakfast together in their castle. The King always loved his toast; it was thin and golden brown. 
One morning, the Queen said to the King, "My dearest, today I shall make you the most special breakfast. You will be served a crown!"
The King was so excited. He had never been served a crown before. 
The Queen then made a very special toast. She used her special butter and jam to make a tiny toasted crown. She carefully cut it into thin slices and presented it to the King.
The King was very impressed with the small crown. He thanked the Queen and enjoyed his breakfast. 
From then on, the King and Queen always had a crown toast served at their breakfast table. They both had a lot of fun eating their crown toast.

One day, a child named Tim went to play outside. He liked to play in the mud. His mom said, "Tim, stay clean today!" Tim said, "Okay, mom."
Tim played with his ball and his toy car. Then, he saw a big mud puddle. He wanted to play in it, but he remembered what his mom said. He tried to stay clean, but his ball went into the mud. Tim tried to get the ball, but he fell in the mud too!
Now Tim was not clean. He was sad. Then, the sun came out and Tim had an idea. He sat in the sun and let the sun dry him. Soon, the mud was dry and Tim brushed it off. He was clean again!
Tim went back to his mom and said, "Look, mom! I am clean!" His mom was happy and surprised. She said, "Good job, Tim! I am proud of you!" And they went inside to have a yummy snack.

One day, a cute little cat named Mimi was out on a walk. She saw her friend, a big dog named Bobo. Bobo was sad. Mimi asked, "Why are you sad, Bobo?"
Bobo said, "I want to go to the march, but my mom said I have to leave soon. I don't want to leave. I want to stay and play." Mimi thought for a moment. She wanted to help her friend.
Mimi had an idea. She said, "Let's play a quick game before you leave. That way, you can have fun and still go to the march." Bobo smiled. They played a fun game of catch. Soon, it was time for Bobo to leave for the march. He was happy now, thanks to Mimi.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to her friend Tim's house to play. They had lots of fun playing with their toys. While they were playing, Lily noticed that her hair was messy. She asked Tim, "Can I borrow your comb, please?"
Tim looked around and found his blue comb. He gave it to Lily and said, "Here you go! Be careful with it, it's my favorite comb." Lily used the comb to fix her hair and make it look nice again. But, as she was combing, the comb broke! Lily felt very sorry and sad.
Lily gave the broken comb back to Tim and said, "I'm so sorry, Tim. I didn't mean to break your comb." Tim looked at the broken comb and then at Lily. He could see that she was really sorry. Tim smiled and said, "It's okay, Lily. We can get a new comb together." They both went to the store and picked out a new comb, and they continued to have a great day playing together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She liked to play outside in her yard. One day, she went out to play and find something new.
While playing, Lucy saw a red brick in the grass. The brick was little and heavy. She picked it up and decided to make it her friend. She named the brick "Bricky" and took it everywhere with her.
Lucy and Bricky played all day. They had fun in the sun and made a little house for Bricky with more bricks. At the end of the day, Lucy took Bricky to her room and they slept together. They were very happy and had many more fun days together.

Once upon a time there was a little boy who lived at the top of a big hill. Every day he would look down at the people who lived at the bottom of the hill. He thought they were so funny and he wished that he could join in their games.
One day, he decided to work his way down the hill and see what it was like to play with the people he'd been observing. When he arrived, he noticed that everyone was doing something unusual. He asked one of the little kids what they were doing, and she told him that they were working on a project together.
The little boy was so excited that he decided to join in and help out. He worked with all his might and soon he was able to help the other kids complete their project. When it was finished, the kids celebrated and thanked the little boy for his help.
The moral of this story is that when you work together, you can accomplish great things!

One day, a little cat named Mimi found a blue hat. She liked the hat very much. Mimi wore the hat all the time. She told her friend, a dog named Spot, "I will wear my blue hat when we play."
Mimi and Spot went to the park to play. They played with a big ball. They were very happy. Mimi's blue hat was too big for her. She had to stretch it to make it fit. Spot said, "Be careful with your hat, Mimi."
One day, Mimi and Spot were playing in the park. The wind blew very hard. Mimi's blue hat flew away. She was very sad. Spot said, "Don't worry, Mimi. We will find your hat." They looked and looked for the blue hat.
Finally, they found the blue hat in a tree. Mimi was very happy. She learned to be more careful with her hat. Spot helped Mimi get her hat down from the tree. They went back to playing in the park with the big ball, and Mimi's hat stayed on her head.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to play outside with his friends. One day, it rained a lot and there were many puddles on the ground. Tim had an idea. He wanted to play a game with the puddles.
Tim and his friends went outside to play. They took turns to push each other into the puddles. They laughed and had fun. But Tim didn't know that one of his friends did not like to get wet. His friend was scared but didn't want to say anything.
After a while, it was the scared friend's turn. Tim pushed him into a big puddle. The friend started to cry. Tim felt very bad and ashamed. He didn't know his friend was scared. Tim said sorry and helped his friend get up. They all went inside to get dry and warm. From that day on, Tim made sure to ask his friends if they liked the game before playing.

One day, a little boy named Tim was ready to eat his lunch. He sat down and opened his lunch box. Inside, he found a sandwich, an apple, and a note from his mom. The note said, "I love you. Have a good day!" Tim smiled and started to eat his sandwich.
While eating, Tim saw a little bug on the ground. The bug looked up at Tim and said, "Hi! I'm Benny the Bug. Can I have a bite of your sandwich?" Tim was surprised, but he shared his sandwich with Benny. As Benny ate, he told Tim that he had a special power. Benny could help people remember things they had forgotten.
Tim thought about a memory he had lost. He asked Benny, "Can you help me remember my mom's special song? I forgot how it goes." Benny nodded and touched Tim's hand. Suddenly, Tim could remember the song! He started to sing it out loud, and Benny joined in.
Just then, Tim's teacher walked by and heard them singing. She was amazed that Tim remembered the song and that Benny could talk! She asked Benny if he could help other kids remember things too. Benny agreed, and from that day on, he became the school's special memory helper. Tim and Benny were very happy to help others and became the best of friends.

Teddy the bear was walking through the park one day when he spotted something in the grass. He leaned down to take a closer look, and saw an adorable little mixer. 
"What an exciting discovery!" Teddy thought out loud. He decided he wanted to take the mixer home with him, so he picked it up.
As he was walking home, Teddy ran into his best friend, Polly the poodle. 
"Hi Teddy! What have you got there?" Polly asked.
"I found an adorable little mixer in the park," Teddy replied. "Would you like to come with me and see it?"
"Yes, of course!" Polly said.
So Teddy and Polly walked back to Teddy's house together. When they arrived, Teddy proudly showed Polly the mixer.
Polly exclaimed, "It's so cute! I'm sure you will have lots of fun playing with it." 
Teddy smiled, knowing that he had made an amazing find. He was sure he was going to have the best time playing with his new mixer.

One day, Tim and Sam were playing in the yard. They saw a ladder near the big tree. Tim wanted to climb the ladder, but Sam said, "No, Tim! It's dangerous!"
Tim didn't listen to Sam. He started to climb the ladder. As he climbed higher and higher, he felt the ladder shake. Suddenly, the ladder started to break! Tim was scared.
Just then, a big, friendly bird flew by and caught Tim before he fell. The bird gently put Tim back on the ground. Tim was safe! He hugged Sam and said, "Thank you for trying to keep me safe. I will listen to you next time." They never played with the dangerous ladder again.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Daisy. She lived in a village with a lot of people. Daisy was naughty and she always marked on the walls.
One day, Daisy went to the village square. All the villagers were gathering around. One of the villagers said, "Daisy, why do you mark the walls?"
Daisy blushed and said, "I just want to show my creativity."
The villager said, "Just be careful. You must not mark any walls!"
Daisy looked around. All the villagers were nodding their heads in agreement.
Daisy felt so guilty for being naughty. She promised that she would never mark on any walls again.
From that day onwards Daisy started expressing her creativity in more appropriate ways. All the villagers appreciated her efforts.
The moral of the story is that we must be careful not to do naughty things that can hurt others.

One day, a small bug named Tim wanted to show his friends how powerful he was. Tim liked to crawl very fast. He thought if he could crawl the fastest, he would have a big victory.
Tim asked his friend, Sam the snail, "Want to race with me?" Sam said, "Yes, let's race!" They went to a long path in the garden to start the race. All their friends came to watch.
Tim and Sam started to crawl. Tim was so fast, and Sam was slow. Tim got to the end first. He was so happy. "I won! I am powerful!" Tim said with a big smile. All his friends clapped, and Tim felt proud of his victory.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Bob and Jan. They both loved playing outside in the sun.
One day Bob said, "Let's play a game today Jan. I want to go fly my kite." Jan smiled and said, "Okay, I'll come with you."
They grabbed their kites and ran outside to the big white field. Bob put out his kite and Jan hers and before long the two were running around in the sunshine, holding onto their kites in the wind.
But then, after a while, Jan started to get tired. Bob saw this and said, "Jan, you look sleepy. Maybe you should take a nap. I'll stay here and fly my kite while you take a nap."
Jan was hesitant at first, but after some encouraging words from Bob she agreed. Bob watched over Jan while she took a nap in the white field. When she woke up, they flew their kites some more until the sun went down and they both went home.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily went on a trip with her family. They had to pack their bags before they left. Lily put her toys, clothes, and a funny hat in her bag. She was very excited to go on this trip.
When they got to the hotel, Lily saw a crazy, big dog. The dog was running and jumping all around. Lily laughed and said, "Look, Mom! That dog is so crazy and funny!" Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, Lily. That dog is having lots of fun!"
At night, Lily and her family ate dinner in the hotel. They had yummy food and talked about their day. Lily told her family about the crazy dog and they all laughed. They had a great time on their trip and made lots of happy memories.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play on the swing at the park. One day, he saw a black cat sitting near the swing. The cat looked sad because it had a hurt paw.
Tim wanted to help the cat feel better. He went to his mom and asked her how to heal the cat's paw. His mom told him to be gentle and put a soft bandage on the paw. Tim did what his mom said.
After a few days, the black cat's paw was all healed. The cat was so happy that it played with Tim on the swing. They became best friends and played together at the park every day.

Once upon a time there were two brothers, called Max and Tom. They were very jolly and liked to do lots of different things together. 
One day, Max found something shiny in the garden. He thought it must belong to someone else and so wanted to give it back. He asked Tom to help him find who it belonged to.
They went around the neighbourhood asking people if they knew anything about the shiny thing. But no one knew where it belonged. 
Max was feeling sad he couldn't find who it belonged to. But Tom had an idea! He suggested they should put a sign up in the park with a picture of the shiny thing and some words about it. Then if anyone knew it belonged to them, they could get in touch.
Max thought this was a great idea. Max and Tom put the sign up in the park, and soon enough someone came along to collect their belonging. Max and Tom were so happy they'd helped, and they hugged each other. Max realised that he and Tom belonged together and that they were the best brothers ever!

Once upon a time, there was a bird with a big wing. The bird loved to fly high in the sky. It was easy for the bird to fly because its wing was so strong.
One day, the bird met a small bug. The bug said, "I wish I could fly like you!" The bird smiled and said, "I can help you. Just hold onto my wing and we will fly together!" The bug was happy and held onto the bird's wing.
As they flew together, the bird told a joke. The bug laughed so much it almost fell off the bird's wing. They had a lot of fun and became good friends. They flew and laughed together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big box with a lock on it. He wanted to unlock it, but he did not know how. He tried and tried, but the lock would not open. Tim was sad.
One day, Tim found a key in his room. He thought, "Maybe this key will unlock the box!" He put the key in the lock and turned it. The lock clicked and the box opened. Tim was so happy!
Inside the box, Tim found a lot of waste. It was not clean. He knew he had to clean it up. Tim worked hard to make the box clear of waste. When he was done, the box was nice and clean. Tim was proud of his work and he knew that he could unlock and clean anything he wanted.

Once upon a time, in a quiet little town, there lived a rare, big bird named Benny. Benny had many friends, but he liked to be alone sometimes. One day, Benny decided to separate from his friends and go on an adventure.
Benny flew high in the sky and saw a small boy named Timmy. Timmy looked sad. Benny asked, "Why are you sad, Timmy?" Timmy said, "I lost my toy, and I can't find it." Benny wanted to help, so he used his big eyes to look for the toy. Together, they found the toy under a big tree.
Timmy was so happy, and he said, "Thank you, Benny!" Benny felt good for helping Timmy. They played together all day. In the end, Benny learned that even if you like being alone, it's nice to help others and make new friends.

Once upon a time, there was a very special game. All the kids loved it and would gather together to get their chance to play.
One day, when the kids were gathering to play the game, they suddenly started to shake with fear. A big, bad monster had come near and it was alive and moving!
The kids screamed and ran away, but the monster followed them. Until, one of the brave kids stood up and threw a stone at it. The stone hit the monster and it fell to the ground, dead.
The kids were happy and relieved. They danced and cheered, and played their special game together all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She was restless and always looking for fun. One day, she went to the park with her mom. Lily played on the swings, slide, and in the sandbox. Her mom sat on a bench with her purse next to her.
While Lily played, a little bird flew down and took her mom's purse. Lily saw the bird and shouted, "Mom, the bird took your purse!" Her mom looked up and saw the bird flying away with her purse. She shouted, "Oh no! We need to get it back!"
Lily and her mom chased the bird through the park. They asked their friends, the squirrel and the rabbit, to help them. The squirrel climbed the tree and the rabbit hopped high. Together, they got the purse back from the bird.
"Thank you for helping us," said Lily's mom. The squirrel and the rabbit smiled. They were happy to help their friends. Lily and her mom went home, and from that day on, they were more careful with their things at the park.

Once upon a time, a small girl named Lily lived in a tiny house. She had a toy, a small machine. She loved her machine very much. It was her own special toy. The machine could make funny noises and move around. Lily played with her machine every day.
One day, while playing with her machine, it stopped working. Lily was very sad. She didn't know what to do. She asked her mom for help. Her mom looked at the machine and saw that it needed new batteries. 
Lily and her mom went to the store to buy new batteries. They put the batteries in the small machine, and it started working again. Lily was so happy. She played with her own small machine all day long. From that day on, Lily took good care of her machine, and they had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time there was a modern man and he wanted to pay for oxygen. He went to the shop and saw two tanks of oxygen. One tank was new and the other one was old. He bought the old tank and went home. 
When he arrived home he realised the old tank was not working. He tried to open it, but it was stuck. He was starting to worry. 
He tried and tried to open it, but he couldn't. He thought he had made a silly mistake and that he wasted his money. 
Just then, his best friend came to help him. He smiled and said, "Don't worry, I know just how to open it." 
He took a tool from his pocket and opened the tank. But it was not empty - it was full of modern oxygen! 
The man was amazed and very happy. He had paid for the oxygen in just the right way!

Once upon a time, there was a funny bunny named Bob. Bob loved to travel and hop around the big green field. One day, Bob found a small mint plant. He thought it smelled very nice.
Bob wanted to take the mint plant to his home. But a big, grumpy bear named Ben also wanted the mint plant. They both did not want to share. They started to fight over the mint plant. They were very mad at each other.
Then, a wise old owl saw them fighting. The owl told them, "You should not fight. It is better to share and be friends." Bob and Ben thought about it. They decided to share the mint plant and be friends. They learned that sharing is good and it made them happy. From that day on, they had lots of fun traveling and hopping around the big green field together.

A little girl named Emma was walking in the woods. It was a warm summer day. She loved to explore the woods with her Daddy. She saw birds singing in the trees, and she felt the gentle breeze on her face.
Then, Emma saw something she didn't like. It was a big pile of wood. She called out to her Daddy, with a tired voice: "I hate that wood!"
Daddy came over to her, and said, "That's OK, sweetheart. Let's move on and find something nicer to look at." He took her hand in his, and they walked away together.
Emma hugged Daddy even tighter. He was so gentle with her. She liked being in the woods with him. As they kept walking, she saw some pretty butterflies and forgot all about the wood.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a mighty girl named Jane. Jane loved to wear her big red boots everywhere she went. One day, Jane saw a little cat stuck in a tree. The cat was scared and needed help.
Jane wanted to surprise her friends by saving the cat. So, she put on her red boots and climbed the tree. It was not easy, but Jane was strong. She reached the cat and held it tight. Jane and the cat climbed down the tree safely.
When Jane's friends saw what she did, they were very happy. They all learned that helping others can bring joy and surprise to everyone. The moral of the story is that we should always try to help others, and our actions can make a big difference.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a thin sheet. It was very special. It could speak! One day, the sheet said, "I will help someone soon."
A little girl named Lily lived in the house. She loved to play with her toys. One day, she lost her toy bear. Lily was very sad. She looked and looked, but she could not find her bear. The speaking sheet saw Lily and knew it was time to help.
The sheet told Lily, "I know where your bear is. It is under your bed." Lily went to her room and looked under her bed. There was her toy bear! She was so happy. The speaking sheet had helped her. From that day on, Lily and the sheet were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She was a great girl who loved to play with her toys. One day, Lily went on a visit to her grandma's house. She was very excited to play with her grandma and have a fun day.
At grandma's house, Lily found a magic toy. The toy was a small, red ball. When Lily played with the ball, it made her shrink! She became very tiny, just like her toys. Lily was scared but also thought it was fun to be small.
Then, Lily had an idea. She wanted to surprise her grandma with her new size. So, she hid behind a cup and waited for her grandma to find her. When grandma saw tiny Lily, she was so surprised! But then, the magic ball made Lily big again. They both laughed and played together for the rest of the visit.

One day, a boy named Tom got a new spear. He was very happy. Tom wanted to show his new spear to his friend, Tim. So, Tom started to walk to Tim's house.
On his way, Tom saw a big cat. The cat was very hungry. The cat wanted to eat Tom's new spear. Tom was scared, but then he had an idea.
Tom gave the cat a big fish. The cat was happy and did not want to eat the spear anymore. Tom and the cat became friends. They walked together to Tim's house. Tim was surprised to see the cat with Tom. They all played together and had a fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who wanted to travel. He wanted to see the sunrise in new places. He had a friend, an ugly frog. The ugly frog wanted to travel too. So, they decided to go together.
The little bird and the ugly frog started their trip. They walked and walked until they were very tired. But they did not stop. They wanted to see the sunrise in a beautiful place. At last, they found a big tree near a pond. They decided to rest there.
The next morning, they woke up early to see the sunrise. The sky was full of colors. The little bird and the ugly frog were so happy. They knew that traveling together made them the best of friends. And they could not wait to see more sunrises together in new places.

One day, a small bird named Bobo lived in a pretty oasis. The oasis was full of water and trees. Bobo had many friends. They liked to play and sing together.
One day, Bobo saw a shiny thing on the ground. It was a pretty stone. He picked it up and showed it to his friend, Lulu. Lulu said, "That is not yours. You should return it." Bobo thought about it and said, "You are right. I will return it."
Bobo looked everywhere to find who lost the pretty stone. At last, he found a sad frog named Toto. Toto was looking for his pretty stone. Bobo gave the stone back to Toto. Toto was happy and said, "Thank you, Bobo!"
The moral value of the story is that it is important to return things that do not belong to us.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the store with his mom. They needed to buy some food for dinner. Tim saw a modest-sized piece of meat and asked his mom, "Can we get this meat, please?" His mom said yes, and they put the meat in their cart.
When they got home, Tim's mom put the meat in the freeze box to keep it cold. Tim watched her and asked, "Why do we freeze the meat, mom?" His mom replied, "We freeze it so it stays good until we cook it for dinner."
Later, Tim's mom took the meat out of the freeze box and cooked it for dinner. Tim was very happy because the meat tasted so good. He said, "Thank you, mom, for the yummy meat!" And they both enjoyed their dinner together.

Once upon a time, there was a nice dog named Spot. Spot was not feeling well. He felt dizzy and did not want to play. Spot's mom said, "We will go see the veterinarian."
At the veterinarian, Spot met a big cat named Fluffy. Fluffy said, "I am here to read a book with my friend." Spot was happy to meet a new friend. They read a book about a bird that could fly high in the sky.
After reading, the veterinarian came to see Spot. She said, "Spot, you are just dizzy because you are hungry." She gave him a treat, and he felt better. Spot and Fluffy played together and became best friends. They promised to read more books and visit the veterinarian together again.

One day, a compassionate cat named Kitty found a bottle of lotion. Kitty was very happy because her skin was very dry. She wanted to share the lotion with her friends. So, she went to her friend Bunny's house.
"Hi Bunny!" Kitty said. "I found this lotion. Do you want to try some?" Bunny nodded and they both tried the lotion. It made their skin feel soft and nice. Then, they saw their friend Bird flying close by.
Kitty and Bunny called Bird to come down. "Hey Bird, we found lotion! Do you want some?" Bird said yes and tried the lotion. As Bird rubbed the lotion on her wings, something unexpected happened. Bird could not fly anymore!
Kitty, Bunny, and Bird were very worried. They did not know what to do. Then, they saw a wise old turtle walking close by. They asked the turtle for help. The turtle told them to wash off the lotion with water.
Bird washed her wings and could fly again. The friends were very happy. They learned that it is good to share, but they should be careful with new things. They thanked the wise old turtle and went to play together.

One day, a little girl named Mia found a talking cat. The cat had long hair. Mia thought the cat was very smart. She wanted to be friends with the cat. Mia asked the cat, "Do you want to be my friend?"
The cat said, "I don't like people. I hate hair. Can you help me cut my hair?" Mia was sad but wanted to help the cat. She got scissors and cut the cat's hair. The cat was happy and said, "Thank you, Mia."
After Mia cut the cat's hair, something strange happened. The cat turned into a little boy. He was an intelligent boy who had been turned into a cat by a mean witch. The boy said, "Now that I am a boy again, I want to be your friend." Mia was happy and they became best friends.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a wise old bird. This bird knew many things and liked to help others. One day, a little rabbit was hopping around and found the wise bird sitting on a seat made of sticks.
"Hello, wise bird," said the rabbit. "I am very thirsty. Can you help me find something to drink?"
The wise bird looked around and saw a small stream nearby. "Yes, little rabbit," said the bird. "You can drink water from that stream over there."
The little rabbit hopped over to the stream and took a long drink. The water was cool and refreshing. The rabbit felt much better after drinking the water.
"Thank you, wise bird!" said the rabbit. "You helped me when I needed it. Now I can go back to playing with my friends."
And so, the little rabbit and the wise bird became good friends. They spent many days together in the forest, helping each other and having fun. And they always knew that if they needed help, they could count on one another.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the creek with her mom. They wanted to cook some yummy food by the water. Lily was very happy and excited. She liked to watch the graceful fish swim in the creek.
While they were cooking, a big dog came near them. The dog wanted their food. Lily was scared, and her mom was worried. They did not want the dog to take their food.
Lily's mom had an idea. She took a big stick and waved it at the dog. The dog ran away, and they were safe. They finished cooking their food and had a great time by the creek.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He was a restless boy who always loved to play. One day, Tim saw a big red envelope on the table. He wanted to know what was inside, but his mom said, "No, Tim. You must sit and wait."
Tim did not like to sit and wait, so he thought of a plan. He asked his mom if he could go outside to play. "Yes, but you must not open the envelope," she said. Tim agreed and went outside.
While playing, Tim found a small key. He was very excited and thought that maybe the key would open the envelope. So, he went back inside and tried the key. To his surprise, the envelope opened! Inside was a beautiful toy car, just like the one he always wanted.
"Wow, Tim! You found the key to your surprise!" his mom said. Tim was very happy and learned that sometimes, being patient and waiting can lead to great things.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a strong tiger named Tom. Tom was very big and had many friends. One day, Tom and his friend, a little bird named Bella, decided to prepare a big meal for all their friends.
Tom and Bella went to find food in the forest. They found yummy fruits, nuts, and berries. Bella said, "Tom, let's take all this food back and prepare a big meal for our friends!" Tom agreed and carried the food in his big, strong mouth.
When they got back, Tom and Bella started to prepare the meal. All their friends came and saw the yummy food. They all said, "Thank you, Tom and Bella, for making this big meal for us!" Tom and Bella were happy to see their friends enjoy the meal they prepared. They all ate together and had a great day in the big forest.

One day, a wealthy lady named Jane wanted to have a big party. She had a big, pretty house and many friends. Jane liked to share her toys and yummy food with her friends. She thought a party would be a fun way to do this.
Jane sent out invites to all her friends. On the invites, she wrote, "Please come to my party. We will have lots of fun and yummy treats." Her friends were very excited and happy to come. 
At the party, Jane and her friends played fun games, ate yummy food, and shared their toys. One friend said, "Thank you for inviting me, Jane. I am having so much fun!" Jane smiled and said, "You're welcome! I am happy you came. Please enjoy the party!" They all played and laughed together, and it was the best party ever.

John was walking his dog through the park one day. His dog was called Max and Max was always happy and friendly. 
John saw a baby crying and he wanted to help. He quickly closed the gate to keep his dog from running away and he walked over to the baby. 
He said to the baby, "What's wrong?". 
The baby replied, "My mommy and daddy told me not to follow the dog and I did, and now I'm lost". 
John smiled at the baby and said, "Don't worry, I'm here now and Max will help us find them". 
John and Max set out walking through the park searching and soon enough they came across the baby's mom and dad. The baby ran over to them, full of joy and hugged them tightly. 
John and Max watched from a distance, John feeling proud and a little ashamed at the same time. 
The moral of the story is to always listen to your parents advice. If the baby had stayed close like he was supposed to, he would not have got himself lost in the park.

Tom and Lily were twins who liked to question everything. They always asked their mom and dad why the sky was blue, why the grass was green, why the dog barked, and so on. Sometimes their parents were happy to answer, but sometimes they were busy or tired and told them to stop asking so many questions.
One day, mom made a big cake for dessert. It smelled very good and looked very yummy. Tom and Lily wanted to eat it right away, but mom said they had to wait until after dinner. She put the cake on the table and told them not to touch it. Then she went to the kitchen to finish cooking.
Tom and Lily looked at the cake with big eyes. They wondered what it tasted like, what it was made of, how mom made it, and if they could have a big slice. They had so many questions, but they knew mom would not like it if they asked her now. They decided to try to find out by themselves.
They went to the table and climbed on the chairs. They reached for the cake and touched it with their fingers. It felt soft and sticky. They licked their fingers and tasted it. It was sweet and creamy. They liked it very much. They wanted more. They took a knife and cut a small piece of the cake. They ate it quickly and quietly. It was delicious. They wanted more. They cut another piece and ate it. Then another, and another, until they had eaten half of the cake.
They were very full and happy. They smiled at each other and wiped their mouths. They thought they had been very clever and useful. They had answered their own questions and had a nice dessert. They did not think mom would notice or mind.
But mom did notice and mind. She came back to the table and saw the cake. She saw the knife, the crumbs, and the empty plates. She saw Tom and Lily's faces, which were still covered with cake. She was very angry and sad. She had worked hard to make the cake for the whole family, and now it was ruined. She asked Tom and Lily why they had done such a bad thing. They did not have an answer. They realized they had made a big mistake. They felt very sorry and ashamed. They wished they had not been so curious and greedy. They wished they had listened to mom and waited for dessert. They learned a hard lesson that day. They said they were sorry and hugged mom. Mom said she loved them, but they had to go to bed without dessert. They agreed and went to their room. They hoped mom would forgive them and make another cake someday. They decided to ask fewer questions and be more patient and respectful. They also decided to brush their teeth very well.

Lily and Ben are friends. They like to play together. Today, they want to play dress up. Lily has a costume box with many things. She picks a princess dress and a crown. She says, "I want to be a princess. You can be a prince."
Ben looks at the costume box. He sees a pirate hat and a sword. He says, "No, I want to be a pirate. You can be a pirate too."
Lily and Ben disagree. They do not want to be the same thing. They do not want to share the costume box. They start to argue. They say, "No, you be this. No, you be that."
They make each other sad. They do not have fun. They do not play together.
Then, Lily's mom comes. She sees Lily and Ben. She says, "What is wrong? Why are you not playing?"
Lily and Ben tell her. They say, "We disagree. We want different costumes. We are sad."
Lily's mom says, "I have an idea. Why don't you both be pirates and princesses? You can mix and match the costumes. You can have fun together."
Lily and Ben think. They say, "That sounds good. We can try that."
They go back to the costume box. They pick different things. Lily wears a pirate hat and a princess dress. Ben wears a pirate sword and a prince coat. They look at each other. They laugh. They say, "We look funny. We like it."
They play together. They pretend to be pirates and princesses. They have fun. They are happy. They agree.

Once upon a time there was a mighty clown who loved to bounce. Everywhere he went he would bounce and it made him so happy.
One day the clown was bouncing at the park when he saw a little girl. The girl was three years old and her name was Sarah. She watched the clown as he bounced around and she wanted to bounce too.
The clown smiled at Sarah and said, "Do you want to learn how to bounce?" Sarah was very excited and said, "Yes! Please teach me."
So, the clown showed her how to bounce. He showed her how to bend her knees and how to jump up high in the air. Sarah tried it and she loved it! She could bounce higher and higher every time.
Sarah smiled at the clown and said, "Thank you. I love to bounce!" Then the clown laughed, because he knew he had made a new friend.

Sam and Ben are brothers. They like to play with their toy sail. They take it to the pond and put it in the water. They watch it go with the wind.
"Look, Sam, our sail is fast!" Ben says.
"Yes, it is, Ben. It is fun to see it move," Sam says.
But then the wind gets stronger. The sail goes too far. Sam and Ben cannot reach it.
"Oh no, Sam, our sail is gone!" Ben says.
"I know, Ben. I am sad. I am anxious. How can we get it back?" Sam says.
They run along the pond. They try to catch the sail. But it is too late. The sail goes into a big pipe. The pipe is dark and scary. Sam and Ben hear a loud splash.
"Sam, Ben, what are you doing?" Their mom says. She sees them by the pipe. She is angry.
"Mom, we lost our sail. It went into the pipe. We are sorry," Sam says.
"Sam, Ben, you are naughty. You should not play near the pipe. It is dangerous. You could get hurt. Come with me. We are going home. No more sail for you," Mom says.
She takes them by the hand. She walks away. Sam and Ben cry. They miss their sail. They wish they could get it back. But they can't. The end.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Lily who lived on a sunny hill. At noon every day she would go outside and admire the bright light.
One day, Lily's mother called from the kitchen window and said "Lily, come and have lunch!"
Lily answered back "Just one more minute!" and continued to admire the beautiful light.
"Lily," her mother called again in a gentle voice, "Dinner will be ready soon. You should come inside now to have lunch."
Lily sighed softly and stopped admiring the light. She turned towards the kitchen and answered "OK, Mommy." She walked slowly to the kitchen, still admiring the light of noon.

One day, a little mole was playing in the yard. He was a fearful mole, always scared of new things. While playing, he saw a boy with a toy. The toy could shoot soft balls. The mole was curious but also scared.
The boy saw the mole and said, "Hi, little mole! Want to play with me?" The mole was scared but wanted to try. The boy showed the mole how to shoot the soft balls. They played together and had fun.
After playing, the mole was no longer fearful. He had made a new friend and learned that trying new things can be fun. They promised to play together again and the mole went back to his home, happy and brave.

Once upon a time, there was a peaceful octopus named Ollie. Ollie lived in the deep blue sea. He had many friends like fish, crab, and starfish. They played together all day long.
One day, Ollie heard his friend, the crab, was sad. The crab lost his shell and needed a new one. Ollie wanted to help. He thought and thought about how he could find a new shell for his friend.
Ollie remembered he saw a big shell near the sea plants. He swam very fast to the sea plants and found the big shell. Ollie picked up the shell with his long arms and took it to the crab.
The crab was so happy to see the shell. He said, "Thank you, Ollie!" and put on the new shell. Now, the crab was not sad anymore. Ollie and his friends played together, happy and peaceful in the deep blue sea.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a very brave boy who loved to study. One day, he was studying in his room when he heard a noise outside. He went to see what it was.
Tim saw a big cat stuck in a tree. "Don't worry, cat! I will help you!" he said. Tim found a long stick with a knife on the end. He used the stick to cut the branch the cat was on. The cat jumped down and was safe.
But the cat was not a cat! It was a magic fairy in disguise. The fairy was so happy that Tim saved her. She said, "Thank you, brave boy! I will give you a wish for helping me." Tim wished for a big, fun party with all his friends. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jessy. She was three years old and liked to play outside with her friends.
One day, Jessy was playing with some of her friends and they got very tired. Everyone wanted to take a break, but they were too afraid to ask.
So Jessy gathered her courage and said "Can we stop now?"
Her friends looked at her but no one said anything. Jessy started to complain.
"I'm tired. We should stop," she said.
One of her friends spoke up. "Okay, let's take a break," he said.
They all stopped and sat down. Jessy was relieved, but she still felt weak from all the playing. They all had a rest, and soon they were ready to play again.
Jessy looked around and smiled. She was happy and she knew that next time, she wouldn't be too afraid to speak up again.

One Sunday, a big cat and a little dog were in the park. The cat was mighty and the dog was small. They were playing with a red ball. The cat said, "I can kick the ball very far!" The dog said, "I can catch it!"
The cat kicked the ball with all his might. It went up, up, up in the sky. The dog ran fast to catch it. The ball seemed to go very far away. But then, something unexpected happened.
A big bird in the sky saw the ball. The bird thought it was a fun toy. So, the bird swooped down and grabbed the ball with its beak. The cat and the dog looked up and saw the bird fly away with their ball. They both said, "Oh no! Our ball is gone!" But they laughed and played together, and they still had a fun Sunday.

Judy and Dan were walking through the park one day. Judy pointed to a rare zoom, flying in the sky. "Look, Dan," she said. "A rare zoom."
"What is it?" asked Dan. Judy told him that it was a special bird.
The zoom stopped flying, and landed in front of them. "Welcome," it said. 
The children were surprised and stood very still. 
The zoom hopped closer to them. "If everyone was friendly and welcoming, like I am, the world would be a much nicer place," it said.
Judy and Dan looked at each other and nodded. They smiled at the zoom, then said together, "Welcome!"
The zoom smiled back, then flew away.
Judy and Dan learned their lesson that day. They understood that being welcoming and friendly was the right thing to do.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She lived in a small hut with her mom and dad. Mia was very important to her family. She loved to play and laugh all day long.
One day, Mia went outside to play. She saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. As she climbed, she heard a loud noise. It made her jump and she got scared. Mia thought it was a big monster coming to get her. She ran back to her hut as fast as she could.
When Mia got home, she told her mom and dad about the noise. They went outside to see what it was. To Mia's surprise, it was just a little bird singing a song. The bird had a beautiful voice.
