Mia was not scared anymore. She learned that sometimes things that seem scary at first can turn out to be nice and fun.

Once upon a time, there was a Little Girl who never wanted to wear anything but her old dress. One day, she went down to the garden with a basket to pick some salad. The Little Girl was very modest, so she picked the biggest and freshest leaves but left some for the other animals living in the garden.
Just then, she heard someone calling out to her. It was the Sun.
"Little Girl," said the Sun, "why don't you wear the new dress your mum bought for you?"
The Little Girl was surprised and replied, "But I like my old dress best. It's comfortable and it keeps me warm."
The Sun replied in a kind voice, "Little Girl, we can learn a lot from the salad you picked. Even though some leaves were bigger and nicer than others, you left some for the other animals in the garden. That was very kind of you! In the same way, it's important to share everything we have, even the clothes we wear. That way, everyone stays comfortable and happy."
The Little Girl smiled and thanked the Sun. From that day on, the Little Girl wore her old dress on some days and her new dress on others. She was happy that she had something to share with others. 
Moral of the story: Sharing is caring!

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a famous tree. This tree was very big and strong. It was the best tree in the town. Everyone liked to sit under the tree and rest.
One day, a big storm came. The wind blew very hard, and the rain fell down. The people in the town were scared. They ran to the famous tree to hide.
The tree was very strong, and it did not fall down in the storm. The people were safe under the tree. They were happy to rest there until the storm was over. After the storm, the sun came out, and the town was happy again.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw a row of ducks near the pond. Tim wanted to feed the ducks, so his mom gave him some bread. "Be careful, Tim," his mom said. "Don't get too close to the water."
Tim walked carefully to the ducks and threw the bread to them. The ducks were happy and ate the bread. Tim saw a big duck that didn't get any bread. He wanted to help the big duck, so he tried to get closer. The big duck saw Tim and said, "Please surrender the bread to me."
Tim was surprised that the duck could talk. He carefully gave the bread to the big duck. The big duck thanked Tim and said, "You are a kind boy. I will give you a wish." Tim thought about it and said, "I wish to be friends with all the ducks."
The big duck smiled and said, "Your wish is granted." From that day on, Tim became friends with all the ducks in the park. He and his mom visited them every day, and they all played together.

One day, a little boy named Tim went out to play in the winter. The snow was cold and white. Tim wore a big, warm coat and a hat. He saw his friend, Sally, and they started to play.
"Let's make a snowman!" said Tim. They rolled snow into big balls and put them together. They gave the snowman a carrot nose and sticks for arms. The snowman looked brilliant!
Tim and Sally wanted to play a game, so they decided to kick snowballs. They took turns kicking the snowballs to see who could kick them the farthest. Tim kicked a snowball very far, and Sally laughed. They played until the sun went down and it was time to go home.
As they walked home, Tim and Sally waved goodbye to their snowman. They were happy they had such a fun day in the winter snow.

Once upon a time there was an ancient tree all alone on a hill. One day, a little girl and her dog named Spot came to visit. Spot ran around the tree and barked.
The little girl said to her dog, "Why are you barking?"
Spot replied, "I can smell something. Maybe it is an owner."
The little girl said, "Let's look!" So they both looked around the tree and, sure enough, they saw a rope hanging from one of the branches.
The little girl said, "This must be an old owner. Maybe he used to hang his hat here."
The little girl and Spot kept exploring the ancient tree and soon they discovered a little house built into one of the branches. They knocked on the door, but no one answered.
So, the little girl and Spot went back home, but they never forgot their adventure beneath the ancient tree.

One day, a little girl named Sue went to the beach with her mom. Sue saw a big white boat on the water. She liked the boat because it was very pretty. Sue's mom said, "Sue, do you want to wave to the people on the boat?" Sue said, "Yes, mom!"
Sue and her mom waved to the people on the boat. The people on the boat waved back. Sue was very happy. She saw a long cord on the boat. Sue's mom said, "That cord is used to tie the boat to the dock." Sue thought that was very interesting.
Later, the big white boat came to the dock. The people on the boat used the long cord to tie the boat. Sue and her mom went to see the boat up close. They said hello to the people on the boat. Sue was so happy that she got to wave to the people and see the big white boat with the long cord.

Sam and Mia are friends. They like to play with flags. They have flags from many nations. Nations are big places with many people. Each nation has a different flag.
One day, Sam and Mia want to make a parade. They carry their flags and march around the yard. They sing songs and wave their flags. They are happy and proud.
But then, a big wind comes. It blows hard and fast. It takes some of the flags away. Sam and Mia are sad and scared. They run after their flags.
They see one flag on a tree. It is a long flag with red and white stripes and blue stars. It is the flag of Sam's nation. Sam climbs the tree and gets the flag. He carries it down and gives it to Mia. Mia says thank you and hugs Sam.
They see another flag on a roof. It is a long flag with green, white and orange stripes. It is the flag of Mia's nation. Mia climbs the ladder and gets the flag. She carries it down and gives it to Sam. Sam says thank you and hugs Mia.
They find the other flags and carry them back to the yard. They are happy and proud again. They make a new parade and sing new songs. They share their flags and their nations. They are friends.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Max. Max had a nice collar. The collar was red and blue. Max liked his collar a lot.
One day, Max saw a big, strong tree. The tree had many leaves. Max liked the tree. Max wanted to admire the tree. So, he sat under the tree and looked at it.
Max was happy under the strong tree. He felt safe. Max's collar was pretty in the sun. Max and the tree were good friends.

One day, a big dog named Max went on a search. He was looking for a huge bone he lost. He looked under the big tree, in the tall grass, and near the small pond. Max was sad because he could not find his huge bone.
Max saw his friend, a small cat named Lily. Lily wanted to play, but Max said, "I need to find my huge bone first." Lily said, "I will help you look." So, they went to search together. But they still could not find the huge bone.
Then, they saw a big bird in the sky. The bird had something in its beak. It was the huge bone! The bird dropped the bone on the ground. Max and Lily were very happy. They did not expect the bird to have the bone. Now, they could play together without any delay.

It was a warm and sunny day in the countryside. All the people of the village were excited to go out and play. But soon, the sky began to darken. Everyone knew that the rain was coming. 
The rain started to pour down on the village. All the villagers scurried to their homes and the games stopped. 
The village children were hungry and their parents had no medicine to cure them. But then, one of the children remembered something his grandmother had told him. She had said that if the village ever needed help, they should find the nearby lake. 
So, the children ran to the lake. And when they arrived, they were amazed to find a beautiful boat of medicine. The children were overjoyed, and quickly filled their baskets with the medicine and ran back to their village.
Thanks to the boat of medicine, the children in the village were successfully cured. The villagers were so pleased with this mysterious help that they started to call the lake "Miracle Lake". It is known to this day as a place of great magic and generosity.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to climb high on trees. One sunny day, Tim saw a big tree with a cord hanging from it. The cord was very high, but Tim wanted to reach it.
"Tim, be careful!" said his friend, Sam. Tim did not listen and tried to climb the tree. He was almost at the top when he slipped and fell. Tim was not hurt, but he was scared.
His mom saw what happened and said, "Tim, you must not climb so high. It is not safe. I have to punish you." Tim was sad, but he knew his mom was right. From that day on, Tim never climbed too high again.

Once upon a time, there was a wealthy bird named Bob. Bob had a big, nice nest in a tall tree. He was very proud of his nest because he worked hard to complete it.
One day, Bob saw a little bird named Tim. Tim did not have a nest. Tim was sad. Bob wanted to help Tim. So, Bob showed Tim how to make a nest.
Tim and Bob worked together to complete Tim's nest. They used sticks, leaves, and soft things. Soon, Tim had a nest just like Bob's. Tim was very happy. Now, both birds had a nice nest in the tall tree. They were good friends and lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a clever baby bear. He liked to explore the world and find new things.
One day, he saw a strange piece of equipment near a pond. He asked his mother, "Mommy, what is that?"
His mother replied, "That looks like a weighing machine. It's used to measure the weight of things. Why don't you try it out?"
The baby bear was excited. He put a small rock onto the weighing machine and looked at the results. He was amazed to see that the machine had weighed the rock!
Then, the baby bear looked for other small things to weigh. He weighed a leaf, a flower, and even a tiny bug. He was so proud of himself.
His mother laughed as she watched him explore. "You're such a clever baby bear!" she said.
The baby bear smiled to himself. He had found a new way to explore the world - by weighing things!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a gifted boy. He loved to build things with his toy blocks. One day, he decided to build a mailbox for his mom.
Tim worked hard to build the mailbox. He asked his mom, "Can I put the mailbox outside?" His mom said, "Yes, Tim! That's a great idea!" She knew Tim was gifted and could do it. She was excited to see what he would build.
Tim finished the mailbox and put it outside. The next day, a letter came for his mom. She was so happy and said, "Thank you, Tim! I love my new mailbox!" Tim felt proud of what he built. He knew he could build anything because he was gifted.

Once upon a time, there was a cute little girl called Nia. She wanted to go to the zoo with her older brother. When they arrived, they saw lots of animals. Nia was so excited and started to run around to see all the animals.
Suddenly, Nia saw a big cage. Inside were two angry lions who were roaring and banging against the bars. Nia became scared and started to cry.
Nia's brother went to speak to the keeper. "Be careful," he said, "these lions are very rude". However Nia didn't understand and tried to reach in and pat the lion's head.
Suddenly the keeper yelled, "No !" but it was too late. As Nia touched the cage, the lock broke and the lions escaped. Everyone was so scared. Nia was really sad as she saw the angry lions running away. 
The zoo keeper was very mad. He yelled at Nia, "You're so rude. You have ruined the zoo!" 
Nia never went to the zoo again.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Charlie and Jake. Charlie loved pizza. Jake was envious of Charlie because he couldn't eat pizza.
One day, Charlie found a giant pizza. He was so excited to eat it all by himself. But when he picked it up, it split in two. 
Charlie said to Jake, "Do you want half of the pizza?"
Jake was so excited. He replied, "Yes, please!"
They split the pizza between them and enjoyed it. However, when they finished eating, they started to feel very sick. They both had stomach aches and felt very ill.
The next day, Charlie and Jake learned their lesson. They didn't share food again.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Bob. Bob was a sleepy bunny who loved to play with his friends in the meadow. One day, Bob was playing with his best friend, a squirrel named Sue. They were playing with their favorite ball, but Bob accidentally threw the ball too far away. Sue was sad and worried that they would lose the ball.
Bob said, "I'm sorry, Sue. I didn't mean to throw the ball too far. Please forgive me." Sue looked at Bob and said, "It's okay, Bob. We all make mistakes. I forgive you. Let's go find the ball together." So, they went on a little adventure to find the ball, and they finally found it near a big tree.
After finding the ball, Bob and Sue continued to play and have fun. They learned that it's important to forgive each other when mistakes happen. No one is perfect, and worrying about small things won't make them better. As the sun began to set, the sleepy friends went home, happy to have learned a valuable lesson about forgiveness and friendship.

Once upon a time, there was a big round ball who loved to roll. One day, it saw a slow guard standing near a door. The ball wanted to play with the guard, so it rolled to him.
The guard saw the ball and thought it wanted to play too. He kicked the ball and it rolled back to him. They played together and had fun. The ball liked the slow guard.
Then, something unexpected happened. The slow guard was not a guard at all! He was a big, friendly dog in a guard costume. The dog wagged its tail and the ball knew they would be best friends. They played and rolled together all day long.

One day, a dog named Max and a cat named Luna were in the park. Max was very alert. He saw a big, yummy treat on the ground. Max wanted to have the treat, but so did Luna.
Max said, "I saw the treat first, so I should have it!" Luna said, "No, I want the treat!" They both tried to grab the treat at the same time. They were not happy with each other.
A big bird saw them fighting. The bird came down and took the treat away. Max and Luna were sad. They did not get the treat. They learned they should share next time.

Lily and Ben were playing in the mud. They made mud pies and mud castles and mud animals. They had a lot of fun, but they also got very dirty.
"Time to go inside and wash up!" Mom called. "You can't eat dinner with all that mud on you!"
Lily and Ben ran to the bathroom. They saw a big tub of water and a bottle of soap. Lily grabbed the soap and poured some on her hands. She rubbed them together and made bubbles.
"Let me have some soap, too!" Ben said. He reached for the bottle, but Lily pulled it away.
"No, it's mine!" Lily said. "You can use water!"
Ben was sad and angry. He wanted to make bubbles, too. He tried to take the bottle from Lily, but she held it tight. They started to tug and pull and shout.
"Stop it, you two!" Mom said. She came into the bathroom and saw the mess. The soap bottle was empty and the water was dirty. Lily and Ben were still fighting.
"What are you doing?" Mom asked. "Why can't you share the soap?"
Lily and Ben looked at each other. They felt ashamed. They knew they had been selfish and rude.
"We're sorry, Mom," they said. "We wanted to make bubbles, but we didn't let each other have some soap."
Mom sighed. She took the empty bottle and threw it in the trash. She filled the tub with fresh water and got a new bottle of soap.
"Here, you can both have some soap," she said. "But you have to promise to share and be nice to each other. Soap is for washing, not for fighting."
Lily and Ben nodded. They said sorry to each other and hugged. They took turns with the soap and made bubbles. They washed their hands and faces and hair. They had fun, but they also learned a lesson.
The moral of the story is: Sharing is caring. Don't be greedy or mean. Soap is for washing, not for fighting.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a mighty king. The king had a big loop in his castle. The loop was very special. The king liked to play with it every day.
One day, a little girl named Lily came to the king. She said, "Please, mighty king, can I play with your loop?" The king looked at her and said, "Yes, but you must pray before you play with it. That is the rule."
Lily prayed and then played with the loop. She had so much fun! She said, "Thank you, mighty king, for letting me play with your loop." The king was happy to see Lily having fun.
The next day, Lily's friend Tom wanted to play with the loop, too. But he did not want to pray. The king said, "No, Tom, you must pray first." Tom decided to pray, and then he had fun playing with the loop, too.
The moral of the story is that we should always follow the rules and be thankful for what we have.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat named Tom. Tom loved to play and explore. One day, he found a restaurant. He was very curious about what was inside.
Tom decided to peek into the restaurant. He saw big people cooking and eating. He thought it looked like fun. But, Tom saw something dangerous too. There was a big, hot stove. Tom knew he should not go near the stove because it could hurt him.
Tom was a smart cat. He knew that playing in the restaurant was not safe. So, he went back to play with his friends outside. They had fun playing in the grass, far away from the dangerous stove. The moral of the story is to always be careful and stay away from dangerous things.

One day, a little boy named Tom went to the park. It was a regular day with the sun shining bright. Tom saw his friend, Sam, sitting on a bench. Sam had a big frown on his face. Tom asked, "Why are you sad, Sam?"
Sam said, "I lost my ball in the dark bushes. I can't find it." Tom wanted to help Sam. They both went to the bushes to look for the ball. The bushes were very dark and big. Tom and Sam were scared, but they wanted to find the ball.
Tom saw something shiny in the dark bushes. He reached in and found the ball! Sam's face lit up with a big smile. They both laughed and played with the ball. The day was not regular anymore. It was a happy day for Tom and Sam.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jack. He was three years old and very eager to explore the world. One day, he wanted to go on an adventure, so he asked his mom if he could go. His mom agreed and Jack put on his shoes.
He stepped outside and ran to the playground. When he got there, he saw a big wreck. He was so eager to check it out that he ran right up to it. He asked his mom what it was, and she said it was an old car.
Jack was so excited. He shouted, "Come and look at the wreck, mom!" His mom smiled as she saw how eager he was to explore. 
The two of them went to the wreck and Jack knocked on the broken car window. He was so curious and happy to find new things. 
Jack and his mom spent the rest of the afternoon exploring the wreck. He was so proud that he could share the adventure with his mom. 
The end.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. Tim saw a small, ugly bug on a flower. He looked very close to observe the bug. The bug was in a tiny cell on the flower, like a little home.
Tim said to his mom, "Mom, look at this ugly bug in a cell on the flower." His mom came over and looked at the bug too. She said, "That is an ugly bug, Tim. But it has a job to do in its cell."
Just then, something unexpected happened. The ugly bug came out of the cell and turned into a beautiful butterfly! Tim and his mom were very surprised. The butterfly flew away, and Tim said, "Wow, mom! That ugly bug changed into a pretty butterfly!"
His mom smiled and said, "Yes, Tim. Sometimes things that seem ugly can turn out to be beautiful. That's why we should never judge something just by how it looks."

Tim and Sam are brothers. They like to play with cars and trucks. They have many toys, but their favorite is a big red fire truck. It has a ladder, a siren, and a hose.
One day, they are playing with the fire truck in the living room. Tim wants to be the driver, and Sam wants to be the mechanic. They pretend that there is a fire and they have to save the people.
"Vroom, vroom, we are the firemen!" Tim says, making the fire truck move on the carpet.
"Beep, beep, I am the mechanic!" Sam says, fixing the fire truck with a screwdriver.
They have fun for a while, but then they start to fight. Tim wants to use the screwdriver, and Sam wants to use the ladder. They pull and tug at the fire truck, and they get angry.
"Give me the screwdriver, it's mine!" Tim shouts.
"No, it's mine, give me the ladder!" Sam shouts.
They are so loud that their mom hears them from the kitchen. She comes to the living room and sees them fighting. She is not happy.
"Boys, boys, what are you doing? Why are you fighting over the fire truck?" she asks.
They both try to explain, but they talk at the same time. Mom can't understand them.
"Stop, stop, I can't hear you. You have to share the fire truck, it's for both of you. You can take turns being the driver and the mechanic. Fighting is not nice, it hurts your feelings and your toys." she says.
Tim and Sam look at each other. They feel sorry for fighting. They don't want to hurt their feelings or their toys. They love their fire truck.
"Sorry, Tim, I was crazy. You can have the screwdriver." Sam says.
"Sorry, Sam, I was crazy too. You can have the ladder." Tim says.
They hug and make up. They decide to share the fire truck and play nicely. Mom smiles and gives them a kiss.
"That's better, boys. I'm proud of you. Now, let's go to the kitchen and have some cookies." she says.
They follow mom to the kitchen, holding the fire truck. They are happy and hungry. They are the best brothers.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to bed. He was very tired. In the morning, Tim would wake up and find a surprise. His mom told him, "When you wake up, you will see something new."
In the morning, Tim woke up and went to the bathroom. He looked in the mirror and saw a new tooth! Tim was so happy. He ran to his mom and said, "Mom, I have a new tooth!" His mom smiled and said, "That's great, Tim! Now you have to keep it healthy."
Tim brushed his new tooth every day to keep it clean and healthy. One day, while brushing his teeth, he heard a tiny voice. It was his new tooth! The tooth said, "Thank you, Tim, for keeping me healthy!" Tim was surprised but happy that his tooth could talk.
From that day on, Tim and his tooth became best friends. They talked and played together. Tim learned that taking care of his teeth was important and fun. And his healthy tooth was always there to remind him to brush and keep his smile bright.

Once upon a time, there was a little gray cat. The cat loved to play with a spark. The spark would jump and dance, and it would excite the cat.
One day, the cat found a spark in the woods. The cat jumped and played with the spark. The cat was very happy and excited. The spark made the cat feel so good.
But then, something bad happened. The spark grew too big and too hot. The woods began to burn. The little gray cat got scared and ran away. The cat was not excited anymore. The end.

Tom liked to play with his toy tank. He made it go vroom vroom on the floor and shoot boom boom at the bad guys. He had a lot of fun with his tank, but he wished he had a friend to play with.
One day, he saw a new boy in the park. He had a big bow and arrow. Tom thought that was cool. He wanted to play with him. He took his tank and went to say hi.
"Hi, I'm Tom. I have a tank. Do you want to play with me?" he asked.
"Hi, I'm Ben. I have a bow and arrow. Sure, I want to play with you," the boy said.
They smiled and became friends. They played with their toys and had a lot of fun. They pretended to be soldiers and heroes.
But then, it got dark. The sun went down and the sky turned black. Tom and Ben got scared. They could not see well. They heard strange noises. They wanted to go home.
"Let's go home, Tom. It's dark and scary here," Ben said.
"OK, let's go home, Ben. But where is my tank? I can't find it," Tom said.
They looked around, but they could not see the tank. It was too dark. They felt sad and worried. They lost the tank.
Suddenly, they saw a big shadow. It was a dog. It had something in its mouth. It was the tank. The dog had taken it. It growled and barked at them.
"Help, help, it's a dog. It has my tank. It's angry," Tom cried.
"Run, run, it's a dog. It's dangerous. It's coming," Ben shouted.
They ran away from the dog. They were very scared. They hoped the dog would not chase them.
But then, they saw a light. It was a flashlight. It was Tom's dad. He had come to look for them. He saw the dog and the tank. He shouted at the dog.
"Hey, hey, drop that tank. Leave those boys alone. Go away," he said.
The dog was afraid of the dad. It dropped the tank and ran away. It did not want to fight.
Tom and Ben were happy. They saw the dad and the tank. They ran to him. They hugged him and thanked him.
"Thank you, dad. You saved us. You found my tank. You are brave," Tom said.
"Thank you, dad. You helped us. You scared the dog. You are kind," Ben said.
The dad smiled and hugged them back. He gave them the tank. He was proud of them.
"You're welcome, boys. I'm glad you're safe. I'm sorry you got scared. You are good friends," he said.
He took them home. He gave them cookies and milk. He read them a story. He tucked them in bed.
They were happy and sleepy. They had a big adventure. They learned a lesson.
They said good night to each other. They said good night to the dad. They said good night to the tank. They said good night to the bow and arrow.
They closed their eyes and dreamed. They dreamed of playing again. They dreamed of being soldiers and heroes. They dreamed of being friends.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park with his dad. Tim had a new kite that he wanted to fly. The park was very quiet and the sky was blue.
Tim and his dad tried to fly the kite, but it would not go up. Tim was sad. His dad said, "Let's solve this problem, Tim." They thought and thought. Then, they saw a big tree with lots of wind.
Tim and his dad went to the tree and tried again. The kite went up, up, up! But then, something unexpected happened. The kite got stuck in the tree! Tim was very sad. His dad said, "Don't worry, we will get it down."
Tim and his dad found a long stick and used it to get the kite out of the tree. The kite was free and they could fly it again. Tim was happy and they had a fun day at the quiet park.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big gate. It was a sunny day, but suddenly it started to rain. Tim felt upset. He did not want to get wet.
Tim ran to the gate and stood under it. A little girl named Sue was also there. She said, "Hi, I am Sue. Do you like the rain?" Tim replied, "No, I am upset. I don't like getting wet."
Just then, the rain stopped, and the sun came out. Tim and Sue looked up and saw a big, beautiful rainbow. They were so happy! The rain had made something wonderful happen. They played together under the rainbow and forgot all about being upset.

Once upon a time there was a small girl called Polly. She loved to play with all the other children in the park, but she wanted to be smarter than all of them.
Every day, Polly would study for hours. She memorized her numbers and letters, and worked hard on her puzzles. But all the other kids in the park would laugh and call her 'nerdy'.
One day, Polly found a small glove in the park. She was so excited that she wanted to keep it forever! She decided to study extra hard with her new glove, so she could show everyone that it was worth something.
She worked and worked, and soon everyone could see that Polly was really smart. The other children stopped calling her names, and instead asked her to help them with puzzles. Polly felt so proud of herself. 
The moral of the story is that nothing is more valuable than hard work. If Polly had not studied hard, she would not have earned the respect of the other children. So, hard work and dedication will always help you in the long run.

Once upon a time, a girl named Sue had a pair of expensive mittens. They were red and soft. Sue loved her mittens very much. She wore them every day when it was cold outside.
One day, Sue wanted to help her mom. Her mom was cutting paper. Sue took the scissors to cut too. But she made a mistake. She cut her expensive mitten! Sue was very sad.
Then, something unexpected happened. Sue's mom looked at the cut mitten and smiled. She took the mitten and sewed it back together. But this time, she added a pocket to the mitten. Now, Sue had a special mitten with a pocket to keep her little toy in. Sue was happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a brave little girl called Milly. Milly lived by the sea and loved to play in the sand on sunny days.
One day, Milly woke up to find it was raining. She went outside and saw the tide was high. But Milly was brave. She put on her boots and danced around in the rain.
The rain got heavier and Milly's boots filled with water. As the tide got higher, the rain kept coming down. The brave little girl kept on dancing until the tide went back down.
It stopped raining and the tide went back out. Milly dried her boots, changed her clothes and went off to play in the sand at the beach. She was happy and brave, just like always. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a big tree. It was in a small forest. Many animals lived in the tree. They were all friends. One day, a man came to the forest. He had an axe. He wanted to cut the big tree down.
The animals were scared. They did not want to lose their home. They thought of a plan. They found a fragile, thin branch. They decided to tear the branch from the tree. They put it in front of the man with the axe.
The man saw the fragile branch. He thought the whole tree was weak. He did not want to cut it down anymore. The animals were happy. They saved their home. The big tree stayed in the forest, and the animals lived happily ever after.

Once there was a boy named Tim. He was only three years old but he was always very careful. One day, his parents told him about a special film they had for him to see. Tim was so excited! He asked his parents to call the film so he could watch it.
They said yes and called the film for him. When it finally arrived, Tim put it in the DVD player and he was so excited. He watched the whole film and he was so happy. He was careful to not touch the DVD, because he didn't want to break it.
When the film was finished, Tim was really tired. His parents were so proud of him for being careful while watching the film. They turned off the DVD player and said goodnight to him. Tim went to bed with a big smile on his face, happy to have watched his special film.

One day, a boy named Tim found a stick in the park. He thought it was a tough stick. Tim wanted to show his friend, Sam, the stick. So, he went to Sam's house.
When Tim got to Sam's house, he said, "Look, Sam! I found a tough stick in the park!" Sam looked at the stick and thought it was cool. They decided to play with the stick.
They played a game where they had to clap the stick on the ground. Each time they clapped the stick, they laughed and had fun. They played with the tough stick all day and had a great time.

Once upon a time, there was a humble dog named Spot. Spot loved to play in his yard. He would run, jump, and bark with joy.
One day, a little bird named Tweet flew into Spot's yard. Tweet was all alone and wanted to find a friend. Spot saw Tweet and wagged his tail. He wanted to play with Tweet.
Spot asked Tweet, "Will you join me and play in my yard?" Tweet was so happy and said, "Yes, I will join you!" They had lots of fun playing together. From that day on, Spot and Tweet were the best of friends.

Once upon a time there was a strong bear who lived in the forest. One day, it felt brave and wanted to show its strength. It decided to release a very loud roar. The noise was so loud that it made all the animals run away. The bear was still feeling brave, so it released another roar. This time the noise was so loud that the trees in the forest began to shake. The loud noise even caused a strong wind to blow. The wind was so strong that it flattened the entire forest. The bear was now so scared that it ran away and never came back. The forest was never the same again.

Once upon a time, in a small country, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was an anxious boy who always wanted to leave his home and explore. One day, he asked his mom, "Can I leave the house and see the world?"
His mom said, "No, Tim. You are too little. You must stay here and be safe."
But Tim did not listen to his mom. He left the house when she was not looking. He walked and walked until he saw a big tree. Tim wanted to see what was on the other side of the tree, but he was very tired. He sat down and fell asleep.
When Tim woke up, it was dark. He was scared and cried, "Mom! Mom! Where are you?" But his mom could not hear him. Tim was all alone in the dark, far from his home.
The moral of the story is to always listen to your mom and dad. They know what is best for you. If Tim had listened to his mom, he would not be lost and scared in the dark.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Sally. Sally loved to skip. Every day she would run around the garden in her bright apron and skip as much as she could.
Sally liked to play a game. She pretended to be different animals and would skip around like them. 
One day her mom called her from inside. "Sally! Come inside and take off your apron please."
"OK, mom," replied Sally. She skipped inside and took off her bright apron. She ran to find her toys and started to play. 
Sally loved skipping and playing in her bright apron. She always had so much fun in her garden.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a tiny, delicate bird named Lily. Lily loved to eat red berries that grew on a special bush. One day, she saw a big bush full of berries. She was very happy.
Lily said to her friend, Sam the squirrel, "Look, so many berries! Let's eat them!" Sam nodded and they started to eat. But suddenly, the berries began to vanish! They were scared and confused.
Then, they saw a little mouse named Max. Max was using a leaf to carry the berries away. They asked Max, "Why are you taking our berries?" Max said, "I am sorry, my family has no food. Can we share?" Lily and Sam agreed to share the berries with Max and his family. They all became good friends and were never hungry again.

Once upon a time, there was a small fish named Jenny. She liked swimming around her fishy friends in the big blue sea. One day, Jenny had a dream. In her dream, she spotted a net in the ocean. She was very scared and she wanted to help the other fish hide from it. 
When she woke up, Jenny quickly swam to find her friends and tell them about her dream. She warned them about the net and asked if they had seen it in the ocean. 
Just then, a compassionate dolphin swam up and said he had seen the net. He said the humans had placed it in the ocean to catch fish.
Jenny was very scared and started to cry. The dolphin told her bravely that she would need to do something to help and told her to watch the net closely.
As Jenny and her friends watched the net, they soon figured out an idea. They started to eat the net bit by bit until it was all gone. Jenny was so happy that she and her friends were able to save the ocean - all thanks to the dolphin's compassionate advice. 
And ever since that day, Jenny and her friends have been very careful with the nets that the humans put in the ocean to remind them to be brave and use their fishy brains.

Once upon a time, there was a tidy bus. It was very clean and shiny. The bus liked to help people go to different places. It was a happy bus.
One day, the bus had to change its way. There was a big tree in the road. The bus went a new way. It was fun for the bus to go a new way.
The bus met new friends on the new way. The friends were a cat, a dog, and a bird. They all liked the tidy bus. The bus took them to their homes. The bus was happy to help its new friends.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly dog named Spot. Spot loved to go for a walk every day. One sunny day, Spot asked his friend, a little bird named Tweet, to join him on his walk.
"Tweet, do you want to go for a walk with me?" Spot asked. Tweet said, "Yes, Spot! I would love to go for a walk with you!"
But before they could start their walk, dark clouds came and it started to rain. Spot and Tweet had to delay their walk. They went under a big tree to stay dry. They waited and waited for the rain to stop.
Finally, the rain stopped and the sun came out again. Spot and Tweet were very happy. They started their walk, jumping and playing along the way. They had a fun and jolly time together, and they couldn't wait to go for another walk the next day.

Once upon a time, there was a big, thick frog. The frog lived near a small pond. Every day, he liked to jump and splash in the water. He had a lot of fun playing in the pond.
One day, as the frog was jumping and splashing, he saw a little fish. The fish was swimming in the water. The frog said, "Hello, little fish! Let's play together!" The fish swam around the frog, and they played together in the pond.
After playing, the frog felt hungry. His stomach made a loud noise. The little fish heard the noise and said, "You must be hungry, frog! Let's find some food to eat." So, they swam together to find food. They found some yummy bugs to eat. After eating, they played some more and had a great day at the pond.

Once upon a time, there was a little rabbit named Bob. Bob loved to dig holes. One day, he dug a big hole in his garden. He thought it was the most beautiful hole he had ever dug.
Bob's friend, a squirrel named Sam, came to see the hole. Sam said, "I don't like your hole, Bob." Bob was sad that Sam didn't like his hole. They disagreed about the hole. But Bob still loved his hole very much.
Then, something unexpected happened. Bob found a big, delicious apple in the hole. He shared it with Sam. Sam took a bite and said, "Wow, this apple is so delicious! I was wrong, Bob. Your hole is great!" And from that day on, Bob and Sam were the best of friends, and they always agreed on everything.

One day, a boy named Tim saw a big ship near the sea. He wanted to climb it and see what was inside. The ship was very tall, but Tim was not afraid.
As Tim started to climb, he met a weak bird on the ship. The bird said, "Hello, I am weak and cannot fly. Can you help me find food?" Tim said, "Yes, I will help you."
Tim and the bird looked for food on the ship. They found some bread and gave it to the bird. The bird ate the bread and felt stronger. The bird said, "Thank you, Tim. I can fly now!" Tim was happy he could help the bird. They played together on the ship all day.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a mysterious book. The book had many pages. Tim loved to whistle while he read the pages.
As Tim read and whistled, a big bird heard him. The bird liked Tim's whistle. It came to see the book too.
Tim and the bird read the book together. They had fun and whistled all day. The mysterious book made them happy.

Lily and Tom were twins who liked to play in the park. One day, they saw a big slide that looked very fun. Lily wanted to go first, but Tom said no. He said he was older and stronger, and he pushed Lily aside.
"Stop it, Tom! That is not nice!" Lily said. She was angry and sad.
"I don't care. I want to slide!" Tom said. He climbed up the ladder and sat on the slide. He smiled and waved at Lily. "Bye bye, Lily!" he said.
But then, something bad happened. The slide was broken and had a sharp edge. When Tom slid down, he cut his leg on the edge. He felt a lot of pain and started to scream.
"Ow, ow, ow! Help me, help me!" he screamed.
Lily heard Tom's scream and ran to him. She saw his leg bleeding and felt worry. She did not know what to do. She looked around and saw a lady sitting on a bench. The lady had gray hair and glasses. She looked wise and kind.
"Help, help! My brother is hurt!" Lily shouted to the lady.
The lady heard Lily and came to them. She saw Tom's leg and shook her head. She said, "Oh, dear. This is very bad. He needs a doctor right away. Where are your parents?"
Lily and Tom did not have parents. They lived in a home with other children who had no parents. They only had a mean lady who took care of them. She did not like them and did not let them go to the park. They had sneaked out when she was not looking.
"We have no parents," Lily said. "We live in a home."
The lady felt sorry for them. She said, "That is very sad. But you still need to go to the hospital. Come with me. I will take you there."
She took Lily and Tom by the hand and led them to her car. She put Tom in the back seat and wrapped his leg with a cloth. She told Lily to sit next to him and hold his hand. She started the car and drove away.
But the lady was not wise and kind. She was evil and cruel. She did not take them to the hospital. She took them to a dark and scary place. She locked them in a room with no windows and no toys. She said, "You are mine now. You will never see the park or the home again. You will work for me and do what I say. If you scream or cry, I will hurt you more."
Lily and Tom were very scared and sad. They wished they had never gone to the slide. They wished they had listened to the mean lady who took care of them. They wished they had someone who loved them and helped them. But they had no one. They were alone and trapped. They did not know what would happen to them. They hugged each other and cried. The end.

Lily and Max were twins who liked to play with their toys. They had many toys of different colors and shapes, but they did not like to share them. Sometimes they would fight over who got to play with what toy, and make a lot of noise.
One day, their mom told them to clean up their toys and put them in the big box. She said they had to be organized and not leave their toys all over the floor. Lily and Max did not want to clean up, they wanted to keep playing. They started to argue and pull the toys from each other.
"Mine!" Lily shouted, holding a yellow ball.
"No, mine!" Max shouted, grabbing a blue car.
"Stop it, both of you!" their mom said, looking angry. "You have to learn to share and cooperate. If you don't clean up your toys, you will have no dessert tonight."
Lily and Max did not like the sound of that. They loved dessert, especially chocolate cake. They looked at each other and decided to stop fighting. They started to put their toys in the big box, one by one. They even helped each other find the matching pieces and sort them by color.
"Good job, Lily and Max!" their mom said, smiling. "You are very organized and smart. Now you can have some dessert and a story before bed."
Lily and Max were happy. They ate their chocolate cake and listened to their mom read them a story about a brave knight and a friendly dragon. They yawned and cuddled with their mom. They said good night and fell asleep, dreaming of their toys and adventures.

Anna and Ben are playing in the park. They have a bottle of bubble soap and a wand. They want to make big bubbles that fly in the air.
Anna goes first. She dips the wand in the soap and blows. A small bubble comes out. It pops on the grass. "That was not a big bubble," Anna says. "Let me try again."
She tries again. She blows harder. A bigger bubble comes out. It flies in the air for a moment. Then a bird flies by and pops it. "Oh no," Anna says. "The bird popped my bubble."
Ben takes the wand. He dips it in the soap and blows. A very big bubble comes out. It is bigger than his head. It flies in the air and shines in the sun. "Wow," Ben says. "Look at my bubble. It is so big and pretty."
Anna claps her hands. She wants to touch the bubble. She runs after it. She reaches out her hand. She is very close to the bubble. She is about to touch it. But then a gust of wind blows the bubble away. It flies over the fence and out of sight. "Oh no," Anna says. "The wind took your bubble."
Ben and Anna are sad. They have no more bubbles. They look at the bottle of soap. It is empty. They look at each other. They hug. They are still friends. They decide to play something else. They see a swing. It is open. They run to the swing. They take turns. They push each other. They laugh. They have fun. They forget about the bubbles. They are happy.

Once upon a time, in a small garden, there was a kind beetle. The beetle had many friends. They played together every day.
One day, the beetle saw a little girl crying. The beetle wanted to help. It went to the girl and tried to make her smile. The beetle danced and jumped around.
The girl saw the beetle and started to smile. She picked up the beetle and thanked it. The girl and the beetle became friends. They played together in the garden every day.

Joe and his mum were in the garden. Joe was looking at a picture of a cat on the wall.
Mum smiled and said, "That's a funny picture isn't it Joe?"
Joe nodded and smiled.
The sky was getting dark. Then all of a sudden Joe saw a big flame in the sky! Joe was scared.
Mum said, "Look Joe, it's ok, it's just the sun burning the sky."
Joe felt better and watched the sun quietly.
The sky lit up in bright colours, oranges, greens and pinks. Joe clapped with excitement.
Mum smiled and said, "That's a funny picture in the sky isn't it Joe?"
Joe smiled and nodded.

Once upon a time, there was a painter. He liked to paint all day. He painted with many colors. One day, he was very fearful. He heard a loud noise close to him.
The painter saw a big bird. The bird said, "Do not be scared. I can help you paint." The painter was happy. He gave the bird a brush. They painted together.
As they painted, the bird's wings made the colors mix in a new way. The painter was amazed. He had never seen such beautiful colors. From that day on, they painted together and made the most wonderful art.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, named Linda and Jack. They were walking in the meadow, under the bright sky. Suddenly, Linda saw something strange and unknown on the grass. She quickly told Jack to come and see.
Jack slowly came closer and saw a small flea. Linda said: "This unknown flea looks so cute, let us keep it as a pet!" Jack agreed, and without hesitation, he carefully put the flea into his pocket.
They continued their walk, and as they went deeper, they heard loud noises from the nearby bushes. Suddenly, something big and unknown jumped out. Linda and Jack looked closer, and realized it was a bigger flea!
Jack shouted "No way! How could you meet a flea bigger than you?" Linda got scared, and started crying. Jack quickly took the small flea out of his pocket and waved it towards the bigger flea.
The bigger flea stopped, and then it slowly backed away. Jack and Linda watched as it jumped away, back into the bushes.
Linda was so happy and relieved, she quickly hugged Jack, thanked him and said "We must never forget this brave meet between the small and the big fleas!"

One day, a proud little chair stood in the middle of the room. It was a very nice chair. The chair liked to be admired by everyone who came to visit.
A little girl named Lucy saw the chair and said, "Wow! I admire your pretty color, chair!" The chair was very happy and proud that Lucy liked it. 
Lucy sat on the chair and played with her toys. She told the chair, "You are my favorite chair!" The chair felt so proud and happy. They became best friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a playful bear. He lived in a big forest. The bear loved to play all day with his friends.
One day, the bear saw a big tree. He had a question. "What is inside the tree?" he asked. He looked and looked, but could not see inside. So, he decided to climb the tree to find out.
When he got to the top, the bear saw a big surprise. There was a big, shiny toy! The bear was so happy. He thought the tree was magic. But then, he saw his friend the squirrel. The squirrel said, "That is my toy!" The bear was sad, but gave the toy back. They played with the toy together and became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play in the dirt. One day, he saw a tiny seed on the ground. Tim thought, "I want to help this seed grow big and strong." So, he dug a small hole and put the seed inside. Tim's hands were filthy from the dirt, but he didn't care.
Every day, Tim would go outside to encourage the seed to grow. He would say, "Grow, little seed, grow!" Tim gave the seed water and made sure it had sunlight. He knew that the seed needed these things to grow. Tim also knew that something special was going to happen soon.
One day, Tim went outside and saw a beautiful flower had grown from the seed. He was so happy! The flower was very pretty, and Tim knew that his hard work had paid off. He had helped the seed grow into a lovely flower by taking care of it every day. Tim learned that with love and care, he could help make wonderful things happen.

One day, a big, graceful fish arrived at the sea. This fish was a shark. The shark was very nice and wanted to make friends with the other fish in the sea.
The shark swam around and said "hi" to all the little fish. The little fish were scared at first, but they saw the shark was kind. So, they started to play with the shark. They had a lot of fun swimming and playing together.
After a while, the sun started to go down. It was time for the shark and the little fish to go home. They said "bye" to each other and went to sleep. The shark was happy to have new friends in the sea.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a polite bird named Bobo. Bobo loved to fly high and soar in the sky. One day, Bobo found a message on the ground. The message said that there was a big party in the forest.
Bobo was very excited to go to the party. He told all his friends about the message. They all wanted to go to the party too. So, they all got ready and started to fly towards the party.
When they got to the party, they saw that it was very dark. They were scared, but they went inside. Suddenly, a big net fell on them. They were trapped! The party was a trap set by a bad man who wanted to catch all the birds. Bobo and his friends could not fly and soar anymore. They were very sad, and they wished they had never found the message.

Lily has a black cat. His name is Tom. Lily likes to speak to Tom. She tells him stories and secrets. Tom listens and purrs.
Yesterday, Lily and Tom went to the park. They saw many things. They saw birds and dogs and flowers. Lily spoke to Tom about them. Tom watched and meowed.
But then, Lily saw a big slide. She wanted to try it. She left Tom under a tree and ran to the slide. She climbed up and slid down. It was fun.
But when she looked for Tom, he was gone. Lily felt sad and scared. She called his name. She ran around the park. She could not find him.
Then, she heard a meow. She followed the sound. She saw Tom in a bush. He was stuck. He had a black thorn in his paw. Lily ran to him. She spoke to him softly. She pulled out the thorn. Tom licked her hand. He was happy.
Lily hugged Tom. She was happy too. She said sorry for leaving him. She said she loved him. Tom purred. He loved her too. They went home together. They were friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside. One day, he saw a big, rough rock with a crack in it. He thought it was a magic rock.
Tim showed the rock to his mom. "Mom, look at this rock! Can it make things vanish?" he asked. His mom said, "I don't know, Tim. Why don't you try it?"
Tim took the rock and wished for his toy to vanish. To his surprise, the toy was gone! He was happy, but soon he missed his toy. He wished for it to come back, and it did. Tim learned that making things vanish is not always good. It's better to be happy with what you have.

Once upon a time, there was a pink fish named Fin. Fin lived in a big pond near a school. Every day, the children would come to the pond to see Fin and his friends.
One sunny day, the children brought a new friend to the pond. The new friend was a big, round balloon. The children wanted to release the balloon into the sky. But as they let go, the wind blew the balloon into the pond! Everyone was surprised.
Fin saw the balloon in the water and thought it was a new friend. He swam up to the balloon and gave it a gentle push. The balloon floated back up to the surface, and the children were happy. They thanked Fin for helping them, and they all had a fun day at the pond.

One day, a little girl named True went for a walk. She wanted to meet new friends. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing. True was very happy.
As she walked, she saw a big, scary cat. The cat looked at her and said, "Hi, I'm Scary. Do you want to be my friend?" True was scared, but she wanted to make friends. So, she said, "Yes, I want to be your friend."
True and Scary played together all day. They had so much fun. They ran, jumped, and laughed. True learned that even if something looks scary, it can still be a good friend.

Once upon a time, there was a graceful bird named Bella. Bella loved to fly in the sky. She had many friends. Bella's best friend was a little girl named Amy. Amy and Bella would play together every day.
One day, Bella and Amy were playing near the dark forest. Amy was scared of the dark. Bella said, "Don't worry, I will be with you. Believe in me." Amy smiled and felt brave with Bella by her side.
They went into the dark forest together. They found a big tree and climbed it. They saw many pretty things. They played, laughed, and had fun. Then, they went home. Amy was happy she had a friend like Bella who made her believe in herself.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play at the park. Her favorite thing to do was to swing on the swings. One sunny day, Sue put on her pink dress and went to the park.
At the park, Sue saw her friend Tom. Tom was alert and looking for someone to play with. Sue said, "Hi Tom! Do you want to swing with me?" Tom smiled and said, "Yes, I would love to swing with you, Sue!"
Sue and Tom took turns pushing each other on the swings. They laughed and had so much fun. After they were done swinging, they played hide and seek. Sue hid behind a big tree, and Tom tried to find her. They played all day until it was time to go home. Sue and Tom said goodbye and promised to play together again soon.

Once there was a very successful man. He was a model to many because of his hard work. He wanted to help others in need, and offered free healing to those who had not been as successful.
One day, a three-year-old boy came to him for help. The boy's father had become sick, and he was very scared.
The successful man asked the boy, "What do you want me to do?"
The boy replied, "I want you to heal my dad. Please help him."
The successful man smiled and said, "I will do my best to help your dad." He put his hand on the boy's shoulder and said, "Your dad will be OK. I promise."
The successful man worked very hard, and eventually the boy's father was healed. The boy was so happy to have his dad back, and he thanked the successful man for helping him.
From then on, the successful man became an even better model for others to follow. He showed that with hard work and determination, you can help others to heal.

Once upon a time, in a small gray hut, there lived a kind old lady. She had a big heart and loved to help others. The hut was in the woods, where the sun would shine through the trees.
One day, a little lost bird came to the hut. The old lady saw it and said, "Oh, poor bird! Let me help you." She took the bird inside the hut and gave it some food and water.
The bird was very happy and started to sing. The sun shone through the window, making the gray hut feel warm and bright. The old lady and the bird became good friends, and they lived happily in the little gray hut.

One day, a small boy named Tim went for a walk. He was a little fearful because it was his first time walking alone. But he was also excited to see new things.
As Tim walked, he saw a big, yummy pie on a table outside a house. He wanted to eat the pie, but he was too scared to take it. Tim thought the pie was for someone else.
Just then, a nice lady came out of the house. She smiled at Tim and said, "Don't be fearful, this pie is for you!" Tim was so happy and thanked the lady. He took the pie home to share with his family.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful trophy. It was all shiny, and the leaves and flowers on it sparkled. Whenever someone saw it, they smiled. But, the trophy always sat in a corner of the room, never being used.
That was until one day, when a three year old child saw the trophy in the corner. She smiled, and wanted to play with it. She went to pick it up and take it to the garden.
However, the child didn't know that the trophy was very special and shouldn't be moved. As soon as she touched it, the trophy folded in half! She was very sorry, and started to cry.
Still, the child was determined to make the trophy look beautiful again. She told her mama and papa and they were amazed! After a few repairs, the trophy looked just like it did before, even better!
From then on, the trophy was used to celebrate special occasions. The three year old was very proud of her work and she became famous in her neighbourhood. Everyone was impressed with the beautiful trophy.

One day, a girl named Sue went for a walk. She liked to skip and jump. While she skipped, she saw a pretty rock. The rock had a symbol on it. Sue did not know what the symbol meant, but she liked it. She took the rock home with her.
That night, Sue put the pretty rock by her bed. She went to sleep and had a dream. In the dream, the rock with the symbol talked to her. The rock told her that it was magic. It could make her skip very high, higher than anyone else.
The next day, Sue went outside. She held the pretty rock and started to skip. With each skip, she went higher and higher. Sue was so happy! She skipped all around town, showing everyone her magic rock. And from that day on, Sue was known as the girl who could skip higher than anyone else.

Tom and Sue were friends who liked to play near the stream. The stream had many fish and frogs and rocks. One day, they saw a big log on the side of the stream. It was brown and soft and had holes.
"Let's play on the log!" Tom said. He ran to the log and climbed on it.
"No, Tom, don't!" Sue said. She was scared of the log. She thought it was old and bad. "The log is rot. It can break and hurt you."
But Tom did not listen to Sue. He jumped on the log and laughed. He said, "The log is fun. It is like a boat. Come on, Sue, join me!"
Sue shook her head. She stayed away from the log. She said, "The log is not fun. It is poor. It can fall and make you wet."
But Tom did not listen to Sue. He jumped on the log again and again. He said, "The log is strong. It can hold me. You are silly, Sue, you are afraid of nothing!"
But Sue was right. The log was not strong. It was rot. It could not hold Tom. The next time Tom jumped on the log, it broke in half. Tom fell into the stream. He got wet and cold and dirty. He cried and screamed.
Sue ran to Tom and helped him out of the stream. She said, "I told you, Tom, the log was rot. It was poor. It could not hold you. You should have listened to me."
Tom was sorry. He said, "You were right, Sue, the log was rot. It was poor. It could not hold me. I should have listened to you. I am sorry, Sue, can you forgive me?"
Sue was kind. She said, "I forgive you, Tom, but you have to be careful. You have to look before you leap. You have to listen to your friends. They want to help you."
Tom nodded. He said, "I will be careful, Sue. I will look before I leap. I will listen to my friends. They want to help me. Thank you, Sue, for being my friend."
Tom and Sue hugged. They went home. They learned a lesson. They never played on the rot log again.

Once upon a time there were two friends, John and Lily. They were both very young and loved to play together. One day, John challenged Lily to a fight. Lily was scared and said no. John kept pressuring her, so she said "Okay, but only if you wink at me!"
John wasn't sure what to do. Lily had never asked for a wink before. But John's wealthy friend, who was always encouraging him to be brave, said "Come on John, show her what you can do!"
So John winked, and Lily smiled. But then, something unexpected happened. They both started laughing and forgot all about the fight.
The moral of this story is that a little wink can go a long way. Sometimes, when we are faced with a challenge, it's better to share a smile than to fight.

Once upon a time, a big, impressive boat was on the sea. The boat liked to ride the waves up and down, up and down. One day, the boat saw a tiny fish in the water.
The tiny fish said, "I can guess your name, big boat!" The boat laughed and said, "You are too small to know my name!" The tiny fish smiled and said, "I will try!"
The tiny fish closed its eyes and thought very hard. Then, it said, "Your name is Big Blue!" The boat was so surprised! The tiny fish guessed the name right! The big, impressive boat and the tiny fish became best friends and rode the waves together forever.

Once upon a time, there was a creative little girl named Lily. She loved to draw and watch cartoons. Her favorite thing to do was to make her own cartoons and share them with her friends.
One day, Lily decided to give her new cartoon to her best friend, Tim. Tim was so happy to see the cartoon. He loved it so much that he wanted to be creative too. So, Tim started to draw his own cartoons, and he gave them to his friends as well.
Soon, all the kids in the neighborhood were making and giving their own cartoons to each other. They learned that sharing their creativity made everyone happy. The moral of the story is that when we give, we can inspire others and spread happiness.

Lily and Ben were hungry. They wanted to eat spaghetti for lunch. Mom said they could help her make it. She gave them a big pot and a wooden spoon.
"First, we need to gather some water," Mom said. "Can you fill the pot with water from the tap?"
Lily and Ben nodded. They took the pot to the sink and turned on the tap. The water was cold and clear. They watched the pot get full.
"Good job!" Mom said. "Now, we need to put the pot on the stove and make the water hot. Be careful, it will be very hot soon."
Mom put the pot on the stove and turned on the fire. The fire was red and yellow. Lily and Ben saw the water start to bubble.
"Next, we need to open the spaghetti box and break the spaghetti in half," Mom said. "Can you do that for me?"
Lily and Ben nodded. They took the spaghetti box from the cupboard and opened it. They saw many long, thin noodles inside. They broke them in half and put them in a bowl.
"Great!" Mom said. "Now, we need to wait for the water to boil. Then, we can put the spaghetti in the pot and cook it. It will take a few minutes."
Lily and Ben waited. They were very hungry. They smelled the spaghetti sauce that Mom had made. It smelled good.
"Can we taste the sauce, Mom?" Lily asked.
"Sure, but be careful, it might be hot," Mom said. She gave them each a small spoon and a little bit of sauce.
Lily and Ben tasted the sauce. It was yummy. They wanted more.
"Can we have more sauce, Mom?" Ben asked.
"Not now, honey. We have to save some for the spaghetti. It's almost ready," Mom said.
Lily and Ben were not happy. They wanted more sauce. They saw the big pot of sauce on the stove. It looked open.
"Let's get more sauce, Lily," Ben whispered.
"OK, Ben," Lily whispered back.
They tiptoed to the stove. They reached for the pot of sauce. They did not see the fire. They did not hear Mom.
They touched the pot. It was very hot. They screamed. The pot fell. The sauce spilled. The fire burned.
Mom heard them. She ran to the stove. She saw the mess. She saw the fire. She saw Lily and Ben. They were hurt. They were crying.
Mom was scared. She was angry. She was sad.
She grabbed Lily and Ben. She turned off the fire. She called for help.
She hugged Lily and Ben. She said sorry. She said she loved them.
But it was too late. The spaghetti was ruined. The lunch was ruined. The day was ruined.
The end.

Once upon a time there was a bunny called Fluffy. She loved going for walks in the park. One day, she was hopping around a bush when she heard a sound. Fluffy stopped and listened. A small voice said, "help me!".
Fluffy hopped closer to the bush and saw a small mouse. "What's the matter?" asked Fluffy. The mouse explained that he was very hungry but he was stuck under the bush.
Fluffy thought for a moment, then she remembered she had a carrot in her pocket. She quickly grabbed it and offered it to the mouse. The mouse was so happy and he quickly devoured it.
Fluffy helped the mouse out from under the bush and said goodbye. The mouse thanked her for her help and scurried away. Fluffy was glad she was able to help the little mouse, even though it was a dead carrot from her pocket.

One day there was a boy named Joe. He was only three years old and loved to explore. Joe was standing in front of an attractive door and he wanted to open it. Joe said to himself, "I wonder what's inside this door? I bet it's something I would love!"
Just then, his mom came around the corner and saw Joe in front of the door. "Joe, you can't go through that door," she said. "It's a door to a store and you aren't allowed to go in."
Joe was disappointed. "But why not mom?" he asked.
"Stores are made for adults," his mom replied. "They are meant to be places where adults go to buy things, so only adults are allowed to go inside."
Joe understood. He felt better knowing that he wasn't allowed in the store because it wasn't meant for him. He thanked his mom for enlightening him and instead of going inside the store, he went for a walk with his mom outside. 
The moral is: respect other people's boundaries and never try to enter a place that is not meant for you.

One day, a little girl named Mia went to the beach with her mom and dad. Mia loved the beach. She liked to play with the sand and look at the big blue sea. She saw a wild seal playing in the water. The seal was gray and had big, cute eyes.
Mia wanted to be friends with the seal. So, she went close to the water and waved her hand. The wild seal saw Mia and swam closer to her. Mia was happy and gave the seal a big smile. Then, she leaned down to give the seal a little kiss on its nose.
But, guess what? The wild seal was not a real seal! It was just a big toy that looked like a seal. Mia's mom and dad laughed and told Mia it was a toy. Mia was surprised but she still liked the toy seal. She played with it all day and gave it lots of kisses.

Once there was a boy named Jack. He wanted to be an artist when he grew up so he loved to paint.
He grabbed his paints from the shelf and he went to the upstairs. His mom was busy downstairs so he was there alone.
Jack painted for a long time and he was very careful. He chose the colors he wanted and made sure each brush stroke was perfect.
But then he got careless. He accidentally dropped his brush near the wall and paint dripped down.
Jack was sad, but he didn't give up. He found a cloth and carefully wiped away the paint on the wall. When he finished, it looked good as new. He smiled and went back to his painting.

Once upon a time, there was a big, filthy pot. It lived in a small house with a nice girl named Sue. Sue loved to play with her toys and have fun. But the filthy pot was always sad because it was too dirty to play with Sue.
One sunny day, Sue saw the filthy pot looking sad. She thought, "I will help the pot be clean and happy again." So, Sue took the pot outside to wash it. She used soap and water to make the pot clean and shiny. The pot was very happy and thanked Sue for her help.
Now that the pot was clean, Sue wanted to show it something fun. She took the pot's handle and lead it to her favorite tree. They climbed the tree together and looked at the pretty birds. The pot was so happy to be clean and play with Sue. From that day on, Sue and the pot were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max liked to play with his ball in the park. One day, Max saw a mask on the ground. He picked it up with his mouth and ran to his friend, a little girl named Lily.
Lily saw Max with the mask and said, "Max, that's a funny mask!" Max wagged his tail and gave the mask to Lily. She put the mask on her face and they both laughed.
Then, they saw a tree with big, red apples. Lily asked Max to help her get the apples. Max jumped high to reach the apples, but he could not. So, they asked a tall man to yield the apples for them. The man picked the apples and gave them to Lily and Max. They said thank you and shared the apples with their friends. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a girl named Amy. Amy had a toy car that she loved to play with. One day, the toy car broke, and Amy was very sad.
Amy's dad saw her sad face and said, "Don't worry, I can repair your toy car!" He took the toy car and worked on it for a minute. Amy watched him and felt alert, hoping her toy car would be fixed soon.
After a minute, Amy's dad gave her the toy car. It was fixed! Amy was very happy and said, "Thank you, Dad!" Now, Amy could play with her toy car again, and she was very alert to make sure it didn't break again. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a thin book. It was a novel. Tim wanted to show it to his friend, Sam. So, he ran to Sam's house.
When Tim got to Sam's house, he said, "Look, Sam! I have a thin book!" Sam looked at the book and said, "Wow! It is a novel! Let's read it together!"
So, Tim and Sam sat down and read the thin novel. They had a lot of fun. They laughed and talked about the story. When they finished, they said, "That was a great book!"

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Mark. Mark was a happy boy who loved to play with his toys. One day, he felt hungry and wanted a snack. He went to the kitchen and saw a big bowl of fruit. Mark picked up a banana and started to eat it. But, he felt uncomfortable in his tummy.
Mark went to his mom and told her about his uncomfortable tummy. His mom said, "Maybe you ate the snack too fast. Let's go for a walk and see if that helps." So, they went for a walk outside. As they walked, Mark saw something shiny on the ground. He picked it up and it was a magic key!
Mark and his mom were very surprised. They took the key home and tried it on every door in their house. Suddenly, a secret door opened! Inside, they found a room full of toys and yummy snacks. Mark's uncomfortable tummy went away, and he and his mom played and ate snacks all day. The magic key made their day full of fun and surprises.

Once upon a time, there was a little orange cat named Tuesday. Tuesday loved to play with his friends outside. One sunny day, he was playing with a big red ball.
Tuesday's friend, a small dog named Max, came running over. "Tuesday, I lost my toy," Max said, looking sad. Tuesday wanted to comfort his friend. "Don't worry, Max. We will find your toy," Tuesday said with a smile.
They looked high and low, and finally found the toy under a green tree. Max was so happy and hugged Tuesday. "Thank you, Tuesday!" Max said. They played together for the rest of the day, feeling happy and safe.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a door with a shiny knob. A little boy named Tim was very enthusiastic to open the door. He wanted to see what was behind it. He had never been in that room before.
One day, Tim decided to remove the knob. He turned and turned it until it came off. The door opened, and Tim saw a room full of colorful toys. He was so happy and excited!
Tim played with the toys all day. He shared them with his friends, and they all had lots of fun. Tim learned that sometimes, being enthusiastic and trying new things can lead to great surprises.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big mouth and liked to poke things. One day, she saw a green frog by the pond. The frog was envious of Lily's big mouth. It wanted to have a big mouth too, so it could eat more bugs.
Lily saw the frog and poked it with a stick. The frog jumped high into the air and landed on Lily's head. Lily laughed and the frog laughed too. They became good friends and played by the pond every day.
One day, when they were playing, the frog jumped into Lily's big mouth. Lily was surprised and didn't know what to do. But then, the frog jumped back out and started to laugh. The frog didn't want a big mouth anymore. It was happy just the way it was. And Lily and the frog stayed friends, playing and laughing together by the pond.

Once upon a time there were two best friends, Mike and Paul. One day they were walking in the park and noticed a big billboard by the side of the road.
Mike said to Paul, "Wow, that billboard looks heavy!" Paul smiled and said, "Let's make a wish on it!"
Mike asked, "What kind of wish should we make?" Paul said, "Let's make a wish that everyone in the world is happy and we look out for each other."
The boys made their wish and stepped back to admire the billboard. Suddenly, it started to glow and they knew that their wish had come true.
The two boys hugged each other and Paul said, "See? When we work together, we can make anything happen!"
The moral of the story is that working together can make wonders happen. Always remember to look out for each other and be kind to one another.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She had a pretty vase in her room. The vase was her favorite thing. It was blue and had flowers on it.
One day, Mia's mom told her that the vase was very intelligent. Mia was very happy to hear this. She loved to talk to her vase and tell it stories. The vase would listen and sometimes it seemed to smile at her.
Mia and her intelligent vase became best friends. They would play together, and Mia would always tell her vase her secrets. They were happy, and Mia knew that her vase was special. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a green frog named Bob. He lived in a small pond. Bob had a special vest that he loved to wear. One day, he put on his vest and went to play with his friend, the fish.
"Hi, fish!" said Bob. "Do you like my vest?"
Fish looked at the vest and said, "Yes, it is very nice, Bob!"
As they played, the sky seemed to get dark. Bob and Fish looked up and saw big, gray clouds. They knew it was going to rain soon.
"Let's go home, Fish. It seems like it will rain," said Bob. So, they said goodbye and went to their homes. Bob was happy that he had his vest to keep him warm during the rain.

One day, a little girl named Mia went to the park. She saw a big slide that looked mysterious. Mia wanted to discover what was at the top of the slide. She climbed up, up, up the steps.
At the top of the slide, Mia found a tiny kitten. "Meow," said the kitten. Mia was surprised to find the kitten there. She asked the kitten, "Are you lost?" The kitten looked at her with big, sad eyes.
Mia decided to help the kitten. She picked it up and they both went down the slide together. They had so much fun! When they reached the bottom, Mia's mommy was waiting for them. The unexpected thing was that Mia's mommy said they could keep the kitten. Mia was so happy! She named the kitten Slide because they found each other on the mysterious slide.

One day, a red cat named Tim was playing in the rain. He loved to jump in wet puddles and make a big splash. Tim saw his friend, a dog named Max, and called out, "Max, come play with me!"
Max came running and joined Tim in the puddles. They were having so much fun, but they didn't see the big wet rock nearby. Tim jumped and slipped on the wet rock, falling down with a thud. "Ouch!" he cried.
Max helped Tim get up and said, "Be careful, Tim. We should look where we jump." Tim learned to be more careful when playing in puddles. Now, Tim and Max always watch for wet rocks, so they don't slip and fall.

Once upon a time, there was a little spider. The spider had many brothers and sisters. They all lived in a big web in a nice garden.
One day, the little spider wanted to be proud. So, the spider decided to behave very well. The spider helped the other spiders in the web. It shared its food and played nice games with them.
Soon, all the spiders in the web were very happy. They liked the little spider a lot. The little spider was very proud of itself. And they all lived happily together in their big web.

Once upon a time, there was a foolish bear who lived in the woods. One day, he was very curious and saw a needle sitting on a tree stump. He was determined to get it, so he pulled and pulled until he could reach it.
The bear held the needle in his hand and admired it. Suddenly, a rabbit appeared and said, "What do you need a needle for?" The bear thought for a moment and replied, "It looks like a nice toy to play with!"
The rabbit shook his head and said, "That's a foolish thing to do! You could hurt yourself!" But, the bear was very stubborn and refused to listen. He just kept on playing with the needle.
After a while, the bear began to feel a sharp pain in his paw. He looked down to see blood dripping from a small hole in his paw. He realized the foolish thing he did and decided to never play with a needle again. The moral of the story is - don't be foolish!

Once upon a time, there was a little octopus. This octopus loved to cook. Every day, he cooked warm food for his friends in the sea.
One day, while the octopus was cooking, a big fish came. The big fish wanted to eat the octopus. The octopus was scared, but he had an idea.
The octopus said, "Wait! I can cook warm food for you too!" The big fish thought about it and said, "Okay." So, the octopus cooked warm food for the big fish. The big fish liked the warm food so much, he became friends with the octopus. From that day on, they cooked warm food together and were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly cat named Tom. Tom liked to sit near the window and watch the birds fly by. He would wave his tail and smile at the birds.
One sunny day, Tom saw a little bird drop a shiny rock outside the window. Tom wanted to play with the shiny rock. So, he jumped out of the window and ran to the rock.
Tom played with the shiny rock all day. He was very jolly and happy. When it was time to go home, Tom took the shiny rock with him. He put it near the window so he could look at it every day. And Tom and the shiny rock lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot had four legs. One day, Spot hurt one of his legs while playing outside. Spot felt very sad and tired.
Spot's mom wanted to help him feel better. She said, "Let's test your leg, Spot." Spot's mom touched his leg very gently. Spot did not cry or bark. His mom said, "Good job, Spot! Your leg will be okay."
