Sue sees that Tim is lying. She is worried about him. She knows he needs help. She decides to tell Mommy anyway. She runs to the bench where Mommy is sitting. She tells Mommy what happened.
Mommy is shocked and scared. She runs to Tim and picks him up. She sees his wounds and feels his fever. She says they need to go to the doctor. She says Tim might have an infection. She says Tim was very foolish and brave.
Tim feels ashamed and scared. He wishes he had listened to Sue. He wishes he had told Mommy. He wishes he had not gone on the slide. He cries and says he is sorry. But it is too late. He has to go to the doctor. He has to get medicine and bandages. He has to stay in bed for a long time. He can't play with Sue anymore. He has a bad ending.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch films on his TV. One day, he found a dirty film under his bed. Tim was sad because he could not watch the dirty film.
Tim went to his mom and started to cry. His mom asked him why he was crying. Tim showed her the dirty film. His mom smiled and said, "Don't worry, Tim. We can clean the film, and then you can watch it."
Tim and his mom cleaned the dirty film together. After they cleaned it, Tim could watch the film on his TV. He was very happy. Tim learned that if he keeps his things clean, he can enjoy them more.

Once upon a time, there was a tidy dragon. The dragon was big and green. The dragon liked to soar high in the sky. He lived in a clean cave.
One day, the dragon met a little bird. The bird said, "Hi, dragon! Let's play!" The dragon said, "Okay, let's soar!" So, the dragon and the bird soared high in the sky. They had fun.
But, the dragon did not see a big tree. He hit the tree and fell down. The bird was sad. The dragon could not soar anymore. The end.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a humble man named Tom. Tom was very kind and always helped others. One day, Tom received a gift from his friend, Sam. The gift was a shiny razor.
Tom was very happy to get the razor. He used it to cut his hair and make it neat. He looked very nice now. Tom thanked Sam for the wonderful gift.
One day, Tom saw a little boy with messy hair. The boy looked sad. Tom wanted to help the boy. He used his humble razor to cut the boy's hair. The boy was very happy and thanked Tom. Everyone in the town loved Tom and his kind heart.

One day, a big car and a small car were on the road. The big car was going very fast. The small car was going slow. The big car did not like this. It wanted to go fast.
The big car said to the small car, "Why are you going so slow? I want to go fast!" The small car replied, "I am low on gas. I need to find a gas place." The big car was not happy. It wanted the small car to go fast so it could go fast too.
The small car found a gas place. It got more gas and felt comfortable. The big car was happy now. The small car went faster. The big car went faster too. They both went fast on the road, and the big car said, "Thank you, small car!" The small car replied, "You're welcome, big car!" And they both went on their way, happy and fast.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom, dad, and her white cat, Snowy. One sunny day, Lily wanted to go to the park to play with her friends.
Before leaving, Lily's mom said, "Lily, don't forget your towel." Lily picked up her blue towel and ran to the park with Snowy. At the park, Lily saw her friends, Jack and Emma. They were having a picnic and studying the ants on the ground.
"Look, Lily!" said Jack. "The ants are working hard." Lily put her towel on the grass and sat down to study the ants too. Snowy sat next to her and watched the ants as well. They all learned how the ants worked together to carry food.
After a while, Lily and her friends got tired. They laid down on their towels and looked at the sky. Snowy curled up on Lily's towel and took a nap. They all had a fun day at the park, and Lily learned something new about ants.

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful spoon. The spoon lived in a big box with other spoons, forks, and knives. They all lived in the kitchen of a nice family.
One day, the cheerful spoon met a new friend named Bow. Bow was a pretty ribbon that lived in the little girl's room. Bow liked to play with the cheerful spoon, and they had a lot of fun together.
The cheerful spoon and Bow played games, sang songs, and danced. When they danced, Bow would spin around the spoon, making it look like the spoon was wearing a dress. They laughed and played all day, and they were very happy. And that is the story of the cheerful spoon and its friend, Bow.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved the color orange. She had an orange dress, an orange hat, and even an orange cat. One day, Mia saw a big orange ball in the sky. She thought it was a big orange candy. She wanted to taste it.
Mia asked her mom, "Can I taste the big orange ball in the sky?" Her mom laughed and said, "That's not candy, Mia. That's the sun. We cannot taste the sun, but we can enjoy its light and warmth."
Mia was sad, but then she saw an orange tree. The tree had lots of big, round orange fruits. Mia's mom picked one for her. "Here, taste this orange fruit. It will be sweet and yummy in your stomach."
Mia tasted the orange fruit and smiled. It was very sweet and juicy. She shared it with her orange cat, and they both loved it. From that day on, Mia and her cat would eat orange fruits together under the big orange ball in the sky. And they were very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toy car. One day, she wanted to make a loop for her car to drive on. She asked her mom, "Can I make a loop, please?" Her mom said, "I permit you to make a loop."
Lily was happy and started to make the loop. But it was not easy. She tried and tried, but the loop kept falling down. This made Lily upset. She did not want to give up. She asked her mom for help.
Her mom came and helped her make the loop. With her mom's help, the loop stayed up! Lily was very happy. She played with her toy car on the loop all day long. And when she was upset, she remembered that her mom was always there to help her.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a boy named Tom. Tom loved to eat pears. One day, he found a big pear tree near his house. He was very happy and picked many pears from the tree.
Tom was a modern boy. He had a big machine that could freeze things. He thought, "I will freeze the pears, so I can eat them later." Tom put all the pears in the machine and waited for them to freeze.
After some time, Tom opened the machine to get the frozen pears. But he found that the machine was broken. All the pears were bad and could not be eaten. Tom was very sad because he could not eat his favorite pears anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a skeleton named Sam. Sam loved to dance and slip around. He was good at it. One day, Sam saw a tasty cake on a table. He wanted a bite, but he knew it was not his.
Sam thought about taking just a little piece. But then, he remembered that taking things without asking is wrong. So, Sam decided to wait and ask if he could have some cake later.
As Sam was dancing and slipping around, a girl named Sue came in. Sue saw the cake too. She asked Sam if he wanted to share the tasty cake with her. Sam was happy that he waited, and they both enjoyed the cake together. The moral of the story is that it's better to wait and ask than to take things without permission.

One day, a little bird named Tim dared to fly near a big house. He saw a tall chimney and wanted to take a closer look. Tim was a careful bird, so he did not fly too close to the chimney.
As Tim flew near the chimney, he saw a small cat named Tom sitting on the roof. Tom looked at Tim and said, "Hi, little bird! Are you being careful?"
Tim replied, "Yes, I am being careful. I just wanted to see the chimney." Tom and Tim talked and became friends. They played together on the roof and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a large cat. This cat loved to play with a ball of yarn. One day, the cat found a pretty pattern on the floor. It was made of yarn too! The cat was very happy.
The cat wanted to tie the yarn from the pattern to the ball of yarn. It tried and tried, but it was hard. The cat did not give up. It kept trying to tie the yarn.
At last, the cat tied the yarn. The large cat was so proud. It played with the ball of yarn and the pretty pattern all day. The cat had so much fun, and it was a very happy day.

Once there was a little girl who was very scared. She was scared of the dark. One night, a spirit appeared. She shouted "I hate you!" The spirit just laughed and said, "Why do you hate me, little one?"
The girl whispered, "I'm scared of you." The spirit said, "I understand but I don't want you to be scared. I'm here to help you." The girl said, "Really?" The spirit said, "Yes, I will protect you and keep you safe. I will never leave you alone."
The girl hugged the spirit and said, "Thank you. I don't hate you anymore." She knew that the spirit was her friend and she wasn't scared anymore. She felt safe and happy. The spirit smiled and they stayed together forever.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play ball with his friends. They had a lot of fun together. Tim was a very good player. He was successful when he played with his friends.
One day, Tim wanted to play with a new ball. He went to his mom and said, "I demand a new ball, please!" His mom told him, "You must be kind and say 'please' when you ask for something."
Tim thought about it and said, "Okay, Mom. Please, can I have a new ball?" His mom smiled and said, "Yes, you can have a new ball because you asked nicely."
Tim was very happy with his new ball. He played with his friends and they all had fun. Tim learned that being kind and saying 'please' is important. This made him a better person and a successful player.

One day, a girl named Sue was excited to help her mom. Her mom was sewing with a needle. Sue asked, "Can I include the red thread, mom?" Her mom said, "Yes, you can help with the red thread."
Sue was happy to help. She took the red thread and gave it to her mom. But then, something unexpected happened. The needle jumped out of her mom's hand and hid in the room. Sue and her mom looked everywhere.
Finally, Sue found the needle under the table. She said, "I found it, mom!" Her mom was proud of Sue. They both laughed and finished sewing together.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a hero. This hero was different. He liked to shoot balls into a hoop. Every day, he played with his friends.
One day, a new friend came to play. He said, "Hello, I want to play too." The hero was happy to have a new friend. They all played together and had fun.
As they played, the hero showed the new friend how to shoot the ball. The new friend tried and tried, and finally, he could shoot the ball into the hoop too. The hero and his friends were very happy. They played all day and became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a famous car. The car was made of shiny metal. It was very fast and everyone loved it. The car had a big race to go to, and it was excited.
On the day of the race, the car went to the track. There were many other cars there too. They all lined up and waited for the race to start. The famous car was a little scared, but it knew it could win.
The race began, and all the cars went very fast. The famous car raced as fast as it could. It passed all the other cars and won the race. Everyone cheered for the famous car. The car was very happy and proud.

Once upon a time, there was a big city. In the city, there was a small dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his ball. One day, Spot's ball rolled far away. Spot had to find his ball.
Spot went to the park. He saw a big tree. Spot thought, "Maybe my ball is there." He looked, but the ball was not there. Spot felt sad. Then, a bird said, "I saw your ball. It is near the big lake." Spot felt happy and urged to go to the lake.
Spot went to the big lake. He saw his ball near the water. He was very happy. Spot got his ball and went back to the city. Now, Spot could play again. And he lived happily ever after.

Tom and Sue like to play with their toy horse. They pretend it is a real horse and they ride on it. They take turns and share the horse. They have fun.
One day, they see a big horse in the field. It is brown and has a long mane. They want to touch it and feed it. They run to the fence and call the horse. The horse comes to them and sniffs their hands. They pet its nose and give it some grass.
"Can we ride the horse?" Tom asks Sue.
"I don't know. It is very big. Maybe it is not friendly," Sue says.
"Let's try. It looks nice. I want to go fast," Tom says.
He climbs over the fence and jumps on the horse's back. The horse does not like it. It neighs and bucks. It runs away with Tom on it. Sue watches and worries.
"Tom, come back! The horse is angry! You will fall!" Sue shouts.
But Tom cannot hear her. He holds on to the horse's mane. He is scared and dizzy. The horse runs faster and faster. It does not stop.
The horse reaches the road. A car is coming. The car does not see the horse. The horse does not see the car. There is a loud crash. Tom and the horse fly in the air. They land on the ground. They do not move.
Sue cries and cries. She runs to Tom and the horse. They are hurt. They are not breathing. They are gone. Sue is alone. She is very sad. She wishes she never saw the horse. She wishes Tom was still with her. She wishes they could play with their toy horse again.

Mary was a girl who was three years old. One day, her father gave her a gift. It was a toy that was hairy. Mary liked it very much. She laughed because it felt funny. She was excited to play with it.
She brought it to the kitchen and showed it to her mom. Mom liked it too. She said it looked funny. Mary hugged it and smiled. She was happy to have a new toy.
Mary and her mom went to the park. She played with her new toy there. She put it in the grass and watched it move around. She laughed because it was funny.
In the evening, Mary put her new hairy toy in her room. She hugged it and said good night. She was glad to have a new gift.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the store with his mom. They needed to buy food for dinner. Tim saw a big green broccoli and wanted it. His mom said no, but Tim felt frustrated.
When they got home, Tim's mom cooked dinner. Tim watched her cook. He saw the broccoli on the table and wanted it more. He tried to take it, but his mom said, "Wait for dinner, Tim."
At dinner, Tim's big sister ate all the broccoli. Tim was very sad and frustrated. His mom saw this and said, "Don't worry, Tim. We will get more broccoli next time." Tim smiled and knew he would get his broccoli soon.

One day, a clean cat named Tim went for a walk. He saw a marble on the ground. Tim thought it was a pretty marble, so he picked it up. He wanted to show it to his friend, Sam the dog.
When Tim arrived at Sam's house, he said, "Look what I found!" Sam looked at the marble and said, "Wow! That's a nice marble!" They played with the marble, rolling it back and forth.
Suddenly, the marble started to grow bigger and bigger! Tim and Sam were very surprised. The big marble turned into a bouncy ball. They laughed and played with the bouncy ball all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly boy named Tim. He liked to play outside and look at the pretty view of the trees and flowers. Every day, he would go out and run around in the big, green yard.
One sunny day, Tim found a big bag of seeds. He wanted to make the yard even prettier. So, he began to scatter the seeds all over the ground. He hoped that they would grow into beautiful plants.
After some days, the seeds started to grow. Tim was so happy to see the new plants. His yard was now more beautiful than ever. Tim loved to play outside even more with his new, pretty plants. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a wide tree with lots of leaves. Tim looked down and saw a small lizard on the ground. The lizard was green and had tiny feet. Tim wanted to play with the lizard.
Tim said, "Hi, lizard! Let's play together." The lizard looked at Tim and nodded. Tim picked up some leaves and started to scatter them around. The lizard ran through the leaves, making them fly in the air.
As they played, Tim and the lizard became good friends. They laughed and had fun under the wide tree. When it was time to go home, Tim said goodbye to his new friend. "Bye, lizard! I will come back and play with you again." The lizard waved its tail and Tim went home with a big smile on his face.

One day, a big fly was flying in the sky. The fly was very spoiled. It did not like to share its food or toys with other flies. The spoiled fly had a friend, a little cat. The cat was always tired and would yawn a lot.
One day, the spoiled fly and the little cat were playing outside. They saw a big, yummy cake. The fly wanted the cake all for itself, but the little cat was hungry too. The cat yawned and said, "Please, can we share the cake?"
Just as the spoiled fly was about to say no, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and took the cake away. The fly and the cat were both sad. They looked at each other and decided to find the cake together.
As they searched, the spoiled fly learned to share and be kind. When they finally found the cake, they shared it and were both very happy. From that day on, the spoiled fly was not spoiled anymore. The fly and the cat became best friends and always shared everything.

One day, a small cat named Tom went for a walk. He saw a beautiful view from the top of a hill. He liked the view very much. He saw many trees, flowers, and birds. Tom was very happy.
Then, he saw a little girl named Amy. Amy was sad. She lost her toy. Tom wanted to help Amy find her toy. "Don't worry, Amy," said Tom. "I will help you find your toy."
They looked everywhere for the toy. Suddenly, Tom saw something under a big rock. He went closer and saw that it was the toy! Tom was surprised because he didn't think the toy could be under the rock. He gave the toy to Amy. She was very happy and said, "Thank you, Tom, for helping me find my toy!"
Tom and Amy became good friends. They played together every day and enjoyed the beautiful view from the hill. And they always helped each other when they needed it.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max was a peaceful dog who loved to nap under the big tree in his yard. One day, his friend, a small bird named Sam, came to visit him.
"Max," said Sam, "I need your help. Can you promise to watch my nest while I find some food for my babies?" Max looked up from his nap and said, "Yes, Sam, I promise to watch your nest and keep your babies safe."
While Sam was away, Max tried to stay awake, but he was so tired. He decided to close his eyes for just a moment. Soon, Max was fast asleep. When Sam returned, he found that Max had not kept his promise. A sneaky cat had come and taken the baby birds away.
Sam was very sad and angry with Max. "You broke your promise, Max! Now my babies are gone!" Max felt terrible and knew he had let his friend down. From that day on, Max never took a nap when he had made a promise to a friend.

Once upon a time, in a big green forest, there lived a raven. The raven had a big black body and liked to roll on the ground. One day, the raven met a small brown dog.
"Hi, I am a dog. I like to roll too," said the small brown dog. "Let's roll together and have fun!"
The raven and the dog rolled on the ground, laughing and playing. They rolled down a hill and found a small cheap toy. The toy was a red ball. The raven and the dog loved the red ball.
"Look at this cheap red ball!" said the raven. "We can roll it and play with it!"
So, the raven and the dog rolled the cheap red ball back up the hill. They played with the red ball all day long. The raven and the dog became best friends, and they rolled and played together every day.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. It was a dark day, but Tim did not complain. He saw a big pole in the park and went to it. He wanted to play with the pole.
A little girl named Sue came to the park too. She saw Tim by the pole and said, "Hi Tim! Can I play with the pole too?" Tim said, "Yes, let's play together!" They were very happy to play with the pole.
But then, something unexpected happened. The pole started to move! It was not a pole, but a very tall man! The man said, "Hello, kids! I am not a pole, I am Mr. Tall!" Tim and Sue were very surprised, but they became friends with Mr. Tall and played together all day.

Lily and Max were playing in the garden. They liked to look for bugs and flowers. They saw a big mushroom under a tree. It was brown and round and had white spots.
"Look, a mushroom!" Lily said. "Can we eat it?"
"No, no, no!" Max said. "Mushrooms can be bad. They can make you sick. We have to ask Mommy first."
But Lily was curious. She wanted to taste the mushroom. She reached out her hand and touched it. It felt soft and wet.
"Maybe just a little bite," she said. "It looks yummy."
She opened her mouth and bit the mushroom. But it was not yummy. It was bitter and sour and made her tongue tingle. She spit it out and made a face.
"Yuck! Yuck! Yuck!" she said. "This mushroom is icky!"
Max laughed. He thought Lily was funny. He gave her a hug and a kiss.
"Are you okay?" he asked. "Do you need some water?"
Lily nodded. She was frustrated. She wanted to find something good to eat. She looked around and saw a red berry on a bush.
"Look, a berry!" she said. "Can we eat it?"
Max shook his head. He remembered what Mommy had told him.
"No, no, no!" he said. "Berries can be bad too. They can have thorns or seeds or poison. We have to ask Mommy first."
But Lily was stubborn. She wanted to taste the berry. She reached out her hand and plucked it. It was shiny and juicy and smelled sweet.
"Maybe just a little bite," she said. "It looks yummy."
She opened her mouth and bit the berry. But it was not yummy. It was spicy and hot and made her throat burn. She spit it out and cried.
"Ow! Ow! Ow!" she said. "This berry is ouchy!"
Max felt sorry for her. He gave her another hug and another kiss.
"Are you okay?" he asked. "Do you need some milk?"
Lily nodded. She was frustrated. She wanted to find something good to eat. She looked around and saw a cookie on a plate.
"Look, a cookie!" she said. "Can we eat it?"
Max smiled. He knew what Mommy had said.
"Yes, yes, yes!" he said. "Cookies are good. They are sweet and crunchy and have chocolate. We can eat them. But we have to share."
Lily smiled too. She was happy. She wanted to share with Max. She broke the cookie in half and gave him one piece.
"Thank you," he said.
"You're welcome," she said.
They ate the cookie and enjoyed it. It was yummy. They gave each other a hug and a kiss.
"Let's go find Mommy," Max said.
"Okay," Lily said.
They ran to the house and found Mommy. They told her what they had done. Mommy was not angry. She was glad they were safe and happy. She gave them a hug and a kiss.
"I love you," she said.
"We love you too," they said.

One day, a cute dog went for a walk. He saw a big, open box. The dog was very curious. He wanted to see what was inside the box.
The dog said, "What will happen if I go inside the box?" He jumped into the open box. The dog was happy to see many toys inside. He played with the toys for a long time.
But then, the open box closed. The cute dog was stuck inside. He tried to push the box open, but it was too heavy. The dog was sad and scared. He could not get out of the box. The cute dog stayed inside the box, and no one found him.

Once upon a time, there was a small boy named Jack. He loved to trade things with his friends. One day, Jack had a very special trade. It was a bright yellow bottle. Jack had a great idea - he would trade the bottle and use it to get some delicious juice to drink.
Jack asked his best friend, Tim, if he wanted to trade. But when Tim saw the bright yellow bottle, he shook his head. "No, I don't think so," he said. "I don't know what's inside the bottle." 
Jack was a bit sad, but then he remembered your Grandma's wise words: "Be careful with the things you trade. Never trade something for something you don't know." Jack nodded and said to Tim, "You're right. I don't know what's inside the bottle. We shouldn't trade it." 
Tim smiled and said, "That's a good lesson. Let's be quiet and not tell anyone else about it. We don't want anyone else to make a mistake!"
Jack nodded in agreement. He was so glad he had listened to his Grandma and remembered her wise words. That's the moral of the story - be careful what you trade and always listen to your wise Grandma!

Tim and Liz liked to play in the old barn. They found many things to have fun with, like hay, ropes and boxes. One day, they saw a big barrel in a corner. They had never seen it before.
"Look, a barrel!" Tim said. "Let's roll it and see what's inside."
"OK, but be careful. It might be heavy or break," Liz said.
They pushed the barrel and it rolled with a loud sound. They followed it until it stopped near a door. They saw a small hole in the barrel and peeked inside.
"Wow, there are apples in here!" Tim said. "Lots of red and green apples. They look good."
"Maybe they are for the farmer. We should not take them," Liz said.
But Tim was hungry and curious. He reached his hand inside the barrel and grabbed an apple. He bit into it and smiled.
"Mmm, this is yummy. Try one, Liz. There are many available," he said.
Liz shook her head. She had a bad feeling. She looked at the door and saw a sign. It said: "Do not touch. These apples are for the cider press. They are sour and hard."
"Tim, stop! Those apples are not for eating. They are for making juice. They are bad for your teeth and tummy," she said.
But it was too late. Tim had eaten two more apples and his face turned red. He felt a pain in his mouth and his stomach. He dropped the apple and cried.
"Ow, ow, ow! These apples hurt me. I want to go home," he said.
Liz felt sorry for him. She helped him get out of the barn and ran to their mom. She told her what happened and mom gave Tim some water and a hug. She said he would be OK, but he had to brush his teeth and rest.
Tim learned his lesson. He never ate apples from a barrel again. He only ate apples from a tree or a basket. And he always listened to Liz. She was a good sister.

Tom and Anna were friends who liked to play together. They had many toys, but their favorite was a big navy boat. The boat was shiny and had many buttons and wheels. They pretended to be sailors and went on adventures.
One day, Tom and Anna wanted to play with the boat, but they could not find it. They looked in their rooms, in the living room, in the kitchen, and in the garden. They asked their moms and dads, but they did not know where the boat was.
They were very sad and bored. They did not want to play with anything else. They sat on the couch and sighed.
"Where is our boat?" Tom asked.
"I don't know. Maybe someone took it," Anna said.
"Who would do that? That's not nice," Tom said.
"Maybe it was Sam. He is always mean to us. He never lets us play with his toys," Anna said.
"Let's go and see. Maybe he has our boat," Tom said.
They put on their shoes and jackets and went to Sam's house. They knocked on the door and Sam's mom opened it.
"Hello, Tom and Anna. What can I do for you?" she asked.
"Hi, Sam's mom. Can we talk to Sam, please?" Tom asked.
"Sure, he is in his room. Come in," she said.
They followed her to Sam's room and saw him playing with their boat. He had put stickers and glue on it and made it look ugly.
"Hey, that's our boat!" Tom shouted.
"No, it's mine. I found it in the trash," Sam lied.
"That's not true. You took it from our house. Give it back!" Anna said.
"No, I won't. It's mine now. Go away!" Sam said.
He pushed them out of his room and slammed the door. Tom and Anna were very angry and sad. They ran to Sam's mom and told her what happened.
"Sam, is that true? Did you take Tom and Anna's boat?" she asked.
Sam did not answer. He knew he was in trouble.
"Sam, open the door right now. You have to apologize and give back the boat. That's not how you treat your friends," she said.
She opened the door and saw the boat. She was shocked by how Sam had ruined it.
"Sam, how could you do this? This is very bad. You have to say sorry and help fix the boat. And you have to share your toys with Tom and Anna. They are your friends and you have to be nice to them," she said.
She made Sam give back the boat and say sorry to Tom and Anna. She also gave them some cookies and juice to cheer them up. She helped them clean the boat and remove the stickers and glue. She also gave them some new buttons and wheels to make it look shiny again.
Tom and Anna were happy to have their boat back. They thanked Sam's mom and forgave Sam. They invited him to play with them and their boat. Sam agreed and said he was sorry. He learned his lesson and promised to be a better friend.
They all played together and had fun. They pretended to be sailors and went on adventures. They were not bored anymore. They were happy.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play with dolls and cars. One day, they see a new toy store. It has many beautiful toys. Lily and Tom want to go inside.
They ask their moms if they can go. Their moms say yes, but they have to pay for the toys. Lily and Tom have some money. They saved it from their birthdays. They are happy. They run to the toy store.
Inside, they see many beautiful toys. They want to buy them all. They pick up dolls and cars and books and games. They do not look at the prices. They go to the cashier. The cashier tells them how much they have to pay. It is a lot of money. More than Lily and Tom have.
Lily and Tom are sad. They do not have enough money. They have to put back some toys. They do not know which ones to choose. They like them all. They start to cry. The cashier is angry. He tells them to hurry up.
Their moms hear them cry. They come to the toy store. They see Lily and Tom with many toys. They are shocked. They ask them what they are doing. Lily and Tom tell them they want to buy the toys. Their moms are angry. They tell them they are greedy and foolish. They tell them they have to pay for the toys they picked up. They tell them they have no more money for other things. They tell them they have bad news.
Lily and Tom are scared. They do not want to pay for the toys. They do not want to have bad news. They do not want to make their moms angry. They wish they never went to the toy store. They wish they played with their old toys. They are sorry. They say they are sorry. But it is too late. They have to pay for the toys. They have to go home with no more money. They have to face the bad news. They have no more fun. They have a bad day. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little mole. The mole loved to play in the dirt. One day, the mole found a big hill. He wanted to roll down the hill.
The mole climbed up the hill. When he got to the top, he looked down. He was excited. The mole started to roll down the hill. As he rolled, he got faster and faster.
When the mole reached the bottom of the hill, he was filthy. He was covered in dirt and leaves. But the mole was very happy. He had so much fun rolling down the hill. And he did it again and again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy store in his room. He loved to play with his toys all day long. One day, Tim found a long rope in the store. He thought it was a fun new toy.
Tim wanted to play with the rope, so he asked his friend, Sam, to come over. When Sam saw the rope, he said, "This rope looks fake. It is not real." Tim and Sam laughed and decided to play with the fake rope anyway.
They took turns pulling the rope and pretending it was a snake. They had so much fun playing with the fake rope from the store. In the end, they put the rope back in the store and played with other toys. They were happy they found the fake rope and had a great time together.

Once upon a time, there was an ordinary cat. This cat lived in a small house. The cat liked to play on the ground. One day, the cat saw a big ball in the yard. The cat wanted to play with the ball.
The cat went to the ball and pushed it. But, the ball did not move. The cat tried again and again. The cat really wanted to play with the ball. The cat did not know what to do.
Then, something unexpected happened. The ball started to move on its own! The cat was surprised. The ball was not an ordinary ball. It was a magic ball! The cat and the magic ball played all day on the ground. They had so much fun together.

Lila likes to look at the stars. She has a book with pictures of planets. She learns their names and colors. She wants to see them for real.
One night, she has a dream. She flies in a big rocket to the sky. She sees the planets up close. They are big and bright and beautiful. She says hello to them.
She comes to a planet that is blue and green. It has water and land and animals. It looks like her home. She likes this planet. She wants to give it a hug.
She finds a big, soft blanket in her rocket. She wraps the planet with the blanket. She thinks the planet will be warm and cozy. She says, "You are a nice planet. I love you."
The planet smiles and says, "Thank you, Lila. You are a nice girl. I love you too."
Lila wakes up. She is happy. She remembers her dream. She looks at her book. She sees the planet that she wrapped. She says, "That is Earth. That is my home."

One day, a little girl named Amy was playing in her room. She looked up and saw a big spider on the ceiling. Amy was scared, but she wanted to be brave. She asked her mom for help to get the spider down.
Her mom was very generous and kind. She got a cup and a paper to catch the spider. She said, "Thank you, spider, for not scaring my little girl too much. Now, let's take you outside." The spider seemed to understand and stayed still in the cup.
Amy was so happy that her mom helped her. She said, "Thank you, Mom, for being so brave and generous." Her mom smiled and said, "You're welcome, Amy. Remember, we should always be kind to all living things, even spiders." Then, they went outside and let the spider go.

Once upon a time, there was a gifted little boy named Tim. Tim could make things with his hands. He made toys, he made art, and he made his friends smile.
One day, Tim found a can of gas. He knew that gas could burn. Tim wanted to use the gas to make a big fire. He thought the fire would be fun to watch and keep him warm.
But Tim's mom saw him with the gas. She took it away and told him not to play with it. She said gas could burn too much and hurt him. Tim listened to his mom and went back to making toys. He was happy to be safe and still make his friends smile.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a book of poems. He loved to read the poems out loud. One day, Tim found a dirty page in his book. He wanted to clean it.
Tim took a cloth and wiped the dirty page. The words on the page started to laugh. Tim was surprised. He laughed too. The laughing made Tim very happy.
Every day, Tim read the poems and laughed with the words. They all became good friends. Tim's book was not dirty anymore, and he had lots of fun with his poem friends. They laughed together every day, and they were all very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a curious and adventurous little boy named Zip. Zip liked to explore new places and try new things. One day, he decided to explore his backyard when he stumbled upon a magical flower. He was so excited and curious about the flower that he immediately touched it.
Zip was in for a surprise when he felt a tingling all over his body. He started to feel better and realized that the flower was special - it had a magical power to heal.
The next day, Zip showed the flower to his friend. His friend said, "What a cool flower! Let's see if it can heal my scraped knee."
Zip was hesitant. He wasn't sure if it was safe to give to his friend. But, in the end, his adventurous and brave side won out. So, he gave his friend the flower and watched as it healed his knee. 
Zip was so happy to see that the flower was able to bring healing to his friend! From then on, Zip kept the magical flower hidden and showed it only to those who needed healing.

Once upon a time, there was a modest city. In this city, there were many people who liked to paint. They painted on paper, on walls, and even on their faces. Everyone in the city loved to see the pretty colors.
One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to paint too. He found a big wall in the city and started to paint. Tim painted a big sun, a green tree, and a blue river. People walking by stopped to watch Tim paint. They smiled and clapped their hands.
Soon, the whole city was covered in beautiful paintings. The people were so happy that they had a big party. They danced, sang, and painted more pictures. The modest city became the most colorful and happy place to be. And it all started with Tim and his love for painting.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a girl named Mia. Mia loved to play outside in every season. Her favorite season was summer because she could eat ice cream and swim all day.
One day, Mia was playing with her friend Tom. Tom was very healthy and strong. He could raise his arms high and lift heavy things. Mia asked Tom, "Can you help me raise my kite up in the sky?" Tom smiled and said, "Of course, Mia! Let's do it together!"
So, Mia and Tom worked together to raise the kite. They ran and laughed as the kite flew high in the sky. They played until the sun went down, and they were very happy. That day, Mia learned that playing with friends and being healthy can make every season fun.

Once upon a time there was a little bird. She was looking for somewhere warm and cozy to live. She saw a nest and it seemed just right, so she flew inside.
But when she tried to settle in, she realized it was too heavy.
The bird looked around and saw an ant carrying some straw. She asked the ant, "Will you help me make this nest lighter?" 
The ant said, "No, that's my house. Find somewhere else to live!" 
The bird felt sad. She flew away to try and find a new home. But it was starting to get dark and she didn't know where to go. 
Then, an owl swooped down and said, "Come with me, the night is too dark for you to fly alone." When they arrived at a new tree, the owl said, "Maybe you can use some of these leaves and twigs to make your own nest." 
The bird was overjoyed. She worked hard to make a nest and it seemed just perfect. Now she had a cozy home of her own. 
The bird and the owl became good friends. They chattered away each night when the sun went down.
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a family who went on a holiday to the sea. They wanted to play in the sand, so they numbered out their buckets and spades. But, when they looked carefully, they noticed something expensive in the sand. It was a shiny ring! 
The children were so excited, they wanted to wear the ring, so they took it quickly. But when they looked up, they saw an angry man. He told them that it was his expensive ring and he was very angry that they had taken it!
The family said sorry over and over again, but the angry man said he was too angry to forgive them. He left with his ring and the family sadly packed up their belongings and went home.
The children learnt a lesson that day. They should never take anything that isn't theirs, no matter how expensive it looks!

Once upon a time, there was a blue bird. The bird loved to spring high into the sky. One day, the bird found a big, soft cloud. The cloud was like a luxury bed.
The bird liked the luxury cloud so much, it wanted to stay. It asked the cloud, "Can I stay with you?" The cloud said, "Yes, you can stay with me, blue bird."
The blue bird was very happy. It could spring high and sleep on the luxury cloud. They became good friends and lived happily in the sky.

Once upon a time, there was a graceful witch. She lived in a small house in the woods. She had a big hat and a long dress. The witch liked to carry things in her big bag.
One day, she met a little girl. The girl was lost in the woods. The witch asked, "Do you need help?" The little girl said, "Yes, I can't find my way home."
The graceful witch wanted to help. She looked in her big bag and found a map. She carried the map to the little girl. Together, they found the way back to the girl's home. The girl was very happy and thanked the witch. From that day, they became good friends.

One day, a cute little rabbit named Bob was hopping in the forest. He saw a big hole in the ground. Bob was curious and wanted to look inside the hole, but he was scared.
Bob called his friend, the wise owl, and asked, "What should I do? I want to see what's in the hole, but I'm scared." The owl said, "Stay brave, Bob. You can look inside, but remain close to the hole, so you can hop away if you need to."
Bob took a deep breath and looked inside the hole. Suddenly, a grumpy bear came out! The bear was not happy to see Bob. "This is my home!" the bear said. "You should not be here!" Bob was scared, but he said sorry to the bear and hopped away quickly. He was happy to be safe and learned to be more careful when exploring the forest.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a fine bird named Bob. Bob was a happy bird who loved to fly high in the sky. One day, Bob decided he wanted to change where he lived. He asked his friends where he should go. They told him to fly to the east.
So, Bob started to fly east. As he flew, he saw many new things. He saw a big river, a tall tree, and a small hill. He was very excited about his new home. He knew it would be a great place to live.
When Bob finally reached the east, he found a beautiful tree to make his new home. He met new friends and played fun games with them. Bob was very happy that he changed where he lived. Now, he could have new adventures and make new memories with his friends in the east.

Tom and Sue like to play with keys. They have many keys of different shapes and colors. Some keys are real and some keys are fake. They can tell the fake keys because they are plastic and light.
One day, they find a big box in the basement. The box has a lock on it. They wonder what is inside the box. They try to unlock the box with their keys, but none of them work. They are sad and frustrated.
Then, Sue has an idea. She remembers that her dad has a big key ring with many keys. She runs upstairs and gets the key ring. She brings it back to Tom and they try the keys one by one. Finally, they find a key that fits the lock. They unlock the box and open it.
Inside the box, they see many toys and books and clothes. They are very happy and excited. They start to take out the things and play with them. They think they have found a treasure.
But then, they hear a voice. It is their dad. He comes down the basement and sees them with the box. He is angry and surprised. He says, "What are you doing with my box? That is not for you. That is for the charity. I was going to give it away to the poor children. You have to put everything back and lock the box again."
Tom and Sue feel ashamed and sorry. They did not know that the box was for the charity. They thought it was a fun game. They say, "We are sorry, dad. We did not mean to be bad. We just wanted to see what was inside the box. We will put everything back and lock the box again."
They do as their dad says. They put everything back and lock the box again. They give the key ring back to their dad. They say, "We are sorry, dad. We will not play with keys again. We will not unlock things that are not ours."
Their dad hugs them and says, "It's okay, kids. I know you were curious. But you have to be careful and respectful. Some things are not for you to unlock. Some things are for other people who need them more. You have enough toys and books and clothes of your own. You should be grateful and generous. Do you understand?"
Tom and Sue nod and say, "We understand, dad. We are grateful and generous. We love you, dad."
Their dad smiles and says, "I love you too, kids. Now, let's go upstairs and have some cookies."

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had many toys in his room. His mom told him to organize his toys to make his room neat and safe. Tim wanted to be a good boy, so he started to put his toys away.
Tim found a tool in his toy box. It was a toy hammer. He used the toy hammer to help him organize his toys. He put his cars and trucks in one place, and his dolls and blocks in another place. He made sure to put his toy hammer back in the toy box when he was done.
When Tim finished organizing his toys, his mom came to see his room. She was very happy with how clean and safe it was. She gave Tim a big hug and said, "Good job, Tim! Your room is so neat and safe now." Tim felt proud of himself for organizing his toys and making his room safe for everyone.

One day, a popular dog named Spot went outside to play on the lawn. He liked to run and jump in the grass. Spot saw a big tree and wanted to play near it.
Spot saw a little bird on the tree. The bird said, "Hi Spot! Want to be friends?" Spot said, "Yes! I like friends." The bird flew down and sat on Spot's head. They played together all day long.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and blew the bird away. Spot was sad. But, the bird came back and gave Spot a kiss. Spot was happy again. They were best friends forever.

Once there was a clever rabbit. He was always trying to invent something new and special. One day he thought of an idea to make a mustache out of carrots.
The rabbit put together all the carrots and tied them together with a ribbon. He looked in the mirror and saw how silly he looked with the carrots on his face. He laughed and laughed.
The rabbit showed his invention to his friends. They all laughed and joked about the silly mustache. Suddenly, the rabbit had an idea. "I know! We can play a game. We can see who can break the mustache the fastest!" Everyone liked the idea and they all began to try and break the mustache.
Some rabbits tried to use their teeth, some tried to bite it and some tried to pull it apart. Finally, the clever rabbit won! He used his paws to break the carrots and he was the first one to break the mustache.
Everyone was very impressed with the clever rabbit's invention. They all celebrated with a silly dance, and the clever rabbit even got to keep the carrot mustache.

Once upon a time, there was a colorful bird who loved to sing. Every day, the bird would sing and bring peace to the forest. All the animals loved the bird's songs.
One day, a little boy came into the forest. He heard the bird sing and wanted to guess what kind of bird it was. He looked up and saw the colorful bird. The boy was so happy to see the bird and wanted to take it home with him.
The boy took the bird home, but the bird stopped singing. The forest was not peaceful anymore, and the boy was sad. He realized that the bird was happiest in the forest. So, the boy took the bird back to the forest. The colorful bird started to sing again, and peace returned to the forest. The boy learned that it is important to let others be happy where they belong.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She loved to swing high in the sky. One day, Lily saw a boy named Tom. Tom was ready to play with Lily. They had fun swinging together.
Tom and Lily grew up and fell in love. They wanted to have a marriage. They were very happy and ready to be together forever. They told their moms and dads about their plans.
But one day, when Lily and Tom were swinging together, a big storm came. The wind blew very hard, and they fell off the swing. They got hurt and couldn't have their marriage. Lily and Tom were very sad, and they never swung together again.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Amy went for a walk in the park. As she walked, she heard a noise in the bushes. She was a bit shy, so she just stayed still. All of a sudden, Amy saw a key come out from the bush. It wasn't a normal key, it glittered like the stars in the night sky. She was so excited!
But then, Amy heard something else come out of the bush. This time, it was a little creature. It had big eyes and small paws. It was so shy, it didn't even want to come out of its hiding spot. Amy was scared for a second, but she knew it was alright.
Amy realized that the key must be from the creature, that's why it was so scared. She wanted to help it, so she stretched out her hand and offered the key back. Instantly, the creature came out of the bush, took the key from Amy's hand and ran off into the night.
Amy smiled. Although she never saw the creature again, she was sure it was happy. After all, with the key, it could open the door to its home.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big nose. Lily was eager to make friends, but the other kids made fun of her nose. This made Lily very sad.
One day, a wise old owl saw Lily crying. The owl asked, "Why are you sad?" Lily told the owl about her big nose and how the other kids made fun of her. The owl thought for a moment and said, "I recommend that you be proud of your big nose. It makes you special."
Lily thought about what the owl said. The next day, she went to school with a big smile on her face. When the kids made fun of her nose, she told them that her big nose made her special. Soon, the other kids saw that Lily was happy and they stopped making fun of her.
In the end, Lily made many friends and they all learned that it is not nice to make fun of others. The moral of the story is to be kind to others and be proud of what makes you different.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was very tired. He played all day in the sun with his friends. When the sun went down, Spot wanted to sleep.
Spot's friend, a small bird, asked him to play more. But Spot said, "No, I am too tired." The bird did not like it. The bird wanted to play all night, but Spot refused to play.
Spot went to sleep near a well. The well was where they got water to drink. The bird was sad but let Spot sleep. When the sun came up, Spot and the bird played again. They were happy and had fun all day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy model car that he loved to play with every day. One day, Tim saw a grumpy old man sitting on a park bench. The man looked sad and lonely.
Tim walked up to the grumpy old man and said, "Hi, my name is Tim. Do you want to play with my model car?" The old man looked at Tim and smiled. "Sure, I would love to play with your car," he said.
Tim lent his model car to the grumpy old man, and they played together all afternoon. The old man was not grumpy anymore. He was very happy to have a new friend. And Tim learned that sharing his toys could make others happy too.

One day, a little boy named Tim was playing outside with his dog, Spot. They were running and jumping in the grass, having lots of fun. Tim started to feel itchy on his arm. He thought, "Oh no, maybe I got a bug bite!" He felt embarrassed because he didn't want anyone to see his itch.
Tim went inside to show his mom. She looked at his arm and said, "Don't worry, it's just a little itch. It will go away soon." Tim felt better, but he still didn't want his friends to see his itch. He decided to stay inside and play with his toys.
While playing, Tim noticed that Spot was scratching too. Tim thought, "Maybe Spot has an itch like me!" But then, something unexpected happened. Tim's mom came in and said, "I found out why you both are itchy! You were playing in the grass, and now you have tiny seeds stuck to your clothes and fur. They are making you itchy!" Tim and Spot laughed, realizing it wasn't bug bites after all. Tim's mom helped them get rid of the seeds, and they went back outside to play, feeling happy and itch-free.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly man named Tom. Tom liked to make things. One day, he made a message. He put the message in a bottle. Tom wanted to send the message to his friend, Sam.
Tom went to the sea and threw the bottle into the water. He said, "Go to Sam, message in a bottle!" The bottle swam away. Tom was happy. He thought Sam would get the message soon.
But something unexpected happened. A big fish came and ate the bottle! The fish swam away with the bottle in its tummy. Tom saw this and was very sad. He said, "Oh no! Sam will never get my message now." And Tom went home with a heavy heart.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Clara. One day she was taken on an unusual adventure. 
Clara's parents took her to a magical landscape. Everything was so beautiful! There were butterflies and flowers everywhere. 
Clara said, "Please can I explore?" 
Her parents said, "Yes, but stay close and be careful". 
Clara explored the landscape, looking at the trees and ponds. Then she found something really unusual. It was a giant caterpillar! 
Clara was so excited, she said "Please can I keep it?" 
Her parents said, "Yes, if you take good care of it". 
Clara kept the caterpillar and looked after it every day. She loved her magical, unusual friend!

One day, a little frog named Tim went for a hop. He liked to hop near the pond. Tim saw a mosquito. The mosquito was very alert. It saw Tim and flew away.
"Wait, mosquito!" Tim called out. "I want to be your friend!" The mosquito stopped and looked at Tim. "Really?" the mosquito asked. "Yes, let's play together!" said Tim.
Tim and the mosquito played all day. They hopped and flew around the pond. They were very happy. At the end of the day, they said goodbye. Tim and the mosquito were now good friends.

Once upon a time there was a little rabbit called Leo. Leo was always looking for a way to explore the world and find something new. One day, he decided to design his own vehicle. He found some fuel and put it in a funnel. Then he added a wheel and a handle.
After he had finished his vehicle, he was so excited. He wanted to try it out, so he sat inside the vehicle and started riding. Suddenly, he had an idea. He wanted to make the ride a bit more special. He put some berries in a cup and added fuel.
He brought the cup to his vehicle and added the mixture to the fuel. It smelled a bit strange, but he decided to try it. As he started to ride, he could feel the fuel getting more and more bitter. He tried to stop, but it was too late. Suddenly, a voice called out to him. It was a friendly rabbit who had been watching him. "Don't worry," it said, "sometimes a bit of adventure can be a bit bitter at first, but it will be delicious in the end." And with that, Leo continued on his adventure.

Anna loves to hug her mommy. She hugs her every morning, every night, and sometimes in between. She likes how her mommy smells like flowers and lotion. She thinks her mommy is very clever, because she can read books, make cookies, and fix toys.
One day, Anna wants to surprise her mommy with a hug. She sneaks into her mommy's room, where she sees a bottle of lotion on the dresser. She thinks, maybe if I put some lotion on me, I will smell like mommy too. She opens the bottle and squeezes some lotion on her hands. Then she rubs it all over her face, her hair, and her clothes.
She feels very sticky and slippery, but she is happy. She runs to find her mommy, who is in the kitchen. She shouts, "Mommy, mommy, I have a hug for you!" She jumps on her mommy and wraps her arms around her.
Her mommy is very surprised. She feels the wet lotion on Anna's face and clothes. She sees the mess on Anna's hair. She says, "Oh, Anna, what did you do? You are all covered in lotion!"
Anna smiles and says, "I wanted to smell like you, mommy. I wanted to give you a hug. Do you like it?"
Her mommy laughs and hugs her back. She says, "I love you, Anna. You are very sweet and clever. But next time, please ask me before you use my lotion. It is not good for your hair and clothes. Come on, let's go wash you up." She takes Anna to the bathroom and helps her clean up. Then she gives her another hug and a kiss. Anna feels happy and loved. She thinks her mommy is the best.

Once there was a boy named Tim. Tim was a lucky boy. He lived near the big ocean. Every day, he played near the water. He liked to watch the fish swim.
One day, Tim saw a big fish. The fish was sad. Tim wanted to help the fish. The fish told Tim that he was lost. Tim said, "I will help you find your family." The fish was happy.
Tim and the fish looked for the fish's family. They looked all day. It was getting dark. The fish said, "Tim, I am sorry. I was bad. I lied. I have no family. Can you forgive me?" Tim was sad, but he forgave the fish. They kept looking for a new family for the fish. But they never found one. The fish was always sad, and Tim was sad too. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a clean ticket. It sat on an table, waiting for someone to pick it up. One day, a 3 year old girl decided to take it. She thought the ticket would be fun to play with.
The girl ran around the house with the ticket, playing a game. She waved the ticket in the air and said "this is my ticket!" Suddenly, Daddy started to shout.
"Put that ticket down, now!" he said.
The girl didn't listen. She continued to play until daddy grabbed the ticket from her and threw it outside. The girl started to cry.
The ticket lay outside in the sun all day and began to rot. By nightfall, it was a pile of mush. Daddy said "we should have put it back on the table". The girl hung her head in sadness.
The ticket was ruined, and the girl was never allowed to have it again. The end.

One day, a jolly cat named Max was playing outside. Max saw a big puddle and decided to jump in it. He jumped and splashed and had so much fun. Max was all wet and soaked from playing in the puddle.
Max went home to find his friend, a little girl named Lisa. Lisa was in the kitchen with a plate of yummy cookies. Max wanted a cookie, but he was all wet. Lisa said, "Max, you are soaked! You need to dry off before you can have a cookie."
Max was sad, but he knew Lisa was right. He went outside to dry off in the sun. While he was drying, he saw something shiny under a bush. Max went to look and found a big, shiny plate. Max was so happy and thought, "I can give this plate to Lisa as a gift!"
Max brought the shiny plate to Lisa, and she was so surprised. "Max, this is a beautiful plate! Thank you so much!" Lisa said. Max was happy that he found a special gift for his friend. Lisa dried Max off with a towel, and they both enjoyed cookies on the new shiny plate together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to ride her bike in the park. One sunny day, while she was riding, she saw a bird on the path.
The bird was very spoiled. It wanted food from Lily. Lily had a cookie in her pocket, so she broke it into small pieces and gave it to the bird. The bird was very happy and ate the cookie bits.
After the bird ate, it sang a pretty song for Lily. Lily smiled and thanked the bird. Then, she rode her bike back home, feeling happy about her new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a restless little bird. She had a feather that was not like the others. This feather was very close to her heart. The little bird liked to fly and play all day.
One day, she met a big bird. The big bird saw her special feather. He said, "Your feather is nice, little bird. Let's be friends!" The little bird was happy to have a friend. They played and flew together in the sky.
At night, the little bird felt tired. She went back to her nest and lay down. The big bird said, "Good night, little bird. Sleep well." The little bird closed her eyes and slept. She was not restless anymore. She had a friend and was very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a clever mixer. The mixer could mix all kinds of things. One day, the sun was shining very bright. The mixer wanted to mix the sun's shine to make a new color.
The mixer said, "Sun, can I mix your shine?" The sun said, "No, you cannot mix my shine. It is too strong for you." The mixer was sad but did not give up.
The mixer went to a wise old tree for help. The tree told the mixer to be patient and wait for the right time. The mixer waited and waited. One day, the sun was not shining so bright. The mixer mixed the sun's shine with other colors and made a beautiful new color. The sun saw this and was happy. They became good friends.
The moral of the story is to be patient and wait for the right time to do things.

Once upon a time there was a thin potato. All alone, the potato stayed in the garden. Every morning the potato looked for supplies to help it grow, but there were none to be found. 
The thin potato felt very sad. It wanted someone to play with and something to eat. The only thing the potato had was hope.
Days passed, and all of a sudden one morning the potato found some supplies in the garden. The potato was so happy! It quickly ate them up, and overnight became bigger and stronger.
In the morning the thin potato was gone, replaced instead with a big, strong potato! The big, strong potato was happy to be in the garden and play with the other potatoes. It enjoyed the supplies it found and thanked them for helping it become the big, strong potato it was today.

Once upon a time, there was a farm. On the farm, there was a cow named Bessie. Bessie was tired. She worked all day and all night. She wanted to change her life.
One day, Bessie asked her friend, the pig, "Can you help me change my life? I am very tired." The pig said, "Yes, let's think of a plan!"
The next day, Bessie and the pig found a magic hat on the farm. They put the hat on Bessie's head. Suddenly, Bessie changed into a big, strong horse! She was not tired anymore. Bessie and the pig were very happy and they played on the farm all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big box full of toys. One day, she decided to unpack her toys and play with them. She took out her dolls, cars, and blocks. She was very happy.
While unpacking, Lily found a small bell. It was shiny and made a pretty sound when she shook it. She put the bell on a string and wore it around her neck. She loved the sound it made when she played.
Lily also found a sour candy in the box. She put the candy in her mouth and made a funny face. The candy was very sour, but Lily liked it. She shared the candy with her friends, and they all made funny faces too. They had lots of fun playing with the toys and eating the sour candy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play outside with his friends. They were always enthusiastic when they played together. Their favorite game was to slide down the big slide at the park.
One sunny day, Tim and his friends found a small box. They decided to bury the box in the sand near the slide. They thought it would be fun to dig it up later and see what was inside.
After they buried the box, they all took turns sliding down the slide. They laughed and played all day. When it was time to go home, they remembered the box they buried. They dug it up and found a note inside. The note said, "Always be kind and share with your friends." Tim and his friends learned the importance of sharing and being kind to one another. They promised to always share their toys and play together happily.

Sara and Tom are friends. They like to play together. But today they disagree. Sara wants to play with the doll. Tom wants to play with the car. They both say "mine" and pull the toy.
Sara and Tom are upset. They cry and shout. They do not share. Mom hears them and comes to see. She says "stop, stop, stop". She says "you have to take turns". She says "you have to be nice".
Sara and Tom listen to Mom. They say "sorry" and hug. They decide to play with the shoe. They pretend it is a boat. They sail it on the rug. They have fun. They are friends again.

Once upon a time, in a tidy little town, there was a restaurant. The restaurant was very clean and bright. Many people liked to go there to eat. One day, a little boy named Tim and his mom went to the restaurant for lunch.
"Mom, can I have a big ice cream?" Tim asked. His mom thought for a moment and then said, "Yes, I will allow you to have a big ice cream, but only if you eat all your food first."
Tim was very happy and ate all his food. He even helped his mom clean the table. The people at the restaurant were very happy to see Tim being so tidy.
After Tim finished his food, his mom let him have a big ice cream. Tim was very happy and said, "Thank you, mom!" They both enjoyed their ice cream and had a great time at the restaurant.
From that day on, Tim always remembered to be tidy and eat all his food before having dessert. And the restaurant continued to be a happy and clean place for everyone to enjoy.

One day, a boy named Tim went to play outside. He saw his neighbor, Sue. Sue was very organized. She liked to keep her toys neat and tidy. Tim wanted to play with Sue, so he asked her, "Can I play with you?"
Sue said, "Yes, but we have to be careful with my toys." They started to play with Sue's toy cars. They made the cars go fast and slow. They had lots of fun.
Then, Tim's mom called him, "Tim, lunch time!" Tim had to pause their play time. He told Sue, "I have to go eat, but I will be back soon." Sue said, "Okay, see you later!" Tim went home to eat and then came back to play with Sue. They played together all day and had a great time.

One day, a little girl named Amy went to the store with her mom. They wanted to buy a card for Amy's friend, Max. Max was having a birthday party. They looked at all the cards that were available.
Amy found a card with a big, funny monster on it. She showed it to her mom and said, "I like this one! Max will laugh!" Her mom agreed and they took the card to the lady at the counter.
At the party, Amy gave the card to Max. When Max opened the card, the monster popped out and screamed, "Happy Birthday!" Max laughed and said, "Thank you, Amy! This is the best card ever!"

Once upon a time there was a mummy bear and baby bear. Mummy bear was always very careful and used to prevent bad things from happening. One day, she decided to take baby bear to explore the woods. 
Mummy bear was very anxious, so she made sure that baby bear didn't wander too far. She said, "Please stay close to me, baby bear!" But baby bear was excited to explore and started running around in motion.
Mummy bear chased after him and said, "No, baby bear! I am trying to prevent you from getting hurt. There are lots of dangerous things in the woods that we can't see. We should always stay together." 
Baby bear nodded and agreed. From then on, he always remembered to stay close to Mummy Bear and listen to her advice.
The moral of this story is to listen to your parents and heed their advice. They only want the best for you, and will try to prevent bad things from happening.

Once there was a little boy, his name was Max. He was only three years old and he loved to do special things.
Max's family was going out to dinner one night and they took him along with them. When they arrived, they saw a big menu on the wall with lots of different options. Max couldn't decide what he wanted, but he eventually settled on the chicken.
Max was so happy when his dinner arrived. He ate so much that he got a very full tummy. But then he noticed something very strange in his dinner. It smelt terrible!
Max's dad returned his dinner to the waiter and told him all about the smelly surprise. The waiter was very apologetic and told him he would make sure everything was alright before they left.
Sure enough, the waiter returned with a fresh plate of dinner for Max and it smelled delicious! Max was so happy and gave the waiter a big smile. He enjoyed his dinner so much that he was sure that he would never forget the smelly surprise!

Once upon a time, there was a noisy dog named Spot. Spot liked to play and make noise all day. One day, Spot saw a big red ball in the park. He wanted to play with it, so he ran to the ball and started to push it with his nose.
A little boy named Tim saw Spot playing with the ball. Tim said, "Hey Spot, can I play with you?" Spot barked happily and they began to play together. They played and played until they were very tired.
As they were about to finish playing, a man came up to them. The man said, "That ball has a price. You need to give me two cookies for it." Tim and Spot were sad, but they wanted to keep the ball. They looked around and found two cookies on a bench. They gave the cookies to the man, and he let them keep the ball. Tim and Spot were so happy, they played with the ball every day after that.

One day, a little duck named Ducky went to the wide pond. He saw his friends, Froggy and Fishy, playing with a band. The band was made of pretty colors. Ducky wanted to play with them too.
"Can I play with the band?" Ducky asked. Froggy and Fishy looked at each other and said, "Yes, but you have to dive into the pond first!" Ducky was scared, but he wanted to play with the band.
Ducky closed his eyes and dived into the pond. He felt the cool water on his feathers. When he came up, he saw that the band was gone. Froggy and Fishy were laughing. "Surprise!" they shouted. "We made a new game for you. Let's find the band together!" Ducky smiled and they all played the new game, having lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a bossy cat named Tom. Tom liked to think he was the best at everything. He lived in a big house with his friend, a dog named Max.
One sunny day, Tom and Max went for a walk. They walked down the street and saw a big tree. Tom said, "I think I can climb that tree faster than you, Max!" Max just wagged his tail and smiled.
Tom climbed up the tree very fast. But when he got to the top, he saw a big bird. The bird said, "You are not the best, Tom! I live in this tree, and I can fly!" Tom felt sad and climbed down. Max was waiting for him. They walked back home, and Tom learned that it is okay not to be the best at everything.

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful teacher named Miss May. She had many pupils in her class. They liked her a lot. They always smiled and played together. One day, a new pupil named Tim came to the class. Tim was very shy and did not talk much. The other pupils did not know how to play with him.
Miss May thought of a fun game to help Tim make friends. She asked the pupils to sign their names on a big paper. Then, they had to find a friend with the same letter in their name. Tim found a girl named Tina. They both had the letter "T" in their names. They started to talk and play together.
But then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and blew the paper away. The pupils were sad and did not know what to do. Miss May said, "Do not worry. We can still be friends without the paper." The pupils learned that they did not need a game to be friends. They just needed to be kind and talk to each other. From that day on, Tim and the other pupils were always happy and played together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He lived with his mom and dad in a small house. They were not wealthy, but they were happy. Tim loved to play with his toys and spend time with his family.
One day, Tim found a magic seed. His mom told him to plant it in their garden. Tim did what his mom said, and the seed started to grow. The plant grew bigger and bigger every day. It was increasing in size so fast!
As the plant grew, it started to give them food. The food made Tim's family happy and healthy. Their life became better and better. They were still not wealthy with money, but they had a lot of love and a magic plant that made them rich in other ways. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a big closet full of toys. He was rich with toys, but he never shared them with his friends. He liked to keep them all for himself.
One day, Tim went to the park and saw his friends playing with a ball. They were all having fun and laughing. Tim wanted to join them, but he didn't have a ball to play with. He started to feel sad and regret not sharing his toys with his friends.
Tim went home and thought about what to do. He decided to share his toys with his friends. The next day, he brought some toys to the park and shared them with his friends. They all played together and had a great time. Tim learned that sharing is better than keeping everything for yourself.

One day, a boy named Tim and a girl named Sue wanted to play a game. They were very competitive. They could not choose which game to play. So, they decided to race up the stair.
Tim said, "I can go up the stair faster than you!" Sue replied, "No, I can go up the stair faster than you!" They both got ready to race. They stood at the bottom of the stair and counted, "One, two, three, go!"
Tim and Sue ran up the stair as fast as they could. They were both very fast, but Sue was a little faster. She reached the top of the stair first and shouted, "I win!" Tim was a little sad, but he was happy for his friend.
The next day, they played more games together. They had a lot of fun, and they both learned that playing together was more important than winning. They were still competitive, but they always remembered to have fun and be good friends.

Once there was a boy who wanted peace. He wanted to be free and happy. He looked around but everywhere he looked he felt tired. He tried to find a way to keep the peace but he couldn't. He locked the door so no one could come in and take away the peace. 
But soon there was shouting outside his door. It was loud and it scared him. He cried and was so afraid he didn't know what to do. He locked the door harder, but it wasn't strong enough. The door broke and the peace he had been looking for broke with it. 
He never found peace again. He was always scared, always tired. He hated what had happened and he could never get it back.

One day, a little bug named Tim wanted to crawl to the big tree. He felt ready for a big adventure. The sun was up, and Tim saw his shadow on the ground. He said to his shadow, "Let's go on a fun trip!"
As they crawled, Tim and his shadow met a big bird. The bird asked, "Where are you going, little bug?" Tim said, "I am going to the big tree with my shadow!" The bird looked at Tim and his shadow and said, "Be careful, the big tree is far away and has many dangers."
Tim and his shadow did not listen to the bird. They kept crawling to the big tree. They saw big rocks and tall grass, but they did not see the danger the bird talked about. They thought they were safe.
But then, a big wind came and blew Tim's shadow away. Tim was very sad and scared. He was not ready for the big tree without his shadow. He tried to crawl back home, but he got lost. Tim never found his shadow or his way back home again.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there lived a boy named Tom. Tom loved to spend time with his best friend, a dog named Max. They would play together all day long. They liked to run, jump, and laugh in the sun.
One day, Tom and Max saw a big tree with tasty red apples. Tom said, "Max, let's pick some apples and take them home." Max barked happily, and they picked many apples from the tree. They filled a big basket with the tasty apples.
When they got home, Tom's mom saw the apples and said, "Wow, these apples look so tasty! Let's make an apple pie." Tom and Max were very happy. They spent the whole day making a big, tasty apple pie. When it was done, they all sat down and enjoyed the delicious treat together.

Once upon a time, in a wild forest, there lived many animals. They were all good friends, and they played together every day. One day, they found a hole that went deep underground.
The animals were curious about the hole, so they decided to unite and explore it together. They held hands and went down into the underground. It was dark, but they were not scared because they were together.
As they walked through the wild underground, they found a big room with a big treasure chest. They opened the chest and found many toys and treats. The animals were very happy and shared everything with each other. From that day on, they were even better friends, and they played together in the wild forest and the underground.

Once upon a time, there was a big table in a small room. On the table, there was a yummy cake. A little boy named Tim saw the cake and wanted to eat it. He was happy and excited.
Tim tried to take a piece of the cake, but he pinched his finger by mistake. "Ouch!" he cried, and he became angry. He did not want to eat the cake anymore. He went to sit on the floor, rubbing his finger.
Then, something unexpected happened. The table started to shake and the cake jumped off the table. The cake was alive! It was a magic cake. The cake said, "I'm sorry, Tim. I didn't mean to hurt you." Tim and the cake became friends and they played together all day. Tim was not angry anymore and they both had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time, in a big blue sea, there was a dolphin named Dolly. Dolly was a very enormous dolphin. She had many friends in the sea. They all liked to play and swim together.
One day, Dolly and her friends decided to prepare for a big race. They practiced swimming very fast. They wanted to see who was the fastest swimmer in the sea. Dolly was excited and happy.
On the day of the race, all the sea animals came to watch. Dolly and her friends swam as fast as they could. In the end, Dolly won the race. Everyone cheered for the enormous dolphin. Dolly was very proud and happy.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play outside at night. They see the moon in the sky. The moon is big and bright and round.
"Look, Ben, the moon is like a ball!" Anna says.
"I want to pick the moon!" Ben says. He jumps and reaches his hands up.
"You can't pick the moon, Ben. It is too far and too high," Anna says.
"But I want to play with the moon!" Ben says. He jumps again and again.
Anna feels cold. She shivers and hugs herself. "Ben, it is cold. Let's go inside and get warm," she says.
"No, I want to pick the moon!" Ben says. He does not listen to Anna.
Anna is sad. She does not want to play alone. She does not want to be cold. She wants Ben to come with her.
She has an idea. She runs to the house and gets a flashlight. She comes back and shines the flashlight on the ground.
"Ben, look! I have a moon for you!" she says.
Ben stops jumping. He sees the light on the ground. It is like a small moon.
"Wow, a moon!" he says. He runs to the light and tries to pick it.
Anna laughs. She moves the flashlight and the light moves too.
"Hey, where did the moon go?" Ben says.
"It is here, Ben. Follow me!" Anna says.
She leads Ben to the house with the flashlight. Ben follows her and tries to pick the light.
They go inside and close the door. They are warm and happy. They have fun with the flashlight and the moon.

Once upon a time, there was a loyal dog. He loved to add chocolate to his day. So every day, he would go to the store. He was always so excited to pick out some yummy chocolate. 
When he would get home, he would add the chocolate to his bowl. Then he would take a nap in his cozy bed. After, he would go outside to play. He loved playing with all his toys!
