But his favorite thing to do was to add the chocolate to his day. He knew that it made him super happy. He was always so loyal to his chocolate! 
Every day, he would get up and go to the store. He was always so excited to pick out some yummy chocolate. He knew that it would make his day even better!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to eat cream. Every day, he would eat cream from the same original big jar. He would repeat this every day because it made him happy.
One day, Tim went to the kitchen to eat his cream. But when he opened the jar, it was empty! Tim was sad and didn't know what to do. He looked everywhere for the cream but could not find it.
Then, Tim saw his cat, Fluffy, licking her paws. She had cream all over her face! Tim laughed and realized that Fluffy had eaten the cream. He wasn't sad anymore because he thought it was funny. Tim decided to share his cream with Fluffy from now on, and they both enjoyed it together.

Once upon a time, in a distant land, there was a small cube. The cube was very happy and liked to play with its friends. They would roll and jump in the warm sun all day long.
One day, the sky became dark and big raindrops started to fall. The cube and its friends were scared, but then they saw a big tree. They all ran under the tree to hide from the rain.
As they sat under the tree, the rain began to soak the ground. The cube and its friends watched as the water made the dirt wet and soft. They started to play with the wet dirt, making fun shapes and having a great time. When the rain stopped, they all went home feeling happy and tired.

Once upon a time, there was a girl called Mabel. She was always feeling lucky, and believed that something wonderful could happen to her.
One day, Mabel went out to play in the park. As she hung from the monkey bars, she suddenly heard a loud noise. Everyone around her stopped to listen. It was silence. No one moved.
Mabel was so scared. She called out, "Is anyone there?" But no one answered. All she could hear was the silence.
Suddenly, a figure appeared. It was a tall man in a hood. Mabel's heart stopped. She knew that he meant trouble.
The man came closer and closer. He said menacingly, "You don't look so lucky now." And then he grabbed Mabel and took her away.
Mabel was never seen again. No one knows what happened to her, but everyone knew that the hooded man was to blame. From then on, they were always aware of the silence, because they knew that someone else might fall victim to this menacing figure.

Once upon a time there was a farmer. He was very tired and wanted to sleep. As he lay in bed, he heard a noise outside. He thought he must be dreaming. Suddenly, he heard a loud bang! He realised it was a gun and jumped out of bed!
The farmer looked out the window. He could see someone far away. They were sending the gun into the air. The farmer wanted to find out what was happening so he quickly put on his shoes and ran outside.
When he arrived, he found a little girl. She was holding the gun and was very tired. The farmer asked the girl what she was doing. The girl said she was trying to shoot stars in the sky.
The farmer smiled and said "I can help you with that." He then took the gun from the girl, picked up some stones and with a few quick throws sent all the stars into the night sky.
The girl was very happy and ran off to show her friends. The farmer was very pleased to have helped her and finally went off to bed to get some rest.

Once upon a time, there was a furry bear named Ben. Ben loved to play with his toy rocket. One day, Ben's friend, a little bird named Tim, came to visit.
"Hi, Ben! What are you playing with?" asked Tim. Ben smiled and said, "I'm playing with my rocket. It's my favorite toy!"
Ben and Tim played with the rocket all day long. They took turns flying it high in the sky. They had so much fun together. Ben knew that his rocket was very special, and he valued it a lot.
As the sun began to set, Ben and Tim said their goodbyes. Ben put his rocket safely away and went to sleep, dreaming of the fun day he had with his friend. He knew that the time spent with Tim made his rocket even more valuable.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Buddy. Buddy was a very anxious dog. One day, Buddy found a message in a bottle. He did not know what the message said, but he wanted to find out.
Buddy took the message to his friend, a wise old cat named Whiskers. Whiskers told Buddy to sit and wait while he read the message. Buddy sat and waited, wagging his tail. He was so excited to hear what the message said.
Whiskers read the message and smiled. He told Buddy that the message was from a far away friend. The friend wanted to meet Buddy and play. Buddy was very happy and not anxious anymore. He knew he had a new friend, and they would have lots of fun together.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They sat on a bench to eat lunch. Tim's mom gave him a sandwich. Tim took a big bite and said, "Mom, this sandwich is spicy!" His mom smiled and said, "I put a little spice in it for fun. I hope you like it."
While they ate, Tim and his mom watched some birds playing in the park. They were having a lot of fun. Suddenly, a big bird flew down and took Tim's spicy sandwich right out of his hand! Tim was so surprised, and he started to cry.
But then, the big bird flew back with the sandwich in its beak. It dropped the sandwich back into Tim's hand and said, "I'm sorry, I thought it was mine. I don't like spicy food." Tim and his mom were very surprised to hear the bird talk, but they laughed and said, "That's okay, Mr. Bird. We forgive you." And they all enjoyed the rest of their day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little fish named Fin. He lived in a big water with his family. Fin was always alert and liked to think a lot.
One day, while Fin was swimming, he saw a big rock. He thought, "I can swim around the rock and find new friends!" So, Fin swam around the rock and found a little crab named Carl. Carl was also alert and liked to think.
Fin and Carl became good friends. They played in the water and thought of fun games to play together. They were always alert, so they stayed safe and had a lot of fun. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a little frog named Tim wanted to play. He saw a dull hoop in the yard. Tim thought it would be fun to hop through the hoop. He called his friend, a small bird named Sam, to play with him.
"Sam, look at this hoop! Let's hop and fly through it!" Tim said. Sam liked the idea and they started to play together. They took turns hopping and flying through the dull hoop. They were having so much fun and they laughed a lot.
As they played, a big dog named Max saw them. Max wanted to play too, but he was too big for the hoop. He tried to hop through the hoop, but he got stuck. Tim and Sam tried to help Max, but they couldn't.
Max started to cry. "I'm stuck and I can't get out!" he said. Tim and Sam felt sad for Max. They called for help, but no one came. The hoop broke and Max was free, but he was hurt.
In the end, Tim, Sam, and Max were sad. They couldn't play anymore. The hoop was broken and Max was hurt. They learned that not all games are good for everyone.

Once upon a time, there was an adventurous elephant named Elly. Elly loved to explore and play with her friends in the jungle. One day, Elly and her friends wanted to part and go on their own adventures.
Elly went to the big river and saw a small mouse stuck on a rock. The mouse was scared and needed help. Elly used her long nose to lift the mouse and put him on the safe ground. The mouse was very happy and thanked Elly for her help.
Elly learned that it is good to help others, even if they are different from you. Her friends came back and saw what Elly did. They all learned that being kind and helpful is important. From that day on, Elly and her friends always helped each other and anyone who needed it. And they lived happily in the jungle, always ready for a new adventure.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to act like a big knight. He had a toy sword that he played with every day. Tim was very good at acting like a knight. One day, Tim saw a poor cat stuck in a tree. He wanted to help the cat.
Tim thought of a plan. He remembered when he acted like a knight who saved a princess. He thought that maybe he could do the same thing for the poor cat. He ran to his room and put on his knight costume. He took his toy sword and went back to the tree.
Tim tried to use his sword to help the cat. He acted like a brave knight and climbed the tree. At the top, he used his sword to reach the cat. The cat was scared, but Tim acted very kind. The cat trusted Tim, and he helped the cat climb down the tree. Tim was very happy that he could use his acting and sword to help the poor cat.

Tom and Sam were friends who liked to play with boats. They had many boats of different colors and sizes. They liked to sail them in the pond near their house. Sometimes they raced their boats and sometimes they pretended they were pirates.
One day, Tom had a new boat. It was big and red and shiny. He was very proud of it and wanted to show it to Sam. He said, "Look at my new boat! It is the best boat ever! It can sail faster and farther than any other boat!"
Sam was not happy. He felt jealous of Tom's new boat. He wanted to have a boat like that too. He said, "Your boat is not so great. It is too big and too red. It does not look normal. I bet it cannot sail in the wind or the waves. I dare you to sail it in the big lake!"
Tom was angry. He did not like Sam's words. He wanted to prove that his boat was the best. He said, "I am not afraid of the big lake. I can sail my boat anywhere. I accept your dare. Come with me and see!"
Tom and Sam took their boats and went to the big lake. The big lake was far from their house and very deep and wide. There were many winds and waves in the big lake. It was not a good place to sail small boats.
Tom put his boat in the water and started to sail it. He pushed it hard and made it go fast. He wanted to show Sam how good his boat was. He did not see the big wave that was coming behind him.
The big wave hit Tom's boat and made it flip over. The boat sank in the water and disappeared. Tom was shocked and sad. He lost his new boat. He cried and cried.
Sam saw what happened and felt sorry. He did not mean to make Tom lose his boat. He wanted to help him. He said, "Tom, I am sorry. I was wrong. Your boat was great. I should not have dared you to sail it in the big lake. Can we still be friends?"
But Tom was too upset to listen. He was angry and hurt. He said, "No, we cannot be friends. You are a bad friend. You made me lose my boat. Go away and leave me alone!"
Tom ran away from the big lake and went back to his house. He did not want to see Sam again. He did not want to play with boats again. He was very unhappy.
Sam was sad too. He lost his friend and his fun. He wished he could take back his words and his dare. He learned a hard lesson. He learned that some dares are not good and some words can hurt. He hoped that one day Tom would forgive him and be his friend again. But he did not know if that would ever happen.

Lily was a compassionate girl who liked to help others. She had a desk in her room where she drew pictures and wrote letters. One day, she saw a new boy in her class who looked sad and lonely. His name was Sam and he had just moved from another town.
Lily wanted to be his friend, so she urged him to sit with her at lunch. "Hi, Sam. I'm Lily. Do you want to share my sandwich and juice?" she asked with a smile.
Sam nodded shyly and followed her to a table. He was hungry and thirsty, but he did not talk much. Lily tried to make him feel welcome by telling him about her desk and her drawings. She showed him a picture of a rainbow that she had made for her mom.
"That's very pretty," Sam said softly. He liked rainbows too, but he missed his old home and his old friends.
Lily sensed that he was sad, so she gave him a hug and said, "I know it's hard to move to a new place, but you'll make new friends here. I'm your friend, and I'll show you around the school and the playground. Maybe you can come to my house after school and see my desk and my toys. Would you like that?"
Sam smiled and nodded again. He felt a little better and a little less lonely. He was glad that Lily was so compassionate and kind. He decided to give her a picture of a rainbow too, as a thank you gift. He hoped they would be good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy little duck. He liked to sail on the water in a small boat. The duck loved to talk to his friends, the fish, the frog, and the bird.
One day, the duck learned a new word. He was so excited to share it with his friends. He sailed to the fish and said, "I know a new word!" The fish wanted to know the word too, so the duck told him. The fish was happy to learn the new word.
Next, the nosy duck sailed to the frog and the bird. He told them the new word too. They all laughed and played together, saying the new word over and over. They had so much fun, and the nosy duck was glad he could share his new word with his friends.

One day, a bright alligator named Ally went out to play. She saw a big, red apple on the ground. Ally was very hungry, so she decided to eat the apple. As she was about to take a bite, a little bird named Billy flew down and said, "Wait, Ally! Can I have some apple too?"
Ally thought for a moment and then said, "Okay, Billy. We can share the apple." They both took turns eating the apple until it was all gone. They were both very happy and full.
Later, Ally and Billy played together in the sun. They were glad they had shared the apple. The moral of the story is that sharing makes everyone happy.

Once upon a time, in a warm summer, a graceful cat lived in a big house. The cat loved to play with her own toys. She had many friends like a dog, a bird, and a little mouse. They all played together in the big house.
One day, when the cat was playing with her own toys, she found a big box. The box was not her own. She was very curious. She called her friends, the dog, the bird, and the little mouse to look at the box. They all gathered around the big box, wondering what was inside.
The dog pushed the box open with his nose. Inside the box, they found a big, colorful ball. The friends were very happy and started to play with the ball. They threw it, rolled it, and bounced it. The ball was not the cat's own toy, but it was a fun surprise for all the friends. And so, they played together all summer long, sharing the big, colorful ball and having lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Pat. Pat had a pet mole, and they were very good friends. Every morning, Pat would stir up some breakfast for them. She would put a mild oatmeal in a bowl and stir it around until it was just right.
One morning, Pat was stir-stir-stirring her oatmeal when her mole came over to take a sniff. Pat greeted her little friend with a smile.
"Do you want some oatmeal?" Pat asked.
The mole nodded his head and grabbed a spoonful with his paws. He ate it up and let out a satisfied sigh.
Pat laughed and said, "That must have been just the right amount of mild!"

Once upon a time, there was a busy little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and run around in the park. One day, Lily found a big bottle with a stick inside. She was very excited and wanted to see what it could do.
Lily pulled the stick out of the bottle and saw it had a round ring at the end. She blew into the ring and, to her surprise, big bubbles came out! The bubbles were shiny and floated in the air. Lily laughed and clapped her hands. She wanted to make more bubbles!
Lily spent the whole day making bubbles in the park. She was very busy pulling the stick from the bottle and blowing into it. The bubbles made her so happy. She knew that she had found a new favorite toy to play with. And from that day on, Lily and her bubble bottle were always together, making the world a more bubbly and fun place.

Once upon a time, there were three little pigs. One day, the three little pigs invited a wolf to come to their house. The wolf was so excited to receive the invite! He was so happy that he wanted to come right away.
When the wolf arrived, the three little pigs became a little scared. He was very loud and rough and the three little pigs were worried that he would cause trouble. Despite their worries, they welcomed the wolf into their house.
The three little pigs offered the wolf a log to sit on. He was so pleased that he thanked them with a big smile. But then something strange happened. The wolf started to rub his log in circles around the house, making the walls shake! The three little pigs started to get very scared and the wolf refused to stop.
The wolf's rubbing made a mild earthquake and the three little pigs lost their house. The house fell apart and the three little pigs ran away, never to be seen again.
The moral of the story is to be kind and polite, but also to be careful with who you invite into your house. Even if someone is friendly, they might not understand the limits of what is acceptable.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toys. One day, he found a big closet in his house. The closet was dark and quiet. Tim was very excited to play in the closet.
Tim went inside the closet with his toys. He played for a long time. Then, he heard a small voice. It was a tiny bug. The bug said, "Hello, Tim. I am so sad. All my friends are dead." Tim felt sad for the bug.
Tim decided to help the bug. He found some new friends for the bug. The bug was very happy. Tim learned that it is good to help others. The moral of the story is: Helping others can make you feel happy and excited.

Once upon a time, there was a little caterpillar named Catty. Catty was very fearful. She was scared of many things, like big birds and loud noises. One day, Catty met a wise old owl named Mr. Hoot. Mr. Hoot was very smart and knew many things.
Mr. Hoot saw that Catty was scared, so he wanted to help her. He said, "Catty, I recommend you go to the big tree at the end of the forest. You will find something special there." Catty was curious but still scared. Mr. Hoot told her not to worry and that she would be safe.
Catty went on a long walk to the big tree. She was scared, but she remembered what Mr. Hoot said. When she got to the big tree, she found a beautiful cocoon. Catty went inside the cocoon and took a long nap. When she woke up, she was not a caterpillar anymore. She was a beautiful butterfly! Catty was not fearful anymore. She was brave and strong, and she flew high in the sky.

There was a fish. It was swimming in the lake. It was a very big lake. The fish was very stupid. It was swimming around and around and around. It wasn't being very careful. Suddenly, it bumped into a big rock in the lake. It hurt its head and it was very scared. But then it swam away and it was ok. It decided to stay away from the big rocks in the lake.

One day, a little boy named Tim and his dog, Spot, went for a walk in the woods. Tim saw a big, dangerous snake. He was scared and ran away. Spot tried to follow Tim, but he lost him.
"Spot, where are you?" Tim cried. "I am lost!" Spot heard Tim but could not find him. The sun went down, and Tim was still lost in the woods. Tim started to cry.
In the morning, Tim woke up and saw the dangerous snake again. He was very scared. The snake came closer and closer. Tim called for Spot, but he never came.

Once upon a time, there was an adorable little dog named Max. Max had a big worry. He could not remember where he left his favorite toy, a small red ball. Max looked everywhere, but he could not find it.
One day, Max went to the park with his friend, a little girl named Lily. Lily saw Max's worry and wanted to help him. They looked for the red ball under the trees and near the swings. Lily asked Max to remember where he played last time.
Max thought and thought. Then, he remembered that he played near the big slide. They went to the slide and found the red ball. Max was so happy! His worry was gone. Max and Lily played with the red ball all day, and they had lots of fun.

One day, a cute little bird named Timmy was looking for a new home. He found an adorable tree with a strong branch. Timmy thought this branch would support a nice nest. So, he started to build his nest on the branch. He used leaves, sticks, and grass to make the nest cozy.
Timmy was very happy with his new nest. It had a nice roof to keep him dry when it rained. He loved to sit in his nest and sing sweet songs. His friends would come to visit and they all liked his new home.
One day, a big storm came. The wind blew hard and the rain fell down. Timmy was scared. He hid inside his nest, but the roof was not strong enough. The rain came in and the nest broke. Timmy fell to the ground and he was very sad. His home was gone and he had to find a new place to live.

Once upon a time, there was a big library. In the library, there lived a graceful cat named Lucy. Lucy loved to drink milk and read books with the kids who visited the library.
One day, a little girl named Amy came to the library. Amy felt thirsty, so she asked Lucy, "Where can I get a drink?" Lucy replied, "I know a place where you can drink water. Follow me!" Lucy led Amy to a water fountain in the library.
After Amy drank the water, she thanked Lucy and they both read books together. The other kids saw them and joined in. Everyone was happy and they all became friends. They learned that sharing and helping others can bring happiness.
The moral of the story is: Be kind and help others, and you will make friends and be happy.

Once upon a time, in a nice little house, there lived a boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside, but winter was coming. It was getting cold, and the trees were losing their leaves. Tim knew the birds would be hungry, so he decided to feed them.
Every day, Tim went out in the cold to give food to the birds. He liked to see them eat and be happy. One day, while Tim was feeding the birds, something unexpected happened. A big, fluffy bird came down from the sky and landed next to Tim. The bird was not like the others. It could talk!
"Thank you for the food, Tim," said the big bird. "I am a magic bird, and I want to give you a gift for being so nice." The bird waved its wings, and suddenly, Tim could talk to all the birds. They became his friends, and they all played together in the winter snow. And from that day on, Tim and the birds had lots of fun, even in the cold winter days.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a favorite sheet that she loved very much. She took her sheet everywhere she went. One day, she went to the park with her mommy and her sheet.
At the park, Lily played with her sheet on the slide and the swings. She was so happy! But then, a big wind came and took her sheet away. Lily was very sad and didn't know what to do. She looked around for her sheet but couldn't find it.
Just then, a nice boy named Tom saw Lily's sheet in a tree. He climbed the tree and got the sheet for her. Lily was so happy and said, "Thank you, Tom!" They played together for the rest of the day, and Lily learned to always say thank you when someone helps her.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there lived a weak old man named Bob. Bob was kind and had a happy spirit. He liked to sit outside his house and talk to the people who walked by.
One day, a little girl named Lily came to visit Bob. She said, "Bob, you seem very weak. Can I help you?" Bob smiled and said, "Yes, Lily. Can you please bring me some water from the well?" Lily nodded and went to get the water.
When Lily came back, she gave the water to Bob. He drank it and felt better. They sat and talked for a while. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing. Bob's spirit was happy, and he didn't feel weak anymore. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a girl named Mia went outside to play in the snow. She saw brown leaves on the ground. She had an idea. Mia wanted to sort the leaves and the snow.
Mia started to sort the brown leaves from the white snow. She made two big piles. One pile was all snow, and the other pile was all brown leaves. Mia was happy with her work.
Suddenly, the brown leaves started to move! Mia was scared at first, but then she saw it was a friendly squirrel. The squirrel had been hiding under the leaves all this time! Mia and the squirrel laughed and played in the snow and brown leaves together.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Flossy. Everywhere Flossy went she loved to find something soft to cuddle up against. She found the best spot in the house to be the old, worn out armchair with the fluffy pillows. 
One day, Flossy decided to explore outside. She ran down to the beach where she saw a large, metal anchor. She touched the edges and it felt so soft and cool! She touched it again and again until she heard a deep voice say, "Hello Flossy! I'm the anchor - don't I feel soft on your fingers?"
Flossy was startled. She stepped back and said, "Who are you? How can you speak?" 
The anchor replied, "I'm an old treasure that has been underwater for many years, but I can still speak!" Flossy asked many questions and learned all about the anchor's many adventures. 
At the end, the anchor said, "Remember Flossy, this anchor will always be your soft friend." From that day on, Flossy stopped by the beach to visit her anchor friend and give it a pat.

Once upon a time, there was an elephant named Red who was very restless. He never seemed to have time to relax. He was always running around and playing with his friends.
One day, Red was so busy playing that he didn't notice a lemon lying on the ground. Without realizing it, he stepped on the lemon and it burst open.
Suddenly, he felt a sharp poke on his foot. He turned around and saw an angry bee. It was mad at Red for squashing the lemon!
Red was very sorry and tried to apologize, but the bee buzzed away.
Red learned a valuable lesson that day. He realized that if he had just taken a few minutes to rest, he wouldn't have stepped on the lemon and not upset the bee. From then on, Red made sure to take time and relax every once in a while!

Once upon a time there was a wild and mischievous bird called Joe. He had a large, soft feather that he always carried with him.
One day, Joe flew very high and was exhausted from all the flapping. He wanted to rest for a while, so he decided to find a tree.
He came across a large tree with lots of branches. He landed on one and closed his eyes. He said, "This is the perfect place for a nap."
But as Joe was resting, there was a noise. He looked down to see a group of little children. They were pointing at Joe and said, "Look, it's a wild bird!"
Joe quickly flew away and felt a bit embarrassed. He thought to himself, "Next time I'll find a better place to rest." 
From then on, Joe found secret places to rest and never let anyone catch him napping!

Once upon a time, there was a polite dog named Spot. Spot loved to play outside all day. He liked to run, jump, and chase his tail. His favorite time to play was during the sunset when the sky turned orange and pink.
One day, Spot found a big box in his yard. He was very curious about the box. Spot decided to cover the box with a soft blanket. He thought it would make the box feel cozy and safe.
The next day, Spot went to check on the box. He found a new friend inside! It was a little kitten named Fluffy. Spot and Fluffy played together in the yard until the beautiful sunset. They became best friends and always played together during sunset.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to a food place with his mom. It was cold outside, so they both wore big coats. Inside the food place, they sat down at a table. A nice lady gave them a menu to look at. The menu had many yummy things to eat.
Tim saw ice cream on the menu. He really wanted it, even though it was cold outside. He said to his mom, "Mom, can I have ice cream, please?" His mom said, "No, Tim. It's too cold for ice cream. Pick something warm to eat." But Tim insisted, "I really want ice cream!"
His mom smiled and said, "Okay, Tim. You can have ice cream after you eat something warm." Tim was happy and picked a warm soup to eat. After he ate his soup, he got his ice cream. Even though it was cold outside, Tim enjoyed his ice cream very much.

Once upon a time, there lived a brilliant fox. It had white fur with glittering eyes. One day, the fox was walking through the forest. Suddenly, it heard a noise. "Split!"
The fox saw that a neck of a bird was stuck in a crack between two rocks. The bird quacked, "Please help me!" The fox thought of a brilliant idea. It gathered small rocks and started building a bridge over the crack. 
When the fox finished building the bridge, the bird said, "That's a brilliant idea! Come, let's cross over together." But the fox kept walking away. 
The bird was surprised and said, "Why don't you come? You don't want to help me?" The fox stopped and said, "I'm not here to help you. I was hoping you'd fall into the crack - that way I could eat you for dinner!" 
The bird was sad and flew away. It learned that not everyone is kind and helpful, no matter how brilliant the idea might be.
Moral of the story: It's always better to help people in need than take advantage of them.

Once upon a time there was a little boy called Tom. He was only three years old but he had a lot of very special friends. One day, he went to visit his friends in the forest. They were tiny creatures with beautiful wings and they needed help.
They asked Tom to supply them with cloth so they could make themselves new clothes. Tom said yes and he went to search for the perfect cloth. After a while, he found the perfect green cloth and he took it back to his friends. 
When they saw the cloth, they were very happy. "It's perfect!", they shouted excitedly. Then they hugged Tom and thanked him for the great present. 
Tom was very proud and happy he could help his friends. And from that day on, all the little creatures in the forest had their very own green cloth.

Once upon a time, in a lovely little town, there was a girl named Sue. Sue had a rag that she always carried with her. One day, Sue and her friend Tom went to play in the park.
In the park, they saw a big tree. Sue and Tom wanted to climb the tree. Sue put her rag on the ground and they started to climb. They were very happy and having lots of fun. But, they did not see the dark clouds coming.
Soon, it started to rain. Sue and Tom climbed down the tree very fast. Sue picked up her wet rag and they ran home. But, they did not yield to the rain and kept playing outside.
The rain got heavier and heavier. Sue and Tom got very wet and cold. They wished they had stopped playing when the rain started. Now, they were both sad and not feeling well.
In the end, Sue and Tom got sick from playing in the rain. They had to stay in bed for many days. They learned that sometimes it is better to yield and go inside when it starts to rain.

Once upon a time, there was a happy little boy named Tim. Tim loved to eat candy. He had a big pocket on his shirt where he kept all his candy. Every day, he would eat candy from his pocket and share it with his friends. Tim and his friends were always happy when they ate candy together.
One sunny day, Tim went to the park to play. He had a lot of candy in his pocket. He played with his friends and they all ate candy. They ran, jumped, and laughed. Tim's pocket was almost empty, but he was still very happy.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big, hungry dog came to the park. The dog saw Tim's pocket and wanted to eat the candy too. The dog jumped on Tim and ate all the candy from his pocket. Tim and his friends were sad and scared. The dog ate the candy and ran away. Tim's pocket was empty, and he couldn't share candy with his friends anymore. They all went home with a bad ending to their day.

Once upon a time, a little sheep had a big dream. The sheep lived on a farm with many friends. But the sheep was grumpy. It wanted to be happy like the other animals.
One day, the grumpy sheep found a magic hat. The hat could make dreams come true. The sheep put on the hat and wished to be happy. The magic hat did something unexpected. It turned the grumpy sheep into a happy bird!
The bird flew high in the sky, singing and laughing. The other animals saw the bird and wanted to be happy too. They all put on the magic hat, one by one. They all turned into happy birds! They all flew away, leaving the farm behind. The grumpy sheep's dream came true, but not in the way it thought.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big rock. The rock was a rare color. It was blue and shiny. Tim wanted to touch the rock.
"Mom, can I touch the rock?" Tim asked. His mom said, "Yes, but be careful." Tim touched the rock. It was very smooth.
All of a sudden, the rock started to talk! "Hi, I am a magic rock!" said the rock. Tim and his mom were very surprised. They did not know rocks could talk. The magic rock made their walk very fun.

Lily was a little girl who loved to sing. She sang every day, in the morning, in the afternoon, and at night. She sang to her toys, to her mom, to her dad, and to her cat. She sang happy songs, sad songs, silly songs, and made-up songs.
One day, Lily's mom told her that they were going to a big place where many people sing and play music. Lily was very excited. She put on her favorite dress and her shiny shoes. She held her mom's hand as they walked to the big place. She saw many people sitting in rows of chairs. She saw a big stage with lights and curtains. She heard a loud sound that made her jump.
"Shh, Lily, that's the bell. It means the show is about to start. We have to be quiet and listen," her mom whispered.
Lily nodded and sat on her mom's lap. She watched as the curtains opened and the singers and musicians came on the stage. They sang and played beautiful songs that made Lily smile and clap. She liked the songs that were fast and loud, and the songs that were slow and soft. She liked the singers who wore colorful costumes and the singers who wore ordinary clothes. She liked the music that made her want to dance and the music that made her want to hug her mom.
After a while, the singers and musicians stopped singing and playing. They bowed to the people and smiled. The people stood up and made a lot of noise with their hands. They were applauding. Lily joined them and applauded too. She was very happy.
"Did you like the show, Lily?" her mom asked.
"I liked it very much, mom. It was amazing. Can we see it again?" Lily asked.
"Maybe another day, sweetie. But now we have to go home. It's getting late and you must be hungry," her mom said.
Lily felt her stomach and realized she was hungry. She had been so busy watching and listening that she had forgotten to eat. She hoped there was something yummy for dinner at home.
"OK, mom. Let's go home. But can I sing one more song on the way?" Lily asked.
"Of course, Lily. You can sing as much as you want. You have a lovely voice," her mom said.
Lily smiled and hugged her mom. She sang one of her favorite songs as they walked home. She was a little girl who loved to sing.

Once upon a time, in a big garden, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to mind the flowers. She had a favorite flower that was amazing. It was big and red. Lily took care of it every day.
One day, Lily's friend Tom came to play. "Tom, come see my amazing flower!" said Lily. "Be careful not to step on it." Tom looked at the flower and said, "Wow, it is so big and red!"
The next day, Lily went to mind her flower. But she saw something was wrong. The amazing flower was not standing up like before. Lily was sad. She remembered what she told Tom.
Lily went to find Tom. "Tom, did you step on my flower?" she asked. Tom said, "No, Lily, I did not. But I saw a big ball near it." They went back to the garden and found the ball. They moved the ball and the amazing flower stood up again. Lily and Tom were happy and played together all day.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a little girl named Lily and her mom. One night, the lights went out and it was very dark. Lily's mom said, "Don't worry, we will use a flashlight."
Lily and her mom looked for the flashlight. They found it under the bed. Lily felt scared because the dark was wild. But her mom said, "It's okay, we have the flashlight to help us see."
Lily and her mom walked around the house with the flashlight. They saw their cat, Max, and he was in a panic. Lily said, "Don't worry, Max. We have the flashlight to make the dark go away."
In the end, the lights came back on and everything was bright again. Lily learned that even when things seem scary, there is always a way to make it better. And now, she knew not to panic when the lights went out, because she had her flashlight and her mom to help her.

Ben and Lily were very happy to go on a tour of the zoo with their mom and dad. They saw many animals, like lions, monkeys, and giraffes. They liked the giraffes the most, because they had long necks and spots. They wanted to feed them some leaves, but the zookeeper said they had to wait for their turn.
When it was their turn, Ben and Lily walked up to the fence with their mom and dad. They each held a leaf in their hand and reached out to the giraffes. The giraffes bent down and took the leaves with their tongues. Ben and Lily giggled and said, "Thank you, giraffes!"
But then, one of the giraffes did something bad. It sneezed very loud and wet. It sneezed right on Ben and Lily's faces. They felt the giraffe's snot and spit on their skin and hair. They screamed and cried. They dropped their leaves and ran back to their mom and dad.
Their mom and dad were shocked and angry. They grabbed some napkins and tried to wipe off the giraffe's sneeze from Ben and Lily's faces. They said, "We're so sorry, kids. That was very rude of the giraffe. Let's go to the bathroom and wash up."
But Ben and Lily were not happy. They did not want to see any more animals. They did not want to be on the tour anymore. They wanted to go home. They said, "We hate the zoo. We hate the giraffes. They ruined our day."
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a soft cat named Tuesday. Tuesday liked to follow a little boy named Tim. They played together every day.
One day, Tim found a big box in his room. He opened the box and saw a big red ball inside. Tuesday looked at the ball and wanted to play with it too. Tim threw the ball and Tuesday followed it.
The ball bounced down the stairs and into the kitchen. Tuesday followed the ball, but when she got to the kitchen, the ball was gone! Instead, there was a big cake on the table. Tim came running in and saw the cake too. They were both surprised.
They looked at each other and laughed. They knew the ball was hiding somewhere. They ate the cake together and had a fun time. In the end, they found the ball behind the fridge. Tuesday and Tim played with the ball and had a great day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a famous dog named Max. Max was famous because he could find anything with his nose. People would ask him to find lost toys, food, and even mints.
One day, a big cat named Tom came to town. Tom liked to fight and cause trouble. He saw Max and wanted to fight him too. Max did not want to fight, but he wanted to keep the town safe and happy.
Max had an idea. He found a mint and gave it to Tom. Tom liked the mint very much. He did not want to fight Max anymore. They became friends and played together. The town was safe and happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a wild plant in the forest. It was not like the other plants. It had big leaves and bright colors. The wild plant was very happy and loved to dance when the wind blew.
One day, a small bird came to the wild plant. The bird said, "You are not like the other plants. You are wild and different." The wild plant felt embarrass. It did not want to be different from the other plants.
But then, a big rain came. The wild plant's big leaves and bright colors helped it grow strong. The other plants saw this and wanted to be like the wild plant too. In the end, the wild plant was not embarrass anymore. It was happy to be wild and different.

Once upon a time, in a small garden, there lived a fat beetle named Bob. Bob was very big, and he had many friends. They all liked to play together in the sun.
One day, a new beetle named Tim entered the garden. Tim was not as big as Bob, but he was very fast. Bob and his friends wanted to play with Tim, but they were scared. They thought Tim might be too fast for them.
Tim saw that Bob and his friends were scared. He wanted to show them that it is good to be friends with different kinds of beetles. So, Tim asked Bob to race with him. Bob agreed, and they started the race. Bob was slow, but he tried his best. In the end, Tim won the race, but he was very nice to Bob.
Bob and his friends learned that it is good to be friends with different beetles. They all became friends with Tim and had lots of fun together. The garden was a happy place for all the beetles, big and small, fast and slow. They all played together and learned from each other. The moral of the story is that we should be friends with everyone, no matter how different they are.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat. The cat was helpless because it was very small. One day, the cat went to a store. In the store, the cat saw a lot of things. The cat wanted to find something to eat.
The cat walked inside the store and saw a big box. The box was very cold. The cat was curious and wanted to know what was inside the box. The cat jumped into the cold box to see.
The cat found some food in the cold box. But the cat was too small to jump out. The cat tried and tried but could not get out. The cat was stuck in the cold box. The cat was very sad and cold.

Lily and Tom were playing with sand at the park. They had buckets and shovels and made a big castle. Lily was very proud of her castle. She raised her bucket high and said, "Look at my castle! It is the best!"
Tom was jealous of Lily's castle. He wanted to have the best castle too. He raised his shovel and said, "No, my castle is better!" He threw some sand at Lily's castle and broke it.
Lily was very sad and angry. She raised her hand and said, "Stop it, Tom! You are mean!" She threw some sand back at Tom and hit his face.
Tom cried and ran away. He had sand in his eyes and mouth. He felt sorry for what he did.
Lily felt sorry too. She realized that throwing sand was not nice. She ran after Tom and said, "I'm sorry, Tom. I should not have thrown sand at you. Can we be friends again?"
Tom nodded and said, "I'm sorry too, Lily. I should not have been jealous of your castle. Can we share our buckets and shovels and make a new castle together?"
Lily smiled and said, "Yes, Tom. That sounds like fun. Sharing is caring."
They hugged and went back to the sand. They worked together and made a bigger and better castle. They were happy and learned a lesson.

Once upon a time there lived a little girl called Mary. Mary was brave and daring and loved exploring the world. One day, Mary wanted to go and explore a dark cave that she had heard about. She filled her pockets with supplies and ventured into the cave.
As soon as Mary stepped into the cave, she heard a loud rumble. The rumbling became more and more deafening and suddenly, the floor started shaking. Mary realised it was an earthquake and screamed. 
When the shaking stopped, Mary looked around. There was dirt all over the floor and the walls were filthy. As Mary tried to make her way out, she stepped into a puddle. She looked down and realised it was not a puddle, it was liquid pouring from the ceiling. 
The liquid was pitch black and it stank really bad. Mary frantically tried to back away but the slippery floor was dangerous and she ended up tripping and falling into the puddle of liquid. Mary was so scared she could hardly breath. 
That is where Mary stayed until she was finally rescued. Sadly, due to the filthy liquid Mary was never the same again and she was forever changed.

One day, a brown cat saw a big envelope on the floor. The cat was very curious. It wanted to know what was inside. So, the cat went to the envelope and tried to open it. But the envelope was too big and too strong for the cat. The cat asked a dog for help. The dog was happy to help.
The dog said, "Let's jump on the envelope together!" The cat agreed. They both jumped on the envelope, and it opened. Inside the envelope was a big, shiny ball. The cat and the dog were very happy. They played with the ball all day long.
At the end of the day, the cat and the dog were tired but happy. They learned that by working together, they can do big things. The cat said, "Thank you, dog, for helping me. We make a good team!" And the dog agreed. They became good friends and shared the shiny ball. The cat and the dog played together every day, and they always helped each other.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a gifted child who loved to learn. He had a special friend, an old owl named Mr. Wise. Mr. Wise was full of wisdom and liked to teach Tim new things.
One day, Tim and Mr. Wise were sitting under a big tree. Tim asked, "Mr. Wise, how do you feel when you teach me things?" Mr. Wise thought for a moment and said, "Tim, I feel happy when I teach you. I also feel good knowing that you will use the wisdom I share with you."
As they talked, a little bird flew to the tree and watched them. The bird wanted to be like Tim and learn from Mr. Wise too. Tim saw the bird and said, "Hello, little bird! Do you want to learn with us?" The bird chirped happily and joined them.
From that day on, Tim, Mr. Wise, and the little bird spent their days learning together. They all felt happy and grateful for the wisdom they shared. Tim knew he was gifted and wanted to share his wisdom with others too. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. It was a cold day, so she wore a big coat. At the park, she saw a big pile of colorful leaves. Lily wanted to play with the leaves, but they were all mixed up. She decided to sort them by color: red, yellow, and brown.
While Lily was sorting the leaves, a boy named Tim came to the park. Tim saw Lily and asked, "What are you doing?" Lily said, "I am sorting leaves by color. Want to help?" Tim said, "Yes!" So, they sorted the leaves together. When they finished, they had three big piles of leaves.
Lily and Tim decided to jump in the piles of leaves. They had so much fun! After playing, they had to wait for their moms to pick them up. While they waited, they talked and became good friends. The moral of the story is that working together and sharing can make new friends and turn work into fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to go to the theater with her mom and dad. They would watch fun plays and laugh together.
One day, Lily found her mom's favorite cup in the kitchen. She wanted to use it, but she dropped it and it broke. Lily felt guilty and sad. She wanted to make her mom happy again.
Lily had an idea. She asked her dad, "Can we replace mom's cup?" Her dad said, "Yes, let's go to the store and find a new one." They went to the store and found a cup just like the one that broke. They brought it home and showed it to mom.
Mom was so happy that Lily tried to fix her mistake. She hugged Lily and said, "Thank you for being honest and trying to make things right." They all went to the theater that night to celebrate and watch a play.
The moral of the story is that it's important to admit when you make a mistake and try to fix it.

Once upon a time there was a little boy named Jack. Jack was troubled and he often felt sad. One day he was walking through a park when he saw a juggling performer. The performer juggled balls, spun plates, and even walked on stilts. 
Jack was excited to watch the performer and he danced around as he watched. When the performer was done, Jack wanted to give him a tip. But all he had was some candy, so he gave the performer a handful of lollipops.
The performer smiled and took the candy. But then he said something that Jack didn't understand. "You think this is enough? This isn't enough! I worked very hard, I need more than just candy". 
Jack was worried, he felt sad and scared. He didn't know what to do so he started to cry. The performer got angry and shouted at him. Jack ran away, he was too scared to stay.
The performer had been troubled before, but now his anger was even worse. Jack's tip had only made things worse. From then on, the little boy was very scared of performers.

Once upon a time, in a nice little house, there lived a boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toys. One day, Tim found a big squash in the garden. He was very happy.
Tim went to his friend, Sue. "Look!" he said. "I found a big squash. Let's play with it!" Sue was excited too. They played with the squash, twisting it and rolling it around. They had a lot of fun.
But then, something unexpected happened. The squash started to grow bigger and bigger! Tim and Sue were scared. They didn't know what to do. Then, Tim had an idea. They twisted the squash one more time, and it stopped growing. They were safe and happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a hungry butterfly. The butterfly did not know where to find food. It flew around looking for something to eat.
One day, the butterfly met a big, friendly bear. The bear knew where to find food. He said, "Come with me, little butterfly. I will show you where to find yummy flowers." The butterfly was very happy and said, "Thank you, big bear!"
The bear took the butterfly to a beautiful garden full of flowers. The butterfly ate and ate until it was not hungry anymore. The butterfly and the bear became good friends. They played together every day and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside with his friends. They had a fun club where they would play games and share toys. Tim was a light-hearted boy who was always happy and kind.
One day, Tim found a shiny rock while playing in his yard. He decided to bury it in the ground so he could find it later. He told his friends in the club about the shiny rock and they all wanted to see it. Tim dug up the shiny rock and showed it to his friends. They all thought it was very special.
Tim's friends wanted to bury their own special things too. They all found something they loved and buried it in the ground. After a while, they dug up their special things and shared them with each other. They learned that sharing made them happy and brought them closer together. The moral of the story is that sharing with friends can bring happiness and make your friendships stronger.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a unique girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the sunshine. Every day, she would put on her favorite dress and run outside to play with her friends.
One sunny day, Lily met a new friend named Max. Max was a funny boy who made Lily laugh a lot. They played together all day in the sunshine. They ran, jumped, and had lots of fun. When they got tired, they sat under a big tree to rest.
Lily said to Max, "I like your shirt!" Max smiled and said, "Thank you! I like your dress too!" They both felt happy and special in their unique clothes. After that day, Lily and Max became best friends and played together in the sunshine every day.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty cat named Lily. Lily loved to sleep on the big, soft couch in the living room. Every day, she would jump up on the couch, curl up, and take a long nap.
One day, while Lily was sleeping, she heard a noise. She opened her eyes and saw a little bird sitting on the couch. The bird looked scared. Lily was surprised, but she wanted to be friends with the bird.
Lily and the bird started to play and have fun. They played all day on the couch. When it was time to sleep, Lily and the bird curled up together on the couch. They became best friends, and from that day on, they always slept together on the big, soft couch.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to sit and watch his mom work in the garden. One sunny day, Tim sat under a tree while his mom worked. The weather was mild and the sun felt warm on his face.
Tim's mom said, "Tim, please help me with the work." Tim didn't want to work. He wanted to sit and play with his toys. But Tim's mom said, "We must finish the work first, then we can play."
Tim decided to help his mom. They worked together and soon the garden looked beautiful. Tim felt happy that he helped his mom. They sat down and played with his toys. Tim learned that helping others with work can be fun and make you feel good.

Lily hated the drawer in her room. It was long and hard to open. She always had to pull and pull to get her toys out. Sometimes the drawer got stuck and she had to ask Mommy or Daddy for help.
One day, Lily wanted to play with her doll. She went to the drawer and tried to open it. But it was too heavy and she could not move it. She got angry and kicked the drawer. "Stupid drawer!" she shouted.
Mommy heard her and came to see what was wrong. She saw Lily crying and the drawer with a dent. "Lily, what did you do?" she asked.
"I hate the drawer! It won't open and it hurts my foot!" Lily said.
Mommy hugged Lily and said, "I'm sorry, sweetie. The drawer is old and needs to be fixed. But you should not kick it or say mean words. That is not nice. How about we find another place for your toys?"
Lily nodded and wiped her tears. Mommy helped her move her toys to a basket. Lily liked the basket. It was easy to carry and had a pretty bow. She thanked Mommy and gave her a kiss. Then she took her doll and went to play. She forgot about the drawer and was happy.

Sara and Ben are twins. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big tree with many green needles. It is a pine tree.
"Can we climb the pine tree?" Sara asks Ben.
Ben looks at the pine tree. He sees that it is very tall and thin. He is afraid to fall.
"No, Sara, it is too high and too thin. We might hurt ourselves," Ben says.
Sara is sad. She wants to climb the pine tree. She thinks it looks fun and pretty.
"Please, Ben, just a little bit. I will be careful," Sara begs.
Ben shakes his head. He does not want to climb the pine tree. He wants to play with his ball.
"Come on, Sara, let's play with the ball. It is more fun and safe," Ben says.
He throws the ball to Sara. Sara catches it. She smiles. She likes to play with the ball too.
"Okay, Ben, let's play with the ball. Maybe another day we can climb the pine tree," Sara says.
They run and laugh and play with the ball. They forget about the pine tree. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a quiet little automobile named Tom. Tom loved to sleep during the day, but at night he would wake up and go on adventures.
One night, Tom met a big red automobile named Sam. Sam asked, "Do you want to play with me?" Tom said, "Yes, let's go!" They drove around the town, laughing and having fun.
As the sun began to rise, Tom and Sam knew it was time to go to sleep. They found a nice spot under a big tree and said goodnight. Tom closed his eyes and went to sleep, dreaming of more fun adventures with his new friend.

Once upon a time there was a helpful witch. She loved to help people, and when she had free time she would rest on her broomstick.
The witch flew around the village looking for people who needed help. One day, she came across an old man with a sore back who lived in a house at the edge of the village. The witch helped the old man carry his bags of food up the stairs to his house. The old man was so happy he thanked the witch and gave her a big hug.
The witch continued around the village helping people with their chores. She watered the gardens and fetched water for the farmers. The farmers were worried their crops would fail without water, but the witch helped them get the water they needed.
At the end of the day the witch was tired, so she returned to her broomstick and flew home. She had been very helpful that day and did a wonderful job. The witch laid down on her broomstick and rested, feeling very happy that she could help others.

Once there was a friendly frog named Fred. Fred lived in a swamp. He liked to hop and play with his friends.
One day, Fred's mom told him not to forget to come home before dark. Fred said, "Okay, Mom!" and went to play with his friends. They played hide and seek and had fun.
Fred was having so much fun that he forgot what his mom said. It got dark and Fred was still in the swamp. He got scared and couldn't find his way home.
Fred called for his mom, but she couldn't hear him. Fred stayed in the dark swamp all night, feeling sad and scared. He wished he didn't forget what his mom told him.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy raft that he loved to play with in the bathtub. The raft was different from his other toys because it could float on water. One day, Tim decided to take his raft to the park.
At the park, Tim met a girl named Sue. Sue had a water bottle that could spray water. "Hi Tim, do you want to play with me?" Sue asked. Tim said, "Yes, let's play with my raft and your water bottle!"
They went to a small pond and put the raft on the water. Sue sprayed the water from her bottle onto the raft, making it move across the pond. Tim and Sue laughed and played together, having so much fun with the different raft and the spray from the water bottle. They became good friends and played at the park every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had an ancient belt. The belt was very old and special. It belonged to his grandpa. Tim loved his belt very much.
One day, Tim was playing with his friend, Sue. Sue saw the belt and asked, "Can I wear your belt, Tim?" Tim thought for a moment and then said, "Okay, I will allow you to wear it."
Sue was so happy to wear the ancient belt. She felt special too. They played all day with the belt. At the end of the day, Tim and Sue gave the belt a big hug. They knew the belt was magic because it made them feel happy and loved.

Lily and Tom like to play near the lake. They see many fish and ducks in the water. They also see some stones on the ground. Lily picks up a stone and throws it into the lake. She likes to see the splash and the ripples. Tom picks up a stone too and throws it into the lake. He likes to hear the plop and the quack.
But then they see a big sign that says "No throwing stones". It has a picture of a duck with a red cross on it. Lily and Tom do not know what the sign means, but they think it is important. They stop throwing stones and look at the sign. They wonder why they cannot throw stones.
A man comes by and sees them. He smiles and says hello. He tells them that the sign means that throwing stones can hurt the fish and the ducks. He says that the fish and the ducks live in the lake and need to be safe. He says that throwing stones can make them scared or injured. He says that Lily and Tom can play with the stones, but not throw them into the lake.
Lily and Tom understand and say sorry. They do not want to hurt the fish and the ducks. They want them to be safe and happy. They put the stones back on the ground and look for other things to play with. They find some sticks and leaves and make a boat. They put the boat on the water and watch it float. They see the fish and the ducks swim by and wave to them. They are glad that they are not throwing stones anymore. They are having fun and being kind.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and eat chocolate. One day, she found a big, harmless bug in her room. The bug was green and had big, round eyes. Lily was not scared because she knew it was a friendly bug.
Lily said to the bug, "Hi, Mr. Bug! Do you want to play with me?" The bug nodded its head and started to play with Lily. They played with her toys and had a lot of fun. After a while, Lily got hungry and went to her mom to ask for some chocolate.
"Mom, can I have some chocolate, please?" asked Lily. Her mom smiled and said, "Of course, sweetie. But don't forget to share with your new friend." Lily took the chocolate and went back to her room. She broke the chocolate into two pieces and said to the bug, "Here, Mr. Bug. Let's pass the chocolate and share it."
Lily and the bug ate the chocolate together. They were very happy and continued to play all day long. From that day on, Lily and the harmless bug became best friends. They played together every day and always shared their chocolate. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a big, strong lion. He was the king of the jungle. All the animals were afraid of him. The strong lion liked to order the animals to do things for him. He would say, "You, get me food!" or "You, clean my home!"
One day, a little mouse came to the strong lion. The mouse was very scared, but he had a promise to keep. He said, "Mr. Lion, I have a promise to give you a big gift. But you must be nice to me and my friends." The strong lion laughed and said, "Okay, little mouse, I will be nice to you."
But the strong lion did not keep his promise. He still ordered the animals around and was not nice to them. The little mouse was sad because the strong lion did not keep his promise. So the mouse decided not to give the strong lion his big gift.
The strong lion never got his big gift and the animals did not like him. The strong lion was sad and alone. And that is how the story ends, with the strong lion learning that it is not good to break promises and order others around.

Once upon a time, there was a rhinoceros. He was big and impressive. He wanted to find somewhere to call home. He hopped through the grass and looked everywhere. He saw a lake and knew it was the perfect spot.
The rhinoceros hopped closer and closer until he reached the lake. The sun shone on the water and the leaves rustled in the wind. It was so peaceful. He hoped that this would be his forever home.
The rhinoceros felt happy and content. Over the days and weeks, he made lots of new friends. He explored his new home, drank from the lake and played in the sun. He was so happy.
He continued to live in his new home for many years. He was so proud of his beautiful lake, and told his friends all about it. They thought it sounded so impressive. The rhinoceros was so happy he had found his forever home. He hoped he would be there forever.

Tom and Sam were brothers. They liked to play with toys, but they had too many toys. Their room was full of junk. Mom said they had to clean up or they would get no cake.
Tom and Sam did not want to clean up. They wanted to eat cake. They had a plan. They would spend some of their coins and buy new toys. Then they would give their old toys to someone else.
They went to the store with their coins. They saw many toys. Some were bright and shiny. Some were soft and fluffy. Some made noises and moved. But some were dull and broken. They cost less coins.
Tom and Sam bought the dull and broken toys. They thought they were smart. They went back home with their new toys. They put their old toys in a big bag. They gave the bag to their neighbor, Lily.
Lily was happy. She thanked Tom and Sam. She opened the bag and saw the toys. She liked them. She said they were not junk. They were treasures. She hugged the toys and smiled.
Tom and Sam went to their room. They looked at their new toys. They were dull and broken. They did not make them happy. They wished they had their old toys back. They felt silly. They had no cake and no fun.

One day, a little fish was swimming in the sea. The fish had a shiny scale on its side. The fish was happy and liked to play with its friends. They played all day in the water.
Then, something happened. The shiny scale fell off! The fish was sad and frustrated. It did not know what to do. It asked its friends for help, but they did not know how to help the fish.
The fish swam around, looking for its scale. It found a big shell on the sea floor. Inside the shell, there was a surprise! It was not the fish's scale, but a new friend! The new friend was a crab. The crab said, "I can help you find your scale!" The fish and the crab looked together and found the shiny scale. The fish was happy again, and now it had a new friend to play with in the sea.

Once upon a time, there was a big, heavy tank. It was very strong and could go fast. One day, the tank went to play with its friends in the park. They played fun games like hide and seek and tag. The tank was very happy.
Then, something unexpected happened. While they were playing, the tank missed its turn and hit a tree. The tree fell down and made a loud noise. All the friends were scared and ran away. The tank felt very sad.
But, the plot twist was that the tree had a big nest with baby birds inside. The baby birds were now on the ground and could not get back to their nest. The tank decided to help the baby birds. It used its strong, heavy body to lift the tree back up. The baby birds were able to go back to their nest. The tank's friends saw how kind and helpful it was and came back to play. They all played together again and had a great day.

Tom was a smart boy who liked to build things with his steel toys. He had many cars, planes, trains and robots that he made with his steel pieces. He also had a big steel box where he kept his toys safe from his little sister, who liked to break them.
One day, Tom saw a new steel toy in the store. It was a shiny rocket that could fly and make noises. Tom wanted the rocket very much, but he did not have enough money to buy it. He asked his mom if he could have it, but she said no. She said he had to save his money and be good.
Tom was sad, but he did not give up. He decided to do extra chores around the house and help his mom and dad. He also sold some of his old toys that he did not play with anymore. He put all his money in a jar and counted it every day. He hoped he would have enough to buy the rocket before it was gone.
After a few weeks, Tom had enough money to buy the rocket. He was very happy and excited. He ran to the store with his mom and found the rocket on the shelf. He grabbed it and took it to the cashier. He paid with his money and thanked the cashier. He hugged his mom and thanked her too. He could not wait to go home and play with his rocket.
Tom ran to his room and opened the rocket. He put the batteries in and turned it on. The rocket made a loud noise and a bright light. Tom smiled and pressed the button to make it fly. The rocket flew up in the air and zoomed around the room. Tom followed it with his eyes and laughed. He was so happy with his new toy. He thought it was the best steel toy ever. He thanked himself for being smart and working hard. He also thanked his steel box for keeping his toys safe. He put his rocket in the box and closed it. He knew he would have many fun adventures with his rocket. He was a smart boy who liked to build things with his steel toys.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy who loved to sort. His favorite thing to sort was blue blocks.
One day, he found a really big box full of colorful blocks. He was so excited!
The little boy laid out all the blocks and began to sort them. One color at a time, he sorted the blocks into neat piles. 
Finally, the little boy reached the blue blocks. He was so enthusiastic that he shouted out loud, "Blue! They're my favorite!"
As he was carefully sorting the blue blocks, his mom stepped into the room. She knelt down beside him and said, "You're sorting so fast! You must love blue blocks a lot."
The little boy smiled and said, "Yes! I love sorting blue blocks!"

Once there was a graceful bunny who loved eating peanut butter. He would search everywhere for the best peanuts to eat. One day, he came up with an idea. He decided to print himself a giant jar of peanut butter. 
He carefully went through the steps, and soon enough he was able to print a big jar. He was so excited, that he raced to grab some peanuts to use it with. But when he returned, he saw a sneaky fox munching away at his jar of peanut butter.
The bunny tried to get him away, but the fox was too clever. He just took off with the jar, leaving the bunny empty-handed. The bunny was so sad, and he ended up just eating a few peanuts with no peanut butter. 
That was the last time the bunny ever tried to print himself a jar of peanut butter again.

Once upon a time, there was a little red bird named Tim. Tim loved to sing and play with his friends. One day, while playing, Tim and his friends saw a big cat. The cat looked like trouble.
Tim's friend, Sam, said, "We need to go away from the cat. It is trouble." But Tim did not listen. He wanted to talk to the cat. Tim flew down to the cat and said, "Hello, cat. I am Tim."
The cat looked at Tim and smiled. It said, "Hello, Tim. I love your red color." Tim was happy, but he knew his friends were scared. So, he asked the cat, "Can you please go away? My friends are scared of you."
The cat understood and went away. Tim and his friends were safe and happy. They learned that sometimes, talking can help solve problems. And they played and sang together all day long.

One day, a little boy named Tim went outside to play. He saw his dad using a grill to cook food. Tim thought it looked fun. He wanted to help his dad. The grass was wet from the rain that morning.
Tim asked his dad, "Can I help with the grill?" His dad smiled and said, "Yes, but be very careful. The grill is hot."
Tim helped his dad put food on the grill. They cooked hot dogs and hamburgers. Tim thought it was fun to watch the food cook. The wet grass made his shoes and socks wet, but he did not mind. He was happy to help his dad.
When the food was done, Tim and his dad ate outside. They had a nice time. Tim thought it was the best day ever. He liked helping his dad with the grill, even if his feet got wet.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play with her toy train. One day, while playing with her train, she saw steam coming out of a tiny hole in the wall. Lily was curious and wanted to see what was inside the hole.
Lily found a magic button next to the hole. She pushed the button and started to shrink. Now, she was the perfect size to fit into the hole. She crawled inside and found a tiny world with tiny people. "Hello," said Lily to the tiny people. "Hello, big girl," they replied.
Lily played with the tiny people all day long. But then, something unexpected happened. The steam from the hole started to make Lily grow big again. She was scared that she would squish her new friends. So, Lily quickly crawled out of the hole and grew back to her normal size. She was happy to be big again, but she would always remember her tiny friends and their perfect little world.

One day, a playful cat named Tom and a little dog named Max were playing with a ball. They liked to pass the ball to each other. Tom would hit the ball with his paw, and Max would catch it in his mouth. They were good friends and liked to play together.
As they played, Tom saw a box of matches on the ground. He did not know what it was, so he asked Max, "What is this?" Max said, "I don't know, but it looks fun. Let's play with it!" They tried to play with the matches, but they could not make them work.
Just then, a big bird flew down from a tree. The bird saw the matches and said, "Don't play with those! They can be dangerous." Tom and Max were surprised because they did not know the bird could talk. The bird showed them how to use the matches safely. They thanked the bird and went back to playing with their ball, knowing that some things are not good to play with.

Once there was a young boy named Jake. Jake wanted to choose something special to do.
As he was looking around he saw an oyster. He had heard about oysters and was curious to learn more so decided to choose that as his activity for the day.
He bent down to take a closer look and noticed it was dead. He was a bit scared at first but then noticed it was not moving so decided it would be safe to touch. He poked it with a stick and nothing happened.
Jake then asked his dad, "What do dead oysters do?", to which his dad replied, "Nothing Jake, they just sit." Jake thought about this for a moment then said to himself, "Choosing dead oysters as an activity might not be that much fun after all."

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a big gift to give to his friend, Sam. Tim wanted to wrap the gift and write a note for Sam's birthday.
One day, while Tim was wrapping the gift, his big sister, Sue, came into the room. Sue was angry because Tim had used her special paper to wrap the gift. Sue took the gift away from Tim and tore the paper off. Tim was sad and cried.
Tim tried to find new paper to wrap the gift, but he could not find any. He did not know what to do. Sam's birthday came, and Tim had no gift to give. Both Tim and Sam were sad.

One day, a small boy went to the park. He wanted to taste a new snack. He saw an unknown man with a big bag. The man gave the boy a whistle. The boy was happy.
The boy blew the whistle. It made a loud noise. Then, something unexpected happened. All the birds flew away. The boy was sad. He wanted the birds to come back.
The boy had an idea. He blew the whistle again. This time, the birds came back. They were not scared anymore. The boy tasted his snack and shared it with the birds. They all became friends.

Once upon a time, there was a busy bee named Buzzy. Buzzy loved to fly around and play with his friend, a little girl named Lily. One day, Buzzy found a shiny marble on the ground. He picked it up and showed it to Lily.
"Look, Lily! I found a marble!" said Buzzy. "It's so pretty and shiny! Let's play with it." Lily smiled and said, "Yes, let's play! We can roll it on the ground and try to catch it."
Buzzy and Lily played with the marble all morning. They rolled it back and forth, laughing and having fun. Soon, they started to feel tired. "Buzzy, I need to take a nap," said Lily. "I'm sleepy."
Buzzy agreed, "Me too, Lily. Let's nap under the big tree." They both lay down under the tree and closed their eyes. As they napped, the marble lay next to them, sparkling in the sun.
When they woke up, Buzzy and Lily felt refreshed and ready to play some more. They picked up the marble and continued their fun day together, happy to have found such a special toy.

Tom and Mia are playing in their room. They have many toys, but they want to make something new. They see a big sheet on the bed. It is white and soft.
"Let's make a tent!" Tom says. He takes the sheet and puts it over two chairs. He makes a door with a pillow.
"Wow, that looks fun!" Mia says. She crawls inside the tent with Tom. They pretend they are camping in the woods. They have a flashlight and a teddy bear.
But then, they hear a loud noise. It is their mom. She is angry. She sees the sheet on the floor. It has a big hole in it.
"Who did this?" she asks. She holds the sheet and shows the hole. "This was a new sheet! You can't tear it like that!"
Tom and Mia are scared. They look at each other. They don't know what to say.
"We are sorry, mom," Tom says. He speaks in a slow voice. He knows he did something bad.
"We just wanted to play," Mia says. She starts to cry. She hugs the teddy bear.
Mom sighs. She is not happy, but she loves her children. She puts the sheet down and hugs them.
"I know you wanted to play, but you have to be careful with things. You can't tear them. They are not toys. Do you understand?" she says.
Tom and Mia nod. They understand. They say sorry again.
Mom smiles. She kisses them and says, "It's okay. I forgive you. But next time, ask me before you use something. And don't tear it, okay?"
Tom and Mia say okay. They hug mom and say they love her. Mom says she loves them too. She helps them clean up the room. They find another sheet to make a tent. This time, they don't tear it. They play nicely and have fun.

One day, a big cat went to a small dog's house. The cat said, "I invite you to my house for a fun play day." The small dog was happy and went with the cat. They played with a smooth ball and had a lot of fun.
After playing, the cat said, "Let's eat some food." The cat gave the small dog a bowl of soup. The small dog tasted it and said, "This is good, but it needs some salt." The cat put some salt in the soup and they both enjoyed their meal.
The small dog said, "Thank you for inviting me to your house and for the salt in the soup." The cat said, "You're welcome. We can be friends and help each other." The small dog and the big cat became best friends. The moral of the story is that we should be kind to others and invite them to our lives.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to run and play all day. One day, while Spot was running, he met a fish named Fin. Fin was sad because he could not run like Spot.
"Hi, Fin!" said Spot. "Why are you sad?" Fin told Spot, "I cannot run like you. I can only swim in the water." Spot thought for a moment and said, "That's okay, Fin. We can still play together. You can swim, and I will run beside you."
So, Spot and Fin played together every day. They had so much fun. One day, they found a yummy treat. Spot said, "Fin, let's share this yummy treat." Fin agreed, and they both enjoyed it.
The moral of the story is that everyone is different, but we can still be friends and share with each other.

One day, a boy named Tim found a big board. He was very happy. Tim wanted to draw on the board. He looked for his color sticks but could not find them. He felt sorry because he lost them.
At night, Tim had a dream. In his dream, he saw a talking color stick. The color stick said, "I am here to help you find your friends." Tim was very excited and said, "Thank you, color stick!"
They looked for the color sticks together. But then, something unexpected happened. The talking color stick turned into a big monster! The monster ate all the color sticks. Tim woke up and knew it was just a dream. He was still sorry he lost his color sticks and now he could not draw on the board.

Once there was a dog named Max who lived with his kind owner. Every day Max's owner would take him for walks in the tidy park nearby. Every morning Max's owner would throw the stick and Max would catch it. This daily routine made Max very happy. 
One day while they both were on a walk, Max's paw suddenly started to hurt. His owner could not bear to see the dog's suffering and took him to the vet. The vet informed the owner that Max's paw was broken and needed to be treated with proper care so it could heal. The owner took all the necessary steps for his beloved Max's paw to heal. 
Max's recovery was slow and tedious, yet his owner never lost patience. Eventually, Max's paw healed back to its healthy state. Max and his owner resumed their daily walks in the park. Max's joy was doubled now that he was free from the pain. From then on, Max never took his little walks for granted. 
The moral of the story is that if you have something you cherish dearly, you must take the necessary steps for it to be healed and safe. With kind care and patience, any injury can heal and bring forth happiness.
