Once upon a time, there was a light bird named Tim. Tim lived near a big cliff. He liked to fly high in the sky and play with his friends.
One day, Tim saw a new sign near the cliff. The sign had a picture of a bird and some words. Tim did not know what the words said, but he was happy to see a bird like him on the sign.
Tim told his friends about the sign. They all went to see it and played near the cliff. They had fun and were very happy. The sign made them feel special. And they lived happily ever after.

Once there was a boy named Tim. He liked to play with his toy cars. One day, he found a different wire in his room. He didn't know where it came from. So, he asked his mom, "What is this wire for?"
His mom looked at the wire and said, "I don't know, let's follow it." They followed the wire and it went outside. Tim was excited to see where it went. They kept walking and walking until they found a big box.
Tim opened the box and saw a big, shiny toy car inside. He was so happy! But then, the car started to talk! "Hello, Tim! I am your new toy car friend!" Tim was surprised but he liked his new talking car. They played together all day and had lots of fun.

Lily and Tom were friends. They liked to play in the park. One day, they saw a lot of mist in the air. They wanted to see what was in the mist.
"Let's go and meet the mist!" Lily said.
"OK, but be careful. The mist might be cold or wet or scary," Tom said.
They ran into the mist. They could not see very well. They could not hear very well. They could not find each other.
"Lily, where are you?" Tom shouted.
"Tom, where are you?" Lily shouted.
They shouted and shouted, but they could not hear each other. They were lost in the mist.
Then, they heard a big roar. It was a lion. The lion was hungry. The lion smelled Lily and Tom. The lion ran towards them.
Lily and Tom were very scared. They tried to run away, but they could not see where to go. They bumped into each other.
"Lily, I found you!" Tom said.
"Tom, I found you!" Lily said.
They hugged each other. They hoped the lion would not find them. But the lion was very fast. The lion was very loud. The lion was not kind.
The lion found Lily and Tom. The lion ate them. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a mysterious bowl. Nobody knew where it came from, but everyone wanted it.
One day a young boy found the bowl. He was so excited and he wanted to show it to his mom. When he picked it up, it made a snapping sound.
The boy was scared and he ran away. But he left the mysterious bowl behind. 
The next day, the boy was brave and he came back. He wanted to take a closer look at the bowl. When he picked it up, he heard the same snapping sound.
He was curious, so the boy held his ear close to the bowl. He heard a quiet ticking inside. He opened the bowl, and found a little watch inside. He was so surprised and excited. 
From then on, the boy and his mom always remembered the mysterious bowl with wonder.

Once upon a time, there was a big, attractive tree. The tree had many pretty cells inside its leaves. The cells helped the tree grow strong and tall.
One sunny day, a happy bird came to the tree. The bird said, "Hi tree! I need to deliver a message to my friend. Can you help me?" The tree nodded and said, "Yes, I can help you."
The bird gave the message to the tree. The tree used its pretty cells to send the message to the bird's friend. The bird's friend got the message and was very happy. The bird thanked the tree and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She lived in a modern house with her mom, dad, and her dog, Spot. Lucy loved to play with Spot in the big yard outside her house. They would run and jump all day long.
One sunny day, Lucy and Spot were playing tag. Lucy was running fast, but then she tripped and hurt her foot. "Ouch!" she cried. Spot ran to her side and licked her face to make her feel better. Lucy's mom heard her cry and came outside to help.
"Please let me see your foot," said mom. Lucy showed her foot to her mom, and mom kissed it to make the pain go away. Then mom put a band-aid on Lucy's foot. Lucy smiled and said, "Thank you, mom!" Now Lucy and Spot could go back to playing and having fun in their modern yard.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty, red car. The car had a shiny license on it. One day, the car was driving down the road with its friend, a blue car.
As they drove, they saw a big rock in the road. The red car and the blue car had to split up to go around the rock. The red car went to the left, and the blue car went to the right. They both made it around the rock safely.
After they passed the rock, the red car and the blue car came back together. They were happy they could still drive together. They both looked very attractive as they drove down the road. They had a fun day driving, and they were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a family of foxes that lived in a forest. The forest was very hot in the summer and the foxes wanted to find a way to stay cool. 
One day, the two fox children were running around in the warm air when suddenly, the older brother stopped. He found a path in the woods that didn't seem like it had been there before.
The foxes followed the path until it came to a little house. Inside, they spotted a big block of ice. It was almost too bright to look at! 
The foxes had found a way to keep cool. They asked the old man who owned the house if they could have some of the ice. The old man said that they could break off a piece.
The foxes did just that. They broke a chunk off the big block of ice and carried it away. The cold fur felt so nice against their skin!
The family was able to keep cool all summer with their piece of ice. They thanked the old man every day and never forgot how hard it is to be hot.

Once upon a time, there were two bears. One bear was called Big Bear and the other Small Bear. Big Bear loved Small Bear very much and always wanted to help him. But Small Bear was very crazy and always wanted to do things on his own.
One day, Small Bear decided he wanted to sort everything in the house. He wanted to sort the books and toys and all the clothes too. But Big Bear thought it was too much for him to do all by himself. 
Big Bear wanted to help Small Bear and tell him he should not try to do it all by himself. But Small Bear was too crazy and he would not listen. He scrunched up his nose and said he wanted to do it all himself.
Big Bear was sad and he knew he could not change Small Bear's mind. So he put on his best smile and said, "That's great! I will help you if you need me." Big Bear was trying to show Small Bear his love. 
The two bears worked together to sort everything and it felt so nice to be working together. Big and Small Bear laughed as they sorted, and in the end, it was all neat and tidy. Small Bear saw that it was easier to work together and he thanked Big Bear for helping him. 
Big Bear was happy and said, "This is what all the love is about, working together and helping each other!"

Once upon a time, there was a huge monster. He had big eyes, a long tail, and many legs. The monster lived in a big cave near a small town. The people in the town were scared of the monster, but the monster was very nice.
One day, a little boy named Tim went to the cave. He saw the monster and said, "Hello, Mr. Monster! Do you want to play with me?" The monster smiled and said, "Yes, Tim! I would love to play with you. What do you want to play?" Tim thought for a moment and said, "Let's have a race!"
So, Tim and the monster went outside to race. They stood at the start line, and when Tim yelled, "Go!", they began to run. The monster was very fast, but Tim was faster. They raced and raced, and in the end, Tim won the race.
The monster was very happy to have a new friend. He said, "Tim, you are very fast! Thank you for playing with me." Tim smiled and said, "You're welcome, Mr. Monster! We can play together every day if you want." And from that day on, Tim and the monster were best friends, and they played together all the time.

Jack was walking through the woods one day. It was a bright and sunny day and Jack was feeling happy. Suddenly, he heard a strange noise. It was a soft, warm whistling sound. Jack looked around and noticed a huge, brown bird in a nearby tree. The bird was holding a big cup of coffee in its beak!
Jack said, "Hi bird, why do you have coffee?"
The bird looked over at Jack and said, "I am drinking the coffee because I'm thirsty. It's so tasty and hot! Don't worry, I won't spill it."
Jack thought that was clever and said, "Wow, you can whistle and drink coffee at the same time!"
The bird smiled and said, "Yes, I can! It's fun!"
Jack smiled and decided to go on his way. As he walked away, he could still hear the bird whistling softly.

Once upon a time, there was a bear who wanted to make some friends. One day, the bear saw a screen in the forest. But then it started to rain. The bear was so upset! 
The rain poured down, and the bear had no one to talk to. So the bear decided to climb up and sit on top of the screen. That way, he could stay dry. 
The bear sat there for a while and watched the rain. He was still upset, but having the screen to sit on made him feel better.
Suddenly, the bear heard some loud noises deep in the forest. He looked down, and he saw some animals hopping around in the rain. They were having so much fun!
The bear smiled, and he was no longer so upset. He had made some new friends.

Once upon a time there was a lazy cat. He slept all day and night and never did anything. Then one day, the cat decided to go out and explore. 
The cat came to a pond that was sparkling in the sunlight. On the shore of the pond, the cat noticed a bar of soap. He thought it would be fun to spin around with the soap. So he picked it up and started spinning.
Suddenly, a big dog appeared and growled at the cat. The frightened cat dropped the soap and ran away. But it was too late. As the cat ran away, the big dog took the soap and ran off too.
The cat sighed and never saw the soap again. The lazy cat learned that day that being lazy can lead to bad endings.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Molly. She was only three years old and she was very curious. Every day she would explore her garden.
One day, Molly saw a pretty cap lying in the middle of the garden. She was so happy that she picked it up and put it on her head.
Suddenly, Molly's mum came out of the house and shouted, "Molly, what are you doing with that cap?"
Molly replied, "I found it in the garden, Mum." 
Molly's mum said, "No, that cap belongs to me. You must give it back to me."
Molly started crying and said, "No, it's so pretty, I want to keep it."
Molly's mum said, "You can keep it, but you must remember to take it back to me." 
Molly was so happy and she hugged her mum. She said, "Yes, Mum, I will remember to take back the pretty cap to you."
The end.

One day, a big dog named Max went for a walk. He walked far from his home. Max was tired and wanted to relax. He saw a big tree and lay down under it. He closed his eyes and began to dream.
In his dream, Max heard a small voice. "Help me, please!" said the voice. Max opened his eyes and saw a little bird. The bird was trying hard to fly, but it could not. Max asked, "What's wrong, little bird?" The bird said, "I hurt my wing and can't fly home."
Max wanted to help the bird. He had an idea. "Climb on my back, and I will take you home," Max said. The bird climbed on Max's back, and they started to walk. They walked and walked, and then they saw something unexpected.
Max and the bird saw a big, beautiful home. It was not the bird's home, but it was a home for both of them. They went inside and found a soft bed. Max and the bird lay down on the bed and began to relax. They were happy in their new home together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom had a flute. He loved to play the flute all day. One day, Tom saw a big, heavy rock. He wanted to move it.
Tom tried to push the heavy rock. It was hard. He pushed and pushed. Tom was tired. He did not give up. He tried again to push the heavy rock.
Then, Tom had an idea. He played his flute. The music made him happy. Tom tried to push the heavy rock again. This time, the rock began to slip. Tom was happy. He had moved the heavy rock with his flute's help.

Once upon a time, there was a kind cat named Kitty. Kitty liked to lay in the warm sun. One day, while she was laying, she saw a little bird. The little bird was a beautiful sight.
"Hi bird," said Kitty. "You are so pretty. Can we be friends?" The little bird looked at Kitty and said, "Yes, Kitty. We can be friends. I am happy to have a kind friend like you."
They played together all day. They had so much fun. Kitty and the little bird were very happy. From that day on, they were the best of friends. And they lived happily ever after.

Anna and Ben were twins who liked to play with their toys. One day, their mom gave them each a shiny coin to buy something at the store. Anna was very happy and put her coin in her pocket. Ben was careless and dropped his coin on the floor.
"Look, Ben, I have a coin and you don't!" Anna said, showing her coin to Ben.
"That's not fair! Give me your coin!" Ben said, trying to grab Anna's coin.
"No, it's mine! Mom gave it to me!" Anna said, holding her coin tight.
They started to fight and pull each other's hair. Their mom heard them and came to see what was wrong.
"What are you doing, Anna and Ben? Why are you fighting?" Mom asked, looking angry.
"Ben lost his coin and he wants mine!" Anna said.
"Anna won't share her coin with me!" Ben said.
Mom sighed and shook her head. "You are both very naughty. You spoiled your chance to buy something nice at the store. Now you have to give me your coins and go to your room. No toys for you today."
Anna and Ben felt very sad and sorry. They gave their coins to Mom and went to their room. They wished they had been more careful and kind.

One day, a little girl named Lily found an ugly blouse in her mom's closet. The blouse was old and had many colors that did not look nice together. Lily thought it was funny and wanted to show her friends.
Lily put the ugly blouse on her cat, Whiskers, to see if he could escape it. Whiskers tried to run away, but the blouse was too big. He looked very funny, and Lily laughed. She called her friend Sam to come and see.
When Sam came over, he saw Whiskers in the ugly blouse and said, "Wow, that's a very ugly blouse! How did Whiskers escape?" Lily giggled and said, "He didn't! I helped him get out of it." They both laughed and played with Whiskers, who was now free from the ugly blouse.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very adventurous. He liked to play outside and explore new places. One day, he decided to go on an adventure in his garage.
In the garage, Tim found many fun things. He saw a big ball, a little car, and a box. He played with the ball and the car, but the box was too high for him to reach. Tim wanted to see what was inside the box, so he tried to jump and snap his fingers to get it.
Tim jumped and snapped his fingers, but the box was still too high. He was sad. Then, his mom came into the garage and saw him trying to get the box. She smiled and helped him by getting the box down. Tim was so happy, and he gave his mom a big hug. They opened the box together, and inside they found more toys for Tim to play with on his next adventure.

Once upon a time, there was a playful kangaroo named Kiki. Kiki loved to jump and stretch her legs. She lived in a big, green field with her friends.
One day, Kiki met a little bird named Benny. Benny was sad because he could not jump like Kiki. Kiki said, "Don't be sad, Benny. I can teach you how to stretch and jump like me!"
Benny was happy and said, "Yes, please teach me!" So Kiki and Benny played together. Kiki showed Benny how to stretch his wings and jump. Benny tried and tried, and soon he could jump too!
Kiki and Benny had lots of fun playing and jumping together. They became best friends and played in the big, green field every day. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small brown cabin, there lived a girl named Amy and her dog, Spot. They loved to play outside in the sun. One day, they decided to go on a walk in the woods.
As they walked, Amy saw a squirrel and said, "Look, Spot! A squirrel!" Spot barked and chased the squirrel up a tree. They laughed and continued walking. But soon, they realized they were lost.
Amy started to cry, but then she remembered her mom had told her, "If you ever get lost, just call for help." So, Amy shouted, "Help! We're lost!" A kind old man heard her and came to help them find their way back to the brown cabin. Amy thanked the man and promised to never forget her way again.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy cat named Tom. Tom loved to sleep all day and eat all night. He would rub his face on his food bowl to show he was hungry.
One day, Tom found a new food bowl in his home. It was filled with yummy food. Tom was so excited to eat it all. He rubbed his face on the new food bowl and started to eat.
But something unexpected happened. As Tom ate, the food bowl moved! It was not a food bowl at all, but a friendly turtle who had come to visit. Tom was surprised, but he and the turtle became good friends. They played together and shared their food, and they were both very happy.

One day, a boy named Tim saw a hidden toy plane in his room. He was very happy. He showed the plane to his friend, Sue. Sue liked the plane too. They decided to play a game with the plane.
They made a race with the plane. Tim said, "Let's see who can make the plane fly the farthest!" Sue said, "Okay!" They took turns throwing the plane. The plane flew far and high.
At the end of the game, Tim's plane flew the farthest. He was so excited. He said, "I win!" Sue clapped and said, "Good job!" They both had a lot of fun playing with the hidden plane.

Once upon a time, there was a big box. In the box, there were many mittens. The mittens were all mixed up. They needed to be sorted so they could be in pairs.
A little girl named Lucy saw the big box of mittens. She wanted to help sort them. She picked up one mitten, then another, and another. She found the ones that matched and put them together.
Soon, all the mittens were sorted into pairs. Lucy was very happy. She showed her mom the big box of mittens, all sorted and neat. Her mom was proud of her. They took the mittens and gave them to friends to keep their hands warm.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to wear his favorite sweater. It was a red sweater with a big blue car on it. But the sweater was stuck in a box with many other clothes. Tim tried to pull it out, but it was not easy.
Tim asked his big sister, Sue, for help. "Sue, can you help me pull my sweater out?" he said. Sue tried to help, but the sweater was still stuck. They both pulled and pulled, but it did not come out.
Finally, Tim had an idea. He asked his dog, Spot, to help. Spot bit the sweater and pulled with all his might. With Tim, Sue, and Spot all pulling together, the sweater came out of the box! Tim was so happy, he put on his sweater and said, "Thank you, Sue and Spot! We did it together!"

Once there was a boy named Ernie. He was three years old and he loved to play games with his friends. Whenever Ernie and his friends would play, they would always behave nicely towards one another.
One day, Ernie wanted to play a special game. He asked his mom if he could have a rich game, like the kind his rich friends owned. His mom said yes, so Ernie went out to buy a rich game. 
He was so excited when he got home and opened the box. Inside was the most glorious game he had ever seen. He couldn't wait to play it with his friends. 
And that's what he did. Ernie and his friends took turns playing the game, and behaved super nicely along the way. They laughed and smiled at each other and had lots of fun. 
Ernie was glad that he had been so nice - because it made his friends happy, and his mom proud. A rich game can really bring friends together if you behave nicely!

One day, a boy named Tim went to the fridge. He wanted to find something to eat. He saw a big, red apple. Tim did not like apples. He said, "I hate apples!"
Tim's mom heard him and said, "Apples are safe to eat. They are good for you." But Tim did not want to eat the apple. He wanted something else. He saw a big box in the fridge. The box was blue and had a picture of a cake on it. Tim liked cake. He took the box out of the fridge.
Tim opened the box and saw the cake. It was big and had lots of colors. He took a big bite of the cake. But the cake did not taste good. Tim felt bad. He fell on the floor. His mom came and saw him. She was sad and said, "That cake was not safe to eat. It was old and bad." Tim learned that he should listen to his mom and eat the safe food.

One day, a big, healthy dog named Max went to the office with his friend, Sam. They were going to help Sam's dad carry some things. Max was very excited to help and be a good dog.
At the office, Sam's dad said, "Max, please carry this box to the other room." Max wagged his tail and carried the box with his strong teeth. When he walked into the other room, he saw a big, shiny ball. Max was so surprised!
Max wanted to play with the ball, so he put the box down and pushed the ball with his nose. The ball rolled out of the office and down the street. Max and Sam laughed and chased the ball together. They had a fun and unexpected adventure that day.

Once upon a time, there was a boring town. In this town, there lived a boy named Tim. Tim was a very unhappy boy. He did not like the boring town. He wanted to have fun and play with his friends. But, there was nothing fun to do in this town.
One day, Tim had a bad dream. It was a nightmare. In his nightmare, everything was going reverse. Cars were going backwards, and people were walking backwards. Tim was very scared. He woke up and thought about the nightmare all day.
Tim wished he could reverse the boring town to make it fun. He tried to play with his friends and make the town fun, but it did not work. The town stayed boring, and Tim stayed unhappy. In the end, Tim could not change the town, and he had to live in the boring town forever.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to walk and play outside. One day, he found a big, round ball in the park. Spot thought the ball was fun and fit just right in his mouth.
Spot carried the round ball all around the park. He walked up and down the hills, and even by the pond. The ball made Spot very happy. He liked to roll it, toss it, and chase after it. The round ball was his new favorite toy.
At the end of the day, Spot was tired from all the walking and playing. He took his round ball home and slept next to it. Spot knew that tomorrow, he would walk and play with his round ball again. And that made him very happy.

Ben and Mia are friends. They like to play together. They have a big map of the world on the wall. The map shows many countries. Countries are places where people live.
One day, Ben and Mia decide to play a game. They take turns to point at a country on the map and say its name. Ben points at a big country with many colors. He says, "This is India. It has a lot of people and animals and food."
Mia points at a small country with a lot of water around it. She says, "This is Japan. It has a lot of islands and mountains and cars."
They keep playing until they reach a country that is white and cold. Ben says, "This is Antarctica. It has a lot of snow and ice and penguins."
Mia says, "I want to go there. It looks fun."
Ben says, "But it is very far and very cold. How will you go there?"
Mia thinks for a moment. Then she says, "I will do this. I will put on a thick coat and a thick hat and a thick scarf. I will take a big boat and a big sled and a big dog. I will sail on the water and slide on the ice and play with the penguins."
Ben says, "That sounds like a good plan. But I will miss you. Can I go with you?"
Mia says, "Of course. You are my friend. We can go together and see the country."
Ben and Mia smile and hug. They look at the map and dream of their adventure.

One day, a happy little girl named Lily went outside to play. She loved to clap her hands and dance on the earth. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing. It was a beautiful day.
Lily saw her friend, Max, playing with a ball. "Hi Max! Can I play with you?" she asked. Max looked at the ball and said, "No, it's my ball. You can't play with it." Lily felt sad and walked away.
Lily decided to clap and dance on her own. As she danced, the earth seemed to dance with her. Max saw how much fun Lily was having and felt bad. He walked over and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. Let's play together." Lily smiled and they both played happily, clapping and dancing on the earth.

Once upon a time, in a big field, there lived a nice sheep. This sheep had smooth, soft wool. One day, a little girl named Lily came to the field. She saw the sheep and said, "Hello, sheep! You have smooth wool. Can I touch it?"
The sheep said, "Yes, you can touch my wool. It is smooth and soft." Lily touched the sheep's wool and she liked it. She played with the sheep all day in the field. They ran and jumped together.
After playing, Lily and the sheep were tired. They found a big tree and sat under it. Lily said, "Sheep, let's seat here and rest." The sheep agreed, and they both sat under the tree. They were happy and had a fun day together.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy cat named Tom. Tom loved to play outside all day long. One day, Tom found a big puddle of oil near his house. He thought it was water, so he jumped in it. Tom got very dirty.
Tom's mom saw him and said, "Tom, you need to clean yourself. You are very dirty from the oil." Tom felt sad and went inside the house. He went to the bathroom and started to clean himself with soap and water. It took a long time, but Tom finally got clean.
After cleaning himself, Tom felt very tired. He went to his bed and lay down. His mom came to his room and gave him a big hug. She said, "Now you are clean, my sleepy little cat." Tom smiled and closed his eyes. He had a good sleep and had sweet dreams.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Tara. She loved spending time outside playing with animals. One day, she decided to go to the forest and make a pretend picnic for her and her friends. Tara bought lots of delicious food for the picnic and even prepared her favourite spoiled fruit salad. 
Just as she was setting up the picnic blanket and getting ready to call her friends, something strange happened. All the food seemed to vanish into thin air! Tara was so confused, she couldn't understand what had happened! She searched everywhere, but the food was nowhere to be seen. 
Tara was sad, but she decided to look on the bright side. After all, she was surrounded by animals and it was a beautiful day. She started laughing and playing with the animals, and soon forgot about the vanishing food. Suddenly, much to her surprise, the food reappeared! The animals had been hiding the food so they could join the picnic too! 
The result was a fun day out with lots of laughs and great memories. Tara was so happy that her spoiled fruit salad hadn't gone to waste. All of them had a wonderful time and the animals were happy too. They all sang and danced until the sun went down. What a magical day!

One day, a gentle cat named Lily wanted to travel. She put on her favorite bracelet and went outside. The sun was warm, and the birds were singing. Lily walked and walked, enjoying her day.
As Lily walked, she met a big dog named Max. Max looked at Lily's bracelet and said, "That is a nice bracelet. Can I see it?" Lily was scared, but she showed Max her bracelet. Max smiled and said, "Thank you, Lily. Your bracelet is very pretty."
Just then, a strong wind came and blew Lily's bracelet away. Lily and Max were sad. They looked and looked, but they could not find the bracelet. Then, a little bird flew down with the bracelet in its beak. The bird said, "I saw your bracelet fly away. I wanted to help, so I brought it back to you." Lily and Max were very happy, and they all became friends.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly cat. The cat loved candy. One day, the cat went for a walk to find some candy.
On the way, the cat met a dog. The dog said, "Hello, cat! Do you want to play?" The cat said, "No, I want to find candy." The dog said, "I know where to find candy. Follow me."
The cat and the dog walked and walked. They found a big box of candy. The cat was so happy. The dog said, "Let's eat all the candy!" They ate and ate until there was no more candy. Then, the cat and the dog felt sick. They should not have eaten all the candy.

Lily and Ben were playing with their toys in the living room. They liked to pretend they were spies and hide things under the sofa. Lily found a big box with a bow on it. She thought it was a present for her.
"Look, Ben, I found a present!" she said, holding the box.
"Let me see!" Ben said, running to her. He tried to grab the box, but Lily pulled it away.
"No, it's mine! I found it first!" she said.
They started to fight over the box, pulling and pushing. The box was heavy and hard to hold. They did not see the tag on the box that said "Danger: Gas".
The box fell to the floor and broke open. A hissing sound came out of the box. A bad smell filled the air. Lily and Ben coughed and choked. They felt dizzy and sick.
"Mama! Papa!" they cried, but no one heard them.
The gas was old and bad. It made them very sick. They fell to the floor and did not get up. The present was not for them. It was a trap.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a toy car. The car could zoom very fast. Tim loved to play with the car. He would push the car and watch it zoom across the room. The car made him feel warm and happy inside.
One day, Tim's friend Sam came over to play. Sam saw the toy car and wanted to play with it too. But Tim did not want to share. "No, this is my car. You can't play with it," Tim said. Sam felt sad and wanted to go home.
Tim's mom saw what happened and talked to Tim. "You should share your toys with your friends," she said. Tim thought about it and decided to share the car with Sam. They both played together, and the car zoomed around the room. They laughed and had fun. Tim felt warm inside, knowing that sharing his car made both him and Sam happy.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a fearful cat named Tom. Tom was always scared of everything. One day, he saw a broken toy on the ground. It was a small car with a wheel that had fallen off. Tom wanted to help fix the toy.
Tom went to his friend, a kind dog named Max. Tom said, "Max, can you help me repair this car? I found some tape in the house." Max replied, "Sure, Tom! Let's use the tape to put the wheel back on the car."
Together, they carefully used the tape to fix the wheel. The car looked as good as new! Tom and Max were very happy. They played with the car all day long. Tom felt less fearful because he had his friend Max by his side. And they both learned that they could fix things when they worked together.

Once upon a time, there was a crazy dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his toy peanut. One day, Spot saw his toy peanut in the kitchen. He was so happy!
Spot ran to the kitchen and jumped to get his toy peanut. But when he jumped, he accidentally slapped his tail on the table. Spot was surprised, but he didn't care because he got his toy peanut.
Spot played with his toy peanut all day long. He would throw it and chase it around the house. He had so much fun with his toy peanut. At the end of the day, Spot was tired and happy. He went to sleep with his toy peanut by his side.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a noisy dog named Max. Max liked to play with his toy cone. He would run, jump, and toss the cone in the air.
One day, Max saw his friend, a big cat named Sam. Sam was sitting by a big pot. Sam liked to stir the pot with a long stick. Max wanted to help Sam stir the pot, but Max was too small.
Max had an idea. He put his toy cone on his head and stood on his back legs. Now Max was tall enough to help Sam stir the pot. They took turns stirring the pot together. Max and Sam were very happy, and they laughed and played all day.

Once there was a tiny blue bird. Its wings were so strong it could fly around high in the sky. 
One day while it was flying, it started to whistle. Its little lungs filled up with air and it sang a happy tune. 
The other birds in the forest heard the whistle and flew closer to see who was singing. 
The tiny blue bird was so proud of its singing that it kept whistling and flapping its wings. 
The birds all flew around the tiny blue bird and enjoyed its song. They flew with it until it was time to go home. 
The tiny blue bird waved goodbye and flew off into the sky, whistling its happy song.

Once upon a time there was a duck. The duck was looking for a nice place to stay, so he kept flying until he saw a big pond. He was so happy when he found it! 
On the edge of the pond, he saw something strange. It was a trap made of sticks and string! The duck thought it was very strange, but he knew it must be useful. 
He waddled closer and heard a voice calling out. It was a little frog! The frog said, "If you stay in this pond, be careful not to get caught in my trap!" 
The duck thanked the frog and promised to be careful. From that day forward, he kept visiting the pond and had many fun adventures.

One day, Tim and his mom were doing laundry. Tim was a small boy who liked to help his mom. He felt dizzy when he saw the clothes spin in the washing machine.
"Mom, why do the clothes spin?" Tim asked.
His mom replied, "The spinning helps clean the clothes, Tim."
Tim wanted to support his mom, so he helped her put the clean clothes into a big basket. They carried the basket together to hang the clothes outside to dry. Tim felt happy that he could help his mom with the laundry.

Jack went for a jog. He ran along the street, looking at the plants and trees around him. He was curious as he ran, wondering what new things he might find.
As Jack jogged along, he noticed a vegetable growing in a nearby garden. He decided to take a closer look. He went up to the garden and saw the shiny vegetable. He bent down to pick it up.
Suddenly, the garden's owner ran from the house. "What are you doing?" he shouted. Jack froze. He tried to explain why he was curious about the vegetable, but the garden's owner was not listening. The garden's owner grabbed Jack. He was very angry and he punished Jack for being so curious.
Jack was sad when he went home. He knew not to be curious about things that weren't his anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a huge dog named Max. Max loved to play games with his best friend, a little boy named Tom. They would run and jump all day long. One day, they found a new game in the park. It was a big ball that they could kick and chase.
But, there was a problem. Max was so big that he would get very hungry after playing the game. Tom tried to feed Max, but the food was too small for him. Max needed more food. Tom was sad because he wanted to keep playing the game with Max.
Tom had an idea. He asked his friends to help him find more food for Max. They all worked together and found lots of big food for Max. They fed Max the huge food and he was happy. Now, Tom and Max could play the game together again. They had lots of fun and were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a restless boy who loved to run and play all day. One day, while playing in the park, he found a quiet corner with a big tree.
In the quiet corner, Tim saw a small, lost kitten. The kitten was sad and dirty, so Tim decided to help it. He found a soft cloth and started to wipe the dirt off the kitten. With each wipe, the kitten became cleaner and happier.
After Tim finished wiping the kitten, they became best friends. They played together in the park every day. The kitten was no longer sad, and Tim was no longer restless. They were both very happy and loved each other very much.

Once, there was a wise cat named Tom. Tom had a best friend, a little fork. They lived in a big house. Tom and the fork liked to play and eat together.
One day, Tom said to the fork, "I will always protect you, my friend." The fork was happy to have a friend like Tom. They played all day long.
But one day, a big dog came into the house. The dog saw the fork and wanted to take it away. Tom was scared, but he remembered his promise to protect the fork. So, Tom jumped on the dog's back and made the dog run away. The fork was safe, and they both were happy. Tom was a brave and wise cat.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to open doors and touch things. One day, Tim went to the park with his mom.
At the park, Tim saw a big box. The box was empty. Tim wanted to open the box and touch what was inside. He asked his mom, "Can I open the box?"
His mom said, "Yes, you can open the box." Tim opened the box and found a big red ball inside. He was so happy! He touched the ball and played with it all day at the park.
In the end, Tim and his mom went home. Tim was tired but happy. He had a fun day opening the box, touching the ball, and playing at the park.

Lily and Tom like to jog with their dog Spot. They jog in the park every day. Spot is fast and smart. He can catch a ball and do tricks.
One day, they see a big cat in the park. Spot wants to chase the cat. He barks and runs after it. Lily and Tom follow him. They think it is fun.
But the cat is not happy. It hisses and scratches. It climbs a tree and jumps on a branch. Spot cannot reach the cat. He whines and jumps.
"Spot, come back!" Lily calls. "The cat does not want to play. It is angry. You are foolish."
Tom agrees. He says, "Yes, Spot, you are foolish. The cat is not a friend. It is a foe. You should leave it alone."
Spot realizes that Lily and Tom are right. He stops jumping and barking. He feels sorry. He runs back to Lily and Tom. He licks their faces and wags his tail.
Lily and Tom hug Spot. They say, "It is OK, Spot. We love you. You are not foolish. You are just curious. But next time, be careful. Some animals are not nice."
Spot nods and barks. He is happy. He learns a lesson. He does not chase the cat anymore. He jogs with Lily and Tom in the park. They have fun.

Once there was a wild wire. It was long and shiny and strong. The wire loved to curl and fold. One day, the wire was trying to fold, but it was too strong. It kept on unfolding! Nothing the wire tried worked.
Finally, the wild wire got so tired it fell asleep. But while it was asleep, the wire kept folding and folding and folding. Until one day the wild wire couldn't fold any more. The wire had folded itself so much it had broken and now it was in two pieces.
The wild wire was so sad! It cried and cried but nothing could fix it. The wire was too broken. There was nothing left to do. The wild wire had no choice but to stay in two pieces forever.

One day, a big dog named Max wanted to play. He saw a ball and had a goal. The goal was to kick the ball into a net. Max was serious about his goal. He ran fast and kicked the ball.
Just then, a little cat named Luna came by. Luna saw Max playing and wanted to join. Max did not know Luna was there. He kicked the ball again and it hit Luna by accident. Luna let out a big sigh. She was not hurt, but the ball went far away.
Max and Luna looked for the ball together. They thought they would never find it. Then, they saw a big bird in the sky. The big bird had the ball in its beak! The bird dropped the ball down to Max and Luna. They were so happy and played together all day. The unexpected friend made Max's goal even more fun.

One day, a pretty girl named Lucy wanted to sort her toys. She had many toys in her room. She wanted to make her room look nice and clean. So, she started to put her toys in different boxes.
While sorting her toys, Lucy found a toy she had not seen for a long time. It was a small, blue car. She was very happy to find it. She said, "I found my blue car! I will show it to Mommy." Lucy took the car and went to find her mommy.
Mommy was in the bathroom. Lucy knocked on the door and said, "Mommy, look! I found my blue car!" Mommy opened the door and smiled at Lucy. She said, "Good job sorting your toys, Lucy! Your room will be very nice and clean now." Lucy felt proud and happy. She went back to her room and continued sorting her toys.

Sara was upset. She did not like the new house. It was big and dark and cold. She missed her old house and her old friends. She wanted to go back.
One night, she heard a noise in her room. She was scared. She hid under her blanket. She heard the noise again. It sounded like a cry. She peeked out of the blanket. She saw a ghost.
The ghost was white and thin and sad. He had big eyes and a small mouth. He looked at Sara. He said, "Hello. I am Tom. I live here. I am lonely. Do you want to be my friend?"
Sara was surprised. She did not know ghosts could talk. She did not know ghosts could be lonely. She felt sorry for Tom. She said, "Hello. I am Sara. I am new here. I am lonely too. I can be your friend."
Tom smiled. He was happy. He said, "Thank you. You are very kind. I have been here a long time. I suffer a lot. I died in a fire. It hurt. I can't leave this house. I can't see my family. I can't play with other kids. You are the first person who can see me and talk to me."
Sara was sad. She did not like to suffer. She did not like to be alone. She said, "I am sorry. That is very bad. I don't like this house either. It is not my home. I want to leave. But I can't. My mom and dad say we have to stay. They say we have no money. They say we have to make the best of it."
Tom nodded. He said, "I understand. It is hard to change. It is hard to lose things. But maybe we can help each other. Maybe we can make this house better. Maybe we can have fun. Maybe we can be happy."
Sara smiled. She liked Tom. He was nice. He was brave. He was funny. She said, "Yes. Maybe we can. Maybe we can be friends. Maybe we can be a team."
Tom and Sara hugged. They were happy. They were not upset anymore. They were not lonely anymore. They had each other. They had a ghost and a girl. They had a story.

Jenny was walking around the park feeling lonely. Nobody had been nice to her all day. 
Suddenly, she heard an excited voice. When she looked up, she saw two people discussing a theater. 
When she heard them mention the theater, she thought she might have finally found a friend. She ran up to them and asked, "Can I come too?"
The two people smiled and welcomed her with open arms. They told her their names were George and Nellie.
George and Nellie discussed going to the theater that evening. Jenny was so excited to share a fun experience with two new friends. 
When the evening came, they went to the theater together. The three friends laughed, sung, and danced all through the night. It was the best night Jenny had ever had. 
From that day forward, Jenny was never lonely again. She had two friends to talk to, play with, and go to the theater with.

Once upon a time, there was a big dinosaur. The dinosaur was very nice and kind. He had a lot of friends in the forest. One day, he saw a little bird who could not fly. The dinosaur felt sad for the bird.
The dinosaur thought and thought. He wanted to help the little bird. The dinosaur had an idea. He would use his long neck to lift the bird up high. The bird was scared at first, but the dinosaur was gentle and kind.
The dinosaur lifted the bird up high. The bird felt the wind on its wings. The bird started to move its wings and it began to fly. The dinosaur was very happy. The bird thanked the dinosaur for helping it learn to fly. All the animals in the forest saw how kind the dinosaur was. The animals learned that being nice and kind is very important.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play outside. One day, he saw a wild cat in his yard. Tim wondered what the cat was doing there. He went up to the cat and said, "Hi, cat! What are you doing here?"
The cat looked at Tim and said, "I'm looking for my friend, the bird. Have you seen her?" Tim thought for a moment and said, "No, I haven't. But I can help you look for her." The cat was happy and they both started looking for the bird.
As they looked around, they saw a big motion in the sky. It was a giant bird! The bird came down and said, "Hello, friends! I was looking for you too!" The wild cat and Tim were surprised to see such a big bird. The bird said, "I am the bird's big sister. Thank you for helping her!" They all became good friends and played together every day.

Lily liked to draw on paper with her crayons. She had many colors and shapes to make pretty pictures. She wanted to show her mom and dad her drawings, so she put them on the wall in the hall with tape. She was very proud of her work.
But when mom and dad came home, they were not happy. They saw the tape on the wall and the drawings that covered it. They said, "Lily, what did you do? You broke the wall with your tape. The tape leaves marks and tears the paint. This is not your paper, this is our wall. You have to be careful with other people's things."
Lily felt sad and sorry. She did not mean to break the wall. She just wanted to share her art. She said, "I'm sorry, mom and dad. I didn't know the tape would break the wall. I wanted to make you happy with my drawings. Can I take them off and put them somewhere else?"
Mom and dad hugged Lily and said, "We love you, Lily, and we love your drawings. But you have to ask us before you put anything on the wall. The wall is not your paper, it is part of our home. We have to keep it clean and original. How about we find a special place for your drawings, like a board or a book, where you can display them and we can see them?"
Lily liked that idea. She helped mom and dad take off the tape and the drawings from the wall. They found a big board and some pins and hung it in Lily's room. They put Lily's drawings on the board and admired them. Lily learned to respect the wall and to ask before she used something that was not hers. She was happy to have her own art space and to share it with her mom and dad.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a boy named Tim. Tim loved to take notes. He had a book where he wrote interesting things he saw every day. One day, he saw a big, red bird outside his window. He thought it was very interesting, so he wrote about it in his book.
Later, Tim met his friend, Sam. Tim said, "Sam, I saw a big, red bird today. It was very interesting!" Sam wanted to see the bird too, so they went to the park to look for it. They searched and searched, but they could not find the bird.
As they were about to leave the park, they saw the big, red bird sitting on a tree branch. They were so happy to find it! But suddenly, a big, mean cat jumped on the branch and caught the bird. Tim and Sam were very sad. They went home and Tim wrote in his book, "Today, we found the big, red bird, but the mean cat took it away. It was a bad day."

One day, a little girl named Mia was in her room. She had a big box of colors. Mia wanted to make a picture with all the colors. She took out her colors and a big sheet of paper. She was very excited.
Mia's mom came into the room and said, "Mia, are you ready to make a crazy picture?" Mia nodded and said, "Yes, Mommy! I will stir all the colors together." Her mom smiled and left the room.
Mia started to stir the colors on the paper. She stirred and stirred until all the colors mixed together. It looked so crazy and fun! Mia was very happy with her picture.
Later, Mia's mom came back into the room. She saw the crazy picture and said, "Wow, Mia! You did a great job stirring all the colors together!" Mia smiled and said, "Thank you, Mommy! I love my crazy picture."
Mia hung her crazy picture on the wall. She was so proud of it. Every time she looked at it, she remembered the fun day she had stirring all the colors together.

Lily likes to write. She has a big book and many crayons. She writes her name, her friends' names, and things she sees. She writes on the book, on the wall, and on the carpet.
One day, Mom sees the carpet. She is not happy. She says, "Lily, why did you write on the carpet? The carpet is not for writing. The carpet is for sitting and playing. You made the carpet dirty. Now we have to clean it."
Lily feels sad. She says, "I'm sorry, Mom. I like to write. I wanted to write a story. The carpet was empty. I thought it was like a big book."
Mom hugs Lily. She says, "I understand, Lily. Writing is good. But you have to write on the right things. The carpet is not the right thing. Next time, ask me before you write on something. I can give you paper or a board. Then you can write your story and show me."
Lily nods. She says, "OK, Mom. I will ask you. Can I help you clean the carpet?"
Mom smiles. She says, "Yes, Lily. You can help me. Thank you. I love you."
They go to the kitchen and get a sponge and a bucket. They go back to the carpet and clean it together. Lily tells Mom her story. Mom listens and says, "That's a nice story, Lily. You are a good writer."

One day, a girl named Sue found a comb. The comb was very pretty. Sue liked to use the comb to brush her long hair. She would brush her hair every day with the comb. It made her feel happy.
One day, Sue was brushing her hair and something unexpected happened. The comb started to change! It was now a dark comb. Sue was very surprised. She did not know how the comb changed.
Sue showed the dark comb to her mom. Her mom said it was magic. They found out that when Sue used the dark comb, she could change things too! Sue was very happy. She could now change her toys and have more fun.

One day, a little girl named Lily had to take an exam at school. She was scared, but her mom gave her a sweet hug and told her she would do great.
Lily went to school and saw her friend, Tim. Tim was also scared about the exam. Lily told Tim about her mom's sweet hug and they both felt better. They both nodded and smiled at each other.
They took the exam and tried their best. After the exam, Lily and Tim played outside and had fun. They were happy that they did their best and now they could relax.

One day, a kind girl named Lily went into the woods to study. She took a big book and a tent with her. She found a nice spot under a big tree and set up her tent. Lily sat down and opened her big book to study.
As she was studying, a little bird flew down and said, "Hi! I am a bird. What are you doing?" Lily looked up and said, "I am studying. I want to learn new things." The bird thought for a moment and said, "I know a secret. Do you want to know?"
Lily was very excited. She closed her big book and said, "Yes, please tell me the secret!" The bird told her to follow him. They walked deeper into the woods until they found a magic tree. The tree had doors and windows, and it was full of other kind animals who wanted to learn too. Lily was very happy. She had found a new place to study with her new friends.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby boy named Tom. Tom loved playing baseball with his friends. One day, he saw a baseball in his front yard. He was so excited that he ran out to pick it up. 
Tom played with the baseball, rolling it around and batting it. His friends came over and they all had so much fun with it! The baseball had even turned a funny colour when they were finished playing. 
At the end of the day, they all put the baseball away safely so they could keep playing with it the next day. It was a very happy time with lots of laughter and running around. 
The end.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play outside in the sun. They run and jump and laugh. They are very happy.
One day, they see a big hill. They want to go up the hill. They hold hands and start to climb. They climb and climb and climb. They are very tired.
They reach the top of the hill. They see a lot of green grass. They lay down on the grass. They feel the wind on their faces. They look at the sky. They see white clouds and blue birds.
"Look, Anna, a cloud looks like a bunny!" Ben says.
"Where, where?" Anna says.
"Over there, see? It has long ears and a fluffy tail!" Ben says.
"I see it, I see it! And look, that cloud looks like a flower!" Anna says.
"Wow, it does! It has pretty petals and a yellow center!" Ben says.
They smile and point at the clouds. They make up stories about the clouds. They have fun.
They hear a loud noise. They turn their heads. They see a big red car. It is coming down the hill. It is very fast. It is very loud. It is very scary.
"Anna, Ben, come here!" They hear a voice. It is their mom. She is waving at them. She is worried.
They get up from the grass. They run to their mom. They hug her. They are safe.
"Mom, we saw a bunny cloud and a flower cloud!" Anna says.
"Mom, we lay down on the grass and felt the wind!" Ben says.
"That sounds nice, but you have to be careful. The hill is not a good place to play. The car is very fast and very dangerous. It can hurt you. Do you understand?" Mom says.
"Yes, mom, we understand. We are sorry. We won't play on the hill again." Anna and Ben say.
"Good. I love you. Now let's go home and have some juice and cookies." Mom says.
"Okay. We love you too. Thank you, mom." Anna and Ben say.
They hold hands and walk with their mom. They are happy. They are safe. They are good.

Once upon a time, there was an obedient dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his toy ball. One day, he was playing near the table when he saw something new. It was a small, bright light. Spot wanted to touch it, but he heard his friend, Tim, say, "No, Spot! That can burn you!"
Spot was sad because he wanted to play with the bright light. He went to his friend Tim and asked, "Why can't I play with the light? It looks fun!" Tim said, "The light is not safe, Spot. It is called fire, and it can hurt you very much."
Spot listened to Tim and stayed away from the fire. He was a good and obedient dog. He played with his toy ball instead. He knew that being safe was more important than playing with something dangerous. And so, Spot and Tim played happily together, far away from the fire.

One day, a clumsy cat named Kitty went for a walk. Kitty saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. She jumped and grabbed a branch. She tried to squeeze into a small hole in the tree, but she was too big.
Kitty looked down and saw a little bird. The bird said, "Hi Kitty, why are you in my tree?" Kitty replied, "I wanted to find a seat to rest." The bird said, "You can sit on the branch next to me."
Kitty moved to the branch and sat down. She was still clumsy, so she almost fell off. The bird helped her by holding her paw. They sat on the branch together and became good friends.

One day, a boy went to the park to play. He saw a kite in the sky. The kite was big and pretty. The boy did not know who the kite belonged to. It was unknown.
The boy wanted to fly the kite too. He asked his mom for help. His mom said they had to work together to fly the kite. The boy was happy to work with his mom.
They both held the kite and ran. The wind helped the kite go up. The boy and his mom worked hard to keep the kite in the sky. They had a fun day at the park with the unknown kite.

Lily was a bee who liked to clean. She cleaned her hive every day. She cleaned the honey, the wax, and the pollen. She cleaned the walls, the floors, and the ceilings. She cleaned with her legs, her tongue, and her wings.
One day, she saw a blue flower outside her hive. She flew to the flower and smelled it. It smelled sweet and fresh. She wanted to take some pollen from the flower. She landed on the flower and rubbed her legs on it.
But the flower was not a flower. It was a trap. A spider had made the flower with silk and dye. The spider wanted to catch bees and eat them. The spider saw Lily and ran to her. He wrapped his silk around her and pulled her to his web.
Lily was scared and sad. She could not fly or clean. She could only buzz and cry. She hoped someone would help her.
Benny was another bee who liked to clean. He cleaned his hive every day. He cleaned the honey, the wax, and the pollen. He cleaned the walls, the floors, and the ceilings. He cleaned with his legs, his tongue, and his wings.
One day, he heard a buzz outside his hive. He flew to the sound and saw a blue flower. He saw Lily in the flower. She was wrapped in silk and stuck to a web. He saw the spider near her. He was angry and brave. He wanted to save Lily.
He flew to the spider and stung him. The spider felt pain and ran away. He flew to Lily and bit the silk. He freed her from the web. He flew with her back to the hive.
Lily was happy and grateful. She thanked Benny for helping her. She hugged him with her wings. She said, "You are a good bee. You are a good friend. You are my hero."
Benny was proud and shy. He smiled at Lily. He said, "You are welcome. You are a good bee. You are a good friend. You are my honey."

Once upon a time, there was a big, ugly snake. The snake was sad because no one wanted to be his friend. All the other animals were scared of him because he was so ugly. The snake would cry every day, wishing he had a friend to care for him.
One day, a little girl named Lily was walking in the woods. She saw the ugly snake crying by a tree. Lily was not scared of the snake. She walked up to him and asked, "Why are you crying, Mr. Snake?" The snake told her how no one wanted to be his friend because he was ugly.
Lily felt sad for the snake. She told him, "I will be your friend, Mr. Snake. I don't care if you are ugly. We can play together and have fun." The snake was so happy to have a friend who cared for him. They played together in the woods every day, and the ugly snake was not sad anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat named Mittens. Mittens was anxious to go outside and play. The sun was shining, and it was a beautiful day.
Mittens went outside and saw a big puddle. She thought, "I want to jump in the puddle!" So, Mittens stretched her legs and jumped high into the air. She landed right in the middle of the puddle with a big splash!
Just then, a friendly frog hopped out of the puddle. "Hello, Mittens!" said the frog. "I was hiding in the puddle. You found me!" Mittens was surprised but happy to make a new friend. They played together all day long, jumping in puddles and having fun.

Once upon a time, in a lively forest, there lived a little duck named Daisy. Daisy had a long neck and loved to take walks by the pond. One sunny day, Daisy met a new friend, a small frog named Freddy.
"Hi, I'm Freddy!" said the frog, hopping up to Daisy. "I like your long neck. Do you want to take a walk with me?"
Daisy smiled and said, "Yes, let's take a walk together!"
So, Daisy and Freddy walked around the pond, talking and laughing. They saw fish in the water and birds in the sky. They had a fun day together. From that day on, Daisy and Freddy were the best of friends.

One day, a big dog and a little cat were playing in the park. They were very happy. The sun was shining and the birds were singing. The dog and the cat liked each other a lot. They liked to run and jump together.
Then, the dog noticed a paper on the ground. It was the news. The dog picked it up and showed it to the cat. The news said there was a hurt bird in the park. The dog and the cat wanted to help the bird. They looked and looked for the hurt bird.
At last, they found the hurt bird. But it was not really hurt! It was just playing a game. The bird wanted to see if the dog and the cat would help it. The dog and the cat laughed and laughed. They were happy to be friends with the bird. Now, they could all play together in the park.

One day, a girl named Lucy went to the store with her mom. They wanted to buy a pretty vase for their home. Lucy saw a big, blue vase and said, "Mom, I like this one!" Her mom agreed and they bought it.
On their way home, Lucy's mom said, "We need to be careful with the vase. We don't want it to break." Lucy held the vase in her hands and walked very slowly. She wanted to make sure it was safe.
When they arrived home, Lucy put the vase on the table. Her mom put some pretty flowers in it. They both smiled and said, "The vase looks so nice with the flowers!" Lucy was happy that she had been careful with the vase.

Once upon a time, there was a big round moon in the sky. The moon was very sad, because it was stuck up high and could not play with the kids on the ground. The kids looked up at the moon and wanted to help it feel happy.
One day, a fit and strong bird saw the sad moon. The bird wanted to rescue the moon and make it smile again. The bird flew up high in the sky, trying to reach the moon. It flapped its wings harder and harder, getting closer each time.
Finally, the bird reached the moon and rescued it from the sky. The moon was so happy to be free and close to the ground. Now, the kids could play with the moon and make it smile. The bird was very proud and the kids cheered. They all played together, and the moon was never sad again.

One day, a curious little boy named Tim found a red wagon. He wanted to play with it. He saw a big rock and had an idea. Tim put the rock in the wagon and pulled it around.
As he walked, he met his friend Sue. Sue asked, "What are you doing, Tim?" Tim said, "I am pulling a big rock in my wagon. Want to help?" Sue smiled and said, "Yes, let's play together."
They played all day, pulling the wagon with the big rock. But then, something unexpected happened. The rock fell out and broke into many small rocks. Tim and Sue were sad at first. But then, they realized they could share the small rocks with their friends.
They went to their friends and gave each one a small rock. Everyone was happy. Tim and Sue learned that sharing can make everyone happy. And that's how the curious boy and his red wagon brought joy to all his friends.

Once there was a little boy who loved going to the gym. It was always so much fun! One day he asked his mom if he could go the gym and his mom said "Yes".
The little boy was so excited! He raced to the gym and when he got there he saw something terrible. It was all broken and smashed up! The little boy was so sad.
He went back to his mom and asked why the gym was so broken. His mom gently answered, "Someone was careless and didn't look after the gym properly".
The little boy was very upset, but he knew what he had to do. He asked his mom if he could help fix the gym and she said yes!
The little boy and his mom worked very hard to clean up the gym and make it look better. When they were finished, it was a perfect gym! The little boy had answered his own question and was so proud of what he had done.

One day, a gifted bird named Blue flew high in the sky. He saw his friend, Red, on a tree. Blue flew down to see Red. They liked to tell each other stories.
Blue said, "I have a story to tell you, Red." Red was excited to hear it. Blue told a story about a kind bear who shared his food with all his friends. Red liked the story and said, "That bear is very nice."
One day, they saw the bear from the story! They could not believe it. The bear said, "Hello, I am the bear from the story. I want to be your friend too." Blue and Red were very happy to have a new friend.
The three friends played and shared their food, just like in the story. They learned that being kind and sharing with others made them happy. And they all lived happily together, teaching others to be kind too.

One day, a little beetle was walking in the woods. He was fearful because he had never been so far from home. He saw a big tree and decided to climb it.
As the beetle climbed, he found a leaf. He thought it would be fun to load the leaf with tiny sticks. He worked hard and soon, the leaf was full of sticks. The beetle was happy with his work.
But then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and blew the leaf away with the beetle still on it! The beetle was scared, but he held on tight. The wind took him back to his home. The beetle was happy to be home and now he had a fun story to tell his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big red truck. This truck loved to serve people. It would help carry things from one place to another. The truck was very happy to do its job. Everyone said it was the perfect truck.
One sunny day, the truck was driving down the road. It was carrying a big box. The truck did not know what was inside the box. Suddenly, the box opened! Out came a lot of balloons. The balloons went up, up, up into the sky. The truck was very surprised.
The truck looked up at the balloons and smiled. It was happy to see the balloons fly high in the sky. The truck knew that it had helped serve someone by carrying the balloons. Even though it was not what the truck expected, it was still a perfect day. The truck continued down the road, ready to help and serve more people.

Once upon a time there was a boy named Bob. He wanted to clap the loudest at school. Bob practiced every day and he got really good at clapping.
On the day of the competition, Bob was so excited. He could feel the excitement in the air. When it was Bob's turn, he clapped his hands so loudly that everyone in the room stopped to notice. 
After Bob finished clapping, the judge said "We have a winner!" Everyone started clapping and Bob was so happy. 
Suddenly, his friend Sarah stepped up and said "What about me? Can I clap too?"
The judge said "Sorry, Sarah. The competition is over. Bob won." 
Sarah was disappointed but she smiled and said "Great job, Bob! I'm so proud of you." 
Bob smiled and said "Thanks Sarah. I'm happy that I won, but I'm also happy that we can still be friends." 
Everyone was happy and they all hugged and clapped. The room was filled with open hearts and big hugs. 
The End.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Anna and Joanna. They were always together but one day they had a disagreement.
Joanna said, "Your dress is so unique, like no other!"
But Anna said, "No, my dress is not unique!"
Then a cop came walking by. He said, "why don't you settle this by wiping off Anna's dress to see if it's unique."
So Joanna wiped Anna's dress and underneath was a beautiful pattern of stars and flowers.
Joanna smiled and said, "see, I told you it was unique!"
Anna smiled too and said, "you're right friend, it is unique!"
And the two friends hugged and continued walking, happy and content.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a delicate umbrella. It was very pretty and had many colors. The umbrella was always wrapped up when not in use. One day, the sun was shining bright and the sky was blue.
A boy named Tim found the umbrella. He opened it and said, "Wow! Look at this!" Tim showed the umbrella to his friend, Sue. Sue said, "That's a nice umbrella! Let's play with it."
They took the umbrella outside and used it to make shade. They had fun playing under the umbrella all day. When they were done, Tim said, "Let's wrap it up and keep it safe."
So they wrapped the umbrella and put it back in the house. The delicate umbrella was happy to be part of their play and knew it would be safe and sound.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to run and play all day. One day, Tim saw a big hill near his house. He wanted to climb all the way to the top.
Tim started to climb the hill. It was not easy, but he did not give up. He felt tired, but he kept going. He knew he would find a fun way to get to the top. 
Finally, Tim made it to the top of the hill. He felt so proud of himself. He looked down and saw his house. It was so small from up there. Tim smiled and knew he could do anything if he tried.

One day, a kind boy named Tom found a big bottle of glue. He wanted to use it to make funny jokes. He thought, "I can put glue on my friend's chair and make them stick!" So, Tom took the glue to school.
At school, Tom's friend, Sue, saw the glue. She asked, "What is that for, Tom?" Tom said, "It is for a funny joke! I will put it on our friend's chair!" Sue laughed and said, "That sounds funny!"
But then, something unexpected happened. The glue bottle opened and spilled all over the floor! Tom and Sue looked at each other and laughed. They knew it was a funny joke, but not the one they planned. Their friends came in and saw the glue on the floor. They all laughed and helped clean it up. Tom learned that sometimes the best jokes happen by accident.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She liked to play outside in her yard. One day, she noticed something new. It was a small pine tree. Sue was very happy to see the tree.
Sue wanted to help the pine tree grow big and strong. She knew it might be difficult, but she was ready to try. Every day, Sue gave the tree water and talked to it. She told the tree stories and sang songs.
As the days went by, the pine tree grew taller and stronger. Sue was proud of her hard work. The tree and Sue became best friends. They spent many happy days together in the yard.

One day, a big dog named Max went for a walk. He saw a small ball near a drain. Max loved to chew on balls, so he picked it up with his mouth. He chewed and chewed on the ball, having so much fun.
Then, a little girl named Lily came up to Max. She said, "That's my ball! Please give it back." Max felt sorry for taking Lily's ball. He dropped the ball and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I didn't know it was yours."
Just then, something unexpected happened. The ball started to bounce by itself! It bounced higher and higher, and then it talked! The ball said, "Thank you, Max and Lily! I was stuck near the drain, and I needed help. Now I can go back to my friends."
Max and Lily were very surprised but happy to help the talking ball. They waved goodbye as the ball bounced away. From that day on, Max and Lily became good friends and always looked out for each other.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved to eat pasta. One day, she asked her mom if she could eat pasta for dinner. Her mom said, "Yes, I permit you to eat pasta tonight."
Lucy was very happy. She put on her apron and helped her mom cook the pasta. They mixed the noodles and sauce together in a big pot. It smelled so good. Lucy could not wait to eat it.
But before they could eat, Lucy's dog, Max, jumped up and ate some pasta from the pot. Lucy's mom said, "Max, you are a naughty dog!" They laughed and made more pasta, and then they all sat down to eat together.

Anna loves her diary. It is pink and has a lock and a key. She writes in it every day. She writes about her friends, her toys, her dreams, and her feelings. She does not want anyone to read her diary. It is her secret.
One day, Anna goes to the park with her mom and dad. She brings her diary with her. She wants to write about the flowers and the birds and the swings. She puts her diary in her backpack and plays with her mom and dad.
But when she goes back to her backpack, she sees a big hole. Her diary is gone. She feels sad and scared. Who took her diary? Will they read it? Will they laugh at her? She needs her diary back. It is fragile. It can break.
She looks around the park. She sees a boy with a bike. He has her diary in his hand. He is trying to open the lock. He does not know the key is in her pocket. She runs to him and shouts, "Stop! That is my diary! Give it back!"
The boy looks at her. He sees her angry face and her tears. He feels sorry. He did not know it was her diary. He thought it was a book. He says, "I'm sorry. I did not mean to hurt you. Here is your diary. I did not read it. I promise."
He gives her diary back. She takes it and hugs it. She says, "Thank you. It is very important to me. It is fragile. Please do not take things that are not yours."
The boy nods. He says, "I'm sorry. I will not do it again. Can we be friends?"
Anna smiles. She says, "Yes, we can be friends. But you have to respect my diary. It is my secret."
They shake hands. They go to the swings and play together. Anna is happy. She has her diary back. She has a new friend. She writes about it in her diary.

Once upon a time, there was a smart boy named Tim. He liked to look at the stars at night. One day, he saw a new planet in the sky. It was very far away, but he could see it. Tim wanted to go to the planet.
So, he built a big rocket to fly to the planet. He asked his friends to help him. They all worked hard to build the rocket. Tim was very happy when the rocket was done.
Tim and his friends went to the planet in the rocket. They saw new things and met new friends. They played and had fun. When it was time to go home, they said goodbye to their new friends. They all went back to Earth in the rocket. Tim and his friends always remember the fun they had on the planet. They learned to mind each other and be kind.

One day, a little boy named Tim and his dog, Spot, went for a walk. They saw a big hill. Tim said, "Let's go up the hill, Spot!" Spot barked happily. They did not know that something incredible was waiting for them at the top of the hill.
As they went up the hill, they saw a little girl named Sue. Sue had a water spray bottle. She said, "Hi Tim and Spot! I found this spray bottle. I think something fun is going to happen!" Tim and Spot said hello and kept going up the hill.
At the top of the hill, they found a big, colorful slide. Tim, Sue, and Spot were very excited. Sue used the spray bottle to make the slide wet and slippery. They all took turns sliding down the incredible slide. They laughed and played all day, and they knew they would remember their fun adventure on the hill forever.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a very intelligent boy. One day, he found a big cookie on the table. He was very happy and wanted to eat it.
Before eating the cookie, Tim decided to examine it. He looked at the cookie very closely. It was round and had many chocolate chips. It smelled very yummy. Tim's mouth watered.
Just then, Tim's mom came into the room. "Mom, can I eat this cookie?" Tim asked. "Yes, you can," his mom said with a smile. Tim took a big bite of the cookie, and it tasted very good. Tim and his mom both laughed and enjoyed the cookie together.

Once upon a time, there was a swan. The swan lived in a big pond. The swan was upset because she could not find her friend, the duck.
The swan looked for the duck. She looked in the water. She looked in the grass. But the swan could not find her friend. She started to cry.
Then, the duck came to the swan. The duck said, "I am back! I had to yield to a big boat. I am sorry I made you upset." The swan was happy to see her friend. They played together in the pond.

Once upon a time, there was a bunny named Bob. Bob loved to eat celery. One day, he found a big, green celery in the garden. But this celery was very tough. Bob tried to bite it, but it was too hard.
Bob thought, "I need to find a way to eat this tough celery." He asked his friend, the bird, for help. The bird told Bob to relax and think. So, Bob sat down and thought about how to eat the celery.
Then, Bob had an idea. He could put the tough celery in water to make it soft. He put the celery in the water and waited. After a while, the celery became soft. Bob was very happy. He ate the celery and shared it with his friends. They all enjoyed the tasty celery together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his red toy car. One day, he saw a big, round ball in his yard. Tim thought it would be fun to play with the ball, but he didn't know how it got there.
Tim went to his friend, Sam, and said, "Sam, I found a big, round ball in my yard. Can you help me pull it?" Sam agreed, and they both went to Tim's yard. They tried to pull the ball, but it was too heavy. They were both tired and sat down to rest.
While they were resting, a foolish dog came running into the yard. The dog saw the big, round ball and started to play with it. The dog pushed the ball with its nose, and it rolled away. Tim and Sam watched the dog and laughed. They learned that sometimes, it's better to push than to pull.

Once upon a time, there was a light bug. The light bug loved to dance. Every night, the light bug would go to the pitch to dance.
One night, while the light bug was dancing, a big wind came. The wind blew the light bug far away from the pitch. The light bug was sad because it could not dance.
Then, a nice bird saw the light bug. The bird helped the light bug get back to the pitch. The light bug was so happy! It danced and danced all night long with the bird. And they became the best of friends.

Once upon a time there was a playful little girl named Emma. She loved to bounce around and play all day. Today, she was especially restless. She ran around the garden, bouncing off the trampoline, the chairs and the table too. Then something caught her eye. She saw an opera singer dressed in a beautiful dress, singing a wonderful song. 
Emma stopped bouncing and stood still. She was mesmerised by the opera singer's beautiful voice. When the song ended, Emma started to bounce again. She twirled around and around and sang along with the singer. The opera singer was so happy to have someone to share her music with! Emma kept dancing and singing until it was time to go. She waved goodbye with a big smile on her face. For the rest of the day, she couldn't stop bouncing and singing happily.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play with their toys. But sometimes they fight over the same toy. Mom says they have to share and take turns. But Lily and Ben do not want to share. They want to keep the toy for themselves.
One day, Mom gives them a new toy. It is a big puzzle with many pieces. Lily and Ben are happy. They want to make the puzzle together. They open the box and dump the pieces on the floor. They start to look for the edges and the corners.
But soon they have a problem. Lily wants to make the top of the puzzle. Ben wants to make the bottom of the puzzle. They both think their part is more fun. They start to pull and tug on the pieces. They do not listen to each other. They start to yell and cry.
Mom hears the noise and comes to see what is wrong. She sees the mess on the floor and the angry faces of Lily and Ben. She is not happy. She says they have to stop fighting and work together. Or she will separate the puzzle and put it away. She says if they can make the puzzle together, they will get a reward. They will get a sticker and a cookie.
Lily and Ben do not want to lose the puzzle. They do not want to make Mom sad. They want to get the reward. They decide to try to work together. They say sorry to each other. They agree to make the top and the bottom of the puzzle together. They help each other find the pieces. They talk and laugh. They have fun.
They finish the puzzle. It is a picture of a farm with animals and flowers. It is very pretty. They are proud of their work. They show Mom their puzzle. Mom is happy. She says they did a good job. She gives them the reward. She gives them a sticker and a cookie. She hugs them and kisses them. Lily and Ben are happy. They share the sticker and the cookie. They say thank you to Mom. They say they love her. They love each other. They love the puzzle.

Once upon a time, there was a swan named Sally. Sally was very pretty, but she was always ashamed. She thought she was not as good as the other swans.
