One day, a new swan named Sam came to the pond. Sally's friend, the duck, said, "Sally, introduce yourself to Sam." Sally was scared, but she went to Sam and said, "Hi, I am Sally." Sam smiled and said, "Hi, Sally! I am Sam. Let's be friends!"
Sally and Sam played together every day. They swam in the pond and had lots of fun. Sally learned that she was just as good as the other swans. She was not ashamed anymore.
The moral of the story is: Don't be ashamed of who you are. Everyone is special in their own way.

One day, there was a little girl named Milly. She was playing at the beach, running in and out of the waves. The sun was sparkling on the water and Milly was having the best day. But then, the sky suddenly changed. Dark clouds came and the wind started to blow.
Milly's mother called her to her side. "You need to resist the wind, sweetheart," her mother said. "Can you do that?"
Milly nodded bravely and kept walking. But soon, the wind was too strong for Milly to resist. Suddenly, she fell down under a big sand dune.
Milly's mother quickly came to her, and saw a small clean door nestled there. "It must be a secret doorway to somewhere," she said. She opened the door and helped Milly inside.
Milly was amazed at what she saw. Inside, there were lots of wonderful rooms! There was a kitchen full of treats, a bedroom lined with pillows, and a studio where Milly could do arts and crafts. Milly couldn't believe her luck!
"You found a special place all by yourself, Milly," her mother said. "I'm so proud of you."
Milly beamed, happy that she had resisted the wind and found such an incredible secret under the sand dune.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She had a big, open room filled with toys. Her favorite toy was a comb. She liked to comb her doll's hair every day.
One day, Amy could not find her comb. She looked all around her room, but it was not there. She was sad. She went to her mom and asked for help. Her mom told her to kneel down and look under her bed. Amy did as her mom said.
When Amy kneeled down and looked under her bed, she found her comb! But she also found a lot of her other toys she had lost. She learned that she should always put her toys away when she was done playing with them. That way, she would not lose them again.

Johnny and his mom were walking in the park. All around them there were vendors selling their goods. Johnny saw a big shop with a wide variety of toys. He was so excited that he begged his mom to take him there to buy something.
"Mom, store! Please!" Johnny shouted.
Johnny's mom was tired and wanted to go back home, but she could see the excitement in her son's eyes. She agreed, and went to the shop with Johnny.
When they entered the store, Johnny ran around and looked at all of the toys. He quickly chose a toy and asked his mom to buy it. His mom reached into her pocket and realized that she had forgotten her wallet at home. She sighed and asked Johnny to come back when they had the money.
Johnny started to cry. He wanted the toy badly, and he was so upset that it would not be his. His mom tried to console him, but nothing worked. Johnny held back his tears and walked away from the store with his mother, never to return.

Once upon a time in a busy forest, there lived a little bird. The bird loved to fly and sing all day. The forest was full of fun and friends.
One day, the bird found some ash near a tree. The ash was soft and gray. The bird thought it would be fun to play in the ash. So, the bird jumped in and rolled around. The bird laughed and had a great time.
After playing in the ash, the bird was dirty. The bird knew it was time to bathe. The bird went to a small pond and splashed in the water. The bird washed off all the ash and felt clean again. The bird flew back to its friends, happy and clean.

Once there was a spike who was very happy. He loved to explore the world and hop around the different places he visited. One day, the spike decided he wanted to meet other spikes and unite with them. 
The spike hopped around and found other spikes who he wanted to unite with. Soon, the first spike was joined by 2 more and then before long, a group of 10 spikes were happily hopping and exploring together. 
The group of spike were very happy playing together through fields and woods and across rivers. Everywhere they went, their group was getting bigger and bigger. One day, the group was so happy that all the spikes in the group got together and created a big spikey circle of friendship. 
The spike were very happy and continued to explore the world as one united group. Together, they could do more and had more fun than just one on its own. 
The end.

One day, a colorful bird found a mystery in the forest. It saw a big box. The bird was very curious. It wanted to know what was inside the box. So, the bird called its friend, the squirrel, to help open the box.
The squirrel said, "Let's open the mystery box together!" They pushed and pulled, and finally, the box opened. Inside the box, they found a big, round fruit. But the fruit was old and starting to rot.
The bird and the squirrel looked at the rotting fruit and decided to throw it away. They did not want to eat it. They were happy they solved the mystery together. The colorful bird and the squirrel went back to playing in the forest, looking for more fun mysteries to solve.

Once upon a time there was a brave little girl. She was excited because she was going on a trail adventure. She had packed her bag and was ready to go.
"Are you ready to go on the trail?" asked her mum.
The girl smiled and nodded.
Mum pointed to the trail, "It is a long trail and you may not find your way back home. So be brave and don't be scared."
The girl whispered to herself, "I'm brave and I won't be scared."
She unpack her bag, took out a map and started to explore the trail. She trekked through the woods and crossed a stream and still the trail went on. She was lost and had no idea where she was.
Suddenly, she heard a noise. It was a rabbit, hopping across the trail. The girl began to follow it and soon she saw a farm. She recognised the farm and knew she was close to her home.
She ran home and her mum was so happy to see her. 
"You were so brave on the trail," said her mum, "I am so proud of you!"

Once upon a time, in a big zoo, there was a playful rhinoceros named Remy. Remy loved to play with his ball all day long. He had many friends in the zoo, but his best friend was a small bird named Billy.
One sunny day, Remy and Billy were playing catch with the ball. Remy would throw the ball with his big nose, and Billy would fly high to catch it. They laughed and had so much fun. Remy said, "Billy, you are my best friend!"
But one day, while they were playing, Remy threw the ball too hard. It went very far away from them. Remy and Billy looked and looked, but they could not find the ball. They were very sad.
Billy said, "Remy, I will fly high and look for our ball." So, Billy flew up and up, but he could not see the ball. While he was looking, a big wind came and took Billy far away.
Remy waited and waited for Billy to come back, but he never did. Remy was very sad and missed his best friend. From that day on, Remy did not play with the ball anymore. He just sat and thought about his friend Billy, wishing they could play together again.

Once upon a time, there was a large rock. This rock was very big and strong. Many small animals lived near the rock because they felt safe. They knew that the rock would protect them from the wind and rain.
One day, a little bird found a pretty blouse near the rock. The blouse was very soft and colorful. The bird wanted to share the blouse with all her friends. So, she decided to make a big nest using the blouse for everyone to sleep in.
The bird and her friends worked together to make the nest. They put the blouse on the large rock and added sticks and leaves. The nest was so big and cozy that all the animals could sleep in it. The little bird was happy to share her find with her friends.
At night, when it was cold, the animals snuggled together in the nest made of the blouse. They were warm and safe because they helped each other and shared what they had. The large rock protected them from the wind and the rain.
The moral of the story is that sharing with others and working together can make everyone happy and safe.

One day, a little girl named Sue went to the park with her mom. Sue liked to play on the swings and see the big trees. She also liked to look at the delicate flowers that grew in the park. The flowers were so pretty and made her smile.
Sue's mom brought a big bottle of juice for them to drink. The juice was sweet and cold. Sue loved drinking the juice after playing on the swings. It made her feel happy and full of energy.
After they finished the juice, Sue and her mom went to see the ducks in the pond. The ducks were so cute and Sue loved to watch them swim. It was a fun day at the park, and Sue couldn't wait to come back again.

Once upon a time, in a land with purple flowers, there was a little stream. The stream was very pretty and had many fish. The fish liked to swim and play all day.
One day, a kind girl named Lily came to the stream. She saw the fish and wanted to play with them. She asked her mom, "Can I play with the fish, please?" Her mom said, "Yes, but be gentle, and let the fish swim."
Lily was very happy. She let the fish swim around her feet and they tickled her toes. She laughed and splashed in the water. The fish liked playing with Lily too.
After a while, Lily's mom said it was time to go home. Lily waved goodbye to the fish and the pretty purple flowers. She knew she would come back to play with the fish in the stream again soon.

Lily wanted to send a gift to her grandma, who lived far away. She found a pretty book with many pictures and stories that she liked. She asked her mom to help her wrap the book with paper and ribbon.
"Can we go to the post office now, mom?" Lily asked.
"Yes, dear, let's go. But we have to be careful with the gift, okay? We don't want it to get damaged or lost," mom said.
They walked to the post office, holding hands. Lily carried the gift in her arms. She was very happy and excited to send it to her grandma.
But when they got to the post office, there was a long line of people waiting. Lily got bored and restless. She started to wiggle and squirm. She dropped the gift on the floor.
"Oh no, Lily, be careful!" mom said. She picked up the gift and checked it. The paper was torn and the ribbon was loose. The book was still okay, but it looked messy.
"I'm sorry, mom. I didn't mean to be careless," Lily said. She felt sad and sorry.
"It's okay, Lily. Accidents happen. But we have to fix the gift before we send it. Let's find some tape and scissors," mom said.
They found some tape and scissors at the counter. Mom helped Lily to fix the gift. They taped the paper and tied the ribbon. The gift looked better, but not as pretty as before.
"Will grandma still like it, mom?" Lily asked.
"Of course she will, Lily. She will love it, because it's from you. And she will know that you tried your best to wrap it. The most important thing is that you love her and want to make her happy," mom said.
They gave the gift to the postman. He put it in a big box with other gifts. He said he would send it to grandma soon.
"Bye bye, gift. Have a safe trip. I love you, grandma," Lily said.
She waved to the gift. She hoped grandma would get it soon and like it. She hugged mom and thanked her for helping her. Mom hugged her back and smiled. She was proud of Lily for being brave and kind.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Anna. She was very curious but sometimes she got confused. One day she was in the bathroom and picked up a bar of soap. She wondered what it did and decided to tear it. "Ouch!" Anna said as she scratched her finger. Her mom heard her cry and came running. "What happened Anna?" her mom asked. Anna was confused and showed her mom the soap she had just torn. "Oh sweetheart, that's not for tearing. It's for cleaning," her mom said. "This is special soap for washing your hands and it won't work if you tear it." Anna listened to her mom and put the soap back in its spot on the shelf. She had learned a useful lesson and from then on remembered not to tear the soap.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lucy went for a walk. She wanted to discover new things. She loved to look at the trees, the flowers, and the birds.
One day, Lucy saw a little bird who looked sad. The bird was frustrated because it could not find any food. Lucy wanted to help the bird, so she looked for some food too.
Together, they discovered a big tree with lots of berries. The bird was happy and not frustrated anymore. Lucy felt good that she could help her new friend. They played and laughed all day long.

Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. Tom is very strong. He has big muscles. Mia is very polite. She says please and thank you.
One day, they see a big dog in the park. The dog has a leash, but no owner. Tom and Mia want to pet the dog, but they are scared. The dog looks friendly, but it is very big.
Tom says, "I will lead the dog to the bench. Maybe we can find its owner there." He takes the leash and walks slowly to the bench. The dog follows him. Mia walks behind them.
The dog is happy. It wags its tail and licks Tom's face. Tom laughs and pets the dog. Mia says, "You are very brave, Tom. You have big muscles, but you are also very kind."
Tom says, "Thank you, Mia. You are very polite. You always say nice things." He smiles and gives Mia the leash. He says, "Do you want to pet the dog too?"
Mia nods and pets the dog. The dog wags its tail and licks Mia's face. Mia laughs and hugs the dog. She says, "You are very sweet, dog. We hope you find your owner soon."
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a pink cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play and have fun. One day, Kitty had an idea. She wanted to create a band with her friends.
Kitty went to her friend, a blue dog named Doggy. Doggy liked to play the drum. They also found a yellow bird named Birdy, who could sing very well. Now, they had a band!
The pink cat, the blue dog, and the yellow bird played music together. They were very happy. Their friends came to listen and dance. Everyone had a great time with the new band.

Tom and Sam are brothers. They like to play in the woods behind their house. One day, they find a big axe in a pile of leaves. Tom picks up the axe and swings it around.
"Look, Sam, I have an axe! I can chop down trees!" Tom says.
"Be careful, Tom, that axe is sharp and heavy. You might hurt yourself or someone else," Sam says.
"No, I won't. I am strong and brave. Watch me chop this tree!" Tom says. He walks to a small tree and tries to hit it with the axe. But the axe is too big for him and he falls down.
"Ow, ow, ow! The axe hit my leg! It hurts!" Tom cries.
Sam runs to Tom and helps him get up. He sees a cut on Tom's leg and some blood. He is scared and worried.
"Tom, you are hurt! We need to go home and tell Mom. She will make it better," Sam says.
"OK, OK, let's go. I'm sorry, Sam. I was silly. The axe is not a toy. It is for grown-ups," Tom says.
They leave the axe in the woods and run home. Sam holds Tom's hand and tells him it will be OK. They manage to get home and tell Mom what happened. Mom hugs them and cleans Tom's wound. She puts a band-aid on it and gives them some cookies.
"Tom, Sam, I'm glad you are safe. But you should never play with an axe or any other dangerous thing. You could have been hurt very badly. Do you understand?" Mom says.
"Yes, Mom, we understand. We are sorry. We won't do it again," Tom and Sam say.
Mom smiles and kisses them. She tells them to be careful and to have fun. Tom and Sam say they love her and go to play with their toys. They are happy and they learn their lesson.

Once upon a time, there was a messy room. In the room, there was a bell. The bell was very old and had not been used for a long time. In the corner of the room, there was a big, brown bag. The bag was starting to rot because it was so old.
One day, a little girl named Lucy found the messy room. She saw the bell and thought it was pretty. She picked it up and said, "I want to clean this bell and make it nice again."
Lucy took the bell outside and started to clean it. As she cleaned, the bell started to shine. She was very happy. Then, she heard a voice. It was a little boy named Tim. He said, "Wow, you made that bell look new again!"
Lucy and Tim became friends. They played with the bell and had lots of fun. Together, they cleaned the messy room and threw away the rotting bag. The room was beautiful again, and the bell rang loud and clear.

Once upon a time, there was a kid named Rachel. Rachel loved to explore the world around her and was very creative. One day, Rachel went outside to the park. She wanted to find a new adventure to explore. As she walked around, she noticed an old tree. She went over to the tree and shouted up to it.
"Tree! I want to climb you!" Rachel demanded.
The tree did not answer as trees can't talk. Rachel looked around and then got a very creative idea. She grabbed some nearby sticks, rocks, and leaves and started to build a ladder. Rachel worked hard until she finished the ladder.
She looked proudly and shouted up to the tree again. "Tree! Now you can let me climb!"
The tree still did not answer but Rachel did not care. She grabbed the ladder and started to climb to the top of the tree. When she got there, she looked around and saw the most beautiful view she had ever seen.
Rachel smiled and decided that this was the perfect place for her new adventures.

Once upon a time, there was a red bird named Bobo. Bobo lived in an igloo. He liked his cold home very much.
One day, Bobo heard a knock on his igloo door. He opened the door and saw a blue fish. The blue fish asked, "Can I come in?" Bobo answered, "Yes, come in and play."
Bobo and the blue fish played all day. They had so much fun together. In the end, they were very happy and tired. They sat in the igloo and said, "What a great day!"

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved to learn about history. She liked to read books about old things. One day, Lucy found a very big and dull history book. The book was not fun, but she wanted to learn.
Lucy took the book to her friend Tom. "Tom, let's read this book together," she said. Tom looked at the book and said, "This book is dull, but we can try." So, they started to read the book. They saw pictures of old houses and people from long ago.
As they read, Lucy and Tom stumbled upon a fun story in the book. It was about a funny dog that lived long ago. The dog liked to play tricks on people. Lucy and Tom laughed as they read the story. The dull history book became fun for them. They learned that even old things can be fun if you look for the good parts.

Once upon a time, in a big green park, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her ball. One sunny day, Lily was playing with her ball when it rolled away. She ran after it and saw a new friend, a boy named Tim.
Lily said, "Hi, I'm Lily! Do you want to play with me?" Tim felt a little upset because he didn't know Lily. But he wanted to make a new friend. So, he said, "Yes, I'm Tim. Welcome! Let's play together."
They played a fun game called score. Lily would kick the ball, and Tim would try to catch it. They laughed and had a great time. In the end, they both scored many times. They were so happy to play together and become friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. Tim wore his favorite coat. It was red and warm. He saw a small bird on the ground. The bird was cold and sad.
Tim's mom was very kind and nice. She wanted to help the bird. She picked up the bird and put it in Tim's coat. The bird felt warm and happy again.
The bird was so happy, it began to sing a pretty song. Tim and his mom clapped their hands for the bird. They were glad they could help the bird. And the bird was glad too.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had many toys, but he still wanted more. One day, he saw a beautiful chair in a store. It was a piece of furniture that he really wanted. Tim was a bit selfish and always asked for more things.
Tim went to his mom and made a request. "Mom, can I have that chair, please?" he asked. His mom said, "No, Tim. You already have many toys and things to play with. You don't need more."
Tim felt sad but he knew his mom was right. He started to play with his old toys and found that they were still fun. He realized that he didn't need the chair to be happy. Tim learned that being selfish was not good.
From that day on, Tim stopped asking for more things. He played with his old toys and shared them with his friends. Tim became a kind and happy boy, and everyone loved playing with him. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Tim. Tim loved to jump all day long. He would jump on his bed, jump in the park, and even jump in the rain.
One day, while Tim was jumping, he met a girl named Sue. Sue liked to jump too! Tim said, "Hi! Do you like to jump?" Sue said, "Yes! Jumping is fun!" They both laughed and jumped together.
But then it started to rain. The rain was harsh and cold. Tim and Sue stopped jumping and ran to find shelter. They found a big tree to hide under. They sat under the tree and talked about their love for jumping. When the rain stopped, they continued to play and jump together. And they became the best of friends.

John was a brave boy. He loved to jog around his garden. One day he saw a basketball. He wanted to play with it. He was so excited. John jogged to the basketball. He grabbed it and put it on the ground. He started to jog around the basketball and moved it in different directions. He was having so much fun. He jogged around the garden chasing the basketball until his parents asked him to come inside. John jogged inside with a smile on his face. He had a lot of fun and felt very brave.

One day, a big cat named Tom found a pipe. He thought it was fun to play with. Tom wanted to teach his friend, a little dog named Max, how to play with the pipe too. So, he went to Max's house.
"Max, look at this pipe! I want to teach you how to play with it," said Tom. Max was excited and they started to play together. They had a lot of fun with the pipe.
Then, something unexpected happened. A tiny bird flew out of the pipe! Tom and Max were very surprised. The bird said, "Thank you for playing with the pipe. It helped me get out!" Tom and Max were happy that they could help the bird. From that day on, they played with the pipe and helped more birds find their way out.

Henry was playing outside one day when he saw something shiny on the ground. It was a small piece of metal. He picked it up and tried to fit it into the other pieces he had. He couldn't quite get it to fit. 
Suddenly, a big man walked by and said, "What are you doing?" 
Henry showed the man the pieces he was trying to fit together. 
The man said, "You need a bigger piece to make that fit. I can show you where to get one if you'd like." 
Henry was pleased and said, "That would be great! Thank you." 
However, the man said in a rude tone, "Have this piece for now. I'll give you a special one later when you're done playing." 
Henry thanked the man and went back to fitting the pieces together. He was happy when the big piece from the man fit perfectly!

Ben and his wife Ann were going to the park. They wanted to play with the ducks and the swings. Ben had a bag of bread for the ducks. He was happy.
"Come on, Ann, let's go to the pond. The ducks are waiting for us," Ben said.
"OK, Ben, but be careful. Don't go too close to the water. It is deep and cold," Ann said.
Ben nodded. He loved his wife. She was smart and kind. He followed her to the pond. He saw the ducks. They were quacking and swimming. Ben smiled. He opened the bag of bread and threw some pieces to the ducks. The ducks came closer. They ate the bread. Ben threw more bread. He wanted to make the ducks happy.
"Ben, that's enough. You are giving them too much bread. They will get sick. And you are wasting the bread. We need some for our lunch," Ann said.
But Ben did not listen. He was having too much fun. He threw the whole bag of bread into the pond. The ducks went crazy. They fought for the bread. They splashed the water. They made a lot of noise.
"Ben, what did you do? You are so foolish. Now we have no bread for our lunch. And the ducks are angry. They might bite you. Come back here. Now," Ann said.
But Ben did not listen. He wanted to touch the ducks. He thought they were soft and cute. He leaned over the edge of the pond. He reached out his hand. He tried to grab a duck.
"Quack! Quack! Quack!" The duck did not like Ben. It bit his finger. It hurt. Ben screamed. He let go of the duck. He fell into the pond. He got wet. He got cold. He got scared.
"Ben! Ben! Ben!" Ann shouted. She ran to the pond. She grabbed Ben's hand. She pulled him out of the pond. She hugged him. She was worried. She was angry. She was sad.
"Ben, are you OK? Are you hurt? Why did you do that? Why did you not listen to me? You are so foolish. You could have drowned. You could have died. I love you. I don't want to lose you. Please, remember. Remember to be careful. Remember to be smart. Remember to listen to your wife," Ann said.
Ben cried. He was sorry. He was ashamed. He was sorry. He loved his wife. He did not want to make her sad. He did not want to be foolish. He wanted to be careful. He wanted to be smart. He wanted to listen to his wife.
"I'm sorry, Ann. I'm sorry. I'm sorry. I love you. I love you. I love you. I will remember. I will remember. I will remember," Ben said.

Once upon a time, there was a mother who had a little boy named Tom. Tom was a good boy who loved to help his mother. One day, his mother asked him to fill a big cup with water. Tom was very happy to help and went right away to do it.
Tom took the big cup to the water and started to fill it. He filled it up, up, up until it was full. He was very careful not to spill any water as he carried it back to his mother. His mother was very happy to see the full cup of water.
"Thank you, Tom," said his mother with a big smile. "You are a very good boy for helping me." Tom felt proud and happy to help his mother. From that day on, Tom always helped his mother with a big smile on his face. And they lived happily ever after.

Once there was a patient with a large book. She was very excited and said to her friend, "Let's read together!" Her friend said, "OK! What should we read?". The patient opened the book and said, "Let's read about animals." They both looked at the page and read about a big lion. They both said, "Wow, that lion is so large!" They turned the page and read about a small mouse. They both said, "Ah, the mouse is so cute!". The patient and her friend laughed and kept reading the book.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was an independent dog who liked to go on walks and play in the park. One sunny day, Spot met a new friend named Fluffy. Fluffy was a very fluffy cat who also liked to play in the park.
Spot said to Fluffy, "Do you want to play with me?" Fluffy said, "Yes, let's play!" They played all day long, running and chasing each other. They had so much fun together. Spot and Fluffy became best friends.
One day, Spot found a big bone. He loved to chew on bones. Spot shared the bone with Fluffy, and they took turns chewing on it. But the bone tasted a little bit salty. Fluffy said, "I like the salt!" Spot said, "Me too!" They chewed on the salty bone until it was all gone. Then they went back to playing in the park, happy to be friends.

Once upon a time, in a land full of snow, there was a reindeer named Remy. Remy had many friends who liked to play in the snow. One day, it was very cold and bitter outside. Remy and his friends did not want to play because it was too cold.
Remy's friend, a little girl named Lily, had an idea. Lily said, "Remy, can you lend me your warm fur coat so I can play outside?" Remy thought about it and said, "Yes, I can lend you my coat, but we must share it so we both can be warm."
So, Remy and Lily shared the warm fur coat. They played in the snow and had lots of fun. They built a big snowman and had a snowball fight. Thanks to Remy lending his coat, they were able to play outside even when it was bitter cold. And that is how Remy the reindeer and Lily became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, in a lovely little town, there lived a girl named Lily. Lily liked to settle down and play with her toys every day. She had a best friend named Tim. They played together all year.
One day, Lily and Tim had a big fight. "I don't want to play with you anymore!" said Lily. Tim felt sad and went away. Lily was all alone with her toys.
As the days went by, Lily missed Tim a lot. She wanted to say sorry, but Tim had moved to another town. Lily never saw Tim again. Now, Lily had to play all by herself, and she was very sad.

Once upon a time, in a small wood, there lived a bossy cat and a nice dog. They were friends, but the cat liked to tell the dog what to do. The dog did not like that, but he still played with the cat because they were friends.
One sunny day, the cat told the dog to take a nap. The dog was not tired, but the cat was bossy, so the dog lay down. The cat went to play alone in the wood. The dog heard the cat having fun and wished he could play too.
After a while, the dog got up and went to find the cat. He saw the cat playing with a big ball. The dog ran to the cat and said, "I don't want to nap! Let's play together!" The cat looked at the dog and said, "Okay, let's play!" They played with the big ball and had a lot of fun. From that day on, the cat was not bossy anymore, and they played together happily in the wood.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Lily. She had a big toy computer that she loved to play with every day. Lily liked to pretend that her computer could talk and play with her.
One sunny day, Lily and her computer went outside to play. They saw a big tree with yummy fruit on it. Lily wanted to eat the fruit, so she climbed the tree and picked some for her and her computer.
Lily ate the fruit and said, "Yum, this is so sweet!" She tried to give some to her computer, but it couldn't eat. So, Lily ate the fruit for both of them. They played and laughed all day long under the big tree.

Once upon a time, there was a wealthy man named Bob. Bob had a big farm with many animals. One day, Bob went to the farm to feed his animals. He saw a little bird on the ground, trying to pick up a worm.
"Hello, little bird," said Bob. "Why are you trying to pick that worm?" The little bird looked up at Bob and said, "I am very hungry, and I need to feed my family."
Bob felt sad for the little bird. He wanted to help, but he didn't know how. Suddenly, he had an idea! He could give the little bird some food from his farm. But, there was a problem. The other animals on the farm did not want to share their food with the little bird.
Bob talked to the animals and told them, "We need to help the little bird. We have so much food, and we can share some with her family." The animals thought about it and agreed to share their food.
In the end, the little bird was able to feed her family, and everyone was happy. The animals learned that sharing can make a big difference and bring happiness to others.

Once upon a time, there was a shy girl named Lily. She had a little book called a diary. Every day, Lily wrote about her fun times in the diary.
One day, Lily went to the park to play. She had so much fun that she forgot her diary on the bench. When she went home, she noticed her diary was gone. "Oh no, I miss my diary!" cried Lily.
The next day, Lily went back to the park. She saw a boy named Tim holding her diary. "Is this your diary?" asked Tim. "Yes, it is! Thank you!" said Lily. They became friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, in a small house, a brave girl named Mia found a pretty jewel. The jewel was shiny and red. Mia loved it very much. She wanted to show it to her best friend, Tom.
Mia went to Tom's house to show him the jewel. Tom liked it too. He wanted to hold it. Mia said, "Be careful, don't squeeze it too hard." But Tom didn't listen. He squeezed the jewel very hard.
Suddenly, the jewel broke into many small pieces. Mia was very sad. She lost her pretty jewel. The brave girl learned a lesson that day. She knew she should be careful with her things and not let others break them.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to play with her toys and call her friends on her toy phone. One day, she was playing in the kitchen and saw the big refrigerator.
Mia thought it would be fun to play with the refrigerator door. She opened it and saw many yummy things inside. Mia wanted to show her toys, so she put them in the refrigerator too. Then, she closed the door and went to play in her room.
After a while, Mia's mom called her for lunch. When Mia's mom opened the refrigerator, she was surprised to see all the toys inside! Mia felt embarrassed and quickly took her toys out. She said sorry to her mom and learned not to play with the refrigerator again. From that day on, Mia only called her friends on her toy phone and played with her toys in her room.

Once upon a time, there was a loyal dog named Spot. Every day, he would run around his backyard and spin around with joy.
One day, Spot saw an object that he had never seen before. He came closer to take a look and it was a shiny spinning top. Spot was mesmerized and he wanted to give it a spin.
He looked up at his owner and barked, "May I spin it? Please?" His owner said, "Of course Spot, you can spin it." Spot joyfully jumped up and started to spin the object. The top spun faster and faster and Spot smiled with delight.
He spun the top until it stopped and he went back to his owner with a wagging tail. His owner was very happy to see how loyal Spot was.
From that day on, Spot and the spinning top were inseparable. Whenever Spot had some free time, he would grab the spinning top and give it a good spin. He was now the happiest and most loyal dog in town.

Once there was a little boy named Jack. He loved robots and was always imagining what it would be like to have a robot of his own. One day, Jack's parents told him he was going on a special trip! Jack was so excited he started to pack his things right away.
The day before he left, Jack was playing with his new robot. It was so cool! It sang, danced, and even said funny jokes. Jack was having the best time, until he heard his parent's voice.
"Jack, it's time to go," they said. "We have to pack up your robot so it can come on the trip with you."
Jack couldn't believe it! He was so excited and packed up the robot very carefully. 
The trip was amazing! Jack went to lots of new places and meet a lot of rich people. He loved showing his robot off to all his new friends, who thought it was amazing.
At the end of his trip, Jack was sad to go home, but he was happy to know that he had packed up his robot to bring back with him. He knew he was going to have a lot of fun playing with it at home.

Once there was a man who wanted to sell a vest. The vest was very pretty and colorful. He went to the market to show his vest to everyone.
In the market, there was a dog. The dog saw the pretty vest and felt jealous. The dog wanted the vest for himself. So, the dog barked and jumped to get the vest from the man.
The man saw the dog and laughed. He gave the dog the vest to wear. Now, the dog was very happy and not jealous anymore. The man and the dog became good friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time there was a brave girl called Lucy. She found a chest in the woods one day. The chest was very old and full of secrets. Lucy was scared but she opened it anyway. Inside the chest was a surprise. A bitter drink! Lucy was brave and yielded a sip. It tasted bad so she quickly put it down. Lucy ran back to her family and told them about her adventure. Together they laughed about her bravely tasting the bitter drink.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She had a wild cat named Tom. Mia loved to draw with her pencil. One day, her mom did not permit her to draw on the walls.
"But Mom, I want to draw a big picture!" Mia said. Her mom replied, "You can draw on paper, but not on the walls."
Mia was sad, but she listened to her mom. She went to her room and drew on paper. Tom, the wild cat, saw the pencil and thought it was a toy. He jumped and grabbed it with his paws.
Mia tried to take the pencil back, but Tom would not let her. "Tom, give me my pencil!" she said. Tom just looked at her and ran away. Mia chased Tom around the house until she finally got her pencil back. She went back to her room and drew a big picture of her wild cat, Tom.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved the color blue. One day, she asked her mom if she could have a blue party. Her mom said yes and they started to plan the party.
Lily invited all her friends to the party. But her friend, Tim, didn't like the color blue. He wanted the party to be red. Lily and Tim did not agree. They were both sad and did not want to go to the party. Lily's mom saw them and asked what was wrong.
Lily's mom told them that it was okay to like different things. She said that they could have both blue and red at the party. Lily and Tim were happy. They learned that it is good to share and be kind to their friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They had a lot of fun playing with a ball and running around. After a while, Tim felt tired and his mom told him it was time for a nap.
"But I want juice," said Tim. His mom smiled and gave him a cup of juice. Tim drank the juice and felt happy. Then, they sat under a big tree and Tim closed his eyes for a nap.
As Tim slept, the sky got dark with clouds. His mom saw this and woke Tim up. "It's time to go home," she said. Tim yawned and they walked back to their house, holding hands, before the rain came.

Once upon a time, there were two friends named Jane and Tom. They loved to be outside and explore the world! One day, they were out in the garden, when they looked up to the sky. There was a big, white cloud up above. Jane said, "I wonder what it's like to be up there?"
Tom thought for a moment. Then he said, "Let's cover that cloud, then we can find out!" Jane was so excited and said, "Yes, let's do it!"
Tom and Jane jumped and made big jumps. With each jump, they got closer and closer to the cloud. Finally, they jumped so high that they were inside the cloud! It was so beautiful and they loved it.
But then something happened. The cloud got broken and Tom and Jane started to fall. Fortunately, they were saved when a bright rainbow caught them both and took them back safely to the ground. 
Tom and Jane were lucky! Even though their adventure ended with a broken cloud, they were able to make a wonderful memory.

Once upon a time, in a warm and sunny village, there lived a kind baker named Tom. Tom loved to make yummy treats for all the village kids. Every day, the kids would come to his shop and say, "Please, Tom, can we have some cake?"
One day, Tom got a big whip from his friend. He wanted to use the whip to remove the bad things from his treats. He thought that if he used the whip, the treats would be even better.
Tom started to use the whip to remove the bad things from his treats. The kids loved the new treats even more. They would say, "Thank you, Tom, these treats are so yummy!" Tom was very happy that the kids loved his treats.
One little girl named Lily asked Tom, "Why are your treats so good now?" Tom told her about the whip and how it helped him remove the bad things from the treats. Lily smiled and said, "That's so cool, Tom! You are very kind to make us happy with your treats."
The moral of the story is that when you try to make things better for others, you can make them happy. And when you make others happy, you will be happy too.

Once upon a time, there was a big, strong man. He had big muscles. He liked to show off his muscles to everyone in the town. One day, he saw a filthy, dirty puddle. He wanted to jump over it.
He backed up and ran fast to jump over the puddle. But as he tried to jump, his foot slipped on the wet ground. He fell into the filthy puddle. He got up and tried to walk, but his muscles hurt from the fall.
The people in the town saw what happened. They did not want to be near the big, strong man anymore. They thought he was too dirty and smelly. The man was sad and lonely, and he could not show off his muscles anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play with his toy cars. One day, he was playing in his room and was very careless. He left his toy cars all over the floor.
His mom came in to give him a case for his toy cars. She did not see the cars on the floor. She walked in and something unexpected happened. She stepped on a car and slipped. Tim's mom had to kneel down to not fall.
Tim saw his mom almost fall and knew he had to be more careful. He put all his toy cars in the case his mom gave him. From that day on, Tim always made sure to put his toys away. He did not want to see his mom almost fall again.

Once upon a time there was a duck. His name was Quack. Quack was very popular. He had lots of friends. All the animals at the pond liked him. 
One day, the other animals noticed that something was different. Quack was very sad. They asked him what was wrong and he said he had lost his hope. The other animals didn't know what that was so they asked him questions. 
Quack told them how he had been flying high in the sky and his wings were too weak to keep him up. He told them how he wanted to feel the wind on his wings, but his wings weren't strong enough anymore. His friends felt so sad for him. 
So, all the animals at the pond decided to help Quack. They wanted to give him back his hope. They all cheered for him and said 'Quack can fly'. 
Quack was filled with hope. He started flapping his wings and finally he was in the air. He flew up higher and higher and everyone cheered with excitement. 
Quack was so happy and everyone was so proud. Now he could feel the wind again and his hope was back.

Once upon a time, in a big, dark forest, there was a brave girl named Lily. She loved to play and explore. One day, she saw a big, old house. It was very dark and scary. Lily wanted to see what was inside.
Lily went into the house. It was very quiet. She saw a skull on the floor. It was very old and dirty. Lily was scared, but she was brave. She said, "I will not scream. I will keep going."
Lily went deeper into the house. She heard a loud noise. "Who's there?" she asked. A big, scary monster came out. It had big teeth and red eyes. The monster looked at Lily and said, "I will eat you!"
Lily was very scared. She tried to run away, but the monster was too fast. The monster caught Lily and ate her up. The big, old house was quiet again. And the skull on the floor was not alone anymore.

Ben and Mia were playing with their toy guns in the backyard. They liked to pretend they were spies on a mission. They hid behind the bushes and the fence and made noises like "pew pew pew" and "bang bang bang".
"Look, there's the exit!" Mia said, pointing to the gate. "We have to shoot the bad guys and escape!"
"OK, let's go!" Ben said, excited. He ran towards the gate, holding his toy gun. But he did not see the big dog that was sleeping next to the gate. The dog woke up and saw Ben running. It barked loudly and chased Ben.
"Help, help!" Ben shouted, scared. He dropped his toy gun and ran back to Mia. The dog followed him, still barking.
"Mia, what do we do?" Ben asked, crying.
"Don't worry, Ben, I have an idea!" Mia said, brave. She picked up Ben's toy gun and aimed at the dog. She made a loud noise like "boom boom boom". The dog was surprised and stopped barking. It looked at Mia and wagged its tail. It thought Mia was playing with it.
"Good dog, good dog!" Mia said, smiling. She threw the toy gun over the fence and the dog ran after it. Mia and Ben hugged each other and laughed.
"We did it, we did it!" they said, happy. They ran to the exit and opened the gate. They saw their mom waiting for them with a big smile.
"Hi, kids, did you have fun?" she asked.
"Yes, mom, we had a lot of fun!" they said. They told her their story and she hugged them and kissed them.
"You are very brave and smart, my little spies!" she said, proud. "Now, let's go inside and have some cookies!"

Once upon a time, there was a red barn on a big farm. The barn was tight and warm, perfect for the animals that lived inside. In the barn, there was a cow, a pig, and a little chick.
One day, the little chick wanted to go outside and play. But the chick was scared. The cow saw the chick and said, "Don't be scared, little chick. I will encourage you." The cow walked with the chick, and together they went outside.
They played all day in the sun. The little chick was happy and not scared anymore. The cow was proud and said, "See, little chick? You can do anything with a friend by your side." And they went back to the tight, warm barn to rest after a fun day.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was three years old and loved to explore. One day while exploring she found something strange. It was an unknown object! She picked it up and started to play with it.
The little girl remembered her daddy had told her about a strange thing called recording. She decided to try it out. She said "Hello, my name is..." and pressed a button on the unknown object. Suddenly, it started recording her voice!
The girl was so excited. She ran back to her daddy to show him what she had found. Daddy said "That's very cool! What is it?"
The little girl replied "It is an unknown recording device! I found it while exploring!". Daddy was so proud of his little girl. He said "You have done an amazing job. Let's keep this recording device forever so we can remember this day." 
The little girl smiled and said "Yay!".
And that's how the little girl and her dad recorded an unknown moment in time.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly boy named Tim. Tim had a drum that he loved to play. He would slap the drum with his hands to make fun sounds. Tim had a best friend, a dog named Max. They liked to play together all the time.
One day, Tim and Max were playing in the park. Tim was slapping his drum, and Max was running around. But then, a big mean boy came and took Tim's drum away. Tim and Max were very sad. Max knew that he had to help his friend get the drum back.
Max had a plan. He saw the big mean boy put the drum under a tree. Max ran to the tree and barked loudly. The big mean boy got scared and ran away. Max took the drum and gave it back to Tim. They were both happy again. Tim slapped his drum and Max wagged his tail. They played together in the park, and the big mean boy never bothered them again.

Once upon a time, there was a mighty lion. The lion lived in a big forest with many friends. They loved to play together every day.
One day, the mighty lion saw a new animal in the forest. It was a small bird with many colors. The lion had never seen a bird like this before. The bird could sing and fly high in the sky.
The mighty lion wanted to learn about the bird's colors. The bird taught the lion about red, blue, and yellow. The lion was happy to learn about colors. They became good friends and played together in the big forest.

One day, a little boy named Tim was standing near the big tree in his yard. He saw a man with a worried face walking down the street. The man was holding a newspaper in his hand.
Tim went up to the man and asked, "Why are you worried?" The man looked at Tim and said, "I lost my dog, and I can't find him. I put an ad in the newspaper, but no one has called me yet."
Tim wanted to help the man, so he said, "Let's look for your dog together!" They walked around the neighborhood, calling the dog's name. After a while, they found the dog hiding behind a bush.
The man was so happy, he hugged Tim and thanked him. Tim felt good for helping the man and making a new friend. They both walked home with big smiles on their faces.

Lily was waiting for her mom to come home. She was playing with her dolls on the floor. She liked to make them talk and hug. She heard a car stop outside. She ran to the window and saw her mom's car. She was so happy!
She opened the door and shouted, "Mom, mom, I missed you!" Her mom smiled and got out of the car. She had a big bag in her hand. She walked to the door and hugged Lily. She said, "I missed you too, my sweet girl. How was your day?"
Lily told her mom about her dolls, her snack, and her nap. She asked her mom, "What is in the bag?" Her mom said, "It is a surprise for you. Do you want to see?" Lily nodded and clapped her hands. Her mom opened the bag and took out a soft, fluffy toy. It was a bunny with long ears and a pink nose.
Lily gasped and said, "A bunny! It is so cute! Can I have it?" Her mom said, "Of course, it is yours. I saw it at the store and I thought of you. You love bunnies, right?" Lily said, "Yes, I do. Thank you, mom. You are the best!" She took the bunny and hugged it. It felt gentle and warm.
Her mom said, "You are welcome, my love. I am glad you like it. What will you name it?" Lily thought for a moment and said, "I will name it Rosie. Because it has a pink nose like a rose." Her mom said, "That is a lovely name. Rosie is lucky to have you as a friend." Lily said, "And I am lucky to have you as a mom." They hugged again and went inside. Lily showed Rosie to her dolls and they all played together. Lily was very happy.

One day, Mama smiled and said, "Let's go outside in the sun!" Little Timmy ran around the yard, feeling so happy. Then, he looked up and saw a big, white cloud. It was so high and seemed to go on forever. Mama said, "Pretty soon, it will be warmer, and we can play even more."
Timmy was so excited, he couldn't stop smiling. But then the sky became dark and harsh. Mama said, "It looks like rain is coming. Let's go back inside." 
Inside, Timmy asked, "Mama, what will rain do?" Mama said, "Rain will make everything all wet, so we should stay inside, to keep dry and be safe."
Timmy was curious but also felt a bit scared. But then soon he heard something outside. It was gentle and shiny. Little Timmy looked outside, and he saw that it was not dark and harsh anymore. But instead the rain had become a beautiful rainbow!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jack. Jack had a green toy box. Inside the box, there a player. It had buttons and music. Whenever Jack pressed the buttons, he heard funny tunes. 
Jack liked to lean against the toy box and listen to the music. He looked up at the sky when he heard the tunes. One day, he leaned too hard, and the box tipped over! Everything tumbled out of the box.
Jack was very sad. He couldn't lean anymore! Then, his mommy came in and saw the mess. She made him help put the toys away. She said, if he was careful, he could lean against the box again.
So Jack put the toys away. When he was done, he leaned against the box. He heard the music, and he was so happy. He smiled and told his mommy he would never lean too hard again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy had a big, red ball. She loved to play with her ball every day. One day, while playing, the ball went into the tall bushes. Amy went to get her ball, but she got a cut on her hand. She felt pain and started to cry.
Amy's mom heard her cry and came running. She saw the cut and the pain in Amy's eyes. Mom knew she had to help her little girl. She took Amy's hand and said, "Don't worry, I will make it better." Mom was very gentle with Amy's hand.
Mom put a soft bandage on Amy's cut. The pain went away and Amy stopped crying. She hugged her mom and said, "Thank you, Mommy." They went back to playing with the big, red ball, but this time, they played away from the tall bushes. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a boy named Tom. Tom loved to play with his toy vehicle. He would push it around the house and make it go fast. One day, Tom's mom said, "Tom, it's very windy outside, so don't go out today." But Tom wanted to play outside with his vehicle.
Tom went outside and the wind was harsh. It blew his hat off his head. Tom had an idea. He went back inside and found some string. Tom said, "I will tie my hat to my head, so it won't blow away." He tied the string to his hat and put it on his head.
Tom went outside again and played with his vehicle. The wind was still harsh, but his hat stayed on his head. Tom's mom saw him playing and said, "Tom, you are very smart for tying your hat to your head." Tom smiled and continued to play with his vehicle, happy that he could enjoy the windy day.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a little girl named Lily. She had a blue ball that she loved very much. She played with the ball every day in her yard. 
One day, Lily's friend, Tim, came over to play. They played with the blue ball together. But Tim accidentally threw the ball too far, and it went into the big tree. Lily and Tim tried to get the ball down, but they could not reach it.
Tim felt very sorry. He said to Lily, "I promise I will find a way to get your blue ball back." They thought and thought. Then, they found a long stick. They used the stick to poke the ball out of the tree. 
Lily was so happy to have her blue ball back. She hugged Tim and said, "Thank you for keeping your promise." They played with the ball for the rest of the day, and they were very careful not to throw it too far again.

Once upon a time, there was a little cow named Mimi. Mimi lived on a farm with her friends. She loved to eat hay and play all day. Mimi wanted to become the healthiest cow in the world. She knew that eating hay and playing with her friends made her happy and healthy.
One sunny day, Mimi was eating hay when she saw a big rainbow in the sky. Mimi was so excited! She thought that if she could find the end of the rainbow, she would become the healthiest cow ever. So, Mimi left her hay and went on a journey to find the end of the rainbow.
Mimi walked and walked, but she couldn't find the end of the rainbow. She started to feel sad and tired. But then, something unexpected happened. Mimi found a big pile of hay at the end of the rainbow! She realized that she didn't need to find the end of the rainbow to become healthy. All she needed was to eat her hay and play with her friends. So, Mimi went back to her farm, ate the hay, and played with her friends. And that's how Mimi became the healthiest cow in the world.

John opened the door and walked out of his house. On the street he saw a billboard. It was available to anyone who wanted it. John liked the billboard and decided to open it. 
He worked hard to open the billboard. After a few hours he finally opened it. Inside the billboard there was a scary creature. The creature was mad at John for opening it. It flew out of the billboard and scared John. 
John ran home and closed the door quickly. The creature was still outside, growling and snarling. John was scared and begged the creature to leave. But the creature just stayed there, waiting for John to open the door again. John stayed in his house, never opening the door again.

Sara and Ben wanted to decorate their room for Mom's birthday. They had paper, scissors, glue and crayons. They cut out stars, hearts and flowers from the paper. They glued them on a long string. They wanted to hang the string on the wall.
"Let's measure how long the string is," Sara said. She held one end of the string and gave the other end to Ben. Ben walked to the other side of the room. He pulled the string tight. He did not see the lamp on the table. He bumped into the lamp. The lamp fell down and broke.
"Oh no!" Sara and Ben said. They heard Mom coming up the stairs. They were very sorry. They hid the string behind the bed. They hoped Mom would not see the broken lamp.
Mom opened the door. She saw the lamp on the floor. She was not happy. She asked Sara and Ben what happened. Sara and Ben told her the truth. They said they were sorry. They said they wanted to decorate the room for her birthday.
Mom looked at them. She saw the string behind the bed. She smiled. She hugged Sara and Ben. She said she was not angry. She said she loved them. She said she wanted to see their decoration. She helped them hang the string on the wall. She said it was beautiful. She said it was the best birthday gift ever. Sara and Ben were happy. They said they loved Mom. They said happy birthday.

Suzie was walking with her family in the park. She noticed a stranger walking nearby. She stopped and waved at the stranger.
"Hello stranger," said Suzie.
The stranger smiled and said, "Hello, little one. How are you today?"
"I'm fine," said Suzie. "Will you please come and play with us?"
The stranger was happy to join them. He and Suzie played on the swings and the climbing frame. They laughed and had lots of fun.
When it was time to go home, the stranger said goodbye.
"Please come and play with us again," said Suzie.
The stranger smiled and said, "I'm sure I will, Suzie. Have a fine day." 
Suzie smiled and said goodbye. She skipped off with her family feeling very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to play and run all day long. One day, Spot saw a big dog doing something called "exercise." The big dog was jumping and running in place. Spot thought it looked fun and wanted to learn how to do it too.
Spot went to the big dog and said, "Hello, can you teach me how to do exercise like you?" The big dog smiled and said, "Of course, Spot! Let's start by jumping up and down." Spot tried to jump like the big dog, but he fell down. Spot felt ashamed because he could not do it right away.
The big dog saw Spot feeling sad and said, "Don't be ashamed, Spot. Everyone has to learn and practice to get better at something. Keep trying and soon you will be good at exercise too." Spot listened to the big dog and kept practicing. After some time, Spot was able to do exercise just like the big dog. They both became good friends and exercised together every day.
Moral: Don't be ashamed if you can't do something right away. Keep trying and you will learn and get better.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Lily was a troubled girl because she always wanted things she did not have. One day, she saw a big piano in a store and she wanted it very much.
Lily asked her mom and dad for the piano, but they did not have enough money to buy it. Lily was very sad. She could not stop thinking about the piano. One day, when she was walking home from school, she found a small box on the ground. Inside the box, there was a tiny piano. Lily was so happy!
Lily played with the tiny piano every day. She found out that the tiny piano could make big music, just like the big piano in the store. Her mom and dad were very proud of her. Lily learned that you do not need big things to be happy. Sometimes, small things can make you just as happy. And that is the end of the story.

Once upon a time, in a small place, there lived a little boy named Tim. He loved to play with his red ball. One day, he saw an incredible big bird in his yard. The bird was very pretty and had many colors. Tim wanted to talk to the bird.
"Hi, bird! Do you want to play with me?" Tim asked. The bird looked at him and replied, "Yes, I want to play with you, Tim." They played with Tim's red ball and had a lot of fun. But then, the sky got dark and it was time for Tim to go home.
Tim said to the bird, "Bye, bird! I will see you tomorrow." The bird replied, "Bye, Tim! I will come back." But the next day, the incredible bird did not come back. Tim was very sad. He waited and waited, but the bird never came back to play with him again.

Lily and Ben are playing outside with chalk. They draw big circles and squares on the sidewalk. They use blue, green, yellow and pink chalk. Lily likes pink the best. She makes a big pink heart and writes her name inside.
"Look, Ben, this is my heart," she says. "Do you like it?"
Ben nods. He likes Lily's heart. He makes a big yellow star and writes his name inside. He shows it to Lily.
"Look, Lily, this is my star," he says. "Do you like it?"
Lily nods. She likes Ben's star. They smile at each other. They are good friends.
But then it starts to rain. The raindrops fall on the sidewalk. They make the chalk wet and blurry. Lily and Ben run to the porch. They watch the rain pour on their drawings.
"Oh no, our chalk is gone!" Lily says. She feels sad. She likes her heart and Ben's star.
Ben feels sad too. He likes his star and Lily's heart. He thinks of something. He runs inside and gets a big umbrella. He comes back and gives it to Lily.
"Here, Lily, take this," he says. "We can still see our chalk."
Lily takes the umbrella. She smiles at Ben. He smiles back. They hold the umbrella together. They walk to the sidewalk. They see their chalk. It is still there, but faint. The rain has not washed it away completely.
"Look, Ben, our chalk is still here!" Lily says. She feels happy. She likes her heart and Ben's star.
"Look, Lily, our chalk is still here!" Ben says. He feels happy. He likes his star and Lily's heart.
They hug each other. They are good friends. They don't mind the rain. They have each other and their chalk.

One day, a bird wanted to fly high in the sky. The bird was very proud of how high it could fly. The bird saw a big car with a driver. The driver was going fast on the road. The bird wanted to say hello to the driver.
"Hello, driver!" said the bird. The driver looked up and saw the bird flying high. The driver was happy to see the bird and said, "Hello, bird! You fly so high! I am proud of you!"
But then, something unexpected happened. The bird flew too close to the car and got stuck in the car's window. The driver was scared and stopped the car. The bird was also scared and did not know what to do.
The driver helped the bird to get out of the window. The bird was free and could fly again. The bird said, "Thank you, driver!" The driver smiled and said, "You're welcome, bird! Be careful when you fly!"
From that day on, the bird was more careful when it flew high in the sky. The bird and the driver became good friends and always said hello to each other when they saw each other.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very forgetful. One day, he was playing with his toy car on the floor. He loved to make the car go fast and make fun noises. Tim's mom called him for dinner. "Tim, come eat!" she said.
Tim ran to the table, but he was so foolish that he forgot his toy car on the floor. After dinner, Tim went back to his room to play some more. He looked for his car, but he could not find it. "Where is my car?" Tim asked himself.
His mom came into the room and saw the toy car on the floor. "Tim, you forgot your car on the floor," she said. Tim felt silly for forgetting his toy. He picked up the car and put it away. From then on, Tim tried to remember where his toys were, so he would not feel foolish again.

One day, a boy went to the park with his mom. It was very hot outside so he asked if he could go in the paddling pool. His mom said yes and he put on his red swimming costume. When he got to the paddling pool, he saw that it was full of cool, blue water. He jumped in and started to splash around.
He then noticed a brown duck swimming in the pool. He ran to the edge and put his hands in the water. The duck swam over to him and he started to gently cup his hands in the water and was amazed as the duck happily soaked up the water.
The boy felt the sun getting hotter and decided to get out the pool and have a massage. His mom had taught him how to do this and he started to rub his arms and legs. He was feeling so relaxed, just like he was having a massage!
When he was done, the boy thanked his mom for taking him to the park. He had had an amazing time, especially with his new brown duck friend!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big, ugly toy. It was a funny-looking bike. He loved to ride it all day long.
One day, Tim went to the park with his mom. He took his ugly bike with him. He rode it around the park, showing it to all his friends. They all thought it was funny, but they liked it too.
As Tim was riding, he saw a girl who was sad. She had hurt her hand. Tim stopped riding his bike and went to help her. He held her hand and walked with her to find her mom. The girl felt better, and they became friends. They played together in the park, and Tim even let her ride his ugly bike.

Once upon a time, there was a clumsy farmer named Tom. He had a farm with many animals. Tom was not good at his job. He always dropped food and fell in the mud. The animals did not like this.
One day, a wise old bird came to the farm. The bird saw Tom's problem and said, "Tom, you need to improve. You are too clumsy. The animals need a better farmer." Tom felt sad, but he knew the bird was right. He wanted to be a better farmer for his animals.
Tom asked the bird for help. The bird said, "First, you must take your time. Do not rush. Be careful with the food and watch where you step." Tom listened and tried his best to improve. Slowly, he became less clumsy. The animals were happy, and Tom was proud. He thanked the wise old bird for helping him become a better farmer.

One day, a little girl named Lily went for a walk. She saw a big gray cat. The cat was sad. Lily wanted to help the cat. She had an idea.
Lily started to act like a cat. She meowed and purred. The gray cat looked at her. It started to act like a cat too. They played and ran around.
After a while, the gray cat was happy. Lily was happy too. They became good friends. They played together every day. And they always acted like cats.

One day, a little girl named Sally went to the park. She saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. Sally tried to climb the tree, but it was hard. She felt frustrated.
Sally saw a spark in the sky. It was a firefly! The firefly flew to the tree and showed her how to climb. Sally watched the firefly and tried again.
This time, Sally was able to climb the tree. She was so happy! She thanked the firefly for its help. Sally and the firefly played together until the sun went down.

Once upon a time, there was a little fish named Fin. Fin lived in a big pond. He had many friends, and they liked to play together. One day, it was very hot and harsh outside. The sun was shining bright in the sky.
Fin and his friends wanted to play a game. They decided to play "slap the water." They would take turns jumping and slapping the water with their tails. When Fin jumped, he slapped the water so hard that it made a big splash!
As Fin played, he saw a big fish. The big fish had a big lung. The big fish wanted to play too. He jumped and slapped the water with his tail. It made an even bigger splash! Fin and his friends laughed and played with the big fish all day long. They had so much fun, and they were happy to make a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little red car. It loved to joke and play with its car friends. They would race around the park and have lots of fun together.
One day, the little red car wanted to go on a big adventure. It needed fuel to go far away. The little red car went to the fuel place and got some. Now, it was ready for the perfect trip.
The little red car drove and drove, seeing new things. It saw big mountains, tall trees, and pretty flowers. The little red car was so happy. It had the perfect adventure and joked with its friends about the fun trip when it got back home. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy staff. He always like to take long naps instead of working. One day, he had a very special visitor. It was a 3 year old child! The staff was so happy that he said a warm welcome. The child smiled and said "thank you". The staff showed her around the building and told her about his work. The child was so impressed that she asked the staff to join her for dinner later. The staff thought it was a wonderful idea! They went to the restaurant and ate delicious food. The staff was very thankful for the child's warm welcome. They had a wonderful time and soon it was time for the child to go home. The staff said goodbye with a nice big hug! The child left with a bright smile on her face.

One day, a big, rough bear went to the woods. He saw a small house. The house had a little pot. The bear was hungry. He wanted to eat.
The bear used his paw to stir the pot. He tasted it with his lip. The pot was hot! The bear said, "Ouch! My lip hurts!"
Then, a little girl came home. She saw the bear. She was not scared. She said, "Mr. Bear, let me help you." The girl put ice on the bear's lip. The bear felt better.
The bear and the girl became friends. They ate from the pot together. They were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a brave little dog named Tiny. Tiny lived in a small house with his family. He loved to play outside, run around, and lie in the warm sun.
One day, Tiny saw a little flame near his house. He knew that the flame was not good and could hurt his family. So, Tiny decided to be brave and do something to help.
Tiny barked and barked until his family came outside. They saw the flame and quickly put it out with water. Tiny's family was so proud of him for being brave and helping them. They gave him a big hug and a treat. And from that day on, Tiny was known as the brave little dog who saved his family.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a little bunny named Ben. Ben was an adventurous bunny who loved to play and jump. One day, he met a squirrel named Sam. Sam was very fast and always won races. Ben wanted to win too, so he asked Sam to race.
"Let's race to that big tree," said Ben. "Okay, let's go!" said Sam. They got ready to run. Ben thought of a way to use spring in his jump to go faster. When they started, Ben used his spring jump and hopped very high and fast. Sam was surprised!
As they raced, Ben was in the lead. But then, something unexpected happened. A big wind blew and made Ben's spring jump go too far! He landed on a big branch up in the tree. Sam stopped and looked up at Ben. "Wow, you won!" said Sam. Ben felt happy and proud. He had a victory, and it was all because of his adventurous spirit and spring jump.

One day, a girl named Amy wanted to go to a fancy party. She put on her pretty dress and shiny shoes. She was very happy and excited to dance and play with her friends.
On her way to the party, Amy saw a doctor. The doctor was kind and smiled at her. He asked her, "Where are you going, Amy?" She said, "I am going to a fancy party to have fun!"
The doctor told her, "That sounds like a great time! But before you go, let me give you a special sticker to make your dress even more fancy." The doctor gave Amy a big, sparkly star sticker. She put it on her dress and said, "Thank you, doctor!"
Amy went to the fancy party and had lots of fun. She danced and played with her friends, and they all loved her sparkly star sticker. When the party was over, Amy went home and told her mom about the kind doctor and the special sticker. Her mom hugged her and said, "I am so glad you had a great time at the fancy party, Amy!"

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to imagine he was in a jungle. He would play with his toy animals and pretend they were real. One day, he found a loyal dog named Spot. Spot was his best friend, and they played in the jungle together.
One day, Tim said to Spot, "I wish we could really go to the jungle and see all the big animals!" Spot barked happily, as if he understood. Tim didn't know that something special was going to happen soon.
The next day, Tim's mom and dad surprised him with a trip to the big zoo. It was just like the jungle! Tim and Spot saw all the big animals they had imagined. They were so happy to see them for real. And just like in their pretend jungle, Tim and Spot stayed loyal friends forever.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a wild cat. The cat was hungry. Tim wanted to help the cat. He went home to get some food.
In the kitchen, Tim found some soup. He put the soup in a bowl. He ran back to the park. The cat saw the soup and paused. The cat said, "I don't like soup. I want fish."
Tim was sad. He wanted to help the cat. He went back home and looked for fish. But he could not find any fish. He had an idea. He took some bread and made a sandwich.
Tim went back to the park. The cat paused again. The cat said, "I still want fish." Just then, a big bird came. The bird had a fish in its mouth. The bird dropped the fish near the cat.
The cat was happy. Tim was happy too. They both ate their food. The cat and Tim became friends. They played in the park every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Emma. Emma had a doll that she loved very much. The doll was made of cloth and had a thread for a smile. Emma would hug her doll every day.
One day, Emma met a boy named Tom. Tom was sometimes rude. He saw Emma's doll and wanted to play with it. Emma said, "Be nice to my doll, please!" Tom said, "OK, I will be nice."
Later, Tom was playing with the doll, but he was not careful. He pulled the thread that made the doll's smile. The thread came off! Emma was sad, and she hugged her doll.
Tom felt bad for being rude. He said, "I am sorry, Emma." He found a new thread and helped Emma put the smile back on her doll. Emma was happy again, and she hugged Tom. From that day on, Tom was always nice to Emma and her doll.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful boy named Tim. Tim lived near a big temple. He liked to help people who came to the temple.
One day, Tim saw a man near the temple. The man was sad. Tim asked, "Why are you sad?" The man said, "I need help. I lost my way to the temple."
Tim wanted to help the man. He took the man's hand and walked with him to the temple. They walked and walked. But they did not find the temple.
Tim and the man were lost. They could not find the temple. They were very sad. The man said, "We needed help, but now we are lost." And they stayed lost forever.

One day, a clever little boy named Tim saw the moon in the sky. He asked his mom, "Mom, why is the moon up there?" His mom said, "The moon is our friend in the sky. It gives us light at night."
Tim wanted to understand more about the moon. So, he went outside to look at it. He saw a big, round, and bright moon. Tim said, "Hello, Moon! Can you come down and play with me?"
To Tim's surprise, the moon answered, "Yes, Tim! I can come down and play with you!" The moon came down from the sky and played with Tim. They had so much fun together. But the moon had to go back to the sky to give light to everyone at night.
Tim was happy that he had a new friend. He now understood more about the moon. From that day on, every night, Tim would look at the moon and smile.

One day, a little cat named Kitty went outside to play. Kitty saw a big tree with many colors on it. She wanted to reach the colors, but they were too high up. So, Kitty tried to stretch her body to touch the colors. But no matter how much she stretched, she could not reach them.
Kitty felt sad and ashamed. She thought, "I am not big enough to touch the colors." Just then, a friendly bird saw Kitty and asked, "Why are you sad, Kitty?" Kitty said, "I want to touch the colors on the tree, but I can't reach them."
The bird thought for a moment and said, "I have an idea! I can help you reach the colors." The bird flew up and broke a branch with colors. Then, the bird gave the branch to Kitty. Kitty was so happy and thanked the bird. They played with the colors together, and Kitty was not ashamed anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia had a scooter that she loved very much. She would ride it every day in the park. But the scooter was old and poor. It had many scratches and the wheels were not so good.
One night, Mia had a dream. In her dream, she saw a shiny new scooter. It was so pretty and fast. Mia wanted that scooter so much. She thought about it all the time, even when she was with her old scooter.
One day, Mia went to the park with her poor scooter. She saw a shiny new scooter just like in her dream. It was near a big tree. Mia left her old scooter and took the new one. She rode it very fast and had so much fun. But soon, a big boy came and said, "That is my scooter!" He was very mad.
Mia felt bad and gave the scooter back to the boy. She went to find her old scooter, but it was gone. Someone else took it. Mia was very sad and walked home without a scooter.

One day, a little boy named Tim set out on a walk. He wore a red hat and big boots. The sky was gray. Tim felt alert, as if rain would come soon.
As he walked, he met a girl named Sue. She had a blue hat and a big smile. "Hi, Tim!" she said. "Do you think it will rain soon?" Tim nodded. "Yes, Sue. I feel alert, like rain is coming. Let's find a place to hide from the rain."
They found a big tree with lots of leaves. They sat under the tree and talked. Soon, the rain came. It fell softly at first, then harder. Tim and Sue stayed dry under the tree. They were happy they had found a good place to hide.

Once upon a time, in a small, gloomy house, there lived a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to read books. He had many books that he would read every day. One day, Tim saw a girl sitting alone under a tree. She looked sad.
Tim wanted to make the girl happy. He thought about what he could do. Tim had an idea! He went to his house and took his favorite book. Tim walked to the girl and said, "I want to give you my book. It makes me happy, and I hope it makes you happy too."
The girl smiled and said, "Thank you, Tim!" They sat under the tree and read the book together. The girl was happy, and Tim was happy too. They became good friends. The moral of the story is that sharing can make others happy and create new friendships.

Once upon a time, there was a soft, little cat named Fluffy. Fluffy liked to play with her ball and look at the birds outside. One day, Fluffy saw a big, red ball in the yard. She wanted to play with it, so she went outside.
As Fluffy got close to the ball, she saw a tiny bug on it. The bug looked at Fluffy and said, "Hi, my name is Tiny! Can you help me? I need to find my home." Fluffy thought for a moment and said, "Okay, I will help you, Tiny."
Fluffy put her finger on the ball and rolled it around the yard. They looked everywhere for Tiny's home. Suddenly, the ball hit a tree and a small door opened in the tree trunk. Tiny's family came out and said, "Thank you, Fluffy, for helping Tiny find her way home!" Fluffy was happy that she could help her new friend. From that day on, Fluffy and Tiny played together all the time.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to wonder about many things. One day, he went to the store with his mom. Tim saw a man who could not hear. He was deaf. Tim was very curious about the man.
Tim wanted to talk to the man, but he did not know how. He thought and thought, and then he had an idea. Tim used his hands to say hello to the deaf man. The man was very happy and said hello back to Tim with his hands too.
