The deaf man and Tim became good friends. They talked with their hands every time they saw each other at the store. Tim learned that even if someone is different, you can still be friends. The moral of the story is to be kind and understanding to everyone, no matter how different they are.

Once upon a time, in an ordinary park, there were two friends, Tim and Sam. They loved to play in the sand. One day, they decided to build a big sandcastle together. They were very excited.
While they were building, a big boy named Jack came over. He said, "I want to build a sandcastle too!" Tim and Sam did not want to share their sand. They said, "No, this is our sand!" Jack was sad and walked away.
Soon, Tim and Sam saw that their sandcastle was not very big. They needed more help. They remembered Jack and felt bad. They called Jack back and said, "Let's all build together!" Jack was happy and they all built a big sandcastle. Everyone was happy and they learned that sharing is good.

Once upon a time there was a bunny. His name was Harry and he liked to hop around looking for wool. He hopped from one field to the next without a care in the world.
One day, he hopped into a meadow that was filled with thick, soft wool. He couldn't believe his luck! He hopped around happily and started to pick the wool up with his furry hands.
Suddenly, he heard a loud noise. He looked around and saw a sheep, standing alert in the corner. "Your wool is mine now, Harry!" the sheep said.
Harry gasped, then cleared his throat to speak. "Please forgive me," he said. "I didn't mean to take your wool."
The sheep looked at Harry and softened his gaze. "I forgive you, my little friend," the sheep said. "But be careful when entering someone else's meadow."
Harry thanked the sheep, then hopped away joyfully, feeling happy and grateful for the sheep's kind forgiveness.

One day, a brave girl named Lily wanted to write a letter to her friend. She took her paper and crayon and started to write. But it was a rainy day, and the rain came inside her house.
Lily did not want her letter to get wet. She had an idea. She took her umbrella and opened it. She put the umbrella over her head and the letter. Now, the rain could not make her letter wet.
Lily was very happy. She finished writing her letter and gave it to her friend. Her friend was happy too. They played under the umbrella and had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim was a little weird. He did not like to eat cereal in the morning. One day, Tim's mom told him he had to eat his cereal before he could go out and play.
Tim did not want to eat the cereal. He tried to remember if he had ever eaten cereal before. He could not remember. Tim felt sad and mad. He did not know what to do.
Tim decided to hide the cereal under his bed. He thought his mom would not find it there. But his mom found the cereal under his bed. She was very mad. Tim could not go out and play. He had to stay in his room all day. This was a bad day for Tim.

One day, a little cat was walking down the street. The cat saw a tall pole. The cat wanted to climb the pole, but it was scared. It felt helpless. The cat looked around for help.
A big dog came by and saw the cat. The dog said, "Why are you sad, little cat?" The cat replied, "I want to climb the pole, but I feel helpless. Can you help me?" The dog thought for a moment and said, "I can help you, but we need to find a way to delay the climb."
The dog found a long rope and tied it to the top of the pole. The dog said, "Hold onto the rope, little cat. I will pull you up." The cat held onto the rope tightly. The dog started to pull the rope, but it was very hard. The dog was tired, but it did not give up. It kept pulling the rope.
Finally, the little cat reached the top of the pole. It was so happy! The cat said, "Thank you, big dog! You helped me and made the climb delay easier." The dog smiled and said, "You're welcome, little cat. We can do anything when we help each other." They both walked away, happy and proud.

Once upon a time, there were two best friends, Brad and Penny. They loved to play together every day. One day, Brad wanted to join Penny's game. 
Unfortunately, Brad felt dizzy and could not join. He asked Penny for help, but she kept playing without him. Brad felt sad and frustrated.
Suddenly, Brad had an idea! He decided to take a rest and then he would be ready to join. He lay down and closed his eyes for five minutes. After that, he felt better.
"Thank you for waiting for me!" said Brad to Penny. He was now ready to join the play. 
Penny smiled and the two friends had a great time playing together. The end!

Once upon a time, there was a lonely lime. The lime lived in a big tree with many other fruits. The other fruits would play and have fun, but the lonely lime was too shy to join them.
One day, a little girl came to the tree to watch the fruits. She saw the lonely lime and felt sad for it. The little girl wanted to help the lime make friends.
The girl picked up the lonely lime and took it to the other fruits. She said, "This lime wants to play too!" The other fruits smiled and let the lime join their fun games. The lonely lime was not lonely anymore, and they all played happily together.

Once upon a time there was a fat puppy. The fat puppy was owned by a small girl called Jo. Jo loved the puppy and they played together every day.
One day when Jo was playing with the puppy, she asked the puppy if it wanted to go for a walk in the park. The puppy was very excited and started to bounce around the room.
Just then, Jo's mother came into the room and saw the puppy. She said no to Jo and told her that the fat puppy was not allowed to go to the park.
Jo and the puppy were very sad. Jo asked her mother why she couldn't take her puppy for a walk. Her mother explained that the park was for people not for pets.
Jo thought for a moment, then she had a great idea. She asked her mother if she could take the puppy for a walk on the sidewalk instead. Her mother agreed and Jo and the fat puppy were very happy. They went for a long, happy walk on the sidewalk.

Once upon a time, there was a big, scary monster. He lived in a dark cave. The monster was very proud of how scary he was. He liked to scare all the little animals in the forest.
One day, the monster did something very bad. He took a little bird's food. The little bird was very sad and hungry. The other animals in the forest did not like what the monster did. They wanted to punish the monster for being mean.
So, the animals made a plan. They all worked together to make the monster feel sorry. They took away his favorite toy. The monster was very sad. He said sorry to the little bird and gave back the food. The monster learned to be nice and not to be proud. The animals were happy, and they all played together.

Tom and Lily like to play in the backyard. They see many bugs and animals there. One day, they see a lizard on the wall. It is green and has a long tail.
"Look, a lizard!" Tom says. "Let's race it!"
"How do we race a lizard?" Lily asks.
"We run next to it and see who is faster!" Tom says. He runs to the wall and tries to catch the lizard.
The lizard is very fast. It runs away from Tom and jumps to another wall. Tom follows it, but he cannot catch it.
Lily laughs. She thinks Tom is funny. She runs after him and the lizard.
"Tom, you cannot race a lizard!" she says. "It is too weird!"
"Weird? Why is it weird?" Tom asks.
"Because it has no legs, only feet!" Lily says. "And it has no hair, only scales!"
Tom stops and looks at the lizard. He sees that Lily is right. The lizard is very different from them.
"Maybe you are right," he says. "The lizard is weird. But it is also cool!"
He smiles and waves at the lizard. The lizard looks at him and flicks its tongue.
"Maybe it likes us," Lily says.
"Maybe," Tom says. "Do you want to be friends with the lizard?"
"Yes!" Lily says. "Let's give it a name!"
They think for a while. Then they say together:
"Let's call it Zip!"

Lila and Tom were playing on the farm. They liked to run and jump and see the animals. They saw the wheat that grew tall and yellow in the field. Lila wanted to make a crown of wheat for her doll. She picked some wheat and tried to tie it together.
"Look, Tom, I made a crown!" she said, holding up the wheat.
But Tom was upset. He wanted to make a crown too, but he did not know how to tie. He tried to copy Lila, but his wheat fell apart.
"I can't tie, Lila, I can't make a crown!" he said, throwing the wheat on the ground.
Lila saw that Tom was upset. She felt sorry for him. She wanted to help him.
"Don't be upset, Tom, I can show you how to tie. It's easy, you just cross and loop and pull. Here, let me help you." she said, picking up some wheat and giving it to Tom.
She held his hands and showed him how to tie the wheat. Tom followed her steps and soon he had a crown too.
"Wow, Lila, you are so smart! Thank you for showing me how to tie. Now we both have crowns!" he said, smiling and putting the crown on his head.
Lila smiled too. She was happy that Tom was happy. She put the crown on her doll and hugged Tom.
"You're welcome, Tom, you are so kind. Now we can play king and queen of the farm!" she said, laughing and running with Tom.

Jack and his mom were walking in the park. Jack saw a big window and wanted to play there. "Please can I go play at the window?" he asked his mom. His mom smiled and said, "Yes Jack, you can. Just be obedient, okay?"
Jack ran to the window and looked out. He saw lots of birds flying in the sky. It was so much fun! He stood there for a long time and watched the birds.
Suddenly, he heard his mom calling from the park. She was saying his name. "Jack, please come back here now!"
Jack knew that his mom was asking nicely, so he ran back obediently. He gave his mom a big hug and said "Thank you for letting me play." His mom smiled and said, "You're welcome."

Once upon a time, there was a brave boy named Tim. Tim was going to a fun party. He put on his red hat and ran to the party with a big smile.
At the party, Tim saw his friend, Sam. Sam was playing with a toy car. "Can I play too?" asked Tim. "Yes, let's play together!" said Sam. They played and laughed, having so much fun.
But then, the toy car went very fast and hit the wall. It broke into pieces. Tim and Sam were sad. "Don't worry," said Tim, "we can fix it." They worked together and fixed the toy car. They were brave and happy again. The party was fun, and everyone played together.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play together, but they also like to do things by themselves. They are independent.
One day, they go to a restaurant with their mom and dad. The restaurant has many different foods. The waiter gives them a menu. A menu is a paper with pictures and words of the foods.
Lily looks at the menu. She sees pizza, pasta, salad, and soup. She likes pizza. She points to the pizza and says, "I want pizza, please."
Ben looks at the menu. He sees burgers, fries, chicken, and fish. He likes burgers. He points to the burger and says, "I want burger, please."
Mom and dad smile. They are happy that Lily and Ben can choose their own foods. They are independent.
But then, mom sees something else on the menu. She sees a special dish. It is called lasagna. It is like pasta, but with cheese and sauce. Mom likes lasagna. She thinks Lily and Ben might like it too.
She suggests, "Why don't you try the lasagna? It is very yummy. You can share it with me and dad."
Lily and Ben look at the lasagna. They are curious. They have never tried it before. They nod and say, "Okay, we can try it."
Mom and dad order the lasagna and the pizza and the burger. They wait for the food. They talk and laugh. They are a happy family.
The food comes. It smells good. Lily and Ben try the lasagna. They like it. It is cheesy and saucy. They say, "Thank you, mom, for suggesting the lasagna. It is very yummy."
Mom and dad smile. They are proud of Lily and Ben. They are independent, but they are also willing to try new things. They are good twins.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely cat named Mimi. Mimi lived in a small house with a big frame on the wall. In the frame was a picture of a happy cat family. Mimi would look at the picture and wish she had a family too.
One day, Mimi heard a knock on her door. She opened it and saw a little mouse. The mouse said, "Hi, I am Tim! I am lonely too. Can we be friends?" Mimi was surprised but happy. She said, "Yes, let's be friends!"
Mimi and Tim played all day. They had so much fun! Then, they looked at the big frame on the wall. Suddenly, the picture started to change. The happy cat family began to vanish! In its place, a new picture appeared. It was a picture of Mimi and Tim, smiling and happy. They were not lonely anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to make dough with her mom. They would put water, flour, and a little salt in a big bowl. Sue liked to mix it all up with her hands. It was fun to play with the dough.
One day, Sue wanted to make dough all by herself. She put too much water in the bowl. The dough was not regular. It was too wet and sticky. Sue was sad. She did not know what to do.
Sue's mom came to help her. She showed Sue how to fix the dough by adding more flour. Sue learned that when she made dough, she needed to be careful with how much water she put in. The moral of the story is to always be careful and ask for help when you need it.

Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Tim. Tim liked to play with his ball made of leather. One sunny day, Tim went to the park to play with his ball.
As Tim played, he saw a big tree. He kicked his ball high, and it got stuck in the tree. Tim was sad. He tried to get the ball down, but he was too small.
Then, something unexpected happened. A squirrel came and pushed the ball out of the tree. But it was not the same ball! It was a new ball made of leather. The old ball was left in the tree to rot. Tim was happy and played with the new ball. And the squirrel became his new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a fair little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and gaze at the pretty flowers in her garden. One day, Lily saw a big, red butterfly. She wanted to catch it and show her mom.
Lily ran inside to get a plate. She thought the butterfly could sit on the plate while she showed her mom. The butterfly saw the plate and sat on it. Lily was very happy.
Lily took the plate with the butterfly to her mom. Her mom smiled and said, "What a pretty butterfly!" Lily felt proud and let the butterfly go back to the flowers. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little dog named Max went for a walk. It was a wet day, and Max loved to play in the puddles. He would jump and splash, making his fur all wet. Max was having so much fun!
As Max walked, he saw a big red ball. "What a surprise!" he thought. Max loved to play with balls. He ran to the ball and bit it. The ball went "pop" and Max jumped back.
Max was sad that the ball was gone, but he kept walking. Soon, he found a new surprise. It was a wet, green frog. Max played with the frog, and they both had fun in the puddles. Max was happy again, and he had a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to paint. He was very creative. One day, he found some old pants in his room. He had an idea. He wanted to paint the pants and make them beautiful.
Tim got his paint and brush. He painted the pants with lots of colors. He made red, blue, and green spots on them. He was very happy with his work. "Look, Mom!" he said, "I made my pants pretty!"
His mom came to see. She smiled at Tim. "You are so creative," she said. "I love your painted pants. Let's show them to your friends." Tim and his mom went outside. They showed the pants to all his friends. Everyone loved Tim's creative pants.

One day, a troubled driver named Tom was driving his big car. He was going to the store to buy some food. He was sad because he lost his hat. He looked everywhere but could not find it. Tom really liked his hat because it was red and big.
On the way to the store, Tom saw a little boy named Tim. Tim was holding a red hat. Tom stopped the car and asked, "Is that my hat?" Tim looked at the hat and said, "Yes, I found it on the road." Tom was very happy. He said, "Thank you, Tim!" Tim gave the hat back to Tom.
Tom put the hat back on his head and drove to the store. He bought food and some toys for Tim. When Tom got back, he gave the toys to Tim. Tim was very happy and said, "Thank you, Tom!" They both smiled and became good friends. From that day on, Tom was not troubled anymore because he had his hat and a new friend.

Once upon a time there was a boy named Tom. Tom was eager to go outside and play a game. He put on his hat, grabbed his swing and a ball, and headed out to the golf course.
At the golf course, Tom had lots of fun. He hit the ball with his swing, and tried his best to get it in the hole. Sometimes he was successful, and sometimes the ball would go too far away.
Tom was so eager that he didn't want to leave the golf course. He kept playing and playing until it was getting dark outside. Then his mom said it was time to go home.
So Tom put his swing and ball in his bag, and went home. At home, he put his hat and bag away, and went to bed. Before he fell asleep, he thought about all the fun he had playing golf. He was eager to go back and play again!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to open things. One day, she found a big, brilliant object in her room. It was a box with a big bow on it. She was so excited and wanted to open it right away.
Lucy called her friends Tom and Sue to come over and see the object. They all gathered around the box, wondering what was inside. They each took turns trying to open it, but it was very hard to do. They pulled and pulled, but the box would not open.
Finally, Lucy's mom came into the room and saw them struggling. She told the children that the box would only open if they worked together. So, Lucy, Tom, and Sue held hands and pulled the bow together. The box opened, and out came a big, beautiful rainbow. The children learned that when they worked together, they could do anything. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, a big dog named Max and a little cat named Luna were the best of friends. They lived in a small house near the woods. One day, they had a fight about who was the fastest runner. They were so mad at each other that they started a war.
Max said, "I am the fastest, and I will win this war!" Luna replied, "No, I am the fastest, and I will win!" They ran and ran, trying to beat each other. But as they ran, they felt sad. They missed being friends and playing together.
After a long time, Max and Luna stopped running. They both felt regret for starting the war. They said sorry to each other and decided to be friends again. To celebrate, they went to a small shop and bought a big, spicy pizza to share. They laughed and played together, happy to be friends again. And they never fought about who was the fastest runner again.

Once upon a time, there was a little ant. The ant was very small. He wanted to grow big and strong. Every day, he ate food and played with his ant friends. They all wanted to grow too. They were ready to be big ants.
One day, the little ant found a big leaf. The leaf was very green and yummy. He thought, "If I eat this leaf, I will grow so big!" So, the little ant started to eat the leaf. He ate and ate and ate. He was very happy.
But then, something unexpected happened. The little ant did not grow big! Instead, he turned green just like the leaf. His ant friends were so surprised. They laughed and laughed. The little green ant was sad. But soon, he realized that being green was special. He was the only green ant in his family. So, the little green ant played with his friends and was happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a little duck named Ducky. Ducky was very excited because he was going to bathe in the big pond for the first time. He had seen his friends do it, and he knew it would be a success.
Ducky ran to the pond and jumped in. He started to bathe and splash water all around. He was having so much fun. But then, something unexpected happened. A big frog jumped out of the water and said, "Hi, Ducky!"
Ducky was surprised and a little scared. But the big frog just wanted to be friends. They played and splashed in the water together. Ducky was happy that he had a new friend. His first time in the big pond was a success, and he couldn't wait to come back and play with his new friend again.

Once upon a time, there was a humble dog named Max. Max had a toy hose that he loved to play with. One day, Max saw a big cat named Tom. Tom wanted to play with Max's hose, but Max did not want to share.
Max called his friend, a small bird named Sam. Sam told Max that sharing is good and that they could all play together. Max thought about it and decided to give it a try. They played with the hose, and Max saw that sharing was fun.
The next day, Max, Tom, and Sam played with the hose again. They had lots of fun together. Max learned that sharing is nice, and it made him happy. The friends played together every day, and they all learned to share and be happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boat. The boat liked to sail on the water. One day, the boat saw a big hill. The boat wanted to sail up the hill.
A loyal dog lived on the hill. The dog liked to help. The dog saw the boat and wanted to help it sail up the hill. The dog pushed the boat with its nose. The boat went up, up, up the hill.
But the hill was too big. The boat and the dog could not go up anymore. They tried and tried. The boat and the dog were sad. They could not go back down the hill. The boat and the dog were stuck on the hill. The end.

Ben and Lily were playing in the park. They liked to examine the flowers, the bugs and the rocks. They learned many things from their games.
One day, they saw a new thing in the park. It was a big red ball. It was shiny and round. They wanted to touch it and play with it.
"Look, Lily, a red ball!" Ben said.
"Wow, Ben, it's so pretty!" Lily said.
They ran to the ball and examined it. It was soft and bouncy. They pushed it and kicked it. They laughed and had fun.
But then, a boy came to the ball. He was older and bigger than Ben and Lily. He was not happy to see them. He was angry and mean.
"Hey, you two, get away from my ball!" he shouted.
"But we just want to play with it!" Ben said.
"It's mine, not yours! Go find your own toys!" he said.
He grabbed the ball and pushed Ben and Lily away. He glared at them and ran away.
Ben and Lily were sad and scared. They did not like the boy. He was not nice. He did not share.
They hugged each other and walked back to their mom. She saw their faces and asked them what happened.
They told her about the red ball and the boy. She listened and comforted them.
"I'm sorry, Ben and Lily. Some people are not kind. They don't know how to share and be friends. But you do. You are good and sweet. You can always play with each other and with me. I love you both very much." she said.
She kissed them and gave them a hug. She took them to the swings and pushed them gently. They smiled and felt better. They forgot about the red ball and the boy. They enjoyed the park and their mom. They were happy and safe.

Once upon a time, there was a happy yellow bird. He sung all day from morning to night. His songs were so sweet and beautiful! 
Every morning the bird would wake up and sing the sun into the sky. It was his special way of saying good morning. All the other animals in the woods loved to hear his voice and they would join in and sing with him. 
The birds songs gave the animals a lot of joy. The birds singing always seemed to make them feel happy. 
One day, the yellow bird left his home in the woods and headed to the city to sing even more songs. Everywhere he went, he was welcomed and cheered by lots of people. Everywhere the bird went, he made people feel happy. 
The yellow bird sang until the sun set and it was time for him to go home. He had had the best day ever, singing his songs to everyone.
The End.

Once upon a time, there was a thoughtful caterpillar named Cathy. Cathy liked to help her friends in the garden. She had many friends like birds, bees, and butterflies.
One hot day, Cathy saw a thirsty flower. The flower needed water. Cathy wanted to help the flower. She found a small cup and poured water on the flower. The flower felt better and thanked Cathy.
Cathy felt happy to help her friend. She went around the garden and poured water on other thirsty flowers too. All the flowers loved Cathy. They called her a thoughtful helper. Cathy the caterpillar was proud to be a friend to everyone in the garden.

Once upon a time, there were two brothers named Joe and Jerry. Joe was always honest and cared about others. He would help anyone in need.
Jerry was different. He liked to twist the truth. He liked to have things his way and no one else.
One day, Joe and Jerry were out for a walk. Suddenly, a stranger stepped out in front of them and asked for help. He said he was lost and didn't know what to do.
Joe quickly offered his help. He asked the stranger which way he should go and tried his best to make him feel better.
Jerry saw an opportunity. He told the stranger to go the wrong way. He wanted the stranger to be thankful to him and not Joe.
But the stranger noticed Jerry's twist and left feeling sad and disappointed.
Joe looked at Jerry and said, "You can't go around twisting the truth to get what you want. That's not how we should treat people, especially those who need our help."
Jerry realized his mistake and decided to be more honest. He listened to Joe and tried his best to help people in need from then on.
The moral of the story is that being honest is always the best and that we should always be kind to others, especially when they are in need of help.

Once, there was a little girl named Emily. She was very cheerful, and had a special place in her heart for the outdoors. One day, she went out to pick some grapes from the vine in her backyard.
As Emily was picking the grapes, she noticed a small bird on the ground. The bird had a broken wing. "Oh no!" She cried. "How can I help you?"
The small bird didn't know how to answer, so Emily took matters into her own hands. She gently covered the bird with both of her hands, like a blanket, and whispered sweet words to the bird. She tried to comfort it and hoped it could heal its wings.
Finally, the little bird flew off into the sky. Emily beamed with pride and joy. She realized that by being kind and gentle, she was able to help someone in need.
The moral of the story is that kindness and compassion can go a long way, even in the smallest of moments. From that day on, Emily chose to be cheerful and cover those in need with kindness.

Anna likes to play with her toy cricket. She calls him Bob and makes him hop and sing. One day, she sees a real cricket in the garden. She runs to catch it, but it is too fast.
"Wait, cricket, wait!" she says. "I want to be your friend!"
The cricket stops and looks at Anna. He is small and green, with long legs and wings. He is not afraid of Anna, but he is not sure if he wants to play with her.
"Why do you want to be my friend?" he asks.
"Because you are like Bob, but real," Anna says. "You can hop and sing, and I can hop and sing too. We can have fun together."
The cricket thinks for a moment. He likes to hop and sing, but he also likes to be free and explore. He does not want to be in a cage or a jar.
"OK, we can be friends, but only if you let me go when I want to," he says.
"OK, OK," Anna says. "I will let you go. But can I hold you for a little bit?"
The cricket agrees. He lets Anna pick him up gently and put him on her hand. He feels her warm skin and her soft fingers. He tickles her with his antennae and makes a chirping sound.
Anna giggles and tries to copy his sound. She makes a high-pitched noise that sounds like a squeak. The cricket laughs and tries to copy her sound. He makes a low-pitched noise that sounds like a grunt.
They make funny noises and laugh together. They are happy and forget about everything else. They are friends.

Jeoby and his mom were walking down the street holding hands. It was a sunny day and Jeoby felt enthusiastic. Suddenly a bright purple bicycle flew by them. Jeoby's eyes lit up as he exclaimed "wow!" His mom laughed and she said, "I wish I could ride a bike like that". Jeoby smiled and said, "I can learn to ride it, I'm sure I can!" His mom smiled and said, "Well we will have to see."
Suddenly, a little girl with long brown hair came out of the bike shop and hopped onto the bike. She waved goodbye to her mom and rode off. Jeoby watched her with a hopeful look in his eyes.
When he turned to his mom to complain, she pulled him into a warm hug. She said, "Don't worry Jeoby, we can practice riding a bike soon. Just be patient and enthusiastic." Jeoby smiled. He knew his mother was right and he hugged her back.

Once upon a time, there was a busy farm. On this farm, there were many animals and plants. One day, a little bunny named Ben hopped through the farm. He saw a cauliflower patch and decided to take a look.
Ben hopped over to his friend, the wise owl named Olly. "Olly, how do I take this cauliflower home?" asked Ben. Olly thought for a moment and said, "You can fold the big leaves around the cauliflower and carry it in your mouth."
Ben went back to the cauliflower patch. He carefully folded the big leaves around the cauliflower. Then, he picked it up with his mouth and hopped back to his home. His family was very happy to see the cauliflower. They all enjoyed a yummy meal together. And from that day on, Ben knew how to carry cauliflower home.

Once upon a time, there was a hero named Tim. Tim was eager to help everyone in his town. One day, he met a little girl named Sally. Sally was sad because she lost her toy.
"Can you tell me what your toy looks like?" asked Tim. Sally told Tim that her toy was small and green. Tim wanted to help Sally find her toy.
They looked everywhere for the toy. Suddenly, they found a big green toy instead of the small one. "This is not my toy," said Sally, "but I like it more!" Tim and Sally were surprised and happy. They played with the big green toy all day long.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. Tim saw an amazing big tree. He wanted to know how tall it was. Tim said, "Mom, I want to measure the tree."
His mom gave him a long string to measure the tree. Tim walked around the tree with the string. He counted one, two, three steps. Tim said, "Mom, the tree is three steps big!"
Mom smiled and said, "Good job, Tim! That's a big tree!" They both laughed and played under the amazing tree. Tim was happy he could measure the tree all by himself.

Once upon a time, there was a big wardrobe in a little boy's room. The boy was scared of the wardrobe. He thought there was a monster inside it. Every night, the boy heard a loud roar coming from the wardrobe. The roar made him very scared, and he could not sleep.
One day, the boy told his mom about the roar. His mom said, "Don't be stupid, there is no monster in the wardrobe." She opened the wardrobe to show him. Inside, there was a big, fluffy cat. The cat was the one making the roar! The boy laughed and felt silly for being scared.
From that day, the boy learned not to be scared of things without knowing what they really were. He understood that sometimes, things are not as scary as they seem. The boy played with the cat and they became best friends. The moral of the story is, don't be afraid of things without knowing the truth.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to bake. His mom helped him bake cookies, cakes, and pies. They were very good at baking together.
One day, Tim and his mom went to the library. The library had many books. Tim found a cheap book about baking. The book had pictures of cookies, cakes, and pies. Tim was very happy to find the book.
Tim and his mom took the cheap baking book home. They baked cookies, cakes, and pies from the book. All their friends loved the treats. Tim and his mom were happy they found the cheap baking book at the library.

Once upon a time, there was a mule named Max. Max liked to wear a big, red hat. One day, Max went for a walk in the woods.
In the woods, Max met a scary bear. The bear saw Max's big, red hat and said, "Why do you wear that hat, Max?"
Max said, "I wear the hat because it is fun and makes me happy." The scary bear looked at Max and said, "Can I try it on?"
Max let the scary bear wear the big, red hat. The bear was not scary anymore. He was happy too. From that day on, Max and the bear became best friends and they wore big, red hats together.

Once upon a time, a bald man lived in a small house. He was not happy in the house. He wanted a big tent to live in. He thought a tent would make him happy.
One day, the bald man saw a big tent in a shop. He wanted the tent very much. He bought the tent and took it home. He set up the tent in his yard. He was very happy.
But then, a big storm came. The wind blew hard, and the rain fell fast. The tent was not strong. The storm broke the tent. The bald man was sad. He wanted his small house back. But it was too late. The house was gone. The bald man was not happy.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom loved to run and play. One sunny day, Tom went to the park with his friends. They were all very happy.
While playing, Tom saw a big trip. He wanted to go on the trip with his friends. They walked and walked until they were very tired. Tom felt dizzy and fell down. His friends helped him get up and made sure he was okay.
Tom learned that it is important to be careful when going on trips. He also learned that friends are always there to help when you fall. From that day on, Tom and his friends always looked out for each other and played safely.

Once upon a time there was an old lollipop. It was a very, very old lollipop. Every day it just sat and waited. No one ever ate it, so it stayed all old and hard. 
One day, a little girl found the lollipop. She was only three years old. She tried to eat the lollipop, but it was too hard. Then she had an idea!
She took the lollipop to her mommy. She said, "Mommy, I have an old lollipop. Can you help me restore it?" Her mom smiled, and said yes!
Mommy put the lollipop in a pot of boiling water. After a few minutes, she took it out. It was soft and perfect. The little girl was so happy. 
She thanked her mommy and ran off with her lollipop. "Yum!" she said, and enjoyed the sweet treat. 
The old lollipop was finally restored and everyone was happy. The End.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a girl named Lily. Lily loved to take sugar and put it on her toast. She liked her toast very sweet. One day, Lily went to the kitchen to take some sugar, but she saw that the sugar was gone!
Lily was sad and went outside to find her sugar. She walked and walked until she found a wild bunny. The wild bunny had her sugar! Lily was surprised. The wild bunny looked at her and said, "I took your sugar, but I will give it back if you play with me."
Lily and the wild bunny played together all day long. They had so much fun. When it was time to go home, the wild bunny gave Lily her sugar back. Lily was happy, and they both became best friends. From that day on, Lily shared her sugar with the wild bunny, and they played together every day.

Once upon a time there was a friendly bear. He liked to walk around the lake and rub the trees. One day while he was rubbing the trees, he heard a noise coming from the lake. He stopped and looked around, but he couldn't find anything. Then the noise started again. He listened carefully and then he saw a little duck swimming in circles in the lake. He was so friendly that he decided to help the little duck.
The bear went into the lake and started to rub the duck's back. Soon, the duck felt much better and was able to swim around the lake again. The bear waved goodbye to the friendly duck and walked away.
From that day on, the bear kept visiting the lake to see the little duck. Every time he went, the duck came closer and closer to the shore. They were soon both happily swimming around the lake and rubbing each other's backs.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Tim. Tim lived in a small, tight cage. One day, Tim heard a noise outside his cage. 
''What's that?'' Tim thought to himself. 
He looked around the corner to see what the noise was. It was a big, swinging door.
''Maybe I can escape!" Tim thought with excitement. 
He flapped his wings and hopped onto the edge of the cage. He began to peck at the latch, but it was too tight. 
Suddenly, Tim heard a voice.
"Are you trying to escape, little bird?"
It was the owner of the cage. Tim tried to be polite.
"Yes sir, if I could get outside, I could fly away!"
The owner smiled.
"I think it's time you left this tight little cage."
He opened the latch and Tim flew out of the cage. He was free!

Once upon a time, in a bright park, there was a little pigeon. The pigeon had many friends who played with him every day. They liked to fly and eat together.
One day, the pigeon saw a little girl. The girl was sad because she lost her toy. The pigeon wanted to help her. So, he went to his friends and requested their help to find the girl's toy.
The pigeon and his friends looked everywhere in the park. They found the toy under a big tree. The girl was very happy to have her toy back. She thanked the pigeon and his friends. From that day on, the girl would come to the park to play with the pigeon and his friends. They all had fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a bald man. He liked to play outside in the sun. One day, he went to the park to have fun.
While playing, he saw a big red ball. He wanted to play with it. He kicked the ball, but it did not move. The man was confused.
Suddenly, the big red ball opened its mouth and bit the man's leg. It was not a ball, it was a big, round, red animal! The man was surprised, but he was okay. He learned to be careful and not to play with things he did not know.

Once upon a time, in a small country, there was a regular girl named Amy. She lived with her mom, dad, and dog, Buddy. They had a happy home, and they played together every day.
One day, Amy did something wrong. She took a cookie from the jar without asking her mom. Her mom saw her and said, "Amy, you must not take cookies without asking." Mom had to punish Amy. She told Amy to sit in her room and think about what she did.
Amy was sad. She knew she did something wrong. She sat in her room and thought about it. After a while, she went to her mom and said, "Mom, I am sorry. I should have asked for the cookie." Mom hugged Amy and said, "I forgive you, Amy. Next time, just ask." Amy learned her lesson, and from that day on, she always asked before taking a cookie.

Once upon a time, in an ordinary little town, there lived a girl named Mia. Mia loved to study the stars in the sky. Every night, she would look up at the universe and wonder what was out there.
One day, Mia met a new friend named Tom. "I study the stars too," said Tom. "Let's study the universe together!" So, they decided to study the stars and learn about the universe every night.
But one night, while they were studying the stars, a big storm came. The wind blew hard and the rain fell fast. Mia and Tom were scared and ran home. They could not study the universe anymore. The storm was too strong and their books were all wet. They were sad because they could not study the stars and the universe anymore.

Sara was a child who liked to play with dolls. She had many dolls, but her favorite was Lily. Lily had long hair, a pretty dress, and a smile on her face. Sara loved to brush Lily's hair and make it into braids or buns.
One day, Sara's cousin Ben came to visit. Ben was a boy who liked to play with cars and trucks. He did not like dolls at all. He thought they were boring and ugly. He saw Sara playing with Lily and said, "What are you doing with that ugly doll? She looks like a rat!"
Sara was very angry and sad. She said, "She is not ugly, she is beautiful! And she is not a rat, she is my friend! Go away and leave us alone!"
But Ben did not go away. He grabbed Lily from Sara and said, "I will show you what I can do with your ugly doll!" He started to twist Lily's hair and pull it out. He twisted her arms and legs and bent them in wrong ways. He twisted her head and made it face backwards. He twisted her dress and tore it. He laughed and said, "Look at your ugly doll now! She is even uglier than before!"
Sara cried and screamed. She tried to get Lily back, but Ben was stronger and faster. He threw Lily on the floor and kicked her. He said, "This is what I do with ugly dolls! I twist them and break them!"
Sara ran to her mom and told her what Ben did. Her mom was very angry and sad too. She scolded Ben and made him apologize. She said, "You should not twist and break other people's toys. That is very mean and rude. How would you like it if someone twisted and broke your cars and trucks?"
Ben said, "I would not like it. I would be very angry and sad too. I am sorry, Sara. I did not mean to hurt you or your doll. I was just playing."
Sara said, "Playing is not twisting and breaking. Playing is sharing and having fun. You hurt me and my doll very much. You made her ugly and broken. I don't want to play with you anymore."
Ben said, "I am sorry, Sara. Please forgive me. Can I help you fix your doll? Maybe we can find some glue and tape and scissors and make her pretty again."
Sara thought for a while. She was still angry and sad, but she also felt sorry for Ben. She said, "Okay, Ben. You can help me fix my doll. But you have to be gentle and careful. And you have to say sorry to Lily too."
Ben said, "Okay, Sara. I will be gentle and careful. And I will say sorry to Lily too. I hope she can be your friend again."
Sara and Ben went to find some glue and tape and scissors. They worked together to fix Lily's hair and arms and legs and head and dress. They made her look as pretty as before. Ben said, "I am sorry, Lily. I was very mean and rude to you. You are not ugly, you are beautiful. And you are not a rat, you are a friend. Please forgive me."
Lily did not say anything, because she was a doll. But Sara smiled and said, "Thank you, Ben. I think Lily forgives you. And I forgive you too. Maybe we can play together again. But not with dolls. Maybe with cars and trucks. Or something else."
Ben smiled and said, "Thank you, Sara. I think that would be nice. Maybe we can play together again. But not with cars and trucks. Maybe with something else. Or maybe with dolls. But only if you want to."
Sara and Ben hugged and said sorry and thank you. They learned to be kind and respectful to each other and to their toys. They learned that playing is not twisting and breaking. Playing is sharing and having fun.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a microscope in his room. He was very happy. He wanted to see small things with it. He looked at his toy, his shirt, and even his hand. The microscope made everything look big!
Tim went to show his friend, Sue. Sue was frightened when she saw the big toy. "What is that?" she asked. Tim said, "It's a microscope! It makes things look big!" Sue wanted to try it too. She looked at her doll, her shoe, and even her hair. She was not frightened anymore. She was happy too.
Then, they heard a noise. It was a surprise! It was Tim's little sister, Amy. She wanted to play with the microscope too. Tim and Sue let Amy try it. They all looked at things together and had fun. They were not frightened anymore. They were all happy with their new microscope.

One day, a big hairy dog named Sam went to play outside. He found a small box on the ground. Sam was very curious and wanted to see if he could fit inside the box. He tried to put his front legs in first, but they did not fit. Then, he tried to put his back legs in, but they did not fit either. Sam was sad because he could not fit in the box.
Sam's friend, a little girl named Sue, came over to play. She saw Sam looking sad and asked, "Sam, why are you sad?" Sam barked and showed her the box. Sue had an idea. She went inside her house and brought out a bar of soap.
Sue rubbed the soap on Sam's legs to make them slippery. Then, she tried to help Sam fit in the box again. This time, Sam's legs slid right in! Sam was so happy that he wagged his tail and barked. But, just as Sam started to enjoy being in the box, the box began to shake and grow bigger. 
To Sam and Sue's surprise, the box turned into a big and beautiful house. It was a magic box! Sam and Sue played inside the new house all day long. They were very happy and amazed by the unexpected magic.

Tim and Sam were brothers who liked to play and have fun. One day, their mom cooked rice and beans for lunch. Tim loved rice and beans, but Sam did not. He wanted pizza or burgers instead.
"Sam, eat your rice and beans. They are good for you and fill your tummy," mom said.
"No, I don't like them. They are yucky and cheap. I want pizza or burgers. They are yummy and cool," Sam said.
He pushed his plate away and made a face. Mom was sad and angry. She said, "Sam, you are being rude and ungrateful. Rice and beans are not cheap. They are what we can afford and what many people eat. Pizza and burgers are not always good for you and they cost a lot of money. You should be happy and thankful for what you have."
Tim ate his rice and beans and said, "Mom, I like them. They are tasty and warm. Thank you for cooking for us."
Mom smiled and said, "You are welcome, Tim. You are being kind and grateful. I am proud of you."
Sam felt bad and ashamed. He saw that Tim was happy and mom was happy. He realized that he was wrong and selfish. He said, "Mom, I am sorry. I was being rude and ungrateful. I will try to eat my rice and beans. They are not yucky and cheap. They are what you made for us and what many people eat. I should be happy and thankful for what I have."
Mom hugged him and said, "I forgive you, Sam. You are being brave and humble. I love you."
Sam ate his rice and beans and said, "Mom, I love you too. Thank you for cooking for us."
The moral of the story is: We should not resist what is good for us and what we have. We should be happy and thankful for what we have and not want what we don't have. We should be kind and grateful to our mom and others who care for us.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a famous house. This house had a tall chimney. Every day, a little bird would sit on the chimney and sing.
One sunny day, a funny clown came to the town. The clown made everyone laugh with his jokes and tricks. The little bird on the chimney saw the clown and wanted to make the town laugh too.
The next day, the little bird thought of a plan. It flew to the chimney and started to sing a funny song. The town people heard the bird's song and began to laugh. The little bird became famous, just like the clown, and they both made the town a happy place to live.

Once there was a boy named Billy. He was only three years old but he was very creative. He loved to make things using whatever materials he could find.
One day, he found an old rake in the shed. He thought it would be fun to play with, so he decided to take it out into the garden.
Billy spent the morning making a design with the rake in the grass - a circle with spokes coming out from it. It looked beautiful when it was finished.
But sadly, he was too creative and too strong; the rake suddenly broke!
Billy was very sad about his broken rake but he was sure he could find a way to fix it.
He looked around for ideas, and he soon found some sticks and some string from the shed.
He carefully used the sticks and the string to tie the pieces of the rake back together, and soon it was as good as new!
Billy was very pleased with his creative repair job and he proudly went back to playing in the garden with his rake.

Once upon a time, there was a naughty dog named Spot. Spot liked to play with things he should not. One day, Spot found a pair of scissors on the floor. He picked them up with his mouth and started running around the house.
Spot's mom saw him with the scissors and shouted, "Spot, stop! That is not a toy!" But Spot did not listen. He kept running and playing. Then, he tripped on a toy and fell. The scissors poked his paw and Spot started to suffer.
Spot's mom came to help him. She took the scissors away and hugged him. She said, "You must not play with things that can hurt you, Spot." Spot learned his lesson and promised to be a good dog from that day on.

Once upon a time, there lived a chubby little bird. He had bright blue feathers, and was always singing.
One day, the little bird flew to a magical land. Everything was sparkly and the ground was made of crystal. He was so happy!
But then, something strange happened. He saw a giant cloud and it was raining crystals. The little bird gasped. How could this be happening?
He flew even closer and then realised why it was raining crystals - someone was crying! The little bird flew towards her and asked: "Why are you crying?".
The girl replied: "I'm worried about my crystal. Someone stole it from me and I want it back!". The little bird was so sorry for the girl. He wanted to help her find her crystal.
So, he decided to look for the crystal with her. Together they searched and searched, until suddenly they found it. The little bird and the girl hugged each other in joy. What an amazing thing had happened!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to throw a ball. One day, he met a girl named Sue. Sue liked to throw a ball too. They decided to play together.
Tim said, "Let's see who can throw the ball the farthest!" Sue agreed. They both wanted to win. They were competitive. They took turns throwing the ball. 
Tim threw the ball very far. Sue threw the ball even farther. They spoke a fun language of laughter and cheers. Tim and Sue played all day. In the end, they both won because they had fun and made a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a playful dog named Spike. Spike liked to run and jump all day long. He was very happy and loved to play with his own toys.
One day, Spike found a big red ball in the yard. He wanted to play with it, so he pushed it with his nose. The ball rolled and bounced, and Spike chased it. He was having so much fun.
At the end of the day, Spike was very tired. He went back to his own bed and curled up with his favorite toy. He closed his eyes and dreamed of more playful days with his big red ball.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very adventurous. He loved to play and guess what was hiding behind trees and rocks. One day, Tim went to the park with his mom.
At the park, Tim saw a big tap. He wanted to play with it. He asked his mom if he could go near the tap. His mom said, "Be careful, Tim." Tim ran to the tap, excited to see what would happen.
Tim turned the tap and water came out. He was happy and played with the water. But then, Tim slipped on the wet ground. He fell and hurt his knee. Tim cried and his mom came to help him. They had to go home, and Tim was sad that his fun day at the park had a bad ending.

Once upon a time, there was a brave, adventurous little girl. She lived on a farm and loved to explore the fields. One day, as she was wandering around, she stumbled across a patch of herbs. She was curious and wanted to find out more about them, so she ignored the warnings from her parents and started to pick some.
The herbs smelled so good, and the little girl had so much fun exploring them. She picked some herbs and added them to her pocket. When she finally went back home, she showed them to her parents. Her parents were worried about her ignoring them, so they scolded her for not listening.
But even though she was sad at being scolded, the little girl never stopped being adventurous. She kept exploring the fields and picking herbs, but this time she made sure she told her parents where she was going. 
The end.

Once upon a time, in a big tree, there was an owl. The owl had big eyes and loved to fly at night. One day, the owl saw a small house. The owl wanted to see what was inside the house.
The owl flew to the house and entered through an open window. Inside the house, the owl saw a little girl. The little girl was sad because her toy was dead. The toy could not move or make noise anymore.
The owl wanted to help the little girl. It found a new toy for her. The little girl was very happy and thanked the owl. The owl flew away, happy that it could help the little girl.

Once upon a time, there was a little pupil named Tim. Tim liked to play with his harmless toy car. One day, while playing, Tim saw a big cat.
The big cat wanted to play too. But Tim did not let the cat play with his toy car. The cat was sad and went away. Tim felt bad for not letting the cat play.
The next day, Tim saw the big cat again. This time, Tim let the cat play with his toy car. They played together and had fun. Then, the big cat showed Tim a secret. The cat could talk! They became best friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time there was a boy. His name was Jim. He wanted to go swimming in the sea. So his mom and dad took him to the beach. 
Jim was very glad to go swimming. He could see the reef in the water. He put on his swimming gear and jumped in the water. 
Jim was so excited. He loved swimming around the reef. He saw all the fish and colourful plants. He was so happy. 
He swam around the reef for hours. When it was time to go, Jim was sad. But he knew he would go swimming again soon. He waved goodbye to the reef and smiled a big, glad smile.

Once upon a time there was a queen. She was very big and had lots of people helping her. Every day she managed lots of things, like taking care of her kingdom. She was very successful. One day, the queen had a brilliant idea. She wanted to paint a picture with lots of different colors. But she wasn't sure if she could manage it all on her own.
Luckily, the queen made a plan. She asked her helpers to get her lots of colors and some brushes. Then she started painting. She painted and painted, but it was hard work. But by the end of the day, she had managed to make a beautiful picture! Everyone was so impressed - it was the most successful painting they had ever seen. The queen was so happy and proud of herself.

Once upon a time there was a girl who was curious. She saw a mysterious candle and had to know what it was. She touched it and it turned on. Suddenly, the room got very dark and strange shadows appeared. The little girl was very scared!
The shadows moved around the room, getting closer and closer. The girl wanted to turn the candle off, but she didn't know how. No matter how hard she tried, nothing worked.
As the shadows got closer, the little girl started to cry. Then, out of the shadows came a scary monster and the little girl screamed. She ran away as fast as she could, leaving the mysterious candle behind. 
The little girl was never seen again. She was never able to turn off the candle, and the mysterious monsters she saw still haunt her dreams.

Once upon a time, there was a blue bird named Bella. Bella loved to sort things. She sorted big rocks, small rocks, and even leaves. One day, Bella saw a beautiful bird named Bobby. Bobby was very nice and they played together. They liked each other a lot.
Bella and Bobby decided to have a marriage. They invited all their friends to the big day. The sun was shining and everyone was very happy. Bella wore a pretty blue dress and Bobby wore a blue bow tie. They looked so nice together.
During the marriage, Bella and Bobby said, "I love you" to each other. Their friends clapped and cheered. After that, they had a big party. Bella and Bobby sorted all the food and toys for their friends to enjoy. They lived happily ever after.

Kim slid down the hill as fast as she could, but she was having such fun that she didn't even realise how quickly she was going. When she reached the bottom, she rolled around in the soft grass and laughed. She looked up at the hill and noticed the thick pine trees there, which blocked the sunlight from her.
Suddenly, a voice called out from behind her - it was her mom. "Kim, why did you slide so quickly? You should have taken it more slowly!"
Kim hung her head and felt guilty. "I'm sorry, mommy," she said. 
Her mom smiled and bent down until she was looking into Kim's eyes. "It's okay, wonderful Kim. Just remember to always be careful, okay?" 
Kim nodded and hugged her mom. She promised she would be more careful in the future. After their hug, Kim and her mom looked out over the valley. As they admired the pine trees, the sun came out from behind the clouds and cast a golden glow over the rolling hills. Kim smiled and held her mom's hand as they watched the beautiful sight together.

Once upon a time, there was a room. It was empty, but the walls were full of possibilities. The room wanted furniture to be organized so it could look nice.
But each time furniture was delivered it was very disorganized and disheveled. The room knew something had to be done. It kept asking the furniture to be neat and organized, but it never worked.
One day, the room got so frustrated that it worked hard to set up the furniture itself. It took a while, but the room was finally organized. The room felt happy and proud.
The moral of the story is that if you want something done, you have to do it yourself. No matter how hard it is, it will always be worth it in the end.

One day, a little dog named Max went for a walk in the park. He saw a big tree and wanted to touch it. Max ran to the tree and put his paw on the bark. He felt a little tremble. Max was not sure what it was, so he kept walking.
Max met a wise old cat named Luna. Max told her about the tremble he felt at the tree. Luna said, "Oh, that is serious. You must be careful. Something big will happen soon."
Later, Max saw a little bird stuck in the tree. The bird was scared and said, "Help me, please!" Max remembered what Luna said about the tremble. He knew this was the big thing she talked about. Max was brave and climbed the tree to help the bird. He saved the bird, and they became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a bell. He loved to throw the bell and hear it ring. One day, Tim went to a smelly place with lots of big bins.
In the smelly place, Tim saw a big, scary dog. The dog wanted to play with Tim's bell. Tim did not want the dog to have his bell. But the dog was fast and took the bell from Tim.
Tim was sad. He tried to get his bell back, but the dog ran away. The bell was gone forever. Tim went home and cried. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play ball. One day, he went to the park with his mom to play.
At the park, Tim saw a big pitch. He wanted to play on it. His mom said, "Let's prepare for the game. It will be easy and fun." Tim was very happy.
They played ball on the pitch. Tim threw the ball and his mom caught it. They had a lot of fun. Tim said, "This is easy!" They played until the sun went down. Then, they went home and had a good night's sleep.

Once upon a time there was a girl called Emma. Emma could speak many languages and was so incredible. Everyone admired her.
Then one day she met a little boy named Sam. Sam could not speak any of the languages Emma spoke. Emma tried to talk to Sam in different languages but it was no use. Sam just did not understand.
The two became good friends anyway and kept playing together. But one day some mean kids saw them. They wanted to make fun of Sam, so they started throwing stones at him.
Emma got so mad that she yelled at the mean kids and asked them to stop. The mean kids did not understand her language and got even more angry. They started throwing bigger stones at Emma and Sam.
But before anything bad could happen, the incredible Emma used her language skills and said something that made the mean kids stop and go away. Emma had saved the day!

The morning was lively for Abigail. She had woken up early and was ready for a day of fun with her family. Abigail quickly put on her summer dress and ran outside.
Everyone was getting ready to go to the beach. Abigail watched as Daddy sorted all the equipment they needed, the towels, the sun cream, buckets and spades. She then spotted her special bucket with a picture of a crab on it. 
Once everything was sorted, they all got into the car and set off. After a short drive, they arrived at the beach. Abigail couldn't believe it. She felt like she was in a dream.
The waves were crashing on the shore and everyone was laughing and playing. Abigail excitedly ran to grab her bucket and spade so she could start building a sandcastle.
By the end of the day, everybody was exhausted from the lively day at the beach. But Abigail was overjoyed and smiled from ear to ear as she snuggled in to the car for the journey home.

Once upon a time, there was a little bear named Ben. Ben loved honey more than anything else. One day, he found a big jar of honey near his home.
Ben was so happy, he started to eat the honey very slow. He wanted to make the honey last as long as he could. Ben ate and ate, but soon the honey started to disappear.
When the honey was all gone, Ben felt sad. He looked around and saw a bee. Ben decided to follow the bee to find more honey. The bee led him to a big tree with a beehive. Ben found more honey and was happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a funny parrot. The parrot liked to talk and play with kids. One day, the parrot met a little boy. The boy said, "Hi, parrot! Let's play together!"
The boy and the funny parrot played all day. They played with a ball, and they played hide and seek. The parrot was very happy, and the boy was happy too. They laughed and had a lot of fun. But then, it was time for the boy to go home.
The boy said, "Bye, funny parrot! Let's play again soon!" But the funny parrot did not want the boy to go. The parrot was sad and flew away. The boy looked for the parrot, but he could not find him. The boy was sad too, and he went home. The funny parrot and the boy never saw each other again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to hang his drawings on the wall. One day, he drew a big sun with a smile. He wanted to hang it up, but he saw a crack on the wall. Tim thought it was a stupid crack because it was in the way.
Tim asked his mom for help. She looked at the crack and smiled. She told him to wait and went away. Tim was sad because he thought his mom did not care about the crack. But then, something unexpected happened.
His mom came back with a big frame. She put the frame on the wall and covered the crack. Now Tim could hang his sun drawing and the crack was gone! Tim was so happy that he hugged his mom. The stupid crack was not a problem anymore, and Tim's room looked even better with the frame.

Once there was a clever swan. He lived on a big lake in a forest. He loved swimming and chirped happily at the other animals.
One day, a big storm came. The wind began to blow and lightning struck the lake. The swan was scared, but he spread out his wings wide and flew as fast as he could to find a safe place.
The swan flew for a long time until he found a cave. But when he went inside, he realized that the cave was already occupied by a very mean bear! The bear had sharp claws and sharp teeth and he wanted to eat the swan.
The swan was very scared, but he was also very clever. He had an idea. He started to sing a sweet song to the bear. The bear liked it so much that he forgot all about eating the swan. The swan was safe!
The bear and the swan became friends after that. They spent lots of time together and the bear always made sure the swan was safe.

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim found a card on the floor. The card had a big number on it. He was worried because he didn't know what the card was for.
Tim showed the card to his friend, Lily. She said, "I know what this card does! When you say 'increase,' the number on the card will grow bigger." Tim said, "Let's try it!" So they both said, "Increase!" The number on the card got bigger, just like Lily said.
Tim and Lily had fun making the number on the card grow bigger and bigger. But then, something unexpected happened. The card started to make everything around them grow bigger too! The trees, the houses, and even their toys were all getting bigger. Tim and Lily were scared. They learned that it's important to be careful with things they don't understand.

Once upon a time, in a big house, there was a huge box. A little boy named Tim found the box in his room. Tim was very excited to see what was inside the box.
Tim opened the box and saw a lot of screws. He took one screw and started to play with it. Tim's mom came into the room and said, "Stop, Tim! Screws are not toys. They can be dangerous."
Tim said, "Okay, Mom. But can we use the screws to build something?" Mom smiled and said, "Yes, we can build a small car." Tim and his mom spent the day building a car with the screws. They had a lot of fun, and Tim learned how to use screws safely.

Milly was feeling lucky as she stepped outside her house. She looked up at the sky and her face lit up with wonder. The twinkling stars were so beautiful and mysterious. She lay back on the grass and began to gaze up at the night sky. Suddenly, she heard a voice.
"You're lucky, Milly" said the voice.
Milly was very surprised and looked around. She saw her neighbour, Mrs. Smith, smiling at her.
"What do you mean?" Milly asked.
"Your lucky to have such a clear night, where you can see the stars so clearly and feel so close to something so special" Mrs. Smith replied.
Milly smiled and thanked Mrs. Smith. She lay back down and continued to gaze at the night sky, feeling very lucky indeed.

Once upon a time, there was a lively dog named Max. Max loved to play with his ball. One day, Max saw a big red ball in the park. He was very happy and wanted to grab it.
Max ran to the ball and said, "Ball, you are my new friend!" He grabbed the ball and started to play. Max was having lots of fun. He jumped and barked with joy.
Soon, a little girl came to the park. She saw Max and the ball. The girl said, "Hi, Max! Can I play with you?" Max wagged his tail and said, "Yes, let's play!" They both had a great start playing together all day.

One day, a little girl named Mia went to the park with her mom. Mia saw a little statue of a dog. She thought it was a toy, so she picked it up to play with it. Mia's mom said, "Be careful, Mia! That statue might break if you drop it!"
Mia played with the statue for a while, but then she accidentally dropped it. The statue broke into two pieces. Mia was very sad and said, "I'm sorry, Mom. I didn't mean to break it." Her mom hugged her and said, "It's okay, Mia. Accidents happen."
Just then, something unexpected happened. The broken statue started to wiggle and move! The little statue turned into a real, live dog! Mia and her mom were so surprised. The dog wagged its tail and licked Mia's face. Mia giggled and said, "I guess it's not so bad that I dropped the statue!" They all played together in the park and had a great day.

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw an open box by a big tree. Tim was curious, so he went to look at the box. Inside the box, there was a snake.
"Hi, snake!" said Tim. The snake looked at Tim and said, "Hi, Tim! Can you help me? I want to leave this box."
Tim thought for a moment. He wanted to help the snake, but he was scared. Then, Tim had an idea. He found a stick and used it to lift the snake out of the box. The snake was happy to be free.
"Thank you, Tim!" said the snake. "You are very kind." Tim smiled and said, "You're welcome, snake! Have a nice day!" And with that, the snake slithered away, and Tim went back to his walk, proud of himself for helping a new friend.

Sara and Ben were twins. They liked to play with their toys and books. But sometimes they got bored. One day, they saw a big magazine on the table. It had many pictures and words. They wanted to look at it.
"Can we see the magazine, Mom?" Sara asked.
"Not now, sweetie. It's Mom's magazine. It's not for kids. You might rip it or make it dirty. Go play with your own things," Mom said.
But Sara and Ben did not listen. They waited until Mom went to the kitchen. Then they took the magazine and ran to their room. They were very quiet. They did not want Mom to hear them.
They opened the magazine and looked at the pictures. They saw cars, animals, people, and places. They tried to read the words, but they were too hard. They made up their own stories.
"Look, Ben, this is a lion. He is very big and strong. He lives in the jungle. He can roar and scare everyone," Sara said.
"Wow, Sara, that's cool. And this is a car. It is very fast and shiny. It can go anywhere. It has wheels and doors and windows," Ben said.
They had fun with the magazine. But they did not see that they were ripping the pages and making them dirty. They did not know that Mom was coming back.
Mom opened the door and saw the mess. She was very angry. She saw the magazine on the floor. It was torn and stained. She saw Sara and Ben with guilty faces.
"What have you done? You took my magazine and ruined it. You did not listen to me. You were very naughty. I'm very disappointed in you," Mom said.
Sara and Ben felt very bad. They knew they did something wrong. They worried that Mom would not love them anymore. They started to cry.
"I'm sorry, Mom. We just wanted to see the pictures. We did not mean to hurt your magazine. Please don't be mad at us. We love you," Sara said.
"Me too, Mom. I'm sorry. We won't do it again. Please forgive us. We love you," Ben said.
Mom sighed. She was still angry, but she also loved her twins. She picked them up and hugged them.
"I love you too, Sara and Ben. But you have to learn to respect other people's things. And you have to listen to me when I tell you something. Do you understand?" Mom said.
"Yes, Mom. We understand. We're sorry," Sara and Ben said.
Mom smiled. She gave them a kiss. She told them to help her clean up the mess. She also told them to pick a book from their shelf. She said she would read them a story. A story that was for kids. A story that they would like.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was lonely because he did not have any friends at school. One day, he decided to step into a new classroom to try and make friends.
In the classroom, Tim saw a girl named Sue. Sue was playing with blocks. Tim walked up to Sue and said, "Hi! Can I play with you?" Sue looked up and smiled. "Yes! Let's build a big tower with the blocks," she said.
Tim and Sue played together and built a tall tower. They laughed and had fun. Tim was not lonely anymore because he made a new friend. From that day on, Tim and Sue played together in the classroom every day.

Once upon a time, in a small cabin in the woods, there lived a group of animals. There was a big bear, a small rabbit, and a fast fox. They were all friends and lived happily together. They always helped each other and were ready to play and have fun.
One day, they decided to have a race to see who was the fastest. The big bear was strong, the small rabbit was quick, and the fast fox was clever. They all thought they could win the race. They started to run as fast as they could through the woods. The big bear was in the lead, the small rabbit was right behind him, and the fast fox was last.
Suddenly, they saw a big river in front of them. They knew they couldn't swim across it. The animals were sad and didn't know what to do. Then, they had an idea. They could unite and work together to build a bridge. The big bear carried the heavy logs, the small rabbit found the long vines, and the fast fox tied everything together. They built a strong bridge and crossed the river together. In the end, they all won the race because they worked together as a team. The animals learned that when they unite, they can do anything.

Once upon a time there was a nosy bunny. He wanted to see what was outside his burrow. He hopped and hopped until he found himself in front of a big house.
The bunny peeked and saw a curtain in one of the windows. He hopped closer to have a better look. But just as he was about to peek through the curtain, he heard a voice.
"Who's there?" the voice asked. The nosy bunny was scared and he hopped back quickly. He didn't know who was talking!
Just then, a woman opened the door and came out. She saw the bunny and smiled. She reached out and gently stroked his furry head.
"Don't be scared little bunny," she said. "You're just too nosy for your own good! Go on now, yield to curiosity another time."
The nosy bunny was happy and he hopped away. He promised to himself that next time he would be extra careful and not get too close to the curtain!

Once upon a time, there was a pilot named Tom. Tom had a big plane. He loved to fly high in the sky. One day, a fierce wind blew. The wind was very strong and made it hard for Tom to fly his plane.
Tom said to himself, "I must resist the wind to keep my plane safe." So, Tom tried very hard to resist the wind. He pushed and pulled on the plane's controls. The wind was very strong, but Tom was stronger.
Finally, Tom won against the wind. He landed his plane safely. Tom was happy that he didn't give up. He learned that when things are hard, you must be strong and resist. And that is how Tom became a great pilot.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. He was three years old and he loved to explore outside. One day he decided to go on an adventure. He was walking through the forest when he heard an enormous wind. He was very scared, but he didn't want to go back home. Tom decided to keep going.
Suddenly, the wind got very strong and started to pick up lots of leaves. Tom wanted to get away from the wind, so he quickly ran in the opposite direction. But, the wind was too strong and it blew Tom into a huge tree.
Tom was stuck in the tree for a long time and he screamed for help. But, no one could hear him. Eventually, the wind stopped blowing and Tom went back home. 
The moral of this story is that it's important to always listen to mommy and daddy. They know what's best for you and it's best to stay out of dangerous situations. Trying to explore can be fun, but it needs to be done with caution.

Once upon a time, there was a little duck named Dee. Dee loved to play in the deep water with her friends. One sunny day, Dee and her friends went to the deep pond to swim and have fun.
As they were playing, Dee found a pretty necklace. She put the necklace on her neck and showed her friends. Dee's friends thought the necklace was very pretty too. They all wanted to find pretty things to put on their necks.
But then, something unexpected happened. When Dee put the necklace on her neck, she turned into a big, beautiful swan! Dee's friends were surprised and didn't know what to do. They looked for more necklaces, but they couldn't find any.
Dee was sad because she missed being a duck and playing with her friends. Then, a wise old turtle told her to put the necklace back in the deep pond. Dee did as the turtle said and turned back into a little duck. Dee was happy to be a duck again and learned that sometimes the best thing to be is just yourself.

Bobby was a gifted little boy. One day, he was playing outside on a hill. It was a sunny day and he was having a lot of fun. Bobby found a big basket sitting nearby. He was excited and decided to slide down the hill with the basket. 
He put the basket in the grass at the top of the hill then slid down it. But something unexpected happened. Bobby couldn't stop! He tumbled into the basket and kept sliding down the hill. The basket bounced around, so Bobby laughed and squealed with joy. 
When he reached the bottom, Bobby was delighted. He looked up the hill and smiled, thinking of all the fun he had. He thought the basket had been a great idea. He knew he was very gifted. Bobby quickly grabbed the basket and raced back up the hill, ready to slide down again.

Once upon a time, there was a brave little girl named Emma. She lived in a beautiful village by the river. One day, on her way home, she came to a big and scary cross. She was very anxious and stopped to look at it. 
Suddenly a deep voice came from the cross and said, "Promise me you will always be brave, and I will give you something special." Emma was not sure what to do, but she was brave and said, "Yes, I promise!" 
The voice then demanded that she follow the path around the cross to find a surprise. Emma took a deep breath and started to walk around the cross. When she got to the other side, she found a shiny necklace with a rainbow-colored stone! Emma was so excited to have a special treat and thanked the voice. 
The voice then said, "Whenever you are feeling anxious, just remember your promise, and that special surprise will help you through." 
Emma smiled and put the necklace around her neck. She now wears it with pride, reminding her to stay brave no matter what life throws her way.

Sam and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. Sam has a red cap. Mia has a blue cap. They run and jump and laugh.
One day, a big dog comes to the park. The dog is fast and loud. It likes to chase things. It sees Sam and Mia and their caps. It runs after them. Sam and Mia are scared.
"Help! Help!" they shout. "The dog wants our caps!"
They run to the swings. They climb up and hold their caps. The dog jumps and barks. It can't reach them.
"Go away, dog!" Sam says. "These are our caps!"
"Maybe it just wants to play," Mia says. "Maybe we can bring it a ball."
She sees a ball near the slide. She waits for the dog to look away. Then she jumps down and runs to the ball. She picks it up and throws it far away.
"Fetch, dog! Fetch!" she says.
The dog sees the ball. It forgets about the caps. It runs after the ball. It catches it and wags its tail.
"Good dog!" Mia says. "You are fast and fun!"
She goes back to the swings. She gives Sam a high five. They put on their caps and smile.
They are not scared anymore. They are happy. They have a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy called Sam. Sam loved to go exploring in the woods. One day, he came across an herb. He had never seen an herb before and he thought it looked normal. So he touched it. As soon as he did, the herb started to rot. 
At first, Sam was worried and he felt sad, but then he noticed something else. As the herb was rotting, it was turning into a really yummy smelling sauce. Sam was so excited and he rushed off to get a bowl. He came back and put the bowl right underneath the herb and let the sauce drip into the bowl. 
Once the bowl was full, Sam was very happy. He served himself a big bowl of the yummy smelling sauce, and it was the best dinner he ever had. From then on, Sam started to go exploring in the woods every day, and he always looked for normal herbs.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a big loop in his yard. He was eager to play with it. He picked it up and ran to his friend, Sue.
"Look what I found!" Tim said to Sue. "Let's play with the loop!"
Sue was happy to play. They took turns throwing the loop and trying to catch it. They laughed and had fun.
