A little dog came running to them. It wanted to play too. The dog jumped and caught the loop in its mouth. Tim and Sue clapped and cheered.
"Good dog!" Tim said. "Come and play with us every day!" The dog wagged its tail and they all played together until the sun went down.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful boy named Tim. Tim loved to help his mom and dad with things around the house. One day, Tim's mom told him he needed a haircut. Tim did not like haircuts, but he knew he had to be helpful.
Tim went to the haircut place with his mom. He sat in the big chair and the haircut lady started to cut his hair. Tim tried to be brave, but he was scared. He wanted to run away, but he did not want to forget to be helpful.
Then, Tim's mom saw that he was scared. She held his hand and told him it was okay. Tim felt better and let the haircut lady finish his haircut. In the end, Tim was happy that he did not forget to be helpful. His mom and dad were proud of him, and he looked great with his new haircut.

One day, a smart cat named Tom wanted to complete a task. He wanted to cook his food on a grill. Tom was very excited to do this.
Tom put his food on the grill and waited. As he was waiting, a big bird came down and took his food. Tom was very sad and did not know what to do.
But then, something unexpected happened. The big bird came back and put the food on the grill. The big bird wanted to help Tom complete his task. They cooked the food together and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a funny bird. The bird liked to sing. It sang all day long. The bird lived in a big tree. The tree had many acorns.
One day, the bird saw a small acorn on the ground. The bird picked it up. The bird wanted to play with the acorn. The bird rolled the acorn with its beak.
The bird sang a happy song. The acorn rolled and rolled. The bird and the acorn had fun. They played all day. The bird was very happy with its new friend, the acorn.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big sack. He liked to put his toys in the sack and carry it around. One day, Tim's sack became weak. It had a hole in it. Tim was sad because his toys could fall out.
Tim went to his mom and said, "Mom, my sack is weak. What should I do?" His mom told him, "Mind the hole, Tim. We can fix it together." Tim felt better. He knew his mom would help him.
They found some cloth and sewed the hole in the sack. It was not weak anymore. Tim was happy. He put his toys back in the sack and played all day. He knew that if he had a problem, his mom would always be there to help.

Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Ella. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Ella was a very modest girl who loved to help others.
One day, Ella saw a cat outside her house. The cat looked hungry and sad. Ella wanted to give the cat some food, but her mom refused. Ella felt sad for the cat and wanted to find a way to help it.
The next day, Ella found a small bag of cat food in her room. She was surprised because she didn't know where it came from. Ella took the food and gave it to the cat. The cat was very happy and became her friend. From that day on, Ella and the cat played together every day, and her mom never knew about the secret cat food.

Once upon a time there was a wise old chicken, and he was very smart. He lived with a lot of other animals in the forest.
One day, one of the animals, the lion, was feeling very angry. He roared, "Roar! I'm so mad!"
The wise old chicken saw the lion and said, "It doesn't feel good to be angry. You should try to be happy and nice."
The lion thought about it and realised the old chicken was right. He said, "You're right. I'll try to be happy and nice."
The moral of the story is that being angry will not help you or any of the other animals in the forest, but being kind and happy will.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy who loved muffins. He wanted a muffin so bad, he went outside and started to look for one. 
The boy followed a dark path, and at the end of it he found a muffin! He was so happy and he started to eat the muffin. 
Then suddenly, he heard a noise and he was scared! There was a big, dark monster coming! The boy tried to run away but he couldn't, so he just stayed there and screamed. 
The monster said, "I guess this muffin belongs to me!" The boy begged the monster to let him keep the muffin, but the monster wouldn't listen. The boy didn't have a choice, and the monster took the muffin. 
The little boy was so sad. He knew he would never find another muffin. So he sadly went home, with no muffin.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was an adorable little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her round, red ball. Every day, she would take it to the park and bounce it up and down.
One day, while Lily was playing with her ball, it rolled away and got stuck in a tree. She tried to get it back but it was too high for her to reach. Just then, a friendly bird flew by and saw Lily looking sad.
The bird wanted to help, so it flew up to the tree and pushed the round ball out. The ball fell to the ground and Lily was very happy. She said, "Thank you, bird!" The bird smiled and said, "You're welcome!" Then the bird flew away.
Lily continued to play with her ball, but this time, she made sure it didn't go too far. She would throw it up and catch it, then repeat the game again and again. She had so much fun playing with her round, red ball.
From that day on, Lily and the bird became good friends. They would play together every day in the park. Lily learned that when you are kind and help others, you can make adorable and lasting friendships.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy had a big cabinet in her room. She liked to put her toys in the cabinet. One day, her friend Tim came to play with her. Tim saw the cabinet and felt jealous. He wanted a cabinet like that too.
Amy and Tim played with the toys. Then, Tim had an idea. He said, "Let's take the toys from the cabinet and hide them." Amy thought it was a fun game. They took the toys out and hid them all around the house.
When Amy's mom saw the empty cabinet and the hidden toys, she was not happy. She told Amy and Tim that they had to put the toys back. Tim did not want to help, but Amy suggested that they should work together. They started to put the toys back, but then something unexpected happened.
While they were putting the toys back, the cabinet fell over and broke. Amy and Tim were very sad. The moral of the story is that we should be careful with our things and not let jealousy make us do things we might regret.

One day, Mum and Dad and baby went to a shop. Baby wanted to decorate. Mum bought a cone and Dad looked at the colours. Baby chose a dark colour. 
Back home, Mum and Dad and baby made a plan. They put the cone on a table and started to decorate. Mum put glue on parts of the cone and Dad put some sparkles on it. Baby put the dark colour on the cone too. 
When it was finished, Mum and Dad and baby all looked at the cone. They smiled because it was beautiful and special. Baby said, "Wow!" Then they all hugged and laughed.

Once upon a time, there was a large bear named Ben. Ben had a special toy called a microscope. He loved to look at things with it.
One day, Ben's friend, a small bird named Lily, came to visit. Lily was sad because her wing was hurt. Ben wanted to care for her and help her feel better.
Ben used his microscope to look at Lily's wing. He saw that it needed a band-aid. He put one on her wing, and Lily felt better. They played together all day, and Ben was happy he could care for his friend.

One day, a little fish named Fin became friends with a big shark named Sam. Fin was very happy to have a new friend, but he was also a bit scared because Sam was so big. They liked to swim and play together in the deep blue sea.
One day, while they were playing, Sam said, "Fin, be careful! Don't be careless when we swim near the big rocks." Fin listened to Sam and was very careful when they played near the rocks.
But one day, Fin was not careful and swam too close to the big rocks. He got stuck and cried for help. Sam heard Fin and quickly came to save him. With his strong tail, Sam pushed the rocks away and set Fin free. Fin thanked Sam and promised to be more careful in the future. They continued to be the best of friends, swimming and playing together in the deep blue sea.

Tom and Lily were playing in the sand. They had a big bucket and a small bucket. They filled the big bucket with sand and turned it over to make a castle. They filled the small bucket with water and poured it over the castle to make it wet.
"Look at our castle!" Lily said. "It is so nice and big!"
Tom nodded. He was happy. He liked playing with Lily. They were friends.
But then, Ben came. Ben was older and bigger. He was not nice. He saw Tom and Lily's castle and kicked it with his foot. The castle broke and the sand flew everywhere.
"Hey!" Tom shouted. "Why did you do that? That was our castle!"
Ben laughed. He had a bitter smile. He did not mind Tom and Lily. He did not care. He liked to make them sad.
"I did it because I can," Ben said. "And I will do it again. Your castle is stupid. You are stupid."
He kicked the big bucket and the small bucket. The buckets flew away. Tom and Lily ran after them. They were scared and angry.
"Stop it, Ben!" Lily cried. "Leave us alone! You are mean!"
Ben chased them. He wanted to kick them too. He thought it was fun.
But then, a big man came. He was Tom and Lily's dad. He saw what Ben was doing and he was not happy. He had a loud voice.
"Hey, you!" he said. "What are you doing? Why are you hurting my children?"
Ben stopped. He was afraid. He did not like the big man. He did not like his voice.
"I was just playing," Ben said. He lied. He hoped the big man would not mind.
But the big man did mind. He did not believe Ben. He knew he was mean.
"You were not playing," he said. "You were bullying. That is not nice. That is wrong. You should say sorry to Tom and Lily. And you should give them back their buckets."
Ben did not want to say sorry. He did not want to give back the buckets. But he had no choice. The big man was stronger and bigger. He made him do it.
Ben said sorry to Tom and Lily. He gave them back their buckets. He did not look at them. He felt ashamed.
Tom and Lily took their buckets. They thanked the big man. They hugged their dad. They were happy.
They went back to the sand. They made a new castle. They poured water over it. They smiled.
They did not mind Ben. They did not care. They liked their castle. They liked each other. They were friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a toy car that he loved very much. One day, while playing, his toy car broke. Tim was very sad and didn't know what to do.
His uncle came to visit and saw the broken toy car. He said, "Don't worry, Tim. I can fix your car." Tim was happy and gave his uncle the broken car. His uncle worked hard to fix it. 
While his uncle was fixing the car, Tim found a piece of paper. He started to tear the paper into small pieces. Then, something unexpected happened. The torn paper turned into a new, shiny toy car! Tim was very surprised and happy. He showed the new car to his uncle and said, "Look, Uncle! The torn paper turned into a new car!" His uncle smiled and said, "Now you have two cars to play with!" And they played happily together with both cars.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sue. Sue loved to dance. She danced in her room, in the yard, and even in the kitchen. One day, Sue saw a dull gray cat sitting on the fence. The cat looked sad.
Sue said to the cat, "Why are you sad, cat?" The cat said, "I want to dance, but I don't know how." Sue thought for a moment, and then she had an idea. "I will teach you to dance!" she said. The cat was happy to hear this.
Sue and the cat danced every day. They danced in the sun, and they danced in the rain. But the cat still could not dance well. One night, Sue prayed, "Please help my cat friend learn to dance." The next day, the cat danced better than ever before. Sue and the cat were so happy, and they danced together all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little squirrel named Peanut. Peanut loved to listen to the birds sing in the trees. Every day, he would sit under the tree and listen to their songs.
One day, while listening to the birds, Peanut met a new friend. It was a little girl named Lily. Lily said, "Hi, Peanut! I have a delicious treat for you." She gave Peanut a yummy nut to eat. Peanut was so happy and said, "Thank you, Lily!"
From that day on, Peanut and Lily would meet under the tree and listen to the birds together. They would share delicious nuts and have lots of fun. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy cat named Tom. Tom lived in a small house with his friend, a little girl named Sue. One day, Sue saw a pretty pot in the yard. She wanted to put a plant in the pot, but she did not know how.
Tom said, "I will try to help you, Sue." So they both tried to put the plant in the pot. But it was hard work, and they could not do it. They felt sad.
Just then, a big, strong dog named Max came by. He saw Tom and Sue, and asked, "Why are you sad?" Sue told him about the pot and the plant. Max said, "I can help you!" He used his strong paws to put the plant in the pot. Sue, Tom, and Max were all happy. They had worked together and solved the problem.

Once upon a time, there was a huge triangle. The triangle lived in a big box. The triangle was very happy in the box. It had many friends. The friends were circle, square, and star. They played all day.
One day, triangle had a question. It wanted to know how to be a better friend. Triangle asked circle, square, and star. They all thought about the question. They wanted to help triangle.
Circle said, "Share your toys." Square said, "Play nice games." Star said, "Always be kind." Triangle listened to its friends. It did what they said. Triangle was a very good friend. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Lily went for a walk. She saw a flower that was about to bloom. She was very happy and said, "Look, the flower is going to bloom soon!"
Lily felt hungry and wanted to eat something yummy. She went to a shop and saw a big pizza. She asked the shop man, "Can I have that pizza, please?" The shop man gave her the pizza, and she ate it all up.
On her way home, Lily saw a foolish dog. The dog was running in circles and barking loudly. The dog made Lily laugh. She said, "Silly dog, you are so foolish!" And then, she went home with a big smile on her face.

Once upon a time, there was a deaf girl named Lily. She loved to paint pictures. One day, she went outside to paint nature. She saw pretty trees, green grass, and blue sky.
Lily sat down and started to paint. She painted the trees, the grass, and the sky. She was very happy. A little bird flew by and saw Lily painting. The bird landed next to her.
"Hi, I like your painting," said the bird. Lily did not hear the bird, but she saw it and smiled. She painted the bird in her picture too. The bird liked being in the painting.
Lily and the bird became friends. They played in nature every day. Lily painted more pictures, and the bird sang songs. Even though Lily was deaf, she loved her friend and their time together.

Billy was walking through the fields, when he stumbled upon a cow. The cow looked very uncomfortable and refused to move. Billy thought this was strange, so he bravely tried to resist the cow and move it. He shouted and he pushed, but the cow refused to move.
"Come on, cow," said Billy. "Maybe I can help you if you just move." The cow stayed still and uncomfortable. It seemed like it was scared, so Billy kept trying to resist it.
Suddenly, the cow started to move and run away. Trying to resist it was too much for Billy, and he stumbled to the ground and scraped his knee. It was a bad ending, but Billy learned his lesson: don't resist a cow!

One day, a little girl called Rose was playing in her garden. She wanted to zoom around on her scooter so she grabbed it and started to wheel it around the garden. Suddenly, she felt something break. She looked down and saw that the wheel of her scooter had snapped off. Rose was very sad.
She ran inside to find her mum. She said, "Mummy, my scooter wheel broke!" Her mum gave her a big hug and said, "Don't worry, it was just a little wheel. We can fix it."
Rose smiled and said, "Please, can I go and get a new one?" Her mum laughed and said, "Yes, of course you can."
The next morning, Rose and her mum went to the store and bought a new wheel. Rose was so excited, she couldn't wait to zoom around the garden again! She help up the new wheel and said, "Thank you Mummy!" 
They went home and fixed the wheel onto the scooter. Rose couldn't believe it. She thanked her mum again and sprinted out into the garden. It felt so good to be able to zoom around on her scooter again!

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spike. Spike loved to play outside in the sun. He would run and jump all day long.
One day, Spike found a big puddle. He jumped in and started to splash. Spike got very wet and muddy. His fur was not clean anymore.
Spike's mom saw him and said, "You need a bath, Spike!" She filled a tub with warm water and let Spike soak in it. Spike liked the warm water. After his bath, Spike was clean again. He was a happy little dog.

Once upon a time, there was a little blue bird named Olive. Olive lived in a big tree with her family. Every day, Olive would fly around and play with her friends.
One day, Olive saw a little boy crying near the tree. She flew down and asked, "Why are you sad?" The little boy said, "I lost my toy. Can you help me find it?" Olive thought for a moment and said, "I promise I will help you find your toy."
Olive and the little boy looked everywhere for the toy. They looked under rocks, behind bushes, and up in the trees. Finally, they found the toy hidden in the tall grass. The little boy was so happy, and he hugged Olive tightly. "Thank you, Olive! You kept your promise!"
Olive smiled and flew back to her tree. She felt proud that she could help the little boy. From that day on, Olive always kept her promises, and she learned that helping others made her feel happy too. And the little boy learned that if you are kind and ask for help, good things can happen.

Once upon a time, there were two friends called Billy and Susie. They were playing in the woods on a beautiful, sunny day.
As they were playing, Susie smelled something sweet in the air. "Do you smell that?" she asked. "It smells like hay!"
Billy and Susie ran to find the source of the smell. When they saw a big, round haystack, their mouths started to water.
Susie said, "I'm so hungry! Let's quit playing and go get some of the hay!"
Billy agreed, and they ran over to the haystack. They reached in and grabbed the soft hay, filling their bellies with its goodness.
When they had finished eating, they lay back in the hay. They were so contented and happy, they did not want to quit.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Stella. She was three years old and had an oyster in her hand.
One day Stella was walking alone through the woods and she noticed an old lady. She smiled at the old lady, but the old lady didn't smile back.
Stella said "Hi, I have an oyster".
The old lady looked very sorry and said to Stella, "I'm so sorry, but I am very hungry. Could I please have your oyster?"
Stella thought carefully and then said, "Yes, you can have my oyster, but only if you pass it on to someone else in need."
The old lady thanked Stella and hugged her tightly.
The moral of the story is that when we are generous, our generosity is passed on and it can make a difference to many people.

Once upon a time, there was a brown chicken. The chicken lived on a farm with many friends. The chicken loved to play in the sun.
One day, the chicken saw a big ball. The chicken wanted to play with the ball. The chicken urged its friends to come and play too. They all came running to have fun.
They all played with the ball, laughing and having a good time. The brown chicken was very happy. The sun was shining, and it was a great day for everyone on the farm.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a chubby baby brother named Sam. They liked to play together in their big, green yard.
One sunny day, Tim and Sam went outside to play. Tim found a big, red ball. He had a fun idea. "Let's roll the ball," said Tim with a big smile. Sam clapped his little hands and laughed.
Tim rolled the ball to Sam. Sam tried to roll it back, but the ball was too big for him. Instead, Sam started to roll on the grass like the ball. Tim laughed and rolled on the grass too. They both had a great time rolling and playing together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Emma. She was getting a wagon for her birthday. She was very excited and wanted to do something special with it.
One day, Emma decided to take the wagon to the park. She was so happy and coludn't wait to explore!
Suddenly, Emma heard a loud noise coming from the nearby playground. When she got closer, she saw that a boy was pushing another boy in the wagon. Emma shouted out "No! Nobody should push anybody else in the wagon!"
The boy pushing the wagon stopped and said, "I'm sorry. I didn't mean to cause trouble.
Emma replied in a calm voice, "It's ok. Let's just have some fun together then."
So they all took turns pushing each other in the wagon and having lots of fun. The park was so peaceful and calm after that.

Once upon a time, there was a big tree. This tree had amazing shade. Many animals liked to sit under the tree. One day, a little girl named Mia came to the tree. She had food to feed the animals.
Mia saw a cat, a dog, and a bird under the tree. She gave food to the cat, the dog, and the bird. They were very happy. Then, something unexpected happened. The tree started to talk!
The tree said, "Thank you, Mia, for feeding my friends." Mia was very surprised. She did not know that trees could talk. The tree told Mia that it was a magic tree. From that day on, Mia and the tree became good friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the jungle with his mom. They saw many big trees and fun animals. Tim liked to watch the monkeys play.
In the jungle, they found a poor, sad monkey. The monkey said, "I am sad because I cannot shut the door to my home." Tim wanted to help the poor monkey.
Tim and his mom helped the monkey shut the door. The monkey was very happy. They all played together in the jungle. The poor monkey was not sad anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a strong girl named Amy. Amy loved to play with her toys. One day, she went to the park with her mom. At the park, Amy saw a big tree. She wanted to put her toy in the tree.
"Why do you want to put your toy in the tree?" her mom asked. Amy said, "I want to see if the toy can fly!" So, Amy put her toy in the tree and waited.
Suddenly, a bird came and took the toy! Amy was sad. But then, the bird flew back and gave the toy to Amy. The bird said, "I can help your toy fly!" Amy was happy and thanked the bird. The bird helped the toy fly, and Amy had a fun day at the park.

Tom and Lily wanted to play in the snow. They put on their warm coats, hats, gloves and boots. They ran outside and saw a big hill of snow. They decided to make a sign that said "Tom and Lily's snow fort". They used sticks and stones to write the words on the snow.
They worked hard and made a big fort with walls and a door. They were very proud of their sign and their fort. They went inside to get some hot chocolate and cookies. They told their mom they would be back soon.
But while they were inside, a big truck came by. The truck was clearing the snow from the road. The truck did not see the sign or the fort. The truck pushed all the snow away with a big plow. The sign and the fort were gone.
Tom and Lily came back outside with their mugs and plates. They looked for their sign and their fort. They could not find them. They saw the truck driving away. They felt very sad and angry. They cried and threw their mugs and plates on the ground. They had no sign, no fort, no snow, no hot chocolate and no cookies. They had a very bad day.

One day, a kind girl named Lily went outside to play. She liked to find things and discover new stuff. While she was playing, she saw a big rock. She thought there might be something under it.
Lily tried to move the rock, but it was too heavy. A friendly dog came to help her. Together, they pushed the rock and found a small hole. The dog said, "I smell something in there!" Lily looked inside and saw something shiny. It was oil!
Lily and the dog were happy they found the oil. They decided to share it with their friends. The dog said, "You are very compassionate, Lily." Lily smiled and said, "Thank you, dog. We should always help each other and be nice to our friends."

Once upon a time, there was a smart cat named Tom. He lived in a small house with a big wardrobe. Tom loved to play in the wardrobe and wear the clothes.
One day, Tom met a dog named Max. They did not like each other and began to quarrel. They barked and hissed, making a lot of noise.
Tom's friend, a kind bird named Sue, saw the quarrel. She told Tom and Max to stop and be friends. They listened to Sue and stopped fighting. From that day on, Tom, Max, and Sue played together and became the best of friends.

One day, a big girl named Sue wanted to bake a cake. She was very generous and wanted to share it with her friends. So, she put on her apron, got her bowl and spoon, and started to mix the cake.
While Sue was baking, her friend Tim came over. "What are you doing?" he asked. "I am baking a cake for our tea time," said Sue. Tim wanted to help, but he didn't know how. He tried to pour some milk into the bowl, but he spilled it all over the floor. Sue was upset, but she didn't want to be mean to her friend.
Then, something unexpected happened. Sue's little brother, Ben, came into the kitchen. He saw the mess and started to giggle. He picked up a mop and began to clean the floor. Sue and Tim were surprised. They didn't know that Ben could be so helpful. They all worked together to finish the cake and clean the kitchen. At tea time, they shared the cake and had a lot of fun.

Mama and Tiny were walking through the park when Tiny saw a shiny jug. He tilted his head and said "Mama, what's that?" Mama smiled and replied, "That's a jug. It's modern and it will protect something special." Tiny squinted his eyes, wondering what it could protect. He was curious but Mama had to move on now.
As they continued their walk, Tiny saw something interesting. It was a big heap of leaves. He said, "Look Mama! It's a pile of leaves, I can jump in!" Mama nodded and said, "Go ahead, but make sure you keep the jug nearby." Tiny was confused, but he didn't ask why.
He jumped in the pile of leaves and played for a few minutes. But then suddenly a squirrel ran up and started to climb the jug. Tiny jumped out of the pile of leaves, ran to the jug and yelled, "Stop! That belongs to me!" The squirrel stopped and ran away.
Mama came over and said, "Let's see what was so special that the jug was meant to protect." Tiny opened the jug, and an old necklace was inside. Mama smiled and said, "See, I told you the jug would protect something special. Now it's yours and you can wear it whenever you'd like!" Tiny was amazed and smiled happily. The modern jug had indeed protected something special.

Once upon a time, there was a little boat. The boat felt helpless because it could not move. It wanted to spend time with the other boats in the water.
One day, a big wind came. The wind pushed the helpless boat into the water. The little boat was so happy! It could finally spend time with the other boats.
But then, something unexpected happened. The little boat was not helpless at all! It had a secret sail. The sail opened, and the little boat went very fast. It was the fastest boat of all! The other boats were so surprised. The little boat was not helpless anymore. It spent lots of time with the other boats, and they all had fun together.

One morning, a young girl decides to visit her Grandpa. She knows that Grandpa has been very important in her life, so she wants to say hello.
The girl puts on her shoes and runs out of the house. She runs past the big trees and down to the big road.
After a short time, the young girl reaches her Grandpa's house. She knocks on the door and hears someone inside move. Then she hears a voice.
Grandpa: "Who is it?"
Young girl: "It's me, Grandpa! I've come to visit you."
Grandpa: "Come in, my dear! I'm so glad you're here."
The young girl opens the door and Grandpa gives her a big hug. They sit down together and talk and laugh. The young girl is very happy to visit her Grandpa.

One day, Tom and his dog Spot were playing in the park. They saw a big black box near a tree. Tom said, "Look, Spot! A present!" They ran to the box and tried to open it, but it was locked.
A mean boy named Jack came to the park. He saw Tom and Spot with the black box. Jack wanted the present too. He said, "Give me the present, or I will not stop being mean to you!" Tom felt sad but did not give the present to Jack.
Tom and Spot went home and found a key under their door. They knew the key would open the black box. They opened it and found new toys inside. Tom said, "Now we can play with our new toys and not worry about Jack." They played all day and were very happy.

Once upon a time there was an old barn in the countryside. Inside the barn lived a family of bunnies. They had lived in the barn for many years and never had any problems.
One day, their mum bunny warned them to stay away from the barn. She told them it was an important place that they mustn't disturb. Even though the bunnies wanted to explore, they still listened to their mum bunny and kept away from the barn.
But one day one of the small bunnies ignored his mum's warnings. He slipped into the barn and noticed that the floor of the barn was full of hay. He thought it would be fun to play in the hay, so he started jumping around.
Just as he was jumping, he heard a noise. He quickly stopped and saw a bigger bunny coming out of a corner of the hay. The bunny was very angry! 
The little bunny apologised and promised to never go into the barn again. He thanked his mum bunny for warning him to stay away. He knew that it was very important to listen to her warnings.

One day, a cool cat named Tim wanted to ride his bike. Tim put on his coat because it was chilly outside. He was very excited to go for a ride around the park.
As Tim rode his bike, he saw many friends. He saw a dog, a bird, and a squirrel. They all said "hi" to Tim. Tim was happy to see his friends and waved back.
After a fun ride, Tim went back home. He took off his coat and went inside. Tim was happy he had a great day. He couldn't wait to ride his bike again tomorrow.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Jack and Jessica. One day they decided to go to the theater. Jack was so excited that he started to clean their seats, but when he bumped into someone he said "Sorry!" The person said, "That's okay".
When the show started, Jessica leaned over to Jack and said, "Isn't it amazing? I'm so glad we came!" Jack smiled and whispered, "Me too."
After the show had finished, Jack and Jessica walked home together. When they arrived at Jessica's house, Jack suddenly said, "I'm sorry I cleaned our seats at the theater. I didn't mean to cause any trouble." Jessica smiled and said, "That's okay, Jack. I don't think anyone minded."

Once upon a time, in a cheerful little town, there lived a brave dog named Max. Max loved to protect his family and friends. He would watch over them all day and night.
One sunny day, Max saw a small cat stuck in a tree. The cat was scared and needed help. Max knew he had to protect the cat. So, he barked and barked to call his family. In just a minute, they came running to see what was wrong.
Max's family saw the cat in the tree and they helped it come down. The cat was so happy and thankful. Max felt proud that he could protect the cat. From that day on, Max and the cat became best friends, and they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there lived a fat frog. He loved to move through his little pool. Every day he would jump and twist around the pool. The water was always nice and clean.
But one day, something terrible happened. It started to rain heavily. When the rain stopped, the frog looked at his pool and it was filthy. The water was filled with dirt and leaves, and it looked horrible.
The poor frog was so sad, he didn't know what to do. He looked around, but he couldn't find a way to clean the pool. He would just have to clean it himself.
The frog wanted to move again through his little pool, but he couldn't. He had to clean it, but it was too dirty. He tried his best to clean the pool, but it was no use. His pool was still filthy and he couldn't move in it anymore.
The poor frog was very sad and could not enjoy his pool again. He had to look for another one but he never found it. He had to stay in a filthy pool forever.

One day, a boy named Tim went to a field. The field was big and had thick, green grass. Tim loved to play in the field with his dog, Spot.
As they played, the sky got dark and it started to rain. The rain made the field wet and muddy. Tim and Spot didn't mind, they liked to play in the mud. They ran and jumped in the puddles, splashing each other with water.
Soon, they were both soaked from the rain and mud. "We're so wet!" said Tim, laughing. Spot barked and wagged his tail, happy to be playing with his best friend. They played until the rain stopped and then went home, leaving the muddy field behind.

Once upon a time, there was a humble cat named Tom. Tom lived in a small house with a big window. He liked to sit by the window and look outside at the birds.
One day, Tom saw a bird sitting on a tree outside the window. He said, "I want to eat that bird." So, Tom opened the window and jumped out. But when he jumped, he fell into a big mud puddle.
Tom looked up and saw the bird flying away. He was sad and dirty. Tom's friend, a little girl named Sue, saw him and said, "Tom, you should not try to eat the birds. They are our friends." Tom listened to Sue and felt bad.
From that day on, Tom never tried to eat the birds again. He still liked to sit by the window and watch them, but now he enjoyed their songs and did not want to eat them. Tom learned that it is important to be kind to all creatures, big and small.

Once upon a time, there was a little orange bird named Tim. Tim lived in a big tree with his family. Tim liked to fly and play with his friends.
One day, Tim saw a small skull on the ground. He was curious and asked his mom, "What is this?" His mom said, "That is a skull. It helps protect an animal's head."
Tim wanted to protect the skull, so he picked it up with his beak and put it in a safe place. He told his friends, "We must protect this skull. It is important."
His friends agreed, and they all took turns watching the skull. They made sure it was safe and protected. Tim and his friends felt proud that they could help protect something important.

John and Mary were friends. They liked to do things together. One day, they had a disagreement.
Mary wanted to play with dolls and John wanted to play with blocks.
John said, "Let's play with blocks!"
Mary disagreed. She said, "No, let's play with dolls!"
John didn't like that. He said, "No! I want to play with blocks!"
Mary felt sad because she wanted to play with dolls. Then she had an idea. She said, "Let's each play with our own toys and make a message with them!"
John thought about it, then he smiled and agreed.
Mary and John each played with their own toys and soon they had a message. It was an original message that only they could understand.
The two friends were so happy to be able to make something together. They played together for the rest of the day.

Once upon a time, there was a stubborn little girl named Lucy. She loved flowers more than anything. One day, she found a very pretty flower in her garden. It was red and yellow and made her smile.
Lucy wanted to celebrate her new flower with her friend, Tim. She went to his house and knocked on the door. Tim opened the door and said, "Hi, Lucy! What do you have there?" Lucy showed him the flower and said, "Look! I found a pretty flower! Let's celebrate!"
Tim and Lucy had a fun day together. They played games, sang songs, and danced around the flower. They were very happy to celebrate their special flower. And from that day on, they always remembered the fun they had with the pretty red and yellow flower.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Mia who was very adventurous. She loved to explore and do new things. 
One day Mia decided she would try baking something. She had never done this before but was excited to get started. She got out some ingredients and made a delicious cake. 
Then she decided to do something special with her cake. She took it to a nearby stadium where a soccer game was going on. 
She proudly put the cake down in the middle of the field and everyone was amazed. They all enjoyed the cake and were so happy to have something so delicious to eat. 
Mia smiled and then ran off, feeling very proud of her adventurous baking adventure.

One day, a little girl named Sue went for a walk. She saw a big rock and on top of it, there was a red strawberry. Sue wanted to eat the strawberry, but it was too high for her to reach.
Sue saw a friendly bird named Bob. She said, "Bob, can you help me get the strawberry from the rock?" Bob said, "Yes, I can help you, Sue." Bob flew up to the rock and picked up the strawberry with his beak. He brought it down to Sue.
Sue was so happy. She said, "Thank you, Bob!" She ate the strawberry, and it was very sweet. Sue and Bob played together all day, and they felt safe and happy. They knew they would always help each other when they needed it.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Meena. Meena really liked math, it was so adventurous and fun. She loved to add and use her numbers in different ways. One day, Meena's Mom said she had a surprise for her; she was going to teach Meena how to make a pie. Meena was so excited, but she was worried too. She had never made anything with math before!
That night, Meena and her Mom were baking the pie together. Meena wrote down all of the ingredients and then had to use math to add them all up. She found it hard at first but with her Mom's help, soon she was an expert at adding.
When the pie was finally done, Meena was so proud of herself. She had gone on an adventurous math journey and learnt something new. Meena was so happy with the success of her pie. She enjoyed eating it so much after all of her hard work!

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a unique cat named Kitty. Kitty had a basket that she loved very much. Every day, she would sit in her basket and listen to the birds sing outside.
One day, Kitty saw a big, red ball outside the window. She wanted to play with it. So, she jumped out of her basket and ran to the door. She opened the door and went outside to play with the big, red ball.
Kitty played with the ball for a long time. She forgot to listen for her mom calling her. When it was time to go home, Kitty could not hear her mom. She was lost and could not find her way back to her basket. Kitty felt sad and scared because she could not go home.

Once upon a time, there was a big bird who loved to soar high in the sky. One sunny day, the bird saw a pepper on the ground. The bird was very hungry, so it flew down to eat the pepper.
As the bird picked up the pepper, it felt something was not right. The pepper looked serious, not like other peppers the bird had seen before. The bird was curious and decided to take the pepper to its friends.
When the bird showed the pepper to its friends, they all laughed. The pepper was not a pepper at all! It was a tiny red hat that looked like a pepper. The bird and its friends had fun trying on the hat and playing with it. From that day on, they all learned that sometimes things are not what they seem.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a folder on the floor. He was frightened because he did not know who it belonged to. He wanted to replace it where it came from, but he could not remember where that was. Tim felt sad.
He asked his friend, Sam, "Do you know who this folder belongs to?" Sam shook his head and said, "No, I don't know." Tim and Sam looked around, but they could not find the owner of the folder.
They saw a big boy named Max. Max was not nice. He took the folder from Tim and said, "This is mine now!" Tim and Sam were very frightened. They did not know what to do. In the end, Max kept the folder, and Tim and Sam felt sad and scared.

One day, a tall driver named Tom went to the park. He saw a little boy trying to ride a bike. The boy was sad because he could not do it. Tom wanted to help the boy.
Tom went to the boy and said, "You can do it! I will help you." He encouraged the boy to try again. The boy got on the bike and Tom held it up. They tried and tried, and the boy started to smile.
Finally, the boy rode the bike all by himself. Tom was very happy for the boy. The boy said, "Thank you, tall driver!" They both laughed and played in the park all day.

Once upon a time, a boy named Tom wanted to ride his bike. He saw a big pot in the distant yard. He thought it was very funny. Tom rode his bike very fast to the pot.
When Tom got to the pot, he saw a girl named Sue. Sue said, "This is my pot. Do you want to play with me?" Tom said, "Yes, let's play!" They played with the pot and had a lot of fun.
But then, a big wind came. The wind blew the pot away. Tom and Sue were sad. They could not play with the pot anymore. The wind was too strong, and they could not find the pot again.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big heart and was very kind to everyone. Lily loved to share her toys and play with her friends.
One day, a new boy named Max moved to the town. Max was very rude and did not want to share. He took Lily's toys and made her sad. Lily's friends did not like Max because he was not nice.
Lily wanted to help Max be a better friend. She insisted that Max say sorry and share the toys. Max listened to Lily and started to change. He said sorry and shared the toys. Soon, Max became a good friend, and everyone was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom and her purple cat, Muffin. One day, Lily saw her mom lift a big cup of coffee. Lily wanted to help her mom, so she asked, "Can I lift the coffee too?"
Her mom smiled and said, "You can try, but be careful. It's very hot." Lily tried to lift the coffee, but it was too heavy for her. She dropped it and the coffee spilled all over the floor. Lily was sad and her mom was upset.
Muffin the cat saw what happened and wanted to help. Muffin jumped on the table and knocked down a box of cookies. The cookies fell on the floor, making a big mess. Lily and her mom looked at each other and started to laugh.
Lily's mom said, "It's okay to make mistakes, as long as we learn from them." They all cleaned up the mess together. From that day on, Lily learned to ask for help when she needed it, and she never tried to lift hot coffee again.

One day, a little dog named Spot was in the park. He saw a big tree with a fake bird on a branch. Spot was scared of the bird and started to tremble.
Spot ran to his friend, a cat named Fluffy. "Fluffy, there is a big scary bird in the tree!" Spot said. Fluffy looked at the tree and saw the bird. "Don't worry, Spot. That bird is fake. It won't hurt you," Fluffy said.
They went closer to the tree and saw a bulb on the ground. Spot and Fluffy dug a hole and put the bulb in it. They knew that soon, a pretty flower would grow. Spot was happy and not scared anymore. The fake bird had helped them find the bulb and make the park even more beautiful.

Tom and Lily went to the park with Mom. They saw a big pond with many birds. They saw ducks, geese and swans. Tom liked the swans. They were white and had long necks. He wanted to touch one.
"Can I touch a swan, Mom?" Tom asked.
"No, Tom, you can't. Swans are not nice. They can bite and push you. They don't like people to touch them. You can look at them, but stay away from them," Mom said.
But Tom did not listen. He saw a swan that was close to the edge of the pond. He ran to it and tried to touch its feathers. The swan did not like that. It hissed and flapped its wings. It pushed Tom with its beak. Tom fell down and cried.
"Ow, ow, ow! The swan pushed me! It hurt me!" Tom shouted.
Mom ran to Tom and picked him up. She hugged him and checked his arm. It was red and had a small mark.
"I'm sorry, Tom. Are you okay?" Mom asked.
"It hurts, Mom. The swan was not nice. It was mean," Tom said.
"I know, Tom. I told you to stay away from the swans. They are not nice. They are wild animals. They don't like people to touch them. You have to be careful and listen to me," Mom said.
"I'm sorry, Mom. I will listen to you next time. I don't like swans anymore. I like ducks. They are nice. Can we feed the ducks?" Tom asked.
"Okay, Tom. We can feed the ducks. But only with the bread that I have. Don't touch the ducks either. They might bite you too. And be nice to them. Don't throw the bread. Break it into small pieces and drop it gently," Mom said.
Tom and Lily nodded. They followed Mom to the other side of the pond. They saw some ducks that were swimming and quacking. They fed the ducks with the bread. The ducks were nice. They ate the bread and came closer. They did not bite or push. Tom and Lily smiled and laughed. They had fun with the ducks. They forgot about the swan.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a boy named Tim. He was a very sleepy boy. One day, Tim's mom asked him to sort his toys. "Tim, please sort your toys," she said.
Tim didn't want to sort his toys. He was too sleepy. But he knew that if he did, he would get a prize. So, he tried to sort his toys, but he was so sleepy that he made a big mess.
Tim's mom saw the mess and was sad. "Oh no, Tim," she said. "You didn't sort your toys well. Now there is no prize for you." Tim felt bad and learned that it's important to do things well, even when you're sleepy.

Lucy was scared. She was in the dark and alone. She looked around and saw a candle on the floor.
"Where it came from?" she whispered to herself.
She walked over to it. It was black and had long legs. She leaned down and picked it up.
Suddenly, the candle started to burn. Lucy jumped back and screamed. The flame was so bright, it scared her.
But soon the flame went out and the room was dark again. Lucy leaned back and hugged the candle. She was so relieved. 
But then she heard a strange noise coming from outside. She leaned in closer to the window and saw three black figures walking towards her house. They were getting closer and closer.
Lucy was so scared she dropped the candle. It broke and the whole house went dark. She never saw the figures again but she never felt safe again.

Lily and Max were twins. They liked to draw and write in their notebooks. They had many notebooks with different colors and pictures on them.
One day, their mom said, "We are going to the park. You can bring one notebook each. Choose the one you like best."
Lily and Max ran to their room. They looked at their notebooks. Lily liked the pink one with flowers. Max liked the blue one with cars. They picked up their notebooks and showed them to their mom.
"Good choice," their mom said. "Now put on your hats and shoes. Let's go to the park."
At the park, Lily and Max found a bench under a big tree. They opened their notebooks and started to draw and write. Lily drew a butterfly and wrote, "I love nature." Max drew a rocket and wrote, "I want to go to space."
They showed each other their drawings and writings. They smiled and said, "You are very good."
They were gentle with their notebooks. They did not tear or stain them. They closed them and put them in their backpacks. They thanked their mom for taking them to the park. They had a fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Bella. Bella had a small nest in a big tree. She loved her nest very much. One day, Bella was hungry. She flew around to find some yummy food.
Bella found a big, red apple. She took a bite and said, "This is so yummy!" A little girl named Anna saw Bella eating the apple. Anna said, "I believe you like that apple, Bella!"
Bella and Anna became friends. They played and ate yummy food together every day. Bella was happy to have a friend who believed in her. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a clever fox named Foxy. Foxy lived in a big forest. One day, Foxy went for a walk to find some food. While walking, he saw a small house. Foxy wanted to meet who lived there.
When Foxy knocked on the door, a kind girl named Lucy opened it. Lucy was happy to meet Foxy. She wanted to give Foxy a gift. Lucy gave Foxy a big, red apple. Foxy loved apples and said "thank you" to Lucy.
Foxy learned that being kind and saying "thank you" made others happy. He went back to the forest and shared the apple with his friends. Foxy and his friends learned to be kind and say "thank you" too. The moral of the story is to always be kind and say "thank you".

One day, a gray cat named Tom went to the store. He wanted to buy sauce for his dinner. Tom saw many sauces at the store. He could not choose which one to buy. So, he started to number them. "One, two, three," Tom said. He counted all the sauce jars.
Just then, a little girl named Sally came to the store. She wanted to help Tom. "Do you need help?" Sally asked. "Yes, please," Tom said. "I want to find the best sauce for my dinner." Sally looked at all the sauces and picked one. "This one is good," she said.
Tom and Sally went to the checkout to pay for the sauce. But, the sauce jar started to shake! It jumped out of Tom's hands and broke on the floor. The sauce was alive! It turned into a big sauce monster. Tom and Sally were very surprised. "Oh no!" they said. But the sauce monster just wanted to be friends. They all laughed and played together. And Tom found a new sauce for his dinner.

One day, a little boy named Will went to the park. He wanted to play with his original toy car. He loved that car because it was special and different from all the other cars. Will was very happy to play with his car.
At the park, Will met a girl named Lily. She had a pretty doll. Will and Lily played together. They had a lot of fun. They laughed and talked. Will said, "I like your doll, Lily." And Lily said, "I like your car, Will."
But then, Will could not find his car. He started to panic. He looked everywhere. He asked Lily, "Have you seen my car?" Lily said, "No, but let's look for it together." They looked under the slide, behind the swings, and in the sandbox.
Finally, they found the car near the big tree. Will was so happy! He thanked Lily for helping him. They played some more and had a great day at the park. Will learned that friends can help when you panic, and it's always more fun to play together.

Once there was a family of squirrels who lived in a shelter in a very tall tree. The mom squirrel had two little baby squirrels. One day, the family decided to go for a walk and explore the woods. The two baby squirrels happily ran and played together, trying to catch each other. 
As they were playing, they noticed an attractive nut that was lying on the ground. Its shiny color made it stand out from the rest of the forest. They both got excited and started to run towards it.
When they were almost there, they heard their mom squirrel shout, "Wait!" They stopped and listened.
Mom said, "I know you want the nut, but it can be dangerous. Someone might have left it there to try and make you bite it. You do not know what it might be. Instead, it is better to just stay away. That's the safest way to go."
The two little squirrels listened to their mom and immediately stopped frolicking towards the nut. They knew that it was important to listen to their mom and be safe. It was attractive, but not worth the danger.

Once upon a time, there was a little crab. The crab lived near the sea. The crab liked to play in the sand and swim in the water. One day, the crab found a delicious apple on the beach. The crab was very happy and wanted to eat the apple.
A little girl saw the crab with the apple. She wanted the apple too. She asked the crab, "Can I have the apple, please?" The crab did not know what to do. The crab did a shrug with its little claws.
The little girl saw the crab shrug and said, "It's okay, we can share the apple." So, the little girl and the crab sat on the sand and ate the delicious apple together. They both were very happy and became good friends. From that day on, they played and shared many things together.

Once upon a time, in a small house with a purple door, there lived a kind man named Tom. Tom liked to repair things. One day, he saw that his porch was broken. He thought, "I need to fix my porch."
Tom went to his friend, Sam. He said, "Sam, can you help me repair my porch?" Sam nodded and said, "Yes, Tom! I will help you." They both went to Tom's house with their tools.
They worked hard to fix the porch. They used hammers and nails. After a while, the porch was fixed. Tom and Sam were happy. They sat on the porch and talked. The sun was shining, and it was a good day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Emily. She was three years old and loved to explore new places. One day, she found a lab and was super excited to go inside.
As she opened the door, Emily felt a chill and started to shiver. Curiously, she looked around and saw a lot of big machines and buttons.
Suddenly, a voice called out from the corner: "Can you help me? I'm so lonely here." Emily looked and saw a robot with big eyes. She was scared but bravely stepped closer.
The robot said, "My name is Bob. I was made here in this lab, but now I'm all alone. Can you help me find a friend?"
Emily smiled and said, "Yes! I know the perfect friend for you. His name is Ted and he's deaf, so you won't even have to worry about talking."
Bob smiled and said, "That sounds great! Let's go find Ted!"
And so, Emily and Bob went off to find Ted and make him a new friend. The end.

Once upon a time, a penguin wanted to build a new home. The penguin was not happy with his old home. He wanted a big and nice home. So, he started to build his new home with ice and snow.
One day, while the penguin was building, he saw something scary. It was a big shadow. The penguin was scared and hid behind his new home. He did not know what the scary thing was.
But then, the scary shadow came closer. The penguin saw that it was not scary at all! It was just a big, friendly whale. The whale wanted to help the penguin build his new home. The penguin was happy, and they built the new home together.

One day, a warm and soft cat named Fluffy went for a walk in the woods. Fluffy liked to sleep on soft things. While walking, Fluffy saw a big tree with soft leaves. Fluffy thought, "I want to sleep on those leaves!"
Fluffy saw an axe next to the tree. Fluffy did not know what an axe was. Fluffy said, "I will use this to get the leaves." Fluffy tried to use the axe, but it was too heavy. Fluffy was sad.
Just then, a friendly bird flew down and said, "Fluffy, I can help you get the leaves." The bird picked up the leaves and gave them to Fluffy. Fluffy was so happy and said, "Thank you, bird!" Fluffy made a bed with the leaves and slept on it. The bird and Fluffy became good friends.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had many toys, but his favorite was a toy gun. He liked to play with it all day long.
One day, Tim's mom told him it was time to clean his room. She said he could only keep some of his toys. Tim had to choose which toys were important to him. He looked at his toy gun and thought about how much fun he had playing with it.
Tim decided that his toy gun was very important to him. He put it in a special box with his other important toys. Then, he gave the rest of his toys to other kids who would love them. Tim was happy because he chose the toy gun and made other kids happy too.

One day, a little boy named Tim wandered into a big room. In the room, there was a large desk. On the desk, there were many things like pens, paper, and a big book. Tim felt very curious about what was in the big book.
He climbed on the desk and opened the big book. Inside, there were many pictures and words. Tim felt very smart looking at the book. A man walked into the room and saw Tim on the desk. The man said, "Tim, you should not be on the desk. That is not a smart thing to do."
Tim felt stupid and sad. He climbed down from the desk and said sorry to the man. The man went away, and Tim was left alone in the room. He wanted to look at the book again, but he knew it was not a good idea. Tim wandered out of the room, feeling very sad.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, Jack and Lucy. They went to the park together every day.
One day, they went to the park and saw a fire. Jack wanted to get close and break it. Lucy said, "No Jack! That's not a good idea!". But Jack didn't listen. He got close and he broke the fire.
The fire burned even bigger and everything around it was on fire. Then, Jack and Lucy both started to cry.
The fireman came and put the fire out. But they had broken the fire and now they were in trouble. They knew they had made a bad choice and they felt very sad. 
The fireman said, "You two were not very good. I'm sorry it ended like this". The fireman then drove away, and Jack and Lucy were all alone.

Once upon a time, there was a smart girl named Lucy. She loved to play with her toy scale. She would put her toys on the scale to see which one was heavy and which one was light.
One day, Lucy's mom told her that it is important to value her toys. She said that all toys are special and we should take care of them. Lucy listened to her mom and promised to value her toys.
From that day on, Lucy played with her scale and her toys with love. She made sure that all her toys were clean and safe. Everyone knew that Lucy was a smart girl who valued her toys.

Once upon a time, a big bear and a little rabbit lived on Earth. They were best friends. They loved to play together and find interesting things in the woods. One day, they found a big, shiny rock. They had never seen anything like it before.
The big bear wanted to keep the shiny rock, but the little rabbit said no. The big bear refused to give it to the rabbit. They both wanted the shiny rock very much. They were sad because they could not agree.
Then, something unexpected happened. A tiny mouse came and took the shiny rock away. The big bear and the little rabbit were surprised. They stopped fighting and became friends again. They learned that their friendship was more important than any shiny rock.

Once upon a time, there was a spoiled little girl called Laura. Every day, she'd wake up and enter the kitchen to get her breakfast. Every now and then, she could hear the phone ringing in the house. 
One day, while Laura was having breakfast, she heard the phone ringing louder than usual. She hopped excitedly off her chair and asked her mom, "Can I answer it?" 
Her mom smiled and said, "Alright, it might be a surprise for you."
Laura picked up the phone and a friendly voice said, "Hello Laura, this is Grandmamma! I wanted to call and say I'm coming to visit this weekend."
Laura was so happy that she screamed with joy. She put her hands to her mouth and ran to tell her mom the news.
"Mommy! Grandmamma is coming to visit!" Laura exclaimed.
Mommy hugged her daughter and said, "That's wonderful! We'd better start getting things ready!"
Laura was so excited to see her Grandmamma that she started dancing around the house and singing. Grandmamma was sure to be pleased with her spoiled little granddaughter!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy car with four wheels. He loved to play with his car and make it go fast. One day, Tim's room was very messy. His mom asked him to clean it up. She would encourage him by saying, "Good job, Tim!"
As Tim was cleaning, he found a big wheel under his bed. He had never seen it before. Tim thought it was a new toy! He put the big wheel on his toy car, and it went very fast. Tim was so happy and excited.
But then, something unexpected happened. The big wheel came off the car and started rolling away! Tim chased after it, but it was too fast. The wheel rolled out the door and into the yard. Tim followed it and found a big bike with one wheel missing. He realized the big wheel was from the bike! Tim put the wheel back on the bike and learned how to ride it. Now, Tim had a new, fun thing to do outside of his messy room.

Once upon a time, there was a little duck named Ducky. Ducky lived in a small pond near his home. One day, Ducky met a new friend, a frog named Froggy. They liked to play and splash in the water together.
One day, Ducky and Froggy had a fight. Ducky said, "I can splash bigger than you!" Froggy said, "No, I can splash bigger!" They were both very mad. They did not play together for the rest of the day.
The next day, Ducky's mom told him, "It is important to be kind to your friends." Ducky thought about it and went to find Froggy. Ducky said, "I am sorry. Let's be friends and splash together again." Froggy was happy and said, "I am sorry too. Let's play!" They played and splashed happily in the water. They learned that being kind to friends is more important than being the best at splashing.

One day, a little boy named Tim was watching his favorite cartoon on TV. He loved the funny animals and the silly things they did. Tim laughed and clapped his hands as he watched the cartoon.
Tim had a big chair in the living room. He liked to lean on it when he watched TV. It was a safe place for him to sit and enjoy his cartoons. His mom always told him to be careful and not to lean too much.
One day, while Tim was leaning on his chair and watching his cartoon, the chair started to wobble. Tim got scared, but he held on tight. Suddenly, the chair stopped wobbling, and Tim felt safe again. He learned to be more careful when leaning on his chair, and he continued to enjoy his favorite cartoons.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big toy box with many toys inside. But there was one toy he wanted to play with the most. It was a long cut of string. The string was not available to play with because it was on a high shelf.
Tim looked up at the high shelf and thought about how to get the string. He dared to try and climb the shelf. But as he started to climb, his mom saw him and said, "No, Tim! That's too high for you!"
Tim was sad but did not give up. He found a tall chair and dared to use it to reach the string. He climbed on the chair and got the string. Tim was very happy to have the string available to play with. His mom saw him and said, "Good job, Tim! But next time, ask for help!"
From that day on, Tim learned to ask for help when he needed it. He and his mom played with the long cut of string, making it into fun shapes and having a great time together.

Once upon a time there was a dependable little girl called Jenna. Jenna loved to help out at her family's market stall every weekend.
At the stall, Jenna was in charge of selling the apples and oranges. She was very good at her job and the customers were always impressed with how quickly Jenna could sell all the fruit.
Jenna had a special selling tactic of offering the customers a free leg of an orange if they bought two boxes. She always managed to sell all the fruit in no time and she was very proud of her achievements.
One day, Jenna was enjoying the sunshine when she fell down and hurt her leg. She was taken to the doctor and had to wear a cast for several months.
Jenna was disappointed that she would not be able to help out at the market stall anymore, but her family reassured her that they would all take it in turns. Jenna was happy to know that they were so dependable.
Soon Jenna was back on her feet and helping out at the market with her family. She felt so proud to be part of such a wonderful team.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Ruth who lived in a small village. Everywhere she looked she saw wide fields with steam coming from the ground. Though she wanted to go outside and play, her mother always said no.
One day, Ruth asked her mother if she could go play in the wide field. Her mother said no again, but this time she said that there was something special in the field she could discover if she waited patiently.
So Ruth waited, and waited, and waited until finally steam started to come out of the wide field. She shouted with excitement and ran out to investigate.
It turned out that the steam was coming from a hot bathtub full of boiling water. A wise old man nearby said, "Be wary of running into hot water, lest you burn yourself!" Ruth thanked the old man and ran home, happy to have learned an important lesson. 
The moral of this story is to be careful and never take risks if you don't know what the consequences may be. Patience and wisdom are the best roads to take in life.

The little girl was very excited. She had been looking forward to having ice-cream all day. Her mom handed her the bowl of ice-cream and she couldn't wait to try it. She licked it and it tasted delicious. 
But then she accidently spilled some. Her mom smiled and said "It's ok sweetie. Let's get a tissue to wipe it up". The little girl got a tissue and wiped up the mess.
After she finished cleaning up, her mom gave her a hug. She kissed her on the head and said "Thank you for being so kind". The little girl smiled and said "You're welcome, mommy." Then she enjoyed eating her delicious ice-cream.

Once upon a time, there was a baby named Tim. Tim was a very creative baby. He loved to play with his toys and make up fun games.
One day, Tim was playing with his toy crab. He saw that the crab could pinch with its claws. Tim thought it would be fun to pretend to be a crab. He walked around the room, pinching the air with his little fingers.
Tim's mom saw him playing and asked, "What are you doing, Tim?" Tim replied, "I'm a crab, Mommy! Pinch, pinch!" Tim's mom laughed and said, "You are a creative little crab, Tim!"
They played together, pinching the air and laughing. Tim was very happy to be a creative baby crab with his mom. And they lived happily ever after.

Ben and Mia like to play sport. They like to run and kick and throw. One day, they go to the park with Mom and Dad. They see a big field with white snow.
"Can we play sport in the snow?" Ben asks.
"Sure, but you need to wear warm clothes and boots," Mom says. "And be careful, the snow can be slippery."
Ben and Mia put on their hats and gloves and coats and boots. They run to the field. They see some kids making a snowman. They see some kids throwing snowballs. They see some kids sliding on the ice.
"Let's play snowball fight!" Mia says.
"OK, but don't hit too hard," Ben says.
They make some snowballs and hide behind a tree. They see a boy and a girl walking by. They throw their snowballs at them.
"Hey, stop that!" the boy says. "That's not nice!"
"Sorry, we just want to play," Mia says.
"Play with us, then," the girl says. "We are playing freeze tag. Do you know how to play?"
"No, how do you play?" Ben asks.
"It's easy. One person is it. They have to chase the others and touch them. If they touch you, you have to freeze and stand still. You can only move again if another player touches you and unfreezes you. The last person to be frozen is the new it. Do you want to try?" the girl explains.
"Sure, that sounds fun," Ben says.
"OK, I'll be it first. Ready, set, go!" the boy says.
He runs after Ben and Mia and the other kids. He touches Mia and she freezes. She looks like a snow statue.
"Help me, Ben!" she calls.
Ben tries to help her, but the boy is fast. He touches Ben and he freezes too. He feels cold and stiff.
"Ha, ha, I got you both!" the boy says.
He looks for the other kids. He sees the girl running away. He chases her. He is about to touch her, when he slips on the ice. He falls down and hurts his knee. He cries out.
"Ow, ow, ow! That's bad!" he says.
The girl stops and runs back to him. She helps him get up.
"Are you OK?" she asks.
"No, my knee hurts. I don't want to play anymore," he says.
He limps to the bench where Mom and Dad are sitting. They check his knee and give him a hug.
Ben and Mia unfreeze. They feel sorry for the boy.
"Are you OK?" they ask.
"No, he's not. He hurt his knee. He needs to rest," the girl says.
"Can we do something to make him feel better?" Mia asks.
"Maybe you can give him a snowman or a snowball as a gift," the girl says.
"OK, let's do that," Ben says.
They make a small snowman and a snowball. They put them next to the boy on the bench.
"Here, these are for you. We hope you feel better soon," they say.
"Thank you. That's very nice of you. I'm sorry I was mean to you before. Do you want to be friends?" the boy says.
"Yes, we do. We like to play sport with you. Maybe next time we can play something else," they say.
They smile and hug. They become friends. They play sport in the snow. They have fun. The end.

Tommy was a 3 year old boy. He loved to explore the world around him. One day, his mommy asked him to help her in the kitchen. She told him to get some oil from the cupboard. But when he opened the door, he heard a strange noise. He was frightened.
"What is it?" he asked his mommy.
She was a bit scared too, but she tried to stay calm so she could help verify what it was.
"It sounds like a mouse," she said.
Tommy slowly reached into the cupboard and slowly, slowly, he grabbed the bottle of oil. But suddenly the mouse jumped out. Tommy screamed and dropped the oil bottle. It spilled all over the floor and made a big mess.
The foreshadowing of the strange noise and the frightened feelings of both Tommy and his mom led to the unfortunate ending of the spilled oil. They both rushed to clean it up, but there was nothing that could be done. It was a mess.

Once upon a time, there was a big, filthy TV. It was so dirty that no one could see the pictures on it. The TV was sad because it wanted to show fun shows to the kids. 
One day, a nice lady came to the house. She saw the filthy TV and wanted to help. The lady got a soft cloth and some polish to make the TV clean and shiny. 
She started to polish the TV very carefully. The dirt went away, and the TV became clean and bright. The kids were so happy to see their favorite shows again. The TV was happy too, because now it could make the kids smile. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once there was a little girl who found it difficult to sleep. Every night, she would lay in bed and think of many things. One day, her mom told her a secret. She said, "When you close your eyes, a kind spirit will help you sleep."
That night, the little girl closed her eyes and waited for the spirit. But instead of the kind spirit, a funny bunny came to her room. The bunny said, "I am here to help you sleep, but first, let's have a little fun!" The bunny showed her how to make shadow animals on the wall with her hands. They made shadow birds, dogs, and cats. The girl laughed and had so much fun with the bunny.
As the night went on, the girl started to feel tired. The bunny noticed and said, "Now it's time to sleep." The girl closed her eyes, and the bunny sang a sweet song. The girl slept well that night. The next morning, she told her mom about the funny bunny. Her mom smiled and said, "The kind spirit sent the bunny to show you that sometimes, having fun and laughing can help you sleep better." From that day on, the little girl always remembered to have fun and laugh, and she never had a difficult time sleeping again.

Tim and Jen are playing soccer in the park. They like to kick the ball and run after it. They are on the same team and they wear red shirts.
"Look, Jen, I can kick the ball very far!" Tim says. He kicks the ball with all his might. The ball flies over the fence and lands on the road.
"Oh no, Tim, you kicked the ball too hard!" Jen says. "How will we get it back?"
They run to the fence and look for the exit. They see a gate that says "Exit". They open the gate and go to the road. They see a big truck coming. The truck is very loud and fast.
"Tim, Jen, come back!" their mom shouts. She sees them on the road. She is scared. She runs to the exit and grabs them. She pulls them back to the park. She hugs them and says, "You are safe now. But you should never go to the road without me. The road is dangerous. Do you understand?"
Tim and Jen nod. They are sorry. They say, "We are sorry, mom. We just wanted to get our ball back. We did not see the truck."
Their mom says, "I know, but you have to be careful. Next time, ask me for help. I will get your ball for you. OK?"
Tim and Jen say, "OK, mom. Thank you."
Their mom says, "You are welcome. Now, let's go and find your ball. Maybe it is still on the road. Or maybe someone picked it up. Come on, let's go."
Tim and Jen follow their mom. They hope they can find their ball. They like to play soccer. They are proud of their kicks. But they also know they have to be safe. They will not go to the road again. They will stay in the park. They will listen to their mom. They will have fun.

Jim and his dad were looking for something special. 
They went on a walk and soon enough they found a lake. 
The lake was very impressive. There were lots of trees and plants around and the water was so blue. 
Jim was so excited to be by the lake. 
He made a little boat out of leaves and put it in the water. 
He watched it float around the lake, and then his dad brought him some chips to eat. 
Jim and his dad sat by the lake and had a wonderful day.

One day, a tall boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw the pretty sunset in the sky. The colors were pink, orange, and yellow. Tim liked to watch the sunset every day.
As he walked, he saw a small cat. The cat was stuck in a tree. Tim heard the cat call, "Meow! Help me, please!" Tim wanted to help the cat. He tried to reach up, but he was not tall enough.
Just then, a big bird flew down from the sky. The bird said, "I can help you save the cat!" Tim was surprised. He did not know that birds could talk! The bird picked up the cat from the tree and put it down on the ground. The cat was happy and thanked Tim and the bird. Tim learned that even if you are not tall enough, friends can help you do big things.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy mule named Bob. Bob lived on a farm with his friend, a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to climb trees and play with Bob. One day, Tim wanted to climb a big tree near the farm.
Tim said, "Bob, let's climb the big tree together!" But Bob was grumpy and did not want to climb. He said, "No, Tim. I don't want to climb the tree. I am grumpy today."
Tim was sad, but he had an idea. He went to the kitchen and found a big, yummy carrot. Tim said, "Bob, if you climb the tree with me, I will give you this carrot." Bob loved carrots, so he agreed to climb the tree.
Tim and Bob climbed the big tree together. They had so much fun, and Bob was not grumpy anymore. When they came down, Tim gave Bob the yummy carrot. They were both very happy and played together all day.

One day, a little flea was very impatient. He wanted to open a door. The door was big and red. The flea was too small to open the door all by himself. He felt sad.
The flea asked his friend, a tiny ant, to help him. They both tried to open the door together. They pushed and pushed, but the door did not open. They were tired and upset.
Then, a big, strong dog walked by. The flea and the ant asked the dog for help. The dog was happy to help. With one big push, the dog opened the door. The flea, the ant, and the dog were all happy and played together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play with his big, round basketball. He would bounce it up and down, and throw it into the hoop. He was very good at it, and it made him very happy.
One day, Tim wanted to play with his basketball outside. He put on his tight shoes and ran out the door. But when he got outside, he saw a big puddle of water. Tim didn't want to spoil his basketball by getting it wet, so he thought of a plan.
Tim found a big basket and put his basketball inside. He carried the basket over the puddle and kept his basketball dry. He was so happy that he could play without spoiling his basketball. And that day, Tim played and played until the sun went down.

Once upon a time, there was a weird cat who loved jazz. The cat would dance and sing all day long. One day, the cat met a dog who also loved jazz.
The cat said, "I love jazz, do you love jazz too?" The dog replied, "Yes, I do! Let's dance together!" So, the cat and the dog danced and sang to the jazz music.
After dancing, the cat had an idea. The cat said, "Let's give a jazz show for all our friends!" The dog agreed, and they invited all their friends. The animals came and watched the cat and dog dance and sing. Everyone had a fun time!

Sara was a three year old girl who loved to make signs. One day, when Sara was looking for things in her house, she found some coal. She thought it was so pretty so she decided to make a sign with it.
As Sara was working on her sign, her mom came into the room. "Oh, what a lovely sign!" said her mom. She asked Sara where she got the coal.
"I found it in the house," said Sara.
"That's too bad," said her mom. "Coal is very expensive and it's not something you can just find around the house. I think it's best if you don't use it for your sign."
Sara was sad about that, but she listened to her mom. She put the coal away and decided to use something else for her sign. She could still make a pretty sign without spending a lot of money.

Once there was a young boy named Tim. He was very sick, so his mom called the nurse. The nurse was very kind and she came to visit him. She hugged Tim and he felt better. 
"Let's gather some flowers," the nurse said. 
So they gathered lots of colorful flowers from the garden. Tim smiled as he watched the flowers sway in the breeze.
Suddenly they saw a pale flower. It was the palest flower they had ever seen. 
The nurse said, "Let's pick it. Maybe it will help you get well!". 
So they picked the pale flower. But as soon as it was taken from the ground, it started to fade. 
The moral of the story is that it's important to respect nature. When people try to take something that doesn't belong to them, they may find that it brings them bad luck. Always stay away from things that don't belong to you.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was a loyal dog who loved his family very much. He liked to play on the floor with his toys and run around in the yard.
One day, Spot saw a big box on the floor. He was very curious and went to sniff it. As he got closer, the box started to shake and make noise. Spot was scared, but he was a brave dog, so he barked at the box to protect his family.
