All of a sudden, the box opened and out came a little girl named Lucy. She started to clap her hands and said "applaud". Spot was surprised but happy to see Lucy. They became best friends and played on the floor together every day.

One day, a clever cat and a dog were playing a game. They wanted to see who could win a big cup of juice. The cat said, "I will win the juice!" The dog said, "No, I will win the juice!"
They both ran very fast to get the juice. But when they got to the juice, they saw a big bird drinking it all. The cat and the dog were very sad. They did not win the juice. The bird said, "I'm sorry, I was very thirsty."
The cat and the dog thought of a new game. They wanted to see who could find the most juice. They looked and looked for more juice. The clever cat found a big box of juice. The dog found a small cup of juice. The cat and the dog were both happy. They had a big juice party and shared all the juice they found. Even the bird was invited to the party. They all had fun and became good friends.

One day, a dolphin named Bob was in the sea. He liked to spin in the water. He spun fast and slow. All his friends liked to watch him spin.
One day, a big fish said, "I can spin too, but I am bad at it. Can you show me how to spin?" Bob said, "Yes, I can help you spin. Let's try it together!" They spun and spun, but the big fish was still bad at spinning.
Then, something unexpected happened. The big fish was not a fish at all! He was a shark! The shark said, "I tricked you, Bob. I wanted to learn to spin so I can catch more fish." Bob was scared, but he had an idea. He said, "If you promise to be nice, I will teach you to spin." The shark thought about it and said, "Okay, I will be nice." So, Bob and the shark spun together, and the shark learned to spin. They became friends and the shark was not bad anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little train. The train was very happy. It liked to go fast and slow. One day, the train saw an angry bird. The bird was mad because it lost its food.
The train wanted to help the bird. So, the train stared at the ground. It looked for food for the bird. The train saw a big worm. The worm was wiggling on the ground.
The train showed the worm to the bird. The bird was not angry anymore. The bird ate the worm and was happy. The train and the bird became friends. They played together every day.

One day, a little cat felt helpless. It was lost and could not find its way home. The cat walked and walked until it saw a porch. On the porch, there was a big dog. The dog saw the cat and said, "Hello, little cat. Why do you look so sad?"
The cat told the dog, "I am lost and I can't find my home. I feel helpless." The dog wanted to provide help, so it asked the cat to stay on the porch with it. They became friends and played together. They were happy.
One day, while they were playing, a big wind came and blew the cat up into a tree. The dog tried to help, but it could not climb the tree. Just then, a little girl came and saw the helpless cat. She climbed the tree and saved the cat. The girl looked at the dog and said, "I know you! You are my lost dog!" The dog was happy to see the girl. The girl took the dog and the cat to her home, and they all lived together. The dog and the cat were not helpless anymore. They had a new home and a new friend.

Once upon a time there lived a little boy called Max. He was only three years old and he loved playing. One day he needed something to play with and so he went to visit Old Jane who lived in the house down the street.
Max asked Old Jane if he could borrow something to play with. Old Jane smiled and said, "I'm sure I have something here that you can borrow." She led him to a dark corner of her house and showed him an old toy. It was a bit scary but Max liked it.
Old Jane said, "You can borrow this if you promise to take good care of it."
Max promised to take care of it and thanked Old Jane. He went home with his new toy and played happily with it all day long.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to get a television for his room. He asked his mom, "Can I get a television, please?" His mom thought about it and said, "Yes, but we must find a harmless one."
They went to the store and saw many televisions. Tim saw a big one and said, "I want that one!" But his mom said, "No, that one is too big. Let's find a smaller, harmless one."
Tim and his mom found a small television that was just right. They took it home and put it in Tim's room. Tim was very happy with his harmless television. He said, "Thank you, Mom!" And they watched fun shows together.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily had a big, wide smile. She liked to play with her toys and skip around her room. One day, Lily decided to skip all the way to the bathroom.
As Lily skipped to the bathroom, she saw her cat, Mittens. Mittens was playing with a ball. Lily laughed and gave Mittens a big hug. Then, she continued to skip to the bathroom.
In the bathroom, Lily saw a pretty rainbow on the wall. It was from the sun shining through the window. Lily felt happy and clapped her hands. She skipped back to her room and told her toys all about the rainbow in the bathroom. They all had a fun day together.

One day, a little boy named Tim and his mom went to the park. Tim wore a coat with a big zip. It was cold outside, so he zipped it up to stay warm. Tim's mom said, "Stay close to me, so you are safe."
At the park, Tim saw a dog. The dog had a zip on its coat too. "Look, Mom! The dog has a zip like me!" Tim said. His mom smiled and said, "Yes, it is a safe coat for the dog too."
While they played, the dog's zip got stuck on a tree. The dog could not move. Tim and his mom went to help. Tim's mom said, "Be safe, Tim." Tim zipped his coat and went to the dog. He helped the dog by unzipping its coat. The dog was free and happy. Tim and his mom went home, feeling safe and warm with their zips.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They had a lot of fun playing with a ball. Tim saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. His mom said, "Tim, don't go too high, or you might fall."
Tim started to climb the tree, but he saw a terrible storm coming. The sky was dark and the wind was strong. Tim was scared and called for his mom. His mom said, "We need to go home, but we can stay under the tree for a little bit to delay getting wet."
As they stayed under the tree, a nice man with an umbrella came over. He said, "I saw you from my house. I can walk you home so you don't get wet." Tim and his mom were very happy and thanked the man.
The unexpected help from the nice man made Tim and his mom feel safe. They learned that sometimes, even in terrible situations, good things can happen, and it's important to help others when you can.

Once upon a time, there was a smart dog named Max. Max liked to dress up in fun clothes. He wore hats, shoes, and even a cape. Max loved to play with his friends in the park.
One day, Max saw a little bird trapped in a cage. The bird wanted to escape and fly away. Max wanted to help the bird. He put on his cape and became a hero.
Max used his smart brain and found the key to open the cage. He dressed fast and used his cape to fly to the bird. Max opened the cage and the bird was free. The bird thanked Max and they became best friends. They played together in the park every day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a wealthy man named Tom. Tom had a big tree in his yard. This tree had many cherries on it. The cherries were red and sweet.
One day, the sun began to rise, and Tom went outside to pick some cherries. He saw a little girl named Lily standing by the tree. Lily looked up at the cherries and said, "I wish I could reach them."
Tom smiled and said, "I can help you." He picked some cherries and gave them to Lily. She was very happy and said, "Thank you, Tom!"
From that day on, Tom and Lily became good friends. They would pick cherries together and share them with everyone in the town. The people in the town were happy and loved Tom and Lily. And the cherry tree grew taller and taller as the sun continued to rise.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. Lily was very excited because today she would make something new. She wanted to make a toy for her little brother, Tim. Lily knew Tim liked soft things, so she decided to use cloth.
Lily found some pretty cloth in her mom's room. It was blue and soft. Lily cut the cloth into two pieces. She sewed the pieces together and filled it with soft, fluffy stuff. Now, she had made a small, soft pillow for Tim.
Lily gave the soft pillow to Tim. Tim was so excited and happy. He hugged the pillow and smiled at Lily. Lily felt happy too because she made her brother happy. They played with the soft pillow all day long, and they had a lot of fun.

One day, a little dog was lost. The dog was poor and had no home. He was sad and hungry. He walked around the town, looking for food and a friend.
A kind girl saw the little dog. She felt bad for him. She gave him some food and water. The dog was very happy. The girl wanted to help the dog find a home.
The girl called the police to help the dog. The police came and took the dog to a safe place. They found a nice family for the dog. The dog was not poor and sad anymore. He was happy with his new family.

Once upon a time, in a big valley, there was a little dog named Max. Max liked to help and serve his friends. One day, Max's friend, a cat named Lily, said, "Max, I am ready to play!"
Max and Lily played in the valley, running and jumping. They were having a lot of fun. But then, something unexpected happened. A big bird came down and took Lily up into the sky!
Max was scared, but he wanted to save his friend. He ran and ran, and found a big tree. Max climbed the tree and saw Lily up high. He jumped and caught Lily just in time. The bird flew away, and Max and Lily were safe.
Max and Lily went back to the valley and told their friends about their adventure. They were happy to be together and ready to play again. And from that day on, the big bird never came back.

Once upon a time, there was a peaceful little town. In this town, there was a girl named Anna. Anna loved to take photos of her friends and family. She had a big box where she put all her photos.
One day, Anna took a photo of her dog, Buddy. Buddy was playing with a ball in the park. Anna thought it was a very nice photo. She put the photo in her big box with all the other photos.
Anna showed the photo to her mom and dad. They liked the photo of Buddy very much. They said, "What a peaceful day in the park with our dog!" Anna felt happy and proud of her photo. And they all had a nice, peaceful day together.

One day, a big animal arrives in the town. It is a spicy animal. It is red and has a big nose. The animal is nice and wants to play with the kids.
The kids see the spicy animal and want to play too. They run to the animal and give it a big hug. The animal is very happy. They play games like catch and hide-and-seek.
At the end of the day, the spicy animal has to go home. The kids are sad but they say bye. They hope the spicy animal will come back to play again soon.

One day, a little dog named Spot wanted to invite his friends to play. He went to his friend Cat's house and said, "Cat, come play with me!" Cat said, "Yes, I want to play too!" So, they went to the park together.
At the park, they saw a big ball. It was a perfect fit for them to play with. They started to play catch with the ball. They were having so much fun. Then, they saw a new sight. A big bird came to the park.
The big bird looked at Spot and Cat. It said, "Can I play with you?" Spot and Cat looked at the big bird and said, "Yes, come play with us!" The big bird was so happy. But then, something unexpected happened. The big bird kicked the ball and it flew far, far away.
Spot, Cat, and the big bird looked at each other. They were sad. But then, the big bird said, "Don't worry, I can fly and find the ball!" The big bird flew high in the sky and found the ball. It brought the ball back and they all played together. They had a very fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a lonely little frog named Freddy. Freddy had no friends because he was always dirty from playing in the mud. One day, a wise old owl saw Freddy looking sad and asked, "Why are you so sad, Freddy?"
Freddy said, "I am lonely and have no friends." The wise old owl thought with his big brain and said, "Freddy, you need to wipe the mud off and be clean. Then, others will want to be your friend."
Freddy listened to the wise old owl and wiped the mud off his body. He jumped into the pond to wash and came out looking clean and shiny. Soon, other animals in the forest saw the clean Freddy and wanted to be his friend. Freddy was no longer lonely and had many friends to play with.
The moral of the story is: when you take care of yourself and stay clean, you will make more friends.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily loved to learn new words. One sunny day, Lily met a big, friendly cat. The cat could talk and knew many perfect words. Lily was very happy to meet her new friend.
"Can you teach me a new word?" Lily asked the cat. The cat smiled and said, "Sure! I will mention a word and you try to say it. The word is 'spell'."
Lily tried to say the word, "Spell." She said it again and again. Soon, she could say it perfectly. Lily and the cat were very proud.
From that day on, Lily and the cat played together every day. They learned many new words and had lots of fun. And Lily's new friend always mentioned perfect words for her to learn.

Once upon a time, there was a big fish. The big fish lived in a deep lake. The big fish liked to swim and play with his friends. One day, the big fish saw a sign in the lake. The sign said, "Do not swim here."
The big fish did not like the sign. He wanted to change it. He thought, "I will swim here anyway." So, the big fish swam past the sign. He swam deeper and deeper into the lake.
As the big fish swam deeper, the water got darker and colder. The big fish started to feel scared. He wanted to go back to his friends, but he could not find his way. The big fish was lost and alone. He wished he had listened to the sign.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a modest ambulance. The ambulance was always ready to help people who were hurt or sick. It had a big heart and loved to make people feel better.
One sunny day, the ambulance was driving down the street when it saw a little girl named Lily. Lily was crying because she had fallen and hurt her knee. The ambulance stopped and said, "Hi Lily, I am here to help you. Let's talk about what happened."
Lily stopped crying and said, "I was running and I fell. My knee hurts a lot." The ambulance smiled and said, "Don't worry, Lily. I will take you to the doctor, and they will make your knee feel better."
Lily felt safe with the modest ambulance and they talked all the way to the doctor. The doctor helped Lily's knee feel better, and she was happy again. The ambulance was so happy it could help Lily and continued to help others in the town.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to decorate his room. He had a lot of fun things to make it look nice. One day, Tim found a big chain in his toy box. The chain was long and shiny. He thought it would be a good thing to use to decorate his room.
Tim hung the chain on his wall. It went up and down, and across his room. Tim thought it looked very impressive. He was happy with the way it made his room look. Tim showed his mom and dad his new decoration. They thought it was impressive too.
Tim's friends came to play at his house. They saw the chain on the wall and thought it was great. They all wanted to decorate their rooms with chains too. Tim and his friends had a fun day playing in his room with the impressive chain. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Mia found a shiny crown in her room. She put it on her head and went outside to show her friends. As she walked, she saw a small bug. The bug looked angry and was blinking its eyes at her. Mia asked the bug, "Why are you angry?"
The bug said, "I am angry because I lost my crown. I can't find it anywhere." Mia felt sad for the bug and wanted to help. She took off her shiny crown and gave it to the bug. The bug was so happy and stopped being angry. 
As the bug put on the crown, something unexpected happened. The bug turned into a beautiful butterfly! The butterfly thanked Mia and flew away. Mia learned that being kind to others can make them happy and sometimes lead to magical surprises.

Once upon a time, there was a big structure made of colorful blocks. It was in a playroom where many kids liked to play. They built the structure together and were very proud of it.
One day, a new kid came to play. He was not fit and had a hard time playing with the other kids. He wanted to play with the structure, too. But he was too big and clumsy. He tried to play with it, but he made a tear in the structure.
The kids saw the tear and were sad. They decided to help the new kid become fit. They played games with him and helped him learn how to play better. Soon, the new kid was fit and could play with the structure without making a tear. The kids were happy and played together every day.

One day, a cat named Tom went for a walk. He saw a big hill and wanted to go up. Tom was very happy to play on the hill. He ran up and down the hill many times. Tom was having a lot of fun.
Tom saw a big tree on the hill. He wanted to rub on the tree. As he rubbed on the tree, he felt something soft. It was a big red ball. Tom was very surprised. He had never seen a ball so big before.
Tom wanted to play with the big red ball. He pushed the ball down the hill. The ball rolled faster and faster. Suddenly, the ball popped! Tom was very embarrassed. He did not mean to pop the ball. But then, lots of small balls came out of the big red ball. Now, Tom had many balls to play with on the hill. He was very happy and not embarrassed anymore.

One day, a curious little bunny named Max hopped near a stream. He saw the water and wanted to play in it. Max was very happy and excited.
A wise old owl saw Max and warned him, "Max, be careful! The stream can be fast and you might get wet." Max listened to the owl and said, "Thank you, Mr. Owl. I will be careful."
Max played by the stream, splashing water with his paws. He had so much fun and stayed safe. The wise old owl watched and smiled. Max was happy he listened to the warning and had a great day.

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful musician named Tom. He loved to play music for everyone. He had a big drum, a small flute, and a shiny bell. Tom made people happy with his songs.
One day, Tom met a little girl named Sue. She was sad because she lost her toy. Tom wanted to help Sue feel better. He said, "I will play a song for you!" He played a happy tune on his flute. Sue started to smile and clap her hands.
Tom and Sue looked for the lost toy together. They looked under rocks and behind trees. At last, they found the toy under a big bush. Sue was so happy! She said, "Thank you, Tom! You are a great friend!" Tom felt proud that he could complete his mission to help Sue. They played music and danced together all day long.

Molly the cat was sitting on the icy rooftop of a big house. She was looking down at the chimney below her.
Molly the cat felt like rolling down the chimney, so she gave it a try. She rolled round and round, down the long and narrow chimney. 
At the bottom, Tom the mouse was talking to his brother. Suddenly they heard a loud thud!
Tom the mouse said, "What was that noise?".
His brother replied, "I think Molly the cat must have rolled down the chimney!"
The two little mice carefully looked up the chimney and sure enough, Molly the cat was looking back at them. 
The two little mice said hello to Molly the cat. Molly the cat had a big smile on her face!

One day, a little rabbit named Remy was playing in his chamber. It was a small, cozy place where he felt safe and warm. Remy liked to spring up and down in his chamber, because it was so much fun.
But one day, Remy felt worried. He heard a big noise outside his chamber. He did not know what it was. So, Remy decided to be brave and find out what was making the big noise.
Remy slowly opened the door and saw a big, friendly bear. The bear was trying to spring like Remy, but he was too big and heavy. Remy was not worried anymore. He showed the bear how to spring in a different way, and they both had fun playing together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy called John. John had a slow friend called Jake. One day, John and Jake decided to play a game.
John said, "Let's separate words!"
"What do you mean?" asked Jake.
"I mean, let's split each word into separate syllables," said John.
"OK! What's the first word?" said Jake, excitedly.
"Cat!" said John.
"C-A-T!" said Jake. "That's easy!"
John and Jake took it in turns to separate words. They had lots of fun and could separate loads of words slowly.
When they had finished playing, John said, "That was fun! Let's play again tomorrow."
"Yes, let's!" agreed Jake.

Ben and his dad were walking in the forest. It was a warm day and Ben was happy to be out with his dad. Suddenly, they came to a clearing. In the clearing, there was a small shelter. Ben's dad said, "Let's sit down and rest here". 
Ben was glad to hear this and he ran over to the shelter. He sat inside and looked around. Outside, he could still hear the birds singing and some small animals in the bushes. Ben was so happy to be there, he wanted to stay longer.
Ben's dad came over and sat down next to him. He said, "It's a nice day for a walk, isn't it? Let's stay here a little longer, I'm glad you are happy." Ben smiled and nodded his head. Together, they enjoyed the warm, sunny day and the peaceful shelter.

One day, a boy named Tim was eager to sail his toy boat. He went to the park with his mom. At the park, there was a big pond. Tim put his boat in the water and watched it sail.
As the boat sailed, it bumped into a brick. The brick said, "Hello, boat! I am a brick. I am not good at sailing. Can you help me sail too?" The boat replied, "Of course, I can help you!"
Tim saw the boat and brick talking. He picked up the brick and put it on the boat. Together, they sailed across the pond. The brick was happy, and Tim was happy too. They all had a fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, Cindy was a happy 3 year old. All of her friends wanted to come to Cindy's house because she always had something fun to do. Today, Cindy had a great idea.
When her friends arrived, Cindy told them, "We are going to start a broccoli patch!" Her friends were concerned; "What is a broccoli patch?", they asked.
Cindy explained that a broccoli patch was where they would plant some broccoli seeds and then watch them grow. Her friends got very excited, and they all agreed to start a broccoli patch.
They went outside and carefully planted the seeds. Once the seeds were in the ground, all the children watered them and left them alone to grow. 
The children waited anxiously for the broccoli to become available, but it seemed like the days were never going to end. Finally, after weeks of waiting, the broccoli was ready! 
The children were so excited that they rushed to the patch and picked the broccoli. They cooked and ate it together, enjoying their delicious reward.
Cindy's friends were so happy with how the broccoli patch had turned out that they wanted to start another one, but Cindy said they would have to wait and see what other fun things she could think of next.

Once upon a time there was a duck. He wanted to drive a car but he did not have a license.
One day he saw a car in a yard, and he asked the owner if he could drive it. The owner said yes, but only if he was careful.
The duck hopped in the car and started driving slowly. He wanted to make sure he followed all of the rules of the road. He drove slowly, not too fast.
After he drove for a while, he came to a stop sign. He let the other cars go first. He was being very careful.
Finally, the duck made it home. He was very proud of himself for driving so slowly and carefully. He hoped he could drive the car again one day.

Once, there was a big desk in a room. A little girl named Sue liked to play on the desk. One day, Sue found a magic button on the desk. She did not know what the button did. She wanted to find out.
Sue pushed the magic button. The desk started to shrink! Sue was surprised. She did not like that the desk was getting smaller. Sue felt frustrated. She wanted her big desk back.
Sue thought hard and remembered that the magic button made the desk shrink. She pushed the button again. The desk grew big again! Sue was happy. She learned that the magic button could make the desk big or small.

Anna liked to play with her toy cricket. It was green and soft and made a funny noise when she squeezed it. She named it Jim and took it everywhere with her. She slept with it, ate with it, and read stories with it.
One day, Anna went to the park with her mom and Jim. She saw a big slide and wanted to try it. She put Jim on the ground and climbed up the ladder. She was very excited and happy. But when she reached the top, she saw a boy with a mean face. He grabbed Jim and ran away.
Anna was very sad and angry. She shouted at the boy and slid down fast. She chased him across the grass and tried to get Jim back. The boy was faster and stronger. He laughed and said, "This is my cricket now. You can't have it. It's too ordinary for you."
Anna felt like crying. She loved Jim and he was not ordinary. He was special and fun and her best friend. She did not give up. She ran after the boy and saw him stop near a bench. He threw Jim on the bench and went to play with something else.
Anna saw her chance. She ran to the bench and grabbed Jim. She hugged him and said, "I'm sorry, Jim. I missed you so much. You are not ordinary. You are amazing and wonderful and my best friend." She removed the dirt and grass from Jim and made sure he was okay.
She smiled and felt happy again. She looked for her mom and saw her waiting for her. She ran to her and showed her Jim. Her mom smiled and said, "I'm proud of you, Anna. You were brave and smart and you got your cricket back. He is not ordinary. He is very special, just like you." She kissed Anna and Jim and took them home. Anna felt very happy and loved. She played with Jim all day and night. She never let him go again.

Tom likes to swim. He has a big pool in his backyard. He swims with his mom and dad and his sister. They splash and play and have fun.
One day, Tom sees a TV show about fish. The fish swim in the ocean. They have many colors and shapes. Tom thinks the fish are cool. He wants to swim like them.
He asks his mom if he can go to the ocean. His mom says no, the ocean is too far and too big. She says he can swim in the pool and pretend he is a fish. Tom is sad. He wants to see the real fish.
The next day, his dad has a surprise for him. He takes him to the aquarium. The aquarium has many fish in big tanks. Tom can see them swim and learn their names. He sees sharks and dolphins and turtles and jellyfish. He thinks they are all cool. He is happy.
He thanks his dad for the surprise. He says he loves the fish. He says he still loves the pool too. He says he can swim in both places and have fun. His dad smiles and hugs him. He says he loves him too. They go home and watch more TV shows about fish.

Once upon a time, there was a rich man who had a house that was so tall it almost touched the sky. He was proud of his house and even though he was generous and kind, he did not share it with anyone. 
One day, a little girl named Emma came to his door. She pleaded with him to open it and let her come inside so she could see the sky from his tall house. The man thought about it for a moment, and then opened the door for her.
He invited her in and told her to look up. She closed her eyes and when she opened them again, she felt very happy. She could see the sky so up close. It was so beautiful. 
The man asked her if she wanted to stay longer, but Emma said that she had to go. The man helped her down and as she left, she thanked him very much. 
At the end of the day, Emma went back home with a happy heart and a smile on her face. She was so thankful that the rich man had let her into his house to see the sky.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a ball that he loved to play with. One day, he kicked the ball very hard and it went into a big yard. The yard had a big gate that was locked. Tim wanted to get his ball back, so he needed to unlock the gate.
Tim saw a key hanging on a tree near the gate. He took the key and put it in the lock. He turned the key and heard a click. The gate was unlocked! Tim went inside the yard and saw a pitch where kids played soccer. He was so happy.
Tim found his ball on the pitch and started to play with the other kids. They all had fun kicking the ball and running around. After playing, Tim said thank you to his new friends and went back home. He locked the gate and hung the key back on the tree. Tim was very happy that he could unlock the gate and play on the pitch with his new friends.

John was excited to go outside and play. As he stepped out of the house he quickly ran and climbed a big tree. Suddenly he stopped as he noticed a white razor on the ground. He carefully picked it up and smiled. He ran back into the house to show it to his mom.
John showed the white razor to his mom and said: "Mom, look what I found!" His mom looked surprised and replied: "John, you shouldn't have taken that! Can you tell me where you found it?". John thought for a minute and then with a big smile he said: "It was under the big tree in the park!". 
John's mom was happy to hear that John had been safe. She gently put the white razor in the trash and said: "Let's go and play, okay honey?". John shouted with delight and they both went outside to have fun.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to a big farm with his mom. They saw many animals and plants. Tim saw a big pumpkin and wanted to take it home. His mom said, "Yes, we can take it home and turn it into a fun pumpkin face."
On their way home, Tim saw a terrible, big cat. The cat wanted to eat the pumpkin. Tim said, "No, cat! This is my pumpkin. You cannot eat it." The cat was sad, but it walked away.
When they got home, Tim and his mom turned the pumpkin into a funny face. They put a light inside and it made them happy. They showed the pumpkin to the cat, and the cat liked it too. They all became friends and had a fun time together.

Lily liked to draw with her crayons. She drew many things: flowers, animals, houses, people. She wanted to show her drawings to her mom and dad. She thought they would be proud of her.
One day, she found a photo of her mom and dad on the table. They were smiling and hugging. Lily thought they looked very happy. She wanted to make them happier. She had an idea. She took her crayons and started to draw on the photo. She drew a big sun, a rainbow, some hearts, and some stars. She thought the photo looked better now.
She ran to her mom and dad with the photo. She said, "Look, look, I improved your photo! Do you like it?" She hoped they would like it.
But her mom and dad did not look happy. They looked surprised and sad. They said, "Oh, Lily, what did you do? This was a special photo of us. You ruined it with your crayons. You should not draw on photos. You should only draw on paper."
Lily felt very embarrassed. She did not mean to ruin the photo. She wanted to make them happy. She said, "I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I didn't know. I thought you would like it. I love you."
Her mom and dad hugged her and said, "We love you too, Lily. We know you were trying to be nice. But you have to be careful with photos. They are not for drawing. They are for remembering. Next time, ask us before you draw on something."
Lily nodded and said, "OK, I will. I'm sorry again." She gave them the photo and said, "Can you fix it?"
Her mom and dad said, "We can try, but it might not be the same. Maybe we can take a new photo of us. And you can draw something for us on a paper. We would love that."
Lily smiled and said, "OK, let's do that. I will draw something nice for you on a paper. And we can take a new photo of us. And we can be happy."

Lily and Ben were camping with Mom and Dad in the big park. They had a tent to sleep in and a fire to cook on. They liked to play in the grass and look at the stars.
One night, they heard a loud noise from the woods. It sounded like a big animal. It was a roar! Lily and Ben were scared. They ran to the tent and zipped it up. They hugged Mom and Dad and hoped the animal would go away.
Mom and Dad told them not to worry. They said it was just a bear looking for food. They said bears were fair and would not hurt them if they left them alone. They said they had a can of spray to scare the bear if it came too close. They said they were safe in the tent.
Lily and Ben felt better. They listened to Mom and Dad's stories and songs. They forgot about the roar. They fell asleep in the tent, warm and cozy. They dreamed of the next day, when they would have more fun in the big park.

One day, a nice dog named Max went for a walk. He saw a cat named Sally. Sally was sad. Max asked, "Why are you sad, Sally?"
Sally said, "I want a massage, but I have no money." Max wanted to help. He said, "I can give you a massage. I value being a good friend."
Max gave Sally a massage. Sally felt nice and happy. They played together all day. But, Max did not see the big hole in the ground. He fell in the hole. Max was stuck and could not get out.
Sally tried to help, but she was too small. They both were sad. The nice day was now bad.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a lazy boy. He did not like to play outside. He liked to sit on his bed and read books. One day, Tim's mom gave him a new book. It had many pages. Tim was very happy.
Tim opened the book and started to read. He read page after page. Soon, he saw a cat sitting by the window. The cat said, "Hi Tim, can I sit with you and read the book?" Tim said, "Yes, come sit with me, cat."
The cat jumped on the bed and sat next to Tim. They read the book together. They laughed and smiled. Tim and the cat read all the pages of the book. After they finished, Tim's mom said, "Good job, Tim! Now, go play outside with your new friend." Tim and the cat went outside to play, and they were not lazy anymore.

One day, a little girl named Mia went to the park. She wanted to collect pretty stones. She saw a big shower in the park. Mia thought it was a good place to find stones.
Mia met a boy named Tim. Tim said, "I want to collect stones too!" They both looked for stones under the shower. But then, the shower turned on. They got all wet! Mia said, "Oh no! We are foolish to look for stones here!"
Mia and Tim laughed. They went to find stones in a dry place. They had fun and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little house. In the house lived a happy family. They had a mom, a dad, a girl, and a boy. They loved to decorate their house with pretty things. One day, they wanted to make their house even more pretty.
The dad went to the store to buy some gas for the car. The mom, the girl, and the boy stayed at home. They were excited to decorate. But they did not know that gas can be dangerous. They were ignorant about it.
The dad came home with the gas. He told the mom, the girl, and the boy to be careful. They learned that gas can be dangerous. They were not ignorant anymore. They decorated their house safely and had a fun time. They all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Molly. Molly wanted to try on her new dress that she had just bought, but her mum refused her. Molly was very sad.
Molly's mum said, "You can't wear your dress now - you need to hang it up first!"
So Molly took her hanger and decided to hang her dress up in her bedroom. But when Molly took the dress off the hanger, it wouldn't work. The hanger was broken.
Molly started to cry - she wanted to wear her new dress so much! Her mum sighed and said, "Maybe this is a sign that it's not the right time yet."
Molly wasn't sure what her mum meant but she decided to put the dress away for now. She used a different hanger and hung her dress up carefully.
The next day, Molly's mum told her she could wear her new dress. Molly was so excited that she quickly got changed. Molly smiled from ear to ear - she knew that the broken hanger was a sign that something good was coming soon.

Once upon a time, in an ancient land, there was a team of animals. The team had a big elephant, a small cat, and a fast dog. They lived in a big house near the forest.
One hot day, the team was feeling very hot. They wanted to find a way to cool down. The big elephant had an idea. He went to the river and filled his trunk with water. Then he walked back to his friends.
The big elephant said, "I will spray you with water to help you cool down!" He sprayed the small cat and fast dog with water from his trunk. They laughed and played in the water. The team was happy and not hot anymore. They had a fun day playing together.

Anna likes to help her mom mail letters. She puts them in the big blue box on the street. She says bye-bye to the letters and hopes they will make someone happy.
One day, Anna finds a prune in her pocket. She does not know how it got there. She thinks it is a funny-looking fruit. She asks her mom what it is.
Mom says it is a prune. It is a dried plum. It is sweet and chewy. Mom says Anna can eat it if she wants.
Anna decides to mail the prune. She thinks someone will like it as a surprise. She is careful not to drop it. She puts it in an envelope and writes her name on it. She puts a stamp on it too.
She goes with her mom to the big blue box. She mails the prune with the letters. She says bye-bye to the prune and hopes it will make someone happy. She smiles and hugs her mom. Mom smiles and hugs her back.

Anna loves fashion. She likes to wear different clothes and colors. She has many dresses, skirts, hats and shoes. She likes to look at magazines and books with fashion pictures. She wants to be a fashion designer when she grows up.
One day, Anna's mom tells her they are going to a fashion show. Anna is very excited. She jumps and claps her hands. She asks her mom what they will see at the fashion show. Her mom says they will see models wearing new clothes and accessories. They will also see lights, music and cameras.
Anna and her mom get ready to go to the fashion show. Anna chooses her favorite dress and shoes. She also puts on a necklace and a bracelet. She looks at herself in the mirror and smiles. She thinks she looks like a model. Her mom says she looks beautiful and gives her a hug.
They arrive at the fashion show. They find their seats and wait for the show to start. Anna is very enthusiastic. She looks around and sees many people. She also sees a big stage with a curtain. She wonders what is behind the curtain. She hears some music and sees some lights.
The curtain opens and the show begins. Anna sees models walking on the stage. They wear different clothes and colors. They also wear hats, scarves, bags and sunglasses. They look happy and confident. Anna likes their clothes and accessories. She claps and cheers for them. She thinks they are amazing. She wants to be like them.
Anna enjoys the fashion show very much. She sees many new and beautiful things. She feels happy and inspired. She tells her mom she loves fashion. Her mom says she loves her. They hug and smile. They have a wonderful time.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big tree with many leaves. Tim thought it would be fun to gather the leaves. So, he started picking them up and made a great big pile.
While Tim was gathering the leaves, he found a small, shiny rock. Tim looked at the rock with wonder. He thought it was the most beautiful rock he had ever seen. Tim put the rock in his pocket and continued to gather more leaves.
When the pile of leaves was very big, Tim jumped in. The leaves went up in the air and fell back down. Tim laughed and played in the leaves until it was time to go home. He showed his mom the great pile of leaves and the rock he found. His mom smiled and told him it was a wonderful day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a famous duck. He was always pretending to be something else on the farm. One day, it was cold outside and all the other animals went inside to get warm. But the famous duck stayed outside, pretending to fly. 
Soon the farmer noticed the duck and asked him why he wasn't inside. The duck said he wanted to pretend to be the most famous bird ever. The farmer admired his enthusiasm and agreed that the duck should stay outside.
But then an even colder weather came and the duck's feet became freezing. He started to shiver and the farmer noticed this, too. The farmer was worried and took the duck inside to get warm. 
The farmer understood why the duck wanted to pretend, but he also knew that the duck needed to stay safe during the cold weather. The farmer made sure the duck had a warm and comfortable place to sleep, and told the duck he could pretend whenever it was safe.

Once upon a time, there was a soldier named Sam. He was a very brave soldier. One day, Sam got an order from his boss. The order was to go to a dangerous place and look for a lost toy.
Sam went to the dangerous place and saw a big, scary monster. He was scared, but he wanted to find the lost toy. He said to the monster, "Please, can you help me find the lost toy?" The monster looked at Sam and said, "Okay, I will help you."
The monster helped Sam look for the toy, and they found it! The monster was not so scary after all. They became friends, and Sam took the toy back to his boss. Everyone was happy, and Sam learned that sometimes, things that seem scary are not so bad when you get to know them.

Once upon a time, there was a weak little dog named Tiny. Tiny loved to play with his ball in the yard, but sometimes he was too weak to play for a long time. One day, Tiny's mom had an idea to help him feel better.
She went to the store and bought a big, shiny grill. She knew that cooking good food on the grill would help Tiny get stronger. So, she sent Tiny's dad to the yard to start the grill, while she went inside to get the food.
Tiny watched as his dad cooked yummy food on the grill. He could smell the tasty smells, and he knew he would eat well today. After they ate, Tiny felt stronger and happier. From that day on, Tiny's mom and dad cooked on the grill often, and Tiny became stronger and stronger.

Mummy was baking a cake for her little girl's birthday. She was getting out the ingredients and carefully folding them into the bowl. Then she added a pinch of chocolate, just the way her little girl liked it.
Suddenly, the little girl walked into the kitchen and saw the cake mixture. She was so excited and asked, "Is that cake for me, Mummy?"
Mummy said, "Yes, little one, it is. How do like it?"
The little girl smiled, but then she realised she only wanted a little bit of the chocolate. She said, "The cake looks great, but could I have just a tiny bit of the chocolate now?"
Mummy smiled, "I'm sorry, darling. We can't eat the chocolate now. It has to go into the cake."
The little girl frowned, but then she smiled. "Ok Mummy. Can I help you fold the ingredients into the bowl?"
Mummy laughed and said, "Of course you can, sweetie!"
So they both worked together folding the ingredients until the cake was ready. 
When it came out of the oven, it was the most delicious chocolate cake the little girl had ever tasted and she was so happy!

One day, a boy named Tim and his dog, Spot, went to play in the park. It was a long Tuesday. Tim and Spot liked to play with a red ball. Tim would throw the ball and Spot would run to get it. They played and played.
"Spot, let's bury the ball and find it!" said Tim. So, they dug a hole and put the ball in it. Then, they put dirt on top. They went to play on the swings before they looked for the ball again.
When they came back, they saw a big tree had grown where they buried the ball! "Wow, Spot, look at that!" said Tim. They dug and dug, and found the red ball under the tree. The ball had turned into a magic ball that made trees grow fast. Tim and Spot played with the magic ball all day.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the zoo with his mom. They saw many animals. They saw a long snake, a big lion, and a tall giraffe. Tim was very happy. He wanted to welcome all the animals to play with him.
At the zoo, they also saw a little bird. The little bird was sad because it could not fly. Tim had an idea. He asked his mom if they could help the bird. His mom said yes, and they took the bird home.
At home, Tim and his mom made a surprise for the bird. They made a big swing for the bird to sit on. The bird was so happy! But then, something unexpected happened. The bird started to fly! The swing helped the bird learn how to fly. The bird thanked Tim and his mom and flew back to the zoo to be with its friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Lily liked to play with her toys and hide behind the curtain in the living room.
One day, Lily found a broken toy on the floor. She didn't know who it belonged to, so she asked her mom and dad. They didn't answer her because they were busy. Lily felt sad and didn't know what to do. She decided to find out who the broken toy belonged to and fix it.
Lily asked her friends if the broken toy was theirs. One friend said yes, and Lily helped her friend fix the toy. They played together and had lots of fun. Lily learned that it is good to help others and ask questions when you don't know something. And everyone was happy in the end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very sleepy. He had a big body and liked to display his toys.
One day, Tim went to the park. He saw a cat. The cat was also sleepy. Tim wanted to play with the cat. But, the cat did not want to play. It was too sleepy.
Then, something unexpected happened! The cat turned into a big, happy dog. The dog was not sleepy. It wanted to play with Tim. They played and played. Tim was not sleepy anymore. He was very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big, generous man named Bob. He lived in a small house in a beautiful land. One day, he found a big box outside his door. He was very excited to see what was inside.
Bob said, "I will open this big box now!" He opened the box, and he found a small, cute dog inside. The dog was very happy to see Bob. "Woof! Woof!" said the dog. Bob was very happy too. He decided to keep the dog and named him Max.
Bob and Max played in the land every day. They had lots of fun together. One day, Max found a small door in the ground. Bob said, "Let's open it!" When they opened the door, they found a secret room full of toys and treats! Max and Bob were very surprised. They played with the toys and shared the treats with their friends in the land. Everyone was happy and loved the generous man Bob and his dog Max.

Once upon a time, there was a little bear. The bear was not fit. He did not know how to run or jump very well. He wanted to play with his friends, but he was too slow.
One day, the bear met a kind bird. The bird said, "I can help you be fit. We can run and jump together." The bear was very happy. They played every day, and the bear started to get fit.
Soon, the bear could run and jump with his friends. He was so happy that he could play with them. He thanked the bird for helping him. The bear now knew how to be fit and have fun with his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom liked to climb on the big pile of junk in his backyard. The junk pile had many things like old toys, boxes, and clothes. Tom thought it was fun to climb and play on the junk.
One hot day, Tom went outside to play on the junk pile. As he climbed higher and higher, he saw a big, red ball on top. Tom said, "I want that ball!" He climbed higher to get the ball. But the sun was very hot, and Tom started to feel hot too.
Tom got the ball, but he was so hot and tired. He tried to climb down, but he slipped and fell. Tom began to cry. His mom came outside and saw Tom on the ground. She said, "You should not climb on the junk pile. It is not safe." Tom was sad and hurt, and he learned not to play on the junk pile anymore.

Once upon a time there was a brave knight. He wore a shining armour and had a magical sword. He was always on the lookout for adventures and quests to solve.
One day, he heard that a fierce dragon lived in a cave nearby. He decided to go and fight the dragon. As he approached the cave, the dragon roared out loud. The knight was not scared of the dragon, so he bravely raised his sword and charged forward.
The knight and the dragon fought a long and hard battle. In the end, the knight used all his strength with his sword and he was able to win the fight. The dragon was dead, and the knight was victorious.
The knight then shouted, "I won! I won!" He was so happy that he had defeated the dragon and saved the village. 
The villagers cheered when they saw the knight and his magical sword. From then on, the knight was known throughout the land as the "Dragon Slayer."

One day, a little dog named Max went for a walk. He felt very peaceful in the park. The sun was shining and the birds were singing. Max saw a line of ants on the ground. He thought it was fun to watch them walk in a line.
Max saw his friend, a cat named Molly. "Look at the ants!" Max said to Molly. Molly looked at the line of ants and felt excited too. They both watched the ants walk in a line, carrying food to their home.
Max and Molly played together in the park. They ran and jumped and had a great time. When it was time to go home, they said goodbye to each other. Max and Molly were both happy they had a peaceful and exciting day at the park.

Once upon a time there was a wild prune. He loved to chew on everything he could find. One day he found a big tree full of juicy prunes. The wild prune was so excited he started to chew on them right away. They were so sweet and tasty that he couldn't stop eating them. After he finished all the prunes, he lay down and took a nap. He was so full and happy that he didn't want to move. The wild prune slept all night and woke up the next morning feeling refreshed. From then on, he nibbled on prunes each day and lived a very happy life.

Once there was a little girl called Molly. She was three years old and she loved going for walks in the park. Today, she had put on her special shoes and was ready to go. 
Molly's mum said, "Don't forget to buckle your shoes before we go outside." Molly looked down and sure enough her shoes weren't buckled up. She started to do it herself but she struggled. 
Molly's mum smiled, "Let me help you with that." She bent down and quickly buckled up her shoes for her. "There we go! All done!" 
On their way home after the walk Molly stopped to look in the sweet shop. "Mmmm, all these yummy sweets, I wish I could buy all of them!" she said.
Molly's mum laughed, "But you have to forget to buy sweets, don't you?" She gave Molly a hug and said, "Come on, we'd better get home now."
And so they went home together, Molly skipping ahead and saying, "Next time I go, I'm not going to forget to buy some yummy sweets!"

One day, a little girl named Amy went to play outside. She saw a big brown hill. Amy wanted to see what was on the other side. So, she walked up the hill.
On the other side, Amy saw a giant. The giant was sad. Amy asked the giant, "Why are you sad?" The giant said, "I lost my toy." Amy wanted to help the giant. She said, "I will find your toy."
Amy looked and looked. She found a toy under a tree. It was a small toy. Amy went to tell the giant she found it. The giant was happy. But when he saw the toy, he said, "This is not my toy."
Just then, a tiny ant came up to them. The ant said, "That's my toy!" It was the ant's toy all along! The giant and Amy laughed. They all played together and had a fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Susie. She was very happy and had lots of friends. One day Susie was playing in the park with her friends, when she saw a sad looking bow in the grass. 
Susie walked over and asked, "What are you doing here?"
The bow replied in a miserable voice, "I'm so sad no one wants to play with me". 
Susie smiled and said, "Don't worry, I will be your friend". She laughed and grabbed the bow.
Susie and the bow went off to play. They ran around and played lots of fun games. They laughed and laughed until they were both very tired.
Susie was very happy that she had a new best friend. She said goodbye to the bow and ran off back home.
The bow smiled from ear to ear and waved goodbye. It was the happiest it had been in a long time.

One day, a curious little girl named Amy found a red brick in her yard. She picked it up and started to rub it with her hands. It felt bumpy and rough. She thought it was fun to touch.
Amy saw her friend, Tom, playing nearby. She said, "Tom, come see this brick! It feels funny when you rub it." Tom walked over and rubbed the brick too. "Wow, it does feel funny!" he said.
Amy and Tom played with the brick and shared it with their friends. They learned that sharing can make playing more fun. The moral of the story is that sharing with others can bring joy and happiness.

One day, a boy named Tom went to the park with his mom. He saw a big fountain that had water spray up and down. He wanted to play with the water, but his mom said no. She said he had to wear his jacket because it was cold and he could get sick.
Tom was angry and ignorant. He did not listen to his mom. He took off his jacket and ran to the fountain. He jumped and splashed and laughed. He felt the water spray on his face and hair. He thought he was having fun.
But soon, he started to shiver and sneeze. He felt cold and wet. He looked for his jacket, but he could not find it. He had left it on the bench, and someone had taken it. He cried and ran to his mom. He said he was sorry and he wanted his jacket.
His mom hugged him and gave him a towel. She said she was glad he was sorry, but he had to learn a lesson. She said he had to be careful and respectful. She said he had to listen to her and not be ignorant. She said she loved him, but he had to be smart and safe.
Tom nodded and said he understood. He said he loved his mom and he would not be ignorant again. He said he would wear his jacket and not play with the water. He said he would be a good boy. His mom smiled and kissed him. She said she was proud of him and they went home.

Once upon a time there was a bald man. He had no hair on his head.
One day the bald man had an idea. He wanted to find a special light.
So he searched and searched, but he couldn't find it! He looked everywhere, but couldn't find it.
Then, he saw a bright light in the sky! He was so excited. He ran over and grabbed it.
When he held the light, it made him feel happy. He liked it so much that he never put it down.
Soon the day ended and the bald man had to go home. He took his special light with him, and he was never sad again. The end.

One day, a little girl named Amy wanted to make a tasty cake. She asked her mom, "Can we make a cake today?" Her mom said, "Yes, we can make a cake!"
Amy and her mom got the mixer and started to pack all the things they needed to make the cake. They put flour, sugar, and eggs in a big bowl. Then, they turned on the mixer and it went round and round, mixing everything together.
As they were mixing, something unexpected happened. A little mouse jumped out of the bowl! Amy and her mom were so surprised. The mouse said, "I'm sorry, I was just looking for some food. I didn't mean to scare you."
Amy's mom said, "That's okay, little mouse. We'll make a new cake and give you some crumbs to eat." So, they made a new cake and shared it with the mouse. The cake was very tasty, and they all had a fun day together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with paint. One day, she found a big jar of purple paint in her room. Lily was so happy!
Lily wanted to paint a pretty picture. She took a big brush and began to splash the purple paint on the paper. But, she splashed too much! The paint went everywhere. It made a big mess on the floor and on her clothes.
Lily's mom came into the room and saw the mess. She was not happy. She told Lily to clean up the mess and be more careful next time. Lily felt sad, but she learned that being careful is important when playing with paint. And from that day on, she always took her time and made beautiful pictures with her purple paint.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She lived in a house with her mommy and daddy. Sue was a happy girl who loved to play with her toys.
One day, Sue wanted to borrow her mommy's pretty vase. She asked, "Mommy, can I borrow your vase?" Mommy said, "Are you ready to be careful with it?" Sue said, "Yes, I am ready!" So, Mommy let Sue borrow the vase.
Sue took the vase to her room and put her favorite flowers in it. She was very careful with the vase. When she was done, she took the vase back to her mommy. Mommy was so proud of Sue for being careful and ready. They put the vase with the flowers on the table and enjoyed looking at them together.

Once upon a time, there were two friends who were playing with a match. It was a long match and they wanted to see how long it could burn. The two friends lit the match and watched it burn.
"Wow, it's burning so long," said one friend. 
The other friend agreed and said, "Let's see how long it will go for."
The match kept burning until it was almost finished. Just before the flame ended, one of the friends said, "Let's blow it out." 
So they both blew out the flame and the match had come to an end. 
The two friends were happy. They had seen the match burn for a long time and they felt satisfied.
The end.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to draw with crayons. Lily has a big box of crayons. Tom has a small box of crayons. But Tom has something that Lily does not have. He has an eraser.
One day, they are drawing on a big paper. Lily draws a house, a tree, and a sun. Tom draws a car, a dog, and a cloud. But Tom makes a mistake. He draws the cloud too big. It covers the sun. He wants to fix it. He uses his eraser. He rubs the cloud away. He makes the sun shine again.
Lily sees what Tom does. She is amazed. She says, "Wow, Tom, you have an eraser! That is so cool! Can I try it?"
Tom says, "Sure, Lily, you can try it. But be careful. It is my only eraser. It is very special. My grandma gave it to me. She said it is a lucky eraser. It can help me with anything."
Lily says, "Thank you, Tom. You are very nice. I will be careful. I want to see if it can help me too."
Lily takes the eraser. She looks at her drawing. She thinks of something she wants to change. She wants to add a flower to her tree. She uses the eraser. She rubs away a part of the tree. She makes a space for the flower. She picks a pink crayon. She draws a flower.
Lily is happy. She says, "Look, Tom, I made a flower! Your eraser is amazing! It can help me too!"
Tom smiles. He says, "You are welcome, Lily. I am glad you like it. You made a beautiful flower. Your drawing is very nice. But do you know what?"
Lily says, "What, Tom?"
Tom says, "You do not need an eraser to make a nice drawing. You are already good at drawing. You have a lot of crayons. You have a lot of ideas. You have a lot of fun. You are lucky too, Lily. You are my friend. And you remind me of my grandma. She is good at drawing too. She is very nice. She has a lot of fun. She is my friend too."
Lily hugs Tom. She says, "Thank you, Tom. You are my friend too. And you remind me of my grandma too. She is good at drawing too. She is very nice. She has a lot of fun. She is my friend too."
They laugh. They keep drawing. They share the eraser. They share the crayons. They share the paper. They share the fun. They are lucky friends.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big flood. A boy named Tim and his dog, Max, had to stay inside their house. They could not go out to play because of the water. Tim and Max were sad but they thought of a fun game to play inside.
Tim and Max played a competitive game of catch. They threw a ball back and forth to each other. Tim was very good at catching, but Max was even better. They laughed and played, forgetting about the flood outside.
But then, something unexpected happened. Max threw the ball too hard, and it broke a window. The water from the flood came into the house very fast. Tim and Max tried to stop the water, but they could not. The flood filled their house, and they had to leave. The game of catch was over, and Tim and Max were sad again.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom liked to wear a big helmet on his head. He wore it when he rode his bike. The helmet was red and shiny.
One day, Tom saw a girl named Sue. Sue did not have a helmet. Tom wanted to help Sue. He was a generous boy. So, he gave Sue his helmet.
Sue was happy to get the helmet. She wore it on her head. Now, Tom and Sue could ride bikes together. They were both safe and had fun. Tom felt good because he was generous and helped his friend.

One sunny day, Tom went to the park with his dad. Tom had a new kite. It was big and red. He was very excited to fly it. But it was difficult to balance the kite in the wind.
Tom's dad said, "Don't give up! Keep trying and you will learn how to balance the kite."
Tom tried again and again. He watched the kite go up and down. He learned how to make the kite dance in the sky. Tom was very happy.
A little girl named Jane came to the park too. She saw Tom's kite and wanted to fly it. Tom shared his kite with Jane. They took turns flying it and had a lot of fun.
The moral of the story is: don't give up when things are difficult, keep trying and you will learn. And when you learn, it's good to share and have fun with others.

Once upon a time, there was a mighty lion named Leo. He lived in a big forest with many trees. Leo had a good friend named Tommy, who was a small mouse. They liked to play together.
One day, Leo found a big scale under a tree. He called Tommy and said, "Look, I found a scale!" Tommy came running and said, "Wow, it's big!" They both decided to play with the scale. They took turns standing on it and seeing how much they weighed.
Later, they found a long cloth. Leo thought it would be fun to wrap Tommy in it like a present. Tommy agreed and laughed as Leo wrapped him up. After wrapping Tommy, Leo pretended to open the present and said, "Oh, it's my best friend, Tommy!" They both laughed and played with the cloth and scale all day.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, a bird and a frog. The frog was hot and tired, so the bird offered to give him a ride on its wing. The bird opened its wing and the frog hopped onto it. They slowly flew away. 
The bird slowly lead the frog over a mountain where the air was hot. The frog started to feel even hotter, and the bird told him just a little bit further there would be a lake. But they never made it to the lake. Suddenly, a gust of wind came and swept the frog off the bird's wing. 
The frog started to fall, he tried to grab hold of the bird's wing, but it was too late. He tumbled down the mountain and got lost in a big forest. No one ever saw the frog again. The bird flew away, sad for his lost friend.

One day, a loyal dog named Max went to the park with his best friend, a little boy named Tim. They loved to play at the park together. They would run, jump, and play on the swings. Max would always stay close to Tim to make sure he was safe.
As they played, Tim saw a big sheet on the ground. "Max, look! A sheet!" Tim said. They ran to the sheet and decided to play with it. They took turns hiding under the sheet and playing peek-a-boo. They had so much fun!
Suddenly, the sheet began to move on its own! Tim and Max were very surprised. They pulled the sheet away and found a little lost kitten underneath. The kitten was scared, but Tim and Max were kind to her. Tim said, "Max, let's take her home and take care of her!" Max barked happily, and they all went home together.

One day, a peaceful cat named Tom lived in a small house. He loved to play with his toy heart. He would pull it with his paws and chase it around the room. Tom was very happy.
One sunny day, Tom's friend, a little girl named Amy, came to visit. She saw Tom playing with his toy heart. "Can I play with you, Tom?" Amy asked. Tom nodded, and they both started to pull and toss the toy heart, laughing and having fun.
Suddenly, the toy heart started to grow bigger and bigger! Tom and Amy were very surprised. They pulled the big heart together, and it opened like a door. Inside, they found a magical land with many new friends to play with. Tom and Amy had even more fun in this new place. And from that day on, they had many more adventures with their magical toy heart.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She was a mild girl who loved to play with her toys. She had a wish to marry a prince. Lily would dream about her big day with the prince.
One day, Lily went to the park with her mom. She saw a boy named Tom. Tom was kind and had a big smile. Lily and Tom played together on the swings and in the sand. They had so much fun!
As they played, Lily told Tom about her wish to marry a prince. Tom said he wanted to be a prince when he grew up. They laughed and played more. Lily and Tom became best friends. And who knows, maybe one day her wish will come true!

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom and baby sister. His baby sister was a tiny infant who liked to laugh and play. Tim was a competitive boy who loved to win games.
At the park, Tim met a friend named Sam. They decided to play a game with sticks. They would stir the dirt and see who could make the biggest pile. "I bet I can make a bigger pile than you!" said Tim. "No way! I'm the best at stirring dirt!" said Sam.
They stirred and stirred, laughing as the dirt piled up. Tim's baby sister watched and clapped her hands. In the end, Tim won the game and they all had fun. They went home tired but happy, ready to play again another day.

Sammy was playing outside in the garden and found a big hole. He got his toy shovel and showed it to his mom.
"Mommy, I can bury my equipment here!" said Sammy, excitedly.
"That's a great idea, Sammy honey," said his mom.
So Sammy started digging the hole and burying his equipment. He worked really fast, and soon he had a big pile of equipment buried in the garden.
After he was done, Sammy said, "I'm done burying my equipment, mommy."
"That was fast, Sammy," she said. "Let's go inside now. It's time for lunch."
So Sammy and his mom went inside and enjoyed a delicious lunch. Sammy was so proud of himself for burying the equipment so quickly. He was happy that he got the job done before lunchtime.

Lily and Tom are twins. They like to play in the park with their dog, Spot. Spot is a big, black dog. He is very intelligent. He can do many tricks.
One day, they go to the park with their mom. They see a big slide, a swing, and a sandbox. They run to the slide and take turns to slide down. They laugh and have fun.
Then, they hear a strange noise. It sounds like a squeak. They look around and see a small, gray mouse. The mouse is hiding under a bench. It looks scared.
"Look, a mouse!" Lily says.
"Let's find it!" Tom says.
They go to the bench and try to see the mouse. Spot follows them. He is curious too. He sniffs the bench and barks.
The mouse hears the bark and runs away. It goes to a hole in the fence. It disappears.
"Bye, mouse!" Lily says.
"Maybe we can find it again another day," Tom says.
They go back to their mom. They tell her about the mouse. She smiles and hugs them.
"You are very intelligent, Lily and Tom. You know how to be gentle with animals. Maybe the mouse will come back and be your friend," she says.
They nod and smile. They love their mom, their dog, and the park. They are happy.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park. He wore a jacket because it was cold. Tim saw a weak bird on the ground. He wanted to help the bird. Tim took the bird in his hands and put it in his jacket to keep it warm.
At the park, Tim met his friend, Jane. "What do you have in your jacket?" Jane asked. "I found a weak bird," Tim said. "I want to help it." Jane wanted to help too. They looked for a warm place for the bird.
They found a big tree with a hole. Tim took the bird out of his jacket and put it in the hole. Suddenly, the weak bird started to sing! It was not weak anymore. The bird flew out of the hole and up into the sky. Tim and Jane were surprised and happy. They knew they had helped the bird feel better.

Once upon a time, there was a generous chicken. This chicken loved to share her food with her friends. Every day, she would wake up early to find food for everyone.
One day, while the chicken was looking for food, she found a big pile of seeds. She was so happy! She called all her friends to come and eat with her. They all came and had a big feast together.
After they ate, the chicken's friends were very thankful. They all decided to help the chicken find food too. From that day on, they all woke up early to find food together. The moral of the story is: when you are generous and share with others, they will help you too.

Once upon a time, there was a band of three friends. They were a cat, a dog, and a bird. They loved to play music together. The cat played the drums, the dog played the guitar, and the bird sang. They wanted to complete a new song, but they needed one more friend to help them.
One day, the cat said, "We need a friend who is a fit for our band." The dog and the bird agreed. They all went out to look for a new friend to join their band. They met many animals, but none of them were the right fit.
Then, they met a little mouse who played the piano. The mouse said, "I can help you complete your song." The cat, the dog, and the bird were happy. They played their new song together, and it was perfect. The mouse was the right fit for their band, and they all became best friends. They played music together every day, and they were the best band in the whole world.

Once there was a little girl named Sarah. She loved to play outside in the garden. One day she went out to the garden and saw something huge and sparkly - it was a diamond! She tried to pick it up, but it wouldn't move. She was really surprised and called for her mom.
Sarah's mom came to the garden. "Mom, look what I found!" said Sarah, pointing at the diamond.
"Oh my! That's a huge diamond! You are so lucky, my little girl" said Mom.
"But, it won't move" said Sarah.
Her mom gave the diamond a gentle twist and it opened up. Inside there was something special - a little golden key! 
Sarah was so excited. "What does this key open, Mommy?" she asked.
"This key opens your heart and mind, Sarah. When you use it, it gives you wisdom, understanding, patience and joy," replied Mom.
Sarah smiled. She had learned an important lesson that day. She knew that even something that looks big and precious on the outside can be something special when you take the time to open it up.

Once upon a time, there was a large train. This train could go very fast. It used steam to move. The train loved to get people from one place to another. It loved to see them happy.
One day, the train saw a big hill. The train thought it could get up the hill. But, as it tried to go up, it started to slow down. The train needed more steam to get up the hill. It looked like the train would not make it.
Then, a small bird came by. The bird saw the train was sad. The bird told the train, "I know where to find more steam!" The train followed the bird and found a large pot of hot water. The train took the steam from the water and went up the hill. The people were happy, and the train was happy too. The train never thought a small bird could help, but it did!

Sara loves to eat cake. She likes the sweet taste and the soft crumbs. She does not like to eat carrots. She thinks they are bitter and hard. Her mom always tells her to eat her carrots. She says they are healthy and good for her eyes.
One day, Sara's mom makes a big cake for her birthday. Sara is very happy. She sees the cake on the table. It has pink frosting and sprinkles. She wants to eat it right away. But her mom says, "Not yet, Sara. You have to eat your dinner first. And that includes your carrots."
Sara frowns. She does not want to eat her carrots. She wants to eat her cake. She says, "But mom, I don't like carrots. They are yucky. Can I have cake instead?"
Her mom shakes her head. She says, "No, Sara. You have to eat your carrots. They are healthy and good for you. If you eat your carrots, you can have a big slice of cake. If you don't eat your carrots, you can have no cake at all."
Sara thinks for a moment. She really wants to have cake. She decides to eat her carrots. She takes a bite. She makes a face. She does not like the taste. But she eats another bite. And another. And another. She finishes her carrots. She says, "There, mom. I ate my carrots. Can I have cake now?"
Her mom smiles. She says, "Yes, Sara. You can have cake now. You are a good girl. You ate your healthy food. Now you can have your sweet treat. Here, have a big slice of cake."
Sara takes the slice of cake. She is very happy. She says, "Thank you, mom. You are the best. I love you. And I love cake."
She takes a bite of cake. She makes a face. She does not like the taste. The cake is not sweet. It is bitter and hard. It has no frosting and no sprinkles. It is made of carrots.
Sara says, "Mom, what is this? This is not cake. This is carrots. I don't like carrots. Why did you give me carrots?"
Her mom says, "Surprise, Sara. This is a carrot cake. I made it for you. It is healthy and good for you. And it has a little bit of sugar and butter. Don't you like it?"
Sara frowns. She does not like it. She says, "No, mom. I don't like it. This is not a surprise. This is a trick. You tricked me. I ate my carrots. I want my real cake. Where is my real cake?"
Her mom says, "I'm sorry, Sara. I thought you would like it. I wanted to make you a special cake. But I guess I made a mistake. There is no real cake. There is only carrot cake."
Sara cries. She says, "Mom, you are not the best. You are the worst. I don't love you. And I don't love cake. I hate carrots. And I hate carrot cake."
She throws the carrot cake on the floor. She runs to her room. She slams the door. She does not come out for a long time.

Once there was a friendly jet. His name was Jet. Jet lived in the sky and loved to fly. Every day Jet would meet new friends! He was always so happy to introduce himself and they were so excited to meet him! One day Jet met some new friends in the sky. They were birds and they were so friendly! With his new friends, Jet played tag and flew around the sky together. They had lots of fun! Jet was really happy to introduce himself and his new friends. They were so excited to be together and knew that they were going to be friends forever!

One day, a clever fox was looking for food in the forest. He saw a big cave and thought there might be something tasty inside. He crept closer and sniffed the air. He smelled a bear!
The fox was scared, but he had an idea. He called out, "Hello, Mr. Bear! Do you want to play a game with me?"
The bear was sleepy and grumpy. He growled, "What game? Go away, little fox!"
The fox said, "It's a fun game. We spin around and see who gets dizzy first. If you win, you can eat me. If I win, you give me some honey."
The bear was curious and hungry. He thought he could easily beat the fox. He said, "Fine, let's play. But you go first."
The fox nodded and ran out of the cave. He spun around and around, faster and faster, until he was a blur. Then he stopped and pretended to be dizzy. He said, "Wow, that was hard. Your turn, Mr. Bear."
The bear came out of the cave and started to spin. He spun around and around, slower and slower, until he was very dizzy. He stumbled and fell to the ground. He said, "Ugh, I don't like this game. You win, little fox."
The fox smiled and ran to the cave. He found a jar of honey and took it. He said, "Thank you, Mr. Bear. You are very kind. Goodbye!" He ran away with the honey, leaving the bear confused and angry.
The fox was very clever. He tricked the bear and got some food. He enjoyed the honey and was happy. The bear was very silly. He fell for the fox's trick and lost his food. He was still dizzy and sad.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a radio. It was not easy to use, but Tim wanted to try. He turned the radio on and music started to play. Tim was very happy and started to dance.
Then, something funny happened. A voice came from the radio. It said, "Hello, Tim! I am a talking radio!" Tim was surprised and said, "Hello, radio! I did not know you can talk!"
The radio and Tim became good friends. They played together and talked every day. Tim was glad he found the radio. And the radio was happy too, because it now had a friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She had a small, weak dog named Spot. Sue and Spot loved to play outside in the sun.
One day, Sue found a brush in the grass. She picked it up and started to brush Spot's fur. Spot liked how the brush felt and wagged his tail. Sue and Spot played with the brush all day.
Sue saw a big tree and pointed at it. She said, "Let's go there, Spot!" They ran to the tree and sat under it. Sue brushed Spot's fur and they both took a nap. When they woke up, they went home and told their mom about their fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a very obedient boy. He always did what his mommy and daddy told him to do. Tim liked to play with his dog, Spot. They played fetch and ran around the yard together.
One day, Tim's mommy asked him to help her find her lost keys. Tim was eager to help. He looked under the bed, in the toy box, and behind the couch. Finally, he found the keys in the kitchen. His mommy was so happy and said, "Thank you, Tim! You are a big success at finding things!"
That night, Tim's daddy asked him a question. "What did you do today, Tim?" Tim was proud to answer, "I helped mommy find her keys! I was a big success!" Tim's mommy and daddy smiled and hugged him. They were so proud of their obedient and helpful little boy.

One day, a big boat was near the water. The boat had a big anchor. The boat man did not know much about anchors. He was ignorant. The boat man said, "I dare to put the anchor in the water."
The boat man put the anchor in the water. The anchor went down, down, down. The boat man was happy. He thought the anchor would hold the boat. But then, something unexpected happened.
A big fish came by. The fish saw the anchor. The fish said, "I dare to play with the anchor." The fish played with the anchor and moved it. The boat started to move too. The boat man was scared. The fish said, "I am sorry, I did not know the anchor was for the boat." The fish helped put the anchor back and the boat man said, "Thank you, fish."
