One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. He saw a shadow on the ground. It was a big shadow. Tim was scared. He thought, "What could make a shadow so big?"
Just then, Tim heard a ring. It was his friend, the little bird. The bird said, "Don't be scared, Tim. I know who makes the big shadow." Tim was grateful for his friend. He asked, "Who makes the big shadow, little bird?"
The little bird took Tim to see the big shadow. They walked and walked. Then, they saw a big tree. The big tree was making the big shadow! Tim laughed. He was not scared anymore. He said, "Thank you, little bird. I am not scared of the big shadow now." The little bird was happy to help Tim. They played in the big shadow all day.

Once upon a time, there was a painter. The painter loved to make pretty pictures. He used many colors to make his pictures. One day, he wanted to make a very tough picture. He thought hard about what to paint.
The painter saw a big tree. He wanted to grab the colors from the tree. He used green, brown, and yellow. He painted the tree with a big smile on his face. He was very happy with his tough picture.
Soon, many people came to see the painter's tough picture. They all liked it a lot. The painter was very proud of his work. From that day on, the painter always tried to make tough pictures that made people happy.

One day, there was an ordinary train. It was going on its own journey. Suddenly, something amazing began to happen. The train started to change! Its wheels started turning faster and it began to go much faster. Everybody on the train was very happy! 
Soon the train was going so fast that it passed lots of other trains. It went so fast that it felt like it was flying! Soon, the ordinary train had become the fastest on the tracks.
When the train arrived, the people were so excited. They thanked the train for taking them on such an exciting journey. The train felt proud of itself and knew that it could do anything if it put its mind to it.
The End.

Lily and Ben went to the park with Mom. They saw many things: swings, slides, flowers, birds, and a big statue. The statue was of a man with a hat and a coat. He looked very serious and still.
"Can we play with the statue?" Lily asked Mom.
"No, sweetie, the statue is not for playing. It is for looking and remembering. The man was a hero who helped many people a long time ago," Mom said.
"But he looks so boring. He never moves or smiles or blinks," Ben said.
"Maybe he is just shy. Maybe he blinks when no one is watching," Lily said.
She had an idea. She whispered to Ben, "Let's hide behind that bush and watch the statue. Maybe we can see him blink."
They ran to the bush and peeked at the statue. They waited and waited, but the statue did not blink.
"This is no fun. He is just a regular statue. Let's go play on the swings," Ben said.
He got up and ran to the swings. Lily stayed behind the bush. She wanted to see the statue blink. She stared and stared, hoping he would blink.
Suddenly, she saw something. The statue winked at her. He winked with one eye and smiled a little. Lily gasped. She blinked and rubbed her eyes. Did she imagine it?
She looked again. The statue was serious and still again. He did not wink or smile. Lily felt confused and excited. She wanted to tell Ben and Mom, but they would not believe her. She decided to keep it a secret. She smiled and winked back at the statue. Then she ran to join Ben and Mom. She felt happy. She had a new friend in the park.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Jake and Emma. Emma was always cheerful and she loved to play outside. 
One day, Jake wanted to show Emma something special. He took her to the car shed, and opened the door. Inside, was a big, shiny car. 
"Look Emma!" said Jake, "This is the driver's car!" 
Emma was amazed. She ran around the car, admiring it. 
"Can we take it for a drive?" asked Emma, her eyes wide and cheerful. 
Jake smiled. "Yes, if you like!" 
He opened the door and they both got in. Jake grabbed the key and gave it a snap. Suddenly, the car roared to life and they set off on their adventure. 
They drove around the city, looking at all the sights. They waved at people as they passed, and felt very happy and cheerful. 
After a while, it was time to head back to the car shed. "Bye bye driver! Thank you for the fun ride!" said Emma, waving goodbye. 
And from that day forward, Jake and Emma became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big closet in his room. One day, Tim wanted to go and play with his toys in the closet. But when he tried to open the closet, it was stuck! Tim was sad and didn't know what to do.
Tim thought about it and had an idea. He would ask his dog, Max, to help him. Max was a strong dog. Tim said, "Max, please help me open the closet." Max tried to pull the closet door with his teeth. Suddenly, the door opened!
When the door opened, Tim and Max saw something unexpected. Instead of toys, there were lots of balloons in the closet! The balloons came out and filled the room. Tim and Max were so happy and surprised. They played with the balloons all day long.

Once upon a time there was a naughty little mouse called Sammy. He used to explore the house every day and one day he entered the Iron Room. He saw the big iron and he was amazed by its shiny surface.
Sammy touched the iron and he quickly recognized how hot it was. Surprised, he quickly backed away. Somehow, he had to get back at it. He thought of a naughty plan.
He started to spin the iron around and around. He had never seen such a sight before. While doing this, he felt a warm gust of air coming from the iron.
Suddenly, his mother appeared in the room. She said, "Sammy! What are you doing? You should not play with the iron! It's very hot and dangerous!"
Sammy recognized his mother's voice and quickly stopped his plan. He was embarrassed and ran away from the Iron Room.

Once upon a time, there was a big cat named Tom. Tom loved to play with his ball of yarn. One day, Tom saw an enormous knot in his yarn. He wanted to untie it so he could play more.
Tom tried to untie the knot, but it was very hard. He would pause, think, and try again. His little friend, a tiny mouse named Jerry, saw him struggle. Jerry wanted to help Tom with the knot.
Together, Tom and Jerry worked on the knot. They would pause, look at it, and try again. After some time, they untied the knot! Tom was happy and gave Jerry a big hug. From that day on, they became best friends, and they always helped each other.

Once upon a time, there was a thin cat named Lily. Lily loved to play with her toys and read books. She had a big bookshelf in her room full of fun books.
One day, Lily saw a new book on the bookshelf. It was very big and didn't fit with the other books. Lily was curious and wanted to read it, but she couldn't reach it. She tried to climb the bookshelf, but it was too high.
Then, something unexpected happened. The bookshelf started to shake and the big book fell down. When it opened, a magic door appeared in the room. Lily was surprised and walked through the door. She found a world full of books and toys, and she played there all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a big, angry bear. He lived in a forest. One day, he wanted to leave his home to find food. He walked and walked until he found a cooler.
Inside the cooler, there were many yummy things to eat. The bear was so happy! He started to eat everything he found. He ate and ate until his big tummy was full.
But then, something unexpected happened. The cooler began to move! The bear was so scared. It turned out that the cooler was not a cooler at all. It was a big, friendly turtle! The turtle just wanted to share his food with the bear. The angry bear was not angry anymore. They became best friends and lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a dancer. The dancer liked to dance all day. The dancer was very good at dancing. The dancer had a friend. The friend was a big dog. The dog was very obedient. The dog liked to watch the dancer dance.
One day, the dancer and the dog went to the park. They saw a big ball. The dancer wanted to play with the ball. The dog wanted to play too. The dancer kicked the ball. The dog ran after the ball. The dog was happy.
The ball went into a tree. The dancer tried to get the ball. The dancer jumped and jumped. The dancer could not reach the ball. The dog had an idea. The dog slapped the tree with its tail. The ball fell down. The dancer and the dog played with the ball all day. They were very happy.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a sad man. The man was sad because he was old and missed being young. He wanted to play and run like the kids in the park. One day, he heard about a magic drink that could make him young again. The drink could restore his youth.
The man was very excited. He went on a long walk to find the magic drink. He looked everywhere, but he could not find it. The man became more sad and miserable. He sat down under a tree and started to cry.
A kind fairy saw the man crying and asked him what was wrong. The man told her about the magic drink. The fairy smiled and gave him a small bottle. She said, "Drink this, and you will be young again." The man drank the magic drink, and he became young again. He was so happy, he thanked the fairy and played with the kids in the park all day.

One day, a small boy named Tim went to search for his lost toy. He walked and walked until he found a stream. Tim saw a big fish in the stream. The fish looked at Tim and said, "Hello, little boy! What are you looking for?"
Tim told the fish, "I am searching for my toy. It is red and has wheels. Have you seen it?" The fish thought for a moment and said, "No, I have not seen your toy. But I can help you look for it."
As they searched, a foolish frog jumped out from behind a rock. The frog laughed and said, "I know where your toy is! I hid it behind that big tree!" Tim and the fish went to the big tree and found the toy. Tim was very happy and thanked the fish and the frog.
But then, the frog jumped on the toy and broke it. Tim was sad, but the fish had an idea. The fish asked the frog to fix the toy. The frog felt bad for being foolish and fixed the toy for Tim. In the end, they all became good friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a library. The library had many colorful books. Kids loved to go there and read.
One day, a magic cat appeared in the library. The cat could talk and loved to read books too. The kids were so happy to see the cat. They read books with the cat and listened to its stories.
The cat became their best friend. They all played and read books together in the colorful library. The kids and the cat lived happily ever after, reading and sharing stories every day.

Once upon a time, in a warm and cozy house, there lived a happy family. There was a mom, a dad, and a little infant named Tim. Tim loved to play games with his mom and dad.
One day, Tim's mom said, "Let's play hide and seek!" Tim clapped his hands and laughed. He was so excited to play. Tim's dad started counting, "One, two, three..."
Tim ran to find a place to hide. He saw a big box in the corner of the room. He crawled inside the box and waited for his dad to find him. Tim giggled quietly, hoping his dad would not hear him.
After a while, Tim heard his dad's footsteps coming closer. His dad looked around the room and said, "Where could Tim be hiding?" Tim tried not to laugh. Then, his dad found the big box and opened it. "There you are, Tim!" he said, smiling. Tim laughed and clapped his hands again. They all had so much fun playing hide and seek together.

Once upon a time, on a wide, wide beach, a little girl named Lily and her dog, Spot, were on holiday. They were playing in the sand and having lots of fun. The sun was shining, and the water was cool.
One day, while they were playing, they saw a baby bird fall from the sky. The baby bird landed in the water and needed help. Lily said, "Oh no, Spot! We need to rescue the baby bird!" Spot barked in agreement.
Lily and Spot ran to the water and swam to the baby bird. Together, they rescued the baby bird and brought it back to the beach. The baby bird was very happy and said, "Thank you, Lily and Spot!" They all played together on the beach for the rest of the holiday.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a dog named Spot. Spot loved to deliver things to his friends. He would carry things in his mouth and take them to his friends' homes. Spot was very good at his job.
One day, Spot had to deliver a big ball to his friend, Max. But the weather was terrible that day. It was raining a lot and very windy. Spot did not want to go outside, but he knew he had to deliver the ball to Max. So, he put on his raincoat and went outside.
Spot walked and walked in the rain. He was wet and cold, but he did not stop. Finally, he got to Max's house. Max was so happy to see Spot and the big ball. They played with the ball inside the house and had lots of fun. Spot was glad he did not stay home, even though the weather was terrible.

Farmer Tom was walking to his barn when he suddenly heard laughter. He looked up and saw a 3 year old girl hopping around a large oak tree in his field, her face beaming with joy. He smiled and waved to her, and she ran over to him, her face lighting up.
"Hi, Farmer Tom! What are you doing here?" she asked.
 Farmer Tom laughed, shaking his head in amusement. "Well, I decided to take a look at my field, to see how the crops are coming along."
The little girl grinned. "Want to see something cool? I want to show you something!" She gestured to the oak tree, and with a deep bow, she held out her hand.
Farmer Tom looked on in amazement. "What is this?"
"It's a view!" the girl exclaimed, pointing up the tree. Farmer Tom looked up to see a stunning sky dotted with clouds. He couldn't help but feel glad, and he smiled down at the girl.
"That is lovely," he said warmly. "Thank you for showing me this view! You made me really glad today."
The little girl beamed. "You're welcome! You can come back any time for another look." She skipped away, calling back, "Now I'm gonna go play!"

Once there was a girl named Mary who had a cube. She kept her cube very tidy and was very proud of it.
One day, Mary was playing with her cube and heard a little voice asking her a question. Mary looked around but she couldn't see anyone. 
She then heard the voice again, but this time it said, "Where did you get such a tidy cube?"
Mary was a bit scared but she answered bravely, "I got it from my mom."
The little voice replied, "Thank you for answering. I love your cube!" 
Mary was even more scared and she asked, "Who are you?"
The voice replied with a chuckle, "I am the Cube Fairy. I love to see tidiness. You take such good care of your cube, so I wanted to tell you."
Mary was so surprised! She thanked the Cube Fairy for her kind words, and she was very happy with her tidy cube.

Tim and Sam were friends. They liked to play with cars and balls. One day, Tim saw a new boy at the park. He had a big red car and a shiny blue tie. Tim wanted to be his friend.
"Hi, I am Tim. What is your name?" Tim asked the new boy.
"I am Max. I am wealthy. My dad has a lot of money. He gave me this car and this tie. They are very expensive. Do you have anything like that?" Max said.
Tim felt sad. He did not have a lot of money. He only had a small yellow car and a green scarf. He thought Max would not like him.
"No, I don't. But I have Sam. He is my best friend. We share everything. We care for each other. Do you have a friend?" Tim said.
Max looked around. He did not see anyone else. He felt lonely. He realized that money and things did not make him happy. He wanted a friend.
"No, I don't. I just have this car and this tie. But they are not fun. They are boring. Can I play with you and Sam?" Max said.
Tim smiled. He was kind. He did not mind that Max was wealthy. He wanted to be his friend.
"Sure, you can. Come on, let's play. Sam, this is Max. He has a big red car and a shiny blue tie. But he also wants to be our friend. Can we share our cars and balls with him?" Tim said.
Sam nodded. He was also kind. He did not care about money and things. He wanted to be Max's friend.
"Hi, Max. I am Sam. I like your car and your tie. They are very pretty. But I also like you. You seem nice. Do you want to play with us?" Sam said.
Max nodded. He was happy. He found two new friends. He did not need his car and his tie. He gave them to Tim and Sam. They gave him their car and scarf. They played together. They had fun. They cared for each other.

Once upon a time, in a big town, there was a tall billboard. The billboard was flexible and could bend. It was next to a park where kids played.
One day, a little girl named Lily found a pretty picture. She wanted to attach the picture to something. She saw the billboard and had an idea. "I want to put my picture on the billboard!" Lily said to her friend, Tom.
Tom said, "We can use tape to attach it!" They got tape and put the picture on the billboard. The wind blew, but the flexible billboard did not break. The picture stayed on the billboard, and everyone could see it. Lily and Tom were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to write. He had a folder where he kept all his drawings and words. Tim loved his folder very much. One day, Tim wanted to draw a big tree.
When Tim opened his folder, he saw a sour apple. The sour apple was big and green. Tim was surprised. He did not know how the apple got in his folder. He wanted to find out, so he asked his mom and dad. They did not know how the apple got there either.
Then, Tim had an idea. He drew a big tree in his folder with a big smile. The next day, he found another sour apple! The tree in his folder was turning his drawings into real things! Tim was so happy. He drew lots of things and they all came true. And every time he found a sour apple, he shared it with his friends.

Once upon a time, in a deep green forest, there lived a little bunny named Ben. Ben was only one year old. He liked to play and hop around with his friends. One day, Ben saw a big, red ball. He wanted to play with it, but it was far away.
Ben thought he should act like a big, strong bunny to get the ball. He hopped and hopped, but he could not reach it. Ben felt sad. He went to his friend, a wise, old owl. The owl told Ben, "You don't need to act like someone else. Just be yourself."
Ben tried again. This time, he did not act like a big, strong bunny. He was just little Ben. He hopped and hopped, and he got the ball! Ben was so happy. He played with the ball all day. The wise, old owl smiled. Ben learned that it is best to be yourself.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy raft that he liked to play with in the water. One day, he took his raft to the lake near his house.
At the lake, Tim saw a duck. The duck was confused and did not know where to go. Tim wanted to help the duck. He thought he could serve the duck by using his raft to take it to the other side of the lake.
Tim put the duck on his raft and pushed it across the water. The duck was happy and not confused anymore. Tim felt good that he could help the duck. He went home with a big smile on his face.

Once upon a time, there was a lovely cat named Kitty. Kitty liked to draw with her crayons. She had many crayons in different colors. Kitty was very happy when she drew pictures.
One day, Kitty met a little dog named Buddy. Buddy saw Kitty's drawings and said, "Wow, your drawings are very pretty! Can you teach me how to draw?" Kitty smiled and said, "Yes, I can teach you. Let's draw together!"
Kitty and Buddy sat down with the crayons. Kitty showed Buddy how to draw a big sun, a tree, and a house. They used all the colors of the crayons. Buddy was very happy and said, "Thank you, Kitty, for teaching me how to draw. Now we can draw lovely pictures together!" And they became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sally. She was a perfect girl who always did her best. One day, Sally had an exam at school. She was very excited to show her teacher how smart she was.
On the way to school, Sally saw a little dog. The dog was lost and looked very sad. Sally wanted to help the dog find its home, but she knew it would delay her going to school. She thought about her exam, but she knew the dog needed her help.
Sally decided to help the dog, even if it meant she would be late for her exam. She took the dog to her teacher, who helped them find the dog's home. Sally was late, but her teacher was proud of her for being kind. In the end, Sally got to take her exam and did a perfect job.

Once there was a shy zebra. She didn't like adventure very much. She stayed in one spot all day long. All the other animals were so much fun, but she stayed alone.
One day, the zebra noticed something. A piece of carrot was lying on the ground. She went over and smelled it. It smelled so yummy and fresh! She took a bite and it tasted so good. 
She kept eating the carrot, until it started to rot. She knew she had stayed in the same spot for too long. She was too shy to move. So the carrot rotted right where she was standing.
The zebra decided it was time to move. She was too shy to go with the other animals. But she knew she needed to go somewhere, before the next carrot started to rot.
So off she went, on her own. She was still shy but she knew she couldn't stay in one spot forever. And any carrot she found would be best eaten quickly!

Tim and Lily were playing outside when they saw dark clouds in the sky. They heard a loud noise like a drum. It was thunder. They saw bright flashes like fire. It was lightning.
"Let's go inside, Tim. I'm scared of the thunder and lightning," Lily said.
"No, Lily. I'm not scared. I'm brave. I want to stay and watch the lightning. It's fun," Tim said.
But soon, the rain started to pour. It was very heavy and cold. Tim and Lily got wet and shivered. They ran to the house, but the door was locked. Mom and Dad were not home yet.
"Help, help! Let us in!" Tim and Lily cried. They knocked on the door and windows, but no one answered.
They saw a big tree near the house. It had a hole in its trunk. They decided to hide in the hole until the rain stopped. They crawled inside and felt a little warmer and drier.
But then, they heard another loud noise. It was louder than the thunder. They saw a bright flash. It was brighter than the lightning. The tree was hit by the lightning. It started to burn.
"Help, help! We're trapped!" Tim and Lily screamed. They tried to get out of the hole, but it was too small. They felt the heat and the smoke. They were scared and sorry.
They wished they had listened to Lily and gone inside earlier. They wished they had not been so stubborn and foolish. They wished they had been more careful and safe.
They learned a hard lesson. They learned that thunder and lightning are not fun. They learned that they should listen to their friends and parents. They learned that they should respect nature and its power.
Luckily, Mom and Dad came home and saw the fire. They called the firemen and they came with a big truck and a loud siren. They used water and axes to put out the fire and free Tim and Lily. They were very happy to see their children alive, but they were also very angry and sad. They hugged them and scolded them.
"Tim and Lily, you were very naughty and reckless. You could have been hurt or killed by the lightning. You should never play outside when there is thunder and lightning. You should always come inside and stay safe. Do you understand?" Mom and Dad said.
"Yes, Mom and Dad. We're sorry. We understand. We won't do it again. We love you," Tim and Lily said.
They were very sorry and ashamed. They were also very grateful and relieved. They thanked the firemen and apologized to Mom and Dad. They promised to be more obedient and wise. They hugged Mom and Dad and each other. They learned their lesson. They learned to be careful and safe.

Once upon a time, in a big valley, there lived a small tree. The tree was not tall like the other trees. The small tree wanted to become tall and strong.
One day, a kind bird came to the small tree. The bird told the tree to be patient and wait. The tree would become tall one day, just like the other trees in the valley. The small tree was happy to hear this.
Over time, the small tree grew and grew. The sun shone, the rain fell, and the small tree became taller and taller. The other trees in the valley were happy too. They all lived happily in the big valley, with the tall tree now standing proud among them.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia was very polite. She always said "please" and "thank you." One day, Mia saw a small box in her room. She started to worry. She did not know what was inside the box.
Mia's big sister, Lily, saw her worry. Lily said, "Do not worry, Mia. I have a surprise for you." Mia felt better. She was excited to see what the surprise was.
Lily helped Mia open the box. Inside the box was a cute, little toy cat. Mia was very happy with her surprise. She gave Lily a big hug and said, "Thank you, Lily!" From that day on, Mia and her toy cat were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a fish named Fin. Fin lived in a pond with his family. One day, the water in the pond started to smell bad. Fin and his friends did not like the bad smell.
Fin wanted to help his friends. He swam to find what was making the bad smell. He saw a big bag in the pond. Fin thought the bag was making the water bad. He knew he had to get the bag out of the pond.
Fin tried to push the bag out, but it was too heavy. He swam to his friends and asked for help. Together, they all managed to push the bag out of the pond. The water was not bad anymore. Fin and his friends could breathe the good oxygen again. They were all happy and played together in the clean water.

Once upon a time, in a small humble house, there lived a little boy named Tim and his mom. They were happy and loved each other very much.
One day, Tim's mom asked him to help her set the table for dinner. She gave him a big plate and a small plate. Tim looked at the plates and asked, "Mom, which plate should I use?"
His mom smiled and said, "Use the big plate, Tim. We have a lot of food to eat tonight." Tim started to put the big plate on the table. He was excited for dinner.
After they ate their dinner, Tim and his mom washed the plates together. They laughed and splashed water on each other. They had so much fun in their humble home. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a cow named Bessie. Bessie liked to eat tasty grass all day. She lived on a big farm with many other cows. The farmer who took care of them was very nice.
One day, Bessie heard the farmer say, "I need to keep Bessie safe. There is a big storm coming." Bessie did not know what a storm was, but she knew the farmer wanted to help her.
The sky got dark and it started to rain. Bessie was scared, but the farmer put her in a warm barn with the other cows. They all felt safe and dry. After the storm, the farmer said, "Bessie, I kept you safe." Bessie was happy and went back to eating her tasty grass.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty whale named Wally. Wally lived in the big blue sea with his friends. They liked to play and swim all day.
One day, Wally and his friends saw some boats. The boats were making the sea dirty. Wally said, "We need to unite and clean our home!" His friends agreed.
So, Wally and his friends worked together. They pushed the dirt and trash away from their home. The sea became clean and pretty again. Wally and his friends were happy. They swam and played in their clean home.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Bobo. Bobo was an ordinary dog who lived in a small house with a big fence. He loved to run and play in his yard.
One day, Bobo saw a big ball outside the fence. He wanted to learn how to get the ball. Bobo tried to jump over the fence, but he was too small. He thought and thought about how to get the ball.
Then, Bobo had an idea. He found a long stick and used it to reach the ball. He pushed the ball with the stick until it rolled through a hole in the fence. Bobo was so happy that he had learned how to get the ball. And from that day on, Bobo knew he could do anything if he tried hard and used his brain.

Once upon a time there was a dolphin who loved to swim in the ocean. One day, the dolphin felt lonely, so it decided to explore. It swam for a very long time, until it saw a distant island.
The dolphin started swimming to the island, but it was tired and couldn't move anymore. It shouted for help, but no one was around. Then suddenly, a kind voice answered.
"Don't worry," said the voice from the sky. "I will forgive you and help you." The dolphin looked up and saw a tiny bird flying above him.
The bird offered to fly the dolphin to the island. The dolphin agreed and flew away with the bird. As they flew, they talked and became best friends.
When they reached the island, the dolphin learned that it was a magical place. Dolphins were free to roam and explore as far as they wanted. The dolphin was so happy, it waved goodbye to its new friend and started to explore.
The dolphin returned to the ocean with a smile, knowing that it was safe and happy. That day, the dolphin learned that it was important to forgive and be kind to others, even when they seem distant.

One day, a curious little girl named Sarah wanted to try something new. She got a cup and went outside. Gently, she let the cup slip out of her tiny hand. Sarah watched as the cup flew away, twirling in the sky. She clapped her hands in delight and ran to catch it, but it was too far for her. 
Sarah's mommy heard her laughing and came to see what was happening. Together, they laughed and enjoyed the sight of the cup dancing in the air. Eventually, it came down and landed safely on the grass. Sarah ran over and carefully picked it up. She was so glad to still have her cup!

Ben and Lily were playing with their toy boats in the pond. They liked to pretend they were sailors and go on adventures. They had a red boat and a blue boat, and they took turns to steer them.
"Look, Lily, I see a big fish!" Ben said, pointing to the water. "Maybe it wants to be our friend."
Lily leaned over to see the fish. It was shiny and green, and it had a long tail. She smiled and waved at it. "Hello, fish! Do you want to play with us?"
The fish did not answer. It swam closer to the boats, and then it opened its mouth. It grabbed the red boat with its teeth and pulled it under the water.
"Hey, that's not nice!" Ben shouted. He was sad and angry. The red boat was his favorite. He tried to reach for it, but it was too late. The fish was gone, and so was the boat.
Lily felt sorry for Ben. She hugged him and said, "I'm sorry, Ben. That was a bad fish. It should not take our toys."
Ben sniffled and nodded. He hugged Lily back and said, "Thank you, Lily. You are a good friend. And a good sailor."
Lily smiled and said, "You are welcome, Ben. You are a good friend too. And a good sailor."
They decided to share the blue boat and continue their adventure. They hoped they would not meet any more bad fish. They had each other, and that was enough.

Once upon a time there was a dry onion. The onion was sad because it had never seen paint. One day, a kind little girl named Sally picked up the onion. Sally had a bucket of colourful paint. She wanted to paint the onion, so she carefully brushed paint over its dry skin. 
The results of Sally's painting were surprising. Something magical happened and the onion began to smile. It could now see in technicolour! After seeing its colourful new look, the onion felt happier than ever before. 
Sally was very proud of her artwork and waved goodbye to the onion as it skipped away in its new beautiful form.
Moral: Kindness brings happiness.

Once upon a time, there was a little mouse named Mimi. Mimi lived in a tiny hole in a big house. One day, Mimi found a piece of cheese on the floor. She was very happy and wanted to eat it.
As Mimi was eating the cheese, a big cat named Tom saw her. Tom wanted to catch Mimi. Mimi was scared and said, "Please don't catch me, Tom!" Tom replied, "I will let you go if you give me the cheese." Mimi thought about it and decided to give Tom the cheese so she could escape.
Mimi learned that it is good to share with others, even if they are scary. Sharing can help make friends and keep everyone safe.

Once upon a time, in an ancient house, there lived a girl named Lily. She loved to write and draw. One day, Lily found a big pick in the garden. She thought it was special and wanted to show her friend, Tom.
Lily ran to Tom's house and said, "Tom, look at this big pick I found! Let's draw with it!" Tom was excited and said, "Yes, let's write our names on the ground with it!"
Together, they used the pick to write their names in the dirt. They laughed and played all day. They drew pictures of the sun, trees, and even their houses. They had so much fun using the ancient pick to write and draw.
As the sun began to set, Lily and Tom knew it was time to go home. They said goodbye and promised to play again soon. Lily took the ancient pick back to her house and put it in a special place, so they could use it again the next time they played together.

Once upon a time, there was a green frog. The green frog lived near a pond. The pond had many bugs. The green frog loved to eat the bugs.
One day, the green frog found a bottle. The bottle had lotion inside. The green frog did not know what lotion was. The green frog thought it was food. So, the green frog tried to open the bottle.
The green frog tried to attach the bottle to a rock. The green frog tried to open the bottle with the rock. But, the bottle did not open. The green frog tried and tried, but it could not open the bottle.
The green frog was very sad. The green frog could not eat the lotion. The green frog could not eat the bugs. The green frog was very hungry. The green frog went away and did not come back.

One day, a little goat named Tim went for a walk. Tim liked to skip and play in the sun. It was easy for him to jump and run.
As Tim skipped, he saw a big tree. Tim wanted to climb the tree, but it was not easy. He tried and tried, but he could not get up.
Just then, a kind bird saw Tim. The bird wanted to help Tim climb the tree. So, the bird took Tim's hand and they flew up together. Tim was happy and said, "Thank you, bird!" They played in the tree all day and had lots of fun.

Lily liked to read books. She had many books on her bookshelf in her room. She liked to look at the pictures and make up stories in her head. Sometimes, her mom or dad would read to her before bed.
One night, Lily wanted to read a new book. She saw a big book on the top of her bookshelf. It had a picture of a dragon on the cover. Lily liked dragons. She wanted to see what was inside the book. She climbed on a chair and reached for the book.
But when she touched the book, it made a loud noise. It said, "ROAR!" Lily was scared. She dropped the book and fell off the chair. She ran to her mom and dad in the living room. She was crying.
"What's wrong, Lily?" her mom asked. She hugged Lily and tried to calm her down.
"There's a scary book in my room!" Lily said. "It roared at me!"
Her dad went to check the book. He came back with a smile. He showed Lily the book. He said, "This is not a scary book, Lily. It's a fun book. It has a button that makes the dragon roar. See?" He pressed the button and the book roared again.
Lily was still scared. She refused to look at the book. She said, "No, no, no! I don't like that book. I want a different book."
Her mom said, "It's okay, Lily. You don't have to read that book if you don't want to. We can pick another book from your bookshelf. How about this one?" She showed Lily a book with a picture of a bunny on the cover.
Lily smiled. She liked bunnies. She said, "Yes, yes, yes! I like that book. Can you read it to me?"
Her mom said, "Of course, sweetie. Let's go to your room and read it together." She took Lily and the book to her room. She tucked Lily in her bed and read her the story of the bunny. Lily felt happy and safe. She fell asleep with the book in her arms.

Once upon a time, there was a rare helicopter named Heli. Heli was not like other helicopters. He could sing! Heli loved to sing happy songs for his friends. All the animals in the forest loved to listen to Heli's songs.
One day, a little bird named Tweet lost her voice. She could not sing anymore. Tweet was very sad. Heli saw that Tweet was sad and wanted to help. Heli thought, "I can teach Tweet to sing again!"
So, Heli and Tweet practiced singing together every day. Slowly, Tweet started to find her voice again. Heli was very patient and kind. Soon, Tweet could sing just as beautifully as before. All the animals in the forest were very happy to hear Tweet's songs again.
The moral of the story is that when we help our friends, we can make them happy. Just like Heli helped Tweet, we can help our friends too. Always be kind and patient, and good things will happen.

One day, a girl named Lucy found a small, interesting scale in her room. She did not know where it came from. Lucy showed it to her friend, Tom. "Look, Tom! I found a scale!" she said.
Tom wanted the scale too. "Give it to me!" he said. Lucy did not want to give it to him. They both wanted the scale, so they started to argue.
Lucy's mom saw them arguing and said, "I will send the scale back to where it came from if you can't share." Lucy and Tom did not want that to happen, so they decided to share the scale and play together.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a mighty boy named Tim. Tim loved to wear his yellow vest every day. He was very strong and could lift heavy things.
One sunny day, Tim went to the park to play. He saw a big log on the ground. Tim wanted to balance on the log. He put one foot on the log and then the other foot. He wobbled a little bit, but he did not fall.
Tim was very happy that he could balance on the log. He felt like a mighty hero in his yellow vest. All his friends clapped and cheered for him. And from that day on, Tim knew he could do anything he set his mind to.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a creative squirrel named Sam. Sam loved to play with his friends and find acorns to eat. One day, while Sam was looking for acorns, he found a big acorn that was stuck in a bush.
Sam tried to pull the acorn out, but it was very hard. He asked his friend, Birdy the bird, for help. "Birdy, can you help me get this acorn out?" Sam said. Birdy was happy to help. They both pulled and pulled, but the acorn did not move. Then, Sam had an idea. He used his sharp teeth to tear the bush around the acorn.
With the bush torn, Sam and Birdy could finally get the acorn out. They were very happy and shared the acorn together. From that day on, Sam and Birdy became best friends, and they always helped each other find acorns in the big forest.

Jenny was so excited to go to the restaurant. She had been looking forward to it all week and could not wait to get there.
Her mom lifted her up into the car and they drove off. When they arrived, Jenny saw that the restaurant was brown. She was so happy to be there!
Inside, they found a big table to sit at. Jenny smiled when she saw it. Then, a waiter came over with a napkin. He asked Jenny to wipe her hands with it.
Jenny laughed and said, "No, thank you! I don't need to wipe my hands." The waiter smiled and walked away. 
Later, Jenny and her mom shared a big, delicious pizza. Jenny smiled as she ate and thought abut how fun it was to be in the restaurant.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside in the sun. One day, while playing, he saw a figure in the distance. The figure was big and looked a little scary. Tim was curious, so he decided to walk closer to see what it was.
As Tim got closer, he saw that the figure was a very big and filthy dog. The dog was covered in dirt and mud. Tim felt bad for the dog and wanted to help. He stared at the dog for a moment, thinking about what to do.
Tim decided to take the dog home and give it a bath. He filled a big tub with warm water and gently washed the dog. After the bath, the dog was clean and happy. Tim and the dog became best friends, and they played together every day. And they lived happily ever after.

Lisa loved to play the piano. She liked to make loud and soft sounds with her fingers. She also liked to sing along with the sounds. She felt happy when she played the piano.
One day, Lisa had a big show at her school. She was going to play the piano in front of many people. She was very excited and nervous. She wanted to do well and make everyone proud.
She went on the stage with her teacher. She saw her mom and dad and her friends in the crowd. She smiled and waved at them. She sat on the piano bench and put her hands on the keys. She was ready to play.
But then, something strange happened. The piano disappeared! Lisa looked around and saw nothing but the empty stage. She did not know what to do. She felt scared and confused. She heard some people laugh and some people gasp. She felt very embarrassed.
She started to cry. She wanted her piano back. She wanted to play her song. She wanted to go home.
Her teacher came to her and hugged her. She told her it was okay. She told her it was a trick. She told her someone had moved the piano with a big cloth and a rope. She told her it was a joke.
Lisa did not think it was funny. She was still sad and angry. She did not want to play anymore. She wanted to leave the stage.
Her teacher said she was sorry. She said she did not know about the trick. She said she would find out who did it and make them say sorry too. She said she would bring the piano back and let Lisa play her song.
Lisa nodded. She wiped her tears. She hoped the piano would come back soon. She hoped the people would stop laughing. She hoped she could still have fun.

One day, a sweet cat named Kitty found a book. Kitty loved to match things. She matched her toys, her food, and her friends. She was very good at it. The book she found had many things to match. Kitty was very happy.
Kitty started to match the things in the book. She matched the ball with the dog, the fish with the water, and the sun with the sky. It was very fun. But then, something unexpected happened. When Kitty matched the last thing, the book started to shake.
The book opened and out came all the things Kitty matched. The ball bounced, the fish swam, and the sun shined. Kitty was surprised, but she was happy too. She had made new friends to play with. And they all lived happily together.

Ben and Mia liked to play with their toys. They had many toys, but their favorite was the jet. The jet was big and shiny and made a loud noise when they pushed a button. They pretended to fly the jet around the room and have adventures.
One day, their mom said, "Ben and Mia, it is time to clean up your toys. Put them in the box, please." Ben and Mia did not want to stop playing. They said, "No, mom, we want to play more." Mom said, "You can play later, but now you have to tidy up. It is good to be neat and helpful."
Ben and Mia did not listen. They hid the jet under the sofa and put the other toys in the box. They thought mom would not see the jet. They ran to the kitchen to have a snack. Mom came after them and said, "Did you put all your toys in the box?" Ben and Mia nodded and smiled.
But mom was not fooled. She said, "What about the jet? Where is the jet?" Ben and Mia looked at each other and felt guilty. They said, "We don't know, mom. We can't find the jet." Mom said, "Let's go and look for it together."
They went back to the living room and mom lifted the sofa. She saw the jet and said, "Here it is. Why did you hide it?" Ben and Mia said, "We wanted to play with it more. We are sorry, mom." Mom said, "I understand, but you have to do what I ask you. The jet is not only a toy, it is also useful. It can teach you about flying and shapes and colors. But if you don't take care of it, it can break or get lost. Then you won't have it anymore. Do you want that?" Ben and Mia said, "No, mom, we don't want that. We love the jet." Mom said, "Then you have to put it in the box with the other toys. And next time, listen to me when I tell you to clean up. OK?" Ben and Mia said, "OK, mom. We are sorry. We will do better." They hugged mom and put the jet in the box. They learned their lesson and were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Buddy. Buddy was a very naughty dog. He loved to run and play all day. One day, Buddy saw a big race with many people running. Buddy wanted to join in and have fun too.
Buddy ran fast and joined the race. The people in the race were very nice and they welcomed Buddy to run with them. They all ran and ran until they reached the finish line. Buddy was so happy to be a part of the race.
At the end of the race, everyone got a medal to remember the fun day. Buddy also got a medal, even though he was a naughty dog. He wore his medal proudly and promised to be a good dog from now on.

Lily and Ben are playing in the living room. They have many toys, but they are bored. They want to do something new and fun.
Lily has an idea. She suggests to Ben, "Let's play with the flowers. We can make them pretty and smell them." Ben likes the idea. He nods and smiles.
They go to the kitchen and find a vase. It is big and blue and shiny. They fill it with water and put it on the table. Then they go to the garden and pick some flowers. They pick red roses, yellow daisies, and purple violets. They are careful not to hurt the flowers or the bees.
They bring the flowers to the living room and put them in the vase. They arrange them in a nice way. They smell the flowers and say, "Ahh, they smell so good." They are happy and proud of their work.
Mom comes in and sees the vase. She is surprised and pleased. She says, "What a beautiful vase of flowers! Who made it?" Lily and Ben say, "We did, Mom. We wanted to make something new and fun." Mom hugs them and says, "You are very clever and kind. The flowers are fine. They make the living room bright and cozy. Thank you, Lily and Ben." Lily and Ben are happy. They love Mom and the flowers.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play in the kitchen. They want to cook a cake for Mom. They find a big bowl and some eggs and flour and sugar.
"Can I crack the eggs?" Lily asks.
"OK, but be careful," Ben says. He pours the flour and sugar in the bowl.
Lily cracks one egg and puts it in the bowl. She cracks another egg and puts it in the bowl. She cracks a third egg and drops it on the floor.
"Oh no!" Lily says. "I made a mess!"
Ben looks at the floor. He sees the egg and the shell. He is not happy.
"Lily, you have to be honest. You have to tell Mom what you did," he says.
"But Mom will be mad at me," Lily says. She feels sad.
"No, Mom will not be mad. She will be glad that you are honest. She will help us clean the floor and cook the cake," Ben says. He hugs Lily.
"OK, I will tell Mom. I am sorry, Ben," Lily says.
They go to find Mom and tell her what happened. Mom smiles and kisses them.
"Thank you for being honest, Lily and Ben. I love you both. Come on, let's clean the floor and cook the cake together," she says.
They clean the floor and cook the cake. The cake is yummy and Mom is proud of them. Lily and Ben are happy. They learned to be honest and to cook.

One day, a little boy named Tim had a bag. He was restless and wanted to go outside to play. He put his toys in the bag and went to the park.
At the park, Tim met a girl named Sue. Sue said, "Hi, I want to play too!" Tim and Sue played with the toys from the bag. They had lots of fun.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and took the bag up in the air! Tim and Sue were sad. But then, a kind bird flew up and got the bag for them. Tim and Sue were happy again and thanked the bird. They played with their toys and had a great day at the park.

Sara was a smart girl. She was playing with her toys outside when she spotted a furry animal. It was a kitty! She was excited and ran over to it. The kitty didn't care and walked away.
Sara was sad. She thought the kitty didn't like her.
Then her mom came over. She smiled and said, "Don't worry. Kitties can be shy sometimes. Try this. Get a gentle rub and show the kitty you care."
Sara followed her mom's advice. She slowly and gently rubbed the kitty's back. The kitty stopped and looked up at Sara. It meowed softly and purred with happiness. Sara and the kitty were friends! She was so happy.
Sara was glad her mom taught her how to care for animals. She was so smart!

Once upon a time, there was a lazy cat named Sam. Sam loved to sleep all day and play all night. One day, Sam saw a big surf by the sea. He wanted to play in the surf, but he was too lazy to move.
Sam's friend, a bird named Tim, called out to him. "Sam, come play in the surf with me!" Sam thought about it and decided to join Tim. They played in the surf, splashing and having fun.
But then, something unexpected happened. A big wave came and took Sam far away from the shore. Tim quickly flew to help Sam. He called out to Sam and grabbed his paw. With a strong pull, Tim saved Sam from the big wave. Sam was not lazy anymore and thanked Tim for saving him. They became best friends and played together every day.

One day, a gifted dog named Pepper went to play in the park. Pepper loved to run and jump on the grass. He saw a little girl with a red ball. The little girl said, "Hi, Pepper! Do you want to play with me?"
Pepper was happy and played with the little girl. They threw the ball and ran around the park. Suddenly, the ball flew very high and got stuck in a tree. The little girl was sad and said, "Oh no, Pepper! How will we get the ball back?"
Just then, a big bird came and took the ball out of the tree. The bird gave the ball back to the little girl. She was so happy and said, "Thank you, bird! You are very kind." Pepper, the little girl, and the bird all played together for the rest of the day.

Once upon a time, there was a clown. He was wearing a purple hat and purple shoes. Everywhere he went, he made people smile and laugh.
One day, the clown was so tired he had to take a break. He found a sunny spot and lay down to yawn.
Suddenly, a mean monster came out of the shadows. "Mwahaha!" chuckled the monster. "You will never make people laugh again!" The monster made the clown's purple hat and shoes disappear.
The clown was so sad, he couldn't even make himself smile. He had nothing left to do but go home and never be a clown again. The end.

One day, a little girl named Amy was playing in the park. She was running and jumping when she fell down. She hurt her knee and started to cry. A thoughtful boy named Tim saw her and came over to help.
"Are you okay?" Tim asked. Amy said, "No, I hurt my knee." Tim looked at her knee and realized it was just a small scrape. He told her, "Don't worry, it's just a little boo-boo. Let's go find your mom."
Amy and Tim walked around the park, but they couldn't find Amy's mom. They started to feel scared. They didn't know what to do. Then, they saw a big tree in the middle of the park and realized that Amy's mom was sitting under it.
Amy and Tim ran to the tree and found Amy's mom. She saw Amy's knee and gave her a hug. "Thank you, Tim, for being so thoughtful and helping Amy," said Amy's mom. Tim smiled and said, "You're welcome. I'm happy to help."

Once upon a time, a little fish named Fin lived in the sea. The sea could be harsh, but Fin had many friends. They liked to play and swim all day.
One day, Fin asked his mom, "Can I go play with my friends in the sea?" His mom said, "Yes, I will allow you to go, but be careful. The sea can be harsh sometimes."
Fin swam and played with his friends. They had so much fun. But then, the sea started to get harsh. The waves got big and scary. Fin's friends said, "We must go home now."
Fin swam back to his mom, safe and sound. He told her all about his day. His mom was happy that he had fun, but she reminded him always to be careful in the harsh sea.

Once upon a time, there was a happy girl named Lily. She loved to dance and talk with her friends. One day, she wanted to have a big dance party. She told her friends, "Let's have a dance party and have fun!"
At the party, everyone was dancing and having a good time. Lily was dancing and talking with her friends. Suddenly, a big, successful cat came to the party. The cat was very good at dancing. Everyone stopped and looked at the cat.
The cat said, "I want to dance too!" At first, Lily and her friends were scared. But then, they saw that the cat was friendly. So, they all danced together and had a great time. The cat became their new friend and they had the best dance party ever.

One day, a cute bunny named Lily went for a walk. She liked to skip and hop in the sunny park. Lily saw a big tree with sweet red apples. She wanted to eat one, but they were too high up.
"Can you help me?" asked Lily to a tall giraffe named Max. Max stretched his long neck and picked an apple with his mouth. He gave it to Lily. "Here you go, Lily," said Max with a smile.
Lily was happy and said, "Thank you, Max!" They both ate apples and played together. Lily learned that asking for help and sharing made her new friend happy. The moral of the story is to be kind and help others.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. Lily saw an old man sitting on a bench. He had a big red balloon. Lily really wanted the balloon, so she walked up to the man.
"Hi, can I have your balloon?" Lily asked. The old man smiled and said, "I think you can. But first, can you tell me what color it is?" Lily thought for a moment and said, "The balloon is red!"
The old man gave Lily the red balloon. She was very happy. Lily and her mom played in the park all day with the red balloon. When it was time to go home, Lily said, "Thank you, old man, for the red balloon!" The old man smiled and waved goodbye.

One day, a farmer was working on his farm. He saw that his land was very rough. He wanted to change it to make it nice and smooth. So, he got his big tractor and started to work.
As he was working, a little bird came and said, "Why do you want to change the rough land? I like it this way." The farmer said, "I want to make it smooth so I can grow more food for everyone."
But then, something unexpected happened. The farmer found a big, shiny treasure box under the rough land. The farmer and the little bird were very surprised. They opened the treasure box and saw many beautiful things inside.
The farmer said, "I will share this treasure with everyone in the village." The little bird was happy and said, "Thank you, farmer. You are very kind. I will tell all my bird friends about your good heart."
From that day on, the farmer and the little bird were good friends. The farmer did not change the rough land, but he still grew lots of food for everyone. And the village was always happy because they had a kind farmer and a treasure to share.

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful girl named Lily. She loved to play with clay. She made a lot of fun things like animals, trees, and houses with the clay.
One day, Lily's friend, Tim, came to her house. He saw her clay animals and said, "Wow, Lily! Your clay animals are so cool! Can you lend me some clay to make my own?" Lily smiled and said, "Of course, Tim! I will lend you some clay."
Tim and Lily played with the clay and made more animals. But then, something unexpected happened. The clay animals started to move and talk! Lily and Tim were so surprised. The clay animals said, "Thank you for making us! We are happy to be alive!" Lily and Tim laughed and played with their new clay friends all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a popular girl named Amy. Everyone wanted to be her friend.
Amy wanted to have even more friends so she decided to increase her popularity. 
Every day she would work to become more popular. 
One day, she had a bad nightmare. She saw herself being all alone, even though she was popular. 
When she opened her eyes, she was very scared. 
So Amy decided that she wouldn't increase her popularity anymore. Instead, she made sure to treat her existing friends extra special. 
She wanted them to know that she would never have a nightmare like that again. 
The end!

Once upon a time there was a silly girl. She liked to joke all the time and her friends thought it was fun.
One day the silly girl went outside in the yard. She saw a big tree with lots leaves and branches. She looked at it and had an idea.
The girl started to joke. She knew funny jokes and made her friends laugh. Her friends started to giggle and clap. 
The silly girl thought it was fun to joke in the yard and did it everyday. Her friends were happy and laughed with her.
The end.

Once there was a big oven. It was so big that it was longer than a house. One day, a mother and her three year old child decided to go and visit the oven. They were both so excited!
When they arrived, the child couldn't believe how big it was. It was almost as tall as an elephant. The mother smiled and opened the door. Inside, it was all warm and cozy. The three year old couldn't wait to explore.
The mother and her child looked around and poked their heads inside to see all the different buttons and knobs of the oven. The child touched and looked at everything.
After an hour of exploring, the mother and the child said good bye to the big oven and went back home. They both had a great time visiting the big oven.

One day, a little dog named Max was playing in the park. Max was very obedient and always listened to his owner, Sam. They were having a fun day, running and playing with a ball.
Max saw a bottle on the ground. He didn't know that it had poison inside. Max was curious and wanted to sniff it. Sam saw Max near the bottle and knew it was dangerous. He shouted, "Max, hurry! Come here!"
Max heard Sam and ran back to him quickly. Sam picked up the bottle and threw it away. "Good boy, Max," Sam said. Max wagged his tail, happy that he listened to his owner.
Together, they continued playing in the park. Max was careful to stay away from things that could hurt him. Sam was proud of his obedient dog, and they had a great day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a delicate flower. It was red and pretty. It lived in a big garden with other flowers. One day, the flower wanted to bloom and be big like the other flowers.
A little rat lived in the garden too. He saw the delicate flower and wanted to help. The rat went to the flower and said, "Hi, I want to help you bloom. Let's be friends." The flower was happy and said, "Yes, let's be friends!"
Together, the rat and the delicate flower worked hard. The rat brought water to the flower every day. The flower grew bigger and bigger. One sunny day, the flower bloomed. It was big and beautiful. The rat and the flower were very happy. They were best friends forever.

One day, a boy named Tim went to play golf with his dad. They were having a lot of fun. Tim was very good at golf and he liked to lean on his golf stick when he was waiting for his dad to hit the ball. Tim's dad was not as good as Tim, but he was very persistent. He kept trying and trying, even when he missed the ball.
"Tim, I will keep trying until I hit the ball very far," said his dad. Tim smiled and said, "I know you can do it, dad!"
Then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and blew Tim's hat away. Tim and his dad ran after the hat, but it was going very fast. They saw the hat go into a big hole in the ground. Tim and his dad looked at each other and decided to go into the hole to get the hat back.
Inside the hole, they found a secret golf game with many kids and their parents playing. Tim and his dad were very surprised. They played golf in the secret place and had even more fun than before. Tim's dad became better at golf and they found Tim's hat too. They were very happy and could not wait to tell their family about the secret golf game.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. They saw a big strawberry on the ground. Lily was very happy and wanted to eat it.
Lily said, "Mom, can I eat the strawberry?" Her mom said, "No, Lily, it's dirty. We must be careful not to be careless with food." Lily was sad but understood.
As they walked, Lily saw a ball. She decided to kick it. She kicked it hard and it flew high into the air. Then, the ball hit the big strawberry and it rolled away. Lily and her mom laughed and continued to play at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a lively little girl named Lily. She liked to play, jump, and dance all day. One day, her mommy asked her, "Lily, do you want to learn something new today?"
Lily said, "Yes, mommy! I want to learn!"
So, mommy showed Lily a big box with a screen. She said, "This is a computer. We can use it to study and learn new things." Lily looked at the computer and was very excited.
Mommy turned on the computer and they started to study. They learned about animals, colors, and shapes. Lily was so happy to learn new things with mommy. She said, "Mommy, I love studying with you and the computer!"
From that day on, Lily and her mommy would study on the computer every day. They had lots of fun learning new things together. And Lily became a very smart and lively little girl.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. It was a cool day, and he saw a small cricket in the grass. He wanted to be friends with the cricket, so he said, "Hi, little cricket! I am Tim. What is your name?"
The cricket looked at Tim and said, "Hi, Tim! My name is Crick. I like to jump and play all day."
Tim thought for a moment and then asked, "Crick, how far can you jump? Can we measure it?" Crick agreed, and they found a stick to help them measure. Tim put the stick on the ground, and Crick jumped as far as he could.
Tim looked at the stick and said, "Wow, Crick! You jumped very far! You are good at jumping." Crick smiled and thanked Tim for measuring his jump.
From that day on, Tim and Crick were best friends. They played together every day, and Tim helped Crick measure his jumps to see if he could jump even farther. They both had lots of fun, and they were always happy when they were together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Mary. Mary had lots of friends, but she never felt really lucky. One day, she decided to go for a walk in the park. As she walked, she saw something bloom in the distance. It was a stranger. 
The stranger had a huge smile on his face. Mary was scared of this stranger, but he said, "Don't worry, I'm not here to hurt you. I just want to give you something great." 
Mary felt a little bit better. She asked the stranger, "What do you want to show me?" 
The stranger reached into his pocket and pulled out a lucky charm. It was really sparkly and it made Mary feel really happy. She thanked the stranger and gave him a hug. 
Mary took the lucky charm home with her and carried it with her everywhere. She now felt really lucky, and with the lucky charm, it only got better. She couldn't wait to show her friends!

Once upon a time, there was a fat, happy cat. The cat loved to play outside. One day, the cat found a swing in the park.
The cat wanted to play on the swing. It jumped on the swing and started to move. The cat felt happy and free as it swung back and forth.
Soon, a little bug came by. The bug wanted to play too. The bug poked the cat's nose. The cat giggled and they played together. The fat cat and the little bug had lots of fun on the swing all day long.

One day, a girl named Lily went for a walk. She loved to observe things around her. Today, she saw many shapes in the park. There were round shapes, square shapes, and even star shapes. Lily liked to look at all the shapes.
As she walked, she found a small, spicy red pepper on the ground. She thought it was a new shape to add to her collection. Lily picked it up and put it in her pocket. She was very happy.
Suddenly, the spicy red pepper jumped out of her pocket! It was not a shape at all, but a tiny spicy red bug! Lily was surprised but she laughed. Now, she had a new friend to observe shapes with her in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a staff. The staff was very tall and clear. It liked to help people.
One day, the staff saw a small cat. The cat was up high in a tree. The cat wanted to come down, but it was scared. The staff wanted to help the cat.
The staff went to the tree and let the cat hang on. The cat hung on tight. The staff helped the cat come down. The cat was happy and said "thank you" to the staff. They became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little mouse named Tim. Tim loved cheese more than anything. One day, he made a promise to his mom that he would find the most useful thing in the world.
Tim went out to search. He looked under a big tree and found a round rock. "This is useful," said Tim, "I can roll it." He took the rock home, but his mom said, "This is not the most useful thing, Tim. Try again."
The next day, Tim looked in a tall bush and found a long stick. "This is useful," said Tim, "I can poke things with it." He took the stick home, but his mom said, "This is not the most useful thing, Tim. Try again."
On the third day, Tim looked by a small pond and found a shiny key. "This is useful," said Tim, "I can open things with it." He took the key home, and his mom smiled.
Just then, something unexpected happened. The key started to wiggle and grow! It turned into a big piece of cheese! Tim and his mom laughed. The cheese was the most useful thing after all. Tim kept his promise, and they both enjoyed the yummy cheese.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a little girl named Mia. Mia was a very tough girl. She liked to learn new things every day. One day, Mia saw a big cross on her way to the park. She wanted to learn how to cross the road.
Mia's mom told her to look left and right before crossing the road. Mia was very good at learning. She looked left and right, just like her mom told her. But Mia didn't see the big puddle in the middle of the road.
As Mia walked across the road, she slipped and fell into the puddle. She was all wet! But Mia was a tough girl, and she didn't cry. She learned that she needed to look down too when crossing the road.
From that day on, Mia always looked left, right, and down before crossing the road. She also helped her friends learn how to cross the road safely. The moral of the story is to always look all around you before crossing the road and to learn from your mistakes.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a toy named Mr. Iron. Mr. Iron was a patient and kind toy. He always listened to Lily when she talked.
One sunny day, Lily and Mr. Iron went to play in the park. They saw a boy crying. Lily asked the boy, "Why are you sad?" The boy said, "I lost my toy and I can't find it." Lily and Mr. Iron decided to help the boy find his toy.
They looked for the toy all around the park. Finally, they found the toy under a big tree. The boy was so happy, he started to laugh. Lily and Mr. Iron laughed too. They all played together for the rest of the day.
The moral of the story is to always be kind and help others when they are in need.

Once upon a time, there was a sad princess. She lived in a big castle. The princess did not like to eat. Her mom and dad, the king and queen, were worried about her.
One day, a funny little cat came to the castle. The cat loved to eat. The princess liked the cat and wanted to play with it. The cat showed the princess a magic plate of food.
The princess tried the food from the magic plate. She liked it! She started to eat more and more. Then, something unexpected happened. The princess turned into a happy cat! Now, she loved to eat and play with her new cat friend. The king and queen were happy too, because the princess was not sad anymore.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a small building. In the building, there lived a little boy named Tim and his mom. Every day, Tim would watch the sun rise from his window. He loved seeing the sky change colors.
One morning, Tim's mom said, "Let's go outside and watch the sun rise together!" Tim was very happy. They put on their coats and went outside. They sat on a bench and waited for the sun to rise.
As the sun began to rise, Tim looked at his mom and said, "Mom, the sun is so pretty!" His mom smiled and said, "Yes, Tim, it is. The sun helps us start a new day." They watched the sun rise and enjoyed the beautiful colors in the sky. Then, they went back to their small building, ready to start their day.

Once upon a time, there was a kind and compassionate cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play with her ball and run around the house. One day, Kitty found a strip of paper on the floor. She picked it up and played with it.
Kitty's friend, Doggy, saw the strip of paper and wanted to play with it too. Doggy took the strip from Kitty and ran away. Kitty seemed sad because she wanted to play with the strip too. There was a big fight between Kitty and Doggy over the strip of paper.
In the end, Kitty and Doggy decided to share the strip of paper. They both played with it together, and they had lots of fun. They learned that sharing is better than fighting, and they became even better friends.

Once upon a time, there was a big, heavy rock near a cliff. One day, a little bird saw the rock and wanted to sit on it. The bird's friend, a squirrel, saw the bird near the cliff and wanted to warn her. 
"Be careful, bird!" the squirrel said. "The cliff is not safe. You may fall." But the bird did not listen and sat on the heavy rock.
Suddenly, the rock began to move! The bird was scared and flew away. The rock rolled down the cliff and landed in a big pile of soft leaves. The squirrel and the bird were surprised. The leaves had saved the day! They laughed and played in the leaves, happy that they were safe.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a group of animals. They were all honest and good friends. They liked to play together all day long. One day, they found a big door with a sign that said "exit."
They were curious about what was behind the door. So, they decided to unite and go through the exit together. They held hands and walked through the door.
Behind the exit, they found a beautiful park full of yummy food and fun games. The honest animal friends were so happy that they united and found this special place. They played and ate all day long. From that day on, they always remembered to unite and help each other. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Mia woke up very early. She was happy because it was a sunny day and she wanted to play outside. She got out of her bed and put on her shoes and coat. She did not want to wake up her mommy and daddy, so she was very quiet.
She opened the door and went outside. She saw many flowers and birds and bugs. She wandered around the garden and smelled the roses. She saw a big butterfly and followed it. The butterfly flew over the fence and into the woods. Mia wanted to see where it went, so she climbed over the fence too.
She walked in the woods and saw many trees and animals. She heard a funny sound and looked around. She saw a little boy sitting on a log. He was playing with a toy car. He looked up and saw Mia.
"Hi, who are you?" he asked.
"Hi, I'm Mia. I followed a butterfly. Who are you?" she answered.
"I'm Leo. I live in that house over there. Do you want to play with me?" he offered.
"Yes, please. I like cars. Can I see yours?" she said.
"Sure, here you go. It's red and fast. Do you have any toys?" he said.
"I have a doll. She's pink and pretty. Do you want to see her?" she said.
"Okay, why not. She looks nice. Do you want to be friends?" he said.
"Yes, I do. You are nice too. Do you want to be friends?" she said.
They smiled and nodded. They played with their toys and talked and laughed. They were happy. They did not know that their mommies and daddies were looking for them. They had wandered too far and they were lost. But they did not feel scared. They had each other. They were friends.

Once upon a time, there was a thick book. The book was a test. This test belonged to a big girl named Sally. Sally was not happy. She did not like tests.
One day, Sally's friend Tim came over. "Sally, I can help you with the test," said Tim. Sally was happy. She thought Tim was very smart. They sat down to look at the thick book.
But Tim was not very good at tests. He did not know the answers. Sally and Tim tried and tried, but they could not do the test. They were sad. The thick book was too hard for them.
In the end, Sally and Tim did not pass the test. They were very sad. They learned that they needed to ask for help from someone who knew more about the test. The thick book was a big challenge for them, but they promised to try harder next time.

One day, a little girl named Lucy found a silly card on the ground. The card had a funny face on it. Lucy wanted to show the card to her friend, Tom. She ran to Tom's house and knocked on the door.
"Look, Tom!" said Lucy, holding up the silly card. "I found this funny card. Let's play with it!"
But when Lucy tried to give the card to Tom, she accidentally dropped it. The card fell into a big puddle. Lucy and Tom looked at the wet card and felt sad.
"I'm sorry, Tom," said Lucy. "I didn't mean to drop the card." Tom looked at Lucy and said, "It's okay, Lucy. We can still play with it. We'll just let it dry first." They both smiled and went inside to play with other toys while they waited for the silly card to dry.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tyler who loved to play outdoors. He'd climb trees and make forts in the sandbox. 
One day, Tyler was out playing with a rope when he had an idea - he wanted to fly like a bird! So, he tied the rope around his middle and started running around, trying to take off. He ran and ran as fast as he could, faster and faster, but the rope just wouldn't lift him off the ground.
Just then, his mom came out of the house and saw him. She was worried and called out, "Tyler! What are you doing!"
Tyler stopped and said, "Mom, I want to fly like a bird!"
His mom smiled sadly and kneeled down beside him. She said in a compassionate voice, "Tyler, sweetheart, you can't actually fly - it's just make believe. But if you want to feel like you're flying, you can swing on the rope if you swing it really hard and hold on tight." 
So Tyler did as his mom said and soon he was swinging high and far. He felt like he was flying, even though he knew it was only pretend. But that was ok, because he was having a blast!

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Amy. She had a toy car with lots of gears. She loved to play with the car and make it go fast. One day, she took her toy car to the park to play with her friend, Tom.
Tom had a soft ball that he liked to squeeze. When Amy saw the ball, she said, "Can I squeeze your ball, Tom?" Tom smiled and said, "Yes, you can squeeze my ball, Amy." They took turns squeezing the ball and playing with Amy's toy car.
As they played, Amy showed Tom how to change the gears on her car to make it go faster. Tom was excited to learn about the gears. They had a lot of fun playing together with the car and the ball. At the end of the day, they were both very happy and tired. They said goodbye and went home, excited to play together again soon.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had an adorable dog named Spot. Tim and Spot loved to play in the park. One day, they went to the park to play. Tim saw a big apple tree. He wanted to pick an apple to eat.
Tim tried to reach the apples, but he was too short. He asked Spot to help him. Spot tried to jump and grab an apple, but he couldn't reach it either. Then, they heard a sound. They both stopped and listened. It was a tiny bird in the tree.
The bird saw Tim and Spot trying to get an apple. The bird wanted to help them. So, the bird flew up and picked an apple from the tree with its beak. The bird dropped the apple down to Tim and Spot. They were so surprised and happy!
Tim and Spot thanked the bird for helping them. They shared the apple with the bird, and they all became good friends. From that day on, Tim, Spot, and the bird played together in the park all the time. And they always listened to each other's stories while eating apples under the apple tree.

Once upon a time, there was a little crab named Tim. He loved to race with his friends on the beach. They would run on the sand near the water when the tide came in. Tim was very fast, and he always won.
One day, a new crab came to the beach. Her name was Sue. Sue was bigger and faster than Tim. When they raced, Sue won every time. Tim was sad, but he was also grateful to have a new friend to play with.
One day, the tide came in very fast. It took all the crabs' toys away. Tim and Sue had to race to save them. They ran as fast as they could. Then, something unexpected happened. Tim found a big shell and used it as a boat. He put all the toys in the shell and floated back to his friends. Sue helped him, and they saved all the toys together. The other crabs cheered for them. Tim learned that winning is not everything, and it is more important to help each other.

Lily and Ben were going on a holiday with their mom and dad. They were very happy and excited. They packed their bags with clothes, toys and books. Mom told them to wear warm coats and hats, because it was cold where they were going.
On the plane, Lily and Ben sat next to a boy and a girl who looked different from them. They had dark skin and curly hair. They spoke a language that Lily and Ben did not understand. Lily and Ben were curious and wanted to talk to them, but the boy and the girl ignored them. They whispered and laughed to each other.
Lily and Ben felt sad and angry. They thought the boy and the girl were rude and ignorant. They did not want to be friends with them. They told their mom and dad what happened. Mom and dad listened and smiled.
They said, "Lily and Ben, you should not judge people by how they look or what they speak. They are not ignorant, they are just different. They have their own culture and history. Maybe they are shy or scared of you. Maybe they want to be friends, but they don't know how. You should try to be kind and respectful to them. You might learn something new and have fun with them. That is the best way to enjoy your holiday."
Lily and Ben thought about what their mom and dad said. They felt sorry for being mean and rude. They decided to give the boy and the girl another chance. They smiled and waved at them. They said, "Hello, we are Lily and Ben. What are your names? Where are you from? Do you want to play with us?"
The boy and the girl smiled and waved back. They said, "Hello, we are Ayo and Nia. We are from Nigeria. We are going to visit our grandma. Yes, we want to play with you."
Lily and Ben were happy and surprised. They found out that Ayo and Nia liked the same things as them. They liked to draw, read and sing. They shared their toys and books. They taught each other some words in their languages. They laughed and had fun.
