Lily and Ben learned a valuable lesson. They learned that people are not ignorant, they are just different. They learned that being kind and respectful is the best way to make friends and enjoy their holiday.

Once upon a time, a mule named Max lived on a farm. Max was a weird mule. He liked to wear a big red hat. One day, Max met a new friend named Sam. Sam was a little boy who lived near the farm.
"Hi, Max," said Sam. "I like your big red hat. Can I try it on?" Max nodded, and Sam put on the hat. They played all day, running and laughing. They had so much fun together.
As the sun began to end the day, Sam had to go home. Max didn't want to say goodbye, so he thought of a fun idea. He took off his big red hat and gave it to Sam. "Keep it," Max said with a smile. Sam was very happy and promised to come back and play with Max again. Now, they would always remember their fun day together.

One day, a big, thick bear named Tom went to find lumber in the woods. He wanted to build a new home. Tom had a friend, a small bird named Sue. Sue flew next to Tom as they looked for lumber.
"Remember, we need strong lumber," said Sue. Tom nodded and they kept looking. They found some thick lumber and started to carry it back home. But then, something unexpected happened.
As they walked, they saw a big pile of lumber that was already cut. Tom and Sue looked at each other and smiled. They did not have to carry the heavy lumber anymore. They used the new pile to build their home and it was perfect.

Once there was a little girl. Her name was Sarah. She loved to play in the water. Every day she went to the big pond and splashed in the cool water.
One day Sarah went to the pond and it was bigger than ever before. It was full of luxurious purple water. There was even a purple slide that went straight into the water. 
Sarah was so happy to see the purple water. She couldn't wait to splash around and have fun. She grabbed the slide and slid into the purple water. Splash!
The water felt so nice and it was just the right temperature for splashing about. Sarah jumped, she ran, and she splashed all around. She had the best time of her life! 
In the end Sarah was even more excited about the purple luxury water than she was before. She couldn't wait to go back and splash around again!

One day, a little bird named Tim was learning how to fly. He lived in a nest with his mom, dad, and two sisters. Their nest was on a high cliff, and Tim could see a lot of trees and a big blue sky.
One morning, Tim's mom said, "It's time for you to learn how to fly." Tim was scared, but he knew he had to try. His mom and dad showed him how to flap his wings and jump. Tim jumped and flapped, but he could not fly. He screamed, "Help me, I'm scared!"
His mom and dad came to help him. They held him and said, "Don't worry, Tim. We will help you learn to fly." They practiced together every day. Tim got better at flapping his wings and jumping off the cliff. One day, he jumped and started to fly. Tim was so happy that he screamed, "I can fly! I can fly!"

One day, a cat and a dog were in a room. They wanted to discuss what to eat. The cat said, "I want fish." The dog said, "I want a bone."
The cat saw a note on the table. The note was from their friend, a bird. The bird said, "I left a mild surprise for you both. Look in the box!" The cat and the dog were happy.
They opened the box and found a big cake! The cat and the dog were surprised. They did not expect a cake. They ate the cake and were very happy. The cat said, "Thank you, bird!" The dog said, "We love the cake!"

Lily and Tom are playing in the park. They have a big ball and a small match. Lily likes to throw the ball high in the air and catch it. Tom likes to throw the match on the ground and watch it burn.
"Look, Lily, look!" Tom says. He throws the match on the grass and it makes a small fire. "It is fun!"
"No, Tom, no!" Lily says. She is scared of the fire. "It is bad! It can hurt us and the park!"
Lily runs to the match and stomps on it with her foot. She puts out the fire. She is angry with Tom. "Don't do that again, Tom! It is not fun, it is dangerous!"
Tom feels sorry. He did not mean to scare Lily or hurt the park. He hugs Lily and says, "I'm sorry, Lily. I won't do that again. I love you and the park."
Lily hugs Tom back and says, "I love you too, Tom. Let's play with the ball instead. It is safer and more fun."
They take the ball and throw it to each other. They laugh and have fun. They are happy.

One day, a little girl named Emily was playing in her garden. She saw a lovely flower and said, "That flower is mine!" She tried to pick it, but it was too high. 
Just then, Emily saw a butterfly. She reached out to catch it in her hand, but it flew away. She said, "Oops, that was helpless." 
Suddenly, a big bird appeared. It began to fly over the garden and it was carrying something in its beak. Emily shouted, "That is mine!" The bird didn't stop and flew away with it. Emily was sad and felt helpless. 
Just then, her mom saw her crying. "What happened?" she asked. Emily told her mom about the flower and the butterfly, and the big bird taking away her thing. Her mom said, "Let's try to get it back." 
They followed the bird and when they finally caught up to it, Emily's mom asked the bird to give back the thing. The bird understood and removed it from its beak. Emily was so happy and thanked the bird. Then the bird flew away. 
The thing was Emily's favourite toy. She was very happy that she got it back. Emily was no longer helpless, and she hugged her toy tightly.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot had a broken leash. His owner, a kind girl named Sally, wanted to take him for a walk. She knew she needed to fix the broken leash first. 
Sally worked hard to fix the broken leash. She tied it together so it would be strong again. Now, Spot could go for a walk with Sally. They were both very happy.
One day, they went to the park. Sally saw a seat and sat down to rest. Spot stayed close to her on his leash. Then, they saw another dog running without a leash. The dog ran away and got lost. Sally and Spot felt sad for the lost dog.
Sally was glad she fixed Spot's leash. She learned that it is important to take care of things and keep them safe. Spot and Sally walked home together, thankful for their strong leash and the lesson they learned.

Once there was a lively little girl named Sally. She loved to play and have fun. One day, Sally saw a costume in the store. It was a pretty dress with lots of colors. She told her mom, "I want that costume!"
Her mom said, "Okay, let's buy it." They took the costume home, and Sally put it on. She danced and twirled around the house. She was so happy!
But then, something unexpected happened. A little dog came into the house. The dog saw Sally in her costume and thought she was a big toy. The dog jumped and played with Sally, but the costume got ripped. Sally was sad, but her mom said, "Don't worry, we can fix it." They sewed the costume back together, and Sally could play in it again.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a girl named Lily. Lily loved to play with her mom's makeup. One gloomy day, Lily decided to have some fun.
She put on red lipstick, blue eyeshadow, and a big pink blush on her cheeks. She looked in the mirror and laughed. She thought she looked so funny.
Lily's mom came into the room and saw her. "Oh, Lily!" she said. "What did you do?" Lily just giggled and said, "I look funny, Mommy!" Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, you do. But let's clean up now."
Lily's mom helped her wash off the makeup. Then, they went outside to play. Lily picked up a ball and threw it to her mom. They played catch and had so much fun, even on a gloomy day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tommy. Tommy was healthy and strong and he loved to explore the jungle. 
One day Tommy was out exploring the jungle when he felt something pinch his leg. He looked down and saw a tiny frog. Tommy gasped and pinched it back carefully. The frog gave a big hop and ran away.
Tommy laughed and ran after it, feeling braver than ever. The frog hopped into a bush, but Tommy heard it giggle. He knew he'd found a friend!
He peered into the bush. "What's your name? Can I play with you?" He asked the frog.
The frog laughed and said, "My name is Frog and yes, let's play together! I know a really fun game that we can play in the jungle. Would you like to play?"
Tommy smiled and said yes. He and Frog spent the whole day playing together in the jungle, running and hopping and laughing. By sunset, Tommy and Frog were both feeling happy and healthy.

One day, a dancer went to the park. She liked to stand on her toes and dance. She was very good at dancing. She danced and danced all day.
A boy saw the dancer and wanted to dance too. But he was a little rude. He did not say please or thank you. The dancer did not like this. She told the boy to be nice.
Then, something unexpected happened. The boy was really a dog! The dog stood on his back legs and danced with the dancer. They danced and danced all day. The dancer was happy, and the dog was nice. They became good friends.

One day, a big helicopter was flying in the sky. It was very happy. The helicopter liked to turn in the sky and make fun shapes. It made the people on the ground laugh and clap.
"Look, Mom! The big helicopter is turning in the sky!" said a little boy. His mom smiled and said, "Yes, it is! It's having so much fun up there!"
But then, something unexpected happened. The big helicopter saw a big bird in the sky. The helicopter did not want to hit the bird, so it turned very fast. The bird was scared and flew away.
The people on the ground were surprised. They did not know what happened. The big helicopter felt bad for scaring the bird. It flew down to the ground and said, "I'm sorry, bird. I did not mean to scare you."
The bird smiled and said, "It's okay, helicopter. Thank you for not hitting me. Let's be friends." And from that day on, the big helicopter and the bird were the best of friends.

Once upon a time there was a fair bunny who was looking for some lotion. He went on a long walk to try and find some. After a while, he came across an old tree in a far away field. He stopped to rest underneath the tree. All of a sudden a fox came out of the tall grass and the bunny couldn't run away. The fox chuckled and said he was going to play a game with the bunny. The bunny had to give the fox two of his carrots if he wanted the lotion. The bunny was so desperate for the lotion so he agreed to play the game. The fox grabbed two of the bunny's carrots and said he'd meet him the next day nearby the tree with the lotion.
The bunny was so excited and couldn't wait to have get the lotion! He waited all night for the fox to meet him, but the fox never showed. The bunny had been tricked! He was so sad and angry that he had been tricked.
The moral of the story is to be careful when you meet people you don't know. Never trust someone too quickly and always think twice before making a decision.

Sara and Tom like to play outside in the fall. They see many red and yellow leaves on the ground. They want to make a big pile of leaves and jump in it. They ask their mom if they can use the rake. Mom says yes, but be careful.
Sara and Tom work hard with the rake. They drag the rake over the grass and gather the leaves. They make a very big pile of leaves. They are happy and excited. They run to the pile and jump in it.
But they do not know that the rake is still in the pile. When they jump in, they feel something sharp and hard. It is the rake. It hurts them. They cry out loud. Mom hears them and runs to them. She sees the rake and the blood. She is scared and angry.
She takes Sara and Tom to the house. She cleans their wounds and puts bandages on them. She tells them they were very careless and foolish. They should have moved the rake away from the pile. They are sorry and ashamed. They are also uncomfortable. They have pain and itch. They cannot play outside for a long time. They wish they had been more careful. They do not like the rake anymore.

Tom and Anna are playing with their toys in the living room. Tom has a car and Anna has a doll. They are having fun, but then Tom sees something shiny on the floor. He picks it up and looks at it. It is a screw.
"Look, Anna, I found a screw!" Tom says. "It is shiny and hard. I can use it for my car."
"No, Tom, that is my screw!" Anna says. "It is part of my doll's dress. Give it back to me!"
Tom does not want to give it back. He likes the screw. He thinks it is cool. He puts it in his pocket and runs away.
"Tom, stop!" Anna cries. She runs after him. She is angry and sad.
They run around the living room, knocking over chairs and books. Their mom hears the noise and comes to see what is going on.
"What are you doing, Tom and Anna?" Mom asks. "Why are you making such a mess?"
Tom and Anna stop running. They look at Mom. They are scared.
"Tom took my screw!" Anna says. She points at Tom's pocket. "It is mine! He has to give it back!"
"Tom, is that true?" Mom asks. "Did you take Anna's screw?"
Tom nods. He feels bad. He takes out the screw and gives it to Anna.
"I'm sorry, Anna," Tom says. "I did not know it was yours. I thought it was a toy."
Anna takes the screw and hugs it. She smiles.
"It's okay, Tom," Anna says. "I forgive you. You can play with it sometimes, if you want."
Tom smiles too. He hugs Anna.
"Thank you, Anna," Tom says. "You are a good sister. I love you."
Mom smiles too. She hugs Tom and Anna.
"I love you too, Tom and Anna," Mom says. "You are good children. But please, be careful with the screw. It is not a toy. It is sharp and small. You can hurt yourself or lose it. And please, clean up the mess you made."
Tom and Anna nod. They say sorry to Mom. They help her clean up the living room. They put their toys away. They are happy and calm. They learned to share and to be honest. They are good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and have fun with her friends. One day, Lily's mom said, "Lily, do you want some juice?" Lily smiled and said, "Yes, please! I love juice!"
Lily's mom gave her a cup of juice. It was great juice, sweet and yummy. Lily drank it all up. After she finished, she wanted to play with her toys again. But then, she remembered something. "Mom, can you remind me to drink more juice later?" she asked. Her mom said, "Of course, I will remind you, dear."
Later that day, Lily played with her friends in the park. They had so much fun running, jumping, and laughing. When they were tired, Lily's mom came with more juice for everyone. She reminded Lily, "Here's more juice for you and your friends!" All the kids drank the juice and felt great. They said, "Thank you, Lily's mom!" And they played happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a pretty dress with a shiny buckle on it. Lily loved her dress very much. One day, she went to play with her friend, Tim.
Tim saw Lily's dress and felt envious. He wanted a shiny buckle too. So, he asked Lily, "Can I borrow your buckle, please?" Lily said, "Okay, but you must give it back to me later."
Tim took the buckle and went away. He played with it all day long. But then, he lost the buckle. Tim looked everywhere, but he could not find it. Lily came back to get her buckle, but Tim said, "I am sorry, I lost your buckle."
Lily was very sad. She did not have her shiny buckle anymore. Tim felt bad too. They both learned that borrowing things can sometimes lead to a bad ending.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had a regular card with a picture of a cat on it. Lucy loved her card and took it everywhere she went.
One day, Lucy went to the park with her card. She saw a boy named Tim playing with a ball. Lucy had a question for Tim. She asked, "Do you like my card?" Tim looked at the card and said, "Yes, I like your cat card!"
Lucy and Tim played together at the park. They took turns asking each other questions about their favorite things. They had so much fun that they became best friends. And from that day on, they always played together with Lucy's regular cat card.

Lila and Ben went to the zoo with Mom and Dad. They saw many animals, like lions, monkeys, and elephants. Lila liked the elephants best, because they had big ears and long noses. Ben liked the monkeys best, because they made funny noises and jumped around.
Then they came to the ostriches. Ostriches are big birds with long necks and legs. They have feathers, but they cannot fly. Lila and Ben wanted to see them closer, so they leaned on the fence. But one ostrich did not like that. It came near them and pecked at their hats.
"Ow, ow, ow!" Lila and Ben cried. "Go away, you smelly ostrich!"
Mom and Dad heard them and ran to help. They pulled Lila and Ben away from the fence and checked their heads. They were not hurt, but their hats were torn and dirty.
"Sorry, kids, the ostrich was probably scared of you. You should not lean on the fence, it is not safe," Mom said.
"But Mom, the ostrich was smelly and mean. It ruined our hats," Ben said.
"Maybe it was smelly because it was hot and sweaty. And maybe it was mean because it was hungry or lonely. You should try to be kind to animals, even if they are different from you," Dad said.
Lila and Ben felt a little sorry for the ostrich. They decided to give it some of their popcorn as a peace offering. The ostrich ate the popcorn and made a soft noise. It looked happier.
"See, it is not so bad. Maybe it just wanted to be friends," Lila said.
"Maybe. But I still like the monkeys better," Ben said.
They waved goodbye to the ostrich and went to see the other animals. They had a fun day at the zoo.

Jack was a three year old boy who loved playing with his blocks. He had so many different colours and shapes, but his favourites were the original blocks made by his grandfather. 
One day, when Jack was playing with the blocks, his dad came into the room. He was holding a box that was filled with even more blocks. 
Jack ran to his dad and squealed with joy when he saw all the new blocks. He asked where his dad had found them. His dad smiled and said he had found them in the attic.
Jack's dad helped him empty the box and set up the blocks. When Jack was finished, he stepped back to admire his work. He had arranged the blocks into an amazing display of different shapes and colours. 
Jack was proud of his work and excited to show it off to his family. He knew that no one else could make a display like his, because it was completely original.

Once upon a time, there were two twin boys named Tim and Tom. They looked the same, and they liked to play together. One day, they found a big, red ball in the park. They both wanted to play with it, but they didn't know whose ball it was. Tim had a troubled look on his face, but Tom just smiled and nodded.
The next day, they went back to the park with their mom. They saw a sad girl who was looking for something. She said, "I lost my big, red ball." Tim and Tom looked at each other and remembered the ball they found. They knew what they had to do.
They took the girl to the ball and gave it back to her. She was very happy, and they all played together. The twins learned that it is good to help others and share. And from that day on, they became good friends with the girl, and they always played together in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a funny ghost named Boo. Boo liked to play with his friends in a big, old house. They would laugh and have fun all day.
One day, Boo and his friends wanted to play a new game. They decided to add more friends to their game. Boo went out to find new friends to play with. He found a cat, a dog, and a bird. They all wanted to play with Boo and his friends.
Boo brought the cat, dog, and bird to the big, old house. They all played together and had lots of fun. The funny ghost and his new friends laughed and played until it was time to go to sleep. They all said goodnight and went to their beds, excited to play together again the next day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. Tom loved to play outside in the wind. One day, he saw a big, scary tree. The tree had no leaves, and its branches looked like arms.
Tom walked up to the tree and said, "Why are you so scary?" The tree did not say anything. Tom wanted to make the tree happy, so he decided to help it.
Tom found some leaves and used them to spring onto the tree's branches. The wind blew and the leaves danced around the tree. The tree started to look less scary. The tree said, "Thank you, Tom. Now I am not scary anymore."
Tom smiled, and he and the tree played in the wind together. They were very happy, and the tree was not scary anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Tim. Tim loved to imagine things. One day, he imagined he was a big bunny chef. He wanted to make a salad for his friends.
Tim felt happy when he made the salad. But he was a little embarrassed because it was his first time. He put in green leaves, red tomatoes, and yellow corn. He hoped his friends would like it.
When Tim shared the salad with his friends, they all loved it! They told Tim he was a great chef. Tim felt proud and not embarrassed anymore. From that day on, he loved to make salads for his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lisa. Lisa was a good girl who loved to help her mom in the kitchen. One day, Lisa's mom was making a big pot of soup. Lisa wanted to help, so her mom gave her a spoon to stir the soup.
As Lisa stirred the soup, she thought it would be fun to improve the taste. She found a jar of spicy red powder and put some in the soup. When her mom tasted the soup, she said, "Oh no! This soup is too spicy now. What can we do?"
Lisa felt sad, but she wanted to fix her mistake. She asked her mom, "Can we add more water and vegetables to make the soup less spicy?" Her mom agreed, and they worked together to improve the soup. In the end, the soup was delicious, and Lisa learned that it's always important to ask for help before trying something new.

Once upon a time, there was a big, weird bed. This bed could talk and walk. It lived in a house with a little girl named Lily.
One day, Lily asked the bed, "Why are you so weird?" The bed replied, "I am weird because I can talk and walk. But I enjoy being your friend and helping you sleep at night."
Lily and the bed played all day. They enjoyed fun games like hide and seek. When it was time for bed, Lily would climb on the weird bed and they would share stories until Lily fell asleep. The weird bed was happy to be Lily's friend and they enjoyed their time together.

Once upon a time, there was a soldier. He was a very cheap soldier. He liked to chew gum all day. One day, he met a little boy. The little boy saw the soldier chewing gum.
"Can I have some gum too?" the little boy asked. The cheap soldier gave the little boy a gum. They both chewed the gum together.
They walked and talked. They became good friends. The soldier and the little boy chewed gum every day. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to school. He was very excited because today was the day of the big exam. Tim wanted to do well because he liked to be the best in his class. He was very competitive.
As Tim sat down in his chair, his teacher said, "It's time to set the exam on your desk." Tim looked at the exam and started to work. All of a sudden, a funny thing happened. The words on the exam started to move and change. Tim was very confused.
Tim raised his hand and said, "Teacher, my exam is changing!" The teacher looked at Tim and smiled. She said, "That's because today's exam is a special magic exam. It's not about being the best, but about having fun and learning together." All the kids in the class laughed and had a great time with the magic exam. They learned that being competitive was not always the most important thing.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Tim. Tim loved to fly high in the sky. He wanted to see the sunrise. The sunrise was very pretty and impressive. Tim knew that to see the sunrise, he had to complete a big task. He had to wake up very early.
One day, Tim went to bed early. He wanted to wake up before the sun came up. He slept and slept. When it was still dark, Tim woke up. He was very excited to see the sunrise.
Tim flew high in the sky. He looked at the sky and saw the sun coming up. The sunrise was very impressive. Tim was happy he completed his task. He saw the pretty sunrise and felt proud. Tim flew back home and told his friends about the impressive sunrise.

Once upon a time, there was a big hospital. In the hospital, there were many useful things to help people feel better. The hospital was a happy place.
One day, it started to rain. The rain was very wet and cold. The people in the hospital needed to stay warm and dry. So, the nurses found a big cover. They put the cover over the hospital to keep everyone safe from the rain.
The cover was very useful. It kept the people in the hospital warm and dry. The rain stopped, and the sun came out. Everyone in the hospital was happy again. They said, "Thank you, cover, for keeping us safe and dry!" And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a cute dog named Spot. Spot had an owner named Tim. Tim and Spot loved to play in their big yard. One day, Tim saw Spot looking uncomfortable. Spot was trying to resist scratching his ear.
Tim asked, "Spot, why are you uncomfortable?" Spot could not talk, but he shook his head and tried to resist scratching again. Tim knew he had to help his friend.
Tim took Spot to a nice lady who could help. The nice lady looked at Spot's ear and gave him something to make him feel better. Soon, Spot was not uncomfortable anymore. He did not need to resist scratching his ear. Tim and Spot went home and played in the yard, happy and healthy.

Once upon a time, in a far away land, there was a little volcano. The volcano lived all by itself and was very independent. It did not need help from anyone. It had a friend named Tim, who was a little boy. Tim liked to visit the volcano and talk to it.
One day, Tim said to the volcano, "Let's play a game!" The volcano agreed, and they played a fun game of catch. Tim threw a big ball, and the volcano caught it with its top. They laughed and played for a long time.
But then, something unexpected happened. The volcano sneezed! When it sneezed, it made a big boom sound, and a little bit of smoke came out. Tim and the volcano were surprised. They did not know that the volcano could sneeze. From that day on, they had a new game to play: the sneezing game. They would play it whenever Tim came to visit, and they had lots of fun together.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a boy named Tim. Tim was always hungry. One day, he went for a walk to find some food. On his way, he found a big apple under a tree. He picked it up and took it with him.
As Tim walked, he saw a girl named Lisa. Lisa was trying to polish her shoes. She was having a hard time. Tim wanted to help. He gave Lisa his apple to hold. Then, he helped her polish her shoes. They became very shiny.
After they finished, Lisa gave Tim a pass to her mom's bakery. Tim was so happy! He went to the bakery and used the pass. He got a big yummy cake. Tim was not hungry anymore. He shared the cake with Lisa, and they became best friends.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. They run and jump and slide and swing. They are happy.
One day, the sun is mild. It is not too hot and not too cold. Anna and Ben see some flowers. They are yellow and pink and purple. They smell good.
Anna picks a yellow flower and gives it to Ben. Ben smiles and says thank you. He picks a pink flower and gives it to Anna. Anna smiles and says thank you. They hug each other.
Then, Anna and Ben do something new. They kiss each other on the cheek. They feel shy and giggle. They say they are best friends.
They hold hands and go back to play. The sun is mild. It is a good day.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little bunny named Lily. She lived in a small house in the woods with her family. Lily loved to play with her friends, the birds and the squirrels.
One day, a big, scary animal came into the woods. All of Lily's friends were scared, and they ran away to hide. Lily felt a threat from the big animal, and she was scared too. She ran back home to her family.
Her mommy and daddy saw that she was scared, so they gave her a big hug to comfort her. They told her that everything would be okay. The big animal went away, and Lily's friends came back. They all played together again, happy and safe.

Ben and Mia like to play in the bath. They have many toys that can float and splash. Sometimes they pretend they are pirates or mermaids. But today they have a new game. They want to soak each other with water.
"Ready, set, go!" Ben says and fills a cup with water. He throws it at Mia, who squeals and laughs. She grabs a sponge and squeezes it over Ben's head. Water drips from his hair and nose. He makes a funny face and Mia giggles.
They keep soaking each other until the water is cold and the floor is wet. They have so much fun that they forget to wash their hair and body. Mom comes to check on them and sees the mess. She is not happy.
"Ben, Mia, what are you doing?" she asks. "You are supposed to take a regular bath, not a water fight. Look at the floor, it is all wet. And your hair and body, they are still dirty. You need to wash them with soap and shampoo."
Ben and Mia feel a little guilty. They know Mom is right. They have an urge to say sorry and hug her. But they also have an urge to soak her too. They look at each other and smile. They have a plan.
"Sorry, Mom," they say together. "We were just having fun. Can you help us wash our hair and body?" They sound very sweet and innocent. Mom smiles and nods. She comes closer to the bath. Ben and Mia grab their cups and sponges. They are ready to soak her. They think it will be funny. But Mom is smarter than they think. She sees their plan and ducks. She grabs a towel and wraps it around her. She laughs and says, "Nice try, you two. But you can't soak me. Now let's wash up and get out of the bath. It's time for bed." Ben and Mia laugh too. They know Mom is not mad. They love her. They wash up and get out of the bath. They dry themselves and put on their pajamas. They hug Mom and say good night. They go to bed and dream of their next bath adventure.

Once upon a time, there was a cute otter. The otter lived in a pond with many fish. One day, the otter saw a big ball near the pond. The otter loved to play with the ball.
The otter was ready to play with the ball. He pushed the ball into the water and swam after it. He had so much fun. But then, the ball floated away from the pond. The otter was sad.
The otter wanted the ball to return. He waited and waited. Then, a kind girl found the ball and brought it back to the otter. The otter was very happy. He played with the ball again and the girl watched him. They both had a great day.

One day, a big bird laid an egg. The egg was not like other eggs. It was disgusting. It was green and had spots. The bird did not like it.
The bird told her friend, the dog, "I want this egg to stop being disgusting." The dog had an idea. He said, "Let's ask the wise old frog for help." So, they went to the frog with the egg.
The frog looked at the egg and said, "This is not a disgusting egg. It is special." The bird did not understand. Then, something unexpected happened. The egg started to shake and crack. A baby dragon came out of the egg. The bird was happy. The dragon was not disgusting. It was beautiful and special.

One day, a powerful king lived in a big castle. He had a number game that he liked to play. Every day, he would count the numbers and have fun.
One sunny day, a stranger came to the castle. He wanted to play the number game with the king. The king was happy and said, "Let's play!"
They played the number game, but something unexpected happened. The stranger was very good at the game. He won! The king was surprised and happy. The stranger became the king's new friend, and they played the number game together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a red shirt that he loved very much. One day, his friend, Sam, asked him, "Can you lend me your red shirt? I need it for a play at school." Tim thought about it and said, "Okay, I will lend you my shirt, but please be careful with it."
The next day, Tim went to watch the play at school. He was excited to see his red shirt on stage. But, something unexpected happened! A big dog ran on the stage and grabbed the red shirt. The dog played with the shirt and it got hurt. Tim was sad to see his red shirt hurt.
After the play, Sam said, "I am sorry, Tim. I didn't know the dog would hurt your shirt." Tim was sad, but he knew it was not Sam's fault. He said, "It's okay, Sam. We can fix the shirt together." So, Tim and Sam worked together to fix the hurt shirt, and they became even better friends.

Once upon a time, there was a curious little bear. The bear loved to collect things. He went around the woods to find things to put in his big box.
One day, the curious bear went to the river. He saw a shiny rock. The bear wanted to collect it. He put his paw in the water to get the rock. But the water was very deep.
The bear fell in the river. He tried to swim, but the water was too strong. The bear was sad and scared. The river took him far away from his home. The curious bear never got to collect the shiny rock.

Once upon a time, there was a lively cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to dress up in different costumes. One day, she found a pretty costume that made her look like a princess.
Kitty walked outside and met a dog named Buddy. Buddy saw Kitty in her costume and said, "Wow, you look like a princess!" Kitty smiled and replied, "Thank you, Buddy! I love my costume too!"
One day, Kitty and Buddy decided they wanted to marry each other. They had a fun party with all their friends. Everyone wore costumes and danced. Kitty and Buddy were very happy and lived together forever.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the garage. Tim saw many toys scatter all over the floor. He found a deep box, and he had an idea.
Tim called his friend, Sue. "Sue, let's put all the toys in the deep box!" Sue came to the garage and saw the mess. "Yes, let's clean up!" she said.
Tim and Sue picked up the toys and put them in the deep box. They worked together and soon, the garage was clean. They were happy and played with the toys all day.

Once upon a time, there was a popular dog named Sam. Sam liked to lead his friends on fun walks. One day, Sam and his friends went to a big park to play.
At the park, Sam saw a ball. He wanted to play with it. But his friend, a wise old cat named Max, said, "No, Sam! That ball has poison on it. It is bad for you."
Sam listened to Max and did not play with the ball. They told their friends about the poison ball, and they all stayed away from it. They played with their own toys and had a great time at the park.
In the end, Sam was happy he listened to Max. He knew that Max was a good friend who cared about him. And Sam continued to lead his friends on fun walks, always being careful of bad things like poison.

Lily liked to choose her own clothes every morning. She wanted to look attractive for her friends at the kindergarten. She opened her closet and saw many dresses, skirts, shirts and pants. She picked a pink dress with flowers and a yellow ribbon. She put it on and looked at herself in the mirror. She smiled and said, "I look pretty!"
She went downstairs to have breakfast with her mom and dad. They praised her dress and gave her a hug and a kiss. They ate cereal and milk and talked about their plans for the day. Lily told them she was going to play with Anna and Ben at the kindergarten. She said they were going to draw pictures, sing songs and build blocks. She was excited and happy.
She grabbed her backpack and followed her mom to the car. They drove to the kindergarten and said goodbye. Lily ran to the playground and saw her friends. They greeted her and complimented her dress. They decided to play on the swings, the slide and the sandbox. They had a lot of fun and laughed a lot.
Then, Lily saw something shiny and colorful on the ground. She walked closer and saw it was a spider. It had eight legs, two eyes and a big body. It was red, blue and green and sparkled in the sun. Lily thought it was the most attractive spider she had ever seen. She wanted to touch it and make it her friend.
She reached out her hand and said, "Hello, spider. You are so pretty. Can I play with you?" But the spider did not like Lily. It felt scared and angry. It raised its legs and showed its fangs. It hissed and spat at Lily. It tried to bite her hand.
Lily screamed and jumped back. She felt scared and hurt. She ran to her friends and told them what happened. They hugged her and comforted her. They told her the spider was not nice and not a good friend. They told her to stay away from it and play with them instead. They gave her a band-aid and a cookie. They made her feel better.
Lily learned a lesson that day. She learned that not everything that looks attractive is good. She learned that some things can be dangerous and mean. She learned that she should be careful and listen to her friends. She also learned that she was still pretty, even with a band-aid on her hand. She smiled and said, "Thank you, friends. You are the best!"

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a little girl named Lucy. Lucy was a very unusual girl because she was not afraid of the dark. One day, Lucy went for a walk in the forest. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing.
As Lucy walked deeper into the forest, the sun began to set, and it started to get dark. Most kids would be scared, but not Lucy. She knew that the dark was not bad. It was just a part of the day. Suddenly, Lucy saw a small, sad animal. It was a baby bear, and it was scared of the dark.
Lucy went to the baby bear and gave it a big hug. The baby bear felt safe and happy. They walked together through the dark forest, and Lucy showed the baby bear that there was nothing to be afraid of. The baby bear learned to be brave, just like Lucy.
The moral of the story is that we can help others be brave by showing them love and support, even in the dark or unusual times.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia liked to play with her orange ball. One day, while playing, she found a green herb near her house. She picked it up and smelled it. It smelled nice!
She ran to her mom and said, "Mom, look what I found! It smells nice!" Her mom smiled and said, "That's a good herb, Mia. It can make our food taste better. Let's put it in the soup for dinner."
Mia was excited to help her mom cook. After they put the herb in the soup, Mia's mom said, "We need to tell Grandma about this herb. Let's write her a letter and mail it to her."
Mia and her mom wrote a letter together. They told Grandma about the orange ball and the nice-smelling herb. They put the letter in an envelope and put a stamp on it. Then they walked to the mailbox and mailed the letter to Grandma.
A few days later, Grandma called Mia. She said, "Thank you for the letter, Mia! I loved hearing about your orange ball and the herb. I will try to find a nice herb for my soup too!" Mia was happy that she could share her story with her Grandma.

Once upon a time there was a person who was very healthy. They lived in a small town and had a cute yellow scarf.
One day the person went outside to take a walk in the park. Suddenly they saw a dog with the same yellow scarf!
The person nodded at the dog and said, "Do you mind if I have my scarf back?"
But the dog just stared at them and ran away with the scarf. The person chased after the dog but couldn't find it.
The person was so sad. They tried to mind the lesson that if something is valuable, then you should take care of it. In the end, they never got their scarf back.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big cardboard box that he loved to play with. He would sit in it and pretend he was in a spaceship, flying to the moon.
One day, Tim's friend, Sam, came over to play. Sam saw the cardboard box and wanted to play with it too. "Can I play with the box, please?" Sam asked. But Tim was selfish and did not want to share. "No, it's mine!" Tim said.
Tim's mom heard them and came to warn them. "Tim, you should share your toys with your friends," she said. Tim thought about it and decided to share the cardboard box with Sam. They both played together, and they had lots of fun. Tim learned that sharing was more fun than being selfish.

One day, Rory and his dad were fishing in the lake. Rory had a fishing license, so they could go fishing. But they also brought some food in case they got hungry.
Suddenly, it started to rain very hard. The rain soaked their clothes and the food. They tried to find some shelter, but there was none.
Rory was very upset. He was getting wet and he was so hungry! But they couldn't eat the soggy food.
Luckily, they saw a boat nearby. They hurried over and knocked on the boat door. An old man opened the door. He smiled and kindly gave them some warm food and a place to rest until the rain stopped.
Rory and his dad enjoyed the warm food, and soon the rain had stopped. They thanked the old man and were on their way. Rory was happy that he no longer had to be hungry or cold and wet!

Once upon a time, there was a lazy boy named Tim. He didn't like to read. He liked to play with his toys all day. One day, Tim found a big toy called a microscope in his room. He didn't know what it was.
Tim's mom said, "Tim, the microscope helps you see very small things. You can read about it in this book." Tim didn't want to read, but he wanted to play with the microscope. So, he tried to read the book. It was hard, but he learned how to use the microscope.
Tim looked at a tiny bug through the microscope. Suddenly, the bug started to talk! "Hello, Tim!" said the bug. Tim was very surprised. The bug told Tim that he was a magic bug. The magic bug helped Tim learn to love reading. They read and played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was an elderly lady named Sue. Sue liked to bake cakes for her friends. One day, she wanted to test a new cake recipe she found.
Sue looked at the recipe and saw she needed to measure the ingredients. She took out her cups and spoons to measure the sugar, flour, and milk. She mixed everything together and put the cake in the oven to bake.
After a while, the cake was ready. Sue took it out of the oven and let it cool. Her friend, Tom, came over to visit. Sue said, "Tom, I made a new cake. Can you try it and tell me if it's good?" Tom took a bite and smiled. "Sue, this cake is very yummy! You did a great job!" Sue was happy that her test was a success and they enjoyed the cake together.

Once upon a time, there was a big bull. He lived on a farm with other animals. He liked to pick flowers with his long horns. All the animals watched him, and they were envious. They wanted to pick flowers too.
One day, a little bird came to the farm. The bird saw the bull picking flowers. The bird had an idea. She flew to the bull and said, "Let me help you pick flowers!" The bull was happy to have a new friend.
The bird and the bull started picking flowers together. But then, something unexpected happened. The bird picked a magical flower. The bull and the bird changed places! Now, the bull could fly, and the bird had long horns. They were both very happy with their new skills, and they played together all day long.

One day, a poor girl named Anna saw a pretty phone in a shop. She wanted to buy it, but she did not have enough money. She was sad and went to her mom. "Mom, can I have some money to buy the phone?" Anna asked. Her mom said, "Not now, dear. We need the money for food."
Anna was walking home when she saw a small cat. The cat was hungry and scared. Anna felt bad for the cat and gave it some food. The cat was happy and followed Anna home. When they got home, Anna's mom saw the cat and smiled. "You did a kind thing, Anna," her mom said.
That night, the cat found a shiny coin under Anna's bed. The cat gave the coin to Anna. The coin was worth a lot of money! Anna and her mom were so surprised. They could now buy the phone and still have money for food. The moral of the story is that when you are kind to others, good things happen to you.

Jazz is a cat who likes to run fast. He runs fast in the house, in the yard, and in the park. He runs fast after birds, balls, and bugs. He is a fast cat.
One day, Jazz sees a big box in the living room. He is curious. He jumps on the box and sniffs it. He hears a noise inside. He scratches the box and tries to open it.
"Jazz, no!" Mom says. "That is a gift for Dad. It is his birthday. You cannot open it."
Jazz looks at Mom. He meows. He wants to see what is in the box.
"Sorry, Jazz," Mom says. "You have to wait. But I have something for you. A gift, too."
Mom goes to the kitchen and comes back with a small bag. She gives it to Jazz. Jazz sniffs the bag. He smells something yummy. He opens the bag and finds some treats. He is happy. He eats the treats and purrs.
"Thank you, Mom," Jazz says. "This is a good gift. I like it."
"You are welcome, Jazz," Mom says. "I love you. You are a good cat. But please, leave the box alone. It is for Dad."
Jazz nods. He loves Mom and Dad. He decides to leave the box alone. He goes to the couch and curls up. He is full and sleepy. He dreams of running fast. He is a fast cat.

Once upon a time there was a mom and a dad that loved each other very much. One day, mommy said to daddy, "I think its time for us to trust our little girl and give her some sauce!" Daddy didn't think this was a good idea because he thought their little girl was too silly. Mommy said, "We can do it. I know our daughter can handle it."
So mommy and daddy decided to let their daughter try some sauce. She was very excited and smiled really big. She scooped some sauce on her spoon and tasted it. She smiled again even bigger after she tasted it and said, "I love it mommy and daddy! Thank you for trusting me!" Mommy and daddy were so happy that their daughter loved the sauce and that they showed her that they trusted her enough to try it.

One day, a little girl named Sue had an ordinary mug. She loved her mug very much. It was her favorite thing to have. Sue and her mom used the mug every day for their tea.
One day, while Sue and her mom were having tea, a funny thing happened. A little bird flew in through the window and said, "Hi Sue! Can I have some tea too?" Sue was very surprised. She had never heard a bird talk before.
Sue said, "Yes, little bird, you can have some tea." She poured some tea into her ordinary mug and gave it to the bird. The bird took a sip and then, something unexpected happened. The bird turned into a big, beautiful butterfly! Sue and her mom were very happy and they all had tea together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to lock doors. He would lock the door to his room, the door to the car, and even the door to his toy box. He thought it was fun to keep things safe in their place.
One day, Tim's mom asked him, "Tim, can you lock the door to the house?" Tim was very happy. He said, "Yes, mom!" He went to the door, turned the key, and locked it. He felt very successful.
Later, Tim's friend came to play. Tim showed his friend how he could lock doors. They played all day, locking and unlocking the doors to their favorite places. Tim's friend said, "You are very good at locking doors, Tim!" Tim smiled and knew he had done a great job.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big, mysterious automobile. It was very strange. Tim wanted to know more about the mysterious automobile, so he went closer. He put his ear on it to listen.
Suddenly, the automobile spoke! "Hello, Tim," it said. Tim was very surprised. "How do you know my name?" he asked. The automobile said, "I am a magic automobile, and I know many things. I can even take you on a fun ride!"
Tim was excited and jumped into the automobile. They went very fast, and Tim laughed. But then, something unexpected happened. The automobile turned into a big, friendly dog! Tim was even more surprised, but he was happy because he always wanted a dog.
Now, Tim and his new dog friend play together every day. They have lots of fun, and Tim never forgets the mysterious automobile that brought them together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a big brush. He liked to use the brush to clean his room. One day, Tim saw a cat. The cat was dead. Tim was sad.
Tim said to his mom, "Mom, the cat is dead. Can we help it?" Mom said, "No, Tim. We cannot help the dead cat. But we can bury it in the yard." So, they buried the cat in the yard.
Later, the sun came out. It was very hot. The sun was so hot that it started to melt things. Tim's brush melted. Tim's mom's ice cream melted. Tim was sad again. He missed the cat and his brush. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toy dragon. His dragon was big and green. But one day, the dragon got very dry.
Tim's mom said, "We need to buy something to make the dragon not dry." Tim was happy because he wanted his dragon to be wet and shiny again. They went to the store to look for something to help the dragon.
At the store, they found a bottle of wet stuff. Tim's mom said, "This will make your dragon not dry!" They bought the wet stuff and went home. Tim put the wet stuff on his dragon, and it was not dry anymore. Tim and his dragon played happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a farm with many cows. The cows gave milk every day. The farmer was happy because the milk was very good.
One day, the farmer saw that the milk was increasing. There were more and more bottles of milk. The farmer was very surprised. He did not know what to do with all the milk.
He decided to take the milk to a distant place, far away from the farm. He gave the milk to people who needed it. Everyone was happy and the farmer felt good. The cows continued to give milk and the farmer shared it with others.

Once upon a time, there was a compassionate boy named Tim. He lived in a small house with his mom, dad, and dog. Tim liked to help people and animals. He was very kind.
One day, Tim found a little bird with a hurt wing. The bird could not fly. Tim wanted to help the bird. He took the bird to his house. Tim's mom put a bandage on the bird's wing.
Tim's dad said they needed to send the bird to a nice doctor. They put the bird in a box with a soft bed. Tim turned a knob to open the door. Tim and his dad took the bird to the doctor. The doctor helped the bird get better. Tim was happy he could help the bird.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He lived in a small house with his mom and dad. Tim had a gloomy room with a big shelf. On the shelf, there were many toys and books. Tim loved to play with his toys.
One day, Tim found a toy gun that could shoot soft balls. He was very happy and showed it to his mom. "Look, mom! I can shoot!" he said. His mom smiled and said, "Be careful, Tim. Don't shoot at people or break things."
The next day, Tim played with his toy gun. He wanted to shoot a ball at the shelf to see if he could hit a toy. But when he shot the ball, something unexpected happened! The shelf started to shake, and all the toys and books fell down. Tim was scared and called his mom.
His mom came into the room and saw the mess. "What happened, Tim?" she asked. Tim told her about shooting the ball at the shelf. His mom was not angry. She smiled and said, "It's okay, Tim. We can fix the shelf together." So, Tim and his mom cleaned the room and fixed the shelf. From that day on, Tim learned to be more careful when playing with his toys.

Once upon a time, there was a big aeroplane. The aeroplane liked to travel high in the sky. It would fly over houses, trees, and cars. The aeroplane was always careful when it flew.
One day, the aeroplane met a little bird. The bird was scared to travel far from its nest. The aeroplane wanted to help the bird. They became good friends and the aeroplane taught the bird to be careful when flying.
The bird learned to be careful and started to travel with the aeroplane. They went on many fun trips together. They saw new places and met new friends. The bird was very happy and thanked the aeroplane for teaching it to be careful. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy cat named Tom. Tom lived in a small house with a kind girl named Sue. Sue liked to read books and play with Tom. But Tom did not like when Sue read her books. He wanted to play all the time.
One day, Sue was reading a big book in her room. Tom jumped on her bed and knocked the book down. The book got dirty, and Sue was sad. She knew she had to clean the book.
Sue got a soft cloth and water to clean the book. Tom watched as Sue cleaned the book. He felt bad for making the book dirty. Tom decided to help Sue clean the book. Together, they cleaned the book until it was like new. Sue was happy, and Tom was not grumpy anymore. From that day on, Tom let Sue read her books, and they both played happily together.

Once upon a time, in a big field, there was a little, fragile poppy. The poppy was red and pretty. It liked to dance with the wind.
One day, a bird flew by the poppy. The bird could whistle very well. The poppy liked the bird's song. The bird liked to see the poppy dance.
Every day, the bird came to whistle for the poppy. They were very happy together. The poppy danced, and the bird sang. And they were best friends forever.

One day, a clever cat named Max went out to play. He saw a long chain on the ground. Max thought it would be fun to play with the chain. He used his paws to stretch the chain out very far.
Max's friend, a dog named Sam, came over. "What are you doing?" Sam asked. Max said, "I am playing with this chain. It is so much fun to stretch it!" Sam wanted to play too, so they both stretched the chain together.
As they pulled the chain, they heard a loud noise. The chain was connected to a big box! The box opened, and out came a lot of colorful balls. Max and Sam were very happy and surprised. They played with the balls and the chain all day long.

One day, a cool owl named Ollie lived in a big tree. Ollie loved to taste new food. He liked sweet fruit and yummy bugs. He flew around to find the best food in the forest.
One day, Ollie saw a little bird named Benny. Benny was sad. "What's wrong?" Ollie asked. "I can't find any food," Benny cried. Ollie knew a lot about food, so he said, "I can help you find some."
Ollie and Benny looked for food together. They found sweet fruit and yummy bugs for Benny. Benny was very happy. They became good friends. They learned that helping others is a good thing to do.

One day, a messy cat named Tom was playing outside. He liked to jump and run in the mud. His fur was always dirty. Tom's friend, a dog named Max, came to play with him.
"Tom, you are so messy!" said Max. "Let's play a game instead of jumping in the mud." Tom agreed, and they started to play tag. They ran and laughed, having lots of fun.
Suddenly, the ground began to shake. It was an earthquake! Tom and Max were scared. They tried to run, but Tom slipped in the mud. Max saw Tom fall and ran to help him.
"Are you okay, Tom?" asked Max. Tom nodded, and they both got up. The earthquake stopped, and they were safe. They went home, happy to be together, even if Tom was still a messy cat.

Once upon a time there was a tall man who lived a peaceful life. Everyday he woke early and worked hard in his fields until the sun went down. He included his children in all his tasks, teaching them the importance of hard work. 
One day, the tall man heard a loud noise in the distance. He told his children to stay inside as he went to investigate. As he approached the horizon, he saw an army of tall soldiers marching through the country. It was a war. 
The tall man knew he needed to protect his land and family. He was scared of what this war would bring, but he was determined to keep them safe. With his family included he worked hard to prepare for the upcoming war. 
After many weeks of hard work and preparation, the tall man and his family were able to survive the war. The tall man was so brave and determined for his family and so thankful for all the hard work they did together. The tall man and his family remained safe throughout the war and enjoyed many more peaceful days in the future.

Once upon a time, there was a rich man named Bob. Bob had many toys and games. He liked to play with his friends. One day, Bob saw a new game in a store. He was very happy and bought the game to play with his friends.
Bob called his friend Sam to come over and play the new game. Sam came to Bob's house and they started to play. They laughed and had fun. Bob said, "Sam, this game is so much fun! We should play it every day!" Sam agreed and they kept playing.
The next day, it was raining outside, and Bob and Sam could not go out to play. They decided to remain inside and play the new game again. They played all day long and had even more fun than before. The rain stopped, but they still wanted to play the game. They knew that they would always have fun with this game, no matter what.

One day, a pale boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big tree and made a wish. He wished for a friend to play with. A little dog came running to him. Tim was happy.
"Hello, dog. Let's play," Tim said. They played all day. They ran and jumped, and had a lot of fun. But then, another big boy came to the park. He wanted to play with the dog too.
"Go away," said the big boy. "I want to play with the dog." Tim did not want to fight, but the big boy pushed him. Tim fell down and cried. The big boy took the dog and left.
Tim was sad. He wished for a friend, but now the dog was gone. The bad ending made Tim cry more. He went home alone, hoping to find a new friend another day.

Once upon a time, there was a funny cat named Tom. Tom had a mitten that he loved very much. One day, Tom's mitten got a big hole in it. Tom was sad.
Tom wanted to fix his mitten. He asked his friend, a little bird named Sam, for help. Sam said, "I can help you fix your mitten, Tom!" So, Tom and Sam worked together to fix the mitten.
As they were fixing the mitten, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and blew the mitten away. Tom and Sam ran after it, but they couldn't catch it. Just when they thought the mitten was gone forever, a nice dog named Max brought it back to them. Tom, Sam, and Max all laughed and played together. And Tom's mitten was fixed and good as new.

Once there was a strong little bird that lived in the forest. Every morning it would fly around and scatter its beautiful song over the trees. Everywhere it flew, it would hear the other animals say how wonderful it sounded.
The bird loved singing and it felt strong flying around and scattering its song. Every day it grew more confident in its song and in its flying.
One day it began to fly further and further away and it started to get lost. The bird called out its beautiful song, but this time it didn't receive any response. The bird flew around desperately but it could not find its way back to the forest.
The bird learned an important lesson - never get too confident and scatter too far away. Always find strength in the familiarity of home.

One day, a bird and a shark were friends. They liked to play near the water. The bird could soar high in the sky. The shark swam fast in the water. They were very happy.
One day, the bird said, "Shark, be careful! There is a big wave coming!" The shark listened and swam away from the wave. The bird soared up high to be safe too. They were both okay.
Then, something unexpected happened. The wave pushed a big ship near them. The bird and the shark looked at the ship. They saw people on the ship. The people waved at the bird and the shark. Now, the bird and the shark had more friends to play with. They all had fun together.

Once upon a time, there was an ordinary jungle. In the jungle, a group of animals were playing.
Suddenly, they stopped and paused. They heard something strange in the distance. It was a rumble!
The animals looked around and saw a giant, orange machine. It was rolling and bouncing through the jungle.
The animals were scared but curious. So, they decided to see what the machine was doing.
The machine rolled up to an old tree and stopped. Then, a big door opened and out stepped a little girl. She was laughing and smiling.
The animals were so surprised. They couldn't believe their eyes! They had never seen anything like it before.
The girl and the animals watched each other for a few moments, and then the girl started to play with the animals. They laughed and ran around, having lots of fun.
The ordinary jungle suddenly came alive with laughter and joy.

Once upon a time, there was a nice witch. She lived in a small house in the woods. She was very generous. She liked to help everyone.
One day, she met a little boy. The boy was lost and sad. The witch wanted to help him. She urged the boy to come with her. The boy was scared, but he knew the witch was kind.
They walked together until they found the boy's home. The boy was happy and thanked the witch. The witch smiled and waved goodbye. The boy knew the generous witch was a good friend.

Once upon a time, there was a clever little fox. The fox had a big smile on its face every day. It liked to play with its friends in the woods. One day, the fox saw a big tree with a lot of yummy fruit.
The fox wanted to get the fruit, but it was too high. It had an idea! The fox would twist its tail around a branch and swing up to get the fruit. It tried and tried, but it could not reach the fruit. The fox felt sad and its smile went away.
Then, a little bird came and saw the fox. The bird wanted to help. It flew up and picked the fruit for the fox. The fox was so happy and its smile came back. The fox and the bird became good friends. They learned that when you help others, you feel happy too.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside. One day, he saw an ancient automobile in his yard. It was big and old. Tim was very excited to see it.
Tim decided to play near the automobile. He found a ball and began to kick it. He kicked the ball very hard, and it went flying towards the ancient automobile. Tim was scared that he might break it.
But, something unexpected happened! When the ball hit the automobile, it made a loud noise. Suddenly, the ancient automobile started to move! It was not broken at all. In fact, it was a magic automobile! Tim was so happy and surprised. He knew that he had found a very special friend in the ancient automobile.

One day, a little boy named Tim saw a big white bird in the sky. He said to his friend, "Look at the bird! It's so big and white!" His friend, Sara, said, "Yes, it's very pretty!"
Tim said, "I want to be a pilot when I grow up. I can imagine flying up high like the bird!" Sara said, "That's a great idea! I want to be a pilot too!"
They played together, pretending to be pilots. They flew high and low, and had lots of fun. One day, a wise old man saw them playing. He said, "You two are very good pilots! But remember, it's important to be kind and help others."
Tim and Sara thought about what the old man said. They decided to be kind pilots, helping people and animals. They knew that being a pilot was not just about flying, but also about being good and helpful.
And so, Tim and Sara grew up to be kind and helpful pilots. They made many friends and always helped others in need. The moral of the story is that it's important to be kind and helpful, no matter what you do.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to play with water. He would wave his hands in the water and watch the water move. One day, Tim found a smooth stone near the tap outside his house. He thought it was a magic stone.
Tim took the smooth stone and put it under the tap. He turned the tap on and water came out. He waved his hands in the water and said, "Magic stone, make me fly!" But nothing happened. Tim tried again and again, but he could not fly.
Tim was sad. He threw the smooth stone away and walked back to his house. He did not see the big hole in the ground. Tim fell into the hole and could not get out. He cried for help, but nobody heard him. The magic stone did not help Tim, and he stayed in the hole.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to walk around his house and play with his toys. One day, while walking, he saw a desk in his room. It was a small desk, just the right size for him to sit and draw pictures.
Tim called out to his mom, "Look, Mom! I found a desk! Can I draw on it?" His mom smiled and said, "Yes, Tim. You can draw on your little desk."
As Tim began to draw, something unexpected happened. The desk started to talk! "Hello, Tim," said the desk. Tim was shocked but also excited. He had never met a talking desk before. The desk told Tim that it was a magic desk and could make his drawings come to life.
Tim decided to draw a big, friendly dog. As soon as he finished drawing, the dog jumped off the paper and started to play with Tim. They had so much fun together. From that day on, Tim and his magic desk went on many adventures, creating new friends and having lots of fun.

One day, a boy and a girl went to the store. They wanted to buy a magazine. The store was big and had many things.
In the store, they saw a very unusual magazine. The boy said, "Look at this one! It is so different." The girl said, "Yes, let's get it!" They were very happy and went to pay for it.
As they paid, the store man said, "Be careful with this magazine. It is magic!" The boy and girl did not believe him. But when they opened the magazine at home, they were in for a big surprise. The pictures in the magazine came to life and started to play with them. The boy and girl had so much fun and knew that the store man was right.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom was a serious boy who liked to play outside. One day, he found a big axe in the woods. He knew he should not touch it, but he dared to pick it up.
Tom carried the axe home to show his mom. His mom saw the axe and was scared. She said, "Tom, you must not dare to touch things like this. It is not safe." Tom felt bad and said sorry to his mom.
Tom's mom took the axe and put it away in a safe place. She told Tom to be more careful next time. Tom promised he would be careful and never dare to touch things like that again. They hugged and went inside to eat some yummy cookies.

Once upon a time, there was a shy little giraffe named Gigi. Gigi had a very long neck, but she was too shy to show it off. She liked to hide behind the big trees in the forest.
One day, Gigi saw some birds who needed help. The birds were trying to get some food from a tall tree, but they could not reach it. Gigi wanted to help the birds, but she was too shy to come out from behind the trees.
Then, Gigi thought about how her long neck could supply the help the birds needed. She took a deep breath and walked out from behind the trees. Gigi used her long neck to reach the food in the tall tree and gave it to the birds. The birds were so happy, and Gigi felt happy too. She learned that it was okay to be shy, but sometimes, being brave can help others.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Bill. He was three years old. His mommy and daddy loved him very much. One day, Bill asked his mommy to show him an image. She said, "Sure thing, Bill!"
She took out her phone and showed Bill an image. It was a picture of a tall tree. The leaves were high in the sky and were shining in the sun. It was very beautiful.
Bill said, "Wow! It looks so high!" His mommy nodded and said, "Yes, it certainly is. Would you like to show it to Daddy?"
Bill smiled. "Yes, please!" So, his mommy showed it to his daddy and they both smiled. They said the picture was very lovely.
Bill was so happy. He smiled and said, "I love you mommy and daddy!" They both said, "We love you too, Bill!"

Once upon a time, there was a big alligator. He lived in a pond. He was very dependable. All the animals liked him.
One day, a small bird came to the alligator. The bird was scared. It was trembling. The alligator asked, "Why are you scared?"
The bird said, "There is a big storm coming. I need help." The alligator said, "Don't worry. I will help you."
The alligator and the bird went to a safe place. They stayed there until the storm was over. The bird was happy. The alligator was a good friend.

Anna and Ben were adventurous twins who loved to play outside. One sunny day, they decided to go to the pool with their mom. They put on their swimsuits, grabbed their towels and ran to the car.
"Are you ready for some fun?" Mom asked as they drove to the pool.
"Yes, yes, yes!" Anna and Ben shouted.
When they got to the pool, they saw many other kids splashing and swimming. Anna and Ben wanted to join them, but they had to wait for Mom to put on sunscreen and find a spot to sit.
"Can we go now, Mom? Please, please, please?" Anna and Ben begged.
"Okay, okay, okay. But be careful and stay close to me. And don't shout too loud, you might disturb the other people," Mom said.
Anna and Ben nodded and ran to the pool. They jumped in and felt the cool water on their skin. They laughed and played with each other, making bubbles and pretending to be fish. They also made some new friends and shared their toys.
They had so much fun that they didn't notice how fast the time passed. Soon, Mom called them to get out of the pool.
"Time to go, Anna and Ben. We have to go home and have lunch," Mom said.
"Aww, do we have to, Mom? We're having so much fun," Anna and Ben whined.
"Yes, you have to. You can come back another day. Now, hurry up and dry yourselves," Mom said.
Anna and Ben obeyed, but they were sad to leave the pool. They said goodbye to their new friends and thanked Mom for taking them. They hoped they could come back soon and have another adventurous day at the pool.

Once upon a time there was a patient bear. Every day he would go out and trade with all the animals in the woods. One day he was going to meet a rabbit for a trade, but as he walked he slipped on a stone. He was careful for the rest of the way, but deep down he knew that something bad was coming soon. 
When he arrived at the rabbit's house, he was shocked to find out that all his trades had been cancelled. Disappointed, the bear went home. But the next day, he decided to try again. 
This time, he made sure he was extra careful, even though it took him a long time to get to the rabbit's house. When he arrived, he was so pleased to find out that all his trades had been saved! The patient bear was so happy that he decided to slip on a stone to celebrate.

Lily and Ben were twins who liked to play in the woods. One day, they saw a black cabin near a big lake. They were curious and wanted to see inside.
"Let's go, Lily. Maybe we can find something fun," Ben said.
"No, Ben. Mama said we should not go to places we do not know. It is not safe," Lily said.
But Ben did not listen. He ran to the cabin and opened the door. Lily followed him, feeling scared.
Inside the cabin, it was dark and dirty. There were spiders and rats and old things. Ben saw a shiny box on a table. He wanted to open it.
"Look, Lily. Maybe there is a treasure inside," Ben said.
He reached for the box, but as soon as he touched it, he felt a sharp pain in his hand. He screamed and dropped the box. It was a trap with teeth that bit him.
Lily ran to him and saw blood on his hand. She felt sorry for him and hugged him.
"Ben, are you okay? We need to go home and tell Mama. She can help you," Lily said.
Ben nodded, feeling sorry too. He realized he was wrong to go to the cabin. He wished he had listened to Lily and Mama.
They left the cabin and ran back to their home. Mama saw them and was worried. She cleaned Ben's hand and put a bandage on it. She hugged them both and said,
"I'm glad you are safe, my loves. But you should never go to places you do not know. It is dangerous and you can get hurt. You should always listen to me and your sister. They care for you and want you to be happy."
Lily and Ben said they were sorry and learned their lesson. They promised to never go to the cabin again. They felt happy to have each other and Mama. They knew they were loved.

Once there was a girl named Jane. Everywhere Jane went, she always insisted that she was happy and she had lots of friends. But the truth was, Jane was very lonely.
One day, Jane decided that she needed more friends. So, she went out to a store and found some labels with pictures of happy people on them. She insisted that if she stuck the labels to her clothes, it would make her happy too.
Jane put the labels all over her clothes and went out to find some friends. While she was out, people noticed the labels. They smiled and waved at her but Jane just felt more lonely than ever.
At the end of the day, Jane realized that the labels weren't enough to make her happy. She took off all of the labels and learned that the only thing that could make her truly happy was to make real friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to record herself singing with a big spoon. The spoon was her pretend microphone. One day, she went to the park with her mom and dad. It was a mild day, and the sun was shining.
At the park, Mia saw a boy named Tim. Tim was playing with a toy car. Mia wanted to sing for Tim, so she took her spoon and started to sing. But Tim didn't want to listen. He said, "I don't want to hear you sing. I want to play with my car."
Mia was sad. She wanted to make Tim happy with her singing. Then, she had an idea. She went to her mom and dad and asked them to help her. They all made a plan to make Tim smile. Mia's mom and dad went to Tim and asked if he wanted to race his car.
Tim was excited and said, "Yes, I want to race my car!" So, Mia's mom and dad started the race. As Tim's car raced, Mia started to sing. This time, Tim listened to Mia's singing and was happy. He said, "Mia, I like your singing now. It made the race more fun!"
Mia was happy that Tim liked her singing. They became friends and played together at the park. From that day on, Mia and Tim always had fun together, with Mia's singing and Tim's toy car.

Once upon a time, there was a big, healthy dog named Spot. Spot loved to play and run in the park. He had a lot of friends, but he also had a big red ball that belonged to him.
One day, Spot saw a little dog playing with his big red ball. Spot did not like this. The little dog did not know that the ball belonged to Spot. Spot felt sad and angry.
Spot went to the little dog and said, "That ball belongs to me!" The little dog was sorry and gave the ball back to Spot. They both decided to play together and became good friends. Now, they share the ball and have lots of fun in the park.

Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they arrive at the park and see a big truck. The truck has many ice creams. Tom and Mia love ice creams. They run to the truck and ask for ice creams.
"Hello, can we have ice creams, please?" Tom says.
"Sure, what flavor do you want?" the truck man says.
"I want chocolate, it is tasty," Mia says.
"I want strawberry, it is tasty too," Tom says.
The truck man gives them ice creams. Tom and Mia say thank you and go to a bench. They lick their ice creams and smile.
"This is the best day ever," Tom says.
"Yes, it is," Mia says.
Then, they have an idea. They want to share their ice creams with their friends. They look around and see Sam and Lily. Sam and Lily are playing with a ball. Tom and Mia run to them and offer them their ice creams.
"Hi, Sam and Lily, do you want some ice creams?" Mia says.
"Wow, yes, thank you!" Sam says.
"Thank you, you are very nice," Lily says.
They take the ice creams and lick them. They say they are tasty. Tom and Mia are happy. They have made their friends happy too. They all play with the ball and the ice creams. They have a lot of fun. They are good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to mix colors to make new ones. One day, she found a box with many colorful bracelets. Lily wanted to make a new bracelet with all her favorite colors.
Lily took all the bracelets and started to mix the colors. She mixed and mixed until she made a very pretty bracelet. But she saw that she used all the other bracelets to make just one. Lily felt guilty because those bracelets were not hers.
Lily knew she had to fix it. She went to her mom and told her what she did. Her mom smiled and helped her put the bracelets back together. Lily learned that it is important to ask before using things that are not hers.

Once upon a time, there was a white bunny. The bunny loved to hop and play in the big green field. In the field, there was a lot of tall corn. The bunny liked to hide in the corn and play with his friends.
One day, the bunny heard about a big race. All the animals in the field wanted to see who was the fastest. The bunny thought he could win. He practiced running every day, and his friends would clap and applaud for him.
The day of the race came, and all the animals lined up. The bunny was very excited. When the race started, the bunny ran as fast as he could. He remembered the corn and had an idea. He hopped through the corn and came out ahead of all the other animals. The bunny won the race, and all his friends cheered and applauded for him.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She had a big, impressive purse. Lily loved her purse very much. One day, she went to the park to play with her friends.
At the park, Lily met a boy named Tom. Tom saw Lily's purse and said, "Wow, your purse is so big and impressive!" Lily smiled and said, "Thank you! I hope you like it too." They played together all day long.
When it was time to go home, Lily could not find her purse. She was very sad. Tom saw her and said, "Don't worry, Lily. I will help you look for it." They looked and looked, and finally, they found the purse under a big tree. Lily was very happy, and she thanked Tom for his help. From that day on, Lily and Tom became the best of friends.

Mama and Papa were sitting outside in the garden one evening. The sun was still bright in the sky, and the air was full of noise from the birds and the bees. 
Mama said: "I think it's time for a snack, don't you Papa?" 
Papa said "Yes, it sounds like a good idea. What do you want to taste?" 
Mama said: "I think I'll have an apple, do you want one too?" 
Papa said: "Yes, an apple would be nice. Let's go get one!" 
So the two of them went over to the apple tree and each took one apple. Mama took a big bite and smiled. 
Papa said: "It tastes so delicious! I'm glad we got these apples." 
Mama said: "Me too. It's nice to take a little snack in the evening like this." 
They finished their apples and then went back inside, but the noise of the birds and the bees stayed outside with them until the night was almost over.

Tom and Sam were brothers. They liked to play in the park. One day, they saw a big tree with a hole. They wanted to see what was inside.
