"Maybe there is a bird or a mouse," Tom said.
"Or maybe there is a treasure or a toy," Sam said.
They ran to the tree and took turns to look inside. They could not see anything. It was very dark.
"I want to reach inside and feel what is there," Tom said.
"No, don't do that. It might be dangerous," Sam said.
But Tom did not listen. He put his hand in the hole and reached as far as he could. He felt something soft and furry. He smiled and pulled it out.
But it was not a bird or a mouse or a treasure or a toy. It was a black cat. And it was very angry and fierce. It scratched Tom's face and bit his hand. It hissed and spat and ran away.
Tom screamed and cried. Sam was scared and ran to get help. Tom's face and hand were bleeding. He had to go to the hospital. He was very sorry he did not listen to Sam. He learned a hard lesson. He never reached inside a hole again.

Once upon a time, there was a smart dog named Max. He loved to play and run in the park with his friends. One day, a new dog arrived at the park. Her name was Bella.
Max went to Bella and said, "Hi, I'm Max. Do you want to play with me?" Bella said, "Yes, let's play!" They played all day and had lots of fun. Max thought Bella was very smart too.
The next day, Max and Bella played a game. They had to find the truth about a big box. Max found the box first, but he couldn't open it. Bella arrived and helped Max open the box. Inside, they found a lot of yummy treats. They shared the treats with their friends and everyone was happy. The truth was that the box had treats for all the dogs to enjoy.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to his classroom. He saw something unusual. There was a big zip on the wall! Tim was very curious about it.
Tim asked his friend, "Do you see the zip on the wall?" His friend said, "Yes, I see it too! What is it?" They both wanted to know what was behind the zip. They tried to open it, but it was stuck.
Then, their teacher came in and saw them trying to open the zip. She said, "Stop! That is not for you to open." Tim and his friend were sad, but they listened to their teacher. They went back to their seats and wondered what was behind the unusual zip in their classroom.

Once upon a time, there was a big red hat. The hat was on a chair. A man wanted to leave his house. He needed his hat. He looked and looked for his hat. He could not find it. He was sad.
The man went outside. It was very cold and bitter. He saw a dog. The dog was wearing the big red hat! The dog was warm and happy. The man was surprised. He did not think the dog would take his hat.
The man asked the dog for his hat back. The dog did not want to give it back. The man was sad. Then, the dog had an idea. The dog found another hat. The dog gave the new hat to the man. Now, both the man and the dog were warm and happy. The man and the dog became good friends. They never felt cold again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Every day, they played games and had fun. One day, Lily's mom said, "Next week, we have a surprise for you!" Lily was very excited and couldn't wait for the surprise.
The week went by, and Lily tried to guess the surprise. "Is it a new toy?" she asked. "Is it a big cake?" But her mom and dad just smiled and said, "You'll see!" Finally, the day came for the surprise. Lily's mom and dad took her to a big park. There were many kids playing and having fun.
Suddenly, Lily heard a loud noise. She looked up and saw a big, colorful balloon in the sky! "Wow! What is that?" she asked. Her dad said, "It's a hot air balloon! The surprise is that we are going for a ride!" Lily was so happy and couldn't believe it. They climbed into the balloon and floated high in the sky. It was the best surprise ever!

Lily and Max are friends. They like to play with their toys. Lily has a furry bear. Max has a shiny car. They share their toys sometimes.
One day, they are playing in the park. Lily sees a big slide. She wants to go on it. She says to Max, "Can I go on the slide? Can you hold my bear?"
Max says, "Yes, you can go on the slide. I will hold your bear. But can I trade my car for your bear? I like your bear. It is soft and furry."
Lily thinks for a moment. She likes her bear, but she also likes Max's car. It is fast and shiny. She says, "OK, we can trade. But only for a little while. Then we trade back."
Max says, "OK, deal." He gives his car to Lily and takes her bear. Lily runs to the slide. She puts the car in her pocket. She climbs up the ladder. She is ready to slide down.
But then, she hears a loud noise. She looks back. She sees Max. He is crying. He has dropped the bear. The bear is on the ground. A big dog is running towards it. The dog looks hungry. It wants to eat the bear.
Lily is scared. She loves her bear. She does not want the dog to eat it. She forgets about the slide. She jumps down. She runs to Max. She says, "Max, are you OK? Where is my bear?"
Max says, "I'm sorry, Lily. I dropped your bear. The dog is coming. It will take your bear. I'm sorry, I'm sorry."
Lily says, "It's OK, Max. Don't cry. We can get the bear back. We can trade the car for the bear. The dog will like the car. It is shiny and noisy. It will leave the bear alone."
Lily takes the car out of her pocket. She throws it to the dog. The dog sees the car. It forgets about the bear. It chases the car. It barks and bites the car.
Lily and Max run to the bear. They pick it up. They hug it. They are happy. They have their bear back. They say, "Thank you, car. You saved our bear. You are a good car."
They trade back their toys. Lily gives the car to Max. Max gives the bear to Lily. They hug each other. They are friends. They say, "We are sorry, too. We should not trade our toys. We love our toys. We love each other."
They go back to the slide. They take turns. They have fun. They are happy. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy little crocodile. He liked to look at what others were doing. One day, he saw a big bird playing with a shiny toy. The crocodile wanted to play too. But the bird did not like the crocodile. The bird said, "You can't play with my toy!"
The crocodile was sad. He wanted to be friends with the bird. So, he thought of a plan. He saw the bird put the toy in a tree. When the bird was not looking, the crocodile took the toy. He wanted to give it back to the bird later and say sorry.
The next day, the crocodile went to the bird with the toy. He said, "I am sorry I took your toy. I just wanted to be friends." The bird looked at the crocodile and the toy. The bird thought for a moment. Then, the bird said, "I forgive you. Let's be friends and play together." And from that day on, the nosy crocodile and the big bird were the best of friends.

Tim liked to play with his toy cars in the yard. He had many cars of different colors and sizes. He made them zoom and crash and honk. He was happy with his cars.
One day, he saw a big black bird on the fence. It was a raven. It had shiny feathers and a sharp beak. It looked at Tim and his cars. Tim was curious. He wanted to see the raven closer.
He left his cars on the grass and walked to the fence. He said hello to the raven. The raven said caw. Tim smiled. He reached out his hand to touch the raven. The raven bit his finger. Tim cried. It hurt a lot.
The raven flew away with one of Tim's cars in its claws. It was Tim's favorite car. It was red and shiny and fast. Tim was sad and angry. He wanted his car back.
He ran to his mom and showed her his finger. His mom put a bandage on it and gave him a hug. She said she was sorry. She said the raven was mean and greedy. She said Tim should not go near the raven again. She said he could receive a new car for his birthday. But Tim did not want a new car. He wanted his red car. He missed his red car. He hated the raven. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a wild fairy named Lily. She lived in a big green forest with many trees and flowers. Lily loved to create things with her magic wand. She made pretty butterflies, sweet fruits, and soft clouds.
One day, Lily met a little boy named Tim. Tim was lost in the forest and was very sad. Lily wanted to help him, so she used her magic wand to create a path for Tim. The path had many colorful flowers and happy birds.
But Lily's magic was too wild. The path she created was not safe for Tim. He followed the path and it led him to a deep, dark hole. Tim fell into the hole and could not get out. Lily tried to help him, but her wild magic could not fix the problem. In the end, Tim stayed in the hole and Lily flew away, feeling very sad.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had a bag of ordinary pebbles. She loved her pebbles very much. One day, she found a big, shiny pebble. It was not like the others. She put it in her bag with the other pebbles.
Lucy went to the store with her mom. She saw a pretty toy she wanted. Her mom said, "You need to pay for it." Lucy did not have any money. She looked at her bag of pebbles and had an idea.
She took out the big, shiny pebble from her bag. She showed it to the store man. The store man liked the shiny pebble very much. He let Lucy pay for the toy with the shiny pebble. Lucy was very happy. She learned that sometimes, even ordinary things can be special.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside with his dog, Spot. One day, while they were playing, Spot started to dig a hole under a big tree. Tim thought it was funny and joined in.
As they dug deeper, Tim found something hidden in the dirt. It was a box! Tim opened the box and saw lots of money inside. He was so happy and wanted to show his mom. He picked up the box and ran inside the house. "Mom, look what Spot and I found!" Tim said as he pointed at the box.
Mom looked at the box and smiled. "Oh, Tim! That's not real money. It's just play money I hid for you and Spot to find. I wanted to give you a fun surprise while you played outside." Tim laughed and hugged his mom. Even though it was not real money, he was still happy because he had a great adventure with Spot.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a drum that he loved to play. He played his drum all day and all night. Sometimes, he played it too loud and it made people feel helpless because they could not hear anything else.
One day, Tim's mom told him to play his drum outside. Tim went outside and saw a little bird with a broken wing. The bird was helpless and could not fly. Tim wanted to help the bird, so he put his drum down and gently picked up the bird.
Tim took the bird to his mom and they helped the bird get better. The bird was happy and flew away. Tim learned that it is important to be kind and help others who are helpless. He also learned to play his drum more quietly, so he could hear when others needed help. And from that day on, Tim always kept his ears open and his heart close to those who needed him.

Once upon a time, there was a little black cat named Boo. Boo loved to play with a small cube. The cube was his favorite toy. He would roll it, toss it, and carry it around in his mouth.
One day, Boo's friend, a tiny bird named Tweet, came to visit. "Boo, what are you playing with?" Tweet asked. "It's my cube!" Boo replied. "I love my cube. It's so much fun!"
Boo and Tweet played with the cube all day. When the sun started to set, they knew it was time to store the cube away. Boo picked up the cube with his mouth and put it in a special box. Tweet helped by pushing the lid closed with his beak.
"Goodbye, cube!" Boo said. "See you tomorrow." Tweet chirped, "Bye, cube!" They both smiled, knowing they would have more fun with the cube the next day. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a lovely mountain. On top of the mountain, there was a big tree with yummy fruits. A little boy named Tim lived near the mountain. He loved to eat the fruits from the tree.
One day, a naughty monkey came to the mountain. He saw the fruits and wanted to eat them too. The monkey started to steal the fruits from the tree. Tim saw the monkey and said, "Hey, monkey! Don't steal the fruits. We can share them."
The monkey listened to Tim and stopped stealing. They both sat under the tree and ate the fruits together. From that day on, Tim and the monkey became good friends. They shared the fruits and played on the lovely mountain every day.

Once upon a time, there was a young mouse named Mimi. Mimi loved cheese more than anything in the world. One day, she found a big piece of cheese in the kitchen. She was so happy and wanted to share it with her friends.
Mimi went to her friends, the cat, the dog, and the bird. She said, "Let's unite and eat this cheese together!" The cat, the dog, and the bird were surprised but happy to share the cheese with Mimi. They all sat down and started eating the yummy cheese.
As they ate, they talked and laughed. They had so much fun being united and sharing the cheese. Mimi and her friends were happy that they could enjoy the cheese together. From that day on, they promised to always unite and share the good things they found. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a bird and a frog were friends. The bird liked to whistle. He whistled all day. The frog liked to sit in the shade. He felt cool and happy there.
The bird said, "Frog, come whistle with me. It is fun!" The frog said, "No, I like the shade. It is hot outside. I feel uncomfortable."
The bird did not listen. He went to whistle near the frog. The frog felt unhappy. He did not like the noise. He left the shade to find a new spot.
The frog looked and looked for a new shade. He felt hot and tired. He could not find a good spot. The frog felt very sad. He missed his old shade and his friend, the bird.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a grumpy cat named Tom. Tom did not like to play with the other cats. One day, Tom saw a brush on the ground. He picked it up and decided to skip around the town with it.
As Tom skipped, he met a little girl named Sue. Sue asked, "Why are you skipping with a brush, Tom?" Tom replied, "I found it, and it makes me happy." Sue smiled and said, "Can I try?" Tom gave the brush to Sue, and they both skipped together.
As they skipped, the other cats saw them and joined in. Everyone was happy and having fun. Tom learned that sharing and playing with others can make him happy too. From that day on, Tom was not grumpy anymore. The moral of the story is that sharing and playing with others can bring happiness to everyone.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there lived an obedient girl named Lily. Lily loved to write and draw. One sunny day, Lily went for a walk in the forest to find some berries.
As she walked, she saw a big bush with bright red berries. "Wow! Look at these berries!" she said to herself. She picked some berries and put them in her basket. Then, she sat down under a tree to write and draw.
As she was drawing, a little bird came by and said, "Hi, Lily! What are you doing?" Lily replied, "I am writing and drawing with my new red berries." The little bird watched as Lily wrote and drew with her berry ink. They had a fun day together in the forest.

Once upon a time there was a little boy called Ben. He had two pets, a cat and a dog. One day, when Ben and his pets were playing in the garden, Ben's dog ran into a wire fence. Ben saw that the wire was thick and it had scratched the dog's fur. Ben was very sad.
Ben's cat Meep noticed the scratch and said to Ben, "What can we do to heal the wound?". 
Ben thought and thought, then he knew the perfect solution. He went inside and got a band-aid and some cream. He carefully put the cream on the scratch and then covered it with the band-aid.
Ben's pets were so happy - the band-aid had healed the wound!
Ben thought to himself that being kind to one another makes everyone happy. Ben hopes that this special lesson will help him and his pets to be kind to every creature, so that it can heal any hurt.

Tom and Anna are friends. They like to play in the hall. The hall has many toys and books. They can run and jump and laugh in the hall.
One day, Tom and Anna are playing in the hall. They are pretending to be pirates. They have hats and swords and a treasure map. They are looking for the treasure in the hall.
"Arrr, matey, where is the treasure?" Tom asks Anna.
"I think it is under the table, captain," Anna says.
They crawl under the table. They find a box. They open the box. They see many shiny things. They are happy. They have found the treasure.
"Wow, look at all the gold and jewels," Tom says.
"We are successful pirates, captain," Anna says.
They take some of the treasure and put it in their pockets. They are thirsty. They want to drink some water.
"Let's go to the kitchen and drink some water," Tom says.
"OK, captain, but we have to be careful. There might be other pirates in the hall," Anna says.
They hold their swords and sneak to the kitchen. They see no one. They are safe. They drink some water. They are refreshed.
They go back to the hall. They play some more. They are happy. They are pirates. They are friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little penguin. He was very helpless. He lived in a cold place with his mom and dad. They were a happy family.
One day, the little penguin went to play with his friends. They found a big box. The penguin wanted to fit in the box. His friends helped him, but he could not fit. The penguin was sad.
Then, something unexpected happened. The box started to move! The little penguin and his friends were scared. The box opened, and out came a big, friendly bear. The bear had been stuck in the box. The little penguin was not helpless anymore. He had a new friend who could help him fit in the box. They all played together and had fun.

One day, a bird and a cat were playing in the park. The bird found a feather on the ground. "Look, cat, I found a feather," said the bird. The cat looked at the feather and wanted to play with it too.
The bird and the cat started to play with the feather. The cat wanted to kick the feather hard. "I want to kick it hard," said the cat. The bird did not want the cat to kick the feather hard. "No, please don't kick it hard," said the bird.
The cat kicked the feather hard anyway. The feather flew far away. The bird was sad and mad at the cat. The cat said sorry to the bird. They both looked for the feather together. They found it and played nice with the feather. The bird and the cat were happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a family of fish. They lived in a deep part of the sea. The family loved to collect pretty shells. One day, the little fish said, "I want to find the biggest shell!"
The family swam together, looking for the biggest shell. But then, they saw a big shark. The shark said, "I want all your shells!" The family was scared. They did not want to give their shells to the shark.
The little fish had an idea. He said, "Let's all swim fast and trick the shark!" So, the family swam in circles around the shark. The shark got dizzy and swam away.
The family was happy and safe. They found the biggest shell and took it home. They put it with their other shells and said, "We are the best shell collectors!"

One day, a bright sun was up in the sky. Tim and Sue went to the park to play. They were very happy. They played with a ball and ran around. After a while, they got hungry. Sue said, "Let's have a snack."
Tim opened his bag to remove the snack. But, oh no! The snack was not there. Tim was sad. Sue was sad too. They wanted the snack. They looked around the park to find the snack.
A squirrel came and said, "I have your snack." Tim and Sue asked, "Can we have it back, please?" The squirrel said, "You can have it if you play with me." They played with the squirrel and had lots of fun. The squirrel gave the snack back to Tim and Sue. They said, "Thank you!" and ate their snack together.

Anna liked to help her mom in the garden. She liked to water the flowers, dig the soil, and plant the seeds. She also liked to watch the birds, bees, and butterflies that came to visit the garden.
One day, Anna saw a big sunflower that was taller than her. She wanted to touch it, but it was too high. She asked her mom, "How can I reach the sunflower?"
Her mom smiled and said, "The sunflower grows every day. It increases in size and height. If you wait and watch, you will see it get bigger and bigger."
Anna was curious and excited. She decided to watch the sunflower every day. She noticed that it increased a little bit every day. She also saw that it followed the sun with its face. She thought it was very pretty and happy.
After a week, the sunflower was so big that it reached the roof of the house. Anna was amazed and proud. She climbed on a chair and touched the sunflower. It felt soft and warm. She said, "Hello, sunflower. You are so big and beautiful. Thank you for making our garden bright and calm." The sunflower seemed to nod and smile at her. Anna felt happy and calm too. She hugged her mom and said, "I love our garden and our sunflower." Her mom hugged her back and said, "I love you, Anna. You are my sunflower."

It was a gloomy day outside, but Sarah wanted to play. She ran to the garden with her bucket, ready to have some fun.
Sarah filled her bucket with water from the pond, then she grabbed a pebble. She looked up at the sky and threw the pebble in the water. It made a huge splash!
"That was fun!" Sarah said with a smile. She filled her bucket up again, this time with more pebbles. She skipped around the pond and threw the pebbles in the water, one after the other. Each time, they made a big splash!
Sarah was getting hungry, so she ran back inside. She spread some delicious raspberry jam on her toast and enjoyed a yummy snack.
"What a nice day," Sarah said with a full tummy. Now she was ready to go back outside and make even more splashes!

Tommy likes to play with cards. He has many cards with pictures of animals and colors. He can make pairs of cards that are the same. He can also make a big tower with the cards. He thinks cards are fun and important.
But his sister Lily does not like cards. She likes to play with dolls and dress them up. She thinks cards are boring and silly. She does not allow Tommy to play with cards in her room. She says cards are only for babies.
One day, Tommy and Lily have to share a room. Their mom and dad are painting their rooms. Tommy wants to play with cards on the floor. Lily wants to play with dolls on the bed. They start to argue and fight. They make a lot of noise.
Their mom and dad hear them and come to the room. They are not happy. They tell Tommy and Lily to stop fighting and share the room. They say cards and dolls are both good toys. They say Tommy and Lily are both important.
They ask Tommy and Lily to play together. They say they can use cards and dolls to make a story. Tommy and Lily think about it. They decide to try. They use cards to make a background for the dolls. They use dolls to act out the story. They have fun and laugh. They are happy. They allow each other to play with cards and dolls. They say sorry and hug. Their mom and dad are proud. They say Tommy and Lily are good children.

One day, a happy fish lived in a big ocean. The fish loved to swim and play with his friends. They were all very enthusiastic and had lots of fun together.
One day, the fish saw something new in the ocean. It was a big, shiny rock. The fish was curious and swam closer to the rock. When he touched the rock, something magical happened. The fish started to change.
The fish changed into a beautiful, colorful fish. His friends were amazed and so happy for him. They all swam and played together, even more enthusiastic than before. They knew the ocean was a special place where magical things could happen.

Once upon a time, there were twin dogs named Tom and Tim. They were very independent and liked to do things on their own. They lived in a small house with a big yard where they could run and play all day long.
One day, Tom and Tim were playing in the yard when they saw a big ball. They both wanted to play with it, but they could not agree on who would get it first. This caused a conflict between the twins. They barked and growled at each other, trying to prevent the other from getting the ball.
Then, their owner came outside and saw the twin dogs fighting. She picked up the ball and said, "Tom and Tim, you must learn to share and play together." She threw the ball, and the twins ran after it, forgetting their conflict. From that day on, they learned to share and prevent fights, and they played happily together.

A little girl was walking in the park. She noticed the soil was very soft.
Suddenly, a clever squirrel jumped up to her shoe. He wanted to eat a nut from her pocket.
The girl smiled and said, "Hello, little squirrel. Would you like a nut?"
The squirrel nodded and stood still, waiting for the nut.
But then a bird flew close by and the squirrel ignored the girl.
He jumped away and ran after the bird. He wanted to catch the bird's feathers.
The girl laughed and said, "That squirrel is too clever for his own good!" 
But the squirrel paid no attention and ignored her completely.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a doll that she loved very much. Every day, she would play with her doll and take it with her wherever she went.
One day, Lily saw a girl named Emma who was sad. Emma did not have any toys to play with. Lily wanted to make Emma happy, so she thought of a plan. She decided to give her doll to Emma. Lily said, "Please, take my doll and play with it. I want you to be happy too."
Emma was very surprised by Lily's generous gift. She took the doll and started to play with it. Soon, Emma was smiling and laughing. Lily felt happy because she had made a new friend. From that day on, Lily and Emma played together every day and shared their toys with each other. They both learned the joy of being generous and kind.

One day, a small boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw a big tree with a small medal hanging on it. Tim asked his mom, "What is that?" His mom said, "That is a medal. People get medals when they do something very good."
Tim wanted the medal, but it was too high for him to reach. He saw a big ball near the tree. Tim had an idea. He picked up the ball and threw it at the medal. The ball hit the medal and it fell down. But something unexpected happened. The ball also hit a big bee nest in the tree.
The bees came out and they were very angry. Tim's mom knew what to do. She took a towel and started to slap the bees away. The bees left them alone. Tim said, "Thank you, mom!" His mom smiled and gave him the medal. "You were very brave," she said. Tim felt happy and proud.

One sunny day, Lucy and her mom were gardening in their backyard. Lucy's mom showed her a vine.
"What is it?" asked Lucy.
"It's a vine," said her mom. "I'm going to put it on the wall."
Then her mom showed her all the other things in the garden, which included flowers and plants.
Mom said, "Look at the clear sky. Isn't it nice?"
"Yes!" said Lucy. She was very happy.
Then, her mom put up the vine on the wall. It created a nice and clear look.
Lucy admired the vine and said, "It looks pretty!"
"Yes, I think so too," said her mom.
And they finished up their gardening together while they watched the vine grow.

Once upon a time, there was a big, ancient tree. Near the tree, there was a small house. In the house, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy liked to eat bananas. She ate a banana every day. One day, she saw a big, yellow banana on the ancient tree.
Lucy wanted the big banana from the tree. She tried to climb the tree, but it was too high. She was sad. She went to her house and found a long stick. Lucy had an idea. She could use the stick to get the banana.
The next day, Lucy went back to the ancient tree. She used the long stick to reach the big banana. She tried and tried until the banana fell down. Lucy was happy. She picked up the banana and opened it. It was the best banana she ever ate. From that day on, Lucy used the long stick to get bananas from the ancient tree.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Lucy. She was only three years old and loved to explore. One day she was playing in her bedroom and found something on the floor. It was her favourite teddy bear! She was so excited and couldn't wait to join it.
Suddenly, there was a knock on the bedroom door. It was her mum. She looked very angry. "Lucy, why did you take my teddy bear without asking?" she asked. 
Lucy felt very guilty and bowed her head. "Sorry," she whispered.
Her mum sighed. "It's alright Lucy, I forgive you. But in future, remember to ask me before you join anything on the floor."

One day, a little girl named Annie was playing in the park. She saw something big and shiny with headlights. It had a long, pointed nose and made a loud honking noise.
Annie went closer and saw that it was a big yellow bus. Annie was excited and asked the bus driver, "What is this?"
The bus driver smiled and said, "This is a bus. I use it to take kids to school, so they can learn new things. See the horn I use to make the noise?"
Annie was very nosy and asked, "Can I honk it?"
The bus driver laughed and said, "Maybe when you are a bit older and learn more."
Annie was sad, but thanked the bus driver for explaining things to her. Then, she went back to playing in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely truck. The truck was very sad because it had no friends. It would drive around the town, looking for someone to play with. But all the other trucks were too busy working.
One day, the lonely truck saw a sharp rock on the road. The rock was big and pointy. The other trucks did not want to drive near it. They were afraid of getting hurt. The lonely truck had an idea. It would help the other trucks by moving the sharp rock.
The lonely truck went to the sharp rock and pushed it with all its strength. The rock moved away from the road. The other trucks were very happy. They thanked the lonely truck for its help. Now, the lonely truck had many friends to play with, and it was not sad anymore.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a loyal dog named Buddy. Buddy loved to help his family with little jobs around the house. He was always happy to give a helping paw.
One day, Buddy's family gave him a new job. They asked Buddy to bring the newspaper from the front yard to the house. Buddy wagged his tail and ran outside to find the newspaper.
Buddy found the newspaper and carried it back to the house in his mouth. He gave the newspaper to his family with a big, happy smile. They were so proud of their loyal dog for doing such a good job. From that day on, Buddy's new job was to bring the newspaper to his family every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a messy room. He liked to play with his toys and leave them all over the floor. One day, his mom told him to clean up his room. Tim didn't want to, but he knew he had to listen to his mom.
As Tim started picking up his toys, he found a big, red ball. He decided to play with it for a little while. Tim threw the ball up in the air and tried to catch it. But, he missed the ball and it went slap on the floor. The loud noise made Tim jump.
Tim's mom heard the slap and came into the room. She saw that Tim was playing instead of cleaning. She told Tim to put the ball away and finish cleaning his room. Tim said sorry and put the ball away. He cleaned up the rest of his toys and soon his room was nice and tidy.

One day, a bossy cat named Tom went to a big steel box. He wanted to see what was inside. "I am the boss of the box," he said, "and I will open it!" He tried to open it, but it was very hard.
Tom saw a little mouse named Jerry who was fast and could rush quickly. "Jerry, help me open the box," Tom said. "I want to see what is inside." Jerry was not sure, but he wanted to help. So, he rushed to the box and tried to open it with Tom.
Suddenly, the box opened! But instead of finding something fun, they found a big dog who was sleeping inside! The dog woke up and said, "I am the boss of the box, not you!" Tom and Jerry were scared and rushed away from the box. They learned that they should not be bossy and should work together as friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Abigail. Abigail was three years old and she had a very special uncle called Paul who she loved very much. 
Every weekend, Uncle Paul and Abigail would go for a walk in the park together. Today was no different, and off they went, hand in hand. 
When they got to the park, Abigail saw a beautiful garden, full of colourful flowers. She was so excited and wanted to pick them all! But Uncle Paul said that she must not pick them, or they would not yield any more flowers.
Abigail understood and nodded, listening carefully to everything Uncle Paul said. Then he showed her something else - a big muddy puddle. Even though it was dirty, Uncle Paul said that they could still have fun, as long as they stayed clean. 
So Abigail and Uncle Paul played in the puddle, careful to stay clean. Then they played some more games, before it was time to go home. Abigail and Uncle Paul hugged goodbye - she was so happy to have spent the day with him and to have learnt something new about how to be kind to nature and to stay clean.

Anna and Ben were playing outside in the sun. They liked to run and jump and laugh. They saw a big bush with red flowers. They wanted to smell the flowers and touch them.
"Let's go to the bush, Ben!" Anna said.
"OK, Anna!" Ben said.
They ran to the bush and bent down to smell the flowers. They smelled sweet and nice. They smiled and reached out to touch the flowers.
But the bush was not nice. It had sharp thorns that hurt their fingers. They cried out and pulled back their hands. They saw blood on their fingers and felt pain.
"Ow, ow, ow!" Anna said.
"Ow, ow, ow!" Ben said.
They were sad and scared. They wanted to go home and get a hug and a band-aid. They looked for their mom and dad, but they did not see them. They were alone in the park.
Then they heard a loud noise. It was thunder. They looked up and saw dark clouds. They felt drops of water on their faces. It was rain.
The rain was cold and wet. It made them shiver and sneeze. It made their clothes and hair and shoes wet. It made their wounds sting and burn. It made them more sad and scared.
They wanted the rain to stop. They wanted the sun to come back. They wanted their mom and dad to come and get them. They wanted the bush to go away.
But the rain did not stop. The sun did not come back. Their mom and dad did not come and get them. The bush did not go away.
They were stuck in the park with the rain and the bush and the pain. They were not grateful. They were unhappy. They wished they never went to the bush. They wished they never played outside. They wished they stayed home. They wished they listened to their mom and dad.
The end.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily loved to build with her blocks. She would stack them high and make incredible towers. One day, she went outside to play in nature. She saw trees, flowers, and butterflies. Lily had an idea.
Lily decided to build a tower with things from nature. She found sticks, leaves, and stones. She worked hard to make her tower stand tall. The animals came to watch her build. They thought it was incredible too.
When Lily finished her nature tower, everyone cheered. The birds sang, the squirrels chattered, and the rabbits hopped. Lily felt very happy. She knew that building with nature was an incredible thing to do.

Once upon a time, there was a loyal dog named Spot. Spot loved to dig in the dirt. One day, he found a big, shiny mineral. It was very pretty. Spot picked it up in his mouth and ran home to show his family.
When Spot got home, he dropped the mineral on the floor. The family looked at it and were very happy. Then, something strange happened. The mineral started to speak! The family was very surprised.
The mineral said, "Thank you for finding me, Spot! I am a magic mineral. I will give you one wish." The family looked at each other and decided to wish for a big, fun park to play in. The magic mineral made their wish come true. Spot and his family played in the park every day and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a pirate named Tom. He had a great big hat and a big laugh. Tom liked to sail on his big boat with his friends. They had fun in the sun and played games all day.
One day, Tom saw a little island. He said to his friends, "Let's go and play on the island!" They all went to the island and found a big tree. Under the tree, they saw a box. Tom said, "Look, a treasure box!"
Tom opened the box and found many toys. He started to laugh and said, "This is the best treasure ever!" All the pirates played with the toys and laughed together. They had a great time on the island. And from that day on, they shared their toys with all the kids they met on their journey.

One day, a girl named Lily wanted to write a letter. She sat down with her pen and paper. She started to write a letter to her friend, Sam. Lily was very good at writing letters. She liked to make her friends happy with her words.
Lily saw her dog, Max, look at her while she wrote. Max looked a little jealous. He wanted to write too! Lily had an idea. She gave Max a pen and paper. Max held the pen in his mouth and tried to write. But it was hard for Max because he was a dog.
Then, something unexpected happened. Max wrote a letter! It was not as good as Lily's, but it was still a letter. Lily was very surprised. She did not know that Max could write. They both laughed and had fun. Now, Lily and Max wrote letters together, making all their friends happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a toy clock that he loved very much. One day, while playing outside, Tim decided to bury his clock in the sand.
Tim dug a hole and put his clock in it. He covered it with sand, but then he forgot where he buried it. Tim felt frustrated because he could not find his clock. He looked and looked, but it was still hidden in the sand.
Tim's big sister, Sue, saw him feeling sad. She asked what was wrong, and Tim told her about his lost clock. Sue helped Tim search for the clock. They dug and dug, and finally, they found it! Tim was so happy to have his clock back. From that day on, Tim promised never to bury his favorite toy again.

Once upon a time, there was a smart cat named Tom. He lived in a small house with his friend, a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily found a shiny copper coin on the ground. She picked it up and showed it to Tom.
"Look, Tom! I found a copper coin!" said Lily. Tom looked at the coin and had an idea. "Let's bring the coin to the store and buy a toy," said Tom. Lily liked the idea, so they went to the store together.
At the store, they found a pretty doll. Lily gave the copper coin to the store man, and he gave them the doll. Lily and Tom were very happy. They brought the doll home and played with it all day. The smart cat and the little girl had a great day together.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went to the park. She had a basket with her. In the basket, there were toys and snacks. Lucy saw a harmless bug on the grass. She said, "Hello, little bug!"
The bug looked at Lucy and said, "Hi, Lucy! Can I play with you?" Lucy said, "Yes, let's start a game!" They played with the toys from the basket. They laughed and had fun.
When it was time to go home, Lucy said, "Bye, little bug!" The bug said, "Bye, Lucy! Let's play again soon!" Lucy smiled and went home with her basket. She had a great day at the park.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who wanted a new pair of shoes. She asked her mom and her mom said she could get them.
The little girl was so happy. She put the shoes on and they fit perfectly. She thanked her mom and smiled.
The little girl wanted to show off her new shoes so she went to the park to find her friends. But when she got there everyone was ready to go home.
The little girl was sad and said to her friends, "But I got my new shoes to show you now!"
Her friends said, "That's okay! We can look at them tomorrow."
The little girl thanked her friends for understanding.
Moral Value: Appreciate what people do for you and don't be too disappointed if things don't go as planned.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big festival. People were happy and having fun. The sun was shining and the sky was calm.
In the middle of the festival, there was a lady who loved to cook. She cooked yummy treats for everyone to eat. She made cookies, cakes, and pies that made everyone smile.
One day, a little girl came to the lady and asked for a cookie. The lady gave her a cookie and the little girl was so happy. They sat together, ate cookies, and enjoyed the calm and fun festival. The end.

Tom liked to play with his flashlight. He liked to turn it on and off and make bright spots on the wall. He liked to number the spots and say them out loud. One, two, three, four, five!
One night, Tom heard a loud noise outside his window. He was scared. He grabbed his flashlight and ran to his mom and dad's room. He knocked on the door and said, "Mom, Dad, there is a noise outside!"
Mom and Dad opened the door and hugged Tom. They said, "Don't worry, Tom, we will check it out." They took Tom and his flashlight and went to the window. They saw a big, black dog barking at a cat on the fence. The dog was mighty and loud.
Tom shone his flashlight at the dog and said, "Go away, dog, leave the cat alone!" The dog saw the bright spot and stopped barking. He tilted his head and looked at Tom. He wagged his tail and ran away. The cat jumped off the fence and ran away too.
Tom smiled and said, "I scared the dog with my flashlight. I am mighty too!" Mom and Dad smiled and said, "You are, Tom, you are. But now it's time to go back to bed. Good night, Tom, we love you." They tucked Tom in his bed and kissed him good night. Tom turned off his flashlight and closed his eyes. He dreamed of being a mighty hero with his flashlight.

Once upon a time, there was a gray bunny named Bob. Bob loved to eat yummy food like carrots and lettuce. One day, Bob saw a sign in the forest that said, "Yummy Food Here."
Bob hopped to the place with the sign. He found many yummy foods like apples and berries. He ate and ate until his tummy was full. But then, he saw another bunny who didn't have any food. The other bunny looked sad and hungry.
Bob thought about the sign and the yummy food. He decided to share his food with the other bunny. They both ate and were happy. The other bunny said, "Thank you, Bob, for sharing your food with me."
From that day on, Bob always shared his food with other bunnies in the forest. They all became good friends and lived happily together. The moral of the story is that sharing with others makes everyone happy.

One day, Tom and Tim were playing in the park. It was Wednesday. Tom was weak, but he wanted to play with Tim. They were having fun with a ball.
Tom said, "I remember when I was weak like you, but I got strong by playing every day."
Tim wanted to be strong too. So, he played with Tom every day. They became best friends.
One day, a big boy came to the park. He wanted to play with Tom and Tim. But he was not nice. He took their ball and ran away.
Tom and Tim were sad, but they remembered that they were strong now. They ran after the big boy and got their ball back. They learned that being strong and kind is better than being mean.

One day, a big dog and a little cat had a fight. The dog was very strong, but the cat was fast. They both thought they were the best and wanted to show it. They were being foolish.
A wise old owl saw them fight and said, "Stop! You should not fight. Sit and talk about your problem." So, the dog and the cat sat down to talk. They were very tired from the fight. While they were talking, they found out they both liked to play with a ball.
Just then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and blew the ball far away. The dog and the cat looked at each other and smiled. They stopped fighting and ran after the ball together. They learned that it was better to be friends and play together than to fight.

Once upon a time, there was a little mouse named Max. Max loved to play outside in the sun. One day, Max found a big piece of cheese. It was so big, it was like a trap. Max wanted to take the cheese home, but it was very hot outside. Max felt uncomfortable in the heat.
As Max tried to move the cheese, something unexpected happened. The cheese began to melt because of the sun. Max was sad because he could not take the cheese home now. But then, Max had an idea. He decided to eat the cheese right there.
Max ate and ate until he was full. He was so happy that he could eat the cheese even if it melted. In the end, Max learned that sometimes things change, but that does not mean they are bad.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park. She saw a huge red ball near the sink. Lily wanted to play with the ball, so she picked it up and threw it high into the sky. The ball went up and up, and it seemed like it would never come back down. Lily was so excited to see the ball rise so high.
As Lily watched the ball, she saw a little bird flying near it. The bird saw the ball too and thought it was a big berry. The bird said, "Yum! That looks like a tasty treat!" The bird flew to the ball and tried to take a bite.
But the ball was not a berry, and the bird was surprised! The bird let go of the ball, and it started to fall back down. The ball fell into the sink with a big splash. Lily ran to the sink and saw the ball was all wet. She laughed and said, "Silly bird, that's not a berry!"
The bird was a little embarrassed, but it laughed too. The bird said, "I'm sorry, I thought it was a yummy berry. Can we play together?" Lily smiled and said, "Yes, let's play!" So, Lily and the bird played with the huge red ball all day long, and they became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a brown dragon. The dragon was very hungry. He wanted to eat something sweet. So, the dragon decided to bake a cake.
The dragon met a little girl named Lily. Lily liked to bake too. "Can I help you bake the cake?" she asked. The dragon said, "Yes, let's bake together!" They mixed the cake and put it in the oven.
As they waited for the cake to bake, they heard a loud noise. It was another dragon! This dragon was green and looked sad. The brown dragon and Lily asked, "Why are you sad?" The green dragon said, "I am hungry and have no food." The brown dragon and Lily smiled. They knew what to do. They all baked another cake together. The green dragon was happy, and they all became friends.

Once upon a time, in a yellow house, lived two best friends, a cat and a dog. They liked to play and have fun all day. One day, they wanted to have a toy fight. So, they started to search for their favorite toys.
While they were searching, they found a big, yellow box. They had never seen it before. They were very curious, so they opened the box. Inside the box, they found many new toys! They were so happy and excited.
They started playing with the new toys, but then something unexpected happened. The toys came to life! The cat and the dog were surprised, but they did not fight with the toys. Instead, they all became friends and played together all day long. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a polite duck named Bob went to the lake. He saw a big fish in the water. The fish was sad. Bob asked the fish, "Why are you sad, big fish?"
The big fish said, "I lost my hat. I can't find it anywhere." Bob wanted to help the big fish. He nodded and said, "I will help you find your hat, big fish."
They looked all around the lake. They saw a small frog. The frog had a hat. It was the big fish's hat! Bob asked the frog, "Can we have the hat back, please?" The frog gave the hat back to the big fish. The big fish was happy. Bob and the big fish played at the lake all day.

Lily was a little girl who liked to dream. Every night, she would close her eyes and imagine wonderful things. She dreamed of flying in the sky, swimming in the sea, and playing with animals. She was happy in her dreams.
One day, she saw a newspaper on the table. She did not know what it was, but it had many pictures and words. She picked it up and looked at it. She saw a picture of a big man with an ugly face. He was angry and shouting. He had a gun in his hand. Lily was scared. She dropped the newspaper and ran to her mom.
"Mom, what is this?" she asked, pointing at the newspaper.
"That is a newspaper, Lily. It tells us what is happening in the world. Sometimes, it has good news, and sometimes, it has bad news. That man is a bad man. He did something very wrong. He hurt many people. He is not nice," her mom explained.
"Why did he do that, mom? Why is he not nice?" Lily asked.
"I don't know, Lily. Some people are not nice because they are angry, or sad, or greedy, or mean. They don't care about others. They don't have good dreams. They have bad dreams," her mom said.
Lily thought about her dreams. She did not want to have bad dreams. She wanted to have good dreams. She wanted to be nice. She hugged her mom and said, "I love you, mom. I want to have good dreams. I want to be nice."
Her mom smiled and hugged her back. She said, "I love you too, Lily. You have good dreams. You are nice. You make me happy."
Lily learned that dreams are important. They can make us happy or sad. They can make us nice or mean. She decided to always have good dreams and be nice to others. She hoped that the bad man would have good dreams too, and be nice someday. She hoped that the world would have more good news than bad news. She hoped that everyone would be happy.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a girl named Amy. She had a toy bunny that she loved very much. One day, her bunny got a big rip and needed to be fixed. Amy's mom said she would replace the bunny with a new one. Amy was sad, but she knew her mom was trying to help.
Amy and her new bunny went outside to play. They saw a little bird trying to escape from a mean cat. The cat was harsh to the bird, but Amy and her bunny scared the cat away. The bird was happy and flew away, singing a thank you song.
From that day on, Amy and her new bunny became the best of friends. They played together every day and helped other animals in need. Amy learned that sometimes change can be good, and that even a new bunny can be a great friend.

One day, a large dog named Max went for a walk. He saw a big red alarm on the wall. Max was curious about the alarm.
Max met a small cat named Lily. She said, "Max, don't touch the alarm. It will make a loud noise!" Max listened to Lily and did not touch the alarm.
Later, it started to rain. Max and Lily used a big leaf to cover themselves. They stayed dry and happy. They became good friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a fast dog named Spot. Spot loved to run and play with his friends in the park. One day, Spot saw a cane lying on the ground. He thought it would be fun to get the cane and play with it.
Spot picked up the cane in his mouth and ran as fast as he could. His friends saw him and wanted to play too. They all ran after Spot, trying to get the cane from him. Spot was very fast and did not let anyone catch him.
After a while, Spot got tired and stopped running. His friends caught up to him and they all took turns playing with the cane. They had a lot of fun that day and, in the end, Spot learned that sharing the cane with his friends was even better than playing with it all by himself.

One day, a big, proud dog saw a thief. The thief took a toy from a little boy. The dog did not like this. The dog wanted to help the little boy.
The proud dog ran fast to the thief. The dog yelled, "Stop!" The thief was scared. He dropped the toy and ran away. The dog picked up the toy with his mouth.
The dog brought the toy back to the little boy. But, the boy was not happy. He said, "That's not my toy!" The dog was confused. Then, another little boy came. He was the real owner of the toy. The first little boy's toy was under a tree. The dog felt happy to help both boys find their toys.

John was in a hurry. His mommy was calling him to go to the store. He grabbed his coat and put it on fast. He was so excited!
Once they got to the store, his mom asked him to get some paper towels. He ran to the aisle and found them right away. His mom was so pleased that he was so organized.
John saw something else that he wanted and asked his mom for it. She used a special word. She said no. John knew it meant he couldn't have it, so he stopped asking.
After the shopping was done, John and his mommy were back in the car and heading home. John's mom thanked him for being so organized in the store and for hurrying. He was so happy to hear his mom use such nice words.

Once upon a time, there was a sailor named Tom. He had a beautiful boat with big sails. Tom loved to sail on the water every day.
One day, Tom saw a mark on his boat. "Oh no!" he said. "Who made this mark?" He looked around and saw a little bird sitting on his boat. The bird said, "I'm sorry, I made the mark."
Tom smiled and said, "That's okay, little bird. You can come sail with me." So, the sailor and the bird became friends. They sailed together on the beautiful boat every day, and they were very happy.

Once there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy had a toy box with many toys. Her favorite toy was a dependable car. The car was red and fast. Lucy loved to play with her car every day.
One day, Lucy's car lost a screw. It made her car not work well. Lucy was sad. She asked her dad to help her find the screw. They looked all around the house for the screw, but they could not find it.
Lucy's dad said, "I have an idea." He went to the garage and found a new screw. He put the new screw in Lucy's car. Lucy's car was fixed. Lucy was happy to accept the new screw. Now, her dependable car was ready to play again.

Once upon a time, there was a good dog. The dog wanted to play with a missile, but he couldn't find one anywhere. He searched and searched but to no avail.
Finally, he decided to tie one of his long ropes to a small tree. He hoped that the rope would become a missile, but it did not. Instead it just stayed in its place. 
The poor dog was very sad. He had tried his best, but it was not enough. He knew he couldn't make a missile out of a rope, so he sadly gave up and went home. 
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly boy named Tim. Tim loved to race with his friends. One day, he found a big toy called a microscope.
Tim said to his friend, "Let's race to see who can look at the microscope first!" His friend, Sam, smiled and said, "Okay, let's go!" They both ran very fast to the microscope.
Tim and Sam took turns looking into the microscope. They saw many tiny things they had never seen before. They were so happy and had lots of fun. After they played with the microscope, they raced all the way back home.

Tom liked to play with his toys in the living room. He had a lot of cars, trains, and animals that he could make noises with. He also liked to sit on the big armchair and pretend he was a king or a pilot.
One day, he found a new toy in his mom's sewing basket. It was shiny and pointy and had a hole in one end. Tom thought it was a rocket or a sword. He took it out and ran around the living room, making whoosh and zing sounds.
But the toy was not a rocket or a sword. It was a needle, and it was very sharp. Tom did not know that, and he waved it too close to his face. He felt a sting on his cheek and saw a drop of red on his skin. He cried out and dropped the needle.
Mom heard him and came running. She saw the needle on the floor and the blood on his cheek. She was scared and angry. She picked up Tom and hugged him. She said, "Tom, what did you do? That is not a toy, that is a needle. It is sharp and can hurt you. You should never touch it. Where did you find it?"
Tom pointed to the sewing basket. He said, "I'm sorry, Mom. I thought it was a rocket. It looked fun and lively. I didn't know it was sharp and bad."
Mom said, "It's okay, Tom. I'm glad you are not hurt badly. But you have to be careful and ask me before you play with something you don't know. Some things are not toys and can be dangerous. Do you understand?"
Tom nodded and said, "Yes, Mom. I understand. I won't touch the needle again. Can I play with my cars now?"
Mom said, "Yes, you can. But first, let me clean your cheek and put a band-aid on it. Then you can play with your cars on the armchair. But be gentle and don't make too much noise. Okay?"
Tom said, "Okay, Mom. Thank you. I love you."
Mom said, "I love you too, Tom. You are my brave and smart boy."

One day, a big parade came to town. It had many people, cars, and animals. All the kids were happy to see the parade. They were restless. They could not sit still. They wanted to see everything.
A girl named Lisa wanted to serve ice cream to her friends. She thought it would be a fun surprise. She went to her house to get the ice cream. When she came back, her friends were gone.
Lisa was sad. She looked for her friends. Then, she saw a big elephant. The elephant was eating her ice cream! Lisa was not sad anymore. She laughed and shared the ice cream with the elephant. The parade was more fun than she thought.

Once upon a time, there was a little brown dog. The dog lived in a small house with a big lock on the door. The dog liked to play with his ball and run in the yard. He was a very happy dog.
One day, the dog lost his ball. He looked everywhere for it but could not find it. The dog was very sad. He wanted his ball back so he could play again. His friend, a kind cat, tried to comfort him. The cat said, "Don't worry, we will find your ball."
The dog and the cat looked all day for the ball. They looked under the bed, behind the big lock, and in the yard. But they could not find the ball. The dog was very sad and the cat could not comfort him anymore.
At the end of the day, the dog and the cat went to sleep. They did not find the ball and the dog was still sad. The cat tried to comfort the dog one more time, but the dog just cried. The dog never found his ball and he was sad forever.

Lily and Ben are playing with blocks in the living room. They like to build towers and bridges and houses with the blocks. They have many colors of blocks: red, blue, yellow, green, and more.
"Look, Lily, I made a big tower!" Ben says, showing his blocks to his sister. "It is very tall and strong!"
Lily smiles and claps her hands. "Wow, Ben, that is a good tower! Can I help you make it taller?"
"Yes, you can, Lily. Here, take some blocks and put them on top. But be careful, do not make it fall!"
Lily and Ben work together to add more blocks to the tower. They stretch their arms to reach the top. They are having fun.
But then, their cat, Fluffy, comes into the living room. Fluffy sees the tower and thinks it is a toy. Fluffy jumps on the tower and tries to catch the blocks. The tower shakes and wobbles and then crashes to the floor. The blocks scatter everywhere. Fluffy runs away.
"Oh no!" Lily and Ben cry. "Our tower is gone! Fluffy is naughty!"
They are sad and angry. But then, they see the light from the window shining on the blocks. The blocks sparkle and glow in different colors. They look pretty and bright.
"Look, Lily, the blocks are light!" Ben says, picking up a yellow block. "They are like suns!"
Lily picks up a blue block. "They are like stars!" she says, holding it up.
Lily and Ben forget about the tower. They start to make new shapes with the light blocks. They make a rainbow, a flower, a car, and more. They laugh and giggle and play. They are happy again.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there lived a little boy named Tim. Tim was a very nervous boy. He was always scared to try new things. One day, his mom told him they were going to a big party in the village. This made Tim very nervous.
At the party, there were many fun games and yummy food. Tim saw his friend, Lily, playing a game. She looked very excited. Tim wanted to play too, but he was still nervous. Lily saw Tim and asked him to join her. They played the game together, and Tim started to feel less nervous.
Soon, Tim was not nervous anymore. He was excited to play more games and eat yummy food with his friend, Lily. The village party was so much fun, and Tim learned that trying new things can be exciting. From that day on, Tim was not so nervous anymore.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a girl with a puppy. The girl said, "Hi, I am Sue. Let me introduce you to my puppy, Max." Tim was happy to meet Max, but he felt impatient. He wanted to play with Max right away.
But Max was not ready to play. He was scared of Tim. Tim did not know what to do. He wanted to be friends with Max, but Max kept running away. Tim felt sad and asked Sue, "Why doesn't Max want to play with me?"
Sue said, "Max is scared because he is a new puppy. He needs time to feel safe." Tim was surprised. He thought all puppies loved to play. Sue had an idea. She told Tim to sit down and be very quiet. Tim did as Sue said, and soon, Max came close to him. Tim and Max became friends and played all day in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was eager to help his mom in the kitchen. One day, his mom asked him to set the table for dinner. Tim was very happy and excited to help.
Tim took the big plates and the small plates from the cupboard. He put a big plate and a small plate in front of each chair. He made sure that everyone had a plate to eat from. Then, he put the forks, spoons, and cups on the table too. Tim felt proud of his work.
When dinner was ready, everyone sat down at the table. They all enjoyed the yummy food that Tim's mom made. After they finished eating, Tim helped to clear the table. He did not leave any plates or cups behind. Tim's mom was very proud of him, and Tim felt happy that he could help.

Ben and Lily were playing in the park. They liked to swing, slide and run. They saw a big yellow post with a sign on it. The sign had many words and pictures.
"What is that?" Lily asked.
"I don't know. Let's go and see," Ben said.
They ran to the post and looked at the sign. It had words like "park rules", "no littering", "keep off the grass" and "have fun". It also had pictures of a dog, a trash can, a flower and a smiley face.
"What does it mean?" Lily asked.
"I think it means what we can and can't do in the park," Ben said. "See, the dog means we can bring our pets, but the trash can means we have to throw away our garbage. The flower means we have to be nice to the plants, and the smiley face means we have to be happy."
"Oh, I see. That makes sense," Lily said.
They heard a voice behind them. It was a park ranger. He wore a hat and a badge.
"Hello, kids. What are you doing?" he asked.
"We are looking at the sign," Ben said.
"That's good. Do you know what the sign is for?" the park ranger asked.
"Yes, it tells us the park rules," Lily said.
"That's right. The sign is to help us keep the park clean and safe for everyone. Do you follow the park rules?" the park ranger asked.
"Yes, we do. We love the park," Ben said.
"Me too. I'm glad you do. Can I recommend something to you?" the park ranger asked.
"What is it?" Lily asked.
"Well, if you look over there, you will see a big yellow tent. That's where we have some fun activities for kids. You can learn more about the park, the animals and the plants. You can also play some games and get some stickers. Would you like to try it?" the park ranger asked.
"Yes, yes, yes!" Ben and Lily said.
"Great. Then follow me. I'll show you the way," the park ranger said.
He led them to the yellow tent. Ben and Lily thanked him and went inside. They had a lot of fun in the yellow tent. They learned, played and got stickers. They followed the park rules and had a happy day.

Once upon a time, there was a big toy rocket. It had a missile on it. The missile was not attached. The rocket wanted to be ready to fly high in the sky.
One day, a little boy found the rocket. He saw the missile and knew it needed to be attached. The little boy was very smart. He knew how to make the rocket ready.
The little boy attached the missile to the rocket. Now, the rocket was ready. It was so happy! The little boy and the rocket played all day. They had so much fun, and the rocket flew high in the sky.

Anna and Ben were best friends. They liked to play catch with a ball in the park. One day, they saw a big palace with many windows and doors. They wanted to see inside.
"Let's go to the palace," Anna said. "Maybe we can find something amazing."
"But we are not allowed to go there," Ben said. "It is for the king and queen only. They might be angry if they see us."
Anna did not listen. She ran to the palace gate and pushed it open. Ben followed her. They went inside the palace and looked around. They saw many beautiful things: paintings, statues, jewels, and clothes.
"Wow, this is amazing," Anna said. "Look at this dress. It is so pretty. I want to try it on."
She took the dress from a closet and put it on. She felt like a princess. She spun around and smiled.
Ben saw a crown on a table. It was shiny and had many gems. He wanted to try it on.
"Look at this crown," Ben said. "It is so cool. I want to try it on."
He took the crown from the table and put it on. He felt like a king. He waved his hand and laughed.
They did not notice that the king and queen had come back to the palace. They heard footsteps and voices. They were scared.
"Quick, let's hide," Anna said. "Maybe they will not find us."
They ran to a corner and hid behind a curtain. They hoped that the king and queen would not see them.
But the king and queen saw the dress and the crown that Anna and Ben had taken. They were angry.
"Who has been in our palace?" the king said. "Who has touched our things?"
They looked around and saw the curtain moving. They pulled it aside and saw Anna and Ben. They were shocked.
"Who are you?" the queen said. "Why are you wearing our dress and crown?"
Anna and Ben were very sorry. They took off the dress and the crown and gave them back to the king and queen. They bowed their heads and said:
"We are sorry, your majesty. We are Anna and Ben. We are best friends. We like to play catch in the park. We saw your palace and wanted to see inside. We thought it was amazing. We did not mean to make you angry. Please forgive us."
The king and queen looked at Anna and Ben. They saw that they were just children who were curious and playful. They decided to be kind and forgive them.
"It is okay, Anna and Ben," the king said. "We understand that you were curious and playful. But you should not go into places that are not yours. You should respect other people's things. You should ask for permission before you touch anything. Do you understand?"
"Yes, your majesty," Anna and Ben said. "We understand. We are sorry. We will not do it again."
The king and queen smiled and said:
"Good. We are glad that you are sorry and that you have learned your lesson. Now, do you want to see more of our palace? We can show you around and tell you some stories. But you have to promise to be careful and respectful. Do you promise?"
"Yes, your majesty," Anna and Ben said. "We promise. Thank you for being kind and forgiving. Thank you for showing us your palace. It is amazing."
The king and queen took Anna and Ben by the hand and showed them more of their palace. They told them some stories and played some games. They had fun and became friends.
Anna and Ben learned that it is wrong to go into places that are not theirs and to touch things that are not theirs. They learned that it is better to ask for permission and to respect other people's things. They also learned that the king and queen were kind and forgiving and that they could be friends with them. They were happy and grateful. They never forgot their adventure in the palace.

Once upon a time, there was a strong man. He was walking down a path to find something good. As he walked along, he seemed to feel something special. He kept walking, until he saw something amazing.
When he reached the end of the path, it seemed like he had arrived at a big castle! The walls were strong, and the moat was filled with water. He stepped inside, and he could see a big treasure room.
The man was so happy that he decided to take all the treasures with him. He was very strong, so he was able to carry all the treasures and take them back down the path.
At the end of the day, the man was very happy. He made sure to cover the path with leaves so that no one would know where he had been, and headed home with all of the treasures. 
The strong man was a very happy man indeed, for he found something special at the end of the path!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. He loved to play with his basketball. One day, he played with his basketball near a big tree. Tom was not careful and his basketball hit a branch. The basketball got a hole and air came out. Tom felt sad and thought it was stupid to play near the tree.
Tom's dad saw him feeling sad. He asked Tom what happened. Tom told him about the basketball. His dad said, "Don't worry, we can fix it." Tom and his dad went to the store to buy a patch. They put the patch on the hole and pumped air into the basketball. The basketball was fixed!
Tom was very happy to play with his basketball again. He learned that it is important to be careful when playing. He also learned that when you make a mistake, you can fix it if you ask for help. Tom never played near the big tree again, and he always shared his toys with his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a lovely race. The race was with crayons and everybody was having a great time. 
A small girl called Joy was getting ready to take part in the race. She was very excited as it was her first time. 
"Mommy, look!", shouted Joy. "Can I join the race?"
"Yes, you can," said Mommy with a bright smile.
Joy grabbed a lovely crayon and lined up with all the other children. "I'm ready!" yelled Joy. 
The whistle blew and everybody started to race around the room. Joy ran as fast as she could, determined to finish first. 
At the end of the race, Joy was the winner. She was so happy and proud of herself. She held up her lovely crayon high, and everybody cheered. 
The race was such a success, and everybody was delighted. Joy's mommy hugged her tight and said, "You did so well! I'm so proud of you." 
Joy knew that with love and determination, she could do anything.

Once upon a time there was a nation. This nation was filled with people who were very happy. But then one day, all of the happiness in the nation vanished. Everyone became very anxious. The people could not understand what had happened. They looked everywhere, but the happiness was nowhere to be found.
The people searched and searched, but they still could not find their lost happiness. They asked each other what had happened, but no one had an answer. Everyone in the nation felt so anxious, and nothing seemed to help.
One day, a little girl had an idea. She said that maybe their happiness had gone somewhere that no one knew about. The people agreed, so they decided to go and look for it. Everyone searched in all different directions, and after a long time, one of the people found something. He had found the lost happiness!
Everyone was so happy that the little girl's idea had worked. The happiness had been found and the nation was happy again. From then on, everyone was careful to never let it vanish again.

Once upon a time, there was a bird with a big wing. The bird loved to carry things in its beak. One day, it found a yummy red fruit. The bird wanted to share it with its friend, the squirrel.
The bird tried to carry the yummy fruit in its beak, but it was too heavy. The bird did not give up. It tried again and again. Then, something unexpected happened! The bird found a stick. The bird put the fruit on the stick and carried it with its wing.
The squirrel saw the bird and was very happy. They both enjoyed the yummy fruit together. The bird learned that if you don't give up, you can find new ways to do things. And that sharing with friends is a good thing to do.

Once upon a time there was a boy named Jim. Jim was three years old and loved to explore and have fun. One day he was playing in his backyard and he saw something very fancy-- a little mail box! 
He ran to his mom and asked, "Mommy, what is that?" 
"That's a mailbox, Jim," his mom said. "You can put letters and cards inside it and mail it to friends, family and people all around the world." 
Jim was amazed and grabbed a piece of paper and a pen. He drew a picture and put it inside the mailbox. Then he put the mailbox on top of a fence post and waved goodbye. 
But, as soon as he left, smoke started filling the backyard. Jim ran back and saw that the mailbox had caught on fire. He quickly grabbed the mailbox, put it in a bucket of water and put out the flames. 
Jim was sad, but he was also glad that he had saved the fancy mailbox. He knew he wanted to keep it forever, even though it wouldn't mail anymore.

One day, a big furry dog went for a walk. The dog saw a cross on the ground. He was very curious, so he went to look at it. As he got closer, he saw a small box under the cross. The dog decided to dig up the box.
When the box was out of the ground, the dog opened it. Inside the box, there was a stick. The dog picked up the stick with his mouth and started to stir the dirt with it. Suddenly, a tiny bird came out of the ground!
"Thank you for helping me!" the bird said to the dog. The dog was very surprised, but happy that he could help the bird. They became good friends and played together all day long. The dog learned that sometimes unexpected things can happen when you are curious and kind.

Once there were two little friends, Jack and Lisa, who were enjoying playing in the park. They were talking about all the fun things they could do when Lisa suddenly stopped and said: "Let's play with the missile."
Jack said: "What missile do you mean?" But Lisa just grinned and said: "Come, I'll show you."
The two of them ran to the other side of the park. When they arrived, they saw a big rocket, which was clear and shiny. Lisa pointed to it and said: "Look, there it is! The missile."
Jack was amazed. He had never seen a missile before. He asked what it was called.
Lisa said: "This kind of missile is called an ICBM. It stands for Intercontinental Ballistic Missile. It can travel very far away."
Jack was quite impressed. He thought it was amazing that a missile could travel so fast and so far. Lisa could tell he was excited and said: "Don't worry. We won't do anything with it. We just wanted to mention it."
Jack was relieved. He was happy they only mentioned the missile and didn't actually do anything with it.
The two little friends went back to their game in the park, with the memory of the big, clear missile in their minds.

Once upon a time, there was a crab. The crab lived in a deep part of the sea. He had many friends, like fish and turtles. They played all day long. The crab was very happy.
One day, the crab and his friends were playing near a big rock. They saw a big box. It was close to the rock. The crab was curious. He wanted to see what was inside the box. His friends were scared, but the crab was brave.
The crab opened the box. Inside, there was a big, shiny pearl. The crab was very surprised. He showed the pearl to his friends. They were all happy. The crab put the pearl inside his shell. Now, the crab was not just a crab. He was a special crab with a shiny pearl. And they all played happily ever after.

Once, there was a little girl who loved to play sports. She was very excited to try new games with her family and friends. One day, she wanted to try a new game but she soon realized it was fake.
The girl asked her Granny, "Granny, what is this game?" Granny said, "That's not a real sport. It's fake." 
The little girl was disappointed. She did not know what to do, until Granny said, "Let's go outside and play a real sport. You can learn a lot and have fun too!"
Then they went outside, and they played a real sport. The little girl learned a lot and had lots of fun. She realized that real sports were more fun and she could learn new skills and become healthier. From then on, the little girl always made sure to only play real sports.

One day, a sweet cat named Kitty and a dog named Max were playing outside. They were very happy and excited. They loved to run and jump in the fresh air.
Kitty said, "Max, I love the oxygen we breathe when we play outside!" Max agreed, "Yes, Kitty! It makes me feel so good and excited!"
One day, they found a big box in the park. They were very curious. They opened the box and found a lot of balloons. They played with the balloons and had so much fun. But then, the balloons started to float away into the sky!
Kitty and Max were sad to see the balloons go. But they learned that it's important to take care of their things and not let them go. They became more careful and enjoyed playing with their toys without losing them.

Anna and Ben were having breakfast with their mom. They liked to eat toast and eggs and juice. But today, mom had a surprise for them. She put a plate of sausages on the table.
"Look, kids, I made some sausages for you. They are very yummy and healthy. They are not real sausages, they are fake sausages. They are made from plants and beans. Do you want to try them?" mom said.
Anna and Ben looked at the sausages. They did not look like the sausages they saw at the store. They looked brown and lumpy and dry. They did not smell good either.
"Yuck, mom, what are these? They are not sausages, they are gross. We don't want to eat them. We want real sausages, like the ones dad makes on the grill. They are juicy and tasty and smell good. Can we have those instead?" Anna said.
"No, sweetie, we don't have any real sausages. Dad is away on a trip, remember? He will be back tomorrow. These are the only sausages we have. They are good for you, they have a lot of protein and fiber and vitamins. They are better than real sausages, which have a lot of fat and salt and chemicals. Please, give them a chance. Just take one bite and see if you like them." mom said.
Anna and Ben did not want to hurt mom's feelings, but they also did not want to eat the fake sausages. They looked at each other and made a plan. They each took a sausage from the plate and pretended to bite it. Then, they quickly removed the sausages from their mouths and hid them under their napkins. They smiled at mom and said, "Mmm, mom, these are very good. Thank you for making them. Can we have some more toast and eggs and juice, please?"
Mom was happy that they liked the sausages. She did not notice the napkins moving on their laps. She gave them more toast and eggs and juice. She said, "You're welcome, kids. I'm glad you enjoyed the sausages. Maybe next time, we can try some other fake foods, like fake cheese or fake bacon or fake chicken nuggets. They are all very yummy and healthy, too."
Anna and Ben nodded and said, "Sure, mom, that sounds great." But they secretly hoped that dad would come back soon and make them some real food. They did not like fake food.
