They thought it was yucky and gross. They wondered what they would do with the sausages under their napkins. They hoped mom would not find out. They hoped the dog would not smell them. They hoped they would not get in trouble. They hoped breakfast would be over soon.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a fierce bear named Ben. Ben was big and strong, but he did not have many friends. He was always hungry and liked to eat popcorn very much.
One day, Ben went to the squirrel family's house to borrow some popcorn. The squirrels were scared of Ben, but they still gave him some popcorn. Ben was happy and went back home to eat it. But he felt lonely and wished he had someone to share it with.
The next day, Ben decided to share his popcorn with the squirrels. They were surprised but happy. They all sat together and ate the popcorn. Ben learned that sharing with others made him feel good and have more friends. From that day on, Ben was not so fierce anymore and became friends with everyone in the forest.

Sara and Ben went to the park with Mom. They saw many animals there. They saw ducks, rabbits, and squirrels. They liked to feed them with bread and nuts.
"Look, Mom!" Sara said. "There is a deer over there! Can we go and see it?"
Mom nodded and smiled. "Yes, we can. But we have to be quiet and gentle. Deer are shy and scared of loud noises."
Sara and Ben followed Mom to the deer. The deer was brown and had big ears and a long nose. It was eating grass under a tree. Sara and Ben wanted to touch it, but Mom stopped them.
"No, we can't touch the deer. It might run away or kick us. We can only look at it from a distance."
Sara and Ben nodded. They watched the deer with wonder. They saw it lift its head and look around. They saw its horns and its tail. They saw it chew and swallow.
"Mom, can you show us how the deer makes sounds?" Ben asked.
Mom nodded and smiled. She made a soft and high sound with her mouth. It sounded like "eeeh". Sara and Ben tried to copy her. They made the same sound. The deer heard them and looked at them. It made the same sound back. Sara and Ben were happy. They felt like they were talking to the deer.
"Wow, Mom, you are so good at deer talk!" Sara said.
Mom laughed and hugged them. "Thank you, Sara. You and Ben are good too. But now we have to go. It's time to go home."
Sara and Ben said goodbye to the deer. They waved and made the sound again. The deer waved its tail and made the sound too. Then it went back to eating grass.
Sara and Ben walked back to the car with Mom. They felt dizzy from spinning and running in the park. They were tired and hungry. But they were also happy. They had a fun day with Mom and the deer.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Emma. She was three years old. One day, Emma was in her garden when she saw something. She saw a newspaper on the ground. She was scared because she had never seen one before.
So she slowly walked up to it and bent down. She gently put her hands underneath it and then lifted it up. When she lifted the newspaper, a butterfly flew out of it. Emma was so excited!
She was so glad that she wasn't scared anymore because the butterfly was so beautiful. Emma started laughing and playing with the butterfly. She chased it around the garden and didn't stop until she was too tired.
After she was done playing, Emma carefully picked up the newspaper and put it in her bedroom. It was her special new friend she wanted to keep for always.

Once upon a time, there was a little fish named Finn. Finn lived in the big ocean with his family. They liked to swim and play hide and seek. One day, Finn and his friends played a game with a hidden treasure.
Finn's friend, Sam the starfish, said, "Let's find the hidden treasure!" They all swam around, looking for it. They looked under rocks and behind plants. They even asked other fish if they had seen it.
But Finn swam too far and got lost. He could not see his friends or his family. He felt sad and scared. Then, he saw a big, friendly whale. The whale asked, "Why are you sad, little fish?"
Finn told the whale, "I am lost. I was playing with my friends and now I can't find them." The whale smiled and said, "Don't worry. I can help you find your friends and family."
The whale took Finn on his back and swam with him. They found Finn's friends and family. Everyone was happy to see Finn again. They thanked the big, friendly whale for helping their friend. From that day on, Finn never swam too far away from his friends and family.

One day, a pale boy named Tom wanted to sell his toys. He had many toys and he thought it would be fun to have a toy shop. Tom asked his mom if he could sell his toys outside their house. His mom said yes and helped him set up a little table.
Tom put all his toys on the table. He had cars, dolls, and balls. He made a sign that said "Toy Shop". Soon, kids came to see his toys. They liked the toys and wanted to buy them. Tom was happy to sell his toys and make new friends.
Tom's mom taught him math, so he could count the money he got from selling his toys. He learned how to add and take away. Tom was proud of his toy shop and his math skills. At the end of the day, he thanked his mom for helping him. They both smiled and hugged.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a man with a big brown mustache. He loved to play hide and seek with his cat, Tom.
One day, the man said, "Tom, let's play hide and seek!" Tom meowed and ran to hide. The man closed his eyes and counted to ten. Then, he opened his eyes and looked for Tom.
He saw a little tail peek from under the bed. The man laughed and said, "I see you, Tom!" Tom came out and meowed again. Now, it was the man's turn to hide.
The man hid behind the big chair. Tom looked and looked. He saw the brown mustache peek from behind the chair. Tom ran to the man and meowed. They both laughed and played hide and seek all day long.

Tom and Lily were playing with their toys in the living room. They had a lot of fun making noises and pretending to be animals. Tom had a toy car that he liked very much. He zoomed it around the floor and made it go fast.
"Look, Lily, my car is so fast!" Tom said.
Lily smiled and picked up her doll. She hugged it and said, "My doll is so pretty. She has a dress and a hat."
Tom and Lily were happy with their toys. But then, Tom's car hit the table leg and broke. The wheel came off and the car stopped moving. Tom was sad and cried.
"Oh no, my car is broken!" Tom said.
Lily saw Tom crying and felt sorry for him. She put down her doll and went to Tom. She took the wheel and the car and said, "Don't cry, Tom. I can fix it for you."
Lily tried to put the wheel back on the car. She pushed and twisted and turned, but it did not work. The wheel was too loose and fell off again. Lily was frustrated and said, "This is hard. I can't fix it."
Tom and Lily looked at the broken car and felt sad. They did not know what to do. They wished they could play with the car again.
Then, they heard a knock on the door. It was their dad. He came home from work and saw them sitting on the floor. He smiled and said, "Hello, Tom and Lily. What are you doing?"
Tom and Lily ran to their dad and hugged him. They showed him the broken car and said, "Dad, can you fix it? Please, please, please!"
Dad looked at the car and said, "Let me see. Hmm, I think I can fix it. But I need some tools. Do you want to help me?"
Tom and Lily nodded and said, "Yes, yes, yes!"
Dad took them to the garage and gave them some tools. He showed them how to use them and how to fix the car. He said, "This is a screwdriver. It can make things tight. This is a hammer. It can make things flat. This is a glue. It can make things stick."
Tom and Lily watched and learned. They helped Dad fix the car. They used the screwdriver, the hammer, and the glue. They worked hard and had fun.
Soon, the car was fixed. The wheel was on and the car could move. Tom and Lily were happy and cheered.
"Yay, we fixed it!" Tom said.
"Thank you, Dad!" Lily said.
Dad smiled and said, "You're welcome, Tom and Lily. You did a great job. You are very smart and brave. Do you want to play with the car now?"
Tom and Lily said, "Yes, yes, yes!"
They took the car and ran to the living room. They zoomed it around the floor and made it go fast. They laughed and had fun.
They were not sad anymore. They were happy. They loved their car. They loved their dad. They loved each other. They had a good day. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She loved to help her mom in the kitchen. Mia was very eager to learn how to cook. One day, her mom had an idea. She asked Mia if she wanted to serve dinner to the family.
Mia was so happy and said yes. Her mom showed her how to make a yummy salad with green leaves, red tomatoes, and orange carrots. Mia mixed it all up in a big bowl. Then, she put it on plates for everyone.
Mia carried the plates to the table and served the salad to her family. They all said, "Thank you, Mia!" and ate the salad. Mia felt proud and was excited to help her mom in the kitchen again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl. She was a daughter. She had a big, scary toy. The toy was in her room. She did not like the toy. It made her feel bad.
One day, the daughter told her mom about the scary toy. Her mom said, "Do not worry. I will help you." The mom took the toy away. The daughter felt happy.
Now, the daughter's room was not scary. She felt good. She played with her other toys. She loved her mom. They lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a mild cat named Mimi. Mimi loved to stretch in the sun. One day, Mimi went to the library with her friend, a dog named Dodo. They wanted to find a book to read.
In the library, Mimi and Dodo saw a big book on the top shelf. Mimi said, "I want to read that book!" Dodo tried to reach the book, but it was too high. Mimi had an idea. She said, "I will stretch and get the book!" Mimi stretched and stretched, and she got the book.
When they opened the book, a magic bird came out! The bird said, "Thank you for setting me free! I will give you one wish." Mimi and Dodo wished to help others. The bird granted their wish, and they became helpers in the library. From that day on, Mimi and Dodo helped other animals find books to read. The moral of the story is that when you help others, good things happen to you.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They saw a big maze made of bushes. They wanted to go inside and find the way out.
"Let's go, Lily!" Ben said. "It looks fun!"
"OK, Ben. But don't go too fast. I don't want to get lost," Lily said.
They went inside the maze. They turned left and right and left and right. They saw many flowers and birds and bugs. They heard a fountain and a bell. They had fun.
But then they felt cold. The sun was going down. The maze was getting dark.
"Ben, I'm cold. Can we go back?" Lily said.
"Sure, Lily. Let's go back. Do you remember the way?" Ben said.
Lily shook her head. She did not remember the way. She was scared.
"Don't worry, Lily. I remember the way. Just follow me," Ben said.
He took her hand and led her through the maze. He followed the fountain and the bell and the flowers and the birds and the bugs. He turned right and left and right and left.
Soon they saw the exit. They ran out of the maze. They saw their mom and dad waiting for them.
"Mom! Dad! We did it! We went through the maze!" Ben said.
"Good job, Ben! And good job, Lily! You followed your brother and stayed together," Mom said.
She hugged them and gave them warm jackets.
"Are you hungry? Let's go home and have some soup," Dad said.
They nodded and smiled. They were happy. They liked the maze. But they liked their mom and dad more.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom, dad, and her best friend, a dog named Max. Max was a reliable friend who always played with Lily and made her laugh.
One day, Lily and Max went to the park to play. They saw a man with a big box. The man said, "Hello, I have a special treat for you! It's a luxury ice cream." Lily and Max were so excited. They couldn't wait to try the ice cream.
As Lily reached for the ice cream, something unexpected happened. The ice cream started to freeze everything around it! Lily and Max were scared. They didn't know what to do. Then, Max had an idea. He barked and jumped on the ice cream, making it fall on the ground and stop freezing everything. Lily and Max were so happy that they saved the day. They learned that sometimes, even luxury things can be tricky, but they were glad to have each other as reliable friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little hanger that lived in a big closet. The hanger was not like the other hangers. It was wild and loved to have fun. One day, the hanger had a great idea. It wanted to roll all around the room.
The wild hanger jumped off the closet rod and started to roll. It rolled under the bed, around the chair, and even over the rug. The hanger was having so much fun, it did not want to stop.
But then, the hanger saw a toy car. It thought, "I can roll even faster with the car!" So, the wild hanger jumped onto the car and rolled all around the room, faster and faster. The little hanger was so happy, it laughed and laughed. And from that day on, the wild hanger and the toy car were the best of friends.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jane. She loved to explore outside on sunny days. One day she was walking in the park when she saw a lovely little umbrella. 
Jane said, "What a beautiful umbrella!" She picked it up and snapped it open and let it go. The umbrella glided in the air and Jane was so excited. 
A bird in the sky said, "That is a very nice umbrella you have." Jane smiled and said, "Yes I found it in the park. Will you help me release it?"
The bird flapped his wings and said, "Of course I will. Just hold it tight, say a final goodbye, and let it go!" 
Jane said goodbye to her little umbrella and released it. The umbrella flew up with the birds and away it went. Jane watched until it disappeared over the horizon and smiled. She was happy to have released her umbrella and enjoyed the beautiful moment.

Once upon a time, there was a family who had a big fireplace. They were very happy and strong. One day, the family wanted to light a fire in the fireplace. They took matches and put them on the logs. Then, they lit the match. The fire was beautiful and the family felt so warm. 
Suddenly, the fire got too big. It quickly spread to the walls and they couldn't stop it. The mother was scared and shouted, "Oh no! We should have been more careful!" The father replied, "Don't worry, I'll call the fireman!"
The fireman came and put out the fire. The family was relieved but also felt sad and regretful. They decided to be more careful next time and be strong. 
The family learned that it was important to be careful when near a fireplace. They were thankful the fire was put out and were happy to be safe.

One day, a team of friends went to the park. They liked to play together. The park was big and had lots of fun things to do. The friends were happy to be there.
In the park, there was an old tree. The tree was very big and tall. The friends liked to play near the old tree. They would run around it and hide behind it when they played games.
The team of friends played all day at the park. They had lots of fun together. When it was time to go home, they said goodbye to the old tree. They knew they would come back to play at the park again soon.

Once upon a time there was a modern mattress. It was in a special room. On one side there were two people who were part of the same family. They were getting ready for bed. 
The father said to the mother: "It's time to part with this mattress. It's too old for us to use."
The mother replied: "But I like our mattress. It's a part of our family!"
The father said: "No, we must get a modern one. We have to part with this one."
The mother was very sad and reluctantly they got rid of the mattress. They immediately regretted it and wished they had not parted with the old one.
The next morning the mother rushed to the old mattress room, but the mattress was gone. Her heart broke and she started to cry. The family had parted with their mattress and there was no turning back.

Once upon a time, there was a smooth rock. The smooth rock lived in a big park. The rock had a friend, a little bird.
One day, the bird found a thin film. The bird did not know what it was. The bird showed the film to the smooth rock. The rock said, "I will protect this film for you."
The bird was happy. The bird and the smooth rock played together in the park. They had lots of fun. The smooth rock always protected the film for the little bird. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a big fish named Bob saw a blackboard deep in the sea. Bob wanted to show his friend, Sam, the blackboard. So, Bob went to Sam's house. Sam was a small fish. Bob said, "Sam, come with me. I want to show you something."
Sam and Bob swam together. They saw the blackboard. Sam said, "Wow, it's a blackboard! What can we do with it?" Bob said, "We can draw and write on it." Sam and Bob decided to dive down and draw on the blackboard. They drew a tasty cake on it.
After drawing, they were hungry. They went to find a tasty cake to eat. They found a cake and shared it. Sam said, "This is the best day ever!" Bob agreed. They swam back to the blackboard and drew more pictures, happy and full.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. Jane had a toy bear that she loved very much. The bear was old and worn out. Jane's friend, Tom, had a new toy car. Jane felt envious of Tom's new toy.
One day, Jane asked her mom, "Can you restore my bear to make it new like Tom's toy car?" Her mom said, "I will try, but I can't promise it will be like new."
Jane's mom tried her best to fix the bear. She sewed it up and put new stuffing inside. Jane was happy to see her bear looking better. But, she still wished it was new like Tom's toy car.
The next day, Jane and Tom were playing outside. Jane wanted to touch Tom's toy car, but Tom said, "No, it's mine!" Jane felt sad and angry. She grabbed the toy car from Tom and threw it on the ground. The toy car broke into pieces.
Tom cried and Jane felt bad. She knew she should not have broken Tom's toy car. Now, both Jane and Tom were sad because their toys were not perfect. Jane learned that being envious and angry can lead to bad things.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a bit selfish. He did not like to share his toys with his friends. One day, Tim lost his favorite toy car. He was very sad.
Tim asked his friend, Sam, "Have you seen my car?" Sam said, "No, but I will help you find it." They looked for the car together. They looked under the bed, in the closet, and behind the door. But they could not find the car.
As they were looking, Tim saw Sam's toy truck. He realized that it was time to stop being selfish. He said, "Sam, I am sorry for not sharing my toys with you. Let's play with your truck together." They played and had fun. Tim learned that sharing was better than being selfish.

One day, a little girl named Lucy was in her room. She saw her mom enter with a thermometer. Lucy was sick and her mom wanted to see if she was getting better. Her mom said, "Let's check if you are still hot." Lucy opened her mouth and her mom put the thermometer in.
After a minute, Lucy's mom took the thermometer out. She looked at it and said, "Good news, Lucy! You are not hot anymore." Lucy felt grateful because she was tired of being sick. She wanted to play with her friends outside.
The next day, Lucy's friends came to play. They all had fun and played nicely together. Lucy remembered how grateful she was when her mom said she was not sick anymore. She decided to be kind and share her toys with her friends. Her friends were happy and they all learned that sharing makes everyone happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. She was playing one day in her garden, when she noticed something very strange. All of a sudden, Jane saw a big, grey hippo walking in front of her. She was so surprised! Jane ran inside to tell her parents.
When her parents heard the news, they were very curious. They all went outside to see the hippo. The hippo was bigger than they thought it would be and it was eating the flowers in the garden.
 
"We have to do something about this," said Jane's dad. He went to talk to the hippo and make it go away. After a few minutes, the hippo left the garden and Jane and her parents were very relieved.
That night, Jane had a strange realization. She realized that she had been brave and proud to take a stand against the hippo. She smiled and felt proud of herself. 
The next day, Jane and her parents told all their friends about what had happened. Everyone said that Jane was very brave and proud. Jane felt happy that she had realized her potential. 
The end.

One day, a big rhinoceros named Ron was feeling sad. He had a regret. He wished he had been more ready to play with his friend, Tom the turtle, the day before. Ron was too slow to say "yes" and Tom had gone to play with another friend.
Ron went to find Tom to say he was sorry. He found Tom near the pond. Ron said, "Tom, I am sorry. I was not ready to play with you yesterday. I regret not playing with you. Can we play today?"
Tom looked at Ron and smiled. "It's okay, Ron. I am happy to play with you today. Let's have fun together!" So Ron and Tom played all day. They played hide and seek and tag. They had lots of fun and laughed a lot.
From that day on, Ron always made sure he was ready to play with his friends. He never wanted to feel regret again. Ron and Tom became the best of friends and always had fun together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Olive. Olive lived with her mom, dad, and her dog, Spot. Olive liked to play in the park with her friends and eat ice cream.
One day, Olive and Spot went to the park. They saw a big tree with lots of leaves. Olive looked up and saw a small bird. The bird was trembling because it was cold. Olive said, "Oh, little bird, why are you trembling?" The bird replied, "I am cold and I miss my mom."
Olive wanted to help the bird. She picked up the bird and held it close to her. Olive said, "Don't worry, little bird. We will find your mom." Olive, Spot, and the bird walked around the park. They asked other animals if they had seen the bird's mom. Finally, they found her near a sour lemon tree.
The bird's mom was so happy to see her baby. She said, "Thank you, Olive, for helping my baby." Olive smiled and said, "You're welcome. I'm glad I could help." Olive, Spot, and the birds played in the park until it was time to go home.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat named Fluffy. Fluffy liked to play with a soft cloth. One day, Fluffy was playing with the cloth when a big dog named Rufus came by. Rufus wanted to play too, but Fluffy was scared.
"Please don't disturb me," said Fluffy to Rufus. Fluffy was very alert because she did not want Rufus to take her cloth. Rufus was sad, but he understood. He went away to find another friend to play with.
Later, Fluffy saw Rufus playing with a ball. She felt bad for not letting him play with her cloth. So, she went over to Rufus and said, "Let's play together!" Rufus was happy, and they played with the cloth and the ball. They became good friends and always played together.

Penny loves to read books. She has many books in her room. She likes to read about animals, and colors, and shapes. She reads every day before she sleeps.
One day, Penny sees a new book on the shelf. It is big and shiny. It has a picture of a castle on the cover. Penny wants to read it. She takes the book and goes to her bed.
But when she opens the book, she hears a voice. It is a loud and angry voice. It says, "Who are you? Why are you reading my book? This is my book, not yours!"
Penny is scared. She looks around. She sees a small man with a long beard and a pointy hat. He is sitting on the book. He is a nosy wizard. He does not like anyone to read his book.
"Please, Mr. Wizard, I am sorry. I did not know this was your book. I just wanted to read it. I like books. Can I read it with you?" Penny asks.
The wizard looks at Penny. He sees that she is a little girl. He sees that she is not mean. He sees that she likes books. He thinks for a moment. Then he says, "Well, maybe you can. But you have to be quiet and listen. This is a very special book. It has magic and secrets. Do you want to hear them?"
Penny nods. She is curious. She wants to hear the magic and secrets. She says, "Yes, please, Mr. Wizard. I want to hear them. Can we be friends?"
The wizard smiles. He says, "Yes, we can be friends. But only if you promise to keep my book safe. And to share it with no one else. Do you promise?"
Penny promises. She says, "I promise, Mr. Wizard. I will keep your book safe. And I will share it with no one else. Thank you for letting me read it with you."
The wizard says, "You are welcome, Penny. Now, let us begin. The first chapter is called 'The Dragon and the Princess'. Are you ready?"
Penny says, "I am ready, Mr. Wizard. Let us begin."
And so they begin to read the book together. And they have a lot of fun. And they learn a lot of things. And they become good friends. And Penny is happy. And the wizard is happy. And the book is happy. And they all live happily ever after. The end.

One day, a little bird was flying in the sky. The bird saw a helpful boy playing in the sand. He was trying to bury a box. The bird flew down to see the boy.
"Hi, can I help you?" asked the bird. The boy looked up and said, "Yes, I want to bury this box, but it is too hard." The bird used its beak to help dig a hole. Together, they buried the box in the sand.
Just then, a big wind came and blew the sand away. The box was not buried anymore! The boy and the bird looked at each other and laughed. They knew they had to try again. This time, they worked even harder, and the box stayed buried. They were happy they could help each other.

Once there was a little girl called Emma. She was three years old and was playing in the garden. It was a sunny summer's day and Emma was having a lot of fun.
Then, the sky started to turn dark. Emma felt a bit nervous. She asked her mum if she could come inside. Her mum said yes.
When they were inside, Emma looked out the window. She saw that it was very dark outside. She was feeling more and more nervous.
Then, Emma's mum turned on the lights and the room was filled with warm light. Emma was so happy and she wasn't scared anymore. 
She saw that the darkness was gone, and the sun was out again. Emma was so relieved, she couldn't help but smile.

Once upon a time, there was a zebra named Zara. Zara was a very happy zebra who lived in the big forest. One day, Zara heard a snap sound. She was scared and wanted to know what made the sound.
Zara walked and walked until she found a little bird. The little bird was crying. "Why are you crying?" asked Zara. The little bird said, "I heard a snap and now I can't find my mommy." Zara thought she could help the little bird find her mommy.
They looked and looked for the mommy bird. Then, they heard another snap! This time, they found the mommy bird by a broken tree branch. The mommy bird was grateful to see her baby. "Thank you, Zara!" said the mommy bird. Zara was happy to help and they all went home to their families.

Once upon a time there was a mommy bird who wanted to take her adorable little chick for a lovely flight.
The mommy bird held her chick in her wings and they flew up, up, up into the sky.
The little chick was so excited to explore the world around them.
"Look, look, Mommy! A big, wet puddle!" said the little chick as they flew down over the wide open fields.
The mommy bird looked down nervously at the puddle. She didn't want her little chick to get wet, but the chick seemed so excited about the possibility of playing in the puddle. She looked back at the chick with concern.
"No, dear, you can't play in the puddle. It's too deep and you might get hurt," the mommy bird said firmly.
The chick looked disappointed but soon after was off in the sky exploring again, with her mommy bird close behind.
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Sally. Sally loved to watch planes fly in the sky. She would sit and look up at the sky all day long.
One day, Sally saw a big plane land near her house. A nice man came out of the plane and said, "Hello, I need help to weigh my plane. Can you help me?" Sally was very excited and said, "Yes, I can help you!"
Sally and the nice man worked together to weigh the plane. It was very heavy! While they were weighing the plane, they found a big bag of candy inside. The nice man said, "This candy is a gift for you, Sally, for helping me." Sally was so happy and shared the candy with all her friends.
The unexpected thing was that the nice man was a pilot who wanted to teach kids about planes. Sally learned that helping others can bring sweet surprises and make new friends. And that was the moral of the story.

Lily and Ben are friends. They like to play and watch movies together. One day, they hear a loud noise from the next house. It sounds like a big roar.
"What is that?" Lily asks.
"Maybe it is a lion," Ben says.
They are curious and nosy. They want to see the lion. They sneak to the fence and peek over. They see a big screen and a projector. The next house is watching a movie. The movie has a lion and other animals.
"Wow, look at that!" Ben says.
"Can we watch too?" Lily asks.
They try to climb the fence, but it is too high. They hear a voice from the next house.
"Hey, kids, what are you doing?" the voice says.
They look and see a man. He is the owner of the next house. He is not angry. He is smiling.
"We want to watch your movie," Lily says.
"It is a movie about the jungle. Do you like the jungle?" the man asks.
"Yes, we do," Ben says.
"Well, why don't you come over and join us? We have popcorn and juice," the man says.
"Really? Thank you!" Lily and Ben say.
They run to the gate and the man lets them in. They sit on the grass and watch the movie. They see the lion and other animals. They hear the roar and the music. They eat the popcorn and drink the juice. They are happy and grateful. They make a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a big cat. The cat was very lazy. He liked to sleep all day. One day, the cat saw a small bug. The bug walked in a zigzag way. The cat wanted to play with the bug.
The cat tried to catch the bug, but the bug was too fast. The cat got very tired. The cat was not happy. He thought the bug was not nice. The cat wanted to be friends with the bug, but he was mad.
Then, the cat saw the bug was sad. The bug said, "I am sorry, I did not want to make you mad." The cat thought about it. He knew it was good to forgive. So, the cat said, "I forgive you, bug. Let's be friends." They played together and had fun. The cat learned that even if someone makes you mad, it is good to forgive and be friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was very lonely because he had no friends to play with. He would walk around the park, hoping to find someone to play with him.
One sunny day, Spot saw a girl named Lily at the park. Lily was playing with a ball. Spot recognized the ball and wanted to play with it too. He ran up to Lily and wagged his tail. Lily saw Spot and smiled. She threw the ball, and Spot ran to catch it. They played together and had lots of fun.
Lily and Spot became best friends. They played together every day. Spot was not lonely anymore. He was happy because he had a friend. Lily was happy too, because she had a friend in Spot. They both knew that their friendship would last forever.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a book. It was a big book, but he did not know it was a novel. Tim was very happy and wanted to read the book. He sat down and started to read. But the words were too big for him. Tim felt foolish because he could not read the big words.
Tim went to his mom and started to complain. "Mom, I can't read this book. The words are too big," he said. His mom smiled and said, "Don't worry, Tim. I will help you read the book." Tim felt better and sat with his mom to read the book.
As they read the book, something unexpected happened. The big words turned into small words that Tim could read. Tim and his mom were surprised. They laughed and continued to read the book together. The novel was full of fun stories, and Tim learned that even if something seems hard, he can always ask for help.

Once upon a time there was a small girl who liked to explore the world. One day she went on an adventure and saw an attractive road. She was so excited to find this special road and she wanted to go down it.
She asked her mom, "Mom, can I go down the road?" "Yes, but be careful and come back soon," her mom replied.
The little girl ran onto the road and as she went further and further, she noticed more and more beautiful things. She wanted to explore and see more of the road but it began to get dark outside.
When she turned around, she saw her mom waiting at the end of the road. Her mom said, "Close, it's time to go home now."
The little girl was sad to leave the road but she felt happy that she had experienced such a beautiful adventure. She knew that she would remember the attractive road forever.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toy automobiles. He had a red one, a blue one, and a yellow one. Tim would drive them all around his room, making car noises as he played.
One day, Tim's friend, Sally, came over to play. Sally liked the yellow automobile the most. She asked Tim, "Can I play with the yellow one, please?" Tim said, "Yes, but you must promise to be careful." Sally promised and they played together.
As they were playing, Sally accidentally broke the yellow automobile. Sally felt very sorry. She said to Tim, "I am so sorry, Tim. I didn't mean to break your toy." Tim was sad, but he knew Sally didn't mean it. He said, "It's okay, Sally. I know you didn't mean to. We can still play with the other automobiles." They continued to play and had a great time together.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot liked to study new things. He would look at them with his big eyes and sniff them with his wet nose. One day, Spot found a big, smelly sock in the yard.
Spot picked up the smelly sock with his mouth. He carried it around the yard, showing it to his friends. The other dogs did not like the smelly sock. They said, "Yuck! That is smelly! Put it down!" But Spot liked the smelly sock and did not want to let it go.
In the end, Spot's mom came outside and saw the smelly sock. She took it away from him and threw it in the trash. Spot was sad, but he knew he would find something new to study tomorrow. And maybe, just maybe, it would not be so smelly.

One day, a little bird named Tim was very busy. He had to fly far away to find food for his family. The sun was not up yet, but Tim knew he had to go early. "I will be back soon," he told his family.
As Tim flew, the sky started to change. The sunrise was coming, and the sky was full of pretty colors. Tim knew he had to be fast to find food before the other birds arrived. He saw a big tree with lots of berries. "This is perfect," he said.
Tim picked many berries and put them in his bag. He was ready to go back home. Suddenly, he heard a loud noise. A big bird arrived at the tree. "You found my berries!" said the big bird. Tim was scared, but he remembered the pretty colors of the sunrise. He knew he had to be brave. "We can share," Tim said. The big bird agreed, and they both went home with full bags.

One day, a girl named Mia and a boy named Tom went to play outside. They found a big, thick door with a lock on it. They wanted to know what was behind the door, so they decided to discuss how to open it.
While they were talking, a cat came by and saw the lock. The cat jumped up and touched the lock with its paw. Suddenly, the lock opened, and the door swung open. Mia and Tom were very surprised.
Behind the door, they found a big room with lots of toys and games. They played and had fun all day. At the end of the day, they thanked the cat for helping them find the room. The cat just smiled and walked away, as if it knew the secret all along.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a girl named Lucy. She loved to paint. One day, Lucy decided to paint her favorite thing, a delicate flower.
As she was painting, her cat, Whiskers, came into the room. Whiskers was thirsty and wanted milk. "Meow," said Whiskers. Lucy heard the meow and knew what Whiskers wanted. "I will get you some milk," she told her cat.
Lucy went to the kitchen and got a bowl of milk for Whiskers. She put the bowl on the floor, and Whiskers happily drank the milk. Lucy smiled and went back to her painting. She finished her delicate flower and showed it to Whiskers. They both felt happy and had a great day.

Once upon a time, there was a yellow animal. The animal was a big bird. The bird had a bell that would ring. The bird liked to ring the bell. The bird lived in a big tree.
One day, the bird met a small cat. The cat said, "Hi, bird! I like your bell. Can I ring it?" The bird said, "No, cat. You cannot ring my bell. It is mine." The cat was sad.
The cat went away and found a big rock. The cat threw the rock at the bird's tree. The tree fell down. The bird and the bell fell too. The bird was hurt. The bell did not ring anymore. The cat was still sad.

One day, a charming fox went for a walk. The fox saw a big tree. He had a question. "Is there something in the tree?" he asked himself.
The fox looked up and saw a big bird. The big bird asked the fox, "Do you want to play with me?" The fox was happy and said "Yes!"
But the big bird was not just a bird. It was a magic bird! The magic bird changed the fox into a small bird. The charming fox was surprised but happy. Now, the charming fox and the magic bird played together in the sky.

One day, a boy named Tim went on a search. He was looking for something colorful. He walked and walked until he saw a big tree. Under the tree, he found a colorful box. Tim was very happy.
"Wow! What is in the box?" Tim said. He opened the box and found a colorful hat. He put the hat on his head and started to dance. "I love my new hat," Tim said with a big smile.
Just then, the hat started to talk! "Hello, Tim!" said the hat. Tim was very surprised. "I am a magic hat. I can help you find more colorful things!" The hat and Tim went on many fun searches together, finding more colorful things and making new friends.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went to the beach. She saw many pretty shells on the sand. She picked up a big, pretty shell. Lucy felt very lucky.
Lucy put the shell close to her ear. She heard a soft ring sound. The sound made Lucy feel happy. She wanted to show her new shell to her best friend, Tom.
Lucy ran to Tom and let him listen to the shell. Tom heard the ring sound too. They both felt lucky and happy. Lucy and Tom played with the shell all day at the beach.

Once upon a time, there was a restless little dog named Max. Max liked to run and play all day. One day, Max saw a big hole in the ground. He was very curious and wanted to see what was inside.
Max went to the hole and decided to enter. He jumped and fell inside. Max was excited to see what was down there. But when he looked around, he saw that it was very dark and he could not see anything. Max started to feel scared.
Then, something unexpected happened. Max heard a loud noise and the hole closed up. Max tried to jump and get out, but he could not. Max was stuck in the hole and no one knew where he was. Max was very sad and wished he had never entered the hole.

Tom and Mia like to play with flags. They have many flags of different colors and shapes. They wave them in the air and pretend to be soldiers or pirates or kings.
One day, they go to the park with their flags. They see a big pond with ducks and fish. They want to play near the pond. They put their flags on the ground and run to the water.
But they do not see a big dog behind them. The dog is hungry and bored. He sees the flags and thinks they are toys. He runs to the flags and grabs them with his teeth. He tears them and chews them and makes them wet and dirty.
Tom and Mia hear the dog and turn around. They see their flags ruined by the dog. They are very sad and angry. They cry and shout at the dog. But the dog does not care. He runs away with some of the flags in his mouth.
Tom and Mia have no flags left to play with. They have to go home with their wet and dirty clothes. They do not have fun at the park. They have a bad day.

Once upon a time, there was a wise old bird who lived in a tree. The wise old bird had a friend, a small cube. The cube could move and talk, and they loved to play together.
One sunny day, the wise old bird and the cube were playing in the park. They were having lots of fun, when suddenly, the cube stopped moving. The wise old bird tried to help the cube move again, but it just wouldn't work.
Just then, a little girl came to the park. She saw the wise old bird and the cube and wanted to play too. The little girl picked up the cube and gave it a hug. To everyone's surprise, the cube started to move again! The wise old bird and the little girl were so happy. They all played together in the park, and they became the best of friends.

One day, a cheerful girl named Lily wanted to have a picnic. She asked her mom for help. Her mom said, "Yes, let's have a picnic in the park."
Lily and her mom went to the park with a big basket of food. They put a soft blanket on the grass. They had yummy sandwiches, sweet fruits, and tasty juice. Lily was very happy.
But then, a big wind came and blew the food away. Lily was sad. Her mom said, "Let's solve this problem together." They found a big tree to block the wind. They put the food back on the blanket and enjoyed their picnic.

One day, a little girl named Lucy found a big, fake bubble. She wanted to play with it, but it did not fit in her toy box. She tried to push it in, but it was too big. Lucy felt sad and did not know what to do.
Lucy's friend, Tom, came over to play. He saw the big, fake bubble and said, "Wow! That's a big bubble! Let's play with it outside!" Lucy smiled and nodded. They took the bubble outside and had lots of fun.
As they played, Lucy's mom came out and said, "I have an idea! We can keep the bubble in the garage. It will fit there." Lucy and Tom were happy. They put the bubble in the garage and played with it whenever they wanted.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to drive his toy car. He had a goal to make it go very fast. Tim was very excited and wild about his car.
Tim said to his mom, "Mom, can I drive my car outside?" His mom smiled and said, "Yes, Tim. Let's go outside and play with your car."
They went outside and Tim started to drive his car. It went so fast, it made him laugh. His mom watched and clapped her hands. Tim reached his goal and felt very happy. They played outside until it was time to go inside for dinner.

Anna likes to sign. She signs with her hands and her face. She signs hello, thank you, and I love you. She signs to her mom, her dad, and her cat. She signs when she is happy, sad, or angry.
One day, Anna goes to the park with her mom. She sees a group of children. They are dancing. They are wearing colorful clothes. They have ribbons, hats, and bells. They look fun and friendly.
Anna wants to join them. She signs to her mom, "Can I dance with them?" Her mom nods and smiles. She says, "Yes, you can. Go ahead and have fun."
Anna runs to the group. She waves her hand and signs hello. The children stop dancing and look at her. They are curious and confused. They do not understand her signs.
One of the children, a boy with a red hat, says, "Hi, what is your name?" Anna signs her name with her fingers. The boy says, "What are you doing with your hands? Are you playing a game?"
Anna shakes her head and signs, "No, this is how I talk. I sign. I cannot hear. I am deaf."
The children are surprised and interested. They want to learn how to sign. They ask Anna to teach them. Anna is happy and excited. She teaches them some signs. They learn how to say their names, how to count, and how to say some animals.
Then, they teach Anna how to dance. They show her how to move her feet, how to swing her arms, and how to follow the music. They give her a ribbon, a hat, and a bell. They dance together. They laugh and smile.
Anna and the children become friends. They sign and dance. They share their colorful clothes. They have fun and learn from each other. Anna's mom watches them and feels proud and happy. She signs to Anna, "I love you." Anna signs back, "I love you too."

Once upon a time, there was a modest house. In this house, there was a little room called an attic. The attic was up high and had many fun things inside.
One day, a boy named Tim went to play in the attic. He found a big box with toys and a funny hat. Tim put on the hat and played with the toys. He had so much fun!
When it was time to go, Tim remembered to close the attic door. He went downstairs to tell his mom about the fun he had. They both smiled and went to eat dinner.

Once upon a time, there was an enormous frog named Fred. He had a big pipe that he liked to play with. One day, Fred saw a little bug near his pipe. The bug was trying to crawl up the pipe, but it was too hard.
Fred said, "Hello, little bug! Do you need help with my pipe?" The bug looked at Fred and said, "Yes, please! I want to see what's up there."
So, Fred managed to help the little bug by lifting it onto his pipe. The bug was very happy and said, "Thank you, Fred! You are very kind."
Together, they played with the pipe all day long. They had a lot of fun and became the best of friends. From that day on, Fred and the little bug always played together and helped each other when they needed it.

Once upon a time, there was a fun game where everyone wanted to win. They were competitive. The game was called "Spin the Passport." All the kids in the town came to play.
In the game, they had to spin a passport on a table. When the passport stopped spinning, the person it pointed to had to do a silly dance. Everyone laughed and clapped while the person danced.
One day, a new kid came to play. He was shy but wanted to join in the fun. He spun the passport, and it pointed at him. He did a funny dance, and everyone laughed. The new kid felt happy and made many friends. They all played "Spin the Passport" together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom lived in a small house with his mom and dad. One day, Tom saw a wide, empty box in his room. He was very happy to find it. Tom had an idea.
Tom called his best friend, Sam. "Sam, I found a wide, empty box! Let's play with it!" Sam came to Tom's house very fast. They both looked at the box and smiled. They knew they could have lots of fun.
Tom and Sam put the wide, empty box in the middle of the room. They pretended it was a big car. They took turns driving the car, making "vroom vroom" sounds. They laughed and played all day long.

Lily and Max were playing with their toys in the living room. Lily had a doll and Max had a car. They were having fun until Max saw a nail on the floor. He picked it up and said, "Look, Lily, a nail! I can make a hole in your doll with this!"
Lily was scared and angry. She loved her doll and did not want Max to hurt it. She said, "No, Max, don't do that! That is mean and bad! I hate you!"
Max was confused. He did not understand why Lily was so upset. He thought it was a funny joke. He said, "Why do you hate me? It is just a nail. It is not a big deal. I was only playing."
Lily did not listen to Max. She grabbed her doll and ran to her mom. She cried and told her what Max did. Her mom hugged her and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. Max was wrong to do that. He should not play with nails. They are dangerous and can hurt you and your toys. I will talk to him and make him say sorry."
Lily felt better and said, "Thank you, mom. I love you." She looked at her doll and said, "I love you, too, doll. You are my best friend." She hoped Max would learn his lesson and be nice to her and her doll.

Once upon a time there was a brilliant spirit. This spirit could make things happen in a special way.
One morning, the spirit wanted to try something new. It decided to reverse everything it had done. So it waved its wand and made magic!
Suddenly, everything in the garden was different. Butterflies were flying upside down, the sun was shining in the night and there were even stars during the day.
The spirit was very happy with what it had done. It felt proud of its brilliant work.
The next day, the spirit used its wand to reverse everything back again. The garden was normal again and the spirit was pleased.
 
That spirit still lives in the garden and sometimes it will use its special powers to make reverse magic.

Once upon a time, there was a little rat. He was a glad rat. He liked to play with his friends. One day, his friends said, "Let's play hide and seek!"
The little rat found a good place to hide. He hid in a box. He was very quiet. He could hear his friends looking for him. They could not find him. The little rat was so glad that he had found a good hiding spot.
After a while, the little rat heard his friends say, "We give up! Come out, little rat!" But the little rat did not want to come out. He wanted to remain in the box. He was having so much fun hiding. His friends looked and looked for him. They finally found the box where the little rat was hiding. They all laughed and were glad to find him. The little rat was glad too. They all played together again, and they all had a great time.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big, round eye that could see everything. Lily loved to play with her best friend, Tom, a cute, fuzzy cat. They played together every day.
One day, Tom did not know where to find his favorite toy. He looked everywhere but could not find it. Feeling sad, Tom asked Lily, "Can you help me find my toy?" Lily, with her big eye, promised to help Tom look for it.
They looked all around the house, and finally, Lily saw the toy under the table. Tom was so happy and thanked Lily. He said, "You are the best friend ever! I promise to help you if you ever need it." From that day on, Lily and Tom learned the importance of helping each other and were never ignorant about their friendship.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sally. Sally was a very modest girl and always tried to behave herself. One day, Sally went to the park with her mom. 
At the park, Sally saw some children playing and eating. She noticed that they were sharing a bag of crunchy peanuts. 
"Mom! Can I have a peanut please?" asked Sally eagerly. 
"Maybe, but first you have to behave before I'll let you have one," said her mom. 
Sally nodded, understanding what her mom said. She then proceeded to play nicely with the other kids and shared her toys with them. 
Her mom smiled approvingly and gave her a peanut. Sally was so happy, she almost couldn't contain her excitement! 
From that day on, Sally made sure to always behave and be a modest girl. She knew that if she behaved well, then she would get her reward.

Once upon a time, in a little town, there was a kind girl named Lily. She liked to help her friends and always said "please" and "thank you". Everyone thought Lily was very polite.
One day, Lily had to choose a toy to play with. She looked at her toys and thought hard. She picked her favorite doll, Daisy. Lily and Daisy played together all day long.
In the town, there was a law that everyone must be nice to each other. Lily liked this law a lot because it made her town a happy place. Everyone followed the law and smiled at each other. And they all lived happily ever after.

Lily and Tom were friends. They liked to play with dolls and cars. One day, they found a big box in the park. They decided to make a house with the box.
"This is our house," Lily said. "We can live here and be happy."
"OK," Tom said. "But can we have a marriage first?"
"A marriage?" Lily asked. "What is that?"
"It is when two people love each other and wear nice clothes and have a cake," Tom said. "My mom and dad had a marriage. It was fun."
Lily thought for a moment. She liked cake, but she did not like nice clothes. They were too tight and itchy. She liked to wear her old jeans and T-shirt.
"I don't want a marriage," Lily said. "I just want to play."
Tom was sad. He wanted a marriage with Lily. He thought she was pretty and smart. He had a ring for her. It was a thin ring made of paper. He had colored it with crayons.
"Please, Lily," Tom said. "Please have a marriage with me. I have a ring for you. See?"
He showed her the ring. Lily looked at it. She did not think it was a nice ring. It was thin and wrinkled. It did not sparkle like her mom's ring.
"No, thank you," Lily said. "I don't like your ring. It is ugly."
She ignored Tom and went inside the box. She closed the flap and pretended it was a door. She did not want to talk to Tom anymore.
Tom felt hurt. He did not understand why Lily did not like his ring. He thought it was beautiful. He had made it with love. He did not want to play with Lily anymore. He took his ring and his cars and left the park. He did not look back.

Once upon a time, there was a gray machine. It lived in a big room with many other machines. The gray machine was always very worry. It did not like to talk to the other machines.
One day, a little girl came into the room. She saw the gray machine and said, "Why are you so worry, gray machine?" The gray machine was scared to talk, but it said, "I am worry because I do not know what I can do."
The little girl thought for a moment. Then she had an idea. "Let's find out what you can do!" she said. They tried many things, but nothing seemed to work. The gray machine was still worry.
Then, something unexpected happened. The little girl accidentally pushed a button on the gray machine. It started to make a beautiful song! The gray machine was so happy. It could make music! The little girl and the gray machine danced and laughed together. The gray machine was not worry anymore.

One day, Lucy was looking for something to play with. She spotted a cute magnet in the kitchen, and quickly grabbed it. She ran outside to the garden with her magnet and began having a great time. 
But, suddenly the magnet flew away! With a gasp, Lucy took off after it. She chased it around the garden, over the fence and down the street.
The magnet stopped under a big tree. Lucy ran over, but it disappeared into a nearby bush. Lucy peeked in to try to find it, and soon heard a small voice.
"Please help me!" cried a tiny voice.
Looking down, Lucy saw a small mouse trapped in the bush. She quickly grabbed the mouse and set it free. 
"Thank you," said the mouse. "That magnet was really just too cute for me to resist!" 
Lucy smiled and said, "No problem!" But, she couldn't help but regret taking the magnet without asking first. 
The mouse seemed to understand, and said with a smile, "Sometimes these things happen. Don't worry about it."
Before Lucy said goodbye, the mouse even gave her a new magnet to take home. 
Lucy thanked him before running back to the garden with her new magnet. She smiled as she remembered how she was able to help the mouse, and vowed never to take things without asking again.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to her room to pick a dress. She saw a pretty dress on a hanger. Lily wanted to wear it. But, the dress was in a locked box. She had to unlock the box to get the dress.
Lily's friend, Tom, came over to play. Tom saw the pretty dress on the hanger. He felt envious. He wanted to help Lily unlock the box. They both looked for the key to open it.
They found the key under Lily's bed. Lily and Tom unlocked the box. Lily took the pretty dress from the hanger and put it on. Tom smiled. They were both happy and played together all day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a magic toy. This toy was very special and original. It could talk and play with Tim.
One day, Tim and his magic toy were playing outside. The toy said, "Tim, I urge you to try something new today." Tim thought for a moment and said, "Okay, let's make a big sand castle."
So, Tim and his magic toy started making the sand castle. They worked hard and made a big, beautiful castle. Tim was very happy.
In the end, Tim and his magic toy sat down and looked at their big sand castle. They were very proud of what they did. They played together every day and always tried new things.

Once upon a time, there was a powerful king who liked to protect his people. He had a big stage in the middle of his land where everyone could see. One day, the king decided to have a big show on the stage for all the people to watch and have fun.
The king asked a funny man to come and make everyone laugh on the stage. The funny man said, "Okay, king. I will make everyone laugh and have a good time." The king was happy and told everyone to come and watch the show.
But, the funny man had a secret plan. He wanted to be the powerful king and protect the people too. So, during the show, he played a trick on the king. The king fell down and could not get up. The people were sad and scared. The funny man became the new king, but he was not a good king like the first one. The people missed the first king who was powerful and protected them.

One day, in a small town, there was a big square. In the middle of the square, there was a box. The box was locked. All the kids in the town wanted to unlock the box, but no one knew how.
One day, a new boy came to the town. His name was Tim. Tim saw the box and asked, "What's inside the box?" The other kids said, "We don't know. We can't unlock it." Tim wanted to help, so he looked for the key.
Tim looked and looked for the key. He asked his new friends to help him. They all looked together. They found the key under a big rock. They were all very happy.
Tim and his friends went to the square. They used the key to unlock the box. When they opened it, they found it was empty! They were all very sad. But then, Tim said, "It's okay. We had fun looking for the key together."
The kids learned that sometimes, it's not about what you find, but the fun you have with your friends. And from that day on, they all played happily in the square.

One day, a little pirate named Tim went on a wild adventure. He had a big hat and a pet bird. They went to find a treasure box. Tim and his bird were very happy.
As they walked, Tim said, "We will find the treasure and be rich!" But then, Tim lost his map. He looked everywhere. His bird said, "Where is the map, Tim?" Tim was sad. They could not find the treasure without the map.
Just when they were about to give up, a big, friendly dog came running with the map in its mouth. The dog had found the map! Tim and his bird were so happy. They thanked the dog, and together, they found the treasure. The wild adventure had a happy end.

Rudy was very scared. He was standing underneath the roof and all he wanted to do was to go home. He looked up at the roof, hoping that he would find a way out.
Suddenly, he saw something. A small red box on top of the roof. In the box was a bright yellow liquid. Rudy wanted to know what it was, so he reached up and took a sip.
It tasted sweet and delicious. He couldn't believe it! He had found a secret drink on top of the roof.
He was so happy that he drank it all up. When he finished, he felt brave and strong. He knew he could find his way home now. With a smile, he started walking back towards home.

Once upon a time, there was a little mole who lived in a hole. He had a big, fair garden with many pretty flowers. The mole loved his garden very much and took good care of it.
One day, a big bird came to the garden. The bird wanted to spoil the flowers. The mole was very sad and asked the bird, "Why do you want to spoil my garden?" The bird did not say anything and just flew away.
The next day, the mole saw the bird again. But this time, the bird was not trying to spoil the garden. The bird was bringing water to help the flowers grow. The mole was happy and surprised. They became friends and took care of the garden together. The moral of the story is, don't judge someone too quickly.

One day, a group of animal friends were playing in the park. There was a big cat, a little dog, and a happy bird. They liked to play together, but sometimes they would get frustrated when they couldn't do the same things.
The cat had an idea to unite and use their will to make a big fun game that they could all play. They all wanted to help make the game, so they started to think of things they could do together. The cat was good at jumping, the dog was good at running, and the bird was good at flying.
So, they made a game where they would take turns doing what they were good at. The cat would jump, the dog would run, and the bird would fly. They all played the game and had lots of fun. They were very happy that they united and used their will to make a game that they could all enjoy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big cardboard box. He loved to play with his box. One day, he decided to make a successful fort with it.
Tim worked hard to build his fort. He cut and taped the cardboard. Soon, the fort was ready. Tim was very happy. He called his friend, Sam, to come and see his fort. "Wow, Tim! Your fort is great!" said Sam.
It was a hot day. The sun was shining bright. Tim and Sam played in the fort all day long. But soon, they felt very hot. "I wish we had ice cream," said Tim. Sam had an idea. They went inside and got some ice cream. They ate it quickly before it could melt. The two friends had a fun day in their successful cardboard fort.

Once upon a time, there was a big, unusual rose. The rose was red and pretty. It lived in a small garden. The rose was always happy and never disturbed.
One sunny day, a little girl came to the garden. She saw the unusual rose and wanted to touch it. The rose was scared and did not want to be disturbed. The girl was sad and walked away.
The next day, the girl came back. She brought a small pot and some dirt. She put the unusual rose in the pot very carefully. The rose was not disturbed. They went home together and became the best friends.

Anna loved to sing. She sang when she woke up, when she played, and when she went to bed. She sang songs about flowers, stars, and animals. She sang so loud that everyone could hear her.
One night, there was a big storm. Anna heard the rain and the wind outside her window. She saw the lightning flash in the sky. She was scared. She hid under her blanket and stopped singing.
Her mom came to her room. She saw Anna shaking and crying. She hugged her and said, "Don't be afraid, my sweetie. The storm will go away soon. The lightning is just a powerful light. It can't hurt you."
Anna looked at her mom. She said, "But it's so loud and bright. It makes me feel small and weak."
Her mom smiled and said, "You are not small and weak. You are brave and strong. You have a powerful voice. You can sing louder than the storm. Sing with me, Anna. Sing your favorite song."
Anna nodded and wiped her tears. She and her mom started to sing. They sang about flowers, stars, and animals. They sang so loud that the storm could hear them. The storm was surprised and impressed. It decided to be quiet and let them sing. The rain and the wind stopped. The lightning flashed one last time and said goodbye.
Anna and her mom looked out the window. They saw the moon and the stars shining. They smiled and hugged each other. They sang one more song and then went to sleep. Anna was not scared anymore. She was happy. She loved to sing.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Tiny. Tiny was a fierce dog, but he was only an inch tall. He lived in a big garden with his family.
One day, Tiny found a hurt bird in the garden. The bird could not fly. Tiny wanted to help the bird heal. He ran to his family and told them about the hurt bird. They all decided to help the bird get better.
With love and care, Tiny and his family helped the bird heal. After some days, the bird could fly again. The bird was so happy and thanked Tiny and his family. They all became good friends. The moral of the story is that even the smallest act of kindness can make a big difference in someone's life.

Once upon a time, there was a little caterpillar. He was different from the other caterpillars. He liked to ride on leaves.
One day, the caterpillar went to ride on a big leaf. He saw a big tree with many leaves. The caterpillar was very happy.
He climbed up the tree to find the best leaf to ride. He found a big green leaf and started to ride on it. The wind blew and the leaf moved. The caterpillar had so much fun.
Later, the caterpillar felt tired. He went back to his home under the tree. He told his caterpillar friends about his fun ride. They all wanted to try it too. And they all had fun riding on leaves together.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big cat named Tom. Tom was very frustrated because he couldn't catch the little mouse, Jerry. Tom believed that he could catch Jerry if he tried hard enough.
One day, Tom said to Jerry, "I will catch you, little mouse!" Jerry laughed and said, "You can try, big cat, but you won't catch me!" Tom tried and tried to catch Jerry, but he always got away.
The people in the town saw Tom and Jerry running around. They talked to the judge about the problem. The judge said, "Tom, if you don't stop chasing Jerry, you must leave the town!" Tom didn't listen and kept chasing Jerry.
One day, Tom finally caught Jerry. But the people in the town were angry. They told Tom he had to leave the town. Tom was sad because he lost his home. The moral of the story is to listen to others and not let your frustration make you do bad things.

Ben and Lily were playing hide and seek in the house. Ben was the seeker and Lily was the hider. Lily ran to the bedroom and looked for a good spot. She saw a big closet with a shiny handle. She opened the door and went inside. She closed the door behind her and smiled. She thought Ben would never find her there.
Ben counted to ten and then shouted, "Ready or not, here I come!" He searched the living room, the kitchen, and the bathroom, but he could not find Lily. He was confused. Where could she be? He scratched his head and thought hard. He remembered that there was one more room he had not checked. He ran to the bedroom and opened the door.
He looked around and saw the bed, the dresser, the lamp, and the closet. He walked to the closet and grabbed the handle. He pulled the door open and saw Lily sitting on the floor. She was holding a teddy bear and a book. She looked surprised and scared.
"Boo!" Ben said and laughed. "I found you, Lily! You are a good hider, but not good enough for me!"
Lily dropped the teddy bear and the book and hugged Ben. She was happy and relieved. "You scared me, Ben! You are a good seeker, but you are also a good brother!"
They giggled and hugged each other. They decided to play another round, but this time they would switch roles. Ben would hide and Lily would seek. They ran out of the closet and closed the door behind them. They had fun playing hide and seek in the house.

Once there was an old lady who had a pet cat. The old lady loved her pet cat very much. One day, when the old lady went for her daily walk, she saw that the cat was dead and lying by her knee. The old lady was very sad. She took the dead cat and held it in her arms. Then she closed her eyes and hugged the cat tight. She said goodbye to her pet cat and then she put it down and walked away. The old lady was very sad. She had lost her pet cat. She closed her eyes and remembered the happy times that she and her pet cat had together.

Once upon a time, there was a humble girl named Jane. She had a purse that she loved very much. One day, she went for a walk in the park with her mom.
In the park, Jane saw a big tree. She told her mom, "I want to play near the tree." Her mom said, "Okay, but don't lose your purse." Jane played and played, but she forgot about her purse.
When it was time to go home, Jane could not find her purse. She felt very sad. Her mom said, "Let's wrap your purse on your arm next time, so you won't lose it."
The next day, they went back to the park. Jane found her purse near the big tree. She was so happy! From that day on, Jane always wrapped her purse on her arm, and she never lost it again.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there were two friends, Tom and Tim. They loved to play and have fun together. They were amazing friends, always ready to help each other.
One day, they met a wise old man who wanted to teach them something important. "When friends unite, they can do anything," he said. Tom and Tim looked at each other and smiled. They knew they were strong when they were together.
One day, while playing near the river, Tom fell into the water. Tim was scared, but he remembered the old man's words. He called all their friends to help. They all united and made a long chain to pull Tom out of the water. They saved Tom and everyone was happy.
Tom and Tim learned that when friends unite, they can do great things. They promised to always help each other and their friends. And they lived happily ever after.
The moral of the story is that unity is strength. When we work together, we can overcome any challenge.

Lily and Tom are playing hide and seek in the garden. Lily is the seeker and Tom is the hider. Lily counts to ten and then opens her eyes. She looks around and tries to find Tom.
"Where are you, Tom?" she calls out. "I will find you!"
Tom is hiding behind a big post. He is very quiet and still. He hopes Lily will not see him. He likes this game. He thinks he is good at hiding.
Lily walks around the garden. She looks behind the trees, under the bench, and in the shed. She does not see Tom. She is alert and listens for any sound. She hears a bird singing, a dog barking, and a car honking.
"Tom, are you here?" she asks. "Are you hiding from me?"
Tom hears Lily's voice. He wants to answer, but he knows that will give him away. He stays silent and holds his breath. He thinks Lily will not find him.
Lily is getting tired of looking. She wonders where Tom is. She thinks he is hiding very well. She decides to try one more place. She walks towards the big post. She sees a flash of red behind it. She smiles and runs to the post.
"Found you, Tom!" she shouts. "I see your red shirt!"
Tom jumps out from behind the post. He is surprised and happy. He laughs and hugs Lily.
"You are a good seeker, Lily!" he says. "You found me!"
Lily and Tom are both happy. They like playing hide and seek. They decide to play again. This time, Tom is the seeker and Lily is the hider. They have fun in the garden.

Once upon a time, there was a modest cow named Bessie. She lived on a farm with many friends. They all liked Bessie because she was nice and always ready to help.
One day, Bessie found a big box near the farm. She did not know what was inside, but she thought it could be something fun. She asked her friends to help her open it. They all tried, but could not open the box. Bessie did not give up. She knew she had to accept the challenge and find a way to open it.
Then, something unexpected happened. A little bird flew down and landed on the box. The bird pushed a button on the box and it opened! Inside the box was a big, bouncy ball. All the animals were very happy and played with the ball all day. Bessie was glad she did not give up, and everyone had a great time.

Once upon a time, in a yellow house, there lived a bear named Bob. Bob loved honey very much. One day, he went to the big tree to get some honey. He saw a bee and said, "Hello, bee! Can I have some honey, please?"
The bee said, "Yes, Bob! But first, you need to wash your hands." So, Bob went to the river and washed his hands. He used water and soap to make them clean. The bee was happy to see Bob's clean hands.
Then, the bee gave Bob a big jar of honey. Bob said, "Thank you, bee!" He took the honey home and ate it with his bread. It was the best honey he ever had. Bob was very happy and always remembered to wash his hands before eating.

Mum was making something in the kitchen. She asked her three year old daughter if she wanted to help. The daughter was very obedient and said yes. 
Mum gave the daughter some powder and asked her to sprinkle it on the plate. She held the plate away from her body so that the powder would not get on her clothes. She told her daughter the powder should be spread evenly. 
The obedient daughter took the spoon and sprinkled the powder on the plate just as her mum had said. 
Mum smiled and said "well done! You were very obedient and told the powder exactly where to go!" 
The daughter was very proud and smiled at her mum. She was happy that she had done something to help.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim lived in an old house with his mom, dad, and pet dog, Spot. Tim loved to play outside with Spot. One day, Tim and Spot went to the park to play.
At the park, they saw a long strip of grass. Tim and Spot ran and played on the strip. They were having so much fun! But then, Tim saw a big puddle of water. He wanted to jump in it.
As Tim ran towards the puddle, he didn't see a rock. Tim's foot hit the rock and he started to slip. But Spot was there to help! Spot caught Tim just in time. Tim was so happy that Spot saved him from falling. They laughed and went back to playing on the strip of grass. Tim and Spot had a great day at the park.

Ben and Sam are friends. They like to play with toys. One day, they find a big box of toys in the garage. They are happy. They want to sort the toys and share them.
Ben sees a gun. It is shiny and black. He likes it. He wants to keep it. He does not want to share it with Sam. He says, "This is my gun. You can have the other toys."
Sam is sad. He likes the gun too. He says, "No, that is not fair. We should share the toys. The gun is not yours. It is in the box."
Ben is angry. He says, "No, it is mine. I saw it first. Go away." He points the gun at Sam. He does not know it is a real gun. He thinks it is a toy.
Sam is scared. He says, "Stop, Ben. That is not a toy. That is a real gun. It can hurt you and me. Put it down."
Ben does not listen. He pulls the trigger. Bang! The gun makes a loud noise. Ben and Sam are shocked. They drop the toys and run away.
Mom and Dad hear the noise. They come to the garage. They see the gun and the toys. They are angry and worried. They say, "What are you doing? That is a dangerous gun. You could have hurt yourself or your friend. You should never touch a gun. You should always ask before you play with things you do not know."
Ben and Sam are sorry. They say, "We are sorry, Mom and Dad. We did not know it was a real gun. We were trying to sort the toys and share them. But we fought over the gun. We were not good friends."
Mom and Dad hug them. They say, "We are glad you are okay. But you should learn to be careful and kind. Guns are not toys. They are for grown-ups. And you should share your toys with your friends. That is what healthy and happy friends do."
Ben and Sam nod. They say, "We understand, Mom and Dad. We will be careful and kind. We will share our toys with our friends. We will be healthy and happy friends."
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a plan to go on a fun trip. He wanted to sleep in a tent with his family. Tim was very excited for his adventure.
One sunny day, Tim and his family went to a big park. They found a nice spot to put up their tent. Tim helped his dad build the tent. It was a little tent, but it was just right for them.
At night, they all slept in the tent. Tim felt happy and safe with his family. They heard the birds sing and the wind blow. In the morning, they woke up and had a yummy breakfast. Tim's plan for a fun trip was a big success.

One day, a little boy was walking in the park. He noticed an elderly man shaking a bag of wheat. The elderly man looked over and said hello.
The little boy asked, "What are you doing, sir?" 
The elderly man replied, "I'm shaking this wheat to separate it from all the dirt." 
The little boy said, "Oh! That looks like fun. Can I help you?" 
The elderly man declined, saying, "Sorry, little one, this is a big job. I can do it myself."
The little boy felt disappointed, but he understood that the elderly man had to do thejob alone. He smiled at the man and promised to come back and help next time.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Lily. Lily loved to go to her class every day. She had many friends in her class, and they all liked to play together.
One day, Lily and her friends found a big red wagon. They wanted to take turns pulling the wagon around the playground. Lily was the first to pull the wagon. She held the handle and pulled it with all her might. The wagon moved slowly, but Lily was happy.
When it was time for her friends to pull the wagon, they all cheered for each other. They laughed and smiled as they took turns pulling the wagon. At the end of the day, they all went home feeling happy and tired. They couldn't wait to come back to class and play together again.

Once upon a time there was a tiger named Tim. Tim wanted to grow big and strong. He was very impatient and wanted to grow right away.
One day Tim was playing in a pond and he saw a fish swimming by. "Hey! I'm as big as you!" he said.
But the fish said, "No you're not! You're very small!"
Tim was getting angry. He wanted to grow bigger than the fish. He tried and tried to make himself bigger, but he just couldn't do it.
He got so mad that he started growling and roaring. He roared so hard and loud that the ground shook.
But it didn't help. Tim was still small and he would never get bigger. He was sad and he never grew bigger.

Today, Mia was feeling delicate. Her mother was working hard, so Mia was by herself. But, she didn't feel lonely. She knew she could make something special with her delicate wings. 
Mia looked all around her room and found what she was looking for - a beautiful silver balloon. She reached out and grabbed it. Then, she closed her eyes and wished for something special.
When she opened her eyes, Mia was flying! She flew around the room, her delicate wings taking her higher and higher until the sunset. 
Mia felt graceful and free. She zoomed around the room, feeling the wind blowing around her. Then, when the sun was setting, she slowly glided down and landed on her pillow.
Mia looked back at the wonderful day. She was so glad that she was free to fly and feel delicate.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to play in her room. One day, she found a dark cupboard. She was so excited to see what was inside.
Lucy opened the cupboard and saw a big box. She guessed there was something fun inside. She opened the box and found a pretty dress and a funny hat. She put them on and danced around her room.
Lucy's mom came into the room and saw her dancing. "What are you wearing?" she asked. Lucy said, "I found these in the dark cupboard! Can you guess what was inside?"
