Her mom laughed and said, "I see you found your dress-up box! Let's play together!" So, Lucy and her mom played dress-up and had a great time.

Lily and Tom were friends. They liked to play in the field near their house. The field had long grass and flowers and bugs. Sometimes they saw a big dog in the field. The dog was brown and had a loud bark. Lily and Tom were scared of the dog.
One day, Lily and Tom went to the field to play. They saw the dog again. The dog was far away, but it saw them too. It started to run towards them. Lily and Tom ran away. They wanted to go back to their house.
"Come on, Tom, run faster!" Lily said.
"I'm trying, Lily, but my legs are tired!" Tom said.
They ran and ran, but the dog was faster. It caught up with them. It jumped on Tom and bit him. Tom screamed. Lily was very scared. She wanted to help Tom, but she did not know how. She ran to the house and called for help.
But it was too late. The dog had hurt Tom very badly. Tom did not join Lily in the house. He did not join her in the field anymore. He did not join her in anything. He was gone. Lily was very sad. She missed Tom. She wished they had never gone to the field. She wished the dog had never joined them.

Once upon a time, there was a duck. The duck liked to swim in the pond. One day, the duck saw something in the water. It was green and looked disgusting. The duck was scared.
The duck wanted to save the pond. It tried to push the green thing away. But the green thing did not move. The duck was sad and did not know what to do. It needed help to save the pond.
Then, something unexpected happened. The green thing started to talk. It said, "I am not bad, I am a friendly frog." The duck was surprised. The frog and the duck became friends. They played together and the pond was not disgusting anymore. The duck was happy it saved the pond.

Once upon a time, there was a little crab with a weak shell. The crab lived by the sea and loved to play in the warm sand. One sunny day, the crab met a new friend, a small fish.
"Hello, little fish," said the crab, "Do you want to play with me?"
"Yes, little crab," the fish replied, "I would love to play with you."
They played all day long, splashing in the water and making sand castles. As the sun grew hotter, the crab started to feel very warm. The crab's weak shell began to melt under the hot sun.
"Oh no, my shell is melting!" cried the crab. "What should I do?"
The little fish had an idea. "Let's go into the water so your shell can cool down," said the fish.
The crab and the fish went into the cool water, and the crab's shell stopped melting. They continued to play together, and the crab learned to be more careful in the sun. From that day on, the crab and the fish were the best of friends.

One day, a cow named Bessie went for a walk in the field. The sun was shining and the grass was green. Bessie was happy. But soon, the sky got dark and it started to rain. Bessie got wet and didn't like it.
Bessie saw a big tree and went under it to stay dry. She looked around and saw her friend, the duck. The duck was playing in the rain and having fun. Bessie started to wonder why the duck liked the rain, but she didn't.
Bessie asked the duck, "Why do you like the rain?" The duck said, "I like the wet because I can swim and play in it." Bessie thought about it and decided to try playing in the rain too. She splashed in the puddles and had fun.
Soon, the rain stopped and the sun came out again. Bessie and the duck were both wet and happy. They played together in the field until it was time to go home. Bessie learned that even when things are wet and not how she likes them, she can still have fun.

Once upon a time, there was a gifted cat named Tom. He could call birds to come to him. One day, Tom found a gear. He thought it was pretty.
Tom showed the gear to his friend, a dog named Max. Max said, "Wow, nice gear! Can I have it?" Tom said, "No, I found it first." Max felt sad and walked away.
Later, Tom saw Max stuck in a tree. Tom called the birds to help get Max down. Max said, "Thank you, Tom!" Tom gave Max the gear as a gift. They were happy friends again.

Mama took her little girl to the park. She wanted her daughter to see what nature had to offer. The little girl began to gaze around in amazement at all of the flowers, grass, and trees. She marveled at the bright green strip of grass that lined the pathways. 
Then, she saw a large tree with branches that seemed to reach the sky. She asked her mom what it was and her mom said it was a maple tree. The little girl asked if she could climb the tree.
Mama said, "If you think you can do it, I'll help you try!"
The little girl cautiously made her way up the tree, clinging to the trunk. When she reached the top, unsteady and dizzy, she let out a little giggle. From up high, she could see the park stretch out for miles and miles.
Mama smiled, proud of her little girl for trying something new. She said, "It looks like you enjoyed your climb."
The little girl smiled and said, "Yes, I did! This was so much fun!"

Once upon a time, there was a giant. The giant was very big and strong. He lived in a wet forest. The forest had many trees, animals, and a big river.
One day, the giant saw that the river was very dry. The animals were thirsty and sad. The giant wanted to help them. He knew he could supply water to the river. He had a big bucket that he could use.
The giant walked to a faraway lake. He filled his big bucket with water. Then, he walked back to the river. The giant poured the water into the river. The river became wet again. The animals were happy and thanked the giant. The giant felt good because he helped his friends.

Once upon a time there was a kind, gentle monster. He liked to read a lot, so every morning he would buy a new newspaper.
One day, the monster was reading his newspaper when all of the words suddenly disappeared! Frustrated, he tried to move the paper around, but nothing happened.
Sighing, the monster decided to try something different. He gently touched his finger to the paper. Suddenly, the words popped back up for him to read!
The monster smiled. From then on, he remembered to always touch the paper gently whenever he wanted to read his newspaper. And it worked every time!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lila. Lila loved to eat tasty food. One day, her mom made a new food for her to try. It was a small, round, green thing. Lila didn't know what it was, so she had to guess. "Is it an olive?" she asked.
Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, it's an olive! Give it a try and see if you like it." Lila took a bite of the olive. It was tasty! She liked it a lot. Lila was happy that she guessed right.
From that day on, Lila asked her mom to put olives in her food. She loved the tasty olives so much. And every time she ate them, she remembered the day she guessed what they were. Lila and her mom were happy eating tasty olives together.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a boy named Tim. Tim had a heavy wallet. He loved his wallet very much. He would put coins in it and carry it everywhere.
One day, Tim's wallet vanished. He looked for it in his room, but he could not find it. Tim was very sad. He asked his mom and dad if they had seen his wallet, but they had not.
Tim went outside to look for his wallet. He saw his friend, Sam, playing with a ball. Tim told Sam about his missing wallet. Sam said, "Let's find it together!" So, Tim and Sam looked everywhere. They found the wallet under a big tree. Tim was so happy! They went back to play with the ball, and Tim's wallet was safe again.

Once there was a brave boy named Jack. He wanted to own something that was far away, west of where he lived. 
So one day, he told his mom: "Mom, I want something west of here, I'm brave and I can do it!"
His mom smiled and said: "I know you are brave, Jack. But you must know, west of here is a long way and it won't be easy." 
But Jack was determined and didn't listen to his mom. So he set out on his journey westward. 
He kept walking, but on the way he got lost and didn't know how to get back home. He was never seen again.
The moral of his story is that brave doesn't always mean successful. We must be brave but also be wise.

Once upon a time, there was a new school. In this school, there were many kids. One day, the teacher said, "It's time for a quiz." All the kids were excited.
The teacher gave the kids two things to choose from for the quiz. They could pick colors or animals. The kids talked to each other. One kid said, "I like colors." Another kid said, "I like animals." They had to choose one.
In the end, they chose animals for the quiz. The teacher asked, "What animal says 'moo'?" The kids all said, "A cow!" They were happy they picked animals. The quiz was fun, and they all learned something new.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy fly. The lazy fly did not like to fly fast. One day, the lazy fly met a big dog. The big dog said, "I have a problem. Can you help me solve it?"
The lazy fly said, "I can try. What is the problem?" The big dog said, "I am very hot. I need to find a cool place to rest. Can you help me find a cool place?" The lazy fly thought and said, "I know a place. Follow me!"
The lazy fly and the big dog went to a big tree. The big tree had a lot of shade. The big dog said, "Thank you, lazy fly. This is a cool place to rest." The lazy fly was happy to help. The lazy fly and the big dog became good friends.

Lily and Ben were playing in the garden. They liked to dig and find things in the dirt. Sometimes they found worms, sometimes they found stones, sometimes they found nothing.
One day, they found something very strange. It was white and hard and had holes and bumps. It looked like a big egg, but it was not an egg.
"What is this?" Lily asked.
"I don't know," Ben said. "Maybe it is a toy."
They picked it up and looked at it. It had two big holes on one side and many small holes on the other side. It also had some teeth inside.
"Maybe it is a skull," Lily said.
"A skull?" Ben said. "What is a skull?"
"A skull is the bone that holds your head and your brain," Lily said. She had learned that from a book.
"Wow, that is cool," Ben said. "But whose skull is this?"
"I don't know," Lily said. "Maybe it is from a dinosaur or a monster or a fairy."
They decided to take the skull to their mom and show her. They thought she would be very proud of them for finding something so unique.
But when they got to the house, their mom was not happy. She was very angry.
"Where did you get that?" she yelled. "That is not a toy. That is not a skull. That is a coconut!"
She took the coconut from them and threw it in the trash.
Lily and Ben were confused and sad. They did not understand why their mom was so mad. They thought the coconut was a skull and they liked it.
They decided to sneak out later and get the coconut back. They wanted to taste it and see if it was yummy or yucky. They hoped it was yummy. They thought it would be fun to eat a skull.

Once upon a time, there was a wild girl. She was very polite and friendly. Everyone liked her around town. 
One day, the wild girl found something special and decided to sell it at the market. Everyone was so excited to see what the wild girl was selling. She had a big smile on her face as all the people around her bought her items. 
When the wild girl counted her money, she found out she had made a lot of money. She was so happy because all her hard work had paid off! She was polite to the customers and thanked them for buying something from her. 
The wild girl was so proud of herself! She celebrated by having a yummy ice-cream and everyone was thrilled to see her so happy. From then on, the wild girl happily sold items at the market every weekend.

One day, a boy named Tim had a bag with a zipper. He liked to put his toys in the bag. The bag was deep, so many toys fit inside. Tim took his bag with him everywhere he went.
One day, Tim could not find his favorite toy car. He looked in his bag, but it was not there. He felt sad. Tim tried to find his toy car, but it seemed to be lost. He asked his mom to help him look for it.
They looked all over the house. At last, they found the toy car under Tim's bed. Tim was so happy! He put the toy car back in his bag with the zipper. From that day on, Tim made sure to always check his bag and keep it close.

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim had a big toy chest. Inside the chest were many toys. Tim loved to play with his toys every day. One day, Tim found a tiny bird near his chest. The bird was hurt and couldn't fly.
Tim said, "Don't worry, little bird. I will help you." He picked up the bird and put it in the chest with his toys. Tim remained alert to make sure the bird was okay.
Days went by, and the little bird began to feel better. Tim talked to the bird every day, and they became friends. One day, the bird said, "Thank you, Tim, for helping me. I can fly now." Tim opened the chest, and the bird flew away. Tim was happy that his friend was okay.

One day, a big red ball was on the playground. A little boy named Tim saw the ball and wanted to play with it. He tried to push the ball, but it was very big and heavy. Tim was serious about playing with the ball, so he tried again.
Tim pushed the ball harder, and it started to roll. He was so happy! But then, a little girl named Sue saw the ball and wanted to play too. Sue and Tim both tried to push the ball at the same time, but they couldn't agree which way to push it. They both wanted to play with the ball, but they didn't know how to share.
Just then, a big wind came and blew the ball into the air. It was a surprise! The ball flew up and away from Tim and Sue. They both looked at each other and laughed. They decided to forget about the ball and play together instead. They became good friends and had lots of fun on the playground.

Once upon a time, there was a princess. She lived in a big castle. The princess was very sad. Her favorite toy was lost. The toy was filthy, but she loved it.
One day, a kind boy came to the castle. He wanted to save the princess from her sadness. The boy looked everywhere for the filthy toy. He looked in the garden, in the kitchen, and even in the big hall.
Finally, the boy found the filthy toy under the princess's bed. The princess was so happy! The boy saved her from sadness. They played with the toy all day long. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Anna. She was three years old and she loved to go to church with her family. Every Sunday morning, Anna and her family would put on their best clothes, and head off to church.
Today, Anna was so excited - it was a lovely day and the sun was shining brightly. As they walked to church, Anna held onto her little brother's hand and they said hello to everyone they passed.
When they reached the church, Anna's dad said to the family, "Let's enjoy this time together."
Inside the church, Anna enjoyed the beautiful music and singing. She watched the people sitting around her, singing and clapping along to the lovely tunes. Anna looked around her, and noticed how happy everyone was.
At the end of the service, Anna said, "I love church."
Anna's dad smiled and said, "Yes, me too. We can come back next week, if you'd like."
Anna smiled and said, "Yes, please!" and she ran outside, happy to be part of such a lovely church family.

One day, a little girl named Lily was playing with her toys. She had a big, terrible mess in her room. Lily's mom came in and saw the mess. She said, "Lily, you need to clean up your toys." Lily did not want to clean up, but she knew she had to.
Lily picked up her toys and put them away. She found a toy she forgot about. It was a small toy stair. Lily loved to play with the stair. She would make her toys walk up and down the stair. Lily's mom saw her playing and said, "Lily, give me the stair so I can put it away."
Lily gave her mom the stair. She was sad to see it go. Her mom said, "Don't worry, you can play with it again later. Now, let's finish cleaning up." Lily and her mom cleaned up the terrible mess together. When they were done, Lily's room was nice and clean.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a favorite shirt. It was red and had a big, happy sun on it. Tim loved to wear his shirt every day. He thought it was very important.
One day, Tim's mom said, "Tim, I recommend you wear a different shirt today. We need to wash your red shirt." Tim was sad, but he knew his mom was right. So, he picked a blue shirt with a funny cat on it.
At the park, Tim met a new friend named Lily. Lily said, "I like your cat shirt!" Tim smiled and said, "Thank you! My mom recommended I wear it today." They played together all day and had lots of fun. Tim learned that sometimes it's good to try new things, even if it's just a different shirt.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his ordinary football. Every day, he would go to the park and test how far he could kick it.
One sunny day, Tim went to the park and saw a big tree. He kicked his football very hard, but it got stuck in the tree! Tim was very sad and did not know what to do.
Then, a friendly bird came and took the football from the tree. The bird dropped the football, and it fell right into Tim's hands. Tim was so happy and thanked the bird. From that day on, Tim and the bird became best friends and played football together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy was a healthy girl who loved to run and play. She lived in a small house with her mom and her pet cat, Fluffy.
One day, Lucy's mom gave her a prune to eat. Lucy did not want to eat the prune. She wanted to eat candy instead. Fluffy the cat saw the prune and wanted it too. Fluffy jumped on the table to get the prune.
Lucy and Fluffy both wanted the prune. They pulled and pulled on the prune. But then, the prune broke in half. Lucy and Fluffy each got a piece of the prune. They ate the prune and found out they liked it. From that day on, Lucy and Fluffy both loved to eat prunes and stayed healthy and happy.

One day, a little girl named Mia was playing outside. She saw an ambulance drive by, making a loud noise. Mia was gentle and kind, so she wanted to help people too. She thought about how she could be helpful like the ambulance.
Mia had an idea. She went to her room and found her toy doctor kit. She took the kit outside and scattered her toys on the grass. Mia pretended to be a doctor and helped her stuffed animals feel better. She put band-aids on them and gave them hugs.
Later that day, Mia's mom saw her playing. She was so proud of her gentle little girl. Mia's mom knew that one day, Mia would grow up to help people just like the ambulance she saw earlier. And when that day came, Mia would be the best helper she could be.

Frankie was worried. He had a big job to do, but he wasn't sure what to do. He looked around for help and then he saw his Daddy. 
"Daddy, can you help me?" Frankie asked.
"Of course, what do you need help with?" his Daddy replied.
Frankie answered, "I need to fill this box, but I don't know how!"
His Daddy smiled, "No worries, I have just the thing. Here's a big tie. Put this tie around the box and then fill it up with whatever you want."
Frankie was overjoyed. He grabbed the tie, put it around the box and filled it up with some of his toys. Frankie was no longer worried and was very happy that he could now finish his job.

Once upon a time, in a big green field, there was a little bunny named Bobo. Bobo loved to hop all day long. One day, while hopping, Bobo found a shiny buckle on the ground. It was very original, like nothing he had ever seen before.
Bobo picked up the buckle and hopped back home. He showed the buckle to his best friend, a bird named Chirpy. "Look, Chirpy! I found a pretty buckle!" said Bobo. Chirpy looked at the buckle and said, "Wow, Bobo! That's very original!"
Together, Bobo and Chirpy decided to make a necklace with the buckle. They used a soft string and put the buckle in the middle. Bobo wore the necklace and hopped around the field, showing it off to all his friends. Everyone loved the original buckle necklace and cheered for Bobo and Chirpy. And from that day on, Bobo and Chirpy became the best of friends, hopping and playing together in the big green field.

Once upon a time, there was a big oak tree in a park. The oak tree had many dull leaves. It was a nice place for kids to play.
One day, a little girl named Lucy came to play near the oak tree. She saw a small hole at the bottom of the tree. Lucy was curious and wanted to discover what was inside the hole.
Lucy called her friend Tom and said, "Tom, come here! I found a hole in the oak tree. Let's discover what's inside." Tom came running and looked at the hole. They both put their hands inside the hole and felt something soft and furry.
To their surprise, they discovered a tiny kitten hiding inside the hole. The kitten was scared but happy to see Lucy and Tom. They took the kitten out of the hole and played with it under the dull leaves of the oak tree.
From that day on, Lucy, Tom, and the kitten became best friends. They always played together near the big oak tree, and they had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy called Sam who had a big dream. Every day, he would our his dream, and often talked about it with his friends.
One day, Sam was sitting at the table for his meal. As he ate, Sam asked his mother if his dream could ever come true. His mother smiled, and told him that if he was brave and kept believing, it could.
Sam was so excited he couldn't eat a bite. He rushed outside to tell his friends all about his dream. His friends smiled and said they hoped his dream would come true.
A few days later, Sam was back at the table for breakfast. He looked across the room and saw a red box. Sam rushed over to the box and opened it. Inside was the most beautiful toy he had ever seen! 
When his mother asked him how he liked it, Sam smiled and said, "It was my dream and now it's come true!"

One day, a little cat named Tom was walking in the park. He saw a big gray rock. Tom was a serious cat, and he wanted to find out why the rock was there. He asked, "Why are you here, gray rock?"
The gray rock did not say anything. Tom felt sad and began to weep. He did not understand why the rock would not talk to him.
Just then, a small bird flew down and sat on the gray rock. The bird looked at Tom and said, "Don't weep, Tom. The rock cannot talk, but I can. I am here to tell you that you are a very special cat. You can make friends with anyone, even a big gray rock!" Tom felt happy and smiled. He realized that he could make friends with anyone, even if they could not talk.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Beth. She was only three years old, and she was always very curious. One day she imagined that she would have a special job of her own someday. 
Beth wanted to practice for her job, so she decided to lean against the wall and watch her mom and dad do their jobs. She watched as her mom cooked delicious food and her dad fixed things around the house. Beth was excited and imagined that her own job would be special and original.
The next day, her parents surprised her with a very special job. They gave Beth a special spoon and asked her to be the official flavor tester for the family's homemade ice cream. Beth was so excited she started to laugh and cheer. 
Beth was so proud and felt like her job was even more special and original than she imagined. Now she knows that if she works hard and leans a little, any job can be special and original.

Once upon a time, in a big building called a university, there lived a wealthy man named Mr. Rich. He had lots of money and fancy things. Mr. Rich liked to show off his money to everyone. He didn't care about how others feel.
One sunny day, Mr. Rich met a nice lady. The nice lady said, "Hello, Mr. Rich. I see you have lots of money. Can you help me? I don't have much and I feel sad." Mr. Rich looked at her and said, "No, I won't help you. I want to keep all my money for myself."
The nice lady walked away and felt very sad. She told her friends about Mr. Rich and how he didn't want to help her. The friends didn't like Mr. Rich anymore because he was not kind.
One day, Mr. Rich lost all his money. He didn't know what to do. He felt scared and lonely. He went to the nice lady and her friends for help. But they remembered how Mr. Rich didn't help them when they needed it.
So, the nice lady and her friends said, "No, we won't help you, Mr. Rich. You didn't care about our feelings when we needed help." Mr. Rich felt very sad and alone. He learned that having lots of money doesn't make you happy if you don't care about others.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to look for stones and make shapes with them. Lily found a big stone that was round and smooth. She showed it to Ben and said, "Look, I found a ball!"
Ben was not impressed. He said, "That is not a ball. That is a stone. It is hard and heavy. You cannot throw it or bounce it. It is a poor stone."
Lily felt sad. She liked her stone. She said, "It is not a poor stone. It is a good stone. It is shiny and pretty. I can fold it in my hand and keep it warm. It is a friend stone."
Ben laughed. He said, "You cannot fold a stone. It is not soft like paper or cloth. It is stiff and solid. You are silly, Lily."
Lily did not like Ben's words. She said, "You are mean, Ben. You do not know how to play. I do not want to play with you anymore." She ran away with her stone.
Ben felt sorry. He did not mean to hurt Lily's feelings. He wanted to play with her. He ran after her and said, "Wait, Lily. I am sorry. I was wrong. You can fold a stone. You can fold it in your heart and make it happy. Can I see your stone? Can we be friends?"
Lily stopped and turned around. She saw Ben's face. He looked sad and sorry. She felt sorry too. She said, "Okay, Ben. I forgive you. You can see my stone. You can touch it and hold it. We can be friends."
They smiled and hugged. They shared the stone and played with it. They made shapes and stories with it. They found more stones and made more friends. They had fun in the park. They learned that stones can be folded in many ways. They learned that words can be folded too. They learned to fold words with kindness and care.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who went to school. She took her folder with her every day. She liked school and all the fun things she learned.
One day, she was walking to school when she heard a loud noise. She jumped and was scared. Suddenly, a big boisterous dog appeared out of nowhere! The dog was barking and seemed very rude.
The girl shouted, "Go away! Don't scare me!" But the dog just kept barking. The girl was scared and started to cry.
Just then, the girl's teacher appeared and saw the little girl being scared. "Let's get inside, little one," the teacher said. She put her arm around the girl and took her inside the school.
The teacher looked at the dog with a stern voice. "You must be very careful," she said. "It's not nice to scare the children."
The dog barked one last time and ran away. The little girl gave her teacher a hug and went into school with her folder. She was happy to be safe and was extra careful from then on. 
The end.

Tom and Lily liked to play on the beach. They liked to make sand castles and look for shells. They also liked to jump in the water when the tide was low. The tide was the water that came and went every day.
One day, Tom and Lily saw a big rock in the water. They wanted to jump on it and see what was on the other side. They waited for the tide to go down and then they ran to the rock. They jumped on it and looked around. They saw some crabs and some fish and some seaweed. They thought it was fun.
But then they heard their mom calling them. She sounded angry. They looked at the beach and saw that the tide was coming up. The water was getting higher and higher. They were scared. They tried to jump off the rock, but the water was too deep. They shouted for help.
Their mom saw them and ran to them. She was worried. She found a big stick and threw it to them. They grabbed the stick and she pulled them to the shore. They were wet and cold and tired. They hugged their mom and said they were sorry.
Their mom was glad they were safe, but she was also mad. She told them they were naughty and they should never jump on the rock again. She said the tide was dangerous and they could have been hurt. They felt guilty. They nodded and said they understood. They promised to listen to their mom and not jump on the rock again. They hoped she would forgive them.

Once upon a time, there were twin bunnies named Bob and Rob. They lived in a big forest. One day, they saw a big box. It was the only available box in the forest. Bob said, "Let's lift the box and see what's inside!"
Bob and Rob tried to lift the box together. It was very heavy. They heard a noise from inside the box. They knew something was in there. They tried harder to lift the box.
Finally, they lifted the box. Inside, they found a big, shiny ball. It was the most beautiful ball they had ever seen. They played with the ball all day and had so much fun. They knew that lifting the box was worth it.

Tom and Lily were twins who liked to play outside. One day, they saw a big truck with many colors and shapes on it. It was a circus truck, and it was going to the park. Tom and Lily wanted to see the circus, so they ran after the truck.
"Look, Lily, there are animals in the truck!" Tom said. He saw a lion, a monkey, and an elephant. They were very big and noisy.
"Wow, Tom, they are unusual!" Lily said. She had never seen such animals before. She wanted to touch them and feed them.
They followed the truck to the park, where the circus people set up a big tent. Tom and Lily wanted to go inside, but they had no money. They saw a man with a hat who was selling tickets.
"Please, mister, can we see the circus?" Tom asked.
"Sorry, kids, you need money to see the circus. It's not free," the man said.
Tom and Lily felt sad. They had no money, and they wanted to see the unusual animals. They sat on a bench and stared at the tent. They hoped someone would give them a ticket.
Then, they saw a lady with a kind face. She had a basket with some bread and cheese. She saw Tom and Lily and smiled.
"Hello, kids, are you hungry?" she asked.
"Yes, ma'am, we are," Tom said.
"Would you like some bread and cheese?" she asked.
"Yes, please, thank you," Lily said.
The lady gave them some bread and cheese, and they ate it happily. They thanked the lady again.
"You're welcome, kids. You have good manners. Do you want to see the circus?" she asked.
"Yes, ma'am, we do, but we have no money," Tom said.
The lady took out two tickets from her pocket and gave them to Tom and Lily.
"Here, these are for you. I have an extra pair. You can see the circus with me," she said.
Tom and Lily were very happy. They hugged the lady and thanked her again.
"Thank you, thank you, you are very nice," they said.
They followed the lady to the tent and saw the unusual animals. They clapped and cheered and had a lot of fun.
They learned that being polite and grateful can bring good things. They also learned that the circus is a wonderful view, but the best view is a kind heart.

One day, on a dry sunny day, Sarah was playing with her toys. Suddenly she heard an alarm going off. She ran to the window and saw that her neighbour's garden was on fire! Everyone was in a rush and needed to act quickly so they could help. 
Sarah saw that the garden had a fountain, but the fountain had no water! No one had any water to put the fire out. Sarah thought quickly and remembered her parents had a big jug of water in the garage. She ran and quickly grabbed the jug, then ran back to the garden so she could help. 
With her jug in hand, Sarah was able to supply the garden with water and put the fire out. Everyone was so thankful for her help. They all gave Sarah a big thank you for being so brave and using her quick thinking.
The moral of the story is to always pay attention to those around you and offer help where you can. Offering a helpful supply can make a big difference and save the day!

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They had a big bag of popcorn to eat. Tim loved popcorn very much. He liked to guess which piece would pop next. It was a fun game for him.
While they were eating the popcorn, a small bird flew down to them. The bird was very hungry. Tim wanted to help the bird, so he gave some popcorn to the bird. The bird tried to eat the popcorn, but it was too hard for the bird to eat. The bird looked sad.
Then, something unexpected happened! A big gust of wind came and blew the popcorn up into the air. The popcorn started to pop in the sky! The bird was very happy and flew up to eat the soft popcorn. Tim and his mom laughed and clapped. They never thought popcorn could pop in the sky, but it did!

One day, a wise old cat and a young pup decided to unite and become friends. They wanted to find a purse that was lost in the big park. They knew that if they worked together, they could find it.
As they walked, the cat said, "Pup, I think we should look near the big tree. I have a feeling we will find the purse there." The pup agreed and they went to the big tree.
When they reached the big tree, they saw the purse! They were so happy they found it. The wise cat and the pup high-fived each other and returned the purse to its owner. The owner was grateful and gave them treats as a thank you. The wise cat and the pup knew that uniting was the best choice they made.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had a big, red button. This button was unusual because it could talk. Lucy loved to play with her talking button.
One day, the button asked Lucy to accept it as her best friend. Lucy said, "Yes, I accept you as my best friend!" They were very happy and played together all day long.
As they played, they met other kids who also had unusual toys. They all became friends and had lots of fun together. And Lucy and her talking button lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty house. In the house, there was a big couch. The couch was very soft and nice. One day, a little girl named Amy came to play in the house.
Amy saw the couch and said, "Wow! This is a big couch!" She jumped on the couch and played all day. She had lots of fun playing on the soft couch.
But then, Amy smelled something bad. She saw smoke coming from the kitchen. "Oh no!" she cried. "Something is burning!" Amy ran to the kitchen and saw a pan on the stove. It was burning! She turned off the stove and the burning stopped.
Amy's mom came into the house and saw what happened. She said, "Amy, you saved the pretty house from a big fire! You are very brave!" Amy was happy that she saved the house and the big couch. From that day on, she always remembered to be careful when she played.

Once there was a wild fox. He was running through the woods and stopped to take a rest. He looked up and saw a big, bright full moon. He was so excited that he wanted to dance around the moon. He started to jump and twirl until he bumped into a rabbit.
The rabbit yelled at the fox, "Hey! What are you doing?" The fox replied, "I'm dancing!" The rabbit shook his head and said, "You're too wild!" The fox was angry and he bit the rabbit's ear.
The rabbit screamed and ran away. The fox felt bad for biting the rabbit, but he knew for sure the rabbit would never forget how wild he was.

Once upon a time, there lived a young boy who loved to play. He often spent time playing and climbing around his house. One day, the young boy was playing around his favourite chair when he remembered something his mum had said. His mum had said that a normal way to sit on the chair was with both feet on the floor.
The young boy wanted to be normal, so every time he found his feet off the floor, he reminded himself to put them back down. This was difficult at first, but slowly and surely, the boy got better and better at sitting properly. His mum was so proud of him and always praised his new normal sitting position.
This story has a moral value which is that it is important to listen to your parents and remember to do the right things. All children should be reminded to sit properly and not get too comfortable and forget their rules.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly dancer named Lily. She loved to dance and carry her teddy bear with her everywhere. One day, Lily saw a big tree and thought it would be fun to climb it. She carried her teddy bear up the tree and they both sat on a big branch.
Lily and her teddy bear saw a little bird in the tree. The bird looked sad. Lily asked the bird, "Why are you sad?" The bird said, "I can't find my nest. I lost it in the wind." Lily wanted to help the bird. She said, "I will carry you and help you find your nest." The bird was happy.
Lily, her teddy bear, and the bird climbed down the tree. They looked and looked for the nest. At last, they found the nest near a pretty flower. The bird was so happy! Lily carried the bird back to its nest. The bird said, "Thank you, Lily!" Lily and her teddy bear danced and laughed. They were happy to help their new friend.

Charlie was a very happy three year old. He had a very fine trophy. Every night he would wipe it off to make sure it stayed shiny. 
One night as Charlie was about to start wiping his trophy, he accidentally knocked it off the table. It fell on the ground and shattered. 
Charlie was very upset. He tried to pick up the pieces and put them back together, but it was no use. His prize was broken and wouldn't fit back together again. 
Charlie cried and cried until his mom came into the room. She hugged him and asked him what had happened. He told her that his prize had broken. 
His mom had an idea to make Charlie feel better. She got out some art supplies and helped him create a new trophy out of paint and paper. When they finished, Charlie was so proud of his new prize and smiled from ear to ear. He promised to wipe it off every night to keep it extra fine.

Once upon a time, there was a smart little bunny called Bob. He was always very happy and loved vegetables. One day, Bob was out in the garden looking for some new vegetables to eat. He found a big juicy carrot and he wanted to eat it.
But then Bob's friend, Rabbit, came along and said that he wanted to eat the carrot too. Bob said no and Rabbit started to cry. Bob felt sad and tried to wipe Rabbit's tears away.
Bob said Rabbit could have the carrot if he was very brave and did something special. Rabbit was very excited and agreed to try. He hopped up to the top of Bob's garden and picked some of the brightest and biggest leaves from the tree.
Bob was so proud of Rabbit and to show it, he gave Rabbit the carrot. Bob and Rabbit were very happy and they shared the carrot together. They both learned that it was smart to share and not fight.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful fish named Finn. Finn lived in the water with his friends. One day, a little girl named Lily came to the pond. She saw Finn and said, "Hi, fish! Can you guess what I have in this box?"
Finn wanted to talk to Lily, but he needed oxygen from the water to breathe. So, he jumped out of the water for a short time and said, "I don't know! What's in the box?"
Lily opened the box, and inside was a new friend for Finn. It was a little frog named Fred! Finn was so happy to have a new friend. But then, they found out that the pond was getting dirty. Finn, Fred, and Lily worked together to clean the pond. They were all helpful friends, and they saved their home.

One day, a dog named Max found a tasty bone. He was very happy. Max wanted to show his friend, Sam the cat. He ran to Sam's house with the bone in his mouth.
Max said, "Sam, look! I found a tasty bone!" Sam looked at the bone and wanted it too. "I want the bone, Max," said Sam. This made Max sad because he did not want to give away his bone.
Max and Sam thought about what to do. They decided to reveal the bone to their friend, Lily the bird. Lily looked at the bone and said, "You can both share the tasty bone!" Max and Sam liked this idea. They were happy to share the bone and play together.

Once upon a time, there was a noisy dog named Max. Max liked to bark and play all day long. He lived in a small house with his best friend, a little girl named Lily.
One day, Lily found a note on the floor. It said, "Do not spoil the cake in the kitchen." Lily wanted to tell Max about the note, but Max was too busy playing with his toys. So, Lily went to the kitchen to see the cake.
As Lily looked at the cake, Max came running into the kitchen. He was very excited and accidentally knocked over the cake! "Oh no, Max!" said Lily, "You spoiled the cake!" Max felt sad and said, "I'm sorry, Lily."
Lily hugged Max and said, "It's okay, Max. We can make a new cake together." Max wagged his tail and barked happily. And so, Lily and Max made a new cake, and they were more careful this time. They had a fun day and learned the importance of reading notes.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Amy went to the park. She saw a small bug on the ground. The bug looked disgusting. Amy wanted to learn more about the bug.
Amy asked her mom, "What is this bug?" Her mom said, "That is a beetle. It has a special power. It can move big things with its strong legs."
Amy watched the beetle move a big leaf. She was happy to learn about the beetle's power. She was not scared of the disgusting bug anymore. Amy knew that even small things can have big powers.

Once upon a time, there was a generous boy named Tom. Tom liked to help people. One day, he wanted to drive his toy car to the store. He wanted to buy butter for his mom.
Tom put on his hat and went outside. He drove his toy car down the street. He saw a cat. The cat was stuck in a tree. Tom stopped his toy car and helped the cat. The cat said "meow" and went on its way.
Tom got to the store and bought the butter. He was very happy. He drove his toy car back home. He gave the butter to his mom. His mom was happy too. Tom felt good because he helped the cat and made his mom happy.

Once upon a time, there was a white dog named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play and learn. One sunny day, Fluffy met a little girl named Lily. Lily was playing with her toys and counting numbers.
"Hi, Lily! Can you teach me numbers?" asked Fluffy. Lily smiled and said, "Of course, Fluffy! Let's start with one, two, and three." Fluffy wagged his tail and listened carefully. Lily held up her fingers and counted, "One, two, three!"
Fluffy barked happily and tried to count too. "One, two, three!" he said. Lily clapped her hands and laughed. "Good job, Fluffy!" They played together, counting numbers and having fun.
As the sun began to set, Fluffy knew it was time to go home. He thanked Lily for teaching him numbers. "Bye, Lily! I had fun learning with you!" Fluffy said. Lily waved goodbye and said, "Bye, Fluffy! See you soon!"
From that day on, Fluffy and Lily became best friends. They played together every day, and Lily continued to teach Fluffy new things. And Fluffy, the white dog, was always happy to learn.

Tim and Sue like to play on the farm. They like to see the cows, the pigs and the chickens. They also like to feed the horses some oats. Oats are small and brown and good for horses.
One day, Tim and Sue find a big bag of oats in the barn. They want to give some to the horses, but the bag is too heavy for them. They have an idea. They see a rope hanging from the ceiling. They tie the bag to the rope and pull it up. They think they are very clever.
But they are careless. They do not tie the rope well. The bag starts to slip. It falls down and hits the ground. The oats spill everywhere. The horses come and eat the oats. They make a big mess. Tim and Sue are scared. They hear the farmer coming. They run and hide. They hope the farmer will not be angry.

Once upon a time, there was a big, wide tree. It had many apples. A little boy named Tim wanted to give apples to his friends. But, it was difficult to get the apples from the tree.
One day, Tim saw a big stick. He thought it would help him get the apples. He tried to use the stick, but it was too heavy. Tim felt sad, but he did not give up.
Tim kept trying to get the apples with the stick. He pulled and pulled, but the stick did not move. Then, the stick fell on Tim's foot. It hurt a lot. Tim cried and could not give apples to his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sarah. She was three-years-old and ever so curious. Sarah was playing in her garden one day when she noticed a bush. She ran over to it and saw there were prunes, which she had never seen before. She was so curious, so she asked her mum what they were.
"Those prunes provide lots of nutrition," mum told her. Sarah was so excited and she ate one straight away. It was so sweet and delicious. Mum smiled at Sarah and said, "Isn't it brilliant?".
Sarah nodded and said, "It sure is! Can I have more, please?" Delighted with her daughter's interest, mum began to pick prunes from the bush and put them into a basket for Sarah to take home.
Sarah hugged her mum, feeling grateful and excited. She had been so amazed by the prunes and she wanted to pick more of them. She thanked her mum for providing such a wonderful snack, and ran off to her garden to pick more prunes. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a soft bunny named Bobo. Bobo lived in a small house near the woods. He had many friends who liked to play with him. One day, Bobo found a shiny watch on the ground. It was round and had a long, thin strap. Bobo did not know what it was, but he thought it was pretty.
Bobo showed the watch to his friends. They all looked at it and tried to guess what it could be. One friend said, "Maybe it is a toy that we can shake." So, they all took turns shaking the watch. They laughed and had fun, but the watch did not do anything when they shook it.
Bobo's friend, a wise old owl, saw them playing with the watch. He told them, "This is a special thing called a watch. It helps people know what time it is." Bobo and his friends were amazed. They thanked the owl for teaching them about the watch. From that day on, Bobo wore the watch on his soft paw and always knew when it was time to play with his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a polite mosquito named Momo. Momo lived in a beautiful garden with many pretty flowers. Momo had many friends who played with him every day.
One sunny day, Momo and his friends decided to bake a yummy cake. They all worked together to mix the cake and put it in the oven. While the cake was baking, Momo said, "Let's share the cake with everyone in the garden when it's ready!" All his friends agreed.
When the cake was ready, Momo and his friends shared it with everyone in the garden. All the animals and insects were very happy and thanked Momo and his friends. The moral of the story is that sharing with others makes everyone happy.

One day, a girl named Sue found a radio. The radio was old and icy. Sue took it home and showed her mom. "Look, Mom! I found a radio!" Sue said.
Mom looked at the radio and smiled. "Let's see if it works," she said. They turned it on, and the radio started to play a song. Sue and her mom danced and laughed. They were very happy.
Later, Sue's dad came home. Sue ran to him and said, "Dad, guess what? I found a radio!" Dad looked at the radio and said, "Wow, that's great! Can you tell me where you found it?" Sue told him about finding the radio outside. They all listened to the music and had a fun time together.

Once upon a time, in a little garden, there was a clever rabbit. This rabbit loved to eat herbs. One day, he saw a big herb that he wanted to eat. But there was a problem. The herb was in a high place, and the rabbit could not reach it.
The rabbit thought, "I need help. Who can help me?" He saw a tall giraffe and asked, "Please, can you help me get the herb?" The giraffe tried to get the herb, but it was still too high. The rabbit felt sad.
Then, the rabbit saw a bird flying in the sky. He asked the bird, "Please, can you help me get the herb?" The bird flew up and got the herb for the rabbit. The rabbit was very happy and said, "Thank you!" The rabbit, the giraffe, and the bird all became friends, and they shared the herb together.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a graceful deer. The deer had many friends, like the birds, rabbits, and squirrels. They all liked to play together.
One day, the deer saw some lumber. The lumber was big and heavy. The deer wanted to tell his friends about it. So, he went to the birds, rabbits, and squirrels and said, "Listen, I found some lumber. Let's build a house for all of us to play in."
They all worked together to build the house with the lumber. They pushed and pulled the big heavy lumber. It was hard work, but they did not give up. And soon, they had a beautiful house to play in.
The friends were very happy with their new house. They learned that when they listen and work together, they can do great things. And they lived happily ever after, always helping each other.

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim got a new toy. It was a big, red truck. He was very happy. Tim wanted to play with his new toy all day.
Tim asked his mom to help him open the box. His mom helped him open it. The new toy truck was inside. Tim was very excited. He loved his new toy truck.
Tim played with his new toy truck all day. He drove it on the floor, and made it go fast. He had so much fun. Tim was very happy with his new toy truck. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a kind woman who lived near a big forest. In the forest, there were many wild animals. The woman loved the animals very much and wanted to help them.
One day, a wild bird came to the woman's house. The bird was hurt and needed help. The woman wanted to bring the bird inside her house to make it feel better. She was very gentle and took good care of the bird.
The woman brought food and water for the wild bird every day. Soon, the bird started to feel better. The bird was very happy and sang a beautiful song for the woman. The woman and the wild bird became best friends and lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was an alligator named Ally. Ally was an honest alligator who lived in a big pond. She had many friends who liked to play with her.
One day, Ally's friend, a little bird named Benny, was worried. Benny said, "Ally, I lost my toy ball. I am sad." Ally wanted to help her friend, so she said, "Don't worry, Benny. I will help you find your ball."
They looked all around the pond, and finally, they found the ball near a tree. Benny was very happy and said, "Thank you, Ally, for being an honest and good friend." They played with the ball together and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a happy teacher named Miss Sue. Miss Sue loved to provide help to her little students. She liked to teach them math. Miss Sue's students also loved math. They always felt happy when they learned new things.
One day, a little girl named Lucy came to Miss Sue. "Miss Sue, can you help me with my math?" asked Lucy. Miss Sue smiled and said, "Of course, Lucy! I am here to provide help to you." Lucy felt happy because Miss Sue was always so nice.
Miss Sue and Lucy sat down and worked on math together. They counted and added numbers. Soon, Lucy understood the math. She said, "Thank you, Miss Sue! You made me happy!" Miss Sue smiled and said, "You're welcome, Lucy! I am happy to help you learn math." And they both had a happy day.

Once upon a time, there was a wild cat named Tom. Tom had a patch on his side that looked like a star. He lived in a small house with a girl named Sue.
One day, Sue found a book and she wanted to read it. But Tom the wild cat wanted to play. He jumped on the book and said, "Play with me, Sue!"
Sue looked at Tom and said, "No, I want to read my book!" Tom felt sad and ran away. He went outside and saw a bird. The bird saw Tom's star patch and said, "You have a pretty patch!" Tom felt happy again.
In the end, Sue finished her book and went outside to play with Tom. They played all day long and had a lot of fun. And every day after that, Sue would read her book and then play with Tom, and they were both very happy.

Tim liked to seat on his big chair and read his books. He had many books with pictures of animals and places. He wanted to see them for real one day. He also had a hat that his grandma gave him. It was blue and had a star on it. He always wore it when he read his books.
One day, he saw a book about lions. He liked lions. They were big and fierce and had a mane. He wanted to be like a lion. He put his hat on his head and said, "I am a lion. Hear me roar!" He roared very loud and ran around the house.
His mom and dad were not happy. They told him to stop roaring and running. They said he was making too much noise and mess. They said he was not a lion, he was a boy. They said he had to be nice and quiet and polite. They said he had to take off his hat and put it away.
Tim was sad and angry. He did not want to be a boy. He wanted to be a lion. He did not want to be nice and quiet and polite. He wanted to be fierce and loud and free. He did not want to take off his hat and put it away. He loved his hat. He said, "No! I am a lion. I will keep my hat. You can't make me!"
He ran away from his mom and dad. He ran to the door and opened it. He ran outside and into the street. He did not see the car that was coming fast. He did not hear the horn that was honking loud. He only felt the pain that hit him hard.
He woke up in the hospital. He had a bandage on his head and a cast on his leg. He saw his mom and dad by his bed. They looked sad and worried. They hugged him and kissed him and said, "We are so glad you are alive. We love you so much. We are sorry we were angry. We only wanted you to be safe."
Tim felt sorry too. He realized he was not a lion. He was a boy. He realized he had to listen to his mom and dad. They knew what was best for him. He realized he had to be careful and smart and kind. He said, "I love you too. I am sorry I was naughty. I will be good. I will do what you say."
He looked at his hat that was on the table. It was torn and dirty. He said, "Can I still keep my hat? It is my favorite hat. It reminds me of grandma."
His mom and dad smiled and said, "Of course you can keep your hat. It is a special hat. It reminds us of grandma too. But you don't have to wear it all the time. You can wear it when you read your books or when you play pretend. But you have to take it off when you go outside or when you eat or when you sleep."
Tim nodded and said, "OK. I will do that. Thank you for my hat. And thank you for saving me."
He put his hat on his head and said, "I am not a lion. I am a boy. But I can still roar sometimes. Can I roar now?"
His mom and dad laughed and said, "Yes, you can roar now. But only a little bit. And only in here. Not outside or in the street."
Tim roared a little bit and smiled. He was happy. He learned a lesson. He learned to be himself and to be careful and to love his family. He learned that being a boy was better than being a lion. He learned that his hat was not only a hat. It was a gift and a memory and a dream.

Once upon a time, in a big forest in the west, there was a little squirrel named Sammy. Sammy was a very helpful and reliable friend to all the animals. One day, Sammy had a big idea. He wanted to organize a fun party for all his friends in the forest.
Sammy went to each of his friends and told them about the party. He asked them to bring something to share. Sammy's friends were very excited and said, "Yes, Sammy, we will bring something!" Sammy knew his friends would not let him down, because they were all reliable too.
The day of the party came, and all the animals brought their treats. There were nuts, berries, and leaves for everyone to eat. Sammy's friends had a great time at the party, and they were so happy that Sammy organized it. The animals knew they could always count on Sammy, their reliable friend.

One day, a little girl named Lily was feeling very lucky. She had a big smile on her face. Lily wanted to do something fun. She dared her friend, Tim, to race her to the big tree. Tim said, "Okay, let's race!"
They ran as fast as they could. Lily and Tim were both very fast, but Lily was a little faster. When they got to the big tree, Lily said, "I won! That was fun!" Tim was happy too, even though he didn't win.
After the race, Lily and Tim were hungry. They went home to have lunch. Lily's mom made them yummy sandwiches. They ate and laughed together. Lily and Tim were so lucky to have each other as friends.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy cat named Tim. Tim did not like to stand up or play. He just liked to sleep all day. One day, Tim's friend, a dog named Sam, came to visit him. Sam was very good at math and liked to count.
"Tim, let's play a game," Sam said. "You stand up, and I will count how many steps you take."
Tim did not want to stand up, but he wanted to make Sam happy. So, he stood up and started to walk. Sam counted, "One, two, three, four..."
Just then, something unexpected happened. Tim found a big box of toys! Tim was so excited that he forgot he was lazy. He started to play with the toys and run around with Sam.
From that day on, Tim was not a lazy cat anymore. He loved to play with Sam and even learned some math. They had so much fun together, and they knew they would be best friends forever.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play baseball with his friends. Every day, they would run, throw, and catch the baseball. Tim was very happy when he played.
One day, Tim found a big, round gum. He thought it would be fun to chew it while he played. But the gum was very harsh. It was hard to chew and made his mouth hurt. Still, Tim kept chewing as he played baseball.
As they played, Tim's friends saw how hard it was for him to chew the harsh gum. They told him to stop and play without it. Tim listened to his friends and threw the gum away. He felt much better and played even better too. The moral of the story is to listen to your friends when they try to help you.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a white cat named Snow. Lily and Snow loved to play in the garden. The garden had many pretty flowers and soft grass. Lily and Snow were very happy there.
One sunny day, Lily and Snow were playing in the garden. A mild wind blew, making the flowers dance. Lily said to Snow, "I love you, Snow. You are my best friend." Snow meowed back, showing love to Lily.
As they played, a little boy named Tom came to the garden. Tom saw Lily and Snow playing and wanted to play too. Lily said, "Hi, Tom! Come play with us!" Tom smiled and joined them. They all played together in the garden, having a fun time. Lily, Snow, and Tom became best friends forever.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a boy named Tom. Tom had a flute that he loved to play. One day, while playing his flute, he saw a scary big dog.
Tom wanted to make friends with the dog, but he did not know how to behave. He thought the dog would like his flute, so he played it very loud. The scary dog did not like it and started to bark.
The dog became angry and chased Tom. Tom ran away, but he tripped and fell. The flute broke into pieces. Tom was sad, and the scary dog went away. Tom learned that he should behave differently when meeting new friends.

One day, a lively bunny named Bob went to the forest. He saw many fruit trees there. He saw red fruit, green fruit, and yellow fruit. He wanted to eat them all.
Bob met a bird named Lily. Lily said, "Hi, Bob! Do you like fruit too?" Bob said, "Yes, I love fruit! They seem so good!" Lily said, "Let's eat fruit together!" So, Bob and Lily ate fruit and played.
They had so much fun in the forest. They played hide and seek, and they sang songs. They were very happy. At the end of the day, they went home with full bellies and big smiles.

Once there was a little girl who was very wise. She loved writing stories and making pictures. One day, she found a magnet while she was playing with her toys. She thought it would be fun to draw pictures on the ground with it.
She took it outside and with the help of her mom she made lots of colorful signs on the sidewalk with it. The signs were so beautiful! Everyone who passed by smiled as they read them and admired her work.
"That's so cool!" said one of her friends who had stopped to take a look at her creations. "You're so wise for making such wonderful pieces!"
The little girl was so proud of herself for being able to write and use the magnet in such a creative way. She smiled and thanked her friend for their kind words. From that day on, the clever little girl used her writing skills and her magnet to make more beautiful pictures and stories.

John was playing in the park with his best friend Debbie. Suddenly, John started to jump up and down and point at something in the ground!
John said: "Look Debbie, a skull!"
John had found a deep, dark and scary looking skull. Debbie was scared. She asked:
"What is that?"
John replied with a proud smile on his face: "It's a skull! I was hoping to find one!"
Debbie started to cry. She said: "I don't like it, let's put it back where it came from!" 
John didn't want to put the skull back. He said: "But it's so cool! I want to keep it!"
Debbie started to get angry. She said: "No, it's not safe! Put it back!"
John didn't want to, he was really hoping to keep his special find! But he knew Debbie was right and reluctantly, he put it back where it came from.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. The sun was shining and the sky was full of big, white clouds. Lily loved to look at the clouds and find shapes in them. She saw one that looked like a bunny and another that looked like a big, round ball.
At the park, Lily met a new friend named Tim. They played together on the swings and the slide. Tim pointed up at the sky and said, "Look, that cloud looks like a big, scary monster!" Lily felt a little uncomfortable, but she did not want Tim to see that she was scared. She said, "No, it looks like a big, fluffy pillow!"
Later, as they played, the big, scary monster cloud got closer and closer. Suddenly, it started to rain! Lily and Tim ran to their moms, laughing and soaking wet. As they said goodbye, Lily looked up at the sky and saw that the big, scary monster cloud was now just a small, fluffy cloud. She smiled and knew that even scary things could turn out to be fun.

One day, a cute cat went outside. The cat saw a big rainbow in the sky. The rainbow had many colors. The cat was very happy to see the rainbow.
The cat looked at the rainbow and blinked. When the cat blinked, the rainbow moved. The cat blinked again, and the rainbow moved again. The cat thought this was fun.
The cat blinked and blinked, and the rainbow moved and moved. The cat played with the rainbow all day. When the sun went down, the rainbow went away. The cat went home, happy and tired.

Once upon a time, there were two best friends: Jack and Kate. Every day, they would play together and shared secrets. One day, Jack had something special. He had a pack of gum.
"Let's share it!", Kate said.
So, they split the gum into two and sat down to eat it. 
Suddenly, Jack had a fancy idea.
"Let's make a promise! Let's save the other half of the gum for tomorrow."
Kate thought it was a very good idea. "I promise!"
The next day, Jack waited for Kate at the park but she never showed up. Jack was so sad and angry. The sky was grey and gloomy. He realized that Kate broke the promise. Jack sadly went home and threw away the gum.
The moral of the story is that it's important to keep our promises. If we don't keep our promises, we could feel sad, angry, and gloomy.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a powerful man named Tom. Tom liked to polish things. One day, he saw a big door. The door was very dirty. Tom wanted to make the door clean and shiny.
Tom went to the door and said, "Hello, door! You are very dirty. I will polish you and make you clean." The door did not talk back, but Tom knew the door was happy.
Tom started to polish the door. He worked hard and the door became shiny. But then, the door fell down on Tom. It was too heavy for him. Tom was sad and hurt. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a big billboard. It had a long ladder next to it. A little boy named Tim saw the ladder and wanted to climb it. He thought it would be fun to be up high.
Tim started to climb the ladder. When he got to the top, he looked down and saw how high he was. He began to panic. "Help!" he cried. His mom heard him and came running. "Don't worry, Tim. I'll help you," she said.
Tim's mom carefully helped him get down. She hugged him and said, "It's not safe to climb things without asking for help first." Tim learned that he should always ask for help before doing something that could be dangerous.

James was a little boy who went to church every Sunday with his family. This Sunday was special, it was a rainy day and the birds were singing. James loved the rain and he wanted to go out and play in it, so he asked his parents if he could. His parents said that he could go out and play, but he had to be careful because the ground was very wet.
James was excited to go outside and he ran out of the house and into the garden. But when he stepped onto the grass he stepped onto an icy patch and slipped and fell.
James was scared and he rubbed his sore knee and started to cry. His mom came running out and she hugged him tightly. His dad said that it was time to go to church and maybe God could make his knee feel better.
James was scared at first but then he remembered that he could pray to God. He closed his eyes and prayed that God would make his knee better. After he stopped praying he thanked God, and then he looked down at his knee and he was happy to see that it was feeling much better!
James was glad that he had prayed as he knew it was a very special way of talking to God. He learned that it was not the things we do that make us feel better, but the faith and trust we put in God.

Once upon a time, there was a light bird who liked to fly high in the sky. One day, she saw a long way that went far away. She was very excited and wanted to see where the way went.
The light bird called her friend, the big dog, to come with her. They went on the long way together, running and flying. They saw many pretty flowers, green trees, and small bugs on their trip.
After a long time, they found a big, shiny pond at the end of the way. The light bird and the big dog were so happy. They played in the water and called their other friends to come and play too. They had the best day ever!

Once upon a time, a little baby lay in his crib. The baby was very gentle and sweet. One day, a big dog came into the room. The dog saw the baby and wagged his tail. The baby saw the dog and started to tremble.
"Don't be scared," said the dog. "I am a nice dog. I will not hurt you." The baby stopped trembling and smiled at the dog. The dog was happy and jumped around the room.
Then, something unexpected happened. The dog found a toy under the crib. The toy was a small dog that looked just like the big dog! The big dog was so surprised and happy. He brought the toy to the baby, and they played together all day long.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a boy named Tom. Tom was a lucky boy because he had a big fruit tree in his yard. The tree had many yummy fruits on it.
One day, Tom saw a girl named Sue standing near the tree. Sue was looking at the fruits, but she did not dare to pick them. Tom asked her, "Why don't you dare to pick the fruits?" Sue said, "I am scared that I might fall."
Tom wanted to help Sue. He said, "Don't be scared. I will help you. We can pick the fruits together." Sue felt happy and with Tom's help, they picked the fruits safely.
In the end, Tom and Sue shared the fruits with their friends. They all had a fun time eating the fruits together. The moral of the story is that it's good to help others and share what we have.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a rare, beautiful bird. The bird had many colors and liked to sing. Every day, the bird would fly and play with its friends.
One day, the bird saw a pretty sunset. The sky was red and orange. The bird was very happy. It wanted to tell its friends about the sunset. So, the bird went to find them.
The bird found its friends and whispered, "Look, a pretty sunset!" The friends looked at the sunset and smiled. They all enjoyed the beautiful sight together. And from that day on, they always watched the sunset together.

Once upon a time, in a big open field, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to dance and play with her friends. One sunny day, Lily put on her pretty dress and went to the field to dance.
As she danced, a small rabbit hopped over to her. "Hello, little rabbit," said Lily. "Do you want to dance with me?" The rabbit nodded and began to dance with Lily. They twirled and jumped together, having so much fun.
Suddenly, the rabbit stopped dancing and looked scared. "What's wrong?" asked Lily. The rabbit whispered, "My friend, the butterfly, has vanished!" Lily wanted to help, so they both searched the open field. They found the butterfly hiding behind a big rock. The butterfly was happy to be found and joined them in their dance. From that day on, Lily, the rabbit, and the butterfly became best friends and danced together in the open field every day.

One day, a funny cat named Zigzag went for a walk. He saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. So, he jumped and went up, up, up. When he reached the top, he looked down and saw his friend, a small dog named Spot.
"Hey Spot!" Zigzag called. "Look at me! I am up in the tree!"
Spot looked up and said, "Wow, Zigzag! You are so high! Be careful!"
Suddenly, the wind blew and the tree started to shake. Zigzag got scared and tried to hold on, but he fell down. But, just before he could hit the ground, a big, soft blanket covered the ground. Zigzag landed on the blanket, safe and sound.
The wind had blown the blanket from a nearby house. Zigzag and Spot laughed and played on the blanket. They were very happy that something unexpected had saved the day.

One day, a tall clown came to play. He had a big bag of toys. He wanted to scatter the toys for the kids. The kids were very happy to see the tall clown and his toys.
But, a big dog came and barked at the tall clown. The tall clown was scared. He did not want to scatter the toys anymore. The kids were sad and wanted to help the tall clown.
The kids gave the big dog a bone. The big dog was happy and stopped barking. The tall clown was not scared anymore. He scattered the toys for the kids. They all played and had fun together.

One day, a girl named Lily was playing with her toys. She saw an empty box and had an idea. She made the box into a pretend phone. Lily was very happy with her new toy phone.
Lily went to her mom and said, "Mom, look at my new phone! Can I talk to Grandma on it?" Her mom smiled and said, "That's a nice phone, but you can't really talk on it. It's just pretend."
Lily felt a little sad but still played with her pretend phone. She went outside and saw her friend, Tim. Tim saw her phone and asked, "Can I talk on your phone too?" Lily said, "It's just pretend, but we can talk to each other with it!"
So, Lily and Tim played with the empty box phone all day. They talked to each other and had lots of fun. In the end, Lily was happy that she could still have fun with her pretend phone.

Tom and Lily were twins who liked to play and laugh. They did not like to eat broccoli. Broccoli was green and hard and smelled bad. They always tried to hide it under their plates or give it to their dog.
One day, their mom made a big pot of broccoli soup for lunch. She said it was good for them and they had to eat it all. Tom and Lily made faces and said no. They did not want broccoli soup. They wanted pizza or cookies or ice cream.
Mom said they could not have anything else until they finished their soup. She put two bowls of soup on the table and went to the kitchen. Tom and Lily looked at the soup and had an idea. They decided to switch their bowls with each other. They thought they were very clever and mom would not notice.
They started to swap their bowls, but they did not see that their dog was under the table. He smelled the soup and jumped up. He knocked over the bowls and spilled the soup all over Tom and Lily. They screamed and got wet and sticky. Mom heard the noise and came back. She saw the mess and the dog licking the soup. She was angry and said, "What are you doing? You are very foolish. Now you have no soup and no pizza or cookies or ice cream. You have to clean up and go to your room."
Tom and Lily felt sorry and ashamed. They said sorry to mom and helped her clean up. They went to their room and cried. They wished they had eaten their broccoli soup. They learned their lesson and never tried to switch their bowls again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. She loved to ride her bike. She rode her bike every day. Jane had a neighbor named Tom. Tom was her best friend. They liked to ride bikes together.
One day, Jane saw a fake snake on the ground. She got scared and fell off her bike. Tom laughed and said, "It's just a fake snake, Jane! Don't be scared." Jane felt silly for being scared of a fake snake. Tom helped her get back on her bike.
The next day, Tom saw a real snake on the ground. He thought it was another fake snake, so he didn't get scared. But the real snake bit Tom! Jane helped Tom and told him, "We should always be careful, even if we think something is fake." Tom learned that it's important to be careful and not always assume things are fake.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a little girl named Amy. She had a friend named Ben. They loved to play together every day.
One day, Amy had a request for Ben. She said, "Ben, can you help me make a big snowman? My body is too small to lift the heavy snow." Ben was happy to help his friend. They started to make the snowman together.
As they worked, a boy named Carl came by. He was ignorant and didn't know how to make a snowman. He asked, "What are you doing?" Amy said, "We are making a snowman. Do you want to help?" Carl was excited and said, "Yes, please!"
So, Amy, Ben, and Carl made the snowman together. They had so much fun and learned to work as a team. The snowman was big and happy. The three friends played together all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a kind, old dog named Max. Max had a job to deliver big boxes to his friends. One day, he had a big box to deliver to his friend, Cat.
On his way, Max met a mean bird. The bird said, "Max, if you don't give me your box, I will be a big threat to you!" Max was scared but he knew he had to deliver the box to Cat.
Max thought of a plan. He told the bird, "I will give you a bigger box if you wait here." The bird agreed. Max went to Cat's house and gave her the big box. Then, he found a big, old box and put a scary toy inside. He took the old box to the mean bird. When the bird opened the box, the scary toy jumped out! The bird got scared and flew away. Max was very happy that he could deliver the box to Cat and also trick the mean bird.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl. She was 3 years old and loved to wander around. Every day, her mom would give her time to explore the world around her. 
One day, while she was wandering, she found a clean lake. She was so excited and ran to tell her mom. "Mom, mom! I found a clean lake! Can we go there?" 
Her mom smiled and agreed. So they went to the clean lake and spent the day playing. 
The little girl was so happy. She enjoyed spending time at the clean lake and she vowed that she would come back every day to wander around and explore.

Once upon a time, a little dog named Spot had a big, boring day. Spot was sad because all of his friends were busy. He wanted to play, but no one was there to play with him.
Spot decided to leave his house and go for a walk. As he walked, he saw a ball. He used his paw to push the ball. The ball rolled and rolled. It was so much fun!
Then, Spot saw a little girl named Sue. Sue was bored too. Spot and Sue played with the ball together. They had a great day. Spot was not bored anymore. They became best friends and played every day.

Once upon a time, there was a big, pink pig. The pig had soft skin. It lived on a farm with many other animals. The pig liked to play with all its friends. They had a lot of fun together.
One day, the pink pig saw a little, lost duck. The duck was sad and needed help. The pig wanted to help the duck find its family. So, the pig decided to lead the duck around the farm to look for its family.
They walked and walked, looking for the duck's family. The pink pig led the duck past the cows, the horses, and the chickens. Finally, they found the duck's family near the pond. The duck was so happy to see its family again. The pink pig was happy too, because it had helped its new friend.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Tom and Tom. They liked to count together, and one day they were counting the clouds in the sky. 
Tom said, "Let's count how many clouds there are!"
Tom said, "Let's see, one, two, three..."
As they were counting, they noticed something different in the clouds. They noticed something strange and amusing. 
Tom said, "What is that?"
Tom said, "I think it's a very fine texture!" 
His friend was surprised, "What do you mean?" 
Tom said, "I think it's a type of material that looks like feathers! It's light and airy and has different colors too!"
Tom smiled, "This fine texture looks like it's here to stay."
The two friends began counting the different colors and shapes of the clouds, and they had so much fun that they forgot to count the clouds in the sky!

Once upon a time, there was a bright little star. The star lived in the sky at night. The star had many friends.
One day, the star wanted to organize a party for all its friends. The star asked the moon, the clouds, and other stars to come to the party. They all said yes.
At the party, everyone had a lot of fun. They played games and danced. The bright star was very happy. The night was filled with laughter and joy. And they all became better friends.

Once there was a boy named George and a girl named Naomi. They were best friends that loved to play together.
One day they decided to explore the forest. As they walked deeper and deeper, they were both amazed by the beautiful view.
Naomi then said, "Let's race each other to the top of the hill!"
George accepted her challenge and they both ran with all their strength. It was a competitive race but in the end Naomi won!
George was surprised by her win and said, "Wow, you're so fast, Naomi!"
Naomi grinned and said, "Thank you, George."
Unfortunately, as Naomi reached the top, she slipped and fell down the hill. George rushed to the bottom and saw that she was hurt. He tried to help her, but it was too late.
Naomi was gone and with her went all their fun memories. George was so sad and surprised.
