Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was an adventurous boy who loved to play outside. One day, Tim wanted to see a big mountain. So, he asked his mom to show him the way to the mountain. His mom said, "Okay, let's go!"
Tim and his mom walked and walked until they saw the big mountain. Tim was so happy to see it. They started to climb up the mountain together. It was hard work, but Tim was brave and strong.
When they reached the top of the mountain, Tim and his mom looked all around. They could see the whole world! Tim felt very proud. He had shown his mom that he was a big, adventurous boy. They went back home and told everyone about their fun day on the big mountain.

One day, a cold wind blew. A boy named Tom put on his coat to go outside. He wanted to jog to stay warm. As he ran, he saw his friend, Sue.
"Hi Sue!" said Tom. "It's cold! Do you want to jog with me?"
Sue said, "Yes! Let's jog and make a game. We can play war with sticks."
Tom and Sue found two sticks. They jogged and played war. They laughed and had fun. The cold wind did not bother them.
After a while, they got tired. They stopped playing war and went home. They knew they could play and jog together, even on cold days.

One day, a little girl named Lucy was playing in her room. She was very enthusiastic, jumping and laughing. She loved to play with her toys and make up stories. Her daddy was in the living room, reading a book.
Lucy wanted to show her daddy her new dance. She ran to the living room and said, "Daddy, Daddy! Look at my dance!" Daddy looked up from his book and smiled. "Okay, Lucy. Show me your dance."
Lucy danced and twirled around the room. Daddy clapped and cheered for her. When she was done, Daddy said, "That was a great dance, Lucy! But now, you must remain in your room and play quietly. I need to finish my book."
Lucy nodded and went back to her room. She played quietly with her toys, but she was still enthusiastic. She knew that when Daddy was done with his book, they could play together again. And that made her very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy fish named Bob. He lived in a beautiful reef with his friends. Bob liked to sleep all day and play all night. His friends always told him to wake up and play with them during the day.
One day, Bob's friend, Sally the seahorse, said, "Bob, please remove the big rock from my home. It is too heavy for me." Bob, being lazy, did not want to help. But he saw that Sally was sad, so he decided to help her.
Bob swam to Sally's home and pushed the big rock away. It was hard work, but he did it! Sally was so happy and said, "Thank you, Bob! You are a good friend." From that day on, Bob was not so lazy anymore. He played with his friends during the day and slept at night, just like everyone else.

Once upon a time, there was a little angel named Amy. She was a happy angel who loved to make others happy too. Amy lived in a beautiful sky with her angel friends.
One day, Amy saw a sad boy on Earth. She wanted to help him change his sad face into a happy one. So, Amy flew down to Earth and gave the boy a big, warm hug. The boy smiled and felt happy again.
Amy learned that helping others can make a big change in their lives. She flew back to the sky and told her angel friends. They all decided to help more people on Earth. And from that day on, the angels made many people happy. The moral of the story is: be kind and help others, and you can change their lives for the better.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy dog named Max. Max loved to polish his red ball. He would polish it every day so it would look shiny and new. One day, when Max was polishing his ball, a big wind came. It was a hurricane!
The hurricane blew Max's ball far away. Max was sad and didn't know what to do. He looked everywhere for his ball, but he couldn't find it. Then, Max met a friendly cat named Luna. Luna saw Max was sad and asked, "Why are you sad, Max?"
Max told Luna about his red ball and the hurricane. Luna wanted to help Max, so they looked for the ball together. They found the ball near a big tree. Max was so happy! He thanked Luna and they became best friends. From that day on, Max and Luna played together and Max always polished his red ball to keep it shiny.

One day, a fat cat named Tom went outside to play. The weather was nice and sunny. Tom saw a big tree with a bird on top. He wanted to taste the bird, so he started to climb the tree.
"Stop, Tom!" said the bird. "I don't want you to taste me!" Tom did not listen and kept climbing. The bird was scared and flew away to another tree. Tom was sad, but he still wanted to taste the bird.
Tom tried to jump to the other tree, but something unexpected happened. He fell into a big pile of leaves! The bird saw this and laughed. "You can't taste me now, Tom!" But then, a big dog came and scared the bird away. Tom was happy because the dog saved him. The dog and Tom became friends, and they played together in the nice weather.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy had a big, empty box. She liked to put her toys in the box. One day, Amy found a brush. The brush was small and soft. She put the brush in the empty box.
Amy's friend, Tim, came over to play. Tim saw the empty box and wanted to play too. He picked up the brush and started to poke the box. Amy laughed and poked the box too. They both poked the box with the brush, making funny sounds.
After a while, they got tired of poking the box. Amy and Tim put the brush back in the empty box. They played with other toys and had a fun day. When it was time for Tim to go home, they said goodbye and put all the toys back in the empty box.

Once upon a time, there was a big festival in a small town. The festival had fun games, yummy food, and lots of happy people. One day, a little girl named Lucy went to the festival with her mom and dad. Lucy was very excited to play games and eat ice cream.
At the festival, Lucy saw a big, scary dog. She was frightened and ran away from the dog. She ran and ran until she found a small, quiet place. In that place, Lucy found a small box. She decided to bury the box in the ground, so she used her hands to dig a hole and put the box inside. Then, she covered the box with dirt.
Lucy's mom and dad found her and asked, "What are you doing, Lucy?" Lucy said, "I buried a box I found. I was frightened by the big, scary dog." Her mom hugged her and said, "Don't worry, Lucy. We will keep you safe." They all went back to the festival, and Lucy had lots of fun playing games and eating ice cream with her mom and dad.

John was walking in the park one sunny afternoon. He was very thoughtful and stopped by the pond. Suddenly, a big balloon appeared. John was so excited and was about to grab it when a voice shouted, "No!" John looked around and saw an old man, who said, "That balloon is mine." John knew he had to go and was about to leave when the old man said, "Wait, I have something for you." He threw a balloon to John and said, "It's a thank you."
John was so happy and thanked the old man. He started to run away with the balloon until he suddenly stopped and felt sad. He turned and saw that the old man had disappeared. John felt confused, his thoughtfulness had not helped him. He had lost the old man, and he now had to go home empty-handed.

Once upon a time, there was a nice queen. She lived in a big castle with a lot of people who support her. The queen liked to help everyone and make them happy.
One day, the queen found a small, lost cat. She took the cat home to the castle. The queen and the cat became best friends. They played together and the cat was very happy. But the queen did not know that the cat had a secret.
The cat was really a bad witch in disguise. The witch wanted to take the queen's castle for herself. The queen did not know this and kept being nice to the cat. One day, the witch showed her true self and took the castle away from the queen.
The queen was very sad. She learned that she should not trust everyone so easily. The people who support her tried to help, but the witch was too strong. The queen lost her castle and her people, all because she was too nice to the wrong person.
The moral of the story is to be careful who you trust. Not everyone is as nice as they seem.

Once upon a time there was a small town. All the people in the town were very nice, except for one man. He was always very rude. One day, he was walking down the street and he saw some kids playing. He yelled at them to be quiet and get out of the way.
The kids were very sad and they ran away. One of them, Samantha, was so mad that she yelled back at the man. She said, "It's not nice to yell at kids! You should be more polite!"
The man was so surprised that a little girl had the courage to stand up to him that he didn't know what to say. He just walked away.
The people in the town were very proud of Samantha for standing up for the kids. From then on, the man always tried to be polite and to use kinder words when talking to people. 
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to read. He read all day long. One day, Tim went outside to play. He saw some soil. It was soft and brown. Tim thought it would be fun to read outside.
Tim sat down on the soil. He read a story about a slow turtle. The turtle was so slow that it took him a long time to get places. Tim felt bad for the turtle. He wished he could help the turtle go faster.
As Tim read, something unexpected happened. The slow turtle from the story came to life! The turtle popped out of the book and landed on the soil. Tim was so surprised! He didn't know that his wish had come true. Tim and the turtle became best friends. They played together every day. Tim was happy that his love for reading brought him a new friend.

One day, a fish named Tim wanted to swim to the theater. He heard that there was a loud show there. Tim was very excited. He swam very fast to get there.
When Tim got to the theater, he saw a big crab. The crab was very loud too. The crab said, "You can't go in, little fish. This show is only for big sea animals!" Tim was sad and asked, "Please, can I go in?"
The crab thought for a moment and then said, "Okay, you can go in. But you have to promise to be quiet during the show." Tim was very happy and said, "I promise!" So Tim went into the theater and enjoyed the loud show with all the big sea animals.

Once upon a time, there was a smart boy named Tim. Tim loved to play chess. He would play chess every day with his friends. Tim was very good at chess and always won.
One day, Tim wanted to walk to the park to play chess. He put his chess set in a bag and began to walk. As he walked, he saw a big dog. The dog looked at Tim and wanted to play too.
Tim tried to walk away, but the dog followed him. The dog saw the chess set and wanted to play. But the dog did not know how to play chess. The dog jumped on the chess set and broke it. Tim was very sad and had no more chess set to play with. The smart boy could not play chess anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a large bird who loved to fly high in the sky. Every day, the bird would wake up at sunrise and fly around to find food. One day, the bird met a little mouse who was very scared.
The little mouse said, "Please, Mr. Bird, don't eat me!" The large bird replied, "I will not eat you, little mouse. But you must help me find food for my family."
So, the mouse helped the bird find food every day. They looked for food together and became good friends. But one day, the mouse could not find any food for the bird's family.
The large bird was very sad and hungry. He looked at the little mouse and thought about his family. Then, the bird replied, "I am sorry, little mouse. I must eat you to feed my family."
And so, the large bird ate the little mouse. The bird's family had food, but the bird lost his friend. It was a bad ending for the little mouse and the bird was very sad.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a boy named Tom. Tom had an unusual tie. It was not like the other ties people wear. It was colorful and had many shapes on it. Tom liked to wear his tie every day.
One sunny day, Tom went to the park. He saw a girl named Lily. Lily saw Tom's unusual tie and said, "Hi Tom, I like your tie. It's very pretty." Tom smiled and said, "Thank you, Lily. I like it too."
Tom and Lily played in the park all day. They became good friends. They were happy that they met each other. From that day on, Tom and Lily always played together, and Tom's unusual tie became a special part of their friendship.

John and his mommy were walking in the woods one day. They saw an ancient vest lying on the ground. John wanted to bring it home, so his mommy said he could.
When they got home, John wanted to bury the vest in the garden. He dug a big, deep hole and put the vest inside. Then he scooped up all the dirt and pushed it back into the hole until the vest was buried. 
John smiled. He liked burying things and feeling the dirt on his hands. Now he had a special memory because of the ancient vest.

Once upon a time, there was a bear called Jack. Jack was very active and loved to do exercises. One day, he was running through the forest when he noticed something difficult. What Jack saw was a big rock. Jack wondered how he could move the rock. He looked around and noticed a rabbit hopping nearby.
Jack used his friendly voice and asked the rabbit, "Hi there! Can you help me move the rock?". The rabbit replied, "Yes, of course! Let me show you."
The rabbit hopped towards the rock and showed Jack what to do. He explained that if they both touched the rock, they could move it. Jack thought that this was a brilliant idea and put his paws on the rock. He and the rabbit pushed and pulled together. With a lot of hard work and determination, they managed to move the rock out of the way!
Jack was amazed and thanked the rabbit for the help. The rabbit smiled and said, "It was a difficult exercise but we did it together!". Jack was very proud of himself for doing something difficult.

Lily and Ben were playing with their toys in the living room. They had many cars, dolls, blocks and animals to play with. But Lily wanted to play with Ben's dinosaur, and Ben wanted to play with Lily's pony. They both reached for the toy they wanted, and pulled hard.
"Give me the dinosaur!" Lily shouted.
"No, it's mine!" Ben shouted back.
They pulled and pulled, until snap! The dinosaur's tail broke off, and the pony's mane came off. Lily and Ben looked at their toys, and felt very sad. They had broken their toys, and now they could not play with them.
They started to cry, and their mom came to see what was wrong. She saw the broken toys, and the angry faces of Lily and Ben. She sighed, and said:
"Lily, Ben, why did you break your toys? You should share and be kind to each other. Toys are not fun if you fight over them. Now, look what you have done. You have made yourselves unhappy, and your toys unhappy too."
She went to get some glue, and said:
"Come, let's fix your toys. But you have to promise me something. You have to promise to say sorry to each other, and to play nicely. Can you do that?"
Lily and Ben nodded, and said:
"Yes, mom. We are sorry. We will share and be kind."
Mom smiled, and helped them glue the dinosaur's tail and the pony's mane. She gave them a hug, and said:
"That's good. I'm proud of you. Now, your toys are happy again, and so are you. Remember, sharing and kindness are better than fighting and breaking."
Lily and Ben hugged their mom, and thanked her. They hugged their toys, and said sorry to them too. Then, they played together with their toys, and had lots of fun. They learned their lesson, and never fought over their toys again.
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a man with a big mustache. He was very sad because no one believed he was nice. They all thought his mustache made him look mean. One day, he decided to do something good to show everyone that he was a nice man.
The man with the mustache saw a cat stuck in a tree. He wanted to help the cat. He climbed the tree and saved the cat. Everyone saw him save the cat, and they were very surprised. They did not think the man with the mustache would do something so kind.
After that day, the people started to believe that the man with the mustache was nice. The man was not sad anymore. He learned that doing good things can change what people think of you. And everyone else learned that you should not judge someone by how they look.

One day, a little boy named Mark went to the park with his mom. They saw a big tree with delicious red apples on it. Mark gazed at the apples and wanted to eat one.
"Mom, can I have an apple?" Mark asked. His mom smiled and said, "Yes, let's pick one together."
They picked a big, delicious apple from the tree. Mark took a big bite and smiled. "This is so yummy!" he said. They enjoyed the apple together and gazed at the beautiful day in the park.

Lily and Tom liked to play near the creek. The creek had water and fish and frogs. They liked to catch the fish and frogs and put them in a bucket. Sometimes they pretended to sell them to other kids.
One day, they went to the creek, but it was dry. There was no water, no fish, no frogs. Only mud and rocks. Lily and Tom were sad.
"Where did the water go?" Lily asked.
"Maybe the sun drank it all," Tom said.
"Maybe the rain forgot to come," Lily said.
They looked around for something else to play with. They saw some sticks and leaves and flowers. They had an idea.
"Let's make a shop," Tom said.
"OK, what can we sell?" Lily asked.
"We can sell sticks for swords, leaves for hats, and flowers for necklaces," Tom said.
They gathered the sticks, leaves, and flowers and arranged them on a big rock. They made a sign that said "Shop" with a crayon. They waited for customers.
Soon, a boy came by. He saw the shop and stopped.
"Hello, do you want to buy something?" Lily asked.
"What do you have?" the boy asked.
"We have swords, hats, and necklaces," Tom said.
"How much are they?" the boy asked.
"One penny each," Lily said.
The boy looked at the sticks, leaves, and flowers. He smiled.
"OK, I'll buy a sword and a hat," he said.
He gave Lily and Tom two pennies. He took a stick and a leaf. He put the leaf on his head and waved the stick.
"Thank you, this is fun," he said.
He ran away, pretending to be a knight.
Lily and Tom were happy. They had sold something. They hoped more kids would come to their shop. They did not mind the dry creek anymore. They had found a new way to play.

Once upon a time, there was a smooth rock. It was very round and shiny. The rock lived by a big tree in a park. Every day, kids would come and play with the rock. They would throw it, kick it, and roll it around. The rock liked to play with the kids too.
One day, a boy named Tim found a long stick. The stick was called a spear. Tim picked up the spear and played with it. He threw it high in the sky and watched it fall back down. The rock saw Tim and began to wonder what it would be like to fly like the spear.
The rock asked Tim, "Can you help me fly like the spear?" Tim smiled and said, "Sure, I can help you!" Tim took the smooth rock and the spear, and he tied them together with a strong string. Then, he threw the spear up in the sky, and the rock flew with it. The rock was so happy to fly like the spear, and it thanked Tim for his help. From that day on, the rock and Tim became best friends, and they played together every day.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play outside with their toys. One day, they see a big truck in front of their house. The truck has many lovely flowers in it. Anna and Ben want to buy some flowers for their mom.
They run to the truck and ask the man, "Can we buy some flowers, please?"
The man smiles and says, "Sure, you can. How much money do you have?"
Anna and Ben show him their piggy bank. They have saved some coins from their dad. The man counts the coins and says, "You have enough to buy two flowers. Which ones do you like?"
Anna and Ben look at the flowers. They are all different colors and shapes. Anna likes the pink ones. Ben likes the yellow ones. They point to the flowers they want and the man gives them to them.
"Thank you, mister!" Anna and Ben say.
"You're welcome, kids. Have a nice day!" the man says.
Anna and Ben run back to their house. They are very happy with their flowers. They want to surprise their mom. They find a vase and fill it with water. They put the flowers in the vase and place it on the table.
Then they wait for their mom to come home. They hide behind the sofa and whisper, "Shh, it's a secret!"
When their mom comes home, she sees the flowers on the table. She is very surprised and happy. She says, "Wow, what lovely flowers! Who gave them to me?"
Anna and Ben jump out from behind the sofa and say, "We did, mommy! We bought them for you with our money!"
Their mom hugs them and kisses them. She says, "Thank you, Anna and Ben! You are the best twins ever! I love you and I love the flowers!"
Anna and Ben smile and say, "We love you too, mommy!"

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue had a toy bird that she loved very much. One day, she put the bird on a hanger to keep it safe.
Sue's mom said, "Sue, don't put your bird on the hanger. It might fall." But Sue did not listen and said, "I want my bird to be safe." She left the bird on the hanger and went to play outside.
While Sue was gone, her bird fell from the hanger. A poor cat saw the bird and thought it was real. The cat tried to eat the bird, but it could not. The bird could talk and said, "Please don't eat me, I am just a toy." The cat was very surprised and said, "I am sorry, I did not know." The cat put the bird back on the hanger.
When Sue came back, she saw her bird on the hanger and the cat. The bird said, "Sue, the cat was nice to me." Sue was happy to see her bird was safe and said, "Thank you, cat, for being nice to my bird."
From that day on, Sue and the cat became friends. Sue learned to listen to her mom, and she never put her bird on the hanger again. The cat would visit Sue and her bird, and they would all play together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to walk to the park with his mom. They would walk and walk until they saw the big slide.
One day, Tim saw a cat by the slide. The cat was very fit and fast. Tim wanted to play with the cat. So, he walked up to the slide with the cat.
Tim and the cat went down the slide together. They had a lot of fun. They walked and played all day. Tim was happy he found a new friend at the slide.

Once upon a time, there was a colorful bird. The bird had a friend, a little boy. They liked to play in the sun. One day, the boy saw a small fire. The fire started to burn.
The boy and the bird wanted to stop the fire. The bird flew to get water. The boy tried to kick dirt on the fire. But, he hurt his toe. The boy was sad.
Then, the bird came back with water. The water put out the fire. The boy's toe felt better. The bird and the boy were happy. They played in the sun again.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy daisy. She loved peeking around and watching other bugs. One day, she saw a bee flying by. She was so nosy that she followed the bee. Suddenly, she heard a loud noise! It was a shoot! The daisy ducked and hid behind a leaf. Then, she saw a big grasshopper with a shoot. He was shooting at the bee! The daisy was so scared that she ran away. She never came back to that place again. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play and act like a big, dangerous lion. She would run around the house and roar like a lion. Her friends, Doggy and Bunny, would laugh and play with her.
One day, Kitty saw a big shadow outside her house. It looked like a threat! Kitty was scared, but she remembered how she acted like a dangerous lion. She thought, "I can be brave like a lion too!" So, she went outside to see what the big shadow was.
When Kitty went outside, she saw that the big shadow was just a big tree branch. It was not a threat at all! Kitty was happy and proud that she was brave like a lion. She ran back inside to tell Doggy and Bunny what she found. They all laughed and played together, and Kitty knew she could be brave when she needed to be.

Once upon a time, there was a lively little cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play in the sun all day. One day, Kitty saw a big tree and wanted to climb it.
Kitty climbed and climbed until she reached the top. At the top, she saw a bird. The bird said, "Hi, Kitty! Be careful of your sharp claws on the tree." Kitty said, "Thank you, bird. I will be careful."
Kitty played on the tree for a while, but then she wanted to go home. She looked down and saw a path to the left. She climbed down the tree and took the left path. At the end of the path, she found her home.
Kitty was happy to be home and told her mom about her adventure. Her mom said, "I'm glad you had fun, but always be careful with your sharp claws." Kitty agreed and they lived happily ever after.

One day, a boy named Tim went to a loud event with his mom. At the event, there were many games and fun things to do. Tim was very happy to be there. He saw a big game with a long line of kids waiting to play. Tim asked his mom, "Can I play too?" His mom said, "Yes, you can play. Let's wait in line."
While they were waiting, Tim saw a big red button on the game. He wondered what it did. When it was finally Tim's turn to play, he was so excited! He had to complete a puzzle before the time ran out. As he was playing, he couldn't stop thinking about the big red button.
Just as Tim was about to complete the puzzle, he decided to press the big red button. Suddenly, the loud event went quiet. All the games stopped, and everyone looked at Tim. It turns out, the big red button was for turning off all the games. Tim felt bad, but his mom said, "It's okay, accidents happen." Soon, the games started again, and everyone went back to having fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was an adventurous boy who loved to play and explore. One day, Tim found a big iron in his house. He did not know what it was for, but he thought it looked interesting.
Tim took the iron to his friend, Sue. He showed her the iron and said, "Look what I found! Do you know what it is?" Sue looked at the iron and said, "I don't know, but it's big and shiny. Let's play with it!" So, Tim and Sue played with the iron all day, pretending it was a magic wand.
As the sun started to set, Tim's mom came outside to find him. She saw Tim and Sue playing with the iron and said, "Oh no, that's not a toy! That's an iron we use to make clothes smooth. It can get very hot and it's not safe for you to play with." Tim and Sue looked at each other and knew they should not play with the iron anymore. They gave it back to Tim's mom and promised to be more careful. From that day on, Tim and Sue continued to have many adventurous days, but they always made sure to play with safe toys.

Once upon a time, there was a dry curtain in a house. It was old and dusty, and it swayed slightly in the breeze from the window.
Suddenly, a small boy called Mike walked up to the curtain and started to pull at it. His mother was nearby and said "No Mike, ignore the curtain." But Mike was too curious and pulled it even more.
Little did he know, but the curtain had a secret. As Mike gave it one last tug, a secret door opened up!
Startled, Mike stepped back and ignored the door, but his curiosity was too strong. He stepped back up to the door and had another look. Then, with a smile on his face, he stepped through the doorway.
He had discovered a whole new world behind the curtain! Mike spent all afternoon exploring and playing, and soon became friends with the creatures he found there.
When his mother finally came to take him home, he told her all about his adventure. She listened carefully and hugged him tight.
No matter what happens, Mike will never ignore a curtain again!

Once upon a time, there was a person named Tim. Tim liked to rock on his chair. One day, while rocking, he saw a little bird outside his window. The bird was very pretty and sang a nice song.
Tim wanted to talk to the bird. He opened the window and said, "Hi, bird! I like your song. Can you teach me to sing like you?" The bird looked at Tim and said, "You are a person, not a bird. You can't sing like me, but I can teach you a fun dance."
Tim was a little ignorant and didn't know what the bird meant. But he was happy to learn something new. So, he danced with the bird, and they both had a great time. Tim learned that even if he couldn't sing like the bird, he could still have fun and make new friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little ant named Tim. Tim had a goal. He wanted to find the biggest leaf in the forest. He thought it would make his family happy. Tim looked for a long time, but he could not find it.
One day, Tim saw a big bug named Bob. Bob was staring at a very long leaf. Tim asked Bob if he could have the leaf for his family. Bob said, "Yes, but you must help me carry it." Tim and Bob worked together to carry the long leaf back to Tim's family.
When they got there, Tim's family was so happy! They all said "thank you" to Bob. Tim learned that working together with others can help reach your goal. And that was the best thing he could do for his family.

Tim and Mia are friends. They like to play in the school. One day, they find a desk in the hall. The desk is big and brown. It has many drawers.
"Look, Tim, a desk!" Mia says. "Let's open the drawers and see what is inside."
"OK, Mia, but we have to be quick. The teacher will come soon." Tim says. They run to the desk and pull the drawers.
The first drawer has papers and pens. The second drawer has books and stickers. The third drawer has nothing. The fourth drawer has something wet and slimy.
"Yuck, what is this?" Mia says. She touches the wet thing. It feels cold and sticky. She screams.
Tim looks at the wet thing. It is a frog. The frog jumps out of the drawer and lands on Tim's face. He screams too.
The teacher hears the screams and comes to the hall. She sees Tim and Mia and the frog and the desk. She is angry.
"Tim and Mia, what are you doing?" She says. "This is not your desk. This is Mr. Green's desk. He is the science teacher. He keeps his frog in the drawer. You should not touch his things. You are in trouble."
She takes Tim and Mia and the frog to the office. She closes the door. Tim and Mia are sad. They have to stay in the office and say sorry to Mr. Green. They do not like the frog. They do not like the desk. They do not like the wet thing. They wish they did not open the drawers. The end.

Lily and Anna are sisters. They like to play in the park. They have a ball and a doll. They are happy.
One day, a big dog comes to the park. It is unknown. It has black fur and sharp teeth. It barks loud. Lily and Anna are scared.
The dog runs to them. It wants the ball. It jumps and snatches the ball from Lily's hand. Lily cries. Anna hugs her.
"Go away, dog!" Anna shouts. "You are bad! You hurt my sister!"
The dog growls. It wants the doll too. It moves closer to Anna and Lily. Anna holds the doll tight. She tries to protect it and her sister.
Then, a man comes to the park. He is the dog's owner. He sees the dog and the sisters. He calls the dog.
"Rex, come here! Bad dog! Leave the girls alone!"
The dog hears the man. It drops the ball and runs to the man. The man puts a leash on the dog. He says sorry to Lily and Anna.
"I'm sorry, girls. Rex is a new dog. He doesn't know how to play nice. He likes balls and dolls, but he shouldn't take them from you. Are you okay?"
Lily and Anna nod. They are still scared, but they are glad the dog is gone. They take the ball and the doll. They hug each other.
"Thank you, man," Anna says. "You are good. You helped us."
The man smiles. He walks away with the dog. Lily and Anna go back to their mom. They tell her what happened. Their mom hugs them.
"You are brave, girls," she says. "You protected each other. I'm proud of you."
Lily and Anna smile. They love their mom. They love their sister. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there lived a naughty dinosaur. He liked to wake up early and cause mischief. One morning, he woke up and decided to go explore. He ran into a little girl, and she cried out in surprise. 
"What are you?" the girl asked.
The naughty dinosaur replied, "I'm a dinosaur! Would you like to join my adventure?"
The girl nodded, and they set out on their journey. They explored many places, and the girl had a lot of fun.
At the end of the day, the naughty dinosaur said goodbye to the girl, and they both went off to sleep. They had such a great time together!

Once upon a time, there was a curious little girl named Amy. She loved to eat cereal every day for breakfast. One morning, Amy found a new box of cereal in the kitchen. She was very excited to try it.
Amy opened the box and poured the cereal into her bowl. But instead of her usual cereal, out came a sharp toy. Amy was very surprised! She had never seen a toy like this before. She picked it up and looked at it closely.
Just then, her mom came into the kitchen and saw the sharp toy in Amy's hand. "Oh, that's not for your cereal, Amy!" her mom said with a smile. "That's a special toy for you to play with, but only when you finish your cereal." Amy laughed and put the toy aside. She was happy to have a new toy, and she enjoyed her cereal even more.

Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch his favorite show on TV. It made him very happy and excited.
One day, Tim saw a new show on TV. It was about a big, red ball that could talk and play. This show excited Tim even more. He laughed and clapped his hands as he watched the big, red ball bounce around.
Tim told all his friends about the new show. They came to his house to watch it with him. They all sat in front of the TV, excited to see the big, red ball. The young friends laughed and played together, just like the big, red ball on the screen.

Sara and Ben are playing in their room. They have many toys and books, but they want to do something new. They see a big paper on the wall. It is a sign that says "Sara and Ben's Room". They like the sign, but they think it is too plain.
"Let's make the sign more fun," Sara says. "We can draw and color on it."
"Good idea," Ben says. "We need crayons and stickers."
They look for crayons and stickers in their drawers and boxes. They find many colors and shapes. They are happy. They go to the sign and start to draw and color. They make flowers, stars, animals, and letters. They stick stickers of cars, dolls, fruits, and hearts. The sign becomes very bright and pretty.
They are almost done, but they want to add one more thing. They see a hanger on the door. It is a thick hanger made of wood. It has a hook on the top.
"Look, Sara," Ben says. "We can use this hanger to hang something on the sign."
"What can we hang?" Sara asks.
Ben thinks for a moment. He sees a scarf on the bed. It is a thick scarf with many colors. He likes the scarf. He takes the scarf and the hanger. He ties the scarf to the hanger. He hangs the hanger on the sign.
"Wow, Ben," Sara says. "That looks great. The scarf makes the sign more cozy and warm."
They smile and hug. They are proud of their sign. They show their sign to their mom and dad. Their mom and dad are proud of them too. They say they are very creative and clever. They take a picture of them and their sign. They put the picture in a frame and hang it on the wall. They all look at the picture and the sign. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play in the mud. He would make mud pies and mud castles. But one day, he found a broken toy in the mud. It was a boring, old toy car. Tim wanted to make it fun again.
Tim asked his dad to help him repair the toy car. They worked together to fix it. They put on new wheels and painted it bright colors. It was no longer boring. It was a fun, new toy for Tim to play with.
Now, Tim and his dad played with the toy car in the mud. They raced it through the mud puddles and made tracks in the mud. Tim was so happy that he could repair the boring toy and make it fun again. And they all lived happily ever after.

Lily and Tom are playing with blocks in the living room. They are building a big tower with different colors. Lily likes the red blocks and Tom likes the blue ones. They are having fun and talking to each other.
"Look, my tower is taller than yours!" Lily says, smiling.
"No, mine is taller!" Tom says, frowning.
They both stand up and measure their towers with their hands. They are almost the same height, but Lily's tower has one more block on top.
"Ha, I win!" Lily says, sticking out her tongue.
Tom feels angry and jealous. He wants to win too. He notices a big ball on the floor. He has an idea. He picks up the ball and throws it at Lily's tower. The ball hits the tower and makes it fall down. The blocks scatter everywhere. Lily's tower is gone.
"Hey, that's not fair!" Lily shouts, feeling sad and mad. "You broke my tower!"
Tom laughs and says, "Now my tower is taller!"
Lily starts to cry. She runs to her mom, who is in the kitchen. She tells her what Tom did. Mom comes to the living room and sees the mess. She is not happy.
"Tom, what did you do?" Mom asks, looking at him.
Tom feels a tap on his shoulder. He turns around and sees his dad, who is home from work. Dad has seen everything too. He is not happy either.
"Tom, that was very mean and rude," Dad says, sounding stern. "You should not throw things at your sister or her toys. You should apologize to her and help her clean up."
Tom feels ashamed. He knows he did something bad. He lowers his head and says, "I'm sorry, Lily. I'm sorry, Mom. I'm sorry, Dad."
He goes to Lily and hugs her. He says he is sorry again. Lily stops crying and hugs him back. She says she forgives him. They both say they love each other.
Mom and Dad smile and hug them too. They say they love them both. They help them pick up the blocks and put them back in the box. They say they can play with them again later, but they have to share and be nice.
Lily and Tom nod and say okay. They have learned their lesson. They decide to play something else together. They are happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a small bird who lived in a tree. One day, it started to rain. The bird was sad because it could not play outside. The bird stayed in the tree, watching the rain fall.
As the rain stopped, the bird saw a small step near the tree. The bird had an idea. It jumped on the step and started to sing. The bird sang a happy song about the rain and the sun. The other animals came to listen.
The animals loved the bird's song. They all started to play together. The small bird learned that even when it rains, there is always something fun to do. The moral of the story is to find joy in every situation, no matter how small it may be.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to move around and play all day. One day, it was very cold outside. Tim's mom said, "Put on your coat, Tim!" Tim put on his coat and ran outside to play.
Tim found a big red apple on the ground. It looked very yummy. He picked up the apple and took a big bite. "Yum!" said Tim. He was very happy to find such a yummy treat. He ate the whole apple and then continued to play.
As Tim played, he started to feel sick. His tummy hurt a lot. Tim ran back to his mom, crying. "Mom, my tummy hurts!" he said. His mom looked worried and said, "Oh no, Tim! You must have eaten something bad." Tim felt very sad and sick. The day did not end well for Tim.

Lily was very hungry. She wanted to eat some soup. She saw a big pot on the stove. She smelled the soup. It smelled good.
She climbed on a chair. She reached for the pot. She stretched her arm. She wanted to get a spoon. But the pot was too hot. She burned her hand. She cried.
"Ow! Ow! Ow!" she shouted.
Mom heard her cry. She ran to the kitchen. She saw Lily on the chair. She saw the pot on the stove. She saw the burn on Lily's hand. She was angry and worried.
"Lily, what are you doing?" she asked. "You should not touch the pot. It is very hot. You could get hurt. You could spill the soup. The soup is for dinner. It is not for you now."
She took Lily off the chair. She put some ice on Lily's hand. She hugged Lily. She said sorry.
Lily felt bad. She said sorry too. She did not want to touch the pot. She did not want to burn her hand. She did not want to make Mom angry. She just wanted to eat some soup.
Mom smiled. She said it was okay. She said she loved Lily. She said she would make some soup for Lily later. She said she would make it better. She said the soup on the stove was terrible. She said she forgot to add salt. She said she would throw it away.
Lily laughed. She said she did not want to eat terrible soup. She said she wanted to eat good soup. She said she would wait for Mom. She said she would help Mom. She said she would not stretch her arm anymore. She said she would be careful. She said she loved Mom too.

John was walking on the beach. He could see an oyster. He was very curious and wanted to have a closer look. He opened the oyster and inside there was the most lovely pearl. 
John said to the oyster: "Oh lovely oyster, can you read?"
The oyster said: "No, John, I can't, but I can give you the gift of a lovely pearl!" 
John was so happy and thanked the oyster. Now he can read and play with the pearl. He took it home and held it close to his heart.
John was thankful, and he was sure he can now always read books and feel the lovely pearl in his pocket!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to jog every day. He would put on his light shoes and run around the park. The sun would shine and Tim would smile as he jogged.
One day, while Tim was jogging, he saw a big stick. He picked up the stick and played with it. Tim swung the stick around, but then he heard a loud snap. Oh no! The stick had broken in half.
Tim felt sad that he broke the stick. He looked around and saw a little girl crying. She told Tim that it was her stick. Tim said sorry and gave her a hug. They became friends and jogged together every day. And they never broke anything again.

One day, a little girl named Sue got a new toy. She was so happy. Sue wanted to show her new toy to her friend, Tom.
"Look, Tom!" Sue said. "I have a new toy. It is so fun to play with."
Tom wanted to play with the toy too. But it started to rain, so they went inside. Sue put her toy next to the shower. "We can play after the rain," she said.
Later, Sue and Tom went to play with the toy again. But Sue forgot that she left it next to the shower. The toy got wet and broke. Sue was sad. She said, "I regret leaving my toy there. Now it is broken."
Tom hugged Sue. "It's okay. We can get a new toy and play together again." Sue smiled and they went to find a new toy to play with.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the farm with his mom. They saw many animals and plants. They also saw corn growing in the field. Tim's mom said, "We need corn for dinner tonight."
Tim wanted to pick the corn all by himself. He did not want to share with his mom. Tim's mom said, "Don't be selfish, Tim. We need to work together."
Just then, a big wind came and blew all the corn away. Tim and his mom were very sad. They did not know what to do. Then, a nice bird flew down and said, "I saw what happened. I will help you find the corn."
Tim and his mom thanked the bird. They all worked together to find the corn. They learned that sharing and helping each other is better than being selfish.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tom. He loved to play with his toy cars. One day, he found a can of oil in the garage. Tom thought it would be fun to play with the oil.
Tom poured the oil on the floor and started to play with his cars. But the oil made the floor very slippery. It was hard for Tom to walk on it. He slipped and fell down. Tom started to cry.
His mom came and saw the mess. She was not happy. She told Tom that it was not safe to play with oil. Tom learned that he should always be careful with things he did not know. The moral of the story is to always be careful and not play with things that can be dangerous.

Once there was a little girl called Lucy. She loved playing all sorts of imaginative games. One day, she wanted to build the tallest tower she had ever seen. 
She went to find all the things she needed. She found some blocks and some glue.
When Lucy started building her tower, she noticed her little brother looking very sad. She asked him what was wrong. 
Her brother said, "I want to help you build your tower, but it's too hard and I'm not very good at it". 
Lucy smiled and said, "That's okay, I know how to sort it out. We can work together to build the tower, it's much more fun that way. Let's get started!"
They worked together to build a beautiful tower. In no time, they had done it. 
Lucy's brother said, "We did it! That was so much fun".
Lucy said, "Yes, we did it! That was so much better than trying to do it all on my own. Trying to do it all by myself would have been so stupid!"

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was playing in his room when he saw a box with a label on it. The label said, "dangerous." Tim was scared but curious.
Tim asked his mom, "Mom, what is in this box?" His mom said, "It is something dangerous, do not touch it." Tim wanted to know what was inside, so he waited for his mom to go away.
When his mom went away, Tim opened the box. Inside, he found a tiny kitten. The kitten was so cute. Tim was confused. Why was the kitten dangerous? He picked up the kitten and it started to weep. Tim felt bad and decided to show his mom.
Tim said, "Mom, look what I found in the dangerous box!" His mom laughed and said, "Oh, Tim! That label was just a joke. The kitten is not dangerous at all. We got it for your birthday. Happy birthday, Tim!" Tim was so happy and hugged his new kitten.

One day, a little girl named Emily was walking down the road. She saw a butterly and stopped to pick it. She held it in her hands, it's wings were soft and tickly. Emily smiled at her new friend and said, "Hi butterfly!"
Suddenly, a loud car went by the road. Emily was scared, but the butterfly was brave. It flew away safe and sound. Emily felt better and she giggled. She then continued walking down the road.
When Emily arrived at her house, she put the butterfly in her garden as a surprise for her mom. Emily opened the garden gate and she yelled, "Mom, come outside! I have a surprise for you!"
Her mom came outside and saw the beautiful butterfly. She said, "Oh my, what an amazing surprise, Emily! Thank you so much!" Emily smiled and hugged her mom. They both had a lovely day.

One day, a little brown bird was flying in the forest. The bird saw a big brown mushroom on the ground. The bird thought the mushroom would be good to eat. So, the bird flew down to the ground to take a bite of the mushroom.
Just as the bird was about to eat the mushroom, a tiny bug came out from under the mushroom. The bug said, "Please don't eat my home!" The bird was scared and let out a scream. The bird did not want to hurt the bug's home.
The bird and the bug talked to each other. The bird said, "I am sorry, I did not know this was your home." The bug said, "That is okay, now you know." They both smiled and became friends. From that day on, the bird and the bug played together in the forest, and the bird never tried to eat the mushroom again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch the cars go by. One day, he saw a fancy car with a big motor. It was very fast and shiny. Tim wanted to have a car like that when he grew up.
Tim worked hard and saved his money. He was always nice to people and helped them when they needed it. Tim's friends saw how hard he worked and they wanted to be like him. They started to work hard too and help others.
One day, Tim was all grown up and he had enough money to buy the fancy car with the big motor. He was very happy. But Tim remembered that being kind and helping others was more important than having a fancy car. So, he used his money to help his friends and family instead. They were all very thankful and happy. And Tim was happy too, because he knew that being kind was the best thing to do.

Once upon a time, there was a big room. In this room, there were many things to see. The room had a display. The display showed pretty colors and shapes. The people who came to see the display liked it a lot. They smiled and clapped their hands.
In the same big room, there was music. The music was called jazz. The jazz was fun to dance to. The people who came to see the display also listened to the jazz. They danced and laughed. They had a good time.
The big room was very organized. Everything was in the right place. The display was on one side, and the jazz was on the other side. The people could see and hear everything very well. They liked the big room and had a lot of fun.

One day, a young bird named Tim saw a big rainbow in the sky. He wanted to find the end of the rainbow. His mom told him to stay home, but Tim insisted on going.
Tim flew and flew, but the rainbow seemed so far away. He met a wise old bird who said, "Rainbows are pretty, but you should listen to your mom." Tim did not listen and kept going.
Soon, Tim was very tired and far from home. He wished he had listened to his mom. Just then, his mom found him and took him home. Tim learned that it is important to listen to those who love you.

Once upon a time, there was a big palace. In the palace lived a king and a queen. They had a little girl named Lily. Lily was very glad because she had many friends in the palace.
One day, Lily met a sad boy named Tom. Tom was sad because he had no friends. Lily said, "I hope you can be my friend." Tom was happy to be Lily's friend. They played together every day.
But one day, Lily and Tom had a fight. They both wanted to play with the same toy. Lily said, "This is my toy!" Tom said, "No, it is my toy!" They were both very mad. The king and queen saw the fight and said, "You must learn to share." But Lily and Tom did not listen.
The next day, Lily and Tom were still mad at each other. They did not want to play together anymore. The palace was not a happy place. The king and queen were sad to see their little girl so unhappy.
The moral of the story is to always share and be kind to your friends, or you might lose them.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the lake with his mom. They saw a big yellow kayak. Tim wanted to go in the kayak, but his mom said, "No, Tim, it is too big for you."
Tim was sad, but he did not give up. He stared at the kayak, hoping his mom would change her mind. Then, a spoiled dog came running and jumped into the kayak. The dog started to eat the food inside the kayak.
Tim's mom saw the dog and laughed. "Well, Tim, I guess the kayak is not too big for you after all. Let's go for a ride!" Tim and his mom went in the kayak, and they had a fun day at the lake with the spoiled dog.

Once upon a time, in a land of green trees and blue skies, there were two friends named Cat and Dog. They liked to play together and have fun. One sunny day, they found a big, red ball in the park. They were both very eager to play with it.
But, Cat and Dog started to quarrel about who should play with the ball first. They both wanted it so much that they forgot how to share. They yelled and yelled, making each other very sad.
A wise old Owl saw them fighting and said, "Friends should not quarrel. You need to learn to share and be kind to each other." Cat and Dog listened to Owl and decided to take turns playing with the ball. They both had fun, and their friendship grew stronger. The moral of the story is that sharing and kindness are important for strong friendships.

Once upon a time, there was a farm. On the farm, there was a swing. The swing was near a big tree. A little girl named Sue loved to play on the swing.
One sunny day, Sue went to the farm to play. She saw a stubborn donkey near the swing. The donkey would not move. Sue said, "Please, donkey, let me swing." But the donkey just shook his head.
Sue thought hard. Then she had an idea. She gave the donkey a big, sweet apple. The donkey ate the apple and moved away. Sue smiled and said, "Thank you, donkey!" Now, Sue could play on the swing all day long.

Ben and Mia were playing in the park with their ball. They liked to kick the ball to each other and run after it. They were having fun and laughing.
Suddenly, they saw a big dog running towards them. The dog was barking and had sharp teeth. Ben and Mia were scared and ran away. They tried to find their mom and dad, but they could not see them.
"Help, help!" Ben shouted. "The dog is coming!"
Mia saw a big tree nearby. She had an idea.
"Ben, come here!" she said. "We can climb the tree and be safe!"
Ben followed Mia and they climbed the tree as fast as they could. The dog could not reach them. It jumped and barked, but it could not climb.
Ben and Mia hugged each other and waited for their mom and dad. They were still scared, but they were glad they had each other.
After a while, they heard a whistle. They looked down and saw a man with a leash. He was the dog's owner. He called the dog and put the leash on it. He looked up and saw Ben and Mia.
"I'm sorry, kids," he said. "My dog is not bad, he is just excited. He likes to play, but he does not know how to be gentle. He has a lung problem and he can't run for too long. He gets tired and dead."
Ben and Mia did not understand what a lung problem was, or what dead meant. But they saw that the dog was panting and lying down. They felt sorry for the dog.
"Can we come down?" Ben asked.
"Yes, of course," the man said. "Don't be afraid, he won't hurt you. He is a good dog, his name is Rex."
Ben and Mia climbed down the tree and went to the man. They petted Rex and gave him some water. Rex licked their hands and wagged his tail. He was happy.
Ben and Mia smiled and said sorry to the man. They said they were just scared and did not know Rex was friendly.
The man said it was okay and thanked them for being kind to Rex. He said he would take Rex to the vet and get him some medicine. He said goodbye and walked away with Rex.
Ben and Mia waved and ran to their mom and dad, who were looking for them. They told them what happened and how they met Rex. They said they were brave and smart.
Their mom and dad hugged them and said they were proud of them. They said they did the right thing and they were good kids.
Ben and Mia felt happy and loved. They learned that sometimes things are not what they seem, and that they should not judge by appearances. They also learned that they could always count on each other. They were best friends.

One day, a little girl named Mia found a thick stick in her yard. She thought it looked like a microphone. Mia loved to sing, so she picked it up and started to sing her favorite song.
Mia's friend, Ben, heard her singing and came to see what was going on. "What are you doing, Mia?" he asked. "I am singing with my new microphone," she said, holding up the thick stick. "Do you want to sing with me?"
Ben smiled and said, "Yes, I want to sing too!" So, Mia and Ben took turns singing into the thick stick microphone. They had so much fun that they decided to make a stage out of a big box. They painted it and put on a show for their families.
Their moms and dads clapped and cheered as Mia and Ben sang their hearts out. They were so happy to have found the thick stick and to have made a fun show for everyone to enjoy.

Once upon a time, in a small pond, there was a young fish named Fin. Fin loved to play with his best friend, Bubbles the frog. One day, they found a big, shiny bubble near the pond. "Wow! Look at that bubble!" Bubbles said.
The next day, Fin and Bubbles went to play near the big bubble again. But when they got there, the bubble was gone! Bubbles started to weep. "Why are you sad?" asked Fin. "I wanted to see the big bubble again," Bubbles said. Fin thought hard and had an idea. "Don't worry, Bubbles. I know how to find the bubble!"
Fin and Bubbles went on a long journey to find the big bubble. They looked everywhere, but they couldn't find it. Then, something unexpected happened. They found a small bubble maker hidden in the pond. "Look, Bubbles! We can make our own big bubble!" said Fin.
Bubbles was so happy that he stopped weeping. They played with the bubble maker all day, making big and small bubbles. And they lived happily ever after, making bubbles in their pond.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to search for yummy food. He walked and walked until he found a big tree with yummy red apples. Tim wanted to pick one, but they were too high for him. He saw a tall man walking by and asked, "Can you help me get an apple, please?"
The tall man smiled and said, "I will help you, but first, let's use our heads to think of a way to get the apples down." Tim thought and thought. Then, he had an idea! He picked up a stick and gave it to the tall man. The tall man used the stick to knock down the apples. Tim was so happy! They both ate the yummy apples together.
As they ate, the tall man told Tim, "It's good to ask for help and use your head to solve problems." Tim nodded and thanked the tall man. From that day on, Tim always remembered to ask for help and think before he acted. And the tall man? He was really a kind giant in disguise! The giant was so happy to help Tim that he would visit the little boy every day to share more yummy food and fun times.

Once upon a time, in a wild forest, there was a little rabbit named Remy. Remy loved to run and play with his friends all day long. One day, Remy found a poem on the ground. He picked it up and read it. The poem talked about the importance of taking naps.
Remy thought about the poem and decided to try taking a nap. He found a cozy spot under a tree and closed his eyes. After a short nap, Remy woke up feeling happy and full of energy.
Remy told his friends about the poem and the nap. They all tried taking naps too, and they found that they had more fun playing together after their naps. The little animals in the wild forest learned that taking naps can make them feel better and enjoy their days more. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a magic cloth. This cloth was very important. It could wipe away any mess or dirt. People in the town loved the magic cloth.
One day, a little girl named Sue found the magic cloth. She said, "Wow, I can clean my room!" She wiped her toys, her bed, and her floor. Her room was very clean. Sue was happy.
But Sue did not know that the magic cloth was very tired. It needed to rest. Sue kept wiping everything. The magic cloth became very weak. One day, it stopped working. Sue cried, "Oh no, the magic is gone!" And the town was sad because the magic cloth was gone forever.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sue. Sue liked to record herself singing. She had a red uniform that she wore when she sang. She thought it was pretty.
One day, Sue saw an ugly bug. The bug was big and had many legs. Sue was scared of the bug. She wanted to run away, but she thought the bug might want to hear her sing.
Sue put on her red uniform and started to record herself. She sang a happy song for the ugly bug. The bug liked the song and danced. Sue was not scared anymore. They became friends and sang together every day.

One day, a big, bossy cat named Max was playing in the yard. He liked to mix dirt and water to make mud pies. Max was having lots of fun making a big mess.
A little dog named Pip came to the yard. Pip was a guard dog, and he wanted to play too. But Max didn't want to share his mud pies. He told Pip to go away.
Pip was sad, but he had an idea. He found some sticks and leaves and started to mix them with the mud. He made a big, beautiful mud castle. Max saw the castle and wanted to play with Pip. They played together, and Max learned to share. They became good friends and had lots of fun making mud pies and castles together.

One day, a little boy named Tim saw the moon in the sky. He wanted to play with the moon, but it was too high up. He had an idea. Tim would bury the moon in the sand, so it would come down to play. He took his shovel and started to dig a hole.
Tim's friend, Sue, saw him digging. She asked, "What are you doing, Tim?" Tim said, "I want to bury the moon in the sand so it can come down and play with us." Sue thought it was a fun idea, so she started to help Tim dig a big hole.
But the moon was persistent and stayed in the sky. Tim and Sue were sad. They wanted the moon to come down and play. They did not know what to do. Tim's mom came outside and saw them. She said, "The moon cannot come down to play, but you can still have fun under its light." Tim and Sue smiled and played in the moonlight, happy to have each other as friends.

Once upon a time, there was an old boat. The boat sailed a long way and reached a reef. It was very beautiful. The boat driver admired it. He thought it was the most special thing in the world.
The boat sailed on and the driver felt ashamed. He wished he could have stayed by the reef longer and enjoyed it more.
But there was something else the driver admired. He saw it in the sky. It was a beautiful rainbow. He was so happy to see it. He admired how colorful it was and smiled.
The driver was so glad he had seen the reef and the rainbow. He realized that beauty was all around, even when it was hard to find!

Once upon a time, there was a thin man who wanted to sell a fountain. The fountain was very pretty and had water that went up and down. The thin man took the fountain to the town to show it to everyone.
In the town, people saw the fountain and thought it was nice. The thin man said, "I want to sell this fountain. Who wants to buy it?" Many people wanted the fountain, but they did not have enough money to buy it.
Finally, a kind lady came and said, "I will buy the fountain." She gave the thin man the money and took the fountain to her home. She put the fountain in her garden and everyone was happy. The thin man was happy because he sold the fountain, and the kind lady was happy because she had a pretty fountain in her garden.

Once upon a time, there was a generous man named Tom. He had a big heart and loved to help everyone. Tom had a special leather hat that he wore every day. He believed that the hat could protect him from anything.
One sunny day, Tom went for a walk in the park. He saw a little bird fall from a tree. Tom wanted to protect the bird, so he took off his leather hat and put the bird inside. He thought the hat would keep the bird safe and warm.
As Tom walked with the bird in his hat, something unexpected happened. The little bird grew very big! It was not a bird at all but a magic creature. The creature thanked Tom for his help and flew away. Tom was happy that he could protect the creature, even if it was not what he thought it was.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a regular belt that he wore every day. One day, Tim's belt broke, and he was sad.
Tim went to his mom and said, "Mom, I need a new belt. My regular belt broke." His mom hugged him and said, "Don't worry, Tim. We will go to the store and find you a new belt."
So, Tim and his mom went to the store. They looked at many belts, but Tim wanted a belt just like his regular one. Finally, they found the perfect belt. Tim was happy, and he said, "Thank you, Mom! Now I have a new regular belt!" They went home, and Tim wore his new belt every day.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a boy named Tim. Tim had very long hair because he did not want a haircut. He thought haircuts were scary. He was also a bit selfish and did not like to share his toys.
One day, a new girl named Sue came to play at Tim's house. Sue saw Tim's long hair and asked, "Why don't you get a haircut?" Tim said, "I am scared, and I don't want to share my hair." Sue told Tim, "If you get a haircut, you will look nice, and we can play better."
Tim thought about what Sue said. He didn't want to miss playing with Sue because she was fun. So, Tim decided to be brave and get a haircut. After the haircut, Tim looked very nice. He was happy and not scared anymore.
Now, Tim and Sue played together all the time. Tim learned to share his toys and not be selfish. They became best friends and had lots of fun together. And Tim never missed a haircut again.

Once upon a time, there was a big tank. The tank was very strong and fierce. It lived in a big forest with many trees and animals. The tank wanted to make friends, but all the animals were scared of it because it was so big and fierce.
One day, a tiny bird came to the tank. The bird was not scared of the tank. The tank was happy to see the bird. The tank offered the bird a ride on its back. The bird said yes, and they went for a ride in the forest.
As they went through the forest, the other animals saw that the tank was not so scary. They saw that the tank was kind and helpful. The tank and the bird became friends with all the animals. And from that day on, the fierce tank and the tiny bird were the best of friends.

Timmy felt a little bit sad. His classroom was closed and he couldn't go to school with his friends. He really missed the fun time they had together in the classroom.
One day, Timmy had an idea. He wanted to open the classroom with his bare hands! He was very strong, so he thought it might work. But when he tried to open the door, it wouldn't budge. He even tried pushing with his feet but it was too strong.
Timmy was very sad and he started to cry. He wanted to be with his friends so badly. He wanted to learn and play in the classroom. 
Luckily, Mum came to the rescue. She found the key and opened the classroom. Timmy was so excited that he cheered. 
The next day, Timmy and his friends had the best time ever in the classroom. His strong hands and feet had helped him out in the end!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to play with his toy cars. One day, Tim's mom gave him a new toy car. This car had a buckle on it. Tim was very happy.
Tim played with his new toy car all day. But then, Tim saw a big hill. He thought it would be fun to roll his car down the hill. But the hill was dangerous. Tim's mom told him not to play near the hill.
Tim did not listen to his mom. He rolled his car down the hill. The car went very fast. It hit a rock and the buckle broke. Tim's toy car suffered. Tim was very sad. He learned that he should listen to his mom and not play near dangerous places.

One day, a cat and a dog were playing in the park. They were having fun, running in zigzags and chasing each other. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing.
Suddenly, the cat saw a big bird in the sky. He felt panic and said, "Dog, look! A big bird is coming! We must be alert!" The dog looked up and saw the bird too. "Oh no!" said the dog. "We need to hide!"
The cat and the dog ran to a big tree and hid behind it. They were scared, but they stayed alert. The big bird flew closer and closer, but it did not see the cat and the dog. It flew away, and the cat and the dog were safe.
They felt happy and started to play again. They knew that they could always help each other when they were in trouble. The cat and the dog were best friends, and they had a great day at the park.

Once upon a time, in a big house, there was a little infant named Tim. He had many toys and loved to play with them all day. Tim was a happy baby and liked to laugh a lot.
One day, Tim's big sister, Ann, wanted to scare him for fun. She put on a great big mask and jumped out from behind the door. She shouted, "Boo!" But Tim didn't get scared. Instead, he laughed and clapped his hands.
Ann was surprised that Tim didn't get scared. She took off the mask and laughed too. From that day on, they played together and had lots of fun. Tim and Ann became the best of friends and always made each other laugh.

Once upon a time, a mom and her daughter went for a walk. They saw a big yawn coming from a cave. The mom said, "We should go see what's inside the cave."
The daughter said, "Okay, but I hope it's not disgusting." They walked into the cave, holding hands. The cave was dark and a little scary.
As they walked deeper into the cave, they saw the big yawn again. They found a big, sleepy bear. The bear looked at them and said, "I'm sorry if my yawn was disgusting. I'm just very sleepy." The mom and the daughter laughed, and they all became friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to think a lot. He thought about what he would do every day. One day, he thought about going to the store with his mom. He wanted to buy a toy.
At the store, Tim saw a big, red ball. He asked his mom, "Can I have the ball, please?" His mom said, "No, Tim. We are here to buy food, not toys." But Tim was persistent. He asked again and again for the ball.
Finally, his mom said, "Okay, Tim. You can have the ball." Tim was very happy. He played with the ball all day and all night. And every time he played with it, he thought about how happy he was that he went to the store with his mom.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to go to the shop with Mom. Mom gives them a coin each. They can buy something they want.
Anna sees a brown bear. It is soft and big. She likes bears. She grabs the bear and hugs it. She says, "Mom, can I have this bear? It is so nice."
Ben sees a brown ball. It is round and bouncy. He likes balls. He grabs the ball and throws it. He says, "Mom, can I have this ball? It is so fun."
Mom smiles and nods. She says, "Yes, you can have the bear and the ball. They are good choices. But first, we have to pay for them. Come with me to the counter."
Anna and Ben follow Mom. They give their coins to the lady. The lady says, "Thank you. Enjoy your bear and your ball." She puts them in a bag.
Anna and Ben say, "Thank you." They take the bag and run to the car. They are happy. They have new toys. They can play with them at home.

Once upon a time, there was a little boat. The boat was very adventurous. It liked to sail on the water all day long.
One day, the boat saw a big, white cream. It thought the cream was a cloud. The boat wanted to sail to the cream. So, it sailed and sailed, getting closer to the cream.
But when the boat got to the cream, it was not a cloud. It was a big, sticky mess. The boat got stuck in the cream and could not sail anymore. The boat was sad because it could not be adventurous again.

Once upon a time, there was a kind bird who loved to sing. She would choose her favorite music and sing for all her friends in the forest. One day, while she was singing, something unexpected happened.
A big storm came and scared all the animals. They ran away to hide from the wind and rain. The kind bird was very sad because her friends were gone, and she couldn't sing her music anymore. She missed her friends, but she had an idea.
The kind bird decided to sing a special song to make her friends feel safe. She chose the happiest music she knew and sang it very loud. Soon, all the animals came back, and they were not scared anymore. They all sang and danced together, and the storm went away. The moral of the story is that being kind and sharing your gifts can help others feel better, even in tough times.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a small hut. The hut was not like the other huts. It was original. Tim went to the hut to look inside. He saw a girl named Sue. Sue seemed sad.
"Why are you sad?" Tim asked Sue. Sue said, "I lost my toy. I cannot find it." Tim wanted to help Sue. They both looked for the toy.
They looked in the hut, but the toy was not there. They looked outside, but the toy was not there. Tim and Sue were tired. They almost gave up. Then, Tim saw something under a tree. It was Sue's toy!
"Look, I found your toy!" Tim said. Sue was happy. They both played with the toy. Tim and Sue became good friends. They played together every day.

Tommy loves his furry panda toy. He sleeps with it every night and hugs it all day. He calls it Pandy and tells it stories and secrets. He thinks Pandy is his best friend.
One day, Tommy goes to the park with his mom and Pandy. He sees a big slide and wants to try it. He runs to the slide and puts Pandy on the ground. He says, "Wait here, Pandy. I'll be back soon."
But when Tommy slides down, he sees a girl holding Pandy. She has curly hair and a pink dress. She says, "Look, mommy, a panda! Can I keep it?"
Tommy feels angry and scared. He wants his Pandy back. He runs to the girl and says, "No, that's my panda! Give it to me!"
The girl says, "No, it's mine! I found it on the ground. It's so soft and cute!"
Tommy and the girl start to pull Pandy from each other. They both cry and shout. Pandy feels sad and hurt.
Mommy hears the noise and comes to see what's wrong. She sees Tommy and the girl fighting over Pandy. She says, "Stop, stop! What's going on here?"
Tommy says, "She took my panda! It's mine!"
The girl says, "No, it's mine! I found it!"
Mommy says, "Calm down, both of you. Let's talk and share. Tommy, did you put your panda on the ground?"
Tommy says, "Yes, but I was going to slide and come back."
Mommy says, "I see. But you should always keep your toys with you. Someone might think they are lost or forgotten. The girl, what's your name?"
The girl says, "I'm Lily."
Mommy says, "Lily, did you ask Tommy if you could play with his panda?"
Lily says, "No, I just saw it and liked it."
Mommy says, "I see. But you should always ask before you take someone else's toy. They might be very attached to it. How would you feel if someone took your favorite toy?"
Lily says, "I would feel sad and mad."
Mommy says, "Exactly. So, how can we solve this problem?"
Tommy and Lily look at each other and at Pandy. They both feel sorry and ashamed. They say, "Sorry."
Mommy says, "That's good. Now, can you give Pandy back to Tommy?"
Lily says, "Yes, here you go, Tommy. I'm sorry I took your panda."
Tommy says, "Thank you, Lily. I'm sorry I yelled at you. Do you want to play with Pandy for a while?"
Lily says, "Yes, please. Can we be friends?"
Tommy says, "Yes, we can. Pandy likes friends too."
Mommy smiles and says, "That's very nice of you, Tommy and Lily. You are both very kind and generous. Why don't you go and play together on the slide? And don't forget to hold Pandy tight."
Tommy and Lily nod and say, "Okay, mommy."
They take Pandy and run to the slide. They take turns sliding and holding Pandy. They laugh and have fun. Pandy feels happy and loved.

Once upon a time, in a jolly little town, there lived a boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toy train. The train made steam when it moved. Tim liked to watch the steam go up in the air.
One day, Tim's friend, Sam, came over to play. "Look at my train!" said Tim. "It makes steam!" Sam was happy to see the train. They played with the train all day long.
As the sun went down, Sam needed to go home. Tim wanted to remember this fun day. So, he took a picture of the train with the steam. Now, every time Tim looks at the picture, he remembers the jolly day he had with Sam.

Once upon a time, in a lively green forest, there lived a little bunny named Bobo. Bobo had many friends, like birds, squirrels, and deer. They loved to play together every day.
One sunny day, Bobo and his friend, Squeaky the squirrel, found a big, shiny axe near a tree. They didn't know what it was, so they went to ask Wise Owl. Wise Owl told them, "This axe is not good for the forest. It can cut down trees. You must hide it where no one can find it."
Bobo and Squeaky decided to hide the axe in a deep hole. They dug and dug until the hole was big enough. After they hid the axe, they heard a loud noise. It was a scary man with another axe, trying to cut down a tree! The animals didn't want their friend, the tree, to be hurt.
Squeaky said, "We must escape and tell the others!" So, Bobo and Squeaky ran to their friends and told them about the scary man. All the animals came together and started making loud noises. The scary man got scared and ran away.
The forest was safe again, and the animals were happy. They thanked Bobo and Squeaky for their help. From that day on, they all played together, knowing they could protect their forest home.

Tom and Mia are playing in the park with their ball. They like to kick the ball to each other and run after it. They are having fun and laughing.
But then the sky gets dark and the wind gets strong. They hear a loud noise. It is thunder. They see a bright light. It is lightning. They are scared and anxious. They want to go home.
"Tom, we need to get shelter!" Mia says. "Where can we go?"
Tom looks around and sees a big tree. He thinks it is a good place to hide from the storm. He grabs Mia's hand and runs to the tree.
"Here, we can stay under the tree until the storm is over," he says.
But the tree is not a good shelter. The wind shakes the branches and the rain falls on them. They are wet and cold. They hear another loud noise. It is a branch breaking. It falls near them. They are more scared and anxious.
"Tom, this is not a good shelter!" Mia says. "We need to find another place!"
Tom agrees. He looks around again and sees a small house. It is the park ranger's house. He thinks it is a better place to hide from the storm. He grabs Mia's hand again and runs to the house.
"Here, we can knock on the door and ask for help," he says.
They knock on the door and a friendly man opens it. He is the park ranger. He sees Tom and Mia and smiles.
"Hello, kids. What are you doing here in the storm?" he asks.
"Please, can we get shelter in your house?" Tom asks. "We are scared and anxious."
"Of course, you can. Come in, come in. You are safe here," the park ranger says.
He lets Tom and Mia in and gives them towels and blankets. He makes them hot chocolate and cookies. He tells them stories and jokes. They are warm and cozy. They are not scared and anxious anymore. They are happy and grateful. They wait for the storm to pass and for their parents to come. They have found a good shelter and a good friend.

Lily and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. They watch the birds and the dogs and the flowers. One day, they see a big brown box near the swings. They are curious. They want to know what is inside.
They go to the box and open it. Inside, they find a small brown rabbit. It has long ears and a fluffy tail. It looks scared. Lily and Ben are happy. They want to pet the rabbit and give it a carrot.
"Hello, rabbit. Don't be afraid. We are nice. Do you want to play with us?" Lily says.
The rabbit does not answer. It jumps out of the box and runs away. It is fast. It does not want to stay in the park. It wants to escape.
"Hey, come back, rabbit. We won't hurt you. We just want to be your friends." Ben says.
But the rabbit does not listen. It hops over the fence and disappears. Lily and Ben are sad. They watch the rabbit escape. They wish they could find it again. They hope it will be safe and happy. They go back to the swings and play. They still like to watch the birds and the dogs and the flowers. But they also miss the rabbit.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. He saw a noisy bird in a tree. The bird was singing a loud song. Tim wanted to make friends with the bird.
