They also helped mom to clean up the track and put it away. They learned their lesson and promised to be good. Mom was proud of them and gave them a kiss. They were happy again.

One day, a small cat named Kitty was playing near a fence. She saw a big, mighty dog on the other side. The dog looked at Kitty and said, "Hi! I am Doggy. Do you want to play with me?"
Kitty was scared of the big dog. She did not want to play. She said, "No, thank you." But Doggy did not listen. He jumped over the fence to play with Kitty.
Kitty was so scared that she let out a loud scream. Her friend, Birdy, heard her scream and came to help. Birdy said, "Doggy, please go back. Kitty is scared."
Doggy felt bad for scaring Kitty. He said, "I am sorry, Kitty. I did not know." Then, Doggy jumped back over the fence. Kitty thanked Birdy for helping her. From that day on, Kitty, Birdy, and Doggy became good friends who played together, but always with the fence between them.

Once upon a time, there was a big red van. It was a very special van because it could record sounds. A kind man named Tom drove the van. He loved to record the sounds of animals and people.
One day, Tom was driving his van when he saw a small dog. The dog looked guilty because it had made a mess on the grass. Tom stopped the van and said, "Hello, little dog! You look guilty. Do you want me to record your bark?"
The dog barked and wagged its tail. Tom recorded the dog's bark with his van. Then, he played the sound back to the dog. The dog was so happy to hear its own bark, it forgot all about the mess it made.
Tom and the dog became best friends. They drove around in the big red van, recording the sounds of other animals and people. Everyone loved to hear their own sounds. And the dog never felt guilty again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. Amy had a deaf dog named Max. Max could not hear, but he was very good at understanding what Amy wanted. They loved to play together.
One day, Amy and Max went for a walk in the woods. They found a big stick that looked like a cross. Amy said to Max, "Let's use this stick to stir the mud by the pond!" Max wagged his tail and followed Amy to the pond.
At the pond, Amy put the cross stick into the mud and started to stir. Max watched and barked happily. They had so much fun stirring the mud and playing together. When they were tired, they went back home and told their mom about their adventure.

Once upon a time, there lived a little girl who loved the heat. No matter the season, she was always willing to go out and feel the sun and the warmth of the air.
One summer, though, it got so hot that even the little girl had to stay inside. Everywhere she looked was too hot for her to resist.
One day, her mom came to her and said "Come with me. Today, we will do something different!"
The little girl followed her mom, wondering what different thing they were going to do. As they stepped outside and were hit with the heat, the little girl knew that the different thing had something to do with the heat. 
They walked until they came to a pool and her mom said, "Let's go swimming!"
The little girl was so happy! Finally, something to do in this heat! With a little splash and a lot of laughs, the little girl used the heat to enjoy the pool with no fear of lingering too long.
The little girl had found her way to resist the heat. From then on, the different things that her mom would do with her during the intensely hot summer days were a reward for the little girl. She was so grateful for the heat and what it had allowed her to do!

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Bobo. Bobo liked to play with pebbles. He had many pebbles in different colors. One day, he found a very big and pretty pebble. He wanted to show it to his friend, Rara the rabbit.
Bobo went to Rara's home and said, "Look, Rara! I found a big pebble!" Rara looked at the pebble and thought it was silly. "Why do you like pebbles, Bobo?" asked Rara. "I like to pass them to my friends," said Bobo. Rara laughed and said, "Okay, let's pass the pebble together!"
Bobo and Rara started to pass the pebble to each other. They were having fun. Soon, other friends joined them. They all passed the pebble and laughed. They played until the sun went down.
The next day, Rara told Bobo, "I was wrong. It was not silly to play with the pebble. We had fun with our friends." Bobo smiled and said, "Yes, Rara. Sometimes, even little things can bring us joy."
The moral of the story is that we should not judge things by their looks. Something that seems silly can bring happiness to everyone.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw an old man sitting on a bench. The old man had a big smile on his face. Tim walked up to the old man and said, "Hi! What is your name?"
The old man replied, "Hello, little one. My name is Mr. Brown. I like to come to the park and watch the birds. Do you like the park too?"
Tim nodded and said, "Yes, I do! But I have to mind my toe. I hurt it yesterday when I was playing with my friends." Tim showed Mr. Brown his toe, which had a small bandage on it.
Mr. Brown looked at Tim's toe and said, "Oh no! I hope your toe gets better soon. Just be careful when you play, and always mind your steps."
Tim smiled and thanked Mr. Brown. They waved goodbye and Tim went back to his mom. They played in the park together, making sure Tim minded his toe. And every time they went to the park, they would visit Mr. Brown and share happy stories.

Once upon a time, there was a bunny named Bob. Bob loved to read books and eat carrots. One day, Bob went to the park to read his new book. He sat under a big tree and started to read. The sun was shining, and the sky was blue.
A little boy named Tim came to the park. Tim saw Bob reading and asked, "What are you reading, bunny?" Bob said, "I am reading a book about clean carrots. I love carrots!" Tim smiled and said, "I love carrots too! Let's be friends."
Bob and Tim played together in the park. They found a big, clean carrot on the ground. They were so happy! But then, something unexpected happened. The carrot started to talk! The carrot said, "Hi, I am Carl the Carrot. I can read too! Can I read with you and be your friend?" Bob and Tim were surprised, but they said yes. And so, Bob, Tim, and Carl the Carrot spent the whole day reading and playing together in the park. They became the best of friends.

Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big hedge. The hedge has many green leaves and red berries.
"Look, Mia, the hedge is so pretty!" Tom says.
"Yes, Tom, it is. Can we pick some berries?" Mia asks.
Tom nods. He and Mia go to the hedge. They reach for the berries. But the hedge is prickly. It hurts their hands.
"Ow, ow, ow!" Tom and Mia cry.
They drop the berries. They run away from the hedge. They see a swing. They want to play on the swing.
"Let me go first, Mia!" Tom says.
He runs to the swing. He sits on the seat. He pushes with his feet. He swings high and low.
"Tom, it's my turn!" Mia says.
She runs to the swing. She tries to grab the seat. But Tom swings too fast. He hits Mia with his feet. Mia falls down. She cries.
"Ow, ow, ow!" Mia cries.
Tom stops the swing. He jumps off the seat. He runs to Mia. He sees her face. It is red and wet.
"Mia, are you okay?" Tom asks.
He feels bad. He did not mean to hurt Mia.
"No, Tom, I'm not okay. You hurt me. You are mean!" Mia says.
She is angry. She does not want to talk to Tom.
Tom is sorry. He wants to make Mia happy. He looks around. He sees a flower. It is yellow and bright. He picks the flower. He goes to Mia. He gives her the flower.
"Mia, I'm sorry. I did not see you. I did not want to hurt you. Please forgive me. Here, this is for you. It is a flower. It is pretty, like you." Tom says.
He smiles. He hopes Mia will smile too.
Mia looks at the flower. She looks at Tom. She sees his smile. She feels his sorry. She takes the flower. She smiles.
"Thank you, Tom. It is a nice flower. You are a good friend. I forgive you." Mia says.
She hugs Tom. Tom hugs her back. They are friends again. They go to play on the slide. They are happy. They forget the hedge. They forget the pain. They only remember the flower. And the sorry. And the smile.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a boy named Tim. Tim was a very ignorant boy. He did not like to learn new things. He liked to play all day and did not want to help others.
One day, Tim found a big rope. He decided to pull the rope for fun. He did not know what the rope was for. He pulled and pulled, not caring about the value of the rope.
As Tim pulled the rope, it made a big tree fall down. The tree crushed many houses in the village. The people were very sad and angry with Tim. The village had lost its value because of the ignorant boy.
The moral of the story is: Do not be ignorant like Tim. Learn the value of things before you play with them. If you do not, bad things can happen.

Pam and Sam went to the beach. They saw a boat and wanted to go on a trip. They hopped on the boat, and it started moving. 
"This is so much fun!" said Pam. 
"I bet the view will improve soon," said Sam.
They saw a delicious fish swim by.
The boat floated faster and faster, rocking back and forth. Suddenly, it started to leak.
Sam said, "Uh-oh. We need to find a way to improve the boat, or else we'll get wet."
The boat rocked even harder, and water started flooding the boat. They tried to keep the boat safe, but it was too late. 
The boat sank, and Pam and Sam had to swim back to the beach. They were very sad because their trip was over.

One day, a little cat named Fluffy found a soft thing called velvet. Fluffy was confused. She did not know what it was. She touched it with her paw and it felt nice.
Fluffy saw her friend, a dog named Spot. Spot saw Fluffy struggle to pick up the velvet. Spot asked, "What is that?" Fluffy said, "I don't know, but it feels nice." Spot touched it too and liked it.
They decided to show their friends. They went to Bunny, Bird, and Frog. All of them touched the velvet and liked it too. They all played with the velvet and had a fun day.

Once upon a time, on a far away planet, there lived a little alien named Zog. Zog was not nice to his friends. He would push and shout at them. His friends did not like it when Zog was rude.
One day, Zog's mom said, "Zog, you must behave. If you don't, you will not have any friends to play with." Zog did not listen to his mom. He thought it was fun to be rude.
Later, Zog went to play with his friends. He pushed one friend and shouted at another. His friends got very sad and left. Zog was all alone. He did not behave, and now he had no friends to play with.
The moral of the story is to always be kind to others and behave well. If you are rude, you might end up all alone.

Once upon a time, there was a curious 3 year old who loved exploring the world around him. He often asked his parents to unlock the door to the garden in their backyard. They did so with a laugh. 
One time when the parents were away, the 3 year old decided to try unlocking the door on his own. He worked hard, but eventually he managed to unlock the door. 
As he stepped outside, he was overcome with a sense of wonder. He noticed the pretty flowers, the attractive bee which was buzzing around, and the interesting shape of a rainbow in the sky. He was so attracted to all the beauty in the garden that he forgot to close the door behind him when he went back inside. 
Meanwhile, an animal had entered the garden and snuck into the house. When the parents returned home that evening, they realised something had gone wrong. 
The moral of the story is that it's important to be careful with what you can unlock - otherwise you may be inviting danger into your home!

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Spot. Spot was a happy dog who loved to play. One day, Spot saw a big ball in the park. He wanted to play with the ball, so he ran to it and barked.
A little girl named Lily was near the ball. She said, "Hi Spot! Do you want to play with me and my ball?" Spot barked and wagged his tail. He was very happy to play with Lily and her ball.
Lily threw the ball far away, and Spot ran to get it. They played for a long time and had lots of fun. When it was time to go home, Lily said, "Spot, you can accept my ball and keep it." Spot barked, wagged his tail, and took the ball home. Now Spot and Lily were both happy because they had a new friend to play with.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom had a bicycle. It was red and shiny. He loved to ride it every day. One day, he saw a big, fierce dog in the park. The dog was barking loudly. Tom was scared.
Tom's mom saw that he was scared. She said, "Tom, don't worry. Kneel down and be quiet. The dog will not hurt you." Tom did what his mom said. He knelt down and waited. The fierce dog came closer, sniffed him, and then walked away.
After the dog left, Tom stood up and smiled. He said, "Thank you, Mom. I'm not scared anymore." Tom's mom smiled too. They walked to the bicycle, and Tom rode it happily. From that day on, Tom was never scared of fierce dogs again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a perfect red ball. She loved to play with her ball every day. She would bounce it on her knee and throw it high in the sky.
One day, Lily's friend Tim came over to play. Tim saw the perfect red ball and said, "Wow! I admire your ball, Lily! Can I play with it too?" Lily smiled and said, "Yes, you can play with my ball, Tim."
Together, they bounced the ball on their knees and threw it high in the sky. They laughed and played all day. At the end of the day, they were tired but happy. They learned that sharing is fun and makes everyone happy. And that was the moral value of the story.

Once upon a time, there was a fierce dog named Max. Max loved to create things with his paws. One day, Max found a big bowl in the yard.
Max had an idea. He said to his friend, Cat, "I will create something in this bowl!" Cat was excited and wanted to help. They both used their paws to put dirt and water into the bowl.
They mixed and mixed until they made mud. Max and Cat laughed and played in the mud. Then, Max said, "Look, Cat! We made mud in the bowl!" Cat smiled and said, "We did it! We are great at creating!" And they played happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a new girl named Carla. Carla was very excited to start school. She wanted to measure how it would feel. On the first day, she had to wait for the bus to take her to school. 
When she got there, she saw a lot of new things: new books, new desks and new friends. She couldn't believe how much there was to measure! Everything looked different. She waited and watched the other kids, and new ideas slowly started to come to her. 
Finally the bell rang and school started. Carla couldn't wait to measure what the day had in store. She loved learning all the new things. Everywhere she looked, there was something new to measure! 
At the end of the day Carla said goodbye to all of the new friends she had made. She was so proud of all she had measured that day! 
The love of learning that Carla had found would last her many years, and she felt excited to measure every new day of school.

Once upon a time, there was a big pit. In the pit, there were many rocks. One day, a little girl named Lucy and her dog, Spot, went to play near the pit.
Lucy picked up a rock and said, "Look, Spot! This rock is so big!" Spot barked and wagged his tail. They played a game where Lucy threw the rock and Spot would bring it back.
After playing for a while, Lucy and Spot got hungry. Lucy's mom had packed a tasty snack for them. They sat down near the pit and ate their tasty food. "Yum, this is so good!" said Lucy. Spot barked happily as he ate his treat.
They played and ate until the sun went down. Then, Lucy and Spot went home, tired but happy from their fun day at the pit.

Once there was a cool fish named Billy. He lived in a bright blue pond with lots of other fish. Every day, Billy would play hide and seek with his best friends, swimming around lots of rocks. 
One day, a big net came down into the pond. The net started to scoop up all the fish in the pond - even Billy! He was scared and tried to swim away, but the net kept getting closer to him. 
Suddenly, a bright blue dolphin appeared! The dolphin slapped the net with its powerful tail, pushing the net away from Billy and the other fish. Relief flooded over Billy as he swam away from the scary net. 
The other fish were so happy to be free. They thanked the dolphin for saving them and invited him to stay and play treasure hunting with them. The dolphin said yes and the fish all celebrated together. They had a lot of fun that day, and the dolphin stayed with them until the sun went down.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a bedroom with a big bed and many toys. He loved to play with his toys all day long.
One day, Tim decided to store his toys in a mighty big box. He put all his toys inside the box and closed the lid. Tim felt happy because his bedroom was now clean and tidy.
Later, Tim wanted to play with his favorite toy, a red car. He opened the mighty big box and found his red car. Tim played with his red car in his bedroom and had lots of fun. When he was done, he stored the red car back in the mighty big box and went to sleep in his big bed.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big slide and wanted to play on it. He climbed the hard steps and got to the top. Tim was so happy and excited to slide down.
As Tim was about to slide, he saw a small rock on the slide. He thought it might hurt him, so he picked it up and put it away. Then, he slid down the slide very fast. Whee! It was so much fun!
But when Tim got to the bottom of the slide, he saw something unexpected. The rock he moved was not just a rock, it was a key! The key opened a secret door in the park. Behind the door was a room full of toys and games. Tim was so surprised and happy. He played there all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Buddy. Buddy was very alert. He liked to watch the birds and play with his ball. One day, Buddy saw a big cat. The cat was not happy.
"Please, can you help me?" the cat requested. "I need a massage. My back hurts a lot." Buddy wanted to help the cat, so he used his paws to give the cat a massage.
"Thank you, Buddy," said the cat. "You are a good friend." Buddy was happy to help the cat. They played together all day and became best friends.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a razor in his dad's room. He knew he shouldn't touch it, but he was very curious. Tim decided to ask his dad about the razor.
"Dad, what is this?" Tim asked, holding the razor carefully. His dad smiled and said, "That is a razor, Tim. It is for grown-ups to use. It can be sharp, so please don't touch it."
Tim was persistent and wanted to know more. He asked his dad, "Can I mail the razor to someone?" His dad thought for a moment and said, "No, Tim. It's not safe to mail sharp things like razors. Let's put it back in my room, and you can help me mail a letter instead."
Tim agreed and helped his dad mail a letter. They went to the mailbox, and Tim dropped the letter inside. Tim felt proud that he could help his dad with something grown-up like mailing a letter.
From that day on, Tim never touched the razor again. He knew it was not safe for little kids like him. Instead, he would help his dad with other things, like mailing letters and picking up toys. Tim was happy to be a helpful little boy.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play with her friends in the big park. One day, as she was playing with her friends, she saw a big jar of butter.
Kitty said to her friends, "Look, I found butter! Let's eat it!" Her friends were happy and wanted to eat the butter too. They all sat down and started to eat the butter together.
As they ate, a big dog named Doggo came up to them. Doggo was not happy. He said, "That is my butter! You should not take things that are not yours." Kitty and her friends were scared, but they knew Doggo was right.
Kitty said, "We are sorry, Doggo. We did not know it was yours." Doggo was kind and said, "It's okay. We can all share the butter and be friends." So they all ate the butter together and became good friends.
In the end, Kitty and her friends learned that it is important to ask before taking something that is not yours. They all played together in the park and shared their things with each other.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. He wanted to find the biggest rock in the universe. He looked and looked, but he could not find it. He saw a big rock, but it was not the biggest rock. Tim felt hurt that he could not find it. He sat down and started to cry.
A little girl named Sue saw Tim crying. She walked up to him and asked, "Why are you crying?" Tim told her, "I want to find the biggest rock in the universe, but I can't find it." Sue said, "Don't cry! Let's look for it together!"
Tim and Sue struggled to look for the rock. They looked under trees and behind bushes. Then, they saw a big, shiny thing in the sky. It was the moon! Tim and Sue laughed. They found the biggest rock in the universe, and it was in the sky all along. They were happy and played together until it was time to go home.

Sara and her mom travel to the market every week. They take the bus and look out the window. Sara likes to see the trees, the cars, and the people. She asks her mom many questions.
At the market, they buy fruits, vegetables, bread, and cheese. Sara helps her mom carry the bags. She also gets a treat: a lovely red apple. She bites it and smiles. It is sweet and juicy.
Sara and her mom meet their friend Anna at the market. Anna has a baby boy. His name is Leo. Sara likes to play with Leo. She gives him her apple. He laughs and gums it. Sara and Anna share their toys and stories.
They have a picnic at the park near the market. They eat their food and drink their juice. They enjoy the sun and the breeze. They watch the birds and the flowers. They are happy and thankful. They hug and say goodbye.
Sara and her mom travel back home on the bus. Sara is tired and sleepy. She rests her head on her mom's shoulder. She dreams of the market and the lovely day.

One day, a boy named Tim found a telephone. It was not a normal telephone. It was big and red. Tim was very happy. He wanted to play with the telephone.
Tim took the telephone to his friend, Lily. "Look, I found a big red telephone!" Tim said. Lily looked at the telephone. She was surprised. "Wow, that's not a normal telephone!" she said. They both laughed.
Tim and Lily played with the telephone. They pretended to accept calls from their friends. They had a lot of fun. At the end of the day, they put the telephone back where Tim found it. They knew they could play with it again another day.

Once upon a time, there was a goose. The goose was big and white. He liked to play with his friends. One day, the goose saw a little boy. The boy was sad. He lost his toy. The goose wanted to help.
The goose and the boy looked for the toy. They looked in the grass. They looked by the water. They looked under the trees. The toy was not available. The boy was still sad.
Then, the goose had an idea. He asked his friends to help. All the animals looked for the toy. They found the toy under a big rock. The boy was happy. The goose and the boy became good friends. They played together every day.

One day, a little girl named Sue found a shiny ornament. It was round and pretty. Sue wanted to make it even more shiny. So, she decided to polish it.
Sue took a soft cloth and rubbed the ornament. She rubbed it so much that it began to look crazy shiny. Sue was very happy with her work. She showed the ornament to her mom and dad.
Her mom and dad loved the crazy shiny ornament. They put it on a special place in their home. Everyone who came to visit saw the ornament and said how nice it was. Sue felt proud and happy.

Once upon a time, there was an enormous dog named Max. Max loved to play outside in the big yard. One day, while playing, he found a small pit in the ground.
Max was curious about the pit, so he started to dig. As he dug, the pit began to increase in size. He dug and dug until the pit was very big. Max was very happy with his new big pit.
Max's friends, the little birds, saw the big pit and came to play with him. They all played together in the enormous pit, having lots of fun. And from that day on, Max and his friends enjoyed playing in the pit every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little frog who lived in a swamp. The frog had many friends, but he was always anxious. He was scared of big animals and loud noises. He wanted to be brave like his friends.
One day, the frog saw a big bird near the swamp. He was very anxious, but he wanted to show his friends he was brave. So, he hopped closer to the big bird. The bird saw the frog and said, "Hello, little frog! Do you want to play with me?"
The frog was still anxious, but he wanted to be brave. So, he said, "Yes, I want to play with you." They played all day in the swamp, and the frog was very happy. He learned that being brave was not so hard after all.

Once upon a time, there was a big chair with a lot of power. Whoever sat on the chair became the boss of everyone. One day, a selfish boy named Tom found the chair in the woods.
Tom sat on the chair and felt the power. He told his friend, Sam, "Now I am the boss. You must do what I say." Sam did not like this, but he listened to Tom because of the chair's power.
Tom became more and more selfish. He made Sam and the other kids do all the work while he sat in the chair. One day, the chair broke because Tom was too heavy. Tom lost the power, and nobody liked him anymore.

Once there was a little boy called Jack. Jack was playing with his toys in his bed one day. Suddenly, his room was filled with a loud noise. Jack was so scared that he scattered his toys around his bed. He got really cross and started to scream and shout.
His mum heard the noise and ran into the room. She hugged him tight and said to him in a gentle voice, "What happened Jack?"
Jack told her about the loud noise. His mum asked why he scattered his toys. He replied that he got scared.
His mum hugged him again and said "It's okay Jack, no one is angry. Next time if you get scared, take a deep breath and try to stay calm".
Jack learnt from his mistake. He learned to keep calm no matter how much he got scared. From that day on, he remembered his mum's gentle advice.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a hairy bug in his bathtub. Tim was scared and called his mom. "Mom, come see the bug!" he said.
Mom came to see the bug. "Oh, it's just a little bug," she said. "Let's catch it and put it outside." Tim and his mom got a cup and a piece of paper.
They caught the hairy bug with the cup and paper. They took it outside and let the bug go. The bug flew away, and Tim felt happy.
From that day on, Tim was not scared of hairy bugs anymore. He knew he could catch them and put them outside. Tim and his mom were a great team.

Sara and Ben were twins who loved to play. They had many toys, but their favorite was a big teddy bear. They named him Fluffy and gave him hugs and kisses every day.
One day, Sara and Ben had a fight. They both wanted to play with Fluffy, but they did not want to share. They pulled and tugged on Fluffy's arms and legs, trying to take him away from each other. Fluffy did not like this. He felt his muscle hurt and his fur rip.
"Stop!" Fluffy said in a soft voice. "You are hurting me. I love you both, but you have to be nice to me and to each other. Why don't you settle down and play something else?"
Sara and Ben were surprised. They did not know Fluffy could talk. They felt sorry for Fluffy and for making him sad. They let go of Fluffy and said they were sorry.
"We are sorry, Fluffy," Sara said. "We love you too. You are our best friend."
"Yes, we are sorry, Fluffy," Ben said. "You are our best friend. You are so adorable and cuddly."
They hugged Fluffy and kissed his nose. Fluffy smiled and hugged them back. He was happy again. He said they could take turns playing with him, or play with him together. Sara and Ben agreed. They said they would not fight anymore. They played with Fluffy and had fun. They were a happy family.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He liked to play with his friends, but he was too anxious to ask them. One day, Tim saw his friends doing exercise in the park. They were jumping and running, and it looked like fun.
Tim went to his big sister, Sue, and asked her to lend him some courage. Sue said, "Tim, you don't need to borrow courage. You have it inside you. Just go and ask your friends to play." Tim felt better and went to the park.
Tim asked his friends if he could join them. They said yes, and they all had fun doing exercise together. Tim learned that he didn't need to be anxious about asking to play. He just needed to be brave and try. And that's how Tim found his courage.

Once there was a girl who liked to joke a lot. She would joke about everything, no matter what the topic was.
One day, the girl was playing outside when she saw a fork lying on the ground. She thought it was silly, so she joked about it.
The girl's mother saw her and got very mad. She thought it was not nice of the girl to make such a joke. She grabbed the fork and told the girl off.
The girl got scared and was very sad. She wished she could make it better. Then, she had a thought. She picked a nice flower from the garden and gave it to her mom, to show her that she was being thoughtful.
The mother was happy and said that the girl can keep the fork as a reminder not to joke about everything.
The girl was sad, but she knew it was a lesson learned. She thanked her mom and kept the fork as a reminder.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a big, red balloon. He was very happy. He wanted to play with the balloon all day. Tim tried to raise the balloon high up in the sky.
But the balloon did not go up. Tim was confused. He tried again to raise the balloon. He jumped and threw the balloon up. But it still did not go up.
Tim's mom saw him and came to help. She tied a long string to the balloon. Now, when Tim raised the balloon, it went up high in the sky. Tim was very happy and played with the balloon all day.

Once there was a little girl and she wanted to dress up like a fancy princess. She went to her room and pulled out a bright pink mask. She put it on and began to twirl around, letting it fly in the air. She looked so fancy!
Next, she searched her dresser to find something else to add. She found a sparkly necklace and some purple tights. She added them to complete her outfit and jumped on the bed. She was so pleased with her fancy look.
The little girl twirled and danced all around the house, showing off her costume. Everyone cheered and smiled as they enjoyed the show. Her mom even took photos, so they could always remember her special outfit. 
The little girl beamed with delight. She had added all the things she needed to feel like a fancy princess.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the store with his mom. They wanted to buy a cheap toy for Tim to play with. Tim was very happy and excited.
In the store, Tim saw a toy he liked. It was a small car. "Mom, can I have this car, please?" Tim asked. "Yes, it is cheap, you can have it," his mom said. Tim was very happy and held the car tightly.
After a minute, they went to pay for the car. But when Tim looked at his hand, the car was gone! It seemed to vanish! Tim was sad and cried. His mom looked around and found the car under a shelf. She gave it back to Tim and he smiled again. They bought the car and went home to play.

Tim and Sam are friends. They like to play with cars and blocks. One day, they find a big bin in the park. The bin is full of old things. Tim and Sam want to see what is inside.
"Look, Sam, a bin!" Tim says. "Maybe we can find something great in there."
"OK, Tim, let's look," Sam says. They run to the bin and start to dig.
They find a hat, a ball, a book, and a doll. Tim likes the hat and the ball. Sam likes the book and the doll.
"Can I have the hat and the ball, Sam?" Tim asks.
"No, Tim, I want the hat and the ball too," Sam says.
"But I saw them first, Sam," Tim says.
"That's not fair, Tim," Sam says.
They start to pull and tug on the hat and the ball. They are not happy.
A lady sees them and comes over. She is the owner of the bin. She is not happy either.
"Hey, you two, stop that!" she says. "What are you doing with my things?"
"Sorry, lady, we just wanted to play," Tim says.
"Well, you can't play with my things. They are not yours. They are mine. I put them in the bin because I don't need them anymore. But you can't take them. That's not nice," she says.
"Sorry, lady, we didn't know," Sam says.
The lady sighs. She sees that Tim and Sam are sad. She has an idea.
"Why don't you suggest something else to play with?" she says. "Maybe you can share the book and the doll. Or you can play with the swings and the slide. Or you can make a sandcastle. There are many things you can do in the park. You don't need to fight over the bin."
Tim and Sam think about it. They agree that the lady is right. They say sorry to the lady and to each other. They put the hat and the ball back in the bin. They decide to play with the book and the doll. They take turns to read and to cuddle. They are happy again.
The lady smiles. She is glad that Tim and Sam are friends again. She thinks they are great kids. She leaves the bin and goes home.

Once upon a time, there was a normal boy named Tim. Tim had a big closet in his room. He liked to play with his toys and put them in the closet when he was done.
One day, Tim saw a sign on the closet door. The sign had a picture of a toy. Tim was very happy and opened the closet to find the toy.
But when he opened the closet, all his toys were gone! Tim was very sad. He looked everywhere, but he could not find his toys. The sign had tricked him, and now his toys were gone forever.

One day, a goat saw a big, round ball. The ball was fake, but the goat did not know. The goat wanted to play with the ball. The goat tried to roll the ball with his head. But the ball did not roll. The goat was sad.
A little boy saw the goat and wanted to help. The boy tried to roll the ball, too. The boy and the goat pushed and pushed, but the ball still did not roll. They did not know why the ball was not rolling.
Then, a big wind came and blew the fake ball away. The boy and the goat were surprised. They saw a real ball under the fake ball. The boy and the goat were happy. They played with the real ball, and it rolled very well. They became good friends and had lots of fun.

One day, a modest frog named Tim lived by a lake. He liked to hang out with his friends. They would play and jump all day.
One day, Tim and his friends saw a big tree near the lake. They decided to hang from the tree's branches. They had lots of fun swinging and hanging.
After playing, they went back to the lake to swim. They splashed and played until the sun went down. Tim and his friends had a great day by the lake.

One day, a girl named Mia went with her mom to buy a rug. They went to a big store with many rugs. Mia was very excited to find the perfect rug for her room.
In the store, they saw a flexible rug. It was soft and easy to bend. Mia liked the flexible rug because it was fun to touch. She told her mom, "I want this rug for my room!"
Mia's mom agreed to buy the flexible rug. They took it home and put it in Mia's room. Mia was very happy to have the new rug. She loved playing on it and showing it to her friends.

One day, Molly and her mom were walking in the park. Molly was excited because she hadn't been in the park for a long time. 
Suddenly, Molly stepped in a big puddle of mud. She started to cry, because her shoes and her socks were all muddy. 
Her mom said, "It's okay, Molly. No need to cry. Let's take your shoes off right here and wash them clean."
Molly felt better. She put her feet in the mild water and they became clean again. 
At the end of the day, Molly was happy again. She said, "Mom, I like walking in the park again. And I even like the mud!" 
Molly's mom smiled and said, "I'm glad you're feeling better, Molly."

Lily was a girl who liked to wonder. She wondered about the stars, the flowers, the animals, and everything else. She liked to ask questions and learn new things. She also liked to make things with her hands. She made original drawings, sculptures, and crafts with paper, clay, and glue.
One day, she found a cricket in the grass. She wondered what it was and how it made its sound. She picked it up gently and put it in a jar. She poked some holes in the lid and gave it some leaves and water. She wanted to keep it as a pet and show it to her friends.
But her brother Tom did not like her idea. He said crickets were not pets. He said they were bugs and they belonged outside. He said Lily was silly and mean for trapping the cricket. He wanted to let it go.
"Please, Tom, don't take my cricket!" Lily cried. "I like it and I want to learn more about it. It is not mean, it is original. It makes a beautiful sound."
"No, Lily, it is not beautiful, it is annoying. It is not original, it is common. It makes a noise, not a sound. And it is not happy in your jar. It wants to be free. Give it to me!" Tom said. He tried to grab the jar from Lily's hands.
Lily and Tom started to fight over the jar. They pulled and pushed and shouted. The jar fell to the ground and broke. The cricket jumped out and ran away. Lily and Tom stopped fighting and looked at the broken jar and the empty grass. They felt sad and sorry.
They realized they had been wrong. They had not listened to each other or to the cricket. They had not been kind or respectful. They had not wondered together. They hugged and said sorry. They decided to look for another cricket and watch it from a distance. They wondered what it would do and where it would go. They learned to appreciate its originality and beauty. They learned to wonder as friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little pony named Pippin. Pippin loved to play in the deep mud puddles on the farm. One day, Pippin jumped into a big, deep mud puddle and got very dirty.
Pippin's mom saw him and said, "Pippin, you must not play in the deep mud. It is not good for you. I must punish you now." Pippin felt sad because he knew he did something wrong.
For his punish, Pippin had to take a bath to clean off all the mud. Pippin did not like the bath, but he knew he had to listen to his mom. After the bath, Pippin promised his mom he would not play in the deep mud puddles again.

One day, a big bull lay in a field. The field was safe and the bull was happy. The sun was bright and warm.
A little boy came to the field. He saw the bull and said, "Hi bull, can I play with you?" The bull did not say anything. The boy went closer and closer to the bull to play.
The bull got up and was not happy. The boy was scared. The bull ran fast at the boy. The boy tried to run away, but the bull was too fast. The boy fell down and the bull was on top of him. The boy was not safe and he was very sad.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved to help her mommy in the kitchen. One day, Lucy's mommy asked her to pour some thick juice into a cup. Lucy was very excited and happy to help.
Lucy opened the drawer and found a big cup. She carefully held the juice bottle and started pouring. "Look, Mommy! I'm pouring the juice!" Lucy said with a big smile. Her mommy smiled back and said, "Good job, Lucy! You are such a great helper."
When Lucy finished pouring the juice, she noticed that some of it had spilled on the table. Lucy felt sad and thought she had made a mistake. But her mommy said, "It's okay, Lucy. We all make mistakes. The important thing is to learn from them and do better next time." Lucy nodded and felt better. Together, they cleaned up the spill and enjoyed their thick juice.
The moral of the story is that it's okay to make mistakes, as long as we learn from them and try to do better next time.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play with their toys in their room. One day, they find a big box in the closet. It has many ties inside. The ties are different colors and shapes.
"Look, Ben, ties!" Lily says. "Let's play with them."
"Okay, Lily, but we have to be careful. These are dad's ties. He wears them to work. He does not like it when we make them dirty or messy." Ben says.
They take out the ties and put them on the floor. They pretend they are snakes, belts, ropes, and scarves. They have fun with the ties. They make noises and laugh.
But then, they hear a sound. It is dad. He is coming home from work. He is walking up the stairs.
"Quick, Lily, we have to put the ties back in the box. Dad is coming. He will be angry if he sees them." Ben says.
They try to put the ties back in the box. But they are too many and too tangled. They cannot do it fast enough.
Dad opens the door and sees the ties on the floor. He is not happy.
"Lily, Ben, what are you doing with my ties? You know you are not supposed to touch them. They are not toys. They are for work. You have made them dirty and messy. How will I wear them now?" Dad says.
Lily and Ben feel bad. They know they have done something wrong. They look at dad and say sorry.
"We are sorry, dad. We just wanted to play with the ties. They are so pretty and fun. We did not mean to make them dirty and messy. We will help you clean them and fix them." Lily and Ben say.
Dad sighs. He is still not happy, but he is not angry anymore. He sees that Lily and Ben are sorry and want to help. He loves them very much.
"Okay, Lily and Ben. I forgive you. But you have to listen to me. You cannot play with my ties again. They are not toys. They are for work. You have to respect my things. Do you understand?" Dad says.
Lily and Ben nod. They understand. They hug dad and say they love him.
"I love you too, Lily and Ben. Now, let's clean up this mess and then we can have some light snacks. How does that sound?" Dad says.
Lily and Ben smile. They like light snacks. They help dad clean up the ties and put them back in the box. Then they go to the kitchen and have some light snacks. They are happy again.

One day, a little dog named Max was walking in the park. He saw a big cat named Tom. Tom was doing something dangerous. He was playing with a ball near the road. Max wanted to help Tom.
Max went to Tom and said, "Tom, it is not good to play near the road. It is dangerous." Tom did not like what Max said. He started to complain, "I can play wherever I want. You are not the boss of me."
Max knew there was a law that said animals should not play near the road. He told Tom, "There is a law that says we cannot play here. It is for our safety." Tom did not like the law, but he knew Max was right. They both went to a safe place in the park to play with the ball. They had lots of fun and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a fragile little fork. It lived in a big kitchen with a kind boy named Tim. Tim loved to cook and always welcomed the fork to help him make yummy food.
One day, Tim wanted to make a cake. He asked his friend, the fork, "Will you help me make a cake?" The little fork was so happy and said, "Yes, I will help you!" Together, they mixed the cake and put it in the oven to bake.
As they waited for the cake to be done, Tim and the fragile fork talked and laughed. They were so excited to eat the yummy cake. When the cake was ready, Tim took it out of the oven and let it cool. Then, they shared the cake with everyone in the house. Everyone welcomed the little fork and thanked it for helping Tim make such a delicious cake.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Sally. One day, Sally was walking in the park when she saw a yellow snake. She was scared and started to cry.
"What's wrong, Sally?" asked her mom.
"I saw a yellow snake!" said Sally.
"It's ok. Snakes aren't so bad, they keep away all the bugs," explained her mom. 
Sally felt better after her mom's reassurance. She then saw a beetle and ran way.
When Sally got home, her mom asked her, "What did you learn today?"
Sally answered, "I learned that it's ok to be scared at first, but if I take a closer look and learn more about something, then I might not need to be so scared".
Moral of the story: We should try to learn more about something before we are scared of it.

One day, a boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a big bush. The bush was green and pretty. Tim wanted to write his name on it. He took a stick and started to write.
A voice said, "Hey! Please don't write on me!" Tim looked around and saw a talking bush! The bush was honest and nice. "I am sorry, I didn't know you could talk," said Tim. The bush said, "It's okay. Let's be friends."
Tim and the bush became good friends. They played games and had fun. One day, the bush said, "I have a secret." Tim was excited. The bush turned into a big, fun slide! Tim and the bush played on the slide all day. They were happy and always stayed friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little white bunny named Bounce. Bounce loved to hop and bounce all day long. He had a big bottle filled with yummy juice that he would drink when he got thirsty from bouncing.
One day, Bounce was bouncing in the green grass when he lost his bottle. He looked under the big tree, behind the red flower, and even in the blue pond, but he could not find his bottle. Bounce felt sad because he was very thirsty.
Just then, a friendly bird saw Bounce and asked, "Why are you sad?" Bounce told the bird about his lost bottle. The bird said, "I will help you find your bottle!" Together, they looked and looked until they found the bottle under a yellow bush. Bounce was so happy and thanked the bird. They became best friends and bounced together every day.

Mandy was a small girl who loved adventures. Today she had the best idea ever. She wanted to go and bathe in the river.
Mandy grabbed her red balloon and ran to the river. As soon as she arrived, she took off her shoes and skipped into the shallow water. It was cold and Mandy was happy. She loved the feel of being in the water.
Mandy played with her balloon and splashed around. She sang a song and laughed as the water tickled her toes. Soon it was time to go home. Mandy had a quick bath, then dried herself off and put her shoes back on.
She took her red balloon and went on her way. It was the best adventure ever!

Once upon a time, there was a big red truck. The truck loved to help people. It would carry things from one place to another. The truck was very successful at its job.
One day, the truck was carrying a big load of toys. The truck realized that it had to go very far, to a place where kids did not have toys. The truck wanted to make the kids happy.
The truck drove for a long time. It went up hills and down hills. It went through rain and sun. Finally, the truck reached the place where the kids were. The kids saw the truck and smiled. They played with the toys, and the truck knew it had done a good job. The truck felt very happy and proud.

Once upon a time, there was a very colorful kite that lived in the sky. It had two big long legs, so it could roll and move around up high.
One day, the kite was happily rolling around in the sky when it spotted two children below.
"Hey, look at me!" the kite shouted. 
The two children stopped and looked up.
"Wow, how beautiful," they said.
The kite smiled and rolled around the sky, showing off its bright, colorful legs. 
The kite kept rolling around and finally came to a stop right in front of the children.
"Let's take turns holding it and flying it!" the children exclaimed. 
So the children took turns holding the kite and flying it high into the sky. The kite happily rolled around in the wind, feeling free and alive. 
And the colorful kite waved goodbye to the two children, thanking them for the wonderful time they had together.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who had a pet cow. Every day the cow would make a request for hay, and the little girl was happy to oblige. She thought it was so yummy to see her cow munching away on the hay.
One day, while the little girl was feeding her cow, a mean fox came along. It was a very fierce fox, and it growled and barked and asked for some of the hay. Even though the little girl was scared, she had to deny the fox's request - the hay was for her cow only.
The fox became very angry and started to chase the little girl and her cow! The fox was so angry he even forgot all about the hay. The little girl was so scared, she ended up running away and the fox got the hay in the end. It was a very bad ending for the poor cow - all the hay was gone!

One day, a boy named Tim found a big pocket on his shirt. He was happy. Tim liked to put things in his pocket. He put a toy car, a red ball, and a small book in it. Tim's pocket was full. He felt good.
Tim went to play with his friends. They played catch with the red ball. Tim threw the ball very far. His arm hurt a little, but he was proud. His friends were amazed. They all had fun.
Then, Tim saw his toy car was gone. He was sad. He looked everywhere. Tim thought he lost it. He was about to cry when he felt something in his pocket. Tim forgot to close his pocket. He opened the small book, and there was his toy car! It was hiding in the book all along. Tim laughed and showed his friends. They all laughed too.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy cat named Max. Max lived in a small house with a girl named Lily. Lily loved to draw with her pastel colors. One day, Max saw the door was open. He wanted to go outside and play.
Max went outside and saw a big tree. He climbed up the tree and sat on a branch. But Max was too grumpy to enjoy the view. He missed Lily and her pastel drawings. Max wanted to go back home, but he was too scared to climb down.
Lily saw Max in the tree and went to help him. She told Max to shut his eyes and trust her. Max did as Lily said, and she helped him down. They went back inside, and Max was happy again. The moral of the story is to trust your friends and be happy with what you have.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a razor in the bathroom. He knew it was bad to touch it, but he was very curious. So, he picked it up and looked at it. 
Suddenly, his big sister, Ann, came into the bathroom. She saw Tim holding the razor and knew it was bad. She wanted to help him, so she took it away from him. Ann told him, "No, Tim! This is bad. Don't touch it!"
Tim felt sad and didn't want to be bad. He wanted to be a good boy. So, he decided to listen to his sister. Ann gave him a soft slap on the hand and said, "Remember, don't touch bad things." Tim nodded and promised to be good.

Once upon a time, in a big universe, there lived a furry cat named Kitty. Kitty had lots of friends, but there was one thing she did not like. She would often say, "I hate getting wet!"
One sunny day, Kitty and her friend, Bunny, went for a walk. They saw pretty flowers and played with a big ball. They had so much fun together. But then, the sky turned gray and rain started to fall.
Kitty said to Bunny, "Oh no! I hate rain! I don't want to get wet!" Bunny thought for a moment and then said, "Let's hide under this big tree. We can stay dry there." Kitty and Bunny hid under the tree and watched the rain fall.
After a while, the rain stopped and the sun came out again. Kitty and Bunny were happy to see the sun. They continued their walk and jumped in puddles. Kitty learned that even though she hated getting wet, she could still have fun with her friend.
At the end of the day, Kitty and Bunny went home. They were tired but happy. Kitty realized that the universe was full of things she might not like, but with good friends, she could still have a great time.

One day, Joey was in his classroom, feeling very adventurous. His teacher said to him, "Joey, it's time to go on an adventure". Joey was so excited! He said, "Yes! Let's go!" 
The teacher said, "Let's look in the hallway". They both ran to the hallway and saw all the pictures of the students on the wall. Joey said, "Wow, it's so pretty!" 
The teacher asked, "Where should we go now?" Joey was thinking very hard. He said, "Let's go to the garden." The teacher smiled and said, "That sounds like a great idea". 
So Joey and his teacher went to the garden. They saw a butterfly fluttering around and a bird singing in a tree. Joey was so happy and said, "This is such an adventurous day!"

Lily and Ben were friends who liked to play together. They had many toys, but Lily's favorite was a big doll with long hair and a pink dress. Ben liked the doll too, but he had a car that made noises and lights.
One day, Lily's mom made rice for lunch. She gave Lily and Ben each a bowl and a spoon. "Eat well, children," she said. "Rice is good for you."
Lily and Ben started to eat, but Ben was not very hungry. He looked at Lily's doll and wanted to play with it. He put his spoon down and reached for the doll. "Can I have your doll, Lily?" he asked.
Lily was not happy. She loved her doll and did not want to share it. She held the doll tightly and said, "No, Ben. This is my doll. You have your car."
Ben felt jealous. He thought Lily's doll was better than his car. He tried to grab the doll from Lily, but Lily pulled it back. They started to fight over the doll. They did not hear Lily's mom coming back to the kitchen.
Lily's mom saw them fighting and was angry. She recognized the doll as a gift from Lily's grandma. She took the doll from them and said, "Stop it, both of you. Fighting is not nice. You should share your toys and be kind to each other."
Lily and Ben felt sorry. They did not want to make Lily's mom angry. They said, "We're sorry, mom. We won't fight again."
Lily's mom smiled and said, "That's good. Now finish your rice and then you can play nicely. And remember, the doll and the car are both special. You can take turns and enjoy them together."
Lily and Ben nodded and ate their rice. They learned their lesson and decided to be better friends. They played with the doll and the car and had fun. They were not jealous anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a calm dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his ball. He would run and jump all day long. Spot's best friend was a little girl named Sue. Sue and Spot were always together.
One day, Sue and Spot were playing in the park. They were having so much fun. But then, something unexpected happened. Spot's ball got a big hole in it! Oh no! The ball was spoiled. Spot was very sad.
Sue wanted to help her friend. She had an idea. Sue found a stitch and fixed the hole in the ball. She gave the ball back to Spot. Spot was so happy! He couldn't believe it. The ball was not spoiled anymore. Now, Spot and Sue could play together again. And they lived happily ever after.

One day, a little boy named Tim was very excited. He saw dark clouds in the sky. His mom said there would be lightning soon. Tim wanted to see the lightning, but his mom told him to stay inside.
Tim stayed inside and looked out the window. He saw the lightning in the sky. It was very bright and fast. Tim clapped his hands and laughed. He liked the lightning show.
When the lightning stopped, Tim went outside to play. He told all his friends about the lightning he saw. They were excited too. They all hoped to see more lightning someday.

One day, a dog went to a lab. In the lab, there were many things to sort. The dog had a job to do. He had to sort the wet and dry toys. He was a big help to the people in the lab.
The dog was sorting the toys when he saw a wet cat. The cat was not happy. The cat did not like being wet. The dog wanted to help the cat. The dog and the cat did not always get along, but the dog wanted to be a good friend.
The dog had an idea. He took the wet cat to a warm, dry place in the lab. The cat became dry and happy. The dog and the cat became friends. They worked together to sort the toys in the lab. The people in the lab were happy too. The dog and the cat learned that they could be friends and help each other.

One day, a clumsy cat named Tom went for a walk. As he walked, he saw a big tree. Tom wanted to climb the tree. So, he leaned on the tree and started to climb.
As Tom climbed, he saw a bird on a branch. The bird said, "Hi, Tom! What are you doing?" Tom said, "I am climbing the tree. I want to see the top." The bird smiled and said, "Be careful, Tom. You are a clumsy cat."
Tom kept climbing, but he was not very good at it. He slipped and fell on a tap. The tap turned on, and water came out. Tom got wet and laughed. The bird laughed too and said, "Told you, Tom! You are a clumsy cat."
In the end, Tom and the bird became good friends. They played by the tap and had a lot of fun. Tom learned that it was okay to be clumsy and have fun with friends.

Once upon a time, there was a new ice-cream shop in town. A little boy named Tim and his mom went to the shop. Tim was very happy.
Inside the shop, Tim saw a big man who owned the shop. The man said, "Hello! I have many ice-creams. What do you want?" Tim looked at all the ice-creams and picked a red one.
Tim's mom bought the ice-cream for him. Tim said, "Thank you, mom!" He ate the ice-cream and it was very yummy. Tim and his mom went home with big smiles. They loved the new ice-cream shop.

Lily and Ben were friends who liked to play with blocks. They made colorful towers and houses with the blocks. One day, they had a big fight over who could use the red blocks. Lily wanted all the red blocks, but Ben wanted some too.
"No, they are mine!" Lily said, grabbing the red blocks from Ben.
"That's not fair!" Ben said, trying to get them back.
They pulled and pushed and shouted at each other. The blocks fell down and made a loud noise. Their teacher came to see what was wrong.
"What are you doing?" the teacher asked, looking at the mess.
Lily and Ben did not reply. They were angry and sad. They did not want to talk to each other or the teacher.
The teacher sighed and said, "You need to share and be kind. Red blocks are not more important than friendship. How would you feel if someone took your favorite blocks?"
Lily and Ben thought about it. They remembered how they used to play with all the blocks together. They felt sorry for being mean.
"Sorry, Lily," Ben said, giving her some red blocks.
"Sorry, Ben," Lily said, giving him some too.
They smiled and hugged. They started to build a new tower with all the colors. The teacher was happy to see them make peace. She knew they would be friends again.

Once upon a time, in a quiet little town, there lived a man named Tim. Tim had a job to work on a big house. He needed a ladder to reach the high parts of the house.
One sunny day, Tim went to the store to buy a ladder. He found a very tall ladder that would help him reach the high parts of the house. He was very happy and took the ladder back to his truck.
The next day, Tim started to work on the big house. He climbed the ladder and reached the high parts of the house. He worked hard all day. At the end of the day, Tim was very tired but happy because he finished his work. The big house looked beautiful, and everyone in the quiet little town was happy too.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy boy named Tim. Tim had a red wagon that he loved to play with. Every day, he would wake up, eat his breakfast, and then run outside to play with his wagon.
One day, while Tim was playing with his wagon, he saw a big dog. The dog was very big and very mean. Tim was scared, but he wanted to be brave. He said to the dog, "Go away, dog! I want to play with my wagon."
But the dog did not go away. Instead, the dog came closer to Tim and his wagon. Tim tried to run away, but the dog was too fast. The dog bit Tim's wagon and broke it into pieces. Tim was very sad and cried.
From that day on, Tim could not play with his wagon anymore. He missed his wagon and felt sad every time he saw the broken pieces. The big, mean dog had taken away Tim's favorite toy, and Tim could not be happy again.

Once upon a time, there was a delicate tutor named Miss Lily. She loved to clean her room and teach little kids. One day, she met a boy named Tom.
Miss Lily said, "Tom, do you like to clean your room?" Tom shook his head and said, "No, I don't like to clean. It's hard and I don't know how to do it."
Miss Lily smiled and said, "I can help you learn to clean your room. It's easy and fun!" Tom looked at her and said, "Okay, I want to learn."
The next day, Miss Lily went to Tom's house. She showed him how to clean his room. They picked up toys, put away clothes, and made the bed. Tom was happy and proud of his clean room.
From that day on, Tom always cleaned his room. He even helped his friends learn how to clean too. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, a little pink ostrich named Ollie lived in a big forest. Ollie had many friends, but his best friend was a small brown bunny named Benny. They liked to play together and have fun all day long.
One sunny day, Ollie and Benny were playing near the river. Benny said, "Ollie, do you remember the time we made a big sand house?" Ollie smiled and said, "Yes, Benny! That was a fun day. Let's do it again!" So, Ollie and Benny started to make a new sand house by the river.
As they were building, a little yellow bird flew down and asked if she could play too. Ollie and Benny said, "Of course, you can play with us!" So, the little yellow bird helped them build the sand house. They all played together and had a great time. And from that day on, Ollie, Benny, and the little yellow bird were the best of friends.

One day, a big wide tree was sad. A small boy saw the tree and said, "Why are you sad, tree?" The tree told the boy, "I am sad because I have poison on me, and I don't want to hurt anyone."
The boy had an idea. He went to his mom and asked for help. His mom knew what to do. She took a big hug cloth and cleaned the tree. The poison was gone!
The tree was happy now. The boy and the tree became good friends. The tree said, "Thank you, little boy." The boy hugged the tree and said, "Always ask for help when you need it." They played together every day, and the tree was never sad again.

Once upon a time there was a ball. It was green and bouncy. Every day, the ball would roll around and have lots of fun. One day, the ball saw a big, funny looking frog. The frog was hopping around in the grass making funny noises. The ball was very excited and it wanted to play with the frog. 
The ball rolled up to the frog and asked if he wanted to play. The frog said yes and the two of them had a great time playing together. They laughed, bounced and hopped around the grass. Then the frog asked the ball for a game of kick.
The ball loved kick and agreed straight away. They decided the frog would kick the ball and the frog kicked it very hard. The ball flew up through the air and landed on a nearby tree branch. The ball was happy and laughed as it looked down. The frog also laughed and it was so funny. 
They continued playing and having fun. Every time the frog kicked the ball, it flew further away. And at the end of the day, the green, bouncy ball was covered with dirt and exhausted but very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little chair. The chair was harmless and loved to play with the kids. One day, the chair saw a big car go zoom! The chair wanted to go zoom too, but it could not move.
The chair was sad. It wanted to find a way to go zoom. One day, a nice bird came to the chair. The bird had an idea. The bird and her friends picked up the chair with their beaks. They flew up, up, up in the sky.
Then, the birds let go of the chair, and the chair went zoom! It was so fun! The chair was happy. But, the birds were not done. They caught the chair and flew it back home. Now, the chair could go zoom with the kids and play all day. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely pig. This pig lived on a farm. The pig had no friends to play with.
One day, the pig went to the store. At the store, the pig met a cat. The cat and the pig became friends. They played all day.
But, surprise! The cat was a magic cat. The magic cat made more friends for the pig. Now, the lonely pig was not lonely anymore. They all played together and lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a grumpy cat named Tom. Tom did not like to play with the other animals. One day, Tom saw a big present near the door. He was very curious about what was inside.
Tom went to the present and started to shake it. As he shook the present, he heard a small voice from inside. "Please don't shake me," said the voice. Tom stopped shaking the present and looked around. He saw a little mouse named Tim standing next to the present.
"Hi, I am Tim," said the little mouse. "I came to make friends and play with you." Tom was not grumpy anymore. He liked his new friend Tim. They played together all day and became best friends. And from that day on, Tom was never grumpy again.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the garage with his dad. Inside, it was big and rough. There were many things like cars, tools, and boxes. Tim saw a ball and wanted to play with it.
"Can I play with the ball, Dad?" Tim asked. Dad said, "Yes, but be careful." Tim was happy and took the ball outside. He kicked it and threw it high in the sky. The ball went far away and landed in the bushes.
Tim went to find the ball. He had to kneel down to look under the bushes. He saw the ball and reached for it. As he grabbed the ball, he heard a tiny voice say, "Hello!" Tim looked around and saw a little bug. The bug said, "Thank you for finding my ball!" Tim smiled and said, "You're welcome!" Then, Tim and the bug played together with the ball.

Once upon a time, there was a funny lady who loved to joke. She lived in a small, clear house near the park. Every day, she would go to the park and make the kids laugh with her silly jokes.
One sunny day, the lady met a little girl named Mia. Mia was sad because she had lost her toy. The lady wanted to help Mia feel better, so she told her a joke. "Why did the chicken cross the road?" the lady asked. Mia looked at her and said, "I don't know. Why?" The lady smiled and said, "To get to the other side!" Mia giggled and started to feel better.
Together, the lady and Mia looked for the lost toy. They searched high and low, near and far. Finally, they found the toy under a big tree. Mia was so happy, she hugged the lady and thanked her for making her laugh and helping her find her toy. From that day on, the lady and Mia became the best of friends, and they always shared jokes and laughter together.

One day, a little girl named Lily saw a big package outside her house. She was very happy and excited. She loved getting packages. Lily thought about what could be in the package all day. She hoped it was a toy she wanted.
That night, Lily had a dream. In her dream, she opened the package and found a sad puppy inside. The puppy was sad because it was all alone. Lily knew she had to make the puppy happy. She played with the puppy and gave it a lot of love. The puppy became very happy.
The next day, Lily opened the package. Just like in her dream, there was a sad puppy inside. Lily remembered her dream and knew what to do. She played with the puppy and gave it lots of love. The puppy became very happy, and they both had lots of fun together.

Lily liked to drink milk every morning. She had a big cup with a pink straw. She drank her milk and felt happy and strong.
Lily also liked to write in her diary. She had a small book with a lock and a key. She wrote about her day and her dreams. She drew pictures of her friends and her toys.
One day, Lily had a very successful day. She learned how to tie her shoes by herself. She helped her mom bake a cake. She played with her brother and shared her toys. She was very proud and happy.
She drank her milk and wrote in her diary. She wrote: "Today I was successful. I did many good things. I love my family and myself." She smiled and locked her diary. She hugged her teddy bear and went to sleep.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby cat named Tom. Tom loved to eat spaghetti all day. One day, Tom's friend, a little bird named Tim, came to visit him.
"Tom, you eat too much spaghetti!" said Tim. "You should reverse and play with me outside."
Tom thought about it and said, "Okay, Tim. Let's go play outside." They played all day and Tom didn't eat any spaghetti.
At the end of the day, Tom felt happy and healthy. He learned that playing with friends was more fun than eating spaghetti all the time. And that was the day Tom learned the moral value of spending time with friends and being active.

One day, Mandy and her Dad went to the beach. Mandy was so excited to go and play in the sand! Dad said she had to stay close.
On the way, they noticed a weird object in the sky. It was getting bigger and bigger. "What is that?", Mandy asked. Dad said it looked like an airplane. Mandy and Dad watched as it flew closer.
The airplane became faster and faster. It was going so fast! Mandy clapped her hands. Then suddenly, the airplane made a weird sound, and flew off into the distance.
Mandy and Dad waved goodbye to the plane. Then Mandy said, "That was strange!" Dad smiled and said, "That was speedy and weird!"

Once upon a time, there was a fearful cat named Lucy. Lucy liked to watch TV all day. She would join her friends, the birds, and the dog to watch their favorite show. They all liked to sit together and have fun.
One day, Lucy saw a big box near the TV. She was scared but also excited. She thought, "What could be inside?" Lucy and her friends decided to open the box together. They all joined and pushed the box to see what was inside.
When they opened the box, they found a new friend! It was a little mouse named Timmy. At first, Lucy was scared of Timmy because she was a fearful cat. But soon, they all became good friends. Now, Lucy, the birds, the dog, and Timmy all join together to watch TV and have fun. And Lucy was not fearful anymore.

Once there was a furry truck. He was a very curious truck and he liked to gaze around the world. One day he was driving through a park and he gazed up into the sky. He saw some dark clouds and he started to feel afraid.
The furry truck decided he should get away from the park as quickly as he could. So he started driving really fast. But as he left the park, he saw a big puddle ahead. He desperately wanted to get around it but he couldn't. He was stuck.
Suddenly some lightning came down from the sky and the furry truck was so scared that he got stuck inside the big puddle. He was stuck in the water for what felt like forever.
Eventually the sun came out from behind the clouds and began to dry up the puddle. The furry truck was so happy as he drove along towards home. He had learned a lesson to always be a bit more careful when he gazed up into the sky.

Once upon a time, there were two little boys called Michael and Simon. They were best friends and always played together. 
One day, Michael received an invitation to a special party. He was very excited! He decided to record the invitation so he could look at it whenever he wanted to. 
Before the party, Michael and Simon went out to the park to have some fun. While they were playing, they got very dirty! 
Michael was worried that he wouldn't be able to go to the party if he was dirty, so he quickly cleaned off his clothes and packed away the invitation in his pocket. 
The moral of the story was that it's important to take care of your things, even when you're having fun, so you can make the most of opportunities.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play with yarn. One day, Kitty found a big box of yarn in the house. She was very happy.
Kitty asked her friend, Doggy, "Do you want to play with me and my yarn?" Doggy said, "Yes, let's play!" So, Kitty and Doggy started to play with the yarn. They had lots of fun.
As they played, they made a big mess. There was yarn everywhere. Kitty and Doggy did not know how to clean up. They tried to load the yarn back into the box, but it was too hard.
Mommy came home and saw the mess. She was not happy. She said, "Kitty and Doggy, you made a big mess! Now you must go to bed early." Kitty and Doggy were sad. They learned that they should always clean up after playing.

Once upon a time, there was a little town. In the town, there was a big cactus. The cactus was very organized. It had many green parts that grew up and up. The people in the town liked the cactus.
One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to zip his bag. He was not good at zipping. He tried and tried, but he could not zip his bag. Tim was sad. He went to the cactus to think.
Tim looked at the cactus. He saw how organized it was. He thought, "I can be like the cactus. I can be organized too." Tim went back to his bag. He tried to zip it again. This time, he was slow and careful. He zipped his bag! Tim was happy. He learned that being organized can help you do things better.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Jane. She was three years old. Jane's mommy and daddy took her to school one day.
When they got there, Jane saw a lot of other children with their parents. Her mommy held her hand as she walked into the classroom.
"It's time for class Jane," said her mommy.
Jane smiled and took off her jacket. She sat down and watched the other kids following their teacher.
Jane's teacher was nice. She taught the class a song and showed them how to draw. Jane tried really hard to follow along, but she was very tired.
Suddenly, Jane heard something in the other room. It sounded like it was coming from the corner. She walked over and saw a broken toy.
Jane picked it up and started to cry. She wanted to play with it, but it was too broken.
Her teacher came over and put her arm around Jane. She said, "Don't worry, I'll help you fix it."
The teacher and Jane worked together to make the toy whole again. Afterwards, Jane thanked her teacher and went back to following the class.
The class finished and Jane went home with her mommy and daddy. She proudly showed them her new toy that was no longer broken.
The End.

The sun was high in the sky and it was a beautiful day. John was happy. He was on his way to the park to find his friends.
John arrived at the park and saw his friends, Louise and Sarah. They were running around, being silly and playing.
John said to them, "Let's go and show you the open space. We can play hide and seek!"
Louise and Sarah were very excited. They followed John to the open space to play. It was an open space full of green grass, trees, and bushes. It was perfect for hide and seek.
John provided a great opportunity for everyone to have a successful game. Everyone had fun running around, and eventually John won the game.
The kids celebrated their successful game of hide and seek and ran home to tell their parents.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went for a walk with her mom. They went to a big park with lots of trees and a big pond. Lucy saw a rough rock near the pond and wanted to sit on it.
As Lucy sat on the rock, she saw a wise old owl in a tree. The owl looked at her and said, "Hello, little girl! I can explain some wisdom to you." Lucy looked at the owl and said, "Okay, Mr. Owl, please tell me your wisdom."
The owl said, "Always be kind to your friends and family. Share your toys and help them when they need you." Lucy smiled and said, "Thank you, Mr. Owl! I will always remember your wisdom." And from that day on, Lucy was always kind and helpful to everyone she met.

Lily and Tom were friends who liked to play in the park. One day, they saw a big pile of leaves and decided to jump in it. They had a lot of fun, throwing leaves in the air and making noises.
But then, Lily felt something hard and cold under her foot. She looked down and saw a skull. It was white and had holes for eyes and teeth. Lily was very scared and started to tremble. She screamed and ran away from the pile.
Tom heard her scream and came to see what was wrong. He saw the skull and was curious. He picked it up and looked at it. He said, "It's just a skull. It's not scary. It's from an animal that died a long time ago. Maybe a deer or a rabbit. See, it has horns and ears."
Lily stopped running and looked at Tom. She saw that he was not afraid. He was smiling and holding the skull. He said, "Do you want to see it? It's cool. You can pretend it's a mask or a hat or a toy."
Lily was still scared, but she was also curious. She walked slowly towards Tom and said, "Are you sure it's not scary? It looks scary."
Tom said, "No, it's not scary. It's just a skull. It can't hurt you or bite you or chase you. It's just bones. Come on, I'll show you. It's fun."
Lily trusted Tom and went closer to him. He gave her the skull and said, "Here, try it. Put it on your head and say 'boo'. It's funny."
Lily did what Tom said. She put the skull on her head and said 'boo'. She felt silly and laughed. Tom laughed too. They both looked jolly and happy.
They played with the skull for a while, making jokes and noises. They forgot their fear and had fun. They learned that some things are not scary if you look at them with curiosity and humor. They also learned that friends can help each other overcome their fears and have fun together. They were good friends who liked to play in the park.

Once there was a little girl named Daisy. Daisy had a long feather she kept in her pocket. She loved the feather and was always playing with it.
One day, Daisy was out in the garden. She held the feather tightly in her hand and started to shout. "Look at my feather!" she shouted.
Suddenly, a bird flew from the tree and landed on the ground next to Daisy. The bird looked very friendly. Daisy smiled at the bird and said, "Hello, birdie!"
The bird smiled back. Then it opened its beak and shouted, "Look at my feathers!" Daisy laughed.
The bird and Daisy played together for a long time. Daisy showed her feather to the bird and the bird showed Daisy its feathers. Daisy was so happy she almost forgot to go home.
When the sun started to go down, Daisy said goodbye to the bird and ran back inside. She put the precious feather back in her pocket and went inside to tell her mommy all about her day.

Two friends, Jack and Lily, were playing in the garden when they saw a mysterious tower standing in the middle of the trees. Lily was so excited to explore the tower that she ran up to it and peaked through the windows. Jack cautiously followed her, not wanting to be left behind. 
Lily put her hand on the door and said, "It looks delicate". Jack replied, "Maybe it's a fairy house?" That gave Lily an even bigger thrill and she opened the door and stepped inside cautiously. She gasped when she saw what it was. 
The inside of the tower was filled with beautiful glowing lights, like music in the air. Lily spoke softly to Jack, "It must be magic". Jack stepped forward, his eyes wide in awe. He whispered, "I can feel it." 
Lily and Jack stood there, spellbound by the magical tower, when suddenly they heard a voice speaking to them. The voice said, "Welcome! I am the magical keeper of this tower and I've been expecting you." Lily and Jack were too shocked to speak.
