One day, a little squirrel and a small bird were not getting along. The wise owl saw them and said, "Let us separate and talk." The little squirrel and the small bird were obedient and went to the wise owl. 
The wise owl told them, "You must learn to share and be kind to each other." The little squirrel and the small bird listened to the wise owl's wisdom. They said, "Thank you, wise owl. We will be kind and share." From that day on, the little squirrel and the small bird were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. Lily liked to perform in front of her friends. She would dance, sing, and make everyone happy. Lily was very good at what she did.
One day, Lily found a big book called a dictionary. The dictionary had many words in it. Lily wanted to learn all the words so she could be even better at performing. She thought the dictionary was brilliant.
Lily practiced and practiced with the dictionary. She learned new words and became very smart. When she performed for her friends again, they were amazed. They thought Lily was the most brilliant performer they had ever seen. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Mia was playing in her room. She had a fun game to play. She wanted to match her toys with the chairs in the house. Mia was glad because she loved this game.
Mia found a small chair for her teddy bear. She found a big chair for her doll. Then, she saw a very big chair she had never seen before. It was a new chair! Mia was very glad and excited.
But when Mia tried to find a toy to match the very big chair, she found out it was not a chair at all! It was a big box with a gift inside. Mia was so surprised and happy. The gift was a new toy to play with, and it matched the new big chair perfectly.

Once upon a time, there was a big ostrich named Olly. Olly had very wide wings. He liked to run and play with his friends in the big, open field.
One day, Olly was playing with his friends when he tripped and fell. Olly started to cry because it hurt. His friends came to help him.
Olly's friends hugged him and told him it would be okay. They helped him stand up and soon, Olly stopped crying. They all went back to playing and having fun together. And Olly was happy again.

One day, Anna and her dad went to the store. It was a big store with lots of things to buy. Anna saw a novel on the shelf and asked her dad to buy it for her. Her dad looked at her and smiled. He said, "That sounds like a great idea Anna! It looks friendly and I'm sure you will enjoy reading it."
Anna was so excited when they got home. She ran to her room and opened the novel. She found lots of colorful pictures and friendly characters inside. Anna was very happy. She couldn't wait to read the novel. 
Every day, Anna and her dad would sit together and read the novel. Her dad was very patient and friendly and he helped Anna with the words she didn't understand. Anna was very thankful. She thanked her dad for buying the novel, and he hugged her with a smile.

Ben and Mia are twins. They like to play with their toys and books. They have a big room with many stars on the wall. They pretend they are in the universe and fly in a rocket.
One night, they are very sleepy. Mom says it is time for bed. Ben and Mia do not want to sleep. They want to play more. They say no to Mom and run to their room. They close the door and lock it.
Mom knocks on the door and says, "Ben and Mia, open the door. You need to sleep. It is late." Ben and Mia do not listen. They laugh and jump on the bed. They throw their toys and books around. They make a big mess.
Mom gets angry. She tries to open the door, but it is locked. She says, "Ben and Mia, stop this. You are being naughty. Open the door now." Ben and Mia do not care. They kick the door and say, "Go away, Mom. We are in the universe. We do not need to sleep."
Mom gets worried. She calls Dad. Dad comes home and sees the locked door. He says, "Ben and Mia, what are you doing? Open the door. We love you. We want to help you." Ben and Mia do not answer. They are too busy playing.
Dad gets a tool. He breaks the door. He sees Ben and Mia on the bed. They are not playing. They are not laughing. They are not moving. They are very pale and cold. They have a fever. They are sick.
Mom and Dad cry. They hug Ben and Mia. They call the doctor. The doctor comes and says, "Ben and Mia have the flu. They need to go to the hospital. They need medicine. They need rest. They need to sleep."
Ben and Mia do not hear the doctor. They do not see Mom and Dad. They do not feel anything. They are in the universe. They are not coming back.

Once upon a time, in a big garden, there lived a peach and an apple. They were best friends. They loved to play together under the warm sun. One day, the peach said to the apple, "I am sad. I want to be with you always, but we have to separate soon."
The apple was sad too. "I don't want to separate," said the apple. "But I have an idea. Let's ask the wise tree if there is a way we can stay together." So, the two friends went to the wise tree for help.
The wise tree listened to their problem and smiled. "You don't have to be jealous of each other," said the wise tree. "You can be together, but not in the way you think." The wise tree then whispered a secret to them. The apple and the peach looked at each other in surprise. They knew what to do now.
The next day, the apple and the peach rolled down the garden and fell into a big pot. A kind lady found them and made a yummy fruit salad. Now, the apple and the peach were together, just like they wanted. They were happy and not jealous anymore. They knew they would never separate again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Amy loved to play with her toys and look at the pretty light that came through the open window.
One sunny day, Amy's mom said, "Amy, let's leave the house and go to the park!" Amy was so excited! She grabbed her ball and ran outside. The sun was shining and the sky was blue. Amy and her mom walked to the park, holding hands.
At the park, Amy saw a big tree with an open space under it. She ran to the tree and shouted, "Mom, look at the light under the tree!" They played under the tree until it was time to go home. Amy didn't want to leave, but her mom said they could come back another day. They walked home, happy and tired from their fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, in a little house, lived a girl named Lily. She loved to sew with her mom. One day, while they were sewing, they found a special thread. It was shiny and pretty. Lily's mom said, "Be careful, this thread is bitter and can break easily."
Lily wanted to sew with the special thread, but her mom had to rush to the store. She told Lily, "Wait for me to come back before using the thread." But Lily didn't listen. She wanted to sew right away.
Lily started to sew with the bitter thread. It broke again and again. She got angry and threw the thread on the floor. When her mom came back, she saw the broken thread and knew Lily didn't wait. She was sad and said, "Lily, you didn't listen, and now the special thread is gone."
The moral of the story is to always listen to your mom and be patient.

One day, a funny dog named Spot went to the park. He saw a big field and wanted to play. Spot saw a ball and ran to get it.
In the field, there was a big cat. The cat did not like Spot. The cat said, "This is my ball! You can't play with it!" Spot was sad, but he still wanted to play.
Spot tried to get the ball, but the cat got mad. They both wanted the ball, so they fought. In the end, the ball popped and no one could play with it. Spot and the cat were both sad and went home.

One day, a big owl sat in a tree. The owl looked harmless, but it had a plan. It wanted to spoil the fun of the little animals that lived in the woods.
The owl saw a small rabbit hopping by. The rabbit was happy and singing a song. The owl called out, "Hey, rabbit! I am going to spoil your fun!" The rabbit stopped and looked at the owl. "Why do you want to do that?" asked the rabbit.
The owl thought for a moment. It didn't know why it wanted to spoil the fun. The rabbit said, "Let's play together instead!" The owl agreed, and they played all day. The big owl learned that it was much better to have fun with friends than to spoil their fun.

Once upon a time, in a far away land, there was a big temple. The temple had many pretty colors on it. People went there to sing and dance. It was a happy place.
One hot day, the sun was shining very bright. The sun was so hot that it started to melt the pretty colors on the temple. The people were sad, but they had a plan.
A fast boy named Tom ran to the river. He filled a big cup with cold water. He ran back to the temple and threw the water on the colors. The melting stopped! The people sang and danced again. The temple was saved and everyone was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a lively dog named Max. Max lived near a big palace. He loved to run, jump, and catch balls. One day, Max saw a little girl outside the palace. The little girl was sad because she lost her ball.
Max wanted to help the little girl. He said, "Don't be sad. I will find your ball." Max looked all around the palace. He saw the ball near a big tree. Max ran fast and caught the ball in his mouth.
Max brought the ball back to the little girl. She was so happy! She said, "Thank you, Max!" They played catch with the ball. Max and the little girl became best friends. They had lots of fun together near the palace.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went for a walk. She saw a big, red strawberry on the ground. Lucy loved strawberries, so she picked it up. She used her mind to imagine what it would be like to eat it. She thought it would be sweet and yummy.
As she walked, she saw a tiny, harmless bug on the strawberry. The bug was very cute and friendly. Lucy decided to keep the bug as a pet. She named the bug Buddy. Lucy and Buddy became best friends.
Lucy and Buddy went on many fun adventures together. One day, as they played, something unexpected happened. The strawberry started to grow bigger and bigger! Lucy and Buddy were surprised. The big strawberry turned into a magical house for Lucy and Buddy to live in. They were so happy and lived in their strawberry house forever.

Once upon a time, there was a little ant named Andy. Andy lived underground in a big ant hill. He had many friends who lived with him.
One day, Andy wanted to be generous and help his friends. He decided to find food for them. Andy went outside and looked around. He saw a big apple on the ground.
Andy tried to push the apple, but it was too heavy. He didn't give up. He tried again and again. Finally, Andy succeed! He pushed the apple all the way to the underground home.
His friends were very happy. They all shared the big apple and thanked Andy for being so generous. They all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat popcorn. One day, she saw a big bag of popcorn in the kitchen. Lily was very happy and excited.
Lily opened the bag and took a handful of popcorn. She put it in her mouth and started to chew. But the popcorn did not taste good. It was sour! Lily did not like sour popcorn. She felt sad and said, "Yuck! This popcorn is sour!"
Lily's mom heard her and came into the kitchen. She saw the sour popcorn and said, "Oh no, Lily! That is not good popcorn. I will make you some yummy popcorn." Lily's mom made new popcorn that was not sour. Lily tasted it and felt very happy. She said, "Thank you, Mom! This popcorn is so yummy!" And they both enjoyed the good popcorn together.

One day, a dog and a cat were friends. They liked to play with a ball. The dog would pass the ball to the cat, and the cat would pass it back. They had lots of fun.
But one day, the cat was impatient. She did not want to wait for the dog to pass the ball. So, she said, "Hurry up, friend! Pass the ball faster!" The dog felt sad. He did not like it when his friend was not happy.
The dog and cat talked. The dog said, "I will try to pass the ball faster." The cat said, "Thank you, friend." They played with the ball again. The dog passed the ball faster, and the cat was happy. They both had fun and stayed friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She was a pupil in a small school. Lily liked to learn new things and play with her friends.
One day, Lily found a small, fragile key on the ground. She picked it up and showed it to her friends. They all wondered what it could unlock. They decided to look for a lock that the key could open.
They searched the school and found a tiny door. Lily put the key in the lock and turned it. The door unlocked and opened. Inside, they found a room full of toys and games. Lily and her friends played all day and had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to paint. One day, he found a big fridge. The fridge was very sad and upset. Tim wanted to make the fridge happy.
Tim had an idea. He took his paint and started to paint the fridge. He painted it with many colors. Red, blue, yellow, and green. The fridge started to look very pretty. The fridge was not upset anymore. It was happy.
But then, something unexpected happened. The fridge started to talk! "Thank you, Tim!" said the fridge. Tim was very surprised. He did not know that the fridge could talk. Now, Tim and the fridge were the best of friends. They painted more things together and made everything happy.

One day, a girl named Lily wanted to create a new seat for her teddy bear. She found a small box and a soft cloth. Lily put the cloth on the box to make it cozy for her teddy bear. She was very happy with her new seat.
Lily and her teddy bear went to the park to play. They saw a big tree that looked fun to climb. Lily thought it would be a great place for her teddy bear to sit. But the tree was very high and dangerous. Lily's mom told her not to climb it.
Then, something unexpected happened. A big gust of wind blew the teddy bear's seat up into the tree. Lily was sad and didn't know what to do. But just then, a friendly squirrel came by. The squirrel climbed the dangerous tree and brought the seat back down to Lily. She was so happy and thanked the squirrel. From that day on, Lily, her teddy bear, and the squirrel became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Emily. She was three-years-old and loved to eat pizza. One day, Emily was very hungry so she asked her mom for a pizza.
"Okay, Emily," said her mom, "but you have to help me make it."
Emily was so excited! She helped her mom mix the tomato sauce, cheese and dough. Then, they placed it in the oven.
Emily wanted to watch the pizza bake, but she was too small to stand in front of the oven. She stood on her tippy toes and frowned.
For what seemed like forever, Emily waited until the pizza was done. When it finally came out, it looked very heavy. Emily and her mom cut it into slices and ate it together. After, she gave her mom a hug and said happily, "Thank you for the pizza, mom!"

One day, a big fan was in a deep box. A small boy found the box and opened it. He saw the big fan and wanted to play with it. The small boy tried to twist the fan, but it was too hard for him. He called his mom for help.
"Mom, can you help me twist the big fan?" asked the small boy. His mom came and tried to twist the fan too, but it was also hard for her. They both tried together, but the fan would not twist.
A small girl saw them and wanted to help. She had a smart idea. She said, "Let's all twist the fan together!" The small boy, his mom, and the small girl all twisted the fan at the same time. The big fan started to spin fast and cool air filled the room.
The small boy and the small girl became good friends. They learned that working together can solve big problems. And the big fan kept them cool and happy all summer long.

Once upon a time, there was a little ant named Andy. Andy loved to support his family by finding food for their meal. One day, he went out to search for food to bring home.
While Andy was looking for food, he saw a big, successful bird named Benny. Benny was eating a meal that looked very yummy. Andy wanted to share it with his family, but he was too small and scared to ask Benny.
Then, something unexpected happened. Benny saw Andy and said, "Hi, little ant! Would you like to share my meal?" Andy was surprised but happy. With Benny's help, Andy was able to bring the yummy meal back to his family. They all enjoyed the meal together and became good friends with Benny.

Once upon a time, there was a gifted little bird named Blue. Blue could sing very well. All the other birds liked to hear Blue sing.
One day, Blue was flying high in the sky. Suddenly, Blue started to fall. He fell down, down, down, and landed on the soft grass. Blue felt a little scared but was not hurt.
Blue saw a big tree and wanted to touch it. He walked to the tree, reached out his wing, and touched it. Blue was so happy that he started to sing again. All the other birds came to listen and they all had a great time.

Once upon a time, there was a thoughtful cat named Tuna. Tuna was a good cat who loved to help. One day, Tuna saw a little bird who was hungry. Tuna wanted to make the bird happy.
Tuna thought, "I will feed the little bird!" So, Tuna found a small bug and gave it to the bird. The bird was very happy and said, "Thank you, Tuna!"
From that day on, Tuna and the bird were best friends. They played together and helped each other. Everyone thought Tuna was a very thoughtful cat. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Sue found a shoe. It was a very pretty shoe. She liked the shoe and wanted to find its friend. Sue thought, "I will look for the other shoe."
Sue looked under the tree and saw a big, nice cat. The cat had the other shoe! Sue said, "Cat, can I have the shoe, please?" The cat gave the shoe to Sue. Sue said, "Thank you, cat!"
Now Sue had two pretty shoes. She put them on and danced around. She was so happy. The cat watched and smiled. Sue had found her new shoes and made a new friend.

Sally watched the mighty herb as it grew in the sunny garden. She was fascinated with it, so she watched it every day. One morning, she saw something amazing! The herb began growing at an incredible rate and soon it was as tall as Sally! She watched and watched, until it became too big for her garden. Finally, it was a large, mighty herb as tall as a house. Sally was so proud of her herb! She watched it, with a big smile on her face.

One day, Tom and his mom went on vacation. They went to a big park with many trees. Tom saw a tree he wanted to climb. His mom said, "Be careful, Tom!"
Tom started to climb the tree. He went up and up. But then, he saw a big, terrible bug. The bug was very big and very scary. Tom was scared. He cried, "Mom, help me!"
His mom heard Tom and came to help. She said, "Don't be scared, Tom. I will help you." She got a long stick and moved the terrible bug away. Tom climbed down from the tree. He was happy that his mom helped him. They played in the park all day and had a great vacation.

Once upon a time, in a normal little town, there was a rock. This rock was not happy. It would always complain, "I am so small and not fun."
One sunny day, a little girl named Mia found the rock. She thought the rock was very special. Mia took the rock home and painted a smiley face on it. The rock started to feel happy.
From that day on, the rock and Mia played together. They had many fun adventures. The rock never complained again. It was happy to be Mia's friend.

Once there was a mild cat with soft fur. The cat loved to play outside. One day, the cat found a big tree.
The cat spread its legs and jumped up the tree. It went up and up. The cat looked down and saw its friend, the dog, with short fur.
The dog said, "Come down, cat!" The cat spread its legs again and jumped down. They played together all day long. They were very happy.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived an enormous bear and a tiny rabbit. They were good friends and loved to play together. One day, they found a big sheet near the river. The bear and the rabbit were very curious about the sheet.
"Let's unite and make a big tent with this sheet," said the bear. The rabbit agreed and they started to work together. They used sticks and stones to make the tent stand up. Soon, the bear and the rabbit had a nice tent to play in.
Inside the tent, the bear and the rabbit had a lot of fun. They played games, told stories, and ate yummy treats. They were so happy that they had united to make a great tent. And from that day on, the bear and the rabbit became even better friends.

Once upon a time, there was a poor boy named Tom. Tom had a guitar that he loved to play. He played his guitar every day and made beautiful music. But one day, Tom made a big mess in his room and his mom was very mad. She said he could not play his guitar until he cleaned his room.
Tom was sad and did not want to clean his room. He wanted to play with his friends outside. So, he went to play and forgot about his guitar. When Tom came back home, his guitar was gone! He felt regret because he did not listen to his mom.
Tom looked everywhere for his guitar, but he could not find it. Then, he saw his mom holding the guitar. She had cleaned his room and found it under a big pile of toys. Tom was so happy and thanked his mom. From that day on, Tom always listened to his mom and kept his room clean. He learned that it is important to listen and do what is right.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty lamp. This lamp had a special power. When it was turned on, it could increase the size of things.
One day, a little girl named Lily found the pretty lamp in her room. She turned it on and saw her toy car grow bigger and bigger. Lily was very happy and surprised.
She played with her big toy car all day. When it was time for bed, Lily turned off the pretty lamp. The toy car became small again. Lily knew she had a new friend in the pretty lamp, and she couldn't wait to play with it again the next day.

Once upon a time, there was a little mouse. The mouse was very lazy. He liked to sleep all day and eat cheese. One day, the mouse heard a big noise. He was scared.
The mouse saw a big cat. The cat said, "I am going to eat you, little mouse!" The mouse did not want to be eaten. He wanted to escape. So, the mouse ran away as fast as he could. The cat chased the mouse, but the mouse was too fast.
Then, something unexpected happened. The mouse found a big hole. He jumped inside and the cat could not follow him. The cat said, "You are a smart mouse. I will not eat you." And from that day on, the lazy mouse and the big cat became friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She wanted to play with her friends but she didn't know how to ask them to come over. 
Lucy was very embarrassed. She wanted to use her mom's phone to call her friends, but she was scared.
So Lucy decided to be brave. She took a deep breath and asked her mom, "Mommy, please can I use the phone?" Her mom said yes, and Lucy was so excited! 
She called her friends and they came over to play. They had so much fun together. 
At the end of the day, Lucy learned an important lesson: it's ok to be scared sometimes, but it's important to be brave and ask for what you need. So if you ever need help, remember to say please and ask someone you trust.

Once upon a time, in a quiet little town, there lived a boy named Tom. Tom loved to eat cake. One day, he heard about a big, yummy cake at the store. He wanted to order the cake for his birthday. He told his mom about the cake and she said, "We will see."
On his birthday, Tom woke up very happy. He thought about the cake all day. He could not wait for his party. Tom's friends came to his house to celebrate. They played games and had lots of fun. But Tom kept looking around for the big cake.
Finally, it was time for cake. Tom's mom brought out a big box. Tom was so excited. He opened the box and there it was - the big, yummy cake he wanted! His mom had ordered it for him. Tom and his friends ate the cake and it was the best birthday ever.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Susan. She was three years old and loved to explore. One day, she decided to climb a tree to see the world from above.
She climbed and climbed until she had nearly reached the top. But then, she felt a little dizzy. She carefully looked down, but her foot slipped and she fell on the ground.
Susan started to cry. Then, something strange happened. She looked up to the same tree she had climbed and saw a little brown squirrel staring at her.
"Why are you staring at me?" Susan asked.
"You look like you need a friend," the squirrel answered. "Let me help you".
Susan was surprised but grateful. With the squirrel's help, Susan was able to get back up on her feet. She smiled at the squirrel, thanked it, and went on her way.

One day, a little red train was going on a long trip. The train was full of people and things. It went chug-chug-chug, and steam came out of its top. The steam was white and harmless. It did not hurt anyone.
As the train went down the tracks, it saw a big, green tree. The tree was in the way. The train said, "Move, tree! I need to go!" But the tree did not move. It refused to move.
A kind man came to help. He pushed the tree with his strong arms. The tree fell down and the train could go on its way. The train said, "Thank you, kind man!" The man smiled and waved goodbye. The little red train went chug-chug-chug, and everyone was happy.

Lily liked to drink water from her cup. She drank water every day, when she was thirsty, when she was hot, when she was sad. She said water was good for her and made her happy.
One day, Lily saw a big bottle on the table. It was shiny and had a picture of a bird on it. Lily thought it was water, but it was not. It was spirit, a drink for grown-ups. Lily did not know that. She wanted to try it. She climbed on a chair and reached for the bottle.
Mama saw Lily and ran to her. She was scared. She took the bottle from Lily and said, "No, no, no, Lily! This is not water, this is spirit. Spirit is not for you, it is bad for you. It can make you sick and hurt you. You must never drink spirit, only water. Do you understand?"
Lily nodded. She was sorry. She did not want to drink spirit. She wanted to drink water. She hugged Mama and said, "I love you, Mama. I love water. Water is good for me and makes me happy."
Mama smiled and kissed Lily. She said, "I love you, too, Lily. You are a good girl. You are impressive. You are my water girl." She gave Lily her cup and filled it with water. Lily drank water and felt good. She was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big bear named Bob. He was very clumsy. He always dropped things and made a mess. One day, he wanted to cook a yummy soup. He had never cooked before, so he worried a lot.
Bob put a big pot on the stove. He put in water, vegetables, and meat. He stirred the soup with a big spoon. But, being clumsy, he spilled some soup on the floor. Bob felt more worry.
Bob's friend, a little bird named Sue, saw him worry. She said, "Don't worry, Bob! I can help you cook." They cooked the soup together. The soup was very yummy. Bob and Sue ate the soup and laughed. They had a great time, and Bob did not worry anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a big, huge farm. On this farm, there was a little girl named Alice. Every day, Alice would visit the farm.
One day, Alice went to visit the farm and saw something very special. There, in the middle of the field, was a huge white pillow. 
Alice was so excited! She ran over to the pillow and asked: "Who is this for?"
A voice came out from nowhere and said: "This is for you, Alice. Take the pillow and enjoy it." 
Alice was so happy! She skipped back to the farmhouse with her huge pillow. She hugged the pillow and was so excited to show it to her family.
Alice put the pillow in her bedroom and took a nap every night with it. The huge pillow was so comfy and cozy, it was her new favorite thing on the farm!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had many rocks that he liked to play with. One day, he decided he wanted to sell some of his rocks to his friends.
Tim went to his friend, Sam, and said, "Sam, do you want to buy a rock? They are very pretty." Sam looked at the rocks and said, "Yes, I want one! I will give you two cookies for one rock." Tim was happy and gave Sam a rock.
Later, Tim went to his friend, Lily. He told her, "Lily, I am selling rocks. Sam bought one. Do you want one too?" Lily wanted to be the best, so she said, "I will give you three cookies for one rock!" Tim smiled and gave her a rock. All his friends wanted to buy rocks from him, and they all tried to give more cookies than the last friend. They were all having a fun, competitive game. In the end, Tim sold all his rocks and had many cookies to eat.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty butterfly. The butterfly loved to blink its wings. One day, it went on a journey to find a new home.
The butterfly flew and flew. It blinked its wings fast and slow. It saw many pretty flowers on its journey. The butterfly was happy.
But then, the sky became dark. The butterfly was scared. It blinked its wings faster, trying to find a safe place. But the wind blew it away. The butterfly was lost, and its journey had a bad ending.

Once there was a girl named Lily. She loved flowers, especially roses. One day, she saw a beautiful rose in her neighbor's garden. Lily wanted the rose for herself, but she knew it was not hers.
Lily went to her neighbor and said, "Give me the rose, I want it!" Her neighbor was sad because Lily was being selfish. The neighbor said, "Lily, if you ask nicely, I can give you a rose from my garden."
Lily thought about it and said, "Please, can I have a rose?" The neighbor smiled and gave her a pretty rose. Lily learned that it is better to ask nicely and not to be selfish. From that day on, she always asked nicely and shared her flowers with others.

Once upon a time, there was a big house with an enormous chimney. A little mouse named Timmy lived in the house. Timmy liked to play in the chimney, but it was very dirty. He wanted to do something to make it clean.
One day, Timmy saw a big brush in the corner. He thought, "I can use this brush to clean the chimney!" So, Timmy tried to push the brush up the chimney, but it was too heavy. He tried and tried, but he could not do it alone.
Timmy asked his friend, a small bird named Sally, to help him. Together, they pushed the brush up the chimney. It was hard work, but they did it! The chimney was now clean, and Timmy was very happy. From that day on, Timmy and Sally played in the clean chimney and had lots of fun.

One day, a girl named May went to the park. She saw a deep hole in the ground. Next to the hole, she found a pretty rose. May thought the rose was special.
May met a boy named Tim. He saw the rose and said, "Wow! That rose is so pretty! Can I see it?" May said, "Yes, but be careful. I think it's special." Tim took the rose and looked at it. He saw words on the rose. He read, "Put me in the deep hole." They thought it was a fun game.
They put the rose in the hole and waited. A big wind came and the hole grew bigger. The wind took May and Tim into the hole. They fell and fell. They tried to get out, but the hole was too deep. They stayed in the hole forever. The special rose was gone, and they never saw it again.

Once upon a time, there was a polite cat named Lily. Lily loved to settle down in her soft bed and listen to her mom tell stories. One day, Lily's mom told her a story about a magic box that held great value. Lily was very excited and wanted to find the magic box.
The next day, Lily went outside and met her friend, a dog named Max. "Max, let's find the magic box," said Lily. "Okay," said Max, "let's go!" They looked and looked, but they could not find the magic box. They were about to give up when they saw a big tree with a hole in it.
Lily and Max went to the tree and looked inside the hole. They found the magic box! But when they opened it, they found something unexpected. Instead of gold or jewels, it was full of toys and treats for them to share. Lily and Max were so happy that they found the magic box and learned that the true value was in friendship and sharing.

One day, a clumsy fish was swimming around the reef. He bumped into a few rocks as he went and his tail was all bent up. He wanted to find something to replace it, but he couldn't find anything that he liked. Then, he spotted a piece of coral sticking out of the reef. The fish thought it looked perfect! He took the coral and used it to replace his broken tail. Now he was the best looking fish in the reef! He swam back and forth, showing off his new tail for all his friends. From then on, he was never clumsy again!

Once upon a time, there was a big ranch. On the ranch, there were many animals. There was a cow named Daisy, a pig named Pinky, and a dog named Buddy.
One day, Daisy said to Pinky and Buddy, "I found something new! It is cheap and will excite us all!" Pinky and Buddy wanted to see what Daisy found. They all went to see the new thing.
Daisy showed them a big ball. They all played with the ball. They had fun and were very excited. They learned that they didn't need expensive things to have fun. The cheap ball made them happy.
At the end of the day, they sat together and talked. Buddy said, "We had fun today and it didn't cost much. We learned that we don't need lots of money to be happy."
The moral of the story is that you don't need expensive things to have fun and be happy.

Lily loved to paint. She had many colors and brushes. She painted flowers, animals, and houses. Sometimes she painted her family and friends.
One day, she wanted to paint something new. She saw a big truck outside her window. It was red and shiny. It had a loud horn and a big door. Lily wanted to paint the truck.
She took her paper and paints. She went outside. She saw a man working on the truck. He had tools and a hat. He was a mechanic. He fixed the truck when it was broken.
"Hello," Lily said. "Can I paint your truck?"
The mechanic looked at Lily. He smiled. He was a wise man. He knew that Lily liked to paint. He also liked to paint when he was a little boy.
"Sure, you can paint my truck," he said. "But not with your paints. They will wash away. You need special paints for the truck. I have some in my toolbox. Do you want to see?"
Lily nodded. She was happy. She followed the mechanic to his toolbox. He showed her different paints. They were bright and shiny. They did not wash away. They were for the truck.
"Can I use these paints?" Lily asked.
"Yes, you can," the mechanic said. "But you have to be careful. They are sticky and hard to clean. You have to wear gloves and a apron. And you have to ask your mom or dad first. Do you understand?"
Lily understood. She was a good girl. She ran to her house. She asked her mom. Her mom said yes. She gave her gloves and a apron. She also gave her a hug and a kiss.
Lily ran back to the truck. She put on the gloves and the apron. She took a paint and a brush. She thanked the mechanic. She started to paint the truck.
She painted flowers, animals, and houses. She painted her family and friends. She painted the mechanic. She painted herself. She painted with joy and love.
The mechanic watched her. He was proud and happy. He helped her when she needed. He praised her when she finished. He gave her a high five and a sticker.
Lily was proud and happy too. She showed her mom her painting. Her mom was amazed and proud. She took a picture and hung it on the fridge.
Lily hugged the mechanic. She thanked him for letting her paint his truck. She said goodbye. She went inside. She washed her hands and face. She put away her paints and brushes.
She had a wonderful day. She learned something new. She made a new friend. She painted a beautiful truck. She was a happy girl.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to collect rocks. One day, he went outside to find more rocks for his collection.
It was a cold day, and Tim wore a big coat to stay warm. He saw a big rock that looked special. He went closer to see it. "Wow!" Tim said, "This rock looks like gold!" But when he picked it up, he knew it was fake. It was just a shiny rock, not real gold.
Tim's friend, Sam, came to play. Sam saw the shiny rock and said, "That's a cool rock, Tim!" Tim smiled and said, "It's a fake gold rock, but I like it." They both laughed and played with the rocks all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a big yellow cat. The cat liked to play with water. One day, the cat found a water spray. The cat was very happy.
The cat went around and sprayed water on everyone. The cat sprayed water on the dog, the bird, and the little girl. Everyone was wet and sad. They all told the cat to stop, but the cat did not listen.
Then, something unexpected happened. The cat saw a big exit door. The cat thought it would be fun to spray water on the door. But when the cat sprayed the water, the door opened! Behind the door, there was a big party for the cat's friends. The cat felt bad for making everyone wet and sad. The cat learned that it is important to listen to others and be kind. From that day on, the cat never sprayed water on anyone again.

One day, a little girl named Mia was playing in her room. She had a big box of toys. Mia loved her toys, but her favorite was a deep blue ball. She played with it all the time.
One day, Mia's mom said, "Mia, it's time to learn history. We will look at pictures of old things." Mia did not want to learn history. She just wanted to play with her deep blue ball. So, Mia hid the ball under her bed and went to learn history with her mom.
Later, Mia went back to her room to play with her deep blue ball. But she could not find it! She looked under her bed, but it was not there. Mia was very sad. She said, "Mom, I miss my deep blue ball. Can you help me find it?" Her mom said, "Of course, Mia. Let's look for it together."
They looked everywhere in the house, but they could not find the deep blue ball. Finally, they found it in the garden. Mia was so happy! She said, "Thank you, Mom!" From that day on, Mia learned that it was okay to learn history and play with her favorite deep blue ball.

John was a dependable three-year-old. One day, his parents permitted him to bring his basket to the park.
When they arrived, John was delighted. He quickly ran to the swings and then to the slide. 
John's parents watched him, smiling.
John's dad said, "John, what would you like to do next?"
John pointed to the sandbox and said, "I want to put things in the basket and put them in the sandbox!".
John's parents permitted him. So, John filled his basket with rocks, shells, and sticks. He happily dumped them in the sandbox. 
John felt so proud of himself and his dependable basket.

One day, a little girl named Lucy went to the store with her mom. They needed to buy some food for dinner. Lucy saw a sausage that she wanted to eat. "Mom, can we get this sausage?" she asked.
Her mom looked at the sausage and said, "That looks like a regular sausage. I think we can get it for dinner tonight." Lucy was very happy. She could not wait to eat the sausage.
When they got home, Lucy's mom asked her to help with dinner. Lucy wanted to write a note to her dad about the sausage. She found a piece of paper and a pen. She wrote, "Dad, we have sausage for dinner. Love, Lucy." She gave the note to her dad, and he smiled. They all enjoyed the regular sausage for dinner.

Once upon a time, there was a man who liked to build. He built many things like houses and toys. One day, he saw a big tree with delicious red apples. He wanted to get the apples, but the tree was too tall.
The man had an idea. He decided to build a ladder to reach the apples. He worked hard, and soon the ladder was ready. He climbed up and picked the delicious apples. He shared the apples with his friends and family, and they were all very happy.
The moral of the story is that when we work hard and build something good, we can share it with others and make them happy too.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a creative girl named Lily. She loved to draw and make fun things. One day, she found a tiny bug, no bigger than an inch. The bug was green and shiny. Lily wanted to be friends with the bug.
Lily said, "Hi bug! My name is Lily. What's your name?" The bug did not talk, but it moved closer to her. It seemed to urge her to play with it. Lily thought it was cute and she decided to call the bug Inchy.
Lily and Inchy played all day. They went outside and drew pictures on the ground with chalk. Inchy was very creative too, and made a beautiful flower. When it was time to go inside, Lily said, "Inchy, you can stay with me in my room."
Lily made a tiny bed for Inchy and they slept together. From that day on, Lily and Inchy were the best of friends. They urged each other to be more creative and have fun every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little cat named Fluffy. Fluffy liked to talk to her friends. She had a best friend named Tiny, who was a harmless little mouse. They liked to play and have fun together.
One day, Fluffy and Tiny found a big bowl of cream. They were very happy. Fluffy said, "Let's eat the cream!" Tiny agreed, and they started to eat the yummy cream. It was so good that they wanted to eat it all day.
After they ate the cream, Fluffy and Tiny felt full and happy. They talked about how much they loved the cream. Then they played and ran around the yard. Fluffy and Tiny were the best of friends, and they had a great day.

One day, a clean ship was out at sea. The ship had many toys on it. The toys were for kids to play with. The sun was shining and the water was very blue. It was a nice day for the ship.
On the ship, there was a big dog and a small cat. The dog said, "Let's play with the toys!" The cat said, "Yes, let's scatter the toys all over the ship!" So, the dog and the cat took the toys out of the boxes and played with them.
But then, something unexpected happened. A big wave came and hit the ship. The toys, the dog, and the cat all fell into the water. The ship was not clean anymore. The dog and the cat were sad because they lost their toys.
The dog and the cat tried to swim back to the ship, but they could not. The ship sailed away without them. The dog and the cat were left in the water with the toys scattered all around them.
In the end, the dog and the cat could not get back on the ship. They had to find a new home. The kids on the ship did not get to play with the toys. The dog and the cat learned that they should not scatter the toys all over the ship.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max loved to teach little kids how to play ball. One day, Max met a small girl named Lily. Lily wanted to learn how to play ball too. Max was very happy to teach her.
Max and Lily went to the park. Max had a folder with pictures of how to play ball. He showed the folder to Lily. They looked at the pictures together. Lily was excited to learn from Max.
They played ball for a long time. Lily learned very fast. Max was a successful teacher. Lily was happy, and Max was proud. They had a lot of fun and became good friends.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to run and jump and slide. But then Ben fell down from the swing. He hurt his knee and it was bleeding. He cried and cried.
Lily ran to him and said, "Don't cry, Ben. I can heal you. I have a magic band-aid. It is unusual. It makes you feel better quick."
She took out a band-aid from her pocket. It was pink and had stars on it. She put it on Ben's knee and said, "There. Now you are fine. See? The band-aid is magic."
Ben stopped crying and looked at his knee. He smiled and said, "Thank you, Lily. You are a good friend. The band-aid is pretty. Can I keep it?"
Lily nodded and said, "Yes, you can keep it. But we have to go home now. It is getting dark. Come on, let's go."
They held hands and walked to the gate. But then a big dog came out of nowhere. It was angry and barked loud. It ran to them and bit Ben's leg. The band-aid fell off and Ben's knee was bleeding again. He screamed and screamed.
Lily was scared and said, "Help, help! Someone help us! The dog is bad. It hurt Ben. The band-aid did not work. It was not magic."
But no one heard them. The dog kept biting and biting. Lily and Ben were in pain and in fear. They wished they had never played in the park. They wished they had stayed home. They wished they had a real magic band-aid. But they did not. And the dog did not stop. And no one came to help. And it was too late.

Tom was a soldier who liked to drive his big truck. He had a hairy dog named Rex who always went with him. One day, Tom had to go to a far place to help his friends. He took Rex and his truck and drove very fast.
On the way, Tom saw a small cat on the road. He did not want to hit the cat, so he tried to stop his truck. But he was going too fast and could not stop in time. He hit the cat and made it hurt. Rex barked and Tom felt bad.
Tom got out of his truck and picked up the cat. He saw that the cat was bleeding and had a broken leg. He looked for the cat's home, but he could not find it. He decided to take the cat with him and find a vet. He put the cat in his truck and drove on.
But then he heard a loud noise behind him. He looked in his mirror and saw that his truck was on fire. He had forgotten to reverse his truck and it had hit a gas tank. He had made a big mistake. He jumped out of his truck and ran away with Rex and the cat. He was lucky that he was not hurt.
Tom found a vet who helped the cat. He also called his friends and told them what happened. They were not angry with him, but they were worried. They told him to be more careful and to reverse his truck when he parked. Tom learned his lesson. He was sorry for the cat and the truck, but he was happy that he saved the cat and Rex. He gave the cat a new home and a new name: Lucky. He was a good soldier and a good friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim lived in a house with his mom and dad. He had a room upstairs with lots of toys.
One day, Tim saw something disgusting on the floor. It was a big, yucky bug! Tim was scared and yelled, "Mom! There's a bug in my room!" His mom came upstairs and saw the bug too.
"I'll help you, Tim," said his mom. She took a tissue and tried to pull the bug away from Tim's toys. But the bug was very fast and didn't want to leave. Tim and his mom chased the bug around the room. Finally, they caught the bug and took it outside. Tim was happy his room was safe again, and they both went downstairs to eat a snack.

One day, a thin cat named Tom went to play by the lake. He saw a small sail on a toy boat. Tom wanted to play with the toy boat, so he jumped into the lake to get it.
As Tom swam to the toy boat, he started to soak. His fur was very wet. He reached the toy boat and pushed the sail with his paw. The toy boat moved fast on the water.
A little boy named Tim saw Tom and said, "Hey, that's my toy boat!" Tom looked at Tim and said, "I like your toy boat. Can we play together?" Tim smiled and said, "Yes, we can be friends and play with the toy boat." So, Tom and Tim played with the toy boat and had a fun day by the lake.

Once upon a time, there was a modest house. In this house, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily had a birthday coming up. She was very excited.
One day, Lily's mom told her to search for a present. Lily looked everywhere. She looked under the bed, in the closet, and behind the chair. She found a box with a bow on it. Lily thought it was her present.
Lily opened the box and found a toy inside. She played with the toy, but it broke. Lily was sad. Her birthday was not fun. The end.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a little girl named Lucy. Lucy was very intelligent. She loved to work hard and help her mom. One day, Lucy's mom asked her to find her red mitten. It was a very cold day, and she needed it to keep her hands warm.
Lucy looked all around the house for the red mitten. She looked under the bed, behind the door, and even in the toy box. She could not find the mitten anywhere. Then, she saw her dog, Spot, playing with something red in the yard. "Spot, what do you have?" Lucy asked.
Spot wagged his tail and dropped the red mitten at Lucy's feet. Lucy was so happy! She picked up the mitten and said, "Thank you, Spot! You are an intelligent dog!" She ran back inside the house and gave the red mitten to her mom.
Her mom smiled and said, "You did a great job, Lucy! You worked hard to find my mitten. Now we can go outside and play in the snow together." Lucy and her mom put on their mittens and had a fun day playing in the snow with Spot.

Once upon a time, there were two kids named Chloe and John. It was a Wednesday and, as usual, Chloe wanted to eat something sweet.
Chloe asked her Mom, "Mom, can I have something sweet?" Her Mom said, "Chloe, we don't have anything sweet at home. We could go to the store, but candy is very expensive!"
Chloe was disappointed, but then John had a good idea. He said, "Mom, why don't we go to the park? I know there are many trees with tasty fruit!"
Chloe and John went to the park and they tasted the fruit. Chloe and John loved the sweet taste, and they both had a great time!

Once upon a time, in a pretty little house, there lived a happy cat. The cat loved to play with its soft ball. One day, the cat wanted to display its ball to its friend, the dog.
The cat rolled its ball to the dog and gave a big wink. The dog was very happy and wagged its tail. They played with the ball all day long, running and jumping in the warm sun.
At the end of the day, the cat and the dog were tired but happy. They sat down under a big tree and shared a yummy treat. The cat gave another wink, and they knew they would be best friends forever.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play in their dad's lab. Their dad is a scientist. He makes new things with his tools and machines.
One day, Lily and Ben find a big bowl of white stuff on the table. It looks like snow, but it is not cold. Lily wants to touch it. Ben wants to stir it.
"Can we play with it?" Lily asks.
"I don't know. Maybe it is for dad's work." Ben says.
"Let's try it. It looks fun." Lily says.
She puts her hand in the bowl. The white stuff is soft and smooth. She makes a ball with it. Ben takes a spoon and stirs the white stuff. It makes a funny sound. He makes a hole with it.
They have fun with the white stuff. They make shapes and letters and animals. They laugh and giggle.
But then, something happens. The white stuff starts to bubble and fizz. It grows bigger and bigger. It spills out of the bowl. It covers the table and the floor and the chairs.
"Oh no! What did we do?" Lily says.
"Run!" Ben says.
They run out of the lab. They see their dad coming. He looks angry.
"What did you do to my experiment?" he says.
"We are sorry, dad. We just wanted to play." Lily and Ben say.
They hug their dad. He hugs them back.
"It's okay. I love you. But next time, don't touch anything in my lab. It can be dangerous." he says.
"Okay, dad. We promise." Lily and Ben say.
They help their dad clean up the mess. They learn a new word: polymer. They say it is a hard word. They prefer to call it the white stuff.

Once upon a time there lived a small musician. He lived near the sea and each night he used to sing sweet tunes. One night he decided to go sailing in his boat. He sailed out to the sea and the stars twinkled in the night sky.
He sang a gentle melody while he sailed and eventually he stopped the boat to rest and take in the beauty of the night. Suddenly he heard a voice singing back to him. It was small but clear and he wondered who it could be.
He called out, "Who is out there?"
A tiny voice replied, "I'm a small fish and I love to listen to your singing."
The musician was delighted and continued playing his music. He and the small fish sang together until the sun came up and then they said goodbye. The small musician sailed back home, his heart full of joy.

Once upon a time, there was a little bug named Bobby. Bobby lived in a small cell under a big tree. He had many friends in the forest. Bobby loved to play with his friends, but sometimes he liked to delay going home.
One day, Bobby was playing with his friend, Sally the squirrel. They were having an easy and fun time. But then, it started to get late. Sally said, "Bobby, we should go home now. It's getting dark."
Bobby didn't want to stop playing, so he said, "Can we delay going home, please? Just a little more time?"
Sally didn't think it was a good idea. She said, "No, Bobby. It's not safe to be out when it's dark. Let's go home now."
Bobby was sad, but he knew Sally was right. They said goodbye and went to their homes. Bobby learned that sometimes it's better not to delay, especially when it's for safety. From that day on, Bobby always went home before it got dark, and he still had lots of fun with his friends.
